eng: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to Jesus. In order to test him they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
local: Yesu talɩ Magaadaɣ peeɖe lɛ, Farɩsɩ ñɩma nɛ Saduki ñɩma powolo pañɩ ɛ-nɔɔ cɔlɔ tɔm. Pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se ɛla ɛsɔdaa hama lakasɩ nasɩyɩ nɛ pana.

eng: But he replied, At evening you say, It will be nice weather because the sky is bright red.
local: Peeɖe ecosi-wɛ se: «Wɩsɩ kɔŋ se sɩɖʋʋ lɛ, ɩtɔŋ se: ‹Alɩwaatʋ wɛɣ camɩyɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛsɔdaa sɛwa.›.

eng: And in the morning you say, There will be bad weather today because the sky is cloudy. You know how to make sense of the skys appearance. But you are unable to recognize the signs that point to what the time is.
local: Tanaŋ cu lɛ, ɩtɔŋ se: ‹Sɔnɔ tɛʋ nɩɣ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛsɔdaa sɛwa nɛ pʋwɛɛ yulee.› Ɩsɩm ɛsɔdaa wɛtʋ tɔbʋʋ lɩzʋʋ, ɛlɛ́, ɩɩpɩzɩɣ se ɩlɩzɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ lakɩ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ pʋbʋ ñɩŋgʋ.

eng: An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign. But it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign. Then he left them and went away.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɛyáa kɛ azaɣ́a, peyeki Ɛsɔ nɛ poluŋuu wondu nɛ ɖoŋ lɛɛŋ tɛɛ. Pañɩnɩɣ se pala ɛsɔdaa hama lakasɩ nasɩyɩ nɛ pana. Ɛlɛ́, paakaɣ yʋsaɣ nakɛyɛ naʋ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ náyʋ Yoonaasɩ ñɩŋga.» Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu ɖɛɛ eyebi-wɛ.

eng: When the disciples arrived on the other side of the lake, they had forgotten to bring bread.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɛtɛzaɣ teŋgu wayɩ nɛ paba sɔɔ tindine yɔɔ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees. .
local: Peeɖe ɛpaɣ-wɛ se: «Ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ ɖeu nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma nɛ Saduki ñɩma pɔ-sɔsɔɔna.».

eng: They discussed this among themselves and said, We didnt bring any bread.
local: Ɛlɛ́, papazɩ ɖama yɔɔdɩnaʋ se: «Yaa ɖɩtakpaɣ tindine yɔɔ ɛkalɩɣ-ɖʋ ḿbʋ́?».

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, You people of weak faith! Why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you dont have any bread?.
local: Yesu nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Lidaʋ ñɩɩɩ tɩnaa mɩ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩyɔɔdʋʋnɩ ɖama se ɩtakpaɣ tindine?.

eng: Dont you understand yet? Dont you remember the five loaves that fed the five thousand and how many baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ ɩɩnɩɣ? Ɩɩtasɩɣ tɔzʋʋ tindine hɔɔlasɩ kagbaanzɩ nzɩ́ pacalɩnɩ ɛyáa kutokiŋ kagbaanzɩ nɛ ɖɔkɩŋ weyi pɩkazaa nɛ ɩkpaɣ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ yaa we?.

eng: And the seven loaves that fed the four thousand and how many large baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Nɛ tindine hɔɔlasɩ lʋbɛ nzɩ́ pacalɩnɩ ɛyáa kutokiŋ naanza nɛ ɖɔkɩŋ weyi pɩkazaa nɛ ɩkpaɣ yɔ, ɩɩtɔzʋʋ pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ yaa we?.

eng: Dont you know that I wasnt talking about bread? But be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees. .
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ ɩtatɩɩnɩda se pɩtɛkɛ tindine tɔm mɔyɔɔdaɣnɩ-mɩ? Meheyi-mɩ se ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma nɛ Saduki ñɩma pɔ-sɔsɔɔna.».

eng: Then they understood that he wasnt telling them to be on their guard for yeast used in making bread. No, he was telling them to watch out for the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Peeɖe panɩwa se pɩtɛkɛ sɔsɔɔna wena palakɩnɩ tindine yɔ, ana eheyaɣ-wɛ se pɔ́ɖɔ́kɩ pa-tɩ nɛ yɛ. Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ Farɩsɩ ñɩma nɛ Saduki ñɩma pawɩlaɣ yɔ, eheyaɣ-wɛ se pɔ́ɖɔ́kɩ pa-tɩ nɛ pʋ.

eng: Now when Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, Who do people say the Human One is? .
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa powolo Sezaaree Filiipu hɔɔlʋʋ taa. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛyáa tɔŋ se Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ ma, ma lɛ anɩ?».

eng: They replied, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the other prophets.
local: Pocosi-i se: «Nabɛyɛ tɔŋ se ña lɛ Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ. Lɛlaa se ña lɛ Elii weyi paaɖaŋaa yɔ nɛ lɛlaa se ña lɛ Yereemii yaa náyaa taa lɛlʋ.».

eng: He said, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? .
local: Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Mɩ yɔ, mɩhʋʋ se ma lɛ anɩ?».

eng: Simon Peter said, You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ cosi-i se: «Ña lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ. Ña lɛ Ɛsɔ wezuu tʋ Pɩyalʋ.».

eng: Then Jesus replied, Happy are you, Simon son of Jonah, because no human has shown this to you. Rather my Father who is in heaven has shown you.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-i se: «Yoonaasɩ pɩyalʋ Siimɔɔ! Ŋhiɣ ña-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ ɛyʋ nɔɔyʋ heyini-ŋ-tʋ. Ɛlɛ́, man-Caa weyi ɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, ɛwɩlɩnɩ-ŋ-tʋ.

eng: I tell you that you are Peter. And Ill build my church on this rock. The gates of the underworld wont be able to stand against it.
local: Ma lɛ, meheyiɣ-ŋ se: ña mayaɣ se Pɩyɛ. Pɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-yɔɔ manɖʋʋ mɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ. Sɩm maɣmaɣ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ pɩlabɩ-ɖɩ pʋyʋ.

eng: Ill give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Anything you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. Anything you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven. .
local: Mɔnkɔŋ-ŋ cɛlʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ nɔnɔɔ ñɩma: mbʋ́ ŋkiziɣ se pataala tataa cɩnɛ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kiziɣ se pataalabɩ-pʋ ɖɔɖɔ, nɛ mbʋ́ ŋtisiɣ se pala tataa cɩnɛ yɔ, etisiɣ se palabɩ-pʋ ɖɔɖɔ.».

eng: Then he ordered the disciples not to tell anybody that he was the Christ.
local: Peeɖe Yesu paɣ́ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se pataala nɛ nɔɔyʋ nɩɩ se ɛnʋ lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ.

eng: From that time Jesus began to show his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and legal experts, and that he had to be killed and raised on the third day.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ Yesu pazɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa yɔɔdɩnaʋ nɛ ɛkaɖɩ-wɛ se pʋwɛɛ se ewolo Yeruusalɛm nɛ ɛtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ Yuda ñɩma sɔsaa nɛ pɔ-cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ pa-paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa pe-nesi taa nɛ pakʋ-ɩ nɛ ɛsɩ nɛ kɩyakɩŋ naadozo wiye lɛ, Ɛsɔ fezi-i.

eng: Then Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him: God forbid, Lord! This wont happen to you.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ ya-ɩ nɛ ɛkɛsɩ-ɩ kɩjaʋ nɛ ɛkalɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ɛsɔ huuzuu ñɔ-yɔɔ. Nabʋyʋ ɛɛlakɩ-ŋ kaʋ!».

eng: But he turned to Peter and said, Get behind me, Satan. You are a stone that could make me stumble, for you are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, ekisini Pɩyɛ yɔɔ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Satanɩ, ɖɛɛ mɔ-yɔɔ! Ŋcaɣ se ŋtuuli-m ma-nʋmɔʋ taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ña-lɩmaɣza tɛkɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩna, ɛyáa ñɩna akɛnaa.».

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu heyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Weyi e-liu wɛɛ se ɛtɩŋɩɣ ma-wayɩ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛsɔɔ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-yɔɔ, etisi nɛ ɛtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ .

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛñɩnɩɣ se ɛya ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-ñʋʋ yɔ, ɛtɛŋnɩ-kʋ lesuu. Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ sɩkɩ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ehikiɣ wezuu.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives? What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Yee ɛyʋ ehiɣ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ñɩm tɩŋa payɩ nɛ elesi e-wezuu lɛ, pʋwazɩɣ-ɩ we? Ɛbɛ pʋdʋ pɩzɩɣ ɛkpaɣ nɛ ɛlɛɣzɩ ku-lone taa?.

eng: For the Human One is about to come with the majesty of his Father with his angels. And then he will repay each one for what that person has done.
local: Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ nɛ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa pɛkpɛndɩɣnɩ ɛ-Caa Ɛsɔ koboyaɣ nɛ pɔkɔɔ nɛ ɛhɛyɩ paa weyi mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see the Human One coming in his kingdom. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se: mbá pɛwɛ cɩnɛ yɔ, pa-taa lɛlaa naɣ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ ma alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mɔnkɔŋ mɔntɔɔ kewiyaɣ yɔ nɛ pʋcɔ pasɩ.».

eng: Boarding a boat, Jesus crossed to the other side of the lake and went to his own city.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu kpa kpɩyʋʋ taa nɛ ɛtɛzɩɣ teŋgu se ɛpɩsɩ ɛ-tɛ.

eng: People brought to him a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man who was paralyzed, Be encouraged, my child, your sins are forgiven. .
local: Ɛtalaa lɛ, ɛnaɣ ɛyáa nabɛyɛ, powokaɣnɩ kalɩkalaɣ nakɛyɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ, kɛhɛnɩ ka-ñalʋʋ yɔɔ. Powokaɣnɩ-kɛ se ɛla nɛ kɔ-yɔɔ cɛ. Ɛnawa se ɛyáa ḿbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ lɛ, eheyi kalɩkalaɣ ŋ́gá se: «Man-taabalʋ! Đɔkɩ ña-tɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, menkpeɣ-ŋ ña-kɩwɛɛkɩm.».

eng: Some legal experts said among themselves, This man is insulting God.
local: Peeɖe paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nabɛyɛ yɔɔdaɣnɩ ɖama se: «Abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛwɛɛkɩɣ Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ!».

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, Why do you fill your minds with evil things?.
local: Yesu na pa-lɩmaɣza ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩwɛnɩ lɩmaɣza kɩdɛkɛdɛɛ ḿbʋ́?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Yee meheyi kalɩkalaɣ ŋ́gá se menkpeɣ-kɛ ka-kɩwɛɛkɩm yaa kakʋyɩ kɔɖɔ, tɩ-naalɛ tɩ-taa lɛ, ndʋ́ tɩfɛyɩ kaɖɛ?.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins —he said to the man who was paralyzed— Get up, take your cot, and go home. .
local: Mancaɣ mawɩlɩ-mɩ se Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ wɛnɩ ɖoŋ se ekpeɣ ɛyáa pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ.» Peeɖe eheyi kalɩkalaɣ ŋ́gá se: «Ña! Kʋyɩ ŋkpaɣ ña-ñalʋʋ nɛ ŋkpe.».

eng: The man got up and went home.
local: Abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛkʋyɩ nɛ ekpe ɛ-tɛ.

eng: When the crowds saw what had happened, they were afraid and praised God, who had given such authority to human beings.
local: Ɛyáa sakɩyɛ mbá paawɛɛ yɔ pana ḿbʋ́ lɛ, sɔɔndʋ kpa-wɛ nɛ pasa Ɛsɔ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛha ɛyáa ɖoŋ ɛnʋ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: As Jesus continued on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. He said to him, Follow me, and he got up and followed him.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tasɩ wobu nɛ ɛsɩndaa nɛ ɛna abalʋ weyi payaɣ se Maatiyee yɔ, ɛcaɣ tikee ɖɩmʋyɛ. Peeɖe Yesu heyi-i se: «Kʋyɩ ŋtɩŋ-m!» Ɛ́lɛ́ kʋyɩ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ ɛtɩŋ-ɩ.

eng: As Jesus sat down to eat in Matthews house, many tax collectors and sinners joined Jesus and his disciples at the table.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yesu wolo ɖɩɣa nɛ ɛcaɣ tɔɔnaɣ ɖɩtɔɔyɛ. Tikee mʋyaa sakɩyɛ nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa lɛlaa paawɛ peeɖe nɛ pa nɛ ɩ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɔtɔkɩ tɔɔnaɣ.

eng: But when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Ŋgʋ́, Farɩsɩ ñɩma nabɛyɛ kana-wɛ ḿbʋ́. Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ mɩ-caa nɛ tikee mʋyaa nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa lɛlaa pɛkpɛndɩ nɛ pɔtɔkɩ?».

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Yesu nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ecosi-wɛ se: «Pɩtɛkɛ alaafɩya tɩnaa pʋpɔzʋʋ se powolo fɛɖaa tɛ, kʋdɔndɩnaa pʋpɔzʋʋ se powolo.

eng: Go and learn what this means: I want mercy and not sacrifice . I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners. .
local: Iwolo ɩñɩnɩ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa tɔm ndʋ́ pamawa yɔ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Ma Ɛsɔ mɔntɔsɔɔlɩ mɩ-kɩlaŋ, ɛlɛ́, me-liu wɛɛ se ɩna ɖama pʋtɔdɩyɛ.› » Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Pɩtɛkɛ mbá pamaɣzɩɣ se pɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzaa yɔ pa-wayɩ mɔnkɔma. Ɛlɛ́, mbá pasɩm pa-tɩ se pɛkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa yɔ pa-wayɩ mɔnkɔm se pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ.».

eng: At that time Johns disciples came and asked Jesus, Why do we and the Pharisees frequently fast, but your disciples never fast.
local: Yesu tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa wolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ-ɩ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ɖa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma ɖɩhɔkʋʋ nɔɔ tam nɛ ñɔ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ñaahɔkʋʋ?».

eng: Jesus responded, The wedding guests cant mourn while the groom is still with them, can they? But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then theyll fast.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Ɩmaɣzɩɣ se mbá paya-wɛ halɩkpaɣyɛ kazandʋ ɖeɖe yɔ, powoki pacaɣnɩ ɛsɛ ñɩŋɩyɛ nɛ pɔhɔkɩ nɔɔ halʋ kɩfalʋ walʋ ɛsɩndaa yaa we? Pɩɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́! Ɛlɛ́, pɔkɔŋ lɩzʋʋ walʋ ɛnʋ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ. Tɩ-taa pɔkɔŋ nɔɔ hɔkʋʋ.».

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes because the patch tears away the cloth and makes a worse tear.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Paakpakɩɣ pɩsaʋ kɩfalʋʋ hɔɔlaɣ nɛ pɔtɔnɩ wonuu kɩbɩnʋʋ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, pɔcɔtʋʋ lɛ, hɔɔlaɣ ŋgá kasʋʋ nɛ kɔhɔ kɩbɩnʋʋ nɛ kɩtɛzɩ cɩyʋʋ.».

eng: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the wineskins would burst, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined. Instead, people pour new wine into new wineskins so that both are kept safe. .
local: Ɛtasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ se: «Paasuu sʋlʋm pɛŋ weyi ɩtɔwaa yɔ ɩ-taa. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, pʋkɔŋ se pɩpɩɣ yɔ, pɩyasɩɣ-ɩ nɛ sʋlʋm wɛɛkɩ pikpe tataa yem. Ɛlɛ́, pɛŋ weyi ɩtɔtɔɔ yɔ, ɩ-taa posuu nɛ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋwɛɛ pi-ɖeɖe camɩyɛ.».

eng: While Jesus was speaking to them, a ruler came and knelt in front of him, saying, My daughter has just died. But come and place your hand on her, and shell live.
local: Yesu yɔɔdaɣnɩ Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yuda ñɩma sɔsɔ nɔɔyʋ wolo eluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Mɛn-pɛlɛ sɩba lɛɛlɛɛyɔ. Ɛlɛ́, kɔɔ ɖiwolo nɛ ŋtɩnɩ ñe-nesi ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ efe.».

eng: So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him.
local: Peeɖe Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pakʋyaa patɩŋ-ɩ.

eng: Then a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and touched the hem of his clothes.
local: Pɛɖɛɣaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, halʋ nɔɔyʋ wobini Yesu wayɩ nɛ etukuni e-toko ndomiye. Halʋ ɛnʋ, calɩm kɛwɛɛ pikpeŋni-i nɛ pɩcɛyɩɣ-ɩ pɩŋŋ pɩnzɩ hiu nɛ naalɛ yɔ.

eng: She thought, If I only touch his robe Ill be healed.
local: Etukuni-i ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛɖʋ ɛ-taa se: «Yee manpɩzaa mehiɣ e-toko nɛ montukuna yɔ, mɔ-yɔɔ cɛkɩ.».

eng: When Jesus turned and saw her, he said, Be encouraged, daughter. Your faith has healed you. And the woman was healed from that time on.
local: Ɛlabɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yesu kisi nɛ ɛna-ɩ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Mɛn-pɛlɛ! Đɔkɩ ña-tɩ! Lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ŋwɛnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, kɩlɩzɩ-ŋ pɩ-taa.» Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, halʋ yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: When Jesus went into the rulers house, he saw the flute players and the distressed crowd.
local: Yesu ɖɛwa nɛ ɛtalɩ Yuda ñɩma sɔsɔ ɛnʋ ɛ-tɛ lɛ, ɛmaɣnɩ hɩsɩ hʋlɩyaa nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ posuni ɖɩɣa nɛ pamakɩ kubusi.

eng: He said, Go away, because the little girl isnt dead but is asleep ; but they laughed at him.
local: Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩlɩɩ cɩnɛ, pɛlɛ ɛnʋ eɖou kuɖou, ɛtasɩ kɩsɩbʋ.» Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, powoŋ-i.

eng: After he had sent the crowd away, Jesus went in and touched her hand, and the little girl rose up.
local: Palɩzɩ ɛyáa awayɩ lɛ, Yesu sʋʋ kuduyuu tɛɛ nɛ ɛ́ɖɔ́kɩ pɛlɛ ɛnʋ e-nesi taa nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ kʋyɩ.

eng: News about this spread throughout that whole region.
local: Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩcɔɔ egeetiye ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa payɩ.

eng: As Jesus departed, two blind men followed him, crying out, Show us mercy, Son of David.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu kʋyɩ Yuda ñɩma sɔsɔ ɛnʋ ɛ-tɛ se ekpeŋ. Ekpeŋaɣ lɛ, yʋlʋmaa naalɛ nabɛyɛ tɩŋɩɣ ɛ-wayɩ nɛ pamakɩ kubusi se: «Wiyaʋ Daaviidi Sayɩ! Na ɖa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ.».

eng: When he came into the house, the blind men approached him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe I can do this? Yes, Lord, they replied.
local: Ɛtalɩ ɖɩɣa lɛ, yʋlʋmaa ḿbá powolo ɛ-cɔlɔ. Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ se manpɩzɩɣ malabɩ-mɩ mbʋ́ mi-liu wɛɛ yɔ yaa we?» Paba cosi-i se: «Ɛɛɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ ŋpɩzɩɣ.».

eng: Then Jesus touched their eyes and said, It will happen for you just as you have believed. .
local: Peeɖe etukuni pɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Pɩla ɛzɩ ɩwɛnaʋ lidaʋ yɔ.».

eng: Their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly warned them, Make sure nobody knows about this. .
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɛ-ɛsɛ kuli. Ɛlɛ́ ɛka pa-ñʋŋ taa se: «Ɩkpa mɩ-tɩ ɖeu, ɩtaaheyi-tʋ nɔɔyʋ.».

eng: But they went out and spread the word about him throughout that whole region.
local: Patɩlɩ peeɖe lɛ, pamaanɩ Yesu tɔm yɔɔ nɛ pɔcɔɔ pe-egeetiye tɩŋa taa payɩ nɛ pasaŋ-ɩ.

eng: As they were leaving, people brought to him a man who was demon-possessed and unable to speak.
local: Yʋlʋmaa ḿbá pɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, nɔɔyʋ woni abalʋ nɔɔyʋ Yesu cɔlɔ. Eleeu nɔɔyʋ wɛ ɛ-wayɩ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ-ɩ kaayɔɔdaɣ.

eng: When Jesus had thrown out the demon, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed and said, Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
local: Yesu ɖɔɔnɩ eleeu ɛnʋ nɛ abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛpazɩ yɔɔdʋʋ nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ yɩɣyɩɣ-ɩ nɛ kɔtɔŋ se: «Đooo yɔ, nabʋyʋ talabɩdɩ Izrɛɛlɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa ɛzʋnɔ!».

eng: But the Pharisees said, He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Peeɖe Farɩsɩ ñɩma tɔm se: «Aleewaa caa hanɩ-ɩ ɖoŋ nɛ ɛɖɔɔnʋʋnɩ aleewaa.».

eng: Jesus traveled among all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, announcing the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness.
local: Yesu cɔwaɣ tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ nɛ cikpetu patɩŋa pa-taa nɛ eheyiɣ ɛyáa Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa nɛ ɛlakɩ nɛ kʋdɔndɩnaa ndɩ ndɩ nɛ ajamaa ndɩ ndɩ patɩŋa pɔ-yɔɔ cɛkɩ.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion for them because they were troubled and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
local: Ɛna ɛyáa samaɣ lɛ, pɩlabɩ-ɩ pʋtɔdɩyɛ nɛ kɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩɩkɩlɩ-wɛ nɛ pekiitiɣ yem ɛzɩ heŋ weyi ɩfɛyɩnɩ ninɖiɣyu yɔ.

eng: Then he said to his disciples, The size of the harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers.
local: Peeɖe eheyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Kʋmtʋ ɖɔwa, ɛlɛ́, kʋnɖaa tɔɖɔɔ.

eng: Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest. .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, itendi tɩ-tʋ nɛ ɛsɔzɩ kʋnɖaa sakɩyɛ nɛ pasɩnɩ-mɩ kʋm.».

eng: A record of the ancestors of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham:.
local: Yesu Krɩstʋ weyi ɛ-cɔzɔ lɛ Daaviidi nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ cɔzɔ lɛ Abraham yɔ, ɛ-cɔzɔnaa hɩla yɔ:.

eng: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
local: Abraham lʋlɩ Izaakɩ, Izaakɩ ñalʋlɩ Yakɔɔbʋ, Yakɔɔbʋ ñalʋlɩ Yuda nɛ ɛ-ɖalaa nɛ e-newaa.

eng: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Aram.
local: Yuda ñalʋlɩ Faarɛɛzɩ nɛ Zaaraa. Payaɣ po-ɖoo se Tamaarɩ. Faarɛɛzɩ ñalʋlɩ Ɛsrɔm, Ɛsrɔm ñalʋlɩ Aram.

eng: Aram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
local: Aram ñalʋlɩ Aminaadaabɩ, Aminaadaabɩ ñalʋlɩ Naasɔɔnɩ, Naasɔɔnɩ ñalʋlɩ Salɩmɔɔ.

eng: Salmon was the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse.
local: Salɩmɔɔ ñalʋlɩ Boozi. Payaɣ Boozi ɛnʋ e-ɖoo se Rahaabɩ. Boozi ñalʋlɩ Obɛɛdɩ. Payaɣ ɛ́lɛ́ ɖoo se Ruti. Obɛɛdɩ ñalʋlɩ Yesee.

eng: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been the wife of Uriah.
local: nɛ Yesee ñalʋlɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Daaviidi. Daaviidi ñalʋlɩ Salomɔɔ. Salomɔɔ ɛnʋ e-ɖoo kɛkɛ sɔɔja Urii halʋ.

eng: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asaph.
local: Salomɔɔ ñalʋlɩ Roboam, Roboam ñalʋlɩ Abiya, Abiya ñalʋlɩ Asafʋ.

eng: Asaph was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram. Joram was the father of Uzziah.
local: Asafʋ ñalʋlɩ Yozafatɩ, Yozafatɩ ñalʋlɩ Yoram, Yoram ñalʋlɩ Oziyaasɩ.

eng: Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
local: Oziyaasɩ ñalʋlɩ Yoatam, Yoatam ñalʋlɩ Ahazɩ, Ahazɩ ñalʋlɩ Ezekiyasɩ.

eng: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amos. Amos was the father of Josiah.
local: Ezekiyasɩ ñalʋlɩ Manaasee, Manaasee ñalʋlɩ Amɔɔnɩ, Amɔɔnɩ ñalʋlɩ Yoziyaasɩ.

eng: Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers. This was at the time of the exile to Babylon.
local: Yoziyaasɩ ñalʋlɩ Yekooniyaasɩ nɛ e-newaa. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa pɛkɛzɩ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma tɛtʋ Babilooni.

eng: After the exile to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
local: Pʋbʋ wayɩ Yekooniyaasɩ ñalʋlɩ Salaatɩyɛlɩ, Salaatɩyɛlɩ ñalʋlɩ Zorobaabɛɛlɩ.

eng: Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
local: Zorobaabɛɛlɩ ñalʋlɩ Abiyudi, Abiyudi ñalʋlɩ Eliyaakim, Eliyaakim ñalʋlɩ Azɔɔrɩ.

eng: Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.
local: Azɔɔrɩ ñalʋlɩ Sadɔɔkɩ, Sadɔɔkɩ ñalʋlɩ Akim, Akim ñalʋlɩ Eliyudi.

eng: Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
local: Eliyudi ñalʋlɩ Eleyazaarɩ, Eleyazaarɩ ñalʋlɩ Maataɣ, Maataɣ ñalʋlɩ Yakɔɔbʋ.

eng: Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary—of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Christ.
local: nɛ Yakɔɔbʋ ñalʋlɩ Yozɛɛfʋ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ Maarɩɩ. Maarɩɩ ɛnʋ ɛlʋlɩnɩ Yesu weyi payaɣ se Krɩstʋ yɔ, pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ.

eng: So there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen generations from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen generations from the exile to Babylon to the Christ.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́, pɩkpaɣʋnɩ Abraham nɛ puwolo pɩsɩɩnɩ Daaviidi yɔ, cɔzɔnaa wɛ hiu nɛ naanza. Pɩkpaɣʋnɩ Daaviidi alɩwaatʋ nɛ puwolo pɩsɩɩnɩ ndʋ́ tɩ-taa pɛkɛzɩ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma tɛtʋ Babilooni yɔ, paba ñɛwɛ hiu nɛ naanza ɖɔɖɔ. Pɩkpaɣʋnɩ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ nɛ puwolo pɩsɩɩnɩ Krɩstʋ yɔ, cɔzɔnaa wɛ hiu nɛ naanza ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: This is how the birth of Jesus Christ took place. When Mary his mother was engaged to Joseph, before they were married, she became pregnant by the Holy Spirit.
local: Ɩna ɛzɩma pɩtɩŋaa nɛ palʋlɩ Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔ. Maarɩɩ weyi ɛlʋlɩ-ɩ yɔ, ɛɛkɛ pɛlɛ weyi posu Yozɛɛfʋ yɔ. Ɛ nɛ ɩ pataacaɣ wiɖiyi. Ɛlɛ́, Maarɩɩ haɣ howa nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖoŋ.

eng: Joseph her husband was a righteous man. Because he didnt want to humiliate her, he decided to call off their engagement quietly.
local: Yozɛɛfʋ ɛnʋ, ɛkɛ ɛyʋ kʋtʋʋzʋ. Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ ɛna-ɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛtɔsɔɔlɩ se ɛɖʋ-ɩ fɛyɛ ɛyáa hɛkʋ taa. Ɛlɛ́, ɛɖʋ se ekiziɣ-i mɛsaɣ tɛɛ.

eng: As he was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, Joseph son of David, dont be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the child she carries was conceived by the Holy Spirit.
local: Ɛwɛɛ ɛmaɣzɩɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ lɩɩ ɛ-yɔɔ ɖoziye taa nɛ eheyi-i se: «Daaviidi sayɩ Yozɛɛfʋ! Sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-ŋ nɛ Maarɩɩ kpaɣʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖoŋ ɛhaɣnɩ howa ŋ́gá.

eng: She will give birth to a son, and you will call him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.
local: Ɛlʋlʋʋ pɩɣa ŋ́gá lɛ, ŋyaa-kɛ se Yesu, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ŋ́gá kɔŋnɩ ɛyáa ñʋʋ yabʋ nɛ kalɩzɩ-wɛ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm taa.».

eng: Now all of this took place so that what the Lord had spoken through the prophet would be fulfilled:.
local: Pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩlaba ḿbʋ́ se tɔm ndʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ katɩŋnɩ náyʋ nɔɔyʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ yɔ, tɩla. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se:.

eng: Look! A virgin will become pregnant and give birth to a son, And they will call him, Emmanuel.
local: «Ɩnaɣ, pɛlɛ nɔɔyʋ kɔŋ haɣʋ nɛ ɛlʋlɩ pɩɣa nɛ payaa-kɛ se Emaanʋyɛɛlɩ.» Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se Ɛsɔ wɛ ɖɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: When Joseph woke up, he did just as an angel from God commanded and took Mary as his wife.
local: Yozɛɛfʋ fema lɛ, ɛkpaɣ Maarɩɩ ɛzɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ tiyiyu heyuu-i yɔ.

eng: But he didnt have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. Joseph called him Jesus.
local: Ɛlɛ́, etotukini-i nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛlʋlɩ ɛ-pɩɣa yɔ. Ɛlʋlɩ-kɛ lɛ, Yozɛɛfʋ ɛnʋ ɛ́yáa-kɛ se Yesu.

eng: Then the Spirit led Jesus up into the wilderness so that the devil might tempt him.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tolisi Yesu nɛ kiwoni-i lakʋ taa se Satanɩ ɛtakɩ-ɩ.

eng: After Jesus had fasted for forty days and forty nights, he was starving.
local: Yesu kɔhɔkɩ nɔɔ kɩyakɩŋ nɩɩnaza. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ñɔɔsɩ wɩ-ɩ.

eng: The tempter came to him and said, Since you are Gods Son, command these stones to become bread.
local: Peeɖe Satanɩ woba ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Yee ŋnaɣ se ŋkɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ yɔ, heyi pɛɛ anɛ se apɩsɩ tindine nɛ ŋtɔɔ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread, but by every word spoken by God . .
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Pɩtɛkɛ tɔɔnaɣ ɖeke ɛyʋ cakɩnɩ wezuu. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se ɛcaɣ-kʋ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ tɩlɩɣnɩ Ɛsɔ nɔɔ taa yɔ.› ».

eng: After that the devil brought him into the holy city and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him.
local: Yesu cosi-i ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Satanɩ ɛnʋ ɛkpaɣ-ɩ ewoni-i Ɛsɔ tɛtʋ taa nɛ ɛkpazɩ-ɩ ɖooo Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa ñʋʋ taa.

eng: Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down; for it is written, I will command my angels concerning you, and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: nɛ eheyi-i se: «Yee ŋkɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ yɔ, kʋyɩ ŋmaa tataa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se ‹Ɛsɔ heyiɣ e-tiyiyaa se petem-ŋ pɔ́ɖɔ́kɩ-ŋ pe-nesi taa nɛ ŋtaatɔlɩ pɩyɛ naɖɩyɛ yɔɔ nɛ pʋkʋ ña-naŋgbaŋaɣ.› ».

eng: Jesus replied, Again its written, Dont test the Lord your God . .
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-i se: «Pama ka-taa ɖɔɖɔ se: ‹Taatakɩ ña-Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ se ŋna ɛ-wayɩ.› ».

eng: Then the devil brought him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
local: Ecosi ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Satanɩ ɛnʋ ɛtasɩ-ɩ kpaɣʋ nɛ ewoni-i pʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kʋkʋyɩ siŋŋ yɔ kʋ-ñʋʋ taa nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-ɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ ajɛya kpeekpe nɛ a-koboyaɣ.

eng: He said, Ill give you all these if you bow down and worship me.
local: nɛ eheyi-i se: «Yee ŋluŋ mɛn-tɛɛ nɛ ŋɖʋ-m hɩɖɛ yɔ, mahaɣ-ŋ pɩtɩŋa payɩ.».

eng: Jesus responded, Go away, Satan, because its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him . .
local: Yesu heyi-i se: «Satanɩ ɖɛɛ mɔ-yɔɔ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Ña-Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ ɖeke koŋ tɛɛ pʋwɛɛ se ŋluŋ nɛ ŋla ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ.› ».

eng: The devil left him, and angels came and took care of him.
local: Peeɖe Satanɩ yebi-i. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa wolo Yesu cɔlɔ nɛ pasɩnɩ-ɩ.

eng: Now when Jesus heard that John was arrested, he went to Galilee.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yesu nɩɩ se pakpa Yohanɛɛsɩ nɛ paɖʋ-ɩ salaka. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛpɩsɩ Galilee egeetiye taa.

eng: He left Nazareth and settled in Capernaum, which lies alongside the sea in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.
local: Ɛtalaa lɛ, ɛtacaɣ Nazarɛɛtɩ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛɖɛɛ Kapɛrnayum. Tɛtʋ ńdʋ́, tɩwɛ Galilee teŋgu nɔɔ, Zabulɔɔ nɛ Nɛftalii pɔ-hɔɔlɩŋ taa.

eng: This fulfilled what Isaiah the prophet said:.
local: Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se pɩla ɛzɩ náyʋ Ezaayii yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ. Ɛyɔɔdaa se:.

eng: Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, alongside the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles.
local: «Ɩnaɣ, Zabulɔɔ nɛ Nɛftalii pɔ-hɔɔlɩŋ taa ñɩma mɩ nɛ mbá mɩ mɩwɛ ɖɔɖɔ teŋgu habɩyɛ nɔɔ nɛ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ wayɩ yɔ! Mbá mɩ ɩkɛ Galilee ñɩma nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɛwɛɛ mɩ-tɛ yɔ!.

eng: the people who lived in the dark have seen a great light, and a light has come upon those who lived in the region and in shadow of death.
local: Ɩnɩɩ camɩyɛ se: ɩɩwɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa nɛ ɩna ñalɩmɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ naɖɩyɛ! Ɩɩwɛ sɩɖaa ɛjaɖɛ taa nɛ ñalɩmɩyɛ ñalɩ mɩ-yɔɔ.».

eng: From that time Jesus began to announce, Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven! .
local: Yesu talɩ Kapɛrnayum lɛ, ɛpazɩ Ɛsɔtɔm susuu nɛ eheyiɣ ɛyáa se: «Ɩlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ wɩzɩ talʋʋ!».

eng: As Jesus walked alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, because they were fishermen.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu ɖɔŋaɣ Galilee teŋgu nɔɔ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛna kpakpasɩ kpayaa naalɛ nabɛyɛ, pɔlɔwaɣ piyiŋ. Ḿbá yɔ: Siimɔɔ weyi payaɣ ɖɔɖɔ se Pɩyɛ yɔ nɛ e-neu Andree.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Ɛna-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩkɔɔ ɩtɩŋ-m nɛ manpɩsɩ-mɩ ɛyáa kpayaa ɛzɩ ɩkpaɣʋ kpakpasɩ yɔ.».

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Peeɖe patiyebi pe-piyiŋ nɛ patɩŋ-ɩ.

eng: Continuing on, he saw another set of brothers, James the son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with Zebedee their father repairing their nets. Jesus called them and.
local: Etuzi ɛ-tɩ ɛsɩndaa lɛ, ɛna Yakubu nɛ e-neu Yohanɛɛsɩ, pa nɛ pa-caa Zebedee pɛwɛ pa-kpɩyʋʋ taa nɛ pɔñɔɔzʋʋ pe-piyiŋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛ́yáa-wɛ.

eng: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the desert of Judea announcing.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ pazɩ susuu Ɛsɔtɔm Yudee lakʋ taa.

eng: immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
local: nɛ patɩyebu pa-caa nɛ kpɩyʋʋ nɛ patɩŋ ɛ-wayɩ.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: Eheyaɣ ɛyáa se: «Ɩlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ wɩzɩ talʋʋ.».

eng: Jesus traveled throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues. He announced the good news of the kingdom and healed every disease and sickness among the people.
local: Yesu cɔwaɣ Galilee egeetiye tɩŋa taa payɩ nɛ eheyiɣ ɛyáa Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ taa nɛ ɛlakɩ nɛ ajamaa ndɩ ndɩ nɛ kʋdɔndɩnaa ndɩ ndɩ patɩŋa pɔ-yɔɔ cɛkɩ.

eng: He was the one of whom Isaiah the prophet spoke when he said: The voice of one shouting in the wilderness, Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight. .
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ tɔm náyʋ Ezaayii yɔɔdaa se: «Nɔɔyʋ susuu lakʋ taa se pɔñɔɔzɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ nʋmɔʋ nɛ pala nɛ kʋtʋʋzɩ camɩyɛ.».

eng: News about him spread throughout Syria. People brought to him all those who had various kinds of diseases, those in pain, those possessed by demons, those with epilepsy, and those who were paralyzed, and he healed them.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-hɩɖɛ sɛ Siirii ɛjaɖɛ tɩŋa taa. Pʋ-yɔɔ pakpakaɣ mbá pɛwɛnɩ kʋdɔmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ yɔ patɩŋa nɛ mbá pʋcɔzʋʋ-wɛ yɔ: eliikenaa nɛ abom tɔlɩyaa nɛ kalɩkalasɩ nɛ powokini ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛlakɩ nɛ pɔ-yɔɔ cɛkɩ.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ ɛnʋ, esuwaɣ toko ŋgʋ́ palʋnɩ kpaŋnɔ siɣá naalɛ tʋ hʋndʋ yɔ nɛ ɛtɔɔlʋʋ tambaɖɛ nɖɩ́ palabɩnɩ tɔnɛ yɔ. Kutoloŋ nɛ tʋŋ num ɛtɔkaɣ.

eng: Large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from the areas beyond the Jordan River.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ ɛyáa samaɣ kpɛzɩnɩ hɔɔlɩŋ tɩŋa nɛ patɩŋɩɣ ɛ-wayɩ. Paalɩnɩ Galilee nɛ Tɛtʋ Hiu egeetiye taa nɛ Yeruusalɛm nɛ Yudee nɛ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ wayɩ.

eng: People from Jerusalem, throughout Judea, and all around the Jordan River came to him.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ susaɣ tɔm lakʋ taa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yeruusalɛm ñɩma nɛ Yudee hɔɔlɩŋ tɩŋa taa ñɩma nɛ hɔɔlɩŋ weyi ɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩñɔtɩnɩ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ yɔ ɩ-taa ñɩma, palɩɣ powoki ɛ-cɔlɔ.

eng: As they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
local: nɛ paa weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋ ɛ-kɩwɛɛkɩm mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ ɛnʋ ɛlakɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ taa.

eng: Now when Jesus had come down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu lɩɩnɩ pʋʋ taa nɛ etiki nɛ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ tɩŋɩɣ ɛ-wayɩ.

eng: Many Pharisees and Sadducees came to be baptized by John. He said to them, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ nawa se Farɩsɩ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma nɛ Saduki ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma mbá powoki ɛ-cɔlɔ se ɛlabɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ yɔ, pɔɖɔwa siŋŋ. Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɛyáa azaɣ́a mɩ! Acɩba-mɩ cɩkpalʋʋ se Ɛsɔ pana kɔŋ-mɩ kpanaʋ yɔ?.

eng: A man with a skin disease came, kneeled before him, and said, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, canaɣ nakɛyɛ wolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ koluŋ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa nɛ kɔtɔ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Yee ŋtisiɣ yɔ, ŋla nɛ mɔ-yɔɔ cɛ nɛ manpɩsɩ ɛyʋ weyi ɛmʋnaa yɔ.».

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives.
local: Ɩla lakasɩ nzɩ́ sɩwɩlɩɣ se ɩlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ yɔ.

eng: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in the territory of Judea during the rule of King Herod, magi came from the east to Jerusalem.
local: Palʋlɩ Yesu Bɛtlɛhɛm Yudee egeetiye taa. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, Eroodi kɛkɛnɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ ɖɩ-taa. Palʋlɩ-ɩ lɛ, lɔŋsɩnɖaa mbá pasɩm tɩɩnzɩ camɩyɛ yɔ, palɩɩnɩ ɖooo wɩsɩ ɖɩlɩyɛ nɛ pɔkɔɔ Yeruusalɛm.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand and touched him, saying, I do want to. Become clean. Instantly his skin disease was cleansed.
local: Peeɖe Yesu posi e-nesi nɛ etukuni-kɛ nɛ eheyi-kɛ se: «Mentisaa! Pɩsɩ ɛyʋ weyi ɛmʋnaa yɔ.» Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, kɔ-yɔɔ tɩcɛbʋ.

eng: And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Ɩtaamaɣzɩ se mɩ-cɔzɔ lɛ Abraham yɔ, pɩtɛma. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se Ɛsɔ pɩzɩɣ ɛla nɛ pɛɛ anɛ apɩsɩ Abraham liɖe taa ñɩma.».

eng: They asked, Where is the newborn king of the Jews? Weve seen his star in the east, and weve come to honor him.
local: Patalaa lɛ, pɔpɔzʋʋ se: «Le pɩɣa ŋgá palʋlɩ-kɛ se kɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ yɔ kɛwɛɛ? Đɩna tɩɩŋa ŋgá kawɩlɩɣ se palʋlɩ pɩɣa ŋ́gá yɔ wɩsɩ ɖɩlɩyɛ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩkɔm se ɖiluŋ kɛ-tɛɛ.».

eng: Be careful that you dont practice your religion in front of people to draw their attention. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Ɩkpa mɩ-tɩ! Ɩtaala mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ se pala Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa yɔ ɛyáa samaɣ taa se pana-mɩ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, ɩɩmʋʋ kɩhɛyʋʋ nakʋyʋ mɩ-Caa weyi ɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ ɛ-cɔlɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-i se: «Taaheyi-tʋ nɔɔyʋ. Ɛlɛ́, wolo cɔjɔ cɔlɔ nɛ ɛna se ñɔ-yɔɔ cɛba, nɛ ŋwoni kileku ɛzɩ Mooyiizi kaɖʋʋ yɔ nɛ pɩwɩlɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa se ñɔ-yɔɔ cɛba.».

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Peeɖe etuu-wɛ eduuye se: «Halɩ pɛtɛm kpaɣʋ laɖɩyɛ se pɛsɛtɩɣ tɩŋ nɛ ɩ-tɛɛ: tɩʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩɩlʋlʋʋ pee kɩbana yɔ, pɛsɛtɩɣ-kʋ nɛ paɖʋ miŋ taa.».

eng: When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and everyone in Jerusalem was troubled with him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Eroodi nɩɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pɩcɛyɩ-ɩ pɩŋŋ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ Yeruusalɛm ñɩma tɩŋa.

eng: Whenever you give to the poor, dont blow your trumpet as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets so that they may get praise from people. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: «Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yee mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛlakɩ kʋñɔndɩnaa kʋjɔʋ yɔ, ɛtaayele se ɛyáa tɩŋa ɩna. Ḿbʋ́ tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ñɩma lakɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa nɛ habɛɛ yɔɔ se pasam-wɛ. Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se pɛtɛm pa-kɩhɛyʋʋ mʋ́ʋ!.

eng: When Jesus went to Capernaum, a centurion approached.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu kʋyɩ se ewoki Kapɛrnayum. Ɛsʋwaɣ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa lɛ, Room sɔɔja sɔsɔ nɔɔyʋ wolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ etendi-i se:.

eng: I baptize with water those of you who have changed your hearts and lives. The one who is coming after me is stronger than I am. Im not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se: «Ma lɛ, malakɩ-mɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ pɩwɩlɩ se ɩlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ. Ma-wayɩ lɛ, weyi ɛkɔŋ yɔ, ɛkɩlɩ-m. Halɩ mantatɩɩmʋna se mankpaɣ ɛ-naataŋgbala nɛ mɔnɖɔkɩ. Ɛkɔŋ-mɩ labʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖoŋ nɛ miŋ.

eng: He gathered all the chief priests and the legal experts and asked them where the Christ was to be born.
local: Peeɖe ekpeɣli ɛ-ɛjaɖɛ taa cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa patɩŋa payɩ nɛ ɛpɔzʋʋ-wɛ se: «Le palʋlʋʋ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ yee?».

eng: But when you give to the poor, dont let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
local: Ña lɛ, yee ŋhaɣ kʋñɔndɩnaa nabʋyʋ yɔ, taayele nɛ ñɛ-ɛgbaɣdɩnaa maɣmaɣ na.

eng: pleading with him, Lord, my servant is flat on his back at home, paralyzed, and his suffering is awful.
local: «Hayɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ! Man-tʋmlaɖʋ pɩsɩ kalɩkalaɣ nɛ ɛwɛɛ ñalʋʋ yɔɔ ɖɩɣa nɛ ɛlakɩ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ.».

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: Ɛwɛ ɛzɩ falɩyʋ ɖɔkʋʋ ɖaakuɖe nɛ ɛfalɩɣ nɛ esuu pee kpou taa nɛ piyele ɛɖʋʋ lɔtʋ miŋ weyi eeɖeŋ wiɖiyi kaʋ yɔ ɛ-taa yɔ.».

eng: They said, In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is what the prophet wrote:.
local: Pocosi-i se: «Bɛtlɛhɛm, Yudee egeetiye taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, náyʋ nɔɔyʋ kamawa se:.

eng: so that you may give to the poor in secret. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Ha-wɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ nɛ ña-Caa weyi ɛnaɣ mbʋ́ ŋlakɩ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ, ɛhɛyɩ-ŋ.».

eng: Jesus responded, Ill come and heal him. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-i se: «Mowoki mala nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ.».

eng: At that time Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan River so that John would baptize him.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ lakaɣ ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ taa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yesu lɩɩnɩ Galilee egeetiye taa nɛ ewolo ɛ-cɔlɔ peeɖe se ɛlabɩ-ɩ-sɩ.

eng: You, Bethlehem, land of Judah, by no means are you least among the rulers of Judah, because from you will come one who governs, who will shepherd my people Israel.
local: ‹Ña Bɛtlɛhɛm tɛtʋ lɛ! Pʋtɔtɔyɩnɩ-ŋ kpem Yudee egeetiye taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ña-taa pɔkɔŋ lʋlʋʋ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ weyi ɛkɔŋ ɖɔkʋʋ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma yɔ.› ».

eng: When you pray, dont be like hypocrites. They love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners so that people will see them. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Yee ɩsɛɣ Ɛsɔ lɛ, ɩtaala ɛzɩ tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ñɩma lakʋʋ yɔ. Pɔsɔɔlɩ sɩŋʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa nɛ habɛɛ nɔsɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɛsɛɣ Ɛsɔ yɔ se ɛyáa ɛna-wɛ. Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se pɛtɛm pa-kɩhɛyʋʋ mʋ́ʋ!.

eng: But the centurion replied, Lord, I dont deserve to have you come under my roof. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Ɛlɛ́, sɔɔja sɔsɔ ɛnʋ eheyi Yesu se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Mantamʋna se ŋsʋʋ man-ɖɩɣa taa. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mentendiɣ-ŋ se ŋyɔɔdɩ tɔm kʋɖʋmtʋ koŋ nɛ man-tʋmlaɖʋ yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: John tried to stop him and said, I need to be baptized by you, yet you come to me.
local: Peeɖe Yohanɛɛsɩ tacaɣ tisuu nɛ ekitini Yesu se: «Ma pʋmʋnaa se mɔnkɔɔ ñɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ŋlabɩ-m-sɩ nɛ ña ñakʋyɩ ŋkɔŋ mɔn-cɔlɔ tuluŋa na!».

eng: Then Herod secretly called for the magi and found out from them the time when the star had first appeared.
local: Peeɖe Eroodi ya lɔŋsɩnɖaa ḿbá mɛsaɣ tɛɛ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ ɖeyi ɖeyi tɩɩŋa ŋ́gá kalɩwa?».

eng: But when you pray, go to your room, shut the door, and pray to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Ña lɛ, yee ŋsɛɣ Ɛsɔ lɛ, ŋsʋʋ ño-kuduyuu tɛɛ nɛ ŋɖɩɣ ñɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ŋsɛɛ Caa weyi paanaɣ-ɩ yɔ. Ɛnʋ naɣnɩ mbʋ́ ŋlakɩ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛhɛyɩ-ŋ.

eng: Im a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Ma lɛ, ma-ñʋʋ yɔɔ ñɩma wɛɛ nɛ mañaŋ-wɛ. Ɛlɛ́, mɛn-tɛɛ ñɩma wɛɛ ɖɔɖɔ. Meheyi pa-taa nɔɔyʋ se ewolo peeɖe yɔ yɔ, ewoki. Maya lɛlʋ se ɛkɔɔ yɔ, ɛkɔŋ ɖɔɖɔ. Meheyi man-tʋmlaɖʋ se ɛla nabʋyʋ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ ñalakɩ-pʋ ɖɔɖɔ.».

eng: Then Pharisees and legal experts came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Farɩsɩ ñɩma nɛ Yuda ñɩma paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa palɩɩnɩ Yeruusalɛm nɛ powolo Yesu cɔlɔ nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se:.

eng: Jesus answered, Allow me to be baptized now. This is necessary to fulfill all righteousness. So John agreed to baptize Jesus.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu cosi-i se: «Yele pɩla ḿbʋ́ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ. Ḿbʋ́ pʋmʋnaa se ɖɩla mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ.» Eheyi Yohanɛɛsɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ tisi nɛ ɛlabɩ-ɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ.

eng: He sent them to Bethlehem, saying, Go and search carefully for the child. When youve found him, report to me so that I too may go and honor him.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, etiyi-wɛ se: «Iwolo Bɛtlɛhɛm nɛ ɩñɩnɩ pɩɣa ŋ́gá camɩyɛ. Ɩna-kɛ lɛ, ɩpɩsɩ iheyi-m nɛ mowolo moluŋ kɛ-tɛɛ ɖɔɖɔ.».

eng: When you pray, dont pour out a flood of empty words, as the Gentiles do. They think that by saying many words theyll be heard.
local: «Yee ɩsɛɣ Ɛsɔ lɛ, ɩtaayɔɔdɩ tɔm nɛ pʋɖɔɔ sakɩyɛ yem ɛzɩ mbá paañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ, palakʋʋ yɔ. Pamaɣzɩɣ se pɔyɔɔdʋʋ sakɩyɛ ḿbʋ́ nɛ Ɛsɔ nɩɩnɩ-wɛ.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he was impressed and said to the people following him, I say to you with all seriousness that even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yesu nɩ sɔɔja ɛnʋ ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛyɩɣyɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛkɛzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛyáa mbá patɩŋaɣ-ɩ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se paa Izrɛɛlɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa maɣmaɣ yɔ, mantanada weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ nɛ pɩtalɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: Why are your disciples breaking the elders rules handed down to us? They dont ritually purify their hands by washing before they eat.
local: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ñɔ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lakɩ mbʋ́ paɖʋ ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa sɔnzɩ taa se pataala yɔ? Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paasaŋɩɣ pe-nesi ɛzɩ paɖʋʋ pɔ-sɔnzɩ taa yɔ nɛ pʋcɔ pɔtɔkɩ tɔɔnaɣ.».

eng: He called his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits to throw them out and to heal every disease and every sickness.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu kpeɣli ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ nɛ ɛha-wɛ ɖoŋ se pɔɖɔɔnɩnɩ aleewaa nɛ pala nɛ ɛyáa kʋdɔmɩŋ tɩŋa nɛ pɛ-ɛjandʋ tɩŋa pɛcɛ.

eng: When Jesus was baptized, he immediately came up out of the water. Heaven was opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ tɩtɛm Yesu labʋ sɔnzɩ ńzɩ́ lɛ, Yesu lɩɣnɩ lɩm taa. Peeɖe ɛsɔdaa tʋlaa nɛ ɛna Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu lɩ ɛzɩ halikuku yɔ nɛ kitiki Yesu ɛnʋ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: When they heard the king, they went; and look, the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stood over the place where the child was.
local: Eroodi tɛm lɔŋsɩnɖaa ḿbá yɔɔdɩnaʋ lɛ, palɩɩ se pɛɖɛɣ Bɛtlɛhɛm. Peeɖe pana tɩɩŋa ŋgá paana wɩsɩ ɖɩlɩyɛ yɔ nɛ patɩŋɩɣ-kɛ. Pana-kɛ lɛ, pɩlabɩ-wɛ leleŋ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ. Katalɩ ɖenɖe pɩɣa ŋ́gá kɛwɛɛ yɔ lɛ, kasɩŋ.

eng: Dont be like them, because your Father knows what you need before you ask.
local: Ɩtaatɩɣzɩnɩ-wɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩ-Caa Ɛsɔ tɛm sɩm mbʋ́ pɩtɛɖɩɣ-mɩ yɔ nɛ pʋcɔ ɩpɔzʋʋ-ɩ-pʋ.

eng: At that time Herod the ruler heard the news about Jesus.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, wiyaʋ Eroodi cɔŋaɣnɩ Galilee egeetiye yɔɔ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛnɩɩ Yesu taŋ.

eng: I say to you that there are many who will come from east and west and sit down to eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kɔŋ lɩnaʋ wɩsɩ ɖɩlɩyɛ nɛ sɩ-ɖɩɖʋyɛ nɛ powolo pa nɛ ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa Abraham nɛ Izaakɩ nɛ Yakɔɔbʋ pɛkpɛndɩ pacaɣ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa nɛ pɔtɔkɩ kpɛdɛlɛɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you break the command of God by keeping the rules handed down to you?.
local: Peeɖe Yesu pɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Nɛ mɩ yɔ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩkpakɩɣ mɩ-cɔzɔnaa sɔnzɩ nɛ ɩwɛɛkɩɣnɩ Ɛsɔ paɣtʋ?.

eng: Here are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, who is called Peter; and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee; and John his brother;.
local: Mbá ɛlɩzaa nɛ ɛ́yáa-wɛ se e-tiyiyaa yɔ, ḿbá yɔ: kajalaɣ lɛ, Siimɔɔ weyi payaɣ ɖɔɖɔ se Pɩyɛ yɔ nɛ e-neu Andree nɛ Zebedee pɩyalaa Yakubu nɛ e-neu Yohanɛɛsɩ.

eng: A voice from heaven said, This is my Son whom I dearly love; I find happiness in him.
local: nɛ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ ɛsɔdaa se: «Ɛnɛ lɛ man-Pɩyalʋ kʋsɔɔlʋ, maahʋʋ ɛ-yɔɔ kaʋ.».

eng: They entered the house and saw the child with Mary his mother. Falling to their knees, they honored him. Then they opened their treasure chests and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
local: nɛ pasʋʋ ɖɩɣa nɛ pana pɩɣa ŋ́gá nɛ ko-ɖoo Maarɩɩ nɛ poluŋ kɛ-tɛɛ paɖʋ-kɛ hɩɖɛ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pɛyɛɖɩ po-wondu nɛ paha-kɛ sika nɛ tɩlaaɖɩ ŋgʋ́ pɔhɔkɩ yɔ nɛ ŋgʋ́ payaɣ-kʋ se miiri yɔ.

eng: Pray like this: Our Father who is in heaven, uphold the holiness of your name.
local: Mɩ lɛ, ɩsɛɛ Ɛsɔ se: ‹Đa-Caa weyi ña ŋwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, ña-hɩɖɛ ɛsɛɛ paa le.

eng: He said to his servants, This is John the Baptist. Hes been raised from the dead. This is why these miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Peeɖe eheyi ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa se: «Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ weyi ɛɛsɩba yɔ, ɛ-tɔm pɔyɔɔdʋʋ, ɛnʋ femna. Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ ɛwɛnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖoŋ nɛ ɛlakɩ hama lakasɩ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: But the children of the kingdom will be thrown outside into the darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, mbá pɩɩwɛɛ se pɛkɛ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa ñɩma yɔ, pɔlɔʋ-wɛ nɛ awayɩ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa. Peeɖe pewiliɣ nɛ pasalɩ pe-kela.».

eng: For God said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Ɛsɔ tɔm se: ‹Ña ña-caa nɛ ño-ɖoo.› Ɛtɔm ɖɔɖɔ se: ‹Weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋ ɛ-caa yaa e-ɖoo tɔm ɛzaɣ́tʋ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se pakʋ-ɩ.›.

eng: When Jesus finished teaching his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
local: Yesu tɛm ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ kɛdʋʋ mbʋ́ etiyiɣ-wɛ se pala yɔ lɛ, ɛɖɛɛ nɛ ɛcɔʋ tɛtʋ ndɩ ndɩ ndʋ́ tɩwɛ egeetiye ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa yɔ tɩ-taa nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ nɛ esusuu ɛyáa Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: Philip; and Bartholomew; Thomas; and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus; and Thaddaeus;.
local: nɛ Filiipu nɛ Bartelemii nɛ Tomaa nɛ tikee mʋyʋ Maatiyee nɛ Alfee pɩyalʋ Yakubu nɛ Taadee.

eng: Bring in your kingdom so that your will is done on earth as its done in heaven.
local: Ñe-kewiyaɣ ɛkɔɔ, yele pala ñe-liu ñɩm tataa cɩnɛ ɛzɩ palakʋʋ ɛsɔdaa yɔ.

eng: Because they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back to their own country by another route.
local: Pɛtɛm labʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Ɛsɔ heyi-wɛ nɛ ɖoziye taa se pataapɩsɩ Eroodi cɔlɔ. Peeɖe pakpaɣ nʋmɔʋ lɛɛkʋ nɛ pekpe.

eng: Jesus said to the centurion, Go; it will be done for you just as you have believed. And his servant was healed that very moment.
local: Peeɖe eheyi sɔɔja sɔsɔ ɛnʋ se: «Wolo ŋkpe. Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ŋwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, malakɩ-ŋ mbʋ́ ŋpɔzɩ-m yɔ.» Ɛtɩyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, tʋmlaɖʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip.
local: Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ sɩm tɔm yɔ. Eroodi kɛlɛɣ e-neu Filiipu halʋ Eroodiyaadɩ. Peeɖe Yohanɛɛsɩ heyi Eroodi se: «Pɩtaha nʋmɔʋ se ŋkpaɣ ñe-neu halʋ.» Pʋyɔɔ eyeba nɛ pakpa Yohanɛɛsɩ, pɔhɔkɩ-ɩ nɛ paɖʋ-ɩ salaka.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother, Everything Im expected to contribute to you Im giving to God as a gift, then you dont have to honor your father.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mɩ mɩwɩlɩɣ se: ‹Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛɖʋwa se mbʋ́ pɩɩwɛɛ se ɛha ɛ-caa yaa e-ɖoo yɔ, ɛhaɣ-pʋ Ɛsɔ› yɔ.

eng: Now when John heard in prison about the things the Christ was doing, he sent word by his disciples to Jesus, asking.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ kɛwɛ salaka taa. Peeɖe ɛnɩ tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ Krɩstʋ lakaɣ yɔ ɖɩ-taŋ nɛ ɛkʋsɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa lɛlaa nɛ etiyi-wɛ se powolo.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up a mountain. He sat down and his disciples came to him.
local: Yesu na ɛyáa samaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛkpa piyaɣ nakɛyɛ yɔɔ nɛ ɛcaɣ. Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa woba ɛ-cɔlɔ.

eng: Simon the Cananaean; and Judas, who betrayed Jesus.
local: nɛ Siimɔɔ weyi payaɣ se Yɔɔ Kandɩyʋ yɔ nɛ Yudaasɩ Iskarɩyɔɔtɩ weyi ekitaa ɛkpa Yesu nɛ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ koyindinaa nesi taa yɔ.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Ha-ɖʋ sɔnɔ kɩyakʋ tɔɔnaɣ.

eng: When the magi had departed, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, Get up. Take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod will soon search for the child in order to kill him.
local: Lɔŋsɩnɖaa ḿbá pɛɖɛwa lɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ lɩɩ Yozɛɛfʋ yɔɔ ɖoziye taa nɛ eheyi-i se: «Eroodi caɣ se ɛkʋ pɩɣa ŋ́gá. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, kʋyɩ ŋkpaɣ ka nɛ ko-ɖoo nɛ ña nɛ wɛ ise iwolo Ejiptɩ nɛ ɩcaɣ peeɖe nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ meheyiɣ-ŋ se ɩpɩsɩ yɔ.».

eng: Jesus went home with Peter and saw Peters mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu wolo Pɩyɛ tɛ nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ kɩnamɩyɛ kpa Pɩyɛ yɔsɔ nɛ ɛhɛnɩ.

eng: At that time Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry so they were picking heads of wheat and eating them.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɔɖɔŋaɣ hayɩm nabʋyʋ taa kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ nakʋyʋ wiye. Paahayɩ tɔɔnaɣ nakɛyɛ pɩ-taa nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɛlɩɣ nɛ nʋmɔʋ nɛ ponuutuu nɛ pasalɩɣ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ñɔɔsɩ wɩɣaɣ-wɛ. Tɔɔnaɣ ŋ́gá, kɛwɛ ɛzɩ amala yɔ.

eng: Although Herod wanted to kill him, he feared the crowd because they thought John was a prophet.
local: Eroodi ñɩnaɣ se ɛkʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ. Ɛlɛ́, eseɣaɣ ɛyáa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɔcɔŋaɣnɩ ɛ́lɛ́ se ɛkɛ náyʋ.

eng: So you do away with Gods Law for the sake of the rules that have been handed down to you.
local: pɩtɛkɛnɩ-ɩ kɩcɛyʋʋ se ɛha-pʋ ɛ-caa yaa e-ɖoo. Pɩwɩlɩɣ se ɩwɛɛkɩɣ Ɛsɔtɔm nɛ ɩɖɔŋ mbʋ́ mɩ-cɔzɔnaa wɩlɩ-mɩ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ ɖeke.

eng: Dont judge, so that you wont be judged.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Ɩtaahʋʋnɩ nɔɔyʋ nɛ Ɛsɔ ɛɛhʋʋnɩ-mɩ.

eng: Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another.
local: pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ nɛ pana se ɛnʋ lɛ weyi ɛ-tɔm paayɔɔdaa se ɛkaɣ kɔm yɔ yaa nɔɔyʋ wɛnɩ wayɩ?.

eng: He taught them, saying:.
local: nɛ ɛpazɩ-wɛ tɔm wɩlʋʋ. Ɛwɩlaɣ-wɛ se:.

eng: Jesus sent these twelve out and commanded them, Dont go among the Gentiles or into a Samaritan city.
local: Yesu tiyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ ɛjaɖɛ taa. Pʋcɔ pɛɖɛɛ lɛ, ɛkam pa-ñʋŋ taa siŋŋ se: «itaawolo hɔɔlɩŋ weyi ɩ-taa mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, pɛwɛɛ yɔ. Ɩtaasʋʋ Samaarɩɩ egeetiye hɔɔlʋʋ nakʋyʋ taa ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Forgive us for the ways we have wronged you, just as we also forgive those who have wronged us.
local: Kpeɣ-ɖʋ ɖa-kɩwɛɛkɩm ɛzɩ ɖikpeɣu ɖɔɖɔ mbá payʋsɩnɩ-ɖʋ yɔ.

eng: Joseph got up and, during the night, took the child and his mother to Egypt.
local: Peeɖe Yozɛɛfʋ kʋyɩ ɖoó ana a-taa nɛ ɛkpaɣ pɩɣa ŋ́gá nɛ ko-ɖoo nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ pɛɖɛɛ Ejiptɩ.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain.
local: Kɩyakɩŋ loɖo ɖɛwa lɛ, Yesu yaa Pɩyɛ nɛ Yakubu nɛ e-neu Yohanɛɛsɩ nɛ ɛkpanɩ-wɛ pʋʋ sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ yɔɔ pe-ɖeke.

eng: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served them.
local: Peeɖe ewobi ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ etukuni e-nesi nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ nɛ ɛkʋyɩ ɛha-wɛ lɩm.

eng: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, Look, your disciples are breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Palakaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Farɩsɩ ñɩma nabɛyɛ na-wɛ nɛ powolo peheyi Yesu se: «Cɔnɩ ŋna ñɔ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lakʋʋ mbʋ́ paɖʋ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ taa se pataala kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye yɔ.».

eng: That day Jesus went out of the house and sat down beside the lake.
local: Kɩyakʋ ŋ́gʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Yesu lɩnɩ ɖɩɣa nɛ ewolo ɛcaɣ teŋgu nɔɔ nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ ɛyáa Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: But at Herods birthday party Herodias daughter danced in front of the guests and thrilled Herod.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye pɔtɔkaɣ Eroodi lʋlʋʋ kazandʋ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Eroodiyaadɩ pɛlɛ paa ɛyáa hɛkʋ taa nɛ pɩla Eroodi leleŋ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ.

eng: Youll receive the same judgment you give. Whatever you deal out will be dealt out to you.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩhʋʋnɩ lɛlaa yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-mɩ hʋnaʋ. Mbʋ́ ɩmaɣzɩɣnɩ lɛlaa yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-mɩ maɣzɩnaʋ.

eng: Hypocrites! Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you.
local: Tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ñɩma mɩ! Náyʋ Ezaayii kɛwɛnɩ toovenim se esusi mɩ-tɔm se:.

eng: Jesus responded, Go, report to John what you hear and see.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Iwolo iheyi-i mbʋ́ ɩnawa nɛ ndʋ́ ɩnɩwa yɔ:.

eng: Go instead to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Ɛlɛ́, iwolo Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma mbá pɛwɛ ɛzɩ heŋ kilebiŋ weyi ɩfɛyɩnɩ ninɖiɣyu yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: Happy are people who are hopeless, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: «Ɛyáa mbá pasɩm pa-tɩ se pɛtɛkɛ pʋyʋ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɛkɛ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa ñɩma.

eng: And dont lead us into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one.
local: Taayele nɛ pɩtakɩ-ɖʋ, ɛlɛ́, lɩzɩ-ɖʋ Satanɩ nesi taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ñatɩnɩ kewiyaɣ nɛ ɖoŋ nɛ koboyaɣ patɩŋa payɩ tam. Pɩla ḿbʋ́!›.

eng: He stayed there until Herod died. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: I have called my son out of Egypt .
local: Pacaɣ peeɖe nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Eroodi sɩba yɔ. Pɩlaba ḿbʋ́ se ndʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ katɩŋnɩ náyʋ nɔɔyʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ yɔ, tɩla. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Maya man-pɩyalʋ nɛ Ejiptɩ se ɛlɩɩ.».

eng: He was transformed in front of them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
local: Peeɖe ɛlɛɣzɩ pɛ-ɛsɩndaa nɛ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa ñɩlɩsɩɣ ɛzɩ wɩsɩ yɔ nɛ e-wondu hʋlʋmɩ layɩ layɩ ɛzɩ fenaɣ yɔ.

eng: That evening people brought to Jesus many who were demon-possessed. He threw the spirits out with just a word. He healed everyone who was sick.
local: Đanaɣ ɖanaa lɛ, ɛyáa kpaɣ eliikenaa sakɩyɛ nɛ powoni Yesu cɔlɔ nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ nɛ aleewaa se pa-wayɩ nɛ ɛla nɛ kʋdɔndɩnaa tɩŋa payɩ yɔɔ cɛ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: But he said to them, Havent you read what David did when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ ɩtakalɩ mbʋ́ Daaviidi kalabɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ñɔɔsɩ wɩɣaɣ ɛ nɛ mbá paawɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ pʋ-tɔm naa we?.

eng: Such large crowds gathered around him that he climbed into a boat and sat down. The whole crowd was standing on the shore.
local: Ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kaakpeɣli ɛ-cɔlɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛkʋyaa ɛkpa kpɩyʋʋ taa nɛ ɛcaɣ nɛ paba ñasɩŋ patɩŋa kidiiliŋ yɔɔ nɛ pewelesiɣ ɛ-tɔm.

eng: Then he swore to give her anything she asked.
local: nɛ eɖuu ɛyáa tɩŋa ɛsɩndaa se yee ɛ́lɛ́ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ paa ɛbɛ yɔ, ɛcɛlɩɣ-ɩ.

eng: Why do you see the splinter thats in your brothers or sisters eye, but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋnaɣ kemaɣ ŋgá kɛwɛ ña-taabalʋ ɛsɩyɛ taa yɔ nɛ ŋŋnaɣ ɖaʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩwɛ ña-ñɩnɖɛ taa yɔ?.

eng: Those who were blind are able to see . Those who were crippled are walking. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. The poor have good news proclaimed to them .
local: yʋlʋmaa naɣ, tala tɩnaa ɖɔŋ camɩyɛ, canasɩ yɔɔ cɛkɩ, ndamaa nɩɣ, sɩɖaa feŋ nɛ posusuu kʋñɔndɩnaa Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: As you go, make this announcement: The kingdom of heaven has come near.
local: Ɩlɩ nʋmɔʋ taa lɛ, isusi ɛyáa se Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ wɩzɩ talʋʋ.

eng: This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: ‹Ɛsɔ tɔm se ɛyáa panɛ, pasaŋ-m nɛ nɔɔ yem. Pa-laŋa taa lɛ, pɔtɔsɔɔlɩ-m paa ñɩɩɩ.

eng: Happy are people who grieve, because they will be made glad.
local: Mbá pɛ-ɛsɛ ñɩŋaa yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kɔŋ pa-laŋa hɛzʋʋ.

eng: If you forgive others their sins, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
local: «Meheyiɣ-mɩ se: yee ikpeɣ ɛyáa lɛlaa pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm mbʋ́ palabɩ-mɩ yɔ yɔ, mɩ-Caa weyi ɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, ekpeɣiɣ-mɩ ɖɔɖɔ mɩ-ñɩmbʋ.

eng: When Herod knew the magi had fooled him, he grew very angry. He sent soldiers to kill all the children in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding territory who were two years old and younger, according to the time that he had learned from the magi.
local: Eroodi kɔm ɛna se lɔŋsɩnɖaa ḿbá pɛcɛtɩ-ɩ kɩcɛtʋ, paapɩsɩɣ ɛ-cɔlɔ lɛ, ɛ-pana kpa pɩŋŋ nɛ ɛkʋsɩ sɔɔjanaa nɛ etiyi-wɛ se pɔcɔɔ Bɛtlɛhɛm tɛtʋ nɛ tɩ-cɔlɔ ñɩndʋ pa-taa nɛ pakʋ abalɩbiya nzɩ́ sɩtɩŋa payɩ sɩwɛnɩ pɩnzɩ naalɛ nɛ pɩkpaɣ pitii yɔ. Eheyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛkɛdɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ lɔŋsɩnɖaa keheyi-i yɔ.

eng: Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Jesus.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ Mooyiizi nɛ Elii palɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pa nɛ Yesu pɔyɔɔdʋʋ.

eng: This happened so that what Isaiah the prophet said would be fulfilled: He is the one who took our illnesses and carried away our diseases.
local: Pɩlaba ḿbʋ́ se pɩla ɛzɩ náyʋ Ezaayii yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Ɛmʋ-ɖʋ ɖɛ-ɛjandʋ nɛ ɖa-kʋdɔmɩŋ» yɔ.

eng: He went into Gods house and broke the law by eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat.
local: Ɛsʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ ɛkpaɣ tindine hɔɔlasɩ nzɩ́ paaha Ɛsɔ yɔ nɛ ɛ nɛ ɛyáa ḿbá pɔtɔɔ. Ŋgʋ́, pɩtaamʋnɩ se pa-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛtɔɔ-sɩ. Ɛlɛ́, cɔjɔnaa ɖeke pɩɩmʋnaa se pɔtɔɔ.

eng: He said many things to them in parables: A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Ɛyɔɔdaɣnɩ-wɛ tɔm sakɩyɛ nɛ aduwa taa. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ wɛnɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ewolo tɔɔnaɣ pee mɩzʋʋ ɛ-hayɩm taa.

eng: At her mothers urging, the girl said, Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a plate.
local: Peeɖe e-ɖoo tasɩ-ɩ tɔm se ɛyɔɔdɩ. Pʋ-yɔɔ eheyi Eroodi se: «Đʋ Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ ñʋʋ ñanaɣ taa nɛ ŋcɛlɩ-m.».

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Let me take the splinter out of your eye, when theres a log in your eye?.
local: Ɛzɩma ŋpɩzɩɣ ŋheyi lɛlʋ se: ‹Sɩŋ malɩzɩ ñɛ-ɛsɩyɛ taa kemaɣ› nɛ ŋŋnaɣ ɖaʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩwɛ ña-ñɩnɖɛ taa yɔ?.

eng: Happy are those who dont stumble and fall because of me. .
local: Weyi eelesiɣ e-lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ehiɣ ɛ-tɩ!».

eng: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those with skin diseases, and throw out demons. You received without having to pay. Therefore, give without demanding payment.
local: Ɩla nɛ kʋdɔndɩnaa yɔɔ cɛ. Ifezi sɩɖaa nɛ ɩla nɛ canasɩ pɩsɩ ɛyáa mbá pamʋnaa yɔ nɛ ɩɖɔɔnɩ aleewaa ɛyáa wayɩ. Maha-mɩ ɖoŋ falaa, pʋwɛɛ se ɩha falaa ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human rules . .
local: Pasaŋ-m nɛ palaɣ kɩlaŋ mɔ-yɔɔ ɛlɛ́ pa-samtʋ fɛyɩ-m kɛzɛɣa nakɛyɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paɣtʋ ndʋ́ pawɩlɩɣnɩ abalɩtʋ yɔ, tɩkɛ ɛyáa paɣtʋ.› ».

eng: Happy are people who are humble, because they will inherit the earth.
local: Mbá pɛwɛ hɛɛɛ yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-wɛ cɛlʋʋ kedeŋa ŋgá ɛɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ ɛ-ñɩma yɔ.

eng: But if you dont forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your sins.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee iikpeɣiɣ lɛlaa pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ, mɩ-Caa eekpeɣiɣ-mɩ ɖɔɖɔ mɩ-ñɩmbʋ.».

eng: This fulfilled the word spoken through Jeremiah the prophet:.
local: Ḿbʋ́ pɩlaba ɛzɩ náyʋ Yereemii kɔyɔɔdʋʋ se:.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Lord, its good that were here. If you want, Ill make three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ heyi Yesu se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Đɩwɛʋ cɩnɛ yɔ, pʋwɛ ɖeu pɩŋŋ. Yee ŋtisiɣ yɔ, mensiɣ kɩɖanɖɩŋ naadozo cɩnɛ: ña lɛɛkʋ, Mooyiizi lɛɛkʋ nɛ Elii lɛɛkʋ.».

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowd, he ordered his disciples to go over to the other side of the lake.
local: Kɩyakʋ nakʋyʋ Yesu wɛ Galilee teŋgu nɔɔ nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ woki ɛ-cɔlɔ. Peeɖe eheyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se ɛ nɛ wɛ pɛtɛzɩ teŋgu kidiiliŋ lɛɛkʋ yɔɔ.

eng: Or havent you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple treat the Sabbath as any other day and are still innocent?.
local: Yaa ɩtakalɩ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ takayɩsɩ taa se: cɔjɔnaa lakɩ tʋmɩyɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa halɩ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye yaa we? Ɛlɛ́, payʋsaa yaa we?.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path, and birds came and ate it.
local: Ɛmɩzaɣ lɛ, lɛɛna tɔlɩ nʋmɔʋ nɔɔ nɛ sumasɩ tii sɩtɔɔ-yɛ.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests he commanded that they give it to her.
local: Eroodi nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ-laŋɩyɛ wɛɛkɩ. Ŋgʋ́, ɛtɛm ɖuúu ɛyáa hɛkʋ taa se paa pɛlɛ ɛnʋ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ ɛbɛ yɔ, ɛcɛlɩɣ-ɩ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛha nɔɔ se.

eng: You deceive yourself! First take the log out of your eye, and then youll see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ tʋ ña! Lɩzɩ ɖaʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩwɛ ñɛ-ɛsɩyɛ taa yɔ nɛ pʋcɔ ŋna camɩyɛ nɛ ŋlɩzɩ kemaɣ ŋgá kɛwɛ ña-taabalʋ ñɩnɖɛ taa yɔ.

eng: When Johns disciples had gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Eheyi Yohanɛɛsɩ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pacaŋ pɛɖɛɛ. Peeɖe ɛpazɩ ɛyáa samaɣ kɛdʋʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ ɛnʋ ɛ-tɔm nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛbɛ iwobi naʋ lakʋ taa? Kañɩŋ́gbayaɣ́ ŋgá heɣlim ciɣdaɣ yɔ yaa we?.

eng: Workers deserve to be fed, so dont gather gold or silver or copper coins for your money belts to take on your trips.
local: Ɩɖɛɣ lɛ, ɩtaakpaɣ liidiye naɖɩyɛ kaʋ.

eng: Jesus called the crowd near and said to them, Listen and understand.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu kpeɣli ɛyáa samaɣ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Iwelesi nɛ ɩnɩɩ mɔn-tɔm camɩyɛ.

eng: Happy are people who are hungry and thirsty for righteousness, because they will be fed until they are full.
local: Mbá pañɩnɩɣ kpem se pala mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-wɛ haʋ mbʋ́ pañɩnɩɣ yɔ.

eng: And when you fast, dont put on a sad face like the hypocrites. They distort their faces so people will know they are fasting. I assure you that they have their reward.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Yee ɩhɔkʋʋ nɔɔ lɛ, ɩtaañɩŋ mɩ-ɛsɩndaa ɛzɩ tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ñɩma lakʋʋ yɔ. Peyeki se pɛ-ɛsɩndaa ɛlɛɣzɩ nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa na-wɛ se pɔhɔkʋʋ nɔɔ. Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se pɛtɛm pa-kɩhɛyʋʋ mʋ́ʋ.

eng: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and much grieving. Rachel weeping for her children, and she did not want to be comforted, because they were no more.
local: «Đɩnɩ nɔɔyʋ makɩ kubusi nɛ ewiliɣ nɛ ɛtɩmɩɣ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ Raamaa tɛtʋ taa. Đo-ɖoo Rakɛɛlɩ wiɣni e-piya nɛ ɛtɔsɔɔlɩ se pɛhɛzɩ ɛ-laŋɩyɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, sifetaa.».

eng: While he was still speaking, look, a bright cloud overshadowed them. A voice from the cloud said, This is my Son whom I dearly love. I am very pleased with him. Listen to him.
local: Ɛyɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛsɔmɩnʋʋ kɩñɩlɩsʋʋ nakʋyʋ kɔɔ kuhuuzi pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ panɩɣ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-wɛ nɛ kɩ-tɛɛ se: «Ɛnɛ lɛ man-Pɩyalʋ kʋsɔɔlʋ, maahʋʋ ɛ-yɔɔ kaʋ. Ɩnɩɩnɩ-ɩ!».

eng: A legal expert came and said to him, Teacher, Ill follow you wherever you go.
local: Ɛlɛ́, paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa taa nɔɔyʋ wolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ eheyiɣ-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Paa le ŋwoki yɔ, mantɩŋɩɣ-ŋ.».

eng: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
local: Ɖɔɖɔ lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se weyi ɛwɛ cɩnɛ yɔ, ɛkɩlɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Lɛɛna ñɔtɔlɩ ɛwaɣ yɔɔ. Tɛtʋ telim lɛ, añɔ lɔŋ.

eng: Then he had John beheaded in prison.
local: powolo salaka taa nɛ pɛsɛtɩ Yohanɛɛsɩ ñʋʋ.

eng: Dont give holy things to dogs, and dont throw your pearls in front of pigs. They will stomp on the pearls, then turn around and attack you.
local: «Ɩtaacɛlɩ Ɛsɔ ñɩm hasɩ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, sɩpɩsɩɣ sɩñasɩ-mɩ. Ɩtaacɛlɩ ɖɔɖɔ afanaa mɩ-lɩtʋ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, pamɩtɩɣ tɩ-yɔɔ.».

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who wear refined clothes are in royal palaces.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛbɛ iwobi naʋ kpem? Nɔɔyʋ suni ñɩɣlɩ́m wondu nɛ ɛwɛɛ peeɖe naa wɛ? Ŋgʋ́, mbá posuu ñɩɣlɩ́m wondu yɔ, pɛwɛɣ awiya ɖɩsɩ taa.

eng: Dont take a backpack for the road or two shirts or sandals or a walking stick.
local: Ɩtaakpaɣ huyuu yaa toko nakʋyʋ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ mbá pɛwɛ mɩ-yɔɔ yɔ yaa naataŋgbala, ɩtaaɖɔkɩ ɖaʋ. Toovenim lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se pɛcɛlɩ tʋmlaɖʋ mbʋ́ ɛcakɩnɩ wezuu yɔ.

eng: Its not what goes into the mouth that contaminates a person in Gods sight. Its what comes out of the mouth that contaminates the person. .
local: Pɩtɛkɛ mbʋ́ ɛyʋ tɔkɩ yɔ, pipisiɣni-i. Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ pɩlɩɣnɩ ɛ-nɔɔ taa yɔ, pipisiɣni-i.».

eng: Happy are people who show mercy, because they will receive mercy.
local: Mbá panaɣ lɛlaa pʋtɔdɩyɛ yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kɔŋ naʋ pa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ.

eng: When you fast, brush your hair and wash your face.
local: Ña lɛ, yee ŋhɔkʋʋ nɔɔ yɔ, ñɔɔzɩ ña-tɩ camɩyɛ.

eng: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.
local: Eroodi sɩba lɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ lɩɩ Yozɛɛfʋ yɔɔ ɖoziye taa Ejiptɩ.

eng: Hearing this, the disciples fell on their faces, filled with awe.
local: Tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa nɩ nɔɔ ŋ́gá lɛ, sɔɔndʋ kpa-wɛ pɩŋŋ nɛ pɔtɔlɩ tataa nɛ papa pɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ tɛtʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens, and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human One has no place to lay his head. .
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Kpayaa ñɛwɛnɩ pɔŋ nɛ sumasɩ ñɛwɛnɩ ɖandʋ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ ma mɛnfɛyɩnɩ ñʋʋ ɖɩɖʋyɛ.».

eng: If you had known what this means, I want mercy and not sacrifice , you wouldnt have condemned the innocent.
local: Yee ɩɩsɩm tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Ma Ɛsɔ mɔntɔsɔɔlɩ mɩ-kɩlaŋ. Ɛlɛ́, me-liu wɛɛ se ɛyáa ɛna ɖama pʋtɔdɩyɛ› yɔ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ yɔ, ɩtaakʋ mbá pɛtɛwɛɛkɩ nabʋyʋ yɔ pɔ-tɔm.

eng: But when the sun came up, it scorched the plants, and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Añɔwa nɛ akpa camɩyɛ fɛlɛ fɛlɛ lɛ, wɩsɩ ñaɣ nɛ awɩlɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, a-hosi taɖaɣlɩ.

eng: They brought his head on a plate and gave it to the young woman, and she brought it to her mother.
local: Pɛsɛtɩ-kʋ lɛ, paɖʋ ñanaɣ taa nɛ pɛcɛlɩ pɛlɛ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ woni ɛcɛlɩ e-ɖoo.

eng: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened to you.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Ɩsʋlʋmɩ Ɛsɔ, ɛhaɣ-mɩ, ɩñɩnɩ ɛ-cɔlɔ, ihikiɣ, ɩma pɔɔyʋʋ yɔɔ, ɛtʋlʋʋ-mɩ.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Ɛbɛ kpem iiwobi naʋ? Náyʋ nɔɔyʋ yaa we? Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɩna weyi yɔ, ɛkɩlɩ náyʋ.

eng: Whatever city or village you go into, find somebody in it who is worthy and stay there until you go on your way.
local: Ɩtalɩ tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ yaa cikpetu natʋyʋ taa lɛ, ɩñɩnɩ weyi etisiɣ nɛ ɛmʋ-mɩ yɔ. Yee ɛmʋ-mɩ lɛ, ɩcaɣ ɛ-tɛ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ ɩkʋyʋʋ yɔ.

eng: Then the disciples came and said to him, Do you know that the Pharisees were offended by what you just said.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa woba ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋnawa se tɔm ndʋ́ ŋyɔɔdaɣ yɔ, tɩwɩ Farɩsɩ ñɩma pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ yaa we?».

eng: Happy are people who have pure hearts, because they will see God.
local: Mbá pa-taa wɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɔkɔŋ Ɛsɔ naʋ.

eng: Then you wont look like you are fasting to people, but only to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
local: nɛ ɛyáa ɛtaatɩlɩ se ŋhɔkʋʋ nɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ña-Caa weyi paanaɣ-ɩ yɔ, ɛnʋ naɣna mbʋ́ pʋwɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛhɛyɩ-ŋ.».

eng: Get up, the angel said, and take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel. Those who were trying to kill the child are dead.
local: nɛ eheyi-i se: «Kʋyɩ ŋkpaɣ pɩɣa nɛ ko-ɖoo nɛ ña nɛ wɛ ɩpɩsɩ Izrɛɛlɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mbá pañɩnaɣ se pakʋ pɩɣa ŋ́gá yɔ, pasɩba.».

eng: But Jesus came and touched them. Get up, he said. Dont be afraid. .
local: Peeɖe ewobi pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛma pɔ-yɔɔ se: «Ɩkʋyɩ, sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-mɩ!».

eng: Another man, one of his disciples, said to him, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa lɛlʋ heyi-i ɖɔɖɔ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Yele mowolo menpi man-caa nɛ pʋcɔ mɔnkɔɔ mantɩŋ-ŋ.».

eng: The Human One is Lord of the Sabbath. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ ma mansɩm mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se pala kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye yɔ.».

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked them.
local: Lɛɛna tɔlɩ kpaŋsɔɔ taa nɛ añɔ ɛzɩ pɩɩkpa camɩyɛ lɛ, kpaŋsɔɔ lɛɣtɩ.

eng: But Johns disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus what had happened.
local: Peeɖe Yohanɛɛsɩ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa woba pakpaɣ-ɩ nɛ pepi. Pɛtɛm-ɩ pim lɛ, powolo pɛkɛdɩ Yesu mbʋ́ pɩlaba yɔ.

eng: For everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛsʋlʋmʋʋ-ɩ yɔ, ɛhaɣ-ɩ, weyi ɛñɩnɩɣ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ, ehikiɣ, weyi ɛ́lɛ́ makɩ pɔɔyʋʋ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛtʋlʋʋ-ɩ.

eng: He is the one of whom it is written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Yohanɛɛsɩ tɔm Ɛsɔ yɔɔdaa nɛ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Na, mancalɩɣ men-tiyiyu tiyuu nɛ ɛñɔɔzɩ ña-nʋmɔʋ.› ».

eng: When you go into a house, say, Peace.
local: Yee ɩsʋ ɖɩɣa lɛ, ɩsɛɛ ka-taa ñɩma se: ‹Ɩwɛɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa!›.

eng: Jesus replied, Every plant that my heavenly Father didnt plant will be pulled up.
local: Ecosi-wɛ se: «Tɩʋ ŋgʋ́ man-Caa weyi ɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, ɛtɔsɔ-kʋ yɔ, pɔkɔŋ-kʋ kpɛzʋʋ.

eng: Happy are people who make peace, because they will be called Gods children.
local: Mbá pañɩnɩɣ laŋhɛzɩyɛ yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-wɛ yaʋ se e-piya.

eng: Stop collecting treasures for your own benefit on earth, where moth and rust eat them and where thieves break in and steal them.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Ɩtaañɩnɩ ñɩm nɛ ikpeɣli-pʋ kedeŋa yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ŋgbam nɛ kálɩkɔ́daɣ pamʋʋ-pʋ yaa mɩ́láa yɔkʋʋ nɛ pamɩlɩ-pʋ.

eng: Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
local: Peeɖe Yozɛɛfʋ kʋyaa ɖɔɖɔ ɛkpaɣ pɩɣa nɛ ko-ɖoo nɛ papɩsɩ Izrɛɛlɩ.

eng: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
local: Pakʋsɩ pɛ-ɛsɛ lɛ, panaɣ Yesu ɖeke.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead. .
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Kɔɔ ŋtɩŋ-m, yele sɩɖaa nɛ pepi pa-sɩɖaa.».

eng: Jesus left that place and went into their synagogue.
local: Yesu tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ peeɖe nɛ ewolo ɛsʋʋ pɛ-Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa.

eng: Other seed fell on good soil and bore fruit, in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Piyele lɛɛna ñɔtɔlɩ tɛtʋ kɩbandʋ taa, alɛ ñɔ nɛ pɩpaɣlɩ tɩŋ nɛ ɩlʋlɩ. Tɩʋ lɛɛkʋ lʋlɩ pee mɩnʋʋ nɛ lɛɛkʋ nɩɩnʋwa nɛ hiu nɛ lɛɛkʋ niidozo.».

eng: When Jesus heard about John, he withdrew in a boat to a deserted place by himself. When the crowds learned this, they followed him on foot from the cities.
local: Yesu nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ewolo ɛkpa kpɩyʋʋ taa se ɛkɛsɩ ɛ-tɩ ɖenɖe ɛyáa fɛyɩ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛyáa samaɣ tɛm nɩʋ. Peeɖe palɩ tɛtʋ ndɩ ndɩ taa nɛ pɔcɔlʋʋ teŋgu nɔɔ se pakatɩ-ɩ ɖenɖe ewoki yɔ.

eng: Who among you will give your children a stone when they ask for bread?.
local: Mɩ-taa anɩ pɩzɩɣ ɛkpaɣ pɩyɛ nɛ ɛcɛlɩ ɛ-pɩɣa alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ kasʋlʋmʋʋ-ɩ tindine yɔ?.

eng: I assure you that no one who has ever been born is greater than John the Baptist. Yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se ɖooo yɔ, patalʋlɩdɩ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɛkɩlɩ Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ. Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, weyi ɛwɛ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa nɛ patakpaɣ-ɩ payaa pʋyʋ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ kɩlɩ-ɩ.

eng: If the house is worthy, give it your blessing of peace. But if the house isnt worthy, take back your blessing.
local: Yee pamʋ-mɩ lɛ, mɩ-laŋhɛzɩyɛ wɛɛ pɔ-cɔlɔ. Yee patamʋ-mɩ lɛ, ɖɩpɩsɩ mɩ-cɔlɔ.

eng: Leave the Pharisees alone. They are blind people who are guides to blind people. But if a blind person leads another blind person, they will both fall into a ditch. .
local: Iyebi-wɛ: pɛwɛ ɛzɩ yʋlʋmaa mbá pɔhɔŋ yʋlʋmaa lɛlaa yɔ! Ŋgʋ́, yee yʋlʋmaa ɛhɔŋ ɖama yɔ, pɔkɔŋ tɔlʋʋ pɔʋ nakʋyʋ taa patɩŋa.».

eng: Happy are people whose lives are harassed because they are righteous, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Mbá ɛyáa wɛ pa-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ se palakɩ mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɛkɛ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa ñɩma.

eng: Instead, collect treasures for yourselves in heaven, where moth and rust dont eat them and where thieves dont break in and steal them.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ikpeɣli-pʋ ɛsɔdaa ɖenɖe ŋgbam nɛ kálɩkɔ́daɣ paapɩzɩɣ pamʋʋ yaa mɩ́láa ɛɛpɩzɩɣ pɔyɔkɩ nɛ pamɩlɩ yɔ.

eng: But when he heard that Archelaus ruled over Judea in place of his father Herod, Joseph was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he went to the area of Galilee.
local: Patalɩ peeɖe lɛ, Yozɛɛfʋ nɩɩ se Arcɛlaayuusi lɛɣzɩnɩ ɛ-caa Eroodi lone taa Yudee egeetiye taa. Pʋ-yɔɔ sɔɔndʋ kpa-ɩ nɛ ɖɩ-taa caɣʋ. Ɛlɛ́, pʋyɔɔdɩnɩ-ɩ ɖoziye taa nɛ ɛkʋyɩ ewolo Galilee egeetiye taa.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, Dont tell anybody about the vision until the Human One is raised from the dead. .
local: Pakʋyɩ pʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ petiki lɛ, ɛpaɣ-wɛ se: «Ɩtaaheyi nɔɔyʋ mbʋ́ ɩnawa yɔ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ fema nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa yɔ.».

eng: When Jesus got into a boat, his disciples followed him.
local: Yesu kpa kpɩyʋʋ taa lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tɩŋ ɛ-wayɩ.

eng: A man with a withered hand was there. Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they asked, Does the Law allow a person to heal on the Sabbath.
local: Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe, e-nesi camaa. Mbá pɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ, pacaɣaɣ se pehiɣ kawanaɣ nakɛyɛ nɛ pakʋyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Pɩha nʋmɔʋ se pala nɛ kʋdɔndʋ nɔɔyʋ yɔɔ cɛ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye yaa pataala?».

eng: Everyone who has ears should pay attention. .
local: Yesu tɛm-wɛ tuuu eduuye lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se: «Weyi ɛwɛnɩ nɩŋgbaaŋ lɛ, ɛnɩɩ.».

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion for them and healed those who were sick.
local: Yesu lɩ kpɩyʋʋ taa nɛ ɛna ɛyáa pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔ-tɔm labɩ-ɩ pʋtɔdɩyɛ nɛ ɛla nɛ kʋdɔndɩnaa mbá powonaa yɔ, pɔ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: Or give them a snake when they ask for fish?.
local: Yaa mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ pɩzɩɣ ɛcɛlɩ ɛ-pɩɣa ɖʋm alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ kasʋlʋmʋʋ-ɩ kpakpayaɣ yɔ yaa we?.

eng: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven is violently attacked as violent people seize it.
local: Pɩkpaɣʋnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Yohanɛɛsɩ ɛnʋ esusi tɔm nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, ɛyáa wɛɛ pɛtɛzɩɣ kʋyʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ yɔɔ nɛ pañɩnɩɣnɩ ɖoŋ se pehiɣ-kɛ.

eng: If anyone refuses to welcome you or listen to your words, shake the dust off your feet as you leave that house or city.
local: Yee iwobi ɖɩɣa nakɛyɛ taa yaa tɛtʋ natʋyʋ taa nɛ nɔɔyʋ kizi se ɛɛmʋʋ-mɩ nɛ ewelesi mɩ-tɔm lɛ, ɩlɩɩ ɖɩɣa ŋ́gá ka-taa yaa tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa nɛ ikpizi mɩ-naŋgbanzɩ yɔɔ mʋzʋʋ nɛ ɩɖɛɛ nɛ pɩwɩlɩ se ɩkɔɔlɩ-wɛ nɛ iyebi-wɛ nɛ pa-sʋʋtʋ.

eng: Then Peter spoke up, Explain this riddle to us.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ pɔzɩ-ɩ se ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ eduuye ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-tɔbʋʋ.

eng: Happy are you when people insult you and harass you and speak all kinds of bad and false things about you, all because of me.
local: «Mbá mɩ patʋʋ-mɩ nɛ pɛwɛɛ mɩ-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ nɛ pɛcɛtɩɣnɩ mɩ-taa tɔm ndɩ ndɩ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ihiɣ mɩ-tɩ!.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖenɖe ña-ñɩm wɛɛ yɔ, peeɖe ɖɔɖɔ ña-lɩmaɣza wɛɛ.».

eng: He settled in a city called Nazareth so that what was spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled: He will be called a Nazarene.
local: nɛ ɛcaɣ tɛtʋ ndʋ́ payaɣ se Nazarɛɛtɩ yɔ tɩ-taa. Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se tɔm ndʋ́ náyaa kɔyɔɔdaa yɔ, tɩla. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Pɔkɔŋ pɩɣa ŋ́gá yaʋ se Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ.».

eng: The disciples asked, Then why do the legal experts say that Elijah must first come.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa tɔŋ se pʋwɛɛ se náyʋ Elii ɛlɛyɩ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ kɔm?».

eng: A huge storm arose on the lake so that waves were sloshing over the boat. But Jesus was asleep.
local: Pɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, heɣlim sɔsɔm nabʋyʋ pazɩ mabʋ ɖoŋ nɛ lɩm sʋʋ kpɩyʋʋ taa. Ɛlɛ́, Yesu ɖowaɣ.

eng: Jesus replied, Who among you has a sheep that falls into a pit on the Sabbath and will not take hold of it and pull it out?.
local: Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Yee mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛwɛnɩ heu kʋɖʋm koŋ nɛ heu ɛnʋ ɛtɔlɩ pɔʋ nakʋyʋ taa kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye, pʋdʋ eewoki ɛlɩzɩ-ɩ yaa we?».

eng: Jesus disciples came and said to him, Why do you use parables when you speak to the crowds.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa woba ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ŋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ ɛyáa nɛ aduwa ḿbʋ́?».

eng: That evening his disciples came and said to him, This is an isolated place and its getting late. Send the crowds away so they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves.
local: Đanaɣ ɖanaa lɛ, Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa wolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ peheyi-i se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, wɩsɩ kpema. Ŋgʋ́, ñɩtʋ hɛkʋ taa ɖɩwɛɛ. Heyi ɛyáa samaɣ ŋgá kɛwɛ cɩnɛ yɔ se kowolo tɛtʋ cikpetu taa nɛ kaya tɔɔnaɣ nɛ kɔtɔɔ.».

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good things to those who ask him.
local: Mbá mɩ ɩkɛ azaɣ́a yɔ, itekizi haʋ mi-piya mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ nɛ pɩcandɩnɩ mɩ-Caa Ɛsɔ weyi ɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ? Ɛhaɣ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ mbá paba sʋlʋmʋʋ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: All the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John came.
local: Náyaa tɩŋa nɛ Mooyiizi pɔyɔɔdɩ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ ŋ́gá kɔ-tɔm nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Yohanɛɛsɩ ɛnʋ ɛkɔma yɔ.

eng: I assure you that it will be more bearable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on Judgment Day than it will be for that city.
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Ɛsɔ hʋʋnɩ ɛyáa yɔ, Sodom nɛ Gomɔɔrɩ ñɩma mbá Ɛsɔ kʋ-wɛ nɛ miŋ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ yɔ, pa-kʋñɔŋ ɛɛtalɩɣ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa ñɩma ñɩŋgʋ.».

eng: Be full of joy and be glad, because you have a great reward in heaven. In the same way, people harassed the prophets who came before you.
local: Mɩ-taa ɛhʋlʋmɩ nɛ ɩmayɩ amaya, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩ-kɩhɛyʋʋ ɖɔ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ. Meheyiɣ-mɩ se ḿbʋ́ paawɛ náyaa mbá pɛlɛyɩ-mɩ kɔm yɔ pa-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ.».

eng: Jesus said, Dont you understand yet?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Mɩ ɖɔɖɔ, halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ ɩɩpɩzɩɣ se ɩnɩɩ tɔm ndʋ́ mɔyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ naa we?.

eng: How much more valuable is a person than a sheep! So the Law allows a person to do what is good on the Sabbath. .
local: Ŋgʋ́, ɛyʋ ñakɩlɩ heu wazaɣ tam sakɩyɛ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pɩha nʋmɔʋ se pala ɛyʋ camɩyɛ lakasɩ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye.

eng: Jesus replied, Because they havent received the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but you have.
local: Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Ɛsɔ taha-wɛ lɛɣtʋ se panɩɩ e-Kewiyaɣ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩwɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ, tɩ-tɔbʋʋ yɔɔ mɔyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-wɛ ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɛ́, mɩ lɛ, ɛha-mɩ lɛɣtʋ se ɩnɩɩ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ.

eng: Look, Im sending you as sheep among wolves. Therefore, be wise as snakes and innocent as doves.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Ɩnɩɩ camɩyɛ se mentiyuu-mɩ yɔ, ɩwɛ ɛzɩ heŋ wɛɣʋ kpemtɔɔyɩsɩ hɛkʋ taa yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se ɩwɛɛ lɔŋ ɛzɩ ɖʋmaa yɔ nɛ ɩmʋna ɛzɩ halikukunaa yɔ.

eng: Therefore, you should treat people in the same way that you want people to treat you; this is the Law and the Prophets.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mbʋ́ ɩcaɣ se ɛyáa ɛlabɩ-mɩ yɔ, mɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɩlabɩ-wɛ ḿbʋ́. Ḿbʋ́ Mooyiizi weyi Ɛsɔ cɛlɩ paɣtʋ yɔ nɛ náyaa paɖʋwa se ɩla.».

eng: They came and woke him, saying, Lord, rescue us! Were going to drown.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa woba ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ pefezi-i se: «Hayɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ya ɖa-ñʋʋ! Đɩtɛŋɛlɩɣ cɩnɛ!».

eng: But Jesus said to them, Theres no need to send them away. You give them something to eat. .
local: Peeɖe ecosi-wɛ se: «Pɩtɔpɔzɩ se pɛɖɛɛ. Ɩha-wɛ tɔɔnaɣ mɩ-maɣmaɣ nɛ pɔtɔɔ.».

eng: Jesus responded, Elijah does come first and will restore all things.
local: Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se Elii ɛlɛyɩnɩ kɔm nɛ ɛɖaɣnɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ.

eng: The eye is the lamp of the body. Therefore, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Ɛyʋ ɛsɛ lɛ e-tomnaɣ kanɖʋ, ana ɛnaɣna. Yee ñɛ-ɛsɛ ɩnaɣ camɩyɛ yɔ, ñatɩŋa payɩ ŋwɛ ñalɩmɩyɛ taa.

eng: If you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
local: Yee mi-liu ɛwɛɛ se itisiɣ mɔn-tɔm yɔ, Yohanɛɛsɩ ɛnʋ lɛ Elii weyi paayɔɔdaa se ɛkɔŋ yɔ.

eng: For those who have will receive more and they will have more than enough. But as for those who dont have, even the little they have will be taken away from them.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pɔtɔŋʋʋ se: ‹Weyi ɛwɛna yɔ, pɔsɔzʋʋ-ɩ nɛ pʋɖɔɔ-ɩ nɛ weyi ɛ́lɛ́ fɛyɩna yɔ, halɩ pɛtɛzɩɣ lɛɣʋ mbʋ́ ɛmaɣzɩɣ se ɛwɛna yɔ.›.

eng: You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Its good for nothing except to be thrown away and trampled under peoples feet.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Mɩ lɛ kedeŋa ɖɔm. Ɛlɛ́, yee ɖɔm leleŋ ɛsɩba lɛ, ɛzɩma palakɩ-pʋ? Pɩfɛyɩ wazaɣ nakɛyɛ ɖɔɖɔ kpem. Pɔlɔʋ-pʋ awayɩ yem nɛ ɛyáa ɖɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: Go in through the narrow gate. The gate that leads to destruction is broad and the road wide, so many people enter through it.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Ɩtɩŋnɩ ɖɛɛmɛnɖɛɛ taa nɛ ihiɣ wezuu. Nɔnɔɔ kɩwalaɣ nɛ nʋmɔʋ kɩwalʋʋ patɩŋnaʋ fɛyɩ kaɖɛ. Yee ɩtɩŋnɩ-wɛ yɔ, ileki. Ɛlɛ́, ɛyáa mbá patɩŋɩɣnɩ-wɛ yɔ pɔɖɔ sakɩyɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and it was made healthy, just like the other one.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi abalʋ ɛnʋ se: «Losi ñe-nesi!» Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ losi-sɩ nɛ sɩ-yɔɔ cɛ nɛ sɩwɛɛ ɛzɩ lɛɛsɩ yɔ.

eng: They replied, We have nothing here except five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Ɛlɛ́, peheyi-i se tindine hɔɔlasɩ kagbaanzɩ nɛ kpakpasɩ naalɛ ɖeke wɛnɩ pɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they didnt know him. But they did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way the Human One is also going to suffer at their hands. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: Elii tɛm kɔm, ɛlɛ́, ɛyáa tasɩm-ɩ, palabɩ-ɩ mbʋ́ pɔsɔɔlaa yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pɔkɔŋ ɖʋʋ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ maɣmaɣ kʋñɔŋ.».

eng: Watch out for people—because they will hand you over to councils and they will beat you in their synagogues.
local: Ɩkpa mɩ-tɩ nɛ ɛyáa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɔkɔŋ-mɩ wonaʋ tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ nɛ polu-mɩ agbazɛɛ pɛ-Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ taa.

eng: He said to them, Why are you afraid, you people of weak faith? Then he got up and gave orders to the winds and the lake, and there was a great calm.
