eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible for human beings. But all things are possible for God. .
local: Peeɖe ɛcɔnɩ-wɛ pɩŋŋ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɛyáa ɩɩpɩzɩɣna, ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ ñapɩzɩɣ ɛla pɩtɩŋa payɩ.».

eng: Then Peter replied, Look, weve left everything and followed you. What will we have.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ heyi-i se: «Cɔnɩ ŋna ɖiyebi mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɖɩwɛna yɔ nɛ ɖɩtɩŋ ña-wayɩ. Ɛbɛ pɩlɩɣ-ɖʋ pɩ-taa?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you who have followed me that, when everything is made new, when the Human One sits on his magnificent throne, you also will sit on twelve thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Peeɖe ecosi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ ma mɔnkɔŋ mancaɣ men-kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ koboyaɣ taa yɔ, mbá mɩ ɩtɩŋaɣ ma-wayɩ yɔ, ɩkɔŋ caɣʋ awiya kpelisi hiu nɛ naalɛ yɔɔ nɛ ɩhʋʋnɩ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma cejewee hiu nɛ naalɛ.

eng: And all who have left houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, children, or farms because of my name will receive one hundred times more and will inherit eternal life.
local: Mbá peyebi pa-ɖɩsɩ yaa pa-ɖalaa yaa pɔ-kɔɔnaa yaa pa-caanaa yaa po-ɖoonaa yaa pe-piya yaa pa-hayɩm mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, pɔkɔŋ wɛnaʋ mbʋ́ mbʋ́ paawɛna yɔ tam mɩnʋʋ nɛ pɛtɛ pehiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: «Ɛlɛ́, ɛyáa mbá pɛkɛ sɔsaa lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, pa-taa sakɩyɛ kɔŋ pɩsʋʋ cikpema nɛ mbá paba kɛ cikpema lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, pa-taa sakɩyɛ ñapɩsɩ sɔsaa.».

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
local: Yesu heyi-wɛ ɖɔɖɔ se: «Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩɩnaɣ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ nɩɣzɩnaa yɔ: ñɩm tʋ nɔɔyʋ lɩnɩ tanaŋ nakʋyʋ cu nɛ ɛmʋ tʋmlaɖaa nabɛyɛ se palabɩ-ɩ tʋmɩyɛ ɛ-tɩŋ weyi popuu i-pee sʋlʋm yɔ ɩ-hayɩm taa.

eng: After he agreed with the workers to pay them a denarion, he sent them into his vineyard.
local: Peeɖe ɛ nɛ wɛ panɩnɩ ɖama se ɛhɛyɩɣ-wɛ liidiye pɔɔyaɣ evemiye taa nɛ ewoni-wɛ pɩ-taa.

eng: Then he went out around nine in the morning and saw others standing around the marketplace doing nothing.
local: Pʋkɔma ɛzɩ ñɩɣtʋ nakʋ yɔ lɛ, ɛlɩɩ samaɣ ɖikpeɣliye nɛ ɛtasɩ nabɛyɛ naʋ, paba sɩŋ yem, paalakɩ pʋyʋ.

eng: He said to them, You also go into the vineyard, and Ill pay you whatever is right.
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-wɛ se: ‹Mɩ ɖɔɖɔ iwolo ɩla tʋmɩyɛ ma-hayɩm taa nɛ mɛhɛyɩ-mɩ mbʋ́ pʋmʋnaa yɔ.›.

eng: And they went. Again around noon and then at three in the afternoon, he did the same thing.
local: Peeɖe ɛ nɛ wɛ patɩŋ kajalaɣ ñɩma wayɩ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛtasɩ lɩʋ wɩsɩ siɣu taa nɛ ɖanaɣ ñɩɣtʋ naadozo yɔɔ nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ lɛlaa ɖɔɖɔ nɛ eheyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́.

eng: Around five in the afternoon he went and found others standing around, and he said to them, Why are you just standing around here doing nothing all day long.
local: Pʋkɔma ɛzɩ ɖanaɣ ñɩɣtʋ kagbaanzɩ yɔ lɛ, ɛtasɩ lɩʋ peeɖe nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ nabɛyɛ sɩŋ ɖɔɖɔ yem. Ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: ‹Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩsɩŋ cɩnɛ kɩyakʋ nɛ kɩ-tɛɛ pilim nɛ ɩɩlakɩ pʋyʋ?›.

eng: Because nobody has hired us, they replied. He responded, You also go into the vineyard.
local: Peeɖe paba cosi-i se: ‹Nɔɔyʋ takpaɣ-ɖʋ se ɖɩlabɩ-ɩ tʋmɩyɛ.› Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-wɛ se: ‹Yee ḿbʋ́ ɩtɩŋ lɛlaa wayɩ ma-hayɩm taa.›.

eng: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, Call the workers and give them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and moving on finally to the first.
local: «Wɩsɩ tɛma lɛ, ñɩm tʋ ɛnʋ eheyi weyi ɛcɔŋnɩ tʋmlaɖaa yɔɔ yɔ se: ‹Yaa-wɛ nɛ ŋhɛyɩ-wɛ pe-liidiye. Ɛlɛ́, pazɩnɩ wayɩ wayɩ ñɩma nɛ pɩɖɩɣnɩ kajalaɣ ñɩma.›.

eng: When those who were hired at five in the afternoon came, each one received a denarion.
local: Mbá powobi hayɩm taa ɖanaɣ ñɩɣtʋ kagbaanzɩ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛhɛyɩ paa pa-taa weyi liidiye pɔɔyaɣ.

eng: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarion.
local: Pɩtalɩnɩ kajalaɣ ñɩma lɛ, ɛhɛyɩ-wɛ ɖɔɖɔ pɔɔyaɣ pɔɔyaɣ. Ŋgʋ́, pamaɣzaɣ se pɛhɛyɩɣ-wɛ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ mbá potowolo lɔŋ yɔ.

eng: When they received it, they grumbled against the landowner.
local: Peeɖe papazɩ pɩɣyɩnaʋ ñɩm tʋ ɛnʋ.

eng: These who were hired last worked one hour, and they received the same pay as we did even though we had to work the whole day in the hot sun.
local: nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: ‹Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋhɛyɩ ɖa nɛ mbá palabɩ tʋmɩyɛ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ ɖeke yɔ liidiye ɖeyi ɖeyi? Ŋgʋ́, ɖɛwɛnɩ tʋmɩyɛ taa ɖooo tanaŋ tɛɛ nɛ ɖɩla tʋmɩyɛ siŋŋ nɛ wɩsɩ ñaɣ-ɖʋ!›.

eng: But he replied to one of them, Friend, I did you no wrong. Didnt I agree to pay you a denarion?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ecosi pa-taa lɛlʋ se: ‹Taŋ! Montomuli-ŋ. Ma nɛ ŋ ɖɩtanɩɩnɩ ɖama se mɛhɛyɩɣ-ŋ liidiye pɔɔyaɣ evemiye taa yaa we?.

eng: Take what belongs to you and go. I want to give to this one who was hired last the same as I give to you.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mʋ ñe-ɖeɖe hɛɛɛ nɛ ŋɖɛɛ. Manɖʋna se mɛhɛyɩɣ ña nɛ wayɩ wayɩ tʋ ɖeyi ɖeyi.

eng: Dont I have the right to do what I want with what belongs to me? Or are you resentful because Im generous.
local: Mɛnfɛyɩnɩ waɖɛ se malabɩnɩ me-liidiye mbʋ́ me-liu wɛɛ yɔ yaa we? Yaa man-camɩyɛ lakasɩ yɔɔ ŋlakɩ ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ ḿbʋ́?› ».

eng: So those who are last will be first. And those who are first will be last. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Ḿbʋ́ wayɩ ñɩma kɔŋ pɩsʋʋ kajalaɣ ñɩma nɛ kajalaɣ ñɩma ñapɩsɩ wayɩ ñɩma.».

eng: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the Twelve aside by themselves on the road. He told them.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pakpaɣaɣ Yeruusalɛm. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛkɛsɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se:.

eng: Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and legal experts. They will condemn him to death.
local: «Ɩna, ɖɩkpaʋ Yeruusalɛm yɔ, pakpaɣ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ nɛ paɖʋzɩ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa pe-nesi taa. Pɔkɔŋ-ɩ hʋnaʋ nɛ pakʋ ɛ-tɔm nɛ paɖʋ se pakʋ-ɩ.

eng: They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be ridiculed, tortured, and crucified. But he will be raised on the third day. .
local: Paɖʋzʋʋ-ɩ mbá paañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ pe-nesi taa nɛ paba woŋ-i nɛ polu-i agbazɛɛ nɛ pakam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɛsɩ. Kɩyakʋ naadozo ñɩŋgʋ wiye lɛ, Ɛsɔ fezi-i.».

eng: Then the mother of Zebedees sons came to Jesus along with her sons. Bowing before him, she asked a favor of him.
local: Yesu tɛm ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa heyuu ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Zebedee halʋ kpaɣ ɛ-pɩyalaa Yakubu nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ nɛ powolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ eluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ nɛ ewini-i.

eng: What do you want? he asked. She responded, Say that these two sons of mine will sit, one on your right hand and one on your left, in your kingdom.
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ɛbɛ ŋcaɣ se malabɩ-ŋ?» Ecosi-i se: «Mancaɣ se ŋkɔm ŋcaɣ ñe-kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ lɛ, ŋyele nɛ man-pɩyalaa panɛ pacaɣ ñɔ-cɔlɔ: lɛlʋ ña-nɩwaŋ yɔɔ nɛ lɛlʋ ña-nɩmɩyɛ yɔɔ.».

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink from the cup that Im about to drink from? They said to him, We can.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Ɩɩsɩŋ mbʋ́ ɩsʋlʋmʋʋ yɔ. Ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ sɔsɔʋ ŋgʋ́ mɔnkɔŋ tɔɔʋ yɔ?» Peeɖe Yakubu nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ pocosi-i se: «Ɛɛɛ! Đɩpɩzɩɣ ɖɩtɔɔ-kʋ.».

eng: He said to them, You will drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom my Father prepared it. .
local: Yesu pɩsɩnɩ-wɛ se: «Toovenim ɩkɔŋ tɔɔʋ kʋñɔŋ ŋgʋ́ mɔntɔkɩ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ma-nɩwaŋ yɔɔ nɛ ma-nɩmɩyɛ yɔɔ caɣʋ ñɩndʋ lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ mahaɣnɩ nʋmɔʋ. Mbá man-Caa liu wɛɛ se pacaɣ lona ana a-taa yɔ, ḿbá cakɩna.».

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with the two brothers.
local: Tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu mbá pɩkazaa yɔ, panɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa-taa wɩɩnɩ pa-naalɛ.

eng: But Jesus called them over and said, You know that those who rule the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Peeɖe Yesu ya patɩŋa nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩtɛm sɩm se mbá pɛkɛ awiya sɔsɔna yɔ, ponuutuu pa-ajɛya taa ñɩma nɛ sɔsaa ñawɩlɩɣ-wɛ ɖɔɖɔ po-ɖoŋ.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Mɩ lɛ, wɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩtaawɛɛ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa. Yee mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛcaɣ se ɛkɛ sɔsɔ yɔ, pʋdʋ ɛpɩsɩ mɩ-tʋmlaɖʋ.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be your slave—.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛcaɣ se ɛkɛ mɩ-ñʋʋdʋ yɔ, pʋdʋ ɛpɩsɩ mɩtɩŋa mi-yom.

eng: just as the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people. .
local: Halɩ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ tɔkɔɔ se ɛyáa ɛlabɩ-ɩ tʋmɩyɛ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛkɔma se ɛlabɩ-wɛ tʋmɩyɛ nɛ ɛkpaɣ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-tɩ ɛhɛyɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ puholi pa-taa sakɩyɛ.».

eng: As Jesus and his disciples were going out of Jericho a large crowd followed him.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa patalɩ Yeriikoo tɛtʋ taa. Palɩ tɩ-taa se pɛɖɛɣ lɛ, ɛyáa samaɣ sɔsɔɔ nakɛyɛ tɩŋɩɣ-wɛ.

eng: When two blind men sitting along the road heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Yʋlʋmaa naalɛ nabɛyɛ kacaɣ peeɖe nʋmɔʋ nɔɔ. Panɩ Yesu taŋ lɛ, pama kubusi se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ, wiyaʋ Daaviidi sayɩ! Na ɖa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ.».

eng: Now the crowd scolded them and told them to be quiet. But they shouted even louder, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Peeɖe ɛyáa samaɣ ñazɩ-wɛ se: «Isu peeɖe!» Ɛlɛ́, pɛtɛzɩ kubusi mabʋ ɖoŋ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ, wiyaʋ Daaviidi Sayɩ! Na ɖa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ.».

eng: Jesus stopped in his tracks and called to them. What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu sɩŋ nɛ ɛ́yáa-wɛ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛbɛ ɩcaɣ se malabɩ-mɩ?».

eng: Lord, we want to see, they replied.
local: Pocosi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Đɩcaɣ se ŋla nɛ ɖɛ-ɛsɛ kuli.».

eng: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they were able to see, and they followed him.
local: Peeɖe Yesu na pa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ nɛ etukuni pɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ patɩpazɩ naʋ nɛ pa-naalɛ patɩŋɩɣ ɛ-wayɩ.

eng: When they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Yesu nɛ ɛyáa mbá patɩŋaɣ-ɩ yɔ, pɔñɔtɩnɩ Yeruusalɛm Bɛtfaajee hɔɔlʋʋ taa Somiŋ Pʋʋ liu tɛɛ lɛ, etiyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa naalɛ se:.

eng: He said to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter, you will find a donkey tied up and a colt with it. Untie them and bring them to me.
local: «Iwolo tɛtʋ cikpetu ndʋ́ tɩwɛ ɛsɩndaa yɔ tɩ-taa. Ɩtalɩɣ lɛ, ɩmakɩnɩ paɖɩ kpaŋaɣ nakɛyɛ nɛ ke-piye. Ɩyɛɖɩ-wɛ nɛ ɩkɔna.

eng: If anyone says anything to you, say that their master needs them. He sent them off right away.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛpɔzɩ-mɩ natʋyʋ lɛ, iheyi-i se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ liu wɛ-wɛ.› Pʋbʋ, pʋdʋ yeki-mɩ nɛ ɩɖɛɛ.».

eng: Now this happened to fulfill what the prophet said.
local: Pʋnɛ pɩlabɩ se tɔm ndʋ́ náyʋ yɔɔdaa yɔ, tɩla. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se:.

eng: Say to Daughter Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble and riding on a donkey, and on a colt the donkeys offspring. .
local: «Iheyi Siiyɔɔ ñɩma se: ɩcɔnɩ ɩna, mi-wiyaʋ sɔsɔ kɔŋ! Ɛwɛ hɛɛɛ. Ɛcaɣ kpaŋaɣ nɛ ke-piye, kpaŋaɣ ŋgá kasɩɣlɩ sʋʋtʋ sakɩyɛ yɔ ke-piye.».

eng: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had ordered them.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ḿbá pɛɖɛwa nɛ pala ɛzɩ eheyuu-wɛ yɔ.

eng: They brought the donkey and the colt and laid their clothes on them. Then he sat on them.
local: Pɔkɔnɩ kpaŋaɣ nɛ ke-piye lɛ, pɔhɔzɩ po-wondu lɛɛtʋ nɛ pɔpɔ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ Yesu kpa ɛcaɣ.

eng: Now a large crowd spread their clothes on the road. Others cut palm branches off the trees and spread them on the road.
local: Peeɖe samaɣ taa ɛyáa sakɩyɛ hɔzɩ po-wondu lɛɛtʋ nɛ pɔpɔ nʋmɔʋ taa. Lɛlaa sɛtɩ hatʋ nɛ pɔpɔ ɖɔɖɔ se paɖʋ-ɩ hɩɖɛ.

eng: The crowds in front of him and behind him shouted, Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Mbá paawɛ Yesu ɛsɩndaa nɛ mbá patɩŋaɣ ɛ-wayɩ yɔ, pokou se: «Ozaana! Wiyaʋ Daaviidi Sayɩ! Ɛsɔ ɛ́wazɩ weyi ɛkɔŋ Kɩpaɣlʋ lone taa yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ. Ozaana! Đooo ɛsɔdaa!».

eng: And when Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up. Who is this? they asked.
local: Yesu talɩ Yeruusalɛm lɛ, piseɣti ɛyáa tɩŋa nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ se: «Anɩ yɔ ɛnɛ?».

eng: The crowds answered, Its the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ ŋgá katɩŋaɣ-ɩ yɔ, kocosi-wɛ se: «Ɛnʋ lɛ náyʋ Yesu, ɛ-tɛ Nazarɛɛtɩ Galilee egeetiye taa.».

eng: Then Jesus went into the temple and threw out all those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Yesu talɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛsʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa kadaɣ taa nɛ ɛɖɔɔnɩ pɛdɩyaa nɛ yaɖaa patɩŋa payɩ. Etuzi liidiye yɔkɩyaa taabɩlɩnaa nɛ halikukunaa pɛdɩyaa kpelisi nɛ ɛpɛɖɩ.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be called a house of prayer. But youve made it a hideout for crooks. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi mbá pɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ se: «Ɛsɔ yɔɔdaa nɛ pama ɛ-Tɔm Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Man-ɖɩɣa kɛɣnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ.› Ŋgʋ́, mɩ mɩpɩsɩ-kɛ mɩ́láa ñɩŋga.».

eng: People who were blind and lame came to Jesus in the temple, and he healed them.
local: Ɛwɛ peeɖe ḿbʋ́ lɛ, yʋlʋmaa nɛ kalɩkalasɩ powolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛla nɛ pɔ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: But when the chief priests and legal experts saw the amazing things he was doing and the children shouting in the temple, Hosanna to the Son of David! they were angry.
local: Cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa paawɛ peeɖe ɖɔɖɔ. Pana hama lakasɩ nzɩ́ Yesu lakaɣ yɔ nɛ panɩɩ piya kou se: «Ozaana! Wiyaʋ Daaviidi Sayɩ» lɛ, pɩwɩzɩ-wɛ.

eng: They said to Jesus, Do you hear what these children are saying? Yes, he answered. Havent you ever read, From the mouths of babies and infants youve arranged praise for yourself? .
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋnɩɣ ndʋ́ piya ńzɩ́ sɩyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ?» Ecosi-wɛ se: «Ɛɛɛ! Yee ḿbʋ́, ɩtakalɩdɩ tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ kaʋ yaa we? Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Ña Ɛsɔ, halɩ ŋyeba nɛ piya sɔsɔsɩ nɛ cikpesi pasam-ŋ.› ».

eng: Then he left them and went out of the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu yebi-wɛ peeɖe nɛ ewolo Betaanii nɛ ɛsʋʋ.

eng: Early in the morning as Jesus was returning to the city, he was hungry.
local: Tɛʋ fema lɛ, Yesu kʋyɩ se ɛpɩsɩɣ Yeruusalɛm nɛ ñɔɔsɩ kpa-ɩ.

eng: He saw a fig tree along the road, but when he came to it, he found nothing except leaves. Then he said to it, Youll never again bear fruit! The fig tree dried up at once.
local: Peeɖe elosi hiyuú nakʋyʋ nɛ ewolo kʋ-cɔlɔ, ɛlɛ́, ɛmaɣnɩ hatʋ ɖeke kʋ-yɔɔ kpemm. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛtɔm se: «Ña! Ŋtasɩ lʋlʋʋ lɛ, maana!» Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, hiyuú ŋ́gʋ́ kɩwɩlɩ.

eng: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed. How did the fig tree dry up so fast? they asked.
local: Ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa na ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩɖɩɣzɩ-wɛ nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ɛzɩma hiyuú ŋ́gʋ́ kɩwɩlɩ kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́?».

eng: Jesus responded, I assure you that if you have faith and dont doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree. You will even say to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the lake. And it will happen.
local: Peeɖe ecosi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se yee ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ nɛ ɩɩpʋtʋʋ yɔ, ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩla mbʋ́ malabɩ hiyuú ŋ́gʋ́ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩɩsɩɣnɩ pi-ɖeke, halɩ ɩpɩzaɣ se iheyi pʋʋ kʋnɛ se: ‹Kpɛzɩ peeɖe nɛ ŋwolo ŋtɔlɩ teŋgu taa› nɛ pɩla ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: If you have faith, you will receive whatever you pray for. .
local: Yee ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛhaɣ-mɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩpɔzʋʋ-ɩ mɩ-Ɛsɔ sɛʋ taa yɔ.».

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, the chief priests and elders of the people came to him as he was teaching. They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu pɩsɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa kadaɣ taa nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ ɛyáa Ɛsɔtɔm. Peeɖe Yuda ñɩma sɔsaa nɛ pɔ-cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa powobi ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Aha-ŋ nɔɔ se ŋɖɔɔnɩ ɛyáa? Aha-ŋ waɖɛ se ŋla ḿbʋ́?».

eng: Jesus replied, I have a question for you. If you tell me the answer, Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Ma mɔnpɔzʋʋ-mɩ tɔm kʋɖʋmtʋ koŋ, yee icosi-m lɛ, meheyi-mɩ weyi ɛha-m nɔɔ se mala ḿbʋ́ yɔ.

eng: Where did John get his authority to baptize? Did he get it from heaven or from humans? They argued among themselves, If we say from heaven, hell say to us, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Mɔnpɔzʋʋ-mɩ se: atiyi Yohanɛɛsɩ se ɛla ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ? Ɛsɔ yaa ɛyáa tiyini-i?» Ɛlɛ́, pɔyɔɔdɩnɩ ɖama se: «Yee ɖicosaa se: ‹Ɛsɔ tiyini-i› yɔ, ɛpɔzʋʋ-ɖʋ se: ‹Nɛ pɩla ɛzɩma ɖɩtamʋ ɛ-tɔm yɔ?›.

eng: But we cant say from humans because were afraid of the crowd, since everyone thinks John was a prophet.
local: Ŋgʋ́, sɔɔndʋ kpaɣ-ɖʋ se ɖɩtɔ se: ‹Ɛyáa tiyini-i,› mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛyáa tɩŋa maɣzɩɣ se Yohanɛɛsɩ kɛ náyʋ.».

eng: Then they replied, We dont know. Jesus also said to them, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pocosi-i se: «Đɩɩsɩŋ.» Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ pɩsɩnɩ-wɛ se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ ma ɖɔɖɔ maaheyiɣ-mɩ weyi ɛha-m nɔɔ ŋ́gá yɔ.».

eng: What do you think? A man had two sons. Now he came to the first and said, Son, go and work in the vineyard today.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ pɔzʋʋ se: «Se we ɩmaɣzɩɣ eduuye ɖɩnɛ ɖɩ-tɔm? Ńɖɩ́ lɛ se: abalʋ nɔɔyʋ wɛnɩ ḿbʋ́, ɛwɛnɩ abalɩbiya naalɛ. Nakʋyʋ wiye lɛ, eheyi sɩ-taa sɔsɔɔ se: ‹Man-pɩyalʋ! Sɔnɔ wolo ŋla tʋmɩyɛ man-tɩŋ weyi popuu i-pee sʋlʋm yɔ ɩ-hayɩm taa.›.

eng: No, I dont want to, he replied. But later he changed his mind and went.
local: Ɛlɛ́, kocosi-i se: ‹Maawoki.› Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, kɛlɛɣzɩ lɩmaɣza nɛ kakʋyɩ kɛɖɛɛ pɩ-taa.

eng: The father said the same thing to the other son, who replied, Yes, sir. But he didnt go.
local: Abalʋ ɛnʋ, eheyi lɛɛka ɖɔɖɔ ḿbʋ́. Kaka cosi-i se: ‹Caa, mowoki.› Ɛlɛ́, kotowolo.».

eng: Which one of these two did his fathers will? They said, The first one. Jesus said to them, I assure you that tax collectors and prostitutes are entering Gods kingdom ahead of you.
local: Peeɖe Yesu pɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Sɩ-naalɛ sɩ-taa lɛ, ŋgá kalabɩ mbʋ́ ka-caa liu wɛɛ yɔ?» Pocosi-i se: «Kajalaɣ ñɩŋga.» Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se tikee mʋyaa nɛ halaa mbá pɛpɛdɩɣ pa-tɩ yɔ, pɛlɛyɩɣ-mɩ sʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa.

eng: For John came to you on the righteous road, and you didnt believe him. But tax collectors and prostitutes believed him. Yet even after you saw this, you didnt change your hearts and lives and you didnt believe him.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ kɔm mɩ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-mɩ mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ pʋ-nʋmɔʋ nɛ ikizi. Ɛlɛ́, tikee mʋyaa nɛ halaa mbá pɛpɛdɩɣ pa-tɩ yɔ, paba ñetisi ɛ-tɔm. Halɩ ɩna ḿbʋ́, ɛlɛ́ ɩtɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ se itisi ɛ-tɔm.».

eng: Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Iwelesi eduuye lɛɛɖɛ: ñɩm tʋ nɔɔyʋ wɛnɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛsɔ tɩŋ weyi popuu i-pee sʋlʋm yɔ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛma koloŋa nɛ ɛcɔzɩ nɛ ɛla ɖenɖe ɛɛñazɩɣ a-lɩm yɔ. Ɛma ɖɔɖɔ tilimiye nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-yɔɔ pasɩŋɩɣ nɛ pefeŋiɣ yɔ. Ɛtɛma lɛ, ɛkpaɣ ɛyáa se pɔcɔŋnɩ tɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛɖɛɛ nʋmɔʋ.

eng: When it was time for harvest, he sent his servants to the tenant farmers to collect his fruit.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, pɩtalɩ tɩŋ ɩnʋ i-pee koyuu. Peeɖe ɛkʋsɩ e-tiyiyaa nabɛyɛ se powolo ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ pamʋ e-ɖeɖe nɛ powona.

eng: But the tenant farmers grabbed his servants. They beat some of them, and some of them they killed. Some of them they stoned to death.
local: Patalaa lɛ, tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá pakpa-wɛ nɛ pama lɛlʋ nɛ pakʋ lɛlʋ nɛ payaɣ lɛlʋ pɛɛ nɛ ɛsɩ.

eng: Again he sent other servants, more than the first group. They treated them in the same way.
local: Hayɩm tʋ ɛnʋ ɛnɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛtasɩ kʋsʋʋ tiyiyaa lɛlaa nɛ pakɩlɩ mbá eetiyaa yɔ ɖɔʋ nɛ etiyi-wɛ. Patalaa lɛ, tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá pakpa-wɛ nɛ palabɩ-wɛ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ paalabʋ kajalaɣ ñɩma yɔ.

eng: Finally he sent his son to them. They will respect my son, he said.
local: Ɛɛnaɣ se ɛla ɛzɩma lɛ, ɛtɛzɩ tiyuu ɛ-pɩyalʋ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛhʋʋ ɛ-taa se: ‹Paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma, pañaŋ-ɩ.›.

eng: But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, This is the heir. Come on, lets kill him and well have his inheritance.
local: Tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá pana-ɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔyɔɔdɩnɩ ɖama se: ‹Ɩnaɣ weyi ɛkɔŋ yɔ, ɛnʋ kaɣnɩ tɩɣyʋʋ ɛ-caa ñɩm. Ɛkɔma lɛ, ɖɩkʋ-ɩ nɛ ɖɩtɩɣyɩ-pʋ!›.

eng: They grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
local: Ɛtalaa lɛ, pakpa-ɩ nɛ palɩzɩ-ɩ nɛ hayɩm ḿbʋ́ pʋ-wayɩ nɛ pakʋ-ɩ.».

eng: When the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenant farmers? .
local: Yesu tɛm tuuu eduuye ńɖɩ́ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Tɔɔ! Hayɩm tʋ ɛnʋ ɛkɔŋ lɛ, ɛbɛ ɛlakɩ tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá?».

eng: They said, He will totally destroy those wicked farmers and rent the vineyard to other tenant farmers who will give him the fruit when its ready.
local: Pocosi-i se: «Ɛkɔŋ ɛkʋ azaɣ́a ḿbá, pɩɩlakɩ-ɩ pʋtɔdɩyɛ kaʋ nɛ piyele ɛcaa ɛyáa kɩfama se pɔcɔnɩ-ɩ ɛ-tɩŋ yɔɔ camɩyɛ nɛ pokoyuu i-pee nɛ powokini-i alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ yɔ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Havent you ever read in the scriptures, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes?.
local: Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɩtakalɩ tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ kaʋ yaa we? Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ kuduyiŋ mayaa kpaɣaɣ pɔlɔ yɔ, ńɖɩ́ pɩsɩnɩ ɖɩkɩlɩ wazaɣ atɩŋa payɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ. Kɩpaɣlʋ labɩna ḿbʋ́. Asaaa! Pʋwɛ ɖeu kpem!›.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that Gods kingdom will be taken away from you and will be given to a people who produce its fruit.
local: Yee pɩcɩbɩ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɛtɔlɩ pɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛmɩzɩɣ kpɛdɛɛɛ. Yee pɩcɩbɩ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɖɩtɔlɩ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩnaŋɩɣ-ɩ naŋa naŋa.» Pʋ-yɔɔ meheyiɣ-mɩ se: mbá mɩ pɩɩwɛɛ se ɩkɛ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa ñɩma yɔ, ɩɩtasɩɣ kɛʋ. Ɛlɛ́, mbá palakɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ yɔ, ḿbá kɛɣnɩnɩ ka-taa ñɩma.

eng: Now when the chief priests and the Pharisees heard the parable, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
local: Cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma panɩ Yesu aduwa ana ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pana se ḿbá etuuni-yɛ.

eng: They were trying to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, who thought he was a prophet.
local: Peeɖe pañɩnaɣ nʋmɔŋ tɩŋa se pakpa-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, peseɣaɣ ɛyáa samaɣ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kamaɣzaɣ se ɛkɛ náyʋ.

eng: Jesus responded by speaking again in parables:.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tasɩ-wɛ tuuu aduwa se:.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding party for his son.
local: «Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ wɛ ɛzɩ eduuye ɖɩnɛ yɔ: wiyaʋ nɔɔyʋ labɩnɩ ɛ-pɩyalʋ halɩkpaɣyɛ kazandʋ.

eng: He sent his servants to call those invited to the wedding party. But they didnt want to come.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, etiyi ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa se powolo payaa mbá paaya-wɛ ti-ɖeɖe yɔ. Paya-wɛ lɛ, paba kizi se paawoki.

eng: Again he sent other servants and said to them, Tell those who have been invited, Look, the meal is all prepared. Ive butchered the oxen and the fattened cattle. Now everythings ready. Come to the wedding party! .
local: Peeɖe wiyaʋ ɛnʋ ɛtasɩ kʋsʋʋ e-tiyiyaa lɛlaa se powolo peheyi-wɛ se ɛtɛm ɛ-naŋ sɔsɔŋ nɛ ɛ-kpɩna wena awɛ num yɔ kʋʋ nɛ ɛla tɔɔnaɣ nɛ ɛɖaŋnɩ-wɛ se powolo pɔtɔɔ halɩkpaɣyɛ kazandʋ!.

eng: But they paid no attention and went away—some to their fields, others to their businesses.
local: Ɛlɛ́, piteheyi mbá paaya-wɛ yɔ natʋyʋ nɛ pɛɖɛɛ pe-ɖeɖe pe-ɖeɖe: lɛlʋ ɛ-hayɩm taa nɛ lɛlʋ ɛ-tadɩyɛ ɖeɖe.

eng: The rest of them grabbed his servants, abused them, and killed them.
local: Pɩsɛɣ mbá yɔ, paba ñakpa tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá nɛ paɖʋ-wɛ kʋñɔŋ nɛ pakʋ-wɛ.

eng: The king was angry. He sent his soldiers to destroy those murderers and set their city on fire.
local: Wiyaʋ nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ-pana kpa pɩŋŋ nɛ etiyi ɛ-sɔɔjanaa nɛ powolo pakʋ ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa kʋyaa ḿbá nɛ piyele pɔsɔ pɛ-tɛtʋ miŋ.

eng: Then he said to his servants, The wedding party is prepared, but those who were invited werent worthy.
local: «Pʋwayɩ lɛ, eheyi ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa mbá pɩsɛɣa yɔ se: ‹Halɩkpaɣyɛ tɔɔnaɣ pɩwa, ɛlɛ́, mbá paaya-wɛ yɔ, pataamʋna.

eng: Therefore, go to the roads on the edge of town and invite everyone you find to the wedding party.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́, iwolo habɛɛ yɔɔ nɛ ɩyaa mbá patɩŋa payɩ ɩkatɩɣ-wɛ yɔ se pɔkɔɔ pɔtɔɔ halɩkpaɣyɛ kazandʋ.›.

eng: Then those servants went to the roads and gathered everyone they found, both evil and good. The wedding party was full of guests.
local: Pɛɖɛwa lɛ, pɔcɔɔ nʋmɔŋ tɩŋa taa payɩ nɛ pekpeɣli ɛyáa mbá patɩŋa panawa yɔ: kɩbama nɛ azaɣ́a nɛ powoni-wɛ nɛ posu kuduyuu ŋgʋ́ kɩ-taa pɔtɔkɩ kazandʋ ńdʋ́ yɔ pam.

eng: Now when the king came in and saw the guests, he spotted a man who wasnt wearing wedding clothes.
local: «Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, wiyaʋ ɛnʋ ɛsʋ pɔ-cɔlɔ se ɛna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pacaɣ tɔɔnaɣ ɖɩtɔɔyɛ yɔ nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ abalʋ nɔɔyʋ peeɖe, etosuu halɩkpaɣyɛ kazandʋ toko.

eng: He said to him, Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes? But he was speechless.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: ‹Taŋ! Ŋgʋ́ ŋtɩŋaa ŋsʋʋ cɩnɛ nɛ ŋtosuu halɩkpaɣyɛ kazandʋ toko?› Ɛlɛ́, abalʋ ɛnʋ esuma.

eng: Then the king said to his servants, Tie his hands and feet and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Peeɖe eheyi ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa se: ‹Ɩhɔkɩ e-nesi nɛ ɛ-naŋgbanzɩ nɛ ɩlɔ-ɩ nɛ awayɩ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa. Peeɖe ewiliɣ nɛ ɛsalɩ e-kela.› ».

eng: Many people are invited, but few people are chosen. .
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ se: «Paya ɛyáa sakɩyɛ, ɛlɛ́, pazɩ palɩzɩɣ pa-taa.».

eng: Then the Pharisees met together to find a way to trap Jesus in his words.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Farɩsɩ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma wolo pala kediɣzaɣ se pana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ paala nɛ pañɩ Yesu nɔɔ cɔlɔ tɔm nɛ pakpa-ɩ yɔ.

eng: They sent their disciples, along with the supporters of Herod, to him. Teacher, they said, we know that you are genuine and that you teach Gods way as it really is. We know that you are not swayed by peoples opinions, because you dont show favoritism.
local: Peeɖe petiyi pɔ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa lɛlaa nɛ Eroodi hɔɔlʋʋ taa ñɩma nabɛyɛ se powolo peheyi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Đɩnawa se ŋyɔɔdʋʋ toovenim nɛ ŋkpɛlɩkɩɣ ɛyáa mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ se pala yɔ pu-toovenim. Sɔɔndʋ ɛɛkpaɣ-ŋ nɛ nɔɔyʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ŋtakpaɣ ɛyáa wɛtʋ nɛ ŋyaa pʋyʋ.

eng: So tell us what you think: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Đɩcaɣ se ŋheyi-ɖʋ ña-lɩmaɣza nɛ ɖɩna se: ɖa-paɣtʋ haɣ waɖɛ se pɛhɛyɩ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ lambuu yaa pataahɛyɩ-ɩ?».

eng: Knowing their evil motives, Jesus replied, Why do you test me, you hypocrites?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu ñɛtɛm sɩm pa-kañatʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ñɩma mɩ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ɩñɩkɩ-m tɔm?.

eng: Show me the coin used to pay the tax. And they brought him a denarion.
local: Ɩkɔnɩ-m lambuu liidiye nɛ mana.» Peeɖe powoni liidiye pɔɔyaɣ nɛ pawɩlɩ-ɩ.

eng: Whose image and inscription is this? he asked.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «A-kɩlɛmʋʋ nɛ a-hɩɖɛ pɛwɛ kɔ-yɔɔ cɩnɛ?».

eng: Caesars, they replied. Then he said, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. .
local: Pocosi-i se: «Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ.» Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Yee ḿbʋ́, ɩcɛlɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ mbʋ́ pɩkɛ ɛ-ñɩm yɔ nɛ piyele ɩcɛlɩ Ɛsɔ mbʋ́ pɩkɛ ɛ́lɛ́ ñɩm yɔ.».

eng: When they heard this they were astonished, and they departed.
local: Eheyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩɖɩɣzɩ-wɛ yem nɛ peyebi-i nɛ pacaŋ pɛɖɛɛ.

eng: That same day Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus.
local: Puwiye ńɖɩ́ ɖɔɖɔ Saduki ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma woba Yesu cɔlɔ. Ḿbá tɔŋna se sɩɖaa eefeŋ. Powoba lɛ, peheyi-i se:.

eng: They asked, Teacher, Moses said, If a man who doesnt have children dies, his brother must marry his wife and produce children for his brother.
local: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Đɔ-cɔzɔ Mooyiizi ɖʋ ɛ-paɣtʋ taa se: ‹Yee ɛyʋ ɛkpaɣ halʋ nɛ ɛsɩ, ɛfɛyɩnɩ pɩɣa yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se e-neu ɛhɔ lelu ɛnʋ nɛ ehiɣni-i pɩɣa ɛ-ɖalʋ ɛnʋ e-lone taa.›.

eng: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married, then died. Because he had no children he left his widow to his brother.
local: Tɔɔ! Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ nɛ e-newaa paawɛ ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa, pɛwɛ lʋbɛ, ɛkpaɣ halʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛkɔɔ ɛsɩ, ɛfɛyɩnɩ pɩɣa. Peeɖe e-neu hɔm lelu ɛnʋ se ehiɣ pɩɣa e-lone taa.

eng: The same thing happened with the second brother and the third, and in fact with all seven brothers.
local: Pɩtalabɩdɩ pʋyʋ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ caŋ ɛsɩ ɖɔɖɔ. Ɛ́lɛ́ wayɩ lɛ, tozo tʋ ñɔhɔm-ɩ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ sɩ ɖɔɖɔ. Pɩla ḿbʋ́ nɛ pɔkɔɔ pasɩ pa-lʋbɛ.

eng: Finally, the woman died.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, halʋ ɛnʋ ɛsɩ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: At the resurrection, which of the seven brothers will be her husband? They were all married to her.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɖɩpɔzʋʋ-ŋ se: sɩɖaa fem wiye lɛ, pa-lʋbɛ pa-taa weyi ɛtɩnɩ halʋ ɛnʋ? Ŋgʋ́, patɩŋa pakpaɣ-ɩ.».

eng: Jesus responded, You are wrong because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Ɩcɛtɩɣ mɩ-tɩ yem, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtanɩɩ Ɛsɔtɔm tɔbʋʋ, ɩtasɩ ɖɔɖɔ e-ɖoŋ.

eng: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and his disciples.
local: Yesu tɛma lɛ, ɛkɛzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se:.

eng: At the resurrection people wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like angels from God.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ sɩɖaa feŋ yɔ, abalaa ɛɛkpakɩɣ halaa, halaa ñaawalɩɣ ɖɔɖɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɛwɛɣ ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa wɛʋ ɛsɔdaa yɔ.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees sit on Moses seat.
local: «Paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma paɖʋwa se palɩzɩ-mɩ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ tɔbʋʋ.

eng: As for the resurrection of the dead, havent you read what God told you.
local: Sɩɖaa fem tɔm yɔɔ lɛ, ɩtakalɩdɩ tɔm ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ yɔɔdɩnɩ-mɩ nɛ pama ɛ-Tɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ yaa we? Ɛyɔɔdaa se:.

eng: Therefore, you must take care to do everything they say. But dont do what they do.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩɖɔkɩ ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ peheyiɣ-mɩ yɔ nɛ ɩɖɔ tɩ-yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɩtaala mbʋ́ palakɩ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paalakɩ mbʋ́ pa-maɣmaɣ pɛkpɛlɩkɩɣ ɛyáa se pala yɔ.

eng: Im the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. .
local: ‹Ma lɛ Abraham nɛ Izaakɩ nɛ Yakɔɔbʋ pɛ-Ɛsɔ.› Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, Ɛsɔ tɛkɛ sɩɖaa Ɛsɔ, ɛlɛ́, wezuu tɩnaa ñɩnʋ ɛkɛnaa.».

eng: For they tie together heavy packs that are impossible to carry. They put them on the shoulders of others, but are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
local: Pasʋkɩ ɛyáa sʋʋtʋ yuŋ ñɩndʋ nɛ paatɩɩɖʋʋ-wɛ nesi paa ñɩɩɩ.

eng: Now when the crowd heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ ŋgá kewelesaɣ Yesu tɔm yɔ, kanɩ ndʋ́ ɛyɔɔdaa yɔ lɛ, pɩɖɩɣzɩ-kɛ yem ɖɩɣ ɖɩɣ.

eng: Everything they do, they do to be noticed by others. They make extra-wide prayer bands for their arms and long tassels for their clothes.
local: Palakɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ se ɛyáa ɛna-wɛ. Pʋ-yɔɔ pamaɣ Ɛsɔtɔm tɔnzɩ nzɩ́ sɩkɩlɩ walʋʋ yɔ sɩ-yɔɔ nɛ pataŋ pe-nesi nɛ pe-kila pa-taa. Pataŋ ɖɔɖɔ po-tokonaa nesi taa toma nɛ akɩlɩɣ lɛlaa tɛ.

eng: When the Pharisees heard that Jesus had left the Sadducees speechless, they met together.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Farɩsɩ ñɩma nɩɩ se Yesu yɔɔdaa nɛ Saduki ñɩma tapɩzɩ pocosi-i natʋyʋ, ɛlɛ́ pekpeɣli ɖama.

eng: They love to sit in places of honor at banquets and in the synagogues.
local: Pañɩnɩɣ ɖɩjaɣyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ kazandʋ ndɩ ndɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ pa-taa.

eng: One of them, a legal expert, tested him.
local: Peeɖe pa-taa paɣtʋ wɩlɩyʋ nɔɔyʋ woba ɛñɩ Yesu nɔɔ cɔlɔ tɔm se ɛna. Ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se:.

eng: They love to be greeted with honor in the markets and to be addressed as Rabbi.
local: Pɔsɔɔlaa se pɛsɛɣ-wɛ nɛ ñamtʋ ɛyáa ɖikpeɣliye taa. Pɔsɔɔlaa ɖɔɖɔ se payaɣ-wɛ se: ‹Wɩlɩyaa.›.

eng: Teacher, what is the greatest commandment in the Law.
local: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Ɛsɔ paɣtʋ tɩŋa taa lɛ, ndʋ́ tɩkɩlaa?».

eng: But you shouldnt be called Rabbi , because you have one teacher, and all of you are brothers and sisters.
local: Mɩ lɛ, mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛtaayele nɛ payaa-ɩ se: ‹Wɩlɩyʋ.› Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩkɛ ɛyáa kʋɖʋmaa, men-ɖeke koŋ lɛ mɩ-Wɩlɩyʋ.

eng: He replied, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, and with all your mind.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: « ‹Sɔɔlɩ ña-Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ nɛ ña-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa nɛ ñe-liu tɩŋa nɛ ña-lɩmaɣza tɩŋa pilim ñe-wezuu caɣʋ taa.›.

eng: Dont call anybody on earth your father, because you have one Father, who is heavenly.
local: Ɩtaayaa nɔɔyʋ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ se: ‹Caa.› Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Caa kʋɖʋm koŋ ɩwɛna, ɛnʋ wɛnɩ ɛsɔdaa.

eng: This is the first and greatest commandment.
local: Ńdʋ́ lɛ kajalaɣ paɣtʋ, ńdʋ́ kɩlɩnɩ lɛɛtʋ tɩŋa payɩ.

eng: Dont be called teacher , because Christ is your one teacher.
local: Mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛtaayele ɖɔɖɔ nɛ payaa-ɩ se: ‹Wiyaʋ.› Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Wiyaʋ kʋɖʋm koŋ ɩwɛna, ɛnʋ lɛ Krɩstʋ.

eng: And the second is like it: You must love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Naalɛ ñɩndʋ ndʋ́ tɩwɛ wazaɣ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ kajalaɣ ñɩndʋ yɔ, ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Sɔɔlɩ ña-taabalʋ ɛzɩ ŋsɔɔlʋʋ ña-maɣmaɣ ña-tɩ yɔ.›.

eng: But the one who is greatest among you will be your servant.
local: Mɩ-taa sɔsɔ ɛpɩsɩ mɩ-tʋmlaɖʋ.

eng: All the Law and the Prophets depend on these two commands. .
local: Paɣtʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-naalɛ tɩkɛnɩ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ nɛ tɔm ndʋ́ náyaa susuu yɔ ti-kiɖe.».

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low. But all who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Weyi ɛkpazɩɣ ɛ-tɩ yɔ, petisiɣ-i. Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ tisiɣ ɛ-tɩ yɔ, pakpazɩ-ɩ.».

eng: Now as the Pharisees were gathering, Jesus asked them.
local: Farɩsɩ ñɩma kpeɣli ɖama lɛ, Yesu pɔzɩ-wɛ se:.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You shut people out of the kingdom of heaven. You dont enter yourselves, and you wont allow those who want to enter to do so.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu kɛzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ñɩma mɩ, pʋtɔɔ-mɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩɖɩkɩ ɛyáa nʋmɔʋ se pataasʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa. Ŋgʋ́, mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩɩsʋʋ, ɛlɛ́, iiyeki ɖɔɖɔ mbá pe-liu wɛ sʋʋ yɔ se paba ɛsʋʋ. .

eng: What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he? Davids son, they replied.
local: «Se we ɩmaɣzɩɣ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ ɛ-tɔm? A-liɖe taa palʋlʋʋ-ɩ?» Pocosi-i se: «Wiyaʋ Daaviidi liɖe taa.».

eng: How terrible it will be for you, legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You travel over sea and land to make one convert. But when theyve been converted, they become twice the child of hell you are.
local: «Paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma mbá mɩ ɩkɛ tɛɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ñɩma yɔ, pʋtɔɔ-mɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩwɛɛ ɩcɔʋ kedeŋa tɩŋa payɩ se ɩñɩnɩ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɩpɩsɩ-ɩ mɩ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ. Ihikiɣ-i lɛ, ɩlabɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ weyi pʋwɛɛ se paɖʋ-ɩ kʋñɔŋ tam naalɛ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ-mɩ, ɖenɖe kʋñɔŋ maatɛŋ wɛɛ yɔ.

eng: He said, Then how is it that David, inspired by the Holy Spirit, called him Lord when he said.
local: Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Yee ḿbʋ́, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tii Daaviidi yɔɔ nɛ ɛpɩzɩ ɛ́yáa-ɩ se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ?›.

eng: How terrible it will be for you blind guides who say, If people swear by the temple, its nothing. But if people swear by the gold in the temple, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: «Mbá mɩ ɩwɛ ɛzɩ yʋlʋmaa nɛ ɩwɩlɩɣ ɛyáa nʋmɔŋ yɔ, pʋtɔɔ-mɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtɔŋ se: ‹Yee nɔɔyʋ eɖuuni Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa se ɛlakɩ pʋyʋ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛnɩ-ɩ kɩcɛyʋʋ se ɛlabɩ-pʋ. Ɛlɛ́, yee pʋdʋ eɖuuni ñɩɣlɩ́m mbʋ́ pacanɩnɩ-kɛ yɔ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛlabɩ-pʋ kpem.›.

eng: The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool?.
local: Daaviidi tɔm se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ heyi man-Kɩpaɣlʋ se: caɣ mɔn-cɔlɔ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ moluzi ño-koyindinaa ñɛ-tɛɛ kpem yɔ.›.

eng: You foolish and blind people! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold holy?.
local: Kɩmɛlɛmɩŋ nɛ yʋlʋmaa mɩ! Ñɩɣlɩ́m nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa ŋgá kapɩsɩ-pʋ ɛ-ñɩm yɔ, pa-taa akɩlɩ wazaɣ?.

eng: If David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son? .
local: Yee Daaviidi ɛyaɣ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ› lɛ, ɛzɩma ɛtasɩɣ kɛʋ Daaviidi ɛnʋ ɛ-sayɩ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ?».

eng: You say, If people swear by the altar, its nothing. But if they swear by the gift on the altar, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Ɩtɔŋ ɖɔɖɔ se: ‹Yee nɔɔyʋ eɖuuni Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa kɩlaŋ ɖɩlayɛ yɔ, pɩɩlakɩ pʋyʋ. Ɛlɛ́, yee eɖuuni kɩlaʋ ŋgʋ́ pala lone ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa yɔ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛla mbʋ́ eɖuwaa se ɛlakɩ yɔ kpem.›.

eng: Nobody was able to answer him. And from that day forward nobody dared to ask him anything.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Farɩsɩ ñɩma taa nɔɔyʋ tapɩzɩ ecosi-i natʋyʋ. Pɩkpaɣʋnɩ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ yɔ, nɔɔyʋ tatasɩ katʋʋ se ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ tɔm.

eng: You blind people! Which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?.
local: Yʋlʋmaa mɩ! Kpɩnɛ nɖɩ́ palawa yɔ nɛ kɩlaŋ ɖɩlayɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩpɩsɩ-ɖɩ Ɛsɔ ñɩm yɔ, pa-taa lɛ, akɩlɩ wazaɣ?.

eng: Therefore, those who swear by the altar swear by it and by everything thats on it.
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se: yee nɔɔyʋ eɖuuni kɩlaŋ ɖɩlayɛ yɔ, eɖuuni lone ńɖɩ́ nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋwɛ ɖɩ-taa yɔ.

eng: Those who swear by the temple swear by it and by everything thats part of it.
local: Weyi eɖuuni Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔ, eɖuuni ɖɩɣa ŋ́gá nɛ weyi ɛwɛ ka-taa yɔ.

eng: Those who swear by heaven swear by Gods throne and by the one who sits on it.
local: Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ɖukuni ɛsɔdaa yɔ, eɖuuni Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ nɛ weyi ɛcaɣ kɔ-yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You give to God a tenth of mint, dill, and cumin, but you forget about the more important matters of the Law: justice, peace, and faith. You ought to give a tenth but without forgetting about those more important matters.
local: «Paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma mɩ, ɩkɛ tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ñɩma, pʋtɔɔ-mɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtayɩɣ nɛ ɩkpɛndɩɣnɩ ɖozi hatʋ maɣmaɣ tam hiu nɛ ɩhaɣ Ɛsɔ pɩtɛ kʋɖʋmam. Ɛlɛ́, ɩɩɖɔŋ paɣtʋ ndʋ́ tɩkɩlɩ wazaɣ yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ: Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa tʋʋzʋʋ nɛ ɖama pʋtɔdɩyɛ naʋ nɛ mbʋ́ ɛyʋ ɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ yɔ pɩ-labʋ. Ŋgʋ́, tɩ-yɔɔ pɩɩwɛɛ se ɩɖɔ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ lɛɛtʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: You blind guides! You filter out an ant but swallow a camel.
local: Mbá mɩ ɩwɛ ɛzɩ yʋlʋmaa nɛ ɩwɩlɩɣ ɛyáa nʋmɔʋ yɔ, ɩwɛ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ mbá pacatɩɣ sʋlʋm nɛ palɩzɩɣ sʋŋñɔɔyɩŋ nɛ piyele pelikini kpaŋnaŋ siɣá naalɛ tɩnaa yɔ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and plate, but inside they are full of violence and pleasure seeking.
local: «Paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma mɩ, ɩkɛ tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ñɩma, pʋtɔɔ-mɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩwɛ ɛzɩ mbá pañalɩɣ pa-ñanzɩ nɛ pe-eyisi sɩ-wayɩ ɖeke nɛ paañalɩɣ sɩ-taa yɔ. Ɩcaɣ se mɩ-yɔɔ ɛwɛɛ ɖeu, ŋgʋ́, mɩlɩm nɛ pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa niye ɖɛlɛsʋʋ pa-lɩmaɣza suni mɩ-taa ɖo.

eng: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup so that the outside of the cup will be clean too.
local: Farɩsɩ ñɩma, yʋlʋmaa mɩ! Ɩcalɩnɩ mɩ-ñanzɩ nɛ mi-eyisi pa-taa ñalʋʋ, pʋbʋ pa-wayɩ wɛɣ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs. They look beautiful on the outside. But inside they are full of dead bones and all kinds of filth.
local: «Paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma mɩ, ɩkɛ tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ñɩma, pʋtɔɔ-mɩ! Ɩwɛ ɛzɩ pɩlaŋ weyi pahʋlʋsɩ-ɩ nɛ ɩwɛɛ ɖeu ñɩlɩ ñɩlɩ yɔ. Ŋgʋ́ ɩ-taa lɛ, sɩɖaa mɔɔ nɛ mbʋ́ pɩɖazaa yɔ, posuni ɖo.

eng: In the same way you look righteous to people. But inside you are full of pretense and rebellion.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɩwɛɛ: ɩwɛ ɛzɩ mbá pɔɖɔŋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ siŋŋ yɔ ɛyáa ɛsɩndaa. Naze lɛ, ɛzaɣ́tʋ nɛ tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ lakasɩ posuni mɩ-taa ɖo.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
local: «Paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma mɩ, ɩkɛ tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ñɩma, pʋtɔɔ-mɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩmaɣ náyaa pɩlaŋ nɛ ɩcanɩɣ mbá palabɩ mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ pa-ñɩŋ.

eng: You say, If we had lived in our ancestors days, we wouldnt have joined them in killing the prophets.
local: nɛ ɩtɔŋ se: ‹Yee ɖɩɩwɛ ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa alɩwaatʋ taa yɔ, ɖitaatisi ɖɩtɩnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖa nɛ wɛ ɖɩkʋ náyaa.›.

eng: You testify against yourselves that you are children of those who murdered the prophets.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɩwɩlɩnɩ mɩ-tɩ se mɩ lɛ náyaa kʋyaa pɩyalaa.

eng: Go ahead, complete what your ancestors did.
local: Tɔɔ! Mbá mɩ ɩtɩŋɩɣ mɩ-cɔzɔnaa nʋmɔʋ yɔ, ɩtɛzɩ-kʋ tɩŋʋʋ!.

eng: You snakes! You children of snakes! How will you be able to escape the judgment of hell?.
local: Đʋmaa mɩ! Ɛzɩma ɩlakɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ ɛtaakʋ mɩ-tɔm nɛ ɛɖʋ-mɩ kʋñɔŋ maatɛŋ taa?.

eng: Therefore, look, Im sending you prophets, wise people, and legal experts. Some of them you will kill and crucify. And some you will beat in your synagogues and chase from city to city.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ meheyiɣ-mɩ se: mentiyiɣni-mɩ náyaa nɛ lɔŋsɩnɖaa nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa. Ɩkɔŋ kʋʋ lɛlaa nɛ ɩka lɛlaa kaamaŋkasɩ yɔɔ nɛ ilu lɛlaa agbazɛɛ mɩ-Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ taa nɛ ɩwɛɛ lɛlaa wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ tɛtʋ ndɩ ndɩ taa.

eng: Therefore, upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been poured out on the earth, from the blood of that righteous man Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ mbá patɩŋa payɩ ɩkʋ-wɛ ɖulum yɔ, pa-sʋʋtʋ tɩŋa pɩsɩɣ mɩ-yɔɔ. Pɩpazɩɣnɩ kʋtʋʋzʋ Aabɛɛlɩ nɛ puwolo pɩsɩɩnɩ Baraacii pɩyalʋ Zakaarii weyi ɩkʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa kadaɣ taa lone kaɖɛ ñɩnɖɛ nɛ kɩlaŋ ɖɩlayɛ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa yɔ.

eng: I assure you that all these things will come upon this generation.
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se: mbá patɩŋa payɩ pakʋ-wɛ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pa-sʋʋtʋ pɩsɩɣ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɛyáa yɔɔ!».

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! You who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you. How often I wanted to gather your people together, just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu heyi Yeruusalɛm ñɩma se: «Hei! Yeruusalɛm ñɩma mɩ, ɩkʋʋ náyaa nɛ ɩyakɩ mbá Ɛsɔ tiyini-mɩ yɔ pɛɛ nɛ pasɩkɩ. Tam ɛzɩma mɔntɔsɔɔlɩ se menkpeɣli-mɩ mɔn-cɔlɔ nɛ mohuuzi mɩ-yɔɔ nɛ pʋyʋ ɛtaalabɩ-mɩ ɛzɩ ɖoyuu papʋʋ kɩ-kalɩmasɩ yɔɔ yɔ nɛ ikizi?.

eng: Look, your house is left to you deserted.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, meyeki-mɩ nɛ mɩ-tɛtʋ.

eng: I tell you, you wont see me until you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name . .
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se: ɩɩtasɩɣ-m naʋ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩyɔɔdʋʋ se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ ɛ́wazɩ weyi ɛkɔŋ e-lone taa yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ› yɔ.».

eng: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten young bridesmaids who took their lamps and went out to meet the groom.
local: Yesu tasɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa heyuu se: «Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, ɛzʋnɔ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ kɔŋ wɛʋ: pɛlaa nabɛyɛ wɛnɩ ḿbʋ́, pɛwɛ hiu. Halɩkpaɣyɛ naɖɩyɛ wiye lɛ, pakpaɣ kanɖʋnaa nɛ powolo paɖaŋ halɩkpaɣyʋ.

eng: Now five of them were wise, and the other five were foolish.
local: Pa-taa lɛ, pɛlaa kagbaanzɩ kɛ kɩmɛlɛmɩŋ nɛ kagbaanzɩ ñɛkɛ lɔŋsɩnɖaa.

eng: The foolish ones took their lamps but didnt bring oil for them.
local: Kɩmɛlɛmɩŋ ɩnʋ, ɩkpaɣ ɩ-kanɖʋnaa lɛ, ɩtɛcɛ kaɖɩsɩm se pɩtɛm ɩ-kanɖʋnaa taa lɛ, ɩɩɖʋ.

eng: But the wise ones took their lamps and also brought containers of oil.
local: Piyele lɔŋsɩnɖaa ñakpaɣ pa-kanɖʋnaa nɛ pɛkpɛndɩnɩ kaɖɩsɩm nɛ powolo.

eng: When the groom was late in coming, they all became drowsy and went to sleep.
local: Ŋgʋ́, halɩkpaɣyʋ tɔkɔɔ lɔŋ nɛ pɛlaa ḿbá patɩŋa payɩ peliisi nɛ poɖoo.

eng: But at midnight there was a cry, Look, the groom! Come out to meet him.
local: «Pʋkɔma ɛzɩ ɖoó hɛkʋ lɛ, panɩɩ kubuka nakɛyɛ se: ‹Ɩɩnaɣ pɛlɛ walʋ! Ɩkʋyɩ nɛ ɩkatɩ-ɩ!›.

eng: Then all those bridesmaids got up and prepared their lamps.
local: Peeɖe pɛlaa ḿbá pakʋyɩ patɩŋa se pɔñɔɔzʋʋ pa-kanɖʋnaa.

eng: But the foolish bridesmaids said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, because our lamps have gone out.
local: nɛ kɩmɛlɛmɩŋ ɩnʋ, ɩpɔzʋʋ lɔŋsɩnɖaa se: ‹Ɩha-ɖʋ mɩ-kaɖɩsɩm pazɩ nɛ ɖɩɖʋ ɖa-kanɖʋnaa taa: pacaɣ ɖem.›.

eng: But the wise bridesmaids replied, No, because if we share with you, there wont be enough for our lamps and yours. We have a better idea. You go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
local: Paba ñocosi-wɛ se: ‹Aayɩ! Đa-kaɖɩsɩm makɩnɩ-ɖʋ, pɩɩkazɩɣ se ɖɩha-mɩ. Iwolo pɛdɩyaa cɔlɔ nɛ ɩcɛ.›.

eng: But while they were gone to buy oil, the groom came. Those who were ready went with him into the wedding. Then the door was shut.
local: Pɛɖɛ cɛbʋ lɛ, pɛlɛ walʋ talɩ. Peeɖe ɛ nɛ mbá pɔñɔɔzɩ pa-tɩ nɛ paɖaŋ-ɩ yɔ, pasʋ halɩkpaɣyɛ kuduyuu tɛɛ nɛ paɖɩɣ pakaɖɩ.

eng: Later the other bridesmaids came and said, Lord, lord, open the door for us.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, kɩmɛlɛmɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩtalaa nɛ ɩmakɩ kubusi se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Kɩpaɣlʋ! Tʋlɩ-ɖʋ!›.

eng: But he replied, I tell you the truth, I dont know you.
local: Peeɖe pɛlɛ walʋ cosi-wɛ se: ‹Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se mantasɩm-mɩ ɖoli.› ».

eng: Therefore, keep alert, because you dont know the day or the hour.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩcaɣnɩ mɩ-ɛsɛ nɛ ɩɖaŋ kpam, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtatɩlɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ yaa ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ ŋgʋ́ kʋ-yɔɔ mɔnkɔŋ yɔ.».

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who was leaving on a trip. He called his servants and handed his possessions over to them.
local: Yesu tasɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa heyuu se: «Ɛzʋnɔ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ kɔŋ wɛʋ ɖɔɖɔ: abalʋ nɔɔyʋ wɛnɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛɖʋ se ewoki nʋmɔʋ. Ɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, ɛ́yáa ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa nɛ ɛtayɩ-wɛ ɛ-ñɩm.

eng: To one he gave five valuable coins, and to another he gave two, and to another he gave one. He gave to each servant according to that servants ability. Then he left on his journey.
local: Ɛcɛlɩ kajalaɣ tʋ sika pɔɔyɩsɩ kapʋsɩ kagbaanzɩ nɛ naalɛ tʋ kapʋsɩ naalɛ nɛ tozo tʋ kʋɖʋmaɣ. Ɛcɛlɩ-wɛ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛmaɣzɩnɩ paa weyi e-ɖoŋ nɛ piyele ɛcaŋ ɛɖɛɛ.

eng: After the man left, the servant who had five valuable coins took them and went to work doing business with them. He gained five more.
local: Ɛɖɛwa lɛ, tʋmlaɖʋ weyi ɛmʋ kapʋsɩ kagbaanzɩ yɔ, ɛtɩwobu nɛ ɛlabɩnɩ-sɩ tadɩyɛ nɛ pɩsɛɛ-ɩ kapʋsɩ kagbaanzɩ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: In the same way, the one who had two valuable coins gained two more.
local: Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ñamʋ kapʋsɩ naalɛ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ la ɖɔɖɔ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pɩsɛɛ-ɩ kapʋsɩ naalɛ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: But the servant who had received the one valuable coin dug a hole in the ground and buried his masters money.
local: Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ñamʋ ɛ-caa sika pɔɔyɩsɩ kapʋwa kʋɖʋmaɣ yɔ, ewolo ehuyi pɔʋ nɛ epim-kɛ.

eng: Now after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
local: «Pileɖaa lɛ, abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛpɩsɩ ɛkɔɔ nɛ ɛ́yáa ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá se powolo peheyi-i mbʋ́ palabɩnɩ sika pɔɔyɩsɩ kapʋsɩ nzɩ́ ɛɛcɛlɩ-wɛ yɔ.

eng: The one who had received five valuable coins came forward with five additional coins. He said, Master, you gave me five valuable coins. Look, Ive gained five more.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ tʋmlaɖʋ weyi ɛmʋ sika pɔɔyɩsɩ kapʋsɩ kagbaanzɩ yɔ, ewobi ɛ-caa ɛnʋ ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-ɩ kapʋsɩ kagbaanzɩ lɛɛsɩ nɛ eheyi-i se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ŋŋcɛlɩ-m sika pɔɔyɩsɩ kapʋsɩ kagbaanzɩ. Na! Pɩsɛ-m kagbaanzɩ ɖɔɖɔ.›.

eng: His master replied, Excellent! You are a good and faithful servant! Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-caa yɩɣyɩ-ɩ nɛ eheyi-i se: ‹Pʋwɛ ɖeu. Ŋkɛ tʋmlaɖʋ kɩbanʋ. Mɛncɛlɩ-ŋ mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ ñɩɩɩ yɔ nɛ ŋɖɔkɩ-pʋ camɩyɛ nɛ ŋlabɩnɩ-pʋ mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, meyekini-ŋ mbʋ́ pʋɖɔwa pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ yɔ. Kɔɔ nɛ ma nɛ ŋ ɖɩwɛɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ taa.›.

eng: The second servant also came forward and said, Master, you gave me two valuable coins. Look, Ive gained two more.
local: Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ñaamʋ kapʋsɩ naalɛ yɔ, ewolo ɖɔɖɔ ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ eheyi-i se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ŋŋcɛlɩ-m sika pɔɔyɩsɩ kapʋsɩ naalɛ. Na! Pɩsɛ-m ɖɔɖɔ naalɛ.›.

eng: His master replied, Well done! You are a good and faithful servant. Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-caa yɩɣyɩ-ɩ nɛ eheyi-i se: ‹Pʋwɛ ɖeu. Ŋkɛ tʋmlaɖʋ kɩbanʋ. Mɛncɛlɩ-ŋ mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ ñɩɩɩ yɔ nɛ ŋɖɔkɩ-pʋ camɩyɛ nɛ ŋlabɩnɩ-pʋ mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mɛncɛlɩɣ-ŋ mbʋ́ pʋɖɔwa yɔ. Kɔɔ nɛ ma nɛ ŋ ɖɩwɛɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ taa.›.

eng: Now the one who had received one valuable coin came and said, Master, I knew that you are a hard man. You harvest grain where you havent sown. You gather crops where you havent spread seed.
local: Pɩɖɩɣnɩ weyi ɛ́lɛ́ mʋ kʋɖʋmaɣ yɔ lɛ, ewolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ eheyi-i se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Manawa se ñɔ-tɔm wɛ kaɖɛ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ. Đenɖe ŋtoɖuu yaa ŋtamɩzɩ yɔ, peeɖe ŋkʋŋ.

eng: So I was afraid. And I hid my valuable coin in the ground. Here, you have whats yours.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ sɔɔndʋ kpa-m nɛ mowoni ñe-sika pɔɔyɩsɩ kapʋwa ŋgá ŋcɛlɩ-m yɔ nɛ mɛmɛsɩ tɛtɛɛ. Ŋ́gá yɔ, mʋ!›.

eng: His master replied, You evil and lazy servant! You knew that I harvest grain where I havent sown and that I gather crops where I havent spread seed?.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-caa cosi-i se: ‹Tʋmlaɖʋ ɛzaɣ́ʋ, fendu tʋ ña! Ŋsɩm ḿbʋ́ se ɖenɖe montoɖúú yaa mantamɩzɩ yɔ, peeɖe mankʋŋ nɛ ɖenɖe mantamɩzɩ yɔ, peeɖe monkuu.

eng: In that case, you should have turned my money over to the bankers so that when I returned, you could give me what belonged to me with interest.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́, pɩɩwɛɛ se ŋwoni men-sika pɔɔyɩsɩ kapʋwa nɛ ŋɖʋ liidiye koozaɣ taa. Yee ŋŋlabɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, mɔnkɔm ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, maawobi mamʋ-kɛ nɛ kɛ-kɛzɛɣa.›.

eng: Therefore, take from him the valuable coin and give it to the one who has ten coins.
local: Peeɖe eheyi tʋmlaɖaa lɛlaa se: ‹Ɩmʋ kapʋwa ŋ́gá lɔŋ e-nesi taa nɛ ɩsɔzɩ weyi ɛwɛnɩ kapʋsɩ hiu yɔ.

eng: Those who have much will receive more, and they will have more than they need. But as for those who dont have much, even the little bit they have will be taken away from them.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛwɛna pʋɖɔɔ yɔ, pɔsɔzʋʋ-ɩ nɛ puhuuni-i. Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛ́lɛ́ fɛyɩnɩ pʋyʋ yɔ, halɩ pɛtɛzɩɣ lɛɣʋ mbʋ́ ɛmaɣzɩɣ se ɛwɛna yɔ.

eng: Now take the worthless servant and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Piyele tʋmlaɖʋ wazaɣ fɛyɩ tʋ ɛnɛ lɛ, ɩkpaɣ-ɩ nɛ ɩlɔ-ɩ awayɩ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa. Peeɖe ewiliɣ nɛ ɛsalɩ e-kela.› ».

eng: Now when the Human One comes in his majesty and all his angels are with him, he will sit on his majestic throne.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Puwiye nɖɩ́ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ nɛ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa tɩŋa pɛkpɛndɩɣnɩ e-koboyaɣ nɛ pɔkɔɔ yɔ, ɛcakɩ e-kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ.

eng: All the nations will be gathered in front of him. He will separate them from each other, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
local: Pɔkɔŋ ajɛya kpeekpe taa ɛyáa kpeɣluu ɛ-ɛsɩndaa nɛ ɛkɛzɩ lɛlaa hɔɔlʋʋ nɛ piyele lɛlaa hɔɔlʋʋ lɛɛkʋ ɛzɩ ninɖiɣyu kpeɣluu heŋ nɛ pʋŋ nɖeke nɖeke yɔ.

eng: He will put the sheep on his right side. But the goats he will put on his left.
local: Ɛkɛzɩɣ lɛlaa ɛ-nɩwaŋ yɔɔ nɛ piyele lɛlaa ɛ-nɩmɩyɛ yɔɔ.

eng: Then the king will say to those on his right, Come, you who will receive good things from my Father. Inherit the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world began.
local: Peeɖe eheyiɣ ɛ-nɩwaŋ yɔɔ ñɩma se: ‹Mbá mɩ man-Caa Ɛsɔ wazɩ mɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɩkɔɔ ɩtɔɔ Kewiyaɣ ŋgá ɛñɔɔzɩ-mɩ ɖooo alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛlabɩ kedeŋa yɔ.

eng: I was hungry and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger and you welcomed me.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ñɔɔsɩ wɩɣaɣ-m nɛ ɩha-m tɔɔnaɣ mɔntɔɔ. Lɔkɔtʋ kɛɖɛ-m, ɩha-m lɩm mɔñɔɔ. Maakɛ ɛgɔm nɛ ɩmʋ-m mɩ-tɛ.

eng: I was naked and you gave me clothes to wear. I was sick and you took care of me. I was in prison and you visited me.
local: Mɔnɖɔŋaɣ ndaakpeɖe nɛ ɩha-m wondu monsuu. Pɩwɩɣaɣ-m nɛ ɩcɔnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ. Maawɛ salaka taa nɛ ɩkɔɔ ɩna-m peeɖe.›.

eng: Then those who are righteous will reply to him, Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you a drink?.
local: Peeɖe mbá patʋʋzaa yɔ, pocosuu-i se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Đooye ɖɩna-ŋ ñɔɔsɩ wɩɣaɣ-ŋ nɛ ɖɩha-ŋ tɔɔnaɣ yaa ɖooye lɔkɔtʋ ɖɛ-ŋ nɛ ɖɩha-ŋ lɩm?.

eng: When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you, or naked and give you clothes to wear?.
local: Đooye ɖɩmʋ-ŋ ɛgɔndʋ ɖɛ-tɛ? Đooye ɖɩna-ŋ ndaakpeɖe nɛ ɖisu-ŋ wondu?.

eng: When did we see you sick or in prison and visit you.
local: Đooye ɖɩna pɩwɩɣaɣ-ŋ yaa ŋwɛ salaka taa nɛ ɖɩkɔɔ ɖɩna-ŋ?›.

eng: Now Jesus left the temple and was going away. His disciples came to point out to him the temple buildings.
local: Yesu tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ lɛ, ɛlɩɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa se ɛɖɛɣ. Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa woba ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ payɩɣyɩɣ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pama Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa ŋ́gá yɔ.

eng: Then the king will reply to them, I assure you that when you have done it for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you have done it for me.
local: Wiyaʋ kaɣ-wɛ cosuu se: ‹Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩlabɩ-pʋ mɛ-ɛyáa mbá patakpaɣ-wɛ payaa pʋyʋ yɔ pa-taa nɔɔyʋ yɔ, man-tɩ tɩŋa ɩlaba.›.

eng: He responded, Do you see all these things? I assure that no stone will be left on another. Everything will be demolished. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩnaʋ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se pɩɩkɔŋ pɩyɛ naɖɩyɛ kazʋʋ lɛɛɖɛ yɔɔ, pɔkɔŋ tuzuu atɩŋa payɩ nɛ pɛpɛɖɩ.».

eng: Then he will say to those on his left, Get away from me, you who will receive terrible things. Go into the unending fire that has been prepared for the devil and his angels.
local: «Ɛkaɣ heyuu ɖɔɖɔ ɛ-nɩmɩyɛ yɔɔ ñɩma se: ‹Ɩkʋyɩ mɔn-cɔlɔ cɩnɛ nɛ ɩɖɛɛ poliŋ, Ɛsɔ wɩlɩ-mɩ kpalʋʋ! Iwolo miŋ weyi eeɖeŋ kaʋ yɔ ɛ-taa. Peeɖe Ɛsɔ ñɔɔzaa nɛ ɛɖaŋnɩ Satanɩ nɛ e-tiyiyaa!.

eng: Now while Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately and said, Tell us, when will these things happen? What will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yesu wolo Somiŋ Pʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛcaɣ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa wolo ɛ-cɔlɔ pe-ɖeke nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Heyi-ɖʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɩkaɣ labʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ wɩlʋʋ se ñɔ-kɔm nɛ kedeŋa tɛm pawɩzaa yɔ.».

eng: I was hungry and you didnt give me food to eat. I was thirsty and you didnt give me anything to drink.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ñɔɔsɩ wɩɣaɣ-m, ɩtaha-m tɔɔnaɣ se mɔntɔɔ. Lɔkɔtʋ ɖɛ-m, ɩtaha-m lɩm se mɔñɔɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ, ɩtaayele nɛ nɔɔyʋ yili-mɩ.

eng: I was a stranger and you didnt welcome me. I was naked and you didnt give me clothes to wear. I was sick and in prison, and you didnt visit me.
local: Mɔnkɔm ɛgɔndʋ, ɩtamʋ-m mɩ-tɛ. Maawɛ ndaakpeɖe, ɩtaha-m wondu se monsuu, pɩwɩɣaɣ-m, ɩtɔcɔnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ. Maawɛ salaka taa, ɩtɔkɔɔ se ɩna-m.›.

eng: Many will come in my name, saying, Im the Christ. They will deceive many people.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kɔŋ kʋɖʋm kʋɖʋm mo-lone taa nɛ peyili ɛyáa sakɩyɛ nɛ peheyi-mɩ se: ‹Ma lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa nɛ etiyini-mɩ› yɔ.

eng: Then they will reply, Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and didnt do anything to help you.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pakaɣ-ɩ cosuu se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Đooye ɖɩna-ŋ ñɔɔsɩ wɩɣaɣ-ŋ yaa lɔkɔtʋ ɖɛ-ŋ yaa ŋkɔm ɛgɔndʋ yaa ŋɖɔŋaɣ ndaakpeɖe yaa pɩwɩɣaɣ-ŋ yaa ŋwɛ salaka taa nɛ ɖɩtasɩnɩ-ŋ?›.

eng: You will hear about wars and reports of wars. Dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Ɩkɔŋ nɩʋ you ŋgʋ́ kʋñɔtɩnɩ-mɩ kpam yɔ kɩ-taŋ nɛ ŋgʋ́ kʋwɛ poliŋ yɔ kʋ-tɔm. Ɛlɛ́, sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-mɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se pɩla ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ tatalɩda.

eng: Then he will answer, I assure you that when you havent done it for one of the least of these, you havent done it for me.
local: Wiyaʋ kaɣ-wɛ cosuu se: ‹Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: yee ɩtalabɩ-pʋ mɛ-ɛyáa mbá patakpaɣ-wɛ payaa pʋyʋ yɔ, pa-taa nɔɔyʋ yɔ, ma ɖɔɖɔ ɩtalabɩ-pʋ.›.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be famines and earthquakes in all sorts of places.
local: Ɛjaɖɛ lɛɛɖɛ kʋyʋʋ lɛɛɖɛ yɔɔ nɛ kewiyaɣ lɛɛka ñakʋyɩ lɛɛka yɔɔ. Ñɔɔsɩ kɔŋ lɩʋ nɛ tɛtʋ ñamsɩ hɔɔlɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ taa.

eng: And they will go away into eternal punishment. But the righteous ones will go into eternal life. .
local: Powokini-wɛ ɖenɖe kʋñɔŋ maatɛŋ wɛɛ yɔ nɛ piyele mbá patʋʋzaa yɔ, paba ñowolo ɖenɖe wezuu maatɛŋ wɛɛ yɔ.».

eng: But all these things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Lakasɩ sɩnɛ sɩtɩŋa payɩ sɩwɛɣ ɛzɩ halʋ wɩzʋʋ lʋlʋʋ nɛ pɩɣa wɩɣ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: They will arrest you, abuse you, and they will kill you. All nations will hate you on account of my name.
local: «Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ paɖʋzʋʋ-mɩ mi-koyindinaa nesi taa nɛ paɖʋ-mɩ kʋñɔŋ nɛ pakʋ-mɩ. Ajɛya kpeekpe taa ɛyáa kɔŋ-mɩ paɖʋʋ mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: Jesus returned to the synagogue. A man with a withered hand was there.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu ɖaɣnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa pɩsʋʋ. Puwiye ńɖɩ́ lɛ, abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe, e-nesi camaa.

eng: At that time many will fall away. They will betray each other and hate each other.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kɔŋ lesuu pe-lidaʋ nɛ palɩɩ ɖama wayɩ nɛ papaɖɩ ɖama.

eng: After a few days, Jesus went back to Capernaum, and people heard that he was at home.
local: Pɩlabɩ kɩyakɩŋ cabɩ lɛ, Yesu pɩsɩ Kapɛrnayum. Ɛyáa nɩwa se ɛwɛ ɖɩɣa lɛ.

eng: Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they were watching Jesus closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
local: Ɛyáa mbá paawɛ peeɖe yɔ, pɔcɔŋnɩ Yesu pɩŋŋ se pana se ɛlakɩ nɛ ɛyʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye yaa ɛɛlakɩ? Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pañɩnaɣ tɔm ndʋ́ paawana nɛ pehiɣ-i se ɛlabɩ tʋmɩyɛ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye yɔ.

eng: Many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.
local: Ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kɔŋ lɩʋ se pɛkɛ náyaa nɛ peyili ɛyáa sakɩyɛ.

eng: So many gathered that there was no longer space, not even near the door. Jesus was speaking the word to them.
local: pekpeɣli pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ nɛ posu nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ awayɩ nɔnɔɔ taa nɛ ɛtasɩ-wɛ heyuu Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: He said to the man with the withered hand, Step up where people can see you. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi ɛyʋ ɛnʋ se: «Kʋyɩ ŋsɩŋ ɛyáa hɛkʋ taa cɩnɛ!».

eng: Because disobedience will expand, the love of many will grow cold.
local: Ɛzaɣ́tʋ tɛzɩɣ ɖɔʋ nɛ halɩ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ lesi pɔ-sɔɔlɩm.

eng: Some people arrived, and four of them were bringing to him a man who was paralyzed.
local: Ɛyáa nabɛyɛ wokaɣnɩ kalɩkalaɣ nakɛyɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ. Ɛyáa naanza kpaɣnɩ-kɛ.

eng: Then he said to them, Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill? But they said nothing.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛpɔzɩ mbá pɔcɔŋaɣnɩ-ɩ yɔ se: «Đa-paɣtʋ taa lɛ, ɛbɛ pʋmʋnaa se pala kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye? Kɩbandʋ naa ɛzaɣ́tʋ? Paya ɛyáa ñʋŋ naa pakʋ-wɛ?» Ɛlɛ́, pɔtɔsɔɔlɩ se pocosi-i.

eng: But the one who endures to the end will be delivered.
local: Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛɖɔkʋʋ ɛ-tɩ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩtalɩ kɛtɛzaɣ kɩyakʋ wiye yɔ, Ɛsɔ yakɩ ɛ-ñʋʋ.

eng: They couldnt carry him through the crowd, so they tore off part of the roof above where Jesus was. When they had made an opening, they lowered the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ yɔɔ lɛ, patana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pala nɛ patanɩ-kɛ yɔ. Peeɖe pakpa kuduyuu ŋgʋ́ ɛɛwɛ kɩ-tɛɛ yɔ kʋ-ñʋʋ taa nɛ patʋʋzɩnɩ ɖenɖe ɛɛwɛɛ yɔ nɛ patʋlɩ pɔʋ nɛ petisi-kɛ nɛ ka-ñalʋʋ.

eng: The beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, Gods Son.
local: Ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm pazaa yɔ.

eng: Looking around at them with anger, deeply grieved at their unyielding hearts, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did, and his hand was made healthy.
local: Peeɖe Yesu pana kpawa nɛ ɛcɔnɩ pɛ-ɛsɩndaa kpɛɛɛ nɛ ɛ-hɔyɛ pɩsɩnɩ pa-laŋkpɩzɩyɛ nɛ eheyi ɛyʋ weyi e-nesi camaa yɔ se: «Losi ñe-nesi.» Ɛ́lɛ́ losi-sɩ nɛ sɩ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: This gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations. Then the end will come.
local: Pɔkɔŋ susuu Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm kedeŋa kpeekpe nɛ pɩkɛnɩ aseɣɖe ajɛya tɩŋa payɩ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, kedeŋa tɛ.».

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, Child, your sins are forgiven! .
local: Yesu nawa se ɛyáa ḿbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ lɛ, eheyi kalɩkalaɣ ŋ́gá se: «Man-taabalʋ! Menkpeɣ-ŋ ña-kɩwɛɛkɩm.».

eng: happened just as it was written about in the prophecy of Isaiah: Look, I am sending my messenger before you . He will prepare your way.
local: Tɩpazɩ ɛzɩ pamaʋ náyʋ Ezaayii Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se Ɛsɔ heyi ɛ-Pɩyalʋ se: «Na, mancalɩɣ men-tiyiyu tiyuu nɛ ɛñɔɔzɩ ña-nʋmɔʋ.».

eng: At that, the Pharisees got together with the supporters of Herod to plan how to destroy Jesus.
local: Peeɖe Farɩsɩ ñɩma lɩwa payaa Eroodi ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma nɛ patɩlabʋ lɩmaɣza se pana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ paala nɛ pakʋ Yesu yɔ.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing that Daniel talked about standing in the holy place .
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ ɩkɔŋ naʋ tɛyɔkɩyʋ weyi ɛtatʋʋzɩ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa kpem yɔ, ɛsɩŋ lone kaɖɛ ñɩnɖɛ taa. Ɛ-tɔm náyʋ Daanɩyɛɛlɩ yɔɔdaa. Weyi ɛkalɩɣ tɔm ńdʋ́ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛnɩɩ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ.

eng: Some legal experts were sitting there, muttering among themselves.
local: Yuda ñɩma paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nabɛyɛ kacaɣ peeɖe nɛ pahʋʋ pa-taa Yesu tɔm se:.

eng: a voice shouting in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight .
local: Tɩpazɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ pamaʋ ka-taa se nɔɔyʋ susuu lakʋ taa se: Pɔñɔɔzɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ nʋmɔʋ! Pala nɛ kʋtʋʋzɩ camɩyɛ» yɔ.

eng: Jesus left with his disciples and went to the lake. A large crowd followed him because they had heard what he was doing. They were from Galilee.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pakʋyɩ se powoki Galilee teŋgu nɔɔ, ɛlɛ́ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ tɩŋ-wɛ. Paalɩnɩ ageeta ndɩ ndɩ taa: Galilee nɛ Yudee nɛ.

eng: then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩtalaa lɛ, mbá pɛwɛ Yudee egeetiye taa yɔ, pese pakpanɩ pʋŋ taa.

eng: Why does he speak this way? Hes insulting God. Only the one God can forgive sins.
local: «Ɛyʋ ɛnɛ ɛcalɩ ɛ-taa se we nɛ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́? Ɛwɛɛkɩɣ Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ! Apɩzɩɣ ekpeɣ kɩwɛɛkɩm nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ Ɛsɔ?».

eng: John the Baptist was in the wilderness calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Ḿbʋ́ Yohanɛɛsɩ kɔm ɛwɛɛ lakʋ taa nɛ ɛyáa woki ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ esusuu-wɛ se pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ nɛ ɛlabɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ kpeɣ-wɛ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm.

eng: Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the area surrounding Tyre and Sidon.
local: Iduumee nɛ egeetiye nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ wayɩ yɔ nɛ lɛɛɖɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ Sidɔɔ tɛtʋ nɛ Tiiri ñɩndʋ pɔ-cɔlɔ yɔ a-taa ɛyáa nɛ Yeruusalɛm ñɩma. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, panɩ mbʋ́ ɛlakaɣ yɔ pʋ-tɔm tɩŋa.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down to grab things from their houses.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛwɛ e-kuduyuu ñʋʋ taa lɛ, ese kpaagbaa. Pɩtaacɩbɩ-ɩ se etiki ɛsʋʋ kɩ-tɛɛ nɛ ɛkpaɣ pʋyʋ nɛ ɛkpɛndɩna.

eng: Jesus immediately recognized what they were discussing, and he said to them, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yesu tɩlɩ tɔm ndʋ́ pahʋwaɣ pa-taa ḿbʋ́ yɔ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ɩhʋʋ mɩ-taa ḿbʋ́?.

eng: Everyone in Judea and all the people of Jerusalem went out to the Jordan River and were being baptized by John as they confessed their sins.
local: Peeɖe Yeruusalɛm ñɩma tɩŋa nɛ Yudee egeetiye taa hɔɔlɩŋ tɩŋa taa ñɩma powokaɣ ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ paa weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋ ɛ-kɩwɛɛkɩm mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ nɛ ɛlakɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ taa.

eng: Jesus told his disciples to get a small boat ready for him so the crowd wouldnt crush him.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se pɔñɔɔzɩ-ɩ kpɩyʋʋ nakʋyʋ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɛyáa samaɣ ñɩkɩɣ-ɩ.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Weyi ɛwɛ hayɩm taa lɛ, ese kpaagbaa. Pɩtaacɩbɩ-ɩ se ekpeŋ ɛ-tɛ nɛ ɛkpaɣ e-toko maɣmaɣ.

eng: Which is easier—to say to a paralyzed person, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up, take up your bed, and walk?.
local: Yee meheyi kalɩkalaɣ ŋ́gá se menkpeɣ-kɛ ka-kɩwɛɛkɩm yaa kakʋyɩ kakpaɣ ka-ñalʋʋ nɛ kɛɖɛɛ, tɩ-naalɛ tɩ-taa lɛ, ndʋ́ tɩfɛyɩ kaɖɛ?.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ ɛnʋ, esuwaɣ toko ŋgʋ́ palʋnɩ kpaŋnɔ siɣá naalɛ tʋ hʋndʋ yɔ. Ɛtɔɔlaɣ tambaɖɛ nɖɩ́ palabɩnɩ tɔnɛ yɔ, nakaŋ nɛ tɩŋ num ɛtɔkaɣ.

eng: He had healed so many people that everyone who was sick pushed forward so that they could touch him.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɛyáa nawa se ɛlakaɣ nɛ kʋdɔndɩnaa yɔɔ cɛkɩ. Pʋ-yɔɔ kʋdɔndɩnaa tɩŋa kpedesaɣ se potukuni-i nɛ pɔ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: When Jesus finished speaking all these words, he said to his disciples.
local: Yesu tɛm ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tɔm ndɩ ndɩ kɛdʋʋ payɩ lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se:.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Pʋkɔŋ tɔɔʋ halaa hosi tɩnaa nɛ asɔŋ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́!.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins —he said to the man who was paralyzed.
local: Mancaɣ mawɩlɩ-mɩ se Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ wɛnɩ ɖoŋ se ekpeɣ ɛyáa pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ.» Peeɖe eheyi kalɩkalaɣ ŋ́gá se:.

eng: He announced, One stronger than I am is coming after me. Im not even worthy to bend over and loosen the strap of his sandals.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ɛyáa samaɣ taa se: «Ma-wayɩ lɛ, weyi ɛkɔŋ yɔ, ɛkɩlɩ-m. Mantatɩɩmʋna se moluŋ nɛ mɛyɛɖɩ ɛ-naataŋgbala tɔnzɩ.

eng: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down at his feet and shouted, You are Gods Son.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɛyáa mbá pa-wayɩ wɛ aleewaa yɔ, polosuu Yesu lɛ, aleewaa tuyuu-wɛ nɛ powoki poluŋuu ɛ-tɛɛ nɛ pamakɩ kubusi se: «Ña lɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ.».

eng: You know that the Passover is two days from now. And the Human One will be handed over to be crucified. .
local: «Ɩnawa se pɩkazɩ kɩyakɩŋ naalɛ se pɔtɔɔ Yuda ñɩma Paska Kazandʋ. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa pɔkɔŋ kpaʋ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ nɛ paɖʋzɩ-ɩ koyindinaa nesi taa nɛ paba kam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ.».

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter or on the Sabbath day.
local: Itendi Ɛsɔ camɩyɛ se ɛtaayele se ise ninkaɣ alɩwaatʋ yaa kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye.

eng: Get up, take your mat, and go home. .
local: «Ña! Kʋyɩ ŋkpaɣ ña-ñalʋʋ nɛ ŋkpe».

eng: Early in the morning all the chief priests and the elders of the people reached the decision to have Jesus put to death.
local: Pakpa Yesu nɛ kife tanaŋ tɛɛ cu lɛ, Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ pɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa pɔyɔɔdɩ patɩŋa payɩ se pakʋ-ɩ.

eng: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
local: Ma lɛ, malakɩ-mɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ, ɛnʋ lɛ, ɛkɔŋ-mɩ labʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖoŋ.».

eng: But he strictly ordered them not to reveal who he was.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu paɣ́-wɛ siŋŋ se pataaheyi nɔɔyʋ weyi ɛkɛnaa yɔ.

eng: Then the chief priests and elders of the people gathered in the courtyard of Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Peeɖe cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ Yuda ñɩma sɔsaa pekpeɣli ɖama cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ weyi payaɣ se Kayifu yɔ, ɛ-ɖɩɣa taa nɛ.

eng: There will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kʋñɔŋ ŋgʋ́ kʋkɔŋ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa yɔ, kɩkɩlɩɣ kʋñɔmɩŋ weyi ɛyáa laba ɖooo ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ labʋ kedeŋa nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ sɔnɔ yɔ ɩtɩŋa payɩ. Nakʋyʋ ɛɛtasɩɣ kɔm nɛ kɩkɩlɩ-kʋ.

eng: Jesus raised him up, and right away he picked up his mat and walked out in front of everybody. They were all amazed and praised God, saying, Weve never seen anything like this.
local: nɛ kakʋyɩ kpaagbaa nɛ kakpaɣ ka-ñalʋʋ ɛyáa tɩŋa ɛsɩndaa nɛ pɔcɔnɩ-kɛ pɩŋŋ nɛ kɛɖɛɛ. Pana-kɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩla patɩŋa piti pɩŋŋ nɛ pasaŋ Ɛsɔ nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Đooo yɔ, ɖɩtanata mbʋ́ Yesu labɩ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ pɩ-ɖaɣʋ.».

eng: They bound him, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate the governor.
local: Peeɖe pɔhɔkɩ-ɩ nɛ powoni-i Room wiyaʋ Pilaatɩ tɛ.

eng: About that time, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa ɖɔɖɔ Yesu lɩnɩ Nazarɛɛtɩ, Galilee egeetiye taa nɛ ewolo Yohanɛɛsɩ cɔlɔ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ taa nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ labɩ-ɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ.

eng: Jesus went up on a mountain and called those he wanted, and they came to him.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu kpa pʋʋ yɔɔ. Peeɖe ɛya mbá ɛsɔɔlaa se powolo ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ nɛ paba wolo ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: They were plotting to arrest Jesus by cunning tricks and to kill him.
local: panɩnɩ ɖama patɩŋa se pakpa Yesu mɛsaɣ tɛɛ nɛ pakʋ-ɩ.

eng: If that time werent shortened, nobody would be rescued. But for the sake of the ones whom God chose, that time will be cut short.
local: Yee Ɛsɔ ɛtaamaɣzɩ nɛ ɛpasɩ kʋñɔŋ kɩyakɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-yɔɔ ɛyáa mbá ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ se pɛkɛ ɛ-ñɩma yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, nɔɔyʋ taawɛnɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ.

eng: Jesus went out beside the lake again. The whole crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu lɩɩ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ Galilee teŋgu nɔɔ nɛ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ wolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ-wɛ Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: When Judas, who betrayed Jesus, saw that Jesus was condemned to die, he felt deep regret. He returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, and.
local: Yudaasɩ weyi ɛɖʋzɩ Yesu koyindinaa nesi taa yɔ, ɛnawa se pakʋʋ Yesu lɛ, ɛpazɩ kpiɖuu nɛ ɛkpaɣ liidiye pɔɔyɩsɩ niidozo nzɩ́ paahɛyɩ-ɩ yɔ se ɛpɩsɩɣna ɛcɛlɩ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ pɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa.

eng: While he was coming up out of the water, Jesus saw heaven splitting open and the Spirit, like a dove, coming down on him.
local: Yesu lɩɣaɣnɩ lɩm taa lɛ, ɛna ɛsɔdaa tʋlaa nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu lɩɩ nɛ kitiki ɛ-yɔɔ ɛzɩ halikuku yɔ.

eng: He appointed twelve and called them apostles. He appointed them to be with him, to be sent out to preach.
local: Powoba lɛ, ɛlɩzɩ pa-taa ɛyáa hiu nɛ naalɛ se pɛkɛ e-tiyiyaa. Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-wɛ se: «Me-liu wɛɛ se ɩwɛɛ mɔn-cɔlɔ nɛ mentiyiɣ-mɩ nɛ isusuu Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the tomb.
local: Kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ ɖɛwa nɛ kife kujuka lɛ, Magdala Maarɩɩ nɛ Maarɩɩ lɛlʋ pakʋyɩ tanaŋ cu nɛ powolo se pana Yesu pɩlaʋ.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the feast so there wouldnt be an uproar among the people.
local: Pɔyɔɔdaɣ se: «Đɩtaakpa-ɩ kazandʋ alɩwaatʋ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, ɛyáa samaɣ kʋyʋʋ ɖɔ-yɔɔ.».

eng: Then if somebody says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or Hes over here, dont believe it.
local: «Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yee nɔɔyʋ eheyi-mɩ se Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa nɛ etiyini-mɩ yɔ, ɛwɛ cɩnɛ yaa peeɖe yɔ yɔ, ɩtaatisi.

eng: As he continued along, he saw Levi, Alphaeus son, sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me. Levi got up and followed him.
local: Ɛtɛma nɛ ɛɖɛɣ lɛ, ɛna Alfee pɩyalʋ Leevi, ɛcaɣ ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ taa tikee ɖɩmʋyɛ. Peeɖe eheyi-i se: «Kʋyɩ ŋtɩŋ-m!» Ɛ́lɛ́ kʋyɩ nɛ ɛtɩŋ-ɩ.

eng: said, I did wrong because I betrayed an innocent man. But they said, What is that to us? Thats your problem.
local: Ewoba lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se: «Malabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm: mankpa ɛyʋ ɛnɛ ɖulum nɛ mɛncɛlɩ se pakʋ-ɩ.» Ɛlɛ́, pocosi-i se: «Ñɔ-cɔlɔ lɛ! Pɩtana-ɖʋ.».

eng: And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: Peeɖe pʋyɔɔdaɣnɩ ɛsɔdaa se: «Ña lɛ man-Pɩyalʋ kʋsɔɔlʋ, maahʋʋ ñɔ-yɔɔ kaʋ.».

eng: and to have authority to throw out demons.
local: Mahaɣ-mɩ ɖoŋ se ɩɖɔɔnɩ aleewaa ɖɔɖɔ.».

eng: Look, there was a great earthquake, for an angel from the Lord came down from heaven. Coming to the stone, he rolled it away and sat on it.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, tɛtʋ ñamsɩ siŋŋ nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ lɩɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ etii epilisi pɩlaʋ nɔɔ pɩyɛ nɛ ɛsɩɩ kɩjaʋ nɛ ɛcaɣ ɖɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: When Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease.
local: Yesu kowobi Betaanii tɛtʋ cikpetu taa nɛ ɛsʋʋ Siimɔɔ weyi ɛɛkɛ canaɣ yɔ ɛ-ɖɩɣa taa.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer great signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, even those whom God has chosen.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mbá pɛcɛtɩɣ se ḿbá lɛ Mbá Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ nɛ mbá paba ñɛcɛtɩɣ se pɛkɛ náyaa yɔ, pɔkɔŋ lɩʋ nɛ pawɩlɩ kawɩlɩsɩ sɔsɔsɩ nɛ pala hama lakasɩ sɔsɔsɩ nɛ halɩ yee papɩzɩɣ yɔ, pɛcɛtɩ mbá Ɛsɔ lɩzaa se pɛkɛ ɛ-ñɩma yɔ.

eng: Jesus sat down to eat at Levis house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples. Indeed, many of them had become his followers.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa nɛ tikee mʋyaa sakɩyɛ nɛ lɛlaa mbá ɛyáa kpakaɣ-wɛ se pɛkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa yɔ, powolo Leevi tɛ nɛ pacaɣ tɔɔnaɣ ɖɩtɔɔyɛ. Pɩtɛ ɛyáa mbá patɩŋaɣ-ɩ yɔ, paaɖɔwa.

eng: Judas threw the silver pieces into the temple and left. Then he went and hanged himself.
local: Peeɖe ɛkpaɣ liidiye ńɖɩ́ nɛ ɛmɩzɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ ewolo ɛɖɩ ɛ-tɩ nimiye nɛ ɛsɩ.

eng: At once the Spirit forced Jesus out into the wilderness.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ŋ́gʋ́ kitolisi-i nɛ ɛɖɛɛ lakʋ taa.

eng: Jesus left that place and came to his hometown. His disciples followed him.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pakʋyɩ ɖenɖe paawɛɛ yɔ nɛ powolo Nazarɛɛtɩ tɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-taa ɛcaɣaɣ nɛ ɛpaɣlɩ yɔ.

eng: He appointed twelve: Peter, a name he gave Simon;.
local: Ɛyáa mbá ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ yɔ, pa-hɩla yɔ: Siimɔɔ weyi ɛkʋm-ɩ hɩɖɛ se Pɩyɛ yɔ nɛ.

eng: Now his face was like lightning and his clothes as white as snow.
local: Ɛñɩlɩsaɣ ɛzɩ tɛʋ ñɩlɩsʋʋ yɔ nɛ e-wondu hʋlʋmɩ layɩ layɩ.

eng: a woman came to him with a vase made of alabaster containing very expensive perfume. She poured it on Jesus head while he was sitting at dinner.
local: Pɔtɔkaɣ tɔɔnaɣ lɛ, halʋ nɔɔyʋ sʋʋ pɔ-cɔlɔ, ɛɖɔkɩ tɩlaaɖɩ ŋgʋ́ kʋwɛ liidiye pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ yɔ, fiɣa kɩñɩlɩsaɣ nakɛyɛ taa ɖo. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛpɩyɩ-kʋ Yesu ñʋʋ taa waaa.

eng: Look, Ive told you ahead of time.
local: Iwelesi camɩyɛ nɛ ɩnɩɩ, meheyi-mɩ se ɩñɔɔzɩ mɩ-tɩ pʋcɔ pʋkɔɔ pɩla ḿbʋ́.

eng: When some of the legal experts from among the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples, Why is he eating with sinners and tax collectors.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, Yuda ñɩma paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa mbá pɛkɛ Farɩsɩ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma yɔ paawɛɛ. Pɛ-ɛsɩndaa Yesu nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa nɛ tikee mʋyaa pɔtɔkaɣ tɔɔnaɣ. Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ɛ nɛ tikee mʋyaa nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa pɛkpɛndɩ pɔtɔkɩ?».

eng: The chief priests picked up the silver pieces and said, According to the Law its not right to put this money in the treasury. Since it was used to pay for someones life, its unclean.
local: Ɛlɛ́, cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa tɔzɩ liidiye ńɖɩ́ nɛ pɔtɔ se: «Đa-paɣtʋ taa lɛ, pɩtaha nʋmɔʋ se paɖʋ-ɖɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa koozaɣ taa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩkɛnɩ ɛyʋ sɩm liidiye.».

eng: He was in the wilderness for forty days, tempted by Satan. He was among the wild animals, and the angels took care of him.
local: Ɛcaɣ peeɖe kɩyakɩŋ nɩɩnaza nɛ Satanɩ wolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛtakɩɣ-ɩ. Tɛtɛ kpɩna hɛkʋ taa ɛɛwɛɛ nɛ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa wolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ pasɩŋnɩ-ɩ.

eng: On the Sabbath, he began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were surprised. Where did this man get all this? Whats this wisdom hes been given? What about the powerful acts accomplished through him?.
local: Pɩtalɩ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye lɛ, ɛpazɩ Ɛsɔtɔm susuu Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa. Ɛyáa sakɩyɛ nɩ-tʋ lɛ, pɩlabɩ-wɛ piti nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Le ɛlɩ lɔŋ ɛnʋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣnɩ ɛyáa nɛ ɛlakɩ Ɛsɔ hama lakasɩ?.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, whom he nicknamed Boanerges, which means sons of Thunder;.
local: Yakubu nɛ e-neu Yohanɛɛsɩ mbá pɛkɛ Zebedee pɩyalaa nɛ ɛkʋm-wɛ hɩɖɛ se Boanɛrjɛsɩ yɔ. Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se tɛʋ tiluu piya.

eng: The guards were so terrified of him that they shook with fear and became like dead men.
local: Peeɖe sɔɔndʋ kpa mbá paaɖaŋ kʋ-yɔɔ yɔ siŋŋ nɛ peseli yem kpa kpa kpa nɛ pe-feziŋ li.

eng: Now when the disciples saw it they were angry and said, Why this waste?.
local: Ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa na ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa-taa wɩɩnɩ halʋ ɛnʋ nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Halʋ ɛnɛ, ɛwɛɛkʋʋ tɩlaaɖɩ ŋ́gʋ́ nɛ ɛlɔʋ yem yɔ, pʋwazɩɣ-ɩ we?.

eng: So if they say to you, Look, hes in the desert, dont go out. And if they say, Look, hes in the rooms deep inside the house, dont believe it.
local: «Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yee peheyi-mɩ se: ‹Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna, ɛwɛ lakʋ taa› yɔ, ɩtaawolo. Yaa peheyi-mɩ se: ‹Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna, ɛwɛ kuduyuu tɛɛ› yɔ, ɩtaatisi.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do. I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners. .
local: Yesu nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ecosi-wɛ se: «Pɩtɛkɛ alaafɩya tɩnaa pʋpɔzʋʋ se powolo fɛɖaa tɛ, kʋdɔndɩnaa pʋpɔzʋʋ se powolo. Pɩtɛkɛ mbá pamaɣzɩɣ se pɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzaa yɔ, pa-wayɩ mɔnkɔma. Ɛlɛ́, mbá pasɩm pa-tɩ se pɛkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa yɔ, pa-wayɩ mɔnkɔm se pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ.».

eng: So they decided to use it to buy the potters field where strangers could be buried.
local: Panɩnɩ ɖama lɛ, pakpaɣ-ɖɩ nɛ payabɩnɩ tɛtʋ pɛŋ mayʋ cɔlɔ se pepi agɔmaa.

eng: After John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee announcing Gods good news.
local: Paɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ salaka wayɩ lɛ, Yesu wolo Galilee egeetiye taa nɛ ɛpazɩ ɛyáa heyuu Ɛsɔ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: Isnt this the carpenter? Isnt he Marys son and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Arent his sisters here with us? They were repulsed by him and fell into sin.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ ɛnʋ lɛ kuduyiŋ kanɖʋ naa we? Pɩtɛkɛ ɛnʋ lɛ Maarɩɩ pɩyalʋ? Yaa pɩtɛkɛ e-newaa lɛ Yakubu nɛ Yoosi nɛ Yuudi nɛ Siimɔɔ? Pɩtɛkɛ ɖa nɛ e-newaa halaa ñɩma ɖɩwɛnɩ cɩnɛ yɔ?» Pa-lɩmaɣza ana ayebina nɛ pa-laŋa wɛɛ kpɩzɩŋ nɛ petetisi Yesu tɔm.

eng: and Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, Alphaeus son; Thaddaeus; Simon the Cananaean;.
local: nɛ Andree nɛ Filiipu nɛ Bartelemii nɛ Maatiyee nɛ Tomaa nɛ Alfee pɩyalʋ Yakubu nɛ Taadee nɛ Siimɔɔ weyi payaɣ se Yɔɔ Kandɩyʋ yɔ.

eng: But the angel said to the women, Dont be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ tiyiyu heyi halaa ḿbá se: «Mɩ lɛ, sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-mɩ. Manawa se Yesu weyi paakam kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ ɩñɩnɩɣ.

eng: Jesus began to teach beside the lake again. Such a large crowd gathered that he climbed into a boat there on the lake. He sat in the boat while the whole crowd was nearby on the shore.
local: Yesu tasaɣ Ɛsɔtɔm susuu Galilee teŋgu nɔɔ lɛ, ɛyáa kpeɣli ɛ-cɔlɔ sakɩyɛ pɩŋŋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛkʋyaa ɛkpa kpɩyʋʋ taa lɩm yɔɔ nɛ ɛcaɣ nɛ ɛyáa wɛɛ teŋgu nɔɔ.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for a lot of money and given to the poor.
local: Yee paapɛdɩ-kʋ, pataayabɩ-kʋ liidiye sɔsɔɖɛ nɛ paha kʋñɔndɩnaa naa we?».

eng: Just as the lightning flashes from the east to the west, so it will be with the coming of the Human One.
local: Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ kɔŋ nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ na-ɩ ɛzɩ tɛʋ ñɩlɩsʋʋ tam kʋɖʋm peleu wɩsɩ ɖɩlɩyɛ nɛ pɩnaɣ paa le yɔ.

eng: Johns disciples and the Pharisees had a habit of fasting. Some people asked Jesus, Why do Johns disciples and the Pharisees disciples fast, but yours dont.
local: Kɩyakʋ nakʋyʋ Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma pɔhɔkaɣ nɔɔ. Peeɖe ɛyáa woba nɛ pɔpɔzɩ Yesu se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma Farɩsɩ ñɩma tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ ñɩma pɔhɔkʋʋ nɔɔ nɛ ña-ñɩma ñaahɔkʋʋ?».

eng: Thats why that field is called Field of Blood to this very day.
local: Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ pɩkpaɣʋnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋ́gʋ́ yɔ, payaɣ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ se: «Sɩm Tɛtʋ.».

eng: saying, Now is the time! Here comes Gods kingdom! Change your hearts and lives, and trust this good news! .
local: Ɛtɔŋaɣ se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, alɩwaatʋ talaa se Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ ɛkɔɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ nɛ itisi ɛ-Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns, among their relatives, and in their own households. .
local: Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Pañaŋ náyʋ paa le. Ɛlɛ́, ɛ-tɛtʋ nɛ e-liɖe nɛ ɛ-hɔʋ pa-taa paañaŋ-ɩ.».

eng: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.
local: nɛ Yudaasɩ Iskarɩyɔɔtɩ. Ɛnʋ kitini ɛkpa Yesu nɛ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ koyindinaa nesi taa.

eng: He isnt here, because hes been raised from the dead, just as he said. Come, see the place where they laid him.
local: Ɛfɛyɩ cɩnɛ, efema ɛzɩ ɛɛyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ. Ɩkɔɔ ɩna ɖenɖe paahɛzɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: He said many things to them in parables. While teaching them, he said.
local: nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ tɔm sakɩyɛ nɛ aduwa taa nɛ eheyi-wɛ se:.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said, Why do you make trouble for the woman? Shes done a good thing for me.
local: Pɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yesu nɩɩ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛbɛ yɔɔ ɩcɛyɩɣ halʋ ɛnʋ ḿbʋ́? Mbʋ́ ɛlabɩ-m yɔ, pʋwɛ ɖeu.

eng: The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ potuu eduuye se: ‹Namɖazʋʋ wayɩ kpeliŋ fɛtɩɣ› yɔ.».

eng: Jesus said, The wedding guests cant fast while the groom is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cant fast.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Ɩmaɣzɩɣ se mbá payaɣ-wɛ halɩkpaɣyɛ kazandʋ ɖeɖe yɔ, powoki pacaɣ ñɔɔsɩ halʋ kɩfalʋ walʋ ɛsɩndaa yaa we? Pɩɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: This fulfilled the words of Jeremiah the prophet: And I took the thirty pieces of silver, the price for the one whose price had been set by some of the Israelites.
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩ náyʋ Yereemii maʋ Yesu tɔm Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Ɛmawa se: «Pakpaɣ liidiye pɔɔyɩsɩ niidozo nzɩ́ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma ɖʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: As Jesus passed alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, for they were fishermen.
local: Kɩyakʋ nakʋyʋ Yesu cɔlaɣ Galilee teŋgu. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛna kpakpasɩ kpayaa naalɛ: Siimɔɔ nɛ e-neu Andree, pɔlɔwaɣ piyuu.
