eng: Many women were watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to serve him.
local: Halaa nabɛyɛ kɛwɛ poliŋ taa nɛ pɔcɔŋnɩ ɖɔɖɔ, pɔɖɔ sakɩyɛ. Ḿbá katɩŋnɩ-ɩ nɛ ɖooo Galilee nɛ pasɩŋnɩ-ɩ.

eng: But all this has happened so that what the prophets said in the scriptures might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left Jesus and ran away.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɩtɩŋa pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se tɔm ndʋ́ náyaa ma Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ, tɩla.» Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tɩŋa payɩ mɩzɩ ɖama nɛ peyebi-i.

eng: When Jesus and his disciples had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret, anchored the boat.
local: Pɛtɛzɩ teŋgu nɛ patalɩ Jeneezaarɛɛtɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa lɛ, paɖɩ pa-kpɩyʋʋ nimiye kidiiliŋ liu tɛɛ.

eng: They came to the synagogue leaders house, and he saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
local: Patalɩ Yayiruusi ɛnʋ ɛ-tɛ lɛ, Yesu maɣnɩ ɛyáa makaɣ kubusi nɛ pependiɣ nɛ pewiliɣ.

eng: Leaving them, he got back in the boat and crossed to the other side of the lake.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu yebi-wɛ nɛ ɛ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa paɖaɣnɩ kpɩyʋʋ taa kpaʋ nɛ pɛtɛzɩ teŋgu kidiiliŋ lɛɛkʋ yɔɔ.

eng: After leaving the region of Tyre, Jesus went through Sidon toward the Galilee Sea through the region of the Ten Cities.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu kʋyɩ Tiiri egeetiye taa nɛ ɛtɩŋnɩ Sidɔɔ ñɩnɖɛ taa nɛ ewolo ɛsʋʋ Tɛtʋ Hiu ñɩnɖɛ taa se ewoki Galilee teŋgu nɔɔ.

eng: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedees sons.
local: Pa-taa lɛlaa yɔ: Magdala tɛtʋ taa Maarɩɩ nɛ Yakubu nɛ Yozɛɛfʋ po-ɖoo Maarɩɩ nɛ Zebedee pɩyalaa ɖoo.

eng: Those who arrested Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest. The legal experts and the elders had gathered there.
local: Pakpa Yesu lɛ, pakpaɣ-ɩ se powokini-i Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ Kayifu tɛ ɖenɖe pɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa nɛ pa-paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa paakpeɣlaa yɔ.

eng: and came ashore. People immediately recognized Jesus.
local: Palɩwa nɛ ɛyáa na Yesu lɛ, patɩlɩ-ɩ kpaagbaa.

eng: He went in and said to them, Whats all this commotion and crying about? The child isnt dead. Shes only sleeping. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛsʋʋ ɖɩɣa nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ipeendiɣ nɛ iwiɣ ḿbʋ́? Pɩɣa kanɛ koɖou kuɖou, katasɩ kɩsɩbʋ.».

eng: Jesus disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, so they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
local: Pɛtɛzaɣ teŋgu lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa kɔsɔ tɔɔnaɣ yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, tindine hɔɔlaɣ kʋɖʋmaɣ kɛwɛnɩ pɔ-cɔlɔ kpɩyʋʋ taa.

eng: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly speak, and they begged him to place his hand on the man for healing.
local: Peeɖe powoni ndam kaayɔɔdaɣ nakɛyɛ nɛ pewiini-i se ɛtɩnɩ nesi kɔ-yɔɔ nɛ kɔ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: That evening a man named Joseph came. He was a rich man from Arimathea who had become a disciple of Jesus.
local: Đanaɣ ɖanaa lɛ, Ariimatee ñɩm tʋ weyi payaɣ se Yozɛɛfʋ nɛ ɛkɛ Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance until he came to the high priests courtyard. He entered that area and sat outside with the officers to see how it would turn out.
local: Pɛɖɛɣaɣnɩ-ɩ lɛ, Pɩyɛ wɛɛ mɛlʋʋ taa mɛlʋʋ taa nɛ ɛtɩŋɩɣ ɛ-wayɩ. Ɛtalɩ cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-taɣ yɔɔ lɛ, ɛcaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyaa cɔlɔ se ɛna mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ tɛmnaʋ yɔ.

eng: and ran around that whole region bringing sick people on their mats to wherever they heard he was.
local: Peeɖe pakpaɣ seya nɛ pɔcɔʋ ɛjaɖɛ tɩŋa nɛ pakpakɩɣ kʋdɔndɩnaa pa-ñalɩŋ yɔɔ nɛ powokini-wɛ ɖenɖe panɩɣaɣ se ɛwɛɛ yɔ.

eng: They laughed at him, but he threw them all out. Then, taking the childs parents and his disciples with him, he went to the room where the child was.
local: Ɛlɛ́, powoŋ-i. Peeɖe ɛɖɔɔnɩ patɩŋa nɛ palɩɩnɩ awayɩ nɛ ɛ́yáa pɩɣa caa nɛ ko-ɖoo nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ḿbá pa-naadozo nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ pasʋʋ ɖenɖe pɛhɛzɩ pɩɣa ŋ́gá yɔ.

eng: He gave them strict orders: Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees as well as the yeast of Herod. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu paɣ́-wɛ se: «Ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ ɖeu nɛ Eroodi nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma pɔ-sɔsɔɔna.».

eng: Jesus took him away from the crowd by himself and put his fingers in the mans ears. Then he spit and touched the mans tongue.
local: Peeɖe ɛkpaɣ-kɛ nɛ ɛkɛzɩ kɩjaʋ nɛ ɛlɛkɩ e-niwee ka-nɩŋgbaaŋ taa nɛ ɛla ndaatʋ nɛ ɛtaa ka-nzʋlʋmɩyɛ yɔɔ.

eng: He came to Pilate and asked for Jesus body. Pilate gave him permission to take it.
local: ewolo Room wiyaʋ Pilaatɩ cɔlɔ. Ewoba lɛ, etendi-i se yee etisiɣ yɔ, ɛcɛlɩ-ɩ Yesu nɛ ewona epi. Ɛlɛ́, Pilaatɩ tisi nɛ ɛha nɔɔ se powolo pakpaɣ-ɩ nɛ pɛcɛlɩ-ɩ.

eng: The chief priests and the whole council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so that they could put him to death.
local: Peeɖe cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ tɔm hʋyaa lɛlaa tɩŋa pañɩnaɣ aseɣɖe tɩnaa se powolo pɛcɛtɩnɩ Yesu taa nɛ pehiɣ-i pakʋ-ɩ.

eng: Wherever he went—villages, cities, or farming communities—they would place the sick in the marketplaces and beg him to allow them to touch even the hem of his clothing. Everyone who touched him was healed.
local: Paa ewobi tɛtʋ cikpetu taa yaa sɔsɔtʋ taa yaa tɛyɛ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛyáa wokini pa-kʋdɔndɩnaa ɖenɖe ɛyáa kpeɣliɣ yɔ. Paɖaŋaɣ-ɩ se pewiini-i nɛ eyele potukuni e-toko nɔɔ maɣmaɣ. Toovenim mbá potukaɣnɩ-kʋ yɔ, patɩŋa pɔ-yɔɔ cɛkaɣ.

eng: Taking her hand, he said to her, Talitha koum , which means, Young woman, get up.
local: Peeɖe Yesu ɖɔkɩ ke-nesi taa nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Taliita kum .» Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se: «Pɩɣa ña! Meheyiɣ-ŋ se ŋkʋyɩ.».

eng: The disciples discussed this among themselves, He said this because we have no bread.
local: Ɛlɛ́, papazɩ ɖama yɔɔdɩnaʋ se: «Yaa ɖɩtakpaɣ tindine yɔɔ ɛkalɩɣ-ɖʋ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: Looking into heaven, Jesus sighed deeply and said, Ephphatha, which means, Open up.
local: Ɛtɛma lɛ, ɛkpazɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ ɛsɔdaa nɛ emuzi nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Efata .» Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se: «Tʋlɩ.».

eng: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
local: Peeɖe ɛkpaɣ-ɩ nɛ ɛhɔkɩ-ɩ pɩsaʋ kɩfalʋʋ kʋhʋlʋmʋʋ nakʋyʋ taa.

eng: They didnt find anything they could use from the many false witnesses who were willing to come forward. But finally they found two.
local: Ɛlɛ́, petehiɣ nɔɔyʋ. Ŋgʋ́, ɛyáa sakɩyɛ woba nɛ pɛcɛtɩnɩ ɛ-taa. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, abalaa naalɛ nabɛyɛ wolo.

eng: Suddenly the young woman got up and began to walk around. She was years old. They were shocked!.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩɣa peleɣa ŋ́gá kakʋyɩ kɔɖɔ nɛ kapazɩ ɖɔm. Kaawɛnɩ pɩnzɩ hiu nɛ naalɛ. Ɛyáa mbá paawɛ peeɖe yɔ, pɩɖɩɣzɩ-wɛ pɩŋŋ.

eng: At once, his ears opened, his twisted tongue was released, and he began to speak clearly.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ndam kaayɔɔdaɣ ŋ́gá, ka-nɩŋgbaaŋ taa tʋlɩ nɛ ka-nzʋlʋmɩyɛ yɛɖɩ nɛ kapazɩ yɔɔdʋʋ camɩyɛ.

eng: who said, This man said, I can destroy Gods temple and rebuild it in three days.
local: nɛ pɔtɔ se: «Abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛtɔm se ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛyɔkɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa nɛ ɛɖaɣnɩ-kɛ maʋ nɛ kɩyakɩŋ naadozo.».

eng: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had carved out of the rock. After he rolled a large stone at the door of the tomb, he went away.
local: nɛ ewona epi pɩlaʋ kɩfalʋ ŋgʋ́ eeyeba nɛ pɛcɛsɩ-ɩ ɛwaɣ taa yɔ kɩ-taa nɛ epilisi pɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ naɖɩyɛ nɛ ɛɖɩɣ kʋ-nɔɔ nɛ ɛcaŋ ɛɖɛɛ.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, Why are you talking about the fact that you dont have any bread? Dont you grasp what has happened? Dont you understand? Are your hearts so resistant to what God is doing?.
local: Yesu nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩyɔɔdʋʋnɩ ɖama se ɩtakpaɣ tindine? Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma mɩ-laŋa wɛɛ kpɩzɩŋ nɛ halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ ɩɩnɩɣ?.

eng: He gave them strict orders that no one should know what had happened. Then he told them to give her something to eat.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu paɣ́ ka-ñɩma se: «Itaala nɛ nɔɔyʋ nɩɩ tɔm tʋnɛ.» Pʋwayɩ lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se: «Iha-kɛ tɔɔnaɣ.».

eng: Jesus gave the people strict orders not to tell anyone. But the more he tried to silence them, the more eagerly they shared the news.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu paɣ́ patɩŋa se pataaheyi-tʋ nɔɔyʋ, ɛlɛ́ pamanɩ ɛ-tɔm yɔɔ nɛ posusi-tʋ siŋŋ.

eng: Then the high priest stood and said to Jesus, Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Peeɖe cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ kʋyaa nɛ ɛpɔzɩ Yesu se: «Tɔm ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛyáa panɛ pɔtɔŋ se ŋyɔɔdaa yɔ, ŋfɛyɩnɩ natʋyʋ se ŋcosuu yaa we?».

eng: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting in front of the tomb.
local: Magdala Maarɩɩ nɛ Maarɩɩ lɛlʋ paacaɣ peeɖe nɛ patʋʋzɩnɩ ɖenɖe pɩlaʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kʋ-nɔɔ wɛna yɔ nɛ pɔcɔŋnɩ-kʋ.

eng: Dont you have eyes? Why cant you see? Dont you have ears? Why cant you hear? Dont you remember?.
local: Ɩwɛnɩ ɛsɛ, ɛlɛ́ ɩɩnaɣ, ɩwɛnɩ nɩŋgbaaŋ, ɛlɛ́ ɩɩnɩɣ. Ɩsɔ mbʋ́ malaba yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ yaa we?.

eng: People were overcome with wonder, saying, He does everything well! He even makes the deaf to hear and gives speech to those who cant speak.
local: Ḿbʋ́ labɩnɩ ɛyáa piti pɩŋŋ nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Mbʋ́ ɛlakɩ yɔ, pɩtɩŋa pʋwɛ ɖeu. Ɛlakɩ nɛ ndamaa nɩɣ nɛ kaayɔɔdasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ.».

eng: But Jesus was silent. The high priest said, By the living God, I demand that you tell us whether you are the Christ, Gods Son.
local: Ɛlɛ́, esuma ɖiɣ ɖiɣ. Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ña lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ yaa we? Ña ɖɔɖɔ lɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ? Yee ḿbʋ́, ɖuúni Ɛsɔ wezuu tʋ.».

eng: The next day, which was the day after Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate.
local: Kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Yuda ñɩma ñɔɔzʋʋ pa-tɩ se pʋcɔ pɩtalɩ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ yɔ, kɩɖɛwa nɛ tɛʋ fe lɛ, cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma powolo pekpeɣli Pilaatɩ tɛ.

eng: When I broke five loaves of bread for those five thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Twelve.
local: Mancɩyɩ tindine hɔɔlasɩ kagbaanzɩ nɛ abalaa kutokiŋ kagbaanzɩ tɔɔ lɛ, ɖɔkɩŋ ɛzɩma taa iku hɔɔlasɩ nzɩ́ sɩkazaa yɔ?» Pocosi-i se: «Hiu nɛ naalɛ.».

eng: You said it, Jesus replied. But I say to you that from now on youll see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds . .
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Ña-tɩ tɩŋa ŋyɔɔdɩnɩ ḿbʋ́. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: pɩkpaɣʋnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, ɩkɔŋ naʋ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ, ɛcaɣ Ɛsɔ Đoŋ Tɩŋa Tʋ cɔlɔ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa. Ɩkɔŋ-ɩ naʋ ɖɔɖɔ ɛsɔmɩndʋ yɔɔ, ɛɖɔŋ kɔm.».

eng: They said, Sir, we remember that while that deceiver was still alive he said, After three days I will arise.
local: nɛ peheyi-i se: «Đajaa! Đɩtɔzʋʋ se alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɔbɔtʋ ɛnɛ ɛɛwɛnɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ yɔ, ɛtɔm se kɩyakɩŋ naadozo wayɩ lɛ, efeŋ.

eng: And when I broke seven loaves of bread for those four thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Seven.
local: Ɛtasɩ-wɛ pɔzʋʋ se: «Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mancɩyɩ tindine hɔɔlasɩ lʋbɛ nɛ ɛyáa kutokiŋ naanza tɔɔ yɔ, ɖɔkɩŋ ɛzɩma taa iku hɔɔlasɩ nzɩ́ sɩkazaa yɔ?» Pocosi-i se: «Đɔkɩŋ lʋbɛ.».

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Hes insulting God! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, youve heard his insult against God.
local: Cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ, ɛnɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ nɛ ɛcɩyɩ ɛ-cɔjɔtʋ toko nɛ pɩwɩlɩ se pɩwɩzɩ-ɩ pɩŋŋ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ ɛyáa lɛlaa se: «Ɛwɛɛkɩ Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ! Ɛbɛ yɔɔ pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩtasɩ ñɩnʋʋ aseɣɖe tɩnaa yɔ? Paa weyi ɛnɩnɩ ɛ-nɩŋgbaaŋ!.

eng: Therefore, order the grave to be sealed until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come and steal the body and tell the people, Hes been raised from the dead. This last deception will be worse than the first.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩpɔzʋʋ-ŋ se ŋlɩzɩ sɔɔjanaa nɛ powolo paɖaŋ ɛ-pɩlaʋ yɔɔ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ kɩyakɩŋ naadozo ɩnʋ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa woki patʋlɩ kʋ-nɔɔ nɛ pamɩlɩ-ɩ nɛ pɔkɔɔ pɛcɛtɩ ɛyáa se efema nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa nɛ pɛ-cɛtɩm ñɔkɔɔ pɩkɩlɩ kajalaɣ ñɩmbʋ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, And you still dont understand? .
local: Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Nɛ pɩla ɛzɩma halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ ɩtatɩɩnɩda yɔ?».

eng: What do you think? And they answered, He deserves to die.
local: Ɩmaɣzɩɣ se we?» Ɛlɛ́, pocosi se: «Ɛ-tɔm sɩba, pʋwɛɛ se pakʋ-ɩ.».

eng: Pilate replied, You have soldiers for guard duty. Go and make it as secure as you know how.
local: Peeɖe Pilaatɩ cosi-wɛ se: «Sɔɔjanaa yɔ! Ɩkpaɣ-wɛ nɛ powolo paɖaŋ pɩlaʋ yɔɔ ɛzɩ ɩsɔɔlʋʋ yɔ.».

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to Jesus and begged him to touch and heal him.
local: Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɛtɛzɩ teŋgu lɛ, patalɩ Bɛtsayɩda. Peeɖe powoni yʋlʋm nɔɔyʋ ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ pewini-i se etukuni-i nɛ ɛ-ɛsɛ kuli.

eng: Then they spit in his face and beat him. They hit him.
local: Peeɖe papazɩ Yesu ɛsɩndaa tɔʋ ndaatʋ nɛ pamakɩ-ɩ ŋguluma nɛ lɛlaa cɛkɩ-ɩ kadanzɩ.

eng: Then they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.
local: Peeɖe powobi pañɩ pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ paɖɩɣ pɩlaʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kʋ-nɔɔ yɔ ɖɩ-yɔɔ se nɔɔyʋ etukuni-ɖɩ lɛ, pana. Pɛtɛma lɛ, pasɩɩ sɔɔjanaa se paɖaŋ kpam.

eng: Taking the blind mans hand, Jesus led him out of the village. After spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on the man, he asked him, Do you see anything? .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu kpa e-nesi taa nɛ ɛhɔm-ɩ ɛlɩzɩ-ɩ tɛtʋ kɩjaʋ yɔɔ nɛ elii ndaatʋ nɛ ɛtaa yʋlʋm ɛnʋ ɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ ɛtɩnɩ e-nesi ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ɛbɛ ŋnaɣ?».

eng: and said, Prophesy for us, Christ! Who hit you.
local: nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Weyi ña Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ, kpeɣ se amabɩ-ŋ?».

eng: The man looked up and said, I see people. They look like trees, only they are walking around.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yʋlʋm kuli ɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ ecosi-i se: «Manaɣ ɛyáa, pɛwɛ ɛzɩ tɩŋ yɔ, pɔɖɔŋ.».

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant woman came and said to him, You were also with Jesus the Galilean.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Pɩyɛ caɣ taɣ yɔɔ yɔ, tɩ-taa cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ tʋmlaɖʋ halʋ ñɩnʋ nɔɔyʋ lɩwa nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ-ɩ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Ña ɖɔɖɔ! Ŋtɩŋaɣ Galilee tʋ Yesu wayɩ.».

eng: Then Jesus placed his hands on the mans eyes again. He looked with his eyes wide open, his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.
local: Yesu tasɩ e-nesi tɩnʋʋ yʋlʋm ɛnʋ ɛ-ɛsɛ yɔɔ lɛ, atɩkuli nɛ ɛcɔna nɛ ɛnaɣ pɩtɩŋa camɩyɛ kpayɩ kpayɩ.

eng: But he denied it in front of all of them, saying, I dont know what you are talking about.
local: Peeɖe ɛkpɛzɩ patɩŋa pɛ-ɛsɩndaa se: «Maasɩŋ ndʋ́ ŋcaɣ yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ.».

eng: Then Jesus sent him home, saying, Dont go into the village! .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-i se: «Đɛɛ ŋkpe, taapɩsɩ tɛtʋ taa.».

eng: When he went over to the gate, another woman saw him and said to those who were there, This man was with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛlɩɩ ewolo nɔnɔɔ taa. Ɛlɛ́, tʋmlaɖʋ halʋ ñɩnʋ lɛlʋ tasɩ-ɩ naʋ nɛ eheyi mbá pɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ se: «Ɛnɛ ɖɔɖɔ ɛtɩŋaɣ Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu wayɩ.».

eng: Jesus and his disciples went into the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, Who do people say that I am? .
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pakʋyɩ se powoki tɛtʋ cikpetu ndɩ ndɩ ndʋ́ tɩwɛ Sezaaree Filiipu tɛtʋ cɔlɔ yɔ tɩ-taa. Palɩ nʋmɔʋ taa lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛyáa maɣzɩɣ se ma lɛ anɩ?».

eng: With a solemn pledge, he denied it again, saying, I dont know the man.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ tasɩ kpɛzʋʋ nɛ eɖuu se: «Mantasɩ abalʋ ɛnʋ.».

eng: They told him, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others one of the prophets.
local: Pocosi-i se: «Nabɛyɛ tɔŋ se ña lɛ Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ. Lɛlaa se ña lɛ Elii weyi paaɖaŋaa yɔ nɛ lɛlaa se ña lɛ náyaa taa lɛlʋ.».

eng: A short time later those standing there came and said to Peter, You must be one of them. The way you talk gives you away.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, mbá pɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ, potuzi pa-tɩ nɛ pɔñɔtɩnɩ-ɩ nɛ peheyi-i se: «Toovenim, ŋkɛ pa-taa lɛlʋ: ñɔ-yɔɔdaɣ wɩlɩɣnɩ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, You are the Christ.
local: Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Mɩ yɔ, mɩhʋʋ se ma lɛ anɩ?» Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ cosi-i se: «Ña lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ.».

eng: Then he cursed and swore, I dont know the man! At that very moment the rooster crowed.
local: Peeɖe ɛkpɛzaa laɣ laɣ nɛ eɖuu se: «Nɛ Ɛsɔ! Mantasɩ abalʋ ɛnʋ!» Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, kalɩmɔʋ koo.

eng: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu paɣ́-wɛ se pataaheyi-tʋ nɔɔyʋ kaʋ.

eng: Peter remembered Jesus words, Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times. And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ tɔzɩ tɔm ndʋ́ Yesu keheyi-i se: «Pʋcɔ kalɩmɔʋ koo lɛ, ŋkpɛzɩɣ tam naadozo se ŋtasɩm-m» yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ. Peeɖe ɛkʋyaa ɛlɩɩ nɛ ewili ɛzɩ pɛcɛbɩ e-liu tɛɛ yɔ.

eng: Then Jesus began to teach his disciples: The Human One must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts, and be killed, and then, after three days, rise from the dead. .
local: Yesu wɩlaɣ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se peɖiɣzini se ɛnʋ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ nɛ Yuda ñɩma sɔsaa nɛ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ pa-paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa pekizi ɛ-tɔm nɛ pakʋ-ɩ ɛsɩ. Kɩyakʋ naadozo ñɩŋgʋ wiye lɛ, Ɛsɔ fezi-i.

eng: He said this plainly. But Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him.
local: Eheyi-wɛ-tʋ camɩyɛ yayɩ yayɩ lɛ, Pɩyɛ yaa-ɩ nɛ pɛkɛɛ kɩjaʋ nɛ ekizini-i-tʋ.

eng: Jesus turned and looked at his disciples, then sternly corrected Peter: Get behind me, Satan. You are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu kisini ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa yɔɔ nɛ ɛkalɩ Pɩyɛ se: «Satanɩ ña! Đɛɛ mɔ-yɔɔ. Ña-lɩmaɣza tɛkɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩna, ɛyáa ñɩna akɛnaa.».

eng: After calling the crowd together with his disciples, Jesus said to them, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu yaa ɛyáa samaɣ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Weyi e-liu wɛɛ se ɛtɩŋɩɣ ma-wayɩ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛsɔɔ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ etisi nɛ ɛtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ .

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me and because of the good news will save them.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛñɩnɩɣ se ɛcaɣ wezuu tam yɔ, ɛtɛŋnɩ-kʋ lesuu. Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ sɩkɩ ma nɛ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ɖɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛcakɩ wezuu tam.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives?.
local: Yee ɛyʋ ehiɣ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ñɩm tɩŋa payɩ nɛ elesi e-wezuu lɛ, pɩwazɩɣ-ɩ we?.

eng: What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Ɛbɛ ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛkpaɣ nɛ ɛlɛɣzɩ ku-lone taa?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this unfaithful and sinful generation, the Human One will be ashamed of that person when he comes in the Fathers glory with the holy angels. .
local: Yee fɛyɛ ɛkpa nɔɔyʋ ma nɛ mɔn-tɔm ɖɔ-yɔɔ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɛyáa mbá pɛkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa nɛ palakɩ acɛyɛ yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ yɔ, ɖɩkaɣ kpaʋ ɖɔɖɔ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ pʋdʋ yɔɔ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛ nɛ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa pɛkpɛndɩɣnɩ ɛ-Caa Ɛsɔ koboyaɣ nɛ pɔkɔɔ yɔ.».

eng: Jesus left that place and went beyond the Jordan and into the region of Judea. Crowds gathered around him again and, as usual, he taught them.
local: Yesu tɛm-wɛ wɩlʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ ewolo Yudee egeetiye taa nɛ ɛtɛzɩ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ wayɩ. Ɛtɛzaa lɛ, ɛyáa samaɣ tasɩ kpeɣluu ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛpazɩ-wɛ wɩlʋʋ ɛzɩ ɛtɩɩlakʋʋ yɔ.

eng: Some Pharisees came and, trying to test him, they asked, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife.
local: Peeɖe Farɩsɩ ñɩma nabɛyɛ woba pañɩ ɛ-nɔɔ cɔlɔ tɔm se: «Đa-paɣtʋ taa lɛ, pɩha nʋmɔʋ se ɛyʋ pɩzɩɣ ɛɖɔɔnɩ ɛ-halʋ naa we?».

eng: Jesus answered, What did Moses command you? .
local: Yesu pɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Paɣtʋ ndʋ́ Mooyiizi ɖʋ-mɩ?».

eng: They said, Moses allowed a man to write a divorce certificate and to divorce his wife.
local: Pocosi-i se: «Mooyiizi ha nʋmɔʋ se ɛyʋ pɩzɩɣ ɛma kizuu takayaɣ nɛ ɛcɛlɩ ɛ-halʋ nɛ ɛɖɔɔnɩ-ɩ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, He wrote this commandment for you because of your unyielding hearts.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Mɩ-laŋa kpɩzɩŋ yɔɔ Mooyiizi ɖʋ-mɩ paɣtʋ ḿbʋ́.

eng: At the beginning of creation, God made them male and female .
local: Đooo kajalaɣ lɛ, Ɛsɔ labɩ pɩtɩŋa nɛ ɛla halʋ nɛ abalʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Because of this, a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ abalʋ yeki ɛ-caa nɛ e-ɖoo nɛ ɛnɩɩnɩ ɛ-halʋ.

eng: and the two will be one flesh. So they are no longer two but one flesh.
local: nɛ pa-naalɛ papɩsɩ ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm. Patatasɩ kɛʋ ɛyáa naalɛ. Ɛlɛ́, papɩsɩ ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm.

eng: When Jesus and his followers approached Jerusalem, they came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives. Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Yesu nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ ŋgá katɩŋaɣ-ɩ yɔ, pɔñɔtɩnɩ Yeruusalɛm Bɛtfaajee nɛ Betaanii pɔ-hɔɔlʋʋ taa Somiŋ Pʋʋ liu tɛɛ lɛ, etiyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa naalɛ se:.

eng: Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together. .
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ pɩtamʋnɩ se ɛyʋ ɛyasɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ kpɛndaa yɔ.».

eng: saying to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: «Iwolo tɛtʋ cikpetu ndʋ́ tɩwɛ ɛsɩndaa yɔ tɩ-taa. Ɩtalɩɣ lɛ, ɩmakɩnɩ paɖɩ kpaŋaɣ piye naɖɩyɛ. Nɔɔyʋ tacaɣdɩ ɖɩ-yɔɔ. Ɩkpɛzɩ-ɖɩ nɛ ɩkɔna.

eng: Inside the house, the disciples asked him again about this.
local: Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pasʋ ɖɩɣa lɛ, paɖaɣnɩ-ɩ pɔzʋʋ tɔm ńdʋ́.

eng: If anyone says to you, Why are you doing this? say, Its master needs it, and he will send it back right away. .
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛpɔzɩ-mɩ se: ‹Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩkpɛzɩɣ-ɖɩ› lɛ, iheyi-i se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ liu wɛ-ɖɩ, ɛpɩsɩɣnɩ-ɖɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ.› ».

eng: He said to them, Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her;.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ecosi-wɛ se: «Yee ɛyʋ ɛɖɔɔnɩ ɛ-halʋ nɛ ɛkpaɣ lɛlʋ yɔ, ɛyʋsɩnɩ kajalaɣ ñɩnʋ, ɛlabɩ acɛyɛ.

eng: They went and found a colt tied to a gate outside on the street, and they untied it.
local: Ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa woba lɛ, pamaɣnɩ paɖɩ-ɖɩ nʋmɔʋ nɔɔ ɖɩɣa nakɛyɛ nɔnɔɔ taa nɛ pacaɣ se pɛkpɛzɩ-ɖɩ.

eng: and if a wife divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery. .
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ yee halʋ nɔɔyʋ ekizi ɛ-walʋ nɛ ɛɖɛɛ ɛwalɩ kɩfalʋ yɔ, ɛlabɩ acɛyɛ.».

eng: Some people standing around said to them, What are you doing, untying the colt.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mbá pɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ, pa-taa nabɛyɛ pɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛbɛ ɩlakɩ peeɖe yɔ? Ɩkpɛzɩɣ kpaŋaɣ piye ńɖɩ́ se ɩlabɩnɩ we?».

eng: People were bringing children to Jesus so that he would bless them. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛyáa wokini piya Yesu cɔlɔ se ɛtɩnɩ sɩ-yɔɔ nesi nɛ ɛwazɩ sɩ-yɔɔ. Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ɖaŋ-wɛ.

eng: They told them just what Jesus said, and they left them alone.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pocosi ɛzɩ Yesu keheyuu-wɛ yɔ nɛ peyebi-wɛ nɛ pɛɖɛnɩ-ɖɩ.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he grew angry and said to them, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Yesu na ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩtɛkɛdɩnɩ-ɩ, ɛlɛ́ ɛkalɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Iyele piya nɛ sɩkɔɔ mɔn-cɔlɔ, ɩtaaɖaŋ-sɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mbá pɛwɛ ɛzɩ piya ńzɩ́ yɔ lɛ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa ñɩma.

eng: They brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes upon it, and he sat on it.
local: Patanɩ kpaŋaɣ piye ńɖɩ́ Yesu cɔlɔ lɛ, pɔhɔzɩ po-wondu lɛɛtʋ nɛ pɔpɔ ɖɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛkpa ɛcaɣ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se yee nɔɔyʋ etetisi ɛzɩ piya cikpesi tisuu se Ɛsɔ tɔkɩnɩ kewiyaɣ yɔ yɔ, ɛɛsʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa.».

eng: Many people spread out their clothes on the road while others spread branches cut from the fields.
local: Ɛyáa sakɩyɛ hɔzɩ po-wondu lɛɛtʋ nɛ pɔpɔ nʋmɔʋ taa nɛ lɛlaa pɔ hatʋ ndʋ́ paacɛbɩ hayɩm taa yɔ se paɖʋ-ɩ hɩɖɛ.

eng: Then he hugged the children and blessed them.
local: Peeɖe Yesu kpaɣ piya ńzɩ́ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛtɩnɩ sɩ-yɔɔ nesi nɛ ɛwazɩ sɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: Those in front of him and those following were shouting, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord!.
local: Mbá paawɛ Yesu ɛsɩndaa nɛ mbá patɩŋaɣ ɛ-wayɩ yɔ, pokou se: «Ozaana! Ɛsɔ ɛ́wazɩ weyi ɛkɔŋ Kɩpaɣlʋ lone taa yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: As Jesus continued down the road, a man ran up, knelt before him, and asked, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Yesu tɛm piya yɔɔ wazʋʋ nɛ ɛkpaɣ nʋmɔʋ lɛ, abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kpaɣ seya nɛ ewolo eluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Wɩlɩyʋ kɩbanʋ! Ɛbɛ pʋwɛɛ kpem se mala nɛ mehiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ?».

eng: Blessings on the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Ɛsɔ ɛ́wazɩ weyi ɛtɔkɩ ɖɔ-cɔzɔ Daaviidi kewiyaɣ yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ. Ozaana! Đooo ɛsɔdaa.».

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋyaɣ-m se kɩbanʋ? Nɔɔyʋ tɛkɛ kɩbanʋ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ Ɛsɔ.

eng: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. After he looked around at everything, because it was already late in the evening, he returned to Bethany with the Twelve.
local: Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ patalɩ Yeruusalɛm lɛ, ɛsʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa. Ɛna mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩlakaɣ yɔ lɛ, ɛlɩɩ nɛ pɛɖɛɛ Betaanii. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, wɩsɩ kpeŋaɣ.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony . Dont cheat. Honor your father and mother . .
local: Paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma, ŋsɩm Ɛsɔ paɣtʋ ndʋ́ paɖʋwa se: taakʋ ɛyʋ, taala acɛyɛ, taamɩlɩ, taacɛtɩnɩ lɛlʋ taa, taacɛzɩ lɛlaa nɛ ŋtɔɔ pa-ñɩm nɛ ŋña ña-caa nɛ ño-ɖoo yɔ.».

eng: The next day, after leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
local: Tɛʋ fema nɛ palɩɩ Betaanii tɛtʋ wayɩ lɛ, ñɔɔsɩ wɩɣ Yesu.

eng: Teacher, he responded, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Peeɖe abalʋ ɛnʋ eheyi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Mɔnɖɔm paɣtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ tɩ-yɔɔ ɖooo me-evebutu taa.».

eng: From far away, he noticed a fig tree in leaf, so he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing except leaves, since it wasnt the season for figs.
local: Peeɖe elosi hiyuú poliŋ taa, kʋlɔba kpemm nɛ ewolo kʋ-cɔlɔ se ɛna se yee kʋlʋlaa yɔ, ɛtɔɔ. Ɛtalɩ kɩ-tɛɛ lɛ, ɛmaɣnɩ hatʋ ɖeke, kɩtalʋlɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ kʋ-lʋlʋʋ alɩwaatʋ.

eng: Jesus looked at him carefully and loved him. He said, You are lacking one thing. Go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me. .
local: Yesu cɔnɩ-ɩ pɩŋŋ lɛ, ɛ-tɔm kɛdɩnɩ-ɩ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Pʋyʋ kʋɖʋm koŋ pɩkazaa se ŋtɛzɩ labʋ. Wolo ŋpɛdɩ mbʋ́ ŋwɛna yɔ pɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ ŋtayɩ kʋñɔndɩnaa pi-liidiye nɛ ŋwɛnɩ ñɩm ɛsɔdaa. Yee ŋlabɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ŋkɔɔ ŋtɩŋ-m.».

eng: So he said to it, No one will ever again eat your fruit! His disciples heard this.
local: Peeɖe Yesu wɩlɩ-kʋ kpalʋʋ se: «Ña! Ŋtasɩ lʋlʋʋ nɛ nɔɔyʋ tɔɔ lɛ, maana.» Ɛlɛ́, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa nɩɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ ḿbʋ́.

eng: But the man was dismayed at this statement and went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Eheyi abalʋ ɛnʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ laŋɩyɛ wɛɛkɩ nɛ ɛñɩŋ ɛsɩndaa nɛ ɛcaŋ ɛɖɛɛ hɛɛɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛwɛnɩ ñɩm pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ.

eng: They came into Jerusalem. After entering the temple, he threw out those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Papɩsɩ Yeruusalɛm lɛ, Yesu sʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa kadaɣ taa nɛ ɛpazɩ ɖɔɔnʋʋ pɛdɩyaa nɛ yaɖaa. Etuzi liidiye yɔkɩyaa taabɩlɩnaa nɛ halikukunaa pɛdɩyaa kpelisi nɛ ɛpɛɖɩ.

eng: Looking around, Jesus said to his disciples, It will be very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom! .
local: Ɛɖɛwa lɛ, Yesu cɔnɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa mbá pɛwɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ pɛ-ɛsɩndaa kpɛɛɛ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Pʋwɛ kaɖɛ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ se ñɩm tɩnaa ɛsʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa.».

eng: He didnt allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
local: Etetisiɣ se nɔɔyʋ ɛsɩɣlɩ pʋyʋ nɛ ɛtɩŋnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa.

eng: His words startled the disciples, so Jesus told them again, Children, its difficult to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩlabɩ-wɛ piti. Peeɖe Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Men-piya, ɩcɔnɩ ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pʋwɛ kaɖɛ kpem mbá pataɣ pa-ñɩm yɔ se pasʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa yɔ.

eng: He taught them, Hasnt it been written, My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But youve turned it into a hideout for crooks . .
local: Peeɖe eheyi mbá pɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ se: «Ɛsɔ yɔɔdaa nɛ pama ɛ-takayaɣ taa se: ‹Man-ɖɩɣa kɛɣnɩ ajɛya kpeekpe Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ.› Ŋgʋ́, mɩ mɩpɩsɩ-kɛ mɩ́láa ñɩŋga.».

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom. .
local: Pʋcɔ ñɩm tʋ nɔɔyʋ sʋʋ ka-taa lɛ, pʋwɛ kaɖɛ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ɛzɩ kpaŋnɔ siɣá naalɛ tʋ sʋʋnɩ ɛjɔʋ pɔʋ taa yɔ.».

eng: The chief priests and legal experts heard this and tried to find a way to destroy him. They regarded him as dangerous because the whole crowd was enthralled at his teaching.
local: Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ pa-paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa panɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pañɩnɩɣ nʋmɔʋ ŋgʋ́ paatɩŋ nɛ pakʋ-ɩ yɔ. Ŋgʋ́ peseɣaɣ-ɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɔm ndʋ́ ɛyɔɔdaɣ yɔ, tɩɩkɛdɩnɩ ɛyáa samaɣ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ.

eng: They were shocked even more and said to each other, Then who can be saved.
local: Peeɖe pɩtɛzɩ-wɛ ɖɩɣzʋʋ nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ ɖama se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ apɩzɩɣ ehiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ yɔ?».

eng: When it was evening, Jesus and his disciples went outside the city.
local: Wɩsɩ wokaɣ ɖʋʋ lɛ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pakʋyɩ Yeruusalɛm peeɖe nɛ pɛɖɛɛ.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible with human beings, but not with God. All things are possible for God. .
local: Yesu cɔnɩ-wɛ pɩŋŋ lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɛyáa ɛɛpɩzɩɣna, ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ ñapɩzɩɣ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛla pɩtɩŋa payɩ.».

eng: Early in the morning, as Jesus and his disciples were walking along, they saw the fig tree withered from the root up.
local: Tɛʋ fem tanaŋ tɛɛ nɛ pɛɖɛɣ lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa na hiyuú ŋ́gʋ́, kɩwɩlɩ wɔɔɔ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ ki-lila.

eng: Peter said to him, Look, weve left everything and followed you.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Cɔnɩ ŋna, ɖa yɔ, ɖiyebi pɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ ɖɩtɩŋ ña-wayɩ, ɛbɛ ɖihikiɣ yɔ?».

eng: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, Rabbi, look how the fig tree you cursed has dried up.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ tɔzɩ mbʋ́ pɩɩlaba yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Wɩlɩyʋ, cɔnɩ ŋna hiyuú ŋgʋ́ ŋwɩlɩ kpalʋʋ yɔ kɩwɩlʋʋ wɔɔɔ.».

eng: Jesus said, I assure you that anyone who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or farms because of me and because of the good news.
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se yee nɔɔyʋ eyebi ɛ-caa yaa e-ɖoo yaa e-piya yaa ɛ-ɖalaa yaa ɛ-kɔɔnaa yaa e-newaa yaa ɛ-hayɩm yaa ɛ-ɖɩɣa yaa mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛwɛna yɔ ma nɛ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ɖɔ-yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: Jesus responded to them, Have faith in God!.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ.

eng: will receive one hundred times as much now in this life—houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and farms —and in the coming age, eternal life.
local: ɛkɔŋ wɛnaʋ mbʋ́ mbʋ́ ɛɛwɛna yɔ pɩ-tam mɩnʋʋ kpaɣnɩ nɔɔnɔɔ ɛjaɖɛ yɔɔ cɩnɛ. Ɛkɔŋ wɛnaʋ ɖɩsɩ, ɖalaa, kɔɔnaa, ɖoonaa, piya nɛ hayɩm sakɩyɛ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɛyáa wɛɣ ɛ-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ. Piyele alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ tɩkɔŋ yɔ tɩ-taa lɛ, ɛkɔŋ hiɣu wezuu maatɛŋ.».

eng: I assure you that whoever says to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the sea—and doesnt waver but believes that what is said will really happen—it will happen.
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se yee nɔɔyʋ eheyi pʋʋ kʋnɛ se: ‹Pʋʋ ña, kpɛzɩ nɛ ŋwolo ŋtɔlɩ teŋgu taa› nɛ yee ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ se ndʋ́ ɛyɔɔdaa yɔ tɩlakɩ nɛ ɛtapʋtɩ ɛ-taa yɔ, pɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Mbá pɛkɛ sɔsaa lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, pa-taa sakɩyɛ kɔŋ pɩsʋʋ cikpema nɛ mbá paba kɛ cikpema lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, pa-taa sakɩyɛ ñapɩsɩ sɔsaa.».

eng: Therefore I say to you, whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you will receive it, and it will be so for you.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ meheyiɣ-mɩ se mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa ɩsʋlʋmʋʋ Ɛsɔ yɔ, ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ se paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma, ɛhaɣ-mɩ-pʋ. Pʋbʋ, pɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, with Jesus in the lead. The disciples were amazed while the others following behind were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he told them what was about to happen to him.
local: Yeruusalɛm Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pakpaɣaɣ nɛ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́. Ɛɛɖɛ-wɛ nɔɔ nɛ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ tɩŋɩɣ-wɛ. Pakpaɣaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa laŋɩyɛ lɩɣ nɛ sɔɔndʋ kpaɣ ɛyáa mbá patɩŋaɣ-wɛ yɔ. Peeɖe ɛya ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ ḿbá nɛ ɛkɛzɩ-wɛ hɔɔlʋʋ nɛ eheyi-wɛ mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ-ɩ tɛmnaʋ alɩwaatʋ pazɩ taa yɔ.

eng: And whenever you stand up to pray, if you have something against anyone, forgive so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your wrongdoings. .
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩkʋyʋʋ se ɩsɛɣ Ɛsɔ yɔ, yee mɩ nɛ nɔɔyʋ ɩwɛ tɔm yɔ, ikpeɣ pʋdʋ. Peeɖe mɩ-Caa weyi ɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, ekpeɣiɣ-mɩ mɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm ɖɔɖɔ. .

eng: Look! he said. Were going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and the legal experts. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: Eheyi-wɛ se: «Đɩkpaɣʋ Yeruusalɛm yɔ, pakpaɣ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ nɛ paɖʋzɩ-ɩ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa pe-nesi taa. Paba kʋʋ ɛ-tɔm nɛ paɖʋ se pakʋ-ɩ nɛ pakpaɣ-ɩ paɖʋzɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pe-nesi taa.

eng: Jesus and his disciples entered Jerusalem again. As Jesus was walking around the temple, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders came to him.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa patasɩ Yeruusalɛm pɩsʋʋ. Patalaa lɛ, ɛsʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa kadaɣ taa nɛ ɛcɔʋ. Peeɖe Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ pa-paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ pɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa powobi ɛ-cɔlɔ.

eng: They will ridicule him, spit on him, torture him, and kill him. After three days, he will rise up. .
local: nɛ paba woŋ-i nɛ pɔtɔ ɛ-yɔɔ ndaatʋ nɛ polu-i agbazɛɛ nɛ pakʋ-ɩ nɛ ɛsɩ. Kɩyakɩŋ naadozo wayɩ lɛ, Ɛsɔ fezi-i.».

eng: They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them.
local: nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Aha-ŋ nɔɔ se ŋɖɔɔnɩ ɛyáa? Aha-ŋ waɖɛ se ŋla ḿbʋ́?».

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, came to Jesus and said, Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask.
local: Yesu tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ lɛ, Zebedee pɩyalaa Yakubu nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ powolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ peheyi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ, ɖɩcaɣ se ŋlabɩ-ɖʋ mbʋ́ ɖɩsʋlʋmʋʋ-ŋ yɔ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, I have a question for you. Give me an answer, then Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Mɔnpɔzʋʋ-mɩ tɔm kʋɖʋmtʋ koŋ nɛ yee icosi-m lɛ, meheyi-mɩ weyi ɛha-m nɔɔ se mala ḿbʋ́ yɔ.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛbɛ ɩcaɣ se malabɩ-mɩ?».

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin? Answer me. .
local: Mɔnpɔzʋʋ-mɩ se: atiyi Yohanɛɛsɩ se ɛla ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ? Ɛsɔ yaa ɛyáa tiyini-i? Icosi nɛ manɩɩ.».

eng: They said, Allow one of us to sit on your right and the other on your left when you enter your glory.
local: Pocosi-i se: «Đe-liu wɛɛ se ŋkɔm ŋcaɣ ñe-kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ lɛ, ŋyele nɛ ɖɩcaɣ ñɔ-cɔlɔ: lɛlʋ ña-nɩwaŋ yɔɔ nɛ lɛlʋ ña-nɩmɩyɛ yɔɔ.».

eng: They argued among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɔyɔɔdɩnɩ ɖama se: «Yee ɖicosaa se: ‹Ɛsɔ tiyini-i› yɔ, ɛpɔzʋʋ-ɖʋ se: ‹Nɛ pɩla ɛzɩma ɖɩtamʋ ɛ-tɔm yɔ?›.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink the cup I drink or receive the baptism I receive? .
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Ɩɩsɩŋ mbʋ́ ɩsʋlʋmʋʋ yɔ. Ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ sɔsɔʋ ŋgʋ́ mɔnkɔŋ tɔɔʋ yɔ? Ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩnɩɩ siziŋ weyi mɔnkɔŋ nɩʋ yɔ?».

eng: But we cant say, Its of earthly origin. They said this because they were afraid of the crowd, because they all thought John was a prophet.
local: Ŋgʋ́, ɖɩtɔm se: ‹Ɛyáa tiyini-i› yɔ, pɩɩlakɩ.» Pahʋwaɣ pa-taa ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, peseɣaɣ ɛyáa mbá paawɛ peeɖe yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paba sɩm Yohanɛɛsɩ se ɛkɛ náyʋ siŋŋ.

eng: We can, they answered. Jesus said, You will drink the cup I drink and receive the baptism I receive.
local: Pocosi-i se: «Ɛɛɛ! Đɩpɩzɩɣ ɖɩtɔɔ-kʋ.» Yesu pɩsɩnɩ-wɛ se: «Toovenim ɩkɔŋ tɔɔʋ kʋñɔŋ sɔsɔʋ ŋgʋ́ mɔntɔkɩ yɔ nɛ ɩnɩɩ siziŋ weyi manɩɣ yɔ.

eng: They answered Jesus, We dont know. Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, papɩsɩnɩ Yesu se: «Đɩɩsɩŋ.» Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ ñocosi-wɛ se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ ma ɖɔɖɔ maaheyiɣ-mɩ weyi ɛha-m nɔɔ ŋ́gá yɔ.».

eng: but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom it has been prepared. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, ma-nɩwaŋ yɔɔ nɛ ma-nɩmɩyɛ yɔɔ caɣʋ ñɩndʋ lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ mahaɣnɩ nʋmɔʋ. Mbá Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ se pacaɣ lona ana a-taa yɔ, ḿbá cakɩna.».

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with James and John.
local: Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu mbá pɩkazaa yɔ, paba nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa-taa wɩɩnɩ Yakubu nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ.

eng: Jesus called them over and said, You know that the ones who are considered the rulers by the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Peeɖe Yesu ya patɩŋa nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩtɛm sɩm se mbá pɔcɔŋnɩ-wɛ se pɛkɛ awiya sɔsɔna yɔ, ḿbá nuutuuni pa-ajɛya taa ñɩma nɛ sɔsaa ñawɩlɩɣ-wɛ po-ɖoŋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Mɩ lɛ, wɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩtaawɛɛ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa. Yee mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛcaɣ se ɛkɛ sɔsɔ yɔ, pʋdʋ ɛpɩsɩ mɩ-tʋmlaɖʋ.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be the slave of all.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛcaɣ se ɛkɛ mɩ-ñʋʋdʋ yɔ, pʋdʋ ɛpɩsɩ mɩtɩŋa mi-yom.

eng: for the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ tɔkɔɔ se ɛyáa ɛlabɩ-ɩ tʋmɩyɛ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛkɔm se ɛla ɛyáa tʋmɩyɛ nɛ ɛkpaɣ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-tɩ ɛhɛyɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ puholi pa-taa sakɩyɛ.».

eng: Jesus and his followers came into Jericho. As Jesus was leaving Jericho, together with his disciples and a sizable crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, Timaeus son, was sitting beside the road.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa patalɩ Yeriikoo tɛtʋ taa. Palɩ tɩ-taa se pɛɖɛɣ nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ tɩŋɩɣ pa-wayɩ lɛ, pamaɣnɩ Tiimee pɩyalʋ yʋlʋm weyi payaɣ se Bartiimee yɔ. Ɛcaɣ nʋmɔʋ nɔɔ nɛ ɛsʋlʋmʋʋ.

eng: When he heard that Jesus of Nazareth was there, he began to shout, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Ɛnɩ Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu taŋ lɛ, ɛma kubuka se: «Wiyaʋ Daaviidi Sayɩ Yesu! Na man-pʋtɔdɩyɛ.».

eng: Many scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Peeɖe ɛyáa sakɩyɛ ñazɩ-ɩ se: «Su peeɖe.» Ɛlɛ́, ɛtɛzɩ kubuka mabʋ ɖoŋ se: «Wiyaʋ Daaviidi Sayɩ! Na man-pʋtɔdɩyɛ.».

eng: Jesus stopped and said, Call him forward. They called the blind man, Be encouraged! Get up! Hes calling you.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu sɩŋ nɛ ɛtɔ se payaa-ɩ, nɛ peheyi yʋlʋm ɛnʋ se: «Ñaɣ pana ŋkʋyɩ, Yesu yaɣ-ŋ.».

eng: Throwing his coat to the side, he jumped up and came to Jesus.
local: Peeɖe ɛlɔ e-toko sɔsɔʋ nɛ ɛkʋyɩ kpaka nɛ ewolo Yesu cɔlɔ.

eng: Jesus asked him, What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said, Teacher, I want to see.
local: Ɛtalaa lɛ, Yesu pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋcaɣ se malabɩ-ŋ we?» Yʋlʋm ɛnʋ ecosi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ, mancaɣ se ŋla nɛ mɛ-ɛsɛ kuli.».

eng: Jesus said, Go, your faith has healed you. At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus on the way.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-i se: «Wolo, lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ŋwɛnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, kɩyabɩ ña-ñʋʋ.» Ɛlɛ́, ɛpazɩ naʋ kpaagbaa nɛ ɛtɩŋɩɣ Yesu wayɩ.

eng: Jesus spoke to them in parables. A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu pazɩ-wɛ tuuu eduuye se: «Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ wɛnɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛsɔ tɩŋ weyi popuu i-pee sʋlʋm yɔ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛma koloŋa nɛ ɛcɔzɩ nɛ ɛla ɖenɖe ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛñazɩ a-lɩm yɔ. Ɛma ɖɔɖɔ tilimiye nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-yɔɔ pasɩŋɩɣ nɛ pefeŋiɣ yɔ. Ɛtɛma lɛ, ɛkpaɣ ɛyáa se pɔcɔŋnɩ-ɩ tɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛɖɛɛ nʋmɔʋ.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
local: «Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, tɩŋ ɩnʋ i-pee koyuu talɩ. Peeɖe ɛkʋsɩ e-tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ se ewolo ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛmʋ e-ɖeɖe nɛ ewona.

eng: But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
local: Tiyiyu ɛnʋ ɛtalaa lɛ, pakpa-ɩ pamabɩ-ɩ nɛ pɔɖɔɔnɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ ndaakpasɩ.

eng: Again the landowner sent another servant to them, but they struck him on the head and treated him disgracefully.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, hayɩm tʋ ɛnʋ ɛtasɩ tiyiyu lɛlʋ kʋsʋʋ nɛ etiyi-i. Ɛ́lɛ́ talaa lɛ, pama ɛ-ñʋʋ nɛ patʋʋ-ɩ.

eng: He sent another one; that one they killed. The landlord sent many other servants, but the tenants beat some and killed others.
local: Hayɩm tʋ ɛnʋ, ɛtasɩ tozo tʋ tiyuu. Ɛ́lɛ́ talaa lɛ, pakpa-ɩ nɛ pakʋ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛtasɩ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ tiyuu nɛ pama lɛlaa nɛ pakʋ lɛlaa pasɩ.

eng: Now the landowner had one son whom he loved dearly. He sent him last, thinking, They will respect my son.
local: Hayɩm tʋ ɛnʋ, ɛ-pɩyalʋ kʋsɔɔlʋ ɖeke koŋ pɩɩkazɩ ɛ-cɔlɔ. Ɛɛnaɣ se ɛla ɛzɩma lɛ, ɛtɛzɩ-ɩ tiyuu pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛhʋʋ ɛ-taa se: ‹Paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma, pañaŋ-ɩ.›.

eng: But those tenant farmers said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
local: Tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá pana-ɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔyɔɔdɩnɩ ɖama se: ‹Ɩnaɣ weyi ɛkɔŋ yɔ, ɛnʋ kaɣnɩ tɩɣyʋʋ ɛ-caa ñɩm. Ɛkɔma lɛ, ɖɩkʋ-ɩ nɛ ɖɩtɩɣyɩ-pʋ!›.

eng: They grabbed him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
local: Ɛtalaa lɛ, pakpa-ɩ pakʋ nɛ pɔlɔnɩ hayɩm ḿbʋ́ pʋ-wayɩ.».

eng: So what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
local: Yesu tɛm tuuu eduuye ńɖɩ́ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Hayɩm tʋ ɛnʋ, ɛbɛ ɛlakɩ ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá? Ɛkɔŋ ɛkʋ-wɛ nɛ ɛcaa ɛyáa kɩfama nɛ ɛlɛɣzɩ po-lone taa.».

eng: Havent you read this scripture, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
local: Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɩtakalɩ tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ yaa we? Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ kuduyiŋ mayaa kpaɣaɣ pɔlɔ yɔ, ńɖɩ́ pɩsɩnɩ ɖɩkɩlɩ wazaɣ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ atɩŋa payɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ.

eng: The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes? .
local: Kɩpaɣlʋ labɩna ḿbʋ́. Asaaa, pɩkɛ tʋmɩyɛ kɩbanɖɛ kpem!› ».

eng: They wanted to arrest Jesus because they knew that he had told the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
local: Yuda ñɩma sɔsaa nɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ lɛ, pañɩnɩɣ nʋmɔʋ ŋgʋ́ paatɩŋ nɛ pakpa Yesu yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pasɩm ḿbʋ́ se ḿbá etuuni eduuye ńɖɩ́. Ɛyáa samaɣ yɔɔ lɛ, paanaɣ se pala ɛzɩma. Ɛlɛ́, peyebi-i nɛ pɛɖɛɛ.

eng: They sent some of the Pharisees and supporters of Herod to trap him in his words.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, petiyi Farɩsɩ ñɩma nɛ Eroodi hɔɔlʋʋ taa ñɩma nabɛyɛ se powolo pañɩ Yesu nɔɔ cɔlɔ tɔm nɛ pʋtɔma yɔ, ɛyɔɔdɩ natʋyʋ nɛ pakpa-ɩ.

eng: They came to him and said, Teacher, we know that youre genuine and you dont worry about what people think. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is. Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay taxes or not.
local: Patalaa lɛ, peheyi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Đɩnawa se ŋyɔɔdʋʋ toovenim, sɔɔndʋ ɛɛkpaɣ-ŋ nɛ nɔɔyʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ŋtakpaɣ ɛyáa wɛtʋ nɛ ŋlabɩnɩ tʋmɩyɛ. Ŋwɩlɩɣ ɛyáa mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ se pala yɔ pu-toovenim. Đa-paɣtʋ haɣ waɖɛ se pɛhɛyɩ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ lambuu yaa pataahɛyɩ-ɩ? Đa yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩhɛyɩ-ɩ yaa ɖɩtaahɛyɩ-ɩ? Cosi-ɖʋ nɛ ɖɩnɩɩ.».

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deceit, he said to them, Why are you testing me? Bring me a coin. Show it to me. .
local: Yesu ñɛtɛm pa-pɩ-tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ lɩmaɣza naʋ ḿbʋ́. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩñɩkɩ mɔ-nɔɔ cɔlɔ tɔm? Ɩkɔnɩ liidiye pɔɔyaɣ nakɛyɛ nɛ mana.».

eng: And they brought one. He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this? Caesars, they replied.
local: Pɔkɔnɩ-kɛ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «A-ñʋʋ nɛ a-hɩɖɛ pɛwɛ kɔ-yɔɔ cɩnɛ?» Pocosi-i se: «Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. His reply left them overcome with wonder.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Ɩcɛlɩ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ mbʋ́ pɩkɛ ɛ-ñɩm yɔ nɛ piyele ɩcɛlɩ Ɛsɔ mbʋ́ pɩkɛ ɛ́lɛ́ ñɩm yɔ.» Eheyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩlabɩ-wɛ piti pɩŋŋ.

eng: Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Saduki ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma wolo Yesu cɔlɔ. Ḿbá tɔŋnɩ se sɩɖaa eefeŋ. Powoba lɛ, peheyi-i se:.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies , leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
local: «Wɩlɩyʋ, ɖɔ-cɔzɔ Mooyiizi ɖʋ ɛ-paɣtʋ taa se: ‹Yee ɛyʋ ɛwɛnɩ ɖalʋ nɛ ɛ-ɖalʋ ɛnʋ ɛsɩ nɛ eyele ɛ-halʋ nɛ ɛfɛyɩnɩ-ɩ pɩɣa yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se pʋdʋ ɛhɔ lelu ɛnʋ nɛ ehiɣni-i pɩɣa ɛ-ɖalʋ lone taa.›.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman; when he died, he left no children.
local: Tɔɔ! Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ nɛ e-newaa pɛwɛnɩ ḿbʋ́, pɛwɛ lʋbɛ. Abalʋ ɛnʋ, ɛkpaɣ halʋ. Pɩlabɩ pɩnzɩ cabɩ lɛ, ɛsɩ, ɛfɛyɩnɩ pɩɣa.

eng: The second married her and died without leaving any children. The third did the same.
local: Peeɖe e-neu hɔm lelu ɛnʋ se ehiɣ pɩɣa e-lone taa. Pɩtalabɩdɩ pʋyʋ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ caŋ ɛsɩ ɖɔɖɔ, halʋ ɛnʋ ɛfɛyɩnɩ-ɩ pɩɣa. Ɛ́lɛ́ wayɩ lɛ, tozo tʋ ñɔhɔm-ɩ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ sɩ ɖɔɖɔ. Halʋ ɛnʋ ɛfɛyɩnɩ-ɩ pɩɣa.

eng: None of the seven left any children. Finally, the woman died.
local: Pɩla ḿbʋ́ nɛ pɔkɔɔ pasɩ pa-lʋbɛ. Ɛfɛyɩnɩ pa-taa nɔɔyʋ nɛ pɩɣa. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, halʋ ɛnʋ ɛsɩ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: At the resurrection, when they all rise up, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɖɩpɔzʋʋ-ŋ se: sɩɖaa fem wiye lɛ, pa-taa weyi ɛtɩnɩ halʋ ɛnʋ? Ŋgʋ́ patɩŋa pakpaɣ-ɩ!».

eng: Jesus said to them, Isnt this the reason you are wrong, because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power?.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Ɩcɛtɩɣ mɩ-tɩ yem, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtanɩɩ Ɛsɔtɔm tɔbʋʋ, ɩtasɩ ɖɔɖɔ e-ɖoŋ.

eng: When people rise from the dead, they wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like Gods angels.
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ sɩɖaa feŋ yɔ, halɩkpaɣyɛ ɛɛtasɩɣ wɛʋ. Halaa nɛ abalaa pɛwɛɣ ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa wɛʋ ɛsɔdaa yɔ.

eng: As for the resurrection from the dead, havent you read in the scroll from Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to Moses, I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?.
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩtɔŋ se sɩɖaa eefeŋ? Ɩtakalɩ Mooyiizi takayaɣ taa ɖenɖe pama hɔɖaɣ mʋwaɣ miŋ yɔ pʋ-tɔm naa we? Ɛsɔ heyi-i nɛ ɛma peeɖe se: ‹Ma lɛ Abraham nɛ Izaakɩ nɛ Yakɔɔbʋ pɛ-Ɛsɔ.›.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. You are seriously mistaken. .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, Ɛsɔ tɛkɛ sɩɖaa Ɛsɔ, ɛlɛ́, wezuu tɩnaa ñɩnʋ ɛkɛnaa. Ɩcɛtɩɣ mɩ-tɩ yem kpem.».

eng: One of the legal experts heard their dispute and saw how well Jesus answered them. He came over and asked him, Which commandment is the most important of all.
local: Yuda ñɩma paɣtʋ wɩlɩyʋ nɔɔyʋ nɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Saduki ñɩma pɔzaɣ Yesu tɔm nɛ ecosuu-wɛ ɖeu yɔ lɛ, ewolo ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ɛsɔ paɣtʋ tɩŋa taa lɛ, ndʋ́ tɩkɩlaa?».

eng: Jesus replied, The most important one is Israel, listen! Our God is the one Lord.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-i se: «Paɣtʋ ndʋ́ tɩkɩlaa yɔ, ńdʋ́ yɔ: ‹Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma mɩ, ɩtɩlɩ se ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ ɖeke koŋ kɛnɩnɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ.

eng: and you must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your mind, and with all your strength.
local: Sɔɔlɩ ña-Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ nɛ ña-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa nɛ ñe-liu tɩŋa nɛ ña-lɩmaɣza tɩŋa nɛ ño-ɖoŋ tɩŋa payɩ ñe-wezuu caɣʋ taa.›.

eng: The second is this, You will love your neighbor as yourself . No other commandment is greater than these. .
local: Tɩ-wayɩ ñɩndʋ lɛ se: ‹Sɔɔlɩ ña-taabalʋ ɛzɩ ŋsɔɔlʋʋ ña-maɣmaɣ ña-tɩ yɔ.› Natʋyʋ fɛyɩ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ tɩkɩlɩ tɩ-naalɛ.».

eng: The legal expert said to him, Well said, Teacher. You have truthfully said that God is one and there is no other besides him.
local: Peeɖe paɣtʋ wɩlɩyʋ ɛnʋ eheyi Yesu se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Ŋcosi ɖeu pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ. Đenɖe ŋyɔɔdaa se ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ ɖeke koŋ kɛnɩnɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔ, pɩkɛ toovenim. Nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ-ɩ.

eng: And to love God with all of the heart, a full understanding, and all of ones strength, and to love ones neighbor as oneself is much more important than all kinds of entirely burned offerings and sacrifices.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛyʋ ɛsɔɔlɩ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛ-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa nɛ ɛ-lɩmaɣza tɩŋa nɛ e-ciyam tɩŋa nɛ e-ɖoŋ tɩŋa payɩ e-wezuu caɣʋ taa nɛ ɛsɔɔlɩ ɛ-taabalʋ ɛzɩ ɛsɔɔlʋʋ ɛ-tɩ yɔ. Pʋwɛ ɖeu nɛ pɩkɩlɩ pahaɣʋ Ɛsɔ kpɩnɛ nɛ paɖʋʋ miŋ taa yɔ nɛ kʋjɔŋ ndɩ ndɩ weyi palakɩ-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ.».

eng: When Jesus saw that he had answered with wisdom, he said to him, You arent far from Gods kingdom. After that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.
local: Yesu nawa se ecosi-i camɩyɛ lɛ, eheyi-i se: «Ŋñɔɔzɩ ña-tɩ camɩyɛ, pɩkazɩ pazɩ, ŋsʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa.» Pʋwayɩ lɛ, nɔɔyʋ tatasɩ katʋʋ se ɛpɔzɩ Yesu natʋyʋ.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, Why do the legal experts say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu pazɩ ɛyáa heyuu Ɛsɔtɔm Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa tɔŋ se Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ ɛkɛ Daaviidi sayɩ?.

eng: David himself, inspired by the Holy Spirit, said, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool.
local: Ŋgʋ́, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yeba nɛ Daaviidi yɔɔdɩ ɛ-tɩ tɩŋa se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ heyi man-Kɩpaɣlʋ se: caɣ mɔn-cɔlɔ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ moluzi ño-koyindinaa ñɛ-tɛɛ kpem yɔ.›.

eng: David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be Davids son? The large crowd listened to him with delight.
local: Daaviidi maɣmaɣ ñayaɣ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ se Kɩpaɣlʋ. Ɛzɩma ɛtasɩɣ kɛʋ Daaviidi ɛnʋ ɛ-sayɩ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ?» Ɛyáa samaɣ welesaɣ Yesu tɔm nɛ pɩkɛdɩɣnɩ-wɛ.

eng: As he was teaching, he said, Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They want to be greeted with honor in the markets.
local: Eheyaɣ-wɛ se: «Ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɔsɔɔlɩ tokonaa sɔsaa suu nɛ pɔcɔʋ. Pɔsɔɔlaa ɖɔɖɔ se pɛsɛɣ-wɛ nɛ ñamtʋ ɛyáa ɖikpeɣliye taa.

eng: They long for places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Pañɩnɩɣ ɖɩjaɣyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ nɛ kazandʋ pa-taa.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly. .
local: Ḿbá ɖɔɖɔ hɔɔzʋʋnɩ lelaa nesi taa mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛna yɔ. Pɛsɛɣ Ɛsɔ nɛ pɩɖaɣlɩɣ se pɩwɩlɩ se ḿbá lɛ ɛyáa kɩbama. Meheyiɣ-mɩ se kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Ɛsɔ kɔŋ se ɛhʋʋnɩ ɛyáa yɔ, ɛɖʋʋ-wɛ kʋñɔŋ ŋgʋ́ kɩfɛyɩ ɖaɣʋ yɔ.».

eng: Jesus sat across from the collection box for the temple treasury and observed how the crowd gave their money. Many rich people were throwing in lots of money.
local: Yesu tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ lɛ, ewolo ɛcaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa haɖɛ aɖakaɣ ɛsɩndaa nɛ ɛcɔŋnɩ ɛyáa mbá powoki paɖʋʋ liidiye ka-taa yɔ. Ñɩm tɩnaa sakɩyɛ wokaɣnɩ liidiye sakɩyɛ nɛ paɖʋʋ.

eng: One poor widow came forward and put in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, lelu kʋñɔndʋ nɔɔyʋ ñowoni liidiye pɔɔyɩsɩ cikpesi naalɛ nɛ ɛɖʋ. Sɩlɩɣ ɛzɩ faɖam naalɛ yɔ.

eng: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than everyone whos been putting money in the treasury.
local: Peeɖe Yesu ya ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se lelu kʋñɔndʋ ɛnɛ, ɛhawa nɛ pɩkɩlɩ lɛlaa tɩŋa.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had, even what she needed to live on. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ñɩm tɩnaa kpaɣ pe-liidiye wena aawazɩɣ-wɛ yɔ nɛ pɔkɔnɩ paɖʋ. Piyele halʋ kʋñɔndʋ ɛnɛ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ mbʋ́ ɛwɛna yɔ pɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ ɛkɔnɩ ɛɖʋ. Pɩtakazɩ-ɩ pʋyʋ se ɛɛya tɔɔnaɣ maɣmaɣ.».

eng: As Jesus left the temple, one of his disciples said to him, Teacher, look! What awesome stones and buildings.
local: Yesu lɩɣaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa lɛlʋ heyi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ, cɔnɩ ŋna pɛɛ kɩbana wena pamanɩ ɖɩɣa sɔsɔɔ kanɛ nɛ kɛwɛɛ ɖeu pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ yɔ.».

eng: Jesus responded, Do you see these enormous buildings? Not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished. .
local: Peeɖe ecosi-i se: «Ŋnaɣʋ-kɛ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, pɔkɔŋ tuzuu pɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ pɛpɛɖɩ. Pɩɩkazɩɣ pɩyɛ naɖɩyɛ lɛɛɖɛ yɔɔ.».

eng: Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately.
local: Yesu yɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛlɩɩ Yeruusalɛm lɛ, ewolo ɛkpa Somiŋ Pʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛcaɣ nɛ ɛha-kɛ nɛ ɛsɛ. Puwiye ńɖɩ́ lɛ, Pɩyɛ nɛ Yakubu nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ nɛ Andree pa-naanza paawɛnɩ ɛ-cɔlɔ. Pɛwɛ pe-ɖeke ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se:.

eng: Tell us, when will these things happen? What sign will show that all these things are about to come to an end.
local: «Heyi-ɖʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɩkaɣ labʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ wɩlʋʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ.».

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu paɣ́-wɛ se: «Ɩkpa mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ, ɩtaayele nɛ nɔɔyʋ cɛtɩ-mɩ yem.

eng: Many people will come in my name, saying, Im the one! They will deceive many people.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kɔŋ kʋɖʋm kʋɖʋm nɛ peheyi-mɩ se: ‹Ma lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa nɛ etiyini-mɩ› yɔ: ḿbʋ́ palakɩ nɛ peyili ɛyáa sakɩyɛ.

eng: When you hear of wars and reports of wars, dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Yee ɩnɩ you taŋ mɩ-cɔlɔ nɛ peheyiɣ-mɩ poliŋ taa ñɩŋgʋ tɔm lɛ, sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-mɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se pɩla ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ tatalɩda.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be earthquakes and famines in all sorts of places. These things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Pʋwɛɣ ɛzɩ halʋ wɩzʋʋ lʋlʋʋ nɛ pɩɣa wɩɣ-ɩ yɔ. Ɛjaɖɛ lɛɛɖɛ kʋyʋʋ lɛɛɖɛ yɔɔ, kewiyaɣ lɛɛka ñakʋyɩ lɛɛka yɔɔ nɛ tɛtʋ ñamsɩ hɔɔlɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ taa nɛ ñɔɔsɩ lɩɩ.

eng: Watch out for yourselves. People will hand you over to the councils. You will be beaten in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me so that you can testify before them.
local: «Mɩ lɛ, ɩkpa mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ. Pɔkɔŋ-mɩ luu agbazɛɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ taa nɛ powoni-mɩ tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ. Payaɣ-mɩ ajɛya ñʋndɩnaa nɛ awiya sɔsɔna pɔ-cɔlɔ mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɩkɛ ma-aseɣɖe tɩnaa pɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: First, the good news must be proclaimed to all the nations.
local: Pʋcɔ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩtalɩ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se posusi Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ajɛya tɩŋa taa.

eng: When they haul you in and hand you over, dont worry ahead of time about what to answer or say. Instead, say whatever is given to you at that moment, for you arent doing the speaking but the Holy Spirit is.
local: Yee pakpa-mɩ nɛ powoni tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ lɛ, ɩtaanɩɣzɩ se ɩɩyɔɔdɩ se we. Yee pɩtalaa lɛ, ɩyɔɔdɩ ndʋ́ tɩkpa mɩ-taa yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖʋʋnɩ-tʋ mɩ-taa, pɩtɛkɛ mɩ-cɔlɔ tɩlɩɣna.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to death. A father will turn in his children. Children will rise up against their parents and have them executed.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, ɛyáa kʋyʋʋ nɛ pawɩlɩ pa-ɖalaa yaa pe-newaa maɣmaɣ se pakʋ-wɛ. Caanaa ñalakɩ pe-piya ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ. Piya ńzɩ́, sɩsɩ kʋyɩ sɩ-ñɩma yɔɔ nɛ sɩla nɛ pakʋ-wɛ.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Mɔ-yɔɔ ɛyáa tɩŋa kɔŋ-mɩ paɖʋʋ. Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛɖɔkʋʋ ɛ-tɩ camɩyɛ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩtalɩ kɛtɛzaɣ kɩyakʋ wiye yɔ, Ɛsɔ yakɩ ɛ-ñʋʋ.».

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing standing where it shouldnt be , then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ ɩkɔŋ naʋ tɛyɔkɩyʋ weyi ɛtatʋʋzɩ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa kpem yɔ, ɛsɩŋ ɖenɖe pɩtamʋnɩ se ɛsɩŋ yɔ. Weyi ɛkalɩɣ tɔm ńdʋ́ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛmaɣzɩ nɛ ɛnɩɩ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ camɩyɛ. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩtalaa lɛ, mbá pɛwɛ Yudee egeetiye taa yɔ, pese pakpanɩ pʋŋ taa.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down or enter their houses to grab anything.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛwɛ e-kuduyuu ñʋʋ taa lɛ, ese kpaagbaa. Pɩtaacɩbɩ-ɩ se etiki ɛsʋʋ kɩ-tɛɛ nɛ ɛkpaɣ pʋyʋ nɛ ɛkpɛndɩna.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Weyi ɛwɛ hayɩm taa lɛ, ese kpaagbaa. Pɩtaacɩbɩ-ɩ se ekpeŋ ɛ-tɛ nɛ ɛkpaɣ e-toko maɣmaɣ.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Pʋkɔŋ tɔɔʋ halaa hosi tɩnaa nɛ asɔŋ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter.
local: Itendi Ɛsɔ se alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩtaakɛnɩ niŋkaɣ alɩwaatʋ.

eng: In those days there will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kʋñɔŋ ŋgʋ́ kʋkɔŋ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa yɔ, kɩkɩlɩɣ kʋñɔmɩŋ weyi ɛyáa laba ɖooo ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ labʋ kedeŋa nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ sɔnɔ yɔ ɩtɩŋa payɩ. Nakʋyʋ ɛɛtasɩɣ kɔm nɛ kɩkɩlɩ-kʋ.

eng: If the Lord hadnt shortened that time, no one would be rescued. But for the sake of the chosen ones, the ones whom God chose, he has cut short the time.
local: Yee Kɩpaɣlʋ ɛtaamaɣzɩ nɛ ɛpasɩ kʋñɔŋ kɩyakɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-yɔɔ ɛyáa mbá ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ se pɛkɛ ɛ-ñɩma yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, nɔɔyʋ taawɛnɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ.

eng: Then if someone says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or There he is, dont believe it.
local: «Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yee nɔɔyʋ eheyi-mɩ se Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa nɛ etiyini-mɩ yɔ, ɛwɛ cɩnɛ yaa peeɖe yɔ yɔ, ɩtaatisi.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, those whom God has chosen.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, cɛtɩyaa mbá peheyiɣ se ḿbá lɛ Mbá Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ nɛ mbá paba ñɛcɛtɩɣ se pɛkɛ náyaa yɔ, pakaɣ kɔm kʋɖʋm kʋɖʋm nɛ pawɩlɩ kawɩlɩsɩ nɛ pala hama lakasɩ nɛ yee papɩzɩɣ yɔ, pɛcɛtɩ Ɛsɔ ñɩma.

eng: But you, watch out! Ive told you everything ahead of time.
local: Mɩ lɛ, mɛntɛm-mɩ heyuu pɩtɩŋa nɛ pɩɖɛɛ. Ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ kpam.».

eng: In those days, after the suffering of that time, the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Kʋñɔmɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-wayɩ lɛ, kɩyakɩŋ weyi ɩkɔŋ yɔ, wɩsɩ ɛɛtasɩɣ ñaɣʋ, fenaɣ ɛɛtasɩɣ ñaɣʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: The stars will fall from the sky, and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Tɩɩnzɩ kpɛzɩɣnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ sɩtɔlɩ nɛ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, pɩñamsɩ.

eng: Then they will see the Human One coming in the clouds with great power and splendor.
local: Peeɖe panaɣ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ nɛ e-ɖoŋ sɔsɔŋ nɛ e-koboyaɣ ɛsɔmɩndʋ yɔɔ, ɛɖɔŋ kɔm.

eng: Then he will send the angels and gather together his chosen people from the four corners of the earth, from the end of the earth to the end of heaven.
local: Etiyiɣ e-tiyiyaa kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ se pekpeɣli mbá Ɛsɔ lɩzaa se pɛkɛ ɛ-ñɩma yɔ.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: «Iwelesi camɩyɛ nɛ ɩnɩɩ mbʋ́ hiyuú wɩlɩɣ-mɩ yɔ: alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ kɩnɛɣdɩɣ yɔ, ɩsɩm kpaagbaa se tɛsoŋiye wɩzaa.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that hes near, at the door.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛɛ. Yee ɩkɔm ɩnaɣ lakasɩ ndɩ ndɩ ńzɩ́ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɩtɩlɩ kpaagbaa se Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ talɩ mɩ-cɔlɔ kpam.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se: ɛyáa mbá pɛwɛnɩ pɛ-ɛsɛ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, paasɩkɩ patɩŋa nɛ pʋcɔ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩla ḿbʋ́.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Ɛsɔdaa nɛ tɛtʋ pamʋkɩ, ɛlɛ́, mɔn-tɔm ñaamʋkɩ kaʋ.».

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the angels in heaven and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Nɔɔyʋ ɛɛsɩŋ kɩyakʋ ŋ́gʋ́ yaa alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́. Paa Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa yaa ɛ-Pɩyalʋ maɣmaɣ. Ɛlɛ́, Caa Ɛsɔ ɖeke koŋ sɩmna.

eng: Watch out! Stay alert! You dont know when the time is coming.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ, ɩtaaɖoo. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtasɩ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́.

eng: It is as if someone took a trip, left the household behind, and put the servants in charge, giving each one a job to do, and told the doorkeeper to stay alert.
local: Tɩwɛ ɛzɩ ɛyʋ nɔɔyʋ ɖɛɣʋ nʋmɔʋ nɛ ɛ́yáa ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa nɛ ɛɖʋ paa weyi tʋmɩyɛ yɔ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, eheyi ɖɩɣa yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyʋ se ɛɖaŋ kpam.».

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know when the head of the household will come, whether in the evening or at midnight, or when the rooster crows in the early morning or at daybreak.
local: Yesu tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, eheyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Ɩtaaɖoo mɩ-tʋma yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtatɩlɩ se tanaŋ naa ɖanaɣ naa ɖoó hɛkʋ taa yaa kalɩmaŋ kou ɖɩɣa tʋ kitiɣ ɛkɔɔ.

eng: Dont let him show up when you werent expecting and find you sleeping.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩtaayele nɛ ɛkɔɔ etudi-mɩ nɛ ɩɖɔŋ ɖou.

eng: What I say to you, I say to all: Stay alert! .
local: Ndʋ́ meheyiɣ-mɩ yɔ, ɛyáa tɩŋa meheyiɣ-tʋ. Ɩcaɣnɩ mɩ-ɛsɛ.».

eng: It was two days before Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and legal experts through cunning tricks were searching for a way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
local: Pɩkazɩ kɩyakɩŋ naalɛ se pɔtɔɔ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma Paska Kazandʋ nɛ ndʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ pɔtɔkɩ tindine nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa fɛyɩ sɔsɔɔna yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ pa-paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa pañɩnaɣ ɛzɩma paala nɛ pakpa Yesu hɛɛɛ nɛ pakʋ-ɩ nɛ nɔɔyʋ ɛtaana yɔ.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the festival; otherwise, there would be an uproar among the people.
local: Peeɖe pɔyɔɔdɩnɩ ɖama se: «Đɩtaakpa-ɩ kazandʋ alɩwaatʋ, pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, ɛyáa kʋyʋʋ ɖɔ-yɔɔ.».

eng: Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease. During dinner, a woman came in with a vase made of alabaster and containing very expensive perfume of pure nard. She broke open the vase and poured the perfume on his head.
local: Yesu kowobi Betaanii tɛtʋ cikpetu taa nɛ ɛsʋʋ Siimɔɔ weyi ɛɛkɛ canaɣ yɔ ɛ-ɖɩɣa taa. Pɔtɔkaɣ tɔɔnaɣ lɛ, halʋ nɔɔyʋ sʋʋ pɔ-cɔlɔ, ɛɖɔkɩ tɩlaaɖɩ ŋgʋ́ petesidi kɩ-taa pʋyʋ yɔ fiɣa kɩñɩlɩsaɣ nakɛyɛ taa. Kʋwɛ liidiye pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛkpalɩ fiɣa ŋ́gá kɔ-nɔɔ nɛ ɛpɩyɩ-kʋ Yesu ñʋʋ taa waaa.

eng: Some grew angry. They said to each other, Why waste the perfume?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mbá pacaɣ peeɖe yɔ, pa-taa lɛlaa taa wɩɩnɩ halʋ ɛnʋ nɛ pɔyɔɔdʋʋ pa-taa pe-ɖeke se: «Halʋ ɛnɛ, ɛwɛɛkʋʋ tɩlaaɖɩ ŋ́gʋ́ nɛ ɛlɔ yem yɔ, pɩwazɩɣ-ɩ we?.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for almost a years pay and the money given to the poor. And they scolded her.
local: Yee paapɛdɩ-kʋ, kɩtaaɖɛɛ ɛzɩ kɩyakɩŋ nasidozo tʋmɩyɛ liidiye yɔ nɛ patayɩ kʋñɔndɩnaa naa we?» Ɛlɛ́, pɛwɛɛ pakʋʋ halʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-tɔm.

eng: Jesus said, Leave her alone. Why do you make trouble for her? She has done a good thing for me.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Iyele halʋ ɛnʋ ɖiɣ ɖiɣ. Ɛbɛ yɔɔ ɩcɛyɩɣ-ɩ ḿbʋ́? Mbʋ́ ɛlabɩ-m yɔ, pʋwɛ ɖeu.

eng: You always have the poor with you; and whenever you want, you can do something good for them. But you wont always have me.
local: Mɩ nɛ kʋñɔndɩnaa ɩwɛna tam. Paa alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ʋ́ ɩsɔɔlaa yɔ, ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩsɩnɩ-wɛ. Ma lɛ, maawɛɣ mɩ-cɔlɔ ḿbʋ́.

eng: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body ahead of time for burial.
local: Mbʋ́ halʋ ɛnɛ ɛpɩzaa yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɛlaba. Ɛɖʋ tɩlaaɖɩ mon-tomnaɣ yɔɔ kpaagbaa se ɛñɔɔzɩ-kɛ nɛ pʋcɔ pepim-m.

eng: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they could go and anoint Jesus dead body.
local: Kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ ɖɛwa lɛ, Yoosi nɛ Yakubu po-ɖoo Maarɩɩ nɛ Magdala Maarɩɩ nɛ Saloomee powolo paya kɔyɛ sɔzɩŋ ñɩnɖɛ se powoni pataa Yesu yɔɔ.

eng: I tell you the truth that, wherever in the whole world the good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se paa le posusuu Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm yɔ, pɔkɔŋ kɛdʋʋ mbʋ́ halʋ ɛnɛ ɛlabɩ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ pʋ-tɔm nɛ ɛyáa ɛtaasɔɔ ɛ-yɔɔ kaʋ.».

eng: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they came to the tomb.
local: Kifem kujuka lɛ, pakʋyɩ tanaŋ cu se patalɩ pɩlaʋ yɔɔ nɛ pʋcɔ wɩsɩ kpa.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to give Jesus up to them.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ taa weyi payaɣ-ɩ se Yudaasɩ Iskarɩyɔɔtɩ yɔ, ewolo eheyi Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa se ɛcaɣ ɛkpa Yesu nɛ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ pe-nesi taa.

eng: They were saying to each other, Whos going to roll the stone away from the entrance for us.
local: Pɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, pɔpɔzʋʋ ɖama se: «Atʋlʋʋ-ɖʋ pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ pɩlaʋ nɔɔ yɔ?».

eng: When they heard it, they were delighted and promised to give him money. So he started looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Panɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩlabɩ-wɛ leleŋ pɩŋŋ nɛ paɖʋ se pɛcɛlɩɣ-ɩ liidiye. Peeɖe ɛpazɩ ñɩnʋʋ nʋmɔʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛɛtɩŋ nɛ ɛkpa Yesu nɛ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ pe-nesi taa yɔ.

eng: When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away.
local: Patalaa lɛ, panaɣ pɩtɛm tʋlʋʋ pɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ ńɖɩ́ yem nɛ pɩsɩɩ-ɖɩ kɩjaʋ.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was sacrificed, the disciples said to Jesus, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Kazandʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ pɔtɔkɩ tindine nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa fɛyɩ sɔsɔɔna yɔ, tɩ-kajalaɣ kɩyakʋ wiye Yuda ñɩma laɣ heɣsi nzɩ́ pɔtɔkɩnɩ Paska Kazandʋ yɔ. Kɩyakʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩtalaa lɛ, Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ɖɩlakɩ-ŋ Paska Kazandʋ tɔɔnaɣ. Le ŋcaɣ ŋtɔɔ-kɛ?».

eng: Going into the tomb, they saw a young man in a white robe seated on the right side; and they were startled.
local: Pasʋ pɩlaʋ taa lɛ, pamaɣnɩ evebu nɔɔyʋ caɣ pa-nɩwaŋ yɔɔ. Esu toko sɔsɔʋ kʋhʋlʋmʋʋ nakʋyʋ waaa, ɛlɛ́ sɔɔndʋ kpa-wɛ.

eng: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, Go into the city. A man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him.
local: Peeɖe etiyi pa-taa ɛyáa naalɛ se: «Iwolo tɛtʋ taa, isuluuni abalʋ nɔɔyʋ, ɛsɩɣlɩ lɩm nɛ peɣa. Isuluni-i lɛ, ɩtɩŋ-ɩ.

eng: But he said to them, Dont be alarmed! You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised. He isnt here. Look, heres the place where they laid him.
local: Peeɖe evebu ɛnʋ eheyi-wɛ se: «Sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-mɩ. Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu weyi paakam kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ ɩñɩnɩɣ yaa we? Efema, ɛfɛyɩ cɩnɛ. Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna ɖenɖe paahɛzɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: Wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, The teacher asks, Where is my guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples? .
local: Ɛsʋ ɖenɖe lɛ, ɩtɩŋ-ɩ nɛ iheyi ɖɩɣa tʋ se: ‹Wɩlɩyʋ tiyini-ɖʋ se ɖɩkɔɔ ŋwɩlɩ-ɖʋ kuduyuu ŋgʋ́ kɩ-taa ɛ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pacakɩ nɛ pɔtɔɔ Yuda ñɩma Paska Kazandʋ yɔ.›.

eng: Go, tell his disciples, especially Peter, that he is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.
local: Iwolo iheyi Pɩyɛ nɛ Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lɛlaa se ɛɖɛ-wɛ nɔɔ nɛ ewolo Galilee egeetiye taa. Peeɖe pamakɩnɩ-ɩ ɛzɩ eeheyuu-wɛ yɔ.».

eng: He will show you a large room upstairs already furnished. Prepare for us there. .
local: Đɩɣa tʋ ɛnʋ ɛwɩlɩɣ-mɩ ɛsɔdaa kuduyuu sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ, paɖaɣnɩ-kʋ camɩyɛ. Kɩ-taa wɛ wondu ndʋ́ palakɩnɩ kazandʋ yɔ tɩtɩŋa payɩ, peeɖe ɩlakɩ-ɖʋ tɔɔnaɣ.».

eng: Overcome with terror and dread, they fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. .
local: Peeɖe pakpaɣ seya nɛ palɩɩnɩ pɩlaʋ taa nɛ sɔɔndʋ nɛ peseliɣ. Ɛlɛ́, peteheyi nɔɔyʋ natʋyʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, sɔɔndʋ kakɩlɩ-wɛ kpaʋ.

eng: The disciples left, came into the city, found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ɖɛwa nɛ powolo tɛtʋ taa lɛ, pamaɣna ɛzɩ eeheyuu-wɛ yɔ nɛ patɩzɩ kazandʋ tɔɔnaɣ peeɖe.

eng: They promptly reported all of the young mans instructions to those who were with Peter. Afterward, through the work of his disciples, Jesus sent out, from the east to the west, the sacred and undying message of eternal salvation. Amen. After Jesus rose up early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom he had cast out seven demons.
local: Kujuka ŋgá ke-wiye tanaŋ cu Yesu fema yɔ, ɛlɩzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛcalɩ wɩlʋʋ Magdala Maarɩɩ weyi ɛ-wayɩ ɛɛɖɔɔnɩ aleewaa lʋbɛ yɔ.

eng: That evening, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
local: Pɩlɛdaa lɛ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ḿbá patalaa lɛ.

eng: She went and reported to the ones who had been with him, who were mourning and weeping.
local: Peeɖe Magdala Maarɩɩ woba ɛkɛdɩ Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa mbʋ́ ɛnawa yɔ. Ɛtalaa lɛ, ɛmaɣnɩ-wɛ, pacaɣ leɣa nɛ pewiliɣ.

eng: During the meal, Jesus said, I assure you that one of you will betray me—someone eating with me. .
local: Pacaɣaɣ se pɔtɔkɩ lɛ, Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ mɩ se mbá mɩ ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩcaɣaɣ nɛ ɖɩtɔkɩ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ kɔŋ-m kpaʋ nɛ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ koyindinaa nesi taa.».

eng: But even after they heard the news, they didnt believe that Jesus was alive and that Mary had seen him.
local: Eheyi-wɛ se: «Yesu fema nɛ ma-maɣmaɣ mana-ɩ nɛ mɛ-ɛsɛ.» Eheyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, petetisi.

eng: Deeply saddened, they asked him, one by one, Its not me, is it.
local: Peeɖe pɩtala ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa leleŋ nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ-ɩ kʋɖʋm kʋɖʋm se: «Ma na?» Ɛnɛ se: «Ma na?».

eng: After that he appeared in a different form to two of them who were walking along in the countryside.
local: Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa naalɛ wokaɣnɩ tɛtʋ wayɩ. Peeɖe Yesu lɩzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ, ɛlɛ́ ɛɛlɛɣzaa.

eng: Jesus answered, Its one of the Twelve, one who is dipping bread with me into this bowl.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Mi-hiu nɛ naalɛ mɩ-taa weyi ma nɛ ɩ ɖɩtɔkɩ ñanaɣ kʋɖʋmaɣ taa yɔ.».

eng: When they returned, they reported it to the others, but they didnt believe them.
local: Tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ḿbá, pa-naalɛ papɩsaa peheyi-tʋ pa-taabalaa lɛ, paba tetisi-tʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born. .
local: «Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ kɔŋ kʋyʋʋ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ ɛ-tɔm Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pʋtɔɔ weyi ɛɖʋzʋʋ-ɩ koyindinaa nesi taa yɔ! Yee pataalʋlɩ pʋdʋ yɔ, pɩɩkɩlɩ-ɩ sɛʋ.».

eng: Finally he appeared to the eleven while they were eating. Jesus criticized their unbelief and stubbornness because they didnt believe those who saw him after he was raised up.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu lɩzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ kʋɖʋm alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pacaɣaɣ pɔtɔkɩ tɔɔnaɣ yɔ nɛ ɛkalɩ-wɛ se pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ pa-laŋa wɛɛ kpɩzɩŋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pataatisi ɛyáa mbá peheyaɣ-wɛ se efema yɔ pɔ-tɔm.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them, and said, Take; this is my body. .
local: Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɔtɔkaɣ tɔɔnaɣ lɛ, ɛkpaɣ tindine nɛ ɛsɛɛ ɖɩ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛcɩyɩ-ɖɩ nɛ ɛcɛlɩ-wɛ se: «Ɩmʋ nɛ ɩtɔɔ, pʋnɛ lɛ mon-tomnaɣ.».

eng: He said to them, Go into the whole world and proclaim the good news to every creature.
local: Pʋbʋ wayɩ lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩcɔɔ kedeŋa kpeekpe nɛ isusi ɛyáa Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛkpaɣ sʋlʋm nɛ caɖaɣ nɛ ɛsɛɛ pʋ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛcɛlɩ-wɛ nɛ pɔñɔɔ patɩŋa payɩ.

eng: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever doesnt believe will be condemned.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ etisaa lɛ, ɩlabɩ-ɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ ya ɛ-ñʋʋ. Ɛlɛ́, yee nɔɔyʋ ekizaa yɔ, Ɛsɔ kʋʋ ɛ-tɔm.

eng: He said to them, This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-wɛ se: «Sʋlʋm pʋnɛ lɛ man-calɩm mbʋ́ pɩkpɛdɩ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ yɔɔ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ tɩŋɩɣna nɛ ɛɖʋ nɔɔ haʋ.».

eng: These signs will be associated with those who believe: they will throw out demons in my name. They will speak in new languages.
local: Ɩɩnaɣ Ɛsɔ hama lakasɩ nzɩ́ mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, pɔkɔŋ labʋ yɔ: pɔɖɔɔnʋʋ aleewaa nɛ mon-ɖoŋ nɛ pɔyɔɔdɩ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: I assure you that I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way in Gods kingdom. .
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se maatasɩɣ sʋlʋm ñɔʋ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ mɔñɔ kɩfam Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa yɔ.».

eng: They will pick up snakes with their hands. If they drink anything poisonous, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on the sick, and they will get well.
local: Papɩzɩɣ pakpa ɖʋmaa mbá pañasɩɣ ɛyáa nɛ pasɩkɩ yɔ nɛ pe-nesi nɛ pɔñɔɔ mbʋ́ pʋkʋʋ ɛyáa yɔ ɖɔɖɔ, ɛlɛ́, pɩɩlakɩ-wɛ pʋyʋ. Patɩnɩɣ kʋdɔndɩnaa yɔɔ nesi ɖɔɖɔ nɛ pɔ-yɔɔ cɛ.».

eng: After singing songs of praise, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ɛtɛm-wɛ heyuu ḿbʋ́ lɛ, petee kazandʋ hendu nɛ pɛɖɛɛ pakpa Somiŋ Pʋʋ yɔɔ.

eng: After the Lord Jesus spoke to them, he was lifted up into heaven and sat down on the right side of God.
local: Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu tɛm ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa yɔɔdɩnaʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩkpazɩ-ɩ ɛsɔdaa yem nɛ ɛcaɣ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa.

eng: Jesus said to them, You will all falter in your faithfulness to me. It is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep will go off in all directions .
local: Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pakpa peeɖe lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se: «Iseɣ mɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ iyebi-m ɖoó anɛ a-taa maɣmaɣ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ yɔɔdaa nɛ pama ɛ-Tɔm Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Mamakɩ ninɖiɣyu sɩbʋ yɔɔ sɩbʋ yɔɔ nɛ piyele heŋ mɩzɩ ɖama.›.

eng: But they went out and proclaimed the message everywhere. The Lord worked with them, confirming the word by the signs associated with them.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ñɛɖɛɛ nɛ posusuu Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm paa le. Kɩpaɣlʋ sɩŋaɣnɩ-wɛ pa-tʋmɩyɛ taa nɛ ɛtɩŋɩɣnɩ hama lakasɩ ndɩ ndɩ yɔɔ nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ se tɔm ndʋ́ posusuu yɔ, tɩkɛ toovenim.

eng: But after Im raised up, I will go before you to Galilee. .
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ menfeŋ yɔ, mowoki manɖaŋ-mɩ Galilee.».

eng: Peter said to him, Even if everyone else stumbles, I wont.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ heyi-i se: ‹Paa lɛlaa eseɣ patɩŋa se peyebi-ŋ yɔ, ma maalakɩ ḿbʋ́.› ».

eng: But Jesus said to him, I assure you that on this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times. .
local: Yesu heyi-i se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-ŋ se ɖoó anɛ a-taa maɣmaɣ, pʋcɔ kalɩmɔʋ koo nɛ kɩlɛlɩ lɛ, ŋkpɛzɩɣ tam naadozo se ŋtasɩm-m.».

eng: But Peter insisted, If I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. And they all said the same thing.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ nɛ ɛsɛ ñɩŋɩyɛ se: «Paa sɩm yɔ, ma nɛ ŋ ɖɩsɩ, maakpɛzɩɣ kaʋ se mantasɩm-ŋ.» Ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lɛlaa yɔɔdɩ patɩŋa ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus said to them, Sit here while I pray. .
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa patalɩ ɖenɖe payaɣ se Jesemaanɩ yɔ. Peeɖe eheyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Ɩcaɣ cɩnɛ nɛ mowolo mɛnsɛɛ Ɛsɔ nɛ mɔnkɔɔ.».

eng: He took Peter, James, and John along with him. He began to feel despair and was anxious.
local: Ɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, ɛ́yáa Pɩyɛ nɛ Yakubu nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ. Pɛɖɛ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lɛlaa yɔɔ pazɩ lɛ, pɩkpɛyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ sɔɔndʋ kpa-ɩ.

eng: He said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert. .
local: nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Mɛ-ɛsɛ ñɩŋaa pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ. Ɩcaɣ cɩnɛ ɩɖaŋ-m, ɩcaɣnɩ mɩ-ɛsɛ, ɩtaaɖoo.».

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell to the ground. He prayed that, if possible, he might be spared the time of suffering.
local: Ɛlɛ́, etuzi ɛ-tɩ ɛsɩndaa pazɩ nɛ eluŋ nɛ etendi Ɛsɔ se: «Man-Caa! Yee ŋtisiɣ nɛ yee pɩsaŋ yɔ, taayele nɛ kʋñɔŋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩtalɩ-m.».

eng: He said, Abba, Father, for you all things are possible. Take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want. .
local: Ɛtasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «manawa se ŋpɩzɩɣ ŋla pɩtɩŋa payɩ. Mentendiɣ-ŋ se ŋla ña-tʋmɩyɛ nɛ mantaasʋʋ kʋñɔŋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-taa. Ɛlɛ́, la mbʋ́ ñe-liu wɛɛ yɔ, taala mbʋ́ me-liu wɛɛ yɔ.».

eng: He came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Simon, are you asleep? Couldnt you stay alert for one hour?.
local: Ɛtɛm Ɛsɔ sɛʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛpɩsɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa naadozo ḿbá pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛmaɣna pɔɖɔŋ ɖou. Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ Pɩyɛ se: «Siimɔɔ, ŋwɛɛ ŋɖou! Ŋtapɩzɩ ŋcaɣnɩ ñɛ-ɛsɛ paa ñɩɩɩ!.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak. .
local: Ɩcaɣnɩ mɩ-ɛsɛ nɛ ɩsɛɛ Ɛsɔ, pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, ɩkɔŋ ɩsʋʋ takʋʋ taa. Ɛyáa liu wɛɛ se pala mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ, ɛlɛ́, pɛwɛnɩ kaapɩzɩtʋ.».

eng: Again, he left them and prayed, repeating the same words.
local: Ɛtasɩ ɛ-tɩ tuzuu lɛ, etendi Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛtasɩ tɔm kʋɖʋmtʋ ńdʋ́ yɔɔdʋʋ.

eng: And, again, when he came back, he found them sleeping, for they couldnt keep their eyes open, and they didnt know how to respond to him.
local: Ɛtasɩ pɩsʋʋ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa cɔlɔ lɛ, ɛmaɣnɩ-wɛ pɔɖɔŋ ɖou ɖɔɖɔ. Patapɩzɩ pacaɣnɩ pɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ paanaɣ se peheyi-i se we.

eng: He came a third time and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Thats enough! The time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Ɛpɩsɩ tozo ɖeɖe lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɩwɛ ɖom yɔɔ tam ḿbʋ́? Tɔɔ, pɩmaɣaɣ! Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ alɩwaatʋ talaa se paɖʋzɩ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa nesi taa.

eng: Get up! Lets go! Look, here comes my betrayer. .
local: Ɩkʋyɩ ɖɩkatɩ weyi ɛkpaɣ-m se ɛɖʋzɩ pe-nesi taa yɔ! Ɛkɔŋ naɣ yɔ!».

eng: Suddenly, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came with a mob carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests, legal experts, and elders.
local: Yesu yɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ taa lɛlʋ weyi payaɣ se Yudaasɩ yɔ nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ nakɛyɛ patalɩ kpaagbaa. Pɔɖɔkɩ sɛsɩ kɩɖaɣlɩsɩ nɛ pɔla. Cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Yuda ñɩma ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa petiyini-wɛ.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss, and take him away under guard.
local: Yudaasɩ ɛnʋ ɛkpaɣnɩ Yesu se ɛɖʋzɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa nesi taa. Ɛɛtɛm kɛdʋʋ ɛyáa samaɣ ŋ́gá ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛlakɩ nɛ pasɩm-ɩ nɛ pakpa-ɩ yɔ. Eheyi-wɛ se: «Yee mowobi mɛnsɛɛ nɔɔyʋ nɛ mamʋzɩ ɛ-laɣzaɣ yɔɔ lɛ, ɛnʋ lɛ. Ɩkpa-ɩ ɩɖɔkɩ-ɩ ɖeu nɛ ɩɖɛnɩ-ɩ.».

eng: As soon as he got there, Judas said to Jesus, Rabbi! Then he kissed him.
local: Ɛtɩtalaa lɛ, ewolo Yesu cɔlɔ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ, sɛɛ ɖeu!» Ɛlɛ́, ɛmʋzɩ ɛ-laɣzaɣ yɔɔ.

eng: Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Peeɖe ɛyáa ḿbá pakpa Yesu.

eng: One of the bystanders drew a sword and struck the high priests slave and cut off his ear.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa lɛlʋ kpɛɛ sɩɣa kɩɖaɣlaɣ nɛ ɛsɛtɩ cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ tʋmlaɖʋ nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ.

eng: Jesus responded, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like an outlaw?.
local: Peeɖe Yesu pɔzɩ ɛyáa ḿbá se: «Ɩkɔm ma-wayɩ nɛ sɛsɩ kɩɖaɣlɩsɩ nɛ pɔla yɔ, mɛnkɛ mɩ́lʋ́ naa we?.

eng: Day after day, I was with you, teaching in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled. .
local: Ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩɩwɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa kɩyakɩŋ tɩŋa nɛ mawɩlɩɣ Ɛsɔtɔm, ɛlɛ́, ɩtakpa-m. Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se pɩla ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ.».

eng: And all his disciples left him and ran away.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tɩŋa mɩzɩ ɖama nɛ peyebi-i.

eng: One young man, a disciple, was wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They grabbed him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɩsɛɣ evebu nɔɔyʋ ɖeke nɛ ɛtɩŋɩɣ ɛ-wayɩ, eehu pɩsaʋ. Pesezaa se pakpaɣ-ɩ lɛ, ɛ-pɩsaʋ cɛ nɛ.

eng: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked.
local: eyebi-kʋ nɛ ese ndaakpeɖe nɛ ɛɖɛɛ.

eng: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and legal experts gathered.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pakpa Yesu se powokini-i Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ tɛ ɖenɖe cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ pɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa nɛ pa-paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa pekpeɣlaa yɔ.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance, right into the high priests courtyard. He was sitting with the guards, warming himself by the fire.
local: Pɛɖɛɣaɣnɩ-ɩ lɛ, Pɩyɛ wɛɛ mɛlʋʋ taa mɛlʋʋ taa nɛ ɛtɩŋɩɣ ɛ-wayɩ. Ɛtalɩ cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-taɣ yɔɔ lɛ, ɛ nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyaa pacaɣ nɛ pohoyuu miŋ.

eng: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death, but they couldnt find any.
local: Peeɖe cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ tɔm hʋyaa lɛlaa tɩŋa pañɩnaɣ aseɣɖe tɩnaa se pɔkɔɔ pɛcɛtɩnɩ Yesu taa nɛ pehiɣ-i pakʋ-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, petehiɣ nɔɔyʋ.

eng: Many brought false testimony against him, but they contradicted each other.
local: Ɛyáa sakɩyɛ woba pɛcɛtɩnɩ ɛ-taa. Ɛlɛ́, pɔ-tɔm tɛkɛ kʋɖʋmtʋ.

eng: Some stood to offer false witness against him, saying.
local: Pa-taa lɛlaa kʋyaa nɛ pɛcɛtɩnɩ ɛ-taa se:.

eng: We heard him saying, I will destroy this temple, constructed by humans, and within three days I will build another, one not made by humans.
local: «Ɛyɔɔdaa se ɛyɔkʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa ŋgá ɛyáa mawa yɔ nɛ ɛɖaɣnɩ maʋ kɩfalaɣ ŋgá ɛyáa ɩɩpɩzɩɣ pama yɔ nɛ kɩyakɩŋ naadozo.».

eng: But their testimonies didnt agree even on this point.
local: Halɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ, pa-aseɣɖe tɛkɛ kʋɖʋmɖɩyɛ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Then the high priest stood up in the middle of the gathering and examined Jesus. Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ kʋyɩ ɛsɩŋ patɩŋa pɛ-ɛsɩndaa nɛ ɛpɔzɩ Yesu se: «Tɔm ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛyáa panɛ pɔtɔŋ se ŋyɔɔdaa yɔ, ŋfɛyɩnɩ natʋyʋ se ŋcosuu yaa we?».

eng: But Jesus was silent and didnt answer. Again, the high priest asked, Are you the Christ, the Son of the blessed one.
