local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu suma, etocosi natʋyʋ kaʋ. Peeɖe cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ, ɛtasɩ-ɩ pɔzʋʋ se: «Ña lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ yaa we? Ña lɛ Ɛsɔ weyi pasaŋ-ɩ yɔ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ yaa we?».

eng: Jesus said, I am. And you will see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds. .
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Ɛɛɛ! Đɔɖɔ lɛ, mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩkɔŋ naʋ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ, ɛcaɣ Ɛsɔ Đoŋ Tɩŋa Tʋ cɔlɔ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa. Ɩkɔŋ-ɩ naʋ ɖɔɖɔ ɛsɔmɩndʋ yɔɔ, ɛɖɔŋ kɔm.».

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Why do we need any more witnesses?.
local: Cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ nɩ Yesu yɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ ɛcɩyɩ ɛ-cɔjɔtʋ toko se pɩwɩlɩ se pɩwɩzɩ-ɩ pɩŋŋ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ ɛyáa lɛlaa se: «Ɛbɛ yɔɔ pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩtasɩ ñɩnʋʋ aseɣɖe tɩnaa yɔ?.

eng: Youve heard his insult against God. What do you think? They all condemned him. He deserves to die.
local: Mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩnɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛwɛɛkɩ Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ yɔ. Ɩmaɣzɩɣ se we?» Patɩŋa pocosi-i se: «Ɛ-tɔm sɩba, pʋwɛɛ se pakʋ-ɩ!».

eng: Some began to spit on him. Some covered his face and hit him, saying, Prophesy! Then the guards took him and beat him.
local: Peeɖe pa-taa lɛlaa pazɩ Yesu yɔɔ tɔʋ ndaatʋ nɛ pohuuzuu ɛ-ɛsɛ yɔɔ nɛ pamakɩ-ɩ ŋguluma nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ-ɩ se: «Kpeɣ se amabɩ-ŋ.» Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyaa tem-i kadanzɩ.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was below in the courtyard. A woman, one of the high priests servants, approached.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Pɩyɛ wɛ taɣ yɔɔ yɔ, tɩ-taa cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ tʋmlaɖʋ halʋ ñɩnʋ nɔɔyʋ lɩwa.

eng: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus.
local: nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ-ɩ, ehoyaɣ miŋ. Ɛcɔnɩ-ɩ pɩŋŋ lɛ, eheyi-i se: «Ña ɖɔɖɔ, ŋtɩŋaɣ Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu wayɩ.».

eng: But he denied it, saying, I dont know what youre talking about. I dont understand what youre saying. And he went outside into the outer courtyard. A rooster crowed.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ kpɛzaa se: «Mantana, maanɩɣ ndʋ́ ŋcaɣ yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ.» Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛlɩɩ ewolo kɩjaŋ taa. Ɛlɛ́, kalɩmɔʋ nakʋyʋ koo.

eng: The female servant saw him and began a second time to say to those standing around, This man is one of them.
local: Peeɖe tʋmlaɖʋ halʋ ñɩnʋ ɛnʋ, ɛɖaɣnɩ-ɩ naʋ nɛ eheyi mbá paawɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ se: «Pa-taa lɛlʋ lɛ abalʋ ɛnɛ.».

eng: But he denied it again. A short time later, those standing around again said to Peter, You must be one of them, because you are also a Galilean.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ tasɩ kpɛzʋʋ. Pɩlabɩ fele lɛ, ɛyáa mbá paawɛ peeɖe yɔ, patasɩ-ɩ heyuu se: «Toovenim! Ŋkɛ pa-taa lɛlʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ŋkɛ Galilee tʋ.».

eng: But he cursed and swore, I dont know this man youre talking about.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ kpɛzɩ laɣ laɣ nɛ eɖuu se: «Nɛ Ɛsɔ! Mantasɩ abalʋ weyi ɩyɔɔdʋʋ ɛ-tɔm ɛzʋnɔ yɔ!».

eng: At that very moment, a rooster crowed a second time. Peter remembered what Jesus told him, Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times. And he broke down, sobbing.
local: Ɛtɩyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, kalɩmɔʋ tasɩ kou. Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ tɔzɩ tɔm ndʋ́ Yesu keheyi-i se: «Pʋcɔ kalɩmɔʋ koo nɛ kɩlɛlɩ lɛ, ŋkpɛzɩɣ tam naadozo se ŋtasɩm-m» yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ, peeɖe ɛtʋlɩ nɔɔ wooo.

eng: In the fifteenth year of the rule of the emperor Tiberius—when Pontius Pilate was governor over Judea and Herod was ruler over Galilee, his brother Philip was ruler over Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was ruler over Abilene.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, pɩlabɩ pɩnzɩ hiu nɛ kagbaanzɩ ɛzɩ Tiibɛɛrɩ caɣʋ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ yɔ. Pɔɔnsɩ Pilaatɩ kɛ Yudee egeetiye ñʋʋdʋ. Eroodi Antipasɩ ñɔcɔŋnɩ Galilee ñɩnɖɛ yɔɔ. Ɛ-ɖalʋ Filiipu cɔŋaɣnɩ Ituuree nɛ Traakooniti pa-ageeta yɔɔ nɛ Liisaniyaasɩ ñɔcɔŋnɩ Abiilɛɛnɩ ñɩnɖɛ yɔɔ.

eng: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas—Gods word came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.
local: Ana nɛ Kayifu paakɛ cɔjɔnaa ñʋndɩnaa. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa Ɛsɔ ɖʋzɩ ɛ-tɔm Zakaarii pɩyalʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ nesi taa lakʋ taa.

eng: John went throughout the region of the Jordan River, calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Ɛsɔ ɖʋzɩ Yohanɛɛsɩ nesi taa ɛ-tɔm ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛpazɩ cɔʋ hɔɔlɩŋ weyi ɩwɛ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ nɔɔ yɔ ɩtɩŋa ɩ-taa nɛ esusuu ɛyáa se: «Ɩlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ nɛ palabɩ-mɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ kpeɣ-mɩ mɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm.».

eng: This is just as it was written in the scroll of the words of Isaiah the prophet, A voice crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight.
local: Ɛ-tɔm náyʋ Ezaayii ma Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Nɔɔyʋ susuu lakʋ taa se pɔñɔɔzɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ nʋmɔʋ nɛ pala nɛ kʋtʋʋzɩ camɩyɛ!.

eng: Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be leveled. The crooked will be made straight and the rough places made smooth.
local: Posuyi kajalanzɩ tɩŋa nɛ pɔyɔlɩsɩ pʋŋ nɛ petiti nɛ pʋwɛɛ ɖeyi ɖeyi. Patʋʋzɩ ɖenɖe ɖenɖe habɩyɛ kotaa yɔ nɛ, pɔñɔɔzɩ nʋmɔŋ weyi ɩwɛɛkaa yɔ camɩyɛ.

eng: All humanity will see Gods salvation. .
local: nɛ paa weyi ɛna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ yakɩ ɛyáa ñʋʋ yɔ.».

eng: Then John said to the crowds who came to be baptized by him, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ yɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛyáa samaɣ woki ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛlakɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ. Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Đʋmaa mɩ! Aheyi-mɩ se ise Ɛsɔ pana wena akɔŋ-mɩ kpanaʋ yɔ?.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives. And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Ɩla lakasɩ nzɩ́ sɩwɩlɩɣ se ɩlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ yɔ. Ɩtaamaɣzɩ se mɩ-cɔzɔ lɛ Abraham yɔ, pɩtɛma. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se Ɛsɔ pɩzɩɣ ɛla nɛ pɛɛ anɛ apɩsɩ Abraham liɖe taa ñɩma.».

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Peeɖe etuu-wɛ eduuye se: «Halɩ pɛtɛm kpaɣʋ laɖɩyɛ se pɛsɛtɩɣ tɩŋ nɛ ɩ-tɛɛ: tɩʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩɩlʋlʋʋ pee kɩbana yɔ, pɛsɛtɩɣ-kʋ nɛ paɖʋ miŋ taa.».

eng: The crowds asked him, What then should we do.
local: Peeɖe ɛyáa samaɣ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ɛzɩma pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩla yɔ?».

eng: He answered, Whoever has two shirts must share with the one who has none, and whoever has food must do the same.
local: Ecosi-wɛ se: «Nɔɔyʋ ɛwɛnɩ tokonaa naalɛ yɔ, ɛpasɩ lɛɛkʋ nɛ ɛha weyi ɛfɛyɩna yɔ. Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛwɛnɩ tɔɔnaɣ yɔ, ɛha ɖɔɖɔ weyi ɛfɛyɩna yɔ.».

eng: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. They said to him, Teacher, what should we do.
local: Pɩkpɛndɩnɩ tikee mʋyaa nɛ powolo ɛ-cɔlɔ se ɛlabɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Wɩlɩyʋ, ɛbɛ pʋmʋnaa se ɖɩla?».

eng: He replied, Collect no more than you are authorized to collect.
local: Peeɖe ecosi-wɛ se: «Ɩtaapɔzɩ ɛyáa nɛ pɩɖɛɛ mbʋ́ paɖʋwa se pɛhɛyɩ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.».

eng: Soldiers asked, What about us? What should we do? He answered, Dont cheat or harass anyone, and be satisfied with your pay.
local: Sɔɔjanaa ñɔpɔzɩ-ɩ ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ se: «Nɛ ɖa yɔ, ɛbɛ pʋmʋnaa se ɖɩla?» Ecosi-wɛ se: «Ɩtaakpa nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɩmʋ e-liidiye yaa ɩtaacɛtɩnɩ nɔɔyʋ taa yem. Ɛlɛ́, mɩ-laŋa ɩhʋlʋmɩnɩ mbʋ́ pɛhɛyɩɣ-mɩ yɔ.».

eng: The people were filled with expectation, and everyone wondered whether John might be the Christ.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ cosi ɛyáa samaɣ ŋ́gá ḿbʋ́ lɛ, kɛtɛzɩ maɣzʋʋ se: «Yaa ɛnʋ lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ?» Peeɖe paa weyi ɛɖaŋ kpam se pʋyʋ sɔsɔ nɔɔyʋ kɔŋ labʋ.

eng: John replied to them all, I baptize you with water, but the one who is more powerful than me is coming. Im not worthy to loosen the strap of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi patɩŋa se: «Ma lɛ, malakɩ-mɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ. Ɛlɛ́, nɔɔyʋ kaɣ kɔm, ɛ́lɛ́ kɩlɩ-m. Mantatɩɩmʋna se mɛyɛɖɩ ɛ-naataŋgbala tɔnzɩ maɣmaɣ. Ɛkɔŋ-mɩ labʋ sɔnzɩ ńzɩ́ nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖoŋ nɛ miŋ.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: Ɛwɛ ɛzɩ falɩyʋ ɖɔkʋʋ ɖaakuɖe nɛ ɛfalɩɣ nɛ esuu pee kpou taa nɛ piyele ɛɖʋʋ lɔtʋ miŋ weyi eeɖeŋ wiɖiyi kaʋ yɔ ɛ-taa yɔ.».

eng: With many other words John appealed to them, proclaiming good news to the people.
local: Tɔm ńdʋ́ nɛ lɛɛtʋ sakɩyɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ sazaɣnɩ ɛyáa samaɣ nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ-kɛ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: But Herod the ruler had been criticized harshly by John because of Herodias, Herods brothers wife, and because of all the evil he had done.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ ɛkalɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Eroodi, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛlɛɣ e-neu halʋ weyi payaɣ se Eroodiyaadɩ yɔ nɛ ɛla kɩwɛɛkɩm lɛɛbʋ ɖɔɖɔ sakɩyɛ.

eng: He added this to the list of his evil deeds: he locked John up in prison.
local: Peeɖe ɛtasɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm lɛɛbʋ labʋ. Ḿbʋ́ lɛ se eyebi pakpa Yohanɛɛsɩ ɛnʋ nɛ paɖʋ-ɩ salaka.

eng: When everyone was being baptized, Jesus also was baptized. While he was praying, heaven was opened.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ labɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ pɩtɛ lɛ, Yesu wolo ɖɔɖɔ nɛ ɛlabɩ-ɩ-sɩ. Ɛtɛm-ɩ-sɩ labʋ lɛ, Yesu sɛɣ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛsɔdaa tʋlɩ.

eng: and the Holy Spirit came down on him in bodily form like a dove. And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu lɩɩ kitiki ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ panaɣ-kʋ ɛzɩ halikuku yɔ. Peeɖe pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ ɛsɔdaa se: «Ña lɛ man-Pɩyalʋ kʋsɔɔlʋ, maahʋʋ ñɔ-yɔɔ kaʋ.».

eng: Jesus was about years old when he began his ministry. People supposed that he was the son of Joseph son of Heli.
local: Yesu kɛwɛnɩ ɛzɩ pɩnzɩ niidozo yɔ nɛ pʋcɔ ɛpazɩ ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ. Ɛyáa maɣzaɣ se ɛkɛ Yozɛɛfʋ pɩyalʋ. Yozɛɛfʋ ñɛkɛ Helii pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Matthat son of Levi son of Melchi son of Jannai son of Joseph.
local: Helii ñɛkɛ Mataatɩ pɩyalʋ, Mataatɩ ñɛkɛ Leevi pɩyalʋ, Leevi ñɛkɛ Mɛlkii pɩyalʋ, Mɛlkii ñɛkɛ Yanayi pɩyalʋ, Yanayi ñɛkɛ Yozɛɛfʋ pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Mattathias son of Amos son of Nahum son of Esli son of Naggai.
local: Yozɛɛfʋ ñɛkɛ Matatɩyaasɩ pɩyalʋ, Matatɩyaasɩ ñɛkɛ Amoosi pɩyalʋ, Amoosi ñɛkɛ Nahum pɩyalʋ, Nahum ñɛkɛ Eslii pɩyalʋ, Eslii ñɛkɛ Nagayii pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Maath son of Mattathias son of Semein son of Josech son of Joda.
local: Nagayii ñɛkɛ Maatɩ pɩyalʋ, Maatɩ ñɛkɛ Matatɩyaasɩ pɩyalʋ, Matatɩyaasɩ ñɛkɛ Semeyiini pɩyalʋ, Semeyiini ñɛkɛ Yozɛɛkɩ pɩyalʋ, Yozɛɛkɩ ñɛkɛ Yodaa pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Joanan son of Rhesa son of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel son of Neri.
local: Yodaa ñɛkɛ Yoaanaanɩ pɩyalʋ, Yoaanaanɩ ñɛkɛ Reezaa pɩyalʋ, Reezaa ñɛkɛ Zorobaabɛɛlɩ pɩyalʋ, Zorobaabɛɛlɩ ñɛkɛ Salaatɩyɛlɩ pɩyalʋ, Salaatɩyɛlɩ ñɛkɛ Neerii pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Melchi son of Addi son of Cosam son of Elmadam son of Er.
local: Neerii ñɛkɛ Mɛlkii pɩyalʋ, Mɛlkii ñɛkɛ Adii pɩyalʋ, Adii ñɛkɛ Koozam pɩyalʋ, Koozam ñɛkɛ Ɛlmaadam pɩyalʋ, Ɛlmaadam ñɛkɛ Ɛɛrɩ pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Joshua son of Eliezer son of Jorim son of Matthat son of Levi.
local: Ɛɛrɩ ñɛkɛ Yesua pɩyalʋ, Yesua ñɛkɛ Eliyeezɛɛrɩ pɩyalʋ, Eliyeezɛɛrɩ ñɛkɛ Yoorim pɩyalʋ, Yoorim ñɛkɛ Mataatɩ pɩyalʋ, Mataatɩ ñɛkɛ Leevi pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Simeon son of Judah son of Joseph son of Jonam son of Eliakim.
local: Leevi ñɛkɛ Siimeyɔɔ pɩyalʋ, Siimeyɔɔ ñɛkɛ Yuda pɩyalʋ, Yuda ñɛkɛ Yozɛɛfʋ pɩyalʋ, Yozɛɛfʋ ñɛkɛ Yonam pɩyalʋ, Yonam ñɛkɛ Eliyaakim pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Melea son of Menna son of Mattatha son of Nathan son of David.
local: Eliyaakim ñɛkɛ Meleya pɩyalʋ, Meleya ñɛkɛ Mɛnaa pɩyalʋ, Mɛnaa ñɛkɛ Matataa pɩyalʋ, Matataa ñɛkɛ Naataɣ pɩyalʋ, Naataɣ ñɛkɛ Daaviidi pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Jesse son of Obed son of Boaz son of Sala son of Nahshon.
local: Daaviidi ñɛkɛ Yesee pɩyalʋ, Yesee ñɛkɛ Obɛɛdɩ pɩyalʋ, Obɛɛdɩ ñɛkɛ Boozi pɩyalʋ, Boozi ñɛkɛ Sala pɩyalʋ, Sala ñɛkɛ Naasɔɔnɩ pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Amminadab son of Admin son of Arni son of Hezron son of Perez son of Judah.
local: Naasɔɔnɩ ñɛkɛ Aminaadaabɩ pɩyalʋ, Aminaadaabɩ ñɛkɛ Admini pɩyalʋ, Admini ñɛkɛ Arnii pɩyalʋ, Arnii ñɛkɛ Ɛsrɔm pɩyalʋ, Ɛsrɔm ñɛkɛ Faarɛɛzɩ pɩyalʋ, Faarɛɛzɩ ñɛkɛ Yuda pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham son of Terah son of Nahor.
local: Yuda ñɛkɛ Yakɔɔbʋ pɩyalʋ, Yakɔɔbʋ ñɛkɛ Izaakɩ pɩyalʋ, Izaakɩ ñɛkɛ Abraham pɩyalʋ, Abraham ñɛkɛ Taaraa pɩyalʋ, Taaraa ñɛkɛ Naacɔɔrɩ pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Serug son of Reu son of Peleg son of Eber son of Shelah.
local: Naacɔɔrɩ ñɛkɛ Seruuki pɩyalʋ, Seruuki ñɛkɛ Raagoo pɩyalʋ, Raagoo ñɛkɛ Faalɛɛkɩ pɩyalʋ, Faalɛɛkɩ ñɛkɛ Eebɛɛrɩ pɩyalʋ, Eebɛɛrɩ ñɛkɛ Sala pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Cainan son of Arphaxad son of Shem son of Noah son of Lamech.
local: Sala ñɛkɛ Kayɩnam pɩyalʋ, Kayɩnam ñɛkɛ Arfaksaadɩ pɩyalʋ, Arfaksaadɩ ñɛkɛ Sem pɩyalʋ, Sem ñɛkɛ Nowee pɩyalʋ, Nowee ñɛkɛ Laamɛɛkɩ pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Methuselah son of Enoch son of Jared son of Mahalalel son of Cainan.
local: Laamɛɛkɩ ñɛkɛ Matuusalaa pɩyalʋ, Matuusalaa ñɛkɛ Enɔɔkɩ pɩyalʋ, Enɔɔkɩ ñɛkɛ Yaarɛɛdɩ pɩyalʋ, Yaarɛɛdɩ ñɛkɛ Maleleyɛɛlɩ pɩyalʋ, Maleleyɛɛlɩ ñɛkɛ Kayɩnaɣ pɩyalʋ.

eng: son of Enos son of Seth son of Adam son of God.
local: Kayɩnaɣ ñɛkɛ Enɔɔsɩ pɩyalʋ, Enɔɔsɩ ñɛkɛ Sɛɛtɩ pɩyalʋ, Sɛɛtɩ ñɛkɛ Aɖam pɩyalʋ nɛ Aɖam lɛ weyi Ɛsɔ mawa yɔ.

eng: Jesus returned from the Jordan River full of the Holy Spirit, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.
local: Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tibi Yesu yɔɔ lɛ, ɛlɩɩnɩ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ nɔɔ nɛ ɛpɩsɩɣ. Peeɖe kitolisi-i nɛ kiwoni-i lakʋ taa.

eng: There he was tempted for forty days by the devil. He ate nothing during those days and afterward Jesus was starving.
local: nɛ ɛcaɣ peeɖe kɩyakɩŋ nɩɩnaza nɛ Satanɩ wɛɛ ɛtakɩɣ-ɩ. Ɛtaatɔɔ nabʋyʋ kɩyakɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-taa. Ɩ-wayɩ lɛ, ñɔɔsɩ kpa-ɩ.

eng: The devil said to him, Since you are Gods Son, command this stone to become a loaf of bread.
local: nɛ Satanɩ ɛnʋ eheyi-i se: «Yee ŋkɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ yɔ, heyi pɩyɛ ɖɩnɛ nɛ ɖɩpɩsɩ tindine nɛ ŋtɔɔ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread .
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se pɩtɛkɛ tɔɔnaɣ ɖeke ɛyʋ cakɩnɩ wezuu.».

eng: Next the devil led him to a high place and showed him in a single instant all the kingdoms of the world.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Satanɩ kpaɣ-ɩ nɛ ɛkpazɩ-ɩ ɖenɖe pɩkɩlɩ kʋyʋʋ yɔ nɛ ɛtɩwɩlɩ-ɩ kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ ajɛya.

eng: The devil said, I will give you this whole domain and the glory of all these kingdoms. Its been entrusted to me and I can give it to anyone I want.
local: nɛ eheyi-i se: «Mahaɣ-ŋ kedeŋa kanɛ kɔ-yɔɔ ɖoŋ nɛ ka-ñɩm tɩŋa payɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, me-nesi taa paɖʋzɩ pɩtɩŋa nɛ weyi mɔnsɔɔlaa yɔ, ɛnʋ mahaɣ.

eng: Therefore, if you will worship me, it will all be yours.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yee ŋluŋ mɛn-tɛɛ yɔ, mankpakɩɣ pɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ manɖʋzɩ ñe-nesi taa.».

eng: Jesus answered, Its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him. .
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Ña-Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ ɖeke koŋ tɛɛ pʋwɛɛ se ŋluŋ nɛ ŋla ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ.› ».

eng: The devil brought him into Jerusalem and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him, Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down from here;.
local: Satanɩ woni Yesu ɖɔɖɔ Yeruusalɛm nɛ ɛkpazɩ-ɩ Ɛsɔ kuduyuu sɔsɔʋ ñʋʋ taa nɛ eheyi-i se: «Yee ŋkɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ yɔ, kʋyɩ ŋmaa tataa.

eng: for its written: He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se Ɛsɔ heyiɣ e-tiyiyaa nɛ pohuuzi ñɔ-yɔɔ nɛ nabʋyʋ ɛtaalabɩ-ŋ.

eng: and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: Pamawa ɖɔɖɔ se pétéɣ-ŋ nɛ pɔ́ɖɔ́kɩ-ŋ camɩyɛ se ŋtaatɔlɩ pɩyɛ naɖɩyɛ yɔɔ nɛ pʋkʋ ña-naŋgbaŋaɣ.».

eng: Jesus answered, Its been said, Dont test the Lord your God. .
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Pama ka-taa ɖɔɖɔ se taatakɩ ña-Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ se ŋna ɛ-wayɩ.».

eng: After finishing every temptation, the devil departed from him until the next opportunity.
local: Satanɩ tɛm-ɩ takʋʋ pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, eyebi-i nɛ ɛcaŋ ɛɖɛɛ se pɩlaba wiɖiyi.

eng: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news about him spread throughout the whole countryside.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu pɩsɩ Galilee egeetiye taa nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖoŋ nɛ ɛ-hɩɖɛ sɛɛ egeetiye ńɖɩ́ ɖɩtɩŋa ɖɩ-taa.

eng: Jesus continued, I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom arrive in power. .
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se mbá pɛwɛ cɩnɛ yɔ, pa-taa lɛlaa naɣ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ kɔŋnɩ ɖoŋ yɔ nɛ pʋcɔ pasɩ.».

eng: He taught in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
local: Ɛwɩlaɣ Ɛsɔtɔm Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ taa nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ yɩɣyɩɣ-ɩ.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain where they were alone. He was transformed in front of them.
local: Kɩyakɩŋ loɖo ɖɛwa lɛ, Yesu yaa Pɩyɛ nɛ Yakubu nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ pakpa pʋʋ sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ yɔɔ pe-ɖeke. Peeɖe ɛlɛɣzɩ pɛ-ɛsɩndaa yem kpaagbaa kpɛlɩ nɛ.

eng: Jesus went to Nazareth, where he had been raised. On the Sabbath he went to the synagogue as he normally did and stood up to read.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu wolo Nazarɛɛtɩ ɖenɖe pɔɖɔkɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛpaɣlɩ yɔ nɛ ɛsʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye ɛzɩ ɛtɩɩlakʋʋ yɔ. Peeɖe ɛkʋyaa ɛsɩŋ se ɛkalɩɣ Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: and his clothes were amazingly bright, brighter than if they had been bleached white.
local: e-wondu hʋlʋmɩ layɩ layɩ nɛ tɩñɩlɩzɩɣ. Nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ se ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛhʋlɩsɩ-tʋ ḿbʋ́.

eng: The synagogue assistant gave him the scroll from the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written:.
local: nɛ pɛcɛlɩ-ɩ náyʋ Ezaayii takayaɣ nɛ ekuli ɖoli nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɛkalɩ. Pama peeɖe se:.

eng: Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ Elii nɛ Mooyiizi palɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pa nɛ Yesu pɔyɔɔdʋʋ.

eng: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me . He has sent me to preach good news to the poor, to proclaim release to the prisoners and recovery of sight to the blind, to liberate the oppressed.
local: «Kɩpaɣlʋ lɩzɩ-m nɛ ɛha-m e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu. Toovenim, ɛlɩzɩ-m nɛ etiyi-m se monsusi kʋñɔndɩnaa Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm. Etiyi-m se meheyi salaka tɩnaa se pɛwɛ pa-tɩ yɔɔ! Meheyi ɖɔɖɔ yʋlʋmaa se pɔkɔŋ pɛ-ɛsɛ kuluu! Etiyi-m se moholi ajamaa mbá panazɩɣ-wɛ yɔ.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Rabbi, its good that were here. Lets make three shrines—one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ heyi Yesu se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Đɩwɛʋ cɩnɛ yɔ, pʋwɛ ɖeu pɩŋŋ. Pʋmʋnaa se ɖisiɣ kɩɖanɖɩŋ naadozo: ña lɛɛkʋ, Mooyiizi lɛɛkʋ nɛ Elii lɛɛkʋ.».

eng: and to proclaim the year of the Lords favor.
local: Etiyi-m ɖɔɖɔ se meheyi ɛyáa tɩŋa pɩnaɣ ŋgá Ɛsɔ naɣ pa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ yɔ kɔ-tɔm.».

eng: He said this because he didnt know how to respond, for the three of them were terrified.
local: Toovenim lɛ se Pɩyɛ ɛɛnaɣ se ɛyɔɔdɩ se we. Sɔɔndʋ kpa ɛ nɛ ɛ-taabalaa lɛlaa.

eng: He rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the synagogue assistant, and sat down. Every eye in the synagogue was fixed on him.
local: Yesu tɛm kalʋʋ lɛ, esuli-kɛ nɛ ɛcɛlɩ tʋmɩyɛ sɩnɩyʋ nɛ pɩsɛɣ ɛcaɣ se ɛpazɩ-wɛ wɩlʋʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛyáa mbá pɛwɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa peeɖe yɔ, patɩŋa pɛkpɛzɩ ɛsɛ nɛ pɔcɔŋnɩ-ɩ.

eng: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice spoke from the cloud, This is my Son, whom I dearly love. Listen to him.
local: Ɛyɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛsɔmɩnʋʋ nakʋyʋ kɔɔ kuhuuzi pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ panɩɩ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-wɛ nɛ kɩ-tɛɛ se: «Ɛnɛ lɛ man-Pɩyalʋ kʋsɔɔlʋ, maahʋʋ ɛ-yɔɔ kaʋ. Ɩnɩɩnɩ-ɩ.».

eng: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
local: Tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ḿbá patɩcɔnɩ pɔ-cɔlɔ kpɛɛɛ lɛ, patatasɩ nɔɔyʋ naʋ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ Yesu.

eng: He began to explain to them, Today, this scripture has been fulfilled just as you heard it. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu pazɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Tɔm ndʋ́ mankalɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, tɩlabɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩnɩɣaɣ-tʋ yɔ.».

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them not to tell anyone what they had seen until after the Human One had risen from the dead.
local: Pakʋyɩ pʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ petiki lɛ, Yesu paɣ́-wɛ se: «Ɩtaaheyi nɔɔyʋ mbʋ́ ɩnawa yɔ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ fema nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa yɔ.».

eng: Everyone was raving about Jesus, so impressed were they by the gracious words flowing from his lips. They said, This is Josephs son, isnt it.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, patɩŋa payɩɣyɩ-ɩ nɛ pɩlabɩ-wɛ piti nɛ lootiye tɔm ndʋ́ eheyi-wɛ yɔ nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Pɩtɛkɛ Yozɛɛfʋ pɩyalʋ yɔ?».

eng: So they kept it to themselves, wondering, Whats this rising from the dead.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɔ́ɖɔ́kɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ ɖama se: «Ɛyɔɔdʋʋ se efeŋ nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa yɔ, pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se we?».

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Undoubtedly, you will quote this saying to me: Doctor, heal yourself. Do here in your hometown what weve heard you did in Capernaum. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, ɩkaɣ-m tuuu eduuye se: ‹Fɛɖʋ, fɛ ña-tɩ ña-maɣmaɣ.› Halɩ ɩkaɣ-m tɔm ɖɔɖɔ se: ‹Đɩnɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa ŋlabɩ Kapɛrnayum yɔ, la ḿbʋ́ ña-tɩ tɩŋa ñɛ-tɛ cɩnɛ.› ».

eng: They asked Jesus, Why do the legal experts say that Elijah must come first.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa tɔŋ se pʋwɛɛ se náyʋ Elii ɛlayɩ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ kɔm?».

eng: He said, I assure you that no prophet is welcome in the prophets hometown.
local: Peeɖe ɛtasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se patakpaɣ náyʋ se ɛkɛ pʋyʋ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-tɛ.

eng: He answered, Elijah does come first to restore all things. Why was it written that the Human One would suffer many things and be rejected?.
local: Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Toovenim, pʋwɛɛ se Elii ɛlayɩ kɔm nɛ ɛɖaɣnɩ pɩtɩŋa camɩyɛ. Meheyiɣ-mɩ ɖɔɖɔ se Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ tɔm pamawa se ɛkɔŋ tɔɔʋ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ nɛ peniɣzi-i.

eng: And I can assure you that there were many widows in Israel during Elijahs time, when it didnt rain for three and a half years and there was a great food shortage in the land.
local: Toovenim tɔm meheyiɣ-mɩ se Elii alɩwaatʋ taa lɛ, lelaa sakɩyɛ kɛwɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, tɛʋ tanɩɩ pɩnzɩ naadozo nɛ fenasɩ loɖo nɛ ñɔɔsɩ sɔsɔsɩ nasɩyɩ lɩɩ ɛjaɖɛ tɩŋa.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has come, but they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it was written about him. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se Elii tɛm kɔm nɛ palabɩ-ɩ mbʋ́ pɔsɔɔlaa yɔ ɛzɩ paamaʋ ɛ-tɔm Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ.».

eng: Yet Elijah was sent to none of them but only to a widow in the city of Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
local: Ŋgʋ́, Ɛsɔ taatiyi Elii lelaa ḿbá pa-taa nɔɔyʋ cɔlɔ. Ɛlɛ́, lelu nɔɔyʋ cɔlɔ etiyi-i. Ɛ́lɛ́ tɛkɛ Yuda tʋ, ɛɛwɛ Sarɛptaa, Sidɔɔ egeetiye taa.

eng: When Jesus, Peter, James, and John approached the other disciples, they saw a large crowd surrounding them and legal experts arguing with them.
local: Petibini pʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ patalɩ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lɛlaa cɔlɔ lɛ, pamaɣnɩ ɛyáa samaɣ kpeɣli peeɖe nɛ pa nɛ Yuda ñɩma paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa pɔhɔŋnɩ ɖama tɔm.

eng: There were also many persons with skin diseases in Israel during the time of the prophet Elisha, but none of them were cleansed. Instead, Naaman the Syrian was cleansed. .
local: Canasɩ sakɩyɛ kɛwɛ ɖɔɖɔ Izrɛɛlɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa náyʋ Eliizee alɩwaatʋ taa. Ŋgʋ́, sɩ-taa nakɛyɛ yɔɔ taacɛ, ɛlɛ́, Siirii ɛjaɖɛ taa tʋ Naamaɣ yɔɔ ɖeke kɛcɛbɩna.».

eng: Suddenly the whole crowd caught sight of Jesus. They ran to greet him, overcome with excitement.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ tɩna Yesu lɛ, pɩɖɩɣzɩ patɩŋa nɛ pese powolo se pɛsɛɛ-ɩ.

eng: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was filled with anger.
local: Mbá patɩŋa paawɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa peeɖe yɔ, panɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩwɩzɩ-wɛ pɩŋŋ.

eng: Jesus asked them, What are you arguing about? .
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛpɔzɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Ɛbɛ yɔɔ mɩ nɛ wɛ ɩhɔŋaɣnɩ ɖama tɔm ḿbʋ́?».

eng: They rose up and ran him out of town. They led him to the crest of the hill on which their town had been built so that they could throw him off the cliff.
local: Peeɖe pakʋyaa pakpa Yesu nɛ palɩzɩ-ɩ nɛ pɛ-tɛtʋ wayɩ nɛ pakpanɩ-ɩ pʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kʋ-yɔɔ pɛ-tɛtʋ wɛɛ yɔ kʋ-ñʋʋ taa se potuzi-i nɛ ɛtɔlɩ kajalaŋa taa.

eng: Someone from the crowd responded, Teacher, I brought my son to you, since he has a spirit that doesnt allow him to speak.
local: Peeɖe ɛyáa samaɣ taa nɔɔyʋ kʋyaa nɛ ɛpazɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Wɩlɩyʋ, mɔnkɔnɩ man-pɩyalʋ ñɔ-cɔlɔ, eleeu nɔɔyʋ wɛ ɛ-wayɩ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ-ɩ kaayɔɔdaɣ.

eng: But he passed through the crowd and went on his way.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu tɩŋnɩ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa hɛɛɛ nɛ ɛcaŋ ɛɖɛɛ.

eng: Wherever it overpowers him, it throws him into a fit. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and stiffens up. So I spoke to your disciples to see if they could throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Paa le, eleeu ɛnʋ ɛkpaɣ-ɩ nɛ ɛyakɩnɩ tɛtʋ nɛ ɛ-nɔɔ ñɛyɩɣ huluu nɛ ɛsalɩɣ e-kela nɛ ɛwɛɛ caŋjanaa. Mewini ñɔ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se pɔɖɔɔnɩ eleeu ɛnʋ ɛ-wayɩ, ɛlɛ́, patapɩzɩ.».

eng: Jesus went down to the city of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people each Sabbath.
local: Yesu ɖɛwa lɛ, ewolo Kapɛrnayum Galilee egeetiye taa nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ ɛyáa Ɛsɔtɔm Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye.

eng: Jesus answered them, You faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring him to me. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu holini-wɛ se: «Ɛyáa lidaʋ fɛyɩ tɩnaa mɩ! Alɩwaatʋ ɛzɩma mantasɩɣ mɩ-cɔlɔ caɣʋ nɛ pʋwɛɛ se mɔnɖɔkɩ mɔ-hɔyɛ ɖɔɖɔ? Ɩkɔnɩ-m pɩɣa ŋ́gá cɩnɛ!».

eng: They were amazed by his teaching because he delivered his message with authority.
local: Peeɖe pɩlabɩ-wɛ piti nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛwɩlaɣ-wɛ yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛwɩlaɣ-wɛ nɛ abalɩtʋ.

eng: They brought him. When the spirit saw Jesus, it immediately threw the boy into a fit. He fell on the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
local: Ɛlɛ́, powoni-kɛ. Eleeu ɛnʋ ɛtɩlosi Yesu lɛ, ɛpazɩ pɩɣa ŋ́gá pɩsʋʋ kpɛndɛɣlɩsɩ nɛ kɔtɔlɩ tataa nɛ kepilimi nɛ huluu ñɛyɩ kɔ-nɔɔ.

eng: A man in the synagogue had the spirit of an unclean demon. He screamed.
local: Puwiye lɛ, abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe, eleeu nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ ɛ-wayɩ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛma kubuka se:.

eng: Jesus asked his father, How long has this been going on? He said, Since he was a child.
local: Yesu pɔzɩ ka-caa se: «Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ʋ́ eleeu pazɩ-kɛ labʋ ḿbʋ́?» Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: «Đooo ka-pɩjatʋ taa.

eng: Hey! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: «Ou! Yesu Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ, ɛbɛ ŋcaɣnɩ-ɖʋ? Ŋkɔm se ŋkʋ-ɖʋ yaa we? Mansɩm mbʋ́ ŋkɛnaa yɔ ɖeu. Ŋkɛ Ɛsɔ Ɛyʋ.».

eng: It has often thrown him into a fire or into water trying to kill him. If you can do anything, help us! Show us compassion.
local: eleeu pazɩ-kɛ tuzuu nɛ ɛpɛɖɩɣ miŋ nɛ lɩm pa-taa se kasɩ. Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ mentendiɣ-ŋ se yee ŋpɩzɩɣ yɔ, ŋna ɖa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ nɛ ŋsɩnɩ-ɖʋ.».

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him! The demon threw the man down before them, then came out of him without harming him.
local: Peeɖe Yesu holini-i se: «Hei! Su peeɖe nɛ ŋse abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-wayɩ. Ɛlɛ́, eleeu co abalʋ ɛnʋ nɛ ɛyaɣ-ɩ nɛ tɛtʋ ɛyáa ɛsɩndaa nɛ ɛcaŋ ese. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtala abalʋ ɛnʋ pʋyʋ.».

eng: Jesus said to him, If you can do anything? All things are possible for the one who has faith. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋtɔŋ se yee manpɩzɩɣ? Weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ, pʋyʋ nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ se Ɛsɔ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛlabɩ-ɩ-pʋ.».

eng: They were all shaken and said to each other, What kind of word is this, that he can command unclean spirits with authority and power, and they leave.
local: Peeɖe pɩɖɩɣzɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ ɖama se: «Tɔm ndʋ́ lɛ ḿbʋ́? Abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛñazɩɣ aleewaa nɛ abalɩtʋ nɛ peseɣ.».

eng: At that the boys father cried out, I have faith; help my lack of faith.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɩɣa ŋ́gá ka-caa tɩmabʋ kubuka se: «Man-caa mɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ, ɛlɛ́ sɩnɩ-m nɛ mɛntɛzɩ wɛnaʋ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ pɩŋŋ.».

eng: Reports about him spread everywhere in the surrounding region.
local: Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ Yesu hɩɖɛ sɛɛ Galilee egeetiye tɩŋa payɩ taa.

eng: Noticing that the crowd had surged together, Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, Mute and deaf spirit, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again. .
local: Yesu na ɛyáa samaɣ seɣaɣ powoki ɛ-cɔlɔ lɛ, eholini eleeu ɛnʋ se: «Weyi ña ŋwɛ pɩɣa ŋ́gá ka-wayɩ nɛ ŋpɩsɩ-kɛ kaayɔɔdaɣ nɛ ŋtɔ ka-nɩŋgbaaŋ taa yɔ, se! Wiɖiyi mantaatasɩ-ŋ naʋ ka-wayɩ.».

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus went home with Simon. Simons mother-in-law was sick with a high fever, and the family asked Jesus to help her.
local: Yesu lɩnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa lɛ, ewolo Siimɔɔ tɛ. Kɩnamɩyɛ kɔɖɔkɩ Siimɔɔ yɔsɔ siŋŋ nɛ petendi Yesu se ɛla nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: After screaming and shaking the boy horribly, the spirit came out. The boy seemed to be dead; in fact, several people said that he had died.
local: Peeɖe eleeu ɛnʋ ɛmabɩ kubusi nɛ ɛñamsɩ-kɛ siŋŋ nɛ ese ka-wayɩ. Ɛlɛ́, kefiɣni nɛ halɩ pʋwɛɛ ɛzɩ kasɩba yɔ nɛ ɛyáa tɔŋ se: «Kasɩba kpem.».

eng: He bent over her and spoke harshly to the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and served them.
local: Peeɖe Eluzi ɛ-tɩ nɛ eholini kɩnamɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ se ɖɩcɛ nɛ ɖɩcɛ ɖɔɖɔ halʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛtɩkʋyɩ ɛha-wɛ lɩm.

eng: But Jesus took his hand, lifted him up, and he arose.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu ɖɔkɩ ke-nesi taa nɛ ɛkʋsɩ-kɛ nɛ kasɩŋ.

eng: When the sun was setting, everyone brought to Jesus relatives and acquaintances with all kinds of diseases. Placing his hands on each of them, he healed them.
local: Wɩsɩ ɖʋwa lɛ, mbá patɩŋa paawɛnɩ kʋdɔndɩnaa ndɩ ndɩ yɔ, powoni-wɛ Yesu cɔlɔ nɛ ɛtɩnɩ e-nesi paa weyi ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: After Jesus went into a house, his disciples asked him privately, Why couldnt we throw this spirit out.
local: Yesu woba ɛsʋʋ ɖɩɣa nɛ ɛ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pacaɣ pe-ɖeke lɛ, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɖɩtapɩzɩ ɖɩɖɔɔnɩ eleeu ɛnʋ pɩɣa ŋ́gá ka-wayɩ?».

eng: Demons also came out of many people. They screamed, You are Gods Son. But he spoke harshly to them and wouldnt allow them to speak because they recognized that he was the Christ.
local: Ɛɖɔɔnɩ aleewaa ɖɔɖɔ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ wayɩ nɛ pamakɩ kubusi se: «Ŋkɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ.» Ɛlɛ́, Yesu paɣ́-wɛ se pataakuli ɛ-wayɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pasɩm-ɩ se ɛnʋ lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, Throwing this kind of spirit out requires prayer. .
local: Peeɖe ecosi-wɛ se: «Ɛsɔ sɛʋ ɖeke pɩzɩɣnɩ pɩha ɛyʋ ɖoŋ nɛ ɛɖɔɔnɩnɩ pɩtɛ eleeu ɛnʋ.».

eng: When daybreak arrived, Jesus went to a deserted place. The crowds were looking for him. When they found him, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
local: Tɛʋ tɩfema lɛ, Yesu lɩɩ ewolo lakʋ taa. Peeɖe ɛyáa samaɣ pazɩ-ɩ ñɩnʋʋ. Pana-ɩ lɛ, powolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ palaɣzɩɣ-ɩ se ɛtaaɖɛɛ.

eng: From there Jesus and his followers went through Galilee, but he didnt want anyone to know it.
local: Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pakʋyɩ ɖɩɣa lɛ, patɩŋnɩ Galilee egeetiye taa. Ɛlɛ́, ɛtaasɔɔlɩ se panɩɩ ɛ-taŋ.

eng: But he said to them, I must preach the good news of Gods kingdom in other cities too, for this is why I was sent. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Pʋwɛɛ se monsusi ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm tɛtʋ lɛɛtʋ taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩ-maɣmaɣ pʋ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ tiyi-m.».

eng: This was because he was teaching his disciples, The Human One will be delivered into human hands. They will kill him. Three days after he is killed he will rise up. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛwɩlaɣ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa. Eheyaɣ-wɛ se: «Pɔkɔŋ kpaʋ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ nɛ paɖʋzɩ-ɩ ɛyáa nesi taa nɛ pakʋ-ɩ nɛ ɛsɩ. Kɩyakɩŋ naadozo wiye lɛ, Ɛsɔ fezi-i.».

eng: So he continued preaching in the Judean synagogues.
local: Peeɖe ɛɖɛwa nɛ esusuu Ɛsɔtɔm Yudee egeetiye taa Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ taa.

eng: But they didnt understand this kind of talk, and they were afraid to ask him.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tanɩɩ pʋ-tɔbʋʋ. Ŋgʋ́, patakatɩɣ se pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ natʋyʋ.

eng: They entered Capernaum. When they had come into a house, he asked them, What were you arguing about during the journey? .
local: Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa patalɩ Kapɛrnayum lɛ, pasʋʋ ɖɩɣa. Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Tɔm ndʋ́ ɩhɔŋaɣnɩ ɖama nʋmɔʋ taa?».

eng: They didnt respond, since on the way they had been debating with each other about who was the greatest.
local: Ɛlɛ́, posuma, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, sɔsɔtʋ yɔɔ poyowaɣ.

eng: He sat down, called the Twelve, and said to them, Whoever wants to be first must be least of all and the servant of all. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu caɣaɣ nɛ ɛ́yáa pe-hiu nɛ naalɛ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Weyi ɛcaɣ se ɛkɛ sɔsɔ yɔ, pʋdʋ ɛpɩsɩ ɛ-tɩ lɛlaa taa pɩɣa nɛ ɛla patɩŋa payɩ tʋmɩyɛ.».

eng: Jesus reached for a little child, placed him among the Twelve, and embraced him. Then he said.
local: Ɛtɛm-wɛ heyuu ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛkpaɣ pɩɣa cikpelaɣ nakɛyɛ nɛ ewoni-kɛ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ ɛkpazɩ-kɛ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se:.

eng: Whoever welcomes one of these children in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me isnt actually welcoming me but rather the one who sent me. .
local: «Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛmʋ piya cikpesi sɩnɛ sɩ-taa nakɛyɛ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, man-tɩ tɩŋa ɛmʋwa. Weyi ɛmʋ-m yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ man-tɩ tɩŋa ɛmʋwa, ɛlɛ́, weyi etiyi-m yɔ ɛmʋwa.».

eng: John said to Jesus, Teacher, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he wasnt following us.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yohanɛɛsɩ heyi Yesu se: «Wɩlɩyʋ, ɖɩna nɔɔyʋ ɖɔɔnaɣ aleewaa nɛ ño-ɖoŋ nɛ ɖɩkʋyɩ se ɖikiziɣni-i se ɛtɛkɛ ɖa-ñɩnʋ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Dont stop him. No one who does powerful acts in my name can quickly turn around and curse me.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Ɩtaaɖaŋ-ɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ se ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛla hama lakasɩ nɛ mon-ɖoŋ nɛ ɛtɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ-m tɔm ɛzaɣ́tʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Whoever isnt against us is for us.
local: Toovenim lɛ, weyi ɛɛkʋyʋʋ ɖɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛkɛ ɖa-ñɩnʋ.

eng: I assure you that whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will certainly be rewarded.
local: Mɩ lɛ, ɩkɛ ma-ñɩma. Ɛlɛ́, meheyiɣ-mɩ se yee nɔɔyʋ ɛha mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ lɩm lɩkpadaɣ maɣmaɣ ma Krɩstʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma, Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-ɩ hɛyʋʋ pu-lone taa.».

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and to be thrown into the lake.
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Yee nɔɔyʋ eyeba nɛ mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ patakpaɣ-wɛ payaa pʋyʋ yɔ pa-taa nɔɔyʋ la kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ, pɩkɩlɩ weewee se pɛlɛɛ e-liu tɛɛ pɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ nɛ powoni pɛpɛɖɩ teŋgu taa.

eng: If your hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter into life crippled than to go away with two hands into the fire of hell, which cant be put out.
local: Yee ñe-nesi eyekini se ŋla kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ, sɛtɩ-sɩ nɛ ŋlɔ. Pɩkɩlɩ ɖeu se ŋwɛnɩ nesi kʋɖʋmsɩ nɛ ŋhiɣ wezuu ɛzɩ ŋŋwɛnɩ ñe-nesi naalɛ nɛ Ɛsɔ pɛɖɩ-ŋ kʋñɔŋ maatɛŋ taa ɖenɖe miŋ eeɖeŋ kaʋ yɔ. .

eng: If your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter life lame than to be thrown into hell with two feet.
local: Yee ña-naŋgbaŋaɣ eyekini se ŋla kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ, sɛtɩ-kɛ nɛ ŋlɔ. Pɩkɩlɩ ɖeu se ŋwɛnɩ naŋgbaŋaɣ kʋɖʋmaɣ nɛ ŋhiɣ wezuu ɛzɩ ŋŋwɛnɩ ña-naŋgbanzɩ naalɛ nɛ Ɛsɔ pɛɖɩ-ŋ ɖenɖe kʋñɔŋ maatɛŋ wɛɛ yɔ. .

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out. Its better for you to enter Gods kingdom with one eye than to be thrown into hell with two.
local: Yee ñɛ-ɛsɩyɛ eyekini se ŋla kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ, hɔɔzɩ-ɖɩ nɛ ŋpɛɖɩ. Pɩkɩlɩ ɖeu se ŋsʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa nɛ ɛsɩyɛ kʋɖʋmɖɩyɛ ɛzɩ ŋŋwɛnɩ ɛsɛ naalɛ nɛ Ɛsɔ pɛɖɩ-ŋ kʋñɔŋ maatɛŋ taa ɖenɖe miŋ eeɖeŋ kaʋ yɔ.

eng: At daybreak, the chief priests—with the elders, legal experts, and the whole Sanhedrin—formed a plan. They bound Jesus, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate.
local: Pakpa Yesu nɛ kife tanaŋ cu lɛ, tɔm hʋyaa tɩŋa payɩ kpeɣli ɖama se pala lɩmaɣza. Ḿbá yɔ: Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ pɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa nɛ pa-paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa. Pɛtɛma lɛ, pɔhɔkɩ Yesu nɛ powoni pɛcɛlɩ Room wiyaʋ Pilaatɩ.

eng: Thats a place where worms dont die and the fire never goes out .
local: Peeɖe ‹sonzoowe wena atɔkɩ ɛyáa nɛ aasɩkɩ kaʋ yɔ nɛ miŋ weyi eeɖeŋ kaʋ yɔ pɛwɛɛ.›.

eng: Pilate questioned him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say. .
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ ñɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ña lɛ Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ naa we?» Yesu cosi-i se: «Ñɔyɔɔdʋʋnɩ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: Everyone will be salted with fire.
local: «Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ paɖʋʋ ɖɔm tɔɔnaɣ taa nɛ kɛwɛɣ leleŋ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ tɩŋɩɣnɩ kʋñɔŋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛñɔɔzɩ paa weyi.

eng: The chief priests were accusing him of many things.
local: Peeɖe Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa kʋyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ se ɛyʋsɩ tɔm sakɩyɛ.

eng: Salt is good; but if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Maintain salt among yourselves and keep peace with each other. .
local: «Đɔm kɛ pʋyʋ kɩbanʋ. Ɛlɛ́, yee pilesi pi-leleŋ lɛ, ɛzɩma palakɩ-pʋ nɛ pɩɖaɣnɩ kpaʋ leleŋ yɔ? Mɩ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se mɩ-taa ɛwɛnɩ ɖama leleŋ pɩŋŋ ɛzɩ ɖɔm wɛʋ leleŋ yɔ nɛ mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩwɛɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa.».

eng: Pilate asked him again, Arent you going to answer? What about all these accusations.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pilaatɩ tasɩ-ɩ pɔzʋʋ se: «Ña-tɩ tɩŋa ŋnɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pakʋyʋʋ ñɔ-yɔɔ yɔ. Ŋfɛyɩnɩ natʋyʋ se ŋcosuu yaa we?».

eng: But Jesus gave no more answers, so that Pilate marveled.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu suma, ɛtatasɩ cosuu natʋyʋ. Peeɖe pɩɖɩɣzɩ Pilaatɩ yem ɖiɣ ɖiɣ.

eng: During the festival, Pilate released one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
local: Paa Yuda ñɩma Paska Kazandʋ ndʋ́ lɛ, Pilaatɩ lɩzɩɣ salaka tɩnaa taa kʋɖʋm weyi ɛyáa samaɣ sɔɔlaa se palɩzɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: A man named Barabbas was locked up with the rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.
local: Ŋgʋ́, abalʋ weyi payaɣ se Baraabaasɩ yɔ nɛ ɛyáa nabɛyɛ paawɛ salaka taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paakʋ ɛyʋ nɔɔyʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-taa ɛyáa kalɩ Room wiyaʋ wayɩ yɔ.

eng: The crowd pushed forward and asked Pilate to release someone, as he regularly did.
local: Peeɖe ɛyáa samaɣ kʋyaa nɛ powolo pana Pilaatɩ se ɛlabɩ-wɛ ɛzɩ ɛtɩɩlakʋʋ yɔ.

eng: Pilate answered them, Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ ñɔpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɩcaɣ se meyele Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ ɛnɛ yaa we?».

eng: He knew that the chief priests had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnawa se ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa laba nɛ pakpa Yesu nɛ powoni-i ɛ-cɔlɔ.

eng: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas to them instead.
local: Cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa ḿbá pasazaɣnɩ ɛyáa se pɔtɔ se mbʋ́ ɛzɩ Pilaatɩ keyele Yesu lɛ, ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ Baraabaasɩ salaka taa.

eng: Pilate replied, Then what do you want me to do with the one you call king of the Jews.
local: Peeɖe Pilaatɩ tasɩ ɛyáa samaɣ pɔzʋʋ se: «Ɛbɛ ɩcaɣ se mala ɛyʋ weyi ɩyaɣ se Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ yɔ?».

eng: They shouted back, Crucify him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pama kubusi se: «Kam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ!».

eng: Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Ɛtasɩ-wɛ pɔzʋʋ se: «Ɛlɛ́ kɩwɛɛkɩm mbʋ́ kpem ɛlaba?» Ɛlɛ́, pɛtɛzɩ kubusi mabʋ ɖoŋ se: «Kam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ!».

eng: Pilate wanted to satisfy the crowd, so he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Pilaatɩ sɔɔlaa se ɛyáa samaɣ taa ɛla leleŋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛlɩzɩ Baraabaasɩ nɛ eyele polu Yesu agbazɛɛ nɛ ɛkpaɣ-ɩ ɛcɛlɩ-wɛ se powoni-i pakam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ.

eng: The soldiers led Jesus away into the courtyard of the palace known as the governors headquarters, and they called together the whole company of soldiers.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, sɔɔjanaa woni Yesu Pilaatɩ tɛ kadaɣ taa. Peeɖe paya ɖama patɩŋa.

eng: They dressed him up in a purple robe and twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on him.
local: nɛ posuu-i awiitoko kɩsɛmʋʋ nakʋyʋ waaa nɛ palʋʋ awiya hulaɣ nɛ sɔɔɔ nɛ papam-ɩ.

eng: They saluted him, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Ɛlɛ́, papazɩ-ɩ sɛʋ nɛ woŋa se: «Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ! Sɛɛ ɖeu!».

eng: Again and again, they struck his head with a stick. They spit on him and knelt before him to honor him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pamakɩ ɛ-ñʋʋ taa ɖaʋ nɛ pɔtɔʋ ɛ-yɔɔ ndaatʋ nɛ poluŋuu ɛ-ɛsɩndaa.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
local: Pɛtɛm-ɩ tɛtɛ pɛŋʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔhɔzɩ toko kɩsɛmʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́ nɛ papɩsɩnɩ e-wondu posuu-i. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pakpaɣ-ɩ se powokini tɛtʋ wayɩ nɛ pakam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ.

eng: Simon, a man from Cyrene, Alexander and Rufus father, was coming in from the countryside. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Pɛɖɛɣaɣnɩ Yesu lɛ, pakatɩ Alɛksandrɩ nɛ Ruufuusi pa-caa, ɛ-tɛ Siirɛɛnɩ egeetiye taa, payaɣ-ɩ se Siimɔɔ. Ɛɛlɩnɩ tɛyɛ yɔɔ nɛ ekpeŋ. Peeɖe sɔɔjanaa kpa-ɩ nɛ ɖoŋ se ɛhɔɔlɩ Yesu kaamaŋkaɣ.

eng: After Jesus finished presenting all his words among the people, he entered Capernaum.
local: Yesu tɛm tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩtɩŋa yɔɔdʋʋ lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ ewolo Kapɛrnayum.

eng: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Powoni Yesu ɖenɖe payaɣ se Goligota yɔ, pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se «ñʋmbɔɔyaɣ lone».

eng: A centurion had a servant who was very important to him, but the servant was ill and about to die.
local: Room sɔɔja sɔsɔ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe. Ɛɛwɛnɩ tʋmlaɖʋ nɛ ɛ-tɔm kɛdɩnɩ-ɩ pɩŋŋ. Tʋmlaɖʋ ɛnʋ, pɩwɩɣaɣ-ɩ sɩbʋ yɔɔ sɩbʋ yɔɔ.

eng: They tried to give him wine mixed with myrrh, but he didnt take it.
local: Patanɩ Yesu lɛ, pɛcɛlɩ-ɩ sʋlʋm mbʋ́ pesidi pɩ-taa kɔyɛ nɖɩ́ payaɣ se miiri yɔ se ɛñɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ekizi.

eng: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to Jesus to ask him to come and heal his servant.
local: Ŋgʋ́, sɔɔja sɔsɔ ɛnʋ, ɛɛnɩ pɔyɔɔdɩ Yesu tɔm se ɛlakɩ nɛ kʋdɔndɩnaa yɔɔ cɛkɩ. Pʋ-yɔɔ etiyi Yuda ñɩma sɔsaa nabɛyɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ se pewini-i nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛya ɛ-tʋmlaɖʋ ñʋʋ.

eng: They crucified him. They divided up his clothes, drawing lots for them to determine who would take what.
local: Peeɖe pakam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ. Pɛtɛma lɛ, papazɩ ɖama e-wondu tayʋʋ. Peeɖe paɖʋ miika se pana ŋgʋ́ paa weyi ɛkpakɩɣ yɔ.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly pleaded with Jesus. He deserves to have you do this for him, they said.
local: Powoba lɛ, pewini-i cɔɔɔ se: «Abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛmʋnaa se ŋsɩnɩ-ɩ.

eng: It was nine in the morning when they crucified him.
local: Pakaŋaɣ-ɩ lɛ, pɩmakaɣ ɛzɩ tanaŋ tɛɛ ñɩɣtʋ nakʋ yɔ.

eng: He loves our people and he built our synagogue for us.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛsɔɔlɩ Yuda ñɩma ɖa nɛ ɛma-ɖʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ.».

eng: The notice of the formal charge against him was written, The king of the Jews.
local: Paama kpata nakʋyʋ yɔɔ mbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ pakam-ɩ yɔ nɛ paka kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ ɛ-ñʋʋ taa. Pamawa se: «Ɛnɛ lɛ Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ.».

eng: Jesus went with them. He had almost reached the house when the centurion sent friends to say to Jesus, Lord, dont be bothered. I dont deserve to have you come under my roof.
local: Peeɖe Yesu tɩŋ-wɛ nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ pɛɖɛɛ. Pɔñɔtɩnɩ ɖɩɣa lɛ, sɔɔja ɛnʋ etiyi ɛ-taabalaa se peheyi Yesu se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ, taakʋ ña-tɩ yem, mantamʋna se ŋsʋʋ man-ɖɩɣa taa.

eng: They crucified two outlaws with him, one on his right and one on his left.
local: Paakam ɖɔɖɔ mɩ́láa naalɛ nabɛyɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ: lɛlʋ ɛ-nɩwaŋ yɔɔ nɛ lɛlʋ ɛ-nɩmɩyɛ yɔɔ. .

eng: In fact, I didnt even consider myself worthy to come to you. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ mantatalɩ man-tɩ tɩŋa ñɔ-cɔlɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ha nɔɔ nɛ man-tʋmlaɖʋ yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: People walking by insulted him, shaking their heads and saying, Ha! So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you?.
local: Nʋmɔɖɔnaa mbá pɛɖɛɣaɣnɩ peeɖe yɔ, pekpiɣyiɣ-i ñʋŋ nɛ patʋʋ-ɩ se: «Hei! Weyi ña ŋtɔŋaɣ se ŋyɔkʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa nɛ ŋɖaɣnɩ-kɛ maʋ nɛ kɩyakɩŋ naadozo yɔ.

eng: Im also a man appointed under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ma lɛ, ma-ñʋʋ yɔɔ ñɩma wɛɛ nɛ mañaŋ-wɛ, mɛn-tɛɛ ñɩma wɛɛ ɖɔɖɔ. Meheyi pa-taa nɔɔyʋ se ewolo peeɖe yɔ yɔ, ewoki. Maya nɔɔyʋ se ɛkɔɔ yɔ, ɛkɔŋ. Meheyi man-tʋmlaɖʋ se ɛla nabʋyʋ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ ñalakɩ-pʋ.».

eng: Save yourself and come down from that cross.
local: ya ña-maɣmaɣ ña-ñʋʋ! Tibini kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩna.».

eng: When Jesus heard these words, he was impressed with the centurion. He turned to the crowd following him and said, I tell you, even in Israel I havent found faith like this. .
local: Powoba nɛ peheyi Yesu ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛyɩɣyɩ sɔɔja sɔsɔ ɛnʋ nɛ ɛkɛzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ ŋgá katɩŋaɣ-ɩ yɔ kɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se paa Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma taa maɣmaɣ, mantanada weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ nɛ pɩtalɩ-ɩ yɔ.».

eng: In the same way, the chief priests were making fun of him among themselves, together with the legal experts. He saved others, they said, but he cant save himself.
local: Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ pa-paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa pɛpɛŋaɣ Yesu tɛtɛ ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ ɖama se: «Ɛyabɩ lɛlaa ñʋʋ ɛzɩma tɛ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ se ɛya ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-ñɩŋgʋ!.

eng: When the centurions friends returned to his house, they found the servant restored to health.
local: Sɔɔja ɛnʋ e-tiyiyaa pɩsɩ ɖɩɣa lɛ, pamaɣnɩ ɛ-tʋmlaɖʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛba.

eng: Soon afterward, Jesus traveled through the cities and villages, preaching and proclaiming the good news of Gods kingdom. The Twelve were with him.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu cɔɔ tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ nɛ cikpetu pa-taa nɛ esusuu Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ.

eng: Let the Christ, the king of Israel, come down from the cross. Then well see and believe. Even those who had been crucified with Jesus insulted him.
local: Weyi ña ŋheyiɣ se ña lɛ Izrɛɛlɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ nɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ, yee ŋtibini kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩna yɔ, pʋbʋ ɖɩwɛɣnɩ lidaʋ ñɔ-yɔɔ.» Halɩ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ mbá paakam-wɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ nɛ patʋʋ-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: A little later Jesus went to a city called Nain. His disciples and a great crowd traveled with him.
local: Tɛʋ fema lɛ, Yesu kʋyɩ se ewoki tɛtʋ ndʋ́ payaɣ se Nayɩnɩ yɔ tɩ-taa. Ewokaɣ lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ sɔsɔɔ nakɛyɛ patɩŋɩɣ ɛ-wayɩ.

eng: along with some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses. Among them were Mary Magdalene .
local: nɛ halaa mbá ɛɛlɩzɩ-wɛ aleewaa nesi taa nɛ ɛla nɛ pa-kʋdɔmɩŋ cɛ yɔ pasɩɣaɣ-ɩ. Halaa ḿbá yɔ: Maarɩɩ weyi payaɣ se Magdala tʋ nɛ ɛɛɖɔɔnɩ aleewaa lʋbɛ ɛ-wayɩ yɔ.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Puwiye ńɖɩ́ wɩsɩ siɣaa lɛ, cɩkpɛndʋʋ yu ɛjaɖɛ tɩŋa kpemm nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ ɖanaɣ ñɩɣtʋ naadozo.

eng: As he approached the city gate, a dead man was being carried out. He was his mothers only son, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was with her.
local: Pɔñɔtɩnɩ-tʋ lɛ, pakatɩ ɛyáa samaɣ nakɛyɛ, kowokaɣnɩ sɩɖʋ nɔɔyʋ pim, ɛɛkɛ lelu nɔɔyʋ pɩɣa ɛgbamɩyɛ.

eng: Joanna , Susanna, and many others who provided for them out of their resources.
local: nɛ Cuuza weyi ɛkɛ Eroodi ɖɩɣa yɔɔ cɔnɩyʋ yɔ ɛ-halʋ Yoana nɛ Sʋzaana nɛ halaa lɛlaa sakɩyɛ mbá pakpakaɣ pa-ñɩm nɛ pasɩŋnɩ Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa yɔ.

eng: At three, Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eloi, eloi, lama sabachthani, which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Peeɖe Yesu mabɩ kubusi se: «Eloyi, Eloyi! Lamaa sapaktanɩɩ?» Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se: «Mɛ-Ɛsɔ, mɛ-Ɛsɔ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋlɔ-m?».

eng: When he saw her, the Lord had compassion for her and said, Dont cry. .
local: Kɩpaɣlʋ na lelu ɛnʋ lɛ, pɩlabɩ-ɩ pʋtɔdɩyɛ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Taawii!».

eng: When a great crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from one city after another, he spoke to them in a parable:.
local: Ɛyáa sakɩyɛ lɩɣaɣnɩ tɛtʋ ndɩ ndɩ taa nɛ powoki Yesu cɔlɔ. Pekpeɣlaa lɛ, etuu-wɛ eduuye se:.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Look! Hes calling Elijah.
local: Mbá paawɛ peeɖe yɔ, pa-taa lɛlaa nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔtɔŋ se: «Ɩnɩɩ ɛyaɣʋ Elii!».

eng: He stepped forward and touched the stretcher on which the dead man was being carried. Those carrying him stood still. Jesus said, Young man, I say to you, get up. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu woba etukuni sɩɖʋ kpataya nɛ sɩɣlɩyaa sɩŋ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Evebu ña! Meheyiɣ-ŋ se ŋkʋyɩ!».

eng: One Sabbath, as Jesus was going through the wheat fields, his disciples were picking the heads of wheat, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
local: Kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɔɖɔŋaɣ hayɩm nabʋyʋ taa. Paahayɩ tɔɔnaɣ nakɛyɛ pɩ-taa nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɛlɩɣ nɛ nʋmɔʋ nɛ ponuutuu nɛ pasalɩɣ. Tɔɔnaɣ ŋ́gá kɛwɛ ɛzɩ amala yɔ.

eng: A farmer went out to scatter his seed. As he was scattering it, some fell on the path where it was crushed, and the birds in the sky came and ate it.
local: «Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ wɛnɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ewolo tɔɔnaɣ pee mɩzʋʋ ɛ-hayɩm taa. Ɛmɩzaɣ lɛ, lɛɛna tɔlɩ nʋmɔʋ nɔɔ nɛ pɛfɛyɩ-yɛ nɛ sumasɩ tii sɩtɔɔ-yɛ.

eng: Someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink, saying, Lets see if Elijah will come to take him down.
local: Peeɖe pa-taa lɛlʋ sewa nɛ ewolo ɛkpaɣ kʋca nɛ ewoni elii sʋlʋm kɩñɩŋɩm nabʋyʋ taa nɛ ɛtʋʋ ɖaʋ nakʋyʋ nɔɔ taa nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ Yesu se ɛmʋzɩ nɛ ɛtɔŋ se: «Iyele ɖɩna se Elii kɔŋ etisi-i yaa we?».

eng: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛkʋyɩ ɛcaɣ nɛ ɛpazɩ yɔɔdʋʋ nɛ Yesu cɛlɩ-ɩ e-ɖoo.

eng: Some Pharisees said, Why are you breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Palakaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Farɩsɩ ñɩma nabɛyɛ na-wɛ nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ɩlakɩ mbʋ́ paɖʋ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ taa se pataala kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye yɔ?».

eng: Other seed fell on rock. As it grew, it dried up because it had no moisture.
local: Lɛɛna ñɔtɔlɩ ɛwaɣ yɔɔ nɛ alɛ ñɔ nɛ awɩlɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, niŋkaɣ fɛyɩ.

eng: But Jesus let out a loud cry and died.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu ma kubuka ɖoŋ nɛ ɛhɛɛ.

eng: Awestruck, everyone praised God. A great prophet has appeared among us, they said. God has come to help his people.
local: Peeɖe sɔɔndʋ kpa patɩŋa nɛ pasaŋ Ɛsɔ nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Náyʋ sɔsɔ nɔɔyʋ kɔm ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa, Ɛsɔ kɔm ɛlɩzɩ ɛ-ɛyáa pɩ-taa.».

eng: Jesus replied, Havent you read what David and his companions did when they were hungry?.
local: Peeɖe Yesu pɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɩtakalɩ mbʋ́ Daaviidi kalabɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ñɔɔsɩ wɩɣaɣ ɛ nɛ mbá paawɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ pʋ-tɔm naa we?.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorns grew with the plants and choked them.
local: Lɛɛna tɔlɩ kpaŋsɔɔ taa nɛ añɔ ɛzɩ aakpa camɩyɛ lɛ, kpaŋsɔɔ lɛɣtɩ.

eng: The curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ pɩsaʋ sɔsɔʋ ŋgʋ́ paakaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa yɔ, kɩkpaɣnɩ ñʋʋ taa faɣʋ nɛ kitii kɩkaɖɩnɩ pɩ-tɛɛ hɔɔlɩŋ naalɛ.

eng: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding region.
local: Mbʋ́ Yesu laba yɔ, pʋ-tɔm cɔɔ Yudee egeetiye tɩŋa taa nɛ ɖɩ-cɔlɔ hɔɔlɩŋ tɩŋa taa payɩ.

eng: He broke the Law by going into Gods house and eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests can eat. He also gave some of the bread to his companions. .
local: Ɛsʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ ɛtɔɔ tindine nɖɩ́ paaha Ɛsɔ yɔ. Ŋgʋ́, pɩfɛyɩ se nɔɔyʋ ɛtɔɔ-ɖɩ yem nɛ ɛtɛkɛ cɔjɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Daaviidi kpaɣ-ɖɩ nɛ ɛ nɛ ɛ-taabalaa pɔtɔɔ».

eng: Still other seed landed on good soil. When it grew, it produced one hundred times more grain than was scattered. As he said this, he called out, Everyone who has ears should pay attention. .
local: Pee lɛɛna ñɔtɔlɩ tɛtʋ kɩbandʋ taa, alɛ ñɔ nɛ apaɣlɩ tɩŋ nɛ paa ŋgʋ́ kʋlʋlɩ pee mɩnʋʋ.» Yesu tɛm-wɛ tuuu eduuye lɛ, eholini-wɛ se: «Weyi ɛwɛnɩ nɩŋgbaaŋ lɛ, ɛnɩɩ.».

eng: When the centurion, who stood facing Jesus, saw how he died, he said, This man was certainly Gods Son.
local: Room sɔɔja sɔsɔ weyi ɛɛsɩŋ Yesu ɛsɩndaa yɔ, ɛna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Yesu mabɩ kubuka nɛ ɛhɛɛ yɔ lɛ, ɛtɔ se: «Toovenim, ɛnɛ ɛɛkɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ!».

eng: Johns disciples informed him about all these things. John called two of his disciples.
local: Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa nɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ lakaɣ yɔ nɛ powolo peheyi pa-caa. Peeɖe ɛya pa-taa naalɛ.

eng: Then he said to them, The Human One is Lord of the Sabbath. .
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ sɩm mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se pala kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye yɔ.».

eng: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɔzɩ-ɩ se ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ ɖomaɣ eduuye ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-tɔbʋʋ.

eng: Some women were watching from a distance, including Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Salome.
local: Halaa nabɛyɛ kɛwɛ poliŋ nɛ pɔcɔŋna ɖɔɖɔ. Pa-taa lɛlaa yɔ: Yoosi nɛ Yakubu cikpelu po-ɖoo Maarɩɩ nɛ Magdala tɛtʋ taa Maarɩɩ nɛ Saloomee.

eng: and sent them to the Lord. They were to ask him, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Etiyi-wɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ cɔlɔ se pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ña lɛ Ñʋʋ Yaɖʋ weyi paayɔɔdɩ ɛ-tɔm se ɛkaɣ kɔm yɔ, yaa nɔɔyʋ wɛnɩ wayɩ?».

eng: On another Sabbath, Jesus entered a synagogue to teach. A man was there whose right hand was withered.
local: Kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ lɛɛkʋ wiye lɛ, Yesu sʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa nɛ ɛpazɩ Ɛsɔtɔm wɩlʋʋ. Puwiye lɛ, abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe, e-nesi kɩbanzɩ camaa.

eng: He said, You have been given the mysteries of Gods kingdom, but these mysteries come to everyone else in parables so that when they see, they cant see, and when they hear, they cant understand .
local: Peeɖe ecosi-wɛ se: «Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ tɔm wɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ. Ɛlɛ́ mɩ lɛ, ɛha-mɩ lɛɣtʋ se ɩnɩɩ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ. Piyele lɛlaa yɔ, panɩɣ-tʋ lɛ, tɩkɛnɩ-wɛ aduwa. Pɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́ se: ‹Pɔcɔnɩ camɩyɛ, ɛlɛ́, pataana nɛ pewelesi camɩyɛ, ɛlɛ́, pataanɩɩ pʋ-tɔbʋʋ.› ».

eng: When Jesus was in Galilee, these women had followed and supported him, along with many other women who had come to Jerusalem with him.
local: Halaa ḿbá patɩŋaɣnɩ Yesu alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛɛwɛ Galilee egeetiye taa yɔ nɛ pasɩŋnɩ-ɩ. Halaa lɛlaa sakɩyɛ mbá ɛ nɛ wɛ paawobi Yeruusalɛm yɔ, paawɛ peeɖe ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: When they reached Jesus, they said, John the Baptist sent us to you. He asks, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Patalɩ Yesu cɔlɔ lɛ, peheyi-i se: «Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ tiyi-ɖʋ se ɖɩkɔɔ ɖɩpɔzɩ-ŋ nɛ ɖɩna se ña lɛ weyi ɛ-tɔm paayɔɔdaa se ɛkaɣ kɔm yɔ yaa nɔɔyʋ wɛnɩ wayɩ?».

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees were watching him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath. They were looking for a reason to bring charges against him.
local: Paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma paawɛɛ nɛ pɔcɔŋnɩ Yesu se pana se ɛlakɩ nɛ nɔɔyʋ yɔɔ cɛ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye yaa ɛɛlakɩ? Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pacaɣ se pehiɣ ndʋ́ paawana nɛ pakpa-ɩ yɔ.

eng: The parable means this: The seed is Gods word.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Ɩɩnaɣ eduuye ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-tɔbʋʋ: tɔɔnaɣ pee ana, awɛ ɛzɩ Ɛsɔtɔm yɔ.

eng: In those days Caesar Augustus declared that everyone throughout the empire should be enrolled in the tax lists.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Ogustɩ ha nɔɔ se pakalɩ ɛyáa mbá ɛcɔŋnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ yɔ patɩŋa.

eng: Since it was late in the afternoon on Preparation Day, just before the Sabbath.
local: Kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se pɔñɔɔzɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ pʋcɔ kife kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ yɔ ki-wiye wɩsɩ tibi ɖʋʋ lɛ.

eng: Right then, Jesus healed many of their diseases, illnesses, and evil spirits, and he gave sight to a number of blind people.
local: Ŋgʋ́, alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ ɖeyi ɖeyi Yesu laba nɛ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kʋdɔmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ cɛ nɛ ɛɖɔɔnɩ aleewaa nɛ ekuli yʋlʋmaa sakɩyɛ ɛsɛ.

eng: Jesus knew their thoughts, so he said to the man with the withered hand, Get up and stand in front of everyone. He got up and stood there.
local: Yesu na pa-lɩmaɣza ḿbʋ́ lɛ, eheyi abalʋ weyi e-nesi camaa yɔ se: «Kʋyɩ ŋsɩŋ ɛyáa tɩŋa hɛkʋ taa cɩnɛ!» Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ kʋyaa ɛsɩŋ.

eng: This first enrollment occurred when Quirinius governed Syria.
local: Kɩkalʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩkɛnɩ kajalaɣ ñɩŋgʋ. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa ɖɔɖɔ Kiriiniyuusi cɔŋaɣnɩ Siirii egeetiye yɔɔ.

eng: Joseph from Arimathea dared to approach Pilate and ask for Jesus body.
local: Ariimatee tʋ weyi payaɣ se Yozɛɛfʋ, ɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma tɔm hʋyaa taa lɛlʋ nɛ ɛɛɖaŋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa ñaŋ-ɩ yɔ, ɛkaɖɩ nɛ ewolo Pilaatɩ cɔlɔ. Ewoba lɛ, etendi-i se yee etisiɣ yɔ, ɛcɛlɩ-ɩ Yesu nɛ ewona epi.

eng: Jesus said to the legal experts and Pharisees, Heres a question for you: Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it? .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Mɔnpɔzʋʋ-mɩ se ɖa-paɣtʋ taa lɛ, ɛbɛ pʋmʋnaa se pala kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye? Kɩbandʋ naa ɛzaɣ́tʋ? Paya ɛyáa ñʋʋ naa pakʋ-wɛ?».

eng: Then he replied to Johns disciples, Go, report to John what you have seen and heard. Those who were blind are able to see. Those who were crippled now walk. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. And good news is preached to the poor.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ecosi-wɛ se: «Iwolo iheyi-i mbʋ́ ɩnawa nɛ ndʋ́ ɩnɩwa yɔ: yʋlʋmaa naɣ nɛ tala tɩnaa ɖɔŋ camɩyɛ nɛ canasɩ yɔɔ cɛkɩ nɛ ndamaa nɩɣ nɛ sɩɖaa feŋ, posusuu kʋñɔndɩnaa Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: The seed on the path are those who hear, but then the devil comes and steals the word from their hearts so that they wont believe and be saved.
local: Ɛyáa nabɛyɛ wɛɛ, panɩɣ Ɛsɔtɔm ńdʋ́ lɛ, Satanɩ tɩŋ ɛhɔɔzɩ-tʋ pa-taa se pataakɔɔ petisi nɛ pehiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ. Ḿbá wɛnɩ ɛzɩ nʋmɔʋ nɔɔ ɖenɖe pee tɔlaa yɔ.

eng: Pilate wondered if Jesus was already dead. He called the centurion and asked him whether Jesus had already died.
local: Peeɖe pɩɖɩɣzɩ Pilaatɩ se Yesu tɛm sɩbʋ, ɛlɛ́ ɛ́yáa sɔɔja sɔsɔ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Yesu sɩbʋ yɔ, pɩtɛm leɖuu naa we?».

eng: Looking around at them all, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and his hand was made healthy.
local: Peeɖe ɛcɔnɩ patɩŋa pɛ-ɛsɩndaa kpɛɛɛ nɛ eheyi abalʋ ɛnʋ se: «Losi ñe-nesi!» Elosi-si lɛ, sɩ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: Everyone went to their own cities to be enrolled.
local: Peeɖe paa weyi ewokaɣ ɛ-tɛ se pama ɛ-hɩɖɛ.

eng: Happy is anyone who doesnt stumble along the way because of me. .
local: Weyi eekiziɣ mɔn-tɔm yɔ, ehiɣ ɛ-tɩ.».

eng: The seed on the rock are those who receive the word joyfully when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while but fall away when they are tempted.
local: Mbá paba wɛ ɛzɩ ɛwaɣ ŋgá kɔ-yɔɔ pee tɔlaa yɔ, panɩɣ-tʋ lɛ, petisi kpaagbaa nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ. Ɛlɛ́, paayeki se tɩlabɩ-wɛ tʋmɩyɛ camɩyɛ. Pɛwɛɣnɩ lidaʋ tɩ-yɔɔ alɩwaatʋ pazɩ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩtakɩɣ-wɛ lɛ, pɔlɔ-kʋ.

eng: They were furious and began talking with each other about what to do to Jesus.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pa-pana kpa pɩŋŋ nɛ pɔyɔɔdʋʋnɩ ɖama ɛzɩma mbʋ́ paala Yesu yɔ.

eng: When he learned from the centurion that Jesus was dead, Pilate gave the dead body to Joseph.
local: Sɔɔja ɛnʋ ecosi-i lɛ, ɛha Yozɛɛfʋ nʋmɔʋ se ewolo etisi-i.

eng: Since Joseph belonged to Davids house and family line, he went up from the city of Nazareth in Galilee to Davids city, called Bethlehem, in Judea.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Yozɛɛfʋ kʋyɩ Nazarɛɛtɩ, Galilee egeetiye taa se ewoki Bɛtlɛhɛm, Yudee egeetiye taa ɖenɖe paalʋlɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Daaviidi yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛkɛ Daaviidi liɖe taa tʋ.

eng: After Johns messengers were gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John. What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Peeɖe Yohanɛɛsɩ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ḿbá pɛɖɛwa nɛ Yesu pazɩ ɛyáa samaɣ kɛdʋʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ ɛnʋ ɛ-tɔm nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛbɛ iwobi naʋ lakʋ taa? Kañɩŋ́gbayaɣ́ ŋgá heɣlim ciɣdaɣ yɔ yaa we? Mantasa.

eng: As for the seed that fell among thorny plants, these are the ones who, as they go about their lives, are choked by the concerns, riches, and pleasures of life, and their fruit never matures.
local: Lɛlaa wɛ ɛzɩ kpaŋsɔɔ taa ɖenɖe pee tɔlaa yɔ. Pɛtɛŋ Ɛsɔtɔm nɩʋ lɛ, lɩmaɣza wena akʋʋ ɛyáa yɔ nɛ ñɩm nɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ wondu sɔɔlʋʋ paɖɩɣ-wɛ nʋmɔʋ nɛ paawɛɣnɩ kɛzɛɣa.

eng: During that time, Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and he prayed to God all night long.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa lɛ, Yesu kpa pʋʋ yɔɔ se ɛsɛɣ Ɛsɔ. Ɛsɛɛ-ɩ ḿbʋ́ tɛʋ nɛ kifem.

eng: He bought a linen cloth, took Jesus down from the cross, wrapped him in the cloth, and laid him in a tomb that had been carved out of rock. He rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ ya pɩsaʋ kɩfalʋʋ kʋhʋlʋmʋʋ nakʋyʋ nɛ etisi Yesu nɛ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɛhɔkɩ-ɩ kɩ-taa nɛ ewoni-i epi pɩlaʋ ŋgʋ́ pɛcɛsɩ ɛwaɣ taa yɔ kɩ-taa nɛ epilisi pɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ naɖɩyɛ nɛ ɛɖɩɣ kʋ-nɔɔ.

eng: He went to be enrolled together with Mary, who was promised to him in marriage and who was pregnant.
local: Ewokaɣ se pama ɛ nɛ e-kususaɣ Maarɩɩ weyi ɛhaɣ howa yɔ pa-yɩla.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who dress in fashionable clothes and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛbɛ iwobi naʋ kpem? Nɔɔyʋ suni wondu kɩbandʋ nɛ ɛwɛɛ peeɖe naa we? Ŋgʋ́, mbá posuu ñɩɣlɩ́m wondu nɛ pɛwɛnɩ pɩtɩŋa yɔ, pɛwɛɣ awiya ɖɩsɩ taa.

eng: The seed that fell on good soil are those who hear the word and commit themselves to it with a good and upright heart. Through their resolve, they bear fruit.
local: Lɛlaa ñɛwɛ ɛzɩ tɛtʋ kɩbandʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-taa pee tɔlaa yɔ. Pewelesiɣ Ɛsɔtɔm nɛ hɔyɛ kɩbanɖɛ nɛ pamʋ-tʋ nesi naalɛ nɛ pañaɣ pana pɔɖɔ tɩ-yɔɔ nɛ tɩwɛɛ-wɛ kɛzɛɣa.».

eng: One day Jesus was standing beside Lake Gennesaret when the crowd pressed in around him to hear Gods word.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu sɩŋ Jeneezaarɛɛtɩ teŋgu nɔɔ nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ ñɩkɩɣ ɖama ɛ-cɔlɔ se panɩɩ Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: At daybreak, he called together his disciples. He chose twelve of them whom he called apostles:.
local: Kifema lɛ, ɛ́yáa ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa nɛ ɛlɩzɩ pa-taa ɛyáa hiu nɛ naalɛ nɛ ɛ́yáa-wɛ se e-tiyiyaa.

eng: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was buried.
local: Yoosi nɛ Yakubu po-ɖoo Maarɩɩ nɛ Magdala Maarɩɩ paawɛɛ nɛ pɔcɔŋnɩ ɖenɖe pepiŋaɣ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: While they were there, the time came for Mary to have her baby.
local: Patalɩ Bɛtlɛhɛm nɛ pɛwɛɛ lɛ, pɩtalɩ se Maarɩɩ ɛlʋlɩ.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Ɛbɛ kpem iiwobi naʋ? Náyʋ nɔɔyʋ yaa we? Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɩna weyi yɔ, ɛkɩlɩ náyʋ.

eng: No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand so that those who enter can see the light.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Nɔɔyʋ ɛɛsɔkɩ kanɖʋ nɛ ɛpam-kʋ nabʋyʋ tɛɛ yaa ɛsʋzɩ-kʋ kaɖʋ tɛɛ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛsɩɣ-kʋ ɖenɖe pʋkʋyɩ camɩyɛ ñaba yɔ nɛ pɩna mbá patɩŋa pasʋʋ ɖɩɣa taa yɔ.

eng: Jesus saw two boats sitting by the lake. The fishermen had gone ashore and were washing their nets.
local: Peeɖe ɛna kpɩyɩŋ naalɛ sɩŋ kidiiliŋ yɔɔ nɛ piyiŋ lɔyaa tii nɛ pañalɩɣ pe-piyiŋ.

eng: Simon, whom he named Peter; his brother Andrew; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew;.
local: Ḿbá yɔ: Siimɔɔ weyi ɛkʋm-ɩ hɩɖɛ se Pɩyɛ yɔ nɛ e-neu Andree nɛ Yakubu nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ nɛ Filiipu nɛ Bartelemii.

eng: She gave birth to her firstborn child, a son, wrapped him snugly, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the guestroom.
local: Peeɖe ɛlʋlɩ ɛ-kajalaɣ pɩɣa, kɛkɛ abalɩbɩɣa. Ɛlʋlɩ-kɛ wondu kalakʋ taa nɛ ɛɖʋ-kɛ pɩsaʋ taa nɛ ɛhɛzɩ-kɛ ɖenɖe paɖʋʋ wondu tɔɔnaɣ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pataahiɣ ɖɩsʋyɛ agɔmaa ɖɩɣa taa.

eng: He is the one of whom its written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ ɛnʋ, ɛ-tɔm Ɛsɔ yɔɔdaa nɛ pama ɛ-Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Na! Mancalɩɣ men-tiyiyu tiyuu nɛ ɛñɔɔzɩ ña-nʋmɔʋ.› ».

eng: Matthew; Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called a zealot;.
local: nɛ Maatiyee nɛ Tomaa nɛ Alfee pɩyalʋ Yakubu nɛ Siimɔɔ weyi payaɣ se Yɔɔ Kandɩyʋ yɔ.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be exposed. Nor is anything concealed that wont be made known and brought to the light.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋwɛ ɛyáa mɛsaɣ tɛɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, pʋkɔŋ pʋ-yɔɔ kuluu nɛ paa weyi ɛna.».

eng: Jesus boarded one of the boats, the one that belonged to Simon, then asked him to row out a little distance from the shore. Jesus sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu kpa lɛɛkʋ taa, Siimɔɔ tɩnɩ-kʋ. Ɛkpawa lɛ, eheyi-i se etuzi-kʋ lɩm taa pazɩ. Peeɖe ɛcaɣaɣ nɛ ɛpazɩ ɛyáa samaɣ Ɛsɔtɔm wɩlʋʋ.

eng: Nearby shepherds were living in the fields, guarding their sheep at night.
local: Ninɖiɣyaa nabɛyɛ kɛwɛ hɔɔlɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-taa. Paawɛ tɛyɛ yɔɔ ɖoó ana a-taa nɛ paɖaŋ pe-heŋ.

eng: I tell you that no greater human being has ever been born than John. Yet whoever is least in Gods kingdom is greater than he. .
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se ɖooo yɔ, patalʋlɩdɩ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɛkɩlɩ Yohanɛɛsɩ ɛnʋ. Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, weyi ɛwɛ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa nɛ patakpaɣ-ɩ payaa pʋyʋ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ kɩlɩ-ɩ.

eng: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
local: nɛ Yakubu pɩyalʋ Yudaasɩ nɛ Yudaasɩ Iskarɩyɔɔtɩ. Ɛnʋ kitini ɛkpa Yesu nɛ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ koyindinaa nesi taa.

eng: Therefore, listen carefully. Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they seem to have will be taken away from them. .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩnɩɣ Ɛsɔtɔm yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pɔtɔŋʋʋ se: «Weyi ɛwɛna, pɔsɔzɩ-ɩ nɛ weyi ɛ́lɛ́ fɛyɩna yɔ, halɩ pɛtɛzɩɣ lɛɣʋ mbʋ́ ɛmaɣzɩɣ se ɛwɛna yɔ.».

eng: When he finished speaking to the crowds, he said to Simon, Row out farther, into the deep water, and drop your nets for a catch. .
local: Ɛtɛma lɛ, eheyi Siimɔɔ se: «Tuzi kpɩyʋʋ nɛ ŋwoni-kʋ ɖenɖe lɩm wɛ cukaŋ yɔ nɛ ña nɛ ña-taabalaa ɩlɔ mi-piyiŋ nɛ ɩkpa kpakpasɩ.».

eng: The Lords angel stood before them, the Lords glory shone around them, and they were terrified.
local: Peeɖe Kɩpaɣlʋ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ lɩ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ ñalɩmɩyɛ ñalɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ sɔɔndʋ kpa-wɛ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ.

eng: Everyone who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged Gods justice because they had been baptized by John.
local: Izrɛɛlɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa ɛyáa tɩŋa welesaɣ Yohanɛɛsɩ tɔm, halɩ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ tikee mʋyaa maɣmaɣ. Pʋ-yɔɔ petisaa nɛ palabɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ. Palabɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pɩwɩlɩ se Ɛsɔ tʋʋzaa.

eng: Jesus came down from the mountain with them and stood on a large area of level ground. A great company of his disciples and a huge crowd of people from all around Judea and Jerusalem and the area around Tyre and Sidon joined him there.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa petiki pʋʋ nɛ patalɩ pasɩŋ kɩ-lalayɛ taa ɖenɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa sakɩyɛ kɛwɛɛ yɔ. Ɛyáa samaɣ ŋgá kalɩnɩ Yudee egeetiye tɩŋa payɩ taa nɛ Yeruusalɛm nɛ tɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩwɛ teŋgu nɔɔ: Tiiri nɛ Sidɔɔ pa-taa yɔ, paawɛ peeɖe ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Jesus mother and brothers came to him but were unable to reach him because of the crowd.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu newaa nɛ e-ɖoo powolo se pana-ɩ. Ɛyáa samaɣ yɔɔ lɛ, patapɩzɩ patalɩ ɛ-cɔlɔ.

eng: Simon replied, Master, weve worked hard all night and caught nothing. But because you say so, Ill drop the nets.
local: Siimɔɔ cosi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ, ɖɩtɛm lɔʋ piyiŋ tɛʋ nɛ kifem nɛ ɖɩwɩɩ, ɖɩtakpa pʋyʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ñɔ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩɖaɣnɩɣ-ɩ lɔʋ.».

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid! Look! I bring good news to you—wonderful, joyous news for all people.
local: Ɛlɛ́, tiyiyu ɛnʋ eheyi-wɛ se: «Sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-mɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɔnkɔm se meheyi-mɩ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ tɔm natʋyʋ. Tɩkɛdɩɣnɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ.».

eng: But the Pharisees and legal experts rejected Gods will for themselves because they hadnt been baptized by John.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Farɩsɩ ñɩma nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa pekizi se ɛtaalabɩ-wɛ-sɩ. Ḿbʋ́ pawɩlɩnaa se pekizi mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ñɩnaɣnɩ-wɛ yɔ.».

eng: They came to hear him and to be healed from their diseases, and those bothered by unclean spirits were healed.
local: Paawobi se panɩɩ tɔm ndʋ́ ɛwɩlɩɣ yɔ nɛ ɛla nɛ pa-kʋdɔmɩŋ cɛ. Mbá aleewaa pɩsaɣ-wɛ kpɛndɛɣlɩsɩ yɔ, pɔ-yɔɔ cɛba ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Someone told him, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.
local: Peeɖe nɔɔyʋ heyi-i se: «Ño-ɖoo nɛ ñe-newaa pɛwɛ awayɩ nɛ pañɩnɩɣ se pana-ŋ.».

eng: Many people have already applied themselves to the task of compiling an account of the events that have been fulfilled among us.
local: Ɛyáa nabɛyɛ na mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ laba ɖɛ-tɛ yɔ ɖooo kajalaɣ taa. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, posusi Ɛsɔtɔm. Pʋbʋ wayɩ lɛ, pɛkɛdɩ-ɖʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ palaba yɔ nɛ ɖa-taa ɛyáa sakɩyɛ pazɩ-tʋ maʋ.

eng: So they dropped the nets and their catch was so huge that their nets were splitting.
local: Pɔlɔ-ɩ lɛ, pakpa kpakpasɩ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ, halɩ pe-piyiŋ caɣ cɛbʋ.

eng: Your savior is born today in Davids city. He is Christ the Lord.
local: Ńdʋ́ lɛ se palʋlɩ-mɩ Ñʋʋ Yaɖʋ ɖoó anɛ a-taa Daaviidi tɛ Bɛtlɛhɛm. Ɛnʋ lɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ, ɛnʋ lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ.

eng: To what will I compare the people of this generation? Jesus asked. What are they like?.
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ se: «Ɛbɛ mɛnkɛdɩɣnɩ pɩtɛ ɛyáa ḿbá lee? Ɛzɩma pɛwɛɛ?.

eng: The whole crowd wanted to touch him, because power was going out from him and he was healing everyone.
local: Paa weyi ɛñɩnaɣ se etukuni-i. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖoŋ nɩɩyɩ lɩɣaɣ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛlakɩ nɛ patɩŋa payɩ pa-kʋdɔmɩŋ cɛkɩ.

eng: He replied, My mother and brothers are those who listen to Gods word and do it. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi mbá pɛwɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ se: «Mbá panɩɣ Ɛsɔtɔm nɛ pɔɖɔŋ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, ḿbá lɛ mon-ɖoonaa nɛ me-newaa.».

eng: Now, after having investigated everything carefully from the beginning, I have also decided to write a carefully ordered account for you, most honorable Theophilus.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ma ɖɔɖɔ mañɩnaa nɛ mana mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩlaba ɖooo kiɖe tɛɛ ɖeyi ɖeyi yɔ lɛ, mana se ña ɖa-caa Tewofiili lɛ, pʋmʋnaa se mama-tʋ ti-lona taa nɛ mɛnkɛdɩ-ŋ.

eng: They signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. They filled both boats so full that they were about to sink.
local: Peeɖe pahadɩ nesi pa-taabalaa piyiŋ lɔyaa lɛlaa kpɩyʋʋ lɛɛkʋ taa se powolo pasɩnɩ-wɛ. Powoba lɛ, palɩzɩ kpakpasɩ nɛ paɖʋ kpɩyɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-naalɛ ɩ-taa nɛ piwiziɣ-i nɛ ɩcaɣ se ɩsʋʋ lɩm tɛɛ.

eng: This is a sign for you: you will find a newborn baby wrapped snugly and lying in a manger.
local: Ɩɩnaɣ mbʋ́ pɩwɩlɩɣ-mɩ nɛ ɩtɩlɩ yɔ: ɩtalɩɣ lɛ, paɖʋ pɩɣa pɩcɩɩcɩyɩm ɛnʋ pɩsaʋ taa nɛ pɛhɛzɩ-kɛ ɖenɖe paɖʋʋ wondu tɔɔnaɣ yɔ.

eng: They are like children sitting in the marketplace calling out to each other, We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt cry.
local: Pɛwɛ ɛzɩ piya nzɩ́ sɩcaɣ ɛyáa samaɣ ɖikpeɣliye nɛ sikou ɖama yɔɔ se: ‹Đɩhʋlɩ-mɩ hɩsɩ se ɩpaa nɛ ikizi. Đitee kʋñɔŋ hendu nɛ itewii.›.

eng: Jesus raised his eyes to his disciples and said: Happy are you who are poor, because Gods kingdom is yours.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu cɔnɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Mbá mɩ ɩkɛ kʋñɔndɩnaa yɔ, ihiɣ mɩ-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩkɛ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa ñɩma.

eng: One day Jesus and his disciples boarded a boat. He said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake. So they set sail.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pakpa kpɩyʋʋ taa. Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Đɩtɛzɩ teŋgu kidiiliŋ lɛɛkʋ yɔɔ.» Ɛlɛ́, pakʋyɩ se pɛɖɛɣ.

eng: I want you to have confidence in the soundness of the instruction you have received.
local: Malakɩ ḿbʋ́ se ŋpɩzɩ ŋtɩlɩ se tɔm ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ pawɩlɩ-ŋ yɔ, tɩkɛ toovenim.

eng: When Simon Peter saw the catch, he fell at Jesus knees and said, Leave me, Lord, for Im a sinner.
local: Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ na ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ewolo eluŋ Yesu tɛɛ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Taakemna-m, mɛnkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖʋ.».

eng: Suddenly a great assembly of the heavenly forces was with the angel praising God. They said.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa samaɣ tii pɔsɔzɩ tiyiyu ɛnʋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ pasaŋ Ɛsɔ nɛ hendu se:.

eng: John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, He has a demon.
local: Ḿbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ: ‹Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ kɔma, ɛɛtɔkɩ tɔɔnaɣ, ɛɛñɔʋ sʋlʋm. Ɛlɛ́, ɩtɔŋ se ekem aleewaa.›.

eng: Happy are you who hunger now, because you will be satisfied. Happy are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
local: Mbá mɩ ñɔɔsɩ wɩɣ-mɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, ihiɣ mɩ-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩkɔŋ haɣʋ! Mbá mɩ iwiɣ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, ihiɣ mɩ-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩkɔŋ woŋuu.

eng: While they were sailing, he fell asleep. Gale-force winds swept down on the lake. The boat was filling up with water and they were in danger.
local: Pɛwɛ lɩm yɔɔ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɖom kpa Yesu nɛ ɛhɛnɩ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, heɣlim sɔsɔm nabʋyʋ pazɩ mabʋ nɛ lɩm sʋʋ kpɩyʋʋ taa nɛ pɩcaɣ se pɩlabɩ-wɛ asɛyʋʋ.

eng: During the rule of King Herod of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Eroodi kɛ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Yudee egeetiye taa yɔ, cɔjɔ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛɛ nɛ payaɣ-ɩ se Zakaarii. Ɛɛkɛ Abiya hɔʋ taa tʋ lɛlʋ. Payaɣ ɛ-halʋ se Elizaabɛɛtɩ, ɛ́lɛ́ ñɛkɛ Aarɔɔnɩ liɖe taa tʋ.

eng: Peter and those with him were overcome with amazement because of the number of fish they caught.
local: Toovenim lɛ se sɔɔndʋ kpa Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ nɛ mbá patɩŋa paawɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kpakpasɩ nzɩ́ pakpawa yɔ, sɩɖɔ siŋŋ.

eng: Glory to God in heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors.
local: «Pasa Ɛsɔ ɖooo ɛsɔdaa nɛ ɛ-laŋhɛzɩyɛ wɛɛ tataa ɛyáa mbá ɛsɔɔlɩ-wɛ yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ.».

eng: Yet the Human One came eating and drinking, and you say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.
local: Piyele Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ ñɔkɔma nɛ ɛtɔkɩ nɛ ɛñɔʋ lɛ, ɩɖaɣnɩɣ yɔɔdʋʋ se: ‹Ɩnaɣʋ ɛyʋ ɛnɛ yɔ, tɔkɩyɛ nɛ ñɔtʋ pɔ-yɔɔ ɖeke ɛmaɣzɩɣ. Ɛkɛ ɖɔɖɔ tikee mʋyaa nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa lɛlaa pa-taabalʋ.›.

eng: Happy are you when people hate you, reject you, insult you, and condemn your name as evil because of the Human One.
local: Yee ɛyáa ɛpaɖɩɣ-mɩ yaa, pekiziɣ-mɩ yaa, patʋʋ-mɩ yaa, pɛwɛɛkɩɣ mɩ-hɩla Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ yɔɔ yɔ, ihiɣ mɩ-tɩ!.

eng: So they went and woke Jesus, shouting, Master, Master, were going to drown! But he got up and gave orders to the wind and the violent waves. The storm died down and it was calm.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa woba ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ pefezi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ, Wɩlɩyʋ, ɖɩsɩkɩ!» Ɛlɛ́, Yesu kʋyɩ nɛ eholini heɣlim nɛ lɩm nɛ pɩtɩŋa pɩhɛɛ kpaagbaa niɣ niɣ.

eng: After these things, the Lord commissioned seventy-two others and sent them on ahead in pairs to every city and place he was about to go.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ tasɩ lɩzʋʋ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa ɛyáa nɩɩnʋwa nɛ nɩɩlɛ nɛ naalɛ nɛ etiyi-wɛ naalɛ naalɛ se pɛɖɛɛ-ɩ nɔɔ nɛ powolo tɛtʋ ndɩ ndɩ nɛ hɔɔlɩŋ weyi ɩ-taa ɛɖʋwa se ewoki yɔ pa-taa.

eng: They were both righteous before God, blameless in their observance of all the Lords commandments and regulations.
local: Pe-wezuu caɣʋ wɛ ɖeu Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa nɛ pɔɖɔŋ Kɩpaɣlʋ paɣtʋ tɩŋa yɔɔ camɩyɛ, natʋyʋ fɛyɩ se paawanɩ-wɛ.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, were Simons partners and they were amazed too. Jesus said to Simon, Dont be afraid. From now on, you will be fishing for people. .
local: Sɔɔndʋ kakpa ɖɔɖɔ Zebedee pɩyalaa: Yakubu nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ. Patɩŋaɣ Siimɔɔ wayɩ. Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi Siimɔɔ ɛnʋ se: «Taasɔɔndɩ! Pɩkpaɣʋnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, ɛyáa ŋkaɣ kpaʋ.».

eng: When the angels returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, Lets go right now to Bethlehem and see whats happened. Lets confirm what the Lord has revealed to us.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa kʋyɩ ninɖiɣyaa cɔlɔ nɛ papɩsɩ ɛsɔdaa. Ɛlɛ́, ninɖiɣyaa ḿbá pɔyɔɔdɩnɩ ɖama se: «Đɩkʋyɩ ɖiwolo Bɛtlɛhɛm peeɖe nɛ ɖɩna mbʋ́ pɩlaba nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ tiyiɣ se peheyi-ɖʋ yɔ.».

eng: But wisdom is proved to be right by all her descendants. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, mbá patɩŋa palakɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ yɔ, pa-lakasɩ wɩlɩɣnɩ se Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ, ɛwɛnɩ lɔŋ siŋŋ.».

eng: Rejoice when that happens! Leap for joy because you have a great reward in heaven. Their ancestors did the same things to the prophets.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩtalaa lɛ, mɩ-laŋa ɩhʋlʋmɩ nɛ ɩmayɩ amaya siŋŋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩ-kɩhɛyʋʋ ɖɔ ɛsɔdaa! Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pɔ-cɔzɔnaa kalabɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩm náyaa mbá Ɛsɔ tiyi-wɛ yɔ.

eng: He said to his disciples, Where is your faith? Filled with awe and wonder, they said to each other, Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him.
local: nɛ ɛpɔzɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ɩwɛnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, kɩwɛ le?» Peeɖe payɩɣyɩ-ɩ nɛ sɔɔndʋ kpa-wɛ nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ pa-tɩ se: «Ɛbɛ ɛnɛ ɛkɛnaa kpem? Halɩ ɛkalɩɣ heɣlim nɛ lɩm nɛ pɩñaŋ-ɩ!».

eng: Jesus called the Twelve together and he gave them power and authority over all demons and to heal sicknesses.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu kpeɣli ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ nɛ ɛha-wɛ ɖoŋ nɛ abalɩtʋ se pɔɖɔɔnɩnɩ aleewaa tɩŋa nɛ pala nɛ kʋdɔndɩnaa yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: He said to them, The harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers. Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Kʋmtʋ ɖɔwa, ɛlɛ́, kʋnɖaa tɔɖɔɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, itendi tɔɔnaɣ ŋ́gá ka-tʋ nɛ ɛsɔzɩ kʋnɖaa nɛ pasɩnɩ-mɩ kʋm.

eng: They had no children because Elizabeth was unable to become pregnant and they both were very old.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɛfɛyɩnɩ pɩɣa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Elizaabɛɛtɩ kɛ kaalʋlaɣ, ŋgʋ́ pa-naalɛ pakpadɩyɩ pɩŋŋ.

eng: As soon as they brought the boats to the shore, they left everything and followed Jesus.
local: Peeɖe palɩzɩ pa-kpɩyɩŋ kidiiliŋ yɔɔ nɛ peyele mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ paawɛna yɔ nɛ patɩŋ Yesu wayɩ.

eng: They went quickly and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
local: Peeɖe pakpaɣ seya nɛ powolo pamaɣnɩ Maarɩɩ nɛ Yozɛɛfʋ nɛ pɩɣa pɩcɩɩcɩyɩm ɛnʋ. Pɛhɛzɩ-ɩ ɖenɖe paɖʋʋ wondu tɔɔnaɣ yɔ.

eng: One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him. After he entered the Pharisees home, he took his place at the table.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Farɩsɩ tʋ nɔɔyʋ ya Yesu se ewolo ɛ-tɛ nɛ pɔtɔɔ tɔɔnaɣ. Ɛlɛ́, Yesu wolo nɛ ɛcaɣ tɔɔnaɣ ɖɩtɔɔyɛ.

eng: But how terrible for you who are rich, because you have already received your comfort.
local: Mbá mɩ ɩwɛnɩ ñɩm yɔ, pʋtɔɔ-mɩ, Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtɛm mɩ-tɩ hiɣu!.

eng: Jesus and his disciples sailed to the Gerasenes land, which is across the lake from Galilee.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, patalɩnɩ pa-kpɩyʋʋ Geraazaa ɛjaɖɛ taa. Đɩwɛ teŋgu kidiiliŋ lɛɛkʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩha ɛsɛ nɛ Galilee egeetiye.

eng: He sent them out to proclaim Gods kingdom and to heal the sick.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, etiyi-wɛ se powolo posusi Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ tɔm nɛ pala nɛ kʋdɔndɩnaa yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: Go! Be warned, though, that Im sending you out as lambs among wolves.
local: Iwolo. Mentiyuu-mɩ yɔ, ɩwɛ ɛzɩ heŋ wɛɣʋ kpemtɔɔyɩsɩ hɛkʋ taa yɔ.

eng: One day Zechariah was serving as a priest before God because his priestly division was on duty.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye lɛ, pɩtalɩnɩ Zakaarii hɔʋ taa ñɩma se pala tʋmɩyɛ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa nɛ Zakaarii wɛɛ ɛlakɩ ɛ-cɔjɔtʋ tʋmɩyɛ.

eng: Jesus was in one of the towns where there was also a man covered with a skin disease. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu woba tɛtʋ natʋyʋ taa. Kʋdɔŋ kɩsɛmʋʋ kɔɖɔkɩ abalʋ nɔɔyʋ tɩ-taa peeɖe siŋŋ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛna Yesu nɛ ewolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ eluŋ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa nɛ etendi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Yee ŋtisiɣ yɔ, ŋla nɛ mɔ-yɔɔ cɛ nɛ manpɩsɩ ɛyʋ weyi ɛmʋnaa yɔ.».

eng: When they saw this, they reported what they had been told about this child.
local: Pana-ɩ lɛ, papazɩ kɛdʋʋ ɛ-tɔm ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu keheyi-wɛ yɔ.

eng: Meanwhile, a woman from the city, a sinner, discovered that Jesus was dining in the Pharisees house. She brought perfumed oil in a vase made of alabaster.
local: Halʋ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa nɛ ɛnɩɩ ḿbʋ́. Peeɖe ɛkpaɣ tɩlaaɖɩ fiɣa kɩñɩlɩsaɣ nakɛyɛ taa ɖo.

eng: How terrible for you who have plenty now, because you will be hungry. How terrible for you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.
local: Pʋtɔɔ-mɩ! Mbá mɩ ɩwɛnɩ pɩtɩŋa pɩtɩŋa lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ñɔɔsɩ kaɣ-mɩ wɩʋ! Mbá mɩ iwoŋuu lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, pʋtɔɔ-mɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩ-laŋa kaɣ wɛɛkʋʋ nɛ iwili!.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a certain man met him. The man was from the city and was possessed by demons. For a long time, he had lived among the tombs, naked and homeless.
local: Pasɩŋaa nɛ Yesu tiki lɛ, tɩ-taa abalʋ nɔɔyʋ wolo se ɛkatɩɣ-ɩ. Abalʋ ɛnʋ, aleewaa wɛ ɛ-wayɩ ɖooo lɔŋ nɛ pɔcɔzʋʋ-ɩ. Ɛtaasuu wondu, ɛɛtasɩɣ ɖɩɣa sʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩlaŋ taa ɛsʋwaɣ.

eng: He told them, Take nothing for the journey—no walking stick, no bag, no bread, no money, not even an extra shirt.
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩɖɛɣ lɛ, ɩtaakpaɣ pʋyʋ: paa ɖaʋ yaa huyuu yaa tɔɔnaɣ yaa liidiye. Nɔɔyʋ ɛtaakpaɣ tokonaa naalɛ.

eng: Carry no wallet, no bag, and no sandals. Dont even greet anyone along the way.
local: Ɩɖɛɣ lɛ, ɩtaakpaɣ liidiye yaa huyuu yaa naataŋgbala. Ɩtaasɩŋ nʋmɔʋ taa se ɩsɛɣ nɔɔyʋ.

eng: Following the customs of priestly service, he was chosen by lottery to go into the Lords sanctuary and burn incense.
local: Peeɖe paɖʋ miika nɛ palɩzɩ-ɩ ɛzɩ palakʋʋ Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa sɔnzɩ taa yɔ se ɛsʋʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ ɖɩɣa taa lone kaɖɛ ñɩnɖɛ taa nɛ ɛɖʋ tɩlaaɖɩ miŋ taa.

eng: Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said, Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu sɛɣaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖoli nɔɔyʋ. Ɛtɛma lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa nɔɔyʋ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Wɩlɩ-ɖʋ Ɛsɔ sɛʋ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ Yohanɛɛsɩ wɩlʋʋ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa yɔ.».
