eng: In this way, you testify that you approve of your ancestors deeds. They killed the prophets, and you build memorials!.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɩlakɩ nɛ pɩwɩlɩɣ se mɩ-cɔzɔnaa lakasɩ kɛdɩnɩ-mɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paba kʋ náyaa nɛ mɩ mɩcanɩɣ pa-pɩlaŋ.

eng: But Jesus replied, Dont stop him, because whoever isnt against you is for you. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-i se: «Ɩtaakizini-i, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛɛkʋyʋʋ mɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛkɛ mɩ-ñɩnʋ.».

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did. .
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɛ́, yee ɩtɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ yɔ, ɩkɔŋ sɩbʋ mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛzɩ ḿbá yɔ.».

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Watch out! Guard yourself against all kinds of greed. After all, ones life isnt determined by ones possessions, even when someone is very wealthy. .
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, eheyi patɩŋa se: «Ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ nɛ ñɩm laɣzaɣ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paa ɛyʋ ɛwɛnɩ ñɩm ɛzɩma yɔ, e-wezuu caɣʋ ɛɛlɩɣnɩ pʋ-cɔlɔ.».

eng: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and then returned to her home.
local: Ɛtɛm yɔɔdʋʋ lɛ, ɛcaɣ Elizaabɛɛtɩ tɛ ɛzɩ fenasɩ naadozo yɔ nɛ pʋcɔ ɛpɩsɩ ekpe.

eng: Therefore, Gods wisdom has said, I will send prophets and apostles to them and they will harass and kill some of them.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ yɔɔdɩ mɩ-tɔm nɛ lɔŋsɩnɖɛ se: ‹Mentiyiɣni-wɛ ɛyáa nabɛyɛ nɛ náyaa. Ɛlɛ́, pakʋʋ pa-taa lɛlaa nɛ pɛwɛɛ lɛlaa wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ.›.

eng: As the time approached when Jesus was to be taken up into heaven, he determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Alɩwaatʋ wɩzɩ talʋʋ se Yesu ɛkpa ɛsɔdaa. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛɖʋwa kpem se ewoki Yeruusalɛm.

eng: Jesus told this parable: A man owned a fig tree planted in his vineyard. He came looking for fruit on it and found none.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tuu-wɛ eduuye se: «Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ hayɩnɩ tɩŋ weyi popuu i-pee sʋlʋm yɔ nɛ ɛsɔ hiyuú ɩ-taa. Nakʋyʋ wiye lɛ, ewolo ki-pee koyuu. Ɛlɛ́, kɩtatɩɩlʋlɩ paa piye.

eng: Then he told them a parable: A certain rich mans land produced a bountiful crop.
local: Peeɖe etuu-wɛ eduuye se: «Ñɩm tʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛnɩ hayɩm nɛ pɩlakɩ tɔɔnaɣ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ.

eng: When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a boy.
local: Elizaabɛɛtɩ fenaɣ talaa lɛ, ɛlʋlɩ abalɩbɩɣa.

eng: As a result, this generation will be charged with the murder of all the prophets since the beginning of time.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, paɖʋʋ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɛyáa mɩ kʋñɔŋ náyaa mbá mɩ-cɔzɔnaa kʋ patɩŋa kpaɣ ɖooo alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ labɩ kedeŋa yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: He sent messengers on ahead of him. Along the way, they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.
local: Peeɖe etiyi nabɛyɛ se pɛɖɛɛ-ɩ nɔɔ nɛ powolo Samaarɩɩ egeetiye taa nɛ pɔñɔɔzɩ-ɩ mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ-ɩ yɔ pɩtɩŋa. Patalaa lɛ, pasʋʋ tɛtʋ cikpetu natʋyʋ taa se pala ḿbʋ́.

eng: He said to his gardener, Look, Ive come looking for fruit on this fig tree for the past three years, and Ive never found any. Cut it down! Why should it continue depleting the soils nutrients.
local: Peeɖe eheyi hayɩm ḿbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ cɔnɩyʋ se: ‹Cɔnɩ ŋna, pɩlabɩ pɩnzɩ naadozo yɔ, mɔnkɔŋ se monkoyuu pee lɛ, maanaɣ pʋyʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, sɛtɩ-kʋ. Ɛbɛ yɔɔ kʋwɛɛkɩɣ tɛtʋ yem.›.

eng: He said to himself, What will I do? I have no place to store my harvest!.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛcaɣ nɛ ɛmaɣzɩ ɛ-taa se: ‹Mala ɛzɩma lee? Kpoŋ ɛɛtalɩɣ-m se monsuu mɔn-tɔɔnaɣ tɩŋa.›.

eng: Her neighbors and relatives celebrated with her because they had heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy.
local: nɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ ñɩma nɛ e-liɖe taa ñɩma panɩɩ se Kɩpaɣlʋ na ɛ-pʋtɔdɩyɛ siŋŋ. Peeɖe pa nɛ ɩ pɛkpɛndaa pamayɩ amaya.

eng: This includes the murder of every prophet—from Abel to Zechariah—who was killed between the altar and the holy place. Yes, Im telling you, this generation will be charged with it.
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se: Ɛsɔ ɖʋʋ-mɩ kʋñɔŋ náyaa mbá pakʋ-wɛ kpaɣ ɖooo pakʋʋ Aabɛɛlɩ nɛ puwolo pɩsɩɩnɩ Zakaarii weyi pakʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa kadaɣ taa lone kaɖɛ ñɩnɖɛ nɛ kɩlaŋ ɖɩlayɛ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: but the Samaritan villagers refused to welcome him because he was determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Ɛlɛ́, peeɖe ñɩma kizi se paamʋʋ-ɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛɖɛɣaɣnɩ Yeruusalɛm kiŋ.

eng: The gardener responded, Lord, give it one more year, and I will dig around it and give it fertilizer.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: ‹Wɩlɩyʋ! Tasɩ-kʋ yebu pɩnaɣ kanɛ nɛ mohuyi kɩ-tɛɛ nɛ manɖʋ huɖe nɛ mana.

eng: Then he thought, Heres what Ill do. Ill tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Thats where Ill store all my grain and goods.
local: Peeɖe ɛtɔm se: ‹Mana mbʋ́ malakɩ yɔ. Mɔyɔkʋʋ mon-kpoŋ ɩnɛ nɛ manɖaɣnɩ maʋ sɔsɔŋ nɛ monsuu mɔn-tɔɔnaɣ tɩŋa nɛ ma-ñɩm lɛɛbʋ tɩŋa.

eng: On the eighth day, it came time to circumcise the child. They wanted to name him Zechariah because that was his fathers name.
local: Pɩɣa ŋgá kalabɩ kɩyakɩŋ lutozo lɛ, powolo se pɛpɛlɩɣ-kɛ nɛ pɔtɔ se payaa-kɛ ka-caa hɩɖɛ se Zakaarii.

eng: How terrible for you legal experts! You snatched away the key of knowledge. You didnt enter yourselves, and you stood in the way of those who were entering. .
local: Paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa mɩ, pʋtɔɔ-mɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩkpɛ nɔnɔɔ ŋgá patɩŋɩɣna nɛ patɩlɩ Ɛsɔ yɔ ka-ñɩmɩyɛ. Ŋgʋ́, mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩɩsʋʋ, ɛlɛ́ ɩɖɩkɩ mbá pe-liu wɛɛ se pasʋʋ yɔ nʋmɔʋ ɖɔɖɔ.».

eng: When the disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to consume them.
local: Ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa Yakubu nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ panɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Yaa ɖiyele nɛ miŋ nɩɩyɩ tibini ɛsɔdaa nɛ enezi-wɛ?».

eng: Maybe it will produce fruit next year; if not, then you can cut it down. .
local: Pʋtɔmaɣ yɔ, cee wayɩ kʋlʋlʋʋ. Yee kɩtalʋlɩ lɛ, ŋsɛtɩ-kʋ.› ».

eng: Ill say to myself, You have stored up plenty of goods, enough for several years. Take it easy! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, meheyi man-tɩ se: lɛɛlɛɛyɔ mɛwɛnɩ ñɩm pɩŋŋ, pɩtalɩɣ-m pɩnzɩ sakɩyɛ. Mancakɩ mɛhɛzɩ nɛ mɔntɔɔ nɛ mɔñɔɔ nɛ mamayɩ amaya.›.

eng: But his mother replied, No, his name will be John.
local: Peeɖe ko-ɖoo cosaa se: «Aayɩ, payaa-kɛ se Yohanɛɛsɩ.».

eng: As he left there, the legal experts and Pharisees began to resent him deeply and to ask him pointed questions about many things.
local: Yesu kʋyɩ peeɖe se ɛɖɛɣ lɛ, paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma pa-pana kpanɩ-ɩ pɩŋŋ nɛ pasɩŋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ tɔm ndɩ ndɩ pɔzʋʋ.

eng: But Jesus turned and spoke sternly to them.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu kɛzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛkalɩ-wɛ .

eng: Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
local: Kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu wɩlaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ naɖɩyɛ taa.

eng: But God said to him, Fool, tonight you will die. Now who will get the things you have prepared for yourself.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ heyi-i se: ‹Kɩmɛlɛŋ ña! Sɔnɔ ɖoó anɛ a-taa maɣmaɣ ŋsɩkɩ. Ɛlɛ́, akaɣ kpaɣʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa ŋɖaɣnaa nɛ ŋsɩɩ ña-tɩ yɔ?› ».

eng: They said to her, None of your relatives have that name.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pocosi-i se: «Patayaa ñe-liɖe taa tʋ nɔɔyʋ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: They plotted against him, trying to trap him in his words.
local: Pañɩkaɣ ɛ-nɔɔ cɔlɔ tɔm se yee etocosi natʋyʋ camɩyɛ lɛ, pehiɣ-i.

eng: and they went on to another village.
local: Peeɖe pakʋyaa se pɛɖɛɣ lɛɛtʋ taa.

eng: A woman was there who had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and couldnt stand up straight.
local: Halʋ kʋdɔndʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe. Eleeu nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ ɛ-wayɩ pɩnzɩ hiu nɛ lutozo yɔ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ-ɩ siɣ́ye tʋ. Ɛtaapɩzɩɣ kpem se ɛsɩŋ yiɣ yiɣ.

eng: This is the way it will be for those who hoard things for themselves and arent rich toward God. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛnɩ weyi ɛcazɩɣ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ñɩm nɛ ɛsɩɣ ɛ-tɩ yɔ. Ɛtɛkɛ ñɩm tʋ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa.».

eng: Then they began gesturing to his father to see what he wanted to call him.
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ ka-caa Zakaarii nɛ nesi wɩlʋʋ taa se e-liu wɛɛ se payaa ɛ-pɩɣa se we?.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples traveled along the road, someone said to him, I will follow you wherever you go.
local: Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, nɔɔyʋ heyi-i se: «Paa le ŋwoki yɔ, mantɩŋɩɣ-ŋ.».

eng: When he saw her, Jesus called her to him and said, Woman, you are set free from your sickness. .
local: Yesu na halʋ ɛnʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ́yáa-ɩ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Halʋ ña! Ña-kʋdɔŋ cɛba.».

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu heyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Pʋ-yɔɔ meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɩtaanɩɣzɩ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá ɩɩtɔɔ yaa wondu ndʋ́ iisuu mi-wezuu caɣʋ taa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: After asking for a tablet, he surprised everyone by writing, His name is John.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Zakaarii wɩlɩ se powoni-i maʋ kpata. Powonaa lɛ, ɛma kʋ-yɔɔ se: «Ka-hɩɖɛ lɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ.» Peeɖe pɩɖɩɣzɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human One has no place to lay his head. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-i se: «Pudanaa wɛnɩ pɔŋ nɛ sumasɩ ñɛwɛnɩ ɖandʋ, ɛlɛ́, Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ fɛyɩnɩ ñʋʋ ɖɩɖʋyɛ.».

eng: He placed his hands on her and she straightened up at once and praised God.
local: Peeɖe ɛtɩnɩ halʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-yɔɔ nesi nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ tɩsɩŋ yiɣ yiɣ nɛ ɛpazɩ Ɛsɔ sam.

eng: There is more to life than food and more to the body than clothing.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, wezuu kɩlɩ tɔɔnaɣ wazaɣ nɛ tomnaɣ ñakɩlɩ wondu wazaɣ.

eng: At that moment, Zechariah was able to speak again, and he began praising God.
local: Zakaarii tɛm maʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ-nzʋlʋmɩyɛ yɛɖɩ nɛ ɛpazɩ yɔɔdʋʋ nɛ ɛsaŋ Ɛsɔ.

eng: Then Jesus said to someone else, Follow me. He replied, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi lɛlʋ se: «Kʋyɩ ŋtɩŋ-m.» Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Yele mowolo menpi man-caa nɛ pʋcɔ mɔnkɔɔ mantɩŋ-ŋ.».

eng: The synagogue leader, incensed that Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, responded, There are six days during which work is permitted. Come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath day.
local: Ɛzɩ Yesu tɩɩlabʋ nɛ halʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye ḿbʋ́ yɔ lɛ, Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa wiyaʋ hɔyɛ pɩsɩ nɛ eheyi ɛyáa samaɣ se: «Kɩyakɩŋ loɖo palakɩ tʋmɩyɛ kɩyakʋ kɩsɩŋʋʋ taa. Yee weyi ɛcaɣ se ɛ-kʋdɔndʋ yɔɔ ɛcɛ yɔ, ɛtʋʋzɩnɩ kɩyakɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-taa nakʋyʋ nɛ ɛkɔɔ. Ɛtaakɔɔ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye.».

eng: Consider the ravens: they neither plant nor harvest, they have no silo or barn, yet God feeds them. You are worth so much more than birds!.
local: Ɩcɔnɩ sumasɩ nɛ ɩna: siiɖuu, sɩɩkʋŋ. Sɩfɛyɩnɩ kpoŋ yaa tɔɔnaɣ ɖɩzɩyɛ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ calɩɣ-sɩ. Ŋgʋ́, mɩkɩlɩ sumasɩ wazaɣ tam sakɩyɛ.

eng: All their neighbors were filled with awe, and everyone throughout the Judean highlands talked about what had happened.
local: Ɛlɛ́, sɔɔndʋ kpa ɛ-cɔlɔ ñɩma nɛ Yudee pʋŋ taa ñɩma tɩŋa kɛdɩɣ ɖama mbʋ́ pɩlaba yɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Let the dead bury their own dead. But you go and spread the news of Gods kingdom. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-i se: «Yele sɩɖaa nɛ pepi pa-sɩɖaa. Ña lɛ, wolo ŋsusi ɛyáa Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ tɔm.».

eng: The Lord replied, Hypocrites! Dont each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from its stall and lead it out to get a drink?.
local: Peeɖe Kɩpaɣlʋ cosi-i se: «Tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ñɩma mɩ! Ɩɩtʋlʋʋ mɩ-naŋ yaa mɩ-kpaŋasɩ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye nɛ iwona se pɔñɔɔ lɩm naa we?.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Mɩ-taa weyi ɛyɔkʋʋ ɛ-ñʋʋ nɛ ɛpɩzɩ etuzi ɛ-sɩm kɩyakʋ?.

eng: All who heard about this considered it carefully. They said, What then will this child be? Indeed, the Lords power was with him.
local: Mbá patɩŋa panɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ yɔ, pamaɣzɩ pɩŋŋ nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ pa-tɩ se: «Ɛbɛ pɩɣa pɩcɩɩcɩyɩm ɛnɛ ɛkaɣ kɛʋ?» Meheyiɣ-mɩ se Kɩpaɣlʋ ɖoŋ kɛwɛ kɔ-yɔɔ siŋŋ.

eng: One Sabbath, when Jesus went to share a meal in the home of one of the leaders of the Pharisees, they were watching him closely.
local: Kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ nakʋyʋ wiye Farɩsɩ ñɩma sɔsɔ nɔɔyʋ ya Yesu ɛ-tɛ se ewolo ɛtɔɔ tɔɔnaɣ nɛ mbá paawɛ peeɖe yɔ, pɛwɛɛ pefeŋiɣ-i.

eng: Someone else said to Jesus, I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say good-bye to those in my house.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, lɛlʋ tasɩ-ɩ heyuu se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Mantɩŋɩɣ-ŋ. Ɛlɛ́, yele mowolo meheyi ma-ñɩma se pɩlabɩ wiɖiyi nɛ mɔnkɔɔ.».

eng: Then isnt it necessary that this woman, a daughter of Abraham, bound by Satan for eighteen long years, be set free from her bondage on the Sabbath day? .
local: Halʋ ɛnɛ, ɛkɛ Abraham sayɩ weyi Satanɩ ɖʋ-ɩ kʋñɔŋ pɩnzɩ hiu nɛ lutozo yɔ. Pɩtaamʋnɩ se malɩzɩ-ɩ kʋñɔŋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-taa kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye yaa we?».

eng: If you cant do such a small thing, why worry about the rest?.
local: Yee ɩɩpɩzɩɣ pʋyʋ cikpelu ɛnɛ labʋ lɛ, ɛbɛ yɔɔ ɩyɔkʋʋ mɩ-ñʋŋ lɛɛbʋ mbʋ́ pɩkazaa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ yɔ?.

eng: Johns father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied.
local: Pɩɣa pɩcɩɩcɩyɩm ɛnʋ ɛ-caa Zakaarii nzʋlʋmɩyɛ yɛɖaa lɛ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tii ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛpazɩ yɔɔdʋʋ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se:.

eng: A man suffering from an abnormal swelling of the body was there.
local: Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe. Pʋwʋaɣ-ɩ nɛ ehuu ɛ-tɩŋa.

eng: Jesus said to him, No one who puts a hand on the plow and looks back is fit for Gods kingdom. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi ɛ́lɛ́ se: «Yee ɛyʋ ɛkpaɣ hakuu se ɛhayɩɣ nɛ eyele ɛnɩɣnɩ lɛɛbʋ yɔ, ɛtamʋna Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa tʋmlaɖʋ.».

eng: When he said these things, all his opponents were put to shame, but all those in the crowd rejoiced at all the extraordinary things he was doing.
local: Yesu yɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, fɛyɛ kpa e-koyindinaa tɩŋa payɩ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛyáa samaɣ tɩŋa laŋɩyɛ hɛɛnɩ tʋma wena ɛlakaɣ yɔ atɩŋa payɩ.

eng: Notice how the lilies grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth. But I say to you that even Solomon in all his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Ɩcɔnɩ tɛyɛ ñɩtʋ lɩzɩɣ hɛtʋ ndʋ́ yɔ nɛ ɩna. Ñɩtʋ ńdʋ́, tɩɩlakɩ tʋmɩyɛ, tɩɩlʋʋ wondu. Ɛlɛ́, meheyiɣ-mɩ se halɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Salomɔɔ nɛ ɛ-ñɩm tɩŋa yɔ, ɛtaawɛnɩ wonuu nakʋyʋ nɛ kɩtalɩ hɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa nakʋyʋ ɖeu.

eng: Bless the Lord God of Israel because he has come to help and has delivered his people.
local: «Pasa ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛkɔm ɛya ɛ-ɛyáa Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma ɖa ɖa-ñʋʋ. Ɛkɔm se ɖɩhɛzɩ.

eng: Jesus asked the lawyers and Pharisees, Does the Law allow healing on the Sabbath or not? .
local: Peeɖe Yesu pɔzɩ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma se: «Pɩha nʋmɔʋ se pala nɛ kʋdɔndʋ nɔɔyʋ yɔɔ cɛ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye yaa pataala?».

eng: Jesus asked, What is Gods kingdom like? To what can I compare it?.
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ wɛ ɛzɩ ɛbɛ lee? Yaa ɛbɛ mɛnkɛdɩɣnɩ-kɛ?.

eng: But they said nothing. Jesus took hold of the sick man, cured him, and then let him go.
local: Ɛlɛ́, potocosi-i natʋyʋ. Peeɖe Yesu hɔm kʋdɔndʋ ɛnʋ nɛ ɛla nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Wolo ŋɖɛɛ.».

eng: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in his servant Davids house.
local: Ɛkɔnɩ-ɖʋ ɖoŋ tʋ ɛ-tʋmlaɖʋ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Daaviidi liɖe taa. Ɛnʋ yakɩnɩ ɖa-ñʋʋ.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, how much more will God do for you, you people of weak faith!.
local: Lidaʋ pazɩ tɩnaa mɩ! Ɛsɔ tekizi haʋ ñɩtʋ ndʋ́ tɩwɛ hayɩm taa sɔnɔ nɛ cee pɔkɔŋ-tʋ ɖʋʋ miŋ taa yɔ ɖeu nɛ pɩcandɩnɩ mɩ na!.

eng: Its like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in a garden. It grew and developed into a tree and the birds in the sky nested in its branches. .
local: Kɛwɛ ɛzɩ ɛyʋ kpakʋʋ piye nɖɩ́ ɖɩkɩlɩ kɔzʋʋ pee tɩŋa taa yɔ nɛ eɖuu ɛ-hayɩm taa nɛ ɖɩñɔ nɛ pɩpɩɩ tɩʋ nɛ sumasɩ ɖʋʋ ɖandʋ ki-pilinzi yɔɔ yɔ.».

eng: He said to them, Suppose your child or ox fell into a ditch on the Sabbath day. Wouldnt you immediately pull it out? .
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Yee mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ pɩɣa yaa ɛ-nɔ ɛtɔlɩ lɔkɔ taa kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye, pʋdʋ yeki-i lɩzʋʋ kpaagbaa se pɩkɛ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ naa we?».

eng: just as he said through the mouths of his holy prophets long ago.
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ eeheyuu ɛ-náyaa ɖooo lɔŋ se peheyi-ɖʋ se.

eng: Dont chase after what you will eat and what you will drink. Stop worrying.
local: Mɩ-ɛsɛ ɛtaasɛɛnɩ mbʋ́ ɩɩtɔɔ nɛ ɩñɔɔ yɔ pɩ-ñɩnʋʋ nɛ lɩmaɣza kʋ-mɩ.

eng: Again he said, To what can I compare Gods kingdom?.
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ se: «Ɛbɛ mɛnkɛdɩɣnɩ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ lee?.

eng: But they had no response.
local: Ɛlɛ́, patapɩzɩ pocosi-i natʋyʋ.

eng: He has brought salvation from our enemies and from the power of all those who hate us.
local: ɛkɔŋ-ɖʋ lɩzʋʋ ɖo-koyindinaa nɛ mbá patɩŋa papaɖɩɣ-ɖʋ yɔ pe-nesi taa yɔ.

eng: All the nations of the world long for these things. Your Father knows that you need them.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, keɖeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ mbá paañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ, ḿbá ñɩnɩɣnɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɛ́ mɩ yɔ, mɩwɛnɩ Caa, ɛnʋ sɩmna mbʋ́ pɩtɛɖɩɣ-mɩ yɔ.

eng: Its like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through the whole. .
local: Kɛwɛ ɛzɩ sɔsɔɔna wena halʋ cozuu nɛ ɛɖʋ sona mʋlʋm kiiloonaa nɩɩlɛ nɛ kagbaanzɩ taa nɛ ehuudi nɛ pʋkɔɔ puhuu yɔ.».

eng: When Jesus noticed how the guests sought out the best seats at the table, he told them a parable.
local: Yesu kana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mbá paya tɔɔnaɣ ɖɩtɔɔyɛ yɔ, palɩzɩɣ ɖɩjaɣyɛ kɩbanɖɛ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ etuu-wɛ eduuye se:.

eng: He has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and remembered his holy covenant.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɛlakɩ ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa ɛ-camɩyɛ lakasɩ nɛ ɛtɔzɩ ɛ-nɔɔ haʋ yɔɔ.

eng: Instead, desire his kingdom and these things will be given to you as well.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se ɩcalɩnɩ ñɩnʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ nɛ ɛtɛzɩ-mɩ haʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɛɖɩɣ-mɩ yɔ pɩtɩŋa payɩ.».

eng: Jesus traveled through cities and villages, teaching and making his way to Jerusalem.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu woki Yeruusalɛm. Ewokaɣ lɛ, ɛɖɔŋnɩ Ɛsɔtɔm susuu tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ nɛ cikpetu ndʋ́ ndʋ́ ɛtɩŋaɣnɩ tɩ-taa yɔ.

eng: When someone invites you to a wedding celebration, dont take your seat in the place of honor. Someone more highly regarded than you could have been invited by your host.
local: «Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛya-ŋ kazandʋ ɖeɖe nɛ ŋwolo yɔ, taahuyi se ŋlɩzɩɣ ɖɩjaɣyɛ kɩbanɖɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋtɔmaɣ yɔ paya nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ kɩlɩ-ŋ.

eng: the solemn pledge he made to our ancestor Abraham. He has granted.
local: Ḿbʋ́ lɛ mbʋ́ ɛɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ ɖɔ-cɔzɔ Abraham yɔ.

eng: Dont be afraid, little flock, because your Father delights in giving you the kingdom.
local: Yesu tasɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa heyuu se: «Mbá mɩ ɩtɔɖɔɔ yɔ, ɩtaasɔɔndɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩ-Caa sɔɔlaa se ɩsʋʋ e-Kewiyaɣ taa.

eng: Someone said to him, Lord, will only a few be saved? Jesus said to them.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, nɔɔyʋ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Pɩkɛ toovenim se ɛyáa pazɩ ɖeke hikiɣni ñʋʋ yabʋ naa we?» Yesu cosi-wɛ nɛ eduuye taa se:.

eng: The host who invited both of you will come and say to you, Give your seat to this other person. Embarrassed, you will take your seat in the least important place.
local: Taayele nɛ weyi ɛya-mɩ yɔ, ɛkɔɔ eheyi-ŋ se: ‹Kʋyɩ ŋkazɩ sɔsɔ ɖɩjaɣyɛ.› Pʋbʋ, ŋkʋyʋʋnɩ fɛyɛ nɛ ŋwolo ŋcaɣnɩ ɖooo wayɩ.

eng: that we would be rescued from the power of our enemies so that we could serve him without fear.
local: Ɛɖʋwa se ɛkɔŋ-ɖʋ lɩzʋʋ ɖo-koyindinaa nesi taa nɛ ɖɩla ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ, sɔɔndʋ ɛɛkpaɣ-ɖʋ.

eng: Sell your possessions and give to those in need. Make for yourselves wallets that dont wear out—a treasure in heaven that never runs out. No thief comes near there, and no moth destroys.
local: Ɩpɛdɩ mɩ-ñɩm nɛ ɩtayɩ kʋñɔndɩnaa pi-liidiye. Ɩwɛnɩ liidiye huyiŋ weyi ɩɩtʋlʋʋ wiɖiyi kaʋ yɔ nɛ ñɩm mbʋ́ pɩɩtɛŋ yɔ ɛsɔdaa ɖenɖe mɩ́láa ɛɛmɩlɩɣ nɛ pʋyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ pɩwɛɛkɩ-pʋ kaʋ yɔ.

eng: Make every effort to enter through the narrow gate. Many, I tell you, will try to enter and wont be able to.
local: «Meheyiɣ-mɩ se: ɩñaɣ pana nɛ ɩtɩŋnɩ ɖɛɛmɛnɖɛɛ taa nɛ ɩsʋʋ nɛ ihiɣ wezuu. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kɔŋ ñɩnʋʋ se pasʋʋ, ɛlɛ́, paapɩzɩɣ.

eng: Instead, when you receive an invitation, go and sit in the least important place. When your host approaches you, he will say, Friend, move up here to a better seat. Then you will be honored in the presence of all your fellow guests.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee paya-ŋ kazandʋ ɖeɖe nɛ ŋwolo yɔ, caɣnɩ wayɩ nɛ weyi ɛya-ŋ yɔ, ɛkɔma lɛ, eeheyi-ŋ se: ‹Man-taabalʋ! Kɔɔ ŋcaɣ ɖɩjaɣyɛ kɩbanɖɛ taa.› Pʋbʋ, paɖʋ-ŋ hɩɖɛ ña nɛ mbá patɩŋa ɩcaɣaɣ yɔ pɛ-ɛsɩndaa.

eng: in holiness and righteousness in Gods eyes, for as long as we live.
local: Đɩkpakɩɣ ɖa-tɩ ɖɩcɛlɩ-ɩ nɛ ɖɩla mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa kɩyakɩŋ tɩŋa ɖe-wezuu caɣʋ taa.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be too.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖenɖe ña-ñɩm wɛɛ yɔ, peeɖe ɖɔɖɔ ña-lɩmaɣza wɛɛ.».

eng: Once the owner of the house gets up and shuts the door, then you will stand outside and knock on the door, saying, Lord, open the door for us. He will reply, I dont know you or where you are from.
local: Alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ kɔŋ nɛ ɖɩɣa tʋ kʋyɩ ɛkaɖɩ nɔnɔɔ. Peeɖe ɩcakɩnɩ awayɩ nɛ ɩmakɩ nɔnɔɔ nɛ ɩtɔŋ se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Tʋlɩ-ɖʋ!› Ɛlɛ́, ecosuu-mɩ se: ‹Mantasɩ ɖenɖe ɩlɩnaa yɔ.›.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛkpazɩɣ ɛ-tɩ yɔ, petisiɣ-i nɛ weyi ɛ́lɛ́ tisiɣ ɛ-tɩ yɔ, pakpazɩ-ɩ.».

eng: You, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his way.
local: Piyele ña man-pɩɣa lɛ, pakaɣ-ŋ yaʋ se Ɛsɔ Caabiyuu náyʋ. Ŋɖɛɣ Kɩpaɣlʋ nɔɔ nɛ ŋñɔɔzɩ ɛ-nʋmɔʋ nɛ.

eng: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps lit.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Ɩñɔɔzɩ mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ nɛ ɩsɔ mɩ-kanɖʋnaa.

eng: Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.
local: Ɛlɛ́, miheyi-i se: ‹Đa nɛ ŋ ɖɩtɔkaɣna nɛ ɖɩñɔʋ nɛ ŋcɔʋ ɖɛ-tɛtʋ taa nɛ ŋwɩlɩɣ Ɛsɔtɔm.›.

eng: Then Jesus said to the person who had invited him, When you host a lunch or dinner, dont invite your friends, your brothers and sisters, your relatives, or rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return and that will be your reward.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu heyi Farɩsɩ tʋ weyi ɛɛya-ɩ ɛ-tɛ yɔ se: «Yee ŋlabɩ kazandʋ yɔ, taayaa ñɛ-ɛgbaɣdɩnaa yaa ña-ñɩma yaa ña-taabalaa ñɩm tɩnaa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, papɩzɩɣ paya-ŋ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ pɩla se pɛhɛyɩ-ŋ ña-kʋjɔʋ.

eng: You will tell his people how to be saved through the forgiveness of their sins.
local: ŋwɩlɩ ɛ-ɛjaɖɛ taa ñɩma ɖa se ɛkɔŋ-ɖʋ kpeɣu ɖa-kɩwɛɛkɩm nɛ ɛya ɖa-ñʋʋ.

eng: Be like people waiting for their master to come home from a wedding celebration, who can immediately open the door for him when he arrives and knocks on the door.
local: ɛzɩ tʋmlaɖaa mbá paɖaŋ pa-caa kpam se ɛlɩɣnɩ halɩkpaɣyɛ ɖeɖe nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛma pɔɔyʋʋ lɛ, patɩŋ patʋlɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: He will respond, I dont know you or where you are from. Go away from me, all you evildoers.
local: Ɛtasɩɣ-mɩ cosuu se: ‹Kɩdɛkɛdɩŋ mɩ! Ɩɖɛɛ mɔ-yɔɔ mɩtɩŋa. Mantasɩ ɖenɖe ɩlɩnaa yɔ.›.

eng: Instead, when you give a banquet, invite the poor, crippled, lame, and blind.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee ŋlabɩ kazandʋ yɔ, yaa kʋñɔndɩnaa nɛ mbá pe-nesi camaa yɔ nɛ kalɩkalasɩ nɛ yʋlʋmaa.

eng: Because of our Gods deep compassion, the dawn from heaven will break upon us.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɛ-Ɛsɔ kɛ pʋtɔdɩyɛ náyʋ siŋŋ: etiyiɣni ɛ-ñalɩmɩyɛ nɛ ɖɩñalɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ ɛzɩ wɩsɩ yɔ.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds waiting up when he arrives. I assure you that, when he arrives, he will dress himself to serve, seat them at the table as honored guests, and wait on them.
local: Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: yee tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá pa-caa ɛtalaa nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ pacaɣnɩ pɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ paɖaŋ-ɩ yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ! Ɛñɔɔzʋʋ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ ɖɩjaɣyɛ se pacaɣ nɛ ɛɖʋ-wɛ tɔɔnaɣ.

eng: There will be weeping and grinding of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in Gods kingdom, but you yourselves will be thrown out.
local: Peeɖe pɔlɔʋ-mɩ nɛ awayɩ nɛ ɩma kubusi nɛ ɩsalɩ mi-kela. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ ɩnaɣ Abraham nɛ Izaakɩ nɛ Yakɔɔbʋ nɛ náyaa tɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa fɛɛɛ.

eng: And you will be blessed because they cant repay you. Instead, you will be repaid when the just are resurrected. .
local: Yee ŋlabɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ŋhiɣ ña-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paapɩzɩɣ pɛhɛyɩ-ŋ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ kaɣnɩ-ŋ hɛyʋʋ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ efeziɣ ɛyáa kʋtʋʋzaa sɩm taa yɔ.».

eng: to give light to those who are sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide us on the path of peace.
local: Ñalɩmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩñalɩɣ mbá pɛwɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa se pɛtɛŋnɩ sɩm yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ. Đɩhɔŋ-ɖʋ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ ɖiwoni laŋhɛzɩyɛ nʋmɔʋ taa.».

eng: All the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around Jesus to listen to him.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye tikee mʋyaa nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa lɛlaa powobi Yesu cɔlɔ patɩŋa payɩ se panɩɩ ɛ-tɔm.

eng: Happy are those whom he finds alert, even if he comes at midnight or just before dawn .
local: Paa ɖoó hɛkʋ taa yaa tanaŋ cu ɛkɔm ɛmaɣnɩ-wɛ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pɛ-ɛsɛ yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ.

eng: People will come from east and west, north and south, and sit down to eat in Gods kingdom.
local: Ɛyáa kɔŋ lɩnaʋ wɩsɩ ɖɩlɩyɛ nɛ sɩ-ɖɩɖʋyɛ nɛ hadɛ nɛ hayo kiŋ nɛ powolo pacaɣ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa nɛ pɔtɔkɩ.

eng: When one of the dinner guests heard Jesus remarks, he said to Jesus, Happy are those who will feast in Gods kingdom.
local: Yesu yɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, mbá paawɛ peeɖe yɔ, pa-taa lɛlʋ heyi-i se: «Weyi ɛkaɣ tɔɔʋ tɔɔnaɣ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa yɔ, ehiɣ ɛ-tɩ!».

eng: The child grew up, becoming strong in character. He was in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
local: Zakaarii pɩɣa ŋ́gá kapaɣlaɣ nɛ kɔ-lɔŋ sɔzʋʋ. Kapaɣlaa lɛ, kowolo kacaɣ lakʋ taa nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ kapazɩ ka-tʋmɩyɛ labʋ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma hɛkʋ taa yɔ.

eng: The Pharisees and legal experts were grumbling, saying, This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.
local: Peeɖe Farɩsɩ ñɩma nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa pa-taa wɩnɩ-ɩ nɛ pɔyɔɔdʋʋ pa-taa pe-ɖeke se: «Ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛ nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa pɛkpɛndaa pɔtɔkɩ yɔ!».

eng: But know this, if the homeowner had known what time the thief was coming, he wouldnt have allowed his home to be broken into.
local: Ɩtɩlɩ ɖɔɖɔ se yee ɖɩɣa tʋ nɔɔyʋ ɛɛsɩm alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ mɩ́lʋ́ kɔŋ yɔ yɔ, ɛtaayebi-i se ɛsʋʋ ɛ-ɖɩɣa taa.

eng: Look! Those who are last will be first and those who are first will be last.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mbá pɛkɛ wayɩ ñɩma lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, pa-taa nabɛyɛ pɩsɩɣ kajalaɣ ñɩma nɛ mbá paba kɛ kajalaɣ ñɩma lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, pa-taa lɛlaa ñapɩsɩ wayɩ ñɩma.».

eng: Jesus replied, A certain man hosted a large dinner and invited many people.
local: Peeɖe Yesu tuu-i eduuye se: «Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ labɩnɩ kazandʋ nɛ ɛ́yáa ɛyáa sakɩyɛ.

eng: Jesus told them this parable:.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu tuu-wɛ eduuye se:.

eng: At that time, some Pharisees approached Jesus and said, Go! Get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you.
local: Yesu yɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Farɩsɩ ñɩma nabɛyɛ wolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ peheyi-i se: «Kʋyɩ cɩnɛ nɛ ŋɖɛɛ ɖoli. Wiyaʋ Eroodi caɣ se ɛkʋ-ŋ.».

eng: You also must be ready, because the Human One is coming at a time when you dont expect him. .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɩñɔɔzɩ mɩ-tɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ kɔŋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩtamaɣzɩ yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ.».

eng: When it was time for the dinner to begin, he sent his servant to tell the invited guests, Come! The dinner is now ready.
local: Tɔɔnaɣ pɩwa lɛ, etiyi ɛ-tʋmlaɖʋ se ewolo eheyi mbá ɛɛya-wɛ yɔ se pɔkɔɔ se alɩwaatʋ talaa.

eng: Suppose someone among you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them. Wouldnt he leave the other ninety-nine in the pasture and search for the lost one until he finds it?.
local: «Yee mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛwɛnɩ heŋ mɩnʋʋ nɛ ɩ-taa kʋɖʋm le, eeyeki kʋɖʋm fɛyɩ mɩnʋʋ weyi ɩkazaa yɔ ñɩtʋ ɖɩtɔɔyɛ nɛ ɛɖɛɛ heu ɛnʋ ɛ-ñɩnʋʋ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛna-ɩ yɔ yaa we?.

eng: Jesus said to them, Go, tell that fox, Look, Im throwing out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will complete my work.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Iwolo iheyi lɔŋ tʋ weyi ɛcɛtɩɣ ɛyáa ɛzɩ kpayʋ yɔ se, mɔntɔm se mɔnɖɔɔnʋʋ aleewaa nɛ mala nɛ kʋdɔndɩnaa yɔɔ cɛ sɔnɔ nɛ cee. Tozo wiye lɛ, mɛntɛzɩ man-tʋmɩyɛ.

eng: Peter said, Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone.
local: Yesu yɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Pɩyɛ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Đe-ɖeke ŋtuuu eduuye ńɖɩ́ yaa ɛyáa tɩŋa.».

eng: One by one, they all began to make excuses. The first one told him, I bought a farm and must go and see it. Please excuse me.
local: Peeɖe patɩŋa papazɩ tenduu kʋɖʋm kʋɖʋm se paanaɣ alɩwaatʋ. Kajalaɣ tʋ heyi tiyiyu ɛnʋ se: ‹Mayabɩ hayɩm nabʋyʋ nɛ mancaɣ se mowolo mana-pʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mentendiɣ-ŋ se maapɩzɩɣ mɔnkɔɔ.›.

eng: And when he finds it, he is thrilled and places it on his shoulders.
local: Ɛnaɣ-ɩ lɛ, ɛ-laŋɩyɛ taa hʋlʋmʋʋ ɖeu nɛ ɛkpaɣ-ɩ ɛhɔɔlɩ ɛ-hazasɩ yɔɔ se ekpeni-i.

eng: However, its necessary for me to travel today, tomorrow, and the next day because its impossible for a prophet to be killed outside of Jerusalem.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se mɔnɖɔ ma-nʋmɔʋ sɔnɔ nɛ cee nɛ ki-kifemuu yɔ, mɛntɛzɩɣ ɖɛʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtamʋna se pakʋ náyʋ ɖoli yem nɛ pɩtɛkɛnɩ Yeruusalɛm.

eng: The Lord replied, Who are the faithful and wise managers whom the master will put in charge of his household servants, to give them their food at the proper time?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛpɩsɩnɩ-ɩ se: «Akɛ tʋmlaɖʋ kɩbanʋ weyi ɛwɛ lɛɣtʋ yɔ? Ɛnʋ lɛ weyi ɛ-caa yekini-i ɖɩɣa se ɛcɔŋnɩ kɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛcɛlɩɣ tʋmlaɖaa lɛlaa tɔɔnaɣ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pʋmʋnaa yɔ.

eng: Another said, I bought five teams of oxen, and Im going to check on them. Please excuse me.
local: Lɛlʋ heyi-i se: ‹Mayabɩ naŋ kɩhayɩŋ hiu, mancaɣ se mamaɣzɩ-ɩ nɛ mana. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mentendiɣ-ŋ se maapɩzɩɣ mɔnkɔɔ.›.

eng: When he arrives home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost sheep.
local: Ɛtalɩɣ ɖɩɣa lɛ, ɛyaɣ ɛ-ɛgbaɣdɩnaa nɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ ñɩma se: ‹Ɩkɔɔ ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩmayɩ amaya. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mana me-heu weyi eeleba yɔ.›.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you! How often I have wanted to gather your people just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Hei! Yeruusalɛm ñɩma mɩ, ɩkʋʋ náyaa nɛ ɩyakɩ ɛyáa mbá Ɛsɔ tiyini-mɩ yɔ pɛɛ nɛ pasɩkɩ. Tam ɛzɩma mɔntɔsɔɔlɩ se menkpeɣli-mɩ mɔn-cɔlɔ nɛ mohuuzi mɩ-yɔɔ nɛ pʋyʋ ɛtaalabɩ-mɩ ɛzɩ ɖoyuu pabʋʋ kɩ-kalɩmasɩ yɔɔ yɔ nɛ ikizi?.

eng: Happy are the servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Tʋmlaɖʋ weyi ɛ-caa pɩsɩɣ nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ-ɩ ɛɖɔŋ ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ labʋ yɔ, ehiɣ ɛ-tɩ!.

eng: Another said, I just got married, so I cant come.
local: Tozo tʋ ñeheyi-i ɖɔɖɔ se: ‹Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ mankpaɣ halʋ, maapɩzɩɣ mɛnɖɛɛ meyebi-i.›.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who changes both heart and life than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to change their hearts and lives.
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛ ɛsɔdaa: yee kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖʋ kʋɖʋm nɔɔyʋ ɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ yɔ, Ɛsɔ nɛ e-tiyiyaa pa-laŋa hʋlʋmʋʋnɩ-ɩ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ɛyáa kʋɖʋm fɛyɩ mɩnʋʋ mbá pamaɣzɩɣ se pɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzaa nɛ pʋtɔpɔzɩ se pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ yɔ.».

eng: Look, your house is abandoned. I tell you, you wont see me until the time comes when you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name . .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, meyeki-mɩ nɛ mɩ-tɛtʋ. Meheyiɣ-mɩ se: ɩɩtasɩɣ-m naʋ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩyɔɔdaa se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ ɛ́wazɩ weyi ɛkɔŋ e-lone taa yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ› yɔ. ».

eng: I assure you that the master will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: ɖɩɣa tʋ ɛnʋ eyekini-i ɛ-ñɩm tɩŋa se ɛcɔnɩ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: When he returned, the servant reported these excuses to his master. The master of the house became angry and said to his servant, Go quickly to the citys streets, the busy ones and the side streets, and bring the poor, crippled, blind, and lame.
local: Tʋmlaɖʋ ɛnʋ, ɛpɩsɩ ɛ-caa cɔlɔ nɛ ɛkɛdɩ-ɩ mbʋ́ pɩlɩwa yɔ. Peeɖe pɩwɩzɩ ɛ́lɛ́ pɩŋŋ nɛ ɛɖaɣnɩ-ɩ tiyuu se: ‹Wolo lɔŋ kɩyakɩŋ nɛ tɛtʋ pa-taa nɛ habɛɛ nɔsɩ nɛ ŋyaa kʋñɔndɩnaa nɛ mbá pe-nesi camaa yɔ nɛ yʋlʋmaa nɛ kalɩkalasɩ nɛ pɔkɔɔ.›.

eng: Or what woman, if she owns ten silver coins and loses one of them, wont light a lamp and sweep the house, searching her home carefully until she finds it?.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tasɩ-wɛ tuuu eduuye se: «Yee halʋ nɔɔyʋ ɛwɛnɩ liidiye pɔɔyɩsɩ hiu nɛ sɩ-taa kʋɖʋmaɣ le, ɛɛsɔkɩ kanɖʋ nɛ ɛhazɩ ɛ-ɖɩɣa tɩŋa nɛ ɛñɩnɩ-kɛ camɩyɛ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛna-kɛ yɔ yaa we?.

eng: But suppose that these servants should say to themselves, My master is taking his time about coming. And suppose they began to beat the servants, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee pɩcɩbɩ tʋmlaɖʋ ɛnʋ nɛ ɛɖʋ ɛ-taa se ɛ-caa ɛɛkaɣ kɔm lɔŋ nɛ ɛtɔkɩ nɛ ɛñɔʋ nɛ pʋkʋʋ-ɩ nɛ ɛsʋʋ tʋmlaɖaa lɛlaa halaa ñɩma nɛ abalaa ñɩma pa-mabʋ yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: When she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost coin.
local: Ɛnaɣ-kɛ lɛ, ɛyaɣ ɛ-ɛgbaɣdɩnaa nɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ ñɩma se: ‹Ɩkɔɔ ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩmayɩ amaya. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mana me-liidiye pɔɔyaɣ ŋgá kaaleba yɔ.›.

eng: The servant said, Master, your instructions have been followed and there is still room.
local: Ɛlabɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛpɩsɩ eheyi-i se: ‹Caa! Malabɩ mbʋ́ ŋheyi-m yɔ nɛ ɖɩjaɣyɛ kazɩ yem.›.

eng: The master of those servants would come on a day when they werent expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict. The master will cut them into pieces and assign them a place with the unfaithful.
local: ɖɩɣa tʋ ɛnʋ, ɛkɔŋ kɩyakʋ nakʋyʋ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛtatɩlɩ yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ nɛ etudi-i nɛ ɛɖʋ-ɩ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ ɛzɩ palakʋʋ mbá paañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, joy breaks out in the presence of Gods angels over one sinner who changes both heart and life. .
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛɛ: yee kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖʋ kʋɖʋm nɔɔyʋ ɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ yɔ, Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa laŋa hʋlʋmʋʋ ɖeu.».

eng: The master said to the servant, Go to the highways and back alleys and urge people to come in so that my house will be filled.
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ tasɩ-ɩ heyuu se: ‹Lɩɩ ŋwolo tɛyɛ nʋmɔŋ taa nɛ ɖenɖe sʋlʋmɩyaa cakɩ pasʋlʋmʋʋ yɔ nɛ ŋna mbá lɛ, ŋcɛyɩ-wɛ nɛ pɔkɔɔ pasʋʋ man-ɖɩɣa taa nɛ kosu.

eng: That servant who knew his masters will but didnt prepare for it or act on it will be beaten severely.
local: Tʋmlaɖʋ weyi ɛsɩm mbʋ́ ɛ-caa liu wɛɛ yɔ nɛ ɛtɔñɔɔzɩ ɛ-tɩ se ɛlabɩ-pʋ yɔ, poluu-i agbazɛɛ ɖeu siŋŋ.

eng: Jesus said, A certain man had two sons.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ tuuu eduuye se: «Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛnɩ pɩyalaa naalɛ.

eng: I tell you, not one of those who were invited will taste my dinner. .
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɛyáa mbá maaɖʋnɩ ñʋʋ yɔ, pa-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛɛkaɣ naʋ man-kazandʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ kaʋ.› ».

eng: The one who didnt know the masters will but who did things deserving punishment will be beaten only a little. Much will be demanded from everyone who has been given much, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked.
local: Ɛlɛ́, tʋmlaɖʋ weyi ɛ́lɛ́ taasɩ mbʋ́ ɛ-caa liu wɛɛ yɔ nɛ ɛla mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ, pamakɩ-ɩ pazɩ. Weyi paha-ɩ sakɩyɛ yɔ, pɔkɔŋ-ɩ pɔzʋʋ sakɩyɛ. Weyi paɖʋzɩ ɛ́lɛ́ nesi taa sakɩyɛ yɔ, pɔkɔŋ-ɩ pɔzʋʋ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: The younger son said to his father, Father, give me my share of the inheritance. Then the father divided his estate between them.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye lɛ, pa-taa cikpelu heyi pa-caa se: ‹Man-caa! Cɛlɩ-m ña-ñɩm mbʋ́ pɩkɛɣ ma-ñɩmbʋ ña-sɩm wayɩ yɔ.› Peeɖe pa-caa tayɩ-wɛ ɛ-ñɩm.

eng: Large crowds were traveling with Jesus. Turning to them, he said.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye ɛyáa samaɣ nɛ Yesu pɔɖɔŋaɣ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛkɛzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se:.

eng: I came to cast fire upon the earth. How I wish that it was already ablaze!.
local: «Mɔnkɔm se manɖʋ kedeŋa miŋ. Ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɔnsɔɔlaa se miŋ ɛnʋ ɛtɛ pazʋʋ mʋ́ʋ yɔ?.

eng: Soon afterward, the younger son gathered everything together and took a trip to a land far away. There, he wasted his wealth through extravagant living.
local: Pɩtalabɩdɩ pʋyʋ lɛ, cikpelu ɛnʋ ekuu ɛ-ñɩm tɩŋa nɛ ɛɖɛɛ ɖooo ɛjaɖɛ naɖɩyɛ taa. Ɛtalɩ peeɖe lɛ, ɛpazɩ tɩkpayɩtʋ labʋ nɛ ɛwɛɛkɩ ɛ-ñɩm ḿbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ.

eng: Whoever comes to me and doesnt hate father and mother, spouse and children, and brothers and sisters—yes, even ones own life—cannot be my disciple.
local: «Weyi ɛcaɣ se ɛtɩŋ-m yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛsɔɔlɩ-m nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ɛ-caa nɛ e-ɖoo nɛ ɛ-halʋ nɛ e-piya nɛ ɛ-ɖalaa nɛ ɛ-kɔɔnaa nɛ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-tɩ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛkɛ mɔn-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ.

eng: I have a baptism I must experience. How I am distressed until its completed!.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se mansʋʋ kʋñɔŋ taa ɛzɩ pamɩɩsʋʋ ɛyʋ lɩm yɔ. Ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɛwɛɣ ɛsɛñɩŋɩyɛ taa nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ!.

eng: When he had used up his resources, a severe food shortage arose in that country and he began to be in need.
local: Ɛtɛm-pʋ wɛɛkʋʋ lɛ, ñɔɔsɩ nasɩyɩ lɩɩ ɛjaɖɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa. Ŋgʋ́, ɛɛwɛ kɛdɛɖaɣ taa kpem.

eng: Whoever doesnt carry their own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.
local: Weyi ɛtatɩŋ ma-wayɩ nɛ ɛtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ yɔ, ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛkɛ mɔn-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ.

eng: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, I have come instead to bring division.
local: Ɩmaɣzɩɣ se laŋhɛzɩyɛ mɔnkɔnɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ yaa we? Meheyiɣ-mɩ se pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɛ́, tayʋʋ mɔnkɔnaa.

eng: He hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
local: Peeɖe ewobi ɛjaɖɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa tʋ nɔɔyʋ cɔlɔ se ɛ́lɛ́ ɛñɩnɩ-ɩ tʋmɩyɛ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ-ɩ ewoni tɛyɛ se ɛcɔŋnɩ-ɩ afanaa yɔɔ.

eng: If one of you wanted to build a tower, wouldnt you first sit down and calculate the cost, to determine whether you have enough money to complete it?.
local: Toovenim lɛ, mɩ-taa weyi ɛmaɣ kuduyuu sɔsɔʋ nɛ ɛtacaɣ se ɛmaɣzɩ nɛ ɛna se e-liidiye talɩɣ-ɩ yaa ɖɩɩtalɩɣ-ɩ nɛ pʋcɔ?.

eng: From now on, a household of five will be divided—three against two and two against three.
local: Pɩkpaɣʋnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, ɛyáa ɛwɛ kagbaanzɩ hɔʋ nakʋyʋ taa yɔ, patayɩɣ ɖama: naadozo kʋyʋʋ naalɛ yɔɔ nɛ naalɛ ñakʋyɩ ɖɔɖɔ naadozo yɔɔ.

eng: He longed to eat his fill from what the pigs ate, but no one gave him anything.
local: Ɛñɩnaɣ se paa afanaa tɔɔnaɣ maɣmaɣ yɔ ɛtɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛhaɣ-ɩ.

eng: Otherwise, when you have laid the foundation but couldnt finish the tower, all who see it will begin to belittle you.
local: Yee ɛtala ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛkʋyɩ yem ɛpazɩ kiɖe sɩʋ nɛ ɛtapɩzɩ ɛma-kʋ nɛ kɩtɛ yɔ, ɛyáa kɔŋ powoŋ-i.

eng: Father will square off against son and son against father; mother against daughter and daughter against mother; and mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law. .
local: Caja kʋyʋʋ ɛ-pɩyalʋ yɔɔ nɛ pɩyalʋ ñakʋyɩ ɛ-caa yɔɔ. Đoyo kʋyʋʋ ɛ-pɛlɛ yɔɔ nɛ pɛlɛ ñakʋyɩ e-ɖoo yɔɔ. Waŋ kʋyʋʋ e-wolu yɔɔ nɛ wolu ñakʋyɩ ɛ-waŋ yɔɔ.».

eng: When he came to his senses, he said, How many of my fathers hired hands have more than enough food, but Im starving to death!.
local: Peeɖe ɛmaɣzɩ ɛ-taa pɩŋŋ nɛ ɛtɔ se: ‹Ŋgʋ́, man-caa wɛnɩ tʋmlaɖaa sakɩyɛ, pɔtɔkɩ paatɛŋ. Piyele ñɔɔsɩ ñakʋʋ-m cɩnɛ yem falaa!.

eng: They will say, Heres the person who began construction and couldnt complete it.
local: nɛ pɔtɔ se: ‹Ɛnɛ ɛma kuduyuu nɛ ɛtapɩzɩ ɛtɛm-kʋ.›.

eng: Jesus also said to the crowds, When you see a cloud forming in the west, you immediately say, Its going to rain. And indeed it does.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tasɩ ɛyáa samaɣ heyuu se: «Yee ɩna tɛʋ piyini wɩsɩ ɖɩɖʋyɛ yɔ, ɩyɔɔdʋʋ kpaagbaa se: ‹Sɔnɔ tɛʋ nɩɣ,› ḿbʋ́ lakɩna.

eng: I will get up and go to my father, and say to him, Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
local: Mankʋyʋʋ manpɩsɩ man-caa cɔlɔ nɛ meheyi-i se: caa! Mayʋsɩnɩ Ɛsɔ nɛ mɛntɛ mayʋsɩnɩ-ŋ.

eng: Or what king would go to war against another king without first sitting down to consider whether his ten thousand soldiers could go up against the twenty thousand coming against him?.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛɛ. Yee wiyaʋ sɔsɔ nɔɔyʋ ewoki se ɛ nɛ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ lɛlʋ poyoo yɔ, ɛcakɩ ɛmaɣzɩ ɖeu nɛ ɛna se yoyaa sakɩyɛ mbá ɛwɛna yɔ, papɩzɩɣ nɛ pa nɛ e-koyindu weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ñɩma kɩlɩ-ɩ tam naalɛ taa yɔ, poyoo yaa paapɩzɩɣ.

eng: And when a south wind blows, you say, A heat wave is coming. And it does.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩnaɣ heɣlim makɩ kpaʋ yɔɔ lɛ, ɩyɔɔdʋʋ se: ‹Sɔnɔ soŋaɣ wɛɣ,› ḿbʋ́ lakɩna.

eng: I no longer deserve to be called your son. Take me on as one of your hired hands. .
local: Mantatasɩ mʋnaʋ se ŋya-m se ña-pɩyalʋ. Ɛlɛ́, kpaɣ-m ɛzɩ ña-tʋmlaɖaa taa lɛlʋ yɔ.›.

eng: And if he didnt think he could win, he would send a representative to discuss terms of peace while his enemy was still a long way off.
local: Yee ɛnawa se ɛɛpɩzɩɣ yɔ, ɛkʋsʋʋ ɛyáa nɛ etiyi-wɛ ɛ́lɛ́ cɔlɔ poliŋ taa se pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se ɛbɛ pʋwɛɛ se ɛla nɛ ɛ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ peceɣ.».

eng: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret conditions on earth and in the sky. How is it that you dont know how to interpret the present time?.
local: Tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ñɩma mɩ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ɩnaɣ mbʋ́ pɩlakɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ tataa yɔ nɛ ɩnɩɣ pʋ-tɔbʋʋ nɛ ɩnaɣ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ lakɩ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ nɛ ɩɩnɩɣ pʋbʋ tɔbʋʋ?».

eng: So he got up and went to his father. While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion. His father ran to him, hugged him, and kissed him.
local: Peeɖe ɛkʋyaa ɛkpaɣ nʋmɔʋ se ekpeŋ ɛ-caa tɛ. Ɛwɩzɩ talʋʋ lɛ, ɛ-caa losi-i poliŋ nɛ pɩlabɩ-ɩ pʋtɔdɩyɛ pɩŋŋ. Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ se lɔŋ ɛkatɩ-ɩ nɛ ekpibi-i nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ.

eng: In the same way, none of you who are unwilling to give up all of your possessions can be my disciple.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛkɛ mɔn-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ nɛ eteyele mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛwɛna yɔ.».

eng: And why dont you judge for yourselves what is right?.
local: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ mɩ-maɣmaɣ iiɖiɣziɣni mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ?.

eng: Then his son said, Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I no longer deserve to be called your son.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛ-pɩyalʋ ɛnʋ eheyi-i se: ‹Caa! Mayʋsɩnɩ Ɛsɔ nɛ mɛntɛ mayʋsɩnɩ-ŋ. Mantatasɩ mʋnaʋ se ŋya-m se ña-pɩyalʋ.›.

eng: Salt is good. But if salt loses its flavor, how will it become salty again?.
local: «Đɔm kɛ pʋyʋ kɩbanʋ. Ɛlɛ́, yee pilesi pi-leleŋ lɛ, ɛzɩma palakɩ-pʋ nɛ pɩɖaɣnɩ leleŋ kpaʋ yɔ?.

eng: As you are going to court with your accuser, make your best effort to reach a settlement along the way. Otherwise, your accuser may bring you before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
local: Yee ŋyʋsɩnɩ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ewokini-ŋ wiyaʋ tɛ yɔ, cɛyɩ ña-tɩ nɛ ña nɛ ɩ ɩñɔɔzɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ nʋmɔʋ taa nɛ ɛtaatanɩ-ŋ tɔm hʋyʋ tɛ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, ɛ́lɛ́ cɛlɩɣ-ŋ sɔɔjanaa nɛ paɖʋ-ŋ salaka.

eng: But the father said to his servants, Quickly, bring out the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!.
local: Patalɩ ɖɩɣa lɛ, ɛ-caa heyi ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa se: ‹Iwolo lɛɛlɛɛ ɩkpaɣ toko kitibuu ŋgʋ́ kɩkɩlɩ ɖeu yɔ nɛ ɩkɔnɩ isuu-i. Ɩlɛɛ-ɩ kokooye nɛ naataŋgbala.

eng: It has no value, neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. People throw it away. Whoever has ears to hear should pay attention. .
local: Halɩ pɩɩwazɩɣ hayɩm taa yaa huɖe taa maɣmaɣ. Pɔlɔʋ-pʋ awayɩ. Meheyiɣ-mɩ se weyi ɛwɛnɩ nɩŋgbaaŋ lɛ, ɛnɩɩ.».

eng: I tell you, you wont get out of there until you have paid the very last cent. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-ŋ se: paɖʋ-ŋ-kɛ yɔ, ŋwɛɣ peeɖe ḿbʋ́ ka ka nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ ŋhɛyɩ ña-kɩmɩyɛ tɩŋa payɩ nɛ pɩtakazɩ paa koba yɔ.».

eng: Fetch the fattened calf and slaughter it. We must celebrate with feasting.
local: Ɩkɔnɩ neɣye nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ num camɩyɛ yɔ nɛ ɩkʋ nɛ ɖɩtɔɔ kazandʋ.

eng: because this son of mine was dead and has come back to life! He was lost and is found! And they began to celebrate.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, man-pɩyalʋ ɛnɛ, pʋwɛ ɛzɩ ɛsɩba nɛ efe yɔ. Eeleba nɛ manɖaɣnɩ-ɩ naʋ.› Peeɖe papazɩ kazandʋ tɔɔʋ.

eng: Now his older son was in the field. Coming in from the field, he approached the house and heard music and dancing.
local: Pɩlakaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ-pɩyalʋ sɔsɔ ñaawɛnɩ tɛyɛ. Ɛ́lɛ́ kpeŋaɣ nɛ ɛwɩzɩnɩ ɖɩɣa lɛ, ɛnɩɩ soo nɛ payɛ pa-taŋ.

eng: He called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
local: Peeɖe ɛya ɛ-caa tʋmlaɖʋ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: ‹Ɛbɛ palakɩ ḿbʋ́?›.

eng: The servant replied, Your brother has arrived, and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he received his son back safe and sound.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: ‹Ñe-neu pɩsɩnɩ ɛkɔɔ nɛ ña-caa kʋ neɣye nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ num camɩyɛ yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛtasɩ-ɩ naʋ nɛ ɛ-alaafɩya.›.

eng: Then the older son was furious and didnt want to enter in, but his father came out and begged him.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-pɩyalʋ sɔsɔ ɛnʋ ɛmʋ pana nɛ ekizi ɖɩɣa sʋʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛ-caa lɩɩ ɛ́yáa-ɩ se ɛsʋʋ.

eng: He answered his father, Look, Ive served you all these years, and I never disobeyed your instruction. Yet youve never given me as much as a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛpɩsɩnɩ ɛ-caa se: ‹Welesi nɛ ŋnɩɩ, pɩnzɩ sakɩyɛ yɔ, mɛwɛɛ malakɩ-ŋ tʋmɩyɛ nɛ paa ŋyɔɔdɩ ndʋ́ yɔ, maakiziɣ.› Wiɖiyi ŋtatɩɩtɔm se: ‹Kpaɣ pimbiɣa kanɛ nɛ ña nɛ ña-taabalaa ɩla kpanzʋʋ.›.

eng: But when this son of yours returned, after gobbling up your estate on prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.
local: Ŋgʋ́, ña-pɩyalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛkpaɣ ña-ñɩm nɛ ɛwɛɛkɩ halaa nʋmɔʋ taa. Sɔnɔ ɛkɔma lɛ, ŋyele pakʋ-ɩ ɖe-neɣye nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ num camɩyɛ yɔ.

eng: Then his father said, Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-caa cosi-i se: ‹Man-pɩɣa! Ñɛwɛnɩ mɔn-cɔlɔ tam. Mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa mɛwɛna yɔ, ñatɩnɩ-pʋ.

eng: But we had to celebrate and be glad because this brother of yours was dead and is alive. He was lost and is found. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, pʋmʋnaa se ɖɩla kazandʋ nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛ ɛzɩ ñe-neu kasɩba nɛ efe yɔ, eeleba nɛ manɖaɣnɩ-ɩ naʋ.› ».

eng: Jesus also said to the disciples, A certain rich man heard that his household manager was wasting his estate.
local: Yesu tasɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tuuu eduuye se: «Ñɩm tʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛnɩ tʋmlaɖʋ nɛ ɛcɔŋnɩ-ɩ ɛ-ñɩm yɔɔ. Nakʋyʋ wiye lɛ, powolo peheyi-i se: ‹Ña-tʋmlaɖʋ wɛɛkɩɣ ña-ñɩm.›.

eng: He called the manager in and said to him, What is this I hear about you? Give me a report of your administration because you can no longer serve as my manager.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛya-ɩ nɛ eheyi-i se: ‹Ña! Manɩɣ pɔyɔɔdʋʋ ñɔ-tɔm se we? Kɛdɩ-m ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ña-tʋmɩyɛ ɖɔŋ yɔ. Ŋŋtasɩɣ kɛʋ man-tʋmlaɖʋ!›.

eng: The household manager said to himself, What will I do now that my master is firing me as his manager? Im not strong enough to dig and too proud to beg.
local: Peeɖe tʋmlaɖʋ ɛnʋ ɛhʋ ɛ-taa se: ‹Nɔɔnɔɔ man-caa lɩzɩɣ-m tʋmɩyɛ taa, malakɩ ɛzɩma lee? Mɛnfɛyɩ ɖoŋ se maahayɩ. Ŋgʋ́, sʋlʋmʋʋ ñɛwɛ-m fɛyɛ.

eng: I know what Ill do so that, when I am removed from my management position, people will welcome me into their houses.
local: Tɔɔ! Mansɩm mbʋ́ malakɩ nɛ yee pɔɖɔɔnɩ-m lɛ, ɛyáa kamʋ-m pɛ-tɛ yɔ.›.

eng: One by one, the manager sent for each person who owed his master money. He said to the first, How much do you owe my master.
local: Peeɖe etiyaa nɛ payaa ɛ-caa ɛnʋ ɛ-kɩndɩyaa kʋɖʋm kʋɖʋm. Pɔkɔma lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ kajalaɣ tʋ se: ‹Ɛzɩma ŋwɛnɩ man-caa kɩmɩyɛ?›.

eng: He said, Nine hundred gallons of olive oil. The manager said to him, Take your contract, sit down quickly, and write four hundred fifty gallons.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ se: ‹Num kɩpam ŋgbaŋ mɩnʋʋ mɛwɛnɩ-ɩ kɩmɩyɛ.› Peeɖe tʋmlaɖʋ ɛnʋ eheyi-i se: ‹Ŋŋnaɣ ña-kɩmɩyɛ takayaɣ! Caɣ lɔŋ nɛ ŋma se ŋgbaŋ nɩɩnʋwa.›.

eng: Then the manager said to another, How much do you owe? He said, One thousand bushels of wheat. He said, Take your contract and write eight hundred.
local: Ɛkʋyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɛmaa lɛlʋ yɔɔ se: ‹Nɛ ña-yɔ, ɛzɩma ña-ñɩnɖɛ kɛnaa?› Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: ‹Mɩla kutokiŋ mɩnʋʋ.› Peeɖe eheyi-i se: ‹Ŋŋnaɣ ña-kɩmɩyɛ takayaɣ! Ma se kutokiŋ nɩɩnʋwa nɛ niidozo.›.

eng: The master commended the dishonest manager because he acted cleverly. People who belong to this world are more clever in dealing with their peers than are people who belong to the light.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, tʋmlaɖʋ ɛzaɣ́ʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-caa yɩɣyɩ-ɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛwɛnɩ lɛɣtʋ. Kedeŋa kanɛ kɔ-yɔɔ ñɩma wɛnɩ lɛɣtʋ pa-lakasɩ taa nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ñalɩmɩyɛ taa ñɩma.

eng: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when its gone, you will be welcomed into the eternal homes.
local: Ma meheyiɣ-mɩ se: ɩkpaɣ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ñɩm mbʋ́ ɩwɛna yɔ nɛ ɩñɩnɩnɩ ɛgbaɣdɩnaa nɛ yee pʋkɔma pɩtɛɖɩɣ-mɩ lɛ, Ɛsɔ kamʋ-mɩ ɛ-ɖɩɣa ŋgá kaayɔkʋʋ kaʋ yɔ ka-taa.

eng: Whoever is faithful with little is also faithful with much, and the one who is dishonest with little is also dishonest with much.
local: Weyi ɛlakɩ mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ yɔ cikpem taa yɔ, ɛlakɩ ɖɔɖɔ mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ yɔ sɔsɔm taa. Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ muluu cikpem taa yɔ, emuluu ɖɔɖɔ sɔsɔm taa.

eng: If you havent been faithful with worldly wealth, who will trust you with true riches?.
local: Yee ɩtalabɩnɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ñɩm mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ yɔ lɛ, acɛlɩɣ-mɩ ñɩm maɣmaɣ siŋŋ yɔ?.

eng: If you havent been faithful with someone elses property, who will give you your own?.
local: Yee ɩtalabɩnɩ lɛlʋ ñɩm mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ yɔ lɛ, acɛlɩɣ-mɩ mɩ-ñɩmbʋ yɔ?.

eng: No household servant can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth. .
local: Tʋmlaɖʋ nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛla caanaa naalɛ tʋma. Yee ɛlakɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɛpaɖɩɣ lɛlʋ nɛ ɛsɔɔlɩ lɛlʋ. Yaa pʋtɔmaɣ yɔ, ɛnɩɣnɩ lɛlʋ nɛ eyele lɛlʋ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛɛ: ɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɩsɔɔlɩ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɩtɛ ɩwɛɛ ñɩm laɣzaɣ.».

eng: The Pharisees, who were money-lovers, heard all this and sneered at Jesus.
local: Yesu yɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Farɩsɩ ñɩma nɩɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩtɩŋa. Peeɖe powoŋ-i, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɛwɛ liidiye laɣzaɣ.

eng: He said to them, You are the ones who justify yourselves before other people, but God knows your hearts. What is highly valued by people is deeply offensive to God.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Ɩcaɣ se ɛyáa ɩna-mɩ se ɩkɛ kʋtʋʋzaa, ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ ñasɩm mɩ-lɩmaɣza tɩŋa. Mbʋ́ ɛyáa maɣzɩɣ se pɩkɩlɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɛɛcaɣ se ɛna-pʋ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa kpem.

eng: Until John, there was only the Law and the Prophets. Since then, the good news of Gods kingdom is preached, and everyone is urged to enter it.
local: Paawɩlɩ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ nɛ náyaa takayɩsɩ taa tɔm nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩtalɩ Lɩm Sɔʋ Sɔnzɩ Laɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ alɩwaatʋ. Đooo alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ papazɩ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm susuu nɛ paa weyi ɛlʋkɩ se ɛsʋʋ ka-taa.

eng: Its easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for the smallest stroke of a pen in the Law to drop out.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɛsɔdaa nɛ tɛtʋ pa-mʋʋ́ʋ fɛyɩ kaɖɛ nɛ pɩtalɩ paɣtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-mayaɣ nakɛyɛ mʋʋ́ʋ.».

eng: Any man who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and a man who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛɖɔɔnɩ ɛ-halʋ nɛ ɛkpaɣ kɩfalʋ yɔ, ɛlabɩ acɛyɛ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, yee pekizi halʋ nɛ nɔɔyʋ kpaɣ-ɩ yɔ, pʋdʋ labɩ acɛyɛ.».

eng: There was a certain rich man who clothed himself in purple and fine linen, and who feasted luxuriously every day.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tuu-wɛ eduuye se: «Abalʋ ñɩm tʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛnɩ ḿbʋ́, esuu awiitokonaa ɖeu ñɩlɩ ñɩlɩ nɛ ɛtɔkɩ leleŋ leleŋ paa ɖooye.

eng: At his gate lay a certain poor man named Lazarus who was covered with sores.
local: Kʋñɔndʋ nɔɔyʋ ñaawɛɛ, payaɣ-ɩ se Lazaarɩ. Ɛtɩŋa ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ heŋ tɔ tɔ nɛ ɛhɛnɩɣ ñɩm tʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-nɔnɔɔ taa.

eng: Lazarus longed to eat the crumbs that fell from the rich mans table. Instead, dogs would come and lick his sores.
local: Ɛɛsɔɔlaa siŋŋ se ɛtɔzɩ mbʋ́ ñɩm tʋ ɛnʋ ɛtɔkɩ nɛ pʋtɔlʋʋ tataa yɔ nɛ ɛtɔɔ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, hasɩ wokaɣ nɛ sineziɣ e-heŋ taa.

eng: The poor man died and was carried by angels to Abrahams side. The rich man also died and was buried.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye lɛ, kʋñɔndʋ ɛnʋ ɛsɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa kpaɣ-ɩ powoni-i lone kɩbanɖɛ taa Abraham cɔlɔ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ñɩm tʋ ɛnʋ ɛsɩ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ pebim-i.

eng: While being tormented in the place of the dead, he looked up and saw Abraham at a distance with Lazarus at his side.
local: nɛ ɛsʋʋ tɛtɛɛ ɖenɖe kʋñɔŋ maatɛŋ wɛɛ yɔ. Ɛwɛ peeɖe ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛkʋsɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ elosi Abraham poliŋ taa, Lazaarɩ wɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ.

eng: He shouted, Father Abraham, have mercy on me. Send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because Im suffering in this flame.
local: Peeɖe ɛmabɩ kubuka se: ‹Hayɩ mɔn-cɔzɔ Abraham! Na man-pʋtɔdɩyɛ nɛ ŋtiyi Lazaarɩ nɛ elii lɩm e-niye nɔɔ taa nɛ elizi me-liu tɛɛ nɛ piniɣdi-m. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, malakɩ kʋñɔŋ pɩŋŋ miŋ ɛnɛ ɛ-taa.›.

eng: But Abraham said, Child, remember that during your lifetime you received good things, whereas Lazarus received terrible things. Now Lazarus is being comforted and you are in great pain.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Abraham cosi-i se: ‹Man-sayɩ! Tɔzɩ se Lazaarɩ lakaɣ kʋñɔŋ kedeŋa yɔɔ lɛ, ñaawɛɛ ŋtɔkɩ leleŋ. Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ Lazaarɩ laŋɩyɛ ñɛhɛ cɩnɛ lɛ, ñalakɩ kʋñɔŋ.

eng: Moreover, a great crevasse has been fixed between us and you. Those who wish to cross over from here to you cannot. Neither can anyone cross from there to us.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, pɔʋ sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ wɛ ɖa nɛ mɩ ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa se paa nɔɔyʋ ɛcaɣ se ɛtɛzɩ mɩ-cɔlɔ yɔ, ɛtaapɩzɩ. Kʋ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ mɩ-cɔlɔ tʋ nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛlɩɩ ɛtɛzɩ ɖɔ-cɔlɔ cɩnɛ.›.

eng: The rich man said, Then I beg you, Father, send Lazarus to my fathers house.
local: Ɛlɛ́, abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛtasɩ-ɩ heyuu se: ‹Caa! Yee ḿbʋ́ mentendiɣ-ŋ se ŋtiyi Lazaarɩ man-caa ɖɩɣa taa.

eng: I have five brothers. He needs to warn them so that they dont come to this place of agony.
local: Mɛwɛnɩ newaa kagbaanzɩ. Ewolo ɛka pa-ñʋŋ taa ɖeu nɛ pataakɔɔ pasʋʋ kʋñɔŋ kʋnɛ kɩ-taa ɖɔɖɔ.›.

eng: Abraham replied, They have Moses and the Prophets. They must listen to them.
local: Abraham cosi-i se: ‹Ñe-newaa wɛnɩ Mooyiizi nɛ náyaa pa-takayɩsɩ. Pakalɩ-sɩ nɛ panɩɩ.›.

eng: The rich man said, No, Father Abraham! But if someone from the dead goes to them, they will change their hearts and lives.
local: Peeɖe abalʋ ɛnʋ eheyi-i se: ‹Caa Abraham! Yee sɩɖaa taa nɔɔyʋ ɛpɩsɩna nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ-wɛ yɔ, pɛlɛɣzɩɣ wɛtʋ.›.

eng: Abraham said, If they dont listen to Moses and the Prophets, then neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Abraham tasɩ-ɩ heyuu se: ‹Yee patanɩɩ Mooyiizi nɛ náyaa pa-takayɩsɩ taa tɔm yɔ, paa sɩɖaa taa nɔɔyʋ efema nɛ ewolo eheyi-wɛ yɔ, paatisiɣ ɛ-tɔm ɖɔɖɔ.› ».

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Things that cause people to trip and fall into sin must happen, but how terrible it is for the person through whom they happen.
local: Yesu tasɩ heyuu ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Mbʋ́ piyeki se ɛyáa ɛla kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ, pʋwɛ tam. Ɛlɛ́, pʋtɔɔ weyi pɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: It would be better for them to be thrown into a lake with a large stone hung around their neck than to cause one of these little ones to trip and fall into sin.
local: Pɩkɩlɩ wee wee se pɛlɛɛ e-liu tɛɛ pɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ nɛ powoni pɛpɛɖɩ teŋgu taa ɖenɖe ɛzɩ eeyele nɛ mbá patakpaɣ-wɛ payaa pʋyʋ yɔ pa-taa nɔɔyʋ la kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ.

eng: Watch yourselves! If your brother or sister sins, warn them to stop. If they change their hearts and lives, forgive them.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ! Yee ña-taabalʋ nɔɔyʋ ɛyʋsɩnɩ-ŋ yɔ, heyi-i se mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu. Yee etisaa nɛ ɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ lɛ, ŋkpaɣ ŋkpeɣ-i.

eng: Even if someone sins against you seven times in one day and returns to you seven times and says, I am changing my ways, you must forgive that person. .
local: Yee evemiye kʋɖʋmɖɩyɛ taa ɛyʋsɩnɩ-ŋ tam lʋbɛ nɛ ɛkɔŋ tam nɛ ewiɣni-ŋ se ɛyʋsɩnɩ-ŋ yɔ, kpaɣ ŋkpeɣ-i.».

eng: The apostles said to the Lord, Increase our faith.
local: Yesu tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, e-tiyiyaa heyi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Yele nɛ ɖɩtɛzɩ lidaʋ wɛnaʋ.».

eng: The Lord replied, If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it would obey you.
local: Peeɖe ecosi-wɛ se: «Yee ɩɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ ñɩɩɩ ɛzɩ piye nɖɩ́ ɖɩkɩlɩ kɔzʋʋ yɔ yɔ, ɩpɩzaɣ se iheyi tɩʋ sɔsɔʋ kʋnɛ se: ‹Kpɛzɩ ŋwolo ŋsɩŋ teŋgu taa!› Nɛ kɩla ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ.».

eng: Would any of you say to your servant, who had just come in from the field after plowing or tending sheep, Come! Sit down for dinner?.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ pɔzʋʋ se: «Yee mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛwɛnɩ tʋmlaɖʋ nɛ ɛhayɩɣ-ɩ yaa eɖiyiɣ-i wondu, ɛlɩɣnɩ hayɩm ɛtalɩ lɛ, ɩmaɣzɩɣ se pʋdʋ heyiɣ-i se we? Eheyiɣ-i se: ‹Caɣ nɛ ŋtɔɔ› yaa.

eng: Wouldnt you say instead, Fix my dinner. Put on the clothes of a table servant and wait on me while I eat and drink. After that, you can eat and drink?.
local: ‹Wolo ŋlɛɣzɩ wondu nɛ ŋwolo ŋlabɩ-m tɔɔnaɣ nɛ ŋkɔna. Mɛntɛm tɔɔʋ nɛ mɔñɔɔ lɛ, ŋŋcaɣ ŋtɔɔ nɛ ŋñɔɔ?›.

eng: You wont thank the servant because the servant did what you asked, will you?.
local: Tʋmlaɖʋ ɛnʋ, ɛ-caa sɛɣ-ɩ mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ yaa we?.

eng: In the same way, when you have done everything required of you, you should say, We servants deserve no special praise. We have only done our duty. .
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛnɩ-mɩ. Yee ɩkɔm ɩla mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ kaɖʋ-mɩ yɔ nɛ pɩtɛ lɛ, ɩtɔ se: ‹Đɩtamʋnɩ se ɖɩkɛ tʋmlaɖaa. Mbʋ́ pɩɩwɛɛ se ɖɩla yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɩlaba.› ».

eng: On the way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
local: Yesu kʋyaa se ewoki Yeruusalɛm lɛ, ɛtɩŋnɩ Samaarɩɩ nɛ Galilee pa-ageeta taa.

eng: As he entered a village, ten men with skin diseases approached him. Keeping their distance from him.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛñɔtɩnɩ tɛtʋ cikpetu natʋyʋ. Ɛsʋwaɣ tɩ-taa lɛ, canasɩ nasɩyɩ hiu kʋyɩ se sɩkatɩɣ-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, sɩsɩŋ poliŋ.

eng: they raised their voices and said, Jesus, Master, show us mercy.
local: nɛ sɩmakɩ kubusi se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Yesu! Na ɖa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ.».

eng: When Jesus saw them, he said, Go, show yourselves to the priests. As they left, they were cleansed.
local: Yesu na-sɩ lɛ, eheyi-sɩ se: «Iwolo ɩwɩlɩ mɩ-tɩ cɔjɔnaa nɛ pana.» Sɩkpaɣ nʋmɔʋ se siwoki lɛ, sɩ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: One of them, when he saw that he had been healed, returned and praised God with a loud voice.
local: Sɩ-taa lɛɛka nawa se kɔ-yɔɔ cɛba lɛ, kakpaɣ nʋmɔʋ pɩsʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ kokou nɛ kasaŋ Ɛsɔ kpamm.

eng: He fell on his face at Jesus feet and thanked him. He was a Samaritan.
local: Katalɩ Yesu cɔlɔ lɛ, koluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ nɛ kɛsɛɛ-ɩ. Ŋgʋ́, kaakɛ Samaarɩɩ tʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, Werent ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?.
local: Peeɖe Yesu tɔm se: «Pɩtɛkɛ ɛyáa hiu yɔɔ cɛbɩna naa we? Nɛ kʋɖʋm fɛyɩ hiu ñɛwɛ le yɔ?.

eng: No one returned to praise God except this foreigner? .
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma pa-taa nɔɔyʋ tatɩɩmaɣzɩ se ɛpɩsɩɣ ɛsa Ɛsɔ ɛzɩ ɛgɔm ɛnɛ yɔ?».

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Get up and go. Your faith has healed you. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi Samaarɩɩ tʋ ɛnʋ se: «Kʋyɩ ŋkpe, ñe-lidaʋ yabɩ ña-ñʋʋ.».

eng: Pharisees asked Jesus when Gods kingdom was coming. He replied, Gods kingdom isnt coming with signs that are easily noticed.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Farɩsɩ ñɩma pɔzɩ Yesu se: «Đooye Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ kaɣ kɔm» Peeɖe ecosi-wɛ se: «Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ kɔŋʋʋ yɔ, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛna-kɛ.

eng: Nor will people say, Look, here it is! or There it is! Dont you see? Gods kingdom is already among you. .
local: Paakaɣ yɔɔdʋʋ se kɛwɛ cɩnɛ yaa peeɖe yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɩtɩlɩ ɖeu se kɛtɛm wɛʋ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa.».

eng: Then Jesus said to the disciples, The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Human One, and you wont see it.
local: Ɛtɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, eheyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ kɔŋ nɛ ɩñɩnɩ se ɩna Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ kɩyakʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ maɣmaɣ, ɛlɛ́, ɩɩnaɣ-ɩ.

eng: People will say to you, Look there! or Look here! Dont leave or go chasing after them.
local: Pɔkɔŋ-mɩ heyuu se ɛwɛ cɩnɛ yaa peeɖe yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɩtaase iwolo se ɩñɩnɩɣ-ɩ.

eng: The Human One will appear on his day in the same way that a flash of lightning lights up the sky from one end to the other.
local: Kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ lɩzɩɣ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ yɔ, pʋwɛɣ ɛzɩ tɛʋ ñɩlɩsʋʋ ɛsɔdaa nɛ pɩnaɣ paa le yɔ.

eng: However, first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pʋcɔ pɩla ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ nɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɛyáa panɛ pekizi-i.

eng: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be during the days of the Human One.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, pɩlakɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ pɩɩlabʋ Nowee alɩwaatʋ taa yɔ:.

eng: People were eating, drinking, marrying, and being given in marriage until the day Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all.
local: ɛyáa tɔkaɣ nɛ pɔñɔʋ nɛ abalaa kpakɩɣ halaa nɛ halaa ñawalɩɣ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Nowee sʋ kpɩyʋʋ sɔsɔʋ taa nɛ tɛʋ sɔsɔʋ pazɩ nɩʋ nɛ kʋkʋ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ yɔ.

eng: Likewise in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, and building.
local: Pɩlakɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ pɩɩlabʋ Looti alɩwaatʋ taa yɔ: ɛyáa tɔkaɣ nɛ pɔñɔʋ nɛ payakɩ wondu nɛ pɛpɛdɩɣ nɛ pahayɩɣ nɛ pamaɣ kuduyiŋ.

eng: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
local: Ɛlɛ́, puwiye nɖɩ́ Looti kʋyɩ Sodom yɔ, miŋ nɛ mamala palɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa ɛzɩ tɛʋ nɩɣʋ yɔ nɛ pitii pɩñaɣ patɩŋa payɩ.

eng: Thats the way it will be on the day the Human One is revealed.
local: Kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ lɩzɩɣ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pɩkaɣ labʋ.

eng: On that day, those on the roof, whose possessions are in the house, shouldnt come down to grab them. Likewise, those in the field shouldnt turn back.
local: Puwiye ńɖɩ́, putudi nɔɔyʋ e-kuduyuu ñʋʋ taa yɔ, pɩtaacɩbɩ-ɩ se etiki ɛsʋʋ kɩ-tɛɛ nɛ ɛkpaɣ ɛ-ñɩm. Yaa yee putudi nɔɔyʋ hayɩm taa yɔ, pɩtaacɩbɩ-ɩ se ɛpɩsɩɣ ɖɩɣa.

eng: Remember Lots wife!.
local: Ɩtɔzɩ Looti halʋ tɔm.

eng: Whoever tries to preserve their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life will preserve it.
local: Weyi ɛñɩnɩɣ se ɛcaɣ wezuu tam yɔ, ɛtɛŋnɩ-kʋ lesuu. Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ lesiɣ-kʋ yɔ, ehikiɣ-kʋ.

eng: I tell you, on that night two people will be in the same bed: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: puwiye ńɖɩ́ lɛ, ɛyáa naalɛ pɩzɩɣ pɛhɛnɩ ñalʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ yɔɔ ɖoó taa nɛ pakpaɣ kʋɖʋm nɛ piyele lɛlʋ.

eng: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left. .
local: Halaa naalɛ pɩzɩɣ pɛwɛɛ namɩyɛ yɔɔ nɛ pakpaɣ kʋɖʋm nɛ piyele lɛlʋ. .

eng: The disciples asked, Where, Lord? Jesus said, The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body. .
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Le pɩkaɣ labʋ ḿbʋ́?» Ɛlɛ́, etuu-wɛ eduuye se: «Namɖazʋʋ wayɩ kpeliŋ fɛtɩɣ.».

eng: Jesus was telling them a parable about their need to pray continuously and not to be discouraged.
local: Yesu tasɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tuuu eduuye se ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ se pʋwɛɛ se pɛwɛɛ Ɛsɔ sɛʋ yɔɔ tam. Pɩtaatii po-toma taa kaʋ.

eng: He said, In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected people.
local: Eduuye ńɖɩ́ yɔ se: «Wiyaʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛnɩ tɛtʋ natʋyʋ taa, ɛɛñaŋ Ɛsɔ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɛɛcɔŋnɩ nɔɔyʋ se ɛkɛ pʋyʋ.

eng: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him, asking, Give me justice in this case against my adversary.
local: Lelu nɔɔyʋ ñaawɛ peeɖe nɛ ewoki wiyaʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-cɔlɔ tam se ɛhʋʋ ɛ nɛ e-koyindu pɔ-tɔm nɛ eɖezi-i.

eng: For a while he refused but finally said to himself, I dont fear God or respect people.
local: Ɛlɛ́, eekeŋni-i. Pʋkɔma pɩnɩɩ wiyaʋ ɛnʋ lɛ, ɛtɔ se: ‹Toovenim lɛ, paa maañaŋ Ɛsɔ nɛ maacɔŋnɩ nɔɔyʋ se ɛkɛ pʋyʋ yɔ.

eng: but I will give this widow justice because she keeps bothering me. Otherwise, there will be no end to her coming here and embarrassing me. .
local: ɖenɖe lelu ɛnɛ ɛkɔŋ tam nɛ ɛcɛyɩɣ-m yɔ, manɩɣnɩ-ɩ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, ɛwɛɣ kɔm yɔɔ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛtɛzɩɣ-m cɛyʋʋ.› ».

eng: The Lord said, Listen to what the unjust judge says.
local: Peeɖe Kɩpaɣlʋ heyi-wɛ se: «Iwelesi camɩyɛ nɛ ɩnɩɩ tɔm ndʋ́ wiyaʋ ɛzaɣ́ʋ ɛnɛ ɛyɔɔdaa yɔ.

eng: Wont God provide justice to his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he be slow to help them?.
local: Ɩmaɣzɩɣ se Ɛsɔ ñaaɖeziɣ ɛ-ñɩma mbá pamakɩ kubusi nɛ payaɣ-ɩ wɩsɩ nɛ ɖoó yɔ yaa we? Pileɖiɣ nɛ pʋcɔ eɖezi-wɛ yaa we?.

eng: I tell you, he will give them justice quickly. But when the Human One comes, will he find faithfulness on earth? .
local: Mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: eɖeziɣ-wɛ lɔŋ. Ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ kɔŋ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ yɔ, ɛnaɣ ɛyáa mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ yaa we?».

eng: Jesus told this parable to certain people who had convinced themselves that they were righteous and who looked on everyone else with disgust:.
local: Yesu tuuu ɖɔɖɔ mbá pamaɣzaɣ se ḿbá lɛ kʋtʋʋzaa Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa nɛ pɔcɔŋnɩ lɛlaa se pɛtɛkɛ pʋyʋ yɔ eduuye se:.

eng: Two people went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
local: «Ɛyáa naalɛ nabɛyɛ wobina Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa se pɛsɛɣ Ɛsɔ. Lɛlʋ kɛ Farɩsɩ tʋ nɛ lɛlʋ ñɛkɛ tikee mʋyʋ.

eng: The Pharisee stood and prayed about himself with these words, God, I thank you that Im not like everyone else—crooks, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
local: Peeɖe Farɩsɩ tʋ sɩŋaa nɛ ɛsɛɛ Ɛsɔ se: ‹Hayɩ Ɛsɔ! Mansaŋ-ŋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɛnfɛyɩ ɛzɩ lɛlaa mbá pamɩlɩɣ nɛ palakɩ ɛzaɣ́tʋ nɛ acɛyɛ yɔ. Mansaŋ-ŋ ɖɔɖɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɛnfɛyɩ ɛzɩ tikee mʋyʋ ɛnɛ yɔ.

eng: I fast twice a week. I give a tenth of everything I receive.
local: Kpɩtaʋ taa lɛ, kɩyakɩŋ naalɛ mɔhɔkʋʋ nɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa mehikiɣ yɔ, mantayɩɣ-pʋ tam hiu nɛ mahaɣ-ŋ hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ.›.

eng: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He wouldnt even lift his eyes to look toward heaven. Rather, he struck his chest and said, God, show mercy to me, a sinner.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, tikee mʋyʋ ñaasɩŋ poliŋ nɛ ɛsɔ ɛ-ñʋʋ nɛ tɛtʋ nɛ ekpiɖiɣ se: ‹Hayɩ Ɛsɔ! Na weyi ma mɛnkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖʋ yɔ man-pʋtɔdɩyɛ!›.

eng: I tell you, this person went down to his home justified rather than the Pharisee. All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up. .
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se Ɛsɔ kpeɣ abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛ-kɩwɛɛkɩm nɛ pʋcɔ ɛpɩsɩ ɛ-tɛ. Ɛlɛ́, eteɖezi Farɩsɩ tʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛkpazɩɣ ɛ-tɩ yɔ, petisiɣ-i. Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ñetisiɣ ɛ-tɩ yɔ, pakpazɩɣ-ɩ.».

eng: People were bringing babies to Jesus so that he would bless them. When the disciples saw this, they scolded them.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛyáa wokini piya ajamaa Yesu cɔlɔ se ɛtɩnɩ sɩ-yɔɔ nesi nɛ ɛwazɩ sɩ-yɔɔ. Ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa na-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, paɖaŋ-wɛ.

eng: Then Jesus called them to him and said, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Peeɖe Yesu tiyaa payaa piya ńzɩ́ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Iyele piya ajamaa nɛ pɔkɔɔ mɔn-cɔlɔ, ɩtaaɖaŋ-wɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mbá pɛwɛ ɛzɩ piya ajamaa ḿbá yɔ lɛ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa ñɩma.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se: yee nɔɔyʋ etetisi se Ɛsɔ tɔkɩnɩ kewiyaɣ ɛzɩ piya cikpesi tisuu yɔ yɔ, ɛɛsʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ e-Kewiyaɣ taa.».

eng: A certain ruler asked Jesus, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ nɔɔyʋ pɔzɩ Yesu se: «Wɩlɩyʋ kɩbanʋ! Ɛbɛ pʋwɛɛ se mala nɛ mehiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ?».

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ecosi-i se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ŋyaɣ-m se kɩbanʋ? Nɔɔyʋ tɛkɛ kɩbanʋ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ Ɛsɔ.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit adultery. Dont murder. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony. Honor your father and mother. .
local: Paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma ŋsɩm Ɛsɔ paɣtʋ ndʋ́ paɖʋwa se: taala acɛyɛ, taakʋ ɛyʋ, taamɩlɩ, taacɛtɩnɩ lɛlʋ taa nɛ ŋña ña-caa nɛ ño-ɖoo yɔ?».

eng: Then the ruler said, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Peeɖe abalʋ ɛnʋ eheyi-i se: «Mɔnɖɔm paɣtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩtɩŋa tɩ-yɔɔ ɖooo me-evebutu taa.».

eng: When Jesus heard this, he said, Theres one more thing. Sell everything you own and distribute the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me. .
local: Eheyi Yesu ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ heyi-i se: «Pʋyʋ kʋɖʋm koŋ pɩkazaa se ŋtɛzɩ labʋ. Wolo ŋpɛdɩ mbʋ́ ŋwɛna yɔ pɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ ŋtayɩ kʋñɔndɩnaa pi-liidiye nɛ ŋwɛnɩ ñɩm ɛsɔdaa. Yee ŋlabɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ŋkɔɔ ŋtɩŋ-m.».

eng: When he heard these words, the man became sad because he was extremely rich.
local: Eheyi-i ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ laŋɩyɛ wɛɛkɩ pɩŋŋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛwɛnɩ ñɩm pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he said, Its very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Yesu nawa se ɛ-laŋɩyɛ wɛɛkaa lɛ, ɛtɔ se: «Pʋwɛ kaɖɛ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ se ñɩm tɩnaa ɛsʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa!.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom. .
local: Pʋcɔ ñɩm tʋ nɔɔyʋ sʋʋ ka-taa lɛ, pʋwɛ kaɖɛ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ɛzɩ kpaŋnɔ siɣá naalɛ tʋ sʋʋnɩ ɛjɔʋ pɔʋ taa yɔ.».

eng: Those who heard this said, Then who can be saved.
local: Peeɖe mbá pewelesaɣnɩ-ɩ yɔ, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Yee ḿbʋ́, apɩzɩɣ ehiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ yɔ?».

eng: Jesus replied, What is impossible for humans is possible for God. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Mbʋ́ ɛyáa ɛɛpɩzɩɣ labʋ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ñapɩzɩɣ ɛlabɩ-pʋ.».

eng: Peter said, Look, we left everything we own and followed you.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Pɩyɛ heyi-i se: «Cɔnɩ ŋna ɖa ɖeyebu mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa ɖɩwɛna yɔ nɛ ɖɩtɩŋ ña-wayɩ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you that anyone who has left house, husband, wife, brothers, sisters, parents, or children because of Gods kingdom.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se yee nɔɔyʋ eyebi ɛ-ɖɩɣa yaa ɛ-halʋ yaa ɛ-ɖalaa yaa e-newaa yaa ɛ-caa nɛ e-ɖoo yaa e-piya Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: will receive many times more in this age and eternal life in the coming age. .
local: Ɛsɔ yeki nɛ ehiɣ alɩwaatʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-taa nɛ pɩkɩlɩ mbʋ́ eyeba yɔ. Piyele ndʋ́ tɩkɔŋ yɔ tɩ-taa lɛ, ehikiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ.».

eng: Jesus took the Twelve aside and said, Look, were going up to Jerusalem, and everything written about the Human One by the prophets will be accomplished.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yesu kɛsɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩna, ɖɩkpaɣ Yeruusalɛm. Peeɖe Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ náyaa kamawa yɔ, tɩkɔŋ labʋ.

eng: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. He will be ridiculed, mistreated, and spit on.
local: Pɔkɔŋ-ɩ kpaʋ nɛ paɖʋzɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pe-nesi taa nɛ powoŋ-i nɛ patʋʋ-ɩ nɛ pɔtɔ ɛ-yɔɔ ndaatʋ.

eng: After torturing him, they will kill him. On the third day, he will rise up. .
local: Pɔkɔŋ-ɩ luu agbazɛɛ nɛ pakʋ-ɩ. Kɩyakɩŋ naadozo wiye lɛ, Ɛsɔ fezi-i.».

eng: But the Twelve understood none of these words. The meaning of this message was hidden from them and they didnt grasp what he was saying.
local: Yesu yɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tatɩɩnɩʋ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɩɩwɛ-wɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ. Pataaɖiɣzini mbʋ́ pʋ-tɔm ɛyɔɔdaɣ yɔ.

eng: As Jesus came to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting beside the road begging.
local: Yesu ɖɛɣaɣ Yeruusalɛm ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛñɔtɩnɩ Yeriikoo. Yʋlʋm nɔɔyʋ kacaɣ nʋmɔʋ nɔɔ nɛ ɛsʋlʋmʋʋ.

eng: When the man heard the crowd passing by, he asked what was happening.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ ɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, ɛnɩɩ ka-taŋ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ se: «Ɛbɛ lakɩɣ?».

eng: They told him, Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.
local: Ɛlɛ́, peheyi-i se: «Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu ɖɛɣna.».

eng: The blind man shouted, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Peeɖe ɛmabɩ kubuka se: «Wiyaʋ Daaviidi Sayɩ Yesu! Na man-pʋtɔdɩyɛ!».

eng: Those leading the procession scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mbá paawɛ nɔɔ yɔ, pakalɩ-ɩ se: «Su peeɖe!» Pɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛtɛzɩ mabʋ kubuka se: «Wiyaʋ Daaviidi Sayɩ! Na man-pʋtɔdɩyɛ!».

eng: Jesus stopped and called for the man to be brought to him. When he was present Jesus asked.
local: Peeɖe Yesu sɩŋaa nɛ ɛha nɔɔ se powolo pɔhɔm-ɩ nɛ powoni-i ɛ-cɔlɔ. Powoni-i lɛ, Yesu pɔzɩ-ɩ se:.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? He said, Lord, I want to see.
local: «Ŋcaɣ se malabɩ-ŋ we?» Ecosi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! La nɛ mɛ-ɛsɛ kuli.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Receive your sight! Your faith has healed you. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-i se: «Ñɛ-ɛsɛ ekuli! Lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ŋwɛnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, kɩyabɩ ña-ñʋʋ.».

eng: At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they praised God too.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ-ɛsɛ kuli nɛ ɛtɩŋɩɣ Yesu wayɩ nɛ ɛsaŋ Ɛsɔ. Ɛyáa samaɣ tɩŋa na ḿbʋ́ lɛ, kasʋʋ Ɛsɔ sam yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through town.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu sʋʋ Yeriikoo tɛtʋ taa se ɛɖɛɣ.

eng: A man there named Zacchaeus, a ruler among tax collectors, was rich.
local: Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe, payaɣ-ɩ se Zaacee. Ɛkɛ tikee mʋyaa sɔsɔ, ɛwɛnɩ ñɩm sakɩyɛ.

eng: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but, being a short man, he couldnt because of the crowd.
local: Ɛñɩnaɣ se ɛna weyi payaɣ se Yesu yɔ, ɛlɛ́, ɛtapɩzɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛyáa kɩlɩ ɖɔʋ. Ŋgʋ́, ɛtaɖaɣlɩ.

eng: So he ran ahead and climbed up a sycamore tree so he could see Jesus, who was about to pass that way.
local: Peeɖe esewa ɛɖɛɛ nɔɔ nɛ ewolo ɛkpa tɩʋ nakʋyʋ yɔɔ se Yesu ɛkɔm ɛɖɛɣ lɛ, ɛna-ɩ.

eng: When Jesus came to that spot, he looked up and said, Zacchaeus, come down at once. I must stay in your home today. .
local: Yesu talɩ tɩʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-tɛɛ lɛ, ɛkpazɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ ɛna-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-i se: «Zaacee tii lɔŋ! Sɔnɔ ñɛ-tɛ mansʋʋ.».

eng: So Zacchaeus came down at once, happy to welcome Jesus.
local: Peeɖe Zaacee titiba nɛ ɛ nɛ Yesu powolo ɛ-tɛ nɛ ɛmʋ-ɩ nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ.

eng: Everyone who saw this grumbled, saying, He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.
local: Mbá patɩŋa pana ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pɩtamaɣ-wɛ nɛ pemidiɣ pa-taa pe-ɖeke se: «Halɩ ɛkʋyaa nɛ ewolo ɛsʋʋ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖʋ tɛ!».

eng: Zacchaeus stopped and said to the Lord, Look, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor. And if I have cheated anyone, I repay them four times as much.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Zaacee kʋyɩ nɛ eheyi Yesu se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Meheyiɣ-ŋ se mantayɩɣ ma-ñɩm tam naalɛ nɛ mankpaɣ hɔɔlʋʋ maha kʋñɔndɩnaa. Yee maamuli nɔɔyʋ nɛ mɔntɔɔ e-liidiye yɔ, mɛhɛyɩ-ɩ ɖɩ-tam naanza.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Today, salvation has come to this household because he too is a son of Abraham.
local: Peeɖe Yesu tɔm se: «Sɔnɔ ɖɩɣa kanɛ ka-taa ɛyáa tɩŋa hiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Zaacee kɛ Abraham sayɩ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: The Human One came to seek and save the lost. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ kɔm se ɛñɩnɩ mbá peleba yɔ nɛ ɛya pa-ñʋʋ.».

eng: As they listened to this, Jesus told them another parable because he was near Jerusalem and they thought Gods kingdom would appear right away.
local: Ɛyáa welesaɣ ndʋ́ Yesu yɔɔdaɣ yɔ. Peeɖe ɛtasɩ-wɛ tuuu eduuye. Toovenim lɛ se pɔñɔtɩnɩ Yeruusalɛm, nɛ ɛyáa maɣzɩɣ se Ɛsɔ kewiyaɣ kɔŋ kpaagbaa nɛ pana-kɛ nɛ pɛ-ɛsɛ. Pʋ-yɔɔ etuu-wɛ eduuye se:.

eng: He said, A certain man who was born into royalty went to a distant land to receive his kingdom and then return.
local: «Nakʋyʋ wiye ɛyʋ sɔsɔ nɔɔyʋ kʋyaa ewolo ɛjaɖɛ poliŋ ñɩnɖɛ naɖɩyɛ taa wiyaʋ sɔsɔ cɔlɔ se ɛcɛlɩ-ɩ kewiyaɣ nɛ ɛɛpɩsɩ ɛcɔnɩ ɛ-ɛjaɖɛ taa ñɩma yɔɔ.

eng: He called together ten servants and gave each of them money worth four months wages. He said, Do business with this until I return.
local: Pʋcɔ ɛɖɛɛ lɛ, ɛya ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa hiu nɛ ɛtayɩ-wɛ sika pɔɔyaɣ pɔɔyaɣ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: ‹Ɩlabɩnɩ tadɩyɛ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mɔnkɔma yɔ.›.

eng: His citizens hated him, so they sent a representative after him who said, We dont want this man to be our king.
local: Ŋgʋ́, ɛ-ɛjaɖɛ taa ñɩma paɖaɣ-ɩ. Ɛɖɛwa lɛ, pakʋsɩ ɛyáa se powolo peheyi wiyaʋ sɔsɔ ɛnʋ se pɔtɔsɔɔlɩ se ɛyʋ ɛnʋ ɛkɛ pe-wiyaʋ.

eng: After receiving his kingdom, he returned and called the servants to whom he had given the money to find out how much they had earned.
local: Ewoba nɛ pɛcɛlɩ-ɩ kewiyaɣ lɛ, ɛpɩsɩ nɛ ekpeɣli ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa mbá ɛɛcɛlɩ-wɛ sika pɔɔyɩsɩ yɔ se ɛna se ɛzɩma ɛzɩma pɩsɛ-wɛ.

eng: The first servant came forward and said, Your money has earned a return of one thousand percent.
local: Peeɖe kajalaɣ tʋ kʋyaa nɛ eheyi-i se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Sika pɔɔyaɣ ŋgá ŋcɛlɩ-m yɔ, pɩsɛ-m kɔ-yɔɔ pɔɔyɩsɩ hiu.›.

eng: The king replied, Excellent! You are a good servant. Because you have been faithful in a small matter, you will have authority over ten cities.
local: Ɛlɛ́, wiyaʋ heyi-i se: ‹Pʋwɛ ɖeu, ŋkɛ tʋmlaɖʋ kɩbanʋ. Đenɖe ŋlabɩ mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ yɔ cikpem taa yɔ, ŋtɔkɩ kewiyaɣ tɛtʋ hiu taa.›.

eng: The second servant came and said, Master, your money has made a return of five hundred percent.
local: Naalɛ tʋ ñakʋyɩ nɛ eheyi-i se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Sika pɔɔyaɣ ŋgá ŋcɛlɩ-m yɔ, pɩsɛ-m kɔ-yɔɔ pɔɔyɩsɩ kagbaanzɩ.›.

eng: To this one, the king said, You will have authority over five cities.
local: Peeɖe wiyaʋ heyi ɛ́lɛ́ se: ‹Ña ñɔtɔkɩ kewiyaɣ tɛtʋ kagbaanzɩ taa.›.

eng: Another servant came and said, Master, here is your money. I wrapped it up in a scarf for safekeeping.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, tozo tʋ ñakʋyɩ nɛ eheyi-i se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Sika pɔɔyaɣ ŋgá ŋcɛlɩ-m yɔ, ŋ́gá yɔ. Mɔhɔkɩ-kɛ pɩsaacemɖe taa nɛ mansɩɩ.

eng: I was afraid of you because you are a stern man. You withdraw what you havent deposited and you harvest what you havent planted.
local: Ŋwɛ-m sɔɔndʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ñɔ-tɔm wɛ kaɖɛ. Mbʋ́ ŋtasɩɩ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ŋkpakɩɣ, nɛ mbʋ́ ŋtoɖuu yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ŋcaɣ se ŋkʋ.›.

eng: The king replied, I will judge you by the words of your own mouth, you worthless servant! You knew, did you, that Im a stern man, withdrawing what I didnt deposit, and harvesting what I didnt plant?.
local: Peeɖe wiyaʋ sɔsɔ ɛnʋ eheyi-i se: ‹Tʋmlaɖʋ ɛzaɣ́ʋ ña! Ñɔ-nɔɔ taa tɔm mahʋʋnɩ-ŋ. Ŋsɩm ḿbʋ́ se mɔn-tɔm wɛ kaɖɛ nɛ mbʋ́ mantasɩɩ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ mankpakɩɣ nɛ mbʋ́ montoɖúú yɔ, ḿbʋ́ mankʋŋ lɛ.

eng: Why then didnt you put my money in the bank? Then when I arrived, at least I could have gotten it back with interest.
local: pɩla ɛzɩma ŋtowoni men-sika pɔɔyaɣ nɛ ŋɖʋ liidiye kozaɣ taa yɔ? Tɛtɛɣ mɔnkɔm ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, maamaɣnɩ pʋsɔzɩ-m kɔ-yɔɔ nɛ mɛnkpɛndɩ mamʋ.›.

eng: He said to his attendants, Take his money and give it to the one who has ten times as much.
local: Peeɖe eheyi ɛ-cɔlɔ ñɩma se: ‹Ɩmʋ pɔɔyaɣ ŋ́gá lɔŋ e-nesi taa nɛ ɩsɔzɩ weyi ɛwɛnɩ pɔɔyɩsɩ hiu yɔ.›.

eng: But Master, they said, he already has ten times as much.
local: Paba heyi-i se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ɛ́lɛ́ tɛm wɛnaʋ pɔɔyɩsɩ hiu!›.

eng: He replied, I say to you that everyone who has will be given more, but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
local: Peeɖe wiyaʋ ɛnʋ ecosi-wɛ se: ‹Meheyiɣ-mɩ se weyi ɛwɛna yɔ, pɔsɔzʋʋ-ɩ nɛ pʋɖɔɔ-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛ́lɛ́ fɛyɩnɩ pʋyʋ yɔ, halɩ pɛtɛzɩɣ lɛɣʋ mbʋ́ ɛmaɣzɩɣ se ɛwɛna yɔ.

eng: As for my enemies who dont want me as their king, bring them here and slaughter them before me. .
local: Piyele mon-koyindinaa mbá pekizaa se mantaatɔɔ kewiyaɣ yɔ, ɩkɔnɩ-wɛ cɩnɛ nɛ ɩkʋ-wɛ mɛ-ɛsɩndaa.› ».

eng: After Jesus said this, he continued on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
local: Yesu tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛɖɛɛ ɛyáa samaɣ nɔɔ se ewoki Yeruusalɛm.

eng: As Jesus came to Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he gave two disciples a task.
local: Pɔñɔtɩnɩ Bɛtfaajee nɛ Betaanii pɔ-hɔɔlʋʋ taa Somiŋ Pʋʋ cɔlɔ lɛ, etiyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa naalɛ se:.

eng: He said, Go into the village over there. When you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: «Iwolo tɛtʋ cikpetu ndʋ́ tɩwɛ ɛsɩndaa yɔ tɩ-taa. Ɩtalɩɣ lɛ, ɩmakɩnɩ paɖɩ kpaŋaɣ piye naɖɩyɛ. Nɔɔyʋ tacaɣdɩ ɖɩ-yɔɔ kaʋ. Ɩkpɛzɩ-ɖɩ nɛ ɩkɔna.

eng: If anyone asks, Why are you untying it? just say, Its master needs it. .
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛpɔzɩ-mɩ se: ‹Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ɩkpɛzɩɣ-ɖɩ› lɛ, icosi-i se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ liu wɛ-ɖɩ.› ».

eng: Those who had been sent found it exactly as he had said.
local: Peeɖe pɛɖɛwa nɛ patalɩ pamaɣnɩ pɩtɩŋa ɛzɩ eeheyuu-wɛ yɔ.

eng: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, Why are you untying the colt.
local: Pɛkpɛzaɣ kpaŋaɣ piye ńɖɩ́ lɛ, ɖɩ-tɩnaa pɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ɩkpɛzɩɣ kpaŋaɣ piye ńɖɩ́?».

eng: They replied, Its master needs it.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pocosi-wɛ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ liu wɛ-ɖɩ.».

eng: They brought it to Jesus, threw their clothes on the colt, and lifted Jesus onto it.
local: Peeɖe powoni-ɖɩ Yesu cɔlɔ nɛ pɔhɔzɩ po-wondu lɛɛtʋ nɛ pɔpɔ ɖɩ-yɔɔ nɛ pakpaɣ-ɩ pacaɣzɩ.

eng: As Jesus rode along, they spread their clothes on the road.
local: Ɛcaɣ ɖɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛɖɛɣ lɛ, ɛyáa hɔzʋʋ po-wondu lɛɛtʋ nɛ pɔpɔʋ nʋmɔʋ taa ɖenɖe ɛtɩŋaɣ yɔ.

eng: As Jesus approached the road leading down from the Mount of Olives, the whole throng of his disciples began rejoicing. They praised God with a loud voice because of all the mighty things they had seen.
local: Ɛñɔtɩnɩ Yeruusalɛm ɛzɩ eetii Somiŋ Pʋʋ lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa sakɩyɛ mbá patɩŋaɣ-ɩ yɔ, pa-laŋa hʋlʋmɩ patɩŋa. Peeɖe papazɩ kou nɛ pasaŋ Ɛsɔ, hama lakasɩ nzɩ́ sɩtɩŋa ɛlaba nɛ pana yɔ sɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: They said, Blessings on the king who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest heavens.
local: Pasaŋaɣ-ɩ se: «Ɛsɔ ɛ́wazɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ weyi ɛkɔŋ Kɩpaɣlʋ lone taa yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ! Laŋhɛzɩyɛ ɛwɛɛ ɖooo ɛsɔdaa nɛ pasam-ɩ peeɖe!».
