eng: Some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to Jesus, Teacher, scold your disciples! Tell them to stop.
local: Peeɖe Farɩsɩ ñɩma mbá paawɛ ɛyáa samaɣ taa peeɖe yɔ, pa-taa nabɛyɛ heyi Yesu se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Kalɩ ñɔ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ḿbá se posu.».

eng: He answered, I tell you, if they were silent, the stones would shout. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Meheyiɣ-mɩ se paa ɛyáa ḿbá posuma yɔ, pɛɛ maɣmaɣ kʋyʋʋ ama kubusi!».

eng: As Jesus came to the city and observed it, he wept over it.
local: Yesu ñɔtɩnɩ Yeruusalɛm tɛtʋ lɛ, ɛna-tʋ. Peeɖe ewi tɩ-taa ñɩma.

eng: He said, If only you knew on this of all days the things that lead to peace. But now they are hidden from your eyes.
local: nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Yee ɩɩtɩɩsɩm mbʋ́ pɩhaɣ-mɩ laŋhɛzɩyɛ sɔnɔ kɩyakʋ kʋnɛ yɔ paa ñɩɩɩ yɔ, iihiɣ-ɖɩ! Ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, pʋwɛ-mɩ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ.

eng: The time will come when your enemies will build fortifications around you, encircle you, and attack you from all sides.
local: Mi-koyindinaa kɔŋ kɩyakɩŋ nɩɩyɩ taa nɛ pama koloŋa sɔsɔɔ nakɛyɛ nɛ kɔcɔɔ-mɩ kata nɛ pakpatɩ-mɩ nɛ.

eng: They will crush you completely, you and the people within you. They wont leave one stone on top of another within you, because you didnt recognize the time of your gracious visit from God. .
local: pakʋ mɩtɩŋa payɩ. Pɔyɔkʋʋ mɩ-ɖɩsɩ, pɩɩkazɩɣ pɩyɛ naɖɩyɛ se ɖɩwɛ lɛɛɖɛ yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtaatɩlɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ kaakɔm se ɛya mɩ-ñʋʋ yɔ.».

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, he threw out those who were selling things there.
local: Yesu talɩ Yeruusalɛm lɛ, ɛsʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa. Ɛsʋwa lɛ, ɛpazɩ tadɩyɛ tɩnaa ɖɔɔnʋʋ.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be a house of prayer, but you have made it a hideout for crooks. .
local: nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɛsɔ yɔɔdaa nɛ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Man-ɖɩɣa kɛɣnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ.› Ŋgʋ́, mɩ mɩpɩsɩ-kɛ mɩ́láa ñɩŋga.».

eng: Jesus was teaching daily in the temple. The chief priests, the legal experts, and the foremost leaders among the people were seeking to kill him.
local: Yesu susaɣ Ɛsɔtɔm Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa paa ɖooye nɛ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Yuda ñɩma sɔsaa pañɩnɩɣ se pakʋ-ɩ.

eng: However, they couldnt find a way to do it because all the people were enthralled with what they heard.
local: Ɛlɛ́, paanaɣ se pala ɛzɩma. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛyáa samaɣ tɩŋa welesaɣ ɛ-tɔm siŋŋ.

eng: On one of the days when Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders approached him.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu wɩlaɣ ɛyáa Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Yuda ñɩma sɔsaa powolo.

eng: They said, Tell us: What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Aha-ŋ nɔɔ se ŋla lakasɩ ńzɩ́? Aha-ŋ waɖɛ se ŋla ḿbʋ́?».

eng: He replied, I have a question for you. Tell me:.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Ma ɖɔɖɔ, mɔnpɔzʋʋ-mɩ tɔm kʋɖʋmtʋ. Iheyi-m nɛ mana se.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin? .
local: Ɛsɔ yaa ɛyáa tiyini Yohanɛɛsɩ se ɛla ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ?».

eng: They discussed among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Why didnt you believe him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɔyɔɔdɩnɩ ɖama se: «Yee ɖicosaa se: ‹Ɛsɔ tiyini-i› yɔ, ɛpɔzʋʋ-ɖʋ se: ‹Nɛ pɩla ɛzɩma ɖɩtamʋ ɛ-tɔm yɔ?›.

eng: But if we say, Its of human origin, all the people will stone us to death because they are convinced that John was a prophet.
local: Ŋgʋ́, ɖɩtɔm se: ‹Ɛyáa tiyini-i› yɔ, ɛyáa samaɣ tɩŋa yakɩ-ɖʋ pɛɛ nɛ ɖɩsɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kɛtɛm sɩm se Yohanɛɛsɩ kɛkɛ náyʋ.».

eng: They answered that they didnt know where it came from.
local: Peeɖe pocosi Yesu se: «Đɩtasɩ weyi etiyi-i yɔ.».

eng: Then Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things. .
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ ñocosi-wɛ se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ ma ɖɔɖɔ maaheyiɣ-mɩ weyi ɛha-m nɔɔ ŋ́gá yɔ.».

eng: Jesus told the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, rented it to tenant farmers, and went on a trip for a long time.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu pazɩ ɛyáa tuuu eduuye se: «Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ wɛnɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛsɔ tɩŋ weyi popuu i-pee sʋlʋm yɔ. Ɛtɛma lɛ, ɛkpaɣ ɛyáa se pɔcɔŋnɩ-ɩ ɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛɖɛɛ nʋmɔʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩ-taa eleɖiɣ yɔ.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants sent him away, beaten and empty-handed.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, pɩtalɩ pee koyuu. Peeɖe ɛkʋsɩ e-tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ se ewolo ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ pehiɣ-i pʋyʋ nɛ ewona. Tiyiyu ɛnʋ ɛtalaa lɛ, pakpa-ɩ pamabɩ-ɩ nɛ pɔɖɔɔnɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ ndaakpasɩ.

eng: The man sent another servant. But they beat him, treated him disgracefully, and sent him away empty-handed as well.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, hayɩm tʋ ɛnʋ ɛtasɩ tiyiyu lɛlʋ kʋsʋʋ nɛ etiyi-i. Ɛlɛ́, tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá pakpa ɛ́lɛ́ ɖɔɖɔ pamabɩ-ɩ nɛ patʋʋ-ɩ nɛ pɔɖɔɔnɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ ndaakpasɩ.

eng: He sent a third servant. They wounded this servant and threw him out.
local: Ɛtasɩ tozo tʋ tiyuu nɛ pama ɛ́lɛ́ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ lɩɩ calɩm nɛ pɔlɔ-ɩ nɛ hayɩm wayɩ.

eng: The owner of the vineyard said, What should I do? Ill send my son, whom I love dearly. Perhaps they will respect him.
local: Peeɖe hayɩm tʋ ɛnʋ ɛtɔm se: ‹Ɛzɩma malakɩ lee? Mɛntɛzɩɣ tiyuu man-pɩyalʋ kʋsɔɔlʋ. Pʋtɔmaɣ yɔ, paañam-ɩ.›.

eng: But when they saw him, they said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him so the inheritance will be ours.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ talaa nɛ pana-ɩ lɛ, peheyi ɖama se: ‹Ɩnaɣ weyi ɛkaɣ tɩɣyʋʋ ɛ-caa ñɩm yɔ. Đɩkʋ-ɩ nɛ ɖɩtɩɣyɩ-pʋ.›.

eng: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What will the owner of the vineyard do to them?.
local: Peeɖe pakpa-ɩ palɩzɩ-ɩ nɛ hayɩm ḿbʋ́ pʋ-wayɩ nɛ pakʋ-ɩ. Tɔɔ! Hayɩm tʋ ɛnʋ, ɛbɛ ɛlakɩ ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa ḿbá?.

eng: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others. When the people heard this, they said, May this never happen.
local: Ɛkɔŋ nɛ ɛkʋ-wɛ nɛ ɛcaa ɛyáa kɩfama nɛ pɔcɔŋnɩ-ɩ ɛ-hayɩm ḿbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ.» Ɛyáa samaɣ nɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ lɛ, kɔtɔ se: «Kaza! Pɩɩtɩɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́ kpem!».

eng: Staring at them, Jesus said, Then what is the meaning of this text of scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cɔnɩ pɛ-ɛsɩndaa pɩŋŋ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ kuduyiŋ mayaa kpaɣaɣ pɔlɔ yɔ, ńɖɩ́ pɩsɩnɩ ɖɩkɩlɩ wazaɣ atɩŋa payɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ› yɔ, tɩ-tɔbʋʋ se we?.

eng: Everyone who falls on that stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on. .
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se: yee pɩcɩbɩ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɛtɔlɩ pɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛmɩzɩɣ kpɛdɛɛɛ. Yee pɩcɩbɩ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɖɩtɔlɩ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩnaŋɩɣ-ɩ naŋa naŋa.».

eng: The legal experts and chief priests wanted to arrest him right then because they knew he had told this parable against them. But they feared the people.
local: Yesu tɩyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa pañɩnɩɣ se pakpa-ɩ kpaagbaa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pasɩm se ḿbá etuuni eduuye ńɖɩ́. Ɛlɛ́, sɔɔndʋ kpaɣaɣ-wɛ nɛ ɛyáa mbá paawɛ peeɖe yɔ.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests were watching Jesus closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They wanted to trap him in his words so they could hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
local: Peeɖe papazɩ Yesu cɔŋʋʋ nɛ paɖʋ mbá peyiliɣ ɛyáa se pɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzaa yɔ se powolo pañɩ ɛ-nɔɔ cɔlɔ tɔm. Palakaɣ ḿbʋ́ se pehiɣ-i pakpa-ɩ nɛ paɖʋzɩ ɛjaɖɛ ñʋndɩnaa nesi taa.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, we know that you are correct in what you say and teach. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ peheyi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Đɩsɩm ɖeu se tɔm ndʋ́ ŋyɔɔdʋʋ nɛ ndʋ́ ŋwɩlɩɣ yɔ, tɩkɛ toovenim. Ŋtakpaɣ ɛyáa tɔm nɛ ŋlabɩnɩ tʋmɩyɛ. Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ se ɛyáa ɛla yɔ, pu-toovenim ŋwɩlɩɣ.

eng: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Heyi-ɖʋ nɛ ɖɩna se ɖa-paɣtʋ haɣ waɖɛ se pɛhɛyɩ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ lambuu yaa pataahɛyɩ-ɩ?».

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deception, he said to them.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu ñɛtɛm nɩʋ pɩ-taa se pañɩkɩ-ɩ tɔm kɩñɩbʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ eheyi-wɛ se:.

eng: Show me a coin. Whose image and inscription does it have on it? Caesars, they replied.
local: «Ɩkɔnɩ-m liidiye pɔɔyaɣ nakɛyɛ nɛ mana.» Powoni-kɛ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «A-ñʋʋ nɛ a-hɩɖɛ pɛwɛ kɔ-yɔɔ cɩnɛ?» Pocosi-i se: «Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ.».

eng: He said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Yee ḿbʋ́, ɩcɛlɩ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ mbʋ́ pɩkɛ ɛ-ñɩm yɔ nɛ piyele ɩcɛlɩ Ɛsɔ mbʋ́ pɩkɛ ɛ́lɛ́ ñɩm yɔ.».

eng: They couldnt trap him in his words in front of the people. Astonished by his answer, they were speechless.
local: Tɔm ndʋ́ Yesu yɔɔdɩ ɛyáa taa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, yiliyaa ḿbá patana kawanaɣ nakɛyɛ tɩ-taa. Peeɖe pɩɖɩɣzɩ-wɛ nɛ Yesu tɔm cosuu nɛ posu ɖiɣ ɖiɣ.

eng: Some Sadducees, who deny that theres a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Saduki ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma mbá pɔtɔŋ se sɩɖaa eefeŋ yɔ, pa-taa nabɛyɛ wolo Yesu cɔlɔ. Powoba lɛ, peheyi-i se:.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother .
local: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Mooyiizi ma ɛ-paɣtʋ taa se: ‹Yee ɛyʋ ɛwɛnɩ ɖalʋ nɛ ɛ-ɖalʋ ɛnʋ ɛsɩ nɛ eyele ɛ-halʋ nɛ ɛfɛyɩnɩ-ɩ pɩɣa yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se pʋdʋ ɛhɔ lelu ɛnʋ nɛ ehiɣni-i pɩɣa ɛ-ɖalʋ lone taa.›.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first man married a woman and then died childless.
local: Tɔɔ! Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ nɛ e-newaa pɛwɛnɩ ḿbʋ́, pɛwɛ lʋbɛ. Abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛkpaɣ halʋ. Pɩlabɩ pɩnzɩ cabɩ lɛ, ɛsɩ, ɛlɛ́ halʋ ɛnʋ ɛfɛyɩnɩ-ɩ pɩɣa.

eng: The second.
local: Peeɖe e-neu hɔm lelu ɛnʋ nɛ pɩlabɩ pazɩ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ sɩ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: and then the third brother married her. Eventually all seven married her, and they all died without leaving any children.
local: Tozo tʋ ñɔhɔm-ɩ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ sɩ ɖɔɖɔ. Pɩla ḿbʋ́ nɛ pɔkɔɔ pasɩ pa-lʋbɛ. Halʋ ɛnʋ ɛfɛyɩnɩ pa-taa nɔɔyʋ nɛ pɩɣa.

eng: Finally, the woman died too.
local: Pa-wayɩ lɛ, halʋ ɛnʋ ɛsɩ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɖɩpɔzʋʋ-ŋ se: sɩɖaa fem wiye lɛ, pa-taa weyi ɛtɩnɩ halʋ ɛnʋ? Ŋgʋ́, patɩŋa pakpaɣ-ɩ!».

eng: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching.
local: Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma Paska Kazandʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ pɔtɔkɩ tindine nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa fɛyɩ sɔsɔɔna yɔ, tɩwɩzaa.

eng: Jesus said to them, People who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ abalaa kpakɩɣ halaa nɛ halaa ñawalɩɣ abalaa.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were looking for a way to kill Jesus, because they were afraid of the people.
local: Cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa pañɩnɩɣ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ paala nɛ pakpa Yesu hɛɛɛ nɛ pakʋ-ɩ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, peseɣaɣ ɛyáa samaɣ.

eng: But those who are considered worthy to participate in that age, that is, in the age of the resurrection from the dead, wont marry nor will they be given in marriage.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛyáa mbá pamʋnaa se pefemni sɩm taa nɛ pasʋʋ Kewiyaɣ ŋgá kakaɣ kɔm yɔ ka-taa yɔ, halɩkpaɣyɛ ɛɛtasɩɣ wɛʋ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa.

eng: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
local: Peeɖe Satanɩ lɩmaɣza sʋ Yudaasɩ weyi pakʋm-ɩ hɩɖɛ se Iskarɩyɔɔtɩ yɔ ɛ-taa. Ɛkɛ ɖɔɖɔ Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ taa lɛlʋ.

eng: They can no longer die, because they are like angels and are Gods children since they share in the resurrection.
local: Paatasɩɣ sɩbʋ ɖɔɖɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɛwɛɣ ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa yɔ. Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-wɛ kpaɣʋ ɛzɩ e-piya yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pasɩba nɛ pefe.

eng: He went out and discussed with the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard how he could hand Jesus over to them.
local: Ɛkʋyɩ faŋŋ nɛ ewolo ɛna cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ cɔnɩyaa ñʋndɩnaa. Ɛna-wɛ nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ pala lɩmaɣza se pana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛɛla nɛ ɛkpa Yesu nɛ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ pe-nesi taa yɔ.

eng: Even Moses demonstrated that the dead are raised—in the passage about the burning bush, when he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob .
local: Piyele sɩɖaa fem tɔm lɛ, Mooyiizi maɣmaɣ kɛtɛm pʋ-tɔm yɔɔdʋʋ ɛ-takayaɣ taa ɖenɖe ɛma hɔɖaɣ miŋ mʋ́ʋ tɔm yɔ. Ɛyɔɔdaa se Kɩpaɣlʋ lɛ Abraham nɛ Izaakɩ nɛ Yakɔɔbʋ pɛ-Ɛsɔ.

eng: They were delighted and arranged payment for him.
local: Panɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa-taa la leleŋ pɩŋŋ nɛ paɖʋ se pɛcɛlɩɣ-ɩ liidiye.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. To him they are all alive. .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, Ɛsɔ tɛkɛ sɩɖaa Ɛsɔ, ɛlɛ́, wezuu tɩnaa ñɩnʋ ɛkɛnaa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-ñɩma tɩŋa payɩ wɛ wezuu.».

eng: He agreed and began looking for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them—a time when the crowds would be absent.
local: Yudaasɩ tisi nɛ ɛpazɩ ñɩnʋʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛyáa ɛɛwɛɣ yɔ se ɛkpa Yesu nɛ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ pe-nesi taa.

eng: Some of the legal experts responded, Teacher, you have answered well.
local: Peeɖe paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa taa nabɛyɛ tɔm se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Ŋyɔɔdɩ camɩyɛ.».

eng: The Day of Unleavened Bread arrived, when the Passover had to be sacrificed.
local: Kazandʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ pɔtɔkɩ tindine nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa fɛyɩ sɔsɔɔna yɔ, tɩ-alɩwaatʋ taa kɩyakʋ nakʋyʋ wɛɛ nɛ Yuda ñɩma laɣ heɣsi nzɩ́ pɔtɔkɩnɩ Paska Kazandʋ yɔ. Kɩyakʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩtalaa lɛ.

eng: No one dared to ask him anything else.
local: Pɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, patakatɩɣ se patasɩ-ɩ tɔm natʋyʋ pɔzʋʋ.

eng: Jesus sent Peter and John with this task: Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover meal. .
local: Yesu tiyi Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ se: «Iwolo ɩtɩzɩ-ɖʋ Paska Kazandʋ tɔɔnaɣ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Why do they say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu pɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ pɔtɔŋ se Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ ɛkɛ Daaviidi sayɩ?.

eng: They said to him, Where do you want us to prepare it.
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Le ŋcaɣ se ɖiwolo ɖɩtɩzɩ-kɛ? Ɛbɛ ŋsɔɔlaa?».

eng: David himself says in the scroll of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side.
local: Ŋgʋ́, Daaviidi maɣmaɣ ma Keɣsi Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ heyi man-Kɩpaɣlʋ se: caɣ mɔn-cɔlɔ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa.

eng: Jesus replied, When you go into the city, a man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters.
local: Ecosi-wɛ se: «Ɩsʋʋ tɛtʋ taa lɛ, isuluuni abalʋ nɔɔyʋ, ɛsɩɣlɩ lɩm nɛ peɣa. Isuluni-i lɛ, ɩtɩŋ-ɩ nɛ ɩsʋʋ ɖɩɣa ŋgá ka-taa ɛsʋʋ yɔ.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
local: nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ moluzi ño-koyindinaa ñɛ-tɛɛ kpem yɔ.›.

eng: Say to the owner of the house, The teacher says to you, Where is the guestroom where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples? .
local: Ɩsʋwa lɛ, ɩpɔzɩ ɖɩɣa tʋ se: ‹Wɩlɩyʋ tiyini-ɖʋ se ɖɩkɔɔ ŋwɩlɩ-ɖʋ kuduyuu ŋgʋ́ kɩ-taa ɛ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pacakɩ nɛ pɔtɔɔ kazandʋ yɔ.›.

eng: Since David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son? .
local: Daaviidi maɣmaɣ ñayaɣ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ se Kɩpaɣlʋ. Ɛzɩma ɛtasɩɣ kɛʋ Daaviidi ɛnʋ ɛ-sayɩ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ?».

eng: He will show you a large upstairs room, already furnished. Make preparations there. .
local: Đɩɣa tʋ ɛnʋ, ɛwɩlɩɣ-mɩ ɛsɔdaa kuduyuu sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ. Kɩ-taa wɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pʋpɔzʋʋ-ɖʋ yɔ. Kɩ-taa peeɖe ɩlakɩ tɔɔnaɣ.».

eng: In the presence of all the people, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ tɩŋa welesaɣ Yesu tɔm ḿbʋ́ lɛ, eheyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se:.

eng: They went and found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Powoba lɛ, pamaɣnɩ ɛzɩ Yesu keheyuu-wɛ yɔ nɛ pala kazandʋ tɔɔnaɣ peeɖe.

eng: Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They love being greeted with honor in the markets. They long for the places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: «Ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɔsɔɔlɩ tokonaa sɔsaa suu nɛ pɔcɔʋ. Pɔsɔɔlaa ɖɔɖɔ se pɛsɛɣ-wɛ nɛ ñamtʋ ɛyáa ɖikpeɣliye taa. Pañɩnɩɣ se pacaɣ ɖɩjaɣyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ taa Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ nɛ kazandʋ pa-taa.

eng: When the time came, Jesus took his place at the table, and the apostles joined him.
local: Alɩwaatʋ talaa lɛ, Yesu nɛ e-tiyiyaa powolo pacaɣ tɔɔnaɣ ɖɩtɔɔyɛ.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly. .
local: Ḿbá ɖɔɖɔ hɔɔzʋʋnɩ lelaa nesi taa mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛna yɔ. Pɛsɛɣ Ɛsɔ nɛ pɩɖaɣlɩɣ se pɩwɩlɩ se ḿbá lɛ ɛyáa kɩbama. Meheyiɣ-mɩ se kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Ɛsɔ kɔŋ se ɛhʋʋnɩ ɛyáa yɔ, ɛɖʋʋ-wɛ kʋñɔŋ ŋgʋ́ kɩfɛyɩ ɖaɣʋ yɔ.».

eng: He said to them, I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
local: Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɔnsɔɔlaa siŋŋ se ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩtɔɔ kazandʋ tʋnɛ nɛ pʋcɔ mansʋʋ kʋñɔŋ taa yɔ!.

eng: I tell you, I wont eat it until it is fulfilled in Gods kingdom. .
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se maatasɩɣ-tʋ tɔɔʋ kaʋ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ kɔma yɔ.».

eng: After taking a cup and giving thanks, he said, Take this and share it among yourselves.
local: Peeɖe ɛkpaɣ sʋlʋm nɛ caɖaɣ nɛ ɛsɛɛ pʋ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛcɛlɩ-wɛ se: «Ɩmʋ-pʋ nɛ ɩtayɩ ɖama nɛ ɩñɔɔ.».

eng: I tell you that from now on I wont drink from the fruit of the vine until Gods kingdom has come. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩkpaɣʋnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, maatasɩɣ sʋlʋm ñɔʋ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ kɔŋ yɔ.

eng: After taking the bread and giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me. .
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛkpaɣ tindine nɛ ɛsɛɛ ɖɩ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛcɩyɩ-ɖɩ nɛ ɛcɛlɩ-wɛ se: «Pʋnɛ lɛ mon-tomnaɣ ŋgá paha mɩ-yɔɔ yɔ. Ɩmʋ ɩtɔɔ nɛ ɩtɔzʋʋnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ.».

eng: In the same way, he took the cup after the meal and said, This cup is the new covenant by my blood, which is poured out for you.
local: Pɛtɛm tɔɔʋ lɛ, ɛkpaɣ ɖɔɖɔ sʋlʋm nɛ caɖaɣ nɛ ɛcɛlɩ-wɛ se: «Sʋlʋm pʋnɛ lɛ man-calɩm mbʋ́ pɩkpɛdɩɣ mɩ-yɔɔ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ tɩŋɩɣna nɛ ɛɖʋ nɔɔ haʋ kɩfam.».

eng: But look! My betrayer is with me; his hand is on this table.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɩcɔnɩ ɩna: «Weyi ɛkɔŋ-m ɖʋzʋʋ koyindinaa nesi taa yɔ, ma nɛ ɩ ɖɩtɔkɩna cɩnɛ!.

eng: The Human One goes just as it has been determined. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays him. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ ɛkʋyɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ ɖʋʋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pʋtɔɔ weyi ɛɖʋzʋʋ-ɩ koyindinaa nesi taa yɔ!».

eng: They began to argue among themselves about which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pazɩ ɖama pɔzʋʋ se patɩlɩ pa-taa weyi ɛkaɣ labʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ.

eng: An argument broke out among the disciples over which one of them should be regarded as the greatest.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pazɩ ɖama hɔm sɔsɔtʋ yɔɔ. Pɔpɔzaɣ pa-tɩ se: «ɖa-taa lɛ, akɛ sɔsɔ?».

eng: But Jesus said to them, The kings of the Gentiles rule over their subjects, and those in authority over them are called friends of the people.
local: Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɛyáa lɛlaa awiya nuutuuni-wɛ nɛ pɔsɔɔlɩ se payaa-wɛ se kɩbama.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Instead, the greatest among you must become like a person of lower status and the leader like a servant.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɩtamʋnɩ se pʋwɛɛ ḿbʋ́ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa. Pʋwɛɛ se mɩ-taa sɔsɔ ɛpɩsɩ ɛ-tɩ ɛzɩ mɩ-taa pɩɣa yɔ. Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ kɛ wiyaʋ yɔ, ɛpɩsɩ ɛzɩ mɩ-tʋmlaɖʋ yɔ.

eng: Looking up, Jesus saw rich people throwing their gifts into the collection box for the temple treasury.
local: Yesu kʋsɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ lɛ, ɛna ñɩm tɩnaa wokaɣ paɖʋʋ liidiye haɖɛ aɖakaɣ taa Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa.

eng: So which one is greater, the one who is seated at the table or the one who serves at the table? Isnt it the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
local: Weyi ɛtɔŋ se palabɩ-ɩ tɔɔnaɣ yɔ nɛ ɛ-tʋmlaɖʋ weyi ɛlakɩ-kɛ yɔ, akɩlaa? Pɩtɛkɛ weyi ɛtɔŋ se palabɩ-ɩ-kɛ yɔ, ɛnʋ kɩlɩna naa we? Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ mɛwɛ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa, mɛwɛ ɛzɩ tʋmlaɖʋ yɔ.

eng: He also saw a poor widow throw in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛna lelu kʋñɔndʋ nɔɔyʋ woni liidiye pɔɔyɩsɩ cikpesi naalɛ nɛ ɛɖʋ.

eng: You are the ones who have continued with me in my trials.
local: Mɩ lɛ, mɩɩwɛnɩ mɔn-cɔlɔ alɩwaatʋ kpeekpe man-kʋñɔmɩŋ tɩŋa taa.

eng: He said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than them all.
local: Peeɖe ɛtɔm se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se lelu kʋñɔndʋ ɛnɛ, ɛhawa nɛ pɩkɩlɩ lɛlaa tɩŋa.

eng: And I confer royal power on you just as my Father granted royal power to me.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mɛncɛlɩɣ-mɩ kewiyaɣ ɛzɩ man-Caa cɛlʋʋ-m-kɛ yɔ.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had to live on. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ñɩm tɩnaa kpaɣ pe-liidiye nɖɩ́ ɖɩɩwazɩɣ-wɛ yɔ nɛ pɔkɔnɩ paɖʋ. Piyele halʋ kʋñɔndʋ ɛnɛ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛwɛna yɔ nɛ ɛkɔnɩ ɛɖʋ.».

eng: Thus you will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Ɩkɔŋ caɣʋ awiya kpelisi yɔɔ nɛ ɩhʋʋnɩ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma cejewee hiu nɛ naalɛ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, mɔnkɔŋ-mɩ yaʋ nɛ ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩcaɣ nɛ ɖɩtɔɔ nɛ ɖɩñɔɔ men-Kewiyaɣ taa.».

eng: Some people were talking about the temple, how it was decorated with beautiful stones and ornaments dedicated to God. Jesus said.
local: Ɛyáa nabɛyɛ yɔɔdaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa tɔm nɛ pɔtɔŋ se pɛɛ wena pamanɩ-kɛ nɛ mbʋ́ mbʋ́ ɛyáa hawa nɛ pacanɩnɩ-kɛ yɔ, piyeba nɛ kɛwɛɛ ɖeu pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ. Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se:.

eng: Simon, Simon, look! Satan has asserted the right to sift you all like wheat.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi Pɩyɛ se: «Siimɔɔ! Siimɔɔ! Tɩlɩ se Satanɩ pɔzɩ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ se ɛtakɩ-mɩ ɛzɩ pafalʋʋ mɩla yɔ nɛ ɛna.

eng: As for the things you are admiring, the time is coming when not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished. .
local: «Mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩnaɣ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ pɔkɔŋ tuzuu pɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ pɛpɛɖɩ. Pɩɩkazɩɣ pɩyɛ naɖɩyɛ lɛɛɖɛ yɔɔ.».

eng: However, I have prayed for you that your faith wont fail. When you have returned, strengthen your brothers and sisters. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, mɛnsɛ Ɛsɔ ñɔ-yɔɔ se ŋtaalesi lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ŋwɛnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ. Ŋkɔm ŋpɩsɩnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ lɛ, ŋla nɛ ña-taabalaa kpa ɖoŋ.».

eng: They asked him, Teacher, when will these things happen? What sign will show that these things are about to happen.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Đooye pɩkaɣ labʋ ḿbʋ́? Ɛbɛ kɔŋ-ɖʋ wɩlʋʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ?».

eng: Peter responded, Lord, Im ready to go with you, both to prison and to death.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ cosi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Mɛntɛm man-tɩ ñɔɔzʋʋ. Paa salaka yɔ, paɖʋ ma nɛ ŋ nɛ yee sɩm yɔ, ma nɛ ŋ ɖɩsɩ.».

eng: The whole assembly got up and led Jesus to Pilate and.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pakʋyɩ patɩŋa nɛ pakpaɣ Yesu nɛ powoni-i Room wiyaʋ Pilaatɩ tɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that you arent deceived. Many will come in my name, saying, Im the one! and Its time! Dont follow them.
local: Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ, ɩtaayele nɛ nɔɔyʋ yili-mɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kɔŋ Kʋɖʋm kʋɖʋm nɛ peyili-mɩ nɛ peheyi-mɩ se: ‹Ma lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa nɛ etiyini-mɩ› yɔ. Pakaɣ-mɩ heyuu ɖɔɖɔ se: ‹Alɩwaatʋ talaa.› Ɛlɛ́, ɩtaamʋ pɔ-tɔm nɛ ɩtɩŋ-wɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, I tell you, Peter, the rooster wont crow today before you have denied three times that you know me. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-i se: «Pɩyɛ! Meheyiɣ-ŋ se: pʋcɔ kalɩmɔʋ koo sɔnɔ lɛ, ŋkpɛzɩɣ tam naadozo se ŋtasɩm-m.».

eng: began to accuse him. They said, We have found this man misleading our people, opposing the payment of taxes to Caesar, and claiming that he is the Christ, a king.
local: Patana-ɩ lɛ, papazɩ ɛ-tɔm kʋʋ Pilaatɩ cɔlɔ se: «Đɩkpa abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛɖʋzʋʋ ɖɛ-ɛyáa nɛ eheyiɣ-wɛ se pataahɛyɩ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ lambuu. Halɩ ɛtɔm se ɛnʋ lɛ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ.».

eng: When you hear of wars and rebellions, dont be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end wont happen immediately. .
local: Yee ɩnɩ pɔyɔɔdʋʋ you nɛ ɖama wayɩ lɩʋ pɔ-tɔm lɛ, sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-mɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se pɩla ḿbʋ́ nɛ pʋcɔ. Ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ tatalɩda.».

eng: Jesus said to them, When I sent you out without a wallet, bag, or sandals, you didnt lack anything, did you? They said, Nothing.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu pɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Maatiyuu-mɩ ndaakpasɩ, ɩtɔɖɔkɩ liidiye yaa huyuu yaa naataŋgbala yɔ, nabʋyʋ tɛɖɩ-mɩ yaa we?» Pocosi-i se: «Aayɩ, nabʋyʋ tɛtɛɖɩ-ɖʋ.».

eng: Pilate asked him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say. .
local: Peeɖe Pilaatɩ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ña lɛ Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ naa we?» Yesu cosi-i se: «Ñɔyɔɔdʋʋnɩ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other.
local: Ɛtasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Ɛjaɖɛ lɛɛɖɛ kʋyʋʋ lɛɛɖɛ yɔɔ nɛ kewiyaɣ lɛɛka ñakʋyɩ lɛɛka yɔɔ.

eng: Then he said to them, But now, whoever has a wallet must take it, and likewise a bag. And those who dont own a sword must sell their clothes and buy one.
local: Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ lɛ, weyi ɛwɛnɩ liidiye yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛ́ɖɔ́kɩ-ɖɩ. Weyi ɛwɛnɩ huyuu yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ ɛkpaɣ-kʋ. Weyi ɛfɛyɩnɩ sɩɣa kɩɖaɣlaɣ yɔ, ɛpɛdɩ e-toko nɛ ɛyabɩ-kɛ.

eng: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no legal basis for action against this man.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pilaatɩ heyi Cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ ŋgá kɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ se: «Maanaɣ ɛzaɣ́tʋ ndʋ́ abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛlaba nɛ pʋwɛɛ se mankʋ ɛ-tɔm yɔ.».

eng: There will be great earthquakes and wide-scale food shortages and epidemics. There will also be terrifying sights and great signs in the sky.
local: Tɛtʋ kɔŋ ñamsʋʋ siŋŋ nɛ ñɔɔsɩ nɛ kʋdɔmɩŋ weyi ɩɖɛɣ ɛyáa yɔ, palɩɩ ajɛya ndɩ ndɩ taa. Pʋkɔŋ wɩlʋʋ kawɩlɩsɩ ndɩ ndɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ sɔɔndʋ kpa ɛyáa pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ.

eng: I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in relation to me: And he was counted among criminals . Indeed, whats written about me is nearing completion. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se pʋwɛɛ se pɩtɩŋnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ tɔm ndʋ́ paama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ, tɩla. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Azaɣ́a hɛkʋ taa paɖʋʋ-ɩ.› Toovenim lɛ, mɔn-tɔm ndʋ́ paamawa yɔ, tɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: But they objected strenuously, saying, He agitates the people with his teaching throughout Judea—starting from Galilee all the way here.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔ-nɔsɩ tɛzɩ kpaʋ ɖoŋ se: «Ɛnʋ wɩlɩɣnɩ ɛyáa se palɩɩ awiya wayɩ. Ɛpazɩnɩ Galilee egeetiye taa nɛ ɛtɩŋnɩ Yudee ñɩnɖɛ tɩŋa taa nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛtalɩ cɩnɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ.».

eng: But before all this occurs, they will take you into custody and harass you because of your faith. They will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pʋcɔ nɛ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩla ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔkɔŋ mɩ-wayɩ wɛʋ kaŋ kaŋ. Pakpaɣ-mɩ nɛ powoni-mɩ nɛ pahʋʋnɩ-mɩ tɔm Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ taa nɛ awiya sɔsɔna nɛ tɛtʋ ñʋndɩnaa pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ paɖʋ-mɩ salaka mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: They said to him, Lord, look, here are two swords. He replied, Enough of that! .
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa heyi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Đɩwɛnɩ sɛsɩ kɩɖaɣlɩsɩ naalɛ.» Ɛ́lɛ́ ecosi-wɛ se: «Pɩmaɣaɣ.».

eng: Hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.
local: Pɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Pilaatɩ pɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛkɛ Galilee tʋ naa we?».

eng: This will provide you with an opportunity to testify.
local: Ḿbʋ́ haɣnɩ-mɩ waɖɛ se ɩlɩzɩ aseɣɖe mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: Jesus left and made his way to the Mount of Olives, as was his custom, and the disciples followed him.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu lɩɩ se ewoki Somiŋ Pʋʋ yɔɔ ɛzɩ etiiwokuu yɔ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tɩŋ ɛ-wayɩ.

eng: When he learned that Jesus was from Herods district, Pilate sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
local: Ɛlɛ́, peheyi-i se ɛ-tɛ lɛ peeɖe. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛtɔm se powoni-i Eroodi cɔlɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ cɔŋaɣnɩnɩ egeetiye ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-yɔɔ. Ŋgʋ́, ɛɛwɛ Yeruusalɛm alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa.

eng: Make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɩɖʋ mɩ-ñʋŋ taa se pɩfɛyɩ se ɩnɩɣzɩ tɔm ndʋ́ ɩɩyɔɔdɩ nɛ ɩlɩɩ pɩ-taa yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: When he arrived, he said to them, Pray that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Patalaa lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se: «Itendi Ɛsɔ se ɛtaayele nɛ pɩtakɩ-mɩ.».

eng: Herod was very glad to see Jesus, for he had heard about Jesus and had wanted to see him for quite some time. He was hoping to see Jesus perform some sign.
local: Patanɩ Yesu lɛ, Eroodi na-ɩ nɛ ɛ-laŋɩyɛ hʋlʋmɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛnɩ ɛ-tɔm nɛ ɛñɩnɩɣ ɖooo lɔŋ se ɛna-ɩ nɛ ɛla hama lakasɩ nasɩyɩ nɛ ɛna.

eng: Ill give you words and wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to counter or contradict.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ma-maɣmaɣ mahaɣnɩ-mɩ lɔŋ nɛ ɩyɔɔdɩ nɛ mi-koyindinaa taa nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩ ecosi yaa ɛkʋ mɩ-tɔm ndʋ́ ɩyɔɔdaa yɔ.

eng: He withdrew from them about a stones throw, knelt down, and prayed.
local: Eheyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, eyebi-wɛ nɛ eposi ɛ-tɩ pazɩ nɛ eluŋ ɛ-nɩmbɔla yɔɔ nɛ etendi Ɛsɔ se:.

eng: Herod questioned Jesus at length, but Jesus didnt respond to him.
local: Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ tɔm sakɩyɛ, ɛlɛ́, etocosi-i natʋyʋ.

eng: You will be betrayed by your parents, brothers and sisters, relatives, and friends. They will execute some of you.
local: Halɩ mɩ-maɣmaɣ mɩ-caanaa nɛ mi-ɖoonaa nɛ mɩ-ɖalaa yaa mi-newaa nɛ mɩ-ñɩma lɛlaa nɛ mɩ-ɛgbaɣdɩnaa pɔkɔŋnɩ-mɩ kpaʋ paɖʋzɩ mi-koyindinaa nesi taa nɛ pakʋ mɩ-taa ɛyáa sakɩyɛ.

eng: Very early in the morning on the first day of the week, the women went to the tomb, bringing the fragrant spices they had prepared.
local: Kujuka wiye tanaŋ tɛɛ cu lɛ, halaa ḿbá pakpaɣ num ndɩ ndɩ nɛ tɩlaaɖɩnaa mbá paañɩnaa yɔ se powoki pɩlaʋ yɔɔ.

eng: He said, Father, if its your will, take this cup of suffering away from me. However, not my will but your will must be done. .
local: «Hayɩ Man-Caa! Yee ŋtisiɣ yɔ, ŋlɩzɩ-m man-kʋñɔŋ sɔsɔʋ kʋnɛ kɩ-taa. Ɛlɛ́, la mbʋ́ ñe-liu wɛɛ yɔ, taala mbʋ́ me-liu wɛɛ yɔ.» .

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were there, fiercely accusing Jesus.
local: Cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa paawɛ peeɖe ɖɔɖɔ nɛ paba tɛzɩ ɛ-yɔɔ kʋyʋʋ kaŋ kaŋ.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛyáa tɩŋa kaɣ-mɩ paɖʋʋ mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
local: Patalaa lɛ, pamaɣnɩ patʋlɩ pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ paaɖɩɣ pɩlaʋ nɔɔ yɔ.

eng: Then a heavenly angel appeared to him and strengthened him.
local: Peeɖe Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ lɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛsɔzɩ-ɩ ɖoŋ.

eng: Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt. Herod mocked him by dressing Jesus in elegant clothes and sent him back to Pilate.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Eroodi nɛ ɛ-sɔɔjanaa powoŋ Yesu nɛ pɛpɛŋ-ɩ tɛtɛ nɛ posuu-i ñɩɣlɩ́m toko. Ḿbʋ́ palabɩ-ɩ nɛ papɩsɩnɩ-ɩ Pilaatɩ cɔlɔ.

eng: Still, not a hair on your heads will be lost.
local: Ɛlɛ́, paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, mɩ-ñʋŋ taa ñɔsɩ wɛʋ yɔ, sɩ-taa nakɛyɛ eeleki yem nɛ Ɛsɔ tatɩlɩ.

eng: but when they went in, they didnt find the body of the Lord Jesus.
local: Pasʋ kɩ-taa lɛ, patana Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu tomnaɣ.

eng: He was in anguish and prayed even more earnestly. His sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, Yesu laŋɩyɛ kɛwɛɛkɩ siŋŋ nɛ ɛ-ɛsɩyɛ sɛɛnɩ Ɛsɔ sɛʋ kaŋ kaŋ. Soŋaɣ kalɩ ɛ-yɔɔ ɛzɩ pʋkʋʋ ɛyʋ nɛ calɩm lɩɣ yɔ nɛ kotosuu tataa ɖoyi ɖoyi.

eng: Pilate and Herod became friends with each other that day. Before this, they had been enemies.
local: Kɩyakʋ ŋ́gʋ́ Eroodi nɛ Pilaatɩ peceɣa. Piyele pataanaɣ ɖama nɛ ɛsɩyɛ.

eng: By holding fast, you will gain your lives.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩñaɣ pana nɛ ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ nɛ ihiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ.».

eng: They didnt know what to make of this. Suddenly, two men were standing beside them in gleaming bright clothing.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɩɖɩɣzɩ-wɛ siŋŋ. Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, abalaa naalɛ nabɛyɛ tɩlɩʋ pɔ-yɔɔ. Posu wondu, nɛ tɩñɩlɩzɩɣ pelu pelu.

eng: When he got up from praying, he went to the disciples. He found them asleep, overcome by grief.
local: Ɛtɛm Ɛsɔ sɛʋ lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ ɛpɩsɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa cɔlɔ nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ pɔɖɔŋ ɖou, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɔ-hɔ pɩsaa.

eng: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people.
local: Papɩsɩnɩ Yesu Pilaatɩ tɛ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ kpeɣli cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ awiya nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ.

eng: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then you will know that its destruction is close at hand.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Sɔɔjanaa kɔŋ cɔʋ Yeruusalɛm tɛtʋ nɛ pata. Ɩna ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɩtɩlɩ se tɩ-yɔkʋʋ alɩwaatʋ wɩzaa.

eng: The women were frightened and bowed their faces toward the ground, but the men said to them, Why do you look for the living among the dead?.
local: Peeɖe sɔɔndʋ kpa halaa ḿbá nɛ paka pɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ tɛtʋ nɛ abalaa ḿbá pɔpɔzʋʋ-wɛ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩñɩnɩɣ weyi ɛwɛnɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ yɔ ɖenɖe sɩɖaa wɛɣ yɔ?.

eng: He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. .
local: Ɛmaɣnɩ-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛbɛ laba nɛ iɖou ḿbʋ́? Ɩkʋyɩ itendi Ɛsɔ se ɛtaayele nɛ pɩtakɩ-mɩ.».

eng: He said to them, You brought this man before me as one who was misleading the people. I have questioned him in your presence and found nothing in this mans conduct that provides a legal basis for the charges you have brought against him.
local: nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩkɔnɩ abalʋ ɛnɛ mɔn-cɔlɔ nɛ iheyi-m se ɛɖʋzʋʋ ɛyáa. Ŋgʋ́, mɔnpɔzɩ-ɩ tɔm mɩ-ɛsɩndaa lɛ, mantana mbʋ́ ɩtɔŋ se ɛwɛɛkaa yɔ pɩ-taa nabʋyʋ kpem.

eng: At that time, those in Judea must flee to the mountains, those in the city must escape, and those in the countryside must not enter the city.
local: Tɩtalaa lɛ, mbá pɛwɛ Yudee egeetiye taa yɔ, pese pakpanɩ pʋŋ taa nɛ mbá paba wɛ Yeruusalɛm yɔ, pese pɛɖɛɛ poliŋ. Piyele mbá paba wɛ tɛyɛ yɔɔ yɔ, pataacazɩ wobu Yeruusalɛm.

eng: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus mother was there, and.
local: Kɩyakɩŋ naalɛ wayɩ lɛ, palakaɣ halɩkpaɣyɛ kazandʋ Kana, Galilee egeetiye taa. Yesu ɖoo kɛwɛ peeɖe.

eng: He isnt here, but has been raised. Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee.
local: Ɛfɛyɩ cɩnɛ, efema. Ɩtɔzɩ tɔm ndʋ́ eeheyi-mɩ Galilee egeetiye taa se:.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd appeared, and the one called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him.
local: Yesu yɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yudaasɩ weyi ɛkɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ taa lɛlʋ yɔ, ɛɖɛɛ ɛyáa samaɣ nakɛyɛ nɔɔ nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ patalɩ. Patalaa lɛ, ewolo se ɛmʋzɩ ɛ-laɣzaɣ yɔɔ.

eng: Neither did Herod, because Herod returned him to us. Hes done nothing that deserves death.
local: Eroodi ɖɔɖɔ ñatana mbʋ́ ɛwɛɛkaa yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛtɔm se papɩsɩnɩ-ɩ mɔn-cɔlɔ cɩnɛ. Toovenim lɛ, abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛtala mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ se pakʋ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: These are the days of punishment, when everything written will find its fulfillment.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ Ɛsɔ wɩlɩɣ ɛ-pana se ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa pama ɛ-Tɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ tɩla.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
local: Paaya Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa kazandʋ ńdʋ́ ti-ɖeɖe.

eng: that the Human One must be handed over to sinners, be crucified, and on the third day rise again.
local: pʋwɛɛ se paɖʋ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa nesi taa nɛ pakam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɛsɩ nɛ kɩyakɩŋ naadozo wiye lɛ, Ɛsɔ fezi-i yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ.».

eng: In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God and the Word was God.
local: Pʋcɔ Ɛsɔ pazɩ kedeŋa labʋ lɛ, weyi payaɣ se Tɔm yɔ, ɛɛwɛɛ. Ɛɛwɛ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ, ɛnʋ lɛ Ɛsɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Judas, would you betray the Human One with a kiss? .
local: Peeɖe Yesu pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Yudaasɩ! Laɣzaɣ yɔɔ mʋzʋʋ ŋwɩlɩɣnɩ koyindinaa se pakpa Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ ma naa we?».

eng: Therefore, Ill have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, meyeki-i. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se polu-i agbazɛɛ nɛ pʋcɔ ɛɖɛɛ.» .

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant or for women who are nursing their children. There will be great agony on the earth and angry judgment on this people.
local: Pʋkɔŋ tɔɔʋ hosi tɩnaa nɛ asɔŋ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kʋñɔŋ sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ sʋʋ ɛjaɖɛ ɖɩnɛ ɖɩ-taa nɛ ɖɩ-taa ñɩma na Ɛsɔ pana.

eng: When the wine ran out, Jesus mother said to him, They dont have any wine.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, sʋlʋm tɛ. Peeɖe Yesu ɖoo woba nɛ eheyi-i se: «Sʋlʋm tɛm ɛyáa cɔlɔ.».

eng: Then they remembered his words.
local: Peeɖe pɔtɔzɩ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: The Word was with God in the beginning.
local: Ɛɛwɛ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ nɛ pʋcɔ ɛ́lɛ́ la kedeŋa.

eng: When those around him recognized what was about to happen, they said, Lord, should we fight with our swords.
local: Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa mbá pɛwɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ, pana mbʋ́ ɛyáa ḿbá pacaɣ labʋ yɔ lɛ, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Đɩsɔbɩ-wɛ sɛsɩ naa we?».

eng: But with one voice they shouted, Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us. .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ patɩŋa pamabɩ kubusi se: «Kʋ abalʋ ɛnɛ! Lɩzɩ-ɖʋ Baraabaasɩ!».

eng: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be plundered by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are concluded.
local: Pakʋʋ pa-taa lɛlaa nɛ sɛsɩ kɩɖaɣlɩsɩ nɛ papɩsɩ lɛlaa yomaa ajɛya tɩŋa taa. Yeruusalɛm lɛ, kɩwɛɣ mbá paañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ pe-nesi taa nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pa-alɩwaatʋ ɖɛwa yɔ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Woman, what does that have to do with me? My time hasnt come yet. .
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Halʋ! Pɩtɛkɛ ñeheyiɣni-m mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se mala yɔ. Ma-alɩwaatʋ tatalɩda.».

eng: When they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and all the others.
local: nɛ palɩɩ pɩlaʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-taa nɛ powolo pɛkɛdɩ Yesu tiyiyaa hiu nɛ kʋɖʋm nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lɛlaa tɩŋa mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa panawa yɔ.

eng: Everything came into being through the Word, and without the Word nothing came into being. What came into being.
local: Ɛ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ tɩŋnaa nɛ ɛla pɩtɩŋa payɩ. Nabʋyʋ fɛyɩ se Ɛsɔ labɩ-pʋ nɛ ɛtatɩŋnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: One of them struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa-taa lɛlʋ sɛtɩ cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ tʋmlaɖʋ nesi kɩbanzɩ nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ.

eng: Barabbas had been thrown into prison because of a riot that had occurred in the city, and for murder.
local: Ŋgʋ́, paaɖʋ ɛ́lɛ́ salaka se ɛhɔyɩ ñaɖʋʋ nakʋyʋ tɛtʋ taa nɛ ɛtɛ ɛkʋ ɛyʋ nɔɔyʋ.

eng: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars. On the earth, there will be dismay among nations in their confusion over the roaring of the sea and surging waves.
local: Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Pɩwɩlɩɣ kawɩlɩsɩ nasɩyɩ wɩsɩ nɛ fenaɣ nɛ tɩɩnzɩ pa-taa ɛsɔdaa. Tataa lɛ, teŋgu nɛ ku-hola pafalɩɣ ɖoŋ nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa laŋa wɛɛkɩ pɩŋŋ.

eng: His mother told the servants, Do whatever he tells you.
local: Peeɖe e-ɖoo heyi tʋmlaɖaa mbá pɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ se: «Ɩla mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛpɔzʋʋ-mɩ yɔ.».

eng: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles.
local: Halaa ḿbá lɛ: Magdala Maarɩɩ nɛ Yaanɩ nɛ Yakubu ɖoo Maarɩɩ. Halaa lɛlaa mbá paawobi peeɖe ɖɔɖɔ yɔ, peheyi Yesu tiyiyaa ḿbá ɖɔɖɔ ḿbʋ́.

eng: through the Word was life, and the life was the light for all people.
local: Ɛ-cɔlɔ wezuu wɛɛ nɛ kiyeki se ɛyáa ɛwɛɛ ñalɩmɩyɛ taa.

eng: Jesus responded, Stop! No more of this! He touched the slaves ear and healed him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Iyele ḿbʋ́, pɩmaɣaɣ.» Peeɖe etukuni nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́ ku-lone taa nɛ kɩpɩsɩ ɛzɩ kɩɩwɛʋ yɔ.

eng: Pilate addressed them again because he wanted to release Jesus.
local: Pilaatɩ caɣaɣ se eyele Yesu lɛ, ɛtasɩ ɛyáa samaɣ heyuu se: «Mantana mbʋ́ abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛwɛɛkaa kpem yɔ.».

eng: The planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken, causing people to faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world.
local: Sɔɔndʋ kɔŋ ɛyáa kpaʋ sɩm yɔɔ sɩm yɔɔ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pamaɣzɩɣ mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ labʋ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖoŋ weyi ɩɖɔkʋʋ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, ɩkɔŋ ñamsʋʋ sɔsɔm.

eng: Nearby were six stone water jars used for the Jewish cleansing ritual, each able to hold about twenty or thirty gallons.
local: Pulona naayɛ loɖo kɛwɛ peeɖe nɛ Yuda ñɩma ɖʋʋ a-taa lɩm nɛ palakɩna pa-cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ wɛtʋ sɔnzɩ. Papɩzaɣ se paɖʋ paa nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa ɛzɩ lɩm litanaa mɩnʋʋ yɔ.

eng: Their words struck the apostles as nonsense, and they didnt believe the women.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pakpaɣ-tʋ se tɩkɛ kɩmɛlɛndʋ tɔm nɛ petetisi-tʋ. .

eng: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness doesnt extinguish the light.
local: Ñalɩmɩyɛ lakɩ nɛ pɩnaɣ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa, ɛlɛ́ cɩkpɛndʋʋ tetisi ñalɩmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-tɔm.

eng: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come to get him, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, as though I were a thief?.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu pɔzɩ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ cɔnɩyaa ñʋndɩnaa nɛ Yuda ñɩma sɔsaa mbá powobi-i kpaʋ yɔ se: «Ma mɩ́lʋ́ nɛ ɩkɔɔ se ɩkpaɣ-m nɛ sɛsɩ kɩɖaɣlɩsɩ nɛ pɔla naa we?.

eng: They kept shouting out, Crucify him! Crucify him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, patɩŋa pama kubusi se: «Kam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ! Kam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ!».

eng: Then they will see the Human One coming on a cloud with power and great splendor.
local: Peeɖe panaɣ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ nɛ e-ɖoŋ sɔsɔŋ nɛ e-koboyaɣ ɛsɔmɩnʋʋ nakʋyʋ yɔɔ, ɛɖɔŋ kɔm.

eng: Jesus said to the servants, Fill the jars with water, and they filled them to the brim.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yesu heyi tʋmlaɖaa se polu lɩm nɛ papɩyɩ a-taa nɛ pusu. Peeɖe paba luwa nɛ pusu a-taa ɖo.

eng: But Peter ran to the tomb. When he bent over to look inside, he saw only the linen cloth. Then he returned home, wondering what had happened.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, Pɩyɛ ñaakʋyaa ɛkpaɣ seya nɛ ewolo pɩlaʋ yɔɔ. Ɛtalaa eluŋ nɛ ewili kɩ-taa lɛ, ɛnaɣ pɩsaŋ weyi paahɔkɩ-ɩ ɩ-taa yɔ i-ɖeke. Peeɖe pɩlabɩ-ɩ piti pɩŋŋ nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩlaba yɔ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ ekpe.

eng: A man named John was sent from God.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Ɛsɔ tiyi abalʋ nɔɔyʋ, payaɣ-ɩ se Yohanɛɛsɩ .

eng: Day after day I was with you in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But this is your time, when darkness rules. .
local: Ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩɩwɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa kɩyakɩŋ tɩŋa, ɛlɛ́ mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ taañɩnɩ se ɛkpa-m. Ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, mɩ nɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ tɛɛ wiyaʋ mɩ-alɩwaatʋ talɩna yɔ.».

eng: For the third time, Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? Ive found no legal basis for the death penalty in his case. Therefore, I will have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Pilaatɩ tasɩ-wɛ pɔzʋʋ tozo ɖeɖe se: «Ɛlɛ́, ɛbɛ kpem ɛwɛɛkaa? Mantana mbʋ́ ɛwɛɛkaa nɛ pʋpɔzʋʋ se pakʋ-ɩ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, meyeki-i. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se polu-i agbazɛɛ nɛ pʋcɔ ɛɖɛɛ.».

eng: Now when these things begin to happen, stand up straight and raise your heads, because your redemption is near. .
local: Ɩkɔm ɩnaɣ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɩkʋyɩ ɩsɩŋ mɩ-nasɩ taa ɖeu, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩ-ñʋʋ yabʋ alɩwaatʋ wɩzɩ talʋʋ.».

eng: Then he told them, Now draw some from them and take it to the headwaiter, and they did.
local: Pɛtɛma lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se pocozi lɩm ḿbʋ́ pazɩ nɛ powoni pɛcɛlɩ halɩkpaɣyɛ kazandʋ yɔɔ cɔnɩyʋ nɛ ɛɖɩŋ. Ɛlɛ́, pala ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: On that same day, two disciples were traveling to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
local: Kɩyakʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́ ki-wiye Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa naalɛ kʋyaa se powoki tɛtʋ cikpetu ndʋ́ payaɣ se Emayuusɩ yɔ tɩ-taa. Tiiposini Yeruusalɛm ɛzɩ kiloomɛtanaa hiu nɛ kʋɖʋm yɔ.

eng: He came as a witness to testify concerning the light, so that through him everyone would believe in the light.
local: Etiyi-i se ɛlɩzɩ ñalɩmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-aseɣɖe nɛ ɛyáa wɛnɩ lidaʋ ɖɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: After they arrested Jesus, they led him away and brought him to the high priests house. Peter followed from a distance.
local: Yesu tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pakpa-ɩ se powokini-i cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ tɛ nɛ Pɩyɛ tɩŋɩɣ pa-wayɩ, ɛlɛ́, eeposini-wɛ.

eng: But they were adamant, shouting their demand that Jesus be crucified. Their voices won out.
local: Peeɖe pɛtɛzaɣ kubusi mabʋ ɖoŋ se: «Kam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ!» Po-kobusi ɖɛ Pilaatɩ lɛ.

eng: Jesus told them a parable: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tuu-wɛ eduuye se: «Ɩcɔnɩ hiyuú nɛ tɩŋ lɛɛŋ tɩŋa nɛ ɩna.

eng: The headwaiter tasted the water that had become wine. He didnt know where it came from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. The headwaiter called the groom.
local: Powonaa pɛcɛlɩ ɛ́lɛ́ nɛ ɛɖɩŋ lɛ, lɩm ḿbʋ́ pɩpɩsɩ sʋlʋm. Ɛtana ɖenɖe pɩlɩnaa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, tʋmlaɖaa mbá pocozi-pʋ nɛ pɛcɛlɩ-ɩ yɔ, paba sɩm ɖenɖe pɩlɩnaa yɔ. Peeɖe ɛya halɩkpaɣyʋ.

eng: They were talking to each other about everything that had happened.
local: Pɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, pɔyɔɔdʋʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pɩlaba yɔ pʋ-tɔm.

eng: He himself wasnt the light, but his mission was to testify concerning the light.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ ɛnʋ, pɩtɛkɛ ɛ-tɩ tɩŋa lɛ ñalɩmɩyɛ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛkɔm se ɛlɩzɩ ñalɩmɩyɛ aseɣɖe.

eng: When they lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
local: Ɛtalaa lɛ, ɛmaɣnɩ paɖʋ miŋ taɣ hɛkʋ taa nɛ ewolo ɛcaɣ mbá pohoyaɣ miŋ ɛnʋ yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: Pilate issued his decision to grant their request.
local: ɛɖʋ se ɛlakɩ-wɛ mbʋ́ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɛnɛɣdɩɣ yɔ, ɩsɩm kpaagbaa se tɛsoŋiye wɩzaa.

eng: and said, Everyone serves the good wine first. They bring out the second-rate wine only when the guests are drinking freely. You kept the good wine until now.
local: nɛ eheyi-i se: «Sʋlʋm kɩbam ɛyáa calɩɣnɩ ñɔʋ. Pahakɩ lɛ, palɩzɩ mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, sʋlʋm kɩbam ñɛwɛɛ ñɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ pɩtalɩ alɩwaatʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-yɔɔ!».

eng: While they were discussing these things, Jesus himself arrived and joined them on their journey.
local: Pɔyɔɔdaɣnɩ ɖama ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yesu ɖɔ nɛ ewolo pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ pɛɖɛɣ.

eng: The true light that shines on all people was coming into the world.
local: Ńɖɩ́ lɛ ñalɩmɩyɛ siŋŋ, ɖɩkɔm kedeŋa yɔɔ se ɖɩñalɩ paa weyi ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: Then a servant woman saw him sitting in the firelight. She stared at him and said, This man was with him too.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, tiyiyu halʋ ñɩnʋ nɔɔyʋ na-ɩ nɛ ɛcɔnɩ-ɩ pɩŋŋ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛtɩŋaɣ Yesu wayɩ ɖɔɖɔ!».

eng: He released the one they asked for, who had been thrown into prison because of a riot and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛlɩzɩ weyi pɔsɔɔlaa se ɛlɩzɩ yɔ nɛ piyele ɛcɛlɩ-wɛ Yesu se palabɩ-ɩ mbʋ́ pɔsɔɔlaa yɔ. Ŋgʋ́, weyi ɛlɩzɩ-ɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, paaɖʋ-ɩ salaka se ɛhɔyɩ ñaɖʋʋ nakʋyʋ nɛ ɛtɛ ɛkʋ ɛyʋ nɔɔyʋ.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that Gods kingdom is near.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛɛ. Yee ɩkɔm ɩnaɣ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɩtɩlɩ kpaagbaa se Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ talɩ mɩ-cɔlɔ kpam.

eng: This was the first miraculous sign that Jesus did in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
local: Ḿbʋ́ Yesu labɩ ɛ-kajalaɣ hama lakasɩ. Kana tɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩwɛ Galilee egeetiye taa yɔ, tɩ-taa ɛlabɩ-sɩ. Ḿbʋ́ ɛwɩlɩnɩ e-ɖoŋ koboyaɣ ñɩŋ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa wɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: They were prevented from recognizing him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, patasɩm-ɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, piihuuzi pɛ-ɛsɛ yɔɔ.

eng: The light was in the world, and the world came into being through the light, but the world didnt recognize the light.
local: Weyi payaɣ se Tɔm yɔ, ɛkɔm kedeŋa yɔɔ. Ɛ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ tɩŋnaa nɛ ɛlabɩ-kɛ. Ɛlɛ́, kɔ-yɔɔ ñɩma tasɩm-ɩ.

eng: But Peter denied it, saying, Woman, I dont know him.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ kpɛzaa se: «Halʋ ña! Mantasɩm-ɩ kaʋ.».

eng: As they led Jesus away, they grabbed Simon, a man from Cyrene, who was coming in from the countryside. They put the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.
local: Powokaɣnɩ Yesu kam lɛ, pakatɩ Siirɛɛnɩ abalʋ nɔɔyʋ, payaɣ-ɩ se Siimɔɔ. Ɛlɩnɩ tɛyɛ. Peeɖe pakpa-ɩ nɛ ɖoŋ nɛ pasʋʋ-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ se ɛtɩŋnɩ-kɛ Yesu wayɩ.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until everything has happened.
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se: ɛyáa mbá pɛwɛnɩ pɛ-ɛsɛ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, paasɩkɩ patɩŋa nɛ pʋcɔ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩla ḿbʋ́.

eng: After this, Jesus and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples went down to Capernaum and stayed there for a few days.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛ nɛ e-ɖoo nɛ e-newaa nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pakʋyɩ powolo Kapɛrnayum nɛ pacaɣ kɩyakɩŋ pazɩ.

eng: He said to them, What are you talking about as you walk along? They stopped, their faces downcast.
local: Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Tɔm ndʋ́ ɩyɔɔdʋʋnɩ ɖama nʋmɔʋ taa ḿbʋ́?» Ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pasɩŋnɩ ɛsɛñɩŋɩyɛ.

eng: The light came to his own people, and his own people didnt welcome him.
local: Ɛkɔm ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-tɛ, ɛlɛ́, ɛ-ñɩma tamʋ-ɩ.

eng: A little while later, someone else saw him and said, You are one of them too. But Peter said, Man, Im not.
local: Pɩlabɩ fele lɛ, abalʋ nɔɔyʋ na-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ tɔ se: «Ña ɖɔɖɔ, ŋkɛ ɛyáa panɛ pa-taa lɛlʋ.» Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ cosi-i se: «Aayɩ! Mɛnfɛyɩ pa-taa.».

eng: A huge crowd of people followed Jesus, including women, who were mourning and wailing for him.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ tɩŋaɣ ɛ-wayɩ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ. Halaa mbá paawɛ ka-taa yɔ, pakʋwaɣ pa-tɩ nɛ wiluu nɛ patɩmɩɣ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Ɛsɔdaa nɛ tɛtʋ pamʋkɩ. Ɛlɛ́, mɔn-tɔm ñaamʋkɩ kaʋ.».

eng: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Yuda ñɩma Paska Kazandʋ ñɔtaa. Pʋ-yɔɔ Yesu kʋyaa nɛ ewolo Yeruusalɛm.

eng: The one named Cleopas replied, Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who is unaware of the things that have taken place there over the last few days.
local: nɛ pa-taa weyi payaɣ se Kleyoopaasɩ yɔ, ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ɛzɩ ñe-ɖeke koŋ ŋwɛnɩ Yeruusalɛm nɛ ŋtanɩɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩlabɩ peeɖe kɩyakɩŋ ɩnɛ ɩ-taa yɔ?».

eng: But those who did welcome him, those who believed in his name, he authorized to become Gods children.
local: Piyele mbá patɩŋa payɩ pamʋ-ɩ nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛha-wɛ ɖoŋ se papɩsɩ Ɛsɔ piya.

eng: An hour or so later, someone else insisted, This man must have been with him, because he is a Galilean too.
local: Pɩtasɩ labʋ ɛzɩ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ yɔ lɛ, abalʋ lɛlʋ yɔɔdɩnɩ abalɩtʋ se: «Abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛtɩŋaɣ Yesu wayɩ toovenim, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛkɛ Galilee tʋ.».

eng: Jesus turned to the women and said, Daughters of Jerusalem, dont cry for me. Rather, cry for yourselves and your children.
local: Peeɖe Yesu kisini halaa ḿbá pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Yeruusalɛm halaa mɩ! Ɩtaawi-m, ɛlɛ́, iwii mɩ nɛ mi-piya.

eng: Take care that your hearts arent dulled by drinking parties, drunkenness, and the anxieties of day-to-day life. Dont let that day fall upon you unexpectedly.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Ɩkpa mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ, ɩɩtaanɩɩnɩ yem yem labʋ nɛ sʋlʋm kpaŋʋʋ nɛ kedeŋa lɩmaɣza wena akʋʋ ɛyáa yɔ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, tɔm hʋʋ kɩyakʋ kɔŋ kutudi-mɩ.

eng: He found in the temple those who were selling cattle, sheep, and doves, as well as those involved in exchanging currency sitting there.
local: Ɛtalaa lɛ, ɛsʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa kadaɣ taa nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ ɛyáa nabɛyɛ, pɛpɛdaɣ naŋ nɛ heŋ nɛ halikukunaa nɛ lɛlaa ñacaɣ kpelisi yɔɔ nɛ pɔyɔkʋʋ liidiye.

eng: He said to them, What things? They said to him, The things about Jesus of Nazareth. Because of his powerful deeds and words, he was recognized by God and all the people as a prophet.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛbɛ laba tɛ?» Pocosi-i se: «Ɛzɩ ŋtanɩɩ mbʋ́ pɩlabɩ Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu yɔ? Ɛɛkɛ náyʋ nɛ ɛlakɩ tʋma nɛ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ tɔm nɛ abalɩtʋ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa pɛ-ɛsɩndaa.

eng: There was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a Jewish leader.
local: Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma ŋgbɛyɛ taa, payaɣ-ɩ se Niikodɛm. Ɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ nɔɔyʋ.

eng: born not from blood nor from human desire or passion, but born from God.
local: Patalʋlɩ-wɛ ɛzɩ ɛyáa sɔɔlʋʋ nɛ palʋlʋʋ pe-piya yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ hanɩ-wɛ wezuu kɩfalʋʋ.

eng: Peter responded, Man, I dont know what you are talking about! At that very moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ cosi-i se: «Abalʋ ña! Mantasɩ tɔm ndʋ́ ŋcaɣ yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ. Ɛtɩyɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, kalɩmɔʋ koo.».

eng: The time will come when they will say, Happy are those who are unable to become pregnant, the wombs that never gave birth, and the breasts that never nursed a child.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ kɔŋ nɛ ɛyáa tɔ se: ‹Kaalʋlɩsɩ nɛ mbá pɔtɔɖɔkɩ kaʋ yaa piya tamʋzɩ-wɛ kaʋ yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ!›.

eng: like a trap. It will come upon everyone who lives on the face of the whole earth.
local: ɛzɩ pɔlɔɔʋ piyuu yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɔm hʋʋ wiye lɛ, pahʋʋnɩ kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ ɛyáa tɩŋa.

eng: He made a whip from ropes and chased them all out of the temple, including the cattle and the sheep. He scattered the coins and overturned the tables of those who exchanged currency.
local: Peeɖe ɛkpaɣ nimee nɛ ɛlʋʋ ɛgbazɩyɛ nɛ ɛɖɔɔnɩnɩ-wɛ ka-taa, pa nɛ pe-heŋ nɛ pa-naŋ nɛ pese palɩɩ nɛ piyele etuzi pa-taabɩlɩnaa ɛpɛɖɩ nɛ liidiye mɩzɩ.

eng: But our chief priests and our leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɖɔ-cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ ɖa-ñʋndɩnaa pakpa-ɩ pɛcɛlɩ nɛ pakʋ ɛ-tɔm nɛ pakam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɛsɩ.

eng: He came to Jesus at night and said to him, Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one could do these miraculous signs that you do unless God is with him.
local: Abalʋ ɛnʋ, ɛkʋyɩ ɖoó naayɛ taa nɛ ewolo Yesu cɔlɔ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Đɩnawa se Ɛsɔ tiyini-ŋ se ŋkɔɔ ŋwɩlɩ-ɖʋ ɛ-tɔm. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ se ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛla hama lakasɩ nzɩ́ ŋlaba yɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ fɛyɩ ɛ-cɔlɔ.».

eng: The Word became flesh and made his home among us. We have seen his glory, glory like that of a fathers only son, full of grace and truth.
local: Weyi payaɣ se Tɔm yɔ, ɛpɩsɩ ɛyʋ nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛwɛɛ ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa. Ɛkɛ lootiye tʋ siŋŋ nɛ ɛtɛ ɛkɛ toovenim tʋ siŋŋ. Đɩna e-koboyaɣ, kɛwɛ ɛzɩ Caa Pɩyalʋ ɛgbamɩyɛ ñɩŋga yɔ.

eng: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the Lords words: Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times. .
local: Kuko ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ kisi nɛ ɛcɔnɩ Pɩyɛ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ tɔzɩ tɔm ndʋ́ eeheyi-i se: «Pʋcɔ kalɩmɔʋ koo sɔnɔ lɛ, ŋkpɛzɩɣ tam naadozo se ŋtasɩm-m» yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: Then they will say to the mountains, Fall on us, and to the hills, Cover us.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ ɛyáa tendiɣ pʋŋ nɛ piyisi se: ‹Ɩkpɛzɩ ihuuzi ɖɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩsɩ.›.

eng: Stay alert at all times, praying that you are strong enough to escape everything that is about to happen and to stand before the Human One. .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩcaɣnɩ mɩ-ɛsɛ nɛ ɩsɛɛ Ɛsɔ alɩwaatʋ kpeekpe nɛ ihiɣ ɖoŋ nɛ ɩpɩzɩ ɩlɩɩ mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ labʋ yɔ pɩ-taa nɛ iwolo Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ cɔlɔ.».

eng: He said to the dove sellers, Get these things out of here! Dont make my Fathers house a place of business. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi halikukunaa pɛdɩyaa se: «Ɩkpaɣ mɩ-halikukunaa nɛ ɩɖɛɛ lɔŋ. Ɩtaapɩsɩ man-Caa ɖɩɣa kɩyakʋ.».

eng: We had hoped he was the one who would redeem Israel. All these things happened three days ago.
local: Ŋgʋ́, ɖɩtaɣaɣ liu se ɛnʋ kɔŋnɩ yabʋ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma ñʋʋ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtɛm labʋ kɩyakɩŋ naadozo yɔ, pɩtɩŋa pɩlabʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born anew, its not possible to see Gods kingdom. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu kaɖɩ-ɩ se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-ŋ se yee pataɖaɣnɩ nɔɔyʋ lʋlʋʋ yɔ, ɛɛkɛɣ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa tʋ.».

eng: John testified about him, crying out, This is the one of whom I said, He who comes after me is greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Ɛ-yɔɔ Yohanɛɛsɩ lɩzɩ aseɣɖe nɛ ɛma kubuka se: «Ɛ-tɔm mɔyɔɔdaɣ se: ‹Ma-wayɩ lɛ, weyi ɛkɔŋ yɔ, ɛkɩlɩ-m, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛwɛɛ nɛ pʋcɔ palʋlɩ-m.› ».

eng: And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Peeɖe ɛkʋyaa ɛlɩɩ nɛ ewili ɛzɩ pɛcɛbɩ e-liu tɛɛ yɔ.

eng: If they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry? .
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se yee palabɩ weyi ma mɛwɛ ɛzɩ tɩʋ lɛŋ ñɩŋgʋ yɔ ɛzʋnɔ lɛ, ɛzɩma paala mbá mɩ ɩwɛ ɛzɩ tɩŋ kɩwɩŋ yɔ?».

eng: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, but he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
local: Yesu wɩlaɣ Ɛsɔtɔm Kɩyakɩŋ tɩŋa Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa wɩsɩ taa. Đoo yuu lɛ, ewolo ɛsʋʋ pʋʋ ŋgʋ́ payaɣ se Somiŋ Pʋʋ yɔ, kʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: His disciples remembered that it is written, Passion for your house consumes me.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tɔzɩ tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Hayɩ Ɛsɔ, mɔnsɔɔlɩ ña-ɖɩɣa sɔɔlʋʋ mbʋ́ yɔ, pʋkɔŋ-m kʋʋ.».

eng: But theres more: Some women from our group have left us stunned. They went to the tomb early this morning.
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɖa-taa halaa nabɛyɛ yeba nɛ pɩɖɩɣzɩ-ɖʋ pɩŋŋ. Pakʋyɩ tanaŋ kʋnɛ kɩ-tɛɛ cu nɛ powolo pasʋʋ pɩlaʋ taa.

eng: Nicodemus asked, How is it possible for an adult to be born? Its impossible to enter the mothers womb for a second time and be born, isnt it.
local: Peeɖe Niikodɛm pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ɛzɩma papɩzɩɣ paɖaɣnɩ lʋlʋʋ ɛyʋ weyi ɛtɛm paɣlʋʋ yɔ? Ŋgʋ́, ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛpɩsɩ e-ɖoo taa nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ tasɩ-ɩ lʋlʋʋ!».

eng: From his fullness we have all received grace upon grace;.
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɛha ɖatɩŋa payɩ mbʋ́ ɛwɛna yɔ pʋ-hɔɔlʋʋ nakʋyʋ, ɛlaba ɖatɩŋa lootiye siŋŋ, nɛ ɛwɛɛ pʋ-yɔɔ paa ɖooye.

eng: The men who were holding Jesus in custody taunted him while they beat him.
local: Ɛyáa mbá pɔɖɔkɩ Yesu yɔ, powoŋaɣ-ɩ nɛ pamakɩ-ɩ.

eng: They also led two other criminals to be executed with Jesus.
local: Paakpɛndɩnɩ ɛyáa azaɣ́a naalɛ nabɛyɛ se pakʋ pa nɛ Yesu.

eng: All the people rose early in the morning to hear him in the temple area.
local: Ɛyáa tɩŋa kʋyaɣ tanamɩŋ tɩŋa tɛɛ cu nɛ powoki Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ pewelesiɣni-i.

eng: Then the Jewish leaders asked him, By what authority are you doing these things? What miraculous sign will you show us.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yuda ñɩma awiya pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Hama lakasɩ nzɩ́ ŋpɩzɩɣ ŋla nɛ ŋwɩlɩnɩ-ɖʋ se pʋwɛɛ se ŋla ḿbʋ́?».

eng: and didnt find his body. They came to us saying that they had even seen a vision of angels who told them he is alive.
local: ɛlɛ́, patana e-tomnaɣ. Peeɖe papɩsaa peheyi-ɖʋ se Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa lɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ peheyi-wɛ se efema.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, its not possible to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-ŋ se weyi paɖaɣnɩɣ-ɩ lʋlʋʋ nɛ pɩtɩŋnɩ lɩm nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu pɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, e-ɖeke ɛkɛɣnɩ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa tʋ.

eng: as the Law was given through Moses, so grace and truth came into being through Jesus Christ.
local: Toovenim lɛ, Mooyiizi yɔɔ Ɛsɔ tɩŋnaa nɛ ɛɖʋ-ɖʋ paɣtʋ. Ɛlɛ́, Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔɔ ɛtɩŋnaa nɛ ɛlabɩ-ɖʋ lootiye nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-ɖʋ toovenim.

eng: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, Prophesy! Who hit you.
local: Pohuuzuu ɛ-ɛsɛ yɔɔ nɛ pamakɩ-ɩ nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ-ɩ se: «Kpeɣ se amabɩ-ŋ?».

eng: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him, along with the criminals, one on his right and the other on his left.
local: Patalɩ ɖenɖe payaɣ se Ñʋmbɔɔyaɣ yɔ lɛ, sɔɔjanaa ka Yesu kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ. Pakam ɖɔɖɔ ɛyáa azaɣ́a ḿbá peeɖe. Lɛlʋ ɛ-nɩwaŋ yɔɔ nɛ lɛlʋ ɛ-nɩmɩyɛ yɔɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, Destroy this temple and in three days Ill raise it up. .
local: Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Yee ɩyɔkɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa kanɛ yɔ, mamaɣ-kɛ nɛ kɩyakɩŋ naadozo.».

eng: Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit is spirit.
local: Meheyiɣ-ŋ se: ɛyʋ lʋlʋʋ ɛyʋ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yeki nɛ ɛyʋ kalɩzaɣ ɖaɣnɩ wɛʋ wezuu.

eng: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found things just as the women said. They didnt see him.
local: Pɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɖa-taa lɛlaa wolo pewili pɩlaʋ taa nɛ pamaɣnɩ ɛzɩ paaheyuu-ɖʋ yɔ. Patana Yesu.».

eng: No one has ever seen God. God the only Son, who is at the Fathers side, has made God known.
local: Nɔɔyʋ tana Ɛsɔ wiɖiyi kaʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛ-Pɩyalʋ ɛgbamɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩ nɛ Caa pɛwɛ ɖama taa nɛ ɖɩkɛ Ɛsɔ yɔ, ńɖɩ́ wɩlɩnɩ-ɖʋ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ kɛnaa yɔ.

eng: Insulting him, they said many other horrible things against him.
local: Paatʋ-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ siŋŋ.

eng: Jesus said, Father, forgive them, for they dont know what theyre doing. They drew lots as a way of dividing up his clothing.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu tɔ se: «Caa! Kpeɣ-wɛ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm, patasɩ mbʋ́ palakɩ yɔ.» Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, paɖʋwaɣ miika se patayɩ ɖama e-wondu.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, It took forty-six years to build this temple, and you will raise it up in three days.
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se ɛzɩma ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛma ɖɩɣa ŋgá pama-kɛ nɛ pɩnzɩ nɩɩnaza nɛ loɖo yɔ nɛ kɩyakɩŋ naadozo?.

eng: Dont be surprised that I said to you, You must be born anew.
local: Pɩtaalabɩ-ŋ piti se mɔntɔm se pʋwɛɛ se paɖaɣnɩ-ŋ lʋlʋʋ.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, You foolish people! Your dull minds keep you from believing all that the prophets talked about.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Kɩmɛlɛmɩŋ mɩ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma mɩ-laŋa wɛɛ kpɩzɩŋ nɛ iitisiɣ tɔm ndʋ́ náyaa kɔyɔɔdaa yɔ?.

eng: This is Johns testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, Who are you.
local: Yuda ñɩma ñʋndɩnaa kʋsɩ pɔ-cɔjɔnaa nɛ Leevi liɖe taa ñɩma nɛ Yeruusalɛm nɛ petiyi-wɛ se powolo pɔpɔzɩ Yohanɛɛsɩ se: «Ña lɛ anɩ?» Ɛlɛ́, ɛlɩzɩ aseɣɖe patɩŋa pɛ-ɛsɩndaa yem kpayɩ se: «Pɩtɛkɛ ma lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ.».

eng: As morning came, the elders of the people, both chief priests and legal experts, came together, and Jesus was brought before their council.
local: Tɛʋ fema lɛ, Yuda ñɩma sɔsaa nɛ pa-paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa pekpeɣli ɖama se powoni Yesu pɔ-tɔm hʋyaa cɔlɔ.

eng: The people were standing around watching, but the leaders sneered at him, saying, He saved others. Let him save himself if he really is the Christ sent from God, the chosen one.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ kasɩŋ peeɖe nɛ kɔcɔŋna nɛ Yuda ñɩma ñʋndɩnaa ñowoŋuu-i nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Yee toovenim se ɛkɛ Krɩstʋ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ yɔ, ɛya ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-ñʋʋ ɛzɩ ɛyabʋ lɛlaa ñɩŋgʋ yɔ!».

eng: But the temple Jesus was talking about was his body.
local: Ŋgʋ́, Yesu yɔɔdʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa tɔm yɔ, ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-tɔm ɛyɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́.

eng: Gods Spirit blows wherever it wishes. You hear its sound, but you dont know where it comes from or where it is going. Its the same with everyone who is born of the Spirit. .
local: Heɣlim makɩ ɖenɖe pʋsɔɔlaa yɔ nɛ ŋnɩɣ kɔkɔɖɛ nɖɩ́ pɩlakɩ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ŋŋnaɣ ɖenɖe pɩlɩnaa yaa ɖenɖe puwoki yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛnɩ weyi Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yeki nɛ ɛwɛnɩ Ɛsɔ wezuu yɔ.».

eng: Wasnt it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then enter into his glory? .
local: Pɩtaawɛɛ se Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ, ɛtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pʋcɔ ɛsʋʋ e-koboyaɣ taa yaa we?».

eng: They asked him, Then who are you? Are you Elijah? John said, Im not. Are you the prophet? John answered, No.
local: Peeɖe patasɩ-ɩ pɔzʋʋ se: «Ña lɛ anɩ kpem? Ña lɛ Elii yaa we?» Ecosi se: «Aayɩ.» Peeɖe patasɩ-ɩ pɔzʋʋ se: «Ña lɛ náyʋ weyi ɖɩɖaŋaa yɔ yaa we?» Ecosi se: «Aayɩ.».

eng: They said, If you are the Christ, tell us! He answered, If I tell you, you wont believe.
local: Powoni-i lɛ, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Heyi-ɖʋ nɛ ɖɩna se ña lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ yaa we?» Peeɖe Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Paa meheyi-mɩ yɔ, iitisiɣni-m.

eng: The soldiers also mocked him. They came up to him, offering him sour wine.
local: Sɔɔjanaa ñaawoŋ-i ɖɔɖɔ nɛ powoni-i sʋlʋm kɩñɩŋɩm nabʋyʋ se ɛñɔɔ nɛ pɔtɔŋ-ɩ se: «Yee ŋkɛ Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ yɔ, ya ña-maɣmaɣ ña-ñʋʋ!».

eng: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered what he had said, and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Yesu femnɩ sɩm taa lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tɔzɩ se eeheyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ nɛ petisi tɔm ndʋ́ eheyi-wɛ yɔ nɛ ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ se tɩkɛ toovenim.

eng: Nicodemus said, How are these things possible.
local: Peeɖe Niikodɛm pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ɛzɩma pɩpɩzɩɣ pɩla ḿbʋ́?».

eng: Then he interpreted for them the things written about himself in all the scriptures, starting with Moses and going through all the Prophets.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛkɛdɩ-wɛ ɖenɖe ɖenɖe paama ɛ-tɔm Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ nɛ ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ. Ɛpazɩnɩ Mooyiizi takayɩsɩ nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛtɛzɩnɩ náyaa ñɩnzɩ tɩŋa.

eng: They asked, Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself.
local: Ɛlɛ́, patasɩ-ɩ pɔzʋʋ se: «Ña lɛ anɩ kpem yɔ? Heyi-ɖʋ nɛ ɖiwolo ɖiheyi mbá petiyi-ɖʋ yɔ. Ña-maɣmaɣ lɛ, ŋmaɣzɩɣ se ŋkɛ anɩ kpem?».

eng: And if I ask you a question, you wont answer.
local: Paa mɔnpɔzɩ-mɩ natʋyʋ yɔ, iicosuu-m ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Above his head was a notice of the formal charge against him. It read This is the king of the Jews.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, paama tɔm nɛ paka ɛ-kaamaŋkaɣ ñʋʋ yɔɔ se: «Ɛnɛ lɛ Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ.».

eng: While Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Passover Festival, many believed in his name because they saw the miraculous signs that he did.
local: Paska Kazandʋ talaa lɛ, ɛyáa na hama lakasɩ nzɩ́ Yesu labɩ Yeruusalɛm yɔ nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, You are a teacher of Israel and you dont know these things?.
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Ŋkɛ Wɩlɩyʋ sɔsɔ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma hɛkʋ taa lɛ, pɩla ɛzɩma ŋŋnɩɣ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ yɔ?».

eng: When they came to Emmaus, he acted as if he was going on ahead.
local: Pɔñɔtɩnɩ tɛtʋ ndʋ́ powokaɣ tɩ-taa yɔ lɛ, Yesu la ɛzɩ ɛɖɛɣ poliŋ yɔ.

eng: John replied, I am a voice crying out in the wilderness, Make the Lords path straight, just as the prophet Isaiah said.
local: Peeɖe Yohanɛɛsɩ cosi-wɛ se ma lɛ weyi náyʋ Ezaayii yɔɔdɩ ɛ-tɔm yɔ. Ɛyɔɔdaa se: «Nɔɔyʋ susuu lakʋ taa se pɔñɔɔzɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ nʋmɔʋ nɛ pala nɛ kʋtʋʋzɩ camɩyɛ.».

eng: But from now on, the Human One will be seated on the right side of the power of God. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɩkpaɣʋnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ cakɩ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa Ɛsɔ Đoŋ Tɩŋa Tʋ cɔlɔ.».

eng: One of the criminals hanging next to Jesus insulted him: Arent you the Christ? Save yourself and us.
local: Ɛyáa azaɣ́a mbá pakam ɛ nɛ wɛ yɔ, pa-taa lɛlʋ tʋʋ-ɩ se: «Pɩcaɣ ña lɛ Krɩstʋ? Ŋŋyakɩ ña-maɣmaɣ ña-ñʋʋ se ŋkpɛndɩnɩ-ɖʋ yɔ?».

eng: But Jesus didnt trust himself to them because he knew all people.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛnʋ tataa-wɛ liu, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛsɩm patɩŋa payɩ pa-lɩmaɣza.

eng: I assure you that we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, but you dont receive our testimony.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-i se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-ŋ se mbʋ́ ɖɩsɩma yɔ, pʋ-tɔm ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ, mbʋ́ ɖɩnanɩ ɖɛ-ɛsɛ yɔ, pɩ-aseɣɖe ɖɩlɩzɩɣ. Ɛlɛ́, mɩ miitisiɣ.

eng: But they urged him, saying, Stay with us. Its nearly evening, and the day is almost over. So he went in to stay with them.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɛcɛyɩ-ɩ se: «Kɔɔ ɖa nɛ ŋ ɖɩsʋʋ cɩnɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖanaɣ ɖanaa nɛ pɩcaɣ yuu.» Peeɖe ewoba nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ pɛwɛɛ.

eng: Those sent by the Pharisees.
local: Mbá petiyi-wɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ cɔlɔ yɔ, Farɩsɩ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma kɛwɛ pa-taa.

eng: They all said, Are you Gods Son, then? He replied, You say that I am. .
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ patɩŋa se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ ŋkɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ?» Ecosi-wɛ se: «Ɩyɔɔdɩ camɩyɛ, ma lɛ ɛnʋ.».

eng: Responding, the other criminal spoke harshly to him, Dont you fear God, seeing that youve also been sentenced to die?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, lɛlʋ ñakalɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋgʋ́, paɖʋ-ŋ kʋñɔŋ ɛzɩ ɛnʋ yɔ, ŋŋñaŋ Ɛsɔ?».

eng: He didnt need anyone to tell him about human nature, for he knew what human nature was.
local: Pɩtaapɔzɩ se pacazɩ-ɩ heyuu ɛyáa tɔm. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛsɩm mbʋ́ pʋwɛ pa-taa yɔ.

eng: If I have told you about earthly things and you dont believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?.
local: Yee meheyiɣ-mɩ mbʋ́ pɩlakɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ yɔ pʋ-tɔm nɛ iitisiɣ lɛ, ɛzɩma ɩpɩzɩɣ itisi alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ meheyiɣ-mɩ mbʋ́ pɩlakɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ pɩ-ñɩndʋ yɔ?.

eng: After he took his seat at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛ nɛ wɛ pacaɣ tɔɔnaɣ ɖɩtɔɔyɛ nɛ ɛkpaɣ tindine nɛ ɛsɛɛ ɖɩ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛcɩyɩ-ɖɩ nɛ ɛcɛlɩ-wɛ.

eng: asked, Why do you baptize if you arent the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet.
local: Paba pɔzɩnɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋtɛkɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ yaa náyʋ weyi ɖɩɖaŋaa yɔ yaa Elii lɛ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋlakɩ ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ?».

eng: Then they said, Why do we need further testimony? Weve heard it from his own lips.
local: Ecosi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔtɔ se: «Ɛbɛ yɔɔ ɖɩtasɩɣ ñɩnʋʋ aseɣɖe tɩnaa yɔ? Pɩlɩ ɛ-nɔɔ taa nɛ ɖa-maɣmaɣ ɖɩnɩɩ.».

eng: Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that he was making more disciples and baptizing more than John .
local: Peheyi Farɩsɩ ñɩma se: «Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa kɩlɩ Yohanɛɛsɩ ñɩma ɖɔʋ. Ɛyáa mbá ɛlakɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ yɔ, pakɩlɩ Yohanɛɛsɩ ñɩma ɖɔʋ ɖɔɖɔ!» Toovenim lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ Yesu lakaɣnɩ ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ, ɛlɛ́ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lakaɣnɩ-wɛ-sɩ. Yesu nɩwa se tɔm ńdʋ́ʋ́ ɛyáa yɔɔdʋʋ lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ Yudee egeetiye taa nɛ ɛpɩsɩ Galilee ñɩnɖɛ taa.

eng: We are rightly condemned, for we are receiving the appropriate sentence for what we did. But this man has done nothing wrong.
local: Đa yɔ, pʋmʋnaa se paɖʋ-ɖʋ-kʋ ḿbʋ́ ɖa-lakasɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩsɩ yɔɔ. Ŋgʋ́ ɛnʋ yɔ, ɛtala kɩwɛɛkɩm nabʋyʋ.

eng: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven, the Human One.
local: Nɔɔyʋ takpa ɛsɔdaa wiɖiyi kaʋ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ, ɛnʋ lɩnɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ etii.

eng: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.
local: Peeɖe pɩcɛbɩ pɛ-ɛsɛ yɔɔ nɛ pasɩm-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛmʋʋ pɛ-ɛsɩndaa.

eng: John answered, I baptize with water. Someone greater stands among you, whom you dont recognize.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yohanɛɛsɩ cosi-wɛ se: «Ma lɛ, malakɩ ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ. Ɛlɛ́, nɔɔyʋ wɛ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa, ɩtasɩm-ɩ.

eng: Jesus had to go through Samaria.
local: Pʋcɔ ewolo Galilee lɛ, pɩɩwɛɛ se ɛtɩŋnɩ Samaarɩɩ egeetiye taa.

eng: Then he said, Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.
local: Peeɖe eheyi Yesu se: «Yesu! Ŋkɔm ñe-kewiyaɣ tɔɔʋ lɛ, ŋtɔzɩ mɔ-yɔɔ.».

eng: They said to each other, Werent our hearts on fire when he spoke to us along the road and when he explained the scriptures for us.
local: Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔyɔɔdɩnɩ ɖama se: «Naze ḿbʋ́ eheyaɣ-ɖʋ Ɛsɔtɔm nʋmɔʋ taa nɛ ɛlɩzɩɣ-ɖʋ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ nɛ ɖa-laŋa hʋlʋmʋʋ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ!».

eng: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so must the Human One be lifted up.
local: Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Mooyiizi kopuzi ɖʋm weyi ɛlabɩnɩ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ yɔ ɖaʋ yɔɔ lakʋ taa, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛɛ se pala Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ.

eng: He comes after me, but Im not worthy to untie his sandal straps.
local: Ma-wayɩ lɛ, ɛnʋ kɔŋna. Ɛkɩlɩ-m, mantamʋna se moluŋ nɛ mɛyɛɖɩ ɛ-naataŋgbala tɔnzɩ.».

eng: He came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, which was near the land Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
local: Ɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, ɛtalɩ Samaarɩɩ egeetiye taa tɛtʋ ndʋ́ payaɣ se Siicaarɩ yɔ tɩ-taa. Tɩwɛ hayɩm mbʋ́ Yakɔɔbʋ ha ɛ-pɩyalʋ Yozɛɛfʋ yɔ pʋ-cɔlɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that today you will be with me in paradise. .
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ ñocosi-i se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-ŋ se sɔnɔ ɖeke lɛ, ma nɛ ŋ ɖiwoki Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ e-koboyaɣ taa.».

eng: They got up right then and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pakʋyɩ papɩsɩ Yeruusalɛm nɛ pamaɣnɩ tiyiyaa ḿbá pe-hiu nɛ kʋɖʋm nɛ pa-taabalaa. Paakpeɣlaa.

eng: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
local: nɛ weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ lɛ, ehiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ.

eng: This encounter took place across the Jordan in Bethany where John was baptizing.
local: Pʋnɛ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩlakaɣ tɛtʋ cikpetu ndʋ́ payaɣ se Betaanii yɔ, tɩ-taa ɖenɖe Yohanɛɛsɩ lakaɣ ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ yɔ. Tɛtʋ ńdʋ́, tɩɩwɛ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ wayɩ.

eng: Jacobs well was there. Jesus was tired from his journey, so he sat down at the well. It was about noon.
local: Ɛtalɩ peeɖe lɛ, pɩtalɩ ɛzɩ wɩsɩ siɣu yɔ. Pɩɩnɩ-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ, nɛ ɛcaɣ Yakɔɔbʋ lɔkɔ nɔɔ.

eng: It was now about noon, and darkness covered the whole earth until about three oclock.
local: Pʋkɔma ɛzɩ wɩsɩ siɣu yɔ lɛ, puhuuzi wɩsɩ yɔɔ nɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ yu ɛjaɖɛ kpeekpe kpemm nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ ɖanaɣ ñɩɣtʋ naadozo. Peeɖe pɩsaʋ sɔsɔʋ ŋgʋ́ paakaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa yɔ, kɩfaɣ kɩ-hɛkʋ taa kpu.

eng: They were saying to each other, The Lord really has risen! He appeared to Simon.
local: nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ fema toovenim nɛ ɛlɩzɩ ɛ-tɩ ɛwɩlɩ Siimɔɔ.».

eng: God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him wont perish but will have eternal life.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ sɔɔlɩ ɛyáa siŋŋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛha ɛ-Pɩyalʋ ɛgbamɩyɛ se weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɛtaatɛŋɛlɩ, ɛlɛ́, ehiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ.

eng: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!.
local: Tɛʋ fema lɛ, Yohanɛɛsɩ na Yesu wokaɣ ɛ-cɔlɔ. Peeɖe eheyi ɛyáa se: «Ɩna Ɛsɔ Heɣa. Ŋ́gá mʋʋnɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm.

eng: A Samaritan woman came to the well to draw water. Jesus said to her, Give me some water to drink. .
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, Samaarɩɩ halʋ nɔɔyʋ wolo lɩm luu nɛ Yesu pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ha-m lɩm nɛ mɔñɔɔ.».

eng: Crying out in a loud voice, Jesus said, Father, into your hands I entrust my life . After he said this, he breathed for the last time.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu ma kubuka ɖoŋ se: «Caa! Manɖʋzɩ man-kalɩzaɣ ñe-nesi taa.» Ɛtɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, etisi fezuu nɛ ɛhɛɛ.

eng: Then the two disciples described what had happened along the road and how Jesus was made known to them as he broke the bread.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ḿbá pa-naalɛ pɛkɛdɩ-wɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pɩlabɩ-wɛ nʋmɔʋ taa yɔ, nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ paasɩm-ɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛcɩyaɣ tindine yɔ.

eng: God didnt send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through him.
local: Toovenim lɛ, Ɛsɔ tetiyi ɛ-Pɩyalʋ ɛyáa cɔlɔ se ɛhʋʋnɩ-wɛ nɛ ɛkʋ pɔ-tɔm. Ɛlɛ́, etiyi-i se ɛya pa-ñʋʋ.

eng: This is the one about whom I said, He who comes after me is really greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Kɔ-tɔm mɔyɔɔdaɣ se ma-wayɩ lɛ, nɔɔyʋ kɔŋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛkɩlɩ-m, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛwɛɛ nɛ pʋcɔ palʋlɩ-m.

eng: His disciples had gone into the city to buy him some food.
local: Ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa kowobi tɛtʋ taa tɔɔnaɣ yabʋ.

eng: When the centurion saw what happened, he praised God, saying, Its really true: this man was righteous.
local: Peeɖe Room sɔɔja sɔsɔ na mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pɩlaba yɔ nɛ ɛsa Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Ɛnɛ, ɛɛkɛ ɛyʋ kʋtʋʋzʋ toovenim!».

eng: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said, Peace be with you! .
local: Pɛkɛdaɣ-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yesu lɩɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩwɛɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa.».

eng: Whoever believes in him isnt judged; whoever doesnt believe in him is already judged, because they dont believe in the name of Gods only Son.
local: Weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ yɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɛɛhʋʋnɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛkʋ ɛ-tɔm. Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛ́lɛ́ fɛyɩnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ tɛm-ɩ hʋnaʋ nɛ ɛkʋ ɛ-tɔm. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛfɛyɩnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ, ŋgʋ́ ɛnʋ lɛ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ ɛgbamɩyɛ.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but I came baptizing with water so that he might be made known to Israel.
local: Mantaasɩm-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, mɔnkɔm mala ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ se Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma tɩŋa ɛsɩm-ɩ.».

eng: The Samaritan woman asked, Why do you, a Jewish man, ask for something to drink from me, a Samaritan woman.
local: Peeɖe halʋ ɛnʋ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋkɛ Yuda tʋ lɛ, ŋcalɩ se we nɛ ŋsʋlʋmʋʋ-m lɩm?» Halʋ ɛnʋ, ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Samaarɩɩ ñɩma nɛ Yuda ñɩma paacaɣ ɖama.

eng: All the crowds who had come together to see this event returned to their homes beating their chests after seeing what had happened.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ ŋgá katɩŋa kaawobi se kana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pakaŋ Yesu yɔ, kana ḿbʋ́ lɛ, katɩmɩɣ kpem yɔɔ.

eng: They were terrified and afraid. They thought they were seeing a ghost.
local: Peeɖe sɔɔndʋ kpa-wɛ pɩŋŋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paasɩm se ñazɩñazʋʋ nakʋyʋ.

eng: After this Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He didnt want to travel in Judea, because the Jewish authorities wanted to kill him.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu kʋyɩ nɛ ɛcɔʋ Galilee egeetiye taa. Ɛtaasɔɔlɩ se ɛcɔɔ Yudee ñɩnɖɛ taa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Yuda ñɩma ñʋndɩnaa ñɩnaɣ se pakʋ-ɩ.

eng: This is the basis for judgment: The light came into the world, and people loved darkness more than the light, for their actions are evil.
local: Mbʋ́ piyeki nɛ pahʋʋnɩ ɛyáa yɔ lɛ se: weyi ɛkɛ ñalɩmɩyɛ yɔ, ɛkɔm ɛyáa kpeekpe cɔlɔ. Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pɔsɔɔlɩ cɩkpɛndʋʋ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ-ɖɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, palakɩ ɛzaɣ́tʋ.

eng: John testified, I saw the Spirit coming down from heaven like a dove, and it rested on him.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Mana Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu, kɩlɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa ɛzɩ halikuku yɔ nɛ kitii kɩcaɣ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: Jesus responded, If you recognized Gods gift and who is saying to you, Give me some water to drink, you would be asking him and he would give you living water. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-i se: «Yee ŋŋsɩm mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ haɣ yɔ nɛ ŋtɩlɩ ɖɔɖɔ weyi ɛsʋlʋmʋʋ-ŋ lɩm yɔ yɔ, ñaasʋlʋmɩnɩ-ɩ, pʋbʋ ɛɛha-ŋ lɩm mbʋ́ pɩhaɣ wezuu yɔ.».

eng: And everyone who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance observing these things.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu ɛgbaɣdɩnaa tɩŋa nɛ halaa mbá patɩŋ-ɩ nɛ ɖooo Galilee yɔ, paba ñaasɩŋ poliŋ nɛ pɔcɔŋnɩ mbʋ́ pɩlakaɣ yɔ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you startled? Why are doubts arising in your hearts?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛbɛ yɔɔ piliɣtiɣ-mɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɩpʋtʋʋ mɩ-taa? Ɛbɛ lakɩ-mɩ?.

eng: When it was almost time for the Jewish Festival of Booths.
local: Ŋgʋ́, pa-kazandʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ pesiki kɩɖanɖɩŋ yɔ, tɩɩwɩzɩ talʋʋ.

eng: All who do wicked things hate the light and dont come to the light for fear that their actions will be exposed to the light.
local: Weyi ɛlakɩ ɛzaɣ́tʋ yɔ, ɛɛcaɣ ñalɩmɩyɛ kpem, ɛɛñɔtʋʋnɩ-ɖɩ ɖɔɖɔ: eseɣ se pataana mbʋ́ ɛlakɩ yɔ nɛ pakʋyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, The one on whom you see the Spirit coming down and resting is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.
local: Mantaasɩmdɩ-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ weyi etiyi-m se mala ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ yɔ, eheyi-m se: ‹Ŋkɔŋ naʋ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tiba nɛ kɩcaɣ nɔɔyʋ yɔɔ. Ɛnʋ kɔŋnɩ ɛyáa labʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖoŋ.›.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, you dont have a bucket and the well is deep. Where would you get this living water?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, halʋ ɛnʋ eheyi Yesu se: «Đajaa! Lɔkɔ wɛ cukaŋ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ, ŋgʋ́ ŋfɛyɩnɩ tɔka ŋgá poluuni lɩm yɔ. Ɛzɩma ŋpɩzɩɣ ŋhiɣ lɩm mbʋ́ pɩhaɣ wezuu yɔ?.

eng: Now there was a man named Joseph who was a member of the council. He was a good and righteous man.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa lɛ, Yuda ñɩma tɔm hʋyaa sɔsaa taa nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe, payaɣ-ɩ se Yozɛɛfʋ. Ɛ-tɛ lɛ Ariimatee, Yudee egeetiye taa. Ɛ-lɩmaɣza kɛwɛ ndɩ nɛ tɔm hʋyaa lɛlaa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛtaatisiɣ se pakʋ Yesu. Ɛɛkɛ ɛyʋ kʋtʋʋzʋ siŋŋ nɛ ɛwɛɛ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ ɖaŋʋʋ yɔɔ.

eng: Look at my hands and my feet. Its really me! Touch me and see, for a ghost doesnt have flesh and bones like you see I have. .
local: Ɩcɔnɩ me-nesi nɛ ma-naŋgbanzɩ nɛ ɩna. Toovenim lɛ, ma! Itukuni-m nɛ ɩna, mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩtɛm sɩm se ñazɩñazʋʋ fɛyɩnɩ tomnaɣ ɛzɩ ma yɔ.» .

eng: Jesus brothers said to him, Leave Galilee. Go to Judea so that your disciples can see the amazing works that you do.
local: Peeɖe e-newaa heyi-i se: «Kʋyɩ cɩnɛ nɛ ŋwolo Yudee egeetiye taa nɛ ñɔ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa na ɖɔɖɔ tʋma wena ŋlakɩ yɔ.

eng: Whoever does the truth comes to the light so that it can be seen that their actions were done in God. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛlakɩ toovenim yɔ, ɛlɩɣ ñalɩmɩyɛ taa se ɛyáa ɛna se mbʋ́ ɛlakɩ yɔ, pɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ.».

eng: I have seen and testified that this one is Gods Son.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ mana ḿbʋ́, pʋ-yɔɔ mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ toovenim se ɛnʋ lɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ.».

eng: You arent greater than our father Jacob, are you? He gave this well to us, and he drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.
local: Đɔ-cɔzɔ Yakɔɔbʋ yebinini-ɖʋ lɔkɔ ŋ́gʋ́. Ɛ nɛ e-piya nɛ e-wondu pɔñɔ kɩ-taa lɩm. Ŋmaɣzɩɣ se ŋkɩlɩ Yakɔɔbʋ yaa we?».

eng: This man went to Pilate and asked for Jesus body.
local: Ɛnʋ wobina ɛna Pilaatɩ nɛ etendi-i se yee etisiɣ yɔ, ɛcɛlɩ-ɩ Yesu nɛ ewoni epi.

eng: As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ e-nesi nɛ ɛ-naŋgbanzɩ.

eng: Those who want to be known publicly dont do things secretly. Since you can do these things, show yourself to the world.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ liu ɛwɛɛ se pasɩm-ɩ yɔ, ɛɛmɛsɩɣ mbʋ́ ɛlakɩ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yele nɛ kedeŋa kpeekpe na mbʋ́ ŋlakɩ yɔ.».

eng: After this Jesus and his disciples went into Judea, where he spent some time with them and was baptizing.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa powolo Yudee egeetiye taa nɛ pacaɣ peeɖe kɩyakɩŋ nɩɩyɩ nɛ palakɩ ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ.

eng: The next day John was standing again with two of his disciples.
local: Tɛʋ fema lɛ, Yohanɛɛsɩ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa naalɛ papɩsɩ lone ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa peeɖe.

eng: Jesus answered, Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-i se: «Weyi ɛñɔʋ lɩm pʋnɛ yɔ, lɔkɔtʋ tasɩɣ-ɩ ɖɔkʋʋ.

eng: Taking it down, he wrapped it in a linen cloth and laid it in a tomb carved out of the rock, in which no one had ever been buried.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ ha-ɩ nɔɔ lɛ, ewolo etisi-i kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɛhɔkɩ-ɩ pɩsaʋ kʋhʋlʋmʋʋ nakʋyʋ taa nɛ ewoni-i epi pɩlaʋ ŋgʋ́ pɛcɛsɩ ɛwaɣ taa nɛ petepimdi nɔɔyʋ kɩ-taa yɔ kɩ-taa.

eng: Because they were wondering and questioning in the midst of their happiness, he said to them, Do you have anything to eat? .
