local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pa-laŋa kahʋlʋmaa siŋŋ nɛ pɩlabɩ-wɛ piti yɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, patapɩzɩ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ kpaagbaa. Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɩwɛnɩ tɔɔnaɣ nakɛyɛ cɩnɛ yaa we?».

eng: His brothers said this because even they didnt believe in him.
local: Toovenim lɛ, e-newaa maɣmaɣ taawɛnɩ ɖɔɖɔ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: After this there was a Jewish festival, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu wolo Yeruusalɛm Yuda ñɩma kazandʋ natʋyʋ yɔɔ.

eng: John was baptizing at Aenon near Salem because there was a lot of water there, and people were coming to him and being baptized. .
local: Ŋgʋ́, Yohanɛɛsɩ ñaawɛ Enɔɔ tɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩñɔtɩnɩ Salɩm ñɩndʋ yɔ tɩ-taa, nɛ ɛyáa woki ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛlakɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, lɩm kɔɖɔ peeɖe.

eng: When he saw Jesus walking along he said, Look! The Lamb of God.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yohanɛɛsɩ na Yesu ɖɛɣaɣ. Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩna Ɛsɔ Heɣa.».

eng: but whoever drinks from the water that I will give will never be thirsty again. The water that I give will become in those who drink it a spring of water that bubbles up into eternal life. .
local: Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ñɔʋ lɩm mbʋ́ ma mahaɣ-ɩ yɔ, lɔkɔtʋ ɛɛtasɩɣ-ɩ ɖɔkʋʋ wiɖiyi kaʋ. Lɩm ḿbʋ́, pʋwɛɣ ɛ-taa ɛzɩ hiɖe nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa lɩm ɛɛtɛŋ kaʋ nɛ pɩhaɣ wezuu maatɛŋ yɔ.».

eng: And Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ɛyáa kpema lɛ, Yesu kʋyɩ nɛ ewolo ɛkpa Somiŋ Pʋʋ yɔɔ.

eng: It was the Preparation Day for the Sabbath, and the Sabbath was quickly approaching.
local: Pɩɩkɛ kemeɣa nakɛyɛ wiye, kifeŋaɣ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ.

eng: They gave him a piece of baked fish.
local: Peeɖe pɛcɛlɩ-ɩ kpakpayaɣ ŋgá pɔhɔbɩ-kɛ yɔ ke-titiɖe.

eng: Jesus replied, For you, anytime is fine. But my time hasnt come yet.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Alɩwaatʋ tatalɩda se mowolo peeɖe. Piyele mɩ lɛ, ɩpɩzɩɣ iwolo paa alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́.

eng: In Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate in the north city wall is a pool with the Aramaic name Bethsaida. It had five covered porches.
local: Lɩm kujomuu nakʋyʋ wɛ Yeruusalɛm tɛtʋ kámaɣ́ yɔɔ nɔnɔɔ ŋgá payaɣ se Heŋ Nɔnɔɔ yɔ kɔ-cɔlɔ. Paasiɣ kʋ-cɔlɔ laŋasɩ kagbaanzɩ nɛ pɛɛ. Payaɣ peeɖe nɛ Ebree taa se Betɛsda.

eng: A debate started between Johns disciples and a certain Jew about cleansing rituals.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa nabɛyɛ nɛ Yuda tʋ nɔɔyʋ, pɔhɔŋaɣnɩ ɖama tɔm cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ wɛtʋ sɔnzɩ yɔɔ.

eng: The two disciples heard what he said, and they followed Jesus.
local: Ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ḿbá panɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pakʋyɩ patɩŋ Yesu wayɩ.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, give me this water, so that I will never be thirsty and will never need to come here to draw water.
local: Peeɖe halʋ ɛnʋ ecosi-i se: «Đajaa! Ha-m lɩm ḿbʋ́ nɛ lɔkɔtʋ ɛtaatasɩ-m ɖɔkʋʋ nɛ mantasɩ kɔm cɩnɛ se moluu lɩm.».

eng: Early in the morning he returned to the temple. All the people gathered around him, and he sat down and taught them.
local: Kifem tanaŋ tɛɛ cu lɛ, ɛpɩsɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ tɩŋa wolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛcaɣ nɛ ɛpazɩ-kɛ wɩlʋʋ Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph. They saw the tomb and how Jesus body was laid in it.
local: Halaa mbá paatɩŋ Yesu nɛ ɖooo Galilee yɔ, patɩŋ Yozɛɛfʋ nɛ powolo pana pɩlaʋ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɛhɛzɩ Yesu yɔ.

eng: Taking it, he ate it in front of them.
local: nɛ ɛmʋ-ɖɩ ɛtɔɔ pɛ-ɛsɩndaa.

eng: The world cant hate you. It hates me, though, because I testify that its works are evil.
local: Kedeŋa ñɩma ɛɛpaɖɩɣ-mɩ, ɛlɛ́, ma papaɖɩɣ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, malɩzɩɣ aseɣɖe se mbʋ́ palakɩ yɔ, pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu.

eng: and a crowd of people who were sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed sat there.
local: Kʋdɔndɩnaa sakɩyɛ hɛnaɣ laŋasɩ ńzɩ́ sɩ-tɛɛ: yʋlʋmaa nɛ tala tɩnaa nɛ kalɩkalasɩ. Paɖaŋaa se lɩm eciɣdi.

eng: They came to John and said, Rabbi, look! The man who was with you across the Jordan, the one about whom you testified, is baptizing and everyone is flocking to him.
local: Peeɖe powobi Yohanɛɛsɩ cɔlɔ nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Ŋtɔzʋʋ abalʋ weyi ña nɛ ɩ ɩɩwɛ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ wayɩ nɛ ŋheyi-ɖʋ ɛ-tɔm yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ yaa we? Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɛlakɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa woki ɛ-cɔlɔ!».

eng: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he asked, What are you looking for? They said, Rabbi , where are you staying.
local: Yesu kisaa nɛ ɛna patɩŋaɣ-ɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛbɛ ɩñɩnɩɣ?» Paba ñɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Raabii! Le ŋwɛɛ?» Raabii tɔbʋʋ se Wɩlɩyʋ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Go, get your husband, and come back here. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-i se: «Wolo ŋyaa ña-walʋ nɛ ña nɛ ɩ ɩkɔɔ.».

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees brought a woman caught in adultery. Placing her in the center of the group.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma patanɩ halʋ nɔɔyʋ. Ɛlakaɣ acɛyɛ nɛ patam-ɩ. Peeɖe pasɩnzɩ-ɩ ɛyáa hɛkʋ taa.

eng: then they went away and prepared fragrant spices and perfumed oils. They rested on the Sabbath, in keeping with the commandment.
local: Pana ḿbʋ́ lɛ, papɩsɩ se pañɩnɩ num ndɩ ndɩ nɛ tɩlaaɖɩnaa nɛ powoni pataa-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, paahɛzɩ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye ɛzɩ paɖʋʋ paɣtʋ taa yɔ nɛ pʋcɔ powolo.

eng: Jesus said to them, These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you—that everything written about me in the Law from Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-wɛ se: «Mbʋ́ pʋ-tɔm maaheyi-mɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩɩwɛɛ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ labɩnɩ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩɩwɛɛ se mɔn-tɔm ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa paama Mooyiizi nɛ náyaa nɛ keɣsi maɖaa pa-takayɩsɩ taa yɔ, tɩla.».

eng: You go up to the festival. Im not going to this one because my time hasnt yet come. .
local: Mɩ lɛ, iwolo kazandʋ taa, ma maapadɩɣ wobu, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ma-alɩwaatʋ tatalɩda.».

eng: A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.
local: Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe. Pɩnzɩ niidozo nɛ lutozo yɔ, pɩwɩɣʋ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: John replied, No one can receive anything unless it is given from heaven.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ cosi-wɛ se: «Mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ha ɛyʋ yɔ, pi-ɖeke koŋ ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛmʋ.

eng: He replied, Come and see. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four oclock in the afternoon.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ecosi-wɛ se: «Ɩkɔɔ nɛ ɩna.» Peeɖe patɩŋ-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ powolo pana ɖenɖe ɛwɛɛ yɔ nɛ pa nɛ ɩ pacaɣ peeɖe kɩyakʋ ŋ́gʋ́. Đanaɣ wɩsɩ wɛlʋʋ pɩlakaɣ ḿbʋ́.

eng: The woman replied, I dont have a husband. You are right to say, I dont have a husband, Jesus answered.
local: Peeɖe halʋ heyi-i se ɛfɛyɩnɩ walʋ. Yesu cosi-i se: «Ŋwɛnɩ toovenim se ŋtɔ se ŋfɛyɩnɩ walʋ.

eng: they said to Jesus, Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of committing adultery.
local: nɛ peheyi Yesu se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Halʋ ɛnɛ, ɛlakaɣ acɛyɛ nɛ ɖɩtam-ɩ.

eng: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures.
local: Peeɖe ekuli pa-lɩmaɣza yɔɔ se panɩɩ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa tɔm tɔbʋʋ.

eng: When Jesus saw him lying there, knowing that he had already been there a long time, he asked him, Do you want to get well? .
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yesu na-ɩ, ɛhɛnaa nɛ ɛtɩlɩ se ɖooo lɔŋ ɛwɛ peeɖe. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ñe-liu wɛɛ se ñɔ-yɔɔ ɛcɛ naa we?».

eng: You yourselves can testify that I said that Im not the Christ but that Im the one sent before him.
local: Mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩlɩzɩ ma-aseɣɖe se mɔntɔm se: ‹Pɩtɛkɛ ma lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ tiyi-m se mɛnɖɛɛ-ɩ nɔɔ.›.

eng: Youve had five husbands, and the man you are with now isnt your husband. Youve spoken the truth. .
local: mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ŋtɛm walʋʋ abalaa kagbaanzɩ nɛ abalʋ weyi ña nɛ ɩ ɩwɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, ɛtɛkɛ ña-walʋ. Ŋyɔɔdɩ toovenim.».

eng: In the Law, Moses commanded us to stone women like this. What do you say.
local: Mooyiizi ɖʋ paɣtʋ taa se payaɣ pɩtɛ halaa ḿbá pɛɛ nɛ pasɩ. Ña yɔ, ñɔtɔŋ se we?».

eng: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
local: Ɛtɛm-wɛ heyuu ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛcaɣ Galilee egeetiye taa peeɖe.

eng: One of the two disciples who heard what John said and followed Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
local: Tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa naalɛ mbá Yohanɛɛsɩ yɔɔdɩ Yesu tɔm nɛ panɩɩ nɛ patɩŋ Yesu ɛnʋ ɛ-wayɩ yɔ, payaɣ pa-taa lɛlʋ se Andree. Ɛnʋ lɛ Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ neu.

eng: He said to them, This is what is written: the Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day.
local: nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ndʋ́ pama ka-taa yɔ lɛ se pʋwɛɛ se Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ, ɛtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ nɛ ɛsɩ nɛ pepim-i. Kɩyakʋ naadozo ñɩŋgʋ wiye lɛ, pefezi-i nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ pɩlaʋ taa.

eng: The sick man answered him, Sir, I dont have anyone who can put me in the water when it is stirred up. When Im trying to get to it, someone else has gotten in ahead of me.
local: Kʋdɔndʋ ɛnʋ ecosi-i se: «Hayɩ Đajaa! Mɛnfɛyɩ nɔɔyʋ se ɛkpaɣ-m nɛ etisi-m lɩm taa alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ peciɣdiɣ-pʋ yɔ. Mañakɩ pana se mowoki lɛ, nɔɔyʋ ñɛtɛm-m layʋʋ.».

eng: The groom is the one who is getting married. The friend of the groom stands close by and, when he hears him, is overjoyed at the grooms voice. Therefore, my joy is now complete.
local: Ɩtɛm naʋ se weyi ɛkpaɣ halʋ yɔ, ɛnʋ lɛ halɩkpaɣyʋ. Meheyiɣ-mɩ se ma mɛwɛ ɛzɩ halɩkpaɣyʋ ɛgbaɣdʋ yɔ. Halɩkpaɣyʋ ɛgbaɣdʋ wɛɣ ɛ-cɔlɔ se ɛsɩnɩ-ɩ. Ɛ-taa hʋlʋmʋʋ siŋŋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛnɩɣ halɩkpaɣyʋ taŋ yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ man-taa tɛzɩ hʋlʋmʋʋ siŋŋ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: The woman said, Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
local: Halʋ ɛnʋ ecosi-i se: «Đajaa! Manaɣ se ŋkɛ náyʋ.

eng: They said this to test him, because they wanted a reason to bring an accusation against him. Jesus bent down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
local: Pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ ḿbʋ́ se pañɩ ɛ-nɔɔ cɔlɔ tɔm nɛ pana ndʋ́ paawana nɛ pakʋyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Yesu luŋ nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ e-niye tataa.

eng: However, after his brothers left for the festival, he went too—not openly but in secret.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yesu newaa kʋyɩ nɛ powolo kazandʋ taa. Pɛɖɛwa lɛ, ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛkʋyɩ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ ewolo. Ɛlɛ́, eteyele se nɔɔyʋ ɛna-ɩ.

eng: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, We have found the Messiah .
local: Mbʋ́ Andree ɛnʋ ɛcalɩ labʋ yɔ lɛ se: ɛñɩnɩ ɛ-ɖalʋ Siimɔɔ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Đɩna Meesii.» Meesii tɔbʋʋ se Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ.

eng: and a change of heart and life for the forgiveness of sins must be preached in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
local: Pʋwɛɛ ɖɔɖɔ se posusini ɛ-nɔɔ ajɛya kpeekpe taa ɛyáa se pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ nɛ Ɛsɔ kpeɣ-wɛ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se papazɩnɩ Yeruusalɛm.

eng: Jesus said to him, Get up! Pick up your mat and walk. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-i se: «Kʋyɩ ŋkpaɣ ña-ñalʋʋ nɛ ŋɖɔ!».

eng: He must increase and I must decrease.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se ɛ́lɛ́ hɩɖɛ ɛsɛɛ nɛ pɔsɔɔ ma mɔ-yɔɔ.».

eng: Our ancestors worshipped on this mountain, but you and your people say that it is necessary to worship in Jerusalem.
local: Samaarɩɩ ñɩma ɖa, ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa luŋaɣ Ɛsɔ tɛɛ pʋʋ kʋnɛ kʋ-yɔɔ nɛ mɩ Yuda ñɩma mɩtɔŋ se Yeruusalɛm pʋwɛɛ se poluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ. Ña ñamaɣzɩɣ se we?».

eng: They continued to question him, so he stood up and replied, Whoever hasnt sinned should throw the first stone.
local: Pɛ-ɛsɩyɛ sɛnɩ pɔzʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Mɩ-taa weyi ɛnaɣ se ɛtala kɩwɛɛkɩm nabʋyʋ kaʋ yɔ, ɛcalɩ kpaɣʋ pɩyɛ nɛ ɛyaɣ halʋ ɛnɛ.».

eng: The Jewish leaders were looking for Jesus at the festival. They kept asking, Where is he.
local: Yuda ñɩma ñʋndɩnaa kɛwɛɛ pañɩnɩɣ-ɩ kazandʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ se: «Le ɛwɛɛ?».

eng: He led him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas .
local: Peeɖe ɛkpaɣ-ɩ nɛ ewoni-i Yesu cɔlɔ. Patalaa lɛ, Yesu cɔnɩ-ɩ pɩŋŋ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Ña lɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ pɩyalʋ weyi payaɣ se Siimɔɔ yɔ, pɔkɔŋ-ŋ yaʋ se Sefaasɩ.» Sefaasɩ tɔbʋʋ se Pɩyɛ.

eng: You are witnesses of these things.
local: Mɩ lɛ mbʋ́ pɩlaba yɔ pɩ-aseɣɖe tɩnaa.

eng: Immediately the man was well, and he picked up his mat and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
local: Ɛtɩyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ nɛ ɛkpaɣ ɛ-ñalʋʋ nɛ ɛpazɩ ɖɔm. Ŋgʋ́, pɩɩkɛ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye.

eng: The one who comes from above is above all things. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all things.
local: «Weyi palʋlɩ-ɩ tataa yɔ, ɛ-tɛ lɛ peeɖe, ɛyɔɔdʋʋ mbʋ́ pɩlakɩ tataa yɔ pʋ-tɔm. Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ lɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, ɛkɩlɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Believe me, woman, the time is coming when you and your people will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-i se: «Halʋ ña! Tisi se alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ kɔŋ nɛ paatasɩɣ Caa Ɛsɔ tɛɛ luŋuu pʋʋ kʋnɛ kʋ-yɔɔ yaa Yeruusalɛm. Ɛlɛ́, poluŋuu ɛ-tɛɛ paa le.

eng: Bending down again, he wrote on the ground.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛɖaɣnɩ luŋuu nɛ ɛwɛɛ ɛmaɣ tataa.

eng: The crowds were murmuring about him. Hes a good man, some said, but others were saying, No, he tricks the people.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ yɔɔdaɣ ɛ-tɔm siŋŋ: nabɛyɛ se: «Ɛkɛ ɛyʋ kɩbanʋ.» Lɛlaa se: «Aayɩ, epeɣziɣ ɛyáa.».

eng: The next day Jesus wanted to go into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, Follow me. .
local: Tɛʋ fema lɛ, Yesu ɖʋ se ewoki Galilee egeetiye taa. Ɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, ɛna Filiipu nɛ eheyi-i se: «Tɩŋ ma-wayɩ.».

eng: Look, Im sending to you what my Father promised, but you are to stay in the city until you have been furnished with heavenly power. .
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se ma lɛ, mentiyiɣni-mɩ ɖoŋ weyi man-Caa ɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ-mɩ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se ɩcaɣ Yeruusalɛm nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖoŋ ɛnʋ etibi mɩ-yɔɔ yɔ.».

eng: The Jewish leaders said to the man who had been healed, Its the Sabbath; you arent allowed to carry your mat.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Yuda ñɩma awiya heyi-i se: «Sɔnɔ pɩkɛ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ, ɖa-paɣtʋ taha nʋmɔʋ se ŋsɩɣlɩ ña-ñalʋʋ.».

eng: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
local: Ɛyɔɔdʋʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛnawa nɛ ɛnɩɩ yɔ pʋ-tɔm. Ɛlɛ́, nɔɔyʋ eetisiɣ aseɣɖe nɖɩ́ ɛlɩzɩɣ yɔ.

eng: You and your people worship what you dont know; we worship what we know because salvation is from the Jews.
local: Samaarɩɩ ñɩma mɩ! Ɩɩsɩŋ Ɛsɔ weyi ɛ-tɛɛ iluŋuu yɔ. Đa Yuda ñɩma lɛ, ɖɩsɩm weyi ɛ-tɛɛ ɖiluŋuu yɔ: ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa ñʋʋ yaɖʋ lɩɣna.

eng: Those who heard him went away, one by one, beginning with the elders. Finally, only Jesus and the woman were left in the middle of the crowd.
local: Panɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa-taa sɔsaa calɩ lɔyʋʋ kʋɖʋm kʋɖʋm nɛ pɛɖɛɛ patɩŋa payɩ nɛ piyele halʋ ɛnʋ e-ɖeke koŋ Yesu ɛsɩndaa.

eng: No one spoke about him publicly, though, for fear of the Jewish authorities.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pɔyɔɔdaɣ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, peseɣaɣ Yuda ñɩma ñʋndɩnaa.

eng: Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
local: Filiipu tɛ lɛ Bɛtsayɩda tɛtʋ cikpetu taa. Andree nɛ Pɩyɛ pɛ-tɛ lɛ peeɖe ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: He led them out as far as Bethany, where he lifted his hands and blessed them.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu woni-wɛ Betaanii tɛtʋ cɔlɔ. Peeɖe ɛkpazɩ e-nesi nɛ ɛwazɩɣ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: He answered, The man who made me well said to me, Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Abalʋ ɛnʋ ecosi-wɛ se: «Weyi ɛlaba nɛ mɔ-yɔɔ cɛ yɔ, eheyi-m se mankpaɣ ma-ñalʋʋ nɛ mɔnɖɔ.».

eng: Whoever accepts his testimony confirms that God is true.
local: Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ñetisiɣ ɛ-aseɣɖe ńɖɩ́ yɔ, ɛñɩɣ nesi se Ɛsɔ lɛ toovenim tʋ.

eng: But the time is coming—and is here!—when true worshippers will worship in spirit and truth. The Father looks for those who worship him this way.
local: Ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ kɔŋ, halɩ tɩtɛm talʋʋ. Tɩ-taa Caa Ɛsɔ haɣ ɛyáa e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu. Peeɖe mbá poluŋuu ɛ-tɛɛ nɛ toovenim yɔ, patɩŋɩɣnɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-yɔɔ nɛ poluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ ɛzɩ ɛ-pɩyalʋ wɩlʋʋ-wɛ yɔ. Toovenim lɛ se, mbá poluŋuu Caa Ɛsɔ tɛɛ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ḿbá ɛñɩnɩɣ. Pʋwɛɛ se patɩŋnɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔɔ nɛ poluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ ɛzɩ ɛ-pɩyalʋ wɩlʋʋ-wɛ yɔ.».

eng: Jesus stood up and said to her, Woman, where are they? Is there no one to condemn you.
local: Peeɖe ɛkʋyaa nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Đɔ-kɔɔ, pɛwɛ le tɛ? Pa-taa nɔɔyʋ tasɩŋ se ɛkʋ ñɔ-tɔm naa we?».

eng: Halfway through the festival, Jesus went up to the temple and started to teach.
local: Kazandʋ kɔm tɩsaa miŋ kpem lɛ, Yesu wolo Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ ɛpazɩ ɛyáa wɩlʋʋ Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who was blind from birth.
local: Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɔɖɔŋaɣ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, pana abalʋ nɔɔyʋ, palʋlɩ-ɩ nɛ yʋlʋmtʋ.

eng: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law and the Prophets: Jesus, Josephs son, from Nazareth.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Filiipu na Nataanayɛɛlɩ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Đɩna weyi Mooyiizi ma ɛ-tɔm paɣtʋ takayaɣ taa nɛ náyaa yɔɔdɩ ɛ-tɔm ɖɔɖɔ yɔ. Ɛnʋ lɛ Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yozɛɛfʋ pɩyalʋ weyi payaɣ se Yesu yɔ.».

eng: As he blessed them, he left them and was taken up to heaven.
local: Ɛwazaɣ pɔ-yɔɔ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pʋkʋsɩ-ɩ pɔ-cɔlɔ peeɖe yem nɛ pɩkpazɩ-ɩ ɛsɔdaa.

eng: They inquired, Who is this man who said to you, Pick it up and walk.
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Anɩ ñeheyi-ŋ se ŋkpaɣ ña-ñalʋʋ nɛ ŋɖɔ?».

eng: The one whom God sent speaks Gods words because God gives the Spirit generously.
local: Weyi Ɛsɔ tiyi-i yɔ, ɛyɔɔdʋʋ Ɛsɔ tɔm. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ tɛzɩ-ɩ haʋ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu.

eng: The woman said, I know that the Messiah is coming, the one who is called the Christ. When he comes, he will teach everything to us.
local: Ɛlɛ́, halʋ ɛnʋ eheyi Yesu se: «Manawa se Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa yɔ ɛkɔŋ, ɛnʋ payaɣ se Krɩstʋ. Ɛkɔŋ lɛ, ɛlɩzɩ-ɖʋ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋ-tɔbʋʋ.».

eng: She said, No one, sir. Jesus said, Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on, dont sin anymore.
local: Halʋ ɛnʋ ecosi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Nɔɔyʋ tasɩŋ.» Peeɖe Yesu heyi-i se: «Wolo ŋɖɛɛ, ma ɖɔɖɔ maakʋʋ ñɔ-tɔm. Ɛlɛ́, taatasɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm labʋ.».

eng: Astonished, the Jewish leaders asked, Hes never been taught! How has he mastered the Law.
local: Peeɖe pɩɖɩɣzɩ Yuda ñɩma nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Ɛnɛ, pɛtɛkpɛlɩkɩ-ɩ lɛ, ɛzɩma ɛlaba nɛ ɛtɩlɩ ḿbʋ́?».

eng: Jesus disciples asked, Rabbi, who sinned so that he was born blind, this man or his parents.
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma palʋlɩ abalʋ ɛnɛ nɛ yʋlʋmtʋ ḿbʋ́? Ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ yaa mbá palʋlɩ-ɩ yɔ pa-ñɩmbʋ yɔɔ?».

eng: Nathanael responded, Can anything from Nazareth be good? Philip said, Come and see.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Nataanayɛɛlɩ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Pʋyʋ kɩbanʋ pɩzɩɣ pɩlɩɩnɩ Nazarɛɛtɩ yaa we?» Filiipu cosi-i se: «Kɔɔ nɛ ŋna.».

eng: They worshipped him and returned to Jerusalem overwhelmed with joy.
local: Peeɖe poluŋaa nɛ paɖʋ-ɩ hɩɖɛ nɛ pacaŋ papɩsɩ Yeruusalɛm nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ.

eng: The man who had been cured didnt know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away from the crowd gathered there.
local: Abalʋ ɛnʋ ecosi-wɛ se ɛtasɩm-ɩ. Toovenim lɛ, ɛtapɩzɩɣ se ɛsɩm-ɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛyáa samaɣ kɛwɛ peeɖe nɛ Yesu sʋʋ ka-taa nɛ ɛɖɛɛ.

eng: The Father loves the Son and gives everything into his hands.
local: Caa Ɛsɔ sɔɔlɩ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ nɛ ɛkpaɣ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ e-nesi taa.

eng: Jesus said to her, I Am—the one who speaks with you. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-i se: «Weyi ma mɔyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-ŋ yɔ, ma lɛ pɩtɛ ɛyʋ.».

eng: Jesus spoke to the people again, saying, I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me wont walk in darkness but will have the light of life. .
local: Yesu tasɩ ɛyáa samaɣ heyuu se: «Ma lɛ ɛyáa kpeekpe ñalɩmɩyɛ. Weyi ɛtɩŋɩɣ ma-wayɩ yɔ, ɛɛɖɔŋ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa kaʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ehikiɣ ñalɩmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩhaɣ wezuu yɔ.».

eng: Jesus responded, My teaching isnt mine but comes from the one who sent me.
local: Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Mbʋ́ mawɩlɩɣ ɛyáa yɔ, pɩtalɩɩnɩ mɔn-cɔlɔ, ɛlɛ́, weyi etiyi-m yɔ, ɛ-cɔlɔ pɩlɩnaa.

eng: Jesus answered, Neither he nor his parents. This happened so that Gods mighty works might be displayed in him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ecosi-wɛ se: «Pɩtɛkɛ ɛ-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ yaa mbá palʋlɩ-ɩ yɔ pa-ñɩmbʋ yɔɔ. Ɛyʋlʋmɩ ḿbʋ́ se ɛyáa ɛpɩzɩ pana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ tɩŋɩɣnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛla ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ yɔ.

eng: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, Here is a genuine Israelite in whom there is no deceit. .
local: Yesu na Nataanayɛɛlɩ wokaɣ ɛ-cɔlɔ lɛ, ɛtɔ se: «Ɩna Izrɛɛlɩ pɩɣa maɣmaɣ. Kaamuluu nɔɔyʋ kaʋ.».

eng: And they were continuously in the temple praising God.
local: nɛ powoki Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa paa ɖooye nɛ pasaŋ Ɛsɔ.

eng: Later Jesus found him in the temple and said, See! You have been made well. Dont sin anymore in case something worse happens to you.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu wolo Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ abalʋ weyi ɛ-yɔɔ cɛba ḿbʋ́ yɔ. Peeɖe eheyi-i se: «Welesi ŋnɩɩ, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ñɔ-yɔɔ cɛba. Taatasɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm labʋ nɛ nabʋyʋ tasɩ-ŋ labʋ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever doesnt believe in the Son wont see life, but the angry judgment of God remains on them.
local: Weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ yɔɔ yɔ, ehiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ. Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ɛɛñaŋ-ɩ yɔ, eehikiɣ-kʋ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ pana wɛɣ-ɩ kpanaʋ yɔɔ.».

eng: Just then, Jesus disciples arrived and were shocked that he was talking with a woman. But no one asked, What do you want? or Why are you talking with her.
local: Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɩsaa lɛ, pamaɣnɩ ɛyɔɔdaɣnɩ halʋ ɛnʋ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pɩɖɩɣzɩ-wɛ yem pɩŋŋ. Ɛlɛ́, pa-taa nɔɔyʋ takatɩ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ se: «Ɛbɛ ŋcaɣnɩ-ɩ?» yaa «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-ɩ?».

eng: Then the Pharisees said to him, Because you are testifying about yourself, your testimony isnt valid.
local: Peeɖe Farɩsɩ ñɩma heyi-i se: «Ña-tɩ tɩŋa ŋlɩzɩɣnɩ ña-aseɣɖe! Yee ḿbʋ́ ñɔ-tɔm tɛkɛ toovenim.».

eng: Whoever wants to do Gods will can tell whether my teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own.
local: Weyi ɛcaɣ se ɛla mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ yɔ, ɛkɔŋ naʋ se mbʋ́ mawɩlɩɣ yɔ, Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ pɩlɩnaa yaa ma-maɣmaɣ mɔn-cɔlɔ pɩlɩnaa.

eng: While its daytime, we must do the works of him who sent me. Night is coming when no one can work.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩla weyi etiyi-m yɔ ɛ-tʋma alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ tɛʋ fema yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖoó kɔŋ yuu nɛ nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛla tʋmɩyɛ.

eng: Nathanael asked him, How do you know me? Jesus answered, Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree. .
local: Peeɖe Nataanayɛɛlɩ pɔzɩ Yesu se: «Ɛzɩma ŋlaba nɛ ŋsɩm-m?» Yesu cosi-i se: «Mana-ŋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ŋŋwɛ hiyuú tɛɛ yɔ nɛ pʋcɔ Filiipu ya-ŋ.».

eng: The man went and proclaimed to the Jewish leaders that Jesus was the man who had made him well.
local: Peeɖe abalʋ ɛnʋ ewobi eheyi Yuda ñɩma awiya se Yesu labɩna nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: The woman put down her water jar and went into the city. She said to the people.
local: Peeɖe halʋ ɛnʋ eyebi ɛ-lɩm peɣa nɛ ɛpɩsɩ tɛtʋ taa nɛ eheyiɣ ɛyáa se:.

eng: Jesus replied, Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, since I know where I came from and where Im going. You dont know where I come from or where Im going.
local: Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Paa malɩzɩɣ man-tɩ tɩŋa ma-aseɣɖe yɔ, mɔyɔɔdʋʋ toovenim. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mansɩm ɖenɖe malɩnaa nɛ ɖenɖe mowoki yɔ. Mɩ lɛ, ɩtasɩ ɖenɖe malɩnaa nɛ ɖenɖe mowoki yɔ.

eng: Those who speak on their own seek glory for themselves. Those who seek the glory of him who sent me are people of truth; theres no falsehood in them.
local: Weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-tɔm yɔ, ɛcaɣ se pasam-ɩ. Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ caɣ se pasa weyi etiyi-i yɔ, ɛkɛ toovenim tʋ, ɛzaɣ́tʋ natʋyʋ fɛyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: After this Jesus went across the Galilee Sea .
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tɛzɩ Galilee teŋgu ŋgʋ́ payaɣ-kʋ ɖɔɖɔ se Tiberiyaadɩ teŋgu yɔ.

eng: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world. .
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mɛwɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ yɔ, ma lɛ ɛyáa kpeekpe ñalɩmɩyɛ.».

eng: Nathanael replied, Rabbi, you are Gods Son. You are the king of Israel.
local: Peeɖe Nataanayɛɛlɩ heyi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Ña lɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ. Ña lɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma wiyaʋ.».

eng: As a result, the Jewish leaders were harassing Jesus, since he had done these things on the Sabbath.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ pakʋyɩ ɛ́lɛ́ yɔɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye ɛlabɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: Come and see a man who has told me everything Ive done! Could this man be the Christ.
local: «Iwolo ɩna abalʋ weyi eheyi-m mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ malaba yɔ. Yaa ɛnʋ lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ?».

eng: You judge according to human standards, but I judge no one.
local: Ɩhʋʋnɩ ɛyáa tɔm ɛzɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa lakʋʋ yɔ. Ma lɛ, maahʋʋnɩ nɔɔyʋ.

eng: Didnt Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you keep the Law. Why do you want to kill me? .
local: Pɩcaɣaɣ Mooyiizi ɖʋ-mɩ paɣtʋ na? Ŋgʋ́, mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛɛɖɔŋ tɩ-yɔɔ. Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩcaɣ se ɩkʋ-m?».

eng: A large crowd followed him, because they had seen the miraculous signs he had done among the sick.
local: Ɛtalɩ peeɖe lɛ, ɛyáa samaɣ tɩŋɩɣ ɛ-wayɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paana hama lakasɩ nzɩ́ ɛlakaɣ nɛ kʋdɔndɩnaa yɔɔ cɛkɩ yɔ.

eng: After he said this, he spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and smeared the mud on the mans eyes.
local: Ɛtɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛtɔ ndaatʋ tataa nɛ enuuti tɛtʋ nɛ ɛtaa yʋlʋm ɛnʋ ɛ-ɛsɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these!.
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Ceeke meheyuu-ŋ se mana-ŋ hiyuú tɛɛ yɔ ŋtisaa se ma lɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ yaa we? Meheyiɣ-ŋ se ŋkɔŋ naʋ mbʋ́ pɩkɩlɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ.».

eng: Jesus replied, My Father is still working, and I am working too. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Man-Caa Ɛsɔ lakɩ tʋmɩyɛ tam, ma ɖɔɖɔ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: They left the city and were on their way to see Jesus.
local: Eheyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, palɩɩ tɛtʋ taa se powoki Yesu cɔlɔ.

eng: Even if I do judge, my judgment is truthful, because Im not alone. My judgments come from me and from the Father who sent me.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee mahʋʋnɩ nɔɔyʋ yɔ, mahʋʋnɩ-ɩ nɛ toovenim. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ men-ɖeke mahʋʋnɩnɩ-ɩ, ɛlɛ́, ma nɛ man-Caa, ɛnʋ tiyini-m.

eng: The crowd answered, You have a demon. Who wants to kill you.
local: Peeɖe ɛyáa samaɣ cosi-i se: «Eleeu nɔɔyʋ wɛ ña-wayɩ. Añɩnɩɣ se ɛkʋ-ŋ?».

eng: Jesus went up a mountain and sat there with his disciples.
local: Peeɖe ɛ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pakpa pʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ pacaɣ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam . So the man went away and washed. When he returned, he could see.
local: nɛ eheyi-i se: «Wolo ŋkɔ lɩm Siloowee kujomuu taa.» Siloowee tɔbʋʋ se Tiyiyu. Ewobi ɛkɔ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ lɛ, ɛ-ɛsɛ kuli.

eng: I assure you that you will see heaven open and Gods angels going up to heaven and down to earth on the Human One. .
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se ɩkɔŋ naʋ ɛsɔdaa tʋlaa nɛ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa tɩŋɩɣnɩ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ ma mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pakpaɣ nɛ petiki.».

eng: For this reason the Jewish leaders wanted even more to kill him—not only because he was doing away with the Sabbath but also because he called God his own Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yuda ñɩma awiya tɛzɩɣ ñɩnʋʋ se pakʋ-ɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtasɩɩnɩ se ɛtɔɖɔ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ, ɛlɛ́, ɛtɔm ɖɔɖɔ se Ɛsɔ lɛ ɛ-Caa nɛ pɩwɩlɩɣ se ɛ nɛ ɩ pɛwɛ kɩmaŋ.

eng: In the meantime the disciples spoke to Jesus, saying, Rabbi, eat.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tendaɣ-ɩ se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Kɔɔ ŋtɔɔ tɔɔnaɣ.».

eng: In your Law it is written that the witness of two people is true.
local: Paɖʋ mɩ-paɣtʋ taa se: yee ɛyáa naalɛ ɩlɩzɩ aseɣɖe kʋɖʋmɖɩyɛ nabʋyʋ yɔɔ yɔ, pɩkɛɣ toovenim.

eng: Jesus replied, I did one work, and you were all astonished.
local: Ecosi-wɛ se: «Hama lakasɩ kʋɖʋmsɩ malaba nɛ pɩɖɩɣzɩ mɩtɩŋa yaa we!.

eng: It was nearly time for Passover, the Jewish festival.
local: Yuda ñɩma kazandʋ ndʋ́ payaɣ-tʋ se Paska yɔ, tɩwɩzaa.

eng: The mans neighbors and those who used to see him when he was a beggar said, Isnt this the man who used to sit and beg.
local: Mbá pɛ-tɛ ñɔtɩnɩ-ɩ yɔ, paawɛ peeɖe. Pa nɛ mbá paana-ɩ ɛsʋlʋmaɣ yɔ, pana-ɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔtɔŋ se: «Pɩtɛkɛ abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛɛcaɣna nɛ ɛsʋlʋmʋʋ yaa we?».

eng: Jesus responded to the Jewish leaders, I assure you that the Son cant do anything by himself except what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
local: Peeɖe Yesu kaɖɩ-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se ma Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ lɛ, maapɩzɩɣ mala nabʋyʋ yem man-tɩ yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ man-Caa Ɛsɔ lakɩ nɛ manaɣ yɔ, pi-ɖeke koŋ malakɩ. Mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ man-Caa lakɩ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ malakɩ.

eng: I am one witness concerning myself, and the Father who sent me is the other. .
local: Malɩzɩɣ man-tɩ tɩŋa ma-aseɣɖe, man-Caa weyi etiyi-m yɔ, ɛlɩzɩɣ-ɖɩ ɖɔɖɔ.».

eng: Jesus looked up and saw the large crowd coming toward him. He asked Philip, Where will we buy food to feed these people? .
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛkʋsɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ ɛna ɛyáa samaɣ sɔsɔɔ nakɛyɛ wokaɣ ɛ-cɔlɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛpɔzɩ Filiipu se: «Le ɖɩpɩzɩɣ ɖɩya tɔɔnaɣ nɛ ɛyáa panɛ patɩŋa payɩ pehiɣ pɔtɔɔ?».

eng: Some said, It is, and others said, No, its someone who looks like him. But the man said, Yes, its me.
local: Nabɛyɛ se: «Ɛnʋ.» Lɛlaa se: «Pɩlɩ! Pɩtɛkɛ ɛnʋ, ɛlɛ́, ɛlɩ-ɩ kɩlɩʋ.» Peeɖe abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ecosi-wɛ se: «Ma lɛ yeli.».

eng: Jesus said to them, I have food to eat that you dont know about. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, ecosi-wɛ se: «Mɔntɔkɩ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá yɔ, ɩtasɩm-kɛ.».

eng: Because Moses gave you the commandment about circumcision , you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
local: Mooyiizi ɖʋwa se ɩpɛlɩ abalɩbiya. Toovenim lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ ɛnʋ calɩnɩ ḿbʋ́ labʋ, ɛlɛ́, mɩ-cɔzɔnaa mbá pɛɖɛ-ɩ nɔɔ yɔ, pacalɩna. Halɩ itisaa se ɩpɛlɩɣ nɔɔyʋ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye.

eng: The Father loves the Son and shows him everything that he does. He will show him greater works than these so that you will marvel.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Caa Ɛsɔ sɔɔlɩ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ ma nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ-m mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛlakɩ yɔ. Ɛkɔŋ-m wɩlʋʋ tʋma sɔsɔna wena akɩlɩ wena malaba ɛzʋnɔ yɔ nɛ mala nɛ pɩɖɩɣzɩ-mɩ.

eng: They asked him, Where is your Father? Jesus answered, You dont know me and you dont know my Father. If you knew me, you would also know my Father. .
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Le ña-Caa wɛɛ?» Ecosi-wɛ se: «Yee ɩɩsɩm-m yɔ, ɩɩsɩm ɖɔɖɔ man-Caa. Ɩtasɩm-m, ɩtasɩ ɖɔɖɔ man-Caa.».

eng: Jesus said this to test him, for he already knew what he was going to do.
local: Ɛyɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ faŋŋ se ɛna ndʋ́ Filiipu cosuu yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛtɛm sɩm mbʋ́ ɛlakɩ yɔ.

eng: So they asked him, How are you now able to see.
local: Pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ɛzɩma pɩlaba nɛ ñɛ-ɛsɛ kuli yɔ?».

eng: The disciples asked each other, Has someone brought him food.
local: Peeɖe tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ḿbá pɔpɔzaɣ ɖama se: «Yaa nɔɔyʋ woni-i tɔɔnaɣ?».

eng: If a man can be circumcised on the Sabbath without breaking Moses Law, why are you angry with me because I made an entire man well on the Sabbath?.
local: Yee ɩpɩzɩɣ se ɩpɛlɩ abalɩbɩɣa nakɛyɛ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye nɛ ɩɖɔ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ yɔɔ lɛ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma mɩ-pana kpaɣnɩ-m se malaba nɛ ɛyʋ maɣmaɣ pilim yɔɔ cɛ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ wiye yɔ?.

eng: As the Father raises the dead and gives life, so too does the Son give life to whomever he wishes.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ efeziɣ sɩɖaa nɛ ɛhaɣ-wɛ wezuu yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ ma, mahaɣ mbá me-liu wɛɛ yɔ wezuu.

eng: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the temple area known as the treasury. No one arrested him, because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Yesu yɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛɛwɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa haɖɛ aɖakaɣ cɔlɔ nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ ɛyáa Ɛsɔtɔm. Nɔɔyʋ taakpa-ɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-alɩwaatʋ taatalɩda.

eng: Philip replied, More than a half years salary worth of food wouldnt be enough for each person to have even a little bit.
local: Filiipu cosi-i se: «Paa ɖɩɩyabɩ tɔɔnaɣ kɩyakɩŋ nasɩlɛ tʋmɩyɛ liidiye yɔ, pɩɩtalɩɣ se pehiɣ pazɩ pazɩ.».

eng: He answered, The man they call Jesus made mud, smeared it on my eyes, and said, Go to the pool of Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.
local: Ecosi-wɛ se: «Abalʋ weyi payaɣ se Yesu yɔ, ɛnʋ labɩna ahɔya pazɩ nɛ ɛtaa mɛ-ɛsɛ yɔɔ nɛ eheyi-m se: ‹Wolo Siloowee nɛ ŋkɔ lɩm.› Peeɖe mowobi mɔnkɔ-pʋ nɛ mɛ-ɛsɛ kuli.».

eng: Jesus said to them, I am fed by doing the will of the one who sent me and by completing his work.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Mɔn-tɔɔnaɣ lɛ se mala mbʋ́ weyi etiyi-m yɔ e-liu wɛɛ yɔ nɛ mala tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɛɖʋ-m yɔ nɛ mɛntɛm-ɖɩ.».

eng: Dont judge according to appearances. Judge with right judgment. .
local: Iyele ɛyáa hʋnaʋ ɛsɛ yɔɔ yem. Ɛlɛ́, ɩhʋʋnɩ-wɛ ɛzɩ pʋmʋnaʋ yɔ.».

eng: The Father doesnt judge anyone, but he has given all judgment to the Son.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, Caa ɛɛhʋʋnɩ nɔɔyʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛha ɛ-Pɩyalʋ ma waɖɛ se mahʋʋnɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ tɔm.

eng: Jesus continued, Im going away. You will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where Im going, you cant come. .
local: Yesu tasɩ ɛyáa samaɣ heyuu se: «Mɛnɖɛɣ, ɩkɔŋ-m ñɩnʋʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɩkɔŋ sɩbʋ mɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm taa. Ɩɩpɩzɩɣ iwolo ɖenɖe mowoki yɔ.».

eng: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peters brother, said.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa lɛlʋ weyi payaɣ se Andree nɛ ɛkɛ Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ neu yɔ, eheyi-i se:.

eng: They asked, Where is this man? He replied, I dont know.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛwɛ le?» Ecosi-wɛ se: «Mantatɩlɩ.».

eng: Dont you have a saying, Four more months and then its time for harvest? Look, I tell you: open your eyes and notice that the fields are already ripe for the harvest.
local: Ɛtasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Mɩ mɩtɔŋ se pɩsɛɣ fenasɩ naanza se pakʋ tɔɔnaɣ, ɛlɛ́, ma meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɩcɔnɩ camɩyɛ, tɔɔnaɣ tɛm pɩʋ hayɩm taa se pakʋm-kɛ.

eng: Some people from Jerusalem said, Isnt he the one they want to kill?.
local: Yeruusalɛm ñɩma nabɛyɛ pɔzaɣ se: «Pɩtɛkɛ abalʋ ɛnɛ pacaɣ se pakʋ yaa we?.

eng: so that everyone will honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever doesnt honor the Son doesnt honor the Father who sent him.
local: nɛ piyele patɩŋa paɖʋ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ hɩɖɛ ɛzɩ paɖʋʋ Caa Ɛsɔ yɔ. Weyi ɛɛɖʋʋ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ hɩɖɛ yɔ, ɛɛɖʋʋ-ɖɩ ɖɔɖɔ Caa Ɛsɔ weyi etiyi-i yɔ.

eng: The Jewish leaders said, He isnt going to kill himself, is he? Is that why he said, Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Peeɖe Yuda ñɩma pɔzɩ pa-tɩ se: «Ɛtɔŋʋʋ se ɖɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɖiwolo ɖenɖe ewoki yɔ, ɛkʋʋ ɛ-tɩ yaa we?».

eng: A youth here has five barley loaves and two fish. But what good is that for a crowd like this.
local: «Abalɩbɩɣa nakɛyɛ wɛ cɩnɛ, kɛwɛnɩ tindine hɔɔlasɩ kagbaanzɩ nɛ kpakpasɩ naalɛ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛzɩma pɩpɩzɩɣ pɩtalɩ samaɣ kanɛ?».

eng: Then they led the man who had been born blind to the Pharisees.
local: Peeɖe pakpaɣ abalʋ weyi pokuli ɛ-ɛsɛ yɔ nɛ powoni Farɩsɩ ñɩma cɔlɔ.

eng: Those who harvest are receiving their pay and gathering fruit for eternal life so that those who sow and those who harvest can celebrate together.
local: Halɩ pɛtɛm kʋnɖʋ hɛyʋʋ nɛ ɛkʋŋ tɔɔnaɣ nɛ ekpeɣliɣ-kɛ se kehiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖuyu nɛ kʋnɖʋ pa-naalɛ pa-laŋa hʋlʋmʋʋ.

eng: Here he is, speaking in public, yet they arent saying anything to him. Could it be that our leaders actually think he is the Christ?.
local: Ɩna, ɛyɔɔdʋʋ ɛyáa ɛsɩndaa keɣlee nɛ paalakɩ-ɩ pʋyʋ! Yaa toovenim ɖa-awiya nawa se ɛnʋ lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ?.

eng: I assure you that whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life and wont come under judgment but has passed from death into life.
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se weyi ewelesiɣ mɔn-tɔm nɛ ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ weyi etiyi-m yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, ehiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ. Paahʋʋnɩ-ɩ. Ɛtɛm lɩʋ sɩm taa nɛ ehiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ.

eng: He said to them, You are from below; Im from above. You are from this world; Im not from this world.
local: Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Mɩ-tɛ lɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ. Ɛlɛ́, ma mɛn-tɛ lɛ ɛsɔdaa. Ɩkɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ñɩma, ɛlɛ́, ma mɛntɛkɛ kɔ-yɔɔ ñɩnʋ.

eng: Jesus said, Have the people sit down. There was plenty of grass there. They sat down, about five thousand of them.
local: Peeɖe Yesu tɔm se: «Iheyi ɛyáa ḿbá se pacaɣ.» Ñɩtʋ kɛwɛ lone ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ. Peheyi-wɛ lɛ, pacaɣ ɖɔɖɔ. Mbá paacaɣaɣ yɔ, pa-taa abalaa katalɩ ɛzɩ kutokiŋ kagbaanzɩ yɔ.

eng: Now Jesus made the mud and smeared it on the mans eyes on a Sabbath day.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu kalabɩ ahɔya nɛ ekulini ɛ-ɛsɛ.

eng: This is a true saying, that one sows and another harvests.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, eduuye nɖɩ́ potuwaa se: ‹Lɛlaa ɖúú nɛ lɛlaa kʋŋ› yɔ, ɖɩkɛ toovenim.

eng: We know where he is from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from.
local: Ŋgʋ́, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ ɛkɔŋ yɔ, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛsɩŋ ɖenɖe ɛlɩɣna yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɖa ɖasɩm ɖenɖe abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛ́lɛ́ ñalɩnaa yɔ.».

eng: I assure you that the time is coming—and is here!—when the dead will hear the voice of Gods Son, and those who hear it will live.
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ kɔŋ, halɩ tɩtɛm talʋʋ. Tɩ-taa sɩɖaa kɔŋ nɩʋ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ nɔɔ nɛ mbá panɩɣ yɔ, pehiɣ wezuu.

eng: This is why I told you that you would die in your sins. If you dont believe that I Am, you will die in your sins. .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ meheyi-mɩ se yee itetisi se: ‹Ma lɛ weyi mɛnkɛnaa yɔ› yɔ, ɩsɩkɩ mɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm taa.».

eng: Then Jesus took the bread. When he had given thanks, he distributed it to those who were sitting there. He did the same with the fish, each getting as much as they wanted.
local: Peeɖe ɛkpaɣ tindine nɛ ɛsa Ɛsɔ ɖɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛtayɩ-ɖɩ ɛyáa ḿbá. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛla ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ kpakpasɩ yɔɔ nɛ ɛtayɩ-wɛ nɛ pɔtɔɔ nɛ pe-liu sɩ.

eng: So Pharisees also asked him how he was able to see. The man told them, He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and now I see.
local: Peeɖe paba ñɔpɔzɩ-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩlaba nɛ ɛ-ɛsɛ kuli yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɛta mɛ-ɛsɛ yɔɔ ahɔya pazɩ nɛ mowolo mɔnkɔ lɩm nɛ manpazɩ naʋ.».

eng: I have sent you to harvest what you didnt work hard for; others worked hard, and you will share in their hard work. .
local: Mentiyi-mɩ nɛ iwolo ɩkʋ hayɩm mbʋ́ pɩ-taa ɩtala tʋmɩyɛ kaʋ yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ lɛlaa labɩ tʋmɩyɛ nɛ mɩ mɩtɔɔ po-soŋaɣ yɔɔ.».

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he exclaimed, You know me and where I am from. I havent come on my own. The one who sent me is true, and you dont know him.
local: Pɔyɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yesu kɛwɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ Ɛsɔtɔm nɛ eholuu se: «Ɩsɩm weyi mɛnkɛnaa nɛ ɖenɖe malɩnaa yɔ toovenim naa we? Pɩtɛkɛ ma-maɣmaɣ mɔn-sɔɔlɩm mɔnkɔmnaa. Ɛlɛ́, nɔɔyʋ tiyini-m, ɛnʋ lɛ toovenim tʋ, ɩtasɩm-ɩ.

eng: Just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
local: Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ wezuu lɩɣnɩ Caa Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ eyebi se kɩlɩɩnɩ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ ma mɔn-cɔlɔ.

eng: Who are you? they asked. Jesus replied, Im exactly who I have claimed to be from the beginning.
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ña lɛ anɩ?» Ecosi-wɛ se: «Se we maacalɩ-mɩ heyuu tɛ?.

eng: When they had plenty to eat, he said to his disciples, Gather up the leftover pieces, so that nothing will be wasted. .
local: Pɔtɔwaa pahaɣ lɛ, Yesu heyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Ikpeɣli hɔɔlasɩ nzɩ́ sɩkazaa yɔ, nakɛyɛ ɛtaale.».

eng: Some Pharisees said, This man isnt from God, because he breaks the Sabbath law. Others said, How can a sinner do miraculous signs like these? So they were divided.
local: Pa-taa nabɛyɛ tɔŋaɣ se: «Abalʋ weyi ɛlabɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛɖɔŋ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ.» Lɛlaa se: «Ɛzɩma kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖʋ pɩzɩɣ ɛla hama lakasɩ ḿbʋ́?» Peeɖe pɩtayɩ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa.

eng: Many Samaritans in that city believed in Jesus because of the womans word when she testified, He told me everything Ive ever done.
local: Halʋ ɛnʋ, ewobi Samaarɩɩ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa nɛ ɛkɛdɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ Yesu labɩ-ɩ yɔ lɛ, ɛyáa sakɩyɛ wɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ́lɛ́ yɔɔ.

eng: I know him because I am from him and he sent me. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, ma mansɩm-ɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-cɔlɔ malɩnaa, ɛnʋ tiyini-m.».

eng: He gives the Son authority to judge, because he is the Human One.
local: Ɛha-m waɖɛ se mahʋʋnɩ ɛyáa tɔm, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ma lɛ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ.

eng: I have many things to say in judgment concerning you. The one who sent me is true, and what I have heard from him I tell the world. .
local: Mɛwɛnɩ tɔm sakɩyɛ se mɔyɔɔdʋʋ nɛ mahʋʋnɩ-mɩ. Ɛlɛ́, weyi etiyi-m yɔ, ɛyɔɔdʋʋ toovenim. Ndʋ́ eheyi-m yɔ, ti-ɖeke meheyiɣ ɛyáa.».

eng: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves that had been left over by those who had eaten.
local: Peeɖe pekpeɣli mbʋ́ pɩkazɩ tindine hɔɔlasɩ kagbaanzɩ ńzɩ́ sɩ-taa yɔ nɛ pusu ɖɔkɩŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ.

eng: Some of the Pharisees questioned the man who had been born blind again: What do you have to say about him, since he healed your eyes? He replied, Hes a prophet.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Farɩsɩ ñɩma tasɩ pɔzʋʋ abalʋ ɛnʋ se: «Nɛ ña yɔ, ŋyɔɔdʋʋ weyi ekuli ñɛ-ɛsɛ yɔ ɛ-tɔm se we?» Ecosi-wɛ se: «Ɛkɛ náyʋ.».

eng: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Samaarɩɩ ñɩma woba ɛ-cɔlɔ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, petendi-i se ɛcaɣ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ etisi ɛcaɣ kɩyakɩŋ naalɛ peeɖe.

eng: So they wanted to seize Jesus, but they couldnt because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Yesu yɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pañɩnɩɣ se pakpa-ɩ, ɛlɛ́, nɔɔyʋ tapɩzɩ etukuni-i. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-alɩwaatʋ taatɩtalɩda.

eng: Dont be surprised by this, because the time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice.
local: Pɩtaaɖɩɣzɩ-mɩ. Alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ kɔŋ nɛ pɩlaŋ taa ñɩma tɩŋa payɩ nɩɩ mɔ-nɔɔ.

eng: They didnt know he was speaking about his Father.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pataaɖiɣzini se ɛ-Caa tɔm ɛyɔɔdaɣ.

eng: When the people saw that he had done a miraculous sign, they said, This is truly the prophet who is coming into the world.
local: Ɛyáa ḿbá, pana hama lakasɩ nzɩ́ Yesu laba yɔ lɛ, pɔtɔ se: «Toovenim, ɛnɛ lɛ náyʋ weyi paayɔɔdaa se ɛkɔŋ kedeŋa yɔɔ yɔ.».

eng: The Jewish leaders didnt believe the man had been blind and received his sight until they called for his parents.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, Yuda ñɩma awiya caɣaɣ se panɩɩ ɛ-ñɩma nɔɔ taa nɛ pʋcɔ petisi se ɛɛyʋlʋmaa nɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɛnaɣ. Pʋ-yɔɔ paya-wɛ.

eng: Many more believed because of his word.
local: Ɛ-tɔm maɣmaɣ yɔɔ lɛ, ɛyáa lɛlaa sakɩyɛ tasɩ wɛnaʋ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: Many from that crowd believed in Jesus. They said, When the Christ comes, will he do more miraculous signs than this man does.
local: Ŋgʋ́ ɛyáa samaɣ taa lɛ, sakɩyɛ kɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ se: «Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ, ɛkɔŋ lɛ, ɛlakɩ hama lakasɩ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ nzɩ́ abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛlaba yɔ yaa we?».

eng: Those who did good things will come out into the resurrection of life, and those who did wicked things into the resurrection of judgment.
local: nɛ pefe. Mbá palabɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ pe-wezuu caɣʋ taa yɔ, pehikiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ nɛ piyele mbá paba labɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩm yɔ, pahʋʋnɩ-wɛ nɛ pakʋ pɔ-tɔm.».

eng: So Jesus said to them, When the Human One is lifted up, then you will know that I Am. Then you will know that I do nothing on my own, but I say just what the Father has taught me.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩkpazɩɣ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ yɔ, ɩtɩlɩɣ se: ‹Ma lɛ weyi mɛnkɛnaa yɔ.› Ɩtɩlɩɣ ɖɔɖɔ se maalakɩ pʋyʋ nɛ ma-maɣmaɣ mɔn-sɔɔlɩm: tɔm ndʋ́ man-Caa wɩlɩ-m yɔ, ńdʋ́ mɔyɔɔdʋʋ.

eng: Jesus understood that they were about to come and force him to be their king, so he took refuge again, alone on a mountain.
local: Ŋgʋ́, Yesu kɛtɛm naʋ se pacaɣ pakpa-ɩ nɛ ɖoŋ nɛ pɛcɛlɩ-ɩ kewiyaɣ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛɖaɣnɩ kpaʋ pʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛcaɣ e-ɖeke.

eng: The Jewish leaders asked them, Is this your son? Are you saying he was born blind? How can he now see.
local: nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Mɩ-pɩyalʋ weyi ɩtɔŋ se ɩlʋlɩ-ɩ nɛ yʋlʋmtʋ yɔ, ɛnʋ yɔ yaa we? Ɛzɩma pɩlaba nɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɛnaɣ?».

eng: and they said to the woman, We no longer believe because of what you said, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this one is truly the savior of the world.
local: Peeɖe peheyi halʋ ɛnʋ se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, pɩtɛkɛ tɔm ndʋ́ ŋheyi-ɖʋ yɔ, ti-ɖeke tɩ-yɔɔ ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛyɔɔdaa nɛ ɖa-maɣmaɣ ɖɩnɩɩ nɛ ɖɩna se toovenim ɛkɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ ñʋʋ yaɖʋ, pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ.».

eng: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about Jesus, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Farɩsɩ ñɩma nɩɩ se ɛyáa samaɣ wɛɛtɩɣ ɖama Yesu tɔm. Peeɖe pa nɛ Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa petiyi Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyaa nabɛyɛ se powolo pakpa-ɩ.

eng: I cant do anything by myself. Whatever I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just. I dont seek my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ se: «Maapɩzɩɣ mala nabʋyʋ yem man-tɩ yɔɔ. Mahʋʋnɩ ɛyáa ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ heyuu-m yɔ. Mɔn-tɔm hʋʋ tʋʋzaa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, maacaɣ se mala me-liu ñɩm. Ɛlɛ́, malakɩ mbʋ́ weyi etiyi-m yɔ e-liu wɛɛ yɔ.».

eng: He who sent me is with me. He doesnt leave me by myself, because I always do what makes him happy. .
local: Weyi etiyi-m yɔ, ɛwɛ mɔn-cɔlɔ, eteyebi-m men-ɖeke. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, malakɩ mbʋ́ pɩkɛdɩnɩ-ɩ yɔ alɩwaatʋ tɩŋa.».

eng: When evening came, Jesus disciples went down to the lake.
local: Đanaɣ ɖanaa lɛ, Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tii powolo teŋgu nɔɔ.

eng: His parents answered, We know he is our son. We know he was born blind.
local: Ɛ-ñɩma cosi-wɛ se: «Đɩsɩm-ɩ se ɛkɛ ɖa-pɩyalʋ, ɖɩlʋlɩ-ɩ nɛ yʋlʋmtʋ.

eng: After two days Jesus left for Galilee. .
local: Yesu caɣ kɩyakɩŋ naalɛ Samaarɩɩ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa peeɖe lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ ɛɖɛɛ Galilee egeetiye taa.

eng: Therefore, Jesus said, Im still with you for a little while before I go to the one who sent me.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu tɔ se: «Ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩtasɩɣ caɣʋ pazɩ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, manpɩsɩɣ weyi etiyi-m yɔ ɛ-cɔlɔ.

eng: If I testify about myself, my testimony isnt true.
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ se: «Yee men-ɖeke maalɩzaɣnɩ man-tɩ tɩŋa ma-aseɣɖe yɔ, pɩɩwɩlaa se pɩtɛkɛ toovenim.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, many people came to believe in him.
local: Yesu yɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛyáa sakɩyɛ wɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: They got into a boat and were crossing the lake to Capernaum. It was already getting dark and Jesus hadnt come to them yet.
local: nɛ pakpa kpɩyʋʋ nakʋyʋ taa se pɛtɛzɩɣ Kapɛrnayum. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, ɖoó kɛtɛm yuu, ɛlɛ́, Yesu taawobidi pɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: But we dont know how he now sees, and we dont know who healed his eyes. Ask him. Hes old enough to speak for himself.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩtana se ɛzɩma pɩlaba nɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɛ-ɛsɛ kuli. Đɩtasɩ ɖɔɖɔ weyi ekuli ɛ-ɛsɛ yɔ. Ɩpɔzɩ-ɩ, ɛtɛkɛ pɩɣa, ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛpɩzɩɣ ecosi-mɩ!».

eng: Jesus himself had testified that prophets have no honor in their own country.
local: Ŋgʋ́, Yesu kɔyɔɔdaa se: «Patakpaɣ náyʋ se ɛkɛ pʋyʋ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-tɛ.».

eng: You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come. .
local: Ɩkɔŋ-m ñɩnʋʋ, ɛlɛ́, ɩɩnaɣ-m. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩɩpɩzɩɣ iwolo ɖenɖe mɔnkɔŋ wɛʋ yɔ.».

eng: There is someone else who testifies about me, and I know his testimony about me is true.
local: Ɛlɛ́, nɔɔyʋ lɩzɩɣnɩ ma-aseɣɖe. Manawa se aseɣɖe nɖɩ́ ɛlɩzɩɣ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩkɛ toovenim.

eng: Jesus said to the Jews who believed in him, You are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teaching.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi Yuda ñɩma mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ se: «Yee ɩwɛɛ ɩɖɔŋ mɔn-tɔm yɔɔ yɔ, ɩkɛɣ mɔn-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa siŋŋ.

eng: The water was getting rough because a strong wind was blowing.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, heɣlim sɔsɔm nabʋyʋ makɩ nɛ teŋgu lɩm taŋgatɩɣ.

eng: His parents said this because they feared the Jewish authorities. This is because the Jewish authorities had already decided that whoever confessed Jesus to be the Christ would be expelled from the synagogue.
local: Sɔɔndʋ kpaɣaɣ-wɛ nɛ Yuda ñɩma awiya yɔɔ pɔyɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paanɩnɩ ɖama se: «Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛyɔɔdaa se Yesu lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ yɔ, paɖaŋɩɣ-ɩ se ɛtaatasɩ wobu Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa.».

eng: When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him because they had seen all the things he had done in Jerusalem during the festival, for they also had been at the festival.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛtalɩ Galilee lɛ, peeɖe ñɩma mʋ-ɩ ɖeu. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paawobi ɖɔɖɔ Yeruusalɛm kazandʋ tɔɔʋ nɛ pana mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛɛlaba yɔ.

eng: The Jewish opposition asked each other, Where does he intend to go that we cant find him? Surely he doesnt intend to go where our people have been scattered and are living among the Greeks! He isnt going to teach the Greeks, is he?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yuda ñɩma pɔzʋʋ ɖama se: «Le ewoki nɛ ɖɩɩpɩzɩɣ se ɖɩna-ɩ? Yaa ewoki Yuda ñɩma mbá pamɩzɩ ɖama Grɛɛkɩ ñɩma ɛjaɖɛ taa yɔ pɛ-tɛ se ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ?.

eng: You sent a delegation to John, and he testified to the truth.
local: Itiyi ɛyáa Yohanɛɛsɩ cɔlɔ se powolo pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ mɔn-tɔm nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ lɩzɩ ma-aseɣɖe toovenim.

eng: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free. .
local: Pʋbʋ, ɩtɩlɩɣ toovenim nɛ toovenim ḿbʋ́ piyele nɛ ɩwɛɛ mɩ-tɩ yɔɔ.».

eng: When the wind had driven them out for about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He was approaching the boat and they were afraid.
local: Pɛɖɛwa ɛzɩ kiloomɛtanaa kagbaanzɩ yaa loɖo yɔ lɛ, panaɣ Yesu ɖɔŋ lɩm yɔɔ nɛ ewoki pɔ-cɔlɔ, ɛlɛ́ sɔɔndʋ kpa-wɛ.

eng: Thats why his parents said, Hes old enough. Ask him.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-ñɩma tɔm se: «Ɛtɛkɛ pɩɣa, ɩpɔzɩ-ɩ!».

eng: He returned to Cana in Galilee where he had turned the water into wine. In Capernaum there was a certain royal official whose son was sick.
local: Peeɖe ɛpɩsɩ Kana tɛtʋ taa, Galilee taa ɖenɖe ɛɛlaba nɛ lɩm pɩsɩ sʋlʋm yɔ. Karɩka sɔsɔɔ nakɛyɛ kɛwɛ peeɖe, pɩwɩɣaɣ ka-pɩyalʋ. Ɛɛwɛ Kapɛrnayum tɛtʋ taa.

eng: What does he mean when he says, You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Ɛtɔm se ɖɩkɔŋ-ɩ ñɩnʋʋ, ɛlɛ́, ɖɩɩnaɣ-ɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɖiwolo ɖenɖe ewoki yɔ, pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se we?».

eng: Although I dont accept human testimony, I say these things so that you can be saved.
local: Ma lɛ, pɩtɛkɛnɩ kɩcɛyʋʋ se ɛyáa ɩlɩzɩ ma-aseɣɖe. Ɛlɛ́, mɔyɔɔdɩ Yohanɛɛsɩ tɔm se pɩya mɩ-ñʋʋ.

eng: They responded, We are Abrahams children; weve never been anyones slaves. How can you say that we will be set free.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pocosi-i se: «Đɩkɛ Abraham liɖe taa ñɩma. Đɩtɛkɛ nɔɔyʋ yomaa kaʋ lɛ, ɛzɩma ŋheyiɣ-ɖʋ se ɖɩkɔŋ ɖa-tɩ yɔɔ wɛʋ yɔ?».

eng: He said to them, I Am. Dont be afraid.
local: Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Ma Yesu, sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa mɩ!».

eng: Therefore, they called a second time for the man who had been born blind and said to him, Give glory to God. We know this man is a sinner.
local: Peeɖe Farɩsɩ ñɩma tasɩ abalʋ ɛnʋ yaʋ nɛ peheyi-i se: «Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa ŋwɛɛ, yɔɔdɩ toovenim. Đa lɛ, ɖɩnawa se weyi ekuli ñɛ-ɛsɛ yɔ, ɛkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖʋ.».

eng: When he heard that Jesus was coming from Judea to Galilee, he went out to meet him and asked Jesus if he would come and heal his son, for his son was about to die.
local: Karɩka ŋ́gá, kanɩwa se Yesu lɩnɩ Yudee egeetiye taa nɛ ewolo Galilee ñɩnɖɛ taa lɛ, kowolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ketendi-i se ewolo Kapɛrnayum nɛ ɛla nɛ ka-pɩyalʋ weyi pɩkazɩ-ɩ fezuu ɖalaa yɔ, ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, All who are thirsty should come to me!.
local: Kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ pɛtɛzɩɣ kazandʋ tɔɔʋ yɔ, ŋ́gʋ́ lɛ kɩyakʋ sɔsɔʋ. Ki-wiye Yesu kʋyaa nɛ ɛsɩŋ ɛyáa samaɣ ɛsɩndaa nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ se: «Yee lɔkɔtʋ ɛ́ɖɔ́kɩ nɔɔyʋ yɔ, ɛkɔɔ mɔn-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛñɔɔ lɩm.

eng: John was a burning and shining lamp, and, at least for a while, you were willing to celebrate in his light.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ ñaawɛ ɛzɩ kanɖʋ ŋgʋ́ pɔsɔba nɛ kɩ-ñalɩmɩyɛ ñalɩ mɩ-yɔɔ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ mɩ-laŋa hʋlʋmɩnɩ ñalɩmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ alɩwaatʋ pazɩ.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you that everyone who sins is a slave to sin.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: weyi ɛlakɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ, ɛkɛ pu-yom.

eng: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and just then the boat reached the land where they had been heading.
local: Pacaɣaɣ se pakpaɣ-ɩ kpɩyʋʋ taa, ɛlɛ́, patɩŋ patalɩ ɖenɖe powokaɣ yɔ.

eng: The man answered, I dont know whether hes a sinner. Heres what I do know: I was blind and now I see.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ecosi-wɛ se: «Mantana se ɛkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖʋ naa ɛtɛkɛ. Ɛlɛ́, pʋyʋ kʋɖʋm mansɩma: maayʋlʋmaa, ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ manaɣ.».

eng: Jesus said to him, Unless you see miraculous signs and wonders, you wont believe. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu pɔzɩ-kɛ se: «Yee ɩtana Ɛsɔ hama lakasɩ nɛ kawɩlɩsɩ, ɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ kaʋ yaa we?».

eng: All who believe in me should drink! As the scriptures said concerning me, Rivers of living water will flow out from within him. .
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: ‹Weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, lɩm mbʋ́ pɩhaɣ wezuu yɔ pɩlɩɣnɩ ɛ-taa nɛ pikpeŋ ɛzɩ pɔɔ yɔ.› ».

eng: I have a witness greater than Johns testimony. The Father has given me works to do so that I might complete them. These works I do testify about me that the Father sent me.
local: Ma lɛ, malɩzɩɣ aseɣɖe nɖɩ́ ɖɩkɩlɩ Yohanɛɛsɩ ñɩnɖɛ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tʋma wena man-Caa ɖʋ-m nɛ malakɩ yɔ, a-maɣmaɣ alɩzɩɣnɩ ma-aseɣɖe se Caa Ɛsɔ tiyini-m.

eng: A slave isnt a permanent member of the household, but a son is.
local: Yom ɛɛwɛɣ ɖɩɣa ŋgá ka-taa ɛwɛɛ yɔ tam. Ɛlɛ́, abalɩbɩɣa ŋgá palʋlɩ peeɖe yɔ, kaka ñɛwɛɣ ka-taa tam.

eng: The next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the lake realized that only one boat had been there. They knew Jesus hadnt gone with his disciples, but that the disciples had gone alone.
local: Tɛʋ fema lɛ, mbá pɛwɛ teŋgu wayɩ yɔ, peɖiɣzina se kpɩyʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ kɛwɛna. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ɖeke kakpanɩ kɩ-taa nɛ pɛɖɛɛ, ɛtaawɛɛ pɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: They questioned him: What did he do to you? How did he heal your eyes.
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ɛbɛ ɛlabɩ-ŋ? Ɛzɩma ekuli ñɛ-ɛsɛ?».

eng: The royal official said to him, Lord, come before my son dies.
local: Ɛlɛ́, kocosi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Wolo mɛn-tɛ nɛ pʋcɔ man-pɩɣa sɩ.».

eng: Jesus said this concerning the Spirit. Those who believed in him would soon receive the Spirit, but they hadnt experienced the Spirit yet since Jesus hadnt yet been glorified.
local: Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tɔm Yesu yɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́. Ŋ́gʋ́ Ɛsɔ kɔŋ haʋ mbá pɛwɛɣnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa lɛ, kɩtatɩɩkɔmda, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ teyebida se ɛ-hɩɖɛ ɛsɛɛ.

eng: And the Father who sent me testifies about me. You have never even heard his voice or seen his form.
local: Man-Caa weyi etiyi-m yɔ, ɛ-tɩ tɩŋa ɛlɩzɩ ma-aseɣɖe ɖɔɖɔ. Ŋgʋ́, wiɖiyi ɩtanɩɩ ɛ-nɔɔ, ɩtana ɛ-ɛsɩndaa ɖɔɖɔ kaʋ.

eng: Therefore, if the Son makes you free, you really will be free.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yee Caa Pɩyalʋ eholi-mɩ yɔ, ɩwɛɣ mɩ-tɩ yɔɔ kpem.

eng: Some boats came from Tiberias, near the place where they had eaten the bread over which the Lord had given thanks.
local: Ɛlɛ́, kpɩyɩŋ lɛɛŋ ñaalɩnɩ Tiberiyaadɩ tɛtʋ taa nɛ ɩtalɩ ɖenɖe Kɩpaɣlʋ kasam Ɛsɔ tindine yɔɔ nɛ pɔtɔɔ-ɖɩ yɔ.

eng: He replied, I already told you, and you didnt listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples too.
local: Ecosi se: «Mɛntɛm-mɩ heyuu, ɛlɛ́ ɩtanɩɩnɩ-m. Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩcaɣ se mantasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ nɛ ɩnɩɩ yɔ? Mɩ ɖɔɖɔ, ɩcaɣ se ɩpɩsɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa naa we?».

eng: Jesus replied, Go home. Your son lives. The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and set out for his home.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-kɛ se: «Kpe! Ña-pɩyalʋ yɔɔ cɛba.» Ɛlɛ́, ketisi se Yesu tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩkɛ toovenim nɛ kacaŋ kɛɖɛɛ.

eng: When some in the crowd heard these words, they said, This man is truly the prophet.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ taa nabɛyɛ nɩ tɔm ndʋ́ ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ lɛ, pɔtɔ se: «Toovenim, ɛnɛ lɛ náyʋ weyi pɩɩwɛɛ se ɛkɔɔ yɔ!».

eng: and you dont have his word dwelling with you because you dont believe the one whom he has sent.
local: Ɩɩɖɔkʋʋ ɛ-tɔm camɩyɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩfɛyɩnɩ lidaʋ weyi ma Caa Ɛsɔ tiyaa yɔ mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: I know that you are Abrahams children, yet you want to kill me because you dont welcome my teaching.
local: Manawa se ɩkɛ Abraham liɖe taa ñɩma. Ɛlɛ́, ɩcaɣ se ɩkʋ-m, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɔn-tɔm fɛyɩnɩ ɖɩjaɣyɛ mɩ-taa.

eng: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ nawa se Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɛfɛyɩ peeɖe lɛ, pa-maɣmaɣ pasʋʋ kpɩyɩŋ ɩnʋ nɛ powolo Kapɛrnayum se pañɩnɩ-ɩ.

eng: They insulted him: You are his disciple, but we are Moses disciples.
local: Peeɖe patʋ-ɩ nɛ peheyi-i se: «Ñɛkɛnɩnɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ! Mooyiizi tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ɖa ɖɛkɛnaa.

eng: While he was on his way, his servants were already coming to meet him. They said, Your son lives.
local: Kekpeŋaɣ lɛ, ka-tʋmlaɖaa katɩ-kɛ nɛ peheyi-kɛ se: «Ña-pɩɣa yɔɔ cɛba!».

eng: Others said, Hes the Christ. But others said, The Christ cant come from Galilee, can he?.
local: Lɛlaa ñɔtɔŋ se: «Ɛnʋ lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ!» Ɛlɛ́, lɛlaa ñapɩsɩna se: «Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ, ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛlɩɩnɩ Galilee egeetiye taa yaa we?.

eng: Examine the scriptures, since you think that in them you have eternal life. They also testify about me.
local: Ɩtazɩɣ tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ tɩ-taa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩmaɣzɩɣ se tɩhaɣ-mɩ wezuu maatɛŋ. Meheyiɣ-mɩ se ńdʋ́ lɩzɩɣnɩ ma-aseɣɖe!.

eng: Im telling you what Ive seen when I am with the Father, but you are doing what youve heard from your father. .
local: Mbʋ́ mana man-Caa cɔlɔ yɔ, pʋ-tɔm mɛnkɛdɩɣ-mɩ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ mɩ mɩlakɩ mbʋ́ mɩ-caa heyi-mɩ yɔ.».

eng: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, Rabbi, when did you get here.
local: Pɛtɛzɩ teŋgu wayɩ nɛ pana Yesu lɛ, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Đooye ŋtalɩ cɩnɛ?».

eng: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we dont know where this man is from.
local: Đɩnawa se Ɛsɔ yɔɔdɩnɩ Mooyiizi. Piyele ɛnʋ yɔ, ɖɩtatɩɩsɩm ɖenɖe ɛlɩnaa yɔ!».

eng: So he asked them at what time his son had started to get better. And they said, The fever left him yesterday at about one oclock in the afternoon.
local: Kɔpɔzɩ-wɛ se alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ʋ́ pɩpazɩ-kɛ labʋ wee wee? Pocosi se: «Đeɖe wɩsɩ taa ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ yɔɔ ka-kɩnamɩyɛ cɛba.».

eng: Didnt the scripture say that the Christ comes from Davids family and from Bethlehem, Davids village.
local: Pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ, ɛkɛɣ Daaviidi liɖe taa tʋ. Palʋlʋʋ-ɩ Bɛtlɛhɛm tɛtʋ taa ɖenɖe Daaviidi kɛwɛɛ yɔ.› ».

eng: yet you dont want to come to me so that you can have life.
local: Ŋgʋ́, ɩɩcaɣ se ɩkɔɔ mɔn-cɔlɔ nɛ ihiɣ wezuu ŋ́gʋ́!.

eng: They replied, Our father is Abraham. Jesus responded, If you were Abrahams children, you would do Abrahams works.
local: Peeɖe pocosi-i se: «Đa-caa lɛ Abraham.» Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ ɩla mbʋ́ Abraham laba yɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that you are looking for me not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate all the food you wanted.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se pɩtɛkɛ hama lakasɩ nzɩ́ malaba nɛ ɩna yɔ, sɩ-yɔɔ ɩñɩnɩɣ-m. Ɛlɛ́, ɩtɔɔ tindine nɛ ɩhaɣ yɔɔ ɩñɩnɩɣ-m.

eng: The man answered, This is incredible! You dont know where he is from, yet he healed my eyes!.
local: Abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛpɩsɩnɩ-wɛ se: «Mbʋ́ pɩɖɩɣzɩɣ-m yɔ lɛ se: ɩɩsɩŋ ɖenɖe ɛlɩnaa yɔ, ɛlɛ́, ekuli mɛ-ɛsɛ!.

eng: Then the father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, Your son lives. And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
local: Peeɖe kɔtɔzaa se alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ ɖeyi ɖeyi Yesu keheyi-kɛ se: «Ña-pɩyalʋ yɔɔ cɛba.» Pʋ-yɔɔ ka nɛ kɔ-hɔʋ taa ñɩma tɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ.

eng: So the crowd was divided over Jesus.
local: Ḿbʋ́ tayʋʋ sʋ ɛyáa hɛkʋ taa Yesu yɔɔ.

eng: I dont accept praise from people.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ ɛyáa samtʋ mañɩnɩɣ.

eng: Instead, you want to kill me, though I am the one who has spoken the truth I heard from God. Abraham didnt do this.
local: Meheyi-mɩ toovenim tɔm ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ heyi-m yɔ, ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɩcaɣ se ɩkʋ-m. Ŋgʋ́, Abraham ñatala nabʋyʋ ḿbʋ́.

eng: Dont work for the food that doesnt last but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Human One will give you. God the Father has confirmed him as his agent to give life. .
local: Ɩtaala tʋmɩyɛ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá kɛwɛɛkɩɣ yɔ kɔ-yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɩla ŋgá kacakɩ tam nɛ kahaɣ wezuu maatɛŋ yɔ kɔ-yɔɔ. Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ kɔŋnɩ-mɩ-kɛ haʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Caa Ɛsɔ ñɩɣ ɛ-yɔɔ nesi.».

eng: We know that God doesnt listen to sinners. God listens to anyone who is devout and does Gods will.
local: Đɩnawa se Ɛsɔ ɛɛnɩɣnɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa, ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛñaŋ-ɩ nɛ ɛlakɩ mbʋ́ e-liu wɛɛ yɔ, ɛnʋ ɛnɩɣna.

eng: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did while going from Judea to Galilee.
local: Hama lakasɩ naalɛ ñɩnzɩ Yesu labɩ Galilee taa ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɩnaʋ Yudee nɛ ɛpɩsɩ yɔ ɛlabɩ-sɩ.

eng: Some wanted to arrest him, but no one grabbed him.
local: Pa-taa lɛlaa caɣaɣ se pakpa-ɩ, ɛlɛ́, nɔɔyʋ tapɩzɩ ɛkpa-ɩ.

eng: but I know you, that you dont have Gods love in you.
local: Mansɩm-mɩ: Ɛsɔ sɔɔlɩm fɛyɩ mɩ-taa!.

eng: You are doing your fathers works. They said, Our ancestry isnt in question! The only Father we have is God.
local: Mbʋ́ mɩ-caa lakɩ yɔ ɩlakɩ.» Peeɖe pocosi-i se: «Đɩtɛkɛ habɩŋ taa piya, Caa kʋɖʋm koŋ ɖɩwɛna, ɛnʋ lɛ Ɛsɔ.».

eng: They asked, What must we do in order to accomplish what God requires.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ɛzɩma pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩtɩŋ nɛ ɖɩpɩzɩ ɖɩla tʋma wena Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ yɔ?».

eng: No one has ever heard of a healing of the eyes of someone born blind.
local: Đooo yɔ, ɖɩtanɩdɩ se nɔɔyʋ kuli weyi palʋlɩ-ɩ nɛ yʋlʋmtʋ yɔ ɛ-ɛsɛ kaʋ.

eng: The guards returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked, Why didnt you bring him.
local: Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ cɔnɩyaa pɩsɩ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma pɔ-cɔlɔ lɛ, pɔpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ɩtakpa Yesu se ɩkɔna?».

eng: If this man wasnt from God, he couldnt do this.
local: Yee abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛtaalɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ, ɛtaapɩzɩ ɛla nabʋyʋ.».

eng: Jesus replied, This is what God requires, that you believe in him whom God sent. .
local: Ecosi-wɛ se: «Tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ se ɩla yɔ lɛ se ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ weyi ma etiyaa yɔ mɔ-yɔɔ.».

eng: Jesus replied, If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God. Here I am. I havent come on my own. God sent me.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Yee toovenim Ɛsɔ ɛɛkɛ mɩ-Caa yɔ, ɩɩsɔɔlɩ-m. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-cɔlɔ malɩnaa nɛ mɔnkɔɔ mɛwɛɛ cɩnɛ. Pɩtɛkɛ mon-ɖoŋ mɔnkɔmnaa, ɛlɛ́, ɛnʋ tiyini-m.

eng: I have come in my Fathers name, and you dont receive me. If others come in their own name, you receive them.
local: Mɔnkɔm man-Caa lone taa, ɛlɛ́, ɩɩcaɣ se ɩmʋ-m. Ŋgʋ́, yee nɔɔyʋ ɛkɔmnɩ ɛ-maɣmaɣ e-ɖoŋ yɔ, ɩmʋʋ-ɩ nesi naalɛ.

eng: The guards answered, No one has ever spoken the way he does.
local: Peeɖe pocosi-wɛ se: «Đooo yɔ, nɔɔyʋ tɔyɔɔdɩ kaʋ ɛzɩ abalʋ ɛnʋ yɔ.».

eng: They responded, You were born completely in sin! How is it that you dare to teach us? Then they expelled him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, papɩsɩnɩ-ɩ se: «Đooo ɛzɩ palʋlʋʋ-ŋ yɔ, kɩwɛɛkɩm taa ŋwɛ ñatɩŋa pilim. Ñacaɣnɩ se ŋkpɛlɩkɩ-ɖʋ lɛɣtʋ naa we?» Peeɖe pɔɖɔɔnɩ-ɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa.

eng: They asked, What miraculous sign will you do, that we can see and believe you? What will you do?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Hama lakasɩ nzɩ́ ŋpɩzɩɣ ŋla nɛ ɖɩna nɛ ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ ñɔ-yɔɔ? Ɛbɛ ŋpɩzɩɣ ŋla?.

eng: Why dont you understand what Im saying? Its because you cant really hear my words.
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩɩnɩɣ tɔm ndʋ́ meheyiɣ-mɩ yɔ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ? Pʋwɛ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩɩpɩzɩɣ iwelesi mɔn-tɔm.

eng: How can you believe when you receive praise from each other but dont seek the praise that comes from the only God?.
local: Mbá mɩ ɩyɩɣyɩɣ ɖama nɛ ɩɩcaɣ se Ɛsɔ ɖeke koŋ ɛyɩɣyɩnɩ-mɩ yɔ, ɛzɩma ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ?.

eng: The Pharisees replied, Have you too been deceived?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Farɩsɩ ñɩma pɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Yaa iyebi mɩ-tɩ nɛ ɛcɛtɩ-mɩ ɖɔɖɔ?.

eng: Jesus heard they had expelled the man born blind. Finding him, Jesus said, Do you believe in the Human One? .
local: Pɔɖɔɔnɩ abalʋ ɛnʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yesu nɩɩ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛkatɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ yɔɔ yaa we?».

eng: Our ancestors ate manna in the wilderness, just as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat .
local: Ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa tɔɔ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá kalɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ payaɣ-kɛ se maanɩ yɔ lakʋ taa. Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: ‹Ɛha-wɛ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá kalɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ se pɔtɔɔ.› ».

eng: Your father is the devil. You are his children, and you want to do what your father wants. He was a murderer from the beginning. He has never stood for the truth, because theres no truth in him. Whenever that liar speaks, he speaks according to his own nature, because hes a liar and the father of liars.
local: Mɩ-caa lɛ eleeu. Mbʋ́ e-liu wɛɛ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɩsɔɔlɩ labʋ. Ɛkɛ ɛyáa kʋyʋ ɖooo kiɖe tɛɛ. Ɛɛɖɔŋ toovenim nʋmɔʋ taa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, toovenim ḿbʋ́, pɩfɛyɩ ɛ-taa. Ɛkʋyʋʋ cɛtʋʋ lɛ, ɛyɔɔdʋʋ ɛzɩ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛwɛʋ yɔ: ɛkɛ cɛtɩyʋ, ɛ-cɔlɔ cɛtɩm lɩɣna.

eng: Dont think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, the one in whom your hope rests.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɩtaamaɣzɩ se mowoki man-Caa Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ nɛ mankʋyɩ mɩ-yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Mooyiizi weyi ɩtaɣ-ɩ liu yɔ, ɛnʋ wokina nɛ ɛkʋyɩ mɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: Have any of the leaders believed in him? Has any Pharisee?.
local: Đa-awiya taa nɔɔyʋ yaa ɖa Farɩsɩ ñɩma, ɖa-taa nɔɔyʋ tisi ɛ-tɔm naa we? Đɩtasa.

eng: He answered, Who is he, sir? I want to believe in him.
local: Ecosi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ɛnʋ lɛ anɩ? Heyi-m nɛ mɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ.».

eng: Jesus told them, I assure you, it wasnt Moses who gave the bread from heaven to you, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se pɩtɛkɛ Mooyiizi hanɩ-mɩ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá kalɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, man-Caa haɣnɩ-mɩ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá kalɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ ka-maɣmaɣ siŋŋ.

eng: Because I speak the truth, you dont believe me.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ma mɔyɔɔdʋʋ toovenim yɔɔ iitisiɣ mɔn-tɔm.

eng: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, because Moses wrote about me.
local: Mɔyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɔn-tɔm ɛmawa. Toovenim lɛ, yee iitisi ɛ-tɔm yɔ, iitisi ma-ñɩndʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: No, only this crowd, which doesnt know the Law. And they are under Gods curse.
local: Samaɣ ŋgá ketisiɣ ɛ-tɔm yɔ, katasɩ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ, Ɛsɔ wɩlɩ-kɛ kpalʋʋ!».

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt enter into the sheep pen through the gate but climbs over the wall is a thief and an outlaw.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yesu yɔɔdɩ se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se: yee nɔɔyʋ ɛtatɩŋnɩ heŋ kaɖaɣ nɔnɔɔ nɛ ɛsʋʋ nɛ ɛmanɩ koloŋa yɔ, ɛkɛ mɩ́lʋ́ nɛ ɛtɛ ɛkɛ ɛzaɣ́ʋ.

eng: Jesus said, You have seen him. In fact, he is the one speaking with you. .
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Ɛnʋ ɖaɣnɩnɩ-ŋ yɔɔdɩnaʋ nɛ ŋnaɣ-ɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ.».

eng: The bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá Ɛsɔ haɣ yɔ lɛ ɛyʋ weyi ɛlɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ ɛhaɣ ɛyáa wezuu yɔ.».

eng: Who among you can show Im guilty of sin? Since I speak the truth, why dont you believe me?.
local: Mɩ-taa anɩ pɩzɩɣ ɛwɩlɩ-m se malabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm? Yee mɔyɔɔdʋʋ toovenim lɛ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma iitisiɣ mɔn-tɔm yɔ?.

eng: If you dont believe the writings of Moses, how will you believe my words? .
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee itetisi tɔm ndʋ́ Mooyiizi mawa yɔ lɛ, ɛzɩma ɩpɩzɩɣ itisi ma-ñɩndʋ yɔ?».

eng: Nicodemus, who was one of them and had come to Jesus earlier, said.
local: Peeɖe Niikodɛm weyi ɛkɛ Farɩsɩ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma taa lɛlʋ nɛ eewobi Yesu cɔlɔ ɖoó taa yɔ, eheyi-wɛ se:.

eng: The one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
local: Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛtɩŋɩɣnɩ nɔnɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ lɛ heŋ ɖiyiyu.

eng: The man said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped Jesus.
local: Peeɖe ɛtɔm se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Mɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ñɔ-yɔɔ.» Ɛlɛ́, eluŋ Yesu tɛɛ nɛ ɛɖʋ-ɩ hɩɖɛ.

eng: They said, Sir, give us this bread all the time.
local: Ɛlɛ́, peheyi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Wɛɛ ŋhaɣ-ɖʋ tɔɔnaɣ ŋ́gá.».

eng: Gods children listen to Gods words. You dont listen to me because you arent Gods children. .
local: Weyi ɛkɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩnʋ yɔ, ewelesiɣ ɛ-tɔm. Ɛlɛ́, mɩ mɩtɛkɛ ɛ-ñɩma, pʋ-yɔɔ iiwelesiɣ ɛ-tɔm.».

eng: Jesus said, I have come into the world to exercise judgment so that those who dont see can see and those who see will become blind. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu tɔm se: «Mɔnkɔm kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ se mahʋʋnɩ ɛyáa nɛ mbá paanaɣ yɔ, pana nɛ mbá paba naɣ yɔ, payʋlʋmɩ.».

eng: Our Law doesnt judge someone without first hearing him and learning what he is doing, does it.
local: «Đa-paɣtʋ taa lɛ, pɩtaha nʋmɔʋ se pakʋ nɔɔyʋ tɔm nɛ patanɩɩ ɛ-nɔɔ taa tɔm yaa patana mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ.».

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and have a demon, werent we.
local: Yesu yɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yuda ñɩma pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Đɩfɛyɩnɩ toovenim se ɖɩyɔɔdɩ se ŋkɛ Samaarɩɩ tʋ nɛ ŋtɛ ŋke aleewaa naa we?».

eng: Jesus replied, I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
local: Peeɖe Yesu kaɖɩ-wɛ se: «Ma lɛ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá kahaɣ wezuu yɔ. Weyi ɛkɔŋ mɔn-cɔlɔ yɔ, ñɔɔsɩ ɛɛwɩɣ-ɩ wiɖiyi kaʋ, weyi ɛ́lɛ́ wɛɣnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, lɔkɔtʋ ɛɛɖɔkʋʋ-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ kaʋ.

eng: The guard at the gate opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
local: Kaɖaɣ yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyʋ tʋlʋʋ-ɩ nɔnɔɔ nɛ ɛ́yáa paa e-heu weyi ɛ-hɩɖɛ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ nɩɩ ɛ-nɔɔ nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ kaɖaɣ taa.

eng: Some Pharisees who were with him heard what he said and asked, Surely we arent blind, are we.
local: Farɩsɩ ñɩma nabɛyɛ kɛwɛ peeɖe. Panɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Đa ɖɔɖɔ ɖɩkɛ yʋlʋmaa naa we?».

eng: They answered him, You are not from Galilee too, are you? Look it up and you will see that the prophet doesnt come from Galilee.
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ña ɖɔɖɔ ŋkɛ Galilee tʋ naa we? Yee ŋkalɩ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ camɩyɛ yɔ, ŋnaɣ se náyʋ nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ se ɛkɛ Galilee egeetiye taa tʋ.» .

eng: I dont have a demon, Jesus replied. But I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ecosi-wɛ se: «Menteke aleewaa, ɛlɛ́, man-Caa manɖʋʋ hɩɖɛ nɛ mɩ mɩwɛɛkɩɣ ma-ñɩnɖɛ!.

eng: But I told you that you have seen me and still dont believe.
local: Ŋgʋ́, meheyi-mɩ nɛ ɩna-m, ɛlɛ́, ɩfɛyɩnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: Whenever he has gathered all of his sheep, he goes before them and they follow him, because they know his voice.
local: Ɩlɩɣ ɩtɩŋa payɩ lɛ, ɛɖɛɛ-ɩ nɔɔ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ tɩŋɩɣ ɛ-wayɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩsɩm ɛ-nɔɔ.

eng: Jesus said to them, If you were blind, you wouldnt have any sin, but now that you say, We see, your sin remains.
local: Peeɖe ecosi-wɛ se: «Yee ɩɩkɛ yʋlʋmaa yɔ, mɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm taawɛɛ mɩ-yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɖenɖe ɩtɔŋ se ɩnaɣ yɔ, mɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm wɛɣ mɩ-yɔɔ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: They each went to their own homes.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, paa weyi ekpe ɛ-tɛ.

eng: Im not trying to bring glory to myself. Theres one who is seeking to glorify me, and hes the judge.
local: Ma lɛ, maañɩnɩɣ mon-koboyaɣ. Ɛlɛ́, nɔɔyʋ wɛɛ, ɛnʋ ñɩnɩɣnɩ-kɛ, ɛnʋ hʋʋnɩ tɔm.

eng: Everyone whom the Father gives to me will come to me, and I wont send away anyone who comes to me.
local: Ɛyáa mbá patɩŋa man-Caa cɛlɩɣ-m yɔ, pɔkɔŋ mɔn-cɔlɔ, maakiziɣ pa-taa nɔɔyʋ kaʋ.

eng: They wont follow a stranger but will run away because they dont know the strangers voice. .
local: Ɩɩtɩŋɩɣ weyi ɩtasɩm-ɩ yɔ kaʋ, ɛlɛ́, iseɣ-i kiseu, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtasɩ ɛ-nɔɔ.».

eng: I assure you that whoever keeps my word will never die. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se: weyi ɛɖɔŋ mɔn-tɔm yɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛsɩkɩ kaʋ.».

eng: Those who heard Jesus use this analogy didnt understand what he was saying.
local: Yesu tuu-wɛ eduuye ńɖɩ́, ɛlɛ́, patanɩɩ ndʋ́ ɛcaɣaɣ-wɛ heyuu yɔ.

eng: I have come down from heaven not to do my will, but the will of him who sent me.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, malɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa mentii se mala mbʋ́ weyi etiyi-m yɔ e-liu wɛɛ yɔ. Mɔntɔkɔɔ se mala mbʋ́ me-liu wɛɛ yɔ.

eng: The Jewish opposition said to Jesus, Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham and the prophets died, yet you say, Whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Peeɖe Yuda ñɩma heyi-i se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɖɩnawa se ŋkem aleewaa yɔ! Abraham sɩba, náyaa ñasɩ nɛ ña ñɔtɔŋ se weyi ɛɖɔŋ ñɔ-tɔm yɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛsɩkɩ kaʋ!.

eng: This is the will of the one who sent me, that I wont lose anything he has given me, but I will raise it up at the last day.
local: Ɩɩnaɣ mbʋ́ weyi etiyi-m yɔ e-liu wɛɛ yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ lɛ se: mantaalesi mbá ɛcɛlɩ-m yɔ pa-taa nɔɔyʋ. Ɛlɛ́, menfezi patɩŋa payɩ kɛtɛzaɣ kɩyakʋ wiye nɛ sɩm taa nɛ pɛwɛɛ wezuu.

eng: So Jesus spoke again, I assure you that I am the gate of the sheep.
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se ma lɛ heŋ kaɖaɣ nɔnɔɔ.

eng: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died and the prophets died, so who do you make yourself out to be.
local: Ŋmaɣzɩɣ se ŋkɩlɩ ɖɔ-cɔzɔ Abraham weyi ɛsɩba yɔ yaa we? Náyaa ñasɩba ɖɔɖɔ, ŋmaɣzɩɣ se ña lɛ anɩ kpem?».

eng: This is my Fathers will: that all who see the Son and believe in him will have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day. .
local: Ɩɩnaɣ mbʋ́ man-Caa liu wɛɛ yɔ: weyi ɛnaɣ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ ma nɛ ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ehikiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ. Menfeziɣ-i kɛtɛzaɣ kɩyakʋ wiye nɛ sɩm taa.».

eng: All who came before me were thieves and outlaws, but the sheep didnt listen to them.
local: Mbá patɩŋa payɩ palayɩ-m kɔm yɔ, pɛkɛ mɩ́láa nɛ azaɣ́a. Heŋ tanɩɩnɩ-wɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is meaningless. My Father, who you say is your God, is the one who glorifies me.
local: Peeɖe Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Yee ma-maɣmaɣ meyekina se ma-hɩɖɛ ɛsɛɛ yɔ, pɩfɛyɩnɩ waɣzaɣ nakɛyɛ. Ɛlɛ́, man-Caa yekina se ɖɩsɛɛ. Ɛnʋ ɩtɔŋ se ɛnʋ lɛ mɩ-Ɛsɔ.

eng: The Jewish opposition grumbled about him because he said, I am the bread that came down from heaven. .
local: Yuda ñɩma midaɣnɩ Yesu pe-liŋ tɛɛ se ɛyɔɔdaa se: «Ma lɛ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá kalɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ ketii yɔ.».

eng: I am the gate. Whoever enters through me will be saved. They will come in and go out and find pasture.
local: Ma lɛ nɔnɔɔ. Weyi ɛtɩŋɩɣnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛsʋʋ yɔ, pɩyakɩ ɛ-ñʋʋ. Ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛsʋʋ nɛ ɛlɩɩ nɛ ehiɣ tɔɔnaɣ.

eng: You dont know him, but I do. If I said I didnt know him, I would be like you, a liar. But I do know him, and I keep his word.
local: Ŋgʋ́, ɩtasɩm-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, ma mansɩm-ɩ. Yee maatɔm se mantasɩm-ɩ yɔ, maakɛ cɛtɩyʋ ɛzɩ mɩ yɔ. Ma lɛ, mansɩm-ɩ nɛ mɔnɖɔŋ ɛ-tɔm yɔɔ.

eng: They asked, Isnt this Jesus, Josephs son, whose mother and father we know? How can he now say, I have come down from heaven.
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzaɣ pa-tɩ se: «Pɩtɛkɛ ɛnʋ lɛ Yozɛɛfʋ pɩyalʋ Yesu? Đɩsɩm ɛ-caa nɛ e-ɖoo, ɛzɩma ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛyɔɔdɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ se ɛlɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ etii» yɔ?.

eng: The thief enters only to steal, kill, and destroy. I came so that they could have life—indeed, so that they could live life to the fullest.
local: Mɩlʋʋ nɛ kʋʋ nɛ wɛɛkʋʋ pe-ɖeke pɔ-yɔɔ mɩ́lʋ́ kɔŋ. Ma lɛ, mɔnkɔm se ɛyáa ihiɣ wezuu nɛ wezuu ŋ́gʋ́ kʋtɔyɩnɩ-wɛ.

eng: Your father Abraham was overjoyed that he would see my day. He saw it and was happy. .
local: Mɩ-cɔzɔ Abraham taa labɩ leleŋ siŋŋ se ɛnaɣ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ mɔnkɔŋ yɔ. Ɛna-kʋ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ ɛ-taa hʋlʋmɩ.».

eng: Jesus responded, Dont grumble among yourselves.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Iyele miduu ḿbʋ́.

eng: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
local: Ma lɛ ninɖiɣyu kɩbanʋ. Ninɖiɣyu kɩbanʋ tisiɣ nɛ ɛsɩ e-heŋ yɔɔ.

eng: You arent even years old! the Jewish opposition replied. How can you say that you have seen Abraham.
local: Peeɖe Yuda ñɩma pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋtatalɩdɩ pɩnzɩ nɩɩnʋwa nɛ ŋtɔŋ se ŋna Abraham yaa we?».

eng: No one can come to me unless they are drawn to me by the Father who sent me, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Man-Caa weyi etiyi-m yɔ, yee ɛtɔkɔnɩ nɔɔyʋ mɔn-cɔlɔ yɔ, pʋdʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛkɔɔ. Weyi ɛkɔŋ mɔn-cɔlɔ yɔ, menfeziɣ-i kɛtɛzaɣ kɩyakʋ wiye nɛ sɩm taa.

eng: When the hired hand sees the wolf coming, he leaves the sheep and runs away. Thats because he isnt the shepherd; the sheep arent really his. So the wolf attacks the sheep and scatters them.
local: Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ tɛkɛ ninɖiɣyu nɛ ɛtatɩɣyɩ heŋ nɛ ɛlakɩ tʋmɩyɛ liidiye ɖeke yɔɔ yɔ, yee ɛna kpemtɔɔyaɣ woki heŋ wayɩ yɔ, eseɣ eyebi-i nɛ kowolo kakpa lɛɛŋ nɛ kamɩzɩ lɛɛŋ nɛ ñɩtʋ.

eng: I assure you, Jesus replied, before Abraham was, I Am. .
local: Ecosi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se maawɛɛ nɛ pʋcɔ palʋlɩ Abraham.».

eng: It is written in the Prophets, And they will all be taught by God. Everyone who has listened to the Father and learned from him comes to me.
local: Náyaa ma pa-takayɩsɩ taa se: ‹Ɛsɔ kɔŋ patɩŋa payɩ kpɛlɩkʋʋ.› Yee ḿbʋ́, paa weyi, ɛnɩ Caa tɔm nɛ ɛkpɛlɩkɩ mbʋ́ ɛwɩlɩ-ɩ yɔ yɔ, ɛkɔŋ mɔn-cɔlɔ.

eng: Hes only a hired hand and the sheep dont matter to him.
local: Eseɣ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, liidiye yɔɔ ɖeke ɛlakɩ tʋmɩyɛ, ɛ-paa fɛyɩnɩ heŋ.

eng: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and left the temple.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ pakpaɣ pɛɛ se payakɩ-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛmɛlɩ nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ ɛɖɛɛ.

eng: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God. He has seen the Father.
local: Pʋnɛ pɩwɩlɩɣ se nɔɔyʋ tana Caa. Weyi ma malɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ, men-ɖeke koŋ mananɩ-ɩ.

eng: I am the good shepherd. I know my own sheep and they know me.
local: Ma lɛ Ninɖiɣyu kɩbanʋ. Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Caa sɩm-m nɛ mansɩm-ɩ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ mansɩm me-heŋ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ sɩm-m. Ɩ-yɔɔ mansɩkɩ.

eng: I assure you, whoever believes has eternal life.
local: Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ehiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ.

eng: I have other sheep that dont belong to this sheep pen. I must lead them too. They will listen to my voice and there will be one flock, with one shepherd.
local: Mɛwɛnɩ ɖɔɖɔ heŋ lɛɛŋ, ɩfɛyɩ kaɖaɣ kanɛ ka-taa. Pʋwɛɛ se menɖiyi-i ɖɔɖɔ. Ɩkɔŋ nɩʋ mɔ-nɔɔ nɛ ɩkpɛndɩ ɩnɛ ɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɩpɩsɩ tikaɣ kʋɖʋmaɣ nɛ ninɖiɣyu kʋɖʋm ɖiyi-i.

eng: I am the bread of life.
local: Ma lɛ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá kahaɣ wezuu yɔ.

eng: This is why the Father loves me: I give up my life so that I can take it up again.
local: Caa sɔɔlɩ-m, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mentisiɣ se mansɩ nɛ piyele manɖaɣnɩ hiɣu wezuu.

eng: Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness and they died.
local: Mɩ-cɔzɔnaa tɔɔ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá kalɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ lakʋ taa, ɛlɛ́, pasɩba.

eng: No one takes it from me, but I give it up because I want to. I have the right to give it up, and I have the right to take it up again. I received this commandment from my Father. .
local: Nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛlɩzɩ me-wezuu. Ɛlɛ́, ma-maɣmaɣ mentisiɣna nɛ mansɩ. Manpɩzɩɣ mansɩ nɛ manpɩzɩ manɖaɣnɩ wezuu hiɣu. Ḿbʋ́ man-Caa ɖʋ-m se mala.».

eng: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that whoever eats from it will never die.
local: Tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá kaka lɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, ŋ́gá yɔ. Weyi ɛtɔɔ-kɛ yɔ, ɛɛsɩkɩ.

eng: There was another division among the Jews because of Jesus words.
local: Tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ lɛ, Yuda ñɩma hɛkʋ taa ɖaɣnɩ tayʋʋ.

eng: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever, and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh. .
local: Ma lɛ wezuu tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá kalɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ. Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛtɔɔ-kɛ yɔ, ɛcakɩ wezuu tam. Tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá mɔnkɔŋ haʋ yɔ, ŋ́gá lɛ mon-tomnaɣ. Mahaɣ-kɛ se kedeŋa kpeekpe ɛyáa ehiɣ wezuu.».

eng: Many of them said, He has a demon and has lost his mind. Why listen to him.
local: Pa-taa sakɩyɛ tɔŋaɣ se: «Ekem aleewaa! Ɛsʋ kpaaŋ! Ɛbɛ yɔɔ ɩnɩɣnɩ-ɩ?».

eng: Then the Jews debated among themselves, asking, How can this man give us his flesh to eat.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yuda ñɩma pazɩ you pa-taa pe-ɖeke nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ pa-tɩ se: «Ɛzɩma ɛnɛ ɛtɔŋ se ɛhaɣ-ɖʋ e-tomnaɣ nɛ ɖɩtɔɔ?».

eng: Others said, These arent the words of someone who has a demon. Can a demon heal the eyes of people who are blind.
local: Lɛlaa se: «Eekem aleewaa yɔ, ɛtaayɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́. Eleeu pɩzɩɣ ekuli yʋlʋmaa ɛsɛ naa we?».

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you, unless you eat the flesh of the Human One and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: yee ɩtɔtɔɔ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ ma mon-tomnaɣ nɛ ɩñɔɔ man-calɩm yɔ, ɩɩwɛɣ wezuu.

eng: The time came for the Festival of Dedication in Jerusalem. It was winter.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye pɔtɔkaɣ Ɛsɔ Đɩɣa Đaɣnʋʋ Kazandʋ Yeruusalɛm. Pɩɩkɛ niŋkaɣ alɩwaatʋ.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Weyi ɛtɔkɩ mon-tomnaɣ nɛ ɛñɔɔ man-calɩm yɔ, ɛtɛm hiɣu wezuu maatɛŋ. Mɔnkɔŋ-ɩ fezuu kɛtɛzaɣ kɩyakʋ wiye nɛ sɩm taa.

eng: and Jesus was in the temple, walking in the covered porch named for Solomon.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yesu cɔʋ ka-taa Salomɔɔ Hɔɔlʋʋ taa.

eng: My flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mon-tomnaɣ lɛ tɔɔnaɣ siŋŋ nɛ man-calɩm lɛ sʋlʋm siŋŋ.

eng: The Jewish opposition circled around him and asked, How long will you test our patience? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.
local: Peeɖe Yuda ñɩma cɔ-ɩ pata nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋyeki-ɖʋ se ɖɩwɛɛ pʋtʋʋ taa ḿbʋ́ nɛ pɩsɩɩnɩ ɖooye? Yee ña lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ yɔ, ŋheyi-ɖʋ ḿbʋ́ kpayɩ.».

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me and I in them.
local: Weyi ɛtɔkɩ mon-tomnaɣ nɛ ɛñɔɔ man-calɩm yɔ, ma nɛ ɩ ɖɩwɛ ɖama taa.

eng: Jesus answered, I have told you, but you dont believe. The works I do in my Fathers name testify about me.
local: Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Mɛntɛm-mɩ heyuu ḿbʋ́, ɛlɛ́, iitisiɣ. Tʋma wena malakɩ man-Caa ɖoŋ taa yɔ, ana lɩzɩɣnɩ ma-aseɣɖe.

eng: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me lives because of me.
local: Caa weyi etiyini-m yɔ, ɛwɛnɩ wezuu, ɛ-yɔɔ mɛwɛnɩ wezuu ɖɔɖɔ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ weyi ɛtɔkɩ-m yɔ, ɛwɛɣnɩ wezuu mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: but you dont believe because you dont belong to my sheep.
local: Mɩ miitisiɣ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩfɛyɩ me-heŋ taa.
