eng: This is the bread that came down from heaven. It isnt like the bread your ancestors ate, and then they died. Whoever eats this bread will live forever. .
local: Tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá kalɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, ŋ́gá lɛ. Kɛfɛyɩ ɛzɩ ŋgá mɩ-cɔzɔnaa tɔwaa yɔ: paba ñasɩba. Weyi ɛtɔkɩ-kɛ yɔ, ɛcakɩ wezuu tam.».

eng: My sheep listen to my voice. I know them and they follow me.
local: Me-heŋ nɩɣ mɔ-nɔɔ. Ma mansɩm-ɩ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ ñatɩŋɩɣ-m.

eng: Jesus said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Yesu wɩlaɣ ɛyáa Ɛsɔtɔm Kapɛrnayum Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa yɔ, ɛyɔɔdɩ tɔm tʋnɛ.

eng: I give them eternal life. They will never die, and no one will snatch them from my hand.
local: Mahaɣ-ɩ wezuu maatɛŋ, ɩɩtɛŋɛlɩɣ kaʋ. Nɔɔyʋ ɛɛkaɣ-ɩ lɛɣʋ me-nesi taa.

eng: Many of his disciples who heard this said, This message is harsh. Who can hear it.
local: Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa-taa sakɩyɛ yɔɔdɩ se: «Tɔm tʋnɛ, tɩwɛ kaɖɛ. Apɩzɩɣ etisi-tʋ?».

eng: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them from my Fathers hand.
local: Man-Caa cɛlɩnɩ-m-ɩ, ɛkɩlɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛlɛɣ nabʋyʋ e-nesi taa.

eng: Jesus knew that the disciples were grumbling about this and he said to them, Does this offend you?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu na ɛ-taa se tɔm ńdʋ́ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa midiɣna nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ndʋ́ mɔyɔɔdaa yɔ, ńdʋ́ cɛyɩɣnɩ-mɩ yaa we?.

eng: I and the Father are one. .
local: Ma nɛ man-Caa ɖɩkɛ kʋɖʋm.».

eng: What if you were to see the Human One going up where he was before?.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛzɩma pɩkaɣ labʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩnaɣ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ kitiɣ ɛkpa ɖenɖe ɛɛwɛɛ yɔ?.

eng: Again the Jewish opposition picked up stones in order to stone him.
local: Peeɖe Yuda ñɩma ɖaɣnɩ kpaɣʋ pɛɛ se payakɩ-ɩ.

eng: The Spirit is the one who gives life and the flesh doesnt help at all. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
local: Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu haɣnɩ wezuu. Ɛyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛla pʋyʋ. Tɔm ndʋ́ meheyi-mɩ yɔ, tɩ-yɔɔ kɩtɩŋɩɣna nɛ kɩhaɣ wezuu.

eng: Jesus responded, I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of those works do you stone me? .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ eheyi-wɛ se: «Malabɩ Caa tʋma kɩbana sakɩyɛ mɩ-ɛsɩndaa. A-taa nɖɩ́ yɔɔ ɩcaɣ se ɩyaɣ-m pɛɛ nɛ mansɩ yɔ?».

eng: Yet some of you dont believe. Jesus knew from the beginning who wouldnt believe and the one who would betray him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mɩ-taa lɛlaa eetisiɣ.» Yesu yɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛtɛm sɩm mbá paatisiɣ yɔ nɛ weyi ɛkɔŋ ɛ-wayɩ lɩʋ yɔ.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We dont stone you for a good work but for insulting God. You are human, yet you make yourself out to be God.
local: Paba cosi-i se: «Pɩtɛkɛ tʋmɩyɛ kɩbanɖɛ naɖɩyɛ yɔɔ ɖɩcaɣ se ɖɩkʋ-ŋ nɛ pɛɛ. Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩkʋʋ-ŋ se ŋwɛɛkɩ Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ: ŋkɛ ɛyʋ yem nɛ ŋyaɣ ña-tɩ se Ɛsɔ.».

eng: He said, For this reason I said to you that none can come to me unless the Father enables them to do so. .
local: Ɛtasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Pʋ-yɔɔ meheyi-mɩ se nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ se ɛkɔŋ mɔn-cɔlɔ nɛ man-Caa taha-ɩ nʋmɔʋ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Isnt it written in your Law, I have said, you are gods?.
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Paɖʋ mɩ-paɣtʋ taa se: ‹Ɛsɔ tɔm se ɩkɛ ɛsɔnaa.›.

eng: Scripture calls those to whom Gods word came gods , and scripture cant be abolished.
local: Đɩnawa se paapɩzɩɣ pahɩzɩ tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Ŋgʋ́, tɩ-taa Ɛsɔ ya mbá ɛyɔɔdaɣnɩ-wɛ yɔ se ɛsɔnaa.

eng: At this, many of his disciples turned away and no longer accompanied him.
local: Pɩkpaɣnɩ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa sakɩyɛ ha-ɩ siɣ́ye nɛ paatasɩɣ-ɩ tɩŋʋʋ.

eng: So how can you say that the one whom the Father has made holy and sent into the world insults God because he said, I am Gods Son?.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma mɔntɔ se: ma lɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ nɛ ɩtɔŋ se mɛwɛɛkɩɣ ɛ-hɩɖɛ yɔ? Ŋgʋ́, ma ɛlɩzaa nɛ etiyi kedeŋa yɔɔ.

eng: Jesus asked the Twelve, Do you also want to leave? .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛpɔzɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ se: «Nɛ mɩ yɔ, miiyeki-m ɖɔɖɔ yaa we?».

eng: If I dont do the works of my Father, dont believe me.
local: Yee maalakɩ man-Caa tʋma yɔ, ɩtaawɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ!.

eng: Simon Peter answered, Lord, where would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
local: Peeɖe Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ cosi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! A-cɔlɔ ɖiwoki? Ñɔ-cɔlɔ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩhaɣ wezuu maatɛŋ yɔ tɩwɛɛ.

eng: But if I do them, and you dont believe me, believe the works so that you can know and recognize that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.
local: Ɛlɛ́, paa malakɩ-yɛ nɛ ɩfɛyɩnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, itisi tʋma wena malakɩ yɔ a-yɔɔ. Peeɖe ɩnaɣ nɛ ɩtɩlɩ kpaagbaa se ma nɛ Caa ɖɩwɛ ɖama taa.».

eng: We believe and know that you are Gods holy one.
local: Đɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ ñɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩtɩlɩ se ña lɛ Ɛsɔ Ñɩnʋ.».

eng: Again, they wanted to arrest him, but he escaped from them.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, paɖaɣnɩɣ ñɩnʋʋ se pakpa-ɩ, ɛlɛ́, ɛtʋlɩ, petehiɣ-i.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I choose you twelve? Yet one of you is a devil. .
local: Ɛlɛ́ Yesu pɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Pɩtɛkɛ malɩzɩnɩ mi-hiu nɛ naalɛ yaa we? Ŋgʋ́, mɩ-taa lɛlʋ kɛ eleeu!».

eng: Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had baptized at first, and he stayed there.
local: Peeɖe Yesu ɖaɣnɩ tɛzʋʋ Yɔɔrdaŋ pɔɔ wayɩ ɖenɖe Yohanɛɛsɩ kalabɩ ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ yɔ nɛ ɛcaɣ peeɖe.

eng: He was speaking of Judas, Simon Iscariots son, for he, one of the Twelve, was going to betray him.
local: Siimɔɔ Iskarɩyɔɔtɩ pɩyalʋ Yudaasɩ tɔm ɛyɔɔdaɣ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paa ɛɛkɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ taa lɛlʋ yɔ, ɛnʋ kɔŋnɩ ɛ-wayɩ lɩʋ.

eng: Many people came to him. John didnt do any miraculous signs, they said, but everything John said about this man was true.
local: Ɛyáa sakɩyɛ wokaɣ ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Yohanɛɛsɩ tala hama lakasɩ nasɩyɩ. Ɛlɛ́, tɔm ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa ɛyɔɔdɩ abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, tɩkɛ toovenim.».

eng: A certain man, Lazarus, was ill. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha. .
local: Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛɛ nɛ payaɣ-ɩ se Lazaarɩ, pɩwɩɣaɣ-ɩ. Ɛ nɛ ɛ-kɔɔnaa Maarɩɩ nɛ Maarta paawɛ tɛtʋ cikpetu ndʋ́ payaɣ se Betaanii yɔ tɩ-taa.

eng: Many believed in Jesus there.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ wɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ.

eng: This was the Mary who anointed the Lord with fragrant oil and wiped his feet with her hair. Her brother Lazarus was ill.
local: Maarɩɩ ɛnʋ ɛɛpɩyɩnɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ naŋgbanzɩ yɔɔ tɩlaaɖɩ nɛ ɛhɛzɩnɩ ɛ-ñɔsɩ. E-neu Lazaarɩ pɩwɩɣaɣ.

eng: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, saying, Lord, the one whom you love is ill.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ halaa ḿbá pa-naalɛ petiyaa se peheyi Yesu se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Pɩwɩɣ weyi ŋsɔɔlɩ-ɩ yɔ.».

eng: When he heard this, Jesus said, This illness isnt fatal. Its for the glory of God so that Gods Son can be glorified through it. .
local: Powobi peheyi-i ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛtɔ se: «Lazaarɩ ɛɛsɩkɩ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩwɩɣ-ɩ ḿbʋ́ se Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ ɛsɛɛ nɛ piyele ɛ-Pɩyalʋ ñɩnɖɛ sɛɛ ɖɔɖɔ.».

eng: Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
local: Yesu kɔsɔɔlɩ Maarta nɛ e-neu, nɛ Lazaarɩ.

eng: When he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed where he was. After two days.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɛnɩwa se pɩwɩɣ Lazaarɩ lɛ, ɛtasɩ caɣʋ kɩyakɩŋ naalɛ ɖenɖe ɛɛwɛɛ yɔ.

eng: he said to his disciples, Lets return to Judea again. .
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, eheyi ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se: «Đɩpɩsɩ Yudee egeetiye taa.».

eng: The disciples replied, Rabbi, the Jewish opposition wants to stone you, but you want to go back.
local: Paba cosi-i se: «Wɩlɩyʋ! Pɩtalabɩdɩ pʋyʋ yɔ, ɖɩ-taa ñɩma ñɩnaɣ se pakʋ-ŋ nɛ pɛɛ nɛ ŋcaɣ se ŋpɩsɩ peeɖe ɖɔɖɔ yaa we?».

eng: Jesus answered, Arent there twelve hours in the day? Whoever walks in the day doesnt stumble because they see the light of the world.
local: Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Tɛʋ feŋ lɛ, wɩsɩ lɩɣ yaa sɩɩlɩɣ? Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛɖɔŋ wɩsɩ taa yɔ, piituuluu-i, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnaɣ ñalɩmɩyɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ.

eng: But whoever walks in the night does stumble because the light isnt in them. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee nɔɔyʋ ɛɖɔŋ ɖoó taa yɔ, putuuluu-i, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛfɛyɩnɩ ñalɩmɩyɛ.».

eng: He continued, Our friend Lazarus is sleeping, but I am going in order to wake him up. .
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛtasɩ se: «Đɛ-ɛgbaɣdʋ Lazaarɩ ɖou, ɛlɛ́, mowoki menfezi-i.».

eng: The disciples said, Lord, if hes sleeping, he will get well.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa tɔm se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Yee eɖou yɔ, ɛ-yɔɔ cɛkɩ.».

eng: They thought Jesus meant that Lazarus was in a deep sleep, but Jesus had spoken about Lazarus death.
local: Ŋgʋ́, Lazaarɩ sɩm tɔm Yesu yɔɔdaɣ, ɛlɛ́, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ñamaɣzɩɣ se ɖom tɩtɩŋa tɔm ɛyɔɔdʋʋ.

eng: Jesus told them plainly, Lazarus has died.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛkaɖɩ kpayɩ se: «Lazaarɩ sɩba.

eng: For your sakes, Im glad I wasnt there so that you can believe. Lets go to him. .
local: Mɩ-yɔɔ lɛ, man-taa hʋlʋmaa se mantaawɛɛ peeɖe alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́. Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ. Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɖiwolo ɛ-cɔlɔ.».

eng: Then Thomas said to the other disciples, Let us go too so that we may die with Jesus.
local: Peeɖe Tomaa weyi payaɣ ɖɔɖɔ se Đom yɔ, eheyi tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lɛlaa se: «Đa ɖɔɖɔ, ɖa nɛ Wɩlɩyʋ ɖiwolo nɛ paa sɩm yɔ ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩsɩ!».

eng: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
local: Yesu talɩ Betaanii lɛ, ɛmaɣnɩ pɛtɛm Lazaarɩ pim nɛ pɩtɛ labʋ kɩyakɩŋ naanza.

eng: Bethany was a little less than two miles from Jerusalem.
local: Betaanii ñɔtɩnɩ Yeruusalɛm. Pɩtatalɩ kiloomɛtanaa naadozo.

eng: Many Jews had come to comfort Martha and Mary after their brothers death.
local: Yuda ñɩma sakɩyɛ kowobi Maarta nɛ Maarɩɩ pɛ-tɛ se pɛsɛɛ-wɛ leɣa.

eng: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, while Mary remained in the house.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Maarta kanɩwa se Yesu woki nɛ ɛkʋyɩ ɛkatɩ-ɩ nɛ piyele Maarɩɩ caɣ ɖɩɣa.

eng: Martha said to Jesus, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Peeɖe eheyi Yesu se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ŋŋwɛ cɩnɛ yɔ, me-neu taasɩ.

eng: Even now I know that whatever you ask God, God will give you.
local: Ɛlɛ́, manawa se: lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, paa ŋpɔzɩ Ɛsɔ ɛbɛ yɔ, ɛhaɣ-ŋ-pʋ.».

eng: Jesus told her, Your brother will rise again. .
local: Yesu heyi-i se: «Ñe-neu feŋ.».

eng: Martha replied, I know that he will rise in the resurrection on the last day.
local: Maarta cosi se: «Toovenim, manawa se ɛkɔŋ fem alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ sɩɖaa feŋ kɛtɛzaɣ kɩyakʋ wiye yɔ.».

eng: Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me will live, even though they die.
local: Yesu heyi-i se: «Mefeziɣni sɩɖaa nɛ mahaɣ-wɛ wezuu. Weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, paa ɛsɩba yɔ, ɛwɛɣ wezuu.

eng: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this? .
local: Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ wɛnɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛsɩkɩ kaʋ. Ŋtisi ḿbʋ́ yaa we?».

eng: She replied, Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Christ, Gods Son, the one who is coming into the world.
local: Maarta cosi-i se: «Ɛɛɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ! Mentisaa se ña lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ. Ña lɛ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ, ña ɛɛɖʋwa se ŋkɔŋ kedeŋa yɔɔ.».

eng: After she said this, she went and spoke privately to her sister Mary, The teacher is here and hes calling for you.
local: Maarta tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛpɩsɩ nɛ ɛ́yáa e-neu Maarɩɩ nɛ eheyiɣ-i mɛsaɣ tɛɛ se: «Wɩlɩyʋ kɔma, ɛyaɣ-ŋ se ŋwolo.».

eng: When Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to Jesus.
local: Maarɩɩ tɩnɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ kpaka se ɛkatɩ-ɩ.

eng: He hadnt entered the village but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
local: Ŋgʋ́, Yesu taatɩtalɩda tɛtʋ taa, ɛlɛ́, ɛɛwɛ ɖenɖe Maarta kakatɩ-ɩ yɔ ḿbʋ́.

eng: When the Jews who were comforting Mary in the house saw her get up quickly and leave, they followed her. They assumed she was going to mourn at the tomb.
local: Yuda ñɩma mbá paawobi Maarɩɩ tɛ se pɛsɛɛ-ɩ leɣa yɔ, pana ɛtɩkʋyɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛlɩɩ lɛ, patɩŋ ɛ-wayɩ. Pamaɣzaɣ se ewoki pɩlaʋ yɔɔ se ewili.

eng: When Mary arrived where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Ɛtalɩ ɖenɖe Yesu kɛwɛɛ yɔ lɛ, ɛna-ɩ. Peeɖe eluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Yee ŋŋwɛ cɩnɛ yɔ, me-neu taasɩ.».

eng: When Jesus saw her crying and the Jews who had come with her crying also, he was deeply disturbed and troubled.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu na se Maarɩɩ wiliɣ nɛ Yuda ñɩma mbá patɩŋaɣ ɛ-wayɩ yɔ, pewiliɣ ɖɔɖɔ. Peeɖe pɩlabɩ-ɩ yem yaaa nɛ ɛ-laŋɩyɛ wɛɛkɩ siŋŋ.

eng: He asked, Where have you laid him? They replied, Lord, come and see.
local: nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Le ipim-i?» Pocosi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Kɔɔ nɛ ŋna.».

eng: Jesus began to cry.
local: Peeɖe Yesu wilaa.

eng: The Jews said, See how much he loved him.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Yuda ñɩma tɔm se: «Ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛɛsɔɔlɩ-ɩ kpem yɔ!».

eng: But some of them said, He healed the eyes of the man born blind. Couldnt he have kept Lazarus from dying.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pa-taa lɛlaa se: «Ɛnʋ weyi ekuli yʋlʋm ɛsɛ yɔ, ɛtaapɩzɩɣ se ɛla ɖɔɖɔ nɛ Lazaarɩ ɛtaasɩ naa we?».

eng: Jesus was deeply disturbed again when he came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone covered the entrance.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu laŋɩyɛ tasɩ wɛɛkʋʋ siŋŋ nɛ ɛkʋyɩ ewolo pɩlaʋ cɔlɔ. Ɛwaɣ taa kɩɩwɛɛ, paɖɩɣ kʋ-nɔɔ pɩyɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Remove the stone. Martha, the sister of the dead man, said, Lord, the smell will be awful! Hes been dead four days.
local: Peeɖe ɛtɔm se: «Ɩtʋlɩ pɩyɛ.» Ɛlɛ́, sɩɖʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-kɔɔ Maarta heyi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma ɛpazɩ sɔŋ lɩʋ: pɩlabɩ kɩyakɩŋ naanza yɔ ɛzɩ pepim-i yɔ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I tell you that if you believe, you will see Gods glory? .
local: Yesu pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Menteheyi-ŋ se yee ŋwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ ŋnaɣ Ɛsɔ koboyaɣ naa we?».

eng: So they removed the stone. Jesus looked up and said, Father, thank you for hearing me.
local: Peeɖe patʋlɩ pɩyɛ. Ɛlɛ́, Yesu kpazɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ ɛsɔdaa nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Caa! Mansaŋ-ŋ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ŋnɩnɩ-m yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: I know you always hear me. I say this for the benefit of the crowd standing here so that they will believe that you sent me. .
local: Ma lɛ, manawa se ŋnɩɣnɩ-m tam. Ɛlɛ́, ɛyáa samaɣ ŋgá kɔcɔ-m kata yɔ, kɔ-yɔɔ mɔyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ se papɩzɩ petisi se ñetiyini-m.».

eng: Having said this, Jesus shouted with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out! .
local: Ɛtɛm Ɛsɔ sam ḿbʋ́ lɛ, eholi ɖoŋ se: «Lazaarɩ! Lɩɩ peeɖe!».

eng: The dead man came out, his feet bound and his hands tied, and his face covered with a cloth. Jesus said to them, Untie him and let him go. .
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ fe nɛ ɛlɩɩ. Paahɔkɩ ɛ-naŋgbanzɩ nɛ e-nesi nɛ pɩsaʋ hɔɔlasɩ nɛ pohuuzi ɛ-ɛsɩndaa pɩsaʋ. Peeɖe Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Ɩyɛɖɩ-ɩ nɛ iyebi-i ɛɖɛɛ.».

eng: Therefore, many of the Jews who came with Mary and saw what Jesus did believed in him.
local: Yuda ñɩma mbá paawobi Maarɩɩ cɔlɔ yɔ, pana mbʋ́ Yesu laba yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ pa-taa sakɩyɛ wɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pa-taa lɛlaa ñaakʋyaa powolo pana Farɩsɩ ñɩma nɛ peheyi-wɛ mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ.

eng: Then the chief priests and Pharisees called together the council and said, What are we going to do? This man is doing many miraculous signs!.
local: Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ paba nɛ Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa pekpeɣli tɔm hʋyaa sɔsaa nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛbɛ pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩla? Abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛlakɩ hama lakasɩ sakɩyɛ!.

eng: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him. Then the Romans will come and take away both our temple and our people.
local: Yee ɖiyebi-i nɛ ɛlakɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɛyáa tɩŋa wɛɣnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ Room ñɩma kɔɔ pɔyɔkɩ ɖɛ-Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa nɛ pɛkpɛndɩnɩ ɖɛ-ɛjaɖɛ!».

eng: One of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, told them, You dont know anything!.
local: Payaɣ pa-taa lɛlʋ se Kayifu, ɛɛkɛ cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ pɩnaɣ ŋ́gá ka-taa. Ɛ́lɛ́ heyini-wɛ se: «Ɩɩnɩɣ natʋyʋ taa kpem!.

eng: You dont see that it is better for you that one man die for the people rather than the whole nation be destroyed.
local: Ɩtanɩɩ se pɩkɩlɩ-mɩ wee wee se ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm ɛsɩ nɛ ɛjaɖɛ kpeekpe ɛtaala asayʋʋ yaa we?».

eng: He didnt say this on his own. As high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would soon die for the nation—.
local: Ŋgʋ́, pɩtɛkɛ ɛ-maɣmaɣ e-liu wɛna nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ tɔm tʋnɛ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛɛkɛ cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ pɩnaɣ ŋ́gá ka-taa yɔɔ eheyi ɛyáa tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ se pʋwɛɛ se Yesu ɛsɩ Yuda ñɩma yɔɔ.

eng: and not only for the nation. Jesus would also die so that Gods children scattered everywhere would be gathered together as one.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɩtɛkɛ pe-ɖeke pɔ-yɔɔ ɛsɩkɩ. Pɩɩwɛɛ se ɛsɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛkpɛndɩ Ɛsɔ piya mbá pamɩzɩ ɖama paa le yɔ nɛ papɩsɩ ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm.

eng: From that day on they plotted to kill him.
local: Pɩkpaɣʋnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋ́gʋ́ yɔ, Yuda ñɩma sɔsaa ɖʋwa se pakʋʋ Yesu.

eng: Therefore, Jesus was no longer active in public ministry among the Jewish leaders. Instead, he left Jerusalem and went to a place near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, where he stayed with his disciples.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛtatasɩɣ cɔʋ Yuda ñɩma hɛkʋ taa keɣlee. Ɛlɛ́, ɛ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pakʋyɩ powolo egeetiye nɖɩ́ ɖɩñɔtɩnɩ lakʋ yɔ ɖɩ-taa, tɛtʋ ndʋ́ payaɣ se Efrayim yɔ, tɩ-taa nɛ pacaɣ peeɖe.

eng: It was almost time for the Jewish Passover, and many people went from the countryside up to Jerusalem to purify themselves through ritual washing before the Passover.
local: Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma Paska Kazandʋ kawɩzɩ talʋʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛjaɖɛ taa ɛyáa sakɩyɛ woba Yeruusalɛm se pala cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ wɛtʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ pʋcɔ tɩtalɩ.

eng: They were looking for Jesus. As they spoke to each other in the temple, they said, What do you think? He wont come to the festival, will he.
local: Pañɩnaɣ Yesu Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ ɖama se: «Ɩmaɣzɩɣ se we? Ɛkɔŋ kazandʋ naa ɛɛkɔŋ?».

eng: The chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where he was should report it, so they could arrest him.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma paha nɔɔ se: yee nɔɔyʋ ɛna ɖenɖe Yesu wɛɛ yɔ yɔ, eheyi nɛ powolo pakpa-ɩ.

eng: Six days before Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, home of Lazarus, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
local: Pɩsɛɣ kɩyakɩŋ loɖo se pɔtɔɔ Paska Kazandʋ. Ɛlɛ́, Yesu wolo Betaanii ɖenɖe Lazaarɩ weyi efezi-i nɛ sɩɖaa taa yɔ, ɛwɛɛ yɔ.

eng: Lazarus and his sisters hosted a dinner for him. Martha served and Lazarus was among those who joined him at the table.
local: Peeɖe palabɩ-ɩ tɔɔnaɣ nɛ Maarta wona. Lazaarɩ kɛwɛ mbá pa nɛ Yesu paacaɣ ɖɩtɔɔyɛ yɔ pa-taa.

eng: Then Mary took an extraordinary amount, almost three-quarters of a pound, of very expensive perfume made of pure nard. She anointed Jesus feet with it, then wiped his feet dry with her hair. The house was filled with the aroma of the perfume.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Maarɩɩ kpaɣ tɩlaaɖɩ ŋgʋ́ palabɩnɩ naarɩ nɛ petesidi kɩ-taa pʋyʋ yɔ ɛzɩ litri hɔɔlʋʋ yɔ nɛ ɛpɩyɩ Yesu naŋgbanzɩ yɔɔ. Ɛtɛma lɛ, ɛmɛlɩ sɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛ-ñɔsɩ nɛ sɔzɩŋ ñɛyɩnɩ ɖɩɣa. Tɩlaaɖɩ ŋ́gʋ́, kɩwɛ liidiye pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples , complained.
local: Peeɖe Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa lɛlʋ weyi payaɣ se Yudaasɩ Iskarɩyɔɔtɩ nɛ ɛkɔŋ-ɩ ɖʋzʋʋ koyindinaa nesi taa yɔ, ɛtɔm se:.

eng: This perfume was worth a years wages! Why wasnt it sold and the money given to the poor? .
local: «Tɩlaaɖɩ ŋ́gʋ́, kɩtalɩ kɩyakɩŋ nasidozo tʋmɩyɛ liidiye! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma pɛtɛpɛdɩ-kʋ se patayɩ kʋñɔndɩnaa?».

eng: He said this not because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief. He carried the money bag and would take what was in it.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ kʋñɔndɩnaa yɔɔ ɛmaɣzaɣ nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɛ́, ɛkɛ mɩ́lʋ́ yɔɔ ɛyɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́: ɛnʋ ɖɔkaɣnɩ liidiye huyuu nɛ ɛmɩlɩɣ mbʋ́ paɖʋwaɣ kɩ-taa yɔ.

eng: Then Jesus said, Leave her alone. This perfume was to be used in preparation for my burial, and this is how she has used it.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-i se: «Yele halʋ ɛnʋ ɖiɣ ɖiɣ! Ɛɛsɩ tɩlaaɖɩ ŋ́gʋ́ nɛ ɛɖaŋnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ pepiŋ-m yɔ.

eng: You will always have the poor among you, but you wont always have me. .
local: Mɩ nɛ kʋñɔndɩnaa ɩwɛnɩ tam. Ɛlɛ́, ma maawɛɣ mɩ-cɔlɔ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: Many Jews learned that he was there. They came not only because of Jesus but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
local: Yuda ñɩma sakɩyɛ kanɩwa se Yesu wɛ Betaanii. Pʋ-yɔɔ pakʋyaa powolo. Pɩtɛkɛ e-ɖeke ɛ-yɔɔ paawoba. Pacaɣaɣ se pana ɖɔɖɔ Lazaarɩ weyi ɛsɩba nɛ efezi-i yɔ.

eng: The chief priests decided that they would kill Lazarus too.
local: Ŋgʋ́, cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa lakaɣ lɩmaɣza se pakʋ Lazaarɩ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: It was because of Lazarus that many of the Jews had deserted them and come to believe in Jesus.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-yɔɔ Yuda ñɩma sakɩyɛ seɣaɣ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ pɛwɛɣnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ.

eng: The next day the great crowd that had come for the festival heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
local: Tɛʋ fema lɛ, ɛyáa samaɣ sɔsɔɔ ŋgá kowobi Paska Kazandʋ tɔɔʋ yɔ, kanɩɩ se Yesu woki Yeruusalɛm.

eng: They took palm branches and went out to meet him. They shouted, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessings on the king of Israel.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pakpaɣ paatʋ nɛ pakatɩɣ-ɩ nɛ pokou se: «Ozaana! Ɛsɔ ɛ́wazɩ weyi ɛkɔŋ Kɩpaɣlʋ lone taa yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ! Ɛsɔ ɛ́wazɩ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ yɔɔ!».

eng: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written.
local: Peeɖe Yesu na kpaŋaɣ piye naɖɩyɛ nɛ ɛkpa ɛcaɣ ɖɩ-yɔɔ. Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se:.

eng: Dont be afraid, Daughter Zion. Look! Your king is coming , sitting on a donkeys colt .
local: «Siiyɔɔ ñɩma mɩ! Sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-mɩ! Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna, mi-wiyaʋ sɔsɔ caɣ kpaŋaɣ piye nɛ ɛkɔŋ.».

eng: His disciples didnt understand these things at first. After he was glorified, they remembered that these things had been written about him and that they had done these things to him.
local: Ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taatɩnɩda mbʋ́ pɩlakaɣ ḿbʋ́ yɔ pʋ-tɔbʋʋ. Ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ yeba nɛ Yesu hɩɖɛ sɛɛ yɔ, pɔtɔzaa se ɛ-tɔm paama ḿbʋ́. Samaɣ laba ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ.

eng: The crowd who had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead were testifying about him.
local: Mbá pa nɛ Yesu paawɛ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛya Lazaarɩ nɛ pɩlaʋ taa nɛ efezi-i nɛ ɛlɩɩ yɔ, pɛkɛdaɣ ɛyáa mbʋ́ panawa yɔ.

eng: Thats why the crowd came to meet him, because they had heard about this miraculous sign that he had done.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛyáa samaɣ kʋyaa se pakatɩɣ-ɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, panɩwa se ɛlabɩ hama lakasɩ ńzɩ́.

eng: Therefore, the Pharisees said to each other, See! Youve accomplished nothing! Look! The whole world is following him.
local: Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma yɔɔdʋʋnɩ ɖama se: «Ɩnaɣ, ɩlakɩ yem: ɛyáa tɩŋa tɩŋɩɣ-ɩ!».

eng: Some Greeks were among those who had come up to worship at the festival.
local: Grɛɛkɩ ñɩma nabɛyɛ kowobi Yeruusalɛm kazandʋ alɩwaatʋ taa ɖɔɖɔ se poluŋ Ɛsɔ tɛɛ.

eng: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and made a request: Sir, we want to see Jesus.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, powolo Filiipu weyi ɛ-tɛ wɛ Bɛtsayɩda Galilee egeetiye taa yɔ ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ peheyi-i se: «Đajaa! Đɩcaɣ ɖɩna Yesu.».

eng: Philip told Andrew, and Andrew and Philip told Jesus.
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ woba eheyi Andree nɛ ɛ nɛ ɩ powolo Yesu cɔlɔ pa-naalɛ nɛ peheyi-i ḿbʋ́.

eng: Jesus replied, The time has come for the Human One to be glorified.
local: Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, alɩwaatʋ talaa se Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ hɩɖɛ ɛsɛɛ.

eng: I assure you that unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it can only be a single seed. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.
local: Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: yee potoɖuu mɩɖɛ piye nɛ ɖɩsɩ yɔ, ɖɩwɛɣ ɖi-ɖeke ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɛ́, yee poɖu-ɖɩ nɛ ɖɩsɩ yɔ, ɖɩlʋlʋʋ pee sakɩyɛ.

eng: Those who love their lives will lose them, and those who hate their lives in this world will keep them forever.
local: Weyi ɛsɔɔlɩ e-wezuu yɔ, ɛtɛŋnɩ-kʋ lesuu. Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ takpaɣ ɛ-ñɩŋgʋ nɛ ɛ́yáa pʋyʋ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ yɔ, eelesiɣ-kʋ, ehikiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ.

eng: Whoever serves me must follow me. Wherever I am, there my servant will also be. My Father will honor whoever serves me.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ liu ɛwɛɛ se ɛlabɩ-m tʋmɩyɛ yɔ, ɛtɩŋ-m. Ḿbʋ́ man-tʋmlaɖʋ kɔŋ wɛʋ ɖenɖe mɛwɛɣ yɔ. Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛlakɩ-m tʋmɩyɛ yɔ, man-Caa ɖʋʋ-ɩ hɩɖɛ.».

eng: Now I am deeply troubled. What should I say? Father, save me from this time? No, for this is the reason I have come to this time.
local: Yesu tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛtɔ se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ma-laŋɩyɛ wɛɛkaa. Ɛlɛ́, se we mɔyɔɔdʋʋ? Mɔyɔɔdʋʋ se: Caa! Taayele nɛ mansʋʋ kʋñɔŋ alɩwaatʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-taa yaa we? Ŋgʋ́, pɩ-maɣmaɣ pʋ-yɔɔ kpem mɔnkɔma.

eng: Father, glorify your name! Then a voice came from heaven, I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.
local: Caa! Yele nɛ ña-hɩɖɛ sɛɛ!» Peeɖe pʋyɔɔdɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa se: «Mɛntɛm yebu nɛ ɖɩsɛɛ, ɛlɛ́, meyeki nɛ ɖɩtasɩ sɛʋ!».

eng: The crowd standing there heard and said, Its thunder. Others said, An angel spoke to him.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ ŋgá kaawɛ peeɖe yɔ, kanɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, kɔtɔ se: «Tɛʋ nazɩna!» Lɛlaa se: «Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ yɔɔdɩnɩnɩ-ɩ!».

eng: Jesus replied, This voice wasnt for my benefit but for yours.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Yesu kaɖɩ-wɛ se: «Pʋyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔɔ pʋyɔɔdʋʋ, ɛlɛ́, mɩ-yɔɔ yɔɔ pʋyɔɔdʋʋ.

eng: Now is the time for judgment of this world. Now this worlds ruler will be thrown out.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, alɩwaatʋ talaa se pahʋʋnɩ kedeŋa ñɩma. Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, pʋwɛɛ se pɔɖɔɔnɩ kedeŋa wiyaʋ.

eng: When I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to me. .
local: Ma lɛ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pakpazɩɣ-m ɖaʋ yɔɔ yɔ, meyeki nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa kɔɔ mɔn-cɔlɔ.».

eng: He said this to show how he was going to die.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ se ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ sɩm mbʋ́ ɛkɔŋ sɩbʋ yɔ.

eng: The crowd responded, We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. How can you say that the Human One must be lifted up? Who is this Human One.
local: Peeɖe ɛyáa samaɣ cosi-i se: «Đɩkalɩ ɖa-paɣtʋ takayaɣ taa se Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa yɔ, ɛwɛɣ wezuu tam lɛ, ɛzɩma ñɔtɔŋ se pʋwɛɛ se pakpazɩ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ ɖaʋ yɔɔ yɔ? Ɛ́lɛ́ lɛ anɩ?».

eng: Jesus replied, The light is with you for only a little while. Walk while you have the light so that darkness doesnt overtake you. Those who walk in the darkness dont know where they are going.
local: Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Ñalɩmɩyɛ wɛ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa ḿbʋ́, ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ pazɩ ɖɩwɛɣ. Ɩɖɔ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ñalɩmɩyɛ wɛ mɩ-cɔlɔ yɔ nɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ ɛtaatudi-mɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛɖɔŋ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa yɔ, ɛɛsɩŋ ɖenɖe ewoki yɔ.

eng: As long as you have the light, believe in the light so that you might become people whose lives are determined by the light. After Jesus said these things, he went away and hid from them.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ ñalɩmɩyɛ yɔɔ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩwɛ mɩ-cɔlɔ yɔ nɛ ɩpɩsɩ ɖɩ-taa ñɩma.» Yesu tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛɖɛɛ poliŋ nɛ ɛmɛlɩ.

eng: Jesus had done many miraculous signs before the people, but they didnt believe in him.
local: Ɛɛlaba hama lakasɩ ndɩ ndɩ Yuda ñɩma ɛsɩndaa, paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pɛfɛyɩnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: This was to fulfill the word of the prophet Isaiah: Lord, who has believed through our message? To whom is the arm of the Lord fully revealed?.
local: Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se tɔm ndʋ́ náyʋ Ezaayii ma ɖooo lɔŋ yɔ, tɩla. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Atisi tɔm ndʋ́ ɖɩyɔɔdaa yɔ? Anɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ wɩlɩ e-ɖoŋ weyi ɛɛwɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ?».

eng: Isaiah explains why they couldnt believe:.
local: Patapɩzɩɣ se petisi, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ezaayii mawa ɖɔɖɔ se:.

eng: He made their eyes blind and closed their minds so that they might not see with their eyes, understand with their minds, and turn their lives around— and I would heal them.
local: «Ɛsɔ yʋlɩsɩ pɛ-ɛsɛ se pataana nɛ ehuuzi pa-lɩmaɣza yɔɔ se pataanɩɩ pʋ-tɔbʋʋ. Ɛlaba ḿbʋ́ se, pɛ-ɛsɛ ɛtaatasɩ naʋ, pataatasɩ natʋyʋ taa nɩʋ, paapɩzɩɣ papɩsɩnɩ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, nɛ ɛla nɛ pɔ-yɔɔ cɛ.».

eng: Isaiah said these things because he saw Jesus glory; he spoke about Jesus.
local: Ezaayii kana Kɩpaɣlʋ koboyaɣ yɔɔ ɛma ɛ-tɔm ḿbʋ́.

eng: Even so, many leaders believed in him, but they wouldnt acknowledge their faith because they feared that the Pharisees would expel them from the synagogue.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, Yuda ñɩma awiya maɣmaɣ taa sakɩyɛ kɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Farɩsɩ ñɩma yɔɔ lɛ, pataasusuu-tʋ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, paɖaŋɩɣ-wɛ se pataatasɩ wobu Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa.

eng: They believed, but they loved human praise more than Gods glory.
local: Paasɔɔlaa se ɛyáa ɛyɩɣyɩ-wɛ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ Ɛsɔ yɩɣyʋʋ-wɛ yɔ.

eng: Jesus shouted, Whoever believes in me doesnt believe in me but in the one who sent me.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yesu holi se: «Weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ men-ɖeke mɔ-yɔɔ ɛwɛnɩ-kʋ, ɛwɛnɩ-kʋ weyi etiyi-m yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: Whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.
local: Weyi ɛnaɣ-m yɔ, ɛnaɣ weyi etiyi-m yɔ.

eng: I have come as a light into the world so that everyone who believes in me wont live in darkness.
local: Ma lɛ, mɔnkɔm kedeŋa yɔɔ ɛzɩ ñalɩmɩyɛ yɔ se nɔɔyʋ ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɛtaawɛɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa.

eng: If people hear my words and dont keep them, I dont judge them. I didnt come to judge the world but to save it.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛnɩ mɔn-tɔm nɛ ɛɛɖɔŋ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ mankʋʋnɩ ɛ-tɔm. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɔntɔkɔɔ kedeŋa yɔɔ se mankʋ ɛyáa tɔm, ɛlɛ́, mɔnkɔm se maya pa-ñʋʋ.

eng: Whoever rejects me and doesnt receive my words will be judged at the last day by the word I have spoken.
local: Weyi ɛlɔʋ-m nɛ ekizi mɔn-tɔm yɔ, ɛnaɣ mbʋ́ pʋkʋʋ ɛ-tɔm yɔ: tɔm ndʋ́ mawɩlaa yɔ, ńdʋ́ kʋʋnɩ ɛ-tɔm kɛtɛzaɣ kɩyakʋ wiye.

eng: I dont speak on my own, but the Father who sent me commanded me regarding what I should speak and say.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ mon-ɖoŋ ɖeke mɔyɔɔdɩnaa. Ɛlɛ́, Caa weyi etiyi-m yɔ, ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛɖʋnɩ tɔm ndʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se mɔyɔɔdɩ nɛ mawɩlɩ yɔ.

eng: I know that his commandment is eternal life. Therefore, whatever I say is just as the Father has said to me. .
local: Manawa se tɔm ndʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se meheyi ɛyáa yɔ, tɩhaɣ wezuu maatɛŋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, tɔm ndʋ́ man-Caa heyi-m yɔ, ńdʋ́ mɔyɔɔdʋʋ.».

eng: Before the Festival of Passover, Jesus knew that his time had come to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them fully.
local: Kifeŋaɣ se pɔtɔɔ Paska Kazandʋ. Ɛlɛ́, Yesu na se ɛ-alɩwaatʋ talaa se ɛkʋyɩ kedeŋa kanɛ kɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ewolo ɛ-Caa cɔlɔ. Ɛɛsɔɔlɩ ɛ-ñɩma mbá pɛwɛ kɔ-yɔɔ yɔ kámaɣ́ fɛyɩ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were sharing the evening meal. The devil had already provoked Judas, Simon Iscariots son, to betray Jesus.
local: Đanaɣ ɖanaa lɛ, ɛ nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa pɔtɔkɩ tɔɔnaɣ. Naze lɛ, Satanɩ tɛm ɖʋʋ Siimɔɔ Iskarɩyɔɔtɩ pɩyalʋ Yudaasɩ taa se ɛkpa-ɩ nɛ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ koyindinaa nesi taa.

eng: Jesus knew the Father had given everything into his hands and that he had come from God and was returning to God.
local: Yesu kɛtɛm naʋ se Caa Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ ɛlɩnaa nɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ ɛkɔŋ pɩsʋʋ. Ɛnawa ɖɔɖɔ se ɛɖʋzɩ e-nesi taa pɩtɩŋa payɩ.

eng: So he got up from the table and took off his robes. Picking up a linen towel, he tied it around his waist.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛkʋyɩ tɔɔnaɣ ɖɩtɔɔyɛ nɛ ɛhɔzɩ e-toko sɔsɔʋ nɛ ɛkpaɣ pɔpɔʋ ɛtɔɔlɩ.

eng: Then he poured water into a washbasin and began to wash the disciples feet, drying them with the towel he was wearing.
local: nɛ ɛɖʋ lɩm ñanaɣ sɔsɔɔ nakɛyɛ taa nɛ ɛpazɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa naŋgbanzɩ kɔʋ nɛ ɛhɛzɩɣnɩ pɔpɔʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛtɔɔlaa yɔ.

eng: When Jesus came to Simon Peter, Peter said to him, Lord, are you going to wash my feet.
local: Ɛtalɩ Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ña pʋwɛɛ se ŋkɔ ma-naŋgbanzɩ lɩm naa we?».

eng: Jesus replied, You dont understand what Im doing now, but you will understand later. .
local: Ecosi-i se: «Mbʋ́ malakɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, ŋŋnɩɣ pʋ-tɔbʋʋ. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwayɩ lɛ, ŋkɔŋ nɩʋ.».

eng: No! Peter said. You will never wash my feet! Jesus replied, Unless I wash you, you wont have a place with me. .
local: Pɩyɛ heyi-i se: «Pɩtatɩɩwɛʋ kpem se ŋkɔ-m ma-naŋgbanzɩ lɩm!» Yesu cosi-i se: «Yee mɔntɔkɔ-ŋ-sɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ma nɛ ŋ ɖɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɖɩkpɛndɩ pʋyʋ.».

eng: Simon Peter said, Lord, not only my feet but also my hands and my head.
local: Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ heyi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Yee ḿbʋ́ taakɔ ma-naŋgbanzɩ ɖeke. Ɛlɛ́, kɔ-m ɖɔɖɔ me-nesi nɛ ma-ñʋʋ!».

eng: Jesus responded, Those who have bathed need only to have their feet washed, because they are completely clean. You disciples are clean, but not every one of you. .
local: Yesu heyi-i se: «Weyi ɛtɛm lɩm sɔʋ yɔ, ɛɛtasɩɣ ñɩnʋʋ se ɛsɔ-pʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛ-naŋgbanzɩ ɖeke ɛkɔʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-yɔɔ wɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ. Mɩ mɔn-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lɛ, ɩwɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ, ɛlɛ́, pɩtɛkɛ mɩtɩŋa.».

eng: He knew who would betray him. Thats why he said, Not every one of you is clean. .
local: Yesu kɛtɛm sɩm weyi ɛkɔŋ-ɩ ɖʋzʋʋ koyindinaa nesi taa yɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ ɛtɔm se: «Pɩtɛkɛ mɩtɩŋa ɩwɛnɩ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ.».

eng: After he washed the disciples feet, he put on his robes and returned to his place at the table. He said to them, Do you know what Ive done for you?.
local: Ɛtɛm ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa naŋgbanzɩ kɔʋ lɛ, esuu e-toko nɛ ɛpɩsɩ ɛcaɣ tɔɔnaɣ ɖɩtɔɔyɛ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɩnɩ mbʋ́ malabɩ-mɩ yɔ pʋ-tɔbʋʋ naa we?.

eng: You call me Teacher and Lord, and you speak correctly, because I am.
local: Ɩyaɣ-m se: ‹Wɩlɩyʋ› nɛ ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ.› Ɩwɛnɩ toovenim, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɛnkɛ ḿbʋ́.

eng: If I, your Lord and teacher, have washed your feet, you too must wash each others feet.
local: Yee weyi ma mɛnkɛ mɩ-Kɩpaɣlʋ nɛ mɩ-Wɩlɩyʋ yɔ, mɔnkɔ mɩ-naŋgbanzɩ lɩm yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se mɩ ɖɔɖɔ, ɩkɔ ɖama naŋgbanzɩ.

eng: I have given you an example: Just as I have done, you also must do.
local: Mawɩlɩ-mɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se ɩla yɔ se ɩpɩzɩ ɩla ɛzɩ malabʋ-mɩ yɔ.

eng: I assure you, servants arent greater than their master, nor are those who are sent greater than the one who sent them.
local: Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: tʋmlaɖʋ nɔɔyʋ takɩlɩ ɛ-caa, tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ takɩlɩ weyi etiyi-i yɔ.

eng: Since you know these things, you will be happy if you do them.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɩsɩm mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se ɩla yɔ. Yee ɩñɩtaa ɩlabɩ-pʋ yɔ, ihikiɣ mɩ-tɩ!.

eng: Im not speaking about all of you. I know those whom Ive chosen. But this is to fulfill the scripture, The one who eats my bread has turned against me.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ mɩtɩŋa mɔyɔɔdʋʋ, mansɩm mbá malɩzaa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ, tɩla. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Weyi ma nɛ ɩ ɖɩtɔkaɣ yɔ, ɛpɩsaa ɛkʋyʋʋ mɔ-yɔɔ.›.

eng: Im telling you this now, before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe that I Am.
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ nɛ pʋcɔ tɩla. Ḿbʋ́ yekina nɛ itisi alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ tɩlakɩ yɔ se: ‹Ma lɛ weyi mɛnkɛnaa yɔ.›.

eng: I assure you that whoever receives someone I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: weyi ɛmʋʋ weyi mentiyi-i yɔ, ɛmʋʋ-m. Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ mʋ́ʋ-m yɔ, ɛmʋʋ weyi etiyi-m yɔ.».

eng: After he said these things, Jesus was deeply disturbed and testified, I assure you, one of you will betray me. .
local: Yesu tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ-laŋɩyɛ wɛɛkɩ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ kɔŋ-m kpaʋ nɛ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ koyindinaa nesi taa.».

eng: His disciples looked at each other, confused about which of them he was talking about.
local: Peeɖe papazɩ ɖama ɛsɛ taa cɔnaʋ nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ ɖama se: «Ɛzɩ anɩ ɛtɔŋ?».

eng: One of the disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was at Jesus side.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, pa-taa weyi Yesu kɩlɩ sɔɔlʋʋ yɔ, ɛɛwɛlɩ hʋyʋʋ yɔɔ ɛ-cɔlɔ tɔɔnaɣ ɖɩtɔɔyɛ.

eng: Simon Peter nodded at him to get him to ask Jesus who he was talking about.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ hadɩ-ɩ nesi nɛ eheyi-i se: «Pɔzɩ-ɩ se anɩ ɛtɔŋ?».

eng: Leaning back toward Jesus, this disciple asked, Lord, who is it.
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ tɛzɩ ɛ-tɩ wɛlʋʋ nɛ Yesu yɔɔ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Anɩ ŋtɔŋ?».

eng: Dont be troubled. Trust in God. Trust also in me.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tasɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa heyuu se: «Mɩ-laŋa ɛtaawɛɛkɩ, ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, ɩwɛnɩ-kʋ ɖɔɖɔ mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, Its the one to whom I will give this piece of bread once I have dipped into the bowl. Then he dipped the piece of bread and gave it to Judas, Simon Iscariots son.
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Ɛnʋ lɛ weyi malɩzɩɣ tindine hɔɔlaɣ melii num nɛ mɛncɛlɩ-ɩ yɔ.» Peeɖe ɛkpaɣ tindine hɔɔlaɣ elii num nɛ ɛcɛlɩ Siimɔɔ Iskarɩyɔɔtɩ pɩyalʋ Yudaasɩ.

eng: My Fathers house has room to spare. If that werent the case, would I have told you that Im going to prepare a place for you?.
local: Đɩjaɣyɛ sakɩyɛ wɛ man-Caa ɖɩɣa taa. Mowoki mɔñɔɔzɩ-mɩ naɖɩyɛ. Pɩtaakɛ toovenim yɔ, mantaaheyi-mɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: After Judas took the bread, Satan entered into him. Jesus told him, What you are about to do, do quickly. .
local: Ɛtɩmʋ-kɛ lɛ, eke Satanɩ. Peeɖe Yesu heyi-i se: «La mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se ŋla yɔ lɔŋ!».

eng: When I go to prepare a place for you, I will return and take you to be with me so that where I am you will be too.
local: Mɛntɛŋ-mɩ-ɖɩ ñɔɔzʋʋ lɛ, manpɩsɩ mɔnkɔɔ mankpaɣ-mɩ nɛ ma nɛ mɩ ɖiwolo ɖenɖe mɛwɛɛ yɔ nɛ mɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɩwɛɛ peeɖe.

eng: No one sitting at the table understood why Jesus said this to him.
local: Mbá paawɛ tɔɔnaɣ ɖɩtɔɔyɛ peeɖe yɔ, pa-taa nɔɔyʋ tana mbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ eheyaɣ Yudaasɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ.

eng: You know the way to the place Im going. .
local: Ɩsɩm nʋmɔʋ ŋgʋ́ kuwoki ɖenɖe mowoki yɔ.».

eng: Some thought that, since Judas kept the money bag, Jesus told him, Go, buy what we need for the feast, or that he should give something to the poor.
local: Yudaasɩ ɖɔkaɣnɩ pe-liidiye huyuu. Pʋ-yɔɔ pa-taa nabɛyɛ maɣzaɣ se Yesu heyaɣ-ɩ se ewolo ɛya kazandʋ wondu ndʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ-wɛ yɔ. Lɛlaa ñamaɣzaɣ se yaa eheyaɣ-ɩ se ɛlɩzɩ nabʋyʋ nɛ ɛha kʋñɔndɩnaa.

eng: Thomas asked, Lord, we dont know where you are going. How can we know the way.
local: Peeɖe Tomaa cosi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Đɩtasɩ ɖenɖe ŋwoki yɔ. Ɛzɩma ɖɩpɩzɩɣ ɖɩtɩlɩ peeɖe nʋmɔʋ yɔ?».

eng: I am the true vine, and my Father is the vineyard keeper.
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Ma lɛ tɩʋ ŋgʋ́ popuu ki-pee sʋlʋm yɔ kɩ-maɣmaɣ nɛ man-Caa lɛ kʋ-tʋ.

eng: So when Judas took the bread, he left immediately. And it was night.
local: Yudaasɩ mʋ tindine hɔɔlaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛtɩkʋyɩ ɛlɩɩ. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, ɖoó koyuwa.

eng: Jesus answered, I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-i se: «Ma lɛ nʋmɔʋ nɛ toovenim nɛ wezuu. Nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ se ewoki Caa cɔlɔ nɛ ɛtatɩŋnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: He removes any of my branches that dont produce fruit, and he trims any branch that produces fruit so that it will produce even more fruit.
local: Ɛsɛtɩɣ pilinzi nzɩ́ sɩwɛ mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ sɩɩlʋlʋʋ yɔ nɛ piyele ɛñɔɔzɩ nzɩ́ sɩlʋlʋʋ yɔ camɩyɛ nɛ sɩtɛzɩ lʋlʋʋ.

eng: When Judas was gone, Jesus said, Now the Human One has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.
local: Yudaasɩ lɩwa lɛ, Yesu yɔɔdɩ se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ hɩɖɛ sɛwa nɛ pɩtɩŋnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩnɖɛ sɛɛ.

eng: If you have really known me, you will also know the Father. From now on you know him and have seen him. .
local: Yee ɩsɩm-m yɔ, ɩsɩŋ ɖɔɖɔ man-Caa. Pɩkpaɣʋnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, ɩna-ɩ nɛ ɩsɩm-ɩ.».

eng: You are already trimmed because of the word I have spoken to you.
local: Tɔm ndʋ́ mawɩlɩ-mɩ yɔ, tɩtɛm-mɩ ñɔɔzʋʋ nɛ ɩwɛɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ.

eng: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify the Human One in himself and will glorify him immediately.
local: Yee pɩtɩŋnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ sɛɛ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ ñeyeki nɛ pɩtɩŋnɩ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛnʋ ñɩnɖɛ ñɛsɛɛ. Pɩkazɩ alɩwaatʋ pazɩ, ɛlakɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: Philip said, Lord, show us the Father; that will be enough for us.
local: Peeɖe Filiipu heyi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Yee ŋwɩlɩ-ɖʋ Caa yɔ, pʋɖɔ-ɖʋ.».

eng: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. A branch cant produce fruit by itself, but must remain in the vine. Likewise, you cant produce fruit unless you remain in me.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩkpɛndɩ ɖama ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɖɩwɛɛ tam. Yee piliŋa ɛwɛɛ nɛ kɛfɛyɩ tɩʋ yɔɔ yɔ, kaapɩzɩɣ kalʋlɩ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛnɩ-mɩ, yee ɩtɛkpɛndɩ mɔ-yɔɔ ḿbʋ́ tam yɔ, ɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɩwɛnɩ wazaɣ.

eng: Little children, Im with you for a little while longer. You will look for me—but, just as I told the Jewish leaders, I also tell you now—Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Men-piya! Ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩtasɩɣ caɣʋ pazɩ yem nɛ pʋcɔ mɛnɖɛɛ. Ɩkɔŋ-m ñɩnʋʋ, ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ meheyiɣ-mɩ tɔm ndʋ́ meheyi Yuda ñɩma sɔsaa yɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ɩɩpɩzɩɣ iwolo ɖenɖe mowoki yɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont you know me, Philip, even after I have been with you all this time? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, Show us the Father?.
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Filiipu! Đooo ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩwɛʋ yɔ, ŋtasɩm-m yaa we? Weyi ɛna-m yɔ, ɛna Caa. Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋtɔŋ se mawɩlɩ-mɩ Caa yɔ?.

eng: I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, then you will produce much fruit. Without me, you cant do anything.
local: Ma lɛ tɩʋ ŋgʋ́ popuu ki-pee sʋlʋm yɔ nɛ mɩ lɛ pilinzi. Weyi ma nɛ ɩ ɖɩkpɛndɩ ɖama tam yɔ, ɛwɛɣ wazaɣ siŋŋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɩla pʋyʋ.

eng: I give you a new commandment: Love each other. Just as I have loved you, so you also must love each other.
local: Manɖʋʋ-mɩ paɣtʋ kɩfatʋ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ɩsɔɔlɩ ɖama. Ɩsɔɔlɩ ɖama ɛzɩ mɔnsɔɔlʋʋ-mɩ yɔ.

eng: Dont you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I have spoken to you I dont speak on my own. The Father who dwells in me does his works.
local: Ŋtetisi se ma nɛ Caa ɖɩwɛ ɖama taa yaa we? Tɔm ndʋ́ meheyiɣ-mɩ yɔ, tɩtalɩɩnɩ mɔn-cɔlɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Caa wɛ mɔn-cɔlɔ, ɛnʋ lakɩnɩ ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ.

eng: If you dont remain in me, you will be like a branch that is thrown out and dries up. Those branches are gathered up, thrown into a fire, and burned.
local: Weyi ɛtɛkpɛndɩ mɔ-yɔɔ tam yɔ, pɔlɔ-ɩ nɛ awayɩ ɛzɩ piliŋa yɔ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ. Pilinzi nzɩ́ sɩwɩlaa yɔ, pɔtɔzʋʋ-sɩ nɛ paɖʋ miŋ taa nɛ sɩñaɣ.

eng: This is how everyone will know that you are my disciples, when you love each other. .
local: Yee ɩsɔɔlɩ ɖama yɔ, pʋbʋ ɛyáa tɩŋa naɣ se ɩkɛ mɔn-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa.».

eng: Trust me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or at least believe on account of the works themselves.
local: Itisi se ma nɛ Caa ɖɩwɛ ɖama taa. Yee iitisiɣ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, tʋma wena malakɩ yɔ a-yɔɔ lɛ, itisi-tʋ.

eng: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask for whatever you want and it will be done for you.
local: Yee ɩkpɛndɩ mɔ-yɔɔ tam nɛ ɩɖɔŋ mɔn-tɔm yɔɔ yɔ, paa ɛbɛ mi-liu ɛwɛɛ nɛ ɩpɔzɩ yɔ, pahaɣ-mɩ-pʋ.

eng: Simon Peter said to Jesus, Lord, where are you going? Jesus answered, Where I am going, you cant follow me now, but you will follow later. .
local: Yesu yɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Le ŋwoki?» Ecosi-i se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ŋŋpɩzɩɣ ŋtɩŋ-m ɖenɖe mowoki yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwayɩ ŋkɔŋ-m tɩŋʋʋ.».

eng: I assure you that whoever believes in me will do the works that I do. They will do even greater works than these because I am going to the Father.
local: Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: weyi ɛtaɣ-m liu yɔ, ɛlakɩ ɖɔɖɔ tʋma wena malakɩ yɔ. Halɩ ɛlakɩ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mowoki mɛwɛɛ man-Caa cɔlɔ.

eng: My Father is glorified when you produce much fruit and in this way prove that you are my disciples.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩwɛɣ wazaɣ siŋŋ nɛ ɩwɩlɩɣ se ɩkɛ mɔn-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa yɔ, man-Caa hɩɖɛ sɛɣ.

eng: Peter asked, Lord, why cant I follow you now? Ill give up my life for you. .
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ maapɩzɩɣ mantɩŋ-ŋ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ? Paa sɩm yɔ, mansɩkɩ ñɔ-yɔɔ!».

eng: I will do whatever you ask for in my name, so that the Father can be glorified in the Son.
local: Mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩpɔzʋʋ-ɩ nɛ mɔ-nɔɔ yɔ, malakɩ-pʋ se pɩtɩŋnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ man-Caa hɩɖɛ sɛɛ.

eng: As the Father loved me, I too have loved you. Remain in my love.
local: Mɔnsɔɔlɩ-mɩ ɛzɩ Caa sɔɔlʋʋ-m yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, iyele nɛ mɔnsɔɔlɩ-mɩ tam.

eng: Jesus replied, Will you give up your life for me? I assure you that you will deny me three times before the rooster crows.
local: Yesu pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Paa sɩm, ŋsɩkɩ mɔ-yɔɔ yaa? Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-ŋ se: pʋcɔ kalɩmɔʋ koo lɛ, ŋkpɛzɩɣ tam naadozo se ŋtasɩm-m.».

eng: When you ask me for anything in my name, I will do it.
local: Mantasɩɣ-mɩ heyuu se: yee ɩpɔzɩ-m pʋyʋ nɛ mɔ-nɔɔ yɔ, malakɩ-mɩ-pʋ.».

eng: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I kept my Fathers commandments and remain in his love.
local: Yee ɩɖɔŋ man-paɣtʋ yɔɔ yɔ, mɔnsɔɔlʋʋ-mɩ tam ɛzɩ mɔnɖɔm man-Caa ñɩndʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ sɔɔlɩ-m tam yɔ.

eng: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Yee ɩsɔɔlɩ-m yɔ, ɩɖɔŋ man-paɣtʋ yɔɔ.

eng: I have said these things to you so that my joy will be in you and your joy will be complete.
local: Meheyi-mɩ ḿbʋ́ se mɩ-taa ɛhʋlʋmɩ ɛzɩ ma yɔ nɛ mɩ-taa tɛzɩ hʋlʋmʋʋ.

eng: I will ask the Father, and he will send another Companion, who will be with you forever.
local: Ma lɛ, mankaɣ tenduu Caa se ɛtasɩ-mɩ tiyinaʋ Sɩnɩyʋ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɛwɛɛ mɩ-cɔlɔ tam:.

eng: This is my commandment: love each other just as I have loved you.
local: Paɣtʋ ndʋ́ manɖʋʋ-mɩ yɔ lɛ se: ɩsɔɔlɩ ɖama ɛzɩ mɔnsɔɔlʋʋ-mɩ yɔ.

eng: This Companion is the Spirit of Truth, whom the world cant receive because it neither sees him nor recognizes him. You know him, because he lives with you and will be with you.
local: ɛnʋ lɛ toovenim Fezuu. Mbá paañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ, paapɩzɩɣ pamʋ-ɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paapɩzɩɣ pana-ɩ yaa pasɩm-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́ mɩ yɔ, mɩsɩŋ-ɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛwɛɣ mɩ-cɔlɔ nɛ mɩ nɛ ɩ ɩwɛɛ ɖama taa.

eng: No one has greater love than to give up ones life for ones friends.
local: Weyi etisiɣ nɛ ɛsɩ ɛ-ɛgbaɣdɩnaa yɔɔ yɔ, ɛsɔɔlɩ-wɛ siŋŋ. Sɔɔlɩm nabʋyʋ fɛyɩ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: I wont leave you as orphans. I will come to you.
local: Maayeki-mɩ mi-ɖeke ɛzɩ solisi yɔ, mankaɣ pɩsʋʋ mɩ-cɔlɔ.

eng: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
local: Yee ɩlakɩ mbʋ́ manɖʋʋ-mɩ yɔ yɔ, ɩkɛɣ mɛ-ɛgbaɣdɩnaa.

eng: Soon the world will no longer see me, but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too.
local: Pɩkazɩ pazɩ, mbá paañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ, paatasɩɣ-m naʋ. Ɛlɛ́ mɩ yɔ, ɩkɔŋ-m naʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɛwɛ wezuu nɛ mɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɩkaɣ wɛʋ wezuu.

eng: I dont call you servants any longer, because servants dont know what their master is doing. Instead, I call you friends, because everything I heard from my Father I have made known to you.
local: Maatasɩɣ-mɩ yaʋ se man-tʋmlaɖaa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tʋmlaɖʋ tasɩ mbʋ́ ɛ-caa lakɩ yɔ. Mayaɣ-mɩ se mɛ-ɛgbaɣdɩnaa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɛnkpɛlɩkɩ-mɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ man-Caa wɩlɩ-m yɔ.

eng: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, you are in me, and I am in you.
local: Puwiye ńɖɩ́ ɩtɩlɩɣ se ma nɛ man-Caa ɖɩwɛ ɖama taa nɛ ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩwɛɛ ɖama taa ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: You didnt choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you could go and produce fruit and so that your fruit could last. As a result, whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give you.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ mɩlɩzɩnɩ-m, ɛlɛ́, ma malɩzɩnɩ-mɩ nɛ mentiyi-mɩ se iwolo ɩla tʋmɩyɛ nɛ ɩwɛnɩ wazaɣ nɛ kɛwɛɛ ḿbʋ́. Peeɖe man-Caa haɣ-mɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩpɔzʋʋ-ɩ nɛ mɔ-nɔɔ yɔ.

eng: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them loves me. Whoever loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them. .
local: Weyi ɛɖɔkʋʋ man-paɣtʋ nɛ ɛɖɔ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ sɔɔlɩnɩ-m. Man-Caa sɔɔlʋʋ weyi ɛsɔɔlɩ-m yɔ. Ma ɖɔɖɔ mɔnsɔɔlʋʋ-ɩ nɛ malɩzɩ man-tɩ mawɩlɩ-ɩ.».

eng: I give you these commandments so that you can love each other.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mbʋ́ manɖʋʋ-mɩ yɔ lɛ se: ɩsɔɔlɩ ɖama.».

eng: Judas asked, Lord, why are you about to reveal yourself to us and not to the world.
local: Yudaasɩ kɛwɛ peeɖe, ɛlɛ́, pɩtɛkɛ Iskarɩyɔɔtɩ pɩyalʋ. Ɛ́lɛ́ pɔzɩnɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ɖa pʋwɛɛ se ŋlɩzɩ ña-tɩ nɛ ŋwɩlɩ, pɩtɛkɛ kedeŋa ñɩma. Se we nɛ ŋlakɩ ḿbʋ́?».

eng: If the world hates you, know that it hated me first.
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Yee kedeŋa ñɩma ɛpaɖɩɣ-mɩ yɔ, ɩtɩlɩ se papaɖɩ-m nɛ pʋcɔ pɩtalɩɣnɩ-mɩ.

eng: Jesus answered, Whoever loves me will keep my word. My Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
local: Yesu cosi-i se: «Weyi ɛsɔɔlɩ-m yɔ, ɛɖɔŋ mɔn-tɔm yɔɔ. Man-Caa sɔɔlʋʋ-ɩ nɛ ma nɛ ɩ ɖiwolo ɖɩwɛɛ pʋdʋ tɛ.

eng: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. However, I have chosen you out of the world, and you dont belong to the world. This is why the world hates you.
local: Yee ɩɩkɛ pa-taa ñɩma yɔ, paasɔɔlɩ-mɩ se ɩkɛ pa-ñɩma. Ɛlɛ́, malɩzɩ-mɩ pa-taa nɛ mɩ nɛ wɛ ɩɩyaɣ ɖama pʋyʋ, pʋ-yɔɔ papaɖɩɣ-mɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: Whoever doesnt love me doesnt keep my words. The word that you hear isnt mine. It is the word of the Father who sent me.
local: Weyi ɛtɔsɔɔlɩ-m yɔ, ɛɛɖɔŋ mɔn-tɔm yɔɔ. Ndʋ́ meheyiɣ-mɩ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, tɩtalɩnɩ mɔn-cɔlɔ, ɛlɛ́, man-Caa cɔlɔ tɩlɩnaa. Ɛnʋ tiyini-m.

eng: Remember what I told you, Servants arent greater than their master. If the world harassed me, it will harass you too. If it kept my word, it will also keep yours.
local: Ɩtɔzɩ tɔm ndʋ́ meheyi-mɩ yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Tʋmlaɖʋ nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ se ɛkɩlɩɣ ɛ-caa.› Yee ɛyáa ɩwɛ ma-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ yɔ, pɔkɔŋ mɩ-wayɩ wɛʋ ɖɔɖɔ ḿbʋ́. Yee pɔɖɔm mɔn-tɔm yɔɔ yɔ, pɔkɔŋ ɖɔm ɖɔɖɔ mɩ-ñɩndʋ yɔɔ.

eng: I have spoken these things to you while I am with you.
local: Ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩwɛ alɩwaatʋ tʋnɛ yɔɔ meheyiɣ-mɩ-tʋ.

eng: The world will do all these things to you on account of my name, because it doesnt know the one who sent me.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mɔ-yɔɔ pɔkɔŋ-mɩ labʋ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ḿbʋ́. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, patasɩ weyi etiyi-m yɔ.

eng: The Companion, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I told you.
local: Sɩnɩyʋ ɛnʋ lɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu. Ŋ́gʋ́ Caa kɔŋ-mɩ tiyinaʋ nɛ mɔ-nɔɔ. Kɩkɔŋ-mɩ wɩlʋʋ pɩtɩŋa nɛ kɩtɔzɩ-mɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ meheyi-mɩ yɔ.

eng: If I hadnt come and spoken to the people of this world, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
local: Yee mantaakɔɔ nɛ mɔyɔɔdɩnɩ-wɛ yɔ, pataakʋ pɔ-tɔm se palakɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm. Ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, pɛfɛyɩnɩ natʋyʋ se paaɖezini pa-tɩ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ.

eng: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give you. I give to you not as the world gives. Dont be troubled or afraid.
local: Meyeki nɛ ɩwɛɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa, mɛhɛzɩɣnɩ mɩ-laŋa. Maahɛzɩɣ-yɛ ɛzɩ kedeŋa ñɩma hɛzʋʋ yɔ. Mɩ-laŋa ɛtaawɛɛkɩ, sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-mɩ.

eng: Whoever hates me also hates the Father.
local: Weyi ɛpaɖɩɣ-m yɔ, ɛpaɖɩɣ ɖɔɖɔ man-Caa.

eng: You have heard me tell you, Im going away and returning to you. If you loved me, you would be happy that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than me.
local: Meheyi-mɩ nɛ ɩnɩɩ se mowoki nɛ menkiti mɔnkɔɔ mɩ-cɔlɔ. Yee ɩɩsɔɔlɩ-m yɔ, mɩ-taa kahʋlʋmɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩnɩwa se mowoki Caa cɔlɔ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛkɩlɩ-m.

eng: If I hadnt done works among them that no one else had done, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
local: Yee mantaala tʋma wena nɔɔyʋ tala pɛ-hɛkʋ taa kaʋ yɔ yɔ, pataakʋ pɔ-tɔm. Ŋgʋ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ pana man-tʋma, ɛlɛ́, papaɖɩɣ ma nɛ man-Caa.

eng: I have told you before it happens so that when it happens you will believe.
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ nɛ pʋcɔ tɩtalɩ. Meheyiɣ-mɩ-tʋ se yee tɩtalaa lɛ, itisi-tʋ.

eng: This fulfills the word written in their Law, They hated me without a reason.
local: Pɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́ se tɔm ndʋ́ pama pa-paɣtʋ takayaɣ taa yɔ, tɩla. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Papaɖɩ-m ɖulum yem.›.

eng: I wont say much more to you because this worlds ruler is coming. He has nothing on me.
local: Maatasɩɣ-mɩ yɔɔdɩnaʋ nɛ pʋɖɔɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kedeŋa wiyaʋ ɛzaɣ́ʋ alɩwaatʋ talaa. Ɛfɛyɩnɩ ɖoŋ nɩɩyɩ mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: When the Companion comes, whom I will send from the Father—the Spirit of Truth who proceeds from the Father—he will testify about me.
local: Sɩnɩyʋ kaɣ kɔm: ɛnʋ lɛ toovenim Fezuu ŋgʋ́ kɩlɩɣnɩ Caa cɔlɔ yɔ. Caa cɛlɩɣnɩ-m-kʋ nɛ mentiyini-mɩ, ŋ́gʋ́ kɔŋnɩ lɩzʋʋ ma-aseɣɖe.

eng: Rather, he comes so that the world will know that I love the Father and do just as the Father has commanded me. Get up. Were leaving this place.
local: Ɛlɛ́, malakɩ ɛzɩ Caa heyuu-m yɔ se kedeŋa ñɩma ɩtɩlɩ se mɔnsɔɔlɩ-ɩ. Ɩkʋyɩ cɩnɛ nɛ ɖɩɖɛɛ!».

eng: You will testify too, because you have been with me from the beginning.
local: Mɩ ɖɔɖɔ lɛ, ɩkɔŋ lɩzʋʋ ma-aseɣɖe, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩɩwɛna ɖooo ɛzɩ manpazʋʋ man-tʋmɩyɛ labʋ yɔ.

eng: I have said these things to you so that you wont fall away.
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ ḿbʋ́ se ɩtaatɔlɩ lidaʋ nʋmɔʋ taa.

eng: They will expel you from the synagogue. The time is coming when those who kill you will think that they are doing a service to God.
local: Pɔkɔŋ-mɩ ɖaŋʋʋ se ɩtaatasɩ wobu Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ naɖɩyɛ taa. Halɩ alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ kɔŋ nɛ mbá pakʋʋ-mɩ yɔ, pamaɣzɩ se Ɛsɔ tʋmɩyɛ palakɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: They will do these things because they dont know the Father or me.
local: Pɔkɔŋ labʋ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, patasɩ Caa yaa ma.

eng: But I have said these things to you so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I didnt say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
local: Ɛlɛ́, meheyi-mɩ ḿbʋ́ se yee pɔkɔm palakɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɩtɔzɩ se maatɛm-mɩ-tʋ heyuu. Mantaaheyi-mɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ ɖooo ɛzɩ manpazʋʋ man-tʋmɩyɛ labʋ yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩɩwɛna.».

eng: But now I go away to the one who sent me. None of you ask me, Where are you going.
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, mowoki weyi etiyi-m yɔ ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɔzʋʋ-m se: ‹Le ŋwoki?›.

eng: Yet because I have said these things to you, you are filled with sorrow.
local: Meheyi-mɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔɔ mɩ-laŋa wɛɛkaa pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he looked up to heaven and said, Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, so that the Son can glorify you.
local: Yesu tɛm yɔɔdɩnaʋ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lɛ, ɛkpazɩ ɛsɛ ɛsɔdaa nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Caa! Alɩwaatʋ talaa. Yele nɛ ña-Pɩyalʋ ma, ma-hɩɖɛ sɛɛ nɛ meyele ɖɔɖɔ ña-ñɩnɖɛ sɛɛ.

eng: I assure you that it is better for you that I go away. If I dont go away, the Companion wont come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, meheyiɣ-mɩ toovenim se: yee mɛnɖɛwa yɔ, pɩkɩlɩɣ-mɩ sɛʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɛntɛɖɛɛ yɔ, Sɩnɩyʋ ɛɛkɔŋ mɩ-cɔlɔ. Ɛlɛ́, yee mɛnɖɛwa yɔ, mentiyiɣni-mɩ-ɩ.

eng: You gave him authority over everyone so that he could give eternal life to everyone you gave him.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ŋha-m waɖɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa yɔɔ se maha mbá patɩŋa payɩ ŋɖʋzɩ me-nesi taa yɔ wezuu maatɛŋ.

eng: When he comes, he will show the world it was wrong about sin, righteousness, and judgment.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛkɔŋ yɔ, ɛwɩlɩɣ kedeŋa ñɩma mbʋ́ pɩkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ hʋʋ tɔm yɔ.

eng: This is eternal life: to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you sent.
local: Ŋgʋ́, wezuu maatɛŋ lɛ se patɩlɩ weyi ña ñe-ɖeke koŋ lɛ Ɛsɔ siŋŋ yɔ nɛ ma Yesu Krɩstʋ weyi ŋtiyaa yɔ.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about sin because they dont believe in me.
local: Ɛwɩlɩɣ-wɛ mbʋ́ pɩkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ, ḿbʋ́ lɛ se pɛfɛyɩnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: I have glorified you on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do.
local: Meyeba nɛ ña-hɩɖɛ sɛɛ tataa cɩnɛ nɛ mɛntɛzɩ labʋ tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ŋɖʋ-m yɔ.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about righteousness because Im going to the Father and you wont see me anymore.
local: Ɛwɩlɩɣ-wɛ mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ, ḿbʋ́ lɛ se mowoki Caa cɔlɔ nɛ ɩɩtasɩɣ-m naʋ.

eng: Now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I shared with you before the world was created.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, Caa! Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, mɔnpɔzʋʋ-ŋ se ŋyele nɛ ma-hɩɖɛ sɛɛ ñɔ-cɔlɔ ɛzɩ ɖɩɩsɛʋ nɛ pʋcɔ ŋla kedeŋa yɔ.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about judgment because this worlds ruler stands condemned.
local: Ɛwɩlɩɣ-wɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ hʋʋ tɔm yɔ, ḿbʋ́ lɛ se ɛtɛm hʋnaʋ kedeŋa wiyaʋ.

eng: I have revealed your name to the people you gave me from this world. They were yours and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
local: Meyeba nɛ ɛyáa mbá ŋlɩzɩ kedeŋa kanɛ kɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ŋɖʋzɩ me-nesi taa yɔ, pasɩm-ŋ. Ñaatɩnɩ-wɛ nɛ ŋɖʋzɩ-wɛ me-nesi taa nɛ pɔɖɔŋ ñɔ-tɔm yɔɔ.

eng: I have much more to say to you, but you cant handle it now.
local: Mantasɩ wɛnaʋ tɔm sakɩyɛ se meheyiɣ-mɩ, ɛlɛ́, ɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɩnɩɩ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ.

eng: Now they know that everything you have given me comes from you.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, panawa se mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ŋɖʋzɩ me-nesi taa yɔ, ñɔ-cɔlɔ pɩlɩnaa.

eng: However, when the Spirit of Truth comes, he will guide you in all truth. He wont speak on his own, but will say whatever he hears and will proclaim to you what is to come.
local: Toovenim Fezuu ŋ́gʋ́, kʋkɔŋ lɛ, kɩwɩlɩ-mɩ toovenim nʋmɔʋ tɩŋa. Pɩtɛkɛ ki-ɖoŋ ɖeke kʋyɔɔdʋʋna. Ɛlɛ́, ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ kɩnɩɣ yɔ, kɩkɛdɩɣ-mɩ nɛ kiheyi-mɩ mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ labʋ yɔ.

eng: This is because I gave them the words that you gave me, and they received them. They truly understood that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, meheyi-wɛ tɔm ndʋ́ ŋheyi-m yɔ nɛ petisi-tʋ. Pana toovenim se ñɔ-cɔlɔ malɩnaa nɛ petisi se ñetiyini-m.

eng: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and proclaim it to you.
local: Kʋkɔŋ yebu nɛ ma-hɩɖɛ sɛɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɔm ndʋ́ mɔyɔɔdaa yɔ, ńdʋ́ kʋkɔŋ-mɩ heyuu.

eng: Im praying for them. Im not praying for the world but for those you gave me, because they are yours.
local: Mentendiɣ-ŋ pɔ-yɔɔ. Maatendiɣ-ŋ kedeŋa ñɩma yɔɔ, ɛlɛ́, mbá ŋɖʋzɩ me-nesi taa yɔ, pɔ-yɔɔ mentendiɣ-ŋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ñatɩnɩ-wɛ.

eng: Everything that the Father has is mine. Thats why I said that the Spirit takes what is mine and will proclaim it to you.
local: Mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ Caa wɛna yɔ, pɩkɛ ma-ñɩm. Pʋ-yɔɔ mɔntɔm se tɔm ndʋ́ mɔyɔɔdaa yɔ, ńdʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu kɔŋ-mɩ heyuu.».

eng: Everything that is mine is yours and everything that is yours is mine; I have been glorified in them.
local: Mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ mɛwɛna yɔ, ñatɩnɩ-pʋ nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ña ñɛwɛna yɔ, mantɩnɩ-pʋ. Ḿbá yekina nɛ ma-hɩɖɛ sɛɛ.

eng: Soon you wont be able to see me; soon after that, you will see me. .
local: Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Pɩkazɩ pazɩ, ɩɩtasɩɣ-m naʋ nɛ pɩtasɩ labʋ pazɩ lɛ, ɩtasɩ-m naʋ.».

eng: Im no longer in the world, but they are in the world, even as Im coming to you. Holy Father, watch over them in your name, the name you gave me, that they will be one just as we are one.
local: Maatasɩɣ wɛʋ kedeŋa yɔɔ, ɛlɛ́, ḿbá ñɛwɛ kɔ-yɔɔ. Ma lɛ, mɔnkɔŋ ñɔ-cɔlɔ. Ɛsɔ Caabiyuu! Ño-ɖoŋ taa lɛ, ɖɔkɩ-wɛ nɛ pala nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ ɛzɩ ma nɛ ŋ ɖɩlabʋ yɔ.

eng: Some of Jesus disciples said to each other, What does he mean: Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me and Because Im going to the Father?.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa lɛlaa pɔzaɣ ɖama se: «Tɔm ndʋ́ ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, tɩ-tɔbʋʋ se we? Nɛ ndʋ́ ɛtasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se ewoki Caa cɔlɔ yɔ, tʋtʋ tɔbʋʋ se we?.

eng: When I was with them, I watched over them in your name, the name you gave to me, and I kept them safe. None of them were lost, except the one who was destined for destruction, so that scripture would be fulfilled.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ma nɛ wɛ ɖɩɩwɛɛ yɔ, ño-ɖoŋ taa mɔncɔŋaɣnɩ mbá ŋɖʋzɩ me-nesi taa yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ: mohuuzi pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pa-taa nɔɔyʋ tele nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ weyi pɩɩwɛɛ se ele nɛ tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ, tɩla yɔ.

eng: What does he mean by soon? We dont understand what hes talking about.
local: Alɩwaatʋ pazɩ ndʋ́ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ tɩ-tɔm yɔ, tɩ-tɔbʋʋ se we? Đɩɩsɩŋ ndʋ́ ɛcaɣ yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ.».

eng: Now Im coming to you and I say these things while Im in the world so that they can share completely in my joy.
local: Ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ mɔnkɔŋ ñɔ-cɔlɔ. Mɛwɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ yɔɔ mɔyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ se pa-taa ɛtɛzɩ hʋlʋmʋʋ ɛzɩ ma yɔ.

eng: Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, so he said, Are you trying to find out from each other what I meant when I said, Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me?.
local: Yesu kanawa se pacaɣ-ɩ tɔm pɔzʋʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Mɔyɔɔdaa se: ‹Pɩkazɩ pazɩ ɩɩtasɩɣ-m naʋ nɛ pɩtasɩ labʋ pazɩ lɛ, ɩtasɩ-m naʋ› yɔɔ ɩwɛɛ ɩpɔzʋʋ ɖama tɔm naa we?.

eng: I gave your word to them and the world hated them, because they dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Mawɩlɩ-wɛ ñɔ-tɔm nɛ kedeŋa ñɩma paɖɩɣ-wɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pa nɛ wɛ patakaa lɩmaɣza ɛzɩ ma nɛ kedeŋa ñɩma ḿbá ɖɩwɛʋ nɛ ɖɩtakaa lɩmaɣza yɔ.

eng: I assure you that you will cry and lament, and the world will be happy. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
local: Toovenim lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: ɩkɔŋ wiluu nɛ ɩtɩmɩ. Ɛlɛ́, kedeŋa ñɩma laŋa hʋlʋmʋʋ. Mɩ-laŋa kɔŋ wɛɛkʋʋ, ɛlɛ́, mɩ-laŋwɛɛkɩyɛ ńɖɩ́, ɖɩkɔŋ pɩsʋʋ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ.

eng: Im not asking that you take them out of this world but that you keep them safe from the evil one.
local: Maatendiɣ-ŋ se ŋkʋsɩ-wɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ, ɛlɛ́, mentendiɣ-ŋ se ŋcɔnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ Satanɩ ɛtaalabɩ-wɛ pʋyʋ.

eng: When a woman gives birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when the child is born, she no longer remembers her distress because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ halʋ lʋlʋʋ yɔ, ɛ-laŋɩyɛ wɛɛkɩɣ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtalaa se ɛtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛtɛŋ lʋlʋʋ lɛ, ɛsɔɔ ɛ-kʋñɔŋ yɔɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-taa hʋlʋmaa se ɛlʋlɩ pɩɣa.

eng: They dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Pa nɛ kedeŋa ñɩma patakaa lɩmaɣza ɛzɩ ma nɛ wɛ ɖɩwɛʋ nɛ ɖɩtakaa lɩmaɣza yɔ.

eng: In the same way, you have sorrow now; but I will see you again, and you will be overjoyed. No one takes away your joy.
local: Mɩ ɖɔɖɔ lɛ, mɩ-laŋa wɛɛkɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mankaɣ-mɩ tasʋʋ naʋ nɛ ahʋlʋmɩ, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛwɛɛkɩ-yɛ ɖɔɖɔ kaʋ.

eng: Make them holy in the truth; your word is truth.
local: Yele se pɩtɩŋnɩ toovenim yɔɔ nɛ pɛkɛ ña-ñɩma pilim. Ñɔ-tɔm lɛ toovenim.

eng: In that day, you wont ask me anything. I assure you that the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
local: Puwiye ńɖɩ́ lɛ, ɩɩtasɩɣ-m pɔzʋʋ natʋyʋ. Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se: mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩsʋlʋmʋʋ Caa nɛ mɔ-nɔɔ yɔ, ɛhaɣ-mɩ-pʋ.

eng: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
local: Mentiyi-wɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ ñɩma hɛkʋ taa ɛzɩ ŋtiyuu-m kɔ-yɔɔ ñɩma hɛkʋ taa yɔ.

eng: Up to now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask and you will receive so that your joy will be complete.
local: Halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ ɩtatɩɩsʋlʋmɩda nabʋyʋ nɛ mɔ-nɔɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩsʋlʋmɩ-ɩ, ɛhaɣ-mɩ nɛ mɩ-laŋa tɛzɩ hʋlʋmʋʋ.».

eng: I made myself holy on their behalf so that they also would be made holy in the truth.
local: Pɔ-yɔɔ lɛ, mɛncɛlɩɣ-ŋ ma maɣmaɣ man-tɩ se pɩtɩŋnɩ toovenim yɔɔ nɛ ḿbá ɖɔɖɔ pɛkɛ ña-ñɩma.

eng: Ive been using figures of speech with you. The time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in such analogies. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
local: Yesu tasɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa heyuu se: «Meheyi-mɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩtɩŋa nɛ aduwa taa. Alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ kaɣ kɔm nɛ maatasɩɣ-mɩ yɔɔdɩnaʋ ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɛ́, meheyiɣ-mɩ Caa tɔm kpayɩ.

eng: Im not praying only for them but also for those who believe in me because of their word.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ mɔn-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa yɔɔ ɖeke mentendiɣ-ŋ. Ɛlɛ́, mentendiɣ-ŋ ɖɔɖɔ mbá pɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ḿbá pɔ-tɔm susuu yɔɔ nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: In that day you will ask in my name. Im not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
local: Pɩtalɩ puwiye ńɖɩ́ lɛ, ɩpɔzɩ Caa nɛ mɔ-nɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, maaheyiɣ-mɩ se mankaɣ-ɩ tenduu mɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: I pray they will be one, Father, just as you are in me and I am in you. I pray that they also will be in us, so that the world will believe that you sent me.
local: Mentendiɣ-ŋ se ŋyele nɛ patɩŋa payɩ pala nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ. Caa! Yele ɖa nɛ wɛ ɖɩwɛɛ ɖama taa ɛzɩ ma nɛ ŋ ɖɩwɛʋ ɖama taa yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ wɩlɩɣnɩ kedeŋa ñɩma se ñetiyini-m.

eng: The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and believed that I came from God.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛsɔɔlɩ-mɩ se ɩsɔɔlɩ-m nɛ ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ se ɛ-cɔlɔ malɩnaa.

eng: Ive given them the glory that you gave me so that they can be one just as we are one.
local: Maha-wɛ koboyaɣ ŋgá ŋha-m yɔ se pala nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ ɛzɩ ma nɛ ŋ ɖɩlabʋ yɔ.

eng: I left the Father and came into the world. I tell you again: I am leaving the world and returning to the Father. .
local: Caa cɔlɔ malɩnaa nɛ mɔnkɔɔ kedeŋa yɔɔ. Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, mankʋyʋʋ kɔ-yɔɔ nɛ manpɩsɩ ɛ-cɔlɔ.».

eng: Im in them and you are in me so that they will be made perfectly one. Then the world will know that you sent me and that you have loved them just as you loved me.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ma nɛ wɛ ɖɩwɛɣ ɖama taa nɛ ma nɛ ŋ ɖɩwɛɛ ɖama taa. Mentendiɣ-ŋ se ŋyele nɛ pala nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ siŋŋ nɛ kedeŋa ñɩma tɩlɩ se ñetiyini-m. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ŋsɔɔlɩ-wɛ ɛzɩ ŋsɔɔlʋʋ-m yɔ.

eng: His disciples said, See! Now you speak plainly; you arent using figures of speech.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa heyi-i se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ŋyɔɔdʋʋ kpayɩ, ŋŋtasɩɣ yɔɔdʋʋnɩ aduwa taa.

eng: Father, I want those you gave me to be with me where I am. Then they can see my glory, which you gave me because you loved me before the creation of the world.
local: Caa! Ñaɖʋzɩnɩ-wɛ me-nesi taa. Me-liu wɛɛ se pɛwɛɛ ɖenɖe mɔnkɔŋ wɛʋ yɔ nɛ pana mon-koboyaɣ. Ñahanɩ-m-kɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ŋŋsɔɔlɩ-m nɛ pʋcɔ ŋla kedeŋa.

eng: Now we know that you know everything and you dont need anyone to ask you. Because of this we believe you have come from God.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɖɩnawa se ŋsɩm pɩtɩŋa payɩ, pɩtacazɩ pɔzʋʋ se pɔpɔzɩ-ŋ natʋyʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ se Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ ŋlɩnaa.».

eng: Righteous Father, even the world didnt know you, but Ive known you, and these believers know that you sent me.
local: Caa kʋtʋʋzʋ! Keɖeŋa ñɩma tasɩm-ŋ. Ɛlɛ́, ma mansɩm-ŋ nɛ mbá ŋɖʋzɩ me-nesi taa yɔ, patɩlɩ se ñetiyini-m.

eng: Jesus replied, Now you believe?.
local: Peeɖe Yesu pɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɩtɔŋ se lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yaa?.

eng: Ive made your name known to them and will continue to make it known so that your love for me will be in them, and I myself will be in them. .
local: Meyeba nɛ pasɩm-ŋ, mɛwɛɣ pʋ-yɔɔ tam. Peeɖe pɔsɔɔlʋʋ ɖama ɛzɩ ŋsɔɔlʋʋ-m yɔ nɛ ma nɛ wɛ ɖɩwɛɛ ɖama taa.».

eng: Look! A time is coming—and is here!—when each of you will be scattered to your own homes and you will leave me alone. Im not really alone, for the Father is with me.
local: Tɔ! Alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ kɔŋ, halɩ tɩtɛm talʋʋ. Tɩ-taa ɩmɩzɩɣ ɖama nɛ paa weyi ekpe ɛ-tɛ nɛ iyele men-ɖeke. Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, piiyeki men-ɖeke koŋ, Caa wɛɣ mɔn-cɔlɔ.

eng: Ive said these things to you so that you will have peace in me. In the world you have distress. But be encouraged! I have conquered the world. .
local: Meheyi-mɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ se ɩwɛɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa ma nɛ mɩ ɖɛ-kpɛndʋʋ taa. Ɩkɔŋ nɩʋ siziŋ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ. Ɛlɛ́, ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ kpam! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɛntɛm kɔ-yɔɔ ɖoŋ tɩŋa wabʋ!».

eng: Then Pilate had Jesus taken and whipped.
local: Pɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Pilaatɩ ha nɔɔ nɛ pakpa Yesu nɛ polu-i agbazɛɛ.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus went out with his disciples and crossed over to the other side of the Kidron Valley. He and his disciples entered a garden there.
local: Yesu tɛm ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa yɔɔ Ɛsɔ tenduu ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ nɛ wɛ powolo pɛtɛzɩ lɔmʋʋ ŋgʋ́ payaɣ se Sedrɔɔ yɔ nɛ pasʋʋ kaɖaɣ ŋgá kɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ ka-taa.

eng: The soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple robe.
local: nɛ sɔɔjanaa lʋʋ awiya hulaɣ nɛ sɔɔ nɛ papam-ɩ. Pakpaɣ ɖɔɖɔ awiitoko kɩsɛmʋʋ nakʋyʋ waaa nɛ posuu-i.

eng: Judas, his betrayer, also knew the place because Jesus often gathered there with his disciples.
local: Yudaasɩ weyi ɛkɔŋ ɖʋzʋʋ Yesu koyindinaa nesi taa yɔ, ɛsɩm ɖɔɖɔ peeɖe. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Yesu nɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa powokaɣ peeɖe tam.

eng: Over and over they went up to him and said, Greetings, king of the Jews! And they slapped him in the face.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, powoki ɛ-ɛsɩndaa nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ! Sɛɛ ɖeu!» Nɛ pɛcɛkɩ-ɩ kadanzɩ.

eng: Judas brought a company of soldiers and some guards from the chief priests and Pharisees. They came there carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yudaasɩ kpɛndɩnɩ sɔɔjanaa tikaɣ nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyaa nɛ ewolo. Paaɖɔkɩ you wondu nɛ kanɖʋnaa nɛ miŋ nɛ koolonzi. Cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma paatiyini-wɛ.

eng: Pilate came out of the palace again and said to the Jewish leaders, Look! Im bringing him out to you to let you know that I find no grounds for a charge against him.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Pilaatɩ ɖaɣnɩ lɩʋ nɛ eheyi ɛyáa samaɣ se: «Iwelesi nɛ ɩnɩɩ, malɩzɩɣ-ɩ mɩ-cɔlɔ awayɩ nɛ pʋcɔ ɩtɩlɩ se maanaɣ kɩdɛkɛdɩm mbʋ́ ɛlaba nɛ pʋpɔzʋʋ se pakʋ ɛ-tɔm yɔ.».

eng: Jesus knew everything that was to happen to him, so he went out and asked, Who are you looking for? .
local: Yesu tɛm naʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋkɔŋ-ɩ labʋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ewobini pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Anɩ ɩñɩnɩɣ?».

eng: When Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe, Pilate said to them, Heres the man.
local: Yesu lɩwa lɛ, ɛpam sɔɔ hulaɣ nɛ esuu awiitoko kɩsɛmʋʋ waaa. Peeɖe Pilaatɩ heyi ɛyáa samaɣ se: «Ɛnʋ yɔ!».

eng: They answered, Jesus the Nazarene. He said to them, I Am. .
local: Pocosi-i se: «Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu.» Ɛlɛ́ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ma yɔ.» Pɩlakaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yudaasɩ ɛnʋ, ɛɛwɛ pɔ-cɔlɔ peeɖe.

eng: When the chief priests and their deputies saw him, they shouted out, Crucify, crucify! Pilate told them, You take him and crucify him. I dont find any grounds for a charge against him.
local: Cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyaa pana-ɩ lɛ, pamakɩ kobusi se: «Kam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ! Kam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ!» Ɛlɛ́, Pilaatɩ heyi-wɛ se: «Ɩkpaɣ-ɩ mɩ-maɣmaɣ nɛ iwoni-i ɩkam-ɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ma mantana mbʋ́ ɛwɛɛkaa nɛ pʋpɔzʋʋ se pakam-ɩ yɔ.».

eng: When he said, I Am, they shrank back and fell to the ground.
local: Yesu heyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pekitini wayɩ wayɩ nɛ pɔtɔlɩ tataa.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, We have a Law, and according to this Law he ought to die because he made himself out to be Gods Son.
local: Pocosi-i se: «Đɩwɛnɩ paɣtʋ natʋyʋ, tɩ-yɔɔ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛsɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛkaɖɩ kpayɩ se ɛnʋ lɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ.».

eng: He asked them again, Who are you looking for? They said, Jesus the Nazarene.
local: Peeɖe ɛtasɩ-wɛ pɔzʋʋ se: «Anɩ ɩñɩnɩɣ yee?» Pocosi se: «Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu.».

eng: When Pilate heard this word, he was even more afraid.
local: Pilaatɩ nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, sɔɔndʋ tɛzɩ-ɩ kpaʋ.

eng: Jesus answered, I told you, I Am. If you are looking for me, then let these people go. .
local: Yesu cosi-wɛ se: «Mɛntɛm-mɩ heyuu se ma yɔ. Yee toovenim se ma ɩñɩnɩɣ yɔ, iyele lɛlaa nɛ pɛɖɛɛ.».

eng: He went back into the residence and spoke to Jesus, Where are you from? Jesus didnt answer.
local: nɛ ɛpɩsɩ ɛsʋʋ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ Yesu se: «Ñɛ-tɛ le?» Ɛlɛ́, Yesu tocosi-i natʋyʋ.

eng: This was so that the word he had spoken might be fulfilled: I didnt lose anyone of those whom you gave me. .
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ se tɔm ndʋ́ ɛɛyɔɔdaa yɔ, tɩla. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Caa, mentelesi ɛyáa mbá ŋɖʋzɩ me-nesi taa yɔ pa-taa nɔɔyʋ.».

eng: So Pilate said, You wont speak to me? Dont you know that I have authority to release you and also to crucify you.
local: Peeɖe eheyi-i se: «Ŋŋcaɣ-m cosuu? Ŋtana se manpɩzɩɣ maha nɔɔ nɛ peyebi-ŋ yaa pakam-ŋ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ yaa we?».

eng: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ kɔɖɔkɩ sɩɣa kɩɖaɣlaɣ nakɛyɛ. Pɩlakaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛkpɛɛ-kɛ nɛ ɛsɛtɩ cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ tʋmlaɖʋ weyi payaɣ se Malcuusi yɔ ɛ-nɩwaŋ nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ.

eng: Jesus replied, You would have no authority over me if it had not been given to you from above. Thats why the one who handed me over to you has the greater sin. .
local: Yesu pɩsɩnɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋfɛyɩnɩ ɖoŋ nɩɩyɩ mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ weyi Ɛsɔ ha-ŋ nɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ weyi ɛkpa-m nɛ ɛkɔnɩ ñɔ-cɔlɔ yɔ, ɛ-kɩwɛɛkɩm kɩlɩ ña-ñɩmbʋ.».

eng: Jesus told Peter, Put your sword away! Am I not to drink the cup the Father has given me? .
local: Peeɖe Yesu heyi-i se: «Pɩsɩnɩ ña-sɩɣa ko-huyuu taa. Ŋmaɣzɩɣ se maatɔkɩ kʋñɔŋ ŋgʋ́ Caa ɖʋwa se mɔntɔɔ yɔ yaa we?».

eng: From that moment on, Pilate wanted to release Jesus. However, the Jewish leaders cried out, saying, If you release this man, you arent a friend of the emperor! Anyone who makes himself out to be a king opposes the emperor.
local: Pɩkpaɣʋ nɛ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ yɔ, Pilaatɩ ñɩnaɣ se eyele Yesu. Ɛlɛ́, Yuda ñɩma pazɩ kubusi mabʋ se: «Yee ŋyebi abalʋ ɛnʋ yɔ, ŋtɛkɛ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ ɛgbaɣdʋ! Weyi ɛtɔŋ se ɛnʋ lɛ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ yɔ, ɛkɛ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ koyindu!».

eng: Then the company of soldiers, the commander, and the guards from the Jewish leaders took Jesus into custody. They bound him.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, sɔɔjanaa mbá nɛ pa-ñʋʋdʋ nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyaa pakpa Yesu nɛ pɔhɔkɩ-ɩ nimee.

eng: When Pilate heard these words, he led Jesus out and seated him on the judges bench at the place called Stone Pavement .
local: Pilaatɩ nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛlɩzɩ Yesu nɛ awayɩ nɛ piyele ɛcaɣ tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ ɖenɖe payaɣ se Pɛɛ Đɩpɔyɛ yɔ. Payaɣ-ɖɩ nɛ Ebree taa se Gabata.

eng: and led him first to Annas. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year. .
local: Pɔhɔkɩ-ɩ lɛ, pacalɩ-ɩ wonaʋ cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ kɩbɩnʋ Ana tɛ. Kayifu kakpaɣ Ana pɛlɛ, ɛnʋ kɛkɛnɩnɩ cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ pɩnaɣ ŋ́gá.

eng: It was about noon on the Preparation Day for the Passover. Pilate said to the Jewish leaders, Heres your king.
local: Kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩɖɛɣaɣ se kife nɛ pɔtɔɔ Paska Kazandʋ yɔ, kɩ-wɩsɩ siɣu pɩlakaɣ ḿbʋ́. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ Pilaatɩ heyi Yuda ñɩma se: «Ɩɩnaɣ mi-wiyaʋ sɔsɔ!».

eng: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jewish leaders that it was better for one person to die for the people.
local: Ɛnʋ ɖɔɖɔ katasɩnɩ Yuda ñɩma ñʋndɩnaa lɔŋ. Eeheyi-wɛ se: «Pɩkɩlɩ wee wee se ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm ɛsɩ nɛ pɩya ɛjaɖɛ kpeekpe ñʋʋ.».

eng: The Jewish leaders cried out, Take him away! Take him away! Crucify him! Pilate responded, What? Do you want me to crucify your king? We have no king except the emperor, the chief priests answered.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pasʋʋ kubusi mabʋ yɔɔ se: «Kʋ-ɩ! Kʋ-ɩ! Kam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ!» Pilaatɩ pɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Pʋwɛɛ se manka mi-wiyaʋ sɔsɔ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ yaa we?» Cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa cosi-i se: «Đɩfɛyɩnɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ nɔɔyʋ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ.».

eng: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Because this other disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the high priests courtyard.
local: Pɛɖɛɣaɣnɩ Yesu cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ tɛ lɛ, Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ nɛ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ nɔɔyʋ patɩŋɩɣ pa-wayɩ. Cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ kasɩm tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ ɛnʋ. Patalaa lɛ, tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ ɛnʋ nɛ Yesu pasʋʋ taɣ yɔɔ.

eng: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified. The soldiers took Jesus prisoner.
local: Peeɖe Pilaatɩ ɖʋzɩ Yesu pe-nesi taa se powoni-i paka kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ pakpaɣ Yesu.

eng: However, Peter stood outside near the gate. Then the other disciple came out and spoke to the woman stationed at the gate, and she brought Peter in.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ sɩŋ nɔnɔɔ taa. Peeɖe tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ ɛnʋ ɛlɩwa ɛyɔɔdɩnɩ halʋ weyi ɛɖaŋ nɔnɔɔ yɔ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ tʋlɩ nɛ Pɩyɛ sʋʋ.

eng: Carrying his cross by himself, he went out to a place called Skull Place .
local: nɛ palɩzɩ-ɩ nɛ tɛtʋ wayɩ nɛ pasʋʋ ɛ-maɣmaɣ kaamaŋkaɣ. Powokaɣnɩ-ɩ se pakam-ɩ ɖenɖe payaɣ se Ñʋmbɔɔyaɣ lone yɔ. Payaɣ peeɖe nɛ Ebree taa se Goligota.

eng: The servant woman stationed at the gate asked Peter, Arent you one of this mans disciples? Im not, he replied.
local: Peeɖe halʋ ɛnʋ ɛpɔzɩ Pɩyɛ se: «Ña ɖɔɖɔ ŋtɛkɛ abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa lɛlʋ naa we?» Pɩyɛ cosi-i se: «Aayɩ, mɛntɛkɛ abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ.».

eng: Thats where they crucified him—and two others with him, one on each side and Jesus in the middle.
local: Patanɩ-ɩ lɛ, sɔɔjanaa kam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ. Paakam ɖɔɖɔ ɛyáa naalɛ nabɛyɛ, paa weyi nɛ ɛ-kaamaŋkaɣ: lɛlʋ ɛ-nɩmɩyɛ yɔɔ nɛ lɛlʋ ɛ-nɩwaŋ yɔɔ nɛ Yesu wɛɛ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa.

eng: The servants and the guards had made a fire because it was cold. They were standing around it, warming themselves. Peter joined them there, standing by the fire and warming himself.
local: Niŋkaɣ kɛwɛ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ nɛ tʋmlaɖaa nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyaa paɖʋ miŋ nɛ pasɩŋ pohoyuu. Peeɖe Pɩyɛ woba ɛsɩŋ ɖɔɖɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ pohoyuu.

eng: Pilate had a public notice written and posted on the cross. It read Jesus the Nazarene, the king of the Jews.
local: Pɛtɛm Yesu kam lɛ, Pilaatɩ ha nɔɔ se pama kpata nakʋyʋ yɔɔ se: «Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu weyi ɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ» yɔ nɛ paka kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ ɛ-ñʋʋ taa.

eng: Meanwhile, the chief priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
local: Powoni Yesu cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ kɩbɩnʋ tɛ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ pɔzɩ-ɩ tɔm ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa nɛ tɔm ndʋ́ ɛwɩlaɣ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: Many of the Jews read this sign, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city and it was written in Aramaic, Latin, and Greek.
local: Yuda ñɩma sakɩyɛ kalɩ tɔm ńdʋ́. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖenɖe pakam Yesu yɔ, pɩɩñɔtɩnɩ Yeruusalɛm tɛtʋ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, paama-tʋ nɛ Ebree, Laatɛɛ nɛ Grɛɛkɩ kʋnʋmɩŋ taa.

eng: Jesus answered, Ive spoken openly to the world. Ive always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews gather. Ive said nothing in private.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu cosi-i se: «Mɔyɔɔdɩnɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa keɣlee nɛ manpam-wɛ wɩlʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ nɛ ɛ-ɖɩɣa pa-taa ɖenɖe Yuda ñɩma tɩŋa kpeɣliɣ yɔ. Mɔntɔyɔɔdɩ natʋyʋ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ kaʋ.

eng: Therefore, the Jewish chief priests complained to Pilate, Dont write, The king of the Jews but This man said, I am the king of the Jews.
local: Peeɖe cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa heyi Pilaatɩ se: «Pɩfɛyɩ se ŋyele tɔm ndʋ́ pama kpata yɔɔ se: ‹Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ› yɔ ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se ŋlɛɣzɩ se: ‹Abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛtɔm se ɛnʋ lɛ Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ.› ».

eng: Why ask me? Ask those who heard what I told them. They know what I said. .
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋpɔzʋʋ-m ḿbʋ́? Pɔzɩ mbá mɔyɔɔdɩnɩ-wɛ yɔ nɛ peheyi-ŋ ndʋ́ meheyi-wɛ yɔ: pasɩm mbʋ́ pʋ-tɔm meheyi-wɛ yɔ.».

eng: Pilate answered, What Ive written, Ive written.
local: Pilaatɩ cosi-wɛ se: «Tɔm ndʋ́ mamawa yɔ, ńdʋ́ wɛɣnɩ kpata yɔɔ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: After Jesus spoke, one of the guards standing there slapped Jesus in the face. Is that how you would answer the high priest? he asked.
local: Yesu yɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyaa mbá paawɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ, pa-taa lɛlʋ cɛbɩ-ɩ kadaŋa nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ḿbʋ́ ŋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ naa we?».

eng: When the soldiers crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and his sandals, and divided them into four shares, one for each soldier. His shirt was seamless, woven as one piece from the top to the bottom.
local: Sɔɔjanaa tɛm Yesu kam lɛ, pakpaɣ e-wondu nɛ patayɩ tam naanza taa nɛ paa weyi ɛkpaɣ hɔɔlʋʋ. Pakpaɣ ɖɔɖɔ e-toko ŋgʋ́ kitiki-i camɩyɛ yɔ. Pɛtɛñɛyɩ-kʋ, ɛlɛ́, palʋ-kʋ kʋlʋʋ waaa.

eng: Jesus replied, If I speak wrongly, testify about what was wrong. But if I speak correctly, why do you strike me? .
local: Peeɖe Yesu pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Yee mɔntɔyɔɔdɩ camɩyɛ yɔ, wɩlɩ-m mbʋ́ mayʋsɩnaa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, yee mɔyɔɔdɩ camɩyɛ lɛ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ŋmakɩ-m yɔ?».

eng: They said to each other, Lets not tear it. Lets cast lots to see who will get it. This was to fulfill the scripture, They divided my clothes among themselves, and they cast lots for my clothing. Thats what the soldiers did.
local: Paaheyi ɖama se: «Đɩtaacɩyɩ-kʋ, ɛlɛ́, ɖɩɖʋ miika nɛ ɖɩna weyi ɛkpakɩɣ-kʋ yɔ.» Ḿbʋ́ tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ, tɩlaba. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Patayɩ ɖama mon-wondu nɛ paɖʋ miika mon-toko yɔɔ se pana se akpakɩɣ-kʋ.» Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ sɔɔjanaa laba yɔ.

eng: Then Annas sent him, bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Peeɖe Ana kpaɣ Yesu nɛ ɛ-hɔkʋtʋ ḿbʋ́ nɛ etiyini-i cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ Kayifu tɛ.

eng: Jesus mother and his mothers sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene stood near the cross.
local: Yesu ɖoo nɛ e-ɖoo neu halʋ ñɩnʋ nɛ Kloopaasɩ halʋ Maarɩɩ nɛ Magdala Maarɩɩ paasɩŋ ɛ-kaamaŋkaɣ cɔlɔ.

eng: Meanwhile, Simon Peter was still standing with the guards, warming himself. They asked, Arent you one of his disciples? Peter denied it, saying, Im not.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ ñaawɛɛ ehoyuu miŋ. Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ña ɖɔɖɔ, ŋtɛkɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa taa lɛlʋ naa we?» Pɩyɛ kpɛzɩ se: «Aayɩ, mɛntɛkɛ abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ.».

eng: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, Woman, here is your son. .
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yesu na e-ɖoo, ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ weyi ɛkɩlɩ sɔɔlʋʋ yɔ, ɛsɩŋ ɛ-cɔlɔ. Peeɖe eheyi e-ɖoo se: «Mon-ɖoo! Kpaɣ-ɩ ɛzɩ ña-pɩyalʋ yɔ.».

eng: A servant of the high priest, a relative of the one whose ear Peter had cut off, said to him, Didnt I see you in the garden with him.
local: Cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ tʋmlaɖaa taa nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe. Ɛkɛ abalʋ weyi Pɩyɛ sɛtɩ ɛ-nɩŋgbaaŋ yɔ ɛ-ñɩnʋ. Ɛnʋ pɔzɩnɩ Pɩyɛ se: «Mantana ña nɛ ɩ kaɖaɣ taa yaa we?».

eng: Then he said to the disciple, Here is your mother. And from that time on, this disciple took her into his home.
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ ɛnʋ se: «Ña ñakpaɣ-ɩ ɛzɩ ño-ɖoo yɔ.» Pɩkpaɣ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ ɛnʋ, ewoni Yesu ɖoo nɛ ɛwɛɛ ɛ-tɛ.

eng: Early in the morning of the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
local: Kujuka wiye tanaŋ tɛɛ cu Magdala Maarɩɩ kʋyaa nɛ ewolo Yesu pɩlaʋ cɔlɔ nɛ pʋcɔ kalɩmaŋ koo. Ewoba lɛ, ɛmaɣnɩ patʋlɩ pɩyɛ kʋ-nɔɔ nɛ pasɩɩ-ɖɩ kɩjaʋ.

eng: Peter denied it again, and immediately a rooster crowed.
