local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ tasɩ kpɛzʋʋ. Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, kalɩmɔʋ nakʋyʋ koo.

eng: After this, knowing that everything was already completed, in order to fulfill the scripture, Jesus said, I am thirsty. .
local: Yesu nawa se mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩɩwɛɛ se pɩla yɔ, pɩlaba lɛ, ɛtɔ se lɔkɔtʋ ɖɛ-m. Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ se pɩla ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ.

eng: She ran to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said, They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we dont know where theyve put him.
local: Peeɖe esewa nɛ ewolo Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ nɛ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ weyi Yesu kɩlɩ sɔɔlʋʋ yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Palɩzɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ ɛ-pɩlaʋ taa nɛ ɖɩɩnaɣ ɖenɖe pasɩ-ɩ yɔ.».

eng: The Jewish leaders led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Roman governors palace. It was early in the morning. So that they could eat the Passover, the Jewish leaders wouldnt enter the palace; entering the palace would have made them ritually impure.
local: Kifema tanaŋ tɛɛ cu lɛ, pakpaɣ Yesu nɛ Kayifu tɛ nɛ powoni-i Room wiyaʋ Pilaatɩ ɖɩɣa taa. Ɛlɛ́, Yuda ñɩma tasʋʋ ka-taa se pɩtaakɔɔ pipisi-wɛ nɛ peyele Paska Kazandʋ tɔɔʋ.

eng: A jar full of sour wine was nearby, so the soldiers soaked a sponge in it, placed it on a hyssop branch, and held it up to his lips.
local: Pɛʋ nakʋyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe, kɩ-taa nɛ sʋlʋm kɩñɩŋɩm nabʋyʋ ɖo. Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, sɔɔjanaa kpaɣ kʋca pelii pɩ-taa nɛ patʋʋ tɩʋ ŋgʋ́ payaɣ se Iizoopu yɔ kɩ-ɖaʋ nɔɔ taa nɛ powoni ɛ-nɔɔ cɔlɔ se ɛmʋzɩ.

eng: Peter and the other disciple left to go to the tomb.
local: Eheyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, paba kʋyɩ se powoki pɩlaʋ cɔlɔ.

eng: So Pilate went out to them and asked, What charge do you bring against this man.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Pilaatɩ lɩ pɔ-cɔlɔ tɩŋ tɛɛ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛbɛ abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛwɛkaa?».

eng: When he had received the sour wine, Jesus said, It is completed. Bowing his head, he gave up his life.
local: Ɛmʋzaa lɛ, ɛtɔ se: «Pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩlaba!» Ɛlɛ́, ɛsɔ ñʋʋ nɛ ɛhɛɛ.

eng: They were running together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and was the first to arrive at the tomb.
local: Pɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, pakpaɣ seya nɛ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ ɛnʋ ɛlɛyɩ Pɩyɛ talʋʋ.

eng: They answered, If he had done nothing wrong, we wouldnt have handed him over to you.
local: Pocosi-i se: «Yee ɛtaakɛ kɩdɛkɛdɩm laɖʋ yɔ, ɖɩtaakɔnɩ-ɩ ñɔ-cɔlɔ.».

eng: It was the Preparation Day and the Jewish leaders didnt want the bodies to remain on the cross on the Sabbath, especially since that Sabbath was an important day. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of those crucified broken and the bodies taken down.
local: Yuda ñɩma ñʋndɩnaa taacaɣ se mbá pakam-wɛ yɔ, pɛwɛɛ kaamaŋkasɩ yɔɔ ḿbʋ́ nɛ kife kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́, kɩkɛ kɩyakʋ sɔsɔʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ powobi Pilaatɩ tɛ se ɛha-wɛ nɔɔ nɛ pɛpɛlɩ pa-naŋgbanzɩ nɛ pasɩ lɔŋ nɛ petisi-wɛ.

eng: Bending down to take a look, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he didnt go in.
local: Ɛtalaa lɛ, eluŋ nɛ ɛcɔnɩ pɩlaʋ taa nɛ ɛna pɩsaŋ weyi paahuuzi Yesu yɔɔ yɔ tataa. Ɛlɛ́, ɛtasʋʋ kɩ-taa.

eng: Pilate responded, Take him yourselves and judge him according to your Law. The Jewish leaders replied, The Law doesnt allow us to kill anyone. .
local: Peeɖe Pilaatɩ heyi-wɛ se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ ɩkpaɣ-ɩ mɩ-maɣmaɣ nɛ ɩhʋʋnɩ-ɩ ɛzɩ paɖʋʋ mɩ-paɣtʋ taa yɔ.» Papɩsɩnɩ-ɩ se: «Pɩtaha-ɖʋ nʋmɔʋ se ɖɩkʋ nɔɔyʋ.».

eng: Therefore, the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men who were crucified with Jesus.
local: Peeɖe ɛha-wɛ-kɛ nɛ sɔɔjanaa wolo pɛpɛlɩ mbá pakam pa nɛ Yesu yɔ, pa-naalɛ pa-naŋgbanzɩ.

eng: Following him, Simon Peter entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
local: Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ ñatalɩ wayɩ wayɩ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ sʋʋ pɩlaʋ taa nɛ ɛmaɣnɩ pɩsaŋ weyi paahuuzi Yesu yɔɔ yɔ tataa.

eng: This was so that Jesus word might be fulfilled when he indicated how he was going to die.
local: Pɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ tɔm ndʋ́ Yesu kɔyɔɔdaa nɛ ɛwɩlɩnɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛkɔŋ sɩbʋ yɔ, tɩla.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they saw that he was already dead so they didnt break his legs.
local: Patalɩ Yesu lɛ, pamaɣnɩ ɛtɛm sɩbʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ pɛtɛpɛlɩ ɛ-ñɩnzɩ.

eng: He also saw the face cloth that had been on Jesus head. It wasnt with the other clothes but was folded up in its own place.
local: Ɛna ɖɔɖɔ pɩsaʋ ŋgʋ́ paahɔkɩ ɛ-ñʋʋ yɔ. Pekpiyi-kʋ nɛ pasɩɩnɩ nɖeke.

eng: Pilate went back into the palace. He summoned Jesus and asked, Are you the king of the Jews.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pilaatɩ pɩsɩ ɛsʋʋ nɛ ɛ́yáa Yesu nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ña lɛ Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ naa we?».

eng: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pa-taa lɛlʋ pɩsɩnɩ kɩgbɛzɛŋ nɛ ɛsɔ ɛ-hʋyʋʋ taa nɛ pʋtʋlɩ nɛ lɩm nɛ calɩm pekpe.

eng: Then the other disciple, the one who arrived at the tomb first, also went inside. He saw and believed.
local: Peeɖe tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ lɛlʋ weyi ɛcalɩ talʋʋ yɔ, ɛsʋ kɩ-taa ɖɔɖɔ nɛ ɛna ḿbʋ́ nɛ etisi.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you say this on your own or have others spoken to you about me? .
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ña-maɣmaɣ ŋmaɣzɩnɩ mɔn-tɔm nɛ ŋyɔɔdʋʋ yaa nabɛyɛ heyini-ŋ-tʋ?».

eng: The one who saw this has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he speaks the truth, and he has testified so that you also can believe.
local: Weyi ɛkɛdɩɣ-mɩ tɔm tʋnɛ yɔ, ɛna mbʋ́ pɩlaba yɔ nɛ ɛ-ɛsɛ, toovenim ɛyɔɔdʋʋ. Ɛnʋ lɛ, ɛnawa se ɛyɔɔdʋʋ toovenim. Ɛyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ se mɩ ɖɔɖɔ itisi-tʋ.

eng: They didnt yet understand the scripture that Jesus must rise from the dead.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pataanɩdɩ tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se pʋwɛɛ se Yesu ɛsɩ nɛ efe yɔ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ.

eng: Pilate responded, Im not a Jew, am I? Your nation and its chief priests handed you over to me. What have you done.
local: Pilaatɩ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Mɛnkɛ Yuda tʋ naa we? Ñɛ-ɛjaɖɛ taa ñɩma nɛ pɔ-cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa pakpanɩ-ŋ nɛ pɔkɔnɩ mɔn-cɔlɔ! Ɛbɛ ŋlaba nɛ pakpa-ŋ?».

eng: These things happened to fulfill the scripture, They wont break any of his bones .
local: Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ, tɩla. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Paakaɣ pɛlʋʋ ɛ-mɔɔyɛ naɖɩyɛ kaʋ.».

eng: Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa mbá pa-naalɛ papɩsɩ pɛ-tɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, My kingdom doesnt originate from this world. If it did, my guards would fight so that I wouldnt have been arrested by the Jewish leaders. My kingdom isnt from here. .
local: Yesu pɩsɩnɩ-ɩ se: «Pɩtɛkɛ kedeŋa kanɛ kɔ-yɔɔ kewiyaɣ mɔntɔwaa. Yee ŋ́gá maatɔwaa yɔ, man-tʋmlaɖaa kalʋbɩ mɔ-yɔɔ se pataaɖʋzɩ-m Yuda ñɩma ñʋndɩnaa nesi taa. Ŋgʋ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ kɛtɛkɛ kedeŋa kanɛ kɔ-yɔɔ ñɩŋga.».

eng: And another scripture says, They will look at him whom they have pierced.
local: Pama ka-taa ɖɔɖɔ se: «Pɔkɔŋ cɔnaʋ weyi patʋlɩ ɛ-hʋyʋʋ taa yɔ.».

eng: Mary stood outside near the tomb, crying. As she cried, she bent down to look into the tomb.
local: Magdala Maarɩɩ ñaasɩŋ pɩlaʋ cɔlɔ nɛ ewiliɣ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, eluŋ nɛ wiluu se ɛcɔŋnɩ kɩ-taa.

eng: So you are a king? Pilate said. Jesus answered, You say that I am a king. I was born and came into the world for this reason: to testify to the truth. Whoever accepts the truth listens to my voice. .
local: Pilaatɩ tasɩ-ɩ pɔzʋʋ se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ ŋkɛ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ na?» Ecosi-i se: «Ñɔyɔɔdʋʋnɩ ḿbʋ́. Ma lɛ, mɔnkɔm kedeŋa yɔɔ nɛ palʋlɩ-m se malɩzɩ aseɣɖe toovenim yɔɔ. Weyi ɛsɔɔlɩ toovenim yɔ, ewelesiɣni-m.».

eng: After this Joseph of Arimathea asked Pilate if he could take away the body of Jesus. Joseph was a disciple of Jesus, but a secret one because he feared the Jewish authorities. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and took the body away.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Ariimatee tʋ weyi payaɣ se Yozɛɛfʋ yɔ, ewolo Pilaatɩ cɔlɔ nɛ etendi-i se yee etisiɣ yɔ, ɛkpaɣ Yesu nɛ ewoni epi. Yozɛɛfʋ ɛnʋ, ɛkɛ Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, eseɣaɣ Yuda ñɩma ñʋndɩnaa. Ewoba lɛ, Pilaatɩ tisi. Peeɖe ewobi ɛkpaɣ Yesu.

eng: She saw two angels dressed in white, seated where the body of Jesus had been, one at the head and one at the foot.
local: Ɛcɔnaa lɛ, ɛna Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa naalɛ nabɛyɛ, posu wondu kʋhʋlʋmʋtʋ nɛ pacaɣ ɖenɖe paahɛzɩ Yesu yɔ: lɛlʋ ɛ-ñʋʋ ɖeɖe nɛ lɛlʋ ɛ-naŋgbanzɩ ɖeɖe.

eng: What is truth? Pilate asked. After Pilate said this, he returned to the Jewish leaders and said, I find no grounds for any charge against him.
local: Peeɖe Pilaatɩ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ɛbɛ ŋtɩɩyaɣ kpem se toovenim?» Pilaatɩ tɛm Yesu pɔzʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛtasɩ lɩʋ Yuda ñɩma cɔlɔ nɛ ɛkaɖɩ-wɛ se: «Ma lɛ, maanaɣ mbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ pʋpɔzʋʋ se pakʋ abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛ-tɔm yɔ.

eng: Later, Jesus himself appeared again to his disciples at the Sea of Tiberias. This is how it happened:.
local: Pɩlabɩ kɩyakɩŋ cabɩ lɛ, Yesu tasɩ ɛ-tɩ wɩlʋʋ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa Tiberiyaadɩ teŋgu nɔɔ. Ɩna ɛzɩma ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ ɛ-tɩ yɔ:.

eng: Nicodemus, the one who at first had come to Jesus at night, was there too. He brought a mixture of myrrh and aloe, nearly seventy-five pounds in all.
local: Abalʋ weyi payaɣ se Niikodɛm nɛ eewobi ɖoó naayɛ taa nɛ ɛna Yesu yɔ, eewobi ɖɔɖɔ peeɖe. Esidi tɩlaaɖɩ ŋgʋ́ payaɣ se alowɛɛ yɔ nɛ ŋgʋ́ payaɣ kʋkʋ se miiri yɔ ɛzɩ kiiloonaa niidozo yɔ nɛ ɛkpɛndɩna.

eng: The angels asked her, Woman, why are you crying? She replied, They have taken away my Lord, and I dont know where theyve put him.
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Đɔ-kɔɔ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋwiliɣ ḿbʋ́?» Ecosi-wɛ se: «Palɩzɩ man-Kɩpaɣlʋ nɛ maanaɣ ɖenɖe pasɩ-ɩ yɔ.».

eng: You have a custom that I release one prisoner for you at Passover. Do you want me to release for you the king of the Jews.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mɩ-sɔnzɩ taa lɛ, paɖʋwa se paa Paska Kazandʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, malɩzɩ salaka tʋ nɔɔyʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mi-liu wɛɛ se malɩzɩ Yuda ñɩma wiyaʋ sɔsɔ naa we?».

eng: Simon Peter, Thomas , Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, Zebedees sons, and two other disciples were together.
local: Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ nɛ Tomaa weyi payaɣ se Đom yɔ nɛ Nataanayɛɛlɩ weyi ɛwɛ Kana Galilee egeetiye taa yɔ nɛ Zebedee pɩyalaa nɛ Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lɛlaa naalɛ, paawɛ ɖoli kʋɖʋm.

eng: Following Jewish burial customs, they took Jesus body and wrapped it, with the spices, in linen cloths.
local: Pa-naalɛ pakpaɣnɩ Yesu nɛ pɔhɔkɩ-ɩ pɩsaŋ taa nɛ pamɩzɩ tɩlaaɖɩnaa ndɩ ndɩ ɛzɩ Yuda ñɩma lakʋʋ sɩɖaa nɛ pʋcɔ pepiŋ-wɛ yɔ.

eng: As soon as she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she didnt know it was Jesus.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ekisi ɛ-wayɩ nɛ ɛna Yesu sɩŋaa. Ɛlɛ́, eteɖiɣzini se ɛnʋ.

eng: They shouted, Not this man! Give us Barabbas.
local: Peeɖe pocosi patɩŋa kɔtɔ kɔtɔ se: «Aayɩ! Lɩzɩ-ɖʋ Baraabaasɩ.» Ŋgʋ́, Baraabaasɩ kɛkɛ ɛzaɣ́ʋ.

eng: Simon Peter told them, Im going fishing. They said, Well go with you. They set out in a boat, but throughout the night they caught nothing.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ heyi-wɛ se: «Mowoki mankpa kpakpasɩ.» Ɛlɛ́, peheyi-i se: «Đa nɛ ŋ ɖiwolo.» Peeɖe pakʋyɩnɩ ɖoó pakpa kpɩyʋʋ taa nɛ powolo, ɛlɛ́, patakpa pʋyʋ.

eng: There was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
local: Kaɖaɣ nakɛyɛ kɛwɛ ɖenɖe pakam Yesu yɔ. Pɩlaʋ kɩfalʋʋ nakʋyʋ kɛwɛ ka-taa peeɖe, petepimdi nɔɔyʋ kɩ-taa.

eng: Jesus said to her, Woman, why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Thinking he was the gardener, she replied, Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him and I will get him.
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Đɔ-kɔɔ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋwiliɣ ḿbʋ́? Anɩ ŋñɩnɩɣ?» Ɛmaɣzaɣ se kaɖaɣ yɔɔ cɔnɩyʋ pɔzʋʋnɩ-ɩ ḿbʋ́. Pʋ-yɔɔ eheyi-i se: «Đajaa! Yee ñalɩzɩnɩ-ɩ yɔ, ŋheyi-m ɖenɖe ŋsɩ-ɩ yɔ nɛ mowolo mankpaɣ-ɩ.».

eng: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples didnt realize it was Jesus.
local: Pɩwɩzɩ ñandʋʋ lɛ, Yesu lɩɩ teŋgu nɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa teɖiɣzini se ɛnʋ.

eng: Because it was the Jewish Preparation Day and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus in it.
local: Kɩ-taa pepim Yesu, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɔñɔɔzaɣ pa-tɩ se pʋcɔ kife pa-kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, kɩɩñɔtɩnɩ peeɖe kpam.

eng: Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned and said to him in Aramaic, Rabbouni .
local: Peeɖe Yesu ya-ɩ se: «Maarɩɩ!» Ɛlɛ́, ekisini ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛsɛɛnɩ Ebree taa se: «Rabuunii!» Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se Wɩlɩyʋ.

eng: Jesus called to them, Children, have you caught anything to eat? They answered him, No.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Men-piya! Ɩkpa kpakpasɩ naa we?» Pocosi-i se: «Aayɩ.».

eng: Jesus said to her, Dont hold on to me, for I havent yet gone up to my Father. Go to my brothers and sisters and tell them, Im going up to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God. .
local: Yesu heyi-i se: «Taalaɣzɩ-m, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mantakpadɩ man-Caa cɔlɔ. Ɛlɛ́, wolo ŋheyi mɛ-ɛgbaɣdɩnaa se mankpaɣ weyi ɛkɛ man-Caa nɛ ɛtɛ ɛkɛ mɛ-Ɛsɔ yɔ ɛ-cɔlɔ. Ɛnʋ ɖɔɖɔ lɛ mɩ-Caa nɛ mɩ-Ɛsɔ.».

eng: He said, Cast your net on the right side of the boat and you will find some. So they did, and there were so many fish that they couldnt haul in the net.
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩlɔ piyuu nɛ kpɩyʋʋ nɩwaŋ yɔɔ, ɩkpaɣ.» Peeɖe pɔlɔ-kʋ nɛ pakpa kpakpasɩ nɛ pusu kɩ-taa ɖo, halɩ paapɩzɩɣ se palɩzɩ-kʋ.

eng: Mary Magdalene left and announced to the disciples, Ive seen the Lord. Then she told them what he said to her.
local: Peeɖe Magdala Maarɩɩ ɛnʋ ewoba nɛ eheyi Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa se ɛna Kɩpaɣlʋ nɛ ɛkɛdɩ-wɛ ɖɔɖɔ ndʋ́ ɛ́lɛ́ keheyi-i yɔ.

eng: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, Its the Lord! When Simon Peter heard it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself and jumped into the water.
local: Peeɖe tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ weyi Yesu kɩlɩ sɔɔlʋʋ yɔ, eheyi Pɩyɛ se: «Naze Kɩpaɣlʋ!» Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ nɩwa se Kɩpaɣlʋ, ŋgʋ́, ɛɛhɔzɩ e-toko nɛ ɛsɩɩ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛkpaɣ-kʋ esuu nɛ ɛfalɩ lɩm taa se ewoki ɛ-cɔlɔ.

eng: It was still the first day of the week. That evening, while the disciples were behind closed doors because they were afraid of the Jewish authorities, Jesus came and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you. .
local: Kujuka ŋ́gá ka-ɖanaɣ Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa woba pekpeɣli kuduyuu nakʋyʋ tɛɛ. Paakaɖɩ pɔ-yɔɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, peseɣaɣ Yuda ñɩma ñʋndɩnaa. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yesu lɩɩ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa yem nɛ ɛsɩŋ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩwɛɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa!».

eng: The other disciples followed in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they werent far from shore, only about one hundred yards.
local: Pataakɩlɩ posinaʋ kidiiliŋ, pɩɩsɛɣ-wɛ ɛzɩ mɛtanaa mɩnʋʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ. Lɛlaa talɩnɩ kpɩyʋʋ, pɔhɔŋaɣ piyuu, kɩ-taa su kpakpasɩ ɖo.

eng: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they were filled with joy.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ e-nesi nɛ ɛ-hʋyʋʋ. Pana ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa-taa hʋlʋmɩ siŋŋ se pana Kɩpaɣlʋ.

eng: When they landed, they saw a fire there, with fish on it, and some bread.
local: Petibini kpɩyʋʋ taa lɛ, pamaɣnɩ miŋ nɩɩyɩ, ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ kpakpasɩ nɛ tindine.

eng: Jesus said to them again, Peace be with you. As the Father sent me, so I am sending you. .
local: Peeɖe Yesu tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Ɩwɛɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa! Mentiyiɣ-mɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ man-Caa tiyuu-m yɔ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Bring some of the fish that youve just caught. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu heyi-wɛ se: «Ɩkɔnɩ kpakpasɩ nzɩ́ ɩkpawa yɔ sɩ-taa nasɩyɩ.».

eng: Then he breathed on them and said, Receive the Holy Spirit.
local: Ɛtɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, efezi pɔ-yɔɔ fʋʋʋ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Ɩmʋ maha-mɩ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu!.

eng: Simon Peter got up and pulled the net to shore. It was full of large fish, one hundred fifty-three of them. Yet the net hadnt torn, even with so many fish.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ kpa kpɩyʋʋ taa nɛ ɛhɔ piyuu nɛ ɛpɛɖɩ tataa, kɩ-taa nɛ kpakpasɩ sɔsɔsɩ ɖo: sɩɩwɛ mɩnʋʋ nɛ nɩɩnʋwa nɛ naadozo. Sɩɩɖɔ piyuu taa siŋŋ, ɛlɛ́, kɩtacɩyɩ.

eng: If you forgive anyones sins, they are forgiven; if you dont forgive them, they arent forgiven. .
local: Mbá ikpeɣiɣ-wɛ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ, Ɛsɔ kpeɣiɣ-wɛ ɖɔɖɔ. Mbá iikpeɣiɣ paba yɔ, eekpeɣiɣ-wɛ ɖɔɖɔ.».

eng: Jesus said to them, Come and have breakfast. None of the disciples could bring themselves to ask him, Who are you? They knew it was the Lord.
local: Peeɖe Yesu ya-wɛ se: «Ɩkɔɔ ɩtɔɔ.» Ɛlɛ́, pa-taa nɔɔyʋ tacazɩ-ɩ pɔzʋʋ se: «Ña anɩ?» Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paatɛm naʋ se ɛnʋ lɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ.

eng: Thomas, the one called Didymus, one of the Twelve, wasnt with the disciples when Jesus came.
local: Yesu woba ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hɛkʋ taa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Tomaa weyi ɛkɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ taa lɛlʋ nɛ payaɣ-ɩ se Đom yɔ, ɛtaawɛɛ.

eng: Jesus came, took the bread, and gave it to them. He did the same with the fish.
local: Ɛya-wɛ lɛ, ɛkpaɣ tindine ńɖɩ́ nɛ kpakpasɩ ńzɩ́ nɛ ɛcɛlɩ-wɛ.

eng: The other disciples told him, Weve seen the Lord! But he replied, Unless I see the nail marks in his hands, put my finger in the wounds left by the nails, and put my hand into his side, I wont believe.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lɛlaa heyi-i se: «Đɩna Kɩpaɣlʋ.» Ɛlɛ́, Tomaa heyi-wɛ se: «Yee mantana ñɩma pɔŋ e-nesi taa nɛ mɛlɛkɩ me-niye ɩ-taa nɛ mɛlɛkɩ ɖɔɖɔ me-nesi ɛ-hʋyʋʋ taa yɔ, maatisiɣ kaʋ.».

eng: This was now the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from the dead.
local: Đooo Yesu fem nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa yɔ, tam naadozo ñɩmbʋ ɛwɩlɩ ɛ-tɩ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ɛzʋnɔ yɔ.

eng: After eight days his disciples were again in a house and Thomas was with them. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus entered and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you. .
local: Kɩyakʋ sɩŋaa lɛ, Yesu tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ɖaɣnɩ kpeɣluu kuduyuu ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-tɛɛ. Puwiye ńɖɩ́ lɛ, Tomaa kɛwɛ pɔ-cɔlɔ. Paaɖɩɣ nɔnɔsɩ nɛ pakaɖɩ. Ɛlɛ́, Yesu ɖaɣnɩ lɩʋ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa yem nɛ ɛsɩŋ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩwɛɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa!».

eng: When they finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, Simon son of John, do you love me more than these? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my lambs. .
local: Pɛtɛm tɔɔʋ lɛ, Yesu pɔzɩ Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ se: «Yohanɛɛsɩ pɩyalʋ Siimɔɔ! Ŋsɔɔlɩ-m nɛ pɩkɩlɩ tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa lɛlaa sɔɔlʋʋ-m yɔ yaa we?» Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: «Ɛɛɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ŋnawa se mɔnsɔɔlɩ-ŋ.» Yesu heyi-i se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ cɔnɩ me-heɣsi yɔɔ camɩyɛ.».

eng: Then he said to Thomas, Put your finger here. Look at my hands. Put your hand into my side. No more disbelief. Believe! .
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, eheyi Tomaa se: «Cɔnɩ me-nesi taa, lɛkɩ ñe-niye, posi ñe-nesi nɛ ŋlɛkɩ ma-hʋyʋʋ taa. Yele ñɔ-hɔyɛ ɖoŋ nɛ ŋwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ.».

eng: Jesus asked a second time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Take care of my sheep. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛtasɩ-ɩ pɔzʋʋ se: «Yohanɛɛsɩ pɩyalʋ Siimɔɔ! Ŋsɔɔlɩ-m yaa we?» Pɩyɛ cosi-i se: «Ɛɛɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ŋnawa se mɔnsɔɔlɩ-ŋ.» Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-i se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ cɔnɩ me-heŋ yɔɔ camɩyɛ.».

eng: Thomas responded to Jesus, My Lord and my God.
local: Peeɖe Tomaa cosi-i se: «Man-Kɩpaɣlʋ! Mɛ-Ɛsɔ!».

eng: He asked a third time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Peter was sad that Jesus asked him a third time, Do you love me? He replied, Lord, you know everything; you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my sheep.
local: Yesu tasɩ-ɩ pɔzʋʋ tozo ɖeɖe se: «Yohanɛɛsɩ pɩyalʋ Siimɔɔ! Ŋsɔɔlɩ-m yaa we?» Ɛtasɩ Pɩyɛ pɔzʋʋ tozo ɖeɖe ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ laŋɩyɛ wɛɛkɩ nɛ ecosi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ŋsɩm pɩtɩŋa payɩ. Ŋna ḿbʋ́ se mɔnsɔɔlɩ-ŋ!» Peeɖe Yesu heyi-i se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ cɔnɩ me-heŋ yɔɔ camɩyɛ.».

eng: Jesus replied, Do you believe because you see me? Happy are those who dont see and yet believe. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋna-m yɔɔ ŋwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ? Mbá patana-m nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ!».

eng: I assure you that when you were younger you tied your own belt and walked around wherever you wanted. When you grow old, you will stretch out your hands and another will tie your belt and lead you where you dont want to go. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-ŋ se: «Ŋŋkɛ evebu lɛ, ŋñɔɔzaɣ ña-tɩ nɛ ŋwoki ɖenɖe ñe-liu wɛɛ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ŋkɔŋ ŋkpadɩyɩ lɛ, ŋposuu ñe-nesi nɛ nɔɔyʋ tɔɔlɩ-ŋ tambaɖɛ nɛ ewoni-ŋ ɖenɖe ñe-liu fɛyɩ yɔ.».

eng: Then Jesus did many other miraculous signs in his disciples presence, signs that arent recorded in this scroll.
local: Yesu kalabɩ hama lakasɩ lɛɛsɩ sakɩyɛ ɛ-tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyaa ɛsɩndaa, ɛlɛ́, patama sɩ-tɔm takayaɣ kanɛ ka-taa.

eng: He said this to show the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. After saying this, Jesus said to Peter, Follow me. .
local: Tɔm tʋnɛ Yesu wɩlɩnɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Pɩyɛ kɔŋ sɩbʋ nɛ Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ sɛɛ yɔ. Ɛtɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, eheyi Pɩyɛ se: «Tɩŋ ma-wayɩ.».

eng: But these things are written so that you will believe that Jesus is the Christ, Gods Son, and that believing, you will have life in his name.
local: Piyele ndʋ́ tʋtʋ wɛ ka-taa yɔ, pama-tʋ se ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ se Yesu lɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ nɛ ɛtɛ ɛkɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ nɛ piyele pɩtɩŋnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ihiɣ wezuu.

eng: Peter turned around and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. This was the one who had leaned against Jesus at the meal and asked him, Lord, who is going to betray you.
local: Yesu yɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Pɩyɛ kisi ɛ-wayɩ nɛ ɛna tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ weyi Yesu kɩlɩ sɔɔlʋʋ yɔ, ewokaɣ pɔ-cɔlɔ. Ɛnʋ kɛwɛlɩnɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ Yesu yɔɔ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɔtɔkaɣ tɔɔnaɣ yɔ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Akɔŋ-ŋ ɖʋzʋʋ koyindinaa nesi taa?».

eng: When Peter saw this disciple, he said to Jesus, Lord, what about him.
local: Pɩyɛ na-ɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ Yesu se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Nɛ ɛnɛ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ tɛŋnɩ we?».

eng: Jesus replied, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you? You must follow me. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu pɩsɩnɩ-ɩ se: «Yee me-liu ɛwɛɛ se ɛcaɣ wezuu nɛ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ manpɩsɩɣ mɔnkɔɔ yɔ lɛ, pɩna-ŋ nɛ we? Ña lɛ, tɩŋ-m!».

eng: Therefore, the word spread among the brothers and sisters that this disciple wouldnt die. However, Jesus didnt say he wouldnt die, but only, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩyɛlɛ haɖaa ḿbá pa-taa se tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ ɛnʋ ɛɛsɩkɩ. Ŋgʋ́, Yesu taaheyi Pɩyɛ se: «Tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ ɛnʋ ɛɛkaɣ sɩbʋ.» Ɛlɛ́, ɛɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Yee me-liu ɛwɛɛ se ɛcaɣ wezuu nɛ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ manpɩsɩɣ mɔnkɔɔ yɔ lɛ, pɩna-ŋ nɛ we?».

eng: This is the disciple who testifies concerning these things and who wrote them down. We know that his testimony is true.
local: Tɔmkpɛlɩkɩyʋ ɛnʋ, ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛlɩzɩnɩ aseɣɖe nɛ ɛma tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ. Đa lɛ, ɖɩnawa se ɛ-aseɣɖe kɛ toovenim.

eng: Jesus did many other things as well. If all of them were recorded, I imagine the world itself wouldnt have enough room for the scrolls that would be written.
local: Mbʋ́ Yesu tasɩ labʋ yɔ, pʋɖɔ sakɩyɛ. Ɛlɛ́, yee paatɔm se pamaɣ pʋ-tɔm piye piye yɔ, takayɩsɩ nzɩ́ sɩ-taa pamaɣ-tʋ yɔ, sɩtaamaɣ kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ.

eng: Peter and John were going up to the temple at three oclock in the afternoon, the established prayer time.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ powokaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa Ɛsɔ sɛʋ ɖanaɣ ñɩɣtʋ naadozo yɔɔ ɛzɩ patɩɩsɛɣʋ yɔ.

eng: Meanwhile, a man crippled since birth was being carried in. Every day, people would place him at the temple gate known as the Beautiful Gate so he could ask for money from those entering the temple.
local: Nabɛyɛ wokaɣnɩ abalʋ nɔɔyʋ paa ɖooye nɛ pacaɣzɩɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa nɔnɔɔ ŋgá payaɣ se Nɔnɔɔ Đeu Ñɩŋga yɔ ka-taa se ɛsʋlʋmɩ mbá pasʋʋ yɔ liidiye. Ɛkɛ kalɩkalaɣ, paalʋlɩ-ɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he began to ask them for a gift.
local: Kalɩkalaɣ ŋ́gá kana Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ pasʋwaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa lɛ, kasʋlʋmɩ-wɛ se paha-kɛ pʋyʋ.

eng: Theophilus, the first scroll I wrote concerned everything Jesus did and taught from the beginning.
local: Man-taabalʋ Tewofiili, man-takayaɣ kajalaɣ ñɩŋga taa lɛ, maatɛm-ŋ kɛdʋʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ Yesu kapazɩ labʋ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ, kpaɣ kiɖe tɛɛ.

eng: Peter and John stared at him. Peter said, Look at us.
local: Peeɖe Paba cɔnɩ-kɛ pɩŋŋ nɛ Pɩyɛ heyi-kɛ se: «Cɔnɩ-ɖʋ.».

eng: right up to the day when he was taken up into heaven. Before he was taken up, working in the power of the Holy Spirit, Jesus instructed the apostles he had chosen.
local: nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Ɛsɔ kpazɩ-ɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ. Ɛɛlɩzɩ ɛyáa nabɛyɛ se pɛkɛ e-tiyiyaa, nɛ pʋcɔ Ɛsɔ kpazɩ-ɩ ɛsɔdaa lɛ, ɛtɩŋnɩ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖoŋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛɖʋ-wɛ paɣtʋ.

eng: So the man gazed at them, expecting to receive something from them.
local: Kaka ñɔcɔnɩ-wɛ pɩŋŋ nɛ kaɖaŋ se paaha-kɛ pʋyʋ.

eng: After his suffering, he showed them that he was alive with many convincing proofs. He appeared to them over a period of forty days, speaking to them about Gods kingdom.
local: Yesu sɩbʋ wayɩ yɔ, ɛwɩlɩ ɛ-tɩ mbá ɛɛlɩzɩ-wɛ se pɛkɛ e-tiyiyaa yɔ. Ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ nɛ wɛtʋ ndɩ ndɩ se ɛwɛ wezuu. Ɛɛwɩlɩ-wɛ ɛ-tɩ kɩyakɩŋ nɩɩnaza, nɛ kɩyakɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-taa, ɛkɛdɩ-wɛ Ɛsɔ kewiyaɣ tɔm.

eng: Peter said, I dont have any money, but I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, rise up and walk.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ heyi-kɛ se: «Mɛnfɛyɩnɩ liidiye yaa sika, ɛlɛ́, mahaɣ-ŋ mbʋ́ mɛwɛna yɔ: Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ ɖoŋ taa lɛ, kʋyɩ ŋɖɔ!».

eng: While they were eating together, he ordered them not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised. He said, This is what you heard from me:.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Yesu nɛ e-tiyiyaa pɔtɔkaɣ tɔɔnaɣ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ɛpaɣ-wɛ se: «Ɩtaakʋyɩ Yeruusalɛm nɛ iwolo ɖoli. Ɛlɛ́, ɩɖaŋ mbʋ́ Caa ɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ-mɩ yɔ. Kʋjɔʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kʋ-tɔm maaheyi-mɩ.

eng: Then he grasped the mans right hand and raised him up. At once his feet and ankles became strong.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, esezi ɛ́ɖɔ́kɩ-kɛ nɛ ɛ-nɩwaŋ nɛ ɛkʋsɩ-kɛ. Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ka-naataŋ nɛ ka-naajʋwa pa-taa kpa ɖoŋ.

eng: John baptized with water, but in only a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Mɔntɔzʋʋ-mɩ se Yohanɛɛsɩ labɩ ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩkazɩ kɩyakɩŋ pazɩ lɛ, Caa Ɛsɔ ñɔkɔŋ-mɩ labʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖoŋ.».

eng: Jumping up, he began to walk around. He entered the temple with them, walking, leaping, and praising God.
local: Peeɖe kama ka-naŋgbanzɩ yɔɔ cim nɛ kapazɩ ɖɔm nɛ ka nɛ wɛ pɛkpɛndɩ pasʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa. Kɔɖɔŋaɣ nɛ kamaɣ nɛ kasaŋ Ɛsɔ.

eng: As a result, those who had gathered together asked Jesus, Lord, are you going to restore the kingdom to Israel now.
local: Pekpeɣli ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Alɩwaatʋ tʋnɛ ŋsɩŋnɩ ɖa Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma se ɖihiɣ ɖa-tɩ yɔɔ caɣʋ naa we?».

eng: All the people saw him walking and praising God.
local: Samaɣ tɩŋa na ɛzɩma mbʋ́ kɔɖɔŋaɣ nɛ kasaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, It isnt for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has set by his own authority.
local: Ecosi-wɛ se: «Pɩtɛkɛ mɩ pʋmʋnaa se ɩtɩlɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ ɖeyi ɖeyi malakɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ. Caa Ɛsɔ ɖʋnɩ-tʋ nɛ ɛ-maɣmaɣ e-ɖoŋ.

eng: They recognized him as the same one who used to sit at the temples Beautiful Gate asking for money. They were filled with amazement and surprise at what had happened to him.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, peɖiɣzini se abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛcakaɣnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa Nɔnɔɔ Đeu Ñɩŋga taa nɛ ɛsʋlʋmʋʋ. Peeɖe pɩlabɩ patɩŋa piti nɛ pɩpazɩ pɩɖɩɣzɩ-wɛ se ɛkʋyaa nɛ ɛɖɔŋ.

eng: Rather, you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.
local: Ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tiki mɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, ihikiɣ ɖoŋ nɛ ɩkɛ ma-aseɣɖe tɩnaa Yeruusalɛm tɛtʋ taa nɛ Yudee nɛ Samaarɩɩ pa-ageeta taa nɛ kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ.».

eng: While the healed man clung to Peter and John, all the people rushed toward them at Solomons Porch, completely amazed.
local: Abalʋ ɛnʋ, ɛwɛ Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ pɔ-cɔlɔ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, samaɣ tɩŋa kpɛzɩ kowolo pɔ-cɔlɔ hɔɔlʋʋ ŋgʋ́ payaɣ se Salomɔɔ Hɔɔlʋʋ yɔ kɩ-taa, pɩɩɖɩɣzɩ-kɛ siŋŋ.

eng: After Jesus said these things, as they were watching, he was lifted up and a cloud took him out of their sight.
local: Yesu tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩkpazɩ-ɩ ɛsɔdaa yem nɛ pɔcɔŋnɩ-ɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛsɔmɩnʋʋ nakʋyʋ wolo kuhuuzi ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ patatasɩ-ɩ naʋ.

eng: Seeing this, Peter addressed the people: You Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why are you staring at us as if we made him walk by our own power or piety?.
local: Pɩyɛ na ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-kɛ se: «Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma mɩ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma pɩɖɩɣzɩɣ-mɩ nɛ abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛbʋ tɔm ḿbʋ́? Ɛbɛ yɔɔ ɩcɔnɩ-ɖʋ ɛzɩ ɖa-maɣmaɣ ɖo-ɖoŋ ɖɩlabɩnaa, yaa ɖɩlakɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ yɔ yɔɔ ɖɩlaba nɛ abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛkʋyɩ ɛɖɔŋ?.

eng: While he was going away and as they were staring toward heaven, suddenly two men in white robes stood next to them.
local: Pɔcɔŋaɣnɩ-ɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛyáa naalɛ nabɛyɛ tɩlɩʋ pɔ-cɔlɔ yem, posu wondu kʋhʋlʋmtʋ.

eng: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the God of our ancestors—has glorified his servant Jesus. This is the one you handed over and denied in Pilates presence, even though he had already decided to release him.
local: Đɔ-cɔzɔnaa Abraham nɛ Izaakɩ nɛ Yakɔɔbʋ pɛ-Ɛsɔ ɖʋnɩ ɛ-tʋmlaɖʋ Yesu hɩɖɛ. Mɩ lɛ, ɩkpa-ɩ mɩ-maɣmaɣ nɛ ɩɖʋzɩ ñʋndɩnaa nesi taa. Wiyaʋ Pilaatɩ tɔm se eyeki-i, ɛlɛ́, ikizi.

eng: They said, Galileans, why are you standing here, looking toward heaven? This Jesus, who was taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way that you saw him go into heaven.
local: nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ-wɛ se: «Galilee ñɩma mɩ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩwɛɛ ɩcɔŋnɩ ɛsɔdaa ḿbʋ́? Yesu ɛnʋ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩlɩzɩ-ɩ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ pɩkpazɩ-ɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ ɩna yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ ɛkɔŋ pɩsʋʋ ɛkɔɔ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa.».

eng: When Pentecost Day arrived, they were all together in one place.
local: Pɩtalɩ Yuda ñɩma Kʋmtʋ Kazandʋ wiye lɛ, taabalaa tɩŋa payɩ kpeɣli ɖama ɖɩɣa nakɛyɛ taa.

eng: You rejected the holy and righteous one, and asked that a murderer be released to you instead.
local: Ikizi Ɛsɔ Ɛyʋ Kʋtʋʋzʋ ɛnʋ nɛ ɩpɔzɩ Pilaatɩ se ɛlɩzɩ-mɩ ɛyáa kʋyʋ.

eng: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is near Jerusalem—a sabbath days journey away.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Yesu tiyiyaa kʋyɩ ɖenɖe paawɛɛ yɔ nɛ papɩsɩ Yeruusalɛm. Somiŋ Pʋʋ yɔɔ paawɛɛ. Kɩtaawɛɛ poliŋ nɛ Yeruusalɛm, pɩɩwɛ ɛzɩ kiloomɛta kʋɖʋmʋʋ yɔ.

eng: Suddenly a sound from heaven like the howling of a fierce wind filled the entire house where they were sitting.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, kɔkɔɖɛ naɖɩyɛ lɩɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa ɛzɩ heɣlim sɔsɔm makʋʋ yɔ nɛ ɖisu ɖɩɣa ŋgá ka-taa paawɛɛ yɔ katɩŋa.

eng: You killed the author of life, the very one whom God raised from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɩlaba nɛ iyele pakʋ Ɛyʋ Kʋtʋʋzʋ ɛnʋ, ŋgʋ́ ɛnʋ haɣnɩ wezuu. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ fezi-i nɛ ɖɩna-ɩ nɛ ɖɛ-ɛsɛ.

eng: When they entered the city, they went to the upstairs room where they were staying. Peter, John, James, and Andrew; Philip and Thomas; Bartholomew and Matthew; James, Alphaeus son; Simon the zealot; and Judas, James son—.
local: Patalaa lɛ, pakpa ɛsɔdaa kuduyuu ŋgʋ́ kɩ-taa paakpeɣlaɣ yɔ kɩ-taa. Mbá pekpeɣlaɣ kɩ-taa yɔ, ḿbá yɔ: Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ nɛ Yakubu nɛ Andree nɛ Filiipu nɛ Tomaa nɛ Bartelemii nɛ Maatiyee nɛ Alfee pɩyalʋ Yakubu nɛ Siimɔɔ weyi payaɣ se Yɔɔ Kandɩyʋ yɔ nɛ Yakubu pɩyalʋ Yuudi.

eng: They saw what seemed to be individual flames of fire alighting on each one of them.
local: Peeɖe pʋyʋ nɔɔyʋ lɩ pɔ-yɔɔ ɛzɩ miŋ nzʋlʋmɛɛ yɔ nɛ pɩmɩzɩ ɖama nɛ pɩcaɣ paa weyi ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees confronted them.
local: Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ pɔyɔɔdaɣnɩ ɛyáa samaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, cɔjɔnaa nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyaa ñʋndɩnaa nɛ Saduki ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma powolo pɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: His name itself has made this man strong. That is, because of faith in Jesus name, God has strengthened this man whom you see and know. The faith that comes through Jesus gave him complete health right before your eyes.
local: Yesu ɛnʋ, e-ɖoŋ nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ lidaʋ wɛnaʋ peyebina nɛ abalʋ weyi ɩsɩm-ɩ nɛ ɩnaɣ-ɩ yɔ, ɛkpa ɖoŋ. Lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛwɛnɩ Yesu yɔɔ yɔ kiyebina nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ camɩyɛ ɛzɩ mɩtɩŋa ɩnaɣʋ-ɩ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ.

eng: all were united in their devotion to prayer, along with some women, including Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
local: nɛ Yesu ɖoo Maarɩɩ nɛ halaa nabɛyɛ nɛ Yesu newaa abalaa ñɩma patɩŋa payɩ paalabɩ nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ nɛ pekpeɣliɣ tam nɛ pɛsɛɣ Ɛsɔ.

eng: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other languages as the Spirit enabled them to speak.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tii kɩwɛɛ patɩŋa pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ papazɩ yɔɔdʋʋ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ weyi kɩha-wɛ ɖoŋ se pɔyɔɔdɩ yɔ.

eng: They were incensed that the apostles were teaching the people and announcing that the resurrection of the dead was happening because of Jesus.
local: Patalaa lɛ, panɩɩ pawɩlɩɣ ɛyáa samaɣ nɛ peheyiɣ-kɛ se Yesu sɩba nɛ efe, ḿbʋ́ wɩlɩɣnɩ se sɩɖaa pɩzɩɣ pefe. Pʋ-yɔɔ pa-pana kpanɩ-wɛ siŋŋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I know you acted in ignorance. So did your rulers.
local: Taabalaa mɩ! Manawa se mɩ nɛ mɩ-awiya ɩtaasɩ mbʋ́ ɩlakaɣ yɔ yɔɔ ɩlabɩ Yesu ḿbʋ́.

eng: During this time, the family of believers was a company of about one hundred twenty persons. Peter stood among them and said.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye taabalaa kpeɣlaa ɛzɩ patɩɩkpeɣluu yɔ, paatalɩ ɛzɩ mɩnʋʋ nɛ nɩɩlɛ yɔ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Pɩyɛ kʋyɩ ɛsɩŋ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ eheyi-wɛ se:.

eng: There were pious Jews from every nation under heaven living in Jerusalem.
local: Yuda ñɩma mbá palakɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ yɔ, paalɩ ajɛya kpeekpe taa nɛ powolo kazandʋ ńdʋ́ ti-ɖeɖe Yeruusalɛm.

eng: They seized Peter and John and put them in prison until the next day.
local: nɛ pakpa-wɛ paɖʋ salaka nɛ tɛʋ kɔɔ kife. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, wɩsɩ kɛtɛm ɖʋʋ.

eng: But this is how God fulfilled what he foretold through all the prophets: that his Christ would suffer.
local: Tɔm ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ keheyi náyaa tɩŋa ɖooo lɔŋ yɔ, ńdʋ́ labɩnɩ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se pʋwɛɛ se Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ ɛtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, the scripture that the Holy Spirit announced beforehand through David had to be fulfilled. This was the scripture concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus.
local: «Taabalaa! Pɩɩwɛɛ se Yudaasɩ weyi ɛwɩlɩ koyindinaa nɛ pakpa Yesu yɔ ɛ-tɔm ndʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tɩŋnɩ Daaviidi yɔɔ nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ yɔ, tɩla.

eng: When they heard this sound, a crowd gathered. They were mystified because everyone heard them speaking in their native languages.
local: Panɩ kɔkɔɖɛ ńɖɩ́ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pacaŋ powolo. Patalaa lɛ, paanaɣ se pɔyɔɔdɩ se we, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paa weyi ɛnɩɣaɣ tɔm ndʋ́ taabalaa yɔɔdaɣ yɔ nɛ ɛ-tɛ taa.

eng: Many who heard the word became believers, and their number grew to about five thousand.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, mbá panɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ yɔ, pa-taa sakɩyɛ tisi-tʋ nɛ pɔsɔzɩ lɛlaa yɔɔ nɛ pɛtɛzɩ ɖɔʋ. Abalaa katalɩ ɛzɩ kutokiŋ kagbaanzɩ yɔ.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Turn back to God so that your sins may be wiped away.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ nɛ ɩpɩsɩnɩ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ nɛ ekpeɣ-mɩ mɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm.

eng: This happened even though he was one of us and received a share of this ministry. .
local: Ŋgʋ́, Yudaasɩ ɛnʋ ɛkɛ ɖa-taa lɛlʋ, ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩlakaɣnɩ tʋmɩyɛ.

eng: They were surprised and amazed, saying, Look, arent all the people who are speaking Galileans, every one of them?.
local: Peeɖe pɩɖɩɣzɩ-wɛ yem ɖɩɣ ɖɩɣ nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Ɛzɩ ɛyáa panɛ pɛtɛkɛ Galilee ñɩma patɩŋa na?.

eng: The next day the leaders, elders, and legal experts gathered in Jerusalem.
local: Kifema lɛ, Yuda ñɩma awiya nɛ pɛ-tɛ sɔsaa nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa pekpeɣli Yeruusalɛm.

eng: Then the Lord will provide a season of relief from the distress of this age and he will send Jesus, whom he handpicked to be your Christ.
local: Peeɖe Kɩpaɣlʋ yeki nɛ ɩhɛzɩ alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ nɛ etiyini-mɩ weyi ɛɛlɩzɩ ɖooo lɔŋ nɛ ɛsɩɩ-mɩ yɔ. Ɛnʋ lɛ Krɩstʋ Yesu.

eng: In fact, he bought a field with the payment he received for his injustice. Falling headfirst, he burst open in the middle and all his intestines spilled out.
local: Pɛhɛyɩ-ɩ liidiye ɛ-ɛzaɣ́tʋ lakasɩ yɔɔ lɛ, ɛkpaɣ-ɖɩ ɛya tɛtʋ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ewolo esiɣ ɛ-ñʋʋ yɔɔ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ nɛ e-lotu ya kpiii nɛ tɩmɩzɩ kpɛdɛɛɛ.

eng: How then can each of us hear them speaking in our native language?.
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ paa ɖa-taa weyi lɛ, ɛnɩɣ ndʋ́ pɔyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ nɛ ɛ-tɛ taa?.

eng: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and others from the high priests family.
local: Cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ Ana nɛ ɛ-hɔʋ taa ñɩma tɩŋa payɩ nɛ Kayifu nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ nɛ Alɛksandrɩ paawɛ peeɖe ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Jesus must remain in heaven until the restoration of all things, about which God spoke long ago through his holy prophets.
local: Pɩkpaɣʋnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛcaɣ ɛsɔdaa nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖaɣnɩ labʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ eeheyi ɛ-náyaa ɖooo lɔŋ nɛ pɔyɔɔdɩ yɔ.

eng: This became known to everyone living in Jerusalem, so they called that field in their own language Hakeldama, or Field of Blood.
local: Yeruusalɛm ñɩma tɩŋa nɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ lɛ, payaa tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ nɛ pɛ-tɛ taa se: ‹Akɩlɩdama.› Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se calɩm tɛtʋ.

eng: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; as well as residents of Mesopotamia, Judea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia.
local: Ŋgʋ́, ɖa-taa cɩnɛ ɖɩnaɣ mbá palɩnɩ Partɩ nɛ Meedii nɛ Elam nɛ Mezoopotamii nɛ Yudee nɛ Kapaadoosi nɛ Pɔntɩ nɛ Aazii egeetiye taa.

eng: They had Peter and John brought before them and asked, By what power or in what name did you do this.
local: Peeɖe palɩzɩ Yesu tiyiyaa ḿbá pɛ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «A-ɖoŋ taa yaa a-hɩɖɛ taa ɩlaba nɛ abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ?».

eng: Moses said, The Lord your God will raise up from your own people a prophet like me. Listen to whatever he tells you. .
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ Mooyiizi yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ. Ɛyɔɔdaa se: ‹Mɩ-Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-mɩ tiyinaʋ náyʋ nɔɔyʋ ɛzɩ ma yɔ, ɛkɛɣ mɩ-tɛ tʋ. Ɩkɔŋ welesuu ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa eheyiɣ-mɩ yɔ.

eng: It is written in the Psalms scroll, Let his home become deserted and let there be no one living in it; and Give his position of leadership to another. .
local: Ŋgʋ́, ɛ-tɔm pama Keɣsi Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Ɛ-ɖɩɣa ɛpɩsɩ njɛyɛ nɛ nɔɔyʋ ɛtaatasɩ sʋʋ ka-taa.› Patasɩ maʋ ɖɔɖɔ se: ‹Nɔɔyʋ ɛlɛɣzɩ e-lone taa nɛ ɛla ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ.›.

eng: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the regions of Libya bordering Cyrene; and visitors from Rome .
local: nɛ Frijii nɛ Pamfilii nɛ Ejiptɩ nɛ Liibii hɔɔlʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kʋwɛ Siirɛɛnɩ yɔ nɛ Room.

eng: Then Peter, inspired by the Holy Spirit, answered, Leaders of the people and elders.
local: Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu kɛwɛ Pɩyɛ yɔɔ, peeɖe ɛyɔɔdaa se: «Ɛjaɖɛ taa awiya nɛ sɔsaa mɩ!.

eng: Whoever doesnt listen to that prophet will be totally cut off from the people. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee nɔɔyʋ etewelesini náyʋ ɛnʋ yɔ, palɩzɩɣ-ɩ Ɛsɔ ñɩma taa nɛ pakʋ-ɩ.›.

eng: Therefore, we must select one of those who have accompanied us during the whole time the Lord Jesus lived among us.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ ɛyáa nabɛyɛ tɩŋaɣ-ɖʋ paa le, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖa nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu ɖɩɩwɛɛ yɔ.

eng: Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the mighty works of God in our own languages.
local: Yuda ñɩma maɣmaɣ nɛ mbá papɩsɩ Yuda ñɩma nɛ palakɩ pɔ-sɔnzɩ yɔ nɛ Krɛɛtɩ ñɩma nɛ Araabii ñɩma patɩŋa payɩ paawɛɛ ɖɔɖɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛ́yáa ḿbá, pɛkɛdɩɣ Ɛsɔ tʋma sɔsɔna tɔm nɛ ɖatɩŋa ɖɩnɩɣ ɖa-kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ taa!».

eng: are we being examined today because something good was done for a sick person, a good deed that healed him?.
local: Đɩlabɩ abalʋ ɛnɛ camɩyɛ nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ. Yee sɔnɔ pɔpɔzʋʋ-ɖʋ pʋ-tɔm yɔ.

eng: All the prophets who spoke—from Samuel forward—announced these days.
local: Náyaa mbá pɔyɔɔdɩ kpaɣ ɖooo náyʋ Samʋyɛɛlɩ alɩwaatʋ yɔ, patɩŋa payɩ pɔyɔɔdɩ kʋɖʋm kʋɖʋm kɩyakɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-tɔm ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: beginning from the baptism of John until the day when Jesus was taken from us. This person must become along with us a witness to his resurrection.
local: Paawɛ ɖɔ-cɔlɔ kpaɣ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Yohanɛɛsɩ labɩ-ɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ yɔ nɛ puwolo pɩsɩɩnɩ puwiye nɖɩ́ Ɛsɔ lɩzɩ-ɩ ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ ɛkpazɩ-ɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se pa-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛsɔzɩnɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩlɩzɩ Yesu sɩm nɛ e-fem pa-aseɣɖe.».

eng: They were all surprised and bewildered. Some asked each other, What does this mean.
local: Peeɖe pɩɖɩɣzɩ patɩŋa yem ɖɩɣ ɖɩɣ, paanaɣ se pɔyɔɔdɩ se we nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ ɖama se: «Tɔm tʋnɛ, tɩ-tɔbʋʋ se we?».

eng: If so, then you and all the people of Israel need to know that this man stands healthy before you because of the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene—whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead.
local: pʋwɛɛ se mɩ nɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma, mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩtɩlɩ se: Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ weyi ɩkam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɛsɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ fezi-i nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa yɔ, e-ɖoŋ taa ɛyʋ ɛnɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛba nɛ ɩnaɣ-ɩ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ ɛ-alaafɩya.

eng: You are the heirs of the prophets and the covenant that God made with your ancestors when he told Abraham, Through your descendants, all the families on earth will be blessed. .
local: Mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa se ɛtɩŋɩɣnɩ náyaa yɔɔ nɛ ɛla yɔ, mɩtɩnɩ-pʋ. Mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ mɩ-cɔzɔnaa alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛha-wɛ nɔɔ yɔ, mɩtɩnɩ-pʋ ɖɔɖɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, eeheyi Abraham se: ‹Matɩŋɩɣnɩ ñe-liɖe taa ñɩma yɔɔ nɛ mawazɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ ɛyáa kpeekpe yɔɔ.›.

eng: So they nominated two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
local: Peeɖe paya ɛyáa ḿbá pa-taa naalɛ: Yozɛɛfʋ weyi payaɣ se Barnaabaasɩ nɛ pakʋm-ɩ hɩɖɛ se Yustuusi yɔ nɛ Maatɩyaasɩ.

eng: Others jeered at them, saying, Theyre full of new wine.
local: Ɛlɛ́, lɛlaa woŋuu-wɛ nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Sʋlʋm kʋʋ-wɛ kpem!».

eng: This Jesus is the stone you builders rejected; he has become the cornerstone!.
local: Yesu ɛnʋ, ɛ-tɔm pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ kuduyiŋ mayaa mɩ ɩkpaɣaɣ ɩlɔ yɔ, ńɖɩ́ pɩsɩnɩ ɖɩkɩlɩ wazaɣ atɩŋa payɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ.› Pɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ lɛ Yesu.

eng: After God raised his servant, he sent him to you first—to bless you by enabling each of you to turn from your evil ways.
local: Ḿbʋ́ Ɛsɔ calɩ ɛ-tʋmlaɖʋ Yesu tiyuu mɩ-cɔlɔ se ɛkɔɔ ɛlɩzɩ-mɩ mɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm taa nɛ pɩwɩlɩ se ɛwazɩ mɩtɩŋa payɩ mɩ-yɔɔ.».

eng: They prayed, Lord, you know everyones deepest thoughts and desires. Show us clearly which one you have chosen from among these two.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɛkpɛndɩ pɛsɛɛ Ɛsɔ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ñanaɣnɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa hɔ taa. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, wɩlɩ-ɖʋ ɛyáa panɛ pa-naalɛ pa-taa weyi ŋlɩzaa.

eng: Peter stood with the other eleven apostles. He raised his voice and declared, Judeans and everyone living in Jerusalem! Know this! Listen carefully to my words!.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ Pɩyɛ nɛ tiyiyaa lɛlaa hiu nɛ kʋɖʋm pakʋyaa pasɩŋ nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩnɩ ɛyáa samaɣ se: «Yuda ñɩma nɛ mbá mɩ mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩwɛ Yeruusalɛm cɩnɛ yɔ! Ɩma nɩŋgbaaŋ ɖeu nɛ ɩnɩɩ tɔm ndʋ́ meheyiɣ-mɩ yɔ.

eng: Salvation can be found in no one else. Throughout the whole world, no other name has been given among humans through which we must be saved.
local: Ñʋʋ yaɖʋ nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ-ɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ talɩzɩ nɔɔyʋ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ se pɩtɩŋnɩ ɛ́lɛ́ yɔɔ nɛ pɩpɩzɩ pɩya ɖa-ñʋʋ.».

eng: to take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas turned away to go to his own place.
local: se ɛkɛ tiyiyu nɛ ɛlɛɣzɩ Yudaasɩ lone taa nɛ ɛla tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɛ́lɛ́ yeba nɛ ɛɖɛɛ e-ɖeɖe yɔ.».

eng: These people arent drunk, as you suspect; after all, its only nine oclock in the morning!.
local: Ɛyáa panɛ, pɩtɛkɛ sʋlʋm kʋʋnɩ-wɛ ɛzɩ ɩyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩkɩlɩ tanaŋ.

eng: The council was caught by surprise by the confidence with which Peter and John spoke. After all, they understood that these apostles were uneducated and inexperienced. They also recognized that they had been followers of Jesus.
local: Tɔm hʋyaa na ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ pɔyɔɔdaɣnɩ abalɩtʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ lɛ, pɩɖɩɣzɩ-wɛ ɖɩɣ ɖɩɣ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, panawa se pɛkɛ ɛyáa yem, pɛ-ɛsɛ tokuli. Ɛlɛ́, paasɩm-wɛ se pa nɛ Yesu paawɛna.

eng: When they cast lots, the lot fell on Matthias. He was added to the eleven apostles.
local: Peeɖe paɖʋ miika nɛ pɩcaɣ Maatɩyaasɩ yɔɔ nɛ ɛsɔzɩ Yesu tiyiyaa hiu nɛ kʋɖʋm ḿbá pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: Rather, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel:.
local: Tɔm ndʋ́ náyʋ Yowɛɛlɩ kɔyɔɔdaa yɔ, ńdʋ́ lakɩnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se:.

eng: However, since the healed man was standing with Peter and John before their own eyes, they had no rebuttal.
local: Ŋgʋ́, panaɣaɣ ɖɔɖɔ weyi ɛ-yɔɔ cɛba yɔ, ɛsɩŋ pɔ-cɔlɔ peeɖe nɛ paanaɣ pɔyɔɔdɩ se we.

eng: After ordering them to wait outside, the council members began to confer with each other.
local: Peeɖe pɔɖɔɔnɩ Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ se palɩɩnɩ awayɩ. Piyebi pe-ɖeke lɛ, papazɩ ɖama yɔɔdɩnaʋ.

eng: In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young will see visions. Your elders will dream dreams. .
local: ‹Ɛsɔ yɔɔdaa se mɔnkɔŋ yebu nɛ men-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tii paa weyi ɛ-yɔɔ kɛtɛzaɣ alɩwaatʋ taa. Mɩ-pɩyalaa nɛ mɩ-pɛlaa pɔkɔŋ kɛʋ náyaa nɛ pɔyɔɔdɩ tɔm ma-hɩɖɛ taa. Malakɩ nɛ mi-evebiya na wondu kɩfatʋ, nɛ mɩ-akpadɩyaa lɛ, paba kɔŋ ɖozuu ɖozee.

eng: What should we do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem is aware of the sign performed through them. Its obvious to everyone and we cant deny it.
local: nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ ɖama se: «Ɛbɛ ɖɩlakɩ ɛyáa panɛ? Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Yeruusalɛm ñɩma tɩŋa na keɣlee se ḿbá labɩnɩ hama lakasɩ ńzɩ́. Ŋgʋ́, ɖɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɖɩkpɛzɩ-tʋ.

eng: Even upon my servants, men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, mɔnkɔŋ yebu kɩyakɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-taa nɛ, men-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tii man-tʋmlaɖaa halaa ñɩma nɛ abalaa ñɩma pɔ-yɔɔ, nɛ pɛkɛ náyaa nɛ pɔyɔɔdɩ tɔm ma-hɩɖɛ taa.

eng: To keep it from spreading further among the people, we need to warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pʋcɔ nɛ ɛyáa panɛ pɔ-tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩtaatɛzɩ cɔʋ ɛjaɖɛ taa lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se ɖikizini-wɛ-tʋ nɛ ɖɩñazɩ-wɛ se pataatasɩ Yesu hɩɖɛ yaʋ nɔɔyʋ cɔlɔ.».

eng: About that time, while the number of disciples continued to increase, a complaint arose. Greek-speaking disciples accused the Aramaic-speaking disciples because their widows were being overlooked in the daily food service.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa lɛ, Yesu wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa kɛwɛɛ pɛtɛzɩɣ ɖɔʋ nɛ pa-taa Yuda ñɩma mbá pɔyɔɔdʋʋ Grɛɛkɩ yɔ, pemidiɣni mbá paba ñɔyɔɔdʋʋ Ebree yɔ pe-liŋ tɛɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paatasɩɣ tayɩnaʋ pe-lelaa tɔɔnaɣ paa evemiye nɖɩ́.

eng: I will cause wonders to occur in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and a cloud of smoke. .
local: Mɔnkɔŋ wɩlʋʋ kawɩlɩsɩ nasɩyɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ mala hama lakasɩ tataa. Calɩm kɔŋ kpɛdʋʋ nɛ miŋ mʋ nɛ ñɔsɩ ñɛyɩ.

eng: When they called Peter and John back, they demanded that they stop all speaking and teaching in the name of Jesus.
local: Peeɖe paya-wɛ nɛ paka pa-ñʋŋ taa se pataatasɩ wɩlʋʋ nɛ Yesu nɔɔ yaa pataatasɩ yaʋ ɛ-hɩɖɛ kaʋ.

eng: The Twelve called a meeting of all the disciples and said, It isnt right for us to set aside proclamation of Gods word in order to serve tables.
local: Peeɖe Yesu tiyiyaa ḿbá pe-hiu nɛ naalɛ pekpeɣli Yesu wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa tɩŋa payɩ nɛ peheyi-wɛ se: «Pɩtamʋnɩ se ɖiyele Ɛsɔtɔm susuu nɛ ɖɩnɩɣnɩ tɔɔnaɣ tɔm.

eng: The sun will be changed into darkness, and the moon will be changed into blood, before the great and spectacular day of the Lord comes. .
local: Wɩsɩ kɔŋ pɩsʋʋ cɩkpɛndʋʋ nɛ fenaɣ ñɛsɛɛ ɛzɩ calɩm yɔ nɛ pʋcɔ ma Kɩpaɣlʋ man-kɩyakʋ sɔsɔʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩfɛyɩ ɖaɣʋ yɔ, kɩtalɩ.

eng: Peter and John responded, Its up to you to determine whether its right before God to obey you rather than God.
local: Ɛlɛ́, paba pɩsɩnɩ-wɛ se: «Mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩmaɣzɩ nɛ ɩna se: pʋwɛ ɖeu ḿbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa se pañam-mɩ nɛ piyele Ɛsɔ yaa we?.

eng: Brothers and sisters, carefully choose seven well-respected men from among you. They must be well-respected and endowed by the Spirit with exceptional wisdom. We will put them in charge of this concern.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, taabalaa! Ɩcɔna nɛ ɩlɩzɩ mɩ-taa abalaa kɩbama lɔŋsɩnɖaa lʋbɛ mbá Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu wɛ pɔ-yɔɔ yɔ nɛ ɖɩɖʋ-wɛ tʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́.

eng: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. .
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, weyi etendiɣ-m yɔ, ehikiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ.› ».

eng: As for us, we cant stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.
local: Đa lɛ, ɖɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɖiyele yɔɔdʋʋ tɔm ndʋ́ ɖɩna tɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩnɩɩ-tʋ yɔ.».

eng: As for us, we will devote ourselves to prayer and the service of proclaiming the word.
local: Đa ɖɛwɛɣ Ɛsɔ sɛʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɖisusuu ɛ-tɔm.».

eng: Fellow Israelites, listen to these words! Jesus the Nazarene was a man whose credentials God proved to you through miracles, wonders, and signs, which God performed through him among you. You yourselves know this.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma mɩ! Iwelesi nɛ ɩnɩɩ ndʋ́ meheyiɣ-mɩ yɔ: mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩtɛm sɩm ḿbʋ́ se Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu lɛ weyi Ɛsɔ wɩlɩ-mɩ e-ɖoŋ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛtɩŋnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛla hama lakasɩ ndɩ ndɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ kawɩlɩsɩ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa.

eng: They threatened them further, then released them. Because of public support for Peter and John, they couldnt find a way to punish them. Everyone was praising God for what had happened.
local: Peeɖe tɔm hʋyaa ḿbá patasɩ-wɛ ñazʋʋ nɛ peyebi-wɛ pɛɖɛɛ. Pataana kawanaɣ ŋgá paahɔmnɩ pa-nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛyáa kpeekpe saŋaɣ Ɛsɔ mbʋ́ pɩlaba yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: This proposal pleased the entire community. They selected Stephen, a man endowed by the Holy Spirit with exceptional faith, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
local: Peeɖe tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩlabɩ patɩŋa leleŋ nɛ palɩzɩ pa-taa abalaa lʋbɛ ɛzɩ peheyuu-wɛ yɔ. Ḿbá yɔ: Etiyɛɛnɩ, abalʋ ɛnʋ, ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ siŋŋ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu wɛɛ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ Filiipu nɛ Prokɔɔrɩ nɛ Nikaanɔɔrɩ nɛ Tiimɔɔ nɛ Parmenaasɩ nɛ Antɩyɔɔsɩ tʋ Niikolaa weyi ɛsɛɣaɣ Ɛsɔ ɛzɩ Yuda ñɩma nɛ pʋcɔ ɛpɩsɩ Yesu wayɩ tɩŋɩyʋ yɔ.

eng: In accordance with Gods established plan and foreknowledge, he was betrayed. You, with the help of wicked men, had Jesus killed by nailing him to a cross.
local: Ɛnʋ paɖʋzɩ mi-nesi taa ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ kamaɣzʋʋ nɛ ɛɖʋ ɖooo lɔŋ yɔ nɛ ɩtɩŋnɩ mbá paaɖɔŋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ nɛ pakam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɩkʋ-ɩ nɛ ɛsɩ.

eng: because the man who had experienced this sign of healing was over years old.
local: Toovenim lɛ, abalʋ weyi palabɩ hama lakasɩ nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɛ-pɩnzɩ kɛɖɛ nɩɩnaza yɔɔ.

eng: The community presented these seven to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
local: Pɛtɛm-wɛ lɩzʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, powoni-wɛ tiyiyaa ḿbá pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ paba sɛɛ pɔ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ nɛ patɩnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nesi.

eng: God raised him up! God freed him from deaths dreadful grip, since it was impossible for death to hang on to him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ fezi-i nɛ ɛlɩzɩ-ɩ nɛ sɩm nesi taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtaapɩzɩɣ se pʋɖɔkɩ-ɩ tam.

eng: After their release, Peter and John returned to the brothers and sisters and reported everything the chief priests and elders had said.
local: Peyebi Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ lɛ, powolo pɛ-ɛgbaɣdɩnaa cɔlɔ nɛ pɛkɛdɩ-wɛ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ ɛjaɖɛ taa sɔsaa peheyi-wɛ yɔ.

eng: Gods word continued to grow. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased significantly. Even a large group of priests embraced the faith.
local: Ɛsɔtɔm tɛzaɣ cɔʋ paa le nɛ Yesu wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa ñɛtɛzɩɣ ɖɔʋ Yeruusalɛm nɛ halɩ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ cɔjɔnaa sakɩyɛ nɛ pɛwɛnɩ ɖɔɖɔ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ.

eng: David says about him, I foresaw that the Lord was always with me; because he is at my right hand I wont be shaken. .
local: Daaviidi yɔɔdɩ Yesu tɔm se: ‹Mɔncɔŋaɣnɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ tam. Toovenim, ɛwɛɣ mɔn-cɔlɔ lɛ, maatɔlʋʋ.

eng: However, a man named Ananias, along with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
local: Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɛ-halʋ paawɛɛ. Payaɣ-ɩ se Anaanɩyaasɩ nɛ payaɣ ɛ-halʋ se Safɩɩra. Paba ñaayɔɔdaa nɛ pɛpɛdɩ pɛ-tɛtʋ natʋyʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: They listened, then lifted their voices in unison to God, Master, you are the one who created the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
local: Paba nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa nɛ wɛ pɛkpɛndɩ patɩŋa nɛ pɛsɛɛ Ɛsɔ se: «Caabiyuu! Ñalabɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ tɛtʋ nɛ lɩm nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋwɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ yɔ.

eng: Stephen, who stood out among the believers for the way Gods grace was at work in his life and for his exceptional endowment with divine power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people.
local: Ɛsɔ kawazɩ Etiyɛɛnɩ yɔɔ nɛ ɛha-ɩ ɖoŋ nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣnɩ kawɩlɩsɩ nɛ ɛlakɩ hama lakasɩ sɔsɔsɩ ɛyáa hɛkʋ taa.

eng: Therefore, my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover, my body will live in hope, .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ man-taa hʋlʋmaa nɛ mɔ-nɔɔ taa tɔm wɛɛ leleŋ nɛ halɩ nɛ paa man-pɩlaʋ taa yɔ, mantaɣ man-tɩ liu.

eng: With his wifes knowledge, he withheld some of the proceeds from the sale. He brought the rest and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Ɛlɛ́, panɩɩnɩ ɖama nɛ patayɩ ti-liidiye taa nɛ abalʋ kpaɣ hɔɔlʋʋ nɛ ewoni ɛcɛlɩ Yesu tiyiyaa se ɖɩtɩŋa lɛ.

eng: You are the one who spoke by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: Why did the Gentiles rage , and the peoples plot in vain? .
local: Ñatɩŋnɩnɩ ñe-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔɔ nɛ ŋyele ɖɔ-cɔzɔ Daaviidi weyi ɛkɛ ña-tʋmlaɖʋ yɔ, ɛyɔɔdɩ se: ‹Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ajɛya taa ñɩma peendiɣ ḿbʋ́? Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma a-taa ñɩma taŋ nɔsɩ nzɩ́ sɩɩwazɩɣ pʋyʋ yɔ?.

eng: Opposition arose from some who belonged to the so-called Synagogue of Former Slaves. Members from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia entered into debate with Stephen.
local: Peeɖe nabɛyɛ kʋyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ. Ḿbá yɔ: Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ nɖɩ́ payaɣ se Puholimwaa ñɩnɖɛ yɔ ɖɩ-taa ñɩma. Pa-taa lɛ: Yuda ñɩma mbá palɩnɩ Siirɛɛnɩ nɛ Alɛksandrii nɛ lɛlaa mbá paba ñalɩnɩ Siliisii nɛ Aazii egeetiye taa yɔ. Pa nɛ Etiyɛɛnɩ pɔhɔŋaɣnɩ ɖama tɔm.

eng: because you wont abandon me to the grave, nor permit your holy one to experience decay. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ña Kɩpaɣlʋ ŋŋyeki-m sɩɖaa taa nɛ mɛntɛŋɛlɩ. Ŋŋyeki ma ña-ñɩnʋ nɛ manɖazɩ.

eng: Peter asked, Ananias, how is it that Satan has influenced you to lie to the Holy Spirit by withholding some of the proceeds from the sale of your land?.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Anaanɩyaasɩ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋyele nɛ Satanɩ peɣzi-ŋ nɛ halɩ ŋcɛtɩnɩ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu taa nɛ ŋsɩɩ ña-tɩ tɛtʋ ndʋ́ ŋpɛdaa yɔ ti-liidiye hɔɔlʋʋ lɛɛkʋ?.

eng: The kings of the earth took their stand and the rulers gathered together as one against the Lord and against his Christ. .
local: Kedeŋa yɔɔ awiya tɩŋa ñɔɔzɩ a-tɩ se aɖɛɣ you. Mbá pɛwɛnɩ ɖoŋ ɛjaɖɛ yɔɔ yɔ, patam nɔɔ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔ nɛ Weyi Ɛlɩzaa Yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ.›.

eng: However, they couldnt resist the wisdom the Spirit gave him as he spoke.
local: Ɛlɛ́, patapɩzɩɣ se palabɩ-ɩ pʋyʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛyɔɔdaɣnɩ lɔŋsɩnɖɛ nɖɩ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ha-ɩ yɔ.

eng: You have shown me the paths of life; your presence will fill me with happiness. .
local: Ŋwɩlɩ-m wezuu nʋmɔʋ, ŋkɔŋ yebu nɛ man-taa wɛɛ leleŋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ŋwɛ mɔn-cɔlɔ tam.› ».

eng: Wasnt that property yours to keep? After you sold it, wasnt the money yours to do with whatever you wanted? What made you think of such a thing? You havent lied to other people but to God.
local: Pʋcɔ ŋpɛdɩ-tʋ lɛ, tɩɩkɛ ña-ñɩm, pɩtɛkɛ ñatɩnɩ ti-liidiye ɖɔɖɔ yaa we? Ɛbɛ laba nɛ ŋla lɩmaɣza kɩdɛkɛdɛɛ anɛ ḿbʋ́? Tɩlɩ se pɩtɛkɛ ɛyáa ɖeke ŋcɛtaa. Ɛlɛ́, ŋcɛtɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɔɖɔ.».

eng: Indeed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with Gentiles and Israelites, did gather in this city against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed.
local: Kɩpaɣlʋ! Pɩkɛ toovenim, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, wiyaʋ Eroodi nɛ wiyaʋ Pɔɔnsɩ Pilaatɩ nɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma yɔ, patam nɔɔ ña-tʋmlaɖʋ kɩbanʋ Yesu weyi ŋlɩzaa yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ tɛtʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-taa.

eng: Then they secretly enticed some people to claim, We heard him insult Moses and God.
local: Peeɖe pɛtɛɣ ɛyáa se pɛcɛtɩnɩ ɛ-taa se: «Đɩnɩ ɛyɔɔdaɣ ɛzaɣ́tʋ tɔm Mooyiizi nɛ Ɛsɔ pɔ-yɔɔ!».

eng: Brothers and sisters, I can speak confidently about the patriarch David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this very day.
local: Pɩyɛ tasɩ-wɛ heyuu ɖɔɖɔ se: «Man-taabalaa! Iyele mayasɩ-mɩ ɖɔ-cɔzɔ Daaviidi tɔm taa kpayɩ nɛ ɩnɩɩ: ɛsɩba nɛ pepim-i nɛ ɛ-pɩlaʋ wɛɛ halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ nɛ ɖɩnaɣ-kʋ.

eng: When Ananias heard these words, he dropped dead. Everyone who heard this conversation was terrified.
local: Anaanɩyaasɩ nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛtɔlɩ nɛ ɛsɩ. Peeɖe sɔɔndʋ kpa mbá panɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ yɔ patɩŋa siŋŋ.

eng: They did what your power and plan had already determined would happen.
local: Ḿbʋ́ palabɩ mbʋ́ ŋmaɣzaa nɛ ŋɖʋ ɖooo lɔŋ nɛ ño-ɖoŋ yɔ.

eng: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the legal experts. They caught Stephen, dragged him away, and brought him before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pasazɩ ɛyáa samaɣ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa sɔsaa nɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa se pakʋyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pakpa-ɩ nɛ ɖoŋ nɛ powoni tɔm hʋyaa sɔsaa cɔlɔ.

eng: Because he was a prophet, he knew that God promised him with a solemn pledge to seat one of his descendants on his throne.
local: Ɛɛkɛ náyʋ nɛ ɛna se Ɛsɔ ɖuwaa se ɛ-sayɩnaa taa nɔɔyʋ kɔŋnɩ tɔɔʋ e-kewiyaɣ.

eng: Some young men stood up, wrapped up his body, carried him out, and buried him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, evebiya wolo pakpaɣ sɩɖʋ ɛnʋ nɛ paɖʋ-ɩ pɩsaʋ taa nɛ powoni-i pepi.

eng: Now, Lord, take note of their threats and enable your servants to speak your word with complete confidence.
local: Kɩpaɣlʋ! Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, cɔnɩ ŋna panazʋʋ ña-tʋmlaɖaa. Ha-wɛ ɖoŋ nɛ posusi ñɔ-tɔm kaalɛyɩtʋ.

eng: Before the council, they presented false witnesses who testified, This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the Law.
local: Paakpɛndɩnɩ ɛyáa mbá pɛtɛɣ-wɛ se pɛcɛtɩnɩ ɛ-taa yɔ nɛ paba lɩzɩ aseɣɖe se: «Abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛwɛɛ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ ɛzaɣ́tʋ tɔm ɖɛ-Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa nɛ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ pɔ-yɔɔ!.

eng: Having seen this beforehand, David spoke about the resurrection of Christ, that he wasnt abandoned to the grave, nor did his body experience decay .
local: Ɛɛnawa se Krɩstʋ kaɣ fem nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛyɔɔdaa se: ‹Ɛsɔ ɛɛkaɣ-ɩ yebu nɛ ɛtɛŋɛlɩ sɩɖaa taa, e-tomnaɣ ɛɛkaɣ ɖazʋʋ pɩlaʋ taa.›.

eng: About three hours later, his wife entered, but she didnt know what had happened to her husband.
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩ ñɩɣtʋ naadozo yɔ lɛ, Anaanɩyaasɩ ɛnʋ ɛ-halʋ talɩ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛtaanɩɩ mbʋ́ pɩlaba yɔ.

eng: Stretch out your hand to bring healing and enable signs and wonders to be performed through the name of Jesus, your holy servant.
local: Wɩlɩ ño-ɖoŋ nɛ ŋla hama lakasɩ nɛ ŋwɩlɩ kawɩlɩsɩ nɛ kʋdɔndɩnaa yɔɔ cɛ. La ḿbʋ́ nɛ ña-tʋmlaɖʋ Yesu nɔɔ.».

eng: In fact, we heard him say that this man Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and alter the customary practices Moses gave us.
local: Đɩnɩ ɛyɔɔdaa se Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu kɔŋ yɔkʋʋ ɖɩɣa ŋ́gá nɛ piyele ɛlɛɣzɩ sɔnzɩ nzɩ́ Mooyiizi ɖʋ-ɖʋ yɔ.».

eng: This Jesus God raised up. We are all witnesses to that fact.
local: Yesu weyi mɔyɔɔdʋʋ ɛ-tɔm ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ fezi-i nɛ ɖa ɖana-ɩ nɛ ɖɛ-ɛsɛ ɖatɩŋa.

eng: Peter asked her, Tell me, did you and your husband receive this price for the field? She responded, Yes, thats the amount.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ pɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Heyi-m nɛ mana se liidiye nɖɩ́ ɩpɛdɩ mɩ-tɛtʋ yɔ, ɖɩtɩŋa yɔ yaa we?» Ɛlɛ́, ecosi se: «Ɛɛɛ, ɖɩtɩŋa lɛ.».

eng: After they prayed, the place where they were gathered was shaken. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking Gods word with confidence.
local: Pɛtɛm Ɛsɔ sɛʋ lɛ, lone nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa paakpeɣlaa yɔ, ɖɩñamsɩ. Peeɖe Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tibi kɩwɛɛ patɩŋa pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ papazɩ Ɛsɔtɔm susuu kaalɛyɩtʋ.

eng: Everyone seated in the council stared at Stephen, and they saw that his face was radiant, just like an angels.
local: Mbá patɩŋa paawɛ tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ peeɖe yɔ, paakpɛzɩ ɛsɛ nɛ pɔcɔŋnɩ Etiyɛɛnɩ, nɛ pana ɛ-ɛsɩndaa. Ɛ-ɛsɩndaa kɛwɛ ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ ɛsɩndaa yɔ.

eng: He was exalted to Gods right side and received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit. He poured out this Spirit, and you are seeing and hearing the results of his having done so.
local: Caa Ɛsɔ kpazɩ-ɩ ɛ-cɔlɔ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa nɛ ɛcɛlɩ-ɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ŋgʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ ɛyáa yɔ: etisi-kʋ ɖɔ-yɔɔ. Ḿbʋ́ ɩnaɣ nɛ ɩnɩɣ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ.

eng: He replied, How could you scheme with each other to challenge the Lords Spirit? Look! The feet of those who buried your husband are at the door. They will carry you out too.
local: Pɩyɛ ɖaɣnɩ-ɩ pɔzʋʋ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ña nɛ ña-walʋ ɩla nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ nɛ ɩtakɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ Fezuu ḿbʋ́? Cɔnɩ ŋna! Mbá pepim ña-walʋ yɔ, pɛtɛm talʋʋ nɔnɔɔ taa, pakpakɩɣ-ŋ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ powoni pepi.».

eng: The community of believers was one in heart and mind. None of them would say, This is mine! about any of their possessions, but held everything in common.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ ŋgá kaawɛnɩ lidaʋ yɔ, kaalabɩ nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ nɛ kɛwɛnɩ lɩmaɣza kʋɖʋmɛɛ. Ka-taa nɔɔyʋ taamaɣzɩɣ ɛ-ñɩm yɔɔ se e-ɖeke ɛtɩnɩ-pʋ. Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ paa weyi ɛwɛna yɔ, pɩɩkɛ patɩŋa pa-ñɩm.

eng: David didnt ascend into heaven. Yet he says, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side, .
local: Daaviidi lɛ, ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛtakpa ɛsɔdaa. Ɛlɛ́, ɛyɔɔdaa se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ heyi man-Kɩpaɣlʋ se: caɣ mɔn-cɔlɔ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa.

eng: The apostles continued to bear powerful witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and an abundance of grace was at work among them all.
local: Ɛsɔ kaha Yesu tiyiyaa ɖoŋ siŋŋ nɛ palɩzɩɣnɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu sɩm nɛ e-fem pa-aseɣɖe nɛ Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ ɛwazɩɣ samaɣ ŋ́gá katɩŋa kɔ-yɔɔ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ.

eng: At that very moment, she dropped dead at his feet. When the young men entered and found her dead, they carried her out and buried her with her husband.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, halʋ ɛnʋ ɛtɔlɩ Pɩyɛ naŋgbanzɩ tɛɛ nɛ ɛsɩ. Evebiya sʋwa lɛ, pamaɣnɩ ɛtɛm sɩbʋ nɛ pakpaɣ-ɩ powoni pepi ɛ-walʋ cɔlɔ.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet. .
local: nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mankpa ño-koyindinaa nɛ moluzi-wɛ ñɛ-tɛɛ yɔ.›.

eng: There were no needy persons among them. Those who owned properties or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds from the sales.
local: Ka-taa nɔɔyʋ taatɛɖɩɣ pʋyʋ: mbá patɩŋa paawɛnɩ hayɩm yaa kuduyiŋ yɔ, pɛpɛdaɣ nɛ powokini pi-liidiye.

eng: Trepidation and dread seized the whole church and all who heard what had happened.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ sɔɔndʋ kpa Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma tɩŋa nɛ mbá patɩŋa payɩ panɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ yɔ siŋŋ.

eng: Therefore, let all Israel know beyond question that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma mɩ mɩtɩŋa, ɩnɩɩ ɖeu se: Yesu weyi ɩkam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa nɛ ɛkɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔ!».

eng: and place them in the care and under the authority of the apostles. Then it was distributed to anyone who was in need.
local: pɛcɛlɩɣ tiyiyaa nɛ paba maɣzɩɣnɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɛɖɩɣ paa weyi yɔ nɛ patayɩɣ.

eng: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. They would come together regularly at Solomons Porch.
local: Ɛsɔ tɩŋaɣnɩ Yesu tiyiyaa yɔɔ nɛ ɛlakɩ hama lakasɩ sakɩyɛ nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ ɛyáa kawɩlɩsɩ. Mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ yɔ, patɩŋa payɩ panɩɣaɣnɩ ɖama nɛ pekpeɣliɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa ɖenɖe payaɣ se Salomɔɔ Hɔɔlʋʋ yɔ.

eng: When the crowd heard this, they were deeply troubled. They said to Peter and the other apostles, Brothers, what should we do.
local: Panɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ lɛ, pitili-wɛ yem yɔɔɔ. Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ Pɩyɛ nɛ tiyiyaa lɛlaa se: «Taabalaa! Ɛbɛ pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩla yɔ?».

eng: Joseph, whom the apostles nicknamed Barnabas , was a Levite from Cyprus.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Leevi liɖe taa tʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ Ciiprɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa, payaɣ-ɩ se Yozɛɛfʋ. Tiyiyaa kakʋm-ɩ hɩɖɛ se Barnaabaasɩ. Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se weyi ɛsazɩɣ lɛlaa yɔ.

eng: No one from outside the church dared to join them, even though the people spoke highly of them.
local: Ɛlɛ́, nɔɔyʋ yem takatɩɣ se ɛsɔzɩ pɔ-yɔɔ. Ŋgʋ́, ɛyáa ñayɩɣyaɣ-wɛ siŋŋ.

eng: Peter replied, Change your hearts and lives. Each of you must be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-wɛ se: «Ɩlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ nɛ pala paa mɩ-taa weyi lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ Yesu Krɩstʋ hɩɖɛ taa nɛ pekpeɣ-mɩ mɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm. Peeɖe Ɛsɔ haɣ-mɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu.

eng: He owned a field, sold it, brought the money, and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Abalʋ ɛnʋ, ɛpɛdɩ ɛ-hayɩm nɛ ewoni pi-liidiye nɛ ɛcɛlɩ tiyiyaa ḿbá.

eng: Indeed, more and more believers in the Lord, large numbers of both men and women, were added to the church.
local: Ɛyáa sakɩyɛ: halaa nɛ abalaa mbá paawɛnɩ lidaʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔ yɔ, ḿbá sɔzaɣnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pɛtɛzɩɣ ɖɔʋ.

eng: This promise is for you, your children, and for all who are far away—as many as the Lord our God invites.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩ nɛ mi-piya nɛ mbá patɩŋa pɛwɛ poliŋ yɔ: mɩtɩŋa payɩ mbá Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ kɔŋ yaʋ yɔ, ɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ kʋjɔʋ ŋ́gʋ́.».

eng: As a result, they would even bring the sick out into the main streets and lay them on cots and mats so that at least Peters shadow could fall on some of them as he passed by.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɛyáa kpakaɣ kʋdɔndɩnaa nɛ palɩɣnɩ pɛhɛzɩɣ-wɛ ñalɩŋ yɔɔ habɛɛ nɔsɩ se Pɩyɛ ɛkɔm ɛɖɛɣ lɛ, paa ɛ-kɩlɛmʋʋ maɣmaɣ yɔ, kiihuuzi pa-taa nabɛyɛ yɔɔ.

eng: With many other words he testified to them and encouraged them, saying, Be saved from this perverse generation.
local: Pɩyɛ tasɩ-wɛ tɔm ndɩ ndɩ heyuu se ɛwɩlɩnɩ-wɛ se pɩkɛ toovenim nɛ pɩlabɩ-wɛ hʋnʋm. Eheyi-wɛ se palɩzɩ pa-tɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ Ɛsɔ ya pa-ñʋʋ.

eng: Even large numbers of persons from towns around Jerusalem would gather, bringing the sick and those harassed by unclean spirits. Everyone was healed.
local: Ɛyáa sakɩyɛ lɩɣaɣnɩ ɖɔɖɔ hɔɔlɩŋ weyi ɩñɔtɩnɩ Yeruusalɛm yɔ ɩ-taa nɛ powokini kʋdɔndɩnaa nɛ mbá aleewaa pɩsaɣ-wɛ kpɛndɛlɩsɩ yɔ nɛ patɩŋa payɩ pɔ-yɔɔ cɛkɩ.

eng: Those who accepted Peters message were baptized. God brought about three thousand people into the community on that day.
local: Peeɖe ɛyáa sakɩyɛ tisi Pɩyɛ tɔm nɛ palabɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ. Mbá pɔsɔzɩ taabalaa yɔɔ puwiye ńɖɩ́ yɔ, patalɩ ɛzɩ kutokiŋ naadozo yɔ.

eng: The high priest, together with his allies, the Sadducees, was overcome with jealousy.
local: Pɩlakaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Saduki ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma tɩŋa payɩ wɛ cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ cɔlɔ. Panɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pala ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ nɛ paɖʋ se pakpaɣ Yesu tiyiyaa.

eng: The believers devoted themselves to the apostles teaching, to the community, to their shared meals, and to their prayers.
local: Ɛyáa ḿbá, pɛ-ɛsɩyɛ sɛnɩ tiyiyaa tɔm welesuu nɛ pa nɛ wɛ panɩɣnɩ ɖama nɛ palakɩ tindine tayʋʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ pɛsɛɣ Ɛsɔ.

eng: They seized the apostles and made a public show of putting them in prison.
local: Peeɖe pakpa-wɛ powoni paɖʋ-wɛ salaka sɔsɔʋ taa.

eng: A sense of awe came over everyone. God performed many wonders and signs through the apostles.
local: Paa pa-taa weyi ɛñaŋaɣ Ɛsɔ nɛ tiyiyaa ñalakɩ hama lakasɩ sakɩyɛ nɛ pawɩlɩɣ kawɩlɩsɩ sakɩyɛ.

eng: An angel from the Lord opened the prison doors during the night and led them out. The angel told them.
local: Đoo yuwa lɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ wolo ɛtʋlɩ salaka kuduyuu nɔnɔsɩ nɛ ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se:.

eng: All the believers were united and shared everything.
local: Mbá patɩŋa paawɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ yɔ, paalabɩ nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ nɛ patayɩɣ ɖama mbʋ́ mbʋ́ pɛwɛna yɔ.

eng: Go, take your place in the temple, and tell the people everything about this new life.
local: «Iwolo Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ ɩkɛdɩ ɛyáa samaɣ wezuu caɣʋ kɩfam tɔm tɩŋa.».

eng: They would sell pieces of property and possessions and distribute the proceeds to everyone who needed them.
local: Pɛpɛdaɣ mbʋ́ paawɛna yɔ nɛ patɩŋa patayɩɣ ɖama pi-liidiye nɛ paa weyi ehikiɣ mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ nɛ ɛwazɩnɩ ɛ-kʋñɔŋ yɔ.

eng: Early in the morning, they went into the temple as they had been told and began to teach. When the high priest and his colleagues gathered, they convened the Jerusalem Council, that is, the full assembly of Israels elders. They sent word to the prison to have the apostles brought before them.
local: Eheyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, petisi nɛ pakʋyɩ tanaŋ wɩsɩ nɛ powolo Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ papazɩ ɛyáa wɩlʋʋ. Ŋgʋ́, cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ nɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ ñɩma paakpeɣli ɖama nɛ payaa tɔm hʋyaa sɔsaa nɛ ɛjaɖɛ taa sɔsaa tɩŋa kediɣzaɣ. Peeɖe pakʋsɩ sɔɔjanaa se powolo palɩzɩ tiyiyaa ḿbá nɛ pɔkɔna.

eng: Every day, they met together in the temple and ate in their homes. They shared food with gladness and simplicity.
local: Panɩɣaɣnɩ ɖama kɩyakɩŋ tɩŋa nɛ papaŋ ɖama kpeɣluu Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa. Pɔcɔwaɣ ɖama tɛ nɛ pɔtɔkɩ nɛ palakɩ tindine tayʋʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ yem fɛɛɛ.

eng: However, the guards didnt find them in the prison. They returned and reported.
local: Paba woba patʋlɩ salaka nɔnɔsɩ lɛ, paanaɣ-wɛ. Peeɖe papɩsaa peheyi-wɛ se:.

eng: They praised God and demonstrated Gods goodness to everyone. The Lord added daily to the community those who were being saved.
local: Pasaŋaɣ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa laŋa hɛɣnɩ-wɛ. Mbá Kɩpaɣlʋ yabɩ pa-ñʋʋ yɔ, pɔsɔzaɣ pɔ-yɔɔ kɩyakɩŋ tɩŋa.

eng: We found the prison locked and well-secured, with guards standing at the doors, but when we opened the doors we found no one inside.
local: «Đɩtalaa lɛ, ɖɩmaɣnɩ paɖɩɣ salaka nɔnɔsɩ kpam nɛ ɖaŋɩyaa ñasɩŋ sɩ-yɔɔ nɛ paɖaŋ. Đɩtʋlɩ-sɩ nɛ ɖɩsʋʋ lɛ, ɖɩɩnaɣ nɔɔyʋ.».

eng: When they received this news, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were baffled and wondered what might be happening.
local: Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa nɔnɔɔ yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyaa ñʋʋdʋ nɛ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa panɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, paanaɣ se pɔyɔɔdɩ se we nɛ pɛwɛɛ pamaɣzɩɣ mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ labʋ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: Just then, someone arrived and announced, Look! The people you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.
local: Peeɖe nɔɔyʋ tɩkɔm yem nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ɩnaɣ! Mbá ɩɖʋ-wɛ salaka yɔ, pɛwɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ pawɩlɩɣ ɛyáa tɔm.».

eng: Then the captain left with his guards and brought the apostles back. They didnt use force because they were afraid the people would stone them.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ heyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɖaŋɩyaa ñʋʋdʋ nɛ ɛ-wayɩ ñɩma powolo pakpa-wɛ nɛ powona. Ɛlɛ́, pototukuni pa-taa nɔɔyʋ yɔɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, peseɣaɣ se ɛyáa samaɣ kayaɣ-wɛ pɛɛ.

eng: The apostles were brought before the council where the high priest confronted them:.
local: Patanɩ-wɛ tɔm hʋyaa sɔsaa cɔlɔ lɛ, cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ pazɩ-wɛ tɔm pɔzʋʋ.

eng: In no uncertain terms, we demanded that you not teach in this name. And look at you! You have filled Jerusalem with your teaching. And you are determined to hold us responsible for this mans death.
local: se: «Đikizini-mɩ kpem se ɩtaatasɩ wɩlʋʋ nɛ abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-nɔɔ lɛ, ɛbɛ ɩlaba yɔ? Iyeba nɛ ɩtɛzɩɣ mɩ-tɔm wɩlʋʋ Yeruusalɛm tɛtʋ tɩŋa payɩ nɛ ɩcaɣ se ɩkpaɣ ɛ-sɩm nɛ ɩsʋʋ-ɖʋ!».

eng: Peter and the apostles replied, We must obey God rather than humans!.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ nɛ ɛ-taabalaa tiyiyaa lɛlaa pocosaa se: «Pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩña Ɛsɔ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ɛyáa.

eng: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus from the dead—whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
local: Đɔ-cɔzɔnaa Ɛsɔ fezi Yesu weyi ɩkam kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɛsɩ yɔ.

eng: God has exalted Jesus to his right side as leader and savior so that he could enable Israel to change its heart and life and to find forgiveness for sins.
local: Efezi-i lɛ, ɛkpazɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛcaɣzɩ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ-ɩ Wiyaʋ Sɔsɔ weyi ɛkɛ Ñʋʋ Yaɖʋ yɔ se ɛwɩlɩ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma nʋmɔʋ nɛ pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ nɛ Ɛsɔ kpeɣ-wɛ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm.

eng: We are witnesses of such things, as is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.
local: Đɩkɛ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩ-aseɣɖe tɩnaa. Pɩtasɩɩnɩ ɖe-ɖeke, ɛlɛ́, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ŋgʋ́ Ɛsɔ ha mbá pañaŋ-ɩ yɔ, kɩkɛ tɩ-aseɣɖe tʋ ɖɔɖɔ.».

eng: When the council members heard this, they became furious and wanted to kill the apostles.
local: Tɔm hʋyaa nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pamʋ miŋ nɛ pacaɣ se pakʋ-wɛ.

eng: One council member, a Pharisee and teacher of the Law named Gamaliel, well-respected by all the people, stood up and ordered that the men be taken outside for a few moments.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Farɩsɩ ŋgbɛyɛ taa tʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa, payaɣ-ɩ se Gamaalɩyɛɛlɩ. Ɛkɛ paɣtʋ wɩlɩyʋ. Ɛyáa tɩŋa ñaŋ-ɩ. Ɛnʋ kʋyɩnɩ ɛsɩŋ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ se palɩzɩ-wɛ nɛ awayɩ nɛ pʋcɔ.

eng: He said, Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you intend to do to these people.
local: Palɩzɩ-wɛ lɛ, eheyi ɛyáa mbá paakpeɣli peeɖe yɔ se: «Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma mɩ! Ɩkpa mɩ-tɩ nɛ mbʋ́ ɩcaɣ se ɩla ɛyáa panɛ yɔ.

eng: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and some four hundred men joined him. After he was killed, all of his followers scattered, and nothing came of that.
local: Pɩtalabɩdɩ pʋyʋ yɔ, Teedaasɩ wɩlaɣ ɛ-tɩ. Abalʋ ɛnʋ, ɛkpazaɣ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋɩɣ ɛ-wayɩ, paatalɩ ɛzɩ nasɩnaza yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pakʋ-ɩ nɛ piyele ɛ-ɛyáa mɩzɩ ɖama patɩŋa payɩ nɛ pɩsɩɩ tɩnaɣ yem ḿbʋ́.

eng: Afterward, at the time of the census, Judas the Galilean appeared and got some people to follow him in a revolt. He was killed too, and all his followers scattered far and wide.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ wayɩ lɛ, Galilee tʋ weyi payaɣ se Yudaasɩ yɔ, ɛhɔ ɛyáa samaɣ nakɛyɛ kɩkalʋʋ alɩwaatʋ taa nɛ katɩŋɩɣ ɛ-wayɩ. Pakʋ ɛ́lɛ́ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ piyele ɛyáa ḿbá pamɩzɩ ɖama patɩŋa.

eng: Heres my recommendation in this case: Distance yourselves from these men. Let them go! If their plan or activity is of human origin, it will end in ruin.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ se: iyele ɛyáa panɛ ɖiɣ ɖiɣ nɛ pɔ-tɔm. Mbʋ́ palakɩ yɔ, yee ɛyáa cɔlɔ pɩlɩnaa yɔ, pʋkɔŋ sɩʋ tɩnaɣ.

eng: If it originates with God, you wont be able to stop them. Instead, you would actually find yourselves fighting God! The council was convinced by his reasoning.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ pɩlɩnaa yɔ, ɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɩɖɩɣ-wɛ nʋmɔʋ. Ɩkpa mɩ-tɩ ɖeu nɛ pɩtaakɔɔ puyozi mɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ!» Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, tɔm hʋyaa tisi ɛ-tɔm.

eng: After calling the apostles back, they had them beaten. They ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, then let them go.
local: Peeɖe paya tiyiyaa ḿbá nɛ polu-wɛ agbazɛɛ nɛ paka pa-ñʋŋ taa se pataatasɩ Yesu hɩɖɛ yaʋ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, peyebi-wɛ se pɛɖɛɛ.

eng: The apostles left the council rejoicing because they had been regarded as worthy to suffer disgrace for the sake of the name.
local: Ɛlɛ́, tiyiyaa ḿbá pakʋyɩ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ palɩɩnɩ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ nawa se pamʋnaa se pɔtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ Yesu yɔɔ.

eng: Every day they continued to teach and proclaim the good news that Jesus is the Christ, both in the temple and in houses.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pʋñɔɔzɩ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tiyiyaa ḿbá pawɩlaɣ nɛ posusuu Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm se Yesu lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ. Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa nɛ ɖɩsɩ pa-taa palakaɣ ḿbʋ́ kɩyakɩŋ tɩŋa.

eng: The high priest asked, Are these accusations true.
local: Pɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ pɔzɩ Etiyɛɛnɩ se: «Toovenim ḿbʋ́ pɩlaba?».

eng: Stephen responded, Brothers and fathers, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he settled in Haran.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: «Man-caanaa nɛ man-taabalaa mɩ! Ɩma nɩŋgbaaŋ nɛ ɩnɩɩ. Ɛsɔ koboyaɣ tʋ kalɩzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ ɖɔ-cɔzɔ Abraham alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛɛwɛ Mezoopotamii yɔ nɛ pʋcɔ ɛ́lɛ́ kʋyɩ ewolo Haraɣ.

eng: God told him, Leave your homeland and kin, and go to the land that I will show you.
local: Eeheyi-i se: ‹Kʋyɩ ñɛ-ɛjaɖɛ taa nɛ ŋyele ña-ñɩma nɛ ŋwolo ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ mawɩlɩɣ-ŋ yɔ ɖɩ-taa.›.

eng: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After Abrahams father died, God had him resettle in this land where you now live.
local: Peeɖe Abraham kʋyɩ Babilooni ñɩma ɛjaɖɛ taa nɛ ewolo ɛcaɣ Haraɣ. Ɛ-caa sɩm wayɩ Ɛsɔ kʋsɩ-ɩ Haraɣ peeɖe nɛ ɛkɔnɩ-ɩ ɖɛ-tɛ Izrɛɛlɩ cɩnɛ.

eng: God didnt give him an inheritance here, not even a square foot of land. However, God did promise to give the land as his possession to him and to his descendants, even though Abraham had no child.
local: Ɛtaaha-ɩ paa tɛtʋ hɔɔlaɣ maɣmaɣ se esobi. Ɛlɛ́, ɛɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ-ɩ ɛjaɖɛ ɖɩnɛ ɖɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ ɛ-sɩm wayɩ lɛ, ɛ-sayɩnaa tɩɣyɩ-ɖɩ. Ŋgʋ́, alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa lɛ, Abraham taawɛnɩ pɩɣa.

eng: God put it this way: His descendants will be strangers in a land that belongs to others, who will enslave them and abuse them for four hundred years. .
local: Tɔm ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ keheyi-i yɔ, ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Ña-sayɩnaa kɔŋ caɣʋ ɛjaɖɛ naɖɩyɛ taa, papɩsɩɣ-wɛ yomaa nɛ panazɩ-wɛ pɩnzɩ nasɩnaza.

eng: And I will condemn the nation they serve as slaves, God said, and afterward they will leave that land and serve me in this place.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mɔnkɔŋ hɔm mbá papɩsɩɣ ña-sayɩnaa ḿbá yomaa yɔ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ña-sayɩnaa ḿbá, pakʋyʋʋ ɛjaɖɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa nɛ papɩsɩ cɩnɛ nɛ poluŋ mɛn-tɛɛ.›.

eng: God gave him the covenant confirmed through circumcision. Accordingly, eight days after Isaacs birth, Abraham circumcised him. Isaac did the same with Jacob, and Jacob with the twelve patriarchs.
local: Ɛsɔ kaha Abraham nɔɔ. Pɛlʋʋ sɔnzɩ wɩlɩɣnɩ se ɛha-ɩ-kɛ. Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ ɛlʋlɩ ɛ-pɩyalʋ Izaakɩ lɛ, ɛpɛlɩ-ɩ kɩyakʋ lutozo ñɩŋgʋ wiye, Izaakɩ ñɛpɛlɩ ɛ́lɛ́ pɩyalʋ Yakɔɔbʋ ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ Yakɔɔbʋ ñɛpɛlɩ ɛ-pɩyalaa hiu nɛ naalɛ mbá pɛkɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma cɔzɔnaa yɔ.

eng: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him into slavery in Egypt. God was with him, however.
local: Cɔzɔnaa ḿbá palabɩnɩ ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ pa-taa lɛlʋ weyi payaɣ se Yozɛɛfʋ yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ pɛpɛdɩ-ɩ nɛ nabɛyɛ nɛ paba woni-i Ejiptɩ nɛ papɩsɩ-ɩ yom. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ taalɔ-ɩ kaʋ.

eng: and rescued him from all his troubles. The grace and wisdom he gave Joseph were recognized by Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over his whole palace.
local: Ɛlɩzaɣ-ɩ ɛ-kʋñɔmɩŋ tɩŋa taa. Patanɩ-ɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Faraɔɔ ɛsɩndaa lɛ, Ɛsɔ kʋɖʋm ɛnʋ eyele nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ laŋɩyɛ hɛɛnɩ ɛ-lɔŋsɩnɖɛ. Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ cɛlɩ-ɩ kewiyaɣ se ɛcɔnɩ Ejiptɩ ɛjaɖɛ yɔɔ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ ɛ-ɖɩɣa tɩŋa.

eng: A famine came upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great hardship came with it. Our ancestors had nothing to eat.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa ñɔɔsɩ nasɩyɩ lɩ Ejiptɩ nɛ Kanaanɩ pa-ajɛya hɔɔlɩŋ tɩŋa. Sɩlɩwa lɛ, ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa wɛɛ kʋñɔŋ sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ taa nɛ paanaɣ pʋyʋ se pɔtɔɔ.

eng: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, pa-caa Yakɔɔbʋ nɩɩ se tɔɔnaɣ wɛ Ejiptɩ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛkʋsɩ-wɛ nɛ etiyi-wɛ.

eng: During their second visit, Joseph told his brothers who he was, and Pharaoh learned about Josephs family.
local: Etiyi-wɛ nabʋlɛ ɖeɖe lɛ, Yozɛɛfʋ yele se ɛ-ɖalaa ɛsɩm-ɩ. Peeɖe wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Faraɔɔ ɛnʋ, ɛtɩlɩ Yozɛɛfʋ ñɩma.

eng: Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives—seventy-five in all—and invited them to live with him.
local: Ḿbʋ́ hanɩ Yozɛɛfʋ waɖɛ nɛ etiyi se powolo pɔkɔnɩ ɛ-caa Yakɔɔbʋ nɛ ɛ-hɔʋ taa ñɩma tɩŋa. Paakpɛndɩ ɛyáa nɩɩnʋwa nɛ nɩɩlɛ nɛ kagbaanzɩ.

eng: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our ancestors died.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Yakɔɔbʋ woba Ejiptɩ ɛcaɣ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ ɛ-sɩm. Peeɖe ɖɔɖɔ ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa sɩba.

eng: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a certain sum of money from Hamors children, who lived in Shechem.
local: Pasɩba lɛ, pepim-wɛ. Pʋwayɩ pakʋyaɣ peeɖe lɛ, pokuli pɔ-mɔɔ nɛ powoni Siicem nɛ pepi pɩlaʋ ŋgʋ́ Abraham kayabɩ Aamɔɔrɩ pɩyalaa cɔlɔ yɔ kɩ-taa.

eng: When it was time for God to keep the promise he made to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt had greatly expanded.
local: Tɔm ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ kaɖʋwa nɛ eheyi Abraham yɔ, tɩ-alɩwaatʋ wɩzaa. Ɖa-ñɩma mbá paawɛ Ejiptɩ yɔ, pɛtɛzɩɣ ɖɔʋ kʋɖɔʋ.

eng: But then another king rose to power over Egypt who didnt know anything about Joseph. .
local: Tɩ-taa ɖɔɖɔ awiya sakɩyɛ kɔtɔɔ kewiyaɣ nɛ pɩɖɛɛ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, wiyaʋ sɔsɔ lɛlʋ ñakpa kpelaɣ yɔɔ Ejiptɩ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛtaatɩlɩ Yozɛɛfʋ.

eng: He exploited our people and abused our ancestors. He even forced them to abandon their newly born babies so they would die.
local: Ɛnʋ kɛcɛtɩnɩ ɖɛ-tɛ ñɩma nɛ ɛnazɩ ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa nɛ halɩ ɛcɛyɩ-wɛ se pɔlɔ pe-piya cikpesi nɛ sɩsɩ.

eng: Thats when Moses was born. He was highly favored by God, and for three months his parents cared for him in their home.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa ɖɔɖɔ palʋlɩ pɩɣa ŋgá payaɣ se Mooyiizi yɔ, kaaɖe Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa pɩŋŋ. Ka-ñɩma kɔɖɔkɩ-kɛ fenasɩ naadozo nɛ pʋcɔ pɔlɔ-kɛ.

eng: After he was abandoned, Pharaohs daughter adopted and cared for him as though he were her own son.
local: Pɔlɔ-kɛ lɛ, wiyaʋ sɔsɔ pɛlɛ hii-kɛ nɛ ɛɖɔkɩ-kɛ ɛzɩ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-pɩyalʋ yɔ.

eng: Moses learned everything Egyptian wisdom had to offer, and he was a man of powerful words and deeds.
local: Ḿbʋ́ hanɩ Mooyiizi waɖɛ nɛ ɛkpɛlɩkɩ mbʋ́ Ejiptɩ ñɩma sɩma yɔ pɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ tɔm nɛ ɛlakɩ tʋma nɛ abalɩtʋ.

eng: When Moses was years old, he decided to visit his family, the Israelites.
local: Ɛkɔm ɛwɛnɩ pɩnzɩ nɩɩnaza lɛ, ɛɖʋ ɛ-taa se ewoki ɛna ɛ-tɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma.

eng: He saw one of them being wronged so he came to his rescue and evened the score by killing the Egyptian.
local: Ewokaɣ lɛ, ɛna Ejiptɩ tʋ nɔɔyʋ makaɣ Izrɛɛlɩ tʋ nɔɔyʋ. Peeɖe ɛtɩnɩ weyi pamakaɣ-ɩ yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛma Ejiptɩ tʋ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ sɩ.

eng: He expected his own kin to understand that God was using him to rescue them, but they didnt.
local: Mooyiizi kɛwɛ yem se ɛ-tɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma ɖiɣzinaa se ɛ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋɩɣna nɛ Ɛsɔ lɩzɩ-wɛ yomiye taa. Naze lɛ, paba tanɩɩ pɩ-taa ḿbʋ́.

eng: The next day he came upon some Israelites who were caught up in an argument. He tried to make peace between them by saying, You are brothers! Why are you harming each other.
local: Kifema lɛ, Mooyiizi tasɩ katʋʋ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma naalɛ nabɛyɛ, poyowaɣ. Peeɖe ewobi se eloluu-wɛ, ɛlɛ́ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: ‹Man-taabalaa! Ɩkɛ ɛyáa kʋɖʋmaa lɛ, ɛbɛ laba nɛ ɩmakɩ ɖama ḿbʋ́?›.
