eng: The one who started the fight against his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? .
local: Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛnazaɣ lɛlʋ yɔ, ɛpɩsɩ etuzi Mooyiizi nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: ‹Asɩ-ŋ wiyaʋ se ŋhʋʋ ɖɔ-tɔm?.

eng: Are you planning to kill me like you killed that Egyptian yesterday? .
local: Ŋcaɣ se ŋkʋ-m ɛzɩ ɖeɖe ŋkʋʋ Ejiptɩ tʋ yɔ yaa we?›.

eng: When Moses heard this, he fled to Midian, where he lived as an immigrant and had two sons.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Mooyiizi se nɛ ewolo ɛcaɣ Maadiyaɣ ɛjaɖɛ taa. Peeɖe ɛkɔm ɛwɛnɩ abalɩbiya naalɛ.

eng: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses in the flame of a burning bush in the wilderness near Mount Sinai.
local: Ɛcaɣ peeɖe pɩnzɩ nɩɩnaza lɛ, nakʋyʋ wiye ewolo lakʋ taa Sinayii pʋʋ cɔlɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ lɩzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ hɔɖaɣ miŋ taa nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-ɩ.

eng: Enthralled by the sight, Moses approached to get a closer look and he heard the Lords voice:.
local: Ɛna ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩlabɩ-ɩ piti. Ɛlɛ́, etuzi ɛ-tɩ pʋ-cɔlɔ se ɛcɔna. Peeɖe ɛnɩɣ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔdʋʋnɩ-ɩ se:.

eng: I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Trembling with fear, Moses didnt dare to investigate any further.
local: ‹Ma lɛ ñɔ-cɔzɔnaa Ɛsɔ. Ma lɛ Abraham nɛ Izaakɩ nɛ Yakɔɔbʋ pɛ-Ɛsɔ.› Mooyiizi nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, sɔɔndʋ kpa-ɩ pɩŋŋ nɛ eseliɣ, halɩ ɛtacazɩ cɔnaʋ.

eng: The Lord continued, Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Kɩpaɣlʋ heyi-i se: ‹Đenɖe ŋsɩŋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pɩkɛ tɛtʋ kaɖɛ ñɩndʋ. Hɔzɩ ña-naataŋgbala.

eng: I have clearly seen the oppression my people have experienced in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning. I have come down to rescue them. Come! I am sending you to Egypt .
local: Mana mɛ-ɛyáa mbá pɛwɛ Ejiptɩ yɔ pa-kʋñɔŋ nɛ manɩɩ pa-atɩma nɛ mentii se moholi-wɛ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, kɔɔ mentiyi-ŋ Ejiptɩ peeɖe.›.

eng: This is the same Moses whom they rejected when they asked, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? This is the Moses whom God sent as leader and deliverer. God did this with the help of the angel who appeared before him in the bush.
local: Ḿbʋ́ Ɛsɔ tiyi Mooyiizi. Ŋgʋ́, Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma kekizi-i nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: ‹Asɩ-ŋ wiyaʋ se ŋhʋʋ ɖɔ-tɔm?› Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ tiyi-i se ewolo ɛkɛ pe-wiyaʋ nɛ ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ yomiye taa. Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛɛlɩzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ hɔɖaɣ miŋ taa nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-ɩ yɔ, e-ɖoŋ yɔɔ ɛtɩŋnaa.

eng: This man led them out after he performed wonders and signs in Egypt at the Red Sea and for forty years in the wilderness.
local: Mooyiizi ɛnʋ ɛlabɩnɩ Ɛsɔ hama lakasɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ kawɩlɩsɩ Ejiptɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa nɛ ɛlɩzɩ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma yomiye taa. Ɛɛlabɩ-sɩ Teŋgu Kɩsɛmʋʋ nɛ lakʋ pa-taa pɩnzɩ nɩɩnaza nzɩ́ pɔɖɔma yɔ.

eng: This is the Moses who told the Israelites, God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your own people. .
local: Ɛnʋ ɖɔɖɔ heyini Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma se: ‹Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-mɩ tiyinaʋ náyʋ nɔɔyʋ ɛzɩ ma yɔ, ɛkɛɣ mɩ-ñɩma taa nɔɔyʋ.›.

eng: This is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness with our ancestors and with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai. He is the one who received life-giving words to give to us.
local: Ɛnʋ kɛwɛnɩ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma mbá paakpeɣli lakʋ taa yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ: ɛ nɛ ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa nɛ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛyɔɔdaɣnɩ-ɩ nɛ Sinayii pʋʋ yɔɔ yɔ paawɛna. Ɛnʋ mʋnɩ wezuu tɔm nɛ ɛcɛlɩ-ɖʋ.

eng: Hes also the one whom our ancestors refused to obey. Instead, they pushed him aside and, in their thoughts and desires, returned to Egypt.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa taacaɣ se pañam-ɩ. Pekizaɣ ɛ-tɔm nɛ pañɩnɩɣ se papɩsɩ Ejiptɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa.

eng: They told Aaron, Make us gods that will lead us. As for this Moses who led us out of Egypt, we dont know whats happened to him! .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ peheyi Aarɔɔnɩ se: ‹Ma-ɖʋ sɩʋ kpelisi nɛ sɩɖɛɛ-ɖʋ nɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩtana mbʋ́ pɩtɛmnɩ ɖo-Mooyiizi weyi ɛlɩzɩ-ɖʋ Ejiptɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa yɔ.›.

eng: Thats when they made an idol in the shape of a calf, offered a sacrifice to it, and began to celebrate what they had made with their own hands.
local: Peeɖe pama neɣye nɛ palaa ɖɩ-yɔɔ nɛ pa-taa hʋlʋmɩnɩ mbʋ́ pa-maɣmaɣ pamanɩ pe-nesi yɔ.

eng: So God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the stars in the sky, just as it is written in the scroll of the Prophets: Did you bring sacrifices and offerings to me for forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel? .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ ha-wɛ siɣ́ye. Eyebi-wɛ nɛ poluŋuu wɩsɩ nɛ fenaɣ nɛ tɩɩnzɩ pɛ-tɛɛ ɛzɩ náyaa maʋ pa-takayɩsɩ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: ‹Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma mɩ! Ma ɩla kpɩna nɛ kɩlaŋ lɛɛŋ ḿbʋ́, lakʋ taa pɩnzɩ nɩɩnaza yaa we?.

eng: No! Instead, you took the tent of Moloch with you, and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made in order to worship them. Therefore, I will send you far away, farther than Babylon. .
local: Pɩtɛkɛ ma. Ɛlɛ́, ɩsɩɣlɩ ɛgɔlɩmɩyɛ Moolɔkɩ sɩʋ kɩɖanɖʋʋ. Ɩsɩɣlɩ ɛgɔlɩmɩyɛ Refaɣ sɩʋ kpelaɣ. Sɩʋ kpelisi ńzɩ́ ɩɩlaba nɛ iluŋuu sɩ-tɛɛ! Pʋ-yɔɔ mɛnkɛzɩɣ-mɩ tɛtʋ, nɛ ɖooo Babilooni ɛjaɖɛ wayɩ!› ».

eng: The tent of testimony was with our ancestors in the wilderness. Moses built it just as he had been instructed by the one who spoke to him and according to the pattern he had seen.
local: Etiyɛɛnɩ tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ se: «Đɔ-cɔzɔnaa kesiɣ kɩɖanɖʋʋ lakʋ taa nɛ kɩwɩlɩɣ se pa nɛ Ɛsɔ pɛwɛna. Pesiɣ-kʋ ɛzɩ ɛ́lɛ́ keheyuu Mooyiizi yɔ: eeheyi-i se esiɣ-kʋ ɛzɩ ŋgʋ́ ɛwɩlɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: In time, when they had received the tent, our ancestors carried it with them when, under Joshuas leadership, they took possession of the land from the nations whom God expelled. This tent remained in the land until the time of David.
local: Đɔ-cɔzɔnaa ḿbá, paayebi kɩɖanɖʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́ nɛ pa-wayɩ ñɩma. Paakpɛndɩnɩ-kʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Yozuwee ketaɣ-wɛ nɛ pɛlɛɣ ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ pɛ-ɛsɩndaa nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɔɔnɩ ɖɩ-taa ñɩma yɔ. Kʋwɛ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pɩtalɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Daaviidi alɩwaatʋ.

eng: God approved of David, who asked that he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
local: Yakɔɔbʋ Ɛsɔ kalabɩ-ɩ lootiye nɛ etendi-i se ɛ́lɛ́ ɛha-ɩ waɖɛ nɛ ɛma-ɩ ɖɩɣa.

eng: But it was Solomon who actually built a house for God.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛ-pɩyalʋ Salomɔɔ manɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa.

eng: However, the Most High doesnt live in houses built by human hands. As the prophet says.
local: Ŋgʋ́, Ɛsɔ Caabiyuu ɛɛsʋʋ kuduyiŋ weyi ɛyáa manɩ pa-maɣmaɣ pe-nesi yɔ ɩ-tɛɛ. Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ tɔm ndʋ́ náyʋ kɔyɔɔdaa yɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se:.

eng: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or where is my resting place? .
local: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔdaa se ɛsɔdaa lɛ men-kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ nɛ tɛtʋ lɛ kpeɖe nɖɩ́ mantɩnɩɣ ma-naŋgbanzɩ ɖɩ-yɔɔ yɔ. Đɩɣa ŋgá ɖɔɖɔ ɩmaɣ-m yɔ? Le manpɩzɩɣ mancaɣ nɛ mɛhɛzɩ?.

eng: Didnt I make all these things with my own hand? .
local: Pɩtɛkɛ ma-maɣmaɣ malabɩnɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ yaa we?› ».

eng: You stubborn people! In your thoughts and hearing, you are like those who have had no part in Gods covenant! You continuously set yourself against the Holy Spirit, just like your ancestors did.
local: Etiyɛɛnɩ tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛtɔ se: «Laŋkpɩzɩyɛ tɩnaa mbá mɩ mɩ-laŋa nɛ mɩ-nɩŋgbaaŋ pɛwɛ ɖoŋ yɔ! Ɩwɛɛ ikiziɣ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tɔm ɛzɩ mɩ-cɔzɔnaa yɔ!.

eng: Was there a single prophet your ancestors didnt harass? They even killed those who predicted the coming of the righteous one, and youve betrayed and murdered him!.
local: Náyʋ weyi ɛ-wayɩ mɩ-cɔzɔnaa keyebi wɛʋ kaŋ kaŋ? Halɩ pakʋ mbá patɩŋa payɩ paayɔɔdaa se pɔkɔŋ lʋlʋʋ Weyi Ɛtʋʋzaa yɔ. Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɛnʋ ɩɖʋzɩ koyindinaa nesi taa nɛ pakʋ-ɩ.

eng: You received the Law given by angels, but you havent kept it.
local: Mbá mɩ Ɛsɔ tɩŋnɩ e-tiyiyaa yɔɔ nɛ ɛcɛlɩ-mɩ ɛ-paɣtʋ yɔ, ɩtɔɖɔ tɩ-yɔɔ!».

eng: Once the council members heard these words, they were enraged and began to grind their teeth at Stephen.
local: Mbá patɩŋa paawɛ tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ peeɖe yɔ, panɩ Etiyɛɛnɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa-pana kpa pɩŋŋ nɛ pɔtɔkɩ po-ndoma yem.

eng: But Stephen, enabled by the Holy Spirit, stared into heaven and saw Gods majesty and Jesus standing at Gods right side.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu kɛwɛ Etiyɛɛnɩ yɔɔ. Pɔtɔkaɣ po-ndoma ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛcɔnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ ɛna Ɛsɔ e-koboyaɣ taa, Yesu ñasɩŋ ɛ-cɔlɔ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa.

eng: He exclaimed, Look! I can see heaven on display and the Human One standing at Gods right side.
local: Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Iwelesi nɛ ɩnɩɩ: ɛsɔdaa tʋlaa nɛ manaɣ Ɛyʋ Pɩyalʋ sɩŋ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa.».

eng: At this, they shrieked and covered their ears. Together, they charged at him.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, papazɩ kou nɛ paɖɩɣ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ! Peeɖe pakʋyɩ patɩŋa pekpibi-i.

eng: threw him out of the city, and began to stone him. The witnesses placed their coats in the care of a young man named Saul.
local: nɛ pɔhɔm-ɩ nɛ palɩnɩ tɛtʋ wayɩ nɛ payakɩ-ɩ pɛɛ se ɛsɩ. Aseɣɖe tɩnaa kɔhɔzɩ po-tokonaa nɛ pasɩɩ evebu weyi payaɣ se Sɔɔlɩ yɔ ɛ-cɔlɔ se ɛcɔnɩ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: As they battered him with stones, Stephen prayed, Lord Jesus, accept my life.
local: Payakaɣ-ɩ pɛɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, etendi Kɩpaɣlʋ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu! Mʋ man-kalɩzaɣ!».

eng: Falling to his knees, he shouted, Lord, dont hold this sin against them! Then he died.
local: Peeɖe eluŋ ɛ-nɩmbɔla yɔɔ nɛ ɛma kubuka ɖoŋ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Taakpaɣ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm nɛ ŋlabɩnɩ tʋmɩyɛ!» Ɛtɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛhɛɛ.

eng: There was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion in the Italian Company.
local: Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ Sezaaree, payaɣ-ɩ se Kɔɔrnɛɛyɩ. Ɛɛkɛ Room sɔɔjanaa tikaɣ ŋgá payaɣ se Itaaliyɛɛ yɔ ka-ñʋʋdʋ.

eng: He and his whole household were pious, Gentile God-worshippers. He gave generously to those in need among the Jewish people and prayed to God constantly.
local: Ɛlakaɣ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ yɔ nɛ ɛ nɛ ɛ-hɔʋ taa ñɩma tɩŋa payɩ pañaŋ Ɛsɔ. Ɛsɩŋaɣnɩ Yuda ñɩma kʋñɔndɩnaa siŋŋ nɛ ɛpaŋ Ɛsɔ sɛʋ.

eng: One day at nearly three oclock in the afternoon, he clearly saw an angel from God in a vision. The angel came to him and said, Cornelius.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye pʋkɔma ɛzɩ ɖanaɣ wɩsɩ wɛlʋʋ yɔ lɛ, eɖozi se ɛna Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ sʋ ɛ-tɛ nɛ ɛ́yáa-ɩ se: «Kɔɔrnɛɛyɩ!».

eng: Startled, he stared at the angel and replied, What is it, Lord? The angel said, Your prayers and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to God.
local: Peeɖe ɛcɔnɩ tiyiyu ɛnʋ nɛ sɔɔndʋ kpa-ɩ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Alaafɩya lɛ?» Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: «Ɛsɔ nɩ ñe-tenduu nɛ ɛna sɩnaʋ mbʋ́ ŋsɩnɩ kʋñɔndɩnaa yɔ, ɛɛsɔʋ ñɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: Send messengers to Joppa at once and summon a certain Simon, the one known as Peter.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, kʋsɩ ɛyáa lɛɛlɛɛyɔ nɛ ŋtiyi-wɛ Yopee nɛ powolo payaa abalʋ weyi payaɣ se Siimɔɔ nɛ pakʋm-ɩ hɩɖɛ se Pɩyɛ yɔ.

eng: He is a guest of Simon the tanner, whose house is near the seacoast.
local: Ɛwɛ naataŋgbala kanɖʋ weyi payaɣ se Siimɔɔ yɔ ɛ-ɖɩɣa taa teŋgu nɔɔ.».

eng: When the angel who was speaking to him had gone, Cornelius summoned two of his household servants along with a pious soldier from his personal staff.
local: Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛyɔɔdɩnɩ Kɔɔrnɛɛyɩ yɔ, ɛɖɛwa lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ tɩkʋsɩ ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa naalɛ nɛ ɛ-sɔɔjanaa mbá ɛ nɛ wɛ pamalɩnɩ ɖama yɔ pa-taa lɛlʋ weyi ɛlakɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ yɔ.

eng: He explained everything to them, then sent them to Joppa.
local: nɛ ɛkɛdɩ-wɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩlaba yɔ nɛ etiyi-wɛ Yopee.

eng: At noon on the following day, as their journey brought them close to the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
local: Tɛʋ fem-wɛ nʋmɔʋ taa nɛ pɔkɔɔ pɔñɔtɩnɩ Yopee lɛ, Pɩyɛ ñaakpa kuduyuu ñʋʋ taa se ɛsɛɣ Ɛsɔ, pɩɩmaɣ ɛzɩ wɩsɩ siɣu yɔ.

eng: He became hungry and wanted to eat. While others were preparing the meal, he had a visionary experience.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, ñɔɔsɩ kpa-ɩ nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ se palabɩ-ɩ tɔɔnaɣ. Palakaɣ-kɛ lɛ, eɖozi se.

eng: Meanwhile, Saul was still spewing out murderous threats against the Lords disciples. He went to the high priest.
local: Sɔɔlɩ ñataanaɣ Kɩpaɣlʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa nɛ ɛsɩyɛ nɛ ɛcaɣ se ɛkʋ-wɛ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ewobi cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ cɔlɔ.

eng: He saw heaven opened up and something like a large linen sheet being lowered to the earth by its four corners.
local: ɛna ɛsɔdaa tʋlaa nɛ pʋyʋ nɔɔyʋ lɩɩ ɛzɩ pɩsaʋ sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ yɔ nɛ pitiki tataa. Patam kɩ-pama naanza taa.

eng: seeking letters to the synagogues in Damascus. If he found persons who belonged to the Way, whether men or women, these letters would authorize him to take them as prisoners to Jerusalem.
local: se ɛ́lɛ́ ɛha-ɩ takayaɣ ŋgá kahaɣ-ɩ waɖɛ se ewolo Damaasɩ Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ taa nɛ yee ɛna halaa yaa abalaa nabɛyɛ patɩŋɩɣ Kɩpaɣlʋ nʋmɔʋ yɔ, ɛkpa-wɛ ɛhɔkɩ-wɛ nɛ ewoni Yeruusalɛm.

eng: Inside the sheet were all kinds of four-legged animals, reptiles, and wild birds.
local: Kpɩna wena awɛnɩ naŋgbanzɩ naanza naanza yɔ atɩŋa payɩ nɛ tɛtɛ ñɩtʋ tɩŋa nɛ sumasɩ ndɩ ndɩ paawɛnɩ kɩ-taa.

eng: During the journey, as he approached Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven encircled him.
local: Ɛkpaɣ nʋmɔʋ nɛ ɛñɔtɩnɩ Damaasɩ lɛ, ñalɩmɩyɛ naɖɩyɛ tɩlɩʋnɩ ɛsɔdaa yem nɛ ɖitii ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: A voice told him, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Peeɖe ɛnɩ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-ɩ se: «Pɩyɛ! Kʋyɩ ŋkpa nɛ ŋkʋ nɛ ŋtɔɔ!».

eng: He fell to the ground and heard a voice asking him, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Peeɖe etibi ɛfalɩ tataa nɛ ɛnɩɣ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-ɩ se: «Sɔɔlɩ! Sɔɔlɩ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋwɛɛ ma-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ ḿbʋ́?».

eng: Peter exclaimed, Absolutely not, Lord! I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ cosi se: «Pɩlɩ! Kɩpaɣlʋ, wiɖiyi mɔntɔtɔɔda mbʋ́ ŋɖʋwa se pataatɔɔ yɔ yaa mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ yɔ.».

eng: Saul asked, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus, whom you are harassing, came the reply.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Sɔɔlɩ pɔzɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ña anɩ?» Pucosi-i se: «Ma Yesu weyi ŋwɛ ɛ-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ yɔ.

eng: The voice spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Peeɖe pɩtasɩ-ɩ heyuu se: «Taakpaɣ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ tɔm se pʋwɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ yɔ se pɩfɛyɩ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ.».

eng: Now get up and enter the city. You will be told what you must do.
local: Kʋyɩ ŋwolo tɛtʋ taa nɛ peheyi-ŋ mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se ŋla yɔ.».

eng: This happened three times, then the object was suddenly pulled back into heaven.
local: Pɩla ḿbʋ́ nɛ pʋɖʋ tozo nɛ pɩtɩkʋsɩ pɩsaʋ ŋ́gʋ́ yem nɛ pɩkpazɩ-kʋ ɛsɔdaa.

eng: Those traveling with him stood there speechless; they heard the voice but saw no one.
local: Mbá ɛ nɛ wɛ pɔɖɔŋaɣ yɔ, pasɩŋ pe-ɖeɖe yem, paanaɣ pɔyɔɔdɩ se we: panɩɣ pʋyɔɔdʋʋ, ɛlɛ́, paanaɣ weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ.

eng: Peter was bewildered about the meaning of the vision. Just then, the messengers sent by Cornelius discovered the whereabouts of Simons house and arrived at the gate.
local: Pɩyɛ kɛwɛɛ ɛmaɣzɩɣ se ɛnɩɩ e-ɖoziye tɔbʋʋ. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa ɛyáa mbá Kɔɔrnɛɛyɩ ketiyaa yɔ pɔpɔzɩ ka ka nɛ pɔkɔɔ patalɩ Siimɔɔ ɖɩɣa nɔnɔɔ taa.

eng: After they picked Saul up from the ground, he opened his eyes but he couldnt see. So they led him by the hand into Damascus.
local: Sɔɔlɩ kʋyɩ tataa peeɖe nɛ ɛcɔŋna lɛ, ɛɛnaɣ. Peeɖe pɔ́ɖɔ́kɩ e-nesi taa nɛ pɔhɔm-ɩ nɛ powoni Damaasɩ.

eng: Calling out, they inquired whether the Simon known as Peter was a guest there.
local: Patalaa lɛ, payaa nɛ pɔpɔzɩ se: «Weyi payaɣ se Siimɔɔ nɛ pakʋm-ɩ hɩɖɛ se Pɩyɛ yɔ, ɛ-tɛ lɛ cɩnɛ yaa we?».

eng: For three days he was blind and neither ate nor drank anything.
local: Ɛlabɩ kɩyakɩŋ naadozo, ɛtana, ɛtɔtɔɔ, ɛtɔñɔɔ.

eng: While Peter was brooding over the vision, the Spirit interrupted him, Look! Three people are looking for you.
local: Pɩyɛ maɣzaɣ se ɛnɩɩ e-ɖoziye tɔbʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu heyi-i se: «Na! Ɛyáa naadozo nabɛyɛ kɔm pañɩnɩɣ-ŋ.

eng: In Damascus there was a certain disciple named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, Ananias! He answered, Yes, Lord.
local: Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ Damaasɩ peeɖe, payaɣ-ɩ se Anaanɩyaasɩ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ lɩzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-ɩ ɖoziye taa nɛ ɛ́yáa-ɩ se: «Anaanɩyaasɩ!» Ɛlɛ́, ɛsɛɛ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ma yɔ.».

eng: Go downstairs. Dont ask questions; just go with them because I have sent them.
local: Kʋyɩ ŋtii nɛ ña nɛ wɛ ɩkpaɣ nʋmɔʋ, taahʋʋ natʋyʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mentiyini-wɛ ña-wayɩ.».

eng: The Lord instructed him, Go to Judas house on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is praying.
local: Peeɖe Kɩpaɣlʋ heyi-i se: «Kʋyɩ kpaka nɛ ŋkpaɣ nʋmɔʋ ŋgʋ́ payaɣ se Kʋtʋʋzʋʋ yɔ nɛ ŋwolo Yudaasɩ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ ŋpɔzɩ Taarsɩ tʋ weyi payaɣ se Sɔɔlɩ yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ, ɛɖɔŋ sɛʋ Ɛsɔ.

eng: So Peter went downstairs and told them, Im the one you are looking for. Why have you come.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ tibi ɛyáa ḿbá pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ma lɛ weyi ɩñɩnɩɣ yɔ. Alaafɩya lɛ?».

eng: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias enter and put his hands on him to restore his sight.
local: Ɛsɛɣaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɩwɩlɩ-ɩ ɖoziye taa abalʋ nɔɔyʋ, payaɣ-ɩ se Anaanɩyaasɩ, ɛsʋwa ɛtɩnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nesi se ɛ-ɛsɛ ekuli.».

eng: They replied, Weve come on behalf of Cornelius, a centurion and righteous man, a God-worshipper who is well-respected by all Jewish people. A holy angel directed him to summon you to his house and to hear what you have to say.
local: Pocosi-i se: «Sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ Kɔɔrnɛɛyɩ tiyini-ɖʋ. Ɛkɛ ɛyʋ kʋtʋʋzʋ, ɛñaŋ Ɛsɔ nɛ Yuda ñɩma tɩŋa lɩzɩɣ aseɣɖe kɩbanɖɛ ɛ-yɔɔ. Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ heyi-i se ɛya-ŋ nɛ ŋwolo ɛ-tɛ nɛ ŋheyi-i ña-taa tɔm.».

eng: Ananias countered, Lord, I have heard many reports about this man. People say he has done horrible things to your holy people in Jerusalem.
local: Anaanɩyaasɩ cosi-i se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ɛyáa sakɩyɛ heyi-m abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-tɔm nɛ pɛkɛdɩ-m ɛzaɣ́tʋ ndʋ́ ɛlabɩ ña-ñɩma mbá pɛwɛ Yeruusalɛm yɔ.

eng: Saul was in full agreement with Stephens murder. At that time, the church in Jerusalem began to be subjected to vicious harassment. Everyone except the apostles was scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Etiyɛɛnɩ sɩm ḿbʋ́, pɩkɛdɩnɩ Sɔɔlɩ. Puwiye ńɖɩ́, Yeruusalɛm Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ sʋ kʋñɔŋ sɔsɔʋ taa. Lidaʋ tayaa tɩŋa se nɛ pɛɖɛɛnɩ Yudee nɛ Samarɩɩ ageeta taa. Yesu tiyiyaa ɖeke pɩɩkazaa.

eng: Peter invited them into the house as his guests. The next day he got up and went with them, together with some of the believers from Joppa.
local: Peheyi Pɩyɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛsʋnɩ-wɛ ɖɩɣa nɛ pasʋʋ ɛ-tɛ puwiye ńɖɩ́. Kifema lɛ, ɛ nɛ wɛ pakpaɣ nʋmɔʋ. Yopee taabalaa nabɛyɛ kasɩ-ɩ.

eng: Hes here with authority from the chief priests to arrest everyone who calls on your name.
local: Ɛkɔm cɩnɛ yɔ, cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa hanɩ-ɩ waɖɛ se ɛkɔɔ ɛkpa mbá patɩŋa payɩ poluŋuu ñɛ-tɛɛ yɔ nɛ ɛhɔkɩ-wɛ.».

eng: Some pious men buried Stephen and deeply grieved over him.
local: Lidaʋ tayaa kpaɣ Etiyɛɛnɩ powona pebi nɛ pewii-i sakɩyɛ.

eng: They arrived in Caesarea the following day. Anticipating their arrival, Cornelius had gathered his relatives and close friends.
local: Kɩyakʋ ŋ́gʋ́ ki-kifemuu patalɩ Sezaaree. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, Kɔɔrnɛɛyɩ kaya ɛ-ñɩma nɛ ɛ-taabalaa nɛ pacaɣ paɖaŋ-wɛ.

eng: The Lord replied, Go! This man is the agent I have chosen to carry my name before Gentiles, kings, and Israelites.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Kɩpaɣlʋ heyi-i se: «Kʋyɩ ŋwolo, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, malɩzɩnɩ-ɩ se pɩtɩŋnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ajɛya lɛɛna taa ñɩma nɛ pa-awiya sɔsɔna nɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma pasɩm-m.

eng: Saul began to wreak havoc against the church. Entering one house after another, he would drag off both men and women and throw them into prison.
local: Sɔɔlɩ ñakaɣ pana se eɖezi Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ: ɛsʋwaɣ ɖɩsɩ taa nɛ ɛkpaɣ halaa nɛ abalaa nɛ ɛɖʋʋ-wɛ salaka.

eng: As Peter entered the house, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet in order to honor him.
local: Pɩyɛ talaa se ɛsʋʋ lɛ, Kɔɔrnɛɛyɩ kʋyɩ ɛkatɩ-ɩ nɛ eluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ nɛ ɛsɛɛ-ɩ nɛ ñamtʋ siŋŋ.

eng: I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.
local: Ma-maɣmaɣ mɔnkɔŋ-ɩ wɩlʋʋ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se ɛtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ.».

eng: Those who had been scattered moved on, preaching the good news along the way.
local: Mbá paamɩzɩ ɖama yɔ, pɔcɔwaɣ ɛjaɖɛ taa nɛ posusuu Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: But Peter lifted him up, saying, Get up! Like you, Im just a human.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ kʋsɩ-ɩ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Kʋyɩ, mɛnkɛ ɛyʋ ɛzɩ ña yɔ.».

eng: Ananias went to the house. He placed his hands on Saul and said, Brother Saul, the Lord sent me—Jesus, who appeared to you on the way as you were coming here. He sent me so that you could see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Peeɖe Anaanɩyaasɩ kpaɣ nʋmɔʋ nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛtalɩ Yudaasɩ tɛ. Ɛsʋ ɖɩɣa lɛ, ɛmaɣnɩ Sɔɔlɩ ɛnʋ nɛ ɛtɩnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nesi nɛ eheyi-i se: «Man-taabalʋ Sɔɔlɩ! Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu weyi ɛlɩzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-ŋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ŋkɔŋaɣ yɔ, etiyini-m se mɔnkɔɔ mala nɛ ñɛ-ɛsɛ kuli nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tii ñɔ-yɔɔ.».

eng: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and began to preach Christ to them.
local: Filiipu ñaawobi Samaarɩɩ tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ taa nɛ eheyiɣ ɛyáa Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ ɛ-tɔm.

eng: As they continued to talk, Peter went inside and found a large gathering of people.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Kɔɔrnɛɛyɩ kʋyɩ nɛ ɛ nɛ Pɩyɛ papazɩ yɔɔdʋʋ nɛ pasʋʋ ɖɩɣa nɛ pamaɣnɩ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kpeɣlaa.

eng: Instantly, flakes fell from Sauls eyes and he could see again. He got up and was baptized.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, nabʋyʋ lɩɩnɩ Sɔɔlɩ ɛsɛ taa ɛzɩ kpakpayaɣ pɔɔya yɔ nɛ pʋtɔlɩ. Peeɖe ɛpazɩ naʋ nɛ ɛkʋyɩ nɛ palabɩ-ɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ.

eng: The crowds were united by what they heard Philip say and the signs they saw him perform, and they gave him their undivided attention.
local: Ɛyɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛlakɩ hama lakasɩ nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ tɩŋa payɩ naɣ-sɩ nɛ pewelesiɣni-i siŋŋ.

eng: He said to them, You all realize that it is forbidden for a Jew to associate or visit with outsiders. However, God has shown me that I should never call a person impure or unclean.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ heyi-wɛ se: «Ɩtɛm sɩm se pɩtaha Yuda tʋ nʋmɔʋ se ɛ nɛ ɛgɔm nɔɔyʋ paɖʋ taabalɩyɛ yaa ɛsʋʋ pʋdʋ tɛ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ ñeheyi-m se mantaakpaɣ nɔɔyʋ se ɛtɛkɛ kɩbanʋ yaa ɛfɛyɩ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ se powolo ɛ-tɛ.

eng: After eating, he regained his strength. He stayed with the disciples in Damascus for several days.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛtɔɔ tɔɔnaɣ nɛ ɛkpa ɖoŋ. Sɔɔlɩ kacaɣ Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa mbá pɛwɛ Damaasɩ yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ kɩyakɩŋ cabɩ.

eng: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many people, and many who were paralyzed or crippled were healed.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, aleewaa makaɣ kubusi ɖoŋ nɛ peseɣ kʋdɔndɩnaa sakɩyɛ wayɩ nɛ kalɩkalasɩ sakɩyɛ nɛ taladɩnaa sakɩyɛ pɔ-yɔɔ cɛkɩ.

eng: For this reason, when you sent for me, I came without objection. I want to know, then, why you sent for me.
local: Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ itiyaa se paya-m lɛ, mankʋyɩ mɔnkɔɔ, mantapʋtɩ. Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, mɔnpɔzʋʋ-mɩ se alaafɩya lɛ ɩya-m ḿbʋ́?».

eng: Right away, he began to preach about Jesus in the synagogues. He is Gods Son, he declared.
local: Pʋwayɩ ɛtɩpazɩ susuu Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ taa se Yesu lɛ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ.

eng: There was great rejoicing in that city.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa ñɩma laŋa hʋlʋmaa siŋŋ.

eng: Cornelius answered, Four days ago at this same time, three oclock in the afternoon, I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in radiant clothing stood before me.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Kɔɔrnɛɛyɩ cosi-i se: «Pɩlabɩ kɩyakɩŋ naanza yɔ, maawɛ mɛn-tɛ nɛ mɛnsɛɣ Ɛsɔ, pɩɩmaɣ ɛzɩ ɖanaɣ wɩsɩ wɛlʋʋ yɔ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, abalʋ nɔɔyʋ tɩlɩʋ mɛ-ɛsɩndaa yem. Esu wondu nɛ tɩñɩlɩzɩɣ.

eng: Everyone who heard him was baffled. They questioned each other, Isnt he the one who was wreaking havoc among those in Jerusalem who called on this name? Hadnt he come here to take those same people as prisoners to the chief priests.
local: Pɩɩɖɩɣzɩ mbá patɩŋa panɩ ɛ-tɔm ńdʋ́ yɔ nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ pa-tɩ se: «Pɩtɛkɛ abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛɛwɛnɩ mbá poluŋuu Yesu tɛɛ yɔ pa-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ Yeruusalɛm yaa we? Pɩtɛkɛ ɛnʋ kɔmnɩ cɩnɛ faŋŋ se ɛkpa-wɛ nɛ ɛhɔkɩ-wɛ nɛ ewoni cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa cɔlɔ yaa we?».

eng: Before Philips arrival, a certain man named Simon had practiced sorcery in that city and baffled the people of Samaria. He claimed to be a great person.
local: Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa ɖooo lɔŋ, payaɣ-ɩ se Siimɔɔ. Ɛlakaɣ hama lakasɩ nɛ pɩɖɩɣzɩɣ Samaarɩɩ ñɩma nɛ ɛmaɣzɩɣ se ɛkɛ sɔsɔ nɔɔyʋ siŋŋ.

eng: He said, Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to him.
local: nɛ eheyi-m se: ‹Kɔɔrnɛɛyɩ! Ɛsɔ nɩ ñe-tenduu nɛ ɛna sɩnaʋ mbʋ́ ŋsɩŋnɩ kʋñɔndɩnaa yɔ.

eng: But Saul grew stronger and stronger. He confused the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, Sɔɔlɩ tɛzaɣ kpaʋ ɖoŋ ɛ-tɔm susuu taa: ɛwɩlaɣ Yuda ñɩma mbá paawɛ Damaasɩ yɔ se Yesu lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ nɛ paba ɛɛtasɩɣ naʋ ndʋ́ paacosi-i yɔ.

eng: Everyone, from the least to the greatest, gave him their undivided attention and referred to him as the power of God called Great.
local: Ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ, kpaɣ piya nɛ puwolo pɩsɩɩnɩ sɔsaa, pewelesaɣnɩ-ɩ nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Ɛnɛ lɛ Ɛsɔ ɖoŋ weyi payaɣ se: ‹Đoŋ sɔsɔŋ› yɔ.».

eng: Therefore, send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, located near the seacoast.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, tiyi nabɛyɛ Yopee nɛ payaa Siimɔɔ weyi pakʋm-ɩ hɩɖɛ se Pɩyɛ yɔ. Ɛwɛ naataŋgbala kanɖʋ weyi payaɣ-ɩ se Siimɔɔ yɔ ɛ-tɛ teŋgu nɔɔ.›.

eng: After this had gone on for some time, the Jews hatched a plot to kill Saul.
local: Pɩlabɩ kɩyakɩŋ cabɩ lɛ, Yuda ñɩma la lɩmaɣza se pakʋ Sɔɔlɩ ɛnʋ.

eng: He had their attention because he had baffled them with sorcery for a long time.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ pɛtɛzaɣ-ɩ welesinaʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛwɛɛ ɛlakɩ hama lakasɩ nɛ pɩɖɩɣzɩɣ-wɛ nɛ sɩ.

eng: I sent for you right away, and you were kind enough to come. Now, here we are, gathered in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has directed you to say.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, mankʋsɩ ɛyáa se pɔkɔɔ paya-ŋ. Pɩlabɩ-m leleŋ se ŋkɔma. Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɖatɩŋa ɖɩwɛ cɩnɛ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa nɛ ɖiwelesiɣ se ɖɩnɩɩ ndʋ́ ɛnʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ etiyi-ŋ se ŋkɔɔ ŋheyi-ɖʋ yɔ.».

eng: However, he found out about their scheme. They were keeping watch at the city gates around the clock so they could assassinate him.
local: Peeɖe papazɩ ɖaŋʋʋ tɛtʋ nɔnɔsɩ taa wɩsɩ nɛ ɖoó se ɛlɩɣ lɛ, pakpa-ɩ pakʋ-ɩ. Ŋgʋ́, Sɔɔlɩ ñaatɛm nɩʋ se patam ɛ-yɔɔ nɔɔ.

eng: After they came to believe Philip, who preached the good news about Gods kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Filiipu kɔm esusi Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ nɛ Yesu Krɩstʋ pa-Laŋhɛzɩyɛ tɔm lɛ, halaa nɛ abalaa petisi-tʋ nɛ palabɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ.

eng: Peter said, I really am learning that God doesnt show partiality to one group of people over another.
local: Kɔɔrnɛɛyɩ yɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Pɩyɛ tɔ se: «Toovenim lɛ, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ manawa se Ɛsɔ tɔsɔɔlɩ nabɛyɛ nɛ eyele lɛlaa:.

eng: But his disciples took him by night and lowered him in a basket through an opening in the city wall.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa kpaɣ-ɩ nɛ ɖoó nɛ paɖʋ ɖɔkʋ taa nɛ powoni-i petisi tɛtʋ kámaɣ́ koloŋa wayɩ.

eng: Even Simon himself came to believe and was baptized. Afterward, he became one of Philips supporters. As he saw firsthand the signs and great miracles that were happening, he was astonished.
local: Halɩ Siimɔɔ maɣmaɣ ñetisi nɛ palabɩ-ɩ-sɩ ɖɔɖɔ. Ɛɛwɛɛ ɛtɩŋɩɣ Filiipu nɛ pɩlakɩ-ɩ piti nɛ hama lakasɩ sɔsɔsɩ nzɩ́ ɛ́lɛ́ lakaɣnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖoŋ yɔ nɛ kawɩlɩsɩ nzɩ́ ɛwɩlaɣnɩ ɖoŋ ɛnʋ nɛ ɛnaɣ yɔ.

eng: Rather, in every nation, whoever worships him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
local: paa anɩ, ɛtɩɩkɛ le tʋ nɛ ɛñaŋ-ɩ nɛ ɛlakɩ mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ yɔ, pʋdʋ tɔm kɛdɩɣnɩ-ɩ.

eng: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him. They didnt believe he was really a disciple.
local: Petisi Sɔɔlɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ewolo Yeruusalɛm nɛ ɛcaɣ se ɛsɔzɩ Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa lɛlaa yɔɔ, ɛlɛ́, paba sɔɔndʋʋ-ɩ patɩŋa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pataatisi se toovenim ɛnʋ ɖɔɖɔ ɛpɩsɩ Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyʋ.

eng: When word reached the apostles in Jerusalem that Samaria had accepted Gods word, they commissioned Peter and John to go to Samaria.
local: Yesu tiyiyaa mbá paawɛ Yeruusalɛm yɔ, panɩwa se Samaarɩɩ ñɩma tisi Ɛsɔtɔm lɛ, pakʋsɩ Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ nɛ petiyi-wɛ pɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: This is the message of peace he sent to the Israelites by proclaiming the good news through Jesus Christ: He is Lord of all!.
local: Ɩtɩlɩ se Ɛsɔ tiyini Yesu Krɩstʋ se ɛkɔɔ eheyi Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm. Yesu Krɩstʋ ɛnʋ lɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ.

eng: Then Barnabas brought Saul to the apostles and told them the story about how Saul saw the Lord on the way and that the Lord had spoken to Saul. He also told them about the confidence with which Saul had preached in the name of Jesus in Damascus.
local: Peeɖe Barnaabaasɩ kpaɣ-ɩ nɛ ɛ nɛ ɩ powolo Yesu tiyiyaa cɔlɔ nɛ ɛkɛdɩ-wɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ kalɩzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ Sɔɔlɩ nʋmɔʋ taa nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩnɩ-ɩ yɔ. Ɛkɛdɩ-wɛ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ eesusi tɔm nɛ Yesu nɔɔ kaalɛyɩtʋ Damaasɩ yɔ.

eng: Peter and John went down to Samaria where they prayed that the new believers would receive the Holy Spirit. .
local: Paba talaa lɛ, petendi Ɛsɔ Samaarɩɩ ñɩma ḿbá pɔ-yɔɔ se ɛha-wɛ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu.

eng: You know what happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism John preached.
local: Paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma, ɩtɛm nɩʋ mbʋ́ pɩlabɩ Galilee egeetiye taa nɛ puwolo Yudee ñɩnɖɛ tɩŋa taa alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Yohanɛɛsɩ susi ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ tɔm yɔ.

eng: After this, Saul moved freely among the disciples in Jerusalem and was speaking with confidence in the name of the Lord.
local: Pɩkpaɣnɩ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, Sɔɔlɩ wɛɛ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ esusuu tɔm nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ nɔɔ kaalɛyɩtʋ Yeruusalɛm.

eng: This was because the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen on any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tetibidi pa-taa nɔɔyʋ yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ ɖeke paalabɩ-wɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu hɩɖɛ taa.

eng: You know about Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and endowed with power. Jesus traveled around doing good and healing everyone oppressed by the devil because God was with him.
local: Ɛsɔ kɛwɛ Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu cɔlɔ nɛ eyele e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tii ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ kɩha-ɩ ɖoŋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛcɔwaɣ lona ndɩ ndɩ taa nɛ ɛlakɩ ɛyáa ɖeu nɛ eholuu mbá patɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛ Satanɩ nesi taa yɔ.

eng: He got into debates with the Greek-speaking Jews as well, but they tried to kill him.
local: Ɛyɔɔdaɣnɩ ɖɔɖɔ Yuda ñɩma mbá pɔyɔɔdʋʋ Grɛɛkɩ yɔ nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ pɔhɔŋnɩ ɖama tɔm nɛ paba ñɩnɩɣ se pakʋ-ɩ.

eng: So Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ patɩnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nesi nɛ petendi Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ ha-wɛ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu.

eng: We are witnesses of everything he did, both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree.
local: Đɩkɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛlabɩ Yeruusalɛm nɛ Yuda ñɩma ɛjaɖɛ tɩŋa taa yɔ pɩ-aseɣɖe tɩnaa. Pakpa-ɩ nɛ pakam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɛsɩ.

eng: When the family of believers learned about this, they escorted him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
local: Taabalaa lɛlaa nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pakpaɣ-ɩ nɛ powoni Sezaaree tɛtʋ taa nɛ ɛɖɛɛ Taarsɩ.

eng: When Simon perceived that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles hands, he offered them money.
local: Patɩnɩ ɛyáa yɔɔ nesi nɛ Ɛsɔ ha-wɛ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Siimɔɔ na. Peeɖe ɛlɩzɩ liidiye se ɛcɛlɩɣ Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ.

eng: but God raised him up on the third day and allowed him to be seen.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ fezi-i kɩyakʋ naadozo ñɩŋgʋ wiye nɛ eyele se ɛlɩzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ ɛyáa.

eng: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria enjoyed a time of peace. God strengthened the church, and its life was marked by reverence for the Lord. Encouraged by the Holy Spirit, the church continued to grow in numbers.
local: Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma kɛwɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa Yudee nɛ Galilee nɛ Samaarɩɩ pa-ageeta hɔɔlɩŋ tɩŋa taa. Pɛtɛzaɣ pɩʋ nɛ pañaŋ Kɩpaɣlʋ nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu sɩŋnɩ-wɛ nɛ pɛtɛzɩɣ ɖɔʋ.

eng: He said, Give me this authority too so that anyone on whom I lay my hands will receive the Holy Spirit.
local: nɛ etendi-wɛ se: «Ɩha-m ɖoŋ ɛnʋ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ yee mantɩnɩ nɔɔyʋ yɔɔ nesi lɛ, Ɛsɔ ha-ɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu.».

eng: not by everyone but by us. We are witnesses whom God chose beforehand, who ate and drank with him after God raised him from the dead.
local: nɛ ɛwɩlɩ mbá Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ ɛɛlɩzaa se pɛkɛ pɩ-aseɣɖe tɩnaa yɔ pe-ɖeke koŋ. Efema lɛ, ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩtɔɔ nɛ ɖɩñɔɔ.

eng: As Peter toured the whole region, he went to visit Gods holy people in Lydda.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, Pɩyɛ cɔwaɣ paa le nɛ esusuu Ɛsɔtɔm. Nakʋyʋ wiye lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ ewolo tɛtʋ ndʋ́ payaɣ se Liidaa yɔ tɩ-taa Ɛsɔ ñɩma cɔlɔ.

eng: Peter responded, May your money be condemned to hell along with you because you believed you could buy Gods gift with money!.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ pɩsɩnɩ-ɩ se: «Ña nɛ ñe-liidiye ɩla asayʋʋ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ŋmaɣzɩɣ se papɩzɩɣ paya Ɛsɔ kʋjɔʋ nɛ liidiye.

eng: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one whom God appointed as judge of the living and the dead.
local: Ɛnʋ ɖʋnɩ-ɖʋ se ɖɩcɔɔ ɖisusi ɛyáa nɛ ɖiheyi-wɛ kpayɩ se ɛ-maɣmaɣ Ɛsɔ lɩzaa se ɛhʋʋnɩ mbá pɛwɛnɩ pɛ-ɛsɛ yɔ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ sɩɖaa.

eng: There he found a man named Aeneas who was paralyzed and had been confined to his bed for eight years.
local: Ɛtalaa lɛ, ɛmaɣnɩ abalʋ weyi payaɣ se Enee yɔ, pɩlabɩ pɩnzɩ lutozo yɔ ɛ́lɛ́ wɛʋ ñalʋʋ yɔɔ yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛkɛ kalɩkalaɣ.

eng: You can have no part or share in Gods word because your heart isnt right with God.
local: Ŋfɛyɩnɩ natʋyʋ yaa waɖɛ naɖɩyɛ tɔm tʋnɛ tɩ-taa, ŋtatʋʋzɩ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa.

eng: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.
local: Náyaa tɩŋa lɩzɩ ɛ-aseɣɖe se: weyi ɛwɛɣnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ kpeɣ pʋdʋ ɛ-kɩwɛɛkɩm.».

eng: Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you! Get up and make your bed. At once he got up.
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-i se: «Enee! Yesu Krɩstʋ yeki nɛ ña-kʋdɔŋ cɛ, kʋyɩ ŋkpiyi ña-ñalʋʋ.» Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Enee kʋyɩ.

eng: Therefore, change your heart and life! Turn from your wickedness! Plead with the Lord in the hope that your wicked intent can be forgiven.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yele ña-lɩmaɣza kɩdɛkɛdɛɛ nɛ ŋtendi Kɩpaɣlʋ nɛ pʋwɛɛ yɔ, eekpeɣ-ŋ ña-lɩmaɣza ana.

eng: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell on everyone who heard the word.
local: Pɩyɛ tosulidi ɛ-nɔɔ lɛ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tii ɛyáa mbá patɩŋa pewelesaɣnɩ-ɩ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: Everyone who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
local: Liidaa ñɩma nɛ Sarɔɔ ñɩma ḿbá, patɩŋa pana Enee ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ nɛ papɩsɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ ñɩma.

eng: for I see that your bitterness has poisoned you and evil has you in chains.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, manawa se kañatʋ kadadaɣyaɣ suni ña-taa ɖo nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm pɩsɩ-ŋ pu-yom.».

eng: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
local: Peeɖe pɩlabɩ Yuda ñɩma mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ paatɩŋ Pɩyɛ wayɩ yɔ piti pɩŋŋ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ha e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma nɛ eyele kitii pɔ-yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha . Her life overflowed with good works and compassionate acts on behalf of those in need.
local: Halʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ Yopee tɛtʋ taa, payaɣ-ɩ se Tabɩɩta. Pɛɖɛzɩɣ hɩɖɛ ńɖɩ́ nɛ Grɛɛkɩ ñɩma kʋnʋŋ taa se: «Dɔɔrkaasɩ.» Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se nɛnzɛm. Ɛɛkɛ Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyʋ nɛ ɛlakɩ camɩyɛ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ nɛ ɛsɩŋnɩ kʋñɔndɩnaa.

eng: Simon replied, All of you, please, plead to the Lord for me so that nothing of what you have said will happen to me.
local: Peeɖe Siimɔɔ heyi Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ se: «Mɩ-maɣmaɣ itendi Kɩpaɣlʋ mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ndʋ́ ɩyɔɔdaa yɔ, tɩ-taa natʋyʋ ɛtaatalɩ-m.».

eng: They heard them speaking in other languages and praising God. Peter asked.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, panɩɣaɣ tɔm ndʋ́ ɛyáa mbá pɔyɔɔdaɣ kʋnʋmɩŋ lɛɛŋ nɛ pasaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ, pɔyɔɔdaɣ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ Pɩyɛ tɔm se:.

eng: About that time, though, she became so ill that she died. After they washed her body, they laid her in an upstairs room.
local: Pɩyɛ wɛ Liidaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, halʋ ɛnʋ ɛhɛnɩ kʋdɔŋ nɛ ɛsɩ. Ɛsɩba lɛ, pɔsɔ-ɩ lɩm nɛ pɔñɔɔzɩ-ɩ nɛ powoni pɛhɛzɩ ɛsɔdaa kuduyuu nakʋyʋ taa.

eng: After the apostles had testified and proclaimed the Lords word, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the good news to many Samaritan villages along the way.
local: Pɛtɛma lɛ, palɩzɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ aseɣɖe nɛ posusi ɛ-tɔm nɛ pakʋyɩ pɔɖɔŋnɩ susuu Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm Samaarɩɩ tɛtʋ cikpetu sakɩyɛ taa nɛ papɩsɩ Yeruusalɛm.

eng: These people have received the Holy Spirit just as we have. Surely no one can stop them from being baptized with water, can they.
local: «Ɛzɩma papɩzɩɣ pekizini mbá Ɛsɔ ha-wɛ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ɖa yɔ se pataalabɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ?».

eng: Since Lydda was near Joppa, when the disciples heard that Peter was there, they sent two people to Peter. They urged, Please come right away.
local: Liidaa taawɛɛ poliŋ nɛ Yopee. Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa nɩwa se Pɩyɛ wɛ peeɖe lɛ, pakʋsɩ pa-taa ɛyáa naalɛ nɛ petiyi-wɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ se peheyi-i se: «Đitendi-ŋ, kɔɔ ɖɛ-tɛ kpɩɩɩ.».

eng: An angel from the Lord spoke to Philip, At noon, take the road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ heyi Filiipu se: «Kʋyɩ ŋkpaɣ hadɛ kiŋ habɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩlɩɣnɩ Yeruusalɛm nɛ ɖitiki Gaazaa yɔ.» Habɩyɛ ńɖɩ́, nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ ɖɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: He directed that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited Peter to stay for several days.
local: Peeɖe ɛha nɔɔ se palabɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ Yesu Krɩstʋ hɩɖɛ taa. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, petendi-i se ɛcaɣ pɔ-cɔlɔ kɩyakɩŋ cabɩ nɛ pʋcɔ ekpe.

eng: Peter went with them. Upon his arrival, he was taken to the upstairs room. All the widows stood beside him, crying as they showed the tunics and other clothing Dorcas made when she was alive.
local: Powobi peheyi-i lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ kpaka nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ pakpaɣ nʋmɔʋ. Patalaa lɛ, pakpanɩ-ɩ ɛsɔdaa kuduyuu ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-taa nɛ lelaa tɩŋa wolo pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ wiluu nɛ pawɩlɩɣ-ɩ tokonaa sɔsaa nɛ lɛlaa mbá Dɔɔrkaasɩ kɛñɛyɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛɛwɛnɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ yɔ patɩŋa.

eng: So he did. Meanwhile, an Ethiopian man was on his way home from Jerusalem, where he had come to worship. He was a eunuch and an official responsible for the entire treasury of Candace.
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ tɩkʋyaa ɛkpaɣ nʋmɔʋ. Ɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, ɛkatɩ Etiyoopii abalʋ nɔɔyʋ, ɛkɛ eleɣye. Ɛnʋ lɛ karɩka sɔsɔɔ ŋgá kɔcɔŋnɩ Etiyoopii abiɖe Kandaasɩ ñɩm yɔɔ yɔ. Eewobi Yeruusalɛm se eluŋ Ɛsɔ tɛɛ.

eng: Peter sent everyone out of the room, then knelt and prayed. He turned to the body and said, Tabitha, get up! She opened her eyes, saw Peter, and sat up.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ ɖɔɔnɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa nɛ palɩɩnɩ awayɩ nɛ pɩsɛɣ e-ɖeke nɛ eluŋ ɛ-nɩmbɔla yɔɔ nɛ etendi Ɛsɔ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, etuzi ɛ-tɩ sɩɖʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ se: «Tabɩɩta! Kʋyɩ!» Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ kuli ɛ-ɛsɛ. Ɛna Pɩyɛ lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ ɛcaɣ.

eng: He was reading the prophet Isaiah while sitting in his carriage.
local: nɛ ɛpɩsɩɣ kpem. Ɛɛcaɣ lɔɔɖɩyɛ nɖɩ́ kpaŋnaŋ hɔŋ yɔ ɖɩ-taa nɛ ɛkalɩɣ náyʋ Ezaayii takayaɣ taa.

eng: He gave her his hand and raised her up. Then he called Gods holy people, including the widows, and presented her alive to them.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɩyɛ sezi ɛ́ɖɔ́kɩ e-nesi taa nɛ ɛkʋsɩ-ɩ. Ɛkʋsɩ-ɩ lɛ, ɛ́yáa Ɛsɔ ñɩma lɛlaa nɛ lelaa nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ-ɩ, ɛ nɛ ɛ-ɛsɛ.

eng: The Spirit told Philip, Approach this carriage and stay with it.
local: Peeɖe Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu heyi Filiipu se: «Tuzi ña-tɩ nɛ ŋñɔtɩnɩ abalʋ ɛnʋ.».

eng: The news spread throughout Joppa, and many put their faith in the Lord.
local: Peeɖe tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩcɔ Yopee tɛtʋ tɩŋa taa payɩ nɛ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ wɛnɩ lidaʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔ.

eng: Running up to the carriage, Philip heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, Do you really understand what you are reading.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ ñese ɛñɔtɩnɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛnɩɣ ɛkalɩɣ náyʋ Ezaayii takayaɣ taa. Peeɖe ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋnɩɣ ndʋ́ ŋkalɩɣ yɔ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ naa we?».

eng: Peter stayed for some time in Joppa with a certain tanner named Simon.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Pɩyɛ caɣ Yopee peeɖe kɩyakɩŋ cabɩ naataŋgbala kanɖʋ weyi payaɣ se Siimɔɔ yɔ ɛ-tɛ.

eng: The man replied, Without someone to guide me, how could I? Then he invited Philip to climb up and sit with him.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: «Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛtalɩzɩ-m tɩ-tɔbʋʋ lɛ, malakɩ ɛzɩma nɛ manɩɩ?» Peeɖe ɛya Filiipu nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ kpa ɛcaɣ ɛ-cɔlɔ lɔɔɖɩyɛ taa nɛ pɛɖɛɣ.

eng: This was the passage of scripture he was reading: Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent so he didnt open his mouth. .
local: Tɔm ndʋ́ Etiyoopii tʋ ɛnʋ ɛkalaɣ náyʋ Ezaayii takayaɣ taa yɔ, ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Pakpa-ɩ nɛ powoni-i ɛzɩ alikizaŋnaa kpaʋ heu se powokini kʋʋ yɔ. Ɛwɛ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ heɣa ŋgá kaawiliɣ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɛsɛtɩɣ kɛ-kpɛm yɔ. Etokuli ɛ-nɔɔ.

eng: In his humiliation justice was taken away from him. Who can tell the story of his descendants because his life was taken from the earth? .
local: Pɛpɛŋ-ɩ tɛtɛ nɛ pakʋ ɛ-tɔm. Atɩɩcazɩɣ ɛ-sayɩnaa tɔm yɔɔdʋʋ? Nɔɔyʋ ɛɛyɔɔdʋʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, palɩzɩ e-wezuu kedeŋa yɔɔ.».

eng: The eunuch asked Philip, Tell me, about whom does the prophet say this? Is he talking about himself or someone else.
local: Etiyoopii tʋ ɛnʋ ɛtɛm kalʋʋ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ Filiipu se: «Mentendi-ŋ, heyi-m weyi ɛ-tɔm náyʋ yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ. Ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-tɔm naa nɔɔyʋ ñɩndʋ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ?».

eng: Starting with that passage, Philip proclaimed the good news about Jesus to him.
local: Peeɖe Filiipu tɩŋnɩ tɔm ndʋ́ ɛkalaɣ yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ nɛ esusi-i Yesu Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: As they went down the road, they came to some water. The eunuch said, Look! Water! What would keep me from being baptized.
local: Pɔɖɔŋaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, patalɩ ɖoli nɔɔyʋ nɛ pamaɣnɩ lɩm nɛ kaɖɩka ŋ́gá kɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Lɩm yɔ! Ɛbɛ ɖɩkɩ-m nʋmɔʋ se pataalabɩ-m lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ?» .

eng: He ordered that the carriage halt. Both Philip and the eunuch went down to the water, where Philip baptized him.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛha nɔɔ se pasɩzɩ lɔɔɖɩyɛ. Peeɖe pa-naalɛ petibi lɩm taa nɛ Filiipu labɩ-ɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ.

eng: When they came up out of the water, the Lords Spirit suddenly took Philip away. The eunuch never saw him again but went on his way rejoicing.
local: Palɩnɩ lɩm taa lɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ Fezuu kpaɣ Filiipu yem nɛ kaɖɩka ŋ́gá katatasɩ-ɩ naʋ. Ɛlɛ́, kɛtɛzɩ ɖɛʋ nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ.

eng: Philip found himself in Azotus. He traveled through that area, preaching the good news in all the cities until he reached Caesarea.
local: nɛ Filiipu ñatɩnaʋ ɛ-tɩ yem Azɔɔtɩ tɛtʋ taa. Peeɖe ɛpazɩ susuu Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ tɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛtɩŋaɣnɩ tɩ-taa yɔ tɩ-taa nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛtalɩ Sezaaree.

eng: About that time King Herod began to harass some who belonged to the church.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ wiyaʋ Eroodi kapazɩ Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma nabɛyɛ wayɩ wɛʋ kaŋ kaŋ.

eng: He had James, Johns brother, killed with a sword.
local: Eyeba nɛ pakʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ ɖalʋ Yakubu nɛ sɩɣa kɩɖaɣlaɣ.

eng: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he arrested Peter as well. This happened during the Festival of Unleavened Bread.
local: Ɛlabɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛna se pɩkɛdɩnɩ Yuda ñɩma. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛkpa Pɩyɛ ɖɔɖɔ. Kazandʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ pɔtɔkɩ tindine nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa fɛyɩ sɔsɔɔna yɔ tɩ-alɩwaatʋ taa pɩlakaɣ ḿbʋ́.

eng: He put Peter in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers, sixteen in all, who guarded him. He planned to charge him publicly after the Passover.
local: Pakpa-ɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, eyele paɖʋ-ɩ salaka. Peeɖe ɛca sɔɔjanaa naanza tam naanza taa se paɖaŋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ pɛtɛm Paska Kazandʋ tɔɔʋ lɛ, ɛhʋʋnɩ-ɩ tɔm ɛyáa tɩŋa ɛsɩndaa.

eng: While Peter was held in prison, the church offered earnest prayer to God for him.
local: Pɩyɛ wɛ salaka taa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma wɛɛ petendiɣ Ɛsɔ ɛ-yɔɔ paa ɖooye.

eng: The night before Herod was going to bring Peters case forward, Peter was asleep between two soldiers and bound with two chains, with soldiers guarding the prison entrance.
local: Đoo wena aɖɛɣaɣ se kife nɛ Eroodi hʋʋnɩ Pɩyɛ tɔm yɔ a-taa lɛ, Pɩyɛ kahɛnaa nɛ eɖou. Sɔɔjanaa naalɛ hɛkʋ taa ɛɛwɛɛ nɛ piyele lɛlaa ñasɩŋ salaka nɔnɔsɩ taa nɛ paɖaŋ. Paahɔkɩ-ɩ yoŋgbasɩ naalɛ.

eng: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared and a light shone in the prison cell. After nudging Peter on his side to awaken him, the angel raised him up and said, Quick! Get up! The chains fell from his wrists.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ tɩlɩʋ ɛ-cɔlɔ salaka kuduyuu taa nɛ ñalɩmɩyɛ naɖɩyɛ ñalɩ kɩ-taa yem ñammm. Peeɖe tiyiyu ɛnʋ ɛmabɩ ɛ-hʋyʋʋ taa se: «Kʋyɩ kpaka!» Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, yoŋgbasɩ nzɩ́ paahɔkɩ e-nesi taa yɔ, sɩyɛɖɩ yem nɛ sɩtɔlɩ.

eng: The angel continued, Get dressed. Put on your sandals. Peter did as he was told. The angel said, Put on your coat and follow me.
local: nɛ tiyiyu ɛnʋ eheyi-i se: «Tɔɔlɩ ña-tambaɖɛ nɛ ŋta ña-naataŋgbala tɔnzɩ.» Pɩyɛ la ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ, ɛlɛ́ ɛtasɩ-ɩ heyuu se: «Suu ño-toko sɔsɔʋ nɛ ŋtɩŋ-m.».

eng: Following the angel, Peter left the prison. However, he didnt realize the angel had actually done all this. He thought he was seeing a vision.
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ tɩŋ-ɩ nɛ palɩɩ. Pɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, pɩlabɩ-ɩ ɛzɩ eɖozuu kuɖozu yɔ. Ɛtaatisi se pɩkɛ toovenim.

eng: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself. After leaving the prison, they proceeded the length of one street, when abruptly the angel was gone.
local: Pɔkɔm pɛɖɛɛ kajalaɣ nɔnɔɔ nɛ naalɛ ñɩŋga nɛ patalɩ ŋgá palabɩ-kɛ nɛ ñɩɣtʋ nɛ ŋɖɛɣ-kɛ nɛ ŋsʋʋ tɛtʋ taa yɔ ka-taa lɛ, katʋlɩ pɛ-ɛsɩndaa yem waaa nɛ palɩɩ. Palɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pakpaɣ nʋmɔʋ nɛ pɛɖɛɣ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, tiyiyu ɛnʋ ɛtɩmʋʋ Pɩyɛ ɛsɩndaa yem.

eng: At that, Peter came to his senses and remarked, Now Im certain that the Lord sent his angel and rescued me from Herod and from everything the Jewish people expected.
local: Peeɖe ɛhʋ ɛ-taa pɩŋŋ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, manawa se toovenim Ɛsɔ tiyini e-tiyiyu nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛlɩzɩ-m Eroodi nesi taa nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa Yuda ñɩma caɣaɣnɩ-m yɔ pɩ-taa.».

eng: Realizing this, he made his way to Marys house. Many believers had gathered there and were praying.
local: Ɛna mbʋ́ pɩ-taa ɛwɛɛ yɔ lɛ, ewolo Yohanɛɛsɩ weyi pakʋm-ɩ hɩɖɛ se Maarkɩ yɔ e-ɖoo Maarɩɩ tɛ. Ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kaakpeɣli peeɖe nɛ pɛsɛɣ Ɛsɔ.

eng: When Peter knocked at the outer gate, a female servant named Rhoda went to answer.
local: Ewoba lɛ, ɛma nɔnɔɔ nɛ tʋmlaɖʋ halʋ ñɩnʋ weyi payaɣ se Roodi yɔ, ewolo se ɛna.

eng: She was so overcome with joy when she recognized Peters voice that she didnt open the gate. Instead, she ran back in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
local: Ɛtalaa lɛ, ɛnɩɩ Pɩyɛ nɔɔ nɛ pɩlabɩ-ɩ leleŋ pɩŋŋ. Đenɖe ɛzɩ ɛɛtʋlaa lɛ, ese ɛpɩsɩ nɛ eheyi se Pɩyɛ kɔma nɛ ɛwɛɛ nɔnɔɔ taa.

eng: The apostles and the brothers and sisters throughout Judea heard that even the Gentiles had welcomed Gods word.
local: Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa nɛ taabalaa mbá pɛwɛ Yudee egeetiye taa yɔ, panɩwa se mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, petisi ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: Youve lost your mind! they responded. She stuck by her story with such determination that they began to say, It must be his guardian angel.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mbá paawɛ peeɖe yɔ, papɩsɩnɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋsʋ kpaaŋ!» Ɛlɛ́, ɛtasɩ-wɛ heyuu se: «Toovenim mɔyɔɔdʋʋ.» Peeɖe pɔtɔm se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ ɛ-wayɩ tʋ.».

eng: When Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him.
local: Pɩyɛ kɔm ɛpɩsɩ Yeruusalɛm lɛ, Yuda ñɩma mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ yɔ, pakʋyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter remained outside, knocking at the gate. They finally opened the gate and saw him there, and they were astounded.
local: Pɩyɛ wɛɛ ɛmakɩ nɔnɔɔ ḿbʋ́. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, powolo pa-maɣmaɣ patʋlɩ nɛ pana-ɩ nɛ pɩlabɩ-wɛ yem yaaa.

eng: They accused him, You went into the home of the uncircumcised and ate with them.
local: se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋwolo mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɛ-tɛ nɛ ña nɛ wɛ ɩtɔɔ?».

eng: He gestured with his hand to quiet them down, then recounted how the Lord led him out of prison. He said, Tell this to James and the brothers and sisters. Then he left for another place.
local: Peeɖe ɛñɩɣ-wɛ nesi se posu. Posuma lɛ, ɛkɛdɩ-wɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ lɩzɩ-ɩ salaka taa yɔ. Ɛtɛm-wɛ kɛdʋʋ lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se: «Iwolo iheyi-tʋ Yakubu nɛ taabalaa lɛlaa.» Eheyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛcaŋ ɛɖɛɛ ɖoli lɛɛɖɛ.

eng: Step-by-step, Peter explained what had happened.
local: Peeɖe Pɩyɛ lɩzɩ-wɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩlaba yɔ pʋ-tɔbʋʋ piye piye se:.

eng: The next morning the soldiers were flustered about what had happened to Peter.
local: Tɛʋ fema lɛ, sɔɔjanaa peendiɣ nɛ pɔpɔzʋʋ ɖama se: «Pɩyɛ mʋʋ́ʋ we?».

eng: I was in the city of Joppa praying when I had a visionary experience. In my vision, I saw something like a large linen sheet being lowered from heaven by its four corners. It came all the way down to me.
local: «Nakʋyʋ wiye maawɛ Yopee nɛ mɛnsɛɣ Ɛsɔ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, monɖozuu se mana pʋyʋ nɔɔyʋ lɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa ɛzɩ pɩsaʋ sɔsɔʋ yɔ, patam pɩsaʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-pama naanza taa nɛ kitii mɔn-cɔlɔ.

eng: Herod called for a thorough search. When Peter didnt turn up, Herod interrogated the guards and had them executed. Afterward, Herod left Judea in order to spend some time in Caesarea.
local: Peeɖe Eroodi ɖʋ ɛyáa se pañɩnɩ-ɩ nɛ paba ñɩnɩ-ɩ, patana-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛ́yáa sɔɔjanaa mbá paaɖaŋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ nɛ ɛhʋʋnɩ-wɛ tɔm nɛ ɛha nɔɔ se pakʋ-wɛ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ Yudee egeetiye taa nɛ ewolo ɛcaɣ Sezaaree tɛtʋ taa alɩwaatʋ cabɩ.

eng: As I stared at it, wondering what it was, I saw four-legged animals—including wild beasts—as well as reptiles and wild birds.
local: Mɔncɔnɩ kɩ-taa pɩŋŋ lɛ, manaɣ kpɩna ndɩ ndɩ: naŋgbanzɩ naanza tɩnaa nɛ ñɩtʋ taa kpɩna nɛ tɛtɛ ñɩtʋ nɛ sumasɩ.

eng: Herod had been furious with the people of Tyre and Sidon for some time. They made a pact to approach him together, since their region depended on the kings realm for its food supply. They persuaded Blastus, the kings personal attendant, to join their cause, then appealed for an end to hostilities.
local: Piiyozi Eroodi nɛ Tiiri nɛ Sidɔɔ pɛ-tɛtʋ taa ñɩma. Paba na ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pala nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ nɛ powolo pana Blastuusi. Ɛnʋ lɛ karɩka sɔsɔɔ ŋgá kaɖaŋɩɣ Eroodi ɖɩɣa yɔɔ yɔ. Pana-ɩ se ɛɖɛɛ-wɛ nɔɔ nɛ powolo petendi-i nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ peceɣ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-ɛjaɖɛ taa pɛ-tɛ ñɩma wokaɣ nɛ pañɩnɩɣ tɔɔnaɣ.

eng: I heard a voice say, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Peeɖe manɩ Pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-m se: ‹Pɩyɛ! Kʋyɩ ŋkpa nɛ ŋkʋ nɛ ŋtɔɔ.›.

eng: On the scheduled day Herod dressed himself in royal attire, seated himself on the throne, and gave a speech to the people.
local: Kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ paɖʋwa se peceɣ yɔ kɩtalaa lɛ, Eroodi suu ɛ-awiya toko nɛ ɛcaɣ e-kpelaɣ taa nɛ ɛpazɩ ɛyáa yɔɔdɩnaʋ.

eng: I responded, Absolutely not, Lord! Nothing impure or unclean has ever entered my mouth.
local: Ɛlɛ́, moncosi se: ‹Pɩlɩ! Kɩpaɣlʋ, wiɖiyi mɔntɔtɔɔda mbʋ́ ŋɖʋwa se pataatɔɔ yɔ yaa mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ yɔ.›.

eng: Those assembled kept shouting, over and over, This is a gods voice, not the voice of a mere human.
local: Peeɖe paba mabɩ kubusi se: «Ɛsɔ yɔɔdʋʋna! Pɩtɛkɛ ɛyʋ!».

eng: The voice from heaven spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Peeɖe pɩtasɩ-m heyuu nɛ ɛsɔdaa se: ‹Taakpaɣ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ tɔm se pʋwɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ yɔ se pɩfɛyɩ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ.›.

eng: Immediately an angel from the Lord struck Herod down, because he didnt give the honor to God. He was eaten by worms and died.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ mabɩ-ɩ nɛ sonzoowe tɔɔ-ɩ nɛ ɛsɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, eyeba nɛ pasam-ɩ ɛzɩ pasaŋʋʋ Ɛsɔ yɔ.

eng: This happened three times, then everything was pulled back into heaven.
local: Pɩla ḿbʋ́ nɛ pʋɖʋ tozo nɛ pʋkʋsɩ pɩtɩŋa yem nɛ pɩkpazɩ ɛsɔdaa.

eng: Gods word continued to grow and increase.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ pɛtɛzaɣ Ɛsɔtɔm susuu paa le nɛ ɛyáa tisiɣ-tʋ.

eng: At that moment three men who had been sent to me from Caesarea arrived at the house where we were staying.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛyáa naadozo nabɛyɛ talɩ pasɩŋ ɖɩɣa ŋgá maawɛ ka-taa yɔ kɔ-nɔnɔɔ taa. Petiyi-wɛ nɛ Sezaaree tɛtʋ taa mɔn-cɔlɔ.

eng: Barnabas and Saul returned to Antioch from Jerusalem after completing their mission, bringing with them John, who was also known as Mark.
local: Barnaabaasɩ nɛ Sɔɔlɩ pɛtɛm labʋ mbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ petiyi-wɛ Yeruusalɛm yɔ lɛ, pa nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ weyi pakʋm hɩɖɛ se Maarkɩ yɔ papɩsɩ Antɩyɔɔsɩ tɛtʋ taa.

eng: The Spirit told me to go with them even though they were Gentiles. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered that mans house.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu heyi-m se mankʋyɩ nɛ ma nɛ ɛyáa ḿbá ɖɩɖɛɛ kpaagbaa, mantaahʋʋ man-taa natʋyʋ. Taabalaa loɖo mbá ɩnaɣ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, ḿbá kʋyɩnɩ Yopee nɛ pasɩ-m nɛ ɖiwolo Kɔɔrnɛɛyɩ tɛ.

eng: He reported to us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, Send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter.
local: Peeɖe ɛkɛdɩ-ɖʋ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛna Ɛsɔ tiyiyu ɛ-tɛ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ heyi-i se etiyi ɛyáa Yopee nɛ paya-m yɔ.

eng: He will tell you how you and your entire household can be saved.
local: Etiyi-wɛ se paya-m nɛ mowolo meheyi ɛ nɛ ɛ-hɔʋ taa ñɩma tɩŋa payɩ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩyakɩ pa-ñʋʋ yɔ.

eng: When I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, just as the Spirit fell on us in the beginning.
local: Manpazɩ yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tii ɛyáa mbá pewelesaɣnɩ-m yɔ patɩŋa pɔ-yɔɔ ɛzɩ kɩɩcalʋʋ tibu ɖa ɖɔ-yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: I remembered the Lords words: John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Peeɖe mɔntɔzɩ tɔm ndʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔdaa yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Yohanɛɛsɩ labɩ ɛyáa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ. Ɛlɛ́, mɩ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-mɩ-sɩ labʋ nɛ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖoŋ.›.

eng: If God gave them the same gift he gave us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, then who am I? Could I stand in Gods way.
local: Yee Ɛsɔ ɛlabɩ-wɛ kʋjɔʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ ŋgʋ́ ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛlabɩ-ɖʋ yɔ lɛ, ma anɩ nɛ manɖɩkɩ-ɩ nʋmɔʋ?».

eng: Once the apostles and other believers heard this, they calmed down. They praised God and concluded, So then God has enabled Gentiles to change their hearts and lives so that they might have new life.
local: Panɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ lɛ, pɛhɛzɩ pa-tɩ nɛ pasaŋ Ɛsɔ se: «Yee ḿbʋ́, Ɛsɔ yebi ɖɔɖɔ se halɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ nɛ pehiɣ wezuu!».

eng: Now those who were scattered as a result of the trouble that occurred because of Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch. They proclaimed the word only to Jews.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pakʋ Etiyɛɛnɩ yɔ, tɩ-taa papazɩ wɛʋ mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ yɔ pa-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ pa-taa lɛlaa sewa nɛ powolo Fenisii nɛ lɛlaa Ciiprɩ nɛ lɛlaa Antɩyɔɔsɩ nɛ posusuu Yuda ñɩma ɖeke Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: Among them were some people from Cyprus and Cyrene. They entered Antioch and began to proclaim the good news about the Lord Jesus also to Gentiles.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ciiprɩ nɛ Siirɛɛnɩ pɛ-tɛtʋ taa ñɩma nabɛyɛ kɛwɛ pa-taa. Paba ñaawobi ɖɔɖɔ Antɩyɔɔsɩ nɛ posusuu Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm nɛ pɛkpɛndɩnɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ.

eng: The same thing happened in Iconium. Paul and Barnabas entered the Jewish synagogue and spoke as they had before. As a result, a huge number of Jews and Greeks believed.
local: Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ pɛwɛ Ikoniyɔm ḿbʋ́ lɛ, nakʋyʋ wiye powolo Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa ɖɔɖɔ nɛ posusi Yesu tɔm siŋŋ nɛ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ: Yuda ñɩma nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ petisi-tʋ nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: The Lords power was with them, and a large number came to believe and turned to the Lord.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ha patɩŋa Ɛsɔ ɖoŋ nɛ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ wɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ.

eng: However, the Jews who rejected the faith stirred up the Gentiles, poisoning their minds against the brothers.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yuda ñɩma mbá petetisi-tʋ yɔ, paba ñasazɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ se pala Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ.

eng: When the church in Jerusalem heard about this, they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Yeruusalɛm Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma nɩɩ ḿbʋ́. Peeɖe pakʋsɩ Barnaabaasɩ nɛ petiyi-i Antɩyɔɔsɩ.

eng: Nevertheless, Paul and Barnabas stayed there for quite some time, confidently speaking about the Lord. And the Lord confirmed the word about his grace by the signs and wonders he enabled them to perform.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, paacaɣ Ikoniyɔm peeɖe ka ka nɛ posusuu ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ lakɩ ɛyáa lootiye yɔ kaalɛyɩtʋ. Eyeba nɛ pawɩlɩ kawɩlɩsɩ nɛ pala hama lakasɩ nɛ pɩwɩlɩ se tɔm ńdʋ́ posusuu yɔ, tɩkɛ toovenim.

eng: When he arrived and saw evidence of Gods grace, he was overjoyed and encouraged everyone to remain fully committed to the Lord.
local: Ɛtalaa lɛ, ɛna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ labɩ lootiye mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ yɔ nɛ ɛ-taa hʋlʋmɩ nɛ ɛsazɩ patɩŋa payɩ se pɔ́ɖɔ́kɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ nɛ laŋɩyɛ kʋɖʋmɖɩyɛ kpam.

eng: The people of the city were divided—some siding with the Jews, others with the Lords messengers.
local: Peeɖe Ikoniyɔm ñɩma tayɩ ɖama tam naalɛ nɛ lɛlaa tɩnɩ Yuda ñɩma yɔɔ nɛ pɩsɛɣ lɛlaa ñatɩnɩ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: Barnabas responded in this way because he was a good man, whom the Holy Spirit had endowed with exceptional faith. A considerable number of people were added to the Lord.
local: Barnaabaasɩ ɛnʋ, ɛɛkɛ ɛyʋ kɩbanʋ nɛ ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ siŋŋ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu wɛɛ ɛ-yɔɔ. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ sɔzɩ mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: Then some Gentiles and Jews, including their leaders, hatched a plot to mistreat and stone Paul and Barnabas.
local: Yuda ñɩma nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ nɛ pa-awiya, pɔñɔɔzɩ pa-tɩ se panazɩ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ nɛ payaɣ-wɛ pɛɛ nɛ pasɩ.

eng: Barnabas went to Tarsus in search of Saul.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Barnaabaasɩ kʋyɩ ewolo Taarsɩ tɛtʋ taa se ɛñɩnɩ Sɔɔlɩ.

eng: When they learned of it, these two messengers fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and the surrounding area.
local: Paba nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pala lɩmaɣza nɛ pakʋyɩ peeɖe nɛ pese powolo Listrɩ nɛ Dɛɛrbɩ pɛ-tɛtʋ nɛ pɔ-hɔɔlɩŋ pa-taa Likaoni egeetiye taa.

eng: When he found him, he brought him to Antioch. They were there for a whole year, meeting with the church and teaching large numbers of people. It was in Antioch where the disciples were first labeled Christians.
local: Ɛna-ɩ lɛ, ɛ nɛ ɩ papɩsɩ Antɩyɔɔsɩ nɛ pɛwɛɛ peeɖe pɩnaɣ nɛ kɛ-tɛɛ pilim Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma cɔlɔ nɛ pawɩlɩɣ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa yɔ. Antɩyɔɔsɩ peeɖe pacalɩ-wɛ kʋm hɩɖɛ se Krɩstʋ ñɩma.

eng: where they continued to proclaim the good news.
local: nɛ posusuu Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: About that time, some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa náyaa nabɛyɛ kʋyɩ Yeruusalɛm nɛ powolo Antɩyɔɔsɩ.

eng: In Lystra there was a certain man who lacked strength in his legs. He had been crippled since birth and had never walked. Sitting there, he.
local: Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ pɛwɛ Listrɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, nakʋyʋ wiye pana abalʋ nɔɔyʋ caɣaɣ, ɛkɛ kalɩkalaɣ. Paalʋlɩ-ɩ ḿbʋ́, ɛtɔɖɔ wiɖiyi kaʋ.

eng: One of them, Agabus, stood up and, inspired by the Spirit, predicted that a severe famine would overtake the entire Roman world.
local: Peeɖe Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu kʋsɩ pa-taa lɛlʋ weyi payaɣ se Agaabusi yɔ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se ñɔɔsɩ sɔsɔsɩ nasɩyɩ kɔŋ lɩʋ ɛjaɖɛ kpeekpe yɔɔ. Toovenim lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se sɩlɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Kloodi kɛ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ yɔ tɩ-taa.

eng: heard Paul speaking. Paul stared at him and saw that he believed he could be healed.
local: Abalʋ ɛnʋ ewelesaɣ tɔm ndʋ́ Pɔɔlɩ yɔɔdaɣ yɔ. Ɛ́lɛ́ cɔnɩ-ɩ pɩŋŋ lɛ, ɛna se ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ se ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛla nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: The disciples decided they would send support to the brothers and sisters in Judea, with everyone contributing to this ministry according to each persons abundance.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ palabɩ lɩmaɣza se paa pa-taa weyi ɛha mbʋ́ ɛpɩzɩɣ yɔ nɛ pasɩnɩ taabalaa mbá pɛwɛ Yudee yɔ.

eng: Raising his voice, Paul said, Stand up straight on your feet! He jumped up and began to walk.
local: Peeɖe Pɔɔlɩ holaa se: «Abalʋ ña, kʋyɩ ŋsɩŋ ña-naŋgbanzɩ yɔɔ!» Ɛlɛ́ abalʋ ɛnʋ, ɛkʋyɩ ɛmaa ɛ-naŋgbanzɩ yɔɔ nɛ ɛpazɩ ɖɔm.

eng: They sent Barnabas and Saul to take this gift to the elders.
local: Pala ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ pakpaɣ mbʋ́ pahawa yɔ nɛ paɖʋzɩ Barnaabaasɩ nɛ Sɔɔlɩ pe-nesi taa se powoni pɛcɛlɩ Yudee Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa.

eng: Seeing what Paul had done, the crowd shouted in the Lycaonian language, The gods have taken human form and come down to visit us.
local: Likaoni egeetiye taa ɛyáa samaɣ na mbʋ́ Pɔɔlɩ laba yɔ lɛ, pamakɩ kubusi nɛ pɔyɔɔdʋʋnɩ pɛ-tɛ taa se: «Agɔlɩma pɩsɩ ɛyáa nɛ akɔɔ ɖɔ-cɔlɔ!».

eng: They referred to Barnabas as Zeus and to Paul as Hermes, since Paul was the main speaker.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ pakʋm Barnaabaasɩ hɩɖɛ se Zeesi, nɛ Pɔɔlɩ se Hɛrmɛsɩ . Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Pɔɔlɩ heyaɣnɩ ɛyáa tɔm.

eng: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was located just outside the city, brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates. Along with the crowds, he wanted to offer sacrifices to them.
local: Ɛgɔlɩmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ payaɣ se Zeesi nɛ ɖɩ-ɖɩɣa wɛ tɛtʋ nɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩ-cɔjɔ weyi ɛkpɛyɩɣ-ɖɩ yɔ, ɛkɔnɩ naŋ abalɩñɩŋ weyi pacanɩ ɩ-yɔɔ nɛ tɩŋ hɛtʋ yɔ se ɛ nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ palaa Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ.

eng: When the Lords messengers Barnabas and Paul found out about this, they tore their clothes in protest and rushed out into the crowd. They shouted.
local: Paba nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pacɩyɩ pɔ-yɔɔ tokonaa sɔsaa nɛ pɩwɩlɩ se pɔtɔsɔɔlɩ se palaa-wɛ. Peeɖe pesewa pasʋʋ ɛyáa hɛkʋ taa nɛ pamakɩ kubusi se:.

eng: People, what are you doing? We are humans too, just like you! We are proclaiming the good news to you: turn to the living God and away from such worthless things. He made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them. .
local: «Taabalaa, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́? Đɩkɛ ɛyáa ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ mɩ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩkɔm se ɖiheyi-mɩ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm nɛ iyele agɔlɩma wena aawazɩɣ pʋyʋ yɔ, a-kpɛyʋʋ nɛ ɩpɩsɩnɩ Ɛsɔ wezuu tʋ yɔɔ. Ɛnʋ labɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ tataa nɛ teŋgu nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋwɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ yɔ.

eng: In the past, he permitted every nation to go its own way.
local: Đooo lɛ, Ɛsɔ kaayebi ajɛya kpeekpe taa ɛyáa yem nɛ palakɩ mbʋ́ pe-liu wɛɛ yɔ.

eng: Nevertheless, he hasnt left himself without a witness. He has blessed you by giving you rain from above as well as seasonal harvests, and satisfying you with food and happiness.
local: Ŋgʋ́, wiɖiyi eteyele ɛ-camɩyɛ lakasɩ labʋ se ɛwɩlɩ mbʋ́ ɛkɛnaa yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ yekina nɛ tɛʋ nɩɣ nɛ ɩkʋŋ tɔɔnasɩ sɩ-alɩwaatʋ taa nɛ ɩtɔkɩ nɛ ɩhakɩ nɛ mɩ-taa hʋlʋmʋʋ.».

eng: Even with these words, they barely kept the crowds from sacrificing to them.
local: Paayɔɔdɩ pɩtɩŋa ḿbʋ́, ɛlɛ́, pɩkazɩ ñɩɩɩ palaɣaɣ-wɛ kɩlaʋ ŋ́gʋ́.

eng: Jews from Antioch and Iconium arrived and won the crowds over. They stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing he was dead.
local: Pʋwayɩ Yuda ñɩma nabɛyɛ lɩnɩ Antɩyɔɔsɩ nɛ Ikoniyɔm pɛ-tɛtʋ taa nɛ powolo Listrɩ peeɖe nɛ pasazɩ ɛyáa samaɣ se ka nɛ wɛ payaɣ Pɔɔlɩ pɛɛ nɛ ɛsɩ. Pamaɣzaa se ɛsɩkɩ lɛ, potuu-i nɛ palɩzɩ nɛ tɛtʋ wayɩ.

eng: When the disciples surrounded him, he got up and entered the city again. The following day he left with Barnabas for Derbe.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa wolo pekpeɣlini-i. Peeɖe ɛtɩkʋyaa nɛ ɛsʋʋ Listrɩ tɛtʋ taa. Tɛʋ fema lɛ, ɛ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ pakʋyɩ Listrɩ peeɖe nɛ powolo Dɛɛrbɩ.

eng: Paul and Barnabas proclaimed the good news to the people in Derbe and made many disciples. Then they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch, where.
local: Powoba lɛ, posusi Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm nɛ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ pɩsɩ Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pakʋyɩ papɩsɩ Listrɩ nɛ Ikoniyɔm nɛ Antɩyɔɔsɩ, Pisidii egeetiye taa.

eng: they strengthened the disciples and urged them to remain firm in the faith. They told them, If we are to enter Gods kingdom, we must pass through many troubles.
local: Patalaa lɛ, peyele pɩla peeɖe Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa hʋnʋm nɛ pa-laŋa hɛɛ. Paaheyi-wɛ se pɔ́ɖɔ́kɩ lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ pɛwɛnɩ Yesu yɔɔ yɔ nesi naalɛ kpam. Paatasɩ-wɛ heyuu se pʋwɛɛ se pɔtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ nɛ pʋcɔ pasʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa.

eng: They appointed elders for each church. With prayer and fasting, they committed these elders to the Lord, in whom they had placed their trust.
local: Paa Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ palɩzaɣ nabɛyɛ se pɛkɛnɩ sɔsaa. Palɩzɩɣ-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa nɛ wɛ pɔhɔkɩ nɔɔ nɛ pɛsɛɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, paɖʋzɩ-wɛ e-nesi taa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-yɔɔ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ.

eng: After Paul and Barnabas traveled through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
local: Pakʋyɩ Pisidii egeetiye taa peeɖe lɛ, powolo Pamfilii ñɩnɖɛ taa.

eng: They proclaimed the word in Perga, then went down to Attalia.
local: Powoba lɛ, peheyi ɛyáa Ɛsɔtɔm Pɛɛrjee tɛtʋ taa, pɛtɛma lɛ, pɛɖɛɛ Atalii ñɩndʋ taa.

eng: From there they sailed to Antioch, where they had been entrusted by Gods grace to the work they had now completed.
local: Peeɖe pasʋ mɛlɛ nɛ papɩsɩ Antɩyɔɔsɩ, Siirii egeetiye taa. Peeɖe paaɖʋzɩ-wɛ Ɛsɔ nesi taa se ɛlabɩ-wɛ lootiye nɛ pala pa-tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ palaba ɛzʋnɔ yɔ.

eng: On their arrival, they gathered the church together and reported everything that God had accomplished through their activity, and how God had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
local: Patalaa lɛ, pekpeɣli Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma nɛ pɛkɛdɩ-wɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa Ɛsɔ tɩŋnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛla yɔ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛha mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ waɖɛ nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: They stayed with the disciples a long time.
local: Pɛtɛm-wɛ kɛdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa nɛ Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa pacaɣ alɩwaatʋ sakɩyɛ Antɩyɔɔsɩ peeɖe.

eng: Some people came down from Judea teaching the family of believers, Unless you are circumcised according to the custom weve received from Moses, you cant be saved.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye ɛyáa nabɛyɛ lɩnɩ Yudee egeetiye taa nɛ powolo Antɩyɔɔsɩ nɛ peheyiɣ taabalaa se yee peteyele nɛ pɛpɛlɩ-wɛ ɛzɩ palakʋʋ sɔnzɩ nzɩ́ Mooyiizi ɖʋwa yɔ sɩ-taa yɔ, paahikiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ.

eng: Paul and Barnabas took sides against these Judeans and argued strongly against their position. The church at Antioch appointed Paul, Barnabas, and several others from Antioch to go up to Jerusalem to set this question before the apostles and the elders.
local: Pɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ pekizini-wɛ-tʋ nɛ pa nɛ wɛ pɔhɔmnɩ ɖama tɔm pɩŋŋ. Peeɖe taabalaa tɔm se: yee ḿbʋ́, Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ nɛ pa-taa nabɛyɛ powolo Yeruusalɛm, Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa nɛ ɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ pana-tʋ.

eng: The church at Antioch included prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon , Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen , and Saul.
local: Náyaa nɛ wɩlɩyaa paawɛ Antɩyɔɔsɩ Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa. Ḿbá yɔ: Barnaabaasɩ nɛ Siimeyɔɔ weyi payaɣ se Kɩkpɛdʋ yɔ nɛ Siirɛɛnɩ tʋ Lusiyuusi nɛ Manayɛɛnɩ weyi paaɖɔkɩ ɛ nɛ wiyaʋ Eroodi pa-pɩcatʋ taa yɔ nɛ Sɔɔlɩ.

eng: The church sent this delegation on their way. They traveled through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling stories about the conversion of the Gentiles to everyone. Their reports thrilled the brothers and sisters.
local: Pɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma cɛlɩ-wɛ mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ-wɛ pa-nʋmɔʋ ɖɔm taa yɔ. Paatɩŋnɩ Fenisii nɛ Samaarɩɩ pa-ageeta taa nɛ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ pɛkɛdɩɣ a-taa ñɩma ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ yeba nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ papɩsɩnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ piye piye nɛ tɩla taabalaa tɩŋa leleŋ pɩŋŋ.

eng: As they were worshipping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, Appoint Barnabas and Saul to the work I have called them to undertake.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye lɛ, pɔhɔkɩ nɔɔ nɛ poluŋuu Kɩpaɣlʋ tɛɛ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu heyi-wɛ se: «Ɩlɩzɩ Barnaabaasɩ nɛ Sɔɔlɩ nɛ pala tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ manɖʋ-wɛ yɔ.».

eng: When they arrived in Jerusalem, the church, the apostles, and the elders all welcomed them. They gave a full report of what God had accomplished through their activity.
local: Patalɩ Yeruusalɛm lɛ, peeɖe Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma nɛ pɔ-sɔsaa nɛ tiyiyaa ḿbá pamʋ-wɛ. Ɛlɛ́, pɛkɛdɩ Yeruusalɛm ñɩma ḿbá mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ katɩŋnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛla yɔ pɩtɩŋa payɩ.

eng: After they fasted and prayed, they laid their hands on these two and sent them off.
local: Pɛtɛm nɔɔ hɔkʋʋ nɛ pɛsɛɛ Ɛsɔ lɛ, patɩnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nesi nɛ peyebi-wɛ nɛ pɛɖɛɛ.

eng: Some believers from among the Pharisees stood up and claimed, The Gentiles must be circumcised. They must be required to keep the Law from Moses.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Farɩsɩ ŋgbɛyɛ taa nabɛyɛ kɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ. Ḿbá kʋyɩna nɛ pɔtɔ se: «Mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se pɛpɛlɩ-wɛ nɛ peheyi-wɛ se pɔɖɔ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ yɔɔ.».

eng: After the Holy Spirit sent them on their way, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.
local: Ḿbʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tiyi Barnaabaasɩ nɛ Sɔɔlɩ ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ lɩm cɔ-ɖɩ pɩta nɛ payaɣ-ɖɩ se Ciiprɩ yɔ ɖɩ-taa. Pakʋyɩ Antɩyɔɔsɩ lɛ, powolo Selesii tɛtʋ taa nɛ pasʋʋ mɛlɛ nɛ pɛɖɛɛ.

eng: The apostles and the elders gathered to consider this matter.
local: Tiyiyaa ḿbá nɛ ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa panɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ lɛ, pekpeɣli ɖama nɛ pamaɣzɩ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: In Salamis they proclaimed Gods word in the Jewish synagogues. John was with them as their assistant.
local: Patalɩ Ciiprɩ peeɖe Salaamiini tɛtʋ taa lɛ, papazɩ Ɛsɔtɔm susuu Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ taa. Yohanɛɛsɩ Maarkɩ kɛwɛ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛsɩŋnɩ-wɛ.

eng: After much debate, Peter stood and addressed them, Fellow believers, you know that, early on, God chose me from among you as the one through whom the Gentiles would hear the word of the gospel and come to believe.
local: Paahɔmnɩ ɖama tɔm siŋŋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ Pɩyɛ kʋyaa nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Taabalaa! Ɩtɛm naʋ mɩ-maɣmaɣ se Ɛsɔ lɩzɩ-m mɩ-hɛkʋ taa cɩnɛ kajalaɣ alɩwaatʋ taa se mowolo meheyi mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ.

eng: They traveled throughout the island until they arrived at Paphos. There they found a certain man named Bar-Jesus, a Jew who was a false prophet and practiced sorcery.
local: Paacɔ Ciiprɩ ɛjaɖɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩtɩŋa nɛ pɔkɔɔ patalɩ Paafɔɔsɩ tɛtʋ taa. Peeɖe pakatɩ tɩʋ nɔɔyʋ, payaɣ-ɩ se Baarɩ-Yesu. Ɛɛkɛ Yuda tʋ nɛ ɛkpakɩɣ ɛ-tɩ se ɛkɛ náyʋ.

eng: God, who knows peoples deepest thoughts and desires, confirmed this by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as he did to us.
local: Ɛsɔ naɣ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɛyáa laŋa taa yɔ, ɛnʋ hanɩ ɛyáa ḿbá e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ɛhaʋ-ɖʋ-kʋ yɔ nɛ pɩwɩlɩ se e-liu wɛɛ se pɛkɛ ɛ-ñɩma.

eng: He kept company with the governor of that province, an intelligent man named Sergius Paulus. The governor sent for Barnabas and Saul since he wanted to hear Gods word.
local: Ɛɛwɛ Ciiprɩ ɛjaɖɛ ñʋʋdʋ Sɛrjiyuusi Pɔɔlusi cɔlɔ. Ɛ́lɛ́ kɛwɛ lɛɣtʋ siŋŋ. Nakʋyʋ wiye lɛ, ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ, etiyi se payaa-ɩ Barnaabaasɩ nɛ Sɔɔlɩ nɛ powolo peheyi-i Ɛsɔtɔm, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛsɔɔlɩ tɩ-nɩʋ.

eng: He made no distinction between us and them, but purified their deepest thoughts and desires through faith.
local: Ɛtɔsɔɔlɩ Yuda ñɩma ɖa nɛ pɩkɩlɩ-wɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mbá patɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛñalɩ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm.

eng: But Elymas the sorcerer opposed them, trying to steer the governor away from the faith.
local: Tɩʋ ɛnʋ, ɛɖɩkaɣ-wɛ nʋmɔʋ nɛ ɛñɩnɩɣ se ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ ɛtaawɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ. Payaɣ-ɩ nɛ Grɛɛkɩ kʋnʋŋ taa se: Eliimaasɩ.

eng: Why then are you now challenging God by placing a burden on the shoulders of these disciples that neither we nor our ancestors could bear?.
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɩtakɩɣ Ɛsɔ se ɩna nɛ ɩsʋkɩ Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa sʋʋtʋ ndʋ́ ɖa-maɣmaɣ nɛ ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa ɖɩtapɩzɩ se ɖɩsɩɣlɩ yɔ?.

eng: Empowered by the Holy Spirit, Saul, also known as Paul, glared at Bar-Jesus and.
local: Peeɖe Sɔɔlɩ weyi payaɣ ɖɔɖɔ se Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu wɛ ɛ-yɔɔ siŋŋ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ yɔ, ɛcɔnɩ-ɩ pɩŋŋ.

eng: On the contrary, we believe that we and they are saved in the same way, by the grace of the Lord Jesus.
local: Đa lɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu lootiye yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ nɛ ɖihiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ ɛzɩ ḿbá yɔ.».

eng: said, You are a deceiver and trickster! You devil! You attack anything that is right! Will you never stop twisting the straight ways of the Lord into crooked paths?.
local: nɛ eheyi-i se: «Cɛtɩyʋ ña, ŋcɛtɩɣ ɛyáa tɩŋa! Kañadʋ ña weyi ŋŋcaɣ se ŋna pʋyʋ kʋtʋʋzʋ ñɛ-ɛsɩndaa kpem yɔ! Đooye ŋcaɣ se ŋyele Kɩpaɣlʋ nɩmaŋ wɛɛkʋʋ?.

eng: The entire assembly fell quiet as they listened to Barnabas and Paul describe all the signs and wonders God did among the Gentiles through their activity.
local: Peeɖe mbá patɩŋa paakpeɣlaa yɔ, posum ɖiɣ ɖiɣ nɛ pewelesiɣni Barnaabaasɩ nɛ Pɔɔlɩ. Paba kɛdaɣ-wɛ kawɩlɩsɩ nzɩ́ Ɛsɔ tɩŋnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ yɔ nɛ hama lakasɩ nzɩ́ ɛtɩŋnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛla mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa yɔ sɩ-tɔm.

eng: Listen! The Lords power is set against you. You will be blind for a while, unable even to see the daylight. At once, Bar-Jesus eyes were darkened, and he began to grope about for someone to lead him around by the hand.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, meheyiɣ-ŋ se Kɩpaɣlʋ ɖʋʋ-ŋ kʋñɔŋ sɔsɔʋ: ŋyʋlʋmʋʋ nɛ ŋŋtasɩɣ wɩsɩ naʋ alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ.» Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Eliimaasɩ yʋlʋmɩ nɛ ekiitiɣ yem nɛ ɛñɩnɩɣ nɔɔyʋ se ɛhɔm-ɩ.

eng: When Barnabas and Paul also fell silent, James responded, Fellow believers, listen to me.
local: Pɛtɛm-wɛ kɛdʋʋ lɛ, Yakubu heyi-wɛ se: «Taabalaa! Iwelesi nɛ ɩnɩɩ.

eng: When the governor saw what had taken place, he came to believe, for he was astonished by the teaching about the Lord.
local: Ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ, ɛna ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔ. Pɩɩɖɩɣzɩ-ɩ nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ tɔm ndʋ́ pawɩlɩ-ɩ yɔ kpem.

eng: Simon reported how, in his kindness, God came to the Gentiles in the first place, to raise up from them a people of God.
local: Siimɔɔ Pɩyɛ kɛdɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ kɔcɔnɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ ɖooo kiɖe tɛɛ nɛ ɛlɩzɩ pa-taa lɛlaa se pɛkɛ ɛ-ñɩma yɔ.

eng: Paul and his companions sailed from Paphos to Perga in Pamphylia. John deserted them there and returned to Jerusalem.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ ɛ-taabalaa pakʋyɩ Paafɔɔsɩ tɛtʋ taa nɛ pasʋʋ mɛlɛ nɛ powolo Pɛɛrjee ñɩndʋ ndʋ́ tɩwɛ Pamfilii egeetiye taa yɔ tɩ-taa. Peeɖe Yohanɛɛsɩ Maarkɩ kʋyɩ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ Yeruusalɛm.

eng: The prophets words agree with this; as it is written.
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ náyaa mbá Ɛsɔ tiyi-wɛ yɔ pa-takayɩsɩ taa yɔ. Pamawa se:.

eng: They went on from Perga and arrived at Antioch in Pisidia. On the Sabbath, they entered and found seats in the synagogue there.
local: Piyebi Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ lɛ, pakʋyɩ Pɛɛrjee peeɖe nɛ powolo Antɩyɔɔsɩ tɛtʋ taa Pisidii egeetiye taa. Kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ talaa lɛ, pasʋʋ Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa nɛ pacaɣ.

eng: After this I will return, and I will rebuild Davids fallen tent; I will rebuild what has been torn down. I will restore it .
local: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ tɔm se: pʋwayɩ lɛ, manpɩsɩɣ mɔnkɔɔ nɛ menkpeɣli wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Daaviidi ñɩma mbá pamɩzɩ ɖama yɔ: pɛwɛ ɛzɩ ɖɩɣa ŋgá kɔyɔkaa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, manɖaɣnɩɣ-kɛ maʋ nɛ mɔñɔɔzɩ-kɛ.

eng: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue leaders invited them, Brothers, if one of you has a sermon for the people, please speak.
local: Đɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ tɩɩkalɩɣ ɛyáa tɩŋa ɛsɩndaa Mooyiizi paɣtʋ nɛ náyaa pa-takayɩsɩ taa. Ɛtɛma lɛ, ɖɩ-taa ñʋndɩnaa tiyi se peheyi Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ se: «Taabalaa! Yee ɩwɛnɩ natʋyʋ se iheyiɣ samaɣ nɛ pɩlabɩ-kɛ hʋnʋm yɔ, ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩyɔɔdɩ-tʋ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ.».

eng: so that the rest of humanity will seek the Lord, even all the Gentiles who belong to me. The Lord says this, the one who does these things .
local: Malakɩ ḿbʋ́ se mbá pɩkazaa yɔ, pañɩnɩ-m nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa, pɩkpɛndɩnɩ agɔmaa mbá manɖʋwa se pɛkɛ ma-ñɩma yɔ patɩŋa payɩ. Tɔm ńdʋ́ ma Kɩpaɣlʋ mɔyɔɔdaa.

eng: Standing up, Paul gestured with his hand and said, Fellow Israelites and Gentile God-worshippers, please listen to me.
local: Peeɖe Pɔɔlɩ kʋyaa nɛ ɛñɩɣ ɛyáa nesi nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma nɛ mbá mɩ ɩñaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ! Iwelesi nɛ ɩnɩɩ:.

eng: known from earliest times.
local: Kɩpaɣlʋ weyi ma meyeba nɛ ɛyáa tɩlɩ pʋnɛ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɖooo lɔŋ yɔ, mɔyɔɔdɩnɩ ḿbʋ́.›.

eng: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors. God made them a great people while they lived as strangers in the land of Egypt. With his great power, he led them out of that country.
local: Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma Ɛsɔ lɩzɩnɩ ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa. Ɛnʋ yebina nɛ pɔɖɔɔ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ paawɛ yomiye taa Ejiptɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa yɔ nɛ pʋcɔ ɛkɔɔ eholi-wɛ nɛ e-ɖoŋ.

eng: Therefore, I conclude that we shouldnt create problems for Gentiles who turn to God.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ma Yakubu meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɩtaakɔnɩ kaɖɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma nɛ pɔsɔɔlɩ se papɩsɩnɩ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: For about forty years, God put up with them in the wilderness.
local: Paa pɔ-tɔm ɛɛwɛ kaɖɛ yɔ, ɛɛcɔnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ lakʋ taa nɛ pɩtalɩ ɛzɩ pɩnzɩ nɩɩnaza yɔ.

eng: Instead, we should write a letter, telling them to avoid the pollution associated with idols, sexual immorality, eating meat from strangled animals, and consuming blood.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩma-wɛ takayaɣ nɛ ɖiheyi-wɛ se pataatɔɔ kɩlaʋ nandʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tipisiɣ ɛyáa. Pataatɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ calɩm nɛ kpɩna wena pɛtɛcɛ a-liŋ tɛɛ yɔ. Pakpa pa-tɩ nɛ yem yem lakasɩ.

eng: God conquered seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave the Israelites their land as an inheritance.
local: Eɖezi Kanaanɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa hɔɔlɩŋ lʋbɛ taa ñɩma nɛ ɛkpaɣ pɛ-tɛtʋ ɛha-wɛ.

eng: After all, Moses has been proclaimed in every city for a long time, and is read aloud every Sabbath in every synagogue.
local: Mɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖooo lɔŋ ɛyáa wɩlaɣ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ paa tɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-taa paa kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ ŋgʋ́ ki-wiye Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ taa.».

eng: This happened over a period of about four hundred fifty years. After this, he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
local: Pɩtɩŋa pɩɩwɛ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩtalɩ ɛzɩ pɩnzɩ nasɩnaza nɛ nɩɩnʋwa ḿbʋ́ yɔ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛha-wɛ nʋmɔɖɛyaa nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ náyʋ Samʋyɛɛlɩ alɩwaatʋ.

eng: The apostles and the elders, along with the entire church, agreed to send some delegates chosen from among themselves to Antioch, together with Paul and Barnabas. They selected Judas Barsabbas and Silas, who were leaders among the brothers and sisters.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa nɛ ɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma nɛ pɔ-sɔsaa, patɩŋa payɩ pala lɩmaɣza se palɩzɩ pa-taa nabɛyɛ nɛ pɔsɔzɩ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ petiyi-wɛ Antɩyɔɔsɩ. Peeɖe palɩzɩ Yuudi weyi pakʋm hɩɖɛ se Barnaabaasɩ yɔ nɛ Silaasɩ. Taabalaa lɛlaa ñaŋaɣ-wɛ siŋŋ.

eng: The Israelites requested a king, so God gave them Saul, Kishs son, from the tribe of Benjamin, and he served as their king for forty years.
local: Pʋbʋ wayɩ lɛ, pɔpɔzɩ Ɛsɔ se ɛsɩɩ-wɛ wiyaʋ. Peeɖe ɛlɩzɩ Kiisi pɩyalʋ Saʋlɩ, Bɛnjamɛɛ cejewiye taa nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ kɛ pe-wiyaʋ sɔsɔ pɩnzɩ nɩɩnaza.

eng: They were to carry this letter: The apostles and the elders, to the Gentile brothers and sisters in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia. Greetings!.
local: Pʋcɔ pɛɖɛɛ lɛ, paɖʋzɩ pe-nesi taa takayaɣ. Pama ka-taa se: «Taabalaa, Krɩstʋ ñɩma mbá mɩ ɩtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma nɛ ɩwɛɛ Antɩyɔɔsɩ tɛtʋ taa nɛ Siirii nɛ Siliisii pa-ageeta taa yɔ! Đa mɩ-taabalaa Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa nɛ Yeruusalɛm ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma nɛ pɔ-sɔsaa ɖamaɣnɩ-mɩ takayaɣ kanɛ, ɖɩsɛ-mɩ!.

eng: After God removed him, he raised up David to be their king. God testified concerning him, I have found David, Jesses son, a man who shares my desires. Whatever my will is, he will do.
local: Ɛsɔ mʋ kewiyaɣ Saʋlɩ cɔlɔ lɛ, ɛcɛlɩ-kɛ Yesee pɩyalʋ Daaviidi nɛ ɛtɔ se: ‹Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, mana weyi me-liu wɛɛ yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ kɔŋnɩ labʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ me-liu wɛɛ se ɛla yɔ.›.

eng: Weve heard that some of our number have disturbed you with unsettling words we didnt authorize.
local: Đɩnɩwa se ɛyáa nabɛyɛ lɩnɩ ɖɛ-tɛ nɛ pɔkɔɔ mɩ-tɛ nɛ peliɣti-mɩ nɛ pɛkpɛzɩ mɩ-kalɩzasɩ nɛ pɔ-tɔm. Đɩtana pɔ-yɔɔ kpem.

eng: From this mans descendants, God brought to Israel a savior, Jesus, just as he promised.
local: E-liɖe taa palʋlɩ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma ñʋʋ yaɖʋ Yesu ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ kaɖʋʋ yɔ.

eng: We reached a united decision to select some delegates and send them to you along with our dear friends Barnabas and Paul.
local: Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ ɖɩnɩɩnɩ ɖama nɛ ɖɩlɩzɩ ɖa-taa nabɛyɛ nɛ ɖitiyi pa nɛ taabalaa kʋsɔɔlaa Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ mɩ-cɔlɔ.

eng: Before Jesus appearance, John proclaimed to all the Israelites a baptism to show they were changing their hearts and lives.
local: Pʋcɔ nɛ Yesu pazɩ ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ lɛ, Yohanɛɛsɩ kosusi Ɛsɔtɔm Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma kpeekpe se pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ nɛ palabɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ.

eng: These people have devoted their lives to the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ḿbá kelemni pa-tɩ sɩm nɔɔ taa ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔɔ.

eng: As John was completing his mission, he said, Who do you think I am? Im not the one you think I am, but he is coming after me. Im not worthy to loosen his sandals.
local: Yohanɛɛsɩ tɛzaɣ ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se: ‹Pɩtɛkɛ ma lɛ weyi ɩɖaŋaa se ɛkɔŋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ma-wayɩ lɛ, ɛkɔŋ. Mantatɩɩmʋna se moluŋ nɛ mɛyɛɖɩ ɛ-naataŋgbala tɔnzɩ maɣmaɣ.›.

eng: Therefore, we are sending Judas and Silas. They will confirm what we have written.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖitiyiɣ Yuudi nɛ Silaasɩ se peheyi-mɩ tɔm ndʋ́ ɖɩma takayaɣ kanɛ ka-taa yɔ nɛ pa-maɣmaɣ pɔ-nɔɔ.

eng: Brothers, children of Abrahams family, and you Gentile God-worshippers, the message about this salvation has been sent to us.
local: Man-taabalaa, mbá mɩ ɩkɛ Abraham sayɩnaa nɛ mbá mɩ ɩwɛ cɩnɛ nɛ ɩñaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ! Đa nɛ mɩ ɖatɩŋa peheyi ñʋʋ yabʋ tɔm tʋnɛ.

eng: The Holy Spirit has led us to the decision that no burden should be placed on you other than these essentials:.
