local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖa nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖɩlabɩ lɩmaɣza se ɖɩtaasʋʋ-mɩ sʋʋtʋ natʋyʋ yem nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ ndʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ-mɩ yɔ.

eng: The people in Jerusalem and their leaders didnt recognize Jesus. By condemning him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.
local: Toovenim lɛ, Yeruusalɛm ñɩma nɛ pa-awiya, pekizi Yesu nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ náyaa tɔm ndʋ́ pakalaɣ paa kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ ŋgʋ́ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, palabɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pahʋnɩ Yesu nɛ pakʋ ɛ-tɔm.

eng: refuse food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality. You will do well to avoid such things. Farewell.
local: Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ɩtaatɔɔ kɩlaʋ nandʋ yaa calɩm yaa kpɩna wena pɛtɛcɛ a-liŋ tɛɛ yɔ. Ɩkpa mɩ-tɩ nɛ yem yem lakasɩ. Ɩlabɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pʋwɛɣ ɖeu. Taabalaa, ɖɩsɛ-mɩ!».

eng: Even though they didnt find a single legal basis for the death penalty, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
local: Ŋgʋ́, patatɩɩnaʋ mbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ pʋwɛɛ se pakʋ-ɩ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, peheyi Pilaatɩ faŋŋ se ɛkʋ-ɩ.

eng: When Barnabas, Paul, and the delegates were sent on their way, they went down to Antioch. They gathered the believers and delivered the letter.
local: Pamʋ takayaɣ ŋ́gá ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pakpaɣ Antɩyɔɔsɩ nʋmɔʋ. Patalaa lɛ, pekpeɣli ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma tɩŋa payɩ nɛ pɛcɛlɩ-wɛ-kɛ.

eng: When they finished doing everything that had been written about him, they took him down from the cross and laid him in a tomb.
local: Ḿbʋ́ palabɩ-ɩ ɛzɩ pamaʋ ɛ-tɔm Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ nɛ petisi-i nɛ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ nɛ powoni pepi.

eng: The people read it, delighted with its encouraging message.
local: Pakalɩ-kɛ patɩŋa pɛ-ɛsɩndaa lɛ, pɩlabɩ-wɛ hʋnʋm nɛ pa-laŋa hʋlʋmɩ.

eng: But God raised him from the dead!.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ fezi-i nɛ sɩɖaa taa.

eng: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they said many things that encouraged and strengthened the brothers and sisters.
local: Yuudi nɛ Silaasɩ pa-maɣmaɣ paakɛ náyaa. Pʋ-yɔɔ paba ñɛkɛdɩ-wɛ tɔm sakɩyɛ se pɩlabɩ-wɛ hʋnʋm nɛ pɛtɛzɩ pɩʋ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa.

eng: He appeared over many days to those who had traveled with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now his witnesses to the people.
local: Ɛlɩzɩ ɛ-tɩ kɩyakɩŋ sakɩyɛ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ ɛyáa mbá paasɩ-ɩ nɛ Galilee egeetiye taa nɛ powoni-i Yeruusalɛm yɔ. Ḿbá lɩzɩɣnɩ ɛ-aseɣɖe lɛɛlɛɛyɔ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma cɔlɔ.

eng: Judas and Silas stayed there awhile, then were sent back with a blessing of peace from the brothers and sisters to those who first sent them.
local: Paacaɣ peeɖe alɩwaatʋ cabɩ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma ḿbá palabɩ-wɛ wele wele se papɩsɩ mbá paatiyi-wɛ yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ. .

eng: We proclaim to you the good news. What God promised to our ancestors.
local: Đa lɛ, ɩɩnaɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ tɔm ndʋ́ ɖiheyiɣ-mɩ yɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa yɔ.

eng: Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, where, together with many others, they taught and proclaimed the good news of the Lords word.
local: Piyebi Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ lɛ, paba ñacaɣ Antɩyɔɔsɩ peeɖe nɛ pa nɛ lɛlaa sakɩyɛ pawɩlɩɣ nɛ posusuu Kɩpaɣlʋ tɔm.

eng: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus. As it was written in the second psalm, You are my son; today I have become your father. .
local: ɖa pa-sayɩnaa ɛlabɩ-pʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, efezi Yesu ɛzɩ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ nɛ pama Keɣsi Takayaɣ ñʋŋ naalɛ taa yɔ. Ɛyɔɔdaa se: ‹Ña lɛ man-Pɩyalʋ. Pɩkpaɣʋnɩ sɔnɔ yɔ, ma lɛ ña-Caa.›.

eng: Some time later, Paul said to Barnabas, Lets go back and visit all the brothers and sisters in every city where we preached the Lords word. Lets see how they are doing.
local: Pacaɣ Antɩyɔɔsɩ kɩyakɩŋ cabɩ lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ heyi Barnaabaasɩ se papɩsɩ taabalaa cɔlɔ tɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa tɩ-taa paasusi Kɩpaɣlʋ tɔm yɔ nɛ pana se pɩsɩnɩ le.

eng: God raised Jesus from the dead, never again to be subjected to deaths decay. Therefore, God said, I will give to you the holy and firm promises I made to David. .
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, Ɛsɔ tɔm se efeziɣ-i nɛ sɩɖaa taa se ɛtaatasɩ sɩbʋ wiɖiyi kaʋ nɛ ɛɖazɩ. Ndʋ́ ɛyɔɔdaa yɔ, ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Mɔnkɔŋ mɩ-yɔɔ wazʋʋ siŋŋ ɛzɩ maaɖʋʋ se mawazɩɣ Daaviidi yɔɔ yɔ. Pɩkɛ toovenim, mɔyɔɔdɩna.›.

eng: Barnabas wanted to take John Mark with them.
local: Peeɖe Barnaabaasɩ tɔm se pɛkpɛndɩnɩ Yohanɛɛsɩ weyi pakʋm hɩɖɛ se Maarkɩ yɔ.

eng: In another place it is said, You will not let your holy one experience deaths decay. .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Daaviidi tasɩ maʋ ɖoli lɛɛɖɛ se: ‹Ña Kɩpaɣlʋ, ŋŋyeki ña-ñɩnʋ nɛ ɛɖazɩ.›.

eng: Paul insisted that they shouldnt take him along, since he had deserted them in Pamphylia and hadnt continued with them in their work.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɔɔlɩ la lɩmaɣza se pataakpɛndɩnɩ-ɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, eesewa nɛ eyebi-wɛ Pamfilii nɛ ɛtatasɩ-wɛ sɩʋ pa-tʋmɩyɛ taa.

eng: David served Gods purpose in his own generation, then he died and was buried with his ancestors. He experienced deaths decay.
local: Daaviidi lɛ, ɛɛlaba mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ siŋŋ yɔ ɛ-alɩwaatʋ taa nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛsɩ nɛ pepim-i ɛ-cɔzɔnaa cɔlɔ nɛ ɛɖazɩ.

eng: Their argument became so intense that they went their separate ways. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ pɔhɔmnɩ ɖama tɔm ka ka nɛ pɩnɩɩ-wɛ nɛ pɛcɛ ɖama. Ɛlɛ́, Barnaabaasɩ kpaɣ Maarkɩ nɛ ɛ nɛ ɩ pasʋʋ mɛlɛ nɛ powolo Ciiprɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa.

eng: but the one whom God has raised up didnt experience deaths decay.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yesu weyi Ɛsɔ fezaa yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ taɖazɩ.

eng: Paul chose Silas and left, entrusted by the brothers and sisters to the Lords grace.
local: Piyele Pɔɔlɩ ñakpaɣ Silaasɩ nɛ taabalaa ɖʋzɩ-wɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ nesi taa se ɛlabɩ-wɛ lootiye. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pacaŋ pɛɖɛɛ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, know this: Through Jesus we proclaim forgiveness of sins to you. From all those sins from which you couldnt be put in right relationship with God through Moses Law.
local: Taabalaa! Pʋwɛɛ se ɩtɩlɩ camɩyɛ se ɛ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ peheyiɣ-mɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm kpeɣu tɔm.

eng: He traveled through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
local: Paatɩŋnɩ Siirii nɛ Siliisii pa-ageeta taa nɛ peyele a-taa Krɩstʋ aŋgba taa ñɩma tɛzɩ pɩʋ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa.

eng: through Jesus everyone who believes is put in right relationship with God.
local: Weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, peɖeziɣ-i Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa Mooyiizi paɣtʋ ñaapɩzɩɣ tiɖezi-i yɔ.

eng: Take care that the prophets words dont apply to you:.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ, ɩtaayele nɛ tɔm ndʋ́ náyaa ma pa-takayɩsɩ taa yɔ, tɩtalɩ-mɩ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Mbá mɩ ɩtakpaɣ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɩyaa pʋyʋ yɔ, iwelesi nɛ ɩnɩɩ nɛ pɩɖɩɣzɩ-mɩ nɛ ɩmʋʋ yem! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, malakɩ tʋmɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ naɖɩyɛ mɩ-alɩwaatʋ taa nɛ paa nɔɔyʋ eheyi-mɩ ɖɩ-tɔm yɔ, iitisiɣ.› » «Maañɩnɩɣ se pɩtalɩ-mɩ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people urged them to speak about these things again on the next Sabbath.
local: Pɔɔlɩ tɛm ɛyáa tɔm heyuu Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa nɛ ɛ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ palɩɩ lɛ, ɛyáa tendi-wɛ se: kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ lɛɛkʋ wiye lɛ, paatasɩ wobu nɛ paɖaɣnɩ-wɛ kɛdʋʋ tɔm ńdʋ́.

eng: When the people in the synagogue were dismissed, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism accompanied Paul and Barnabas, who urged them to remain faithful to the message of Gods grace.
local: Ɛyáa tɩŋa lɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ peeɖe lɛ, Yuda ñɩma sakɩyɛ nɛ mbá palakaɣ Yuda ñɩma sɔnzɩ yɔ, patɩŋ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ pa-wayɩ. Peeɖe paba sazɩ-wɛ se pɔ́ɖɔ́kɩ pa-tɩ camɩyɛ kpam nɛ nɔɔyʋ ɛtaakɔɔ elesi-wɛ Ɛsɔ lootiye nʋmɔʋ taa.

eng: On the next Sabbath, almost everyone in the city gathered to hear the Lords word.
local: Pɩtalɩ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ lɛɛkʋ wiye lɛ, Antɩyɔɔsɩ tɛtʋ taa ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ kpeɣli ɖama Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa se pewelesi Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: When the Jews saw the crowds, they were overcome with jealousy. They argued against what Paul was saying by slandering him.
local: Yuda ñɩma na ɛyáa samaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, papazɩ ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ labʋ Pɔɔlɩ yɔɔ nɛ patʋʋ-ɩ nɛ pɛkpɛzɩɣnɩ-ɩ.

eng: Speaking courageously, Paul and Barnabas said, We had to speak Gods word to you first. Since you reject it and show that you are unworthy to receive eternal life, we will turn to the Gentiles.
local: Peeɖe ɛ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ peheyi-wɛ kpayɩ se: «Mɩ pɩɩmʋnaa se ɖɩlɛyɩnɩ Ɛsɔtɔm heyuu nɛ pʋcɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɖenɖe ikizi-tʋ nɛ ɩmaɣzɩɣ se ɩtamʋnɩ se ihiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ yɔ, ɖiyeki-mɩ ɖiwolo mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: This is what the Lord commanded us: I have made you a light for the Gentiles, so that you could bring salvation to the end of the earth .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɔm ndʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ heyi-ɖʋ yɔ, ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Malɩzɩ-mɩ se ɩkɛnɩ ajɛya kpeekpe taa ɛyáa ñalɩmɩyɛ nɛ piyele patɩŋa payɩ pehiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ.› ».

eng: When the Gentiles heard this, they rejoiced and honored the Lords word. Everyone who was appointed for eternal life believed.
local: Mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, panɩ-tʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa-taa labɩ-tʋ nɛ leleŋ pɩŋŋ nɛ pɔtɔ se Kɩpaɣlʋ tɔm wɛ ɖeu. Peeɖe mbá patɩŋa pɩɩwɛɛ se pehiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ yɔ, pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ.

eng: and the Lords word was broadcast throughout the entire region.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ tɔm pazɩ cɔʋ egeetiye ńɖɩ́ ɖɩtɩŋa ɖɩ-taa.

eng: However, the Jews provoked the prominent women among the Gentile God-worshippers, as well as the citys leaders. They instigated others to harass Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their district.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yuda ñɩma ḿbá, pasazɩ abila wena awokaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa yɔ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ tɛtʋ ñʋndɩnaa nɛ pakʋyɩ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Barnaabaasɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pɔɖɔɔnɩ-wɛ pɛ-ɛjaɖɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa.

eng: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from their feet and went to Iconium.
local: Peeɖe paba kpizi-wɛ pa-naŋgbanzɩ taa mʋzʋʋ nɛ pɩwɩlɩ se peyebi-wɛ nɛ pa-sʋʋtʋ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pakʋyɩ Antɩyɔɔsɩ Pisidii egeetiye taa peeɖe nɛ powolo pacaɣ Ikoniyɔm tɛtʋ taa.

eng: Because of the abundant presence of the Holy Spirit in their lives, the disciples were overflowing with happiness.
local: Piyele Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa mbá pɛwɛ Antɩyɔɔsɩ peeɖe yɔ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu kɛwɛ pɔ-yɔɔ siŋŋ nɛ pɛwɛɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ taa kpem.

eng: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ kʋyɩ Atɛɛnɩ nɛ ewolo Kɔrɛntɩ tɛtʋ taa.

eng: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus. He had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul visited with them.
local: Peeɖe ɛkatɩ Yuda tʋ nɔɔyʋ, palʋlɩ-ɩ Pɔntɩ egeetiye taa. Payaɣ-ɩ se Akilaasɩ. Ɛ nɛ ɛ-halʋ Prisiili patalʋʋ peeɖe yɔ, piteleɖida. Itaalii ɛjaɖɛ taa palɩnaa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ weyi payaɣ se Kloodi yɔ ɛɛha nɔɔ se pataatasɩ naʋ Yuda tʋ nɔɔyʋ Room tɛtʋ taa. Pɔɔlɩ na-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ewolo pɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: Because they practiced the same trade, he stayed and worked with them. They all worked with leather.
local: nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ pɛwɛɛ nɛ palakɩ tʋmɩyɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paakɛ kɩɖanɖɩŋ laɖaa ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ɛnʋ yɔ.

eng: Every Sabbath he interacted with people in the synagogue, trying to convince both Jews and Greeks.
local: Paa kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ ŋgʋ́ lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ wokaɣ Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ nɛ Grɛɛkɩ ñɩma palakɩ lɩmaɣza Yesu tɔm yɔɔ nɛ ɛñɩnɩɣ se petisi-tʋ.

eng: Once Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul devoted himself fully to the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Silaasɩ nɛ ɛ-taabalʋ Timootee pɔkɔm palɩɩnɩ Masedonii nɛ patalɩ Pɔɔlɩ cɔlɔ Kɔrɛntɩ lɛ, pɩha-ɩ waɖɛ nɛ esusuu Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm alɩwaatʋ kpeekpe nɛ eheyiɣ Yuda ñɩma ḿbá se Yesu lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ.

eng: When they opposed and slandered him, he shook the dust from his clothes in protest and said to them, You are responsible for your own fates! Im innocent! From now on Ill go to the Gentiles.
local: Ɛlɛ́, paba ñakʋyʋʋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ patʋʋ-ɩ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ekpizi-wɛ e-wondu yɔɔ mʋzʋʋ nɛ pɩwɩlɩ se ɛkɔɔlɩ-wɛ nɛ eyebi-wɛ nɛ pa-sʋʋtʋ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Yee ɩkɔm ile yɔ, ɩtɩlɩ ɖeu se mɩ-maɣmaɣ mɩ-cɔlɔ pɩlɩnaa, pɩtatasɩ kɛʋ ma-ñɩndʋ. Pɩkpaɣʋnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, mɛnkɛɣnɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ.».

eng: He left the synagogue and went next door to the home of Titius Justus, a Gentile God-worshipper.
local: Peeɖe ɛkʋyɩ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ewolo abalʋ weyi payaɣ se Tiitiyuusi Yustuusi yɔ ɛ-tɛ. Pɩɩñɔtɩnɩ Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ kpam, abalʋ ɛnʋ eluŋaɣ Ɛsɔ tɛɛ.

eng: Crispus, the synagogue leader, and his entire household came to believe in the Lord. Many Corinthians believed and were baptized after listening to Paul.
local: Krispuusi weyi ɛkɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa ñʋʋdʋ yɔ, ɛɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ ɛ-ñɩma tɩŋa payɩ. Kɔrɛntɩ ñɩma sakɩyɛ mbá pewelesaɣ Pɔɔlɩ tɔm yɔ, paawɛnɩ ɖɔɖɔ lidaʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔ nɛ palabɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ.

eng: One night the Lord said to Paul in a vision, Dont be afraid. Continue speaking. Dont be silent.
local: Đoo naayɛ taa lɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ heyi Pɔɔlɩ ɖoziye taa se: «Taasɔɔndɩ, wɛɛ ŋyɔɔdʋʋ, taasu.

eng: Im with you and no one who attacks you will harm you, for I have many people in this city.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ma-maɣmaɣ mɛwɛ ñɔ-cɔlɔ. Nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛkpa-ŋ nɛ ɛlabɩ-ŋ kɩdɛkɛdɩm nabʋyʋ. Mbá pɔkɔŋ kɛʋ ma-ñɩma yɔ, pɔɖɔ tɛtʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-taa.».

eng: So he stayed there for eighteen months, teaching Gods word among them.
local: Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ Pɔɔlɩ caɣ Kɔrɛntɩ peeɖe pɩnaɣ nɛ hɔɔlʋʋ nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ ɛyáa Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: Now when Gallio was the governor of the province of Achaia, the Jews united in their opposition against Paul and brought him before the court.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Room tʋ weyi payaɣ se Galiyɔɔ yɔ ɛtɔɔ kewiyaɣ Akayɩ egeetiye taa yɔ, tɩ-taa Yuda ñɩma tam nɔɔ nɛ pakʋyɩ Pɔɔlɩ yɔɔ nɛ pakpa-ɩ se powokini-i tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ.

eng: This man is persuading others to worship God unlawfully, they declared.
local: Patanɩ-ɩ lɛ, pɔtɔ se: «Abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛwɩlɩɣ ɛyáa se poluŋ Ɛsɔ tɛɛ luŋuu mbʋ́ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́ paɖʋ paɣtʋ taa.».

eng: Just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, If there had been some sort of injury or criminal behavior, I would have reason to accept your complaint.
local: Pɔɔlɩ caɣaɣ se ekuli ɛ-nɔɔ lɛ, Galiyɔɔ maɣmaɣ cosi-wɛ se: «Yuda ñɩma mɩ! Yee ɛzaɣ́tʋ yaa kɩwɛɛkɩm sɔsɔm nabʋyʋ ɛɛlaba yɔ, pʋbʋ, maaɖɔkɩ mɔ-hɔyɛ nɛ mewelesini-mɩ ɛzɩ pʋmʋnaʋ yɔ.

eng: However, since these are squabbles about a message, names, and your own Law, deal with them yourselves. I have no desire to sit in judgment over such things.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɖenɖe pɩkɛnɩ tɔm hɔm yem tɔm pee nɛ hɩla nɛ mɩ-maɣmaɣ mɩ-paɣtʋ pɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, mɩ-cɔlɔ lɛ. Maahʋʋ pɩtɛ tɔm ńdʋ́!».

eng: He expelled them from the court.
local: Eheyi-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛɖɔɔnɩ-wɛ tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ.

eng: but everyone seized Sosthenes, the synagogue leader, and gave him a beating in the presence of the governor. None of this mattered to Gallio.
local: Peeɖe patɩŋa pakpa pɛ-Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa ñʋʋdʋ Sɔstɛɛnɩ tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ peeɖe nɛ papazɩ-ɩ mabʋ. Ɛlɛ́, piteheyi wiyaʋ Galiyɔɔ natʋyʋ.

eng: After Paul stayed in Corinth for some time, he said good-bye to the brothers and sisters. At the Corinthian seaport of Cenchreae he had his head shaved, since he had made a solemn promise. Then, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila, he sailed away to Syria.
local: Pɔɔlɩ katasɩ caɣʋ Kɔrɛntɩ peeɖe alɩwaatʋ sakɩyɛ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ taabalaa cɔlɔ nɛ ɛ nɛ Prisiili nɛ ɛ-walʋ Akilaasɩ pasʋʋ mɛlɛ se powoki Siirii ɛjaɖɛ taa. Pʋcɔ pɛɖɛɛ lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ kahʋm ɛ-ñʋʋ Sankree, mbʋ́ eɖuuni Ɛsɔ se ɛlakɩ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: After they arrived in Ephesus, he left Priscilla and Aquila and entered the synagogue and interacted with the Jews.
local: Pɔkɔm patalɩ Efɛɛzɩ tɛtʋ taa lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ yebi-wɛ nɛ ewolo Yuda ñɩma cɔlɔ pɛ-Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ palakɩ lɩmaɣza Yesu tɔm yɔɔ.

eng: They asked him to stay longer, but he declined.
local: Peeɖe paba tendi-i se ɛtasɩ caɣʋ pɔ-cɔlɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ekizi.

eng: As he said farewell to them, though, he added, God willing, I will return. Then he sailed off from Ephesus.
local: Ɛkʋyɩ pɔ-cɔlɔ se ɛɖɛɣ lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se: «Yee Ɛsɔ etisaa yɔ, manpɩsɩɣ mɩ-cɔlɔ.» Ɛtɛm-wɛ heyuu ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛsʋʋ mɛlɛ peeɖe nɛ ɛɖɛɛ.

eng: He arrived in Caesarea, went up to Jerusalem and greeted the church, and then went down to Antioch.
local: Ɛkɔm ɛtalɩ Sezaaree nɛ etii lɛ, ɛkpaɣ nʋmɔʋ nɛ ewolo Yeruusalɛm nɛ ɛsɛɛ Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ewolo Antɩyɔɔsɩ.

eng: After some time there he left and traveled from place to place in the region of Galatia and the district of Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.
local: Ɛcaɣ peeɖe alɩwaatʋ cabɩ lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ ɛtɩŋnɩ Galaasii nɛ Frijii pa-ageeta taa nɛ eyele nɛ a-taa Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa tɩŋa kpa ɖoŋ pe-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ taa.

eng: Meanwhile, a certain Jew named Apollos arrived in Ephesus. He was a native of Alexandria and was well-educated and effective in his use of the scriptures.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye Yuda tʋ nɔɔyʋ woba Efɛɛzɩ. Paalʋlɩ-ɩ Alɛksandrii tɛtʋ taa, payaɣ-ɩ se Apoloosi. Ɛsɩm Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa tɔm siŋŋ nɛ ɛsɩ yɔɔdʋʋ camɩyɛ.

eng: He had been instructed in the way of the Lord and spoke as one stirred up by the Spirit. He taught accurately the things about Jesus, even though he was aware only of the baptism John proclaimed and practiced.
local: Ɛɛkpɛlɩkɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ wayɩ tɩŋʋʋ tɔm camɩyɛ nɛ ɛwɛnɩ kpekpeka nɛ esusuu Yesu tɔm nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ-tʋ tɩ-nasɩ taa. Ɛlɛ́, Yohanɛɛsɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ ɖeke tɔm ɛɛsɩma.

eng: He began speaking with confidence in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they received him into their circle of friends and explained to him Gods way more accurately.
local: Ewobi peeɖe ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛsʋʋ Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa nɛ ɛpazɩ Ɛsɔtɔm susuu kaalɛyɩtʋ. Prisiili nɛ ɛ-walʋ Akilaasɩ panɩ ɛyɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, payaa-ɩ nɛ pɛkɛzɩ-ɩ hɔɔlʋʋ nɛ pɛtɛzɩ-ɩ kɛdʋʋ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se ɛyʋ ɛtɩŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ.

eng: When he wanted to travel to Achaia, the brothers and sisters encouraged him and wrote to the disciples so they would open their homes to him. Once he arrived, he was of great help to those who had come to believe through grace.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛɖʋ se ewoki Akayɩ egeetiye taa. Ɛlɛ́, taabalaa yele nɛ pɩlabɩ-ɩ hʋnʋm nɛ pama egeetiye ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa taabalaa takayaɣ se ɛtalaa lɛ, pamʋ-ɩ ɖeu. Ɛtalaa lɛ, ɛwɛɛ wazaɣ siŋŋ mbá pɩtɩŋnɩ Ɛsɔ lootiye yɔɔ nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: He would vigorously defeat Jewish arguments in public debate, using the scriptures to prove that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ekizini Yuda ñɩma tɔm nɛ abalɩtʋ ɛyáa tɩŋa ɛsɩndaa kaŋŋ nɛ ɛtɩŋnɩ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa tɔm yɔɔ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ se Yesu lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ.

eng: Paul reached Derbe, and then Lystra, where there was a disciple named Timothy. He was the son of a believing Jewish woman and a Greek father.
local: Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Silaasɩ paatɩŋnɩ Dɛɛrbɩ nɛ powolo Listrɩ. Patalaa lɛ, pamaɣnɩ Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyʋ nɔɔyʋ, payaɣ-ɩ se Timootee. E-ɖoo kɛ Yuda tʋ, ɛ́lɛ́ wɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ. Ɛ-caa ñɛkɛ Grɛɛkɩ tʋ.

eng: Paul and Silas journeyed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, then came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.
local: Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Silaasɩ pakʋyɩ Liidii tɛ lɛ, patɩŋnɩ Amfipolii nɛ Apolooni pɛ-tɛtʋ taa nɛ pɔkɔɔ patalɩ Tesalooniki ñɩndʋ taa. Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ naɖɩyɛ kɛwɛ peeɖe.

eng: The brothers and sisters in Lystra and Iconium spoke well of him.
local: Listrɩ taabalaa nɛ Ikoniyɔm ñɩma palɩzaɣ aseɣɖe kɩbanɖɛ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: As was Pauls custom, he entered the synagogue and for three Sabbaths interacted with them on the basis of the scriptures.
local: Patalaa nɛ pɩtalɩ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ wolo Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa ɛzɩ ɛtɩɩlakʋʋ yɔ. Peeɖe ɛ nɛ Yuda ñɩma palabɩ lɩmaɣza Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa tɔm yɔɔ kɩyakɩŋ kɩhɛzɩŋ naadozo.

eng: Paul wanted to take Timothy with him, so he circumcised him. This was because of the Jews who lived in those areas, for they all knew Timothys father was Greek.
local: Pɔɔlɩ liu kɛwɛɛ se ɛkpɛndɩnɩ-ɩ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛkpaɣ-ɩ nɛ ɛpɛlɩ-ɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Yuda ñɩma mbá paawɛ ageeta ana a-taa yɔ, pana patɩŋa se ɛ-caa kɛ Grɛɛkɩ tʋ.

eng: Through his interpretation of the scriptures, he demonstrated that the Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead. He declared, This Jesus whom I proclaim to you is the Christ.
local: Ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ nɛ ɛtɩŋnɩ tɩ-yɔɔ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ, pɩɩwɛ ḿbʋ́ se ɛtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛsɩ nɛ efe nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa. Eheyi-wɛ se Yesu weyi ɛkɛdɩɣ-wɛ ɛ-tɔm yɔ, ɛnʋ lɛ Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ.

eng: As Paul and his companions traveled through the cities, they instructed Gentile believers to keep the regulations put in place by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem.
local: Pɛɖɛɣaɣ nɛ patɩŋnɩ tɛtʋ ndɩ ndɩ ndʋ́ tɩ-taa yɔ, peheyaɣ tɩ-taa ñɩma mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ yɔ tɔm ndʋ́ e-tiyiyaa nɛ Yeruusalɛm Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa paaɖʋwa yɔ. Paakam pa-ñʋŋ taa se pɔɖɔ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: Some were convinced and joined Paul and Silas, including a larger number of Greek God-worshippers and quite a few prominent women.
local: Ɛyáa ḿbá, pa-taa lɛlaa tisi tɔm ńdʋ́ nɛ patɩŋ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Silaasɩ pa-wayɩ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ Grɛɛkɩ ñɩma sakɩyɛ mbá pañaŋaɣ Ɛsɔ yɔ nɛ abila naayɛ.

eng: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and every day their numbers flourished.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, Krɩstʋ aŋgba taa ñɩma tɛzaɣ kpaʋ ɖoŋ pe-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ taa nɛ pɛtɛzɩɣ ɖɔʋ kɩyakɩŋ tɩŋa.

eng: But the Jews became jealous and brought along some thugs who were hanging out in the marketplace. They formed a mob and started a riot in the city. They attacked Jasons house, intending to bring Paul and Silas before the people.
local: Peeɖe Yuda ñɩma labɩ ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ nɛ pekpeɣli añɔndɔmwaa nabɛyɛ nɛ pa nɛ wɛ peyele ɛyáa kpeɣli Tesalooniki tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa nɛ peseɣti-wɛ. Halɩ paaku ɖama nɛ powolo abalʋ weyi payaɣ se Yaasɔɔ yɔ ɛ-tɛ nɛ pañɩnɩɣ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Silaasɩ se pakpa-wɛ nɛ pahʋʋnɩ-wɛ tɔm samaɣ ɛsɩndaa.

eng: Paul and his companions traveled throughout the regions of Phrygia and Galatia because the Holy Spirit kept them from speaking the word in the province of Asia.
local: Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu taaha Pɔɔlɩ nɛ ɛ-taabalaa nʋmɔʋ se posusi Ɛsɔtɔm Aazii egeetiye taa. Pʋ-yɔɔ pakpaɣ nʋmɔʋ nɛ patɩŋnɩ Frijii egeetiye nɛ Galaasii ɛjaɖɛ pa-taa se pɛɖɛɣ.

eng: When they didnt find them, they dragged Jason and some believers before the city officials. They were shouting, These people who have been disturbing the peace throughout the empire have also come here.
local: Ɛyáa ḿbá powoba lɛ, patana-wɛ. Peeɖe pakpa Yaasɔɔ nɛ pɛkpɛndɩnɩ taabalaa nabɛyɛ nɛ pɔhɔm-wɛ nɛ powoni tɛtʋ ñʋndɩnaa cɔlɔ nɛ poholuu se: «Ɛyáa panɛ, pɛwɛɛkɩɣ kedeŋa kpeekpe, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, patalɩ ɖɛ-tɛ cɩnɛ.

eng: When they approached the province of Mysia, they tried to enter the province of Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus wouldnt let them.
local: Pɔñɔtɩnɩ Miisii egeetiye lɛ, pala ka ka se powoki Bitinii ñɩnɖɛ taa, ɛlɛ́, Yesu Fezuu taha-wɛ nʋmɔʋ.

eng: What is more, Jason has welcomed them into his home. Every one of them does what is contrary to Caesars decrees by naming someone else as king: Jesus.
local: nɛ Yaasɔɔ mʋ-wɛ nɛ pɛwɛɛ ɛ-tɛ! Pa-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛɛɖɔŋ mbʋ́ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ ɖʋwa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ, pɔyɔɔdʋʋ se nɔɔyʋ wɛɛ ɖɔɖɔ, ɛkɛ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ, ɛnʋ lɛ Yesu.».

eng: Passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas instead.
local: Peeɖe pɛtɛzɩ Miisii ñɩnɖɛ taa tɩŋnaʋ nɛ powolo Trowaasɩ. Tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa mɛlɛnaa sɩŋɩɣ.

eng: This provoked the crowd and the city officials even more.
local: Tɔm ńdʋ́, tɩɩcɛyɩ ñʋndɩnaa ḿbá nɛ ɛyáa samaɣ siŋŋ.

eng: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul took a route through the interior and came to Ephesus, where he found some disciples.
local: Apoloosi wɛ Kɔrɛntɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ ñatɩŋnɩ pʋŋ hɔɔlɩŋ taa nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛtalɩ Efɛɛzɩ. Peeɖe ɛkatɩ Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa nabɛyɛ.

eng: A vision of a man from Macedonia came to Paul during the night. He stood urging Paul, Come over to Macedonia and help us.
local: Đoo yuwa lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ ɖozi se Masedonii tʋ nɔɔyʋ kɔm ɛsɩŋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ etendi-i se: «Kʋyɩ ŋtɛzɩ ɖɛ-tɛ nɛ ŋsɩnɩ-ɖʋ.».

eng: After Jason and the others posted bail, they released them.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɛpɛlɩ Yaasɔɔ nɛ ɛ-taabalaa kɩmɩyɛ nɛ pɛhɛyɩ-ɖɩ nɛ pʋcɔ peyebi-wɛ.

eng: He asked them, Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you came to believe? They replied, Weve not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.
local: nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Ɛsɔ ha-mɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ yɔ yaa we?» Paba cosi-i se: «Aayɩ! Wiɖiyi ɖɩtanɩɩ se Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu nakʋyʋ wɛɛ.».

eng: Immediately after he saw the vision, we prepared to leave for the province of Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
local: Pɔɔlɩ tɛm ɖozuu ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ nɛ ɛ-taabalaa nɛ ma Luka ɖɩtɩkʋyɩ nɛ ɖɩpazɩ ñɩnʋʋ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɖɩɩla nɛ ɖiwolo Masedonii egeetiye taa yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩtɛm naʋ se Ɛsɔ yaɣnɩ-ɖʋ se ɖiwolo peeɖe nɛ ɖisusi Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: As soon as it was dark, the brothers and sisters sent Paul and Silas on to Beroea. When they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
local: Đoo tɩyuwa lɛ, taabalaa heyi Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Silaasɩ se pɛɖɛɛ Beree tɛtʋ taa. Patalaa lɛ, powolo Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa.

eng: Then he said, What baptism did you receive, then? They answered, Johns baptism.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɔɔlɩ tasɩ-wɛ pɔzʋʋ se: «Sɔnzɩ nzɩ́ palabɩ-mɩ yɔ?» Pocosi-i se: «Yohanɛɛsɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ palabɩ-ɖʋ.».

eng: We sailed from Troas straight for Samothrace and came to Neapolis the following day.
local: Peeɖe ɖɩsʋ mɛlɛ Trowaasɩ nɛ ɖɩɖɛɛ Samootrasɩ ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ lɩm cɔ-ɖɩ pɩta yɔ ɖɩ-hɔɔlʋʋ taa kpaagbaa. Tɛʋ fema lɛ, ɖiwolo Neyapoliisi.

eng: The Beroean Jews were more honorable than those in Thessalonica. This was evident in the great eagerness with which they accepted the word and examined the scriptures each day to see whether Paul and Silas teaching was true.
local: Peeɖe Yuda ñɩma lɩmaɣza kɛwɛ ɖeu nɛ pɩkɩlɩ Tesalooniki ñɩma, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paatisi Ɛsɔtɔm nɛ pa-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa pilim. Paa ɖooye pañɩnaɣ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se pana se tɔm ndʋ́ Pɔɔlɩ yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ, tɩkɛ toovenim na.

eng: Paul explained, John baptized with a baptism by which people showed they were changing their hearts and lives. It was a baptism that told people about the one who was coming after him. This is the one in whom they were to believe. This one is Jesus.
local: Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se: «Toovenim lɛ, Yohanɛɛsɩ ñalakaɣ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ se pɩwɩlɩ se pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ. Eeheyi-wɛ se ɛ-wayɩ lɛ, nɔɔyʋ kɔŋ, pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ. Ɛnʋ lɛ Yesu.».

eng: From there we went to Philippi, a city of Macedonias first district and a Roman colony. We stayed in that city several days.
local: Đɩkʋyɩ Neyapoliisi lɛ, ɖiwolo Room ñɩma tɛtʋ taa Masedonii egeetiye taa, payaɣ-tʋ se Filiipu. Ńdʋ́ kakɩlɩnɩ wazaɣ. Tɩ-taa peeɖe ɖɩcaɣ kɩyakɩŋ cabɩ.

eng: Many came to believe, including a number of reputable Greek women and many Greek men.
local: Pa-taa ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ, pɩkpɛndɩnɩ Grɛɛkɩ ñɩma: abila nɛ abalaa sakɩyɛ, nɛ petisi se tɩkɛ toovenim.

eng: After they listened to Paul, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Pɔɔlɩ yɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ panɩɩ lɛ, peyele nɛ palabɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu hɩɖɛ taa.

eng: On the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to the riverbank, where we thought there might be a place for prayer. We sat down and began to talk with the women who had gathered.
local: Pɩtalɩ kɩyakʋ kɩhɛzʋʋ lɛ, ɖɩlɩɩnɩ tɩ-taa nɛ ɖiwolo pɔɔ nɔɔ ɖenɖe ɖɩmaɣzaɣ se Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ naɖɩyɛ wɛɛ yɔ. Đɩtalaa lɛ, ɖɩmaɣnɩ halaa kpeɣlaa, ɛlɛ́ ɖɩcaɣ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɖiheyiɣ-wɛ Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: The Jews from Thessalonica learned that Paul also proclaimed Gods word in Beroea, so they went there too and were upsetting and disturbing the crowds.
local: Yuda ñɩma mbá pɛwɛ Tesalooniki yɔ, panɩwa se Pɔɔlɩ wɛɛ nɛ esusuu Ɛsɔtɔm Beree ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pakʋyɩ powolo peeɖe nɛ peseɣtiɣ ɛyáa nɛ peliɣtiɣ pa-lɩmaɣza.

eng: When Paul placed his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they began speaking in other languages and prophesying.
local: Peeɖe Pɔɔlɩ tɩnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nesi nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tii pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ papazɩ yɔɔdʋʋ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ nɛ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ.

eng: One of those women was Lydia, a Gentile God-worshipper from the city of Thyatira, a dealer in purple cloth. As she listened, the Lord enabled her to embrace Pauls message.
local: Halaa ḿbá, payaɣ pa-taa lɛlʋ se Liidii, ɛkɛ Tiyaatiiri tʋ nɛ ɛpɛdɩɣ pɩsaŋ kɩsɛmɩŋ. Ɛñaŋaɣ Ɛsɔ, Kɩpaɣlʋ kuli ɛ-lɩmaɣza yɔɔ se ɛnɩɩnɩ Pɔɔlɩ camɩyɛ.

eng: The brothers and sisters sent Paul away to the seacoast at once, but Silas and Timothy remained at Beroea.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ taabalaa heyi Pɔɔlɩ se ɛɖɛɛ teŋgu hɔɔlʋʋ taa kpaagbaa. Ɛlɛ́, Silaasɩ nɛ Timootee paba ñacaɣ peeɖe.

eng: Altogether, there were about twelve people.
local: Paatalɩ ɛzɩ ɛyáa hiu nɛ naalɛ yɔ.

eng: Once she and her household were baptized, she urged, Now that you have decided that I am a believer in the Lord, come and stay in my house. And she persuaded us.
local: Peeɖe ɖɩlabɩ-ɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ ɛ-hɔʋ taa ñɩma tɩŋa. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛ́yáa-ɖʋ nɛ eheyi-ɖʋ se: «Yee toovenim taa itisaa se mɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ, ɩkɔɔ ɩwɛɛ mɛn-tɛ.» Ɛlɛ́, ɛcɛyɩ-ɖʋ nɛ ɖitisi.

eng: Those who escorted Paul led him as far as Athens, then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to come to him as quickly as possible.
local: Ɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, nabɛyɛ sɩɩ-ɩ nɛ patanɩ-ɩ Atɛɛnɩ tɛtʋ taa. Papɩsaɣ lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se patalaa lɛ, peheyi Silaasɩ nɛ Timootee se pakʋyɩ kpaka nɛ patɩŋ ɛ-wayɩ.

eng: Paul went to the synagogue and spoke confidently for the next three months. He interacted with those present and offered convincing arguments concerning the nature of Gods kingdom.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ wolo Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa nɛ esusi Ɛsɔtɔm kaalɛyɩtʋ. Fenasɩ naadozo ɛɛlaba ḿbʋ́. Eheyaɣ-wɛ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ tɔm nɛ ɛñɩnɩɣ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛɛla nɛ petisi-tʋ yɔ.

eng: One day, when we were on the way to the place for prayer, we met a slave woman. She had a spirit that enabled her to predict the future. She made a lot of money for her owners through fortune-telling.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye ɖa nɛ Pɔɔlɩ ɖiwokaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa lɛ, pɛlɛ nɔɔyʋ katɩ-ɖʋ, ɛkɛ tʋmlaɖʋ. Ekem eleeu nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɛcɔʋ ɛtɩyɩɣ nɛ eheyiɣ ɛyáa mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ labʋ yɔ nɛ ɛkɔŋnɩ ɛ-caanaa liidiye sakɩyɛ.

eng: While Paul waited for them in Athens, he was deeply distressed to find that the city was flooded with idols.
local: Pɔɔlɩ kaawɛ Atɛɛnɩ nɛ ɛɖaŋ Silaasɩ nɛ Timootee lɛ, ɛna se agɔlɩma coka ɖeke suni tɩ-taa. Pʋ-yɔɔ pɩwɩzɩ-ɩ.

eng: Some people had closed their minds, though. They refused to believe and publicly slandered the Way. As a result, Paul left them, took the disciples with him, and continued his daily interactions in Tyrannus lecture hall.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pa-taa sakɩyɛ la laŋa kpɩzɩŋ nɛ pekizi-tʋ faŋŋ nɛ peniɣziɣ Kɩpaɣlʋ wayɩ tɩŋʋʋ tɔm ɛyáa tɩŋa ɛsɩndaa. Peeɖe Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa peyebi-wɛ nɛ pekpeɣliɣ ɖama abalʋ weyi payaɣ se Tiraanuusi yɔ e-sukuli taa nɛ esusuu ɛyáa Kɩpaɣlʋ tɔm kɩyakɩŋ tɩŋa.

eng: She began following Paul and us, shouting, These people are servants of the Most High God! They are proclaiming a way of salvation to you.
local: Pɛlɛ ɛnʋ, ɛna-ɖʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛtɩŋɩɣ-ɖʋ nɛ ɛmakɩ kubusi se: «Ɛyáa panɛ pɛkɛ Ɛsɔ Caabiyuu tʋmlaɖaa, posusuu-mɩ ñʋʋ yabʋ tɔm.».

eng: He began to interact with the Jews and Gentile God-worshippers in the synagogue. He also addressed whoever happened to be in the marketplace each day.
local: nɛ ɛtɛzɩ ɖɛʋ ewolo Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ taa nɛ ɛ nɛ Yuda ñɩma nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma nɛ pañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ, palakɩ lɩmaɣza Yesu tɔm yɔɔ. Paa ɖooye, ewokaɣ ɛyáa samaɣ ɖikpeɣliye nɛ ɛ nɛ mbá pɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ pɔyɔɔdʋʋ.

eng: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the disciples, encouraged them, said good-bye, and left for the province of Macedonia.
local: Ñaɖʋʋ cɛba Efɛɛzɩ peeɖe lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ kpeɣli Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩnɩ-wɛ se pɩlabɩ-wɛ hʋnʋm. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛsɛɛ-wɛ nɛ ɛcaŋ ɛɖɛɛ Masedonii ɛjaɖɛ taa.

eng: This went on for two years, so that everyone living in the province of Asia—both Jews and Greeks—heard the Lords word.
local: Ɛlabɩ ḿbʋ́ pɩnzɩ naalɛ. Pʋ-yɔɔ Yuda ñɩma nɛ Grɛɛkɩ ñɩma mbá paawɛ Aazii egeetiye taa yɔ, patɩŋa payɩ panɩ tɔm ńdʋ́.

eng: She did this for many days. This annoyed Paul so much that he finally turned and said to the spirit, In the name of Jesus Christ, I command you to leave her! It left her at that very moment.
local: Ɛmakaɣ kubusi ḿbʋ́ kɩyakɩŋ sakɩyɛ. Pʋkɔma pɩɖɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ lɛ, ɛpɩsɩnɩ pɛlɛ ɛnʋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ eholini eleeu weyi ɛwɛ ɛ-wayɩ yɔ se: «Yesu Krɩstʋ ɖoŋ taa lɛ, meheyiɣ-ŋ se ŋse pɛlɛ ɛnʋ ɛ-wayɩ!» Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, eleeu ɛnʋ ese.

eng: Certain Epicurean and Stoic philosophers engaged him in discussion too. Some said, What an amateur! Whats he trying to say? Others remarked, He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign gods.
local: Peeɖe lɔŋsɩnɖaa mbá pɛkɛ Epikuri nɛ Sitoyiisi pa-aŋgba taa ñɩma yɔ, powobi Pɔɔlɩ cɔlɔ nɛ pa nɛ ɩ pɔyɔɔdʋʋ nɛ pa-taa lɛlaa tɔŋ se: «Abalʋ kɔkɔɖɛ yaɖʋ ɛnɛ, se we ɛyɔɔdʋʋ yee?» Lɛlaa ñanɩ ɛyɔɔdaɣ Yesu sɩm nɛ e-fem pɔ-tɔm ḿbʋ́ lɛ, paba ñɔtɔŋ se: «Paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma, agɔlɩma kɩfana tɔm ɛyɔɔdʋʋ.».

eng: He traveled through that region with a message of encouragement. When he came to Greece.
local: Ɛtalaa lɛ, ɛɖɔŋnɩ sɩŋʋʋ sɩŋʋʋ nɛ ɛkɛdɩɣ Ɛsɔtɔm sakɩyɛ nɛ pɩlakɩ ɛyáa hʋnʋm. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ewolo Grɛɛsɩ ñɩnɖɛ taa.

eng: God was doing unusual miracles through Paul.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, Ɛsɔ tɩŋaɣnɩ Pɔɔlɩ yɔɔ nɛ ɛlakɩ hama lakasɩ sɔsɔsɩ.

eng: Her owners realized that their hope for making money was gone. They grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them before the officials in the city center.
local: Pɛlɛ ɛnʋ ɛ-caanaa nawa se ɛɛtasɩɣ-wɛ liidiye cazʋʋ lɛ, pakpa Pɔɔlɩ nɛ ɛ-taabalʋ Silaasɩ nɛ pɔhɔm-wɛ nɛ powoni ɛyáa samaɣ ɖikpeɣliye, tɛtʋ ñʋndɩnaa cɔlɔ.

eng: They took him into custody and brought him to the council on Mars Hill. What is this new teaching? Can we learn what you are talking about?.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ pakpaɣ-ɩ powoni ɛyáa samaɣ ɖikpeɣliye ɖenɖe payaɣ se Areyoopajɩ yɔ nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Tɔm kɩfatʋ ndʋ́ ŋwɩlɩɣ ɛyáa ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, tʋtʋ lɛ ndʋ́ kpem?.

eng: he stayed for three months. Because the Jews hatched a plot against Paul as he was about to sail for Syria, he decided instead to return through Macedonia.
local: nɛ ɛcaɣ peeɖe fenasɩ naadozo. Ɛkʋyaa lɛ, ewolo se ɛsʋʋ kpɩyʋʋ nɛ ɛɖɛɛ Siirii ɛjaɖɛ taa. Ɛlɛ́, ɛnɩɩ se Yuda ñɩma nabɛyɛ tam ɛ-yɔɔ nɔɔ se pakʋʋ-ɩ. Peeɖe ɛtɩlɛɣzɩ lɩmaɣza nɛ ekiti ɛtɩŋnɩ Masedonii.

eng: Even the small towels and aprons that had touched his skin were taken to the sick, and their diseases were cured and the evil spirits left them.
local: Halɩ pakpakɩɣ tokonaa nɛ kɛdɛɛkasɩ mbá potukuni-i yɔ nɛ potukuuni kʋdɔndɩnaa nɛ pɔ-yɔɔ cɛkɩ nɛ aleewaa seɣ pa-wayɩ.

eng: When her owners approached the legal authorities, they said, These people are causing an uproar in our city. They are Jews.
local: Patanɩ-wɛ Room ñʋndɩnaa cɔlɔ lɛ, peheyi-wɛ se: «Ɛyáa panɛ, pɛwɛɛ nɛ pɛwɛɛkɩɣ ɖɛ-tɛtʋ. Pɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma.

eng: Youve told us some strange things and we want to know what they mean. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɔm ndʋ́ ŋheyiɣ-ɖʋ yɔ, ɖɩtanɩdɩ-tʋ kaʋ, pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩcaɣ se ɖɩnɩɩ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ.».

eng: He was accompanied by Sopater, Pyrrhus son from Beroea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
local: Ɛ nɛ mbá pɔɖɔŋaɣ yɔ, ḿbá yɔ: Beree abalʋ weyi payaɣ se Piruusi yɔ ɛ-pɩyalʋ Sopatɛɛrɩ nɛ Tesalooniki tɛtʋ taa ñɩma: Aristaarkɩ nɛ Sekundusi nɛ Dɛɛrbɩ tʋ Gayuusi nɛ Timootee nɛ Aazii ñɩma: Ticiiki nɛ Trofim.

eng: There were some Jews who traveled around throwing out evil spirits. They tried to use the power of the name of the Lord Jesus against some people with evil spirits. They said, In the name of the Jesus whom Paul preaches, I command you.
local: Peeɖe Yuda ñɩma mbá pɔcɔwaɣ nɛ pɔɖɔɔnʋʋ aleewaa ɛyáa wayɩ yɔ, pakʋyaa ɖɔɖɔ se payaɣ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu hɩɖɛ nɛ pɔɖɔɔnɩnɩ aleewaa ɛyáa wayɩ nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Yesu weyi Pɔɔlɩ susuu ɛ-tɔm yɔ ɛ-hɩɖɛ taa lɛ, aleewaa mɩ! Điheyiɣ-mɩ se ise ɛyáa panɛ pa-wayɩ!».

eng: who promote customs that we Romans cant accept or practice.
local: nɛ pawɩlɩɣ-ɖʋ sɔnzɩ nzɩ́ pɩtaha ɖa Room ñɩma nʋmɔʋ se ɖitisi yaa ɖɩla yɔ.».

eng: They said this because all Athenians as well as the foreigners who live in Athens used to spend their time doing nothing but talking about or listening to the newest thing.
local: Toovenim lɛ, Atɛɛnɩ ñɩma tɩŋa payɩ nɛ agɔmaa mbá pɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ, pa-tʋmɩyɛ lɛ se tɛʋ efema lɛ, pɛwɛɛ tɔm taa tazʋʋ nɛ tɩ-susuu yɔɔ.

eng: They went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.
local: Ḿbá kɛɖɛnɩ ma nɛ ɩ nɔɔ nɛ powolo paɖaŋ-ɖʋ Trowaasɩ tɛtʋ taa.

eng: The seven sons of Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, were doing this.
local: Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa taa lɛlʋ weyi payaɣ se Seva yɔ, ɛ-pɩyalaa lʋbɛ lakaɣnɩ tʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́.

eng: The crowd joined in the attacks against Paul and Silas, so the authorities ordered that they be stripped of their clothes and beaten with a rod.
local: Peeɖe ɛyáa samaɣ kʋyɩ pɔ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ ñʋndɩnaa ha nɔɔ se pacɩyɩ pɔ-yɔɔ wondu nɛ piyele polu-wɛ agbazɛɛ.

eng: Paul stood up in the middle of the council on Mars Hill and said, People of Athens, I see that you are very religious in every way.
local: Ḿbʋ́ hanɩ Pɔɔlɩ waɖɛ nɛ ewolo ɛsɩŋ Areyoopajɩ hɛkʋ taa nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Atɛɛnɩ ñɩma mɩ! Manawa se ɩkɛ agɔlɩma tɛɛ luŋiyaa siŋŋ.

eng: We sailed from Philippi after the Festival of Unleavened Bread and met them five days later in Troas, where we stayed for a week.
local: Kazandʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ pɔtɔkɩ tindine nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa fɛyɩ sɔsɔɔna yɔ tɩɖɛwa lɛ, ɖɩkʋyɩ ɖiwolo Filiipu nɛ ɖɩsʋʋ mɛlɛ. Kɩyakɩŋ kagbaanzɩ wayɩ lɛ, ɖɩtalɩ pɔ-cɔlɔ Trowaasɩ nɛ ɖa nɛ wɛ ɖɩcaɣ peeɖe kɩyakɩŋ lʋbɛ.

eng: The evil spirit replied, I know Jesus and Im familiar with Paul, but who are you.
local: Patalɩ eliikenu nɔɔyʋ tɛ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ pɩsɩnɩ-wɛ se: «Yesu lɛ, mansɩm-ɩ nɛ mantɩlɩ mbʋ́ Pɔɔlɩ kɛnaa yɔ. Mɩ yɔ, mɩ lɛ mbá kpem?».

eng: When Paul and Silas had been severely beaten, the authorities threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to secure them with great care.
local: Pɛtɛm-wɛ luu siŋŋ lɛ, powoni-wɛ paɖʋ salaka nɛ peheyi weyi ɛɖaŋɩɣ kɔ-nɔnɔɔ yɔ se ɛɖaŋ-wɛ camɩyɛ kpam.

eng: As I was walking through town and carefully observing your objects of worship, I even found an altar with this inscription: To an unknown God. What you worship as unknown, I now proclaim to you.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɔncɔwaɣ mɩ-tɛtʋ taa lɛ, mana agɔlɩma wena a-tɛɛ iluŋaɣ yɔ a-coka. Halɩ mana ɖoli nɔɔyʋ cokiye naɖɩyɛ, pama ɖɩ-yɔɔ se: ‹Ɛgɔlɩmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩtasɩm-ɖɩ yɔ ɖi-cokiye yɔ.› Tɔɔ! Pʋwɛ ɖeu. Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ iluŋuu pɩ-tɛɛ nɛ ɩtasɩm-pʋ yɔ, pʋ-tɔm ma Pɔɔlɩ mɔnkɔm-mɩ heyuu: ḿbʋ́ lɛ.

eng: On the first day of the week, as we gathered together for a meal, Paul was holding a discussion with them. Since he was leaving the next day, he continued talking until midnight.
local: Kujuka wiye lɛ, ɖikpeɣli ɖama se ɖɩlakɩ tindine tayʋʋ sɔnzɩ. Kifeŋaɣ se Pɔɔlɩ ekpe. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛɛwɛɛ nɛ ɛyɔɔdʋʋnɩ mbá pɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩtalɩ ɖoó hɛkʋ.

eng: The person who had an evil spirit jumped on them and overpowered them all with such force that they ran out of that house naked and wounded.
local: Peeɖe ekpibi-wɛ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ patɩŋa tɔɔzɩm taañam nɛ pamʋ heŋ nɛ pese yem yem nɛ palɩɩ ndaakpela.

eng: When he received these instructions, he threw them into the innermost cell and secured their feet in stocks.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ-wɛ nɛ ɛsʋnɩ ɖooo pɩ-taa kuduyuu taa nɛ ɛka pa-naŋgbanzɩ ɖaŋ pɔŋ taa.

eng: God, who made the world and everything in it, is Lord of heaven and earth. He doesnt live in temples made with human hands.
local: Ɛsɔ. Ɛnʋ labɩnɩ kedeŋa kpeekpe nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pʋwɛ kɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ lɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ ɛsɔdaa nɛ tataa. Ɛɛcakɩ coka wena ɛyáa manɩ pe-nesi yɔ a-tɛɛ.

eng: There were many lamps in the upstairs room where we had gathered.
local: Ɛsɔdaa kuduyuu nakʋyʋ taa ɖiikpeɣlaa. Paasɔbɩ kanɖʋnaa sakɩyɛ nɛ pasɩɩ kɩ-taa.

eng: This became known to the Jews and Greeks living in Ephesus. Everyone was seized with fear and they held the name of the Lord Jesus in the highest regard.
local: Efɛɛzɩ ñɩma tɩŋa nɛ Yuda ñɩma nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ panɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ patɩŋa payɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, sɔɔndʋ kpa-wɛ nɛ peyele Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu hɩɖɛ sɛɛ.

eng: Around midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them.
local: Pɩkɛdɩ ɖoó hɛkʋ taa lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Silaasɩ pɛsɛɣ Ɛsɔ nɛ pasaŋ-ɩ nɛ hendu nɛ salaka tɩnaa lɛlaa welesiɣ.

eng: Nor is God served by human hands, as though he needed something, since he is the one who gives life, breath, and everything else.
local: Ɛfɛyɩ kɛtɛɖaɣ taa nɛ pʋpɔzʋʋ se ɛyáa ɛlabɩ-ɩ pʋyʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ hanɩ patɩŋa payɩ wezuu nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pʋwɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: A young man named Eutychus was sitting in the window. He was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. When he was sound asleep, he fell from the third floor and died.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, evebu weyi payaɣ se Eticii yɔ, ɛɛcaɣ mundoɖe yɔɔ. Pɔɔlɩ wɛɛ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ ka ka ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɖom kpaɣ-ɩ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, pɩlabɩ-ɩ liii nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ ɖɩ-yɔɔ ɖooo ɛsɔdaa kuduyuu naadozo ñɩŋgʋ taa nɛ etii ɛfalɩ tataa. Powobi pakʋsɩ-ɩ lɛ, e-fezuu tɛm ɖɛʋ.

eng: Many of those who had come to believe came, confessing their past practices.
local: Pa-taa mbá paapazɩ wɛnaʋ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ yɔ, pa-taa sakɩyɛ heyaɣ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm ɛyáa ɛsɩndaa keɣlee.

eng: All at once there was such a violent earthquake that it shook the prisons foundations. The doors flew open and everyones chains came loose.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, tɛtʋ tɩñamsɩ siŋŋ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ salaka kuduyuu nɛ kʋ-nɔnɔsɩ tɩŋa tɩtʋlɩ yem nɛ yoŋgbasɩ nzɩ́ paaɖɩ salaka tɩnaa tɩŋa yɔ sɩtɩcɛbʋ.

eng: From one person God created every human nation to live on the whole earth, having determined their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands.
local: Ɛtɩŋnɩ ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm weyi ɛlayɩ labʋ yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛla ɛyáa kpeekpe kedeŋa yɔɔ. Ɛɖʋ-wɛ alɩwaatʋ ndɩ ndɩ nɛ ɛtayɩ-wɛ ajɛya.

eng: Paul went down, fell on him and embraced him, then said, Dont be alarmed. Hes alive.
local: Peeɖe Pɔɔlɩ tɩlɩwa etii nɛ eluŋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛkpaɣ-ɩ e-nesi taa nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-mɩ: ɛ nɛ ɛ-ɛsɛ!».

eng: This included a number of people who practiced sorcery. They collected their sorcery texts and burned them publicly. The value of those materials was calculated at more than someone might make if they worked for one hundred sixty-five years.
local: nɛ fadɩŋ laɖaa taa sakɩyɛ ñɔkɔnɩ pa-fadɩŋ takayɩsɩ nɛ pañaɣzɩ-sɩ miŋ ɛyáa ɛsɩndaa. Patʋʋzɩ si-liidiye lɛ, ɖɩɖɔwa pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ: ɖɩɩtalɩ ɛzɩ pe-liidiye pɔɔyɩsɩ kutokiŋ nɩɩnʋwa yɔ.

eng: When the jailer awoke and saw the open doors of the prison, he thought the prisoners had escaped, so he drew his sword and was about to kill himself.
local: Peeɖe kɔ-yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyʋ fema nɛ ɛnaɣ kɔ-nɔnɔsɩ tɩŋa tʋlɩ yem haaa. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛlɩzɩ ɛ-sɩɣa se ɛtɛzɩɣ ɛ-tɩ kʋʋ nɛ ɛhɛzɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛmaɣzaɣ se paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma yɔ, salaka tɩnaa tɩŋa lɩwa pese.

eng: God made the nations so they would seek him, perhaps even reach out to him and find him. In fact, God isnt far away from any of us.
local: Ɛlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se ɛyáa ɛñɩnɩ-ɩ kpeɣu taa kpeɣu taa nɛ pʋtɔmaɣ yɔ, pana-ɩ. Ŋgʋ́, Ɛsɔ toposini ɖa-taa nɔɔyʋ.

eng: Then Paul went back upstairs and ate. He talked for a long time—right up until daybreak—then he left.
local: Peeɖe Pɔɔlɩ pɩsaa ɛkpa nɛ ɛcɩyɩ tindine nɛ ɛtɔɔ. Pɛtɛm sɔnzɩ ńzɩ́ labʋ lɛ, ɛɖaɣnɩ yɔɔdʋʋ ka ka nɛ tɛʋ kɔɔ kife. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛɖɛɛ.

eng: In this way the Lords word grew abundantly and strengthened powerfully.
local: Ḿbʋ́ pɩtɩŋnɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ ɖoŋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛ-tɔm tɛzɩ cɔʋ siŋŋ.

eng: But Paul shouted loudly, Dont harm yourself! Were all here.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɔɔlɩ ma kubuka leɣ leɣ se: «Đatɩŋa ɖɩwɛɛ yooo, taakʋ ña-tɩ!».

eng: In God we live, move, and exist. As some of your own poets said, We are his offspring.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-yɔɔ ɖɩwɛ wezuu nɛ ɖɩlakɩ pɩtɩŋa. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ mɩ-tɛ hendu teyaa nabɛyɛ kɔyɔɔdaa. Pɔyɔɔdaa se: ‹Đa ɖɔɖɔ ɖɩkɛ e-liɖe taa ñɩma.›.

eng: They took the young man away alive, and they were greatly comforted.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, evebu ɛnʋ efe nɛ pekpeni-i nɛ ɛ-alaafɩya tɩŋa. Ɛyáa na-ɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, patɩŋa pa-laŋa hɛɛ niɣ niɣ.

eng: Once these things had come to an end, Paul, guided by the Spirit, decided to return to Jerusalem, taking a route that would carry him through the provinces of Macedonia and Achaia. He said, After I have been there, I must visit Rome as well.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ la lɩmaɣza se ɛkʋyʋʋ ɛtɩŋnɩ Masedonii nɛ Akayɩ pa-ageeta taa nɛ ewolo Yeruusalɛm. Ɛtɔm se ɛtalaa yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ewolo ɖɔɖɔ Room nɛ ɛna.

eng: Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense.
local: «Man-caanaa nɛ man-ɖalaa mɩ! Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, mahʋʋ mɔn-tɔm, iwelesi nɛ ɩnɩɩ.».

eng: The jailer called for some lights, rushed in, and fell trembling before Paul and Silas.
local: Peeɖe ɖaŋɩyʋ ɛnʋ ɛtɔm se pɔkɔnɩ-ɩ miŋ. Pɔkɔnɩ-ɩ lɛ, ɛtɩsʋʋ ɖooo pɩ-taa ɖenɖe ɛɖɩɣ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Silaasɩ yɔ nɛ eluŋ pɛ-tɛɛ. Sɔɔndʋ kakpa-ɩ nɛ ɛ-nandʋ paɣ́.

eng: Therefore, as Gods offspring, we have no need to imagine that the divine being is like a gold, silver, or stone image made by human skill and thought.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ se ɖɩmaɣzɩ se papɩzɩɣ pakpaɣ-ɩ nɛ pɛkɛdɩnɩ sɩʋ kpelaɣ ŋgá ɛyáa maɣzaa nɛ polubini sika yaa liidiye yaa pɩyɛ nɛ pacanɩ-kɛ yɔ.

eng: We went on to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we intended to take Paul on board. Paul had arranged this, since he intended to make his way there by land.
local: Pɔɔlɩ ɖɛwa nɛ piyebi-ɖʋ lɛ, ɖɩsʋʋ mɛlɛ nɛ ɖɩɖɛɛ nɔɔ nɛ ɖiwolo ɖɩɖaŋ-ɩ Asoosi tɛtʋ taa. Ɛɛɖʋwa se ɛɖɔŋ naŋgbanzɩ nɛ ɖɩkatɩ-ɩ peeɖe.

eng: He sent two of his assistants, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, while he remained awhile in the province of Asia.
local: Peeɖe ɛkʋsɩ mbá pasɩŋaɣnɩ-ɩ tʋmɩyɛ yɔ pa-taa ɛyáa naalɛ nɛ etiyi-wɛ Masedonii nɛ piyele ɛtasɩ caɣʋ alɩwaatʋ cabɩ Aazii egeetiye taa. Ḿbá lɛ Timootee nɛ Eraastɩ.

eng: When they heard him address them in Aramaic, they became even more quiet.
local: Pɔɔlɩ tɩkuli ɛ-nɔɔ nɛ panɩɩ se pɛ-tɛ Ebree taa ɛyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-wɛ lɛ, pɛtɛzɩ sum ɖiɣ ɖiɣ. Peeɖe eheyi-wɛ se:.

eng: He led them outside and asked, Honorable masters, what must I do to be rescued.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ nɛ awayɩ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Caawaa! Ɛbɛ pʋwɛɛ se mala nɛ mehiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ?».

eng: After we tore ourselves away from them, we set sail on a straight course to Cos, reaching Rhodes the next day, and then Patara.
local: Đa nɛ mbá pasɩ Pɔɔlɩ yɔ ɖɩyasɩ ɖama lɛ, ɖɩsʋʋ mɛlɛ nɛ ɖɩɖɛɛ Kɔɔsɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa kpaagbaa. Kifema lɛ, ɖɩkʋyɩ peeɖe nɛ ɖiwolo Roodi ñɩnɖɛ taa nɛ ɖɩkʋyɩ ɖɩɖɩ taa nɛ ɖiwolo Pataraa tɛtʋ taa.

eng: God overlooks ignorance of these things in times past, but now directs everyone everywhere to change their hearts and lives.
local: Ɛsɔ ɛɛtasɩɣ kpaɣʋ mbʋ́ ɛyáa kalabɩ ɖooo lɔŋ nɛ patatɩlɩ yɔ nɛ ɛlabɩnɩ tʋmɩyɛ. Ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ eheyiɣ patɩŋa payɩ paa le se pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ.

eng: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.
local: Ɛkatɩ-ɖʋ lɛ, ɛkpa ɖɔ-cɔlɔ mɛlɛ taa nɛ ɖiwolo Mitiilɛɛnɩ ñɩndʋ taa.

eng: At that time a great disturbance erupted about the Way.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Pɔɔlɩ wɛ Efɛɛzɩ tɛtʋ taa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pɩɩkɔnɩ ñaɖʋʋ sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ wayɩ tɩŋʋʋ nʋmɔʋ yɔɔ.

eng: Paul continued, Im a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia but raised in this city. Under Gamaliels instruction, I was trained in the strict interpretation of our ancestral Law. I am passionately loyal to God, just like you who are gathered here today.
local: «Ma lɛ, mɛnkɛ Yuda tʋ, palʋlɩ-m Taarsɩ Siliisii ɛjaɖɛ taa. Ɛlɛ́, Yeruusalɛm cɩnɛ mancaɣ abalʋ weyi payaɣ se Gamaalɩyɛɛlɩ yɔ ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛkpɛlɩkɩ-m ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se mɔnɖɔ ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa paɣtʋ yɔɔ ɖeyi ɖeyi yɔ nɛ mɔnkɔɔ manpaɣlɩ. Mɛ-ɛsɩyɛ kɛsɛnɩ Ɛsɔ tʋmɩyɛ ɛzɩ mɩtɩŋa mɩ-ñɩna sɛʋnɩ-ɖɩ sɔnɔ yɔ.

eng: They replied, Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved—you and your entire household.
local: Paba cosi-i se: «Wɛnɩ lidaʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu yɔɔ nɛ ɛya ña-ñʋʋ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ ñɔ-hɔʋ taa ñɩma.».

eng: We found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, boarded, and put out to sea.
local: Đɩtalɩ peeɖe lɛ, ɖɩmaɣnɩ mɛlɛ ŋgʋ́ kuwoki Fenisii ɛjaɖɛ taa yɔ nɛ ɖɩsʋʋ-kʋ nɛ ɖɩɖɛɛ.

eng: This is because God has set a day when he intends to judge the world justly by a man he has appointed. God has given proof of this to everyone by raising him from the dead.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛtɛm ɖʋʋ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛkɔŋ hʋnaʋ kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ ɛyáa nɛ toovenim yɔ. Ɛyʋ weyi ɛlɩzaa yɔ, ɛ-yɔɔ ɛtɩŋɩɣna nɛ ɛhʋʋnɩ-wɛ tɔm. Mbʋ́ ɛwɩlɩnɩ paa weyi se ɛkɔŋ-pʋ labʋ yɔ lɛ se efezi-i nɛ sɩɖaa taa.».

eng: The next day we sailed from there and arrived opposite Chios. On the day after, we sailed to Samos, and on the following day we came to Miletus.
local: Đɩkʋyɩ peeɖe lɛ, ɖɩtalɩ Ciyoo tɛtʋ cɔlɔ tɛʋ kifemuu. Tozo wiye lɛ, ɖɩtalɩ Samɔɔsɩ ñɩndʋ taa. Naza wiye lɛ, ɖɩtalɩ Miilɛɛ ñɩndʋ taa lɛ.

eng: There was a silversmith named Demetrius. He made silver models of Artemis temple, and his business generated a lot of profit for the craftspeople.
local: Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe, payaɣ-ɩ se Demetriyuusi, ɛkɛ nɩŋgbacaɣlɩnaa luɖu. Elukaɣ ɛgɔlɩmɩyɛ halɩñɩnɖɛ nɖɩ́ payaɣ se Artemiisi yɔ ɖɩ-ɖɩɣa lɛzɩŋ cikpeŋ nɛ liidiye ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ nɛ ɛpɛdɩɣ. Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ ɛ nɛ ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa pehiɣ pa-tɩ siŋŋ.

eng: I harassed those who followed this Way to their death, arresting and delivering both men and women into prison.
local: Maawɛ mbá pɔɖɔŋ Yesu nʋmɔʋ taa yɔ pa-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ nɛ mañɩnɩɣ se mankʋ-wɛ. Maakpa halaa nɛ abalaa nɛ mɔhɔkɩ-wɛ nɛ manɖʋ salaka.

eng: They spoke the Lords word to him and everyone else in his house.
local: Peeɖe ɛkpaɣ-wɛ nɛ ɖoó ana nɛ ewoni ɛ-tɛ nɛ peheyi ɛ nɛ ɛ-hɔʋ taa ñɩma tɩŋa Kɩpaɣlʋ tɔm.

eng: We spotted Cyprus, but passed by it on our left. We sailed on to the province of Syria and landed in Tyre, where the ship was to unload its cargo.
local: Đɩkɔm ɖɩtalɩ Ciiprɩ ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ lɩm cɔ-ɖɩ pɩta yɔ ɖɩ-hadɛ kiŋ lɛ, ɖiyebi-ɖɩ nɩmɩyɛ yɔɔ nɛ ɖiwolo Tiiri tɛtʋ taa Siirii ɛjaɖɛ taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, peeɖe pʋwɛɛ se palɩzɩ mɛlɛ taa wondu.

eng: When they heard about the resurrection from the dead, some began to ridicule Paul. However, others said, Well hear from you about this again.
local: Mbá pɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ, panɩ Pɔɔlɩ yɔɔdaɣ sɩɖaa fem tɔm ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa-taa lɛlaa woŋ-i nɛ lɛlaa ñɔtɔŋ se: «Wiɖiyi lɛ, ŋŋtasɩ kɔm nɛ ŋheyi-ɖʋ-tʋ.».

eng: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so that he wouldnt need to spend too much time in the province of Asia. He was hurrying to reach Jerusalem, if possible, by Pentecost Day.
local: Pɔɔlɩ kaɖʋwa se ɛɛsɩŋɩɣ Efɛɛzɩ Aazii egeetiye taa, pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, elesiɣ alɩwaatʋ yem. Ekpedesaɣ se pʋwɛɛ yɔ, ɛtalɩ Yeruusalɛm Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tibu Kazandʋ wiye.

eng: He called a meeting with these craftspeople and others working in related trades and said, Friends, you know that we make an easy living from this business.
local: Nakʋyʋ wiye lɛ, ekpeɣli pa nɛ mbá palakɩ tʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-lɛzʋʋ yɔ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Taabalaa! Ɩtɛm sɩm se ɖa-tʋmɩyɛ yɔɔ ɖihikiɣ ɖa-tɩ.

eng: The high priest and the whole Jerusalem Council can testify about me. I received letters from them, addressed to our associates in Damascus, then went there to bring those who were arrested to Jerusalem so they could be punished.
local: Tɔm ndʋ́ mɔyɔɔdʋʋ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ nɛ tɔm hʋyaa sɔsaa papɩzɩɣ palɩzɩ ma-aseɣɖe. Halɩ maawobi mamʋ takayɩsɩ pa-maɣmaɣ pɔ-cɔlɔ se mowoni ɖɛ-tɛ ñɩma cɔlɔ Damaasɩ nɛ pɩsɛɣ mankpa patɩŋa nɛ mɔnkɔnɩ-wɛ Yeruusalɛm cɩnɛ nɛ pɔhɔ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ.».

eng: Right then, in the middle of the night, the jailer welcomed them and washed their wounds. He and everyone in his household were immediately baptized.
local: Pɛtɛm-wɛ heyuu lɛ, ɛñalɩ pa-kɩcɩkpɩŋ taa. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Silaasɩ pala ɛ nɛ ɛ-ñɩma ḿbá patɩŋa lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ.

eng: We found the disciples there and stayed with them for a week. Compelled by the Spirit, they kept telling Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
local: Đɩtalaa lɛ, ɖɩmaɣnɩ Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa nɛ ɖa nɛ wɛ ɖɩcaɣ kpɩtaʋ nɛ kɩ-tɛɛ. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔɔdɩnɩ-wɛ nɛ peheyi Pɔɔlɩ se ɛtaatasɩ pɩsʋʋ Yeruusalɛm.

eng: At that, Paul left the council.
local: Peeɖe ɛkʋyɩ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ ɛɖɛɛ.

eng: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus calling for the churchs elders to meet him.
local: Pɔɔlɩ talɩ Miilɛɛ lɛ, etiyi Efɛɛzɩ se payaa Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa.

eng: And you can see and hear that this Paul has convinced and misled a lot of people, not only in Ephesus but also throughout most of the province of Asia. He says that gods made by human hands arent really gods.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩnaɣ nɛ ɩnɩɣ nɛ mɩ-nɩŋgbaaŋ ndʋ́ mɩ-Pɔɔlɩ ɛnʋ ɛwɛɛ esusuu Efɛɛzɩ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ Aazii egeetiye hɔɔlɩŋ sakɩyɛ taa yɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se agɔlɩma wena ɛyáa manɩ pe-nesi yɔ, atɛkɛ pʋyʋ kpem. Tɔm ńdʋ́ ɛcɛtɩɣnɩ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ nɛ paba tisiɣ-tʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: During that journey, about noon, as I approached Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven encircled me.
local: Pɔɔlɩ tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Pʋ-yɔɔ mankpaɣ nʋmɔʋ nɛ mɔnkɔɔ mɔñɔtɩnɩ Damaasɩ. Pʋkɔma ɛzɩ wɩsɩ siɣu yɔ lɛ, ñalɩmɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ naɖɩyɛ tɩlɩʋnɩ ɛsɔdaa yem nɛ ɖitii mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: He brought them into his home and gave them a meal. He was overjoyed because he and everyone in his household had come to believe in God.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ewoni-wɛ e-kuduyuu tɛɛ nɛ ɛha-wɛ tɔɔnaɣ nɛ pɔtɔɔ nɛ ɛ nɛ ɛ-hɔʋ taa ñɩma tɩŋa payɩ pa-taa hʋlʋmɩ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ.

eng: When our time had come to an end, we departed. All of them, including women and children, accompanied us out of town where we knelt on the beach and prayed.
local: Ɛlɛ́, kpɩtaʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kʋkɔm kɩɖɛɛ lɛ, ɖɩɖaɣnɩ ɖa-nʋmɔʋ pazʋʋ. Đɩɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, pasɩɩ-ɖʋ patɩŋa, pa nɛ pa-halaa nɛ pe-piya nɛ palɩnɩ-ɖʋ tɛtʋ wayɩ. Peeɖe ɖiluŋ ɖa-nʋmbɔla yɔɔ teŋgu nɔɔ nɛ ɖɩsɛɛ Ɛsɔ.

eng: Some people joined him and came to believe, including Dionysius, a member of the council on Mars Hill, a woman named Damaris, and several others.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, nabɛyɛ kanɩnɩ-ɩ nɛ petisi ɛ-tɔm nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ. Pa-taa yɔ: Denisi weyi ɛkɛ Areyoopajɩ tɔm hʋyaa taa lɛlʋ yɔ nɛ halʋ weyi payaɣ se Damaari yɔ nɛ lɛlaa ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: When they arrived, he said to them, You know how I lived among you the whole time I was with you, beginning with the first day I arrived in the province of Asia.
local: Paba talɩ ɛ-cɔlɔ lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se: «Man-taabalaa! Ɩtɛm sɩm mɔn-ɖɔnɛ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩɩwɛɛ kpaɣ ɖooo kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ mancalɩ fɛyʋʋ mɩ-tɛ Aazii egeetiye taa yɔ.

eng: This poses a danger not only by discrediting our trade but also by completely dishonoring the great goddess Artemis. The whole province of Asia—indeed, the entire civilized world—worships her, but her splendor will soon be extinguished.
local: Manaʋ yɔ, yee ɖɩtakʋyɩ ɖɩsɩŋ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɖa-tʋmɩyɛ ɖɩnɛ ɖɩsɩɣ tɩnaɣ! Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɖɛ-ɛgɔlɩmɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ Artemiisi nɖɩ́ Aazii ñɩma tɩŋa nɛ ajɛya kpeekpe ɛyáa poluŋuu ɖɩ-tɛɛ yɔ, peniɣziɣ ɖɩ-ɖɩɣa nɛ nɔɔyʋ ɛɛtasɩɣ-ɖɩ kpaɣʋ nɛ ɛ́yáa pʋyʋ!».

eng: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking me, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Peeɖe mɔntɔlɩ tataa kpʋʋʋ nɛ manɩɣ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-m se: ‹Sɔɔlɩ! Sɔɔlɩ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋwɛɛ ma-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ ḿbʋ́?›.

eng: The next morning the legal authorities sent the police to the jailer with the order Release those people.
local: Tɛʋ fema lɛ, Room ñʋndɩnaa tiyi karɩsɩ nasɩyɩ se siwolo siheyi salaka yɔɔ ɖaŋɩyʋ ɛnʋ se ɛlɩzɩ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Silaasɩ salaka taa.

eng: We said good-bye to each other, then we boarded the ship and they returned to their homes.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɖa nɛ wɛ ɖɩsɛɛ ɖama nɛ ɖa ɖasʋʋ mɛlɛ nɛ piyele ḿbá ñapɩsɩ pɛ-tɛ.

eng: I served the Lord with great humility and with tears in the midst of trials that came upon me because of the Jews schemes.
local: Mentisi man-tɩ nɛ mala Kɩpaɣlʋ tʋmɩyɛ nɛ ɛsɩtɛɣlɩm nɛ kʋñɔŋ ŋgʋ́ mɔntɔɔ Yuda ñɩma nesi taa yɔ kɩ-taa. Ḿbá kɔlɔnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ nɔɔ kɩdɛkɛdaɣ tam sakɩyɛ.

eng: So the jailer reported this to Paul, informing him, The authorities sent word that you both are to be released. You can leave now. Go in peace.
local: Peeɖe ɛkʋyɩ hɛɛɛ nɛ ewolo eheyi Pɔɔlɩ se: «Ñʋndɩnaa tiyaa nɛ peheyi-m se malɩzɩ-mɩ salaka taa. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩlɩɩ ikpemni laŋhɛzɩyɛ.».

eng: Continuing our voyage, we sailed from Tyre and arrived in Ptolemais. We greeted the brothers and sisters there and spent a day with them.
local: Đɩkʋyɩ Tiiri peeɖe lɛ, ɖɩtɛzɩ ɖa-nʋmɔʋ teŋgu yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩkɔɔ ɖɩtalɩ Pitolemayisi tɛtʋ taa. Đɩtalaa lɛ, ɖɩsɛɛ taabalaa nɛ ɖa nɛ wɛ ɖɩcaɣ kɩyakʋ nɛ kɩ-tɛɛ.

eng: I answered, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are harassing, he replied.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mɔnpɔzɩ se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ña anɩ?› Pucosi-m se: ‹Ma Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu weyi ŋwɛ ɛ-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ yɔ.›.

eng: Once they heard this, they were beside themselves with anger and began to shout, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians.
local: Ɛyáa mbá paakpeɣlaa yɔ, panɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pa-pana kpa siŋŋ nɛ pamakɩ kubusi se: «Efɛɛzɩ ñɩma ɛgɔlɩmɩyɛ Artemiisi kɩlɩnɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ!».

eng: You know I held back nothing that would be helpful so that I could proclaim to you and teach you both publicly and privately in your homes.
local: Mɛntɛmɛsɩ-mɩ natʋyʋ. Maakɛdɩ-mɩ nɛ mawɩlɩ-mɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋwɛ-mɩ wazaɣ yɔ samaɣ nɛ mɩ-ɖɩsɩ pa-taa.

eng: Paul told the police, Even though we are Roman citizens, they beat us publicly without first finding us guilty of a crime, and they threw us into prison. And now they want to send us away secretly? No way! They themselves will have to come and escort us out.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɩtamaɣ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ eheyi karɩsɩ ńzɩ́ se: «Mɩ-ñʋndɩnaa yeba nɛ pamabɩ-ɖʋ ɛyáa tɩŋa hɛkʋ taa nɛ patahʋʋ ɖɔ-tɔm natʋyʋ kaʋ nɛ powoni-ɖʋ paɖʋ salaka. Ŋgʋ́ ɖa ɖɔɖɔ ɖɩkɛ Room ñɩma . Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ petiyiɣ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ se peyebi-ɖʋ yaa we? Pɩɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́ kpem! Iwolo iheyi-wɛ se pɔkɔɔ pa-maɣmaɣ nɛ palɩzɩ-ɖʋ.».

eng: The next day we left and came to Caesarea. We went to the house of Philip the evangelist, one of the Seven, and stayed with him.
local: Kifema lɛ, ɖɩkpaɣ nʋmɔʋ nɛ ɖɩkɔɔ ɖɩtalɩ Sezaaree. Peeɖe ɖiwoba ɖɩwɛɛ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm susiyu Filiipu tɛ. Abalʋ ɛnʋ, ɛɛkɛ ɛyáa lʋbɛ mbá palɩzɩ Yeruusalɛm se pɛkɛ Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa tayɩyaa yɔ pa-taa lɛlʋ.

eng: My traveling companions saw the light, but they didnt hear the voice of the one who spoke to me.
local: Mbá ma nɛ wɛ ɖɩɩwɛ peeɖe yɔ, pana ñalɩmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɔɖɔ. Ɛlɛ́, patanɩɩ weyi ɛyɔɔdaɣnɩ-m yɔ ɛ-nɔɔ.

eng: The city was thrown into turmoil. They rushed as one into the theater. They seized Gaius and Aristarchus, Pauls traveling companions from the province of Macedonia.
local: Peeɖe pɩñɩŋnɩ Efɛɛzɩ tɛtʋ tɩŋa. Ɛlɛ́, tɩ-taa ñɩma kpa Masedonii ñɩma naalɛ nabɛyɛ: Gayuusi nɛ Aristaarkɩ mbá pa nɛ Pɔɔlɩ pɔɖɔŋaɣ yɔ, nɛ pɔhɔm-wɛ ɖinɖa ɖinɖa nɛ powoni Efɛɛzɩ ñɩma ḿbá pa-ɖikpeɣliye ɖenɖe palakɩ aleɣya yɔ.

eng: You know I have testified to both Jews and Greeks that they must change their hearts and lives as they turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
local: Meheyi Yuda ñɩma nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ se pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu yɔɔ.

eng: The police reported this to the legal authorities, who were alarmed to learn that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens.
local: Paba pɩsaa peheyi-wɛ se Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Silaasɩ pɛkɛ Room ñɩma ɖɔɖɔ lɛ, sɔɔndʋ kpa-wɛ.

eng: He had four unmarried daughters who were involved in the work of prophecy.
local: Ɛwɛnɩ pɛlaa naanza, patawalɩda, pɛkɛ náyaa.

eng: I asked, What should I do, Lord? Get up, the Lord replied, and go into Damascus. There you will be told everything you have been appointed to do.
local: Peeɖe mɔnpɔzaa se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ɛbɛ pʋwɛɛ se mala?› Ɛlɛ́, ecosi-m se: ‹Kʋyɩ ŋwolo Damaasɩ, peeɖe peheyiɣ-ŋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa se ŋla yɔ.›.

eng: Paul wanted to appear before the assembly, but the disciples wouldnt allow him.
local: Pɔɔlɩ caɣaɣ se ɛlɩɩ ɛyáa samaɣ ɛsɩndaa. Ɛlɛ́, Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa lɛlaa kizini-i.

eng: Now, compelled by the Spirit, Im going to Jerusalem. I dont know what will happen to me there.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, pɩkɛnɩ-m kɩcɛyʋʋ se mowolo Yeruusalɛm ɛzɩ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu heyuu-m yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mantana mbʋ́ pɩkaɣ-m tɛmnaʋ peeɖe yɔ.

eng: They came and consoled Paul and Silas, escorting them out of prison and begging them to leave the city.
local: Peeɖe pakʋyɩ pa-maɣmaɣ nɛ powolo pɛhɛzɩ pa-laŋa nɛ palɩzɩ-wɛ salaka taa nɛ petendi-wɛ se pakʋyɩ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa.

eng: After staying there for several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
local: Đɩɩwɛ peeɖe kɩyakɩŋ cabɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, nakʋyʋ wiye náyʋ weyi payaɣ se Agaabusi yɔ, ɛlɩɩnɩ Yudee egeetiye taa nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɖɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: I couldnt see because of the brightness of that light, so my companions led me by the hand into Damascus.
local: Ñalɩmɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ ńɖɩ́, ɖɩɩyʋlɩsɩ mɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ maatasɩɣ naʋ. Ɛlɛ́, man-taabalaa ɖɔkɩ me-nesi taa nɛ pɔhɔm-m nɛ powoni Damaasɩ.

eng: Even some officials of the province of Asia, who were Pauls friends, sent word to him, urging him not to risk going into the theater.
local: Halɩ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ Aazii karɩsɩ sɔsɔsɩ nasɩyɩ nzɩ́ sɩkɛ ɛ-ɛgbaɣdɩnaa yɔ nɛ sɩkʋsɩ ɛyáa se powolo peheyi-i se pɩtaacɩbɩ-ɩ se ewoki ɛyáa samaɣ ɖikpeɣliye taa peeɖe.

eng: What I do know is that the Holy Spirit testifies to me from city to city that prisons and troubles await me.
local: Ndʋ́ manawa yɔ lɛ se: paa tɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-taa mantalaa yɔ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu wɩlɩɣ-m nɛ kiheyiɣ-m se salaka nɛ kʋñɔŋ sɔsɔʋ pɩtɛŋnɩ-m.

eng: Paul and Silas left the prison and made their way to Lydias house where they encouraged the brothers and sisters. Then they left Philippi.
local: Pɔɔlɩ nɛ Silaasɩ palɩ salaka taa peeɖe lɛ, powolo Liidii tɛ. Peeɖe pana taabalaa nɛ peyele pɩla paba hʋnʋm. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pɛɖɛɛ.

eng: He came to us, took Pauls belt, tied his own feet and hands, and said, This is what the Holy Spirit says: In Jerusalem the Jews will bind the man who owns this belt, and they will hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: Ɛtalaa lɛ, ɛkpaɣ Pɔɔlɩ tambaɖɛ nɛ ɛhɔkɩ ɛ-naŋgbanzɩ nɛ e-nesi pa-taa nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Iwelesi nɛ ɩnɩɩ ndʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tiyi-m se mɔnkɔɔ meheyi-mɩ yɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: weyi ɛtɩnɩ tambaɖɛ ɖɩnɛ yɔ, ɛzʋnɔ Yuda ñɩma kɔŋ-ɩ hɔkʋʋ Yeruusalɛm nɛ paɖʋzɩ-ɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pe-nesi taa.».

eng: There was a certain man named Ananias. According to the standards of the Law, he was a pious man who enjoyed the respect of all the Jews living there.
local: Abalʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛwɛ peeɖe, payaɣ-ɩ se Anaanɩyaasɩ. Ɛɖɔŋ Ɛsɔ paɣtʋ yɔɔ camɩyɛ nɛ Yuda ñɩma tɩŋa lɩzɩɣ ɛ-yɔɔ aseɣɖe kɩbanɖɛ.

eng: Meanwhile, the assembly was in a state of confusion. Some shouted one thing, others shouted something else, and most of the crowd didnt know why they had gathered.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ kpeɣlaa lɛ, ɛyáa wɛɛ pokou yem: paa weyi nɛ ɛ-ñɩndʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, piliɣtaɣ-wɛ. Pa-taa sakɩyɛ tatasɩ ɖiɣzinaʋ mbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ paakpeɣlaa yɔ.

eng: But nothing, not even my life, is more important than my completing my mission. This is nothing other than the ministry I received from the Lord Jesus: to testify about the good news of Gods grace.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mañɩtaa mɛntɛ man-tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu ɖʋ-m yɔ yɔ, me-wezuu tɛkɛnɩ-m pʋyʋ. Ńɖɩ́ lɛ se: monsusi Ɛsɔ lootiye Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: When we heard this, we and the local believers urged Paul not to go up to Jerusalem.
local: Đa nɛ Sezaaree ñɩma ɖɩnɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɖitendi Pɔɔlɩ se ɛtaatasɩ pɩsʋʋ Yeruusalɛm.

eng: The Jews sent Alexander to the front, and some of the crowd directed their words toward him. He gestured that he wanted to offer a defense before the assembly.
local: Peeɖe Yuda ñɩma nabɛyɛ lɩ ɛyáa samaɣ taa nɛ powolo pɛwɛɛtɩ weyi payaɣ se Alɛksandrɩ yɔ tɔm nɛ pɔhɔm-ɩ palɩzɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa ɛsɩndaa se ɛyɔɔdɩnɩ pɔ-nɔɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛ́lɛ́ kpazɩ nesi se paha-ɩ nʋmɔʋ nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ.

eng: He came and stood beside me. Brother Saul, receive your sight! he said. Instantly, I regained my sight and I could see him.
local: Ɛnʋ kɔmnɩ mɔn-cɔlɔ nɛ eheyi-m se: ‹Man-taabalʋ Sɔɔlɩ! Ñɛ-ɛsɛ ekuli nɛ ŋna!› Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, mɛ-ɛsɛ kuli nɛ manaɣ-ɩ camɩyɛ.

eng: I know that none of you will see me again—you among whom I traveled and proclaimed the kingdom.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, manawa se mbá mɩ mɩtɩŋa payɩ mɔnkɔm mɩ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ monsusi Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ tɔm yɔ, mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛɛtasɩɣ naʋ mɛ-ɛsɩbiye taa kaʋ.

eng: Paul replied, Why are you doing this? Why are you weeping and breaking my heart? Im ready not only to be arrested but even to die in Jerusalem for the sake of the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ kitini-ɖʋ se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ iwiliɣ nɛ ɩñɩnɩɣ se itisi mon-toma taa ḿbʋ́? Ma lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ hɔkʋʋ yɔɔ ɖeke mɔñɔɔzɩ man-tɩ nɛ manɖaŋ. Ɛlɛ́, manɖaŋaa ɖɔɖɔ se paa sɩm yɔ, mansɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu yɔɔ Yeruusalɛm.».

eng: but when they realized he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians! This continued for about two hours.
local: Ɛlɛ́, peɖiɣzini se Alɛksandrɩ ɛnʋ, ɛkɛ Yuda tʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛyáa samaɣ pazɩ kou katɩŋa payɩ se: «Efɛɛzɩ ñɩma ɛgɔlɩmɩyɛ Artemiisi kɩlɩnɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ!» Pɛwɛɛ kou yɔɔ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩtalɩ ɛzɩ ñɩɣtʋ naalɛ yɔ.

eng: He said, The God of our ancestors has selected you to know his will, to see the righteous one, and to hear his voice.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛtasɩ-m heyuu se ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa Ɛsɔ lɩzɩ-m ɖooo lɔŋ se mantɩlɩ mbʋ́ e-liu wɛɛ yɔ nɛ mana Weyi Ɛtʋʋzaa yɔ nɛ manɩɩ ɛ́lɛ́ nɔɔ taa tɔm.

eng: Therefore, today I testify to you that Im not responsible for anyones fate.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ sɔnɔ kɩyakʋ kʋnɛ se: yee mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ eleba yɔ, ma-ñɩndʋ natʋyʋ fɛyɩ pɩ-taa.

eng: Since we couldnt talk him out of it, the only thing we could say was, The Lords will be done.
local: Đɩtapɩzɩ ɛ-tɔm lɛ, ɖɩtɛzɩ-ɩ yebu nɛ ɖɩtɔ se: «Mbʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ liu wɛɛ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɛlabɩna!».

eng: The city manager brought order to the crowd and said, People of Ephesus, doesnt everyone know that the city of Ephesus is guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and of her image, which fell from heaven?.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, takayaɣ taa mayʋ susi-wɛ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-wɛ se: «Efɛɛzɩ ñɩma mɩ! Anɩ pɩsɛɣ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ se eteɖiɣzini se ɛgɔlɩmɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ Artemiisi nɛ ɖɩ-sɩʋ kpelaɣ ŋgá kalɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ ketii yɔ, ɖɛ-tɛ Efɛɛzɩ ñɩma cɔŋnɩnɩ pa-ɖɩɣa yɔɔ?.

eng: You will be his witness to everyone concerning what you have seen and heard.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ma pʋwɛɛ se mɛnkɛnɩ ɛ́lɛ́ aseɣɖe tʋ nɛ monsusi ɛyáa tɩŋa mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa manawa nɛ manɩɩ yɔ pʋ-tɔm.

eng: I havent avoided proclaiming the entire plan of God to you.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, meheyi-mɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa se ɛkɔŋ labʋ yɔ pɩtɩŋa payɩ, mɛntɛmɛsɩ-mɩ natʋyʋ.

eng: After this, we got ready and made our way up to Jerusalem.
local: Đɩcaɣ Sezaaree peeɖe kɩyakɩŋ cabɩ lɛ, ɖɩñɔɔzɩ ɖa-tɩ se ɖiwoki Yeruusalɛm.

eng: Therefore, since these facts are undeniable, you must calm down. Dont be reckless.
local: Tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩfɛyɩ kpɛzʋʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se iyele nɛ mɩ-yɔɔ hɛɛ, ɩtaakpedesi nabʋyʋ taa kaʋ.

eng: What are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized, and wash away your sins as you call on his name.
local: Anaanɩyaasɩ tɛm-m heyuu ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ-m se lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɛbɛ manɖaŋaa ɖɔɖɔ yɔ? Pʋwɛɛ se mowolo palabɩ-m lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ mentendi Kɩpaɣlʋ nɛ ɛñalɩ-m man-kɩwɛɛkɩm.».

eng: Watch yourselves and the whole flock, in which the Holy Spirit has placed you as supervisors, to shepherd Gods church, which he obtained with the death of his own Son.
local: Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu lɩzɩnɩ-mɩ se ɩkɛ sɔsaa nɛ ɩcɔnɩ Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma yɔɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɩcɔnɩ mɩ-maɣmaɣ mɩ-tɩ yɔɔ camɩyɛ nɛ ɩcɔnɩ ɖɔɖɔ mbá pɛwɛ ɖɩ-taa yɔ patɩŋa pɔ-yɔɔ ɛzɩ ninɖiɣyu cɔŋʋʋnɩ e-heŋ yɔɔ yɔ. Ɛsɔ tɩŋnɩnɩ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ calɩm yɔɔ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ-wɛ ɛ-ñɩma.

eng: Some of the disciples from Caesarea accompanied us and led us to Mnasons home, where we were guests. He was from Cyprus and had been a disciple a long time.
local: Đɩɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa nabɛyɛ sɩɩ-ɖʋ nɛ powoni-ɖʋ Yeruusalɛm peeɖe Ciiprɩ tʋ weyi ɛ-tɛ paaɖʋwa se ɖɩsʋʋ yɔ ɛ-tɛ. Payaɣ-ɩ se Mɩnaasɔɔ, ɛkɛ Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyʋ ɖooo lɔŋ.

eng: The men you brought here have neither robbed the temple nor slandered our goddess.
local: Ɩkɔnɩ ɛyáa panɛ cɩnɛ, ŋgʋ́, patakpaɣ pʋyʋ ɖɛ-ɛgɔlɩmɩyɛ ɖɩɣa taa, pɔtɔyɔɔdɩ ɖɩ-yɔɔ tɔm ɛzaɣ́tʋ natʋyʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: When I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I had a visionary experience.
local: Pɔɔlɩ tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ se: «Mɔnkɔm manpɩsɩ Yeruusalɛm lɛ, mowolo Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ mɛnsɛɣ-ɩ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, pɩlabɩ-m ɛzɩ ɖoziye yɔ.

eng: I know that, after my departure, savage wolves will come in among you and wont spare the flock.
local: Ma Pɔɔlɩ mɛntɛm naʋ se ma-wayɩ lɛ, mbá pɛwɛ ɛzɩ kpemtɔɔyɩsɩ nzɩ́ ñɔɔsɩ wɩɣ-sɩ yɔ, pɔkɔŋ lɩʋ mɩ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa, paakaɣ sɔm nɔɔyʋ kaʋ.

eng: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers and sisters welcomed us warmly.
local: Đɩtalɩ Yeruusalɛm lɛ, taabalaa mʋ-ɖʋ nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ.

eng: I saw the Lord speaking to me. Hurry! he said. Leave Jerusalem at once because they wont accept your testimony about me.
local: nɛ mana Kɩpaɣlʋ nɛ eheyiɣ-m se: ‹Kʋyɩ kpaka nɛ ŋlɩɩ Yeruusalɛm tɛtʋ wayɩ lɔŋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Yuda ñɩma ɛɛkaɣ tisuu mɔn-tɔm ndʋ́ ŋheyiɣ-wɛ yɔ.›.

eng: On the next day Paul and the rest of us went to see James. All of the elders were present.
local: Kifema lɛ, ɖa nɛ Pɔɔlɩ ɖiwolo Yakubu tɛ. Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa lɛlaa tɩŋa kekpeɣli peeɖe.

eng: Some of your own people will distort the word in order to lure followers after them.
local: Halɩ mɩ-maɣmaɣ mɩ-taa lɛlaa kɔŋ pɩsʋʋ cɛtɩyaa nɛ pepeɣzi Krɩstʋ wayɩ tɩŋɩyaa nɛ paba tɩŋ-wɛ.

eng: I responded, Lord, these people know I used to go from one synagogue to the next, beating those who believe in you and throwing them into prison.
local: Ɛlɛ́, moncosi se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Naa pa-maɣmaɣ pɛtɛm sɩm se mɔncɔwaɣnɩ Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ ndɩ ndɩ taa nɛ mankpaɣ mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ñɔ-yɔɔ yɔ nɛ mamakɩ-wɛ nɛ maɖʋʋ-wɛ salaka!.

eng: After greeting them, he gave them a detailed report of what God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
local: Đɩtalaa lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ sɛɛ-wɛ nɛ ɛkɛdɩ-wɛ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ tɩŋnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛla mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa yɔ pɩtɩŋa pʋ-tɔm piye piye.

eng: Stay alert! Remember that for three years I constantly and tearfully warned each one of you. I never stopped warning you!.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩcɔnɩ mɩ-tɩ yɔɔ nɛ ɩtɔzʋʋ se pɩnzɩ naadozo yɔ, menteyele mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ paɣ́ʋ wɩsɩ nɛ ɖoó nɛ halɩ mentisi ɛsɩtɛɣlɩm.

eng: When Stephen your witness was being killed, I stood there giving my approval, even watching the clothes that belonged to those who were killing him.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pakʋ ña-aseɣɖe tʋ Etiyɛɛnɩ yɔ, ma ɖɔɖɔ maawɛ peeɖe nɛ mantɩnɩ pa-taa mbá pakʋwaɣ-ɩ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ. Maaɖaŋnɩ-wɛ po-wondu.›.

eng: Those who heard this praised God. Then they said to him, Brother, you see how many thousands of Jews have become believers, and all of them keep the Law passionately.
local: Panɩ-tʋ lɛ, pasa Ɛsɔ pɩŋŋ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, peheyi-i se: «Taabalʋ! Ŋnaɣ, Yuda ñɩma ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ yɔ, patɩŋa payɩ pɔɖɔkɩ paɣtʋ nesi naalɛ kpam.

eng: Therefore, if Demetrius and the craftspeople with him have a charge against anyone, the courts are in session and governors are available. They can press charges against each other there.
local: Yee nɩŋgbacaɣlɩnaa luɖu Demetriyuusi nɛ ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa pa-taa ɛwɛnɩ nɔɔyʋ yɔ, kɩyakɩŋ weyi paɖʋwa se pahʋʋ tɔm yɔ, ɩwɛɛ. Ñʋndɩnaa ñɛwɛɣ i-wiye ɖɔɖɔ. Ɩ-taa nakʋyʋ wiye papɩzɩɣ powolo payaa pʋdʋ!.

eng: Then the Lord said to me, Go! I will send you far away to the Gentiles.
local: Peeɖe eheyi-m se: ‹Kʋyɩ! Mentiyiɣ-ŋ poliŋ, mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ.› ».

eng: Now I entrust you to God and the message of his grace, which is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all whom God has made holy.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, manɖʋzʋʋ mɩtɩŋa payɩ Ɛsɔ nɛ e-lootiye tɔm pe-nesi taa. Ɛlabɩ-mɩ lootiye. Ɛnʋ pɩzɩɣnɩ eyele nɛ ɩkpa ɖoŋ nɛ ɛha-mɩ ñɩm mbʋ́ ɛsɩwa se ɛhaɣ mbá patɩŋa payɩ eyeba nɛ papɩsɩ ɛ-ñɩma yɔ.

eng: They have been informed that you teach all the Jews who live among the Gentiles to reject Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children nor to live according to our customs.
local: Ŋgʋ́, peheyi-wɛ se ŋwɩlɩɣ Yuda ñɩma mbá pɛwɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɛ-tɛ se paha Mooyiizi paɣtʋ siɣ́ye nɛ ŋheyiɣ-wɛ se pataapɛlɩ pe-piya nɛ pataaɖɔ pɔ-sɔnzɩ yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Additional disputes can be resolved in a legal assembly.
local: Yee ɩwɛnɩ natʋyʋ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ, ɩpɩzɩɣ iwoni-tʋ tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ nɛ pɔñɔɔzɩ-mɩ-tʋ ɛzɩ paɖʋʋ ɖa-paɣtʋ taa yɔ.

eng: The crowd listened to Paul until he said this. Then they shouted, Away with this man! Hes not fit to live.
local: Ɛyáa samaɣ welesaɣnɩ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛyɔɔdɩ tɔm pee ana yɔ. Peeɖe papazɩ kubusi mabʋ se: «Abalʋ ɛnɛ, pakʋsɩ-ɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ! Pɩtamʋnɩ se ɛtasɩ caɣʋ wezuu!».

eng: I havent craved anyones silver, gold, or clothing.
local: Nɔɔyʋ liidiye yaa e-sika yaa e-wondu talabɩ-m liu.

eng: What about this? Without a doubt, they will hear that you have arrived.
local: Ɛzɩma ɖɩlakɩ yɔ? Paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma ɛyáa nɩɣ se ŋkɔma.

eng: As they were screaming, throwing off their garments, and flinging dust into the air.
local: Pamakaɣ kubusi nɛ pɔlɔʋ po-wondu ɛsɔdaa nɛ pakʋsʋʋ mʋzʋʋ se pɩwɩlɩ se pɩkɩlɩ-wɛ wɩzʋʋ.

eng: You yourselves know that I have provided for my own needs and for those of my companions with my own hands.
local: Mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩnawa se malabɩ tʋmɩyɛ nɛ ma-maɣmaɣ me-nesi nɛ mehiɣ mbʋ́ pɩtɛɖaɣ ma nɛ man-taabalaa yɔ.

eng: As for us, we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since we cant justify this unruly gathering.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, tɔm ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa tɩlabɩ sɔnɔ yɔ, Room ñɩma kɔŋ pakʋyɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ se ɖalɩzɩnɩ ñaɖʋʋ nɛ ɖɩkɔɔ ɖihiɣ tɔm sɔsɔtʋ natʋyʋ yem. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩfɛyɩnɩ natʋyʋ se ɖiiheyi-wɛ mbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ ɖikpeɣlaa yɔ.».

eng: You must therefore do what we tell you. Four men among us have made a solemn promise.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, la mbʋ́ ɖiheyiɣ-ŋ yɔ. Abalaa naanza nabɛyɛ wɛ ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa cɩnɛ. Pa nɛ Ɛsɔ paapɛlɩ ñɩyʋʋ.

eng: the commander directed that Paul be taken into the military headquarters. He ordered that Paul be questioned under the whip so that he could find out why they were shouting at him like this.
local: Peeɖe Room sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ sɔsɔ ha nɔɔ se pasʋnɩ Pɔɔlɩ pa-pɩlɩʋ taa nɛ polu-i agbazɛɛ nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ nɛ panɩɩ mbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ ɛyáa samaɣ wɛɛ kamakɩnɩ-ɩ kubusi ḿbʋ́ yɔ.

eng: In everything I have shown you that, by working hard, we must help the weak. In this way we remember the Lord Jesus words: It is more blessed to give than to receive.
local: Mawɩlɩ-mɩ pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa se ḿbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se ɩla tʋmɩyɛ nɛ ɩsɩnɩ kʋñɔndɩnaa nɛ ɖatɩŋa ɖɩtɔzɩ tɔm ndʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu maɣmaɣ yɔɔdaa yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Ɛyʋ haʋ lɛlʋ pʋyʋ yɔ, pɩlakɩ-ɩ leleŋ pɩŋŋ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ɛzɩ pahaʋ ɛ-maɣmaɣ yɔ.› ».

eng: After he said this, he dismissed the assembly.
local: Ɛtɛm-wɛ yɔɔdɩnaʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛɖɔɔnɩ ɛyáa samaɣ ŋ́gá.

eng: Take them with you, go through the purification ritual with them, and pay the cost of having their heads shaved. Everyone will know there is nothing to those reports about you but that you too live a life in keeping with the Law.
local: Kpaɣ-wɛ nɛ ña nɛ wɛ iwolo ɩla cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ wɛtʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ ŋhɛyɩ liidiye pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ papɩzɩ powolo pahʋ pa-ñʋŋ. Pʋbʋ, pɩwɩlɩɣ se ña ɖɔɖɔ ŋɖɔŋ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ yɔɔ. Peeɖe ɛyáa tɩŋa ɖiɣziɣni se ñɔ-tɔm ndʋ́ pɔyɔɔdaɣ yɔ, tɩtɛkɛ toovenim.

eng: As they were stretching him out and tying him down with straps, Paul said to the centurion standing there, Can you legally whip a Roman citizen who hasnt been found guilty in court.
local: Pɔkɔm pɔhɔkɩ-ɩ se poluu-i lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ weyi ɛ́lɛ́ kɛwɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ peeɖe yɔ se: «Pɩha-mɩ nʋmɔʋ se ɩma Room tʋ yem nɛ ɩtahʋʋnɩ-ɩ nɛ ɩkʋ ɛ-tɔm naa we?».

eng: After he said these things, he knelt down with all of them to pray.
local: Pɔɔlɩ tɛm ɛyáa yɔɔdɩnaʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ nɛ wɛ poluŋ pa-nʋmbɔla yɔɔ nɛ pɛsɛɛ Ɛsɔ.

eng: As for the Gentile believers, we wrote a letter about what we decided, that they avoid food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality.
local: Piyele mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩma-wɛ takayaɣ nɛ ɖiheyi-wɛ mbʋ́ ɖɩɖʋwa yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ lɛ se pataatɔɔ kɩlaʋ nandʋ yaa calɩm yaa kpɩna wena pɛtɛcɛ a-liŋ tɛɛ yɔ a-nandʋ. Peyele ɖɔɖɔ acɛyɛ.».

eng: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and reported it. He asked, What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ewolo eheyi pa-ñʋʋdʋ sɔsɔ se: «Abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛkɛ Room tʋ! Ɛzɩma ŋlakɩ kpem yɔ?».

eng: They cried uncontrollably as everyone embraced and kissed Paul.
local: Pɛtɛma lɛ, patɩŋa pɛsɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ siŋŋ nesi naalɛ nɛ palabɩ-ɩ wele wele nɛ pɛ-ɛsɩtɛɣlɩm kpe cɔɔɔ.

eng: The following day Paul took the men with him and went through the purification ritual with them. He entered the temple and publicly announced the completion of the days of purification, when the offering would be presented for each one of them.
local: Pɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ kife lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ kpaɣ-wɛ nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ powolo nɛ palakɩ sɔnzɩ ńzɩ́. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ewolo Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ eheyi ɛyáa alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ sɔnzɩ ńzɩ́ sɩtɛŋ yɔ. Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ papɩzɩɣ palaa paa pa-taa weyi ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: The commander went to Paul and demanded, Tell me! Are you a Roman citizen? He said, Yes.
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ ñowobi Pɔɔlɩ cɔlɔ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Cosi-m nɛ manɩɩ se ŋkɛ Room tʋ na?» Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: «Ɛɛɛ!».

eng: They were especially grieved by his statement that they would never see him again. Then they accompanied him to the ship.
local: Mbʋ́ piyebi kpem nɛ pɛ-ɛsɛ ñɩŋ yɔ lɛ se: eheyi-wɛ se paatasɩɣ naʋ ɛ-ɛsɩbiye taa kaʋ. Pʋwayɩ pasɩ-ɩ nɛ patanɩ-ɩ mɛlɛ cɔlɔ.

eng: When the seven days of purification were almost over, the Jews from the province of Asia saw Paul in the temple. Grabbing him, they threw the whole crowd into confusion by shouting.
local: Kɩyakɩŋ lʋbɛ palakɩnɩ sɔnzɩ ńzɩ́. Kɩyakɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩkɔm se ɩɖɛɣ, ŋgʋ́, Yuda ñɩma nabɛyɛ kɛwɛ peeɖe. Palɩnɩ Aazii egeetiye taa. Pana Pɔɔlɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pasazɩ ɛyáa samaɣ tɩŋa se kakpa-ɩ.

eng: The commander replied, It cost me a lot of money to buy my citizenship. Paul said, Im a citizen by birth.
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ ñekitini-i se: «Ma lɛ, mɛhɛyɩ liidiye sɔsɔɖɛ nɛ pʋcɔ manpɩsɩ Room tʋ.» Pɔɔlɩ ñekitini-i se: «Ma lɛ, ɖooo palʋlʋʋ-m yɔ, mɛnkɛ Room tʋ.».

eng: Fellow Israelites! Help! This is the man who teaches everyone everywhere against our people, the Law, and this place. Not only that, he has even brought Greeks into the temple and defiled this holy place. .
local: Paawɛɛ pamakɩ kubusi se: «Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma, ɩkɔɔ ɩsɩnɩ-ɖʋ! Ɛyʋ weyi esusuu ɛyáa tɩŋa paa le se pataaɖɔ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa pɔ-yɔɔ tɔm ɛzaɣ́tʋ yɔ, ɛnʋ yɔ. Halɩ ɛkpaɣ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ nɛ ewoni Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ pepisi-kɛ!».

eng: At once those who were about to examine him stepped away. The commander was alarmed when he realized he had bound a Roman citizen.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, mbá pɩɩwɛɛ se polu-i agbazɛɛ nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ yɔ, pɛɖɛɛ peyebi-i. Pa-ñʋʋdʋ sɔsɔ ɛnʋ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛnawa se ɛkɛ Room tʋ lɛ, sɔɔndʋ kpa-ɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ kɔtɔmnɩ se pɔhɔkɩ-ɩ.

eng: They said this because they had seen Trophimus the Ephesian in the city with him earlier, and they assumed Paul had brought him into the temple.
local: Pɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paana ɛ nɛ Efɛɛzɩ tʋ Trofim tɛtʋ taa nɛ pamaɣzɩ se paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma, ɛsʋnɩ-ɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa.

eng: The commander still wanted to know the truth about why Paul was being accused by the Jews. Therefore, the next day he ordered the chief priests and the entire Jerusalem Council to assemble. Then he took Paul out of prison and had him stand before them.
local: Tɛʋ fema lɛ, sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ sɔsɔ ɛnʋ ɛñɩnɩɣ se ɛna mbʋ́ Pɔɔlɩ labɩ kpem nɛ Yuda ñɩma kʋyʋʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ekpeɣli cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ tɔm hʋyaa sɔsaa tɩŋa payɩ nɛ ɛha nɔɔ nɛ palɩzɩ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ powoni-i pɛ-hɛkʋ taa.

eng: The entire city was stirred up. The people came rushing, seized Paul, and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the gates were closed.
local: Piiseɣti ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ tɛtʋ hɔɔlɩŋ tɩŋa taa nɛ pese powolo pakpa Pɔɔlɩ ɛnʋ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa peeɖe nɛ pɔhɔm-ɩ palɩzɩnɩ awayɩ nɛ patɩɖɩʋ nɔnɔsɩ.

eng: While they were trying to kill him, a report reached the commander of a company of soldiers that all Jerusalem was in a state of confusion.
local: Pañɩnaɣ se pakʋ-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, nɔɔyʋ wolo eheyi Room sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ se pɩsa miŋ Yeruusalɛm tɛtʋ tɩŋa.

eng: Without a moments hesitation, he took some soldiers and officers and ran down to the mob. When the mob saw the commander and his soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ ɛ-sɔɔjanaa nabɛyɛ nɛ paba ñʋndɩnaa nɛ ɛ nɛ wɛ pese powolo ɛyáa ḿbá pa-wayɩ. Paba na-wɛ lɛ, peyele Pɔɔlɩ mabʋ.

eng: When the commander arrived, he arrested Paul and ordered him to be bound with two chains. Only then did he begin to ask who Paul was and what he had done.
local: Peeɖe sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ etuzi ɛ-tɩ Pɔɔlɩ cɔlɔ nɛ ɛha nɔɔ se pakpa-ɩ pɔhɔkɩ-ɩ yoŋgbasɩ naalɛ. Pɛtɛm-ɩ hɔkʋʋ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ se: ɛnʋ lɛ anɩ? Ɛbɛ ɛlaba?.
