eng: Some in the crowd shouted one thing, others shouted something else. Because of the commotion, he couldnt learn the truth, so he ordered that Paul be taken to the military headquarters.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, ɛyáa samaɣ wɛɛ kokou yem: paa weyi nɛ ɛ-ñɩndʋ. Sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ, ɛtapɩzɩɣ se ɛnɩɩ natʋyʋ kɔkɔɖɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa. Peeɖe eheyi ɛ-sɔɔjanaa se powoni Pɔɔlɩ pa-pɩlɩʋ taa.

eng: When Paul reached the steps, he had to be carried by the soldiers in order to protect him from the violence of the crowd.
local: Patalɩ tendemee yɔɔ lɛ, pʋpɔzɩ se sɔɔjanaa ɛkpaɣ-ɩ ɛyáa samaɣ helu helu lakasɩ yɔɔ.

eng: The mob that followed kept screaming, Away with him.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, katɩŋaɣ ɛ-wayɩ nɛ kamakɩ kubusi se: «Pʋwɛɛ se pakʋ-ɩ!».

eng: As Paul was about to be taken into the military headquarters, he asked the commander, May I speak with you? He answered, Do you know Greek?.
local: Pasʋwaɣnɩ Pɔɔlɩ pɩlʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-taa lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ nɛ Grɛɛkɩ taa se: «Manpɩzɩɣ mɔyɔɔdɩ tɔm natʋyʋ yaa we?» Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ ñɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Naze ŋnɩɣ Grɛɛkɩ tɔm nɩɩ?.

eng: Arent you the Egyptian who started a revolt and led four thousand terrorists into the desert some time ago.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ pɩtɛkɛ ña lɛ Ejiptɩ tʋ weyi ɛkɔm kɩyakɩŋ ɩnɛ ɩ-taa nɛ ɛlɩzɩ ñaɖʋʋ nɛ ɛkpaɣ ɛyáa kʋyaa kutokiŋ naanza nɛ ewoni lakʋ taa yɔ?».

eng: Paul replied, Im a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city. Please, let me speak to the people.
local: Pɔɔlɩ cosi-i se: «Ma mɛnkɛ Yuda tʋ, palʋlɩ-m Taarsɩ tɛtʋ taa Siliisii ɛjaɖɛ taa, tɩ-hɩɖɛ sɛwa, mɛnkɛ tɩ-taa tʋ. Mentendiɣ-ŋ se ŋha-m nʋmɔʋ nɛ mɔyɔɔdɩnɩ samaɣ.».

eng: With the commanders permission, Paul stood on the steps and gestured to the people. When they were quiet, he addressed them in Aramaic.
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ ha-ɩ nʋmɔʋ se ɛyɔɔdɩ. Ɛlɛ́, Pɔɔlɩ sɩŋ tendene yɔɔ nɛ ɛñɩɣ samaɣ nesi se kosu. Kosum ɖiɣ ɖiɣ lɛ, ɛpazɩ-wɛ yɔɔdɩnaʋ nɛ pɛ-tɛ Ebree taa. Ndʋ́ ɛyɔɔdaa yɔ, ńdʋ́ lɛ se:.

eng: Paul stared at the council and said, Brothers, I have lived my life with an altogether clear conscience right up to this very day.
local: Peeɖe Pɔɔlɩ kʋsɩ ɛsɛ nɛ ɛcɔnɩ tɔm hʋyaa ḿbá pɩŋŋ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Man-taabalaa mɩ! Me-wezuu caɣʋ taa lɛ, man-taa wɛ ɖeu Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ.».

eng: The high priest Ananias ordered those standing beside Paul to strike him in the mouth.
local: Ɛyɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ Anaanɩyaasɩ heyi mbá paawɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ se pɛcɛ ɛ-nɔɔ kadaŋa.

eng: Then Paul said to him, God is about to strike you, you whitewashed wall! You sit and judge me according to the Law, yet disobey the Law by ordering that I be struck.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɔɔlɩ kitini-i se: «Ŋwɛ ɛzɩ koloŋa ɛzaɣ́ʋ ŋgá pata akalʋʋ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kɔŋnɩ-ŋ mabʋ! Ŋcaɣ cɩnɛ se ŋhʋʋnɩ-m tɔm ɛzɩ paɖʋʋ paɣtʋ taa yɔ nɛ ŋkiti ŋtɔŋ se pamabɩ-m!».

eng: Those standing near him asked, You dare to insult Gods high priest.
local: Mbá paawɛ peeɖe yɔ, panɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pekitini-i se: «Ɛsɔ cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ ŋtʋʋ ḿbʋ́?».

eng: Paul replied, Brothers, I wasnt aware that he was the high priest. It is written, You will not speak evil about a ruler of your people .
local: Pɔɔlɩ cosi-wɛ se: «Taabalaa! Mantaana se cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, mantaatʋʋ-ɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: ‹Pɩtaha nʋmɔʋ se ɛyʋ ɛtʋʋ ɛ-ɛjaɖɛ taa wiyaʋ.› ».

eng: Knowing that some of them were Sadducees and the others Pharisees, Paul exclaimed in the council, Brothers, Im a Pharisee and a descendant of Pharisees. I am on trial because of my hope in the resurrection of the dead.
local: Pɔɔlɩ kɛtɛm sɩm ḿbʋ́ se tɔm hʋyaa ḿbá, pa-taa lɛlaa kɛ Saduki ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma nɛ lɛlaa ñɛkɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩnɖɛ taa ñɩma. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛyɔɔdɩ ɖoŋ pɛ-ɛsɩndaa se: «Man-taabalaa mɩ! Ma ɖɔɖɔ mɛnkɛ Farɩsɩ ŋgbɛyɛ taa tʋ, Farɩsɩ tʋ lʋlɩnɩ-m. Lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ mɛwɛnɩ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ se efeziɣ sɩɖaa yɔ, kʋ-yɔɔ ɩhʋʋnɩ-m tɔm ɛzʋnɔ.».

eng: These words aroused a dispute between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Farɩsɩ nɛ Saduki pa-aŋgba taa ñɩma pazɩ you pa-taa pe-ɖeke nɛ patayɩ ɖama nabʋlɛ taa.

eng: This is because Sadducees say that theres no resurrection, angel, or spirit, but Pharisees affirm them all.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Saduki ñɩma eetisiɣ se sɩɖaa feŋ. Paatisiɣ ɖɔɖɔ se Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa yaa feziŋ nɩɩyɩ pɛwɛɛ. Ŋgʋ́, Farɩsɩ ñɩma ñetisiɣ ḿbʋ́.

eng: Council members were shouting loudly. Some Pharisees who were legal experts stood up and insisted forcefully, We find nothing wrong with this man! What if a spirit or angel has spoken to him.
local: Peeɖe pasʋ you yɔɔ. Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Farɩsɩ ñɩma hɔɔlʋʋ taa paɣtʋ wɩlɩyaa nabɛyɛ kʋyɩ nɛ pa nɛ wɛ pɔhɔŋnɩ ɖama tɔm nɛ pɔtɔ se: «Abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɖɩtana ɛ-kɩwɛɛkɩm nabʋyʋ kaʋ. Pʋtɔmaɣ yɔ, fezuu nakʋyʋ yaa Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ yɔɔdɩnɩnɩ-ɩ.».

eng: The dispute became so heated that the commander feared they might tear Paul to pieces. He ordered soldiers to go down and remove him by force from their midst. Then they took him back to the military headquarters.
local: Ɛlɛ́, you wɛɛ kɩtɛzɩɣ saʋ miŋ nɛ sɔɔndʋ kpa sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ se pɩtaakɛdɩ patayɩ Pɔɔlɩ ɛnʋ nanʋʋ nanʋʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ eheyi ɛ-sɔɔjanaa se powolo lɔŋ nɛ pakpaɣ-ɩ ɛyáa samaɣ hɛkʋ taa nɛ papɩsɩnɩ-ɩ pa-pɩlʋʋ taa.

eng: The following night the Lord stood near Paul and said, Be encouraged! Just as you have testified about me in Jerusalem, so too you must testify in Rome.
local: Papɩsɩnɩ-ɩ nɛ ɖoó yu lɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ wolo ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Đɔkɩ ña-tɩ kpam! Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ŋlɩzɩ ma-aseɣɖe Yeruusalɛm yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛɛ se ŋlɩzɩ-ɖɩ Room.».

eng: The next morning some Jewish leaders formulated a plot and solemnly promised that they wouldnt eat or drink until they had killed Paul.
local: Kifema lɛ, Yuda ñɩma nabɛyɛ ta nɔɔ nɛ poɖuuni Ɛsɔ se yee patakʋ Pɔɔlɩ ɛnʋ yɔ, pʋyʋ nɔɔyʋ ɛɛkpaɣ pɔ-nɔɔ taa kaʋ.

eng: More than forty people were involved in the conspiracy.
local: Mbá poɖuu ḿbʋ́ yɔ, paaɖɛ ɛyáa nɩɩnaza yɔɔ.

eng: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, We have solemnly promised to eat nothing until we have killed Paul.
local: Paakʋyaa powolo peheyi cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ ɛjaɖɛ taa sɔsaa se: «Đa lɛ, ɖiɖuuni Ɛsɔ se yee ɖɩtakʋ Pɔɔlɩ yɔ, pʋyʋ nɔɔyʋ ɛɛkpaɣ ɖɔ-nɔɔ taa kaʋ.

eng: You and the council must explain to the commander that you need Paul brought down to you. Pretend that you want to examine his case more closely. Were prepared to kill him before he arrives.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mɩ nɛ tɔm hʋyaa sɔsaa iwolo iheyi sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ se ɛkɔnɩ-mɩ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩtazɩ ɛ-tɔm taa siŋŋ nɛ ɩna. Piyele ɖa lɛ, ɖɩñɔɔzɩ ɖa-tɩ se ɖɩkʋʋ-ɩ nʋmɔʋ taa.».

eng: Pauls sister had a son who heard about the ambush and he came to the military headquarters and reported it to Paul.
local: Pɔyɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ koou nɩɩ. Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ woba ɛsʋʋ sɔɔjanaa pɩlʋʋ taa nɛ eheyi-i-tʋ.

eng: Paul called for one of the centurions and said, Take this young man to the commander because he has something to report to him.
local: Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ Pɔɔlɩ yaa sɔɔjanaa taa sɔsɔ lɛlʋ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Kpaɣ evebu ɛnɛ nɛ ŋwoni-i mɩ-ñʋʋdʋ cɔlɔ, ɛwɛnɩ natʋyʋ se eheyiɣ-i.».

eng: He took him to the commander and said, The prisoner Paul asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.
local: Ɛlɛ́, sɔɔja ɛnʋ, ɛkpaɣ evebu ɛnʋ nɛ ewoni-i ɛ́lɛ́ cɔlɔ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Salaka tʋ Pɔɔlɩ yanɩ-m nɛ eheyi-m se mɔnkɔnɩ evebu ɛnɛ ñɔ-cɔlɔ se ɛwɛnɩ natʋyʋ se eheyiɣ-ŋ.».

eng: The commander took him by the hand and withdrew to a place where they could speak privately. He asked, What do you have to report to me.
local: Peeɖe sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ, ɛɖɔkɩ evebu ɛnʋ e-nesi taa nɛ ɛ nɛ ɩ pɛkɛɛ hɔɔlʋʋ nɛ ɛpɔzɩ-ɩ se: «Ndʋ́ ŋwɛnɩ se ŋheyiɣ-m yee?».

eng: He replied, The Jewish leaders have conspired to ask that you bring Paul down to the council tomorrow. They will pretend that they want to investigate his case more closely.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: «Yuda ñɩma tam nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ se pɔkɔŋ pɛcɛtɩ petendi-ŋ nɛ cee ŋwoni Pɔɔlɩ tɔm hʋyaa sɔsaa ɛsɩndaa nɛ paɖaɣnɩ ɛ-tɔm taa tazʋʋ siŋŋ nɛ pana.

eng: Dont fall for it! More than forty of them are waiting to ambush him. They have solemnly promised not to eat or drink until they have killed him. They are ready now, awaiting your consent.
local: Pɔkɔma lɛ, taanɩɩnɩ-wɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pa-taa nabɛyɛ mɛlɩɣ nʋmɔʋ taa se pakʋ-ɩ. Pɛɖɛ ɛyáa nɩɩnaza yɔɔ. Poɖuuni Ɛsɔ se yee patakʋ-ɩ yɔ, pʋyʋ nɔɔyʋ ɛɛkpaɣ pɔ-nɔɔ taa kaʋ. Đajaa, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ñɔ-nɔɔ taa tɔm ɖeke paɖaŋaa. Pɛtɛm pa-tɩ ñɔɔzʋʋ nɛ paɖaŋ kpam.».

eng: The commander dismissed the young man, ordering him, Dont tell anyone that you brought this to my attention.
local: Peeɖe sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ eheyi-i se: «Đɛɛ! Manɩwa. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtaacɩbɩ-ŋ nɛ ŋla nɛ nɔɔyʋ nɩɩ se ŋheyi-m-tʋ.».

eng: The commander called two centurions and said, Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy horsemen, and two hundred spearmen to leave for Caesarea at nine oclock tonight.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ ɛnʋ, ɛ́yáa ɛ-sɔɔjanaa sɔsaa taa ɛyáa naalɛ nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Ikpeɣli sɔɔjanaa nasɩlɛ nɛ sɔɔjanaa mbá pacakɩ kpaŋnaŋ yɔ nɩɩnʋwa nɛ nɩɩlɛ nɛ mbá paba youni kɩkpɛzɛmɩŋ yɔ nasɩlɛ nɛ mɩtɩŋa ɩñɔɔzɩ mɩ-tɩ nɛ iwolo Sezaaree tɛtʋ taa ɖoó taa ñɩɣtʋ nakʋ yɔɔ.

eng: Have horses ready for Paul to ride, so they may take him safely to Governor Felix.
local: Ɩɖɛɣ lɛ, ɩlɩzɩ ɖɔɖɔ kpaŋnaŋ nɩɩyɩ. Ɩ-yɔɔ Pɔɔlɩ cakɩ nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛtalɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Feliksɩ tɛ nɛ ɛ-alaafɩya.».

eng: He wrote the following letter:.
local: Pɛɖɛɣaɣ lɛ, ɛma Feliksɩ takayaɣ se:.

eng: Claudius Lysias, to the most honorable Governor Felix: Greetings.
local: «Đajaa wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Feliksɩ! Ma Kloodi Lisiyaasɩ mamaɣnɩ-ŋ takayaɣ kanɛ, mɛnsɛ-ŋ!.

eng: This man was seized by the Jews and was almost killed by them. I was nearby with a unit of soldiers, and I rescued him when I discovered that he was a Roman citizen.
local: Mancaɣ meheyi-ŋ se ɛyʋ weyi pɔkɔŋnɩ-ŋ yɔ, Yuda ñɩma kakpa-ɩ se pakʋʋ-ɩ nɛ ma nɛ mɔn-sɔɔjanaa ɖɩlɛɣ-ɩ pe-nesi taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩnɩwa se ɛkɛ Room tʋ.

eng: I wanted to find out why they were accusing him, so I brought him to their council.
local: Mancaɣaɣ se mana mbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ Yuda ñɩma kʋyʋʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ lɛ, mankpaɣ-ɩ nɛ mowoni pɔ-tɔm hʋyaa sɔsaa cɔlɔ.

eng: I discovered that they were accusing him about questions related to their Law. I found no charge deserving of death or imprisonment.
local: Pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ lɛ, mana se ɛtala kɩwɛɛkɩm nabʋyʋ nɛ pʋpɔzʋʋ se pakʋ-ɩ yaa paɖʋ-ɩ salaka. Ɛlɛ́, pa-maɣmaɣ pa-paɣtʋ yɔɔ pakpa-ɩ.

eng: When I was informed of a conspiracy against his life, I sent him to you at once and ordered his accusers to bring their case against him before you.
local: Manɩwa se Yuda ñɩma ḿbá pa-taa nabɛyɛ tam ɛ-yɔɔ nɔɔ se pakʋʋ-ɩ. Pʋ-yɔɔ mankpaɣ-ɩ nɛ mentiyini-ŋ kpaagbaa nɛ meheyi ɛ-tɔm tɩnaa ḿbá se pɔkɔɔ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ tɔm ñɛ-ɛsɩndaa!».

eng: Following their orders, the soldiers took Paul during the night and brought him to Antipatris.
local: Sɔɔjanaa mʋ takayaɣ ŋ́gá lɛ, pala mbʋ́ pa-ñʋʋdʋ heyi-wɛ yɔ: pa nɛ Pɔɔlɩ pakpaɣ nʋmɔʋ ɖoó ana a-taa ɖeke nɛ pɔkɔɔ patalɩ Antipatrii tɛtʋ taa.

eng: The following day they let the horsemen continue on with Paul while they returned to the military headquarters in Jerusalem.
local: Tɛʋ fema lɛ, sɔɔjanaa mbá pɔɖɔŋaɣ naŋgbanzɩ yɔ, papɩsɩ pa-pɩlʋʋ taa nɛ pɩsɛɣ kpaŋnaŋ tɩnaa nɛ pa nɛ Pɔɔlɩ pɛɖɛɛ.

eng: The horsemen entered Caesarea, delivered the letter to the governor, and brought Paul before him.
local: Patalɩ Sezaaree wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Feliksɩ ɛnʋ ɛ-tɛ lɛ, pakpaɣ takayaɣ ŋ́gá nɛ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ paɖʋzɩ e-nesi taa.

eng: After he read the letter, he asked Paul about his home province. When he learned that he was from Cilicia.
local: Ɛtɛm-kɛ kalʋʋ lɛ, ɛpɔzɩ Pɔɔlɩ se: «Ñɛ-tɛ egeetiye nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa?» Ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: «Mɛn-tɛ Siliisii egeetiye taa.».

eng: the governor said, I will hear your case when your accusers arrive. Then he ordered that Paul be kept in custody in Herods palace.
local: Peeɖe eheyi-i se: «Ñɔ-tɔm tɩnaa ɛkɔma lɛ, maapɔzɩ-ŋ nɛ manɩɩ ñɔ-nɔɔ taa tɔm.» Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛha nɔɔ se pakpaɣ Pɔɔlɩ ɛnʋ nɛ powoni-i wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Eroodi ɖɩɣa taa nɛ paɖaŋ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: Three days after arriving in the province, Festus went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.
local: Fɛstuusi kɔm Sezaaree egeetiye taa nɛ pɩla kɩyakɩŋ naadozo lɛ, ɛkʋyɩ nɛ ewolo Yeruusalɛm.

eng: The chief priests and Jewish leaders presented their case against Paul. Appealing to him.
local: Peeɖe cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ ɛjaɖɛ ñʋndɩnaa pakʋyaa nɛ powoni Pɔɔlɩ tɔm ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ pɔpɔzɩ-ɩ se.

eng: they asked as a favor from Festus that he summon Paul to Jerusalem. They were planning to ambush and kill him along the way.
local: ɛlabɩ-wɛ lootiye nɛ ɛha-wɛ nʋmɔʋ nɛ pakpaɣ-ɩ nɛ powoni Yeruusalɛm peeɖe. Paatam nɔɔ se yee pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pakʋ-ɩ nʋmɔʋ taa.

eng: But Festus responded by keeping Paul in Caesarea, since he was to return there very soon himself.
local: Fɛstuusi cosi-wɛ se Pɔɔlɩ wɛ salaka taa Sezaaree. Ɛlɛ́, ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛpɩsɩɣ peeɖe alɩwaatʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-taa.

eng: Five days later the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a lawyer named Tertullus. They pressed charges against Paul before the governor.
local: Pɩlabɩ kɩyakɩŋ kagbaanzɩ lɛ, cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ Anaanɩyaasɩ nɛ ɛjaɖɛ taa sɔsaa nabɛyɛ nɛ tɔm loluyu Tɛrtuluusi patalɩ Sezaaree wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Feliksɩ tɛ se pakʋyɩ Pɔɔlɩ yɔɔ.

eng: Some of your leaders can come down with me, he said. If hes done anything wrong, they can bring charges against him.
local: Ɛtasɩ-wɛ heyuu se yee Pɔɔlɩ ɛlabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm nabʋyʋ yɔ, ɛ nɛ pa-taa sɔsaa powolo Sezaaree nɛ pakʋyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: After the governor summoned Paul, Tertullus began to make his case against him. He declared, Under your leadership, we have experienced substantial peace, and your administration has brought reforms to our nation.
local: Peeɖe wiyaʋ tiyaa nɛ payaa Pɔɔlɩ. Ɛkɔma lɛ, Tɛrtuluusi kʋyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ: eheyi wiyaʋ se: «Đajaa wiyaʋ sɔsɔ! Ñɔ-yɔɔ ɖɩwɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa siŋŋ. Ñɔ-lɔŋsɩnɖɛ yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ sakɩyɛ ɛjaɖɛ ɖɩnɛ ɖɩ-taa se piɖeni-ɖɩ.

eng: He stayed with them for no more than eight or ten days, then went down to Caesarea. The following day he took his seat in the court and ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Fɛstuusi caɣ Yeruusalɛm peeɖe ɛzɩ kɩyakɩŋ lutozo yaa hiu yɔ lɛ, ɛpɩsɩ Sezaaree. Tɛʋ fema lɛ, ewolo ɛcaɣ tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ nɛ etiyi se powoni Pɔɔlɩ.

eng: Always and everywhere, most honorable Felix, we acknowledge this with deep gratitude.
local: Đɩsɛɣ-ŋ siŋŋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa ŋwɛɛ ŋlakɩ alɩwaatʋ tɩŋa paa le yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: When he arrived, many Jews who had come down from Jerusalem surrounded him. They brought serious charges against him, but they couldnt prove them.
local: Pɔkɔnɩ-ɩ lɛ, Yuda ñɩma mbá paalɩnɩ Yeruusalɛm nɛ powolo yɔ, pɔcɔɔ-ɩ pata nɛ pakʋyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ siŋŋ nɛ tɔm sɔsɔtʋ. Ŋgʋ́, pa-maɣmaɣ pataapɩzɩɣ se pɔyɔɔdɩ nɛ pawɩlɩ se ndʋ́ pɔyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ tɩkɛ toovenim.

eng: I dont want to take too much of your time, so I ask that you listen with your usual courtesy to our brief statement of the facts.
local: Ɛlɛ́, maakɩlɩɣ-ŋ lesuu alɩwaatʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ monkuluu-ŋ hulaɣ se ña-camɩyɛ lakasɩ yɔɔ lɛ, ŋwelesini-ɖʋ pazɩ yem nɛ ŋnɩɩ ndʋ́ ɖɩkɔmnaa yɔ.

eng: In his own defense, Paul said, Ive done nothing wrong against the Jewish Law, against the temple, or against Caesar.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Pɔɔlɩ tetisi tɔm ńdʋ́ nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ se: «Mantayʋsɩnɩ Yuda ñɩma paɣtʋ yaa Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yaa Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ.».

eng: We have found this man to be a troublemaker who stirs up riots among all the Jews throughout the empire. Hes a ringleader of the Nazarene faction.
local: Abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛkɛ kɩdɛkɛdʋʋ: ɛwɛɛ eyozuu Yuda ñɩma tɩŋa kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ. Ɛkɛ Nazarɛɛtɩ ñɩma ŋgbɛyɛ ɛzaɣ́ʋ ñʋʋdʋ.

eng: Festus, wanting to put the Jews in his debt, asked Paul, Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem to stand trial before me concerning these things.
local: Fɛstuusi ñɩnaɣ se ɛla nɛ pɩkɛdɩnɩ Yuda ñɩma ḿbá. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛpɔzɩ Pɔɔlɩ se: «Ŋtisiɣ se powoni-ŋ Yeruusalɛm nɛ pahʋʋnɩ-ŋ tɔm ńdʋ́ mɛ-ɛsɩndaa yaa we?».

eng: and even tried to defile the temple. Thats when we arrested him.
local: Halɩ ɛcaɣaɣ se episi Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa, ɛlɛ́ ɖɩkpa-ɩ. Đɩcaɣaɣ se ɖɩhʋʋnɩ-ɩ tɔm ɛzɩ paɖʋʋ ɖa-paɣtʋ taa yɔ.

eng: Paul replied, Im standing before Caesars court. I ought to be tried here. I have done nothing wrong to the Jews, as you well know.
local: Ecosi se: «Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ mɛwɛ cɩnɛ. Ɛnʋ pʋwɛɛ se ɛhʋʋ mɔn-tɔm. Mantala Yuda ñɩma kɩwɛɛkɩm nabʋyʋ ɛzɩ ña-maɣmaɣ ŋtɛm sɩm siŋŋ yɔ.

eng: By examining him yourself, you will be able to verify the allegations we are bringing against him.
local: nɛ eheyi-ɖʋ se ɖɩkɔɔ ñɔ-cɔlɔ. Đajaa, ña-maɣmaɣ ŋpɔzʋʋ-ɩ lɛ, eheyiɣ-ŋ mbʋ́ pɩlaba nɛ ɖɩkʋyʋʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ pɩtɩŋa.».

eng: If Im guilty and have done something that deserves death, then I wont try to avoid death. But if there is nothing to their accusations against me, no one has the authority to hand me over to them. I appeal to Caesar.
local: Yee malabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm nabʋyʋ nɛ pʋwɛɛ se pakʋ-m yɔ, maaseɣ sɩm. Ɛlɛ́, yee toovenim ɛfɛyɩ mbʋ́ pɔtɔŋ se malaba yɔ pɩ-taa yɔ, pɩtaha nɔɔyʋ nʋmɔʋ se ɛ́ɖʋzɩ-m pe-nesi taa. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mɔnpɔzʋʋ se powoni-m Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ cɔlɔ.».

eng: The Jews reinforced the action against Paul, affirming the truth of these accusations.
local: Tɛrtuluusi yɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Yuda ñɩma ḿbá patɩnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ se toovenim ḿbʋ́ ɖeyi ɖeyi pɩlaba.

eng: After Festus conferred with his advisors, he responded, You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you will go.
local: Peeɖe Fɛstuusi nɛ ɛ-tɔm tasɩyaa pacaɣaɣ nɛ pala lɩmaɣza. Pɛtɛma lɛ, ecosi Pɔɔlɩ se: «Ŋtɔm se patanɩ-ŋ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ, peeɖe ŋwoki ɖɔɖɔ.».

eng: The governor nodded at Paul, giving him permission to speak. He responded, I know that you have been judge over this nation for many years, so I gladly offer my own defense.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, wiyaʋ sɔsɔ wɩlɩ Pɔɔlɩ nesi se ɛyɔɔdɩ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ kaɖɩ se: «Đajaa, manawa se pɩlaba pɩnzɩ sakɩyɛ yɔ ŋhʋʋ tɔm ɛjaɖɛ ɖɩnɛ ɖɩ-taa yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ mɔyɔɔdʋʋ mɔn-tɔm nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ.

eng: After several days had passed, King Agrippa and Bernice arrived in Caesarea to welcome Festus.
local: Pɩlabɩ kɩyakɩŋ cabɩ lɛ, wiyaʋ Agriipaa nɛ ɛ-kɔɔ Bereniisi powolo Sezaaree se pɛsɛɛ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Fɛstuusi.

eng: You can verify that I went up to worship in Jerusalem no more than twelve days ago.
local: Ña-maɣmaɣ ŋpɩzɩɣ ŋpɔzɩ nɛ ŋna, maawobu Ɛsɔ tɛɛ luŋuu Yeruusalɛm yɔ, pɩlaba kɩyakɩŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ yɔ ɖeyi ɖeyi.

eng: Since they were staying there for many days, Festus discussed the case against Paul with the king. He said, There is a man whom Felix left in prison.
local: Pacaɣ kɩyakɩŋ cabɩ lɛ, ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ Pɔɔlɩ tɔm nɛ ɛkɛdɩ Agriipaa se: «Wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Feliksɩ weyi mɛlɛɣzaa yɔ, ɛɖʋ abalʋ nɔɔyʋ salaka halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ.

eng: They didnt find me arguing with anyone in the temple or stirring up a crowd, whether in the synagogue or anywhere else in the city.
local: Wiɖiyi nɔɔyʋ takatɩ-m Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa yaa Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ naɖɩyɛ taa yaa tɛtʋ taa se ma nɛ nɔɔyʋ ɖiɖiɣni tɔm hɔm yaa mansazaɣ samaɣ se kalɩɩ ɛyáa wayɩ.

eng: When I was in Jerusalem, the Jewish chief priests and elders brought charges against him and requested a guilty verdict in his case.
local: Mowobi Yeruusalɛm lɛ, cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa nɛ ɛjaɖɛ taa sɔsaa pɔkɔnɩ ɛ-tɔm mɔn-cɔlɔ se mahʋʋnɩ-ɩ nɛ mankʋ-tʋ.

eng: Nor can they prove to you the allegations they are now bringing against me.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, paa ŋpɔzɩ ɛyáa mbá yɔ, paapɩzɩɣ pɔyɔɔdɩ nɛ pawɩlɩ-ŋ mbʋ́ malaba nɛ pakʋyʋʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: I told them it is contrary to Roman practice to hand someone over before they have faced their accusers and had opportunity to offer a defense against the charges.
local: Peeɖe moncosi-wɛ se Room ñɩma tɛ lɛ, pɩtaha nʋmɔʋ se nɔɔyʋ ɛlabɩ pʋyʋ yɔ, pakʋ-ɩ yem nɛ ɛ nɛ mbá pɛwɛ tɔm yɔ potosuli ɖama nɛ ekuli ɛ-nɔɔ se tɔm yɔ, tɩ-ɖaɣʋ yɔ.

eng: I do admit this to you, that I am a follower of the Way, which they call a faction. Accordingly, I worship the God of our ancestors and believe everything set out in the Law and written in the Prophets.
local: Đajaa, ndʋ́ mankaɖɩɣ ñɛ-ɛsɩndaa yɔ lɛ se: ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa Ɛsɔ tɛɛ moluŋuu, mentisiɣ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ pama Mooyiizi paɣtʋ nɛ náyaa pa-takayɩsɩ taa yɔ. Moluŋuu ɛ-tɛɛ ɛzɩ mbá payaɣ se ŋgbɛyɛ ɛzaɣ́ʋ taa ñɩma yɔ palakʋʋ yɔ.

eng: When they came here, I didnt put them off. The very next day I took my seat in the court and ordered that the man be brought before me.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ patɩŋ-m nɛ pɔkɔɔ cɩnɛ. Tɔm ndʋ́ peheyi-m yɔ, montoɖoo tɩ-yɔɔ: kifema lɛ, mowolo mancaɣ tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ nɛ mentiyi se pɔkɔnɩ abalʋ ɛnʋ.

eng: The hope I have in God I also share with my accusers, that there will be a resurrection of both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: Pa-maɣmaɣ liu ŋgʋ́ pataɣ yɔ, ŋ́gʋ́ mantaɣ ɖɔɖɔ. Ŋ́gʋ́ lɛ se: ɛyáa kʋtʋʋzaa nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa pasɩba, ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-wɛ fezuu.

eng: When the accusers took the floor, they didnt charge him with any of the crimes I had expected.
local: Ɛ-tɔm tɩnaa kɔma lɛ, patatɩɩyɔɔdɩ se ɛlabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm mbʋ́ mamaɣzaɣ yɔ pɩ-taa nabʋyʋ kaʋ.

eng: On account of this, I have committed myself to maintaining a clear conscience before God and with all people.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ mañakɩ pana se man-taa ɛwɛɛ ɖeu Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛyáa pɛ-ɛsɩndaa tam.

eng: Instead, they quibbled with him about their own religion and about some dead man named Jesus, who Paul claimed was alive.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pa-maɣmaɣ pɔ-sɔnzɩ nɛ abalʋ weyi payaɣ se Yesu, ɛsɩba nɛ Pɔɔlɩ ñɔtɔŋ se ɛ nɛ ɛ-ɛsɛ yɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ pɔhɔŋaɣnɩ ɖama tɔm.

eng: After an absence of several years, I came to Jerusalem to bring gifts for the poor of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
local: Pɩlabɩ pɩnzɩ cabɩ yɔ, mɛnfɛyɩ Yeruusalɛm. Pʋ-yɔɔ manpɩsaa mɔnkɔɔ se maha mɛn-tɛ ñɩma haɖɛ nɛ malaa kɩlaʋ.

eng: Since I had no idea how to investigate these matters, I asked if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem to stand trial there on these issues.
local: Ma-maɣmaɣ lɛ, menteɖiɣzini ɛzɩma mbʋ́ maahʋʋ tɔm ńdʋ́ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ mɔnpɔzɩ Pɔɔlɩ se e-liu wɛɛ se ewolo Yeruusalɛm nɛ pahʋʋ ɛ-tɔm ńdʋ́ peeɖe naa we?.

eng: When they found me in the temple, I was ritually pure. There was no crowd and no disturbance.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ malakaɣ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pana-m Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa, ɛlɛ́, maatɛm cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ wɛtʋ sɔnzɩ labʋ. Ñaɖʋʋ yaa you nakʋyʋ taawɛɛ.

eng: However, Paul appealed that he be held in custody pending a decision from His Majesty the emperor, so I ordered that he be held until I could send him to Caesar.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ecosi se peyebi-i salaka taa ḿbʋ́ nɛ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ hʋʋ ɛ-tɔm. Pʋ-yɔɔ mɔntɔm se: yee ḿbʋ́, peyebi-i ka-taa nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mowoni-i Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ.».

eng: But there were some Jews from the province of Asia. They should be here making their accusations, if indeed they have something against me.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yuda ñɩma nabɛyɛ lɩnɩnɩ Aazii egeetiye taa nɛ pɔkɔɔ pamaɣnɩ-m. Yee ḿbá ɛɛwɛnɩnɩ natʋyʋ se pakʋyʋʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ḿbá pɩɩmʋnaa se pɔkɔɔ ñɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, I want to hear the man myself. Tomorrow, Festus replied, you will hear him.
local: Peeɖe Agriipaa heyi Fɛstuusi se: «Me-liu wɛɛ se pɩcaɣ ɛzɩma yɔ, manɩɩ abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-nɔɔ taa.» Fɛstuusi cosi se: «Yee ḿbʋ́ cee lɛ ɛɛyɔɔdɩ nɛ ŋnɩɩ.».

eng: In their absence, have these people who are here declare what crime they found when I stood before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Yaa mbá pɛwɛ cɩnɛ yɔ, pa-maɣmaɣ pɔyɔɔdɩ nɛ ɖɩnɩɩ se pahʋnɩ-m tɔm hʋyaa sɔsaa cɔlɔ lɛ, kɩwɛɛkɩm mbʋ́ pana mɔ-yɔɔ?.

eng: The next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great fanfare. They entered the auditorium with the military commanders and the citys most prominent men. Festus then ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Kifema lɛ, Agriipaa nɛ ɛ-halʋ Bereniisi nɛ sɔɔjanaa ñʋndɩnaa nɛ tɛtʋ ñʋndɩnaa patɩŋa payɩ powolo wiii nɛ pasʋʋ tɔm ɖɩhʋyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ taa. Peeɖe Fɛstuusi tiyaa se powoni Pɔɔlɩ.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, You may speak for yourself. So Paul gestured with his hand and began his defense.
local: Fɛstuusi tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Agriipaa heyi Pɔɔlɩ se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, paha-ŋ nʋmɔʋ, yɔɔdɩ ñɔ-tɔm lɛ.» Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ kpazɩ nesi nɛ ɛpazɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se:.

eng: Perhaps it concerns this one statement that I blurted out when I was with them: I am on trial before you today because of the resurrection of the dead.
local: Mantala nabʋyʋ nɛ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ tɔm kʋɖʋmtʋ kɔŋ ndʋ́ mansɩŋ pɛ-ɛsɩndaa nɛ moholi yɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Mantaɣ liu se sɩɖaa kɔŋ fem. Pʋ-yɔɔ sɔnɔ pahʋʋnɩ-m mɩ-ɛsɩndaa ɛzʋnɔ.› ».

eng: Festus said, King Agrippa and everyone present with us: You see this man! The entire Jewish community, both here and in Jerusalem, has appealed to me concerning him. Theyve been calling for his immediate death.
local: Powonɩ-ɩ lɛ, Fɛstuusi yɔɔdɩ se: «Wiyaʋ Agriipaa nɛ mbá mɩ mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɖa nɛ mɩ ɖɩwɛ cɩnɛ yɔ, ɩnaɣ abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛ-yɔɔ Yuda ñɩma samaɣ kɔm mɔn-cɔlɔ Yeruusalɛm cɩnɛ nɛ pamakɩ kubusi se pɩtamʋnɩ se ɛtasɩ wezuu caɣʋ.

eng: King Agrippa, I consider myself especially fortunate that I stand before you today as I offer my defense concerning all the accusations the Jews have brought against me.
local: «Wiyaʋ Agriipaa! Sɔnɔ pɩlabɩ-m leleŋ pɩŋŋ se mɔyɔɔdʋʋ kʋyʋʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ Yuda ñɩma kʋyʋʋ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ pʋ-tɔm ñɛ-ɛsɩndaa.

eng: Felix, who had an accurate understanding of the Way, adjourned the meeting. He said, When Lysias the commander arrives from Jerusalem, I will decide this case.
local: Feliksɩ kɛtɛm sɩm Yesu wayɩ tɩŋʋʋ tɔm camɩyɛ. Pʋ-yɔɔ eheyi ɛyáa ḿbá se pekpe nɛ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ Lisiyaasɩ ɛkɔma lɛ, ɛɛtazɩ pɔ-tɔm taa nɛ ɛna.

eng: This is because you understand well all the Jewish customs and controversies. Therefore, I ask you to listen to me patiently.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ŋsɩm Yuda ñɩma sɔnzɩ tɩŋa siŋŋ nɛ mbʋ́ pʋ-tɔm pɔyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, monkuluu-ŋ hulaɣ se ŋɖɔkɩ ñɔ-hɔyɛ nɛ ŋwelesi mɔn-tɔm.

eng: Ive found that he has done nothing deserving death. When he appealed to His Majesty, I decided to send him to Rome.
local: Mɔncɔnɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa lɛ, ɛtala kɩwɛɛkɩm mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ se pakʋ-ɩ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛpɔzaa se powoni-i Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ, mentisaa ɖɔɖɔ se powoni-i.

eng: He arranged for a centurion to guard Paul. He was to give Paul some freedom, and his friends were not to be hindered in their efforts to provide for him.
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi sɔɔjanaa ñʋʋdʋ weyi ɛɖaŋaɣ Pɔɔlɩ yɔɔ yɔ se ɛcɔnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ, ɛlɛ́, ɛha-ɩ waɖɛ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, eyele ɛ-taabalaa nɛ pasɩnɩ-ɩ.

eng: Every Jew knows the way of life I have followed since my youth because, from the beginning, I was among my people and in Jerusalem.
local: Yuda ñɩma tɩŋa sɩm mbʋ́ mɛnkɛ ɖooo me-evebutu taa yɔ. Pasɩm ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ maacaɣ wezuu Yeruusalɛm mɛ-ɛjaɖɛ taa ñɩma hɛkʋ taa yɔ.

eng: I have nothing definite to write to our lord emperor. Therefore, Ive brought him before all of you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that after this investigation, I might have something to write.
local: Mɛnfɛyɩnɩ natʋyʋ siŋŋ se mamaɣ me-wiyaʋ sɔsɔ nɛ meheyi-i abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-tɔm. Pʋ-yɔɔ mɔnkɔnɩ-ɩ mɩ-ɛsɩndaa. Ɛlɛ́, ña wiyaʋ Agriipaa, ña-maɣmaɣ ñɛ-ɛsɩndaa mɔnkɔnɩ-ɩ se yee ŋtɛm-ɩ tɔm pɔzʋʋ lɛ, mana ndʋ́ maama takayaɣ taa yɔ.

eng: After several days, Felix came with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, and summoned Paul. He listened to him talk about faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Pɔɔlɩ wɛ peeɖe ḿbʋ́ nɛ pɩla kɩyakɩŋ cabɩ lɛ, Feliksɩ nɛ ɛ-halʋ Druusiili pɔkɔɔ nɛ etiyi se payaa Pɔɔlɩ. Halʋ ɛnʋ ɛkɛ Yuda tʋ. Ɛ́lɛ́ kɔma lɛ, Feliksɩ heyi-i se ɛkɛdɩ-ɩ Krɩstʋ Yesu yɔɔ lidaʋ wɛnaʋ tɔm. Peeɖe ɛpazɩ-ɩ-tʋ kɛdʋʋ.

eng: They have known me for a long time. If they wanted to, they could testify that I followed the way of life set out by the most exacting group of our religion. I am a Pharisee.
local: Pasɩm-m ɖooo lɔŋ, yee petisiɣ yɔ, papɩzɩɣ palɩzɩ aseɣɖe se maakɛ Farɩsɩ ñɩma ŋgbɛyɛ taa tʋ. Maaɖɔm ɖɩ-taa sɔnzɩ yɔɔ. Ńzɩ́ kɩlɩnɩ kaɖɛ ɖɛ-Ɛsɔ sam taa.

eng: After all, it would be foolish to send a prisoner without specifying the charges against him.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtamʋnɩ se mowoni salaka tʋ nɔɔyʋ yem Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ tɛ nɛ mantawɩlɩ mbʋ́ ɛlaba nɛ pakʋyʋʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ.».

eng: When he spoke about upright behavior, self-control, and the coming judgment, Felix became fearful and said, Go away for now! When I have time, Ill send for you.
local: Ɛkɛdaɣ-wɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se ɛyʋ ɛcaɣ wezuu kʋtʋʋzʋʋ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa yɔ nɛ tɩ ɖɔkʋʋ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ kɔŋ ɛyáa tɔm hʋnaʋ yɔ pɩtɩŋa pʋ-tɔm. Peeɖe sɔɔndʋ kpa wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Feliksɩ ɛnʋ nɛ eheyi-i se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ŋpɩzɩɣ ŋɖɛɛ nɛ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ mehiɣ alɩwaatʋ lɛ, maaɖaɣnɩ-ŋ yaʋ.».

eng: Today I am standing trial because of the hope in the promise God gave our ancestors.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, pahʋʋnɩ-m tɔm. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mantaɣ liu mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ heyi ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa se ɛlakɩ yɔ.

eng: This is the promise our twelve tribes hope to receive as they earnestly worship night and day. The Jews are accusing me, King Agrippa, because of this hope!.
local: Đɛ-ɛjaɖɛ taa cejewee hiu nɛ naalɛ taa ñɩma taɣ liu se panaɣ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ heyi ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa se ɛlakɩ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ pɛwɛɛ poluŋuu Ɛsɔ tɛɛ wɩsɩ nɛ ɖoó. Wiyaʋ! Liu taʋ ḿbʋ́, pʋ-yɔɔ Yuda ñɩma kʋyʋʋ mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: At the same time, he was hoping that Paul would offer him some money, so he often sent for him and talked with him.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, Feliksɩ maɣzaɣ se pʋtɔmaɣ yɔ, pɩkɛdɩɣ Pɔɔlɩ ha-ɩ liidiye. Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ ɛpaŋ-ɩ yaʋ se ewolo nɛ ɛ nɛ ɩ pala faawɩyɛ.

eng: Why is it inconceivable to you that God raises the dead?.
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ɩmaɣzɩɣ se Ɛsɔ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ efezi sɩɖaa yɔ?.

eng: When two years had passed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. Since Felix wanted to grant a favor to the Jews, he left Paul in prison.
local: Pɩnzɩ naalɛ kɔm sɩɖɛɛ lɛ, abalʋ weyi payaɣ se Pɔɔrsiyuusi Fɛstuusi yɔ, ɛlɛɣzɩ Feliksɩ lone taa nɛ ɛkɛ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ. Feliksɩ caɣaɣ se pɩla Yuda ñɩma leleŋ lɛ, eyele Pɔɔlɩ salaka taa ḿbʋ́.

eng: I really thought that I ought to oppose the name of Jesus the Nazarene in every way possible.
local: Ma lɛ, maamaɣzaa se manɖɩkɩ nʋmɔŋ tɩŋa payɩ nɛ nɔɔyʋ ɛtaatasɩ yaʋ Nazarɛɛtɩ tʋ Yesu hɩɖɛ.

eng: And thats exactly what I did in Jerusalem. I locked up many of Gods holy people in prison under the authority of the chief priests. When they were condemned to death, I voted against them.
local: Ḿbʋ́ malabɩ Yeruusalɛm nɛ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa ha-m nʋmɔʋ nɛ ma-maɣmaɣ mankpa Yesu ñɩma sakɩyɛ nɛ manɖʋ-wɛ salaka. Pɔtɔm se pakʋʋ-wɛ lɛ, mentisi.

eng: In one synagogue after another—indeed, in all the synagogues—I would often torture them, compelling them to slander God. My rage bordered on the hysterical as I pursued them, even to foreign cities.
local: Maawɛɛ mɔhɔŋ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ Yuda ñɩma Ɛsɔ ɖɩsɛyɛ tɩŋa payɩ taa paa ɖooye. Malakaɣ-wɛ ḿbʋ́ se pɔyɔɔdɩ ɛzaɣ́tʋ tɔm pe-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ yɔɔ. Man-pana kakpanɩ-wɛ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ nɛ mɛwɛɛ pa-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ ḿbʋ́ nɛ halɩ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ ajɛya lɛɛna tɛtʋ taa.».

eng: On one such journey, I was going to Damascus with the full authority of the chief priests.
local: Pɔɔlɩ tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Pʋ-yɔɔ cɔjɔnaa sɔsaa kɛcɛlɩ-m takayaɣ nɛ paha-m nʋmɔʋ nakʋyʋ wiye se mowolo Damaasɩ.

eng: While on the road at midday, King Agrippa, I saw a light from heaven shining around me and my traveling companions. That light was brighter than the sun.
local: Wiyaʋ! Mɛwɛ nʋmɔʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-taa nɛ pʋkɔɔ ɛzɩ wɩsɩ siɣu yɔ lɛ, mana ñalɩmɩyɛ naɖɩyɛ lɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa yem nɛ ɖɩñalɩ ma nɛ man-taabalaa ɖɔ-yɔɔ ñammm. Ñalɩmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́, ɖɩɩkɩlɩ wɩsɩ ñalɩmɩyɛ ɖoŋ.

eng: We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice that said to me in Aramaic, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me? Its hard for you to kick against a spear.
local: Peeɖe ɖatɩŋa payɩ ɖɩtɔlɩ tataa nɛ manɩɣ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-m nɛ ɖɛ-tɛ Ebree taa se: ‹Sɔɔlɩ! Sɔɔlɩ! Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋwɛɛ ma-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ nɛ ŋɖʋʋ ña-tɩ kʋñɔŋ nɛ ña-nɩŋgbaaŋ ɖoŋ ḿbʋ́?›.

eng: Then I said, Who are you, Lord? The Lord replied, I am Jesus, whom you are harassing.
local: Peeɖe mɔnpɔzaa se: ‹Kɩpaɣlʋ! Ña anɩ?› Ɛlɛ́, ecosi-m se: ‹Ma Yesu weyi ŋwɛ ɛ-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ yɔ.

eng: Get up! Stand on your feet! I have appeared to you for this purpose: to appoint you as my servant and witness of what you have seen and what I will show you.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, kʋyɩ ŋsɩŋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, malɩzɩ man-tɩ mawɩlɩ-ŋ se ŋpɩsɩ man-tʋmlaɖʋ nɛ ŋcɔɔ ŋheyi lɛlaa ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ŋna-m sɔnɔ yɔ nɛ ŋkɛdɩ-wɛ mbʋ́ mbʋ́ mantasɩɣ-ŋ wɩlʋʋ yɔ.

eng: I will rescue you from your own people and from the Gentiles. I am sending you.
local: Ñɛ-tɛ Yuda ñɩma nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pa-taa malɩzɩ-ŋ nɛ mentiyi-ŋ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: to open their eyes. Then they can turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, and receive forgiveness of sins and a place among those who are made holy by faith in me.
local: Mentiyiɣ-ŋ se ŋwolo ŋkuli pɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ ŋlɩzɩ-wɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa nɛ ŋwoni-wɛ ñalɩmɩyɛ taa. Lɩzɩ-wɛ ɖɔɖɔ Satanɩ nesi taa nɛ ŋwoni-wɛ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ. Yee pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɔ-yɔɔ lɛ, Ɛsɔ kpeɣ-wɛ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm nɛ ɛha-wɛ ñɩm mbʋ́ ɛsɩwa se ɛhaɣ ɛ-ñɩma yɔ.› ».

eng: So, King Agrippa, I wasnt disobedient to that heavenly vision.
local: «Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, wiyaʋ Agriipaa! Mentekizi mbʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ wɩlɩ-m nɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ.

eng: Instead, I proclaimed first to those in Damascus and Jerusalem, then to the whole region of Judea and to the Gentiles. My message was that they should change their hearts and lives and turn to God, and that they should demonstrate this change in their behavior.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Damaasɩ ñɩma manpazɩnɩ Ɛsɔtɔm susuu. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, monsusi-tʋ ɖɔɖɔ Yeruusalɛm ñɩma nɛ Yudee egeetiye taa ñɩma tɩŋa nɛ ajɛya lɛɛna taa ñɩma. Meheyi-wɛ se pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ nɛ papɩsɩnɩ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ nɛ pala mbʋ́ pɩwɩlɩɣ se toovenim pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ yɔ.

eng: Because of this, some Jews seized me in the temple and tried to murder me.
local: Tɔm tʋnɛ tɩ-yɔɔ Yuda ñɩma kpa-m Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa se pakʋʋ-m.

eng: God has helped me up to this very day. Therefore, I stand here and bear witness to the lowly and the great. Im saying nothing more than what the Prophets and Moses declared would happen:.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ huuzi mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩtalɩnɩ sɔnɔ nɛ mɛwɛɛ malɩzɩɣ sɔsaa nɛ piya patɩŋa payɩ tɔm ndʋ́ náyaa nɛ Mooyiizi pɔyɔɔdaa se tɩkɔŋ labʋ kpem yɔ tɩ-aseɣɖe. Maayɔɔdʋʋ natʋyʋ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ-tʋ.

eng: that the Christ would suffer and that, as the first to rise from the dead, he would proclaim light both to my people and to the Gentiles.
local: Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ‹Weyi Ɛsɔ Lɩzaa Yɔ, ɛkɔŋ siziŋ nɩʋ nɛ ɛsɩ. Ɛnʋ calɩɣnɩ fem nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa nɛ ɛkɛdɩ Yuda ñɩma nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ ñalɩmɩyɛ tɔm.› ».

eng: At this point in Pauls defense, Festus declared with a loud voice, Youve lost your mind, Paul! Too much learning is driving you mad.
local: Pɔɔlɩ yɔɔdaɣ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Fɛstuusi holini-i se: «Pɔɔlɩ ŋsʋ kpaaŋ! Ŋkɩlɩ kpɛlɩkʋʋ pʋɖɔɔ nɛ pʋsʋzɩ-ŋ kpaaŋ!».

eng: But Paul replied, Im not mad, most honorable Festus! Im speaking what is sound and true.
local: Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ cosi-i se: «Đajaa! Mantasʋʋ kpaaŋ nakʋyʋ, ɛlɛ́, tɔm ndʋ́ mɔyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ, tɩkɛ tɔm kɩbandʋ nɛ toovenim tɔm siŋŋ.

eng: King Agrippa knows about these things, and I have been speaking openly to him. Im certain that none of these things have escaped his attention. This didnt happen secretly or in some out-of-the-way place.
local: Wiyaʋ Agriipaa kɛtɛm nɩʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pɩlaba yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, manpɩzɩɣ mɔyɔɔdɩ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa yem faaa. Manawa se ɛnɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɩtala mɛsaɣ tɛɛ kʋlɔmʋʋ nakʋyʋ taa.

eng: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know you do.
local: Wiyaʋ Agriipaa! Ŋtisiɣ tɔm ndʋ́ náyaa yɔɔdaa yɔ yaa we? Manawa se ŋtisaa!».

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, Are you trying to convince me that, in such a short time, youve made me a Christian.
local: Peeɖe Agriipaa pɩsɩnɩ-ɩ se: «Ŋcaɣ se manpɩsɩ Krɩstʋ tʋ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yaa we?».

eng: Paul responded, Whether it is a short or a long time, I pray to God that not only you but also all who are listening to me today will become like me, except for these chains.
local: Pɔɔlɩ cosi-i se: «Paa lɛɛlɛɛyɔ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yaa ɛzɩmtaa yɔ, mentendiɣ Ɛsɔ se ña nɛ mbá ɩwɛ cɩnɛ nɛ iwelesiɣ mɔn-tɔm sɔnɔ yɔ, mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩpɩsɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ma yɔ nɛ pɩsɛɣ yoŋgbasɩ sɩnɛ si-ɖeke!».

eng: The king stood up, as did the governor, Bernice, and those sitting with them.
local: Peeɖe wiyaʋ Agriipaa nɛ ɛ-halʋ Bereniisi nɛ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Fɛstuusi nɛ mbá paawɛ peeɖe yɔ, pakʋyaa.

eng: As they left, they were saying to each other, This man is doing nothing that deserves death or imprisonment.
local: pɛkɛzɩ pa-tɩ kɩjaʋ nɛ pɔyɔɔdʋʋ pa-taa pe-ɖeke se: «Abalʋ ɛnɛ, ɛtala kɩwɛɛkɩm mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ se pakʋ-ɩ yaa paɖʋ-ɩ salaka yɔ.».

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, This man could have been released if he hadnt appealed to Caesar.
local: Peeɖe Agriipaa heyi Fɛstuusi se: «Yee abalʋ ɛnɛ ɛtaapɔzɩ se patanɩ-ɩ Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ, paayebi-i!».

eng: When it was determined that we were to sail to Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were placed in the custody of a centurion named Julius of the Imperial Company.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, paɖʋ se ɖɩsʋʋ mɛlɛ Sezaaree nɛ ɖiwolo Itaalii. Peeɖe pakpaɣ Pɔɔlɩ nɛ salaka tɩnaa lɛlaa nabɛyɛ nɛ paɖʋzɩ sɔɔja sɔsɔ weyi payaɣ se Yuliyuusi yɔ e-nesi taa. Ɛkɛ sɔɔjanaa tikaɣ ŋgá payaɣ se: Ogustɩ sɔɔjanaa yɔ, pa-ñʋndɩnaa taa lɛlʋ.

eng: We boarded a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail for ports along the coast of the province of Asia. So we put out to sea. Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica, came with us.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɖa nɛ wɛ ɖɩsʋʋ Adramiiti tɛtʋ taa mɛlɛ nɛ ɖɩɖɛɛ, kuwokaɣ Aazii egeetiye taa mɛlɛnaa ɖɩsɩŋɩyɛ ndɩ ndɩ taa. Đa nɛ Masedonii tʋ Aristaarkɩ weyi ɛ-tɛ Tesalooniki tɛtʋ taa yɔ ɖɩɩsʋnɩ-kʋ.

eng: The next day we landed in Sidon. Julius treated Paul kindly and permitted him to go to some friends so they could take care of him.
local: Kifema lɛ, ɖɩtalɩ Sidɔɔ nɛ Yuliyuusi liu wɛɛ se ɛla Pɔɔlɩ camɩyɛ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛha-ɩ nʋmɔʋ se ewolo ɛna ɛ-ɛgbaɣdɩnaa nɛ paha-ɩ mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: From there we sailed off. We passed Cyprus, using the island to shelter us from the headwinds.
local: Đɩkʋyɩ peeɖe lɛ, heɣlim makaɣ tuliŋa nɛ pɩcɛyɩɣ-ɖʋ. Pʋyɔɔ ɖɩtɩŋnɩ Ciiprɩ ɛjaɖɛ teŋgu hɔɔlʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩ-taa pɩsaŋaɣ ɖɔm yɔ nɛ ɖɩɖɛɛ.

eng: We sailed across the open sea off the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, and landed in Myra in Lycia.
local: Đɩkɔm ɖɩɖɛɛ Siliisii nɛ Pamfilii pa-ageeta yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩtalɩ Miiraa tɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩwɛ Liisii ñɩnɖɛ taa yɔ tɩ-taa.

eng: There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship headed for Italy and put us on board.
local: Peeɖe sɔɔja sɔsɔ ɛnʋ ɛna mɛlɛ ŋgʋ́ kɩlɩnɩ Alɛksandrii nɛ kuwoki Itaalii yɔ nɛ ɛsʋzɩ-ɖʋ-kʋ.

eng: After many days of slow and difficult sailing, we arrived off the coast of Cnidus. The wind wouldnt allow us to go farther, so we sailed under the shelter of Crete off Salmone.
local: Heɣlim cɛyaɣ-ɖʋ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ nɛ ɖɩla hɛɛɛ hɛɛɛ kɩyakɩŋ sakɩyɛ ɖa-nʋmɔʋ ɖɔm taa teŋgu yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ nɛ pʋcɔ ɖɩkɔɔ ɖɩtalɩ Kiniidi. Pɩcɛyaɣ-ɖʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɖiyele ɖɩtɩŋnɩ Salmonee nɛ ɖɩmalɩ Krɛɛtɩ ɛjaɖɛ nɛ hadɛ kiŋ.

eng: We sailed along the coast only with difficulty until we came to a place called Good Harbors, near the city of Lasea.
local: Đɩlabɩ kʋñɔŋ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pʋcɔ ɖɩtalɩ ɖɩsɩŋ ɖenɖe payaɣ se Mɛlɛnaa Đɩsɩŋɩyɛ Kɩbanɖɛ yɔ Laasee tɛtʋ cɔlɔ.

eng: Much time had been lost, and the voyage was now dangerous since the Day of Reconciliation had already passed. Paul warned them.
local: Điilesi alɩwaatʋ sakɩyɛ ɖa-nʋmɔʋ ɖɔm ḿbʋ́ pɩ-taa nɛ teŋgu yɔɔ ɖɔm ñɛwɛɛ sɔɔndʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩɩkɛ yolim taa: nɔɔ hɔkʋʋ kɛtɛm ɖɛʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ Pɔɔlɩ paɣ́ mɛlɛ taa ñɩma se:.

eng: Men, I see that our voyage will suffer damage and great loss, not only for the cargo and ship but also for our lives.
local: «Taabalaa! Manaɣ se ɖa-nʋmɔʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kʋwɛɣ kaɖɛ, yee ɖɩtakpa ɖa-tɩ yɔ, mɛlɛ kʋnɛ nɛ kɩ-taa wondu sakɩyɛ pɔkɔŋ wɛɛkʋʋ, halɩ ɖa-maɣmaɣ ɖɩpɩzɩɣ ɖifeti wezuu.».

eng: But the centurion was persuaded more by the ships pilot and captain than by Pauls advice.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Room sɔɔja sɔsɔ ɛnʋ, ɛɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ mɛlɛ sayʋ nɛ kʋ-tʋ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ndʋ́ Pɔɔlɩ yɔɔdaa yɔ.

eng: Since the harbor was unsuitable for spending the winter, the majority supported a plan to put out to sea from there. They thought they might reach Phoenix in Crete and spend the winter in its harbor, which faced southwest and northwest.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, mɛlɛnaa ɖɩsɩŋɩyɛ taawɛɛ ɖeu se ɛyáa ɛcaɣ niŋkaɣ alɩwaatʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ mbá paawɛ kɩ-taa yɔ, pa-taa sakɩyɛ tisaa se pakʋyɩ peeɖe nɛ pɛɖɛɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pacaɣaɣ se pʋwɛ ɛzɩma yɔ, paatalɩ Krɛɛtɩ mɛlɛnaa ɖɩsɩŋɩyɛ nɖɩ́ payaɣ se Feniksɩ yɔ ɖɩ-taa. Đɩha ɛsɛ nɛ hadɛ kiŋ wɩsɩ ɖɩɖʋyɛ nɛ hayo kiŋ wɩsɩ ɖɩɖʋyɛ pɔ-hɔɔlʋʋ taa. Peeɖe ɛyáa pɩzɩɣ pacaɣ niŋkaɣ alɩwaatʋ.

eng: When a gentle south wind began to blow, they thought they could carry out their plan. They pulled up anchor and sailed closely along the coast of Crete.
local: Mɛlɛ sɩŋ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pɩkɛdɩ lɛ, heɣlim nabʋyʋ lɩɩnɩ hadɛ kiŋ nɛ pɩmakɩ fɛlɛ fɛlɛ nɛ kɩ-taa ñɩma maɣzɩ se pɩpɩzɩɣ pɩsɩnɩ-wɛ nɛ pala mbʋ́ paaɖʋwa yɔ. Peeɖe pɛkpɛzɩ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ ŋgʋ́ paakamnɩ-kʋ yɔ nɛ pamalɩ Krɛɛtɩ kidiiliŋ kpɛɛɛ nɛ pɛɖɛɣ.

eng: Before long, a hurricane-strength wind known as a northeaster swept down from Crete.
local: Pɩtala pʋyʋ lɛ, heɣlim sɔsɔm mbʋ́ payaɣ se Eraakɩlɔɔ yɔ, pɩlɩɩnɩ hayo kiŋ nɛ pɩmakɩ ɖoŋ.

eng: The ship was caught in the storm and couldnt be turned into the wind. So we gave in to it, and it carried us along.
local: nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ ɖa nɛ mɛlɛ ŋ́gʋ́ nɛ pɩɖɛɣna. Nabʋyʋ taapɩzɩɣ se pɩkpa-kʋ kpem nɛ ɖɩtɛzɩ ɖa-tɩ yebu nɛ pɩɖɛnɩ-ɖʋ.

eng: After sailing under the shelter of an island called Cauda, we were able to control the lifeboat only with difficulty.
local: Peeɖe ɖɩtɩŋnɩ ɛjaɖɛ cikpeɖe nɖɩ́ lɩm cɔ-ɖɩ pɩta nɛ payaɣ-ɖɩ se Koodaa yɔ ɖɩ-hadɛ kiŋ nɛ ɖɩhɛzɩnɩ heɣlim pazɩ. Halɩ ɖɩɩlaba kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ nɛ pʋcɔ ɖɩlɩzɩ kpɩyʋʋ cikpeluu ŋgʋ́ palɩzɩɣnɩ ɛyáa yɔ.

eng: They brought the lifeboat aboard, then began to wrap the ship with cables to hold it together. Fearing they might run aground on the sandbars of the Gulf of Syrtis, they lowered the anchor and let the ship be carried along.
local: Peeɖe mɛlɛ taa tʋmlaɖaa pɩsɩnɩ-kʋ mɛlɛ taa. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pɔhɔkɩ mɛlɛ ŋ́gʋ́ nimee kpam. Sɔɔndʋ kpaɣaɣ-wɛ se pataawolo pakpa Liibii kidiiliŋ kañɩŋa huwa yɔɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ petisi pɩsaʋ sɔsɔʋ ŋgʋ́ popuzuu se heɣlim ɛsʋʋ yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ piyebi mɛlɛ nɛ kɩɖɛɣ.

eng: We were so battered by the violent storm that the next day the men began throwing cargo overboard.
local: Heɣlim kɛwɛ mabʋ yɔɔ nɛ pʋcɔzʋʋ-ɖʋ pɩŋŋ. Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ tɛʋ fema lɛ, papazɩ mɛlɛ taa wondu lɩzʋʋ nɛ pɔlɔʋ teŋgu taa.

eng: On the third day, they picked up the ships gear and hurled it into the sea.
local: Tozo wiye lɛ, pa-maɣmaɣ pɛyɛɖɩ mɛlɛ yɔɔ wondu nɛ pɔlɔʋ.

eng: When neither the sun nor the moon appeared for many days and the raging storm continued to pound us, all hope of our being saved from this peril faded.
local: Pɩɩlabɩ kɩyakɩŋ sakɩyɛ, wɩsɩ nɛ tɩɩnzɩ patalɩɩ. Ɛlɛ́, heɣlim ḿbʋ́ pʋwɛnɩ pɩmakɩ ɖoŋ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɖɩtɛzɩ lɔʋ liu se ɖɔ-tɔm tɛma.

eng: For a long time no one had eaten. Paul stood up among them and said, Men, you should have complied with my instructions not to sail from Crete. Then we would have avoided this damage and loss.
local: Pileɖaa yɔ, pa-taa nɔɔyʋ takpazɩ pʋyʋ nɛ ɛ-nɔɔ. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ Pɔɔlɩ kʋyaa ɛsɩŋ pɛ-ɛsɩndaa nɛ eheyi-wɛ se: «Taabalaa! Yee ɩɩnɩnɩ-m nɛ ɖɩcaɣ Krɛɛtɩ yɔ, ɖɩtaasʋʋ asayʋʋ kʋnɛ kɩ-taa nɛ ɖilesi wondu ɛzʋnɔ.

eng: Now I urge you to be encouraged. Not one of your lives will be lost, though we will lose the ship.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, mansazɩɣ-mɩ se ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ kpam. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ eelesiɣ e-wezuu. Ɛlɛ́, mɛlɛ ɖeke kɔŋnɩ labʋ asayʋʋ.

eng: Last night an angel from the God to whom I belong and whom I worship stood beside me.
local: Ɛsɔ weyi ɛtɩnɩ-m nɛ moluŋuu ɛ-tɛɛ yɔ, etiyi e-tiyiyu mɔn-cɔlɔ ɖoó ana a-taa.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Paul! You must stand before Caesar! Indeed, God has also graciously given you everyone sailing with you.
local: nɛ eheyi-m se: ‹Pɔɔlɩ! Sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-ŋ. Pʋwɛɛ se ŋwolo Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ cɔlɔ. Ɛsɔ lakɩ-ŋ lootiye nɛ pɩtɩŋnɩ ñɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pʋyʋ ɛɛlakɩ ña nɛ mbá ɩɖɔŋ yɔ pa-taa nɔɔyʋ.›.

eng: Be encouraged, men! I have faith in God that it will be exactly as he told me.
local: Taabalaa! Pʋ-yɔɔ meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ kpam, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mantaɣ Ɛsɔ liu se ndʋ́ ɛyɔɔdaa yɔ, tɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: However, we must run aground on some island.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pʋkɔŋ-ɖʋ lɩzʋʋ nɛ pʋlɔ ajɛya cikpena wena lɩm cɔ-yɛ pɩta yɔ a-taa naɖɩyɛ kidiiliŋ yɔɔ.».

eng: On the fourteenth night, we were being carried across the Adriatic Sea. Around midnight the sailors began to suspect that land was near.
local: Pɩlabɩ ɖoó hiu nɛ naanza yɔ, ɖɩwɛʋ Mediteraanee teŋgu yɔɔ nɛ heɣlim cɔzʋʋ-ɖʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ. Pʋkɔma ɛzɩ ɖoó hɛkʋ yɔ lɛ, pɩla mɛlɛ taa tʋmlaɖaa ɛzɩ ɖɩñɔtɩnɩ tɛtʋ natʋyʋ yɔ.

eng: They dropped a weighted line to take soundings and found the water to be about one hundred twenty feet deep. After proceeding a little farther, we took soundings again and found the water to be about ninety feet deep.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pakpaɣ nimaɣ ŋgá pamaɣzɩɣnɩ lɩm yɔ nɛ pɛpɛɖɩ pɩ-taa se pamaɣzɩ-pʋ. Peeɖe peɖiɣzinaa se pu-cukaŋ taa wɛ ɛzɩ haŋ nɩɩlɛ yɔ. Patasɩ wobu ɛsɩndaa pazɩ lɛ, patasɩ maɣzʋʋ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pana haŋ hiu nɛ kagbaanzɩ.

eng: Afraid that we might run aground somewhere on the rocks, they hurled out four anchors from the stern and began to pray for daylight.
local: Sɔɔndʋ kpaɣaɣ-wɛ se mɛlɛ ɛtaakɔɔ kuyoni ɛwasɩ nasɩyɩ. Pʋ-yɔɔ petisi ñɩɣtʋ ndʋ́ pasɩzɩɣnɩ mɛlɛnaa yɔ naanza mɛlɛ wayɩ nɛ pacaɣnɩ pɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ paɖaŋ kpam se tɛʋ efe.

eng: The sailors tried to abandon the ship by lowering the lifeboat into the sea, pretending they were going to lower anchors from the bow.
local: Mɛlɛ taa tʋmlaɖaa ñɩnaɣ se pese. Pʋ-yɔɔ petisi kpɩyʋʋ ŋgʋ́ palɩzɩɣnɩ ɛyáa yɔ nɛ pɛcɛtɩ se powoki petisi ndʋ́ tʋtʋ wɛ ɛsɩndaa yɔ.

eng: Paul said to the centurion and his soldiers, Unless they stay in the ship, you cant be saved from peril.
local: Peeɖe Pɔɔlɩ heyi Room sɔɔja sɔsɔ nɛ sɔɔjanaa lɛlaa se: «Yee ɛyáa ḿbá patacaɣ mɛlɛ taa cɩnɛ yɔ, ɩɩpɩzɩɣ ihiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ.».

eng: The soldiers then cut the ropes to the lifeboat and let it drift away.
local: Ɛyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, sɔɔjanaa wolo pɛsɛtɩ kpɩyʋʋ nimee nɛ peyebi-kʋ kɩɖɛɛ.

eng: Just before daybreak, Paul urged everyone to eat. He said, This is the fourteenth day youve lived in suspense, and youve not had even a bite to eat.
local: Paawɛ peeɖe ḿbʋ́ nɛ tɛʋ wɩzɩ fem lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ sazɩ patɩŋa se pɔtɔɔ tɔɔnaɣ, eheyaɣ-wɛ se: «Pɩlabɩ kɩyakɩŋ hiu nɛ naanza yɔ, ɩwɛ ɖaŋʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɩtɔtɔɔ pʋyʋ.

eng: I urge you to take some food. Your health depends on it. None of you will lose a single hair from his head.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɩtɔɔ tɔɔnaɣ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɩtɔɔ nɛ ihiɣ wezuu, mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛɛkaɣ lesuu ɛ-ñʋʋ taa ñɔyaɣ maɣmaɣ.».

eng: After reaching land safely, we learned that the island was called Malta.
local: Đɩlɩ cɩŋɩlɩm ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɖiɖiɣzini se ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ lɩm cɔ-ɖɩ pɩta nɛ payaɣ-ɖɩ se Maltɩ yɔ, ɖɩ-taa ɖɩwɛɛ.

eng: After he said these things, he took bread, gave thanks to God in front of them all, then broke it and began to eat.
local: Pɔɔlɩ tɛm yɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛkpaɣ tindine nɛ ɛsɛɛ ɖɩ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ patɩŋa pɛ-ɛsɩndaa nɛ ɛcɩyɩ-ɖɩ nɛ ɛpazɩ tɔɔʋ.

eng: The islanders showed us extraordinary kindness. Because it was rainy and cold, they built a fire and welcomed all of us.
local: Đɩ-taa ñɩma kamʋ-ɖʋ ɖeu siŋŋ: paɖʋ miŋ sɔsɔŋ nɩɩyɩ nɛ ɖikpeɣli ɛ-cɔlɔ ɖatɩŋa nɛ ɖihoyuu. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɛʋ nakʋyʋ kapazɩ nɩʋ nɛ niŋkaɣ wɛɛ.

eng: Everyone was encouraged and took some food. .
local: Peeɖe pɩlabɩ mɛlɛ taa ɛyáa tɩŋa hʋnʋm nɛ pɔtɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Paul gathered a bunch of dry sticks and put them on the fire. As he did, a poisonous snake, driven out by the heat, latched on to his hand.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ kpaɣ ɖasɩ ñʋʋ nakʋyʋ se ɛɖʋʋ miŋ taa, naze lɛ, pazɩyɛ naɖɩyɛ kɛwɛ kɩ-taa. Miŋ hohou kpa-ɖɩ lɛ, ɖɩlɩɩ nɛ ɖɩñasɩ-ɩ nɛ ɖisusini e-nesi.

eng: In all, there were two hundred seventy-six of us on the ship.
local: Mbá ɖa ɖɩɩwɛ mɛlɛ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-taa yɔ, ɖɩɩkpɛndɩ ɛyáa nasɩlɛ nɛ nɩɩnʋwa nɛ nɩɩlɛ nɛ loɖo.

eng: When the islanders saw the snake hanging from his hand, they said to each other, This man must be a murderer! He was rescued from the sea, but the goddess Justice hasnt let him live.
local: Peeɖe ñɩma na ɖisusini e-nesi ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɔyɔɔdʋʋ pa-taa pe-ɖeke se: «Abalʋ ɛnɛ, paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma ɛkɛ ɛyáa kʋyʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paa pɩlɩzɩ-ɩ teŋgu taa yɔ, Ɛsɔ taɖʋ se ɛtasɩ caɣʋ wezuu.».

eng: When they had eaten as much as they wanted, they lightened the ship by throwing the grain into the sea.
local: Pɛtɛm tɔɔʋ nɛ pahaɣ lɛ, papazɩ lɩzʋʋ mɩla nɛ pɔlɔʋ teŋgu taa se mɛlɛ ɛtaakɩlɩ yuŋ.

eng: Paul shook the snake into the fire and suffered no harm.
local: Peeɖe ɛmɩsɩ e-nesi nɛ ɖʋm ɛnʋ ɛlɩɩ ɛtɔlɩ miŋ taa. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtala Pɔɔlɩ nabʋyʋ.

eng: In the morning light they saw a bay with a sandy beach. They didnt know what land it was, but they thought they might possibly be able to run the ship aground.
local: Tɛʋ fema lɛ, mɛlɛ taa ñɩma ɛɛsɩŋ ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ pɔñɔtɩnaa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, polosuu kidiiliŋ sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ kotaaa, kʋ-yɔɔ nɛ kañɩŋa nɛ paɖʋ se yee papɩzɩɣ yɔ, powoni mɛlɛ peeɖe nɛ kɩkaɖɩnɩ-kɛ.

eng: They expected him to swell up with fever or suddenly drop dead. After waiting a long time and seeing nothing unusual happen to him, they changed their minds and began to claim that he was a god.
local: Ɛyáa ḿbá, paaɖaŋaa se Pɔɔlɩ nesi húú yaa pɩkɛdɩɣ ɛtɔlɩ yem nɛ ɛsɩ. Pʋkɔma pileɖi nɛ paanɩɣ natʋyʋ lɛ, peyele pɔtɔŋ se abalʋ ɛnʋ ɛkɛ ɛsɔ nɔɔyʋ kpem.

eng: They cut the anchors loose and left them in the sea. At the same time, they untied the ropes that ran back to the rudders. They raised the foresail to catch the wind and made for the beach.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ pɛyɛɖɩ ñɩɣtʋ nɛ peyebi-tʋ teŋgu taa nɛ pɛyɛɖɩ ɖɔɖɔ nimee wena paahɔkɩnɩ ɖaŋ weyi pasaɣnɩ mɛlɛ yɔ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pata pɩsaʋ cikpeluu ŋgʋ́ kɩwɛɣ mɛlɛ ɛsɩndaa yɔ nɛ pakpazɩ se heɣlim eketi-kʋ nɛ puwoni kidiiliŋ cɔlɔ.

eng: From Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for Gods good news.
local: Ma Krɩstʋ Yesu tʋmlaɖʋ Pɔɔlɩ, mamaɣnɩ-mɩ takayaɣ kanɛ, mɛnsɛ-mɩ. Ɛsɔ ya-m se mɛnkɛ Yesu tiyiyu nɛ ɛsɩ-m nɛ hɔɔlʋʋ se monsusi ɛ-Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: Publius, the islands most prominent person, owned a large estate in that area. He welcomed us warmly into his home as his guests for three days.
local: Ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa paawɛ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɖɩ-taa wiyaʋ weyi payaɣ se Publiyuusi yɔ, ɛɛwɛnɩ ɖɩɣa nakɛyɛ pʋ-cɔlɔ peeɖe nɛ ɛmʋ-ɖʋ nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ nɛ ɖɩsʋʋ ɛ-tɛ kɩyakɩŋ naadozo.

eng: But they struck a sandbar and the ship ran aground. The bow was stuck and wouldnt move, and the stern was broken into pieces by the force of the waves.
local: Ɛlɛ́, puwoni-kʋ nɛ puyoni kañɩŋa huuye naɖɩyɛ nɛ kʋ-nɔɔ sʋʋ ka-taa nɛ pɩfɛyɩ ciɣduu nɛ lɩm hola sɔsɔna ñɛpɛlɩ-kʋ nɛ wayɩ.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: Ɛsɔ katɩŋnɩ náyaa yɔɔ ɛ-Tɔm Takayaɣ taa ɖooo lɔŋ nɛ ɛɖʋ nɛ pama se ɛcɛlɩɣ-ɖʋ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ńdʋ́.

eng: Publius father was bedridden, sick with a fever and dysentery. Paul went to see him and prayed. He placed his hand on him and healed him.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, kɩnamɩyɛ nɛ anɩza paakpa wiyaʋ Publiyuusi caa nɛ ɛhɛnɩ. Pɔɔlɩ nɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛsʋʋ ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɛtɩnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nesi nɛ ɛsɛɛ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming to shore and escaping.
local: Peeɖe sɔɔjanaa nɩnɩ ɖama se pakʋ salaka tɩnaa nɛ pa-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛtaaya lɩm nɛ ɛtʋlɩ.

eng: He was publicly identified as Gods Son with power through his resurrection from the dead, which was based on the Spirit of holiness. This Son is Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: ɛlɛ́, efem nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa yɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ ya-ɩ se ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ weyi pɩtɩŋnɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔɔ nɛ ɛwɛnɩ ɖoŋ yɔ.

eng: Once this happened, the rest of the sick on the island came to him and were healed.
local: Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Maltɩ ɛjaɖɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa kʋdɔndɩnaa lɛlaa wolo ɖɔɖɔ Pɔɔlɩ cɔlɔ nɛ pɔ-yɔɔ cɛ.

eng: However, the centurion wanted to save Paul, so he stopped them from carrying out their plan. He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and head for land.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Room sɔɔja sɔsɔ kizini-wɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, e-liu wɛɛ se ɛya Pɔɔlɩ ñʋʋ. Peeɖe ɛha nɔɔ se mbá pasɩm lɩm yaʋ yɔ, pacalɩ falʋʋ pɩ-taa nɛ paya nɛ palɩɩ kidiiliŋ yɔɔ.

eng: Through him we have received Gods grace and our appointment to be apostles. This was to bring all Gentiles to faithful obedience for his names sake.
local: Ɛ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ Ɛsɔ labɩ-m lootiye nɛ ɛha-m waɖɛ se mɛnkɛ Yesu ɛnʋ e-tiyiyu nɛ meyele nɛ ajɛya kpeekpe taa ɛyáa wɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ pañam-ɩ. Ḿbʋ́ yekina nɛ paɖʋ-ɩ hɩɖɛ.

eng: They honored us in many ways. When we were getting ready to sail again, they supplied us with what we needed.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛyáa ɖʋ-ɖʋ hɩɖɛ siŋŋ, ɖɩcaɣaɣ se ɖɩsʋʋ mɛlɛ lɛɛkʋ nɛ ɖɩɖɛɛ lɛ, paha-ɖʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pʋpɔzʋʋ-ɖʋ ɖa-nʋmɔʋ ɖɔm taa yɔ.

eng: He ordered the rest to grab hold of planks or debris from the ship. In this way, everyone reached land safely.
local: nɛ piyele lɛlaa ñɔñɔkɔyɩnɩ kpoŋgbolonaa yaa mɛlɛ hɔɔlasɩ nɛ patɩŋ pa-wayɩ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ palaba nɛ patɩŋa payɩ palɩɩ kidiiliŋ yɔɔ, pɩtacɩdɩlɩ nɔɔyʋ.

eng: You who are called by Jesus Christ are also included among these Gentiles.
local: Mbá mɩ Yesu Krɩstʋ ya-mɩ yɔ, ɩwɛ ɖɔɖɔ ɛyáa ḿbá pa-taa.

eng: After three months we put out to sea in a ship that had spent the winter at the island. It was an Alexandrian ship with carvings of the twin gods Castor and Pollux as its figurehead.
local: Fenasɩ naadozo ɖɛwa lɛ, ɖɩsʋʋ Alɛksandrii tɛtʋ taa mɛlɛ ŋgʋ́ payaɣ se Đomaa Ñʋŋ yɔ nɛ ɖɩɖɛɛ. Kɩɩsɩŋ Maltɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa niŋkaɣ alɩwaatʋ taa.

eng: To those in Rome who are dearly loved by God and called to be Gods people. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Room ñɩma mbá mɩ Ɛsɔ sɔɔlɩ-mɩ siŋŋ nɛ ɛ́yáa-mɩ se ɩkɛ ɛ-ñɩma yɔ, mɩtɩŋa payɩ mamaɣ takayaɣ kanɛ. Đa-Caa Ɛsɔ nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ palabɩ-mɩ lootiye nɛ pɛhɛzɩ mɩ-laŋa!.

eng: We landed in Syracuse where we stayed three days.
local: Đɩtalɩ Siraakuusi lɛ, ɖɩcaɣ kɩyakɩŋ naadozo.

eng: First of all, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because the news about your faithfulness is being spread throughout the whole world.
local: Kajalaɣ lɛ, mantɩŋɩɣnɩ Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ mansaŋ Ɛsɔ mɩtɩŋa payɩ mɩ-yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɔyɔɔdʋʋ lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ɩwɛnɩ yɔ kʋ-tɔm kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ.

eng: From there we sailed to Rhegium. After one day a south wind came up, and we arrived on the second day in Puteoli.
local: Đɩkʋyɩ peeɖe lɛ, ɖɩmalɩ kidiiliŋ kpɛɛɛ nɛ ɖiwolo Rejiyoo. Tɛʋ fema lɛ, hadɛ kiŋ heɣlim pazɩ mabʋ nɛ ɖɩɖʋ kɩyakɩŋ naalɛ nɛ pʋcɔ ɖɩtalɩ Puuzɔɔlɩ.

eng: I serve God in my spirit by preaching the good news about Gods Son, and God is my witness that I continually mention you.
local: Ɛsɔ weyi malakɩ ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ nɛ ma-lɩmaɣza tɩŋa nɛ monsusuu ɛ-Pɩyalʋ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm yɔ, ɛnawa se mɛwɛɛ mamaɣzɩɣ mɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: There we found brothers and sisters who urged us to stay with them for a week. In this way we came to Rome.
local: Peeɖe ɖɩmaɣnɩ taabalaa nabɛyɛ nɛ paba tendi-ɖʋ se ɖa nɛ wɛ ɖɩcaɣ kɩyakɩŋ lʋbɛ. Pʋwayɩ ɖɩkpaɣ nʋmɔʋ nɛ ɖɩɖɛɣ Room.

eng: in all my prayers. Im always asking that somehow, by Gods will, I might succeed in visiting you at last.
local: mɛ-Ɛsɔ sɛʋ taa. Mɔnpɔzʋʋ-ɩ paa ɖooye se yee etisiɣ yɔ, ɛtɛzɩ-m haʋ nʋmɔʋ nɛ mɔnkɔɔ mɩ-tɛ.

eng: When the brothers and sisters there heard about us, they came as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns to meet us. When Paul saw them, he gave thanks to God and was encouraged.
local: Peeɖe taabalaa nɩwa se ɖiwoki lɛ, pa-taa lɛlaa katɩ-ɖʋ Apiyuusi kɩyakʋ taa nɛ lɛlaa ñakatɩ-ɖʋ ɖenɖe payaɣ se Agɔmaa Đɩsɩ Naadozo yɔ. Pɔɔlɩ na-wɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛsa Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛkpa ɖoŋ.

eng: I really want to see you to pass along some spiritual gift to you so that you can be strengthened.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, me-liu wɛɛ kpem se mɔnkɔɔ nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tɩŋnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ kɩlabɩ-mɩ kʋjɔʋ nɛ ɩkpa ɖoŋ.

eng: When we entered Rome, Paul was permitted to live by himself, with a soldier guarding him.
local: Đɩtalɩ Room lɛ, paha Pɔɔlɩ nʋmɔʋ se ɛ nɛ sɔɔja weyi ɛɖaŋɩɣ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, pacaɣnɩ nɖeke.

eng: What I mean is that we can mutually encourage each other while I am with you. We can be encouraged by the faithfulness we find in each other, both your faithfulness and mine.
local: Ndʋ́ mancaɣ yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ lɛ se mɔnkɔŋ mɩ-tɛ se lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩwɛna yɔ, kiyele nɛ pɩla ma nɛ mɩ hʋnʋm.

eng: Three days later, Paul called the Jewish leaders together. When they gathered, he said, Brothers, although I have done nothing against our people or the customs of our ancestors, Im a prisoner from Jerusalem. They handed me over to the Romans.
local: Patalɩ Room nɛ pɩla kɩyakɩŋ naadozo lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ yaa Yuda ñɩma ñʋndɩnaa mbá pɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ ɛ-tɛ. Pekpeɣlaa lɛ, eheyi-wɛ se: «Man-taabalaa! Mantayʋsɩnɩ ɖɛ-ɛjaɖɛ taa ñɩma yaa ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa sɔnzɩ. Ɛlɛ́, pakpa-m ɖulum Yeruusalɛm nɛ paɖʋzɩ-m Room ñɩma nesi taa.

eng: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that I planned to visit you many times, although I have been prevented from coming until now. I want to harvest some fruit among you, just as I have done among the other Gentiles.
local: Man-taabalaa! Me-liu wɛɛ se ɩtɩlɩ se manpam ɖʋʋ se mɔnkɔŋ mɩ-tɛ nɛ mala tʋmɩyɛ nɛ pɩlɩɩ pʋyʋ ɛzɩ malabʋ ajɛya lɛɛna taa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ pɩlaɣzɩɣ-m halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ.

eng: who intended to release me after they examined me, because they couldnt find any reason for putting me to death.
local: Peeɖe pɔpɔzɩ-m tɔm nɛ pɔtɔ se peyeki-m, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, patana mbʋ́ mayʋsaa nɛ pʋpɔzʋʋ se pakʋ-m yɔ.

eng: I have a responsibility both to Greeks and to those who dont speak Greek, both to the wise and to the foolish.
local: Pɩkɛnɩ-m kɩcɛyʋʋ se mowolo mbá pɛ-ɛsɛ kulaa nɛ mbá pɛ-ɛsɛ tokuli yɔ, nɛ lɔŋsɩnɖaa nɛ mbá patasɩ lɔŋ yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: When the Jews objected, I was forced to appeal to Caesar. Dont think I appealed to Caesar because I had any reason to bring charges against my nation.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yuda ñɩma kizi se pataayebi-m. Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ mɔnpɔzɩ se patanɩ-m Room wiyaʋ sɔsɔ cɔlɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtɛkɛ se mancaɣ mankʋyɩ mɛ-ɛjaɖɛ taa ñɩma yɔɔ.

eng: Thats why Im ready to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
local: Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ me-liu wɛɛ siŋŋ se monsusi ɖɔɖɔ mbá mɩ ɩwɛ Room yɔ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: This is why I asked to see you and speak with you: its because of the hope of Israel that I am bound with this chain.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ maya-mɩ se mɔyɔɔdɩnɩ-mɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mbʋ́ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma taɣ liu yɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ paɖɩ-m yoŋgbasɩ sɩnɛ.».

eng: Im not ashamed of the gospel: it is Gods own power for salvation to all who have faith in God, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, fɛyɛ ɛɛkpaɣ-m nɛ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ńdʋ́: tɩkɛ ɖoŋ weyi Ɛsɔ tɩŋɩɣnɩ ɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛya mbá patɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛɣnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ pa-ñʋʋ yɔ. Ɛpazɩɣnɩ Yuda ñɩma nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩtamnɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ.

eng: They responded, We havent received any letters about you from Judea, nor have any of our brothers come and reported or said anything bad about you.
local: Peeɖe pekitini-i se: «Yudee egeetiye taa tʋ nɔɔyʋ tetiyini-ɖʋ takayaɣ nakɛyɛ ñɔ-tɔm taa. Đa-taabalaa mbá pɛwɛ peeɖe yɔ, wiɖiyi pa-taa nɔɔyʋ tɔkɔɔ ɛkɛdɩ-ɖʋ natʋyʋ yaa eheyi-ɖʋ se ŋlabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm nabʋyʋ.

eng: Gods righteousness is being revealed in the gospel, from faithfulness for faith, as it is written, The righteous person will live by faith. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, ńdʋ́ wɩlɩɣnɩ tam ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ tʋʋzaa nɛ ɛtɩŋɩɣnɩ ɛyáa lidaʋ wɛnaʋ ɖeke koŋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛpɩsɩɣ-wɛ kʋtʋʋzaa yɔ. Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: «Weyi ɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ e-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛwɛɣ wezuu.».

eng: But we think its important to hear what you think, for we know that people everywhere are speaking against this faction.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɖe-liu wɛɛ se ɖɩnɩɩ ndʋ́ ña-maɣmaɣ ŋmaɣzɩɣ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩnawa se ɛyáa kizi ŋgbɛyɛ nɖɩ́ ŋwɛ ɖɩ-taa yɔ ɖɩ-tɔm paa le.».

eng: Gods wrath is being revealed from heaven against all the ungodly behavior and the injustice of human beings who silence the truth with injustice.
local: Đɩna kaŋŋ se Ɛsɔ pana kpa ɖooo ɛsɔdaa. Akpanɩ mbá patɩŋa payɩ pa-paa fɛyɩnɩ-ɩ nɛ palakɩ mbʋ́ pɩtatʋʋzɩ yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɩkɩnɩ ɛyáa nʋmɔʋ se pataatɩlɩ e-toovenim.

eng: On the day scheduled for this purpose, many people came to the place where he was staying. From morning until evening, he explained and testified concerning Gods kingdom and tried to convince them about Jesus through appealing to the Law from Moses and the Prophets.
local: Peeɖe Pɔɔlɩ nɛ ɛyáa ḿbá paɖʋ kɩyakʋ se pakatɩ ɖama. Pɩtalɩ ki-wiye lɛ, powolo sakɩyɛ ɛ-tɛ nɛ ɛkɛdɩ-wɛ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ tɔm wɩsɩ nɛ sikpem: ɛtɩŋaɣnɩ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ nɛ náyaa takayɩsɩ taa tɔm pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛñɩnɩɣ se petisi Yesu tɔm.

eng: This is because what is known about God should be plain to them because God made it plain to them.
local: Ɛ-pana kpaɣnɩ-wɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ mbʋ́ papɩzɩɣ patɩlɩ ɛ-tɔm taa yɔ.

eng: Some were persuaded by what he said, but others refused to believe.
local: Lɛlaa tisi-tʋ nɛ lɛlaa ñekizi-tʋ.

eng: Ever since the creation of the world, Gods invisible qualities—Gods eternal power and divine nature—have been clearly seen, because they are understood through the things God has made. So humans are without excuse.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɖooo ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ labʋ kedeŋa yɔ, ɛlabɩ-kɛ labʋ mbʋ́ yɔ, piyeba nɛ panaɣ ɛ-wɛtʋ ndʋ́ paanaɣ yɔ kaŋŋ nɛ e-ɖoŋ weyi ɛfɛyɩ tɩnaɣ yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ ɛkɛnaa kpem yɔ.

eng: They disagreed with each other and were starting to leave when Paul made one more statement: The Holy Spirit spoke correctly when he said to your ancestors through Isaiah the prophet.
local: Pacaɣaɣ kpem nɛ patanɩɩnɩ ɖama lɛ, Pɔɔlɩ heyi-wɛ tɔm tobi se: «Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu kɛwɛnɩ toovenim alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ kɩtɩŋnɩ náyʋ Ezaayii yɔɔ nɛ kɩyɔɔdɩnɩ mɩ-cɔzɔnaa yɔ! Kɩyɔɔdɩnɩ-wɛ se:.

eng: Although they knew God, they didnt honor God as God or thank him. Instead, their reasoning became pointless, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, paapɩzɩɣ pawaanɩ natʋyʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pasɩm Ɛsɔ, ɛlɛ́, pataɖʋ-ɩ hɩɖɛ ɛzɩ pʋmʋnaʋ yɔ, patasam-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ. Pa-lɩmaɣza nɛ pa-laŋa taa wɛɛkaa, paasɩŋ pʋyʋ.

eng: Go to this people and say: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing. .
local: ‹Wolo ŋheyi ɛjaɖɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa ñɩma se: pɔkɔŋ welesuu camɩyɛ, ɛlɛ́, paanɩɣ pʋ-tɔbʋʋ. Pɔkɔŋ cɔnaʋ camɩyɛ, ɛlɛ́, paanaɣ.

eng: While they were claiming to be wise, they made fools of themselves.
local: Pakpazaɣ pa-tɩ se pɛkɛ lɔŋsɩnɖaa nɛ papɩsɩ kpaanzɩyaa.

eng: This peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛjaɖɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa ñɩma laŋa wɛ kpɩzɩŋ nɛ pɔtɔ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ se pataanɩɩ, nɛ pohuuzi pɛ-ɛsɛ yɔɔ se pataana. Pataasɔɔlɩ se pana nɛ pɛ-ɛsɛ, panɩɩ nɛ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ, nɛ paɖʋ sɔɔlɩm nɛ panɩɩ pɩ-taa. Pataasɔɔlɩ se papɩsɩnɩ ma Ɛsɔ mɔ-yɔɔ. Ḿbʋ́ labɩna mantapɩzɩ mala nɛ pɔ-yɔɔ cɛ.› ».

eng: They exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images that look like mortal humans: birds, animals, and reptiles.
local: Ɛsɔ ɛɛsɩkɩ kaʋ, ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ ɛzɩ paaluŋ e-koboyaɣ tɛɛ lɛ, peyele poluŋuu ɛyʋ weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ñasɩkɩ yɔ nɛ sumasɩ nɛ kpɩna nɛ tɛtɛ ñɩtʋ pa-sɩʋ kpelisi tɛɛ.

eng: Therefore, be certain of this: Gods salvation has been sent to the Gentiles. They will listen.
local: Pɔɔlɩ tasɩ-wɛ heyuu se pʋwɛɛ se patɩlɩ se mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, ḿbá Ɛsɔ tiyini ñʋʋ yabʋ tɔm tʋnɛ. Paba kɔŋnɩ-tʋ welesuu!.

eng: So God abandoned them to their hearts desires, which led to the moral corruption of degrading their own bodies with each other.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ yebi-wɛ nɛ palakɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩm ɛzɩ paɖʋʋ pa-taa yɔ nɛ paɖʋʋ ɖama tomnasɩ fɛyɛ.

eng: Paul lived in his own rented quarters for two full years and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
local: Pɔɔlɩ kacaɣ ɖɩɣa ŋgá ɛɛlaba haya yɔ ka-taa pɩnzɩ naalɛ pilim. Ɛmʋwaɣ mbá patɩŋa payɩ powokaɣ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ.

eng: They traded Gods truth for a lie, and they worshipped and served the creation instead of the creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Pakpaɣ cɛtɩm nɛ pɛlɛɣzɩ Ɛsɔ toovenim lone taa. Poluŋuu mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ laba yɔ pɩ-tɛɛ nɛ palakɩ pɩ-tʋmɩyɛ nɛ peyeki Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ ɛ-maɣmaɣ. Ŋgʋ́, laɖʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-tɛɛ pɩɩwɛɛ se poluŋ tam. Pɩla ḿbʋ́!.

eng: Unhindered and with complete confidence, he continued to preach Gods kingdom and to teach about the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Esusaɣ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ tɔm nɛ ɛwɩlɩɣ ɛyáa Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ tɔm kaalɛyɩtʋ yem fɛɛɛ.

eng: Thats why God abandoned them to degrading lust. Their females traded natural sexual relations for unnatural sexual relations.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ yebi-wɛ nɛ pañɩnɩɣ kpem se pala mbʋ́ pʋɖʋʋ ɛyáa fɛyɛ yɔ: halaa yebi pa-walaa cɔlɔ sʋʋ nɛ panɩɣnɩ ɖama caʋ.

eng: Also, in the same way, the males traded natural sexual relations with females, and burned with lust for each other. Males performed shameful actions with males, and they were paid back with the penalty they deserved for their mistake in their own bodies.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ abalaa ñeyebi pa-halaa yɔɔ sʋʋ nɛ panɩɣnɩ ɖama caʋ ɖeke kpem. Ḿbʋ́ pɛwɛɛ nɛ palakɩ ɖama mbʋ́ pɩkpaɣ ɛyáa fɛyɛ yɔ nɛ pamʋʋ pa-nʋmɔʋ lebu kɩhɛyʋʋ ŋgʋ́ pʋmʋnaa yɔ.

eng: Since they didnt think it was worthwhile to acknowledge God, God abandoned them to a defective mind to do inappropriate things.
local: Pɔtɔsɔɔlɩ se patɩlɩ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ yebi-wɛ nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lɩmaɣza kɩdɛkɛdɛɛ nɛ palakɩ mbʋ́ pɩtamʋna yɔ.

eng: So they were filled with all injustice, wicked behavior, greed, and evil behavior. They are full of jealousy, murder, fighting, deception, and malice. They are gossips.
local: Ḿbʋ́ suni pa-taa ɖo. Ḿbʋ́ lɛ: mbʋ́ pɩtatʋʋzɩ yɔ pɩtɩŋa nɛ kɩdɛkɛdɩm nɛ ɛsɛ naɣnaɣ nɛ kañatʋ nɛ ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ nɛ ɛyáa kʋʋ nɛ eyoziye nɛ ɛyáa cɛtʋʋ nɛ ɛzaɣ́tʋ nɛ ɛyáa tɔm yɔɔdʋʋ.

eng: they slander people, and they hate God. They are rude and proud, and they brag. They invent ways to be evil, and they are disobedient to their parents.
local: Pɛwɛɛkɩɣ ɖama hɩla. Pɛkɛ Ɛsɔ koyindinaa, pa-paa fɛyɩnɩ nɔɔyʋ, paasɩɣ pa-tɩ pʋ-cɔlɔ, pakpazɩɣ pa-tɩ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ. Pañɩnɩɣ nʋmɔŋ kɩfaŋ weyi patɩŋ nɛ pɛtɛzɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩm labʋ yɔ nɛ paañaŋ pa-ñɩma.

eng: They are without understanding, disloyal, without affection, and without mercy.
local: Pa-ñʋŋ taa fɛyɩ, paalakɩ mbʋ́ paɖʋwa se palakɩ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ pa-laŋa wɛ kpɩzɩŋ nɛ paanaɣ lɛlaa pʋtɔdɩyɛ.

eng: Though they know Gods decision that those who persist in such practices deserve death, they not only keep doing these things but also approve others who practice them.
local: Ŋgʋ́, pasɩm paɣtʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa yɔ ɖeu. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: mbá palakɩ lakasɩ ńzɩ́ yɔ, pʋmʋnaa se pakʋ-wɛ. Pɩtasɩɩnɩ se pɛwɛɛ palakɩ lakasɩ ńzɩ́, ɛlɛ́, pɛwɛɛ patɩnɩɣ ɖɔɖɔ mbá palakɩ-sɩ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: So whats the advantage of being a Jew? Or whats the benefit of circumcision?.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ ɛyʋ ɛkɛ Yuda tʋ, ɛwɛnɩ kɛzɛɣa nakɛyɛ yaa we? Pɛlʋʋ ñɛwɛ wazaɣ nakɛyɛ yaa we?.

eng: Plenty in every way. First of all, the Jews were trusted with Gods revelations.
local: Kɛzɛɣa sɔsɔɔ nakɛyɛ wɛ pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa siŋŋ. Kajalaɣ ñɩŋga lɛ se Yuda ñɩma nesi taa Ɛsɔ ɖʋzɩ ɛ-tɔm.

eng: What does it matter, then, if some werent faithful? Their lack of faith wont cancel Gods faithfulness, will it?.
local: Yee pa-taa nabɛyɛ ɛtɔɖɔ tɩ-yɔɔ lɛ, pɩwɩlɩɣ se Ɛsɔ ɛɛlakɩ mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ yɔ yaa we?.

eng: Absolutely not! God must be true, even if every human being is a liar, as it is written: So that it can show that you are right in your words; and you will triumph when you are judged. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́! Pʋwɛɛ se paa weyi ɛtɩlɩ se paa ɛyáa tɩŋa ɛcɛtɩɣ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ñɛkɛ toovenim tʋ. Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: «Pʋwɛɛ se patɩlɩ-ŋ se ŋkɛ toovenim tʋ ñɔ-yɔɔdaɣ taa, nɛ yee pahʋʋnɩ-ŋ yɔ, ŋwakɩ.».

eng: But if our lack of righteousness confirms Gods justice, what will we say? That God, who brings wrath upon us, isnt just ?.
local: Mbʋ́ ɖɩlakɩ nɛ pɩtatʋʋzɩ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ wɩlɩɣnɩ se Ɛsɔ tʋʋzaa. Yee ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩyɔɔdɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ lɛlaa yɔɔdʋʋ se Ɛsɔ tatʋʋzɩ yɔ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛhɔŋ ɖa-nɩŋgbaaŋ yɔ yaa we?.

eng: Absolutely not! If God werent just, how could he judge the world?.
local: Toovenim lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́! Yee ɛtatʋʋzɩ lɛ, ɛzɩma ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛhʋʋnɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa tɔm yɔ?.

eng: But if Gods truth is demonstrated by my lie and it increases his glory, why am I still judged as a sinner?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee mɛn-cɛtɩm ɛwɩlɩɣnɩ Ɛsɔ toovenim kaŋŋ nɛ piyeki se pasam-ɩ lɛ, ɛbɛ yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛɛ se pahʋʋnɩ-m nɛ pakʋ mɔn-tɔm ɛzɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖʋ yɔ?.

eng: Why not say, Lets do evil things so that good things will come out of it?.
local: Yee pɩɩkɛ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pʋbʋ ɖɩɩyɔɔdaa se: «Đɩla kɩdɛkɛdɩm nɛ pɩlɩɩ-ɖʋ kɩbandʋ.» Ḿbʋ́ mbá pɛcɛtɩɣnɩ ɖa-taa yɔ pɔtɔŋ se ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ. Ɛsɔ kɔŋ pɩtɛ ɛyáa ḿbá hʋnaʋ nɛ ɛkʋ pɔ-tɔm. Ḿbʋ́ pʋmʋnaa se pɛtɛmna.

eng: So what are we saying? Are we better off? Not at all. We have already stated the charge: both Jews and Greeks are all under the power of sin.
local: Se we pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩyɔɔdɩ yɔ? Đa Yuda ñɩma ɖakɩlɩnɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ yaa we? Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́ kpem! Maatɛm yɔɔdʋʋ se Yuda ñɩma nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, patɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm nesi taa.

eng: As it is written, There is no righteous person, not even one. .
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: «Nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ se ɛtʋʋzaa, paa kʋɖʋm maɣmaɣ!.

eng: There is no one who understands. There is no one who looks for God. .
local: Nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ se ɛwɛ lɛɣtʋ. Nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ ɖɔɖɔ se ɛñɩnɩɣ Ɛsɔ.

eng: They all turned away. They have become worthless together. There is no one who shows kindness. There is not even one. .
local: Patɩŋa payɩ pelebi nʋmɔʋ, patɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛɛkaa. Nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ se ɛlakɩ kɩbandʋ, paa kʋɖʋm maɣmaɣ!.

eng: Their throat is a grave that has been opened. They are deceitful with their tongues, and the poison of vipers is under their lips. .
local: Pokuli pɔ-nɔsɩ yɔ, pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pɩlaʋ ŋgʋ́ pokuli kʋ-nɔɔ yɔ. Pa-nzʋlʋmɛɛ pepeɣziɣni ɛyáa. Tɔm ndʋ́ pɔyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ, tɩkʋʋ ɛyáa ɛzɩ ɖʋmaa sɔtʋ yɔ.

eng: Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness. .
local: Ɛzaɣ́tʋ nɛ kpalʋʋ wɩlʋʋ tɔm posuni pɔ-nɔsɩ taa ɖo.

eng: Their feet are quick to shed blood; .
local: Pɛwɛ kpekpeka ɛyáa ɖɩkʋyɛ.

eng: destruction and misery are in their ways; .
local: Paa le patalaa yɔ, pɛwɛɛkɩɣ wondu nɛ peyele pɩnɩɩ ɛyáa siŋŋ.

eng: and they dont know the way of peace. .
local: Paasɩŋ mbʋ́ paala nɛ pa nɛ lɛlaa pɛwɛɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa yɔ.

eng: There is no fear of God in their view of the world. .
local: Pa-paa fɛyɩnɩ Ɛsɔ pe-wezuu caɣʋ taa.».

eng: Now we know that whatever the Law says, it speaks to those who are under the Law, in order to shut every mouth and make it so the whole world has to answer to God.
local: Đɩnawa se mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ paɖʋ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ taa yɔ, mbá pasɩm-tʋ yɔ, ḿbá paɖʋ-tʋ. Pʋwɛ ḿbʋ́ se nɔɔyʋ ɛtaapɩzɩ ehiɣ ndʋ́ ɛwana yɔ nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa tɩlɩ se payʋsɩ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa.

eng: It follows that no human being will be treated as righteous in his presence by doing what the Law says, because the knowledge of sin comes through the Law.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɛɛpɩsɩɣ nɔɔyʋ kʋtʋʋzʋ se ɛɖɔm paɣtʋ yɔɔ camɩyɛ. Ɛlɛ́, paɣtʋ yekina nɛ ɛyʋ tɩlɩ se ɛlabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm.

eng: But now Gods righteousness has been revealed apart from the Law, which is confirmed by the Law and the Prophets.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ Ɛsɔ wɩlɩ-ɖʋ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛpɩsɩɣ-ɖʋ kʋtʋʋzaa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtɛkɛ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ yɔɔ ɛtɩŋnaa. Pama pʋ-tɔm paɣtʋ nɛ náyaa pa-takayɩsɩ taa.

eng: Gods righteousness comes through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ for all who have faith in him. Theres no distinction.
local: Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: mbá patɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ pɩsɩɣ-wɛ kʋtʋʋzaa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɛfɛyɩ ndɩ nɛ ɖama:.

eng: All have sinned and fall short of Gods glory.
local: ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ labɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm, paakaɣ naʋ Ɛsɔ koboyaɣ kaʋ.

eng: but all are treated as righteous freely by his grace because of a ransom that was paid by Christ Jesus.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ lakɩ-wɛ lootiye nɛ ɛpɩsɩ-wɛ kʋtʋʋzaa falaa yem. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Krɩstʋ Yesu ha ɛ-tɩ nɛ pɩya pa-ñʋʋ.

eng: Through his faithfulness, God displayed Jesus as the place of sacrifice where mercy is found by means of his blood. He did this to demonstrate his righteousness in passing over sins that happened before.
local: Ɛnʋ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa se pɛkpɛdɩ ɛ-calɩm nɛ pɩtɩŋnɩ pʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛñalɩ mbá patɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm. Ḿbʋ́ ɛwɩlɩnɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛtʋʋzaa yɔ. Ɛɖɔkɩ ɛ-hɔyɛ ɖooo lɔŋ nɛ ɛtɔhɔ mbá paalabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ. Ɛñɩnɩɣ se ɛwɩlɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛtʋʋzaa yɔ alɩwaatʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-taa nɛ paa weyi ɛsɩm-ɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛkpɛndɩ ɛpɩsɩ weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ yɔ kʋtʋʋzʋ.

eng: What happens to our bragging? Its thrown out. With which law? With what we have accomplished under the Law?.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛbɛ yɔɔ ɛyáa kʋndʋʋ yɔ? Nabʋyʋ fɛyɩ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛbɛ yɔɔ kpem? Papɩzɩɣ pakʋndɩ se pɔɖɔŋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ yaa we? Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́! Ɛlɛ́, pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ yɔɔ papɩzɩɣ pakʋndɩ.

eng: No, not at all, but through the law of faith. We consider that a person is treated as righteous by faith, apart from what is accomplished under the Law.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩnawa se lidaʋ yɔɔ Ɛsɔ pɩsɩɣ ɛyʋ kʋtʋʋzʋ. Pɩtɛkɛ paɣtʋ yɔɔ ɖɔm yɔɔ.

eng: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isnt God the God of Gentiles also? Yes, God is also the God of Gentiles.
local: Ɛsɔ wɛʋ yɔ, Yuda ñɩma ɖeke Ɛsɔ ɛkɛnaa yaa we? Ɛtɛkɛ ɖɔɖɔ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɛ-Ɛsɔ yaa we? Toovenim lɛ, ɛkɛ ɖɔɖɔ pa-ñɩnʋ.

eng: Since God is one, then the one who makes the circumcised righteous by faith will also make the one who isnt circumcised righteous through faith.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kʋɖʋm koŋ wɛna. Ɛpɩsɩɣ Yuda ñɩma kʋtʋʋzaa lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ pɛwɛna yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ ɛpɩsɩɣ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ kʋtʋʋzaa lidaʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: Do we then cancel the Law through this faith? Absolutely not! Instead, we confirm the Law.
local: Pɩwɩlɩɣ se lidaʋ yɔɔ lɛ, paɣtʋ tɛkɛ pʋyʋ yaa we? Toovenim lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́! Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ yɔ, ɖɩwɩlɩɣ mbʋ́ paɣtʋ kɛnaa kpem yɔ.

eng: Therefore, since we have been made righteous through his faithfulness, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Đe-lidaʋ yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ Ɛsɔ pɩsɩ-ɖʋ kʋtʋʋzaa. Pʋ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnɩ ɖa nɛ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ ɖɛ-kpɛndʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɖa nɛ ɩ pʋwɛɛ leleŋ.

eng: We have access by faith into this grace in which we stand through him, and we boast in the hope of Gods glory.
local: Ɛ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ ɖɩpɩzɩ ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ nɛ pɩha-ɖʋ waɖɛ se ɖɩtɩlɩ lootiye nɖɩ́ ɛlabɩ-ɖʋ nɛ ɖɩhaɣ-ɖʋ ɖoŋ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩkʋndʋʋ nɛ ɖɩtaɣ liu se ɖa nɛ Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ ɖɩkɔŋ wɛʋ e-koboyaɣ taa.

eng: But not only that! We even take pride in our problems, because we know that trouble produces endurance.
local: Halɩ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ kʋñɔŋ taa maɣmaɣ, ɖɩkʋndʋʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩnawa se kɩ-taa ɛyʋ kpɛlɩkɩɣ ɛ-hɔyɛ ɖɔkʋʋ.

eng: endurance produces character, and character produces hope.
local: Ɛɖɔkʋʋ ɛ-hɔyɛ lɛ, ɛpɩɩ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa. Ɛpɩɣ lɛ, ɛsʋʋ liu taʋ yɔɔ.

eng: This hope doesnt put us to shame, because the love of God has been poured out in our hearts through the Holy Spirit, who has been given to us.
local: Ŋgʋ́, liu taʋ ḿbʋ́, piipeɣziɣ-ɖʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ tɩŋnɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ŋgʋ́ ɛha-ɖʋ yɔ kɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛɖʋ ɛ-sɔɔlɩm ɖa-taa.

eng: While we were still weak, at the right moment, Christ died for ungodly people.
local: Toovenim lɛ, Krɩstʋ sɩba mbá ɖa ɖa-paa fɛyɩnɩ Ɛsɔ yɔ ɖɔ-yɔɔ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa yɔ. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, ɖɩtatɩɩwɛnɩdɩ ɖoŋ nɩɩyɩ.

eng: It isnt often that someone will die for a righteous person, though maybe someone might dare to die for a good person.
local: Paa ɛyʋ ɛtʋʋzaa yɔ, pʋwɛ kaɖɛ siŋŋ se nɔɔyʋ etisi nɛ ɛsɩ ɛ-yɔɔ. Pʋtɔmaɣ yɔ, ɛyʋ kɩbanʋ yɔɔ nɔɔyʋ pɩzɩɣ etisi nɛ ɛsɩ.

eng: But God shows his love for us, because while we were still sinners Christ died for us.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ ñawɩlɩ-ɖʋ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛsɔɔlɩ-ɖʋ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩɩkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa yɔ eyeba nɛ Krɩstʋ sɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: So, now that we have been made righteous by his blood, we can be even more certain that we will be saved from Gods wrath through him.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, yee Ɛsɔ ɛtɩŋnɩ ɛ́lɛ́ calɩm kpɛndʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ-ɖʋ kʋtʋʋzaa lɛ, ɛzɩma ɛɛyakɩ ɖa-ñʋʋ se ɛ-pana ɛtaatasɩ-ɖʋ kpanaʋ yɔ?.

eng: If we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son while we were still enemies, now that we have been reconciled, how much more certain is it that we will be saved by his life?.
local: Đɩɩkɛ Ɛsɔ koyindinaa. Ɛlɛ́, eyele nɛ pɩtɩŋnɩ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ sɩm yɔɔ nɛ ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa ñɔɔzɩ. Yee ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa ɛñɔɔzaa lɛ, ɛzɩma ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛtɩŋnɩ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ wezuu yɔɔ nɛ ɛya ɖa-ñʋʋ yɔ?.

eng: And not only that: we even take pride in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, the one through whom we now have a restored relationship with God.
local: Pɩtasɩɩnɩ pi-ɖeke, ɛlɛ́, ɖɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩkʋndʋʋnɩ Ɛsɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ ɖa nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa ñɔɔzɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ.

eng: Just as through one human being sin came into the world, and death came through sin, so death has come to everyone, since everyone has sinned.
local: Ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm koŋ yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm sʋʋ kedeŋa taa: ɛnʋ lɛ Aɖam. Sɩm ñatɩŋnɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ nɛ pʋbʋ ñasʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ pʋkʋʋ ɛyáa tɩŋa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, patɩŋa payɩ palabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm.

eng: Although sin was in the world, since there was no Law, it wasnt taken into account until the Law came.
local: Pʋcɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ cɛlɩ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ lɛ, kɩwɛɛkɩm kɛtɛm wɛʋ kedeŋa yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ takpaɣ-pʋ ɛ́yáa pʋyʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ paɣtʋ fɛyɩ yɔ.

eng: But death ruled from Adam until Moses, even over those who didnt sin in the same way Adam did—Adam was a type of the one who was coming.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, kpaɣ Aɖam alɩwaatʋ nɛ puwolo pɩsɩɩnɩ Mooyiizi ñɩndʋ lɛ, sɩm ñaawɛɛ pʋkʋʋ ɛyáa. Halɩ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ mbá patala kɩwɛɛkɩm mbʋ́ Aɖam weyi ɛtɔɖɔ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ kaɖʋwa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛlaba yɔ. Aɖam ɛnʋ lɛ weyi pʋwɛɛ se ɛkɔɔ yɔ ɛ-lɛzʋʋ.

eng: But the free gift of Christ isnt like Adams failure. If many people died through what one person did wrong, Gods grace is multiplied even more for many people with the gift—of the one person Jesus Christ—that comes through grace.
local: Ɛlɛ́, kɩwɛɛkɩm ḿbʋ́, paatɩɩkpakɩɣ-pʋ se pɛkɛdɩɣnɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ha-ɖʋ falaa yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee pɩtɩŋnɩ ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm koŋ kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ nɛ sakɩyɛ sɩkɩ lɛ, ɛzɩma Ɛsɔ lootiye nɛ kʋjɔʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛtɩŋnɩ ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm koŋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛlabɩ-ɖʋ falaa yɔ, paapɩzɩɣ pɛwɛɛ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ cɔlɔ siŋŋ yɔ? Ɛyʋ ɛnʋ lɛ Yesu Krɩstʋ.

eng: The gift isnt like the consequences of one persons sin. The judgment that came from one persons sin led to punishment, but the free gift that came out of many failures led to the verdict of acquittal.
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se kɩwɛɛkɩm mbʋ́ ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm koŋ laba yɔ, paatɩɩkpakɩɣ-pʋ se pɛkɛdɩɣnɩ Ɛsɔ kʋjɔʋ ŋ́gʋ́. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kɩwɛɛkɩm kʋɖʋmʋm koŋ yɔɔ lɛ, pahʋʋnɩ ɛyʋ nɛ pakʋ ɛ-tɔm. Ŋgʋ́, mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ haɣ ɛyʋ falaa ɛ-kɩwɛɛkɩm sakɩyɛ wayɩ yɔ, piyeki nɛ ɛpɩsɩ-ɩ kʋtʋʋzʋ.

eng: If death ruled because of one persons failure, those who receive the multiplied grace and the gift of righteousness will even more certainly rule in life through the one person Jesus Christ.
local: Yee ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm koŋ kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ sɩm tɔɔ kewiyaɣ lɛ, ɛzɩma pɩɩpɩzɩɣ pɩtɩŋnɩ Yesu Krɩstʋ ɖeke koŋ yɔɔ nɛ mbá Ɛsɔ lakɩ-wɛ lootiye siŋŋ nɛ ɛpɩsɩɣ-wɛ kʋtʋʋzaa falaa yɔ, paba ñacaɣ wezuu nɛ pɔtɔɔ kewiyaɣ yɔ?.

eng: So what are we going to say? Should we continue sinning so grace will multiply?.
local: Se we ɖɩkaɣ yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ? Đɩwɛɣ kɩwɛɛkɩm labʋ yɔɔ ḿbʋ́ se Ɛsɔ lootiye ɛtɛzɩ ɖɔʋ naa we?.

eng: So now the righteous requirements necessary for life are met for everyone through the righteous act of one person, just as judgment fell on everyone through the failure of one person.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋnɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm kʋɖʋmʋm koŋ yɔɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ hʋʋnɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa nɛ ɛkʋ pɔ-tɔm yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ lakasɩ kɩbanzɩ kʋɖʋmsɩ koŋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ kʋtʋʋzaa nɛ pehiɣ wezuu.

eng: Absolutely not! All of us died to sin. How can we still live in it?.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́ kpem! Mbá ɖa ɖɩwɛ ɛzɩ sɩɖaa nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm tatasɩ wɛnaʋ ɖoŋ ɖɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛzɩma ɖɩpɩzɩɣ ɖɩwɛɛ pɩ-taa ɖɔɖɔ yɔ?.

eng: Many people were made righteous through the obedience of one person, just as many people were made sinners through the disobedience of one person.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋnɩ ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm koŋ kaañamtʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ pɩsɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm koŋ ñamtʋ yɔɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ pɩsɩ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kʋtʋʋzaa.

eng: Or dont you know that all who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?.
