local: Lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ kpɛndɩ ɖa nɛ Krɩstʋ Yesu. Sɩ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩkpɛndɩ ɛ-sɩm taa. Mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩtatɩlɩ ḿbʋ́ yaa we?.

eng: The Law stepped in to amplify the failure, but where sin increased, grace multiplied even more.
local: Paɖʋ paɣtʋ lɛ, kɩwɛɛkɩm tɛzɩ ɖɔʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɖenɖe kɩwɛɛkɩm tɛzɩ ɖɔʋ yɔ, lootiye ñɔɖɔwa nɛ pɩkɩlɩ.

eng: Therefore, we were buried together with him through baptism into his death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too can walk in newness of life.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ palabɩ-ɖʋ-sɩ yɔ, pɩwɩlɩɣ se ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩsɩbɩna nɛ pepim-ɖʋ. Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se ɖa ɖɔɖɔ ɖihiɣ wezuu caɣʋ kɩfam ɛzɩ pɩtɩŋnaʋ Caa ɖoŋ taa nɛ Krɩstʋ fe nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa yɔ.

eng: The result is that grace will rule through Gods righteousness, leading to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord, just as sin ruled in death.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋnɩ sɩm yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩwɛɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm nesi taa yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ Ɛsɔ tʋʋzʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩwɛɛ e-nesi taa se pɩtɩŋnɩ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ lootiye yɔɔ nɛ ɖihiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ.

eng: So what are we going to say? Are we going to find that Abraham is our ancestor on the basis of genealogy?.
local: Đɩyɔɔdʋʋ ɖɔ-cɔzɔ Abraham tɔm se we yɔ? Ɛbɛ ehiɣ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ?.

eng: If we were united together in a death like his, we will also be united together in a resurrection like his.
local: Yee ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩkpɛndɩ ɛ-sɩm taa yɔ, ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩkpɛndɩɣ ɖɔɖɔ ɖife.

eng: Because if Abraham was made righteous because of his actions, he would have had a reason to brag, but not in front of God.
local: Toovenim lɛ, yee Ɛsɔ ɛɛpɩsɩ Abraham kʋtʋʋzʋ tʋma wena ɛlaba yɔ a-yɔɔ yɔ, pʋbʋ ɛɛkʋndaa. Ŋgʋ́, pɩtamʋnɩ ḿbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa.

eng: This is what we know: the person that we used to be was crucified with him in order to get rid of the corpse that had been controlled by sin. That way we wouldnt be slaves to sin anymore.
local: Đɩsɩm ɖeu se: wɛtʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩɩwɛna ɖooo lɔŋ yɔ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pakam Krɩstʋ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ yɔ, pɛkpɛndɩnɩ-tʋ nɛ paka. Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se ɖɛ-wɛtʋ ńdʋ́, tɩtaatasɩ wɛnaʋ ɖoŋ ɖɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩtasɩ kɛʋ kɩwɛɛkɩm yomaa.

eng: What does the scripture say? Abraham had faith in God, and it was credited to him as righteousness .
local: Ɛlɛ́, pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Abraham wɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ-ɩ se ɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ.».

eng: because a person who has died has been freed from sins power.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛsɩba yɔ, ɛlɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm nesi taa.

eng: Workers salaries arent credited to them on the basis of an employers grace but rather on the basis of what they deserve.
local: Ŋgʋ́, weyi ɛlabɩ tʋmɩyɛ nɛ pɛhɛyɩ-ɩ yɔ, paatɔŋ se kʋjɔʋ palabɩ-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛ-kɩmɩyɛ pɛhɛyɩ-ɩ.

eng: But if we died with Christ, we have faith that we will also live with him.
local: Ŋgʋ́, yee ɖa nɛ Krɩstʋ ɖɩsɩbɩna yɔ, ɖɩtaɣ liu se ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩkɔŋ wɛʋ wezuu ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: But faith is credited as righteousness to those who dont work, because they have faith in God who makes the ungodly righteous.
local: Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ñaalakɩ naɖɩyɛ se papɩsɩ-ɩ kʋtʋʋzʋ, ɛlɛ́, ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛpɩsɩɣ kʋtʋʋzʋ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ pɩsɩɣnɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa kʋtʋʋzaa.

eng: We know that Christ has been raised from the dead and he will never die again. Death no longer has power over him.
local: Đɩnawa se Krɩstʋ weyi ɛsɩba nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa yɔ, ɛɛtasɩɣ sɩbʋ ɖɔɖɔ. Sɩm tatasɩ wɛnaʋ ɖoŋ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: In the same way, David also pronounces a blessing on the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from actions:.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Daaviidi yɔɔdɩ weyi Ɛsɔ pɩsɩ-ɩ kʋtʋʋzʋ yɔ ɛ-tɩ hiɣu tɔm. Ŋgʋ́, ɛ́lɛ́ taakpaɣ ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ nɛ ɛ́yáa pʋyʋ. Ɛyɔɔdaa se:.

eng: He died to sin once and for all with his death, but he lives for God with his life.
local: Ɛsɩbʋ yɔ, ɛsɩba kpaagbaa sɩbʋ se kɩwɛɛkɩm ɛsɩɩ tɩnaɣ. Ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɛwɛʋ wezuu yɔ, ɛwɛ-kʋ se pɩkɛdɩnɩ Ɛsɔ.

eng: Happy are those whose actions outside the Law are forgiven, and whose sins are covered. .
local: «Mbá pɛwɛ koboyaɣ taa yɔ, ḿbá yɔ: mbá payʋsaa nɛ Ɛsɔ kpeɣ-wɛ nɛ ehuuzi pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: In the same way, you also should consider yourselves dead to sin but alive for God in Christ Jesus.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩkpaɣ mɩ-tɩ se ɩwɛ ɛzɩ sɩɖaa yɔ nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm tatasɩ wɛnaʋ ɖoŋ mɩ-yɔɔ. Ɩkpaɣ mɩ-tɩ ɖɔɖɔ se ɩwɛ wezuu mɩ nɛ Krɩstʋ Yesu mɩ-kpɛndʋʋ taa se pɩkɛdɩnɩ Ɛsɔ.

eng: Happy are those whose sin isnt counted against them by the Lord .
local: Weyi ɛwɛ koboyaɣ taa yɔ, ɛnʋ yɔ: Kɩpaɣlʋ takpaɣ ɛ-kɩwɛɛkɩm nɛ ɛ́yáa pʋyʋ.».

eng: So then, dont let sin rule your body, so that you do what it wants.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ se mi-tomnasɩ ɛtasɩ wɛʋ kɩwɛɛkɩm nesi taa nɛ piyele nɛ ɩla mbʋ́ sɩsɔɔlaa yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, sɩsɩkɩ.

eng: Is this state of happiness only for the circumcised or is it also for those who arent circumcised? We say, Faith was credited to Abraham as righteousness.
local: Yuda ñɩma koboyaɣ ɖeke tɔm pɔyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ yaa we? Yaa kedeŋa yɔɔ ɛyáa lɛlaa pɩzɩɣ pehiɣ koboyaɣ ŋ́gá ɖɔɖɔ? Ɖɩyɔɔdɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ se: «Abraham wɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔɔ ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ-ɩ se ɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ.».

eng: Dont offer parts of your body to sin, to be used as weapons to do wrong. Instead, present yourselves to God as people who have been brought back to life from the dead, and offer all the parts of your body to God to be used as weapons to do right.
local: Ɩtaakpaɣ mi-pilinzi nɛ ɩlabɩnɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm ɛzɩ mbʋ́ palakɩnɩ mbʋ́ pɩtatʋʋzɩ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩkpaɣ mɩ-tɩ nɛ ɩcɛlɩ Ɛsɔ ɛzɩ mbá pasɩba nɛ pefe yɔ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɩkpaɣ mi-pilinzi ńzɩ́ nɛ ɩcɛlɩ-ɩ ɛzɩ mbʋ́ palakɩnɩ mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ.

eng: So how was it credited? When he was circumcised, or when he wasnt circumcised? In fact, it was credited while he still wasnt circumcised, not after he was circumcised.
local: Ɛzɩma ɛɛwɛ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ kpaɣ-ɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ? Paapɛlɩ-ɩ yaa we? Aayɩ, pataapɛlɩ-ɩ nɛ pʋcɔ.

eng: Sin will have no power over you, because you arent under Law but under grace.
local: Pɩfɛyɩ se ɩtasɩ wɛʋ kɩwɛɛkɩm nesi taa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paɣtʋ tatasɩ wɛnaʋ ɖoŋ mɩ-yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ lootiye ɖoŋ taa ɩwɛɛ.

eng: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that comes from the faith he had while he still wasnt circumcised. It happened this way so that Abraham could be the ancestor of all those people who arent circumcised, who have faith in God, and so are counted as righteous.
local: Pʋwayɩ pɛpɛlɩ-ɩ nɛ pɩkɛnɩ-ɩ yʋsaɣ: ŋ́gá wɩlɩɣna se Ɛsɔ kpaɣ-ɩ se ɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛɛwɛna nɛ pʋcɔ pɛpɛlɩ-ɩ yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ. Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ Abraham kɛ mbá pɛtɛpɛlɩ-wɛ nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ-wɛ se pɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzaa yɔ patɩŋa payɩ pɔ-cɔzɔ.

eng: So what? Should we sin because we arent under Law but under grace? Absolutely not!.
local: Se we ɖɩkaɣ yɔɔdʋʋ kpem yɔ? Paɣtʋ tatasɩ ɖoŋ wɛnaʋ ɖɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩwɛɛ Ɛsɔ lootiye taa yɔɔ pɩha-ɖʋ nʋmɔʋ se ɖɩla kɩwɛɛkɩm naa we? Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́ kpem!.

eng: He could also be the ancestor of those circumcised people, who arent only circumcised but who also walk in the path of faith, like our ancestor Abraham did while he wasnt circumcised.
local: Ɛkɛ ɖɔɖɔ mbá pɛpɛlɩ-wɛ yɔ pɔ-cɔzɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtasɩnɩ pɛ-pɛlʋʋ ɖeke, pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ ɛzɩ ɖɔ-cɔzɔ Abraham kɛwɛnaʋ nɛ pʋcɔ pɛpɛlɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: Dont you know that if you offer yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, that you are slaves of the one whom you obey? Thats true whether you serve as slaves of sin, which leads to death, or as slaves of the kind of obedience that leads to righteousness.
local: Ɩtatɩlɩ se yee ɩkpaɣ mɩ-tɩ nɛ ɩcɛlɩ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɩkɛ e-yomaa nɛ ɩñaŋ-ɩ yɔ, pʋbʋ, ɩkɛ e-yomaa kpem yaa we? Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm yomaa yaa Ɛsɔ yomaa. Ɛlɛ́, yee ɛyʋ ɛkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm yom yɔ, puwokini-i sɩm taa. Yee ɛkɛ Ɛsɔ yom yɔ, piyeki nɛ ɛla mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ.

eng: The promise to Abraham and to his descendants, that he would inherit the world, didnt come through the Law but through the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Toovenim lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ paɣtʋ yɔɔ ɖɔm yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ Ɛsɔ heyi Abraham nɛ ɛ-sayɩnaa se pɔkɔŋ tɩɣyʋʋ kedeŋa kpeekpe. Ɛlɛ́, ɛwɛʋnɩ lidaʋ nɛ Ɛsɔ kpaɣ-ɩ se ɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ yɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa.

eng: But thank God that although you used to be slaves of sin, you gave wholehearted obedience to the teaching that was handed down to you, which provides a pattern.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩsa Ɛsɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mbá mɩ ɩɩkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm yomaa yɔ, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɩmʋ tɔm ndʋ́ pawɩlɩ-mɩ yɔ nesi naalɛ nɛ ɩɖɔŋ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: If they inherit because of the Law, then faith has no effect and the promise has been canceled.
local: Yee mbá pɔɖɔŋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ se Ɛsɔ ɛkpaɣ-wɛ se pɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzaa yɔ ɛɖʋwa se patɩɣyɩ-kɛ yɔ, pʋbʋ pɩɩwɩlaa se lidaʋ nɛ mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ yɔ, pɩɩwazɩɣ pʋyʋ.

eng: Now that you have been set free from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness.
local: Puholi-mɩ nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm nesi taa nɛ ɩpɩsɩ mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ pu-yomaa.

eng: The Law brings about wrath. But when there isnt any law, there isnt any violation of the law.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paɣtʋ kulusuu Ɛsɔ pana. Ɛlɛ́, ɖenɖe tɩfɛyɩ yɔ, paatɔŋ se pɔtɔɖɔ tɩ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Once, you offered the parts of your body to be used as slaves to impurity and to lawless behavior that leads to still more lawless behavior. Now, you should present the parts of your body as slaves to righteousness, which makes your lives holy.
local: Ɩɩnɩɣ tɔm taa lɔŋ yɔɔ mankpakɩɣ mbʋ́ ɛyáa lakɩ yɔ se malɩzɩnɩ-mɩ pɩ-taa. Đooo lɛ, iiyeba nɛ mi-pilinzi pɩsɩ mbʋ́ pipisiɣ ɛyáa yɔ nɛ kɩdɛkɛdɩm po-yomaa nɛ ɩtɛzɩ pɩsʋʋ kɩdɛkɛdɩm laɖaa kpem. Ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, iyebi sɩpɩsɩ mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ pu-yomaa nɛ ɩtɛzɩ pɩsʋʋ Ɛsɔ ñɩma kpem.

eng: Thats why the inheritance comes through faith, so that it will be on the basis of Gods grace. In that way, the promise is secure for all of Abrahams descendants, not just for those who are related by Law but also for those who are related by the faith of Abraham, who is the father of all of us.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ɖɩwɛnɩ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ, kʋ-yɔɔ ɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ-ɖʋ pʋyʋ. Ɛlakɩ-ɖʋ-pʋ falaa se pʋwazɩ Abraham sayɩnaa tɩŋa payɩ. Pɩtɛkɛ mbá pɛcɛlɩ-wɛ paɣtʋ yɔ, pe-ɖeke pʋwazɩɣ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩkpɛndɩnɩ mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ɖɔ-cɔzɔ Abraham yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ lɛ ɖatɩŋa payɩ ɖɔ-cɔzɔ.

eng: When you were slaves of sin, you were free from the control of righteousness.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩɩkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm yomaa yɔ, pɩtaakɛnɩ-mɩ kɩcɛyʋʋ se ɩla mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ.

eng: As it is written: I have appointed you to be the father of many nations. So Abraham is our father in the eyes of God in whom he had faith, the God who gives life to the dead and calls things that dont exist into existence.
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: «Malɩzɩnɩ-ŋ se ŋkɛ ajɛya ndɩ ndɩ taa ñɩma cɔzɔ.» Ɛkɛ ɖɔ-cɔzɔ Ɛsɔ weyi ɛwɛnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ lidaʋ yɔ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa. Ɛnʋ feziɣni sɩɖaa nɛ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtaawɛɛ yɔ, pʋwɛɣ.

eng: What consequences did you get from doing things that you are now ashamed of? The outcome of those things is death.
local: Lakasɩ nzɩ́ ɩlaba nɛ sɩkpaɣ-mɩ fɛyɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, pɩlɩ-mɩ we sɩ-taa? Siwokini-mɩ sɩm taa!.

eng: When it was beyond hope, he had faith in the hope that he would become the father of many nations, in keeping with the promise God spoke to him: Thats how many descendants you will have. .
local: Abraham kɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ nɛ ɛɖaŋ nɛ ɛkɔɔ ɛkɛ ajɛya ndɩ ndɩ taa ñɩma cɔzɔ ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ keheyuu-i yɔ. Eeheyi-i se: «Ḿbʋ́ ña-sayɩnaa ɖɔʋ.».

eng: But now that you have been set free from sin and become slaves to God, you have the consequence of a holy life, and the outcome is eternal life.
local: Ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, Ɛsɔ holi-mɩ nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm nesi taa nɛ ɩpɩsɩ e-yomaa. Mbʋ́ pɩlɩ-mɩ pɩ-taa yɔ lɛ se ɩpɩsɩ ɛ-ñɩma nɛ pɩtɛmna se ihikiɣ wezuu maatɛŋ.

eng: Without losing faith, Abraham, who was nearly years old, took into account his own body, which was as good as dead, and Sarahs womb, which was dead.
local: Abraham kɛwɛna ɛzɩ pɩnzɩ mɩnʋʋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtaalabɩ-ɩ ɛjandʋ e-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ taa, ŋgʋ́, ɛɛñɔtɩnɩ pɩlaʋ kpam. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɛɛnawa se ɛ-halʋ Saara tapɩzɩɣ se ɛlʋlɩ.

eng: The wages that sin pays are death, but Gods gift is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Kɩwɛɛkɩm kɩhɛyʋʋ lɛ sɩm. Ɛlɛ́, kʋjɔʋ ŋgʋ́ Ɛsɔ lakɩ-ɖʋ falaa yɔ lɛ wezuu maatɛŋ ŋgʋ́ ɖihiɣ ɖa nɛ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Krɩstʋ Yesu ɖɛ-kpɛndʋʋ taa yɔ.

eng: He didnt hesitate with a lack of faith in Gods promise, but he grew strong in faith and gave glory to God.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́, ɛcɔnɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ-ɩ yɔ lɛ, ɛtapʋtɩ. Ɛlɛ́, e-lidaʋ yele nɛ ɛkpa ɖoŋ nɛ ɛsa Ɛsɔ.

eng: He was fully convinced that God was able to do what he promised.
local: Ɛnawa kpem se Ɛsɔ pɩzɩɣ ɛla mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ yɔ.

eng: Therefore, it was credited to him as righteousness.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ-ɩ se ɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ.

eng: But the scripture that says it was credited to him wasnt written only for Abrahams sake.
local: Ɛlɛ́, tɔm ndʋ́ pamawa se: «Ɛsɔ kpaɣ-ɩ se ɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ» yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ e-ɖeke ɛ-yɔɔ pama-tʋ.

eng: It was written also for our sake, because it is going to be credited to us too. It will be credited to those of us who have faith in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead.
local: Mbá ɖa ɛpɩsɩ-ɖʋ kʋtʋʋzaa yɔ, ɖɔ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ pama-tʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ weyi efezi ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu nɛ sɩm taa yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: He was handed over because of our mistakes, and he was raised to meet the requirements of righteousness for us.
local: Đa-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ eyeba nɛ pakʋ-ɩ, ɛlɛ́, efezi-i se ɛpɩsɩ-ɖʋ kʋtʋʋzaa.

eng: So now there isnt any condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
local: Tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩtɩŋa tɩ-yɔɔ lɛ, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ paatasɩɣ kʋʋ mbá pa nɛ Krɩstʋ Yesu palabɩ nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ yɔ pɔ-tɔm kaʋ.

eng: The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɛwɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ŋgʋ́ kɩtɩŋnɩ Krɩstʋ Yesu yɔɔ nɛ kɩhaɣ-ɖʋ wezuu yɔ ki-nesi taa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, puholi-m nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm nɛ sɩm pe-nesi taa.

eng: God has done what was impossible for the Law, since it was weak because of selfishness. God condemned sin in the body by sending his own Son to deal with sin in the same body as humans, who are controlled by sin.
local: Ɛsɔ labɩ mbʋ́ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ tapɩzɩɣ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, wɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-taa ɛyʋ wɛɛ yɔ, tiyebina nɛ paɣtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩfɛyɩ ɖoŋ: Ɛsɔ tɩŋnɩ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ weyi etiyi kedeŋa yɔɔ nɛ ɛwɛnɩ tomnaɣ ɛzɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa ɖa yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ palaa-ɩ nɛ ɛkɔ pʋ-ñʋʋ.

eng: He did this so that the righteous requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us. Now the way we live is based on the Spirit, not based on selfishness.
local: Ɛlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se ɖɩpɩzɩ ɖɩla mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ ɛzɩ paɖʋʋ paɣtʋ taa yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩɩɖɔŋ ɛzɩ ɖe-liu wɛʋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩɖɔŋ ɛzɩ pɩkɛdɩnaʋ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔ.

eng: People whose lives are based on selfishness think about selfish things, but people whose lives are based on the Spirit think about things that are related to the Spirit.
local: Toovenim lɛ, mbá pacakɩ wezuu ɛzɩ pɩkɛdɩnaʋ-wɛ yɔ, panɩɣnɩ mbʋ́ pe-liu wɛɛ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mbá paba ñacakɩ-kʋ ɛzɩ pɩkɛdɩnaʋ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔ, panɩɣnɩ mbʋ́ kaka liu wɛɛ yɔ.

eng: The attitude that comes from selfishness leads to death, but the attitude that comes from the Spirit leads to life and peace.
local: Yee ɛyʋ ɛnɩɣʋnɩ mbʋ́ pɩkɛdɩnɩ ɛ-maɣmaɣ yɔ yɔ, puwokini-i sɩm taa. Ɛlɛ́, ɛnɩɣʋnɩ mbʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu pɔzʋʋ yɔ yɔ, puwokini-i wezuu nɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ pa-taa.

eng: So the attitude that comes from selfishness is hostile to God. It doesnt submit to Gods Law, because it cant.
local: Mbá panɩɣnɩ mbʋ́ pa-maɣmaɣ pe-liu wɛɛ yɔ, pɛkɛ Ɛsɔ koyindinaa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paaɖɔŋ Ɛsɔ paɣtʋ yɔɔ. Halɩ paatɩɩpɩzɩɣ pɔɖɔ tɩ-yɔɔ kpem.

eng: People who are self-centered arent able to please God.
local: Mbá pacakɩ wezuu ɛzɩ pɩkɛdɩnaʋ-wɛ yɔ, pɔ-tɔm ɛɛpɩzɩɣ tɩkɛdɩnɩ Ɛsɔ.

eng: But you arent self-centered. Instead you are in the Spirit, if in fact Gods Spirit lives in you. If anyone doesnt have the Spirit of Christ, they dont belong to him.
local: Mɩ lɛ, ɩɩcakɩ wezuu ɛzɩ pɩkɛdɩnaʋ mɩ-maɣmaɣ yɔ, ɩcakɩ-kʋ ɛzɩ pɩkɛdɩnaʋ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu wɛ mɩ-cɔlɔ. Yee Krɩstʋ Fezuu ɛfɛyɩ nɔɔyʋ cɔlɔ yɔ, ɛtɛkɛ Krɩstʋ tʋ.

eng: If Christ is in you, the Spirit is your life because of Gods righteousness, but the body is dead because of sin.
local: Yee Krɩstʋ ɛwɛ mɩ-cɔlɔ yɔ, paa mi-tomnaɣ ɛtɛŋnɩ sɩm kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ yɔ, mɩ-kalɩzaɣ wɛɣ wezuu. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩpɩsɩ kʋtʋʋzaa Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa.

eng: If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead lives in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your human bodies also, through his Spirit that lives in you.
local: Yee Ɛsɔ weyi efezi Yesu nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa yɔ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɛwɛ mɩ-cɔlɔ lɛ, ɛzɩma ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛtɩŋnɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ŋgʋ́ kɩwɛ mɩ-cɔlɔ yɔ kɩ-yɔɔ nɛ efezi ɖɔɖɔ mi-tomnasɩ nzɩ́ sɩsɩkɩ yɔ?.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we have an obligation, but it isnt an obligation to ourselves to live our lives on the basis of selfishness.
local: Man-taabalaa mɩ, pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pɩkɛnɩ-ɖʋ kɩcɛyʋʋ se ɖɩla mbʋ́ pɩkɛdɩnɩ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtɛkɛnɩ-ɖʋ kɩcɛyʋʋ se ɖɩcaɣ wezuu ɛzɩ pɩkɛdɩnaʋ ɖa-maɣmaɣ yɔ.

eng: If you live on the basis of selfishness, you are going to die. But if by the Spirit you put to death the actions of the body, you will live.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee ɩcaɣ wezuu ɛzɩ pɩkɛdɩnaʋ mɩ-maɣmaɣ yɔ yɔ, ɩsɩkɩ. Ɛlɛ́, yee pɩtɩŋnɩ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔɔ nɛ ɩkɔ mɩ-ɛzaɣ́tʋ lakasɩ ñʋʋ yɔ, ɩwɛɣ wezuu.

eng: All who are led by Gods Spirit are Gods sons and daughters.
local: Mbá patɩŋa payɩ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu wɩlɩɣ-wɛ nʋmɔʋ yɔ, pɛkɛ Ɛsɔ piya.

eng: You didnt receive a spirit of slavery to lead you back again into fear, but you received a Spirit that shows you are adopted as his children. With this Spirit, we cry, Abba, Father.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Fezuu ŋgʋ́ Ɛsɔ ha-mɩ yɔ, kɩtɛkɛ ŋgʋ́ kɩpɩsɩɣ-mɩ yomaa nɛ kiyeki se sɔɔndʋ ɛtasɩ-mɩ kpaʋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ŋ́gʋ́ lɛ e-Fezuu ŋgʋ́ kɩpɩsɩɣ-mɩ e-piya nɛ kɩhaɣ paa ɖa-taa weyi waɖɛ se ɛ́yáa-ɩ se: «Man-Caa!» yɔ.

eng: The same Spirit agrees with our spirit, that we are Gods children.
local: Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu maɣmaɣ heyi-ɖʋ se ɖɩkɛ Ɛsɔ piya.

eng: But if we are children, we are also heirs. We are Gods heirs and fellow heirs with Christ, if we really suffer with him so that we can also be glorified with him.
local: Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɖɩkɛ e-piya yɔ, ɖatɩɣyɩɣnɩ ɛ-ñɩm mbʋ́ ɛñɔɔzaa nɛ ɛsɩɩ-ɖʋ yɔ. Đa nɛ Krɩstʋ ɖɩtɩɣyɩɣnɩ-pʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩtɔkɩnɩ ɛ-kʋñɔŋ yɔ, ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩkɔŋnɩ wɛʋ ɖɔɖɔ e-koboyaɣ taa.

eng: I believe that the present suffering is nothing compared to the coming glory that is going to be revealed to us.
local: Manawa se kʋñɔŋ ŋgʋ́ ɖɩtɔkɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, paapɩzɩɣ pamaɣzɩ-kʋ nɛ koboyaɣ ŋgá Ɛsɔ kaɣ-ɖʋ wɩlʋʋ yɔ.

eng: The whole creation waits breathless with anticipation for the revelation of Gods sons and daughters.
local: Mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ Ɛsɔ labɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ yɔ, pɩɖaŋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ lɩzɩɣ e-piya nɛ pana-sɩ yɔ kpam.

eng: Creation was subjected to frustration, not by its own choice—it was the choice of the one who subjected it—but in the hope.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ labɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ yɔ, pɩpɩsɩ mbʋ́ pɩɩwazɩɣ pʋyʋ yɔ. Pɩtɛkɛ pɩ-maɣmaɣ pi-liu wɛnɩ ḿbʋ́, ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ ɖʋnɩ-pʋ ḿbʋ́. Ɛɖaŋaa se:.

eng: that the creation itself will be set free from slavery to decay and brought into the glorious freedom of Gods children.
local: ɛkɔŋ yebu nɛ pʋwɛɛ pɩ-tɩ yɔɔ nɛ pɩtaatasɩ wɛɛkʋʋ ɛzɩ e-piya wɛʋ sɩ-tɩ yɔɔ e-koboyaɣ taa yɔ.

eng: We know that the whole creation is groaning together and suffering labor pains up until now.
local: Ŋgʋ́, ɖɩna mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ labɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ yɔ pɩtɩŋa payɩ, pɩlakɩ kʋñɔŋ nɛ pisisiɣ halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ ɛzɩ halʋ caɣʋ lʋlʋʋ nɛ ɛlakɩ yɔ.

eng: Im speaking the truth in Christ—Im not lying, as my conscience assures me with the Holy Spirit:.
local: Mɔyɔɔdʋʋ toovenim, mɛnkɛ Krɩstʋ tʋ, maacɛtɩɣ. Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu wɩlɩɣ-m se pɩkɛ toovenim, ma-laŋɩyɛ ɛɛmaɣ:.

eng: And its not only the creation. We ourselves who have the Spirit as the first crop of the harvest also groan inside as we wait to be adopted and for our bodies to be set free.
local: Pɩtasɩɩnɩ mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ pi-ɖeke. Ɛlɛ́, mbá ɖa ɛcalɩnɩ haʋ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu nɛ kɩwɛɛ ɖɔ-cɔlɔ yɔ, ɖisisiɣ ɖɔɖɔ ɖa-taa nɛ ɖɩɖaŋ se Ɛsɔ ɛpɩsɩ-ɖʋ e-piya nɛ ɛya ɖa-ñʋʋ kpaagbaa kpem.

eng: I have great sadness and constant pain in my heart.
local: toovenim ḿbʋ́ lɛ se: mɔ-hɔyɛ pɩsaa siŋŋ nɛ pʋwɛɛ pɩcɛyɩɣ-m.

eng: We were saved in hope. If we see what we hope for, that isnt hope. Who hopes for what they already see?.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ yabɩ ɖa-ñʋʋ, ɛlɛ́, ɖɩtana ɖa-ñʋʋ yabʋ tɩnaɣ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩɖaŋaa. Yee ɛyʋ ɛna mbʋ́ ɛɖaŋaa yɔ yɔ, ɖaŋʋʋ tɔm tatasɩ wɛʋ ɖɔɖɔ: anɩ pʋdʋ ñɛwɛɣ ɛɖaŋɩɣ mbʋ́ ɛnaɣ yɔ?.

eng: I wish I could be cursed, cut off from Christ if it helped my brothers and sisters, who are my flesh-and-blood relatives.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee pʋwɛɛ se Ɛsɔ ɛwɩlɩ ma-maɣmaɣ kpalʋʋ nɛ ma nɛ Krɩstʋ ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa tayɩ nɛ pɩsɩnɩ man-taabalaa Yuda ñɩma yɔ, pɩkɛdɩɣnɩ-m.

eng: But if we hope for what we dont see, we wait for it with patience.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee mbʋ́ ɖɩɩnaɣ yɔ ɖɩɖaŋaa yɔ, ɖɩɖaŋɩɣ-pʋ nesi naalɛ kpam.

eng: They are Israelites. The adoption as Gods children, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the Law, the worship, and the promises belong to them.
local: Pɛkɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma: Ɛsɔ pɩsɩ-wɛ e-piya nɛ eyele nɛ pɛwɛɛ e-koboyaɣ taa. Ɛha-wɛ nɔsɩ nɛ ɛɖʋ-wɛ paɣtʋ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ poluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ yɔ nɛ ɛɖʋ mbʋ́ mbʋ́ ɛlakɩ-wɛ yɔ.

eng: In the same way, the Spirit comes to help our weakness. We dont know what we should pray, but the Spirit himself pleads our case with unexpressed groans.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛɛ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu kɔŋ se kɩsɩnɩ-ɖʋ ɖɛ-ɛjandʋ taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩɩsɩŋ mbʋ́ pʋmʋnaa se ɖɩpɔzɩ ɖɛ-Ɛsɔ sɛʋ taa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu maɣmaɣ sɛɣ-ɩ nɛ ɖɔ-nɔɔ nɛ kisisiɣ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ.

eng: The Jewish ancestors are theirs, and the Christ descended from those ancestors. He is the one who rules over all things, who is God, and who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Pɛkɛ Abraham nɛ Izaakɩ nɛ Yakɔɔbʋ pa-sayɩnaa. Đɩkpaɣ Krɩstʋ ɛsɛ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛkɛ pe-liɖe taa tʋ. Ŋgʋ́, ɛnʋ lɛ Ɛsɔ weyi ɛkɩlɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ yɔ. Pasam-ɩ tam. Pɩla ḿbʋ́!.

eng: The one who searches hearts knows how the Spirit thinks, because he pleads for the saints, consistent with Gods will.
local: Peeɖe Ɛsɔ weyi ɛnaɣ ɛyáa laŋa taa yɔ, ɛtɩlɩɣ mbʋ́ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu caɣ pɔzʋʋ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kɩsɛɣ-ɩ ɛ-ñɩma yɔɔ ɛzɩ e-liu wɛʋ yɔ.

eng: But its not as though Gods word has failed. Not all who are descended from Israel are part of Israel.
local: Ŋgʋ́, pɩtɛkɛ mɔntɔŋ se mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ yɔ pɩsayaa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma tɩŋa kɛnɩnɩ Ɛsɔ ñɩma siŋŋ.

eng: We know that God works all things together for good for the ones who love God, for those who are called according to his purpose.
local: Đɩsɩm ḿbʋ́ se Ɛsɔ lakɩ tʋmɩyɛ pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa se pʋwazɩ mbá pɔsɔɔlɩ-ɩ yɔ. Ḿbá lɛ mbá ɛya-wɛ ɛzɩ ɛɖʋʋ yɔ.

eng: Not all of Abrahams children are called Abrahams descendants, but instead your descendants will be named through Isaac. .
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Abraham liɖe taa ñɩma tɛkɛ Ɛsɔ piya patɩŋa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ heyi Abraham se: «Izaakɩ liɖe taa ñɩma ɖeke pakaɣ yaʋ se ñe-piya.».

eng: We know this because God knew them in advance, and he decided in advance that they would be conformed to the image of his Son. That way his Son would be the first of many brothers and sisters.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mbá ɛɛlɩzɩ ɖooo lɔŋ yɔ ɛɛɖʋwa se eyeki nɛ pɛwɛɛ ɛzɩ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ yɔ, nɛ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ ɛnʋ, ɛkɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩma ɖalʋ. Pɔɖɔ sakɩyɛ.

eng: That means it isnt the natural children who are Gods children, but it is the children from the promise who are counted as descendants.
local: Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se pɩtɛkɛ Abraham piya tɩŋa lɛ Ɛsɔ piya, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ tɔm se e-piya maɣmaɣ lɛ nzɩ́ palʋlɩ-sɩ ɛzɩ ɛɖʋʋ yɔ.

eng: Those who God decided in advance would be conformed to his Son, he also called. Those whom he called, he also made righteous. Those whom he made righteous, he also glorified.
local: Mbá ɛɛɖʋ ɖooo lɔŋ se ɛlakɩ-wɛ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɛya-wɛ ɖɔɖɔ, nɛ mbá ɛya-wɛ yɔ, ɛpɩsɩ-wɛ kʋtʋʋzaa, nɛ mbá ɛpɩsɩ-wɛ kʋtʋʋzaa yɔ, eyele nɛ pɛwɛɛ e-Koboyaɣ taa.

eng: The words in the promise were: A year from now I will return, and Sarah will have a son .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kaɖʋwa se: «Pɩɩtalɩɣ alɩwaatʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-yɔɔ nɛ manpɩsɩɣ lɛ, Saara lʋlɩ abalɩbɩɣa.».

eng: So what are we going to say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?.
local: Se we ɖɩtasɩɣ yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ? Yee Ɛsɔ ɛwɛ ɖɔ-yɔɔ lɛ, anɩ pʋdʋ ñapɩzɩɣ ekizini-ɖʋ yɔ?.

eng: Not only that, but also Rebecca conceived children with one man, our ancestor Isaac.
local: Pɩtasɩɩnɩ pi-ɖeke. Abalɩbiya naalɛ nzɩ́ Rebeka lʋlaa yɔ, sɩ-caa kʋɖʋm, ɛnʋ lɛ ɖɔ-cɔzɔ Izaakɩ.

eng: He didnt spare his own Son but gave him up for us all. Wont he also freely give us all things with him?.
local: Ɛnʋ weyi etohuuzi ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ yɔɔ nɛ eyele palaa-ɩ ɖatɩŋa payɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛzɩma ɛɛhaɣ-ɖʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋwɛ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ falaa?.

eng: When they hadnt been born yet and when they hadnt yet done anything good or bad, it was shown that Gods purpose would continue because it was based on his choice.
local: Pataatɩɩlʋlɩda piya ńzɩ́ nɛ pʋcɔ paatɔ se sɩlabɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ yaa mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ heyi Izaakɩ se: «Kajalaɣ ñɩŋga kaɣ ka-tɩ luzuu cikpelaɣ tɛɛ». Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Mɔnsɔɔlɩ Yakɔɔbʋ nɛ manpaɖɩ Ezaayuu» yɔ. Pɩlaba ḿbʋ́ se mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa se pɩlakɩ yɔ, pɩla. Yee ḿbʋ́ ɛnʋ ɖʋna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛlɩzɩɣ ɛyʋ weyi ɛsɔɔlaa yɔ. E-liu wɛna nɛ ɛlɩzɩ-ɩ. Pɩtɛkɛ pʋdʋ tʋma yɔɔ ɛlɩzɩ-ɩ.

eng: Who will bring a charge against Gods elect people? It is God who acquits them.
local: Apɩzɩɣ ɛkʋyɩ mbá Ɛsɔ lɩzaa yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ? Nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɖezini-wɛ.

eng: So what are we going to say? Isnt this unfair on Gods part? Absolutely not!.
local: Se we ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ? Đɩyɔɔdʋʋ se Ɛsɔ kɛ weyi ɛtatʋʋzɩ yɔ yaa we? Đɩɩtɩɩyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ kpem!.

eng: Who is going to convict them? It is Christ Jesus who died, even more, who was raised, and who also is at Gods right side. It is Christ Jesus who also pleads our case for us.
local: Apɩzɩɣ ɛkʋ pɔ-tɔm? Nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Krɩstʋ Yesu sɩbɩna. Pɩtasɩɩnɩ pi-ɖeke, ɛlɛ́, efema nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa. Ɛwɛ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa nɛ etendiɣ-i nɛ ɖɔ-nɔɔ.

eng: He says to Moses, Ill have mercy on whomever I choose to have mercy, and Ill show compassion to whomever I choose to show compassion .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, eheyi Mooyiizi se: weyi me-liu wɛɛ se manaɣ ɛ-pʋtɔdɩyɛ nɛ malabɩ-ɩ ɖeu yɔ, manaɣ ɛ-pʋtɔdɩyɛ nɛ malabɩ-ɩ ɖeu.

eng: Who will separate us from Christs love? Will we be separated by trouble, or distress, or harassment, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?.
local: Ɛbɛ pɩzɩɣ pɩlɩzɩ-ɖʋ Krɩstʋ weyi ɛsɔɔlɩ-ɖʋ yɔ e-nesi taa? Kʋñɔŋ naa sɔɔndʋ kadadaɣyaɣ naa wayɩ wɛʋ kaŋ kaŋ naa ñɔɔsɩ naa kʋñɔndʋtʋ naa tɩ lem sɩm nɔɔ taa yaa sɩm?.

eng: So then, it doesnt depend on a persons desire or effort. It depends entirely on God, who shows mercy.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pɩtalɩɩnɩ mbʋ́ ɛyʋ liu wɛɛ yɔ pʋ-cɔlɔ yaa pana wena ɛñakɩ yɔ a-cɔlɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ weyi ɛnaɣ ɛyáa pʋtɔdɩyɛ yɔ, e-ɖeke koŋ ɛ-cɔlɔ pɩlɩnaa.

eng: As it is written, We are being put to death all day long for your sake. We are treated like sheep for slaughter. .
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: «Ñɔ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩwɛ sɩm nɔɔ taa wɩsɩ nɛ ɖoó nɛ palakɩ-ɖʋ ɛzɩ heŋ weyi powokini alikizaŋ tɛ se pakʋ yɔ.».

eng: Scripture says to Pharaoh, I have put you in this position for this very thing: so I can show my power in you and so that my name can be spread through the entire earth. .
local: Pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se Ɛsɔ heyi Ejiptɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ se: «Ña-maɣmaɣ mɛncɛlɩ kewiyaɣ se mantɩŋnɩ ñɔ-yɔɔ nɛ mawɩlɩ ɛyáa mon-ɖoŋ nɛ ma-hɩɖɛ sɛɛ kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ.».

eng: But in all these things we win a sweeping victory through the one who loved us.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩ-taa lɛ, pɩtɩŋnɩ weyi ɛsɔɔlɩ-ɖʋ yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩwa kpaagbaa kpem.

eng: So then, God has mercy on whomever he wants to, but he makes resistant whomever he wants to.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yee pɩmaɣ Ɛsɔ se ɛnaɣ nɔɔyʋ pʋtɔdɩyɛ yɔ, ɛnaɣ-ɖɩ. Pɩmaɣ-ɩ se ɛlakɩ nɛ nɔɔyʋ ɛtaakemni-i yɔ, ɛlakɩ ɖɔɖɔ, pʋdʋ eekeŋni-i.

eng: Im convinced that nothing can separate us from Gods love in Christ Jesus our Lord: not death or life, not angels or rulers, not present things or future things, not powers.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, manawa siŋŋ se: paa sɩm yaa wezuu yaa Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa yaa ɛsɔdaa awiya yaa ɛsɔdaa ɖoŋ yaa mbʋ́ pʋwɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yaa pɩkaɣ kɔm yɔ yaa mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɛsɔdaa yaa tɛtɛɛ yɔ yaa mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ laba yɔ, pɩ-taa nabʋyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ pɩyasɩ-ɖʋ nɛ Ɛsɔ sɔɔlɩm mbʋ́ ɛtɩŋnɩ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Krɩstʋ Yesu yɔɔ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-ɖʋ yɔ.

eng: So you are going to say to me, Then why does he still blame people? Who has ever resisted his will.
local: Pʋtɔmaɣ yɔ, pɔpɔzʋʋ-m se: «Yee pʋwɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛbɛ yɔɔ Ɛsɔ kalɩɣ ɛyáa yɔ? Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, apɩzɩɣ ekizi mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ yɔ?».

eng: You are only a human being. Who do you think you are to talk back to God? Does the clay say to the potter, Why did you make me like this? .
local: Ña ɛyʋ lɛ, ña anɩ nɛ ŋtɔ se ŋkɛlɛsɩɣ Ɛsɔ? Cʋʋ ñanaɣ pɔzʋʋ weyi ɛma-kɛ yɔ se: pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋma-m ḿbʋ́ yaa we?.

eng: Doesnt the potter have the power over the clay to make one pot for special purposes and another for garbage from the same lump of clay?.
local: Cʋʋ mayʋ pɩzɩɣ ɛlabɩnɩ cʋʋ mbʋ́ e-liu wɛɛ yɔ: ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛkpaɣ cʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ nɛ ɛmanɩ ñanaɣ kɩbaŋga ŋgá kɛwɛ liidiye siŋŋ yɔ nɛ yem ñɩŋga ŋgá kɛfɛyɩ liidiye yɔ.

eng: What if God very patiently puts up with pots made for wrath that were designed for destruction, because he wanted to show his wrath and to make his power known?.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛnɩ Ɛsɔ, ɛcaɣaɣ se ɛwɩlɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛ-pana kpawa yɔ. Ɛcaɣaɣ ɖɔɖɔ se patɩlɩ e-ɖoŋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛɖɔkɩ ɛ-hɔyɛ siŋŋ nɛ ɛ́ɖɔ́kɩ mbá pɩɩwɛɛ se ɛ-pana ɛkpanɩ-wɛ nɛ pasʋʋ asayʋʋ taa yɔ.

eng: What if he did this to make the wealth of his glory known toward pots made for mercy, which he prepared in advance for glory?.
local: Ɛcaɣaɣ ɖɔɖɔ se ɛwɩlɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ e-koboyaɣ paɣlaa yɔ. Ɛ nɛ mbá ɛna pa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ yɔ, pɛwɛɣnɩ ka-taa. Ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ ɖooo lɔŋ se pasʋʋ ka-taa.

eng: We are the ones God has called. We dont come only from the Jews but we also come from the Gentiles.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɖa lɛ ɛyáa ḿbá. Paa ɖɩkɛ Yuda ñɩma yaa ɖɩtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, ɖa ɛyawa.

eng: As it says also in Hosea, I will call my people those who arent my people, and the one who isnt well loved, I will call loved one. .
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ nɛ náyʋ Ozee ma ɛ-takayaɣ taa yɔ. Ɛmawa se: «Ɛyáa mbá pataakɛ ma-ñɩma yɔ, mɔnkɔŋ-wɛ yaʋ se ma-ñɩma nɛ ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ mantaasɔɔlɩ ɖɩ-taa ñɩma yɔ, mayaa ɖɩɖɩ se ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ mɔnsɔɔlɩ ɖɩ-taa ñɩma siŋŋ yɔ.

eng: And in the place where it was said to them, You arent my people, there they will be called the living Gods children .
local: Nɛ ɖenɖe paaheyi ɛyáa se: ‹Ɩtɛkɛ ma-ñɩma yɔ,› peeɖe maɣmaɣ pɔkɔŋ-wɛ yaʋ se Ɛsɔ weyi ɛwɛ wezuu yɔ e-piya.».

eng: But Isaiah cries out for Israel, Though the number of Israels children will be like the sand of the sea, only a remaining part will be saved, .
local: Ezaayii lɛ, Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma tɔm ɛ́lɛ́ susaa. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Paa Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma ɛɛɖɔ sakɩyɛ ɛzɩ teŋgu nɔɔ kañɩŋa yɔ yɔ, pa-taa ɛyáa pazɩ ɖeke ñʋʋ payakɩ.

eng: because the Lord does what he says completely and quickly. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ kɔŋ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa hʋnaʋ kɛlɛʋ.».

eng: As Isaiah prophesied, If the Lord of the heavenly forces had not left descendants for us, we would have been like Sodom, and we would have become like Gomorrah. .
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Ezaayii ɛnʋ ɛɛyɔɔdɩ ɖooo lɔŋ se: «Yee Ɛsɔ Caabiyuu ɛtaakazɩ ɖe-liɖe taa ñɩma nabɛyɛ yɔ, ɖɩɩpɩsɩ ɛzɩ Sodom ñɩma yɔ. Đɩɩnɩɣzɩnɩ Gomɔɔrɩ ñɩma.».

eng: So what are we going to say? Gentiles who werent striving for righteousness achieved righteousness, the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Se we ɖɩtasɩɣ yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ? Đɩyɔɔdʋʋ se: mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma nɛ pataacaɣ se papɩsɩ kʋtʋʋzaa Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa yɔ, pɩtɩŋnɩ lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ pɛwɛnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ nɛ papɩsɩ kʋtʋʋzaa ɛ-ɛsɩndaa.

eng: But though Israel was striving for a Law of righteousness, they didnt arrive.
local: Pɩsɛɣ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma mbá pañakaɣ pana siŋŋ se pɔɖɔ paɣtʋ yɔɔ nɛ papɩsɩ kʋtʋʋzaa Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa yɔ, pɔtɔlɩ nʋmɔʋ taa.

eng: Why? Its because they didnt go for it by faith but they went for it as if it could be reached by doing something. They have tripped over a stumbling block.
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ pɩlabɩ-wɛ ḿbʋ́? Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, patañɩnɩɣ se pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pañɩnaɣ se pɩtɩŋnɩ pa-tʋma yɔɔ nɛ papɩsɩ kʋtʋʋzaa ɛ-ɛsɩndaa. Peeɖe powobi poyoni pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖituuluu ɛyáa yɔ.

eng: As it is written: Look! Im putting a stumbling block in Zion, which is a rock that offends people. And the one who has faith in him will not be put to shame. .
local: Đɩ-tɔm pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Ɩɩnaɣ, mansɩɣ Siiyɔɔ tɛtʋ taa pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖituuluu ɛyáa yɔ nɛ, ɛwaɣ ŋgá kɛpɛɖɩɣ-wɛ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛwɛɣnɩ lidaʋ weyi ɛkɛ pɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛkaɣ fɛyɛ tɔɔʋ.».

eng: Brothers and sisters, Im talking to you as people who know the Law. Dont you know that the Law has power over someone only as long as he or she lives?.
local: Taabalaa, ɩtɛm sɩm tɔm ndʋ́ meheyiɣ-mɩ yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩsɩm paɣtʋ: ɩsɩm ḿbʋ́ se alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛyʋ wɛɣ wezuu yɔ pʋwɛɛ se ɛɖɔ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: A married woman is united with her husband under the Law while he is alive. But if her husband dies, she is released from the Law concerning her husband.
local: Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna, halʋ weyi ɛwalaa yɔ, paɣtʋ yekina se ɛ nɛ ɛ-walʋ pataayasɩ ɖama alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛ́lɛ́ wɛnɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, yee walʋ ɛnʋ ɛsɩba yɔ, puholi halʋ ɛnʋ nɛ paɣtʋ ndʋ́ tɩɩwɛ ɛ nɛ ɛ-walʋ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa yɔ tɩ-taa.

eng: So then, if she lives with another man while her husband is alive, shes committing adultery. But if her husband dies, shes free from the Law, so she wont be committing adultery if she marries someone else.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee ɛ-walʋ ɛwɛnɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ ɛkʋyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛɖɛɛ ɛwalɩ nɔɔyʋ yɔ, payaɣ-ɩ se acɛyɛ tʋ. Ɛlɛ́, yee ɛ-walʋ ɛsɩba yɔ, puholi-i nɛ paɣtʋ ńdʋ́ ti-nesi taa. Ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛwalɩ abalʋ lɛlʋ, paatɔŋ se ɛkɛ acɛyɛ tʋ.

eng: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, you also died with respect to the Law through the body of Christ, so that you could be united with someone else. You are united with the one who was raised from the dead so that we can bear fruit for God.
local: Man-taabalaa, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛnɩ-mɩ. Paɣtʋ tatasɩ wɛnaʋ ɖoŋ mɩ-yɔɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩwɛ ɛzɩ sɩɖaa yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩ nɛ Krɩstʋ weyi ɛsɩba yɔ, ɩkpɛndaa nɛ ɩwɛɛ e-nesi taa. Ɛnʋ lɛ weyi ɛsɩba nɛ Ɛsɔ fezi-i yɔ. Đa nɛ ɩ ɖɩkpɛndaa se ɖa-tʋmɩyɛ ɛwɛɛ Ɛsɔ wazaɣ.

eng: When we were self-centered, the sinful passions aroused through the Law were at work in all the parts of our body, so that we bore fruit for death.
local: Toovenim lɛ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩlakaɣ ɛzɩ ɖe-liu wɛʋ yɔ, paɣtʋ yɔɔ pɩtɛzaɣ-ɖʋ labʋ liu se ɖɩla kɩwɛɛkɩm. Ŋgʋ́, pɩtɛŋnɩ-ɖʋ sɩm.

eng: But now we have been released from the Law. We have died with respect to the thing that controlled us, so that we can be slaves in the new life under the Spirit, not in the old life under the written Law.
local: Đooo lɛ, ɖɩɩwɛ ɛzɩ yomaa yɔ paɣtʋ nesi taa. Ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɖɩwɛ ɛzɩ sɩɖaa yɔ nɛ tɩtatasɩ ɖoŋ wɛnaʋ ɖɔ-yɔɔ. Ɛsɔ lɩzɩ-ɖʋ ti-nesi taa. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩlɛɣzɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɖɩlakaɣ Ɛsɔ tʋmɩyɛ yɔ ɛzɩ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu wɩlʋʋ-ɖʋ yɔ. Đɩɩtasɩɣ-ɖɩ labʋ ɛzɩ paɖʋʋ paɣtʋ taa yɔ.

eng: So what are we going to say? That the Law is sin? Absolutely not! But I wouldnt have known sin except through the Law. I wouldnt have known the desire for what others have if the Law had not said, Dont desire to take what others have .
local: Se we ɖɩɩyɔɔdɩ yɔ? Mooyiizi paɣtʋ kɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm naa we? Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́ kpem! Ɛlɛ́, tɩ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ mantɩlɩ mbʋ́ pɩkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee pataaɖʋ tɩ-taa se: «Taaɖʋ ɛsɩyɛ lɛlʋ ñɩm yɔɔ» yɔ, mantaatɩlɩ mbʋ́ ɛsɩyɛ ɖʋʋ kɛnaa yɔ.

eng: But sin seized the opportunity and used this commandment to produce all kinds of desires in me. Sin is dead without the Law.
local: Peeɖe kɩwɛɛkɩm tɩŋnɩ waɖɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ mbʋ́ paɖʋ paɣtʋ taa yɔ pɩ-taa nabʋyʋ yɔɔ nɛ pɩkpa ɖoŋ man-taa nɛ piyele manɖʋʋ ɛsɛ wondu ndɩ ndɩ yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paɣtʋ ɛfɛyɩ yɔ, kɩwɛɛkɩm fɛyɩnɩ ɖoŋ.

eng: I used to be alive without the Law, but when the commandment came, sin sprang to life.
local: Đooo mantaatɩlɩ paɣtʋ lɛ, maawɛ wezuu. Ɛlɛ́, mansɩm-tʋ lɛ, kɩwɛɛkɩm kpa ɖoŋ man-taa.

eng: and I died. So the commandment that was intended to give life brought death.
local: nɛ mantɩpɩsɩ ɛzɩ sɩɖʋ yɔ: paɣtʋ ndʋ́ pɩɩwɛɛ se tɩha-m wezuu yɔ, tiwoni-m sɩm taa.

eng: Sin seized the opportunity through the commandment, deceived me, and killed me.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, waɖɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm tɩŋnɩ mbʋ́ paɖʋ paɣtʋ taa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ nɛ pɩcɛtɩ-m nɛ pʋkʋ-m.

eng: So the Law itself is holy, and the commandment is holy, righteous, and good.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, Ɛsɔ ɖʋnɩ paɣtʋ, ɛnʋ ɖʋnɩ ɖɔɖɔ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ tɩ-taa yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ wɩlɩɣnɩ-ɖʋ mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ. Paɣtʋ wɛ ɖeu nɛ tɩwɛɛ siɣsiɣ.

eng: So did something good bring death to me? Absolutely not! But sin caused my death through something good so that sin would be exposed as sin. That way sin would become even more thoroughly sinful through the commandment.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ, pɩpɩsɩnɩ mbʋ́ pʋkʋʋ ma yɔ yaa we? Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́ kpem! Kɩwɛɛkɩm kɔnɩnɩ-pʋ. Ḿbʋ́ kɩwɛɛkɩm wɩlɩ pɩ-maɣmaɣ pɩ-tɩ keɣlee: pɩtɩŋnɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ nɛ pʋkʋ-m. Ḿbʋ́ pɩtɩŋnɩ mbʋ́ paɖʋ paɣtʋ taa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ nɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm tɛzɩ pɩ-tɩ wɩlʋʋ kpaagbaa keɣlee se pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu kpem.

eng: We know that the Law is spiritual, but Im made of flesh and blood, and Im sold as a slave to sin.
local: Đɩna toovenim se Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖʋnɩ paɣtʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ma mɛnkɛ ɛjam weyi kɩwɛɛkɩm yabɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛwɛɛ pi-nesi taa ɛzɩ yom yɔ.

eng: I dont know what Im doing, because I dont do what I want to do. Instead, I do the thing that I hate.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, maanɩɣ man-tɩ tɔm. Maalakɩ mbʋ́ me-liu wɛɛ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ maacaɣ kpem yɔ malakɩ.

eng: But if Im doing the thing that I dont want to do, Im agreeing that the Law is right.
local: Yee malakɩ mbʋ́ maacaɣ yɔ yɔ, mentisaa se paɣtʋ wɛ ɖeu.

eng: But now Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, its sin that lives in me.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ pɩtɛkɛ malakɩnɩ ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɛ́, kɩwɛɛkɩm mbʋ́ pʋwɛ man-taa yɔ pɩlakɩna.

eng: I know that good doesnt live in me—that is, in my body. The desire to do good is inside of me, but I cant do it.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, manawa se mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ, pɩfɛyɩ man-taa: pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se wɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-taa mɛwɛɛ yɔ, mɛnfɛyɩ ɖoŋ lidaʋ nʋmɔʋ taa. Toovenim lɛ, me-liu wɛɛ se mala mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, maapɩzɩɣ.

eng: I dont do the good that I want to do, but I do the evil that I dont want to do.
local: Maalakɩ ɖeu mbʋ́ me-liu wɛɛ yɔ, ɛlɛ́, malakɩ ɛzaɣ́tʋ ndʋ́ maacaɣ yɔ.

eng: But if I do the very thing that I dont want to do, then Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, it is sin that lives in me that is doing it.
local: Yee malakɩ mbʋ́ maacaɣ yɔ yɔ, pɩwɩlɩɣ se pɩtɛkɛ malakɩnɩ-pʋ. Ɛlɛ́, kɩwɛɛkɩm mbʋ́ pʋwɛ man-taa yɔ pɩlakɩna.

eng: So I find that, as a rule, when I want to do what is good, evil is right there with me.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ mana man-taa se: alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa mancaɣ se mala ɖeu yɔ, ɛzaɣ́tʋ ɖeke manpɩzɩɣ labʋ.

eng: I gladly agree with the Law on the inside.
local: Ma-maɣmaɣ man-taa hʋlʋmɩnɩ Ɛsɔ paɣtʋ.

eng: but I see a different law at work in my body. It wages a war against the law of my mind and takes me prisoner with the law of sin that is in my body.
local: Ɛlɛ́, manaɣ se nabʋyʋ wɛ man-taa ɖɔɖɔ, pʋ nɛ mbʋ́ pɩkɛdɩnɩ-m ma-lɩmaɣza taa yɔ palʋkɩ. Ḿbʋ́ pɩsɩɣnɩ-m kɩwɛɛkɩm mbʋ́ pʋwɛ man-taa yɔ pu-yom.

eng: Im a miserable human being. Who will deliver me from this dead corpse?.
local: Mɔn-tɔm wɛ pʋtɔdɩyɛ! Mon-tomnaɣ yɔɔ lɛ, mansɩkɩ. Alɩzɩɣ-m ka-taa?.

eng: Thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then Im a slave to Gods Law in my mind, but Im a slave to sins law in my body.
local: Ɛsɔ lɩzɩɣnɩ-m! Đɩtɩŋnɩ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩsam-ɩ! Yee ḿbʋ́ ma-maɣmaɣ manpɩzɩɣ mɔnɖɔ Ɛsɔ paɣtʋ yɔɔ ma-lɩmaɣza taa. Ɛlɛ́, mɛ-wɛtʋ ñɔɖɔŋ kɩwɛɛkɩm nʋmɔʋ taa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, my hearts desire is for Israels salvation. Thats my prayer to God for them.
local: Man-taabalaa, mbʋ́ mañɩnɩɣnɩ ma-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa nɛ mentendiɣ Ɛsɔ Yuda ñɩma yɔɔ yɔ lɛ se: ɛya pa-ñʋʋ.

eng: I can vouch for them: they are enthusiastic about God. However, it isnt informed by knowledge.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, manpɩzɩɣ malɩzɩ pa-aseɣɖe se pañakɩ pana siŋŋ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa. Ɛlɛ́, patasɩm-kʋ.

eng: They dont submit to Gods righteousness because they dont understand his righteousness, and they try to establish their own righteousness.
local: Toovenim lɛ, patakpaɣ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ pɩsɩɣ ɛyáa kʋtʋʋzaa yɔ nɛ payaa pʋyʋ nɛ pa-maɣmaɣ pañɩnɩɣ mbʋ́ paala nɛ papɩsɩ kʋtʋʋzaa yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ pɔtɔɖɔ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ pɩsɩɣ ɛyáa kʋtʋʋzaa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: Christ is the goal of the Law, which leads to righteousness for all who have faith in God.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Krɩstʋ yɔɔ paɣtʋ tatasɩ wɛnaʋ ɖoŋ se mbá patɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ, papɩsɩ kʋtʋʋzaa ɛ-ɛsɩndaa.

eng: Moses writes about the righteousness that comes from the Law: The person who does these things will live by them. .
local: Tɔm ndʋ́ Mooyiizi ma ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋnɩ paɣtʋ yɔɔ ɖɔm yɔɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ pɩsɩ ɛyʋ kʋtʋʋzʋ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ yɔ, ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Weyi ɛɖɔŋ mbʋ́ paɖʋ paɣtʋ taa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ pʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛwɛɛ wezuu.».

eng: But the righteousness that comes from faith talks like this: Dont say in your heart , Who will go up into heaven? .
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɔtɔm se Ɛsɔ tɩŋɩɣnɩ lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛyʋ wɛnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ-ɩ kʋtʋʋzʋ yɔ, ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Taapɔzɩ ña-maɣmaɣ ña-tɩ se akaɣ kpaʋ ɛsɔdaa?» Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se akpaɣ ɛsɔdaa nɛ etisi Krɩstʋ.

eng: or Who will go down into the region below? .
local: Taapɔzɩ ña-maɣmaɣ ña-tɩ ɖɔɖɔ se: «Akaɣ tibu tɛtɛɛ?» Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se atiki ɛlɩzɩ Krɩstʋ nɛ sɩɖaa taa.

eng: But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart .
local: Se we pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ? Pamawa se: «Ñɔ-cɔlɔ tɔm wɛɛ, tɩwɛ ñɔ-nɔɔ taa nɛ tɩwɛɛ ña-taa.» Ŋgʋ́, ńdʋ́ lɛ lidaʋ tɔm ndʋ́ ɖisusuu yɔ.

eng: Because if you confess with your mouth Jesus is Lord and in your heart you have faith that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
local: Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: yee ŋyɔɔdɩnɩ ñɔ-nɔɔ ɛyáa tɩŋa ɛsɩndaa se Yesu lɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ nɛ ña-taa lɛ, ŋwɛnɩ lidaʋ se Ɛsɔ fezi-i nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa yɔ, Ɛsɔ yakɩ ña-ñʋʋ.

eng: Trusting with the heart leads to righteousness, and confessing with the mouth leads to salvation.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ ɛyʋ wɛɣnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-taa nɛ Ɛsɔ pɩsɩ-ɩ kʋtʋʋzʋ, nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩnɩ ɛ-nɔɔ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ ya ɛ-ñʋʋ.

eng: The scripture says, All who have faith in him wont be put to shame .
local: Ḿbʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Paa weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛkaɣ fɛyɛ tɔɔʋ.».

eng: There is no distinction between Jew and Greek, because the same Lord is Lord of all, who gives richly to all who call on him.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, tayʋʋ nabʋyʋ fɛyɩ Yuda ñɩma nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa: patɩŋa pa-Kɩpaɣlʋ kʋɖʋm, ɛhaɣ ɛ-ñɩm mbá payaɣ-ɩ se ɛsɩnɩ-wɛ yɔ patɩŋa.

eng: All who call on the Lords name will be saved. .
local: Ḿbʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Weyi ɛyaɣ Kɩpaɣlʋ se ɛsɩnɩ-ɩ yɔ, ɛyakɩ ɛ-ñʋʋ.».

eng: So how can they call on someone they dont have faith in? And how can they have faith in someone they havent heard of? And how can they hear without a preacher?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛzɩma papɩzɩɣ payaa-ɩ se ɛsɩnɩ-wɛ nɛ pɛfɛyɩnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ? Ɛzɩma papɩzɩɣ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ patanɩɩ ɛ-tɔm nɔɔyʋ cɔlɔ? Ɛzɩma papɩzɩɣ panɩɩ ɛ-tɔm nɛ nɔɔyʋ tosusi-tʋ?.

eng: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written, How beautiful are the feet of those who announce the good news. .
local: Ɛzɩma tɔm susiyaa wɛɣ nɛ petetiyi nabɛyɛ se posusi-tʋ? Ḿbʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Tɔm susiyaa wokuuni ɛyʋ tɔm kɩbandʋ yɔ, pɩlabɩ ɖeu lɛ yee!».

eng: But everyone hasnt obeyed the good news. As Isaiah says, Lord, who has had faith in our message? .
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɩtɛkɛ patɩŋa paatisini Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm. Toovenim lɛ, Ezaayii ma ɛ-takayaɣ taa se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ, atisi tɔm ndʋ́ ɖisusaa yɔ?».

eng: So, faith comes from listening, but its listening by means of Christs message.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, lidaʋ lɩɣnɩ tɔm ndʋ́ ɛyʋ welesiɣ yɔ tɩ-cɔlɔ. Tɔm ndʋ́ ewelesiɣ yɔ lɛ Krɩstʋ tɔm ndʋ́ posusuu yɔ.

eng: But I ask you, didnt they hear it? Definitely! Their voice has gone out into the entire earth, and their message has gone out to the corners of the inhabited world. .
local: Ḿbʋ́ mɔnpɔzʋʋ se: Yuda ñɩma tanɩɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ yaa we? Panɩ-tʋ. Ḿbʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Ɛyáa nɩ pa-taŋ paa le kedeŋa yɔɔ, nɛ halɩ ndʋ́ pɔyɔɔdaa yɔ, tɩtalɩ ɖooo kedeŋa tɩnaɣ yɔɔ.».

eng: But I ask you again, didnt Israel understand? First, Moses says, I will make you jealous of those who arent a people, of a people without understanding. .
local: Mantasɩɣ pɔzʋʋ se: Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma tanɩɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa yaa we? Mooyiizi layɩ cosuu se: «Ɛsɔ yɔɔdaa se: ‹Meyeki nɛ ɩla sʋsɩ ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ patakpaɣ payaa pʋyʋ yɔ ɖɩ-taa ñɩma. Mansazɩɣ-mɩ nɛ mɩ-pana kpanɩ ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa ñɩma fɛyɩ lɛɣtʋ yɔ.› ».

eng: And Isaiah even dares to say, I was found by those who didnt look for me; I revealed myself to those who didnt ask for me. .
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Ezaayii ñakatɩ nɛ esusuu se: «Ɛsɔ yɔɔdaa se: ‹Mbá pataañɩnɩɣ-m yɔ, pana-m. Mawɩlɩ man-tɩ mbá pataasʋlʋmʋʋ-m pʋyʋ yɔ.› ».

eng: But he says about Israel, All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma ñɩndʋ lɛ, ɛyɔɔdaa se: «Mañɩnɩ kɩyakʋ nɛ kɩ-tɛɛ pilim se mɔñɔɔzɩ ma nɛ mbá paañaŋ-m nɛ pɛkɛlɛsɩɣ-m yɔ ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa.».

eng: So, brothers and sisters, because of Gods mercies, I encourage you to present your bodies as a living sacrifice that is holy and pleasing to God. This is your appropriate priestly service.
local: Man-taabalaa, Ɛsɔ na ɖa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ naʋ mbʋ́ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se pɩlabɩ-mɩ hʋnʋm nɛ ɩkpaɣ mɩ-maɣmaɣ mɩ-tɩ pilim mi-wezuu caɣʋ taa nɛ ɩha-ɩ nɛ ɩwɛɛ ɛzɩ kpɩna wena palaɣ-ɩ yɔ . Ɩkɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩma kpaagbaa kpem nɛ mɩ-tɔm kɛdɩnɩ-ɩ. Ḿbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ kpem se iluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ.

eng: Dont be conformed to the patterns of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your minds so that you can figure out what Gods will is—what is good and pleasing and mature.
local: Ɩtaawɛɛ ɩlakɩ ɛzɩ mbá paañaŋ Krɩstʋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɩha Ɛsɔ waɖɛ nɛ ɛha-mɩ lɩmaɣza kɩfana kpem nɛ pɩlɛɣzɩ-mɩ. Peeɖe ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩtɩlɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ yɔ: ḿbʋ́ lɛ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu nɛ pɩkɛdɩnɩ-ɩ nɛ pʋmʋna yɔ.

eng: Because of the grace that God gave me, I can say to each one of you: dont think of yourself more highly than you ought to think. Instead, be reasonable since God has measured out a portion of faith to each one of you.
local: Lootiye nɖɩ́ Ɛsɔ labɩ-m yɔ ɖɩ-yɔɔ lɛ, pɩha-m waɖɛ se meheyi paa mɩ-taa weyi se ɛtaakpazɩ ɛ-tɩ ɖenɖe ɛtatalɩ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛwɛnɩ lɩmaɣza wena amaɣnaa yɔ. Paa weyi ɛsɩɩnɩ lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ Ɛsɔ ha-ɩ yɔ kɩ-kámaɣ́ yɔɔ.

eng: We have many parts in one body, but the parts dont all have the same function.
local: Ɛyʋ wɛnɩ tomnaɣ kʋɖʋmaɣ, ɛlɛ́, kɛwɛnɩ hɔɔlɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ. Paa ŋ́gʋ́ nɛ kʋ-tʋmɩyɛ.

eng: In the same way, though there are many of us, we are one body in Christ, and individually we belong to each other.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ paa ɖɩɖɔwa yɔ, ɖɩwɛ ɛzɩ tomnaɣ kʋɖʋmaɣ ɖa nɛ Krɩstʋ ɖɛ-kpɛndʋʋ taa nɛ ɖatɩŋa payɩ ɖɩɖɔkɩ ɖama ɛzɩ ɛyʋ tomnaɣ hɔɔlɩŋ ɖɔkʋʋnɩ ɖama yɔ.

eng: We have different gifts that are consistent with Gods grace that has been given to us. If your gift is prophecy, you should prophesy in proportion to your faith.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ ha paa weyi nɛ e-ɖeɖe ɛzɩ ɛlabʋ-ɖʋ lootiye yɔ. Yee ɛha ɖa-taa nɔɔyʋ se ɛkɛ ɛ-náyʋ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se pʋdʋ esusi tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ ɛ-cɔlɔ yɔ nɛ pɩtalɩ lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛha-ɩ yɔ kɩ-kámaɣ́ yɔɔ.

eng: If your gift is service, devote yourself to serving. If your gift is teaching, devote yourself to teaching.
local: Yee Ɛsɔ ɛha nɔɔyʋ se ɛla tʋmɩyɛ Yesu ŋgbɛyɛ taa yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛlabɩ-ɖɩ. Weyi ɛha-ɩ se ɛwɩlɩ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛwɩlɩ.

eng: If your gift is encouragement, devote yourself to encouraging. The one giving should do it with no strings attached. The leader should lead with passion. The one showing mercy should be cheerful.
local: Weyi ɛha ɛ́lɛ́ se ɛyɔɔdɩnɩ lɛlaa nɛ pɩlabɩ-wɛ hʋnʋm yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛ́lɛ́ ɛla ḿbʋ́. Pʋwɛɛ se weyi ɛhaɣ ɛ-ñɩm yɔ, ɛha-pʋ nɛ ɛ-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa. Weyi ɛcɔŋnɩ lɛlaa yɔɔ yɔ, ɛcɔnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ camɩyɛ. Weyi ɛsɩŋnɩ pɩlabɩmwaa yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ ɛsɩnɩ-wɛ nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ.

eng: Love should be shown without pretending. Hate evil, and hold on to what is good.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se ɩsɔɔlɩ ɛyáa nɛ mɩ-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa pilim. Ikizi mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ kpem. Mɩ-ɛsɩyɛ ɛsɛɛnɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ.

eng: Love each other like the members of your family. Be the best at showing honor to each other.
local: Ɩsɔɔlɩ ɖama ɛzɩ pʋmʋnaʋ se Yesu ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma ɛsɔɔlɩ ɖama yɔ. Pɩlabɩ-mɩ hʋnʋm nɛ ɩña ɖama.

eng: Dont hesitate to be enthusiastic—be on fire in the Spirit as you serve the Lord!.
local: Ɩla tʋmɩyɛ, fendu ɛtaakpa-mɩ. Ɩla Kɩpaɣlʋ tʋmɩyɛ nɛ mɩ-laŋa tɩŋa pilim.

eng: Be happy in your hope, stand your ground when youre in trouble, and devote yourselves to prayer.
local: Mɩ-laŋa ɛhʋlʋmɩ mɩ-ɖaŋʋʋ yɔɔ. Ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-hɔ kʋñɔmɩŋ taa. Ɩwɛɛ Ɛsɔ sɛʋ yɔɔ tam.

eng: Contribute to the needs of Gods people, and welcome strangers into your home.
local: Ɩsɩnɩ mɩ-taabalaa Ɛsɔ ñɩma mbá pɛwɛ kʋñɔŋ taa yɔ. Ɩmʋ mbá pɔkɔŋ mɩ-tɛ yɔ camɩyɛ.

eng: Bless people who harass you—bless and dont curse them.
local: Itendi Ɛsɔ se ɛ́wazɩ mbá pɛwɛ mɩ-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ. Itendi-i se ɛ́wazɩ pɔ-yɔɔ, ɩtaawɩlɩ-wɛ kpalʋʋ.

eng: Be happy with those who are happy, and cry with those who are crying.
local: Mɩ nɛ mbá pamayɩɣ amaya yɔ, ɩkpɛndɩ ɩmayɩ. Mɩ nɛ mbá paba wiliɣ yɔ, ɩkpɛndɩ iwili.

eng: Consider everyone as equal, and dont think that youre better than anyone else. Instead, associate with people who have no status. Dont think that youre so smart.
local: Ɩnɩɩnɩ ɖama camɩyɛ mi-wezuu caɣʋ taa. Ɩtaakpazɩ mɩ-tɩ, ɛlɛ́, ɩmaɣzɩ mbʋ́ patakpaɣ payaa pʋyʋ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ. Ɩtaakpaɣ mɩ-tɩ se ɩkɛ lɔŋsɩnɖaa.

eng: Dont pay back anyone for their evil actions with evil actions, but show respect for what everyone else believes is good.
local: Ɩtaala nɔɔyʋ taŋ labɩ-m malakɩ-ɩ. Ɩñaɣ pana nɛ ɩla mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ paa weyi ɛ-ɛsɩndaa.

eng: If possible, to the best of your ability, live at peace with all people.
local: Ɩñaɣ pana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩpɩzɩɣ yɔ nɛ mɩ nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ pʋwɛɛ leleŋ.

eng: Dont try to get revenge for yourselves, my dear friends, but leave room for Gods wrath. It is written, Revenge belongs to me; I will pay it back, says the Lord .
local: Man-taabalaa kʋsɔɔlaa, mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩtaahɛyɩ ɛyáa mbʋ́ palabɩ-mɩ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, iyele se Ɛsɔ pana ɛkpanɩnɩ-wɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Mɛhɛyɩɣnɩ ɛyáa mbʋ́ palabɩ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ.» Đa-Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔdɩnɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: Instead, If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink. By doing this, you will pile burning coals of fire upon his head .
local: Pamawa ɖɔɖɔ se: «Yee ñɔɔsɩ ɛwɩɣ ño-koyindu yɔ, ha-ɩ tɔɔnaɣ nɛ ɛtɔɔ. Yee lɔkɔtʋ ɛkpa-ɩ yɔ, ha-ɩ lɩm nɛ ɛñɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee ŋlabɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pʋwɛɣ ɛzɩ ŋhuti ɛ-ñʋʋ taa miŋ nɛ mamala yɔ.».

eng: Dont be defeated by evil, but defeat evil with good.
local: Taayele ña-tɩ nɛ kɩdɛkɛdɩm wabɩ-ŋ. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se ŋla mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ nɛ ŋwabɩnɩ-pʋ.

eng: So I ask you, has God rejected his people? Absolutely not! Im an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ mɔnpɔzʋʋ se: Ɛsɔ lɔ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma naa we? Pɩtatɩɩkɛʋ ḿbʋ́ kpem! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ma-maɣmaɣ mɛnkɛ Izrɛɛlɩ tʋ. Mɛnkɛ Abraham liɖe taa tʋ, mɛwɛ Bɛnjamɛɛ cejewiye taa.

eng: God hasnt rejected his people, whom he knew in advance. Or dont you know what the scripture says in the case of Elijah, when he pleads with God against Israel?.
local: Ɛsɔ tɔlɔ ɛ-ñɩma mbá ɛɛlɩzɩ ɖooo lɔŋ yɔ. Pɩlakɩ-m ɛzɩ ɩsɩm Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa ɖenɖe pamawa se Elii kpiɖaɣ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma yɔɔ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa yɔ.

eng: Lord, they have killed your prophets, and they have torn down your altars. Im the only one left, and they are trying to take my life. .
local: Ekpiɖaɣ nɛ ɛtɔŋ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ, pakʋ ña-náyaa nɛ pɔyɔkɩ ɖenɖe ɖenɖe palaɣ-ŋ yɔ. Men-ɖeke koŋ pɩsɛɣa nɛ pacaɣ se pɛtɛzɩ-m.».

eng: But what is Gods reply to him? I have kept for myself seven thousand people who havent bowed their knees to Baal. .
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ cosi-i se we? Ecosi-i se: «Mansɩ man-tɩ ɛyáa kutokiŋ lʋbɛ, potoluŋ sɩʋ ŋgʋ́ payaɣ se Baalɩfa yɔ kɩ-tɛɛ.».

eng: So also in the present time there is a remaining group by the choice of Gods grace.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ alɩwaatʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-taa: Yuda ñɩma hɛkʋ taa lɛ, Ɛsɔ labɩ nabɛyɛ lootiye nɛ ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ nɛ ɛsɩɩ-wɛ ɛ-tɩ.

eng: But if it is by grace, it isnt by whats done anymore. If it were, Gods grace wouldnt be grace.
local: E-lootiye yɔɔ ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ, pɩtɛkɛ pa-tʋmɩyɛ yɔɔ ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, Ɛsɔ lootiye ɛɛtasɩɣ kɛʋ lootiye siŋŋ.

eng: So what? Israel didnt find what it was looking for. Those who were chosen found it, but the others were resistant.
local: Đɩyɔɔdʋʋ se we yɔ? Đɩtɔŋ se Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma tehiɣ mbʋ́ pañɩnaɣ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mbá Ɛsɔ lɩzɩ pa-taa yɔ, pe-ɖeke pehiɣni-pʋ nɛ pɩsɛɣ pʋtɔ lɛlaa nɩŋgbaaŋ taa se pataanɩɩ.

eng: As it is written, God gave them a dull spirit, so that their eyes would not see and their ears not hear, right up until the present day. .
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: «Ɛsɔ yeba nɛ pa-ñʋŋ taa kpa ɖoŋ nɛ ehuuzi pɛ-ɛsɛ yɔɔ se pataana nɛ ɛtɔ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ taa se pataanɩɩ nɛ pɛwɛɛ ḿbʋ́ halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ.».

eng: And David says, Their table should become a pitfall and a trap, a stumbling block and payback to them for what they have done. .
local: Daaviidi mawa ɖɔɖɔ se: «Pɔ-tɔɔnasɩ ɛpɩsɩ kaamʋʋ nɛ piyuu nɛ pɩkpa-wɛ nɛ pɔtɔlɩ nɛ pɔhɔ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ.

eng: Their eyes should be darkened so they cant see, and their backs always bent. .
local: Pɛ-ɛsɛ ɛyʋlʋmɩ nɛ pataatasɩ naʋ kaʋ! Nɛ pʋwɛɛ pɩnazɩɣ-wɛ.».

eng: So Im asking you: They havent stumbled so that theyve fallen permanently, have they? Absolutely not! But salvation has come to the Gentiles by their failure, in order to make Israel jealous.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ mɔnpɔzʋʋ se: pucoɖuu Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma nɛ pɔtɔlɩ yɔ, pɔtɔlaa se kʋyʋʋ fɛyɩ yaa we? Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́ kpem! Ɛlɛ́, pɛ-ɛzaɣ́tʋ yɔɔ lɛ, mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, papɩzaa pehiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ se Yuda ñɩma ɛsɛzɩ ɛsɩyɛ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: But if their failure brings riches to the world, and their defeat brings riches to the Gentiles, how much more will come from the completion of their number!.
local: Mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, Yuda ñɩma ɛzaɣ́tʋ nɛ pɔ-tɔlʋʋ pɔ-yɔɔ pehiɣ Ɛsɔ ñɩm. Yee pɩkɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛzɩma pʋwɛɣ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ patɩŋa payɩ papɩsɩɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ nʋmɔʋ taa yɔ?.

eng: Im speaking to you Gentiles. Considering that Im an apostle to the Gentiles, I publicize my own ministry.
local: Mbá mɩ ɩtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, mɩ mɔyɔɔdʋʋnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ: ma lɛ Krɩstʋ tiyiyu weyi ɛlakɩ tʋmɩyɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mamaɣzɩɣ se man-tʋmɩyɛ wɛ wazaɣ siŋŋ.

eng: in the hope that somehow I might make my own people jealous and save some of them.
local: Malakɩ ḿbʋ́ se pʋtɔmaɣ yɔ, me-liɖe taa ñɩma kaatɛzɩ wɛʋ ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ nɛ pɩya pa-taa nabɛyɛ ñʋʋ.

eng: If their rejection has brought about a close relationship between God and the world, how can their acceptance mean anything less than life from the dead?.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ lɔʋ Yuda ñɩma ḿbá yɔ, ɛ nɛ ɛyáa lɛlaa peceɣaa. Yee pɩkɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛzɩma pʋwɛɣ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛɖaɣnɩɣ-wɛ mʋ́ʋ yɔ? Pʋwɛɣ ɛzɩ ɛyʋ sɩkʋʋ nɛ efe nɛ pʋwɛɣ yɔ.

eng: But if part of a batch of dough is offered to God as holy, the whole batch of dough is holy too. If a root is holy, the branches will be holy too.
local: Ɩtɩlɩ se: yee pafaɣ tindine hɔɔlaɣ kajalaɣ ñɩŋga nɛ paha-kɛ Ɛsɔ yɔ, pɩwɩlɩɣ se ɛnʋ tɩnɩ tindine ńɖɩ́ ɖɩtɩŋa ɖɔɖɔ. Yee pakpaɣ tɩʋ nakʋyʋ lila nɛ paha Ɛsɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ tɩnɩ ki-pilinzi ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: If some of the branches were broken off, and you were a wild olive branch, and you were grafted in among the other branches and shared the root that produces the rich oil of the olive tree.
local: Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma wɛ ɛzɩ somuu ŋgʋ́ pɔsɔwa nɛ pɛsɛtɩ ki-pilinzi nasɩyɩ yɔ. Weyi ña ŋtɛkɛ Yuda tʋ yɔ, ŋwɛ ɛzɩ somuu ŋgʋ́ kʋñɔ tɛyɛ yɔɔ yem yɔ ki-piliŋa. Pɛsɛtɩ-kɛ nɛ pɔsɔ ŋgʋ́ pɔsɔ kʋsɔʋ yɔ ki-pilinzi nzɩ́ pɛsɛtaa yɔ si-lone taa: lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ŋtɔkɩ ɖɔɖɔ liɖe nɖɩ́ ɖɩcalɩɣ-kʋ yɔ ɖɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: then dont brag like youre better than the other branches. If you do brag, be careful: its not you that sustains the root, but its the root that sustains you.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, taakpaɣ pilinzi nzɩ́ pɛsɛtaa yɔ se sɩtɛkɛ pʋyʋ. Ɛbɛ yɔɔ ŋkpazɩɣ ña-tɩ yɔ? Pɩtɛkɛ ñɔ-yɔɔ lila wɛɛ, ɛlɛ́, ñɛwɛnɩ lila yɔɔ.

eng: You will say then, Branches were broken off so that I could be grafted in.
local: Ŋkaɣ-m heyuu se «Pɛsɛtɩ pilinzi ńzɩ́ se pɔsɔ-m si-lone taa.».

eng: Fine. They were broken off because they werent faithful, but you stand only by your faithfulness. So dont think in a proud way; instead be afraid.
local: Pɩkɛ toovenim. Pɛsɛtɩ-sɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ńzɩ́ lɛ lidaʋ fɛyɩwaa nɛ ña ñɛwɛɛ lone nɖɩ́ pɔsɔ-ŋ ɖɩ-taa yɔ ñe-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ yɔɔ. Taakpazɩ ña-tɩ, ɛlɛ́, sɔɔndʋ ɛkpa-ŋ.

eng: If God didnt spare the natural branches, he wont spare you either.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee Ɛsɔ etohuuzi Yuda ñɩma mbá pɛwɛ ɛzɩ kajalaɣ pilinzi yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, eehuuzuu ña ɖɔɖɔ ñɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: So look at Gods kindness and harshness. Its harshness toward those who fell, but its Gods kindness for you, provided you continue in his kindness; otherwise, you could be cut off too.
local: Cɔnɩ ŋna Ɛsɔ camɩyɛ lakasɩ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛhʋʋnɩ ɛyáa nɛ ɛhɔŋ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ: ɛhʋʋnɩ mbá palabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ nɛ ɛlakɩ ña ɖeu. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se ŋwɛɛ Ɛsɔ camɩyɛ lakasɩ nʋmɔʋ taa. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, ɛsɛtɩɣ-ŋ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ pɛsɛtʋʋ piliŋa yɔ.

eng: And even those who were cut off will be grafted back in if they dont continue to be unfaithful, because God is able to graft them in again.
local: Yee Yuda ñɩma eyebi pa-kaañamtʋ nɛ paɖaɣnɩ wɛnaʋ lidaʋ yɔ, Ɛsɔ pɩsɩɣnɩ-wɛ ɖenɖe paawɛɛ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛwɛnɩ ɖoŋ se ɛɖaɣnɩ-wɛ sɔʋ.

eng: If you were naturally part of a wild olive tree and you were cut off from it, and then, contrary to nature, you were grafted into the cultivated olive tree, wont these natural branches stand an even better chance of being grafted back onto their own olive tree?.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, weyi ña ŋtɛkɛ Yuda tʋ yɔ, ŋwɛ ɛzɩ somuu ŋgʋ́ kʋñɔ tɛyɛ yɔɔ yem yɔ ki-piliŋa maɣmaɣ: pɛsɛtɩ-kɛ nɛ pɔsɔ-kɛ ŋgʋ́ pɔsɔ kʋsɔʋ yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ. Ŋgʋ́, ŋtɛkɛ kʋkʋ piliŋa. Yee Ɛsɔ ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛla ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛzɩma ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛpɩsɩnɩ Yuda ñɩma mbá pɛwɛ ɛzɩ somuu ŋgʋ́ pɔsɔ kʋkʋ kʋsɔʋ yɔ ki-pilinzi maɣmaɣ nɛ ɛpɩsɩnɩ ɛsɔ si-lona taa?.

eng: I dont want you to be unaware of this secret, brothers and sisters. That way you wont think too highly of yourselves. A part of Israel has become resistant until the full number of the Gentiles comes in.
local: Man-taabalaa, mancaɣ se mawɩlɩ-mɩ mbʋ́ pɩɩwɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ nɛ ɩtaakpaɣ mɩ-tɩ se ɩkɛ lɔŋsɩnɖaa: Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma lɛlaa tamaa, paapɩzɩɣ panɩɩ. Ɛlɛ́, pɛwɛɣ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mbá Ɛsɔ lɩzɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pa-taa yɔ patɩŋa payɩ pehiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ yɔ.

eng: In this way, all Israel will be saved, as it is written: The deliverer will come from Zion. He will remove ungodly behavior from Jacob. .
local: Peeɖe Ɛsɔ kɔŋ yabʋ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma tɩŋa ñʋʋ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: «Ñʋʋ yaɖʋ kaɣ lɩnaʋ Yeruusalɛm. Ɛhɛzɩɣ Yakɔɔbʋ liɖe taa ñɩma kɩwɛɛkɩm.

eng: This is my covenant with them, when I take away their sins .
local: Ḿbʋ́ lɛ nɔɔ haʋ mbʋ́ meyeki nɛ pʋwɛɛ ma nɛ wɛ ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ menkpeɣiɣ-wɛ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ.».

eng: According to the gospel, they are enemies for your sake, but according to Gods choice, they are loved for the sake of their ancestors.
local: Yee ɛyʋ ɛkpaɣ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Yuda ñɩma kizi Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm yɔ yɔ, pɩwɩlɩɣ se pɛkɛ Ɛsɔ koyindinaa. Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se pʋwazɩ-mɩ. Ɛlɛ́, yee ɛyʋ ɛkpaɣ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ lɩzɩ-wɛ yɔ yɔ, pɩwɩlɩɣ se pɛkɛ mbá Ɛsɔ sɔɔlɩ-wɛ mbʋ́ ɛɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ pɔ-cɔzɔnaa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: Gods gifts and calling cant be taken back.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɛɛmʋʋ mbʋ́ ɛhawa yɔ kaʋ. Ɛɛlɛɣzɩɣ ɖɔɖɔ lɩmaɣza mbá ɛya-wɛ yɔ pɔ-tɔm taa.

eng: Once you were disobedient to God, but now you have mercy because they were disobedient.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɩtaña Ɛsɔ ɖooo lɔŋ. Ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ pɩtɩŋnɩ pa-kaañamtʋ yɔɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ na mɩ-pʋtɔdɩyɛ.

eng: In the same way, they have also been disobedient because of the mercy that you received, so now they can receive mercy too.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ patañam-ɩ nɛ ɛna mɩ mɩ-pʋtɔdɩyɛ. Đɩ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋɩɣna nɛ ɛpɩzɩ ɛna pa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: God has locked up all people in disobedience, in order to have mercy on all of them.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ yeba nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa wɛɛ ɛzɩ yomaa yɔ pa-kaañamtʋ yɔɔ se ɛna patɩŋa payɩ pa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ.

eng: Gods riches, wisdom, and knowledge are so deep! They are as mysterious as his judgments, and they are as hard to track as his paths!.
local: Ɛsɔ camɩyɛ lakasɩ ɖɔwa siŋŋ! Ɛ-lɔŋsɩnɖɛ nɛ ɛ-lɛɣtʋ pɛfɛyɩ kámaɣ́! Apɩzɩɣ ɛlɩzɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋ ɛ-taa yɔ pʋ-tɔbʋʋ? Apɩzɩɣ ɛnɩɩ mbʋ́ ɛlakɩ yɔ pʋ-tɔbʋʋ?.

eng: Who has known the Lords mind? Or who has been his mentor? .
local: Pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Asɩm Ɛsɔ lɩmaɣza? Apɩzɩɣ ɛkɛ ɛ-tɔm tasɩyʋ?.

eng: Or who has given him a gift and has been paid back by him? .
local: Apɩzaa ɛcalɩ-ɩ haʋ pʋyʋ nɛ pʋwɛɛ se ɛhɛyɩ pʋdʋ pʋ-yɔɔ?».

eng: All things are from him and through him and for him. May the glory be to him forever. Amen.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-cɔlɔ pɩtɩŋa pɩlɩɣna. Ɛnʋ yebina nɛ pɩtɩŋa pʋwɛɛ se pɩkɛ ɛ-ñɩm. Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ ɛsɛɛ paa ɖooye! Pɩla ḿbʋ́!.

eng: Every person should place themselves under the authority of the government. There isnt any authority unless it comes from God, and the authorities that are there have been put in place by God.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se paa weyi ɛña ñʋndɩnaa mbá pɔcɔŋnɩ ajɛya yɔɔ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mbá patɩŋa payɩ pɛkɛ ñʋndɩnaa yɔ, Ɛsɔ hanɩ-wɛ waɖɛ. Ɛsɔ sɩnɩ ñʋndɩnaa mbá pɛwɛɛ yɔ patɩŋa.

eng: So anyone who opposes the authority is standing against what God has established. People who take this kind of stand will get punished.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, weyi ɛɛñaŋ ñʋʋdʋ yɔ, ɛtaña mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa yɔ. Mbá palakɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pa-maɣmaɣ peyeki se pahʋʋnɩ-wɛ.

eng: The authorities dont frighten people who are doing the right thing. Rather, they frighten people who are doing wrong. Would you rather not be afraid of authority? Do whats right, and you will receive its approval.
local: Toovenim lɛ, mbá palakɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ, sɔɔndʋ ɛɛkpaɣ-wɛ nɛ ñʋndɩnaa. Ɛlɛ́, mbá palakɩ mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ, ḿbá seɣni-wɛ. Ñe-liu wɛɛ se sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-ŋ nɛ ñʋndɩnaa naa we? Yee ḿbʋ́ la mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ nɛ pasam-ŋ.

eng: Welcome the person who is weak in faith—but not in order to argue about differences of opinion.
local: Ɩmʋ weyi ɛwɛnɩ ɛjandʋ e-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ taa yɔ ɛ-tɔm, ɩtaakpɛzɩnɩ-ɩ.

eng: It is Gods servant given for your benefit. But if you do whats wrong, be afraid because it doesnt have weapons to enforce the law for nothing. It is Gods servant put in place to carry out his punishment on those who do what is wrong.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ tʋmɩyɛ taa pɛwɛɛ yɔɔ pañɩnɩɣ se ŋla mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu. Ɛlɛ́, yee ŋlakɩ mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ yɔ, sɔɔndʋ ɛkpa-ŋ nɛ wɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee pɛwɛnɩ waɖɛ se pɔhɔ ɛyáa nɩŋgbaaŋ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ yem: pɛkɛ Ɛsɔ tʋmlaɖaa se pahʋʋnɩ mbá palakɩ mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ nɛ ɛ-nɔɔ nɛ pɔhɔ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ.

eng: One person believes in eating everything, while the weak person eats only vegetables.
local: Nabʋyʋ taa, nɔɔyʋ maɣzɩɣ se pɩha-ɩ waɖɛ se ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛtɔɔ paa tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá. Ɛlɛ́, lɛlʋ weyi ɛkɛ ɛjam e-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ taa yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ ñɔtɔkɩ ɖozi hatʋ ɖeke.

eng: That is why it is necessary to place yourself under the governments authority, not only to avoid Gods punishment but also for the sake of your conscience.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se paa weyi ɛña ñʋndɩnaa. Pɩtɛkɛ nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ hɔm yɔɔ ɖeke pʋwɛɛ se ɩñam-wɛ. Ɛlɛ́, ɩñam-wɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩnaɣ ɖɔɖɔ mɩ-taa se pʋmʋnɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: Those who eat must not look down on the ones who dont, and the ones who dont eat must not judge the ones who do, because God has accepted them.
local: Pɩfɛyɩ se weyi ɛtɔkɩ paa tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá yɔ, eniɣzi weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ɛɛtɔkɩ lɛɛsɩ yɔ. Weyi ɛɛtɔkɩ tɔɔnasɩ lɛɛsɩ yɔ, pɩfɛyɩ se ɛkpɛzɩnɩ weyi ɛ́lɛ́ tɔkɩ sɩtɩŋa yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ñamʋ-ɩ nesi naalɛ.

eng: You should also pay taxes for the same reason, because the authorities are Gods assistants, concerned with this very thing.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ ɩhɛyɩɣ lambuu. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɖʋ ñʋndɩnaa se pala tʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ camɩyɛ.

eng: Who are you to judge someone elses servants? They stand or fall before their own Lord .
local: Ña anɩ nɛ ŋkpɛzɩɣnɩ lɛlʋ tʋmlaɖʋ? Yee ɛlakɩ ɖeu ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ taa yaa ɛɛlakɩ lɛ, pɩna-ŋ nɛ we? Ɛ-caa cɔlɔ lɛ. Ɛkɔŋ labʋ ɖeu, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Kɩpaɣlʋ wɛnɩ ɖoŋ se ɛsɩnɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛla mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ.

eng: So pay everyone what you owe them. Pay the taxes you owe, pay the duties you are charged, give respect to those you should respect, and honor those you should honor.
local: Ɩhɛyɩ paa weyi mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se ɩhɛyɩ-ɩ yɔ. Ɩhɛyɩ lambuu mʋyaa pa-lambuu nɛ ɩhɛyɩ tikee mʋyaa paba tikee. Ɩña weyi pʋmʋnaa se ɩñam-ɩ yɔ nɛ ɩɖʋ hɩɖɛ weyi pʋmʋnaa se ɩɖʋ-ɩ-ɖɩ yɔ.

eng: One person considers some days to be more sacred than others, while another person considers all days to be the same. Each person must have their own convictions.
local: Nabɛyɛ maɣzɩɣ se kɩyakɩŋ nɩɩyɩ kɩlɩ lɛɛŋ. Ŋgʋ́, lɛlaa ñamaɣzɩɣ se ɛwɛ kɩmaŋ. Pʋwɛɛ se paa weyi ɛtɩlɩ mbʋ́ ɛmaɣzɩɣ yɔ camɩyɛ, pʋtʋʋ ɛtaawɛɛ pɩ-taa.

eng: Dont be in debt to anyone, except for the obligation to love each other. Whoever loves another person has fulfilled the Law.
local: Ɩtaatɔɔ nɔɔyʋ kɩmɩyɛ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ sɔɔlɩm kɩmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɩtɔɔ ɖama yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛsɔɔlɩ lɛlaa yɔ, ɛɖɔm mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ paɖʋ paɣtʋ taa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: Someone who thinks that a day is sacred, thinks that way for the Lord. Those who eat, eat for the Lord, because they thank God. And those who dont eat, dont eat for the Lord, and they thank the Lord too.
local: Weyi ɛmaɣzɩɣ se kɩyakʋ nakʋyʋ kɩlɩ lɛɛŋ yɔ, ɛlakɩ ḿbʋ́ se ɛɖʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ hɩɖɛ. Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ tɔkɩ paa tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá yɔ, ɛcaɣ ɖɔɖɔ se ɛɖʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ hɩɖɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛsaŋ Ɛsɔ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá ɛtɔwaa yɔ kɔ-yɔɔ. Weyi ɛɛtɔkɩ tɔɔnasɩ nasɩyɩ yɔ, ɛlakɩ ḿbʋ́ se ɛɖʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ hɩɖɛ ɖɔɖɔ. Ɛsaŋ ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá ɛtɔwaa yɔ kɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: The commandments, Dont commit adultery, dont murder, dont steal, dont desire what others have, and any other commandments, are all summed up in one word: You must love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, paɣtʋ ndʋ́ paɖʋwa se: taala acɛyɛ, taakʋ ɛyʋ, taamɩlɩ, taaɖʋ ɛsɩyɛ lɛlaa ñɩm yɔɔ nɛ lɛɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩwɛɛ yɔ tɩtɩŋa payɩ tɩwɛ paɣtʋ ndʋ́ paɖʋwa se: sɔɔlɩ ña-taabalʋ ɛzɩ ŋsɔɔlʋʋ ña-maɣmaɣ ña-tɩ yɔ tɩ-taa.

eng: We dont live for ourselves and we dont die for ourselves.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɖa-taa nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ se ɛwɛ wezuu ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-yɔɔ. Nɔɔyʋ ɛɛsɩkɩ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Love doesnt do anything wrong to a neighbor; therefore, love is what fulfills the Law.
local: Weyi ɛsɔɔlɩ ɛ-taabalʋ yɔ, ɛɛlakɩ-ɩ mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, weyi ɛsɔɔlʋʋ lɛlaa yɔ, ɛɖɔŋ paɣtʋ ndʋ́ tɩwɛɛ yɔ tɩtɩŋa payɩ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: If we live, we live for the Lord, and if we die, we die for the Lord. Therefore, whether we live or die, we belong to God.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee ɖɩwɛ wezuu yɔ, ɖɩwɛɛ se ɖɩɖʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ hɩɖɛ. Yee ɖɩsɩkɩ yɔ, ɖɩsɩkɩ se ɖɩɖʋ-ɩ hɩɖɛ ɖɔɖɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, paa ɖɩwɛ wezuu yaa ɖɩsɩba yɔ, ɖɩkɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ ñɩma.

eng: As you do all this, you know what time it is. The hour has already come for you to wake up from your sleep. Now our salvation is nearer than when we first had faith.
local: Ɩla ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩnawa se alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ yakɩ ɖa-ñʋʋ yɔ, tɩtalaa se ifemni mi-ɖom yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖa-ñʋʋ yabʋ ñɔtɩnɩ-ɖʋ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ɛzɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩɩcalɩ wɛnaʋ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: This is why Christ died and lived: so that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Krɩstʋ sɩba nɛ efe se ɛkɛ sɩɖaa nɛ ɛsɛ ñɩma pa-Kɩpaɣlʋ.

eng: The night is almost over, and the day is near. So lets get rid of the actions that belong to the darkness and put on the weapons of light.
local: Đoo ɖɛwa nɛ tɛʋ caɣ fem. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩhɔzɩ mbʋ́ ɖɩlakaɣ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa yɔ nɛ ɖɩlɔ nɛ pɩsɛɣ ɖɩkpaɣ hɔzɩŋ weyi ɖɩɩlabɩnɩ you wɩsɩ taa yɔ.

eng: But why do you judge your brother or sister? Or why do you look down on your brother or sister? We all will stand in front of the judgment seat of God.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ña ñɛkpɛzɩɣnɩ ña-taabalʋ? Ña yɔ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ñɛpɛŋɩɣ ña-taabalʋ tɛtɛ? Đatɩŋa payɩ ɖɩkaɣ wobu Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa nɛ ɛhʋʋnɩ-ɖʋ tɔm.

eng: Lets behave appropriately as people who live in the day, not in partying and getting drunk, not in sleeping around and obscene behavior, not in fighting and obsession.
local: Đɩɖɔ camɩyɛ siɣsiɣ ɛzɩ pʋmʋnaʋ se ɛyʋ ɛɖɔ ñalɩmɩyɛ taa yɔ. Đɩɖɔkɩ ɖa-tɩ nɛ kujoŋjoŋ labʋ nɛ sʋlʋm kpaŋʋʋ nɛ acɛyɛ labʋ nɛ ɛzaɣ́tʋ labʋ nɛ you nɛ ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ.

eng: Because it is written, As I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow to me, and every tongue will give praise to God. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Ma Kɩpaɣlʋ lɛ, mankaɖɩɣ kpayɩ se: ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ kaɣ luŋuu mɛn-tɛɛ nɛ paa weyi ɛyɔɔdɩ nɛ pʋkʋyɩ ɛsɔdaa se ma lɛ Ɛsɔ.».

eng: Instead, dress yourself with the Lord Jesus Christ, and dont plan to indulge your selfish desires.
local: Ɩkpaɣ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ wɛtʋ ɛzɩ mi-you wondu yɔ. Ɩtaatasɩ yebu nɛ mɩ-maɣmaɣ mɩ-wɛtʋ hɔm-mɩ nɛ ɩla mbʋ́ mi-liu wɛɛ yɔ.

eng: So then, each of us will give an account of ourselves to God.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, paa ɖa-taa weyi lɛ, ɛkɔŋ heyuu Ɛsɔ mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ nɛ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-nɔɔ.

eng: So stop judging each other. Instead, this is what you should decide: never put a stumbling block or obstacle in the way of your brother or sister.
local: Điyele ɖama tɔm hʋnaʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɩɖʋ kpem se: ɩɩlakɩ nabʋyʋ nɛ piyele putuuli mɩ-taabalʋ yaa pɩpɛɖɩ-ɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm taa.

eng: I know and Im convinced in the Lord Jesus that nothing is wrong to eat in itself. But if someone thinks something is wrong to eat, it becomes wrong for that person.
local: Ma nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu ɖɛ-kpɛndʋʋ taa lɛ, manawa nɛ mantɩlɩ se nabʋyʋ fɛyɩ se pɩfɛyɩ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ. Ɛlɛ́, yee nɔɔyʋ ɛmaɣzɩɣ se nabʋyʋ fɛyɩ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ yɔ, pɩtɛ ɛyʋ pɩfɛyɩ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ.

eng: If your brother or sister is upset by your food, you are no longer walking in love. Dont let your food destroy someone for whom Christ died.
local: Yee ŋpɩsɩ ña-taabalʋ hɔyɛ nɛ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá ŋtɔkɩ yɔ yɔ, pɩwɩlɩɣ se ŋtɔɖɔ sɔɔlɩm nʋmɔʋ taa. Taatɩŋnɩ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá ŋtɔkɩ yɔ kɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ŋɖʋzɩ weyi Krɩstʋ sɩbɩ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: And dont let something you consider to be good be criticized as wrong.
local: Pɩfɛyɩ se mbʋ́ ŋmaɣzɩɣ se pʋmʋnaa se ŋtɔɔ yɔ, pɩha nɔɔyʋ waɖɛ nɛ ɛyɔɔdɩ tɔm ɛzaɣ́tʋ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: When I came to you, brothers and sisters, I didnt come preaching Gods secrets to you like I was an expert in speech or wisdom.
local: Taabalaa, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mɔnkɔm mɩ-tɛ se meheyi-mɩ Ɛsɔ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩwɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ tɔm sɔsɔtʋ natʋyʋ yaa lɔŋsɩnɖɛ sɔsɔɖɛ naɖɩyɛ meheyini-mɩ-tʋ.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about eating food and drinking but about righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ tɔm tɛkɛ tɔɔnaɣ nɛ sʋlʋm pa-ñɩndʋ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩkɛ mbʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu haɣ yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ yɔ: tʋʋzʋʋ nɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ.

eng: I had made up my mind not to think about anything while I was with you except Jesus Christ, and to preach him as crucified.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋcɔ mɔnkɔɔ mɩ-tɛ lɛ, manɖʋwa se alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mɛwɛɣ mɩ-tɛ yɔ, maaheyiɣ-mɩ natʋyʋ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ Yesu Krɩstʋ tɔm. Kɔzɩkɔzɩ sɩm mbʋ́ ɛsɩbɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ yɔ pʋ-tɔm.

eng: Whoever serves Christ this way pleases God and gets human approval.
local: Weyi ɛlakɩ Krɩstʋ tʋmɩyɛ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɛ-tɔm kɛdɩnɩ Ɛsɔ. Ɛyáa tɔŋ ɖɔɖɔ se ɛlakɩ ɖeu.

eng: I stood in front of you with weakness, fear, and a lot of shaking.
local: Man-tɩ tɩŋa maawɛʋ mɩ-cɔlɔ yɔ, mɛwɛ ɛjandʋ taa nɛ sɔɔndʋ kpaɣ-m pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ nɛ menseliɣ.

eng: So lets strive for the things that bring peace and the things that build each other up.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩñɩnɩ mbʋ́ pɩhɛzɩɣ ɖa-laŋa nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɖɩsɩnɩ ɖama nɛ ɖɩtɛzɩ pɩʋ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa yɔ.

eng: My message and my preaching werent presented with convincing wise words but with a demonstration of the Spirit and of power.
local: Monsusaɣ-mɩ Ɛsɔtɔm lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ ɛyáa lɔŋsɩnɖɛ monsusaɣnɩ-mɩ-tʋ se mawabɩnɩ-mɩ. Ɛlɛ́, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖoŋ wɩlɩnɩ-mɩ se tɔm ndʋ́ monsusuu yɔ, tɩkɛ toovenim.

eng: Dont destroy what God has done because of food. All food is acceptable, but its a bad thing if it trips someone else.
local: Taawɛɛkɩ Ɛsɔ tʋmɩyɛ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá ŋtɔkɩ yɔ kɔ-yɔɔ. Toovenim lɛ, tɔɔnasɩ tɩŋa mʋnaa se pɔtɔɔ-sɩ. Ɛlɛ́, yee ŋnawa se pɩpɩzɩɣ pɩtɩŋnɩ nakɛyɛ yɔɔ nɛ ña-taabalʋ tɔlɩ yɔ, pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu se ŋtɔɔ-kɛ.

eng: I did this so that your faith might not depend on the wisdom of people but on the power of God.
local: Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ ɖoŋ yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtɛkɛ ɛyáa lɔŋsɩnɖɛ yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa.

eng: Its a good thing not to eat meat or drink wine or to do anything that trips your brother or sister.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ lɛ se: taakpazɩnɩ ñɔ-nɔɔ mbʋ́ pɩpɩzɩɣ pɩpɛɖɩ ña-taabalʋ kɩwɛɛkɩm taa yɔ kaʋ. Paa nandʋ yaa sʋlʋm yaa paa ɛbɛ.

eng: What we say is wisdom to people who are mature. It isnt a wisdom that comes from the present day or from todays leaders who are being reduced to nothing.
local: Ŋgʋ́, lɔŋsɩnɖɛ tɔm ɖisusuu ɛyáa mbá papɩ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa siŋŋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtɛkɛ kedeŋa ñɩma lɔŋsɩnɖɛ yaa kedeŋa awiya mbá pɔkɔŋ labʋ asayʋʋ yɔ pa-ñɩnɖɛ.

eng: Keep the belief that you have to yourself—its between you and God. People are blessed who dont convict themselves by the things they approve.
local: Mbʋ́ ña-maɣmaɣ ŋmaɣzɩɣ se pʋwɛ ɖeu se ŋpɩzɩɣ ŋlabɩ-pʋ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ ña-taa Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa. Weyi ɛkʋyʋʋ se ɛlakɩ pʋyʋ nɛ pɩɩcɛyɩɣ-ɩ yɔ, ɛwɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ taa.

eng: We talk about Gods wisdom, which has been hidden as a secret. God determined this wisdom in advance, before time began, for our glory.
local: Ɛsɔ lɔŋsɩnɖɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩɩwɛ ɛyáa mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ, ɖɩ-tɔm ɖisusuu. Tɩ-taa wɛ cukaŋ. Pʋcɔ Ɛsɔ la kedeŋa lɛ, ɛɛɖʋwa se pɩtɩŋnɩ ɖɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩwɛɛ e-koboyaɣ taa.

eng: But those who have doubts are convicted if they go ahead and eat, because they arent acting on the basis of faith. Everything that isnt based on faith is sin.
local: Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛpʋtʋʋ tɔɔnaɣ nakɛyɛ yɔɔ yɔ, yee ɛtɔɔ-kɛ yɔ, ɛ-tɔm sɩkɩ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛfɛyɩnɩ lidaʋ se mbʋ́ ɛlakɩ yɔ pʋwɛ ɖeu. Mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛyʋ lakɩ nɛ ɛpʋtʋʋ pʋ-yɔɔ yɔ, pɩkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm.

eng: It is a wisdom that none of the present-day rulers have understood, because if they did understand it, they would never have crucified the Lord of glory!.
local: Kedeŋa awiya taa nɔɔyʋ tasɩ lɔŋsɩnɖɛ ńɖɩ́. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee paasɩm-ɖɩ yɔ, pataaka Kɩpaɣlʋ weyi ɛtɩnɩ koboyaɣ yɔ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ.

eng: But this is precisely what is written: God has prepared things for those who love him that no eye has seen, or ear has heard, or that havent crossed the mind of any human being .
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: «Mbʋ́ ɛyʋ tana wiɖiyi kaʋ yaa ɛtanɩɩ yɔ, mbʋ́ ɛyʋ tamaɣzɩ pʋ-yɔɔ wiɖiyi kaʋ yɔ, toovenim lɛ, ḿbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ñɔɔzaa nɛ ɛsɩɩ mbá pɔsɔɔlɩ-ɩ yɔ.».

eng: God has revealed these things to us through the Spirit. The Spirit searches everything, including the depths of God.
local: Đa Ɛsɔ tɩŋnɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔɔ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-pʋ. Toovenim lɛ se Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu sɩm pɩtɩŋa payɩ, halɩ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖooo Ɛsɔ taa yɔ.

eng: Who knows a persons depths except their own spirit that lives in them? In the same way, no one has known the depths of God except Gods Spirit.
local: Apɩzɩɣ ɛtɩlɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ lɛlʋ taa yɔ? Ɛlɛ́, paa weyi ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-kalɩzaɣ tɩlɩɣnɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɛ-taa yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ Ɛsɔ taa yɔ, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛtɩlɩ-pʋ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ ɛ-maɣmaɣ e-Fezuu.

eng: We havent received the worlds spirit but Gods Spirit so that we can know the things given to us by God.
local: Ŋgʋ́ ɖa lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ kedeŋa fezuu paha-ɖʋ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ hanɩ-ɖʋ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu se ɖɩpɩzɩ ɖɩtɩlɩ ɛ-ñɩm mbʋ́ paha-ɖʋ falaa yɔ.

eng: These are the things we are talking about—not with words taught by human wisdom but with words taught by the Spirit—we are interpreting spiritual things to spiritual people.
local: Đɩɩyɔɔdʋʋ pʋ-tɔm nɛ tɔm ndʋ́ kedeŋa lɔŋsɩnɖaa wɩlɩɣ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ndʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu wɩlɩɣ yɔ ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋna. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩlɩzɩɣ kʋ-tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ mbá Ɛsɔ ha-wɛ-kʋ yɔ.

eng: But people who are unspiritual dont accept the things from Gods Spirit. They are foolishness to them and cant be understood, because they can only be comprehended in a spiritual way.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mbá ɛtaha-wɛ-kʋ yɔ, paapɩzɩɣ petisi toovenim tɔm ndʋ́ kiheyiɣ ɛyáa yɔ. Pamaɣzɩɣ se tɩkɛ kɩmɛlɛndʋ tɔm. Paapɩzɩɣ panɩɩ-tʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mbá Ɛsɔ ha-wɛ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔ pe-ɖeke papɩzɩɣna panɩɩ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ.

eng: Spiritual people comprehend everything, but they themselves arent understood by anyone.
local: Mbá ɛha-wɛ-kʋ yɔ, papɩzɩɣ patɩlɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yaa mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ. Nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛkpɛzɩnɩ-wɛ.

eng: Who has known the mind of the Lord, who will advise him? But we have the mind of Christ.
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: «Asɩm Kɩpaɣlʋ lɩmaɣza? Apɩzɩɣ ɛtasɩ-ɩ tɔm?» Ŋgʋ́ ɖa yɔ, ɖɩmaɣzɩɣ ɛzɩ Krɩstʋ maɣzʋʋ yɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I couldnt talk to you like spiritual people but like unspiritual people, like babies in Christ.
local: Taabalaa, mantaapɩzɩɣ se mɔyɔɔdɩnɩ-mɩ ɛzɩ mbá Ɛsɔ ha-wɛ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mɔyɔɔdɩnɩ-mɩ ɛzɩ mbá palakɩ ɛzɩ ɖooo paalakʋʋ yɔ yaa ɛzɩ mbá pɛkɛ piya pɩcɩɩcɩyɩmwaa Krɩstʋ nʋmɔʋ taa yɔ.

eng: I gave you milk to drink instead of solid food, because you werent up to it yet.
local: Tɔm ndʋ́ mɛnkɛdɩ-mɩ yɔ, tɩwɛ ɛzɩ naalɩm maha-mɩ yɔ, tɩfɛyɩ ɛzɩ tɔɔnaɣ yuŋ ñɩŋga yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtaapɩzɩɣ se ɩtɔɔ-kɛ. Halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ ɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɩtɔɔ-kɛ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Now you are still not up to it because you are still unspiritual. When jealousy and fighting exist between you, arent you unspiritual and living by human standards?.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mi-wezuu caɣʋ taa lɛ, ɩlakɩ ɛzɩ ɩɩwɛʋ yɔ. Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ nɛ you pɛwɛ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa yɔ, ɩfɛyɩ ɛzɩ ɩɩwɛʋ yɔ yaa we? Yaa mi-wezuu caɣʋ ñɛfɛyɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ɛyáa lɛlaa yɔ?.

eng: When someone says, I belong to Paul, and someone else says, I belong to Apollos, arent you acting like people without the Spirit?.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ yɔɔdʋʋ se ɛwɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɔɔ nɛ lɛlʋ se ɛ́lɛ́ ñɛwɛ Apoloosi yɔɔ yɔ, paalakɩ ɛzɩ kedeŋa ñɩma lakʋʋ yɔ yaa we?.

eng: After all, what is Apollos? What is Paul? They are servants who helped you to believe. Each one had a role given to them by the Lord:.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ anɩ lɛ Apoloosi? Ma Pɔɔlɩ, ma lɛ anɩ? Đɩkɛ Ɛsɔ tʋmlaɖaa yem. Ɖɔ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ. Paa ɖa-taa weyi ɛlakɩ tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ ɖʋ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: I planted, Apollos watered, but God made it grow.
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ mɔsɔnɩ tɩʋ nɛ Apoloosi mɩzɩ kʋ-yɔɔ lɩm yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ lakɩna nɛ kɩpaɣlɩɣ.

eng: Because of this, neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but the only one who is anything is God who makes it grow.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ weyi ɛsɔʋ tɩʋ yaa weyi ɛmɩzɩɣ kʋ-yɔɔ lɩm yɔ, ɛkɩlɩnɩ wazaɣ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ weyi ɛlakɩ nɛ kɩpaɣlɩɣ yɔ, e-ɖeke koŋ ɛkɩlɩnɩ wazaɣ.

eng: The one who plants and the one who waters work together, but each one will receive their own reward for their own labor.
local: Weyi ɛsɔʋ tɩʋ yɔ nɛ weyi ɛmɩzɩɣ kʋ-yɔɔ lɩm yɔ, pɛwɛ kɩmaŋ. Paa pa-taa weyi lɛ, tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɛlaba yɔ, ɖɩ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-ɩ hɛyʋʋ.

eng: We are Gods coworkers, and you are Gods field, Gods building.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖatɩŋa ɖɩkpɛndaa nɛ ɖɩlakɩ Ɛsɔ tʋmɩyɛ nɛ mɩ mɩwɛɛ ɛzɩ ɛ-hayɩm yɔ. Ɩwɛ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ɛ-ɖɩɣa yɔ.

eng: I laid a foundation like a wise master builder according to Gods grace that was given to me, but someone else is building on top of it. Each person needs to pay attention to the way they build on it.
local: Ma lɛ, Ɛsɔ labɩ-m lootiye nɛ mɛwɛɛ ɛzɩ kuduyiŋ mayʋ kɩbanʋ weyi ɛsɩ tigiɖe nɛ lɛlʋ tɩnɩɣ ɖɩ-yɔɔ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se paa weyi ɛtɩlɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛtɩnɩɣ ɖɩ-yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: No one can lay any other foundation besides the one that is already laid, which is Jesus Christ.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛtasɩ tigiɖe naɖɩyɛ sɩʋ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ nɖɩ́ pɛtɛm sɩʋ yɔ. Ńɖɩ́ lɛ Yesu Krɩstʋ.

eng: So, whether someone builds on top of the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, grass, or hay.
local: Paa nɔɔyʋ ɛtɩnɩ ɖɩ-yɔɔ nɛ sika yaa ñɩɣlɩ́m yaa pɛɛ kɩñɩlɩzɛɛ yaa ɖasɩ yaa kesi yaa ñɩtʋ yɔ.
