local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ yɔ, pɩsɩŋnɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛpɩɩ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa. Weyi esusuu tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ, Yesu ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma ɛ́lɛ́ yeki se papɩɩ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa.

eng: We were all baptized by one Spirit into one body, whether Jew or Greek, or slave or free, and we all were given one Spirit to drink.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu kʋɖʋmʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ pala ɖatɩŋa payɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ nɛ ɖɩpɩsɩ tomnaɣ kʋɖʋmaɣ. Paa ɖɩkɛ Yuda ñɩma yaa mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, yaa yomaa yaa mbá pɛwɛ pa-tɩ yɔɔ yɔ, ɖatɩŋa payɩ Fezuu kʋɖʋmʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-lɩm ɖɩñɔwa.

eng: I wish that all of you spoke in tongues, but Id rather you could prophesy. Those who prophesy are more important than those who speak in tongues, unless they are able to interpret them so that the church might be built up.
local: Me-liu wɛɛ se mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩyɔɔdɩ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ. Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ me-liu wɛɛ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ yɔ lɛ se isusi tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ. Weyi esusuu-tʋ yɔ, ɛkɩlɩ wazaɣ weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋ kʋnʋmɩŋ ɩnʋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, yee nɔɔyʋ ɛpɩzɩɣ se ɛlɩzɩ ndʋ́ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ nɛ pɩsɩnɩ Yesu ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma nɛ papɩɩ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa yɔ, ḿbʋ́ lɛ.

eng: Certainly the body isnt one part but many.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ piliŋa kʋɖʋmaɣ wɛnɩ tomnaɣ yɔɔ, ɛlɛ́, sɩɖɔ sakɩyɛ.

eng: After all, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I help you unless I speak to you with a revelation, some knowledge, a prophecy, or a teaching?.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, taabalaa, mɔnpɔzʋʋ-mɩ se: yee mɔnkɔm mɩ-tɛ nɛ mɔyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-mɩ kʋnʋmɩŋ weyi ɩɩnɩɣ yɔ nɛ menteheyi-mɩ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩɩwɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ tɩ-taa natʋyʋ yaa lɔŋsɩnɖɛ tɔm yaa tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ yaa mantawɩlɩ-mɩ natʋyʋ lɛ, mawazɩ-mɩ we?.

eng: If the foot says, Im not part of the body because Im not a hand, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Paa naŋgbaŋaɣ ɛtɔm se: «Mɛntɛkɛ nesi, pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mɛnfɛyɩ tomnaɣ yɔɔ» yɔ, kaapɩzɩɣ keyele tomnaɣ piliŋa kɛʋ.

eng: Likewise, things that arent alive like a harp or a lyre can make a sound, but if there arent different notes in the sounds they make, how will the tune from the harp or the lyre be recognized?.
local: Đɩkpaɣ soo wondu ɛzɩ hɩlaɣ yaa cɩmʋʋ yɔ: yee paawiɣ camɩyɛ lɛ, ɛzɩma peɖiɣziɣni ndʋ́ pahʋlʋʋ hɩlaɣ taa yaa ndʋ́ pamakɩ cɩmʋʋ yɔɔ yɔ?.

eng: If the ear says, Im not part of the body because Im not an eye, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Paa nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ ɛtɔm se: «Mɛntɛkɛ ɛsɩyɛ, pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mɛnfɛyɩ tomnaɣ yɔɔ» yɔ, kɩɩpɩzɩɣ kiyele tomnaɣ piliŋa kɛʋ.

eng: And if a trumpet call is unrecognizable, then who will prepare for battle?.
local: Yee nɔndɔʋ hʋlɩyʋ ɛtayaa-kʋ camɩyɛ lɛ, akaɣ ɛ-tɩ ñɔɔzʋʋ se ɛɖɛɛ you yɔ?.

eng: If the whole body were an eye, what would happen to the hearing? And if the whole body were an ear, what would happen to the sense of smell?.
local: Yee ɛyʋ tomnaɣ tɩŋa ɛɛkɛ ɛsɩyɛ lɛ, ɛbɛ ɛɛnɩnaa yɔ? Yee kaakɛ nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ lɛ, ɛbɛ ɛɛsɔŋnaa yɔ?.

eng: Its the same way with you: If you dont use language that is easy to understand when you speak in a tongue, then how will anyone understand what is said?.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, yee ɩpaŋnɩ yɔɔdʋʋ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ lɛ, ɛzɩma patɩlɩɣ ndʋ́ ɩcaɣ yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ? Pʋbʋ ɩlɔʋ mɩ-tɔm yem.

eng: But as it is, God has placed each one of the parts in the body just like he wanted.
local: Ɛsɔ ɖʋnɩ paa piliŋa ŋgá tomnaɣ yɔɔ ḿbʋ́ ɛzɩ e-liu wɛʋ yɔ.

eng: It will be as if you are speaking into the air! There are probably many language families in the world, and none of them are without meaning.
local: Kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ wɛnɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɩ-taa nakʋyʋ fɛyɩ se kɩfɛyɩnɩ tɔbʋʋ.

eng: If all were one and the same body part, what would happen to the body?.
local: Yee sɩtɩŋa payɩ sɩɩkɛ piliŋa kʋɖʋmaɣ lɛ, le tomnaɣ ñaawɛɛ yɔ?.

eng: So if I dont know the meaning of the language, then I will be like a foreigner to those who speak it, and they will be like foreigners to me.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, yee maanɩɣ kʋnʋŋ ŋgʋ́ pɔyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-m yɔ yɔ, mɛwɛ ɛzɩ ɛgɔm yɔ weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋ-kʋ yɔ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa nɛ pʋdʋ ñɛwɛɛ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ɛgɔm yɔ mɛ-ɛsɩndaa.

eng: But as it is, there are many parts but one body.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pilinzi ndɩ ndɩ nɛ tomnaɣ kʋɖʋmaɣ koŋ pɛwɛna.

eng: The same holds true for you: since you are ambitious for spiritual gifts, use your ambition to try to work toward being the best at building up the church.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛnɩ-mɩ. Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩsɔɔlɩ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu kʋjɔŋ siŋŋ yɔ, ɩñaɣ pana nɛ ihiɣ-i camɩyɛ. Ɩkpaɣ-ɩ nɛ ɩsɩnɩnɩ Yesu ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma nɛ papɩɩ pe-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ taa.

eng: So the eye cant say to the hand, I dont need you, or in turn, the head cant say to the feet, I dont need you.
local: Ɛsɩyɛ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɖiheyi nesi se: «Man-paa fɛyɩnɩ-ŋ.» Yaa ñʋʋ ñaapɩzɩɣ kiheyi naŋgbanzɩ se: «Man-paa fɛyɩnɩ-mɩ.».

eng: Therefore, those who speak in a tongue should pray to be able to interpret.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛsɛɛ Ɛsɔ se ɛlabɩ-ɩ kʋjɔʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛɛlɩzɩnɩ ɩ-tɔbɩŋ yɔ.

eng: Instead, the parts of the body that people think are the weakest are the most necessary.
local: Halɩ tomnaɣ yɔɔ pilinzi nzɩ́ ɖɩkpakɩɣ se sɩkɛ ajamaa kpem yɔ, sɩwɛ-ɖʋ wazaɣ siŋŋ.

eng: If I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind isnt productive.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee mɛnsɛɣ Ɛsɔ nɛ kʋnʋmɩŋ ɩnʋ yɔ, man-kalɩzaɣ wɛ Ɛsɔ sɛʋ yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mɛ-lɛɣtʋ eehikiɣ kɛzɛɣa nakɛyɛ.

eng: The parts of the body that we think are less honorable are the ones we honor the most. The private parts of our body that arent presentable are the ones that are given the most dignity.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, tomnaɣ yɔɔ pilinzi nzɩ́ ɖɩmaɣzɩɣ se sɩkɛ-ɖʋ fɛyɛ pʋyʋ yɔ, sɩ-yɔɔ ɖɩkɩlɩɣ huuzuu. Halɩ nzɩ́ sɩfɛyɩ cɔnaʋ kpem yɔ, sɩ-yɔɔ ɖɩkɩlɩɣ cɔnaʋ kpem.

eng: What should I do? Ill pray in the Spirit, but Ill pray with my mind too; Ill sing a psalm in the Spirit, but Ill sing the psalm with my mind too.
local: Ɛbɛ pʋwɛɛ se mala yɔ? Pʋwɛɛ se mɛnsɛɛ Ɛsɔ nɛ man-kalɩzaɣ. Ɛlɛ́, mɛnsɛɣ-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ mɛ-lɛɣtʋ. Menteɣ hendu nɛ man-kalɩzaɣ. Ɛlɛ́, menteɣ-tʋ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ mɛ-lɛɣtʋ.

eng: The parts of our body that are presentable dont need this. But God has put the body together, giving greater honor to the part with less honor.
local: Ŋgʋ́, pɩtɛkɛnɩ-ɖʋ kɩcɛyʋʋ se ɖɩcɔŋnɩ nzɩ́ sɩwɛ cɔnaʋ yɔ sɩ-yɔɔ ḿbʋ́. Ɛsɔ labɩ tomnaɣ nɛ ɛɖʋ ke-pilinzi nzɩ́ sɩfɛyɩ cɔnaʋ yɔ hɩɖɛ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ.

eng: After all, if you praise God in the Spirit, how will the people who arent trained in that language say Amen! to your thanksgiving, when they dont know what you are saying?.
local: Toovenim lɛ, yee ŋsaŋ Ɛsɔ nɛ ña-kalɩzaɣ ɖeke lɛ, ɛzɩma weyi ɛɛnɩɣ kʋnʋŋ ŋ́gʋ́ yɔ, ɛpɩzɩɣ etisi se: «pɩla ḿbʋ́» ñɛ-Ɛsɔ sam ḿbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ? Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛnɩɣ ndʋ́ ŋyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ.

eng: so that there wont be division in the body and so the parts might have mutual concern for each other.
local: se tayʋʋ ɛtaawɛɛ tomnaɣ yɔɔ. Ɛlabɩ ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ se pilinzi ɛwɛnɩ lɩmaɣza kʋɖʋmɛɛ nɛ sɩtɔzʋʋ ɖama yɔɔ.

eng: You may offer a beautiful prayer of thanksgiving, but the other person is not being built up.
local: Paa ñɛ-Ɛsɔ sam ḿbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu ɛzɩma yɔ, piiyeki se lɛlʋ ɛpɩɩ e-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ taa.

eng: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part gets the glory, all the parts celebrate with it.
local: Yee piliŋa nakɛyɛ ɛnɩɣ siziŋ yɔ, pilinzi tɩŋa nɩɣ siziŋ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ŋ́gá yɔ. Yee pasam piliŋa nakɛyɛ yɔ, ka nɛ pilinzi tɩŋa pamayɩɣ amaya.

eng: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
local: Mansaŋ Ɛsɔ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ eyeba nɛ mɔyɔɔdʋʋ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ mɩtɩŋa payɩ yɔ.

eng: You are the body of Christ and parts of each other.
local: Mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩkɛ Krɩstʋ tomnaɣ nɛ paa mɩ-taa weyi ɛkɛ tomnaɣ ŋ́gá ke-piliŋa.

eng: But in the church Id rather speak five words in my right mind than speak thousands of words in a tongue so that I can teach others.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Yesu ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma kpeɣliɣ yɔ, me-liu kɩlɩ wɛʋ se mɔyɔɔdɩ tɔm pazɩ nɛ lɛɣtʋ nɛ mɛnkpɛlɩkɩnɩ lɛlaa nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ɛzɩ maayɔɔdɩnɩ-wɛ tɔm huwa huwa nɛ kʋnʋmɩŋ weyi paanɩɣ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: In the church, God has appointed first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, the ability to help others, leadership skills, different kinds of tongues.
local: Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa Ɛsɔ calɩnɩ lɩzʋʋ Yesu ŋgbɛyɛ taa. Pa-wayɩ lɛ, náyaa, paba wayɩ lɛ, wɩlɩyaa. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛla lɛlaa kʋjɔŋ ndɩ ndɩ: ḿbá lɛ mbá palakɩ hama lakasɩ nɛ mbá peyeki se kʋdɔndɩnaa yɔɔ ɛcɛ yɔ nɛ mbá pasɩŋnɩ lɛlaa nɛ mbá pɔcɔŋnɩ lɛlaa yɔɔ yɔ nɛ mbá pɔyɔɔdʋʋ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ yɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont be like children in the way you think. Well, be babies when it comes to evil, but be adults in your thinking.
local: Taabalaa, ɩtaawɛnɩ pɩcatʋ lɩmaɣza. Kɩdɛkɛdɩm ɖeɖe lɛ, ɩwɛɛ ɛzɩ piya yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɩmaɣzɩ ɛzɩ sɔsaa yɔ.

eng: All arent apostles, are they? All arent prophets, are they? All arent teachers, are they? All dont perform miracles, do they?.
local: Patɩŋa payɩ pɛkɛ Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa naa we? Pɛkɛ náyaa naa we? Pɛkɛ wɩlɩyaa naa we? Palakɩ hama lakasɩ naa we?.

eng: In the Law it is written: I will speak to this people with foreign languages and foreigners lips, but they will not even listen to me this way , says the Lord.
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔdʋʋ nɛ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: «Ɛyáa mbá pɔyɔɔdʋʋ kʋnʋmɩŋ lɛɛŋ yɔ nɛ agɔmaa, pɔ-yɔɔ mɔnkɔŋ tɩŋnaʋ nɛ mɔyɔɔdɩnɩ mɛ-ɛyáa Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma. Halɩ paakaɣ-m welesinaʋ.».

eng: All dont have gifts of healing, do they? All dont speak in different tongues, do they? All dont interpret, do they?.
local: Pɩha patɩŋa payɩ se peyele kʋdɔndɩnaa yɔɔ cɛ naa we? Pɩha patɩŋa payɩ se pɔyɔɔdɩ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ yaa we? Pɩha patɩŋa payɩ se pɛɖɛzɩ tɔm kʋnʋmɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-taa yaa we?.

eng: So then, tongues are a sign for those who dont believe, not for those who believe. But prophecy is a sign for believers, not for those who dont believe.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mbá pañaŋ Krɩstʋ yɔ, kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ yɔɔdʋʋ tɛkɛnɩ-wɛ yʋsaɣ. Ɛlɛ́, mbá paañaŋ Krɩstʋ yɔ pɩkɛnɩ yʋsaɣ. Piyele tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ ti-susuu tɛkɛnɩ yʋsaɣ mbá paañaŋ Krɩstʋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mbá pañaŋ-ɩ yɔ pɩkɛnɩ yʋsaɣ.

eng: Use your ambition to try to get the greater gifts. And Im going to show you an even better way.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩñɩnɩ kʋjɔŋ weyi ɩkɩlɩ lɛɛŋ yɔ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, mantasɩɣ-mɩ wɩlʋʋ nʋmɔʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩkɩlɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ yɔ.

eng: Follow my example, just like I follow Christs.
local: Ɩla ɛzɩ ma yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ma-maɣmaɣ malakɩ ɛzɩ Krɩstʋ yɔ.

eng: So suppose that the whole church is meeting and everyone is speaking in tongues. If people come in who are outsiders or unbelievers, wont they say that you are out of your minds?.
local: Mɩ lɛ, yee ikpeɣli ɖama nɛ mɩtɩŋa ɩyɔɔdʋʋ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ nɛ nabɛyɛ yem yaa mbá paañaŋ Krɩstʋ yɔ patalɩ, paatɔŋ se ɩkɛ kpaanzɩyaa naa we?.

eng: I praise you because you remember all my instructions, and you hold on to the traditions exactly as I handed them on to you.
local: Mansaŋ-mɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩtɔzʋʋ mɔ-yɔɔ paa ɖooye nɛ ɩɖɔkɩ ma-lakasɩ nɛ tɔm ndʋ́ pawɩlɩ-m nɛ mawɩlɩ-mɩ yɔ, pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: But if everyone is prophesying when an unbeliever or outsider comes in, they are tested by all and called to account by all.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, yee patɩŋa posusuu tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ nɛ weyi ɛɛñaŋ Krɩstʋ yɔ yaa nɔɔyʋ yem talɩ peeɖe yɔ, paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma yɔ, ɛnɩɣ natʋyʋ nɛ tɩlabɩ-ɩ tʋmɩyɛ nɛ ɛtɩlɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se ɛla yɔ.

eng: Now I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, me-liu wɛɛ se ɩtɩlɩ se: Krɩstʋ lɛ paa abalʋ weyi e-wiyaʋ nɛ abalʋ lɛ halʋ wiyaʋ nɛ Ɛsɔ lɛ Krɩstʋ wiyaʋ.

eng: The secrets of their hearts are brought to light. When that happens, they will fall on their faces and worship God, proclaiming out loud that truly God is among you!.
local: Peeɖe pukuluu ɛ-lɩmaɣza wena awɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ a-yɔɔ nɛ ɛpa ɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ tɛtʋ nɛ ɛsa Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛkaɖɩ kpayɩ se: «Toovenim, Ɛsɔ wɛ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa.».

eng: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered shames his head.
local: Abalʋ weyi ɛsɛɣ Ɛsɔ yaa esusuu tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ, ehuuzi ɛ-ñʋʋ yɔɔ pʋyʋ yɔ, ɛɖʋʋ e-wiyaʋ Krɩstʋ fɛyɛ.

eng: What is the outcome of this, brothers and sisters? When you meet together, each one has a psalm, a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. All these things must be done to build up the church.
local: Taabalaa, ɛbɛ pʋwɛɛ se ɩla yɔ? Pʋwɛɛ se paa mɩ-taa weyi ɛwɛnɩ nabʋyʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ikpeɣliɣ ɖama yɔ. Pɩpɩzaɣ se pɩkɛ hendu yaa tɔm natʋyʋ se ɛwɩlɩɣ yaa Ɛsɔ wɩlɩ-ɩ nabʋyʋ yaa ɛwɛnɩ tɔm natʋyʋ se ɛyɔɔdʋʋnɩ kʋnʋŋ ŋgʋ́ ɛyáa ɛɛnɩɣ yɔ kɩ-taa nɛ lɛlʋ ɖɛzɩɣ-tʋ. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩsɩnɩ lɛlaa nɛ papɩɩ pe-lidaʋ nʋmɔʋ taa.

eng: Every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered disgraces her head. It is the same thing as having her head shaved.
local: Ɛlɛ́, halʋ weyi ɛsɛɣ Ɛsɔ yaa esusuu tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ nɛ etohuuzi ɛ-ñʋʋ yɔɔ pʋyʋ yɔ, ɛɖʋʋ ɛ-walʋ fɛyɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnɩɣzɩnɩ halʋ weyi pahʋm-ɩ koloŋgolo se paɖʋ-ɩ fɛyɛ yɔ.

eng: If some speak in a tongue, then let two or at most three speak, one at a time, and someone must interpret.
local: Yee mɩ-taa nabɛyɛ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ yɔ, pɛwɛɛ naalɛ yaa naadozo, pataaɖɛɛ pʋ-yɔɔ. Pɔyɔɔdɩ kʋɖʋm kʋɖʋm. Pʋwɛɛ ɖɔɖɔ se nɔɔyʋ ɛwɛɛ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ ɖɛzɩɣ ndʋ́ pɔyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ.

eng: If a woman doesnt cover her head, then she should have her hair cut off. If it is disgraceful for a woman to have short hair or to be shaved, then she should keep her head covered.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yee halʋ nɔɔyʋ etohuuzi pʋyʋ ɛ-ñʋʋ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛtɛzɩ ɛ-ñɔsɩ hʋm. Ŋgʋ́, yee pɩkɛnɩ halʋ fɛyɛ se ɛhʋ ɛ-ñɔsɩ yaa pahʋm-ɩ koloŋgolo yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ehuuzi pʋyʋ ɛ-ñʋʋ yɔɔ.

eng: However, if there is no interpreter, then they should keep quiet in the meeting. They should speak privately to themselves and to God.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛfɛyɩ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa peeɖe se ɛɖɛzɩɣ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se pɛtɛzɩ sum. Pɔyɔɔdɩnɩ pa-maɣmaɣ pa-tɩ nɛ pɔyɔɔdɩnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: A man shouldnt have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is mans glory.
local: Pɩfɛyɩ se abalʋ ehuuzi pʋyʋ ɛ-ñʋʋ yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ lɛ Ɛsɔ lɛzʋʋ nɛ e-koboyaɣ. Piyele halʋ lɛ abalʋ koboyaɣ.

eng: In the case of prophets, let two or three speak and have the rest evaluate what is said.
local: Náyaa lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se naalɛ yaa naadozo ɛyɔɔdɩna nɛ lɛlaa maɣzɩ nɛ pana.

eng: Man didnt have his origin from woman, but woman from man;.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ halʋ yɔɔ Ɛsɔ tɩŋnaa nɛ ɛla abalʋ. Ɛlɛ́, abalʋ yɔɔ ɛtɩŋnaa nɛ ɛla halʋ.

eng: And if some revelation comes to someone else who is sitting down, the first one should be quiet.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee Ɛsɔ ɛwɩlɩ nɔɔyʋ natʋyʋ peeɖe yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se weyi ɛyɔɔdaɣ yɔ esu nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ ñɔyɔɔdɩ.

eng: and man wasnt created for the sake of the woman, but the woman for the sake of the man.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ halʋ yɔɔ Ɛsɔ labɩ abalʋ, ɛlɛ́, abalʋ yɔɔ ɛlabɩ halʋ.

eng: You can all prophesy one at a time so that everyone can learn and be encouraged.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩyɔɔdɩ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ kʋɖʋm kʋɖʋm nɛ ɩwɩlɩnɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ lɔŋ nɛ pɩlabɩ-wɛ hʋnʋm.

eng: Because of this a woman should have authority over her head, because of the angels.
local: Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa yɔɔ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se halʋ ehuuzi pʋyʋ ɛ-ñʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ pɩwɩlɩ se eluzi ɛ-tɩ ɛ-walʋ tɛɛ.

eng: The spirits of prophets are under the control of the prophets.
local: Náyaa wɛnɩ ɖoŋ kʋjɔʋ ŋgʋ́ Ɛsɔ labɩ-wɛ yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: However, woman isnt independent from man, and man isnt independent from woman in the Lord.
local: Ŋgʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ ɛsɩndaa lɛ, halʋ fɛyɩ ɛ-tɩ yɔɔ abalʋ cɔlɔ. Abalʋ ɖɔɖɔ ñɛfɛyɩ ɛ-tɩ yɔɔ halʋ cɔlɔ.

eng: God isnt a God of disorder but of peace. Like in all the churches of Gods people.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ tayaa-ɖʋ se ɖɩla yem yem, ɛlɛ́, ɛya-ɖʋ se ɖɩwɛɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa. Ɛzɩ pʋwɛʋ Ɛsɔ ñɩma aŋgba tɩŋa taa payɩ yɔ.

eng: As woman came from man so also man comes from woman. But everything comes from God.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paa abalʋ yɔɔ Ɛsɔ ɛtɩŋnaa nɛ ɛla halʋ yɔ, halʋ lʋlʋʋnɩ abalʋ. Ŋgʋ́, halʋ nɛ abalʋ nɛ pɩtɩŋa payɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ pɩlɩnaa.

eng: the women should be quiet during the meeting. They are not allowed to talk. Instead, they need to get under control, just as the Law says.
local: pʋwɛɛ se halaa esu alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Yesu aŋgba taa ñɩma kpeɣliɣ yɔ. Pɩtaha-wɛ nʋmɔʋ se pɔyɔɔdɩ. Pʋwɛɛ se poluzi pa-tɩ ɛzɩ paɖʋʋ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ taa ɖɔɖɔ yɔ.

eng: Judge for yourselves: Is it appropriate for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?.
local: Mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩmaɣzɩ nɛ ɩna: pʋwɛ ɖeu ḿbʋ́ se halʋ ɛsɛɛ Ɛsɔ nɛ etohuuzi pʋyʋ ɛ-ñʋʋ yɔɔ yaa we?.

eng: If they want to learn something, they should ask their husbands at home. It is disgraceful for a woman to talk during the meeting.
local: Yee pe-liu ɛwɛɛ se pɔpɔzʋʋ tɔm natʋyʋ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se pɔpɔzɩ-tʋ pa-maɣmaɣ pa-walaa ɖɩsɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛ fɛyɛ se halʋ ɛyɔɔdɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Yesu ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma kpeɣliɣ yɔ.

eng: Doesnt nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him;.
local: Mbʋ́ mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩnaɣ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ yɔ, pɩɩwɩlɩɣ-mɩ se pɩkɛnɩ abalʋ fɛyɛ se eyele ɛ-ñɔsɩ nɛ susu yaa we?.

eng: Did the word of God originate with you? Has it come only to you?.
local: Mɩ-tɛ Kɔrɛntɩ Ɛsɔtɔm lɩnaa yaa we? Yaa mi-ɖeke tɩtalaa?.

eng: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? This is because her long hair is given to her for a covering.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pɩkɛnɩ halʋ hɩɖɛ ɖʋʋ se ɛ́lɛ́ ñɩnzɩ esu. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩha-ɩ ñɔsɩ se sihuuzi ɛ-ñʋʋ yɔɔ.

eng: If anyone thinks that they are prophets or spiritual people, then let them recognize that what Im writing to you is the Lords command.
local: Yee mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛmaɣzɩɣ se ɛkɛ náyʋ yaa ɛwɛnɩ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu kʋjɔʋ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛtɩlɩ se ndʋ́ mama-mɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, tɩkɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ paɣtʋ.

eng: But if someone wants to argue about this, we dont have such a custom, nor do Gods churches.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ liu ɛwɛɛ se ɛkpɛzɩɣnɩ tɔm tʋnɛ yɔ, ɖa ɖaalakɩ ḿbʋ́. Paalakɩ ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ aŋgba taa.

eng: If someone doesnt recognize this, they arent recognized.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛtakpaɣ-tʋ ɛ́yáa pʋyʋ yɔ, paakpakɩɣ pʋdʋ payaa pʋyʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Now I dont praise you as I give the following instruction because when you meet together, it does more harm than good.
local: Tɔm tʋnɛ tɩ-wayɩ lɛ, maasaŋ-mɩ lɛɛtʋ ndʋ́ manpazɩɣ yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ tɩ-taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, piiɖeɣni-mɩ mi-kpeɣluu taa. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛkɩɣ-mɩ kɩwɛɛkʋ.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, use your ambition to try to get the gift of prophecy, but dont prevent speaking in tongues.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, taabalaa, meheyiɣ-mɩ se tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ ti-susuu ɛwɛɛ-mɩ liu. Ɩtaaɖaŋ ɛyáa se pataayɔɔdɩ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ.

eng: First of all, when you meet together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.
local: Kajalaɣ lɛ, manɩwa se alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ikpeɣliɣ ɖama yɔ, tayʋʋ wɛ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa. Peheyi-m-tʋ nɛ mamaɣzɩɣ se pɩkɛɣ toovenim.

eng: Everything should be done with dignity and in proper order.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se pala pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛzɩ pʋmʋnaʋ yɔ nɛ pʋtʋʋzɩ.

eng: Its necessary that there are groups among you, to make it clear who is genuine.
local: Pʋwɛɛ kpem se tayʋʋ ɛwɛɛ ḿbʋ́ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ papɩzɩ patɩlɩ mbá pɔɖɔkʋʋ pa-tɩ yɔ.

eng: So when you get together in one place, it isnt to eat the Lords meal.
local: Paa ɩmaɣzɩɣ se ɩlakɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ sɩm yɔɔ tɔzʋʋ sɔnzɩ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ ńzɩ́ ɩlakɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ikpeɣliɣ ɖama yɔ.

eng: Each of you goes ahead and eats a private meal. One person goes hungry while another is drunk.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩcakɩ sɩ-ɖɩlaɖɛ lɛ, paa weyi ekpedesiɣ se ɛtɔɔ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-tɔɔnaɣ. Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ ñɔɔsɩ ñawɩɣ nabɛyɛ nɛ sʋlʋm ñakʋʋ lɛlaa.

eng: Dont you have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you look down on Gods churches and humiliate those who have nothing? What can I say to you? Will I praise you? No, I dont praise you in this.
local: Ɩfɛyɩnɩ ɖɩsɩ se ɩtɔɔ nɛ ɩñɔɔ sɩ-taa yaa we? Yaa Ɛsɔ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma iniɣziɣ nɛ ɩɖʋʋ fɛyɛ mbá pɛfɛyɩ pʋyʋ yɔ? Se we pʋwɛɛ se meheyi-mɩ yɔ? Mansam-mɩ yaa we? Aayɩ, maapɩzɩɣ mansam-mɩ kpem.

eng: I received a tradition from the Lord, which I also handed on to you: on the night on which he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɔm ndʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ heyi-m nɛ meheyi-mɩ yɔ, ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ɖoó wena a-taa pakpa Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu yɔ, pʋcɔ pakpa-ɩ lɛ, ɛɛkpaɣ tindine.

eng: After giving thanks, he broke it and said, This is my body, which is for you; do this to remember me.
local: nɛ ɛsɛɛ ɖɩ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ lɛ, ɛcɩyɩ-ɖɩ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Pʋnɛ lɛ mon-tomnaɣ ŋgá paha mɩ-yɔɔ yɔ. Ɩtɔɔ-ɖɩ nɛ ɩtɔzʋʋnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ.».

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want to call your attention to the good news that I preached to you, which you also received and in which you stand.
local: Taabalaa, mɔntɔzʋʋ-mɩ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ndʋ́ monsusi-mɩ yɔ. Ńdʋ́ itisaa nɛ ɩɖɔkɩ-tʋ nesi naalɛ kpam.

eng: He did the same thing with the cup, after they had eaten, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Every time you drink it, do this to remember me.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pɛtɛm tɔɔnaɣ tɔɔʋ lɛ, ɛkpaɣ sʋlʋm nɛ caɖaɣ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Sʋlʋm caɖaɣ kanɛ lɛ nɔɔ haʋ kɩfam mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋnɩ man-calɩm yɔɔ nɛ pʋwɛɛ yɔ. Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩñɔʋ-pʋ yɔ, ɩla ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɩtɔzʋʋnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ.».

eng: You are being saved through it if you hold on to the message I preached to you, unless somehow you believed it for nothing.
local: Yee ɩɖɔkɩ-tʋ ḿbʋ́ camɩyɛ kpam ɛzɩ monsusuu-mɩ-tʋ yɔ yɔ, tɩyakɩ mɩ-ñʋʋ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ɩɩwɛnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ monsusi-mɩ-tʋ yɔ, kɩɩwazɩɣ-mɩ pʋyʋ.

eng: Every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you broadcast the death of the Lord until he comes.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩtɔkɩ tindine ɖɩnɛ nɛ ɩñɔʋ sʋlʋm caɖaɣ kanɛ yɔ, isusuu Kɩpaɣlʋ sɩm nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ ɛ-kɔm.

eng: I passed on to you as most important what I also received: Christ died for our sins in line with the scriptures.
local: Tɔm ndʋ́ maanɩwa nɛ mentisi nɛ tɩkɩlɩ wazaɣ yɔ, ńdʋ́ meheyi-mɩ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ɖa-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ Krɩstʋ sɩba. Ḿbʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa ɖooo lɔŋ.

eng: This is why those who eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord inappropriately will be guilty of the Lords body and blood.
local: Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ yee nɔɔyʋ ɛɛtɔkɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ tindine nɛ ɛɛñɔʋ ɛ-sʋlʋm caɖaɣ ɛzɩ pʋmʋnaʋ yɔ yɔ, ɛyʋsɩnɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ tomnaɣ nɛ ɛ-calɩm.

eng: he was buried, and he rose on the third day in line with the scriptures.
local: Pepim-i nɛ efe kɩyakʋ naadozo ñɩŋgʋ wiye ɛzɩ paamaʋ ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ.

eng: Each individual should test himself or herself, and eat from the bread and drink from the cup in that way.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se paa weyi ɛmaɣzɩ ɛ-taa camɩyɛ nɛ ɛna nɛ pʋcɔ ɛtɔɔ tindine ńɖɩ́ nɛ ɛñɔɔ sʋlʋm caɖaɣ ŋ́gá.

eng: He appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
local: Efema lɛ, ɛwɩlɩ ɛ-tɩ Pɩyɛ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛwɩlɩ ɛ-tɩ e-tiyiyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ.

eng: Concerning the collection of money for Gods people: You should do what I have directed the churches in Galatia to do.
local: Haɖɛ nɖɩ́ pahaɣ se pasɩnɩ Ɛsɔ ñɩma yɔ ɖɩ-tɔm taa lɛ, mɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɩla ɛzɩ meheyuu Yesu aŋgba taa ñɩma mbá pɛwɛ Galaasii yɔ.

eng: Those who eat and drink without correctly understanding the body are eating and drinking their own judgment.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛtɔkɩ nɛ ɛñɔɔ yem nɛ ɛtamaɣzɩ camɩyɛ se ɛna mbʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ tomnaɣ kɛwa yɔ, mbʋ́ ɛtɔkɩ nɛ ɛñɔʋ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ kʋʋnɩ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-tɔm.

eng: and then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at once—most of them are still alive to this day, though some have died.
local: Paba wayɩ lɛ, ɛwɩlɩ ɛ-tɩ taabalaa mbá nɛ pɛkpɛndɩ-ɩ naʋ yɔ, pɛɖɛ ɛzɩ taŋga yɔ. Paa pa-taa lɛlaa ɛsɩba yɔ, pa-taa ɛyáa sakɩyɛ wɛ wezuu halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ.

eng: On the first day of the week, each of you should set aside whatever you can afford from what you earn so that the collection wont be delayed until I come.
local: Kpɩtaʋ taa kajalaɣ kɩyakʋ wiye lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se paa mɩ-taa weyi ɛsɩɩ mbʋ́ ɛpɩzɩɣ se ɛha yɔ nɛ hɔɔlʋʋ nɛ maakɔŋ mantalɩɣ lɛ, ɩtɛm cɔʋ pɩtɩŋa.

eng: Because of this, many of you are weak and sick, and quite a few have died.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ mɩ-taa ɛyáa sakɩyɛ hɛnɩ kʋdɔmɩŋ nɛ papɩsɩ ajamaa nɛ mɩ-taa nabɛyɛ sɩ.

eng: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
local: Pʋwayɩ ɛwɩlɩ ɛ-tɩ Yakubu. Pʋbʋ wayɩ lɛ, ɛwɩlɩ ɛ-tɩ e-tiyiyaa lɛlaa tɩŋa.

eng: Then when I get there, Ill send whomever you approve to Jerusalem with letters of recommendation to bring your gift.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mantalɩɣ mɩ-tɛ yɔ, mamaɣ takayɩsɩ nɛ mɛncɛlɩ mbá ɩlɩzɩ-wɛ se powoni mɩ-haɖɛ Yeruusalɛm yɔ nɛ pɛkpɛndɩna.

eng: But if we had judged ourselves, we wouldnt be judged.
local: Yee ɖa-maɣmaɣ ɖɩmaɣzaa nɛ ɖɩtɩlɩ ɖa-tɩ nɛ pʋcɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɛɛhʋʋnɩ-ɖʋ.

eng: and last of all he appeared to me, as if I were born at the wrong time.
local: Patɩŋa payɩ pa-wayɩ lɛ, ɛwɩlɩ-m ɛ-tɩ kɛtɛzaɣ taa. Ŋgʋ́, mɛwɛnɩ ɛzɩ pɩɣa ŋgá palʋlɩ-kɛ kɩtapɩpʋʋ yɔ.

eng: If it seems right for me to go too, theyll travel with me.
local: Yee pʋwɛɛ se mowolo ma-maɣmaɣ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ, ma nɛ wɛ ɖiwolo.

eng: However, we are disciplined by the Lord when we are judged so that we wont be judged and condemned along with the whole world.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Kɩpaɣlʋ hʋʋnɩ-ɖʋ nɛ ɛhɔŋ ɖa-nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ se ɛñɔɔzɩ-ɖʋ nɛ pataakɔɔ pakʋ mbá paañaŋ Krɩstʋ yɔ pɔ-tɔm nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ-ɖʋ.

eng: Im the least important of the apostles. I dont deserve to be called an apostle, because I harassed Gods church.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɛnkɛnɩ Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa taa tɔyaɣ. Toovenim lɛ, mantamʋna se paya-m se Krɩstʋ tiyiyu. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, maawɛnɩ ɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ.

eng: Ill come to you after I go through Macedonia, and because Im going through Macedonia.
local: Mantɩŋɩɣnɩ Masedonii egeetiye taa nɛ mɔnkɔɔ mɩ-tɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se mantɩŋnɩ peeɖe nɛ pʋcɔ.

eng: For these reasons, my brothers and sisters, when you get together to eat, wait for each other.
local: Man-taabalaa, pʋ-yɔɔ meheyiɣ-mɩ se: ɩɖaŋ ɖama alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ikpeɣliɣ se ɩtɔɔ Kɩpaɣlʋ tɔɔnaɣ yɔ.

eng: I am what I am by Gods grace, and Gods grace hasnt been for nothing. In fact, I have worked harder than all the others—that is, it wasnt me but the grace of God that is with me.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ lootiye yɔɔ lɛ, mɛnkɛ mbʋ́ mɛnkɛnaa yɔ. Lootiye nɖɩ́ ɛlabɩ-m yɔ, ɖɩwɛnɩ kɛzɛɣa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, malabɩ tʋmɩyɛ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa lɛlaa tɩŋa payɩ. Pɩtɛkɛ mon-ɖoŋ malabɩnaa, ɛlɛ́, lootiye nɖɩ́ ɛlabɩ-m yɔ ɖɩ-yɔɔ malabɩ-ɖɩ.

eng: I may stay with you or even spend the winter there in Corinth so that you can send me on my way to wherever Im off to next.
local: Pʋtɔmaɣ yɔ, mɔnkɔŋ sɩŋʋʋ mɩ-cɔlɔ pazɩ. Halɩ mancakɩ niŋkaɣ alɩwaatʋ tɩŋa nɛ ɩsɩnɩ-m nɛ mowolo ɖenɖe pʋwɛɛ se mowolo yɔ.

eng: If some of you are hungry, they should eat at home so that getting together doesnt lead to judgment. I will give directions about the other things when I come.
local: Yee ñɔɔsɩ ɛwɩɣ nɔɔyʋ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛtɔɔ ɛ-tɛ nɛ ɛtaakɔɔ eyele nɛ Ɛsɔ kʋ mɩ-tɔm alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ikpeɣliɣ ɖama yɔ. Tɔm ndʋ́ tɩkazaa yɔ, mɔñɔɔzʋʋ-tʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mantalɩɣ mɩ-tɛ yɔ.

eng: So then, whether you heard the message from me or them, this is what we preach and this is what you have believed.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, paa pɩɩkɛ ma yaa ḿbá yɔ, tɔm ndʋ́ ɖisusuu-mɩ yɔ, tɩ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ.

eng: I dont want to make a quick visit to you, since I hope to spend some time with you if the Lord lets it happen.
local: Me-liu fɛyɩ se mana-mɩ ɖɛʋ taa yem. Ɛlɛ́, me-liu wɛɛ se yee Kɩpaɣlʋ etisiɣ yɔ, mancaɣ mɩ-cɔlɔ pazɩ.

eng: So if the message that is preached says that Christ has been raised from the dead, then how can some of you say, Theres no resurrection of the dead?.
local: Yee ɖisusuu se Krɩstʋ sɩba nɛ efe lɛ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma mɩ-taa lɛlaa ñɔtɔŋ se sɩɖaa eefeŋ?.

eng: Ill stay here in Ephesus until the Festival of Pentecost.
local: Paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma yɔ, mancakɩ Efɛɛzɩ cɩnɛ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tibu Kazandʋ wiye.

eng: If theres no resurrection of the dead, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Yee toovenim se sɩɖaa eefeŋ yɔ, pʋbʋ Krɩstʋ tefe ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: In spite of the fact that there are many opponents, a big and productive opportunity has opened up for my mission here.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paa koyindinaa ɛɖɔwa yɔ, pɩha-m waɖɛ se mala tʋmɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ wazaɣ yɔ.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then our preaching is useless and your faith is useless.
local: Yee Krɩstʋ etefe yɔ, tɔm ndʋ́ ɖisusuu yɔ, tɩfɛyɩnɩ wazaɣ nakɛyɛ nɛ mi-lidaʋ fɛyɩnɩ wazaɣ nakɛyɛ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: If Timothy comes to you, be sure that he has no reason to be afraid while hes with you, because he does the work of the Lord just like I do.
local: Yee Timootee ɛtalɩ mɩ-tɛ lɛ, ɩmʋ-ɩ camɩyɛ nɛ pɩkɛdɩnɩ-ɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛlakɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ tʋmɩyɛ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ma yɔ.

eng: So I decided that, for my own sake, I wouldnt visit you again while I was upset.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ manɖʋwa se maakɔŋ mɩ-tɛ nɛ mantasɩ mɩ-hɔ pɩsʋʋ.

eng: We are found to be false witnesses about God, because we testified against God that he raised Christ, when he didnt raise him if its the case that the dead arent raised.
local: Yee toovenim sɩɖaa eefeŋ yɔ, pʋbʋ Ɛsɔ tefezi Krɩstʋ ɖɔɖɔ. Halɩ aseɣɖe nɖɩ́ ɖɩlɩzɩɣ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ se Ɛsɔ fezi-i kpem yɔ, ɖɩcɛtɩnɩ Ɛsɔ taa.

eng: So dont let anyone disrespect him, but send him on in peace so he can join me. Im waiting for him along with the brothers and sisters.
local: Pɩfɛyɩ se nɔɔyʋ enim-i. Ɛlɛ́, ɩsɩnɩ-ɩ nɛ ɛɖɔ ɛ-nʋmɔʋ nɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ nɛ ɛpɩsɩ ɛkɔɔ mɔn-cɔlɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ma nɛ taabalaa ɖɩɖaŋ-ɩ.

eng: If I make you sad, who will be there to make me glad when you are sad because of me?.
local: Yee mɔnkɔm mɩ-tɛ nɛ mɩ-hɔ pɩsɩ lɛ, apɩzɩɣ eyele nɛ ma-laŋɩyɛ hʋlʋmɩ yɔ? Pɩɩkɛɣnɩ mɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩtɩŋa payɩ mɩ-hɔ pɩsaa.

eng: If the dead arent raised, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee sɩɖaa eefeŋ yɔ, Krɩstʋ tefe ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Concerning Apollos our brother: I strongly encouraged him to visit you with the brothers and sisters, but he didnt want to go now. Hell come when he has an opportunity.
local: Piyele ɖa-taabalʋ Apoloosi lɛ, mɔyɔɔdɩnɩ-ɩ se pɩlabɩ-ɩ hʋnʋm nɛ ɛ nɛ taabalaa lɛlaa pɔkɔɔ mɩ-tɛ. Ɛlɛ́, e-liu fɛyɩ se ɛkɔɔ alɩwaatʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-taa. Ɛkaɣ kɔm alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɩhaɣ-ɩ nʋmɔʋ yɔ.

eng: Thats why I wrote this very thing to you, so that when I came I wouldnt be made sad by the ones who ought to make me happy. I have confidence in you, that my happiness means your happiness.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ mama-mɩ takayaɣ lɛɛka ŋ́gá se mantalaa lɛ, mɔ-hɔyɛ ɛtaapɩsɩnɩ mbá pɩɩwɛɛ se peyele nɛ ma-laŋɩyɛ hʋlʋmɩ yɔ. Ŋgʋ́, manawa se mɩtɩŋa payɩ mɩ-laŋa hʋlʋmʋʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ma-ñɩnɖɛ hʋlʋmʋʋ yɔ.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then your faith is worthless; you are still in your sins.
local: Nɛ yee Krɩstʋ etefe yɔ, mi-lidaʋ fɛyɩnɩ kɛzɛɣa nakɛyɛ. Đɩnɛ ɩwɛ mɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm nesi taa ḿbʋ́.

eng: Stay awake, stand firm in your faith, be brave, be strong.
local: Ɩcaɣnɩ mɩ-ɛsɛ nɛ ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ kpam mi-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ taa. Ɩwɛɛ abalɩtʋ nɛ ɩwɛɛ ɖoŋ.

eng: I wrote to you in tears, with a very troubled and anxious heart. I didnt write to make you sad but so you would know the overwhelming love that I have for you.
local: Mama-mɩ takayaɣ ŋ́gá nɛ ɛsɛñɩŋɩyɛ siŋŋ nɛ laŋpɩsɩyɛ nɛ ɛsɩtɛɣlɩm. Mantama-mɩ se mɩ-hɔ ɛpɩsɩ. Ɛlɛ́, mancaɣaɣ se ɩtɩlɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɔnsɔɔlɩ-mɩ siŋŋ yɔ.

eng: and whats more, those who have died in Christ are gone forever.
local: Pɩwɩlɩɣ ɖɔɖɔ se mbá paawɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ pʋcɔ pasɩ yɔ, peleba.

eng: Everything should be done in love.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se ɩla pɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ sɔɔlɩm.

eng: But if someone has made anyone sad, that person hasnt hurt me but all of you to some degree .
local: Toovenim lɛ, yee nɔɔyʋ eyeba nɛ ɛyáa hɔ pɩsɩ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ ma-maɣmaɣ mɔ-hɔyɛ ɛpɩsaa. Ɛlɛ́, mɩtɩŋa payɩ mɩ-hɔ ɛpɩsaa. Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩtaaɖɛlɛsɩ niye tɔm tʋnɛ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: If we have a hope in Christ only in this life, then we deserve to be pitied more than anyone else.
local: Yee kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ wezuu caɣʋ ɖeke yɔɔ ɖɩtaɣ Krɩstʋ yɔ, ɖɩwɛ pʋtɔdɩyɛ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I encourage you to do something else. You know that the people in Stephanas household were the first crop of the harvest to come from the mission to Achaia. They have dedicated themselves to the service of Gods people.
local: Taabalaa, ɩnawa se Stefanaasɩ nɛ ɛ-hɔʋ taa ñɩma pacalɩnɩ kɛʋ Krɩstʋ ñɩma Akayɩ egeetiye taa nɛ palakɩ Ɛsɔ ñɩma tʋmɩyɛ. Pʋ-yɔɔ mentendiɣ-mɩ se:.

eng: The punishment handed out by the majority is enough for this person.
local: Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɩ-taa ɛyáa sakɩyɛ tɛm hɔm pʋdʋ nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ yɔ, pʋɖɔwa.

eng: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead. Hes the first crop of the harvest of those who have died.
local: Ɛlɛ́ toovenim taa lɛ, Ɛsɔ fezi Krɩstʋ nɛ sɩɖaa taa. Ɛnʋ lɛ sɩɖaa taa kajalaɣ tʋ weyi efezaa yɔ.

eng: So accept the authority of people like them and of anyone who cooperates and works hard.
local: iluzi mɩ-tɩ pa nɛ mbá patɩŋa payɩ palakɩ tʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ yɔ pɛ-tɛɛ.

eng: This is why you should try your best to forgive and to comfort this person now instead, so that this person isnt overwhelmed by too much sorrow.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɩtɛzɩ-ɩ kpeɣu nɛ ɩhɛzɩ ɛ-laŋɩyɛ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, pʋkɔŋ pɩkɩlɩ ɛ-hɔyɛ pɩsʋʋ nɛ ɛlɔ liu.

eng: Since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead came through one too.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋnɩ ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm yɔɔ nɛ ɛyáa sɩkɩ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm yɔɔ nɛ sɩɖaa fe.

eng: Im so happy that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have arrived; theyve made up for my missing you.
local: Man-taa hʋlʋmɩnɩ Stefanaasɩ nɛ Fɔɔrtunaatusi nɛ Akayikuusi pɔ-kɔm. Pasɩnɩ-m alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩfɛyɩ mɔn-cɔlɔ yɔ.

eng: So I encourage you to show your love for this person.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mentendiɣ-mɩ se ɩla mbʋ́ pɩwɩlɩɣ se ɩsɔɔlɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: In the same way that everyone dies in Adam, so also everyone will be given life in Christ.
local: Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋnɩ Aɖam yɔɔ nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa sɩkɩ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ sɩɖaa tɩŋa fe.

eng: Indeed theyve provided my spirit and yours with a much-needed rest. Therefore, give them proper recognition.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, peyeba nɛ ma nɛ mɩ ɖa-laŋa hɛɛ. Pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩyɩɣyɩ-wɛ.

eng: This is another reason why I wrote you. I wanted to test you and see if you are obedient in everything.
local: Mbʋ́ piiyeba nɛ mama-mɩ takayaɣ ŋ́gá yɔ lɛ se: me-liu wɛɛ se mantakɩ-mɩ nɛ mana se ndʋ́ meheyiɣ-mɩ yɔ, ɩlakɩ tɩtɩŋa tɩ-yɔɔ yaa ɩɩlakɩ.

eng: Each event will happen in the right order: Christ, the first crop of the harvest, then those who belong to Christ at his coming.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ kɔŋ fezuu paa mbá nɛ pa-alɩwaatʋ: Krɩstʋ ɛcalɩnɩ fezuu. Ɛ-wayɩ lɛ, mbá pɛkɛ Krɩstʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-ñɩma yɔ, efeziɣ-wɛ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Krɩstʋ pɩsɩɣ ɛkɔɔ yɔ.

eng: The churches in the province of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you warmly in the Lord, together with the church that meets in their house.
local: Yesu aŋgba taa ñɩma mbá pɛwɛ Aazii egeetiye taa yɔ, pɛsɛ-mɩ. Akilaasɩ nɛ Prisiili nɛ Yesu ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma mbá pekpeɣliɣ pa-ɖɩɣa taa yɔ, pɛsɛ-mɩ ɖeu Kɩpaɣlʋ weyi ɛkpɛndɩɣ-ɖʋ yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ.

eng: If you forgive anyone for anything, I do too. And whatever Ive forgiven , I did it for you in the presence of Christ.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛyʋsaa nɛ ikpeɣ-i yɔ, ma ɖɔɖɔ menkpeɣiɣ-i. Ma lɛ, yee pɩɩwɛɛ se menkpeɣ nɔɔyʋ yɔ, mɛntɛm-ɩ kpeɣu mɩ-yɔɔ ɛzɩ pʋmʋnaʋ se mala Krɩstʋ ɛsɩndaa yɔ.

eng: and then the end, when Christ hands over the kingdom to God the Father, when he brings every form of rule, every authority and power to an end.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, kɛtɛzaɣ alɩwaatʋ talɩ: alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ Krɩstʋ kpakɩɣ kewiyaɣ nɛ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ Caa Ɛsɔ nesi taa. Pʋcɔ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ-kɛ lɛ, eɖeziɣ kewiyisi tɩŋa nɛ ɛyáa ɖoŋ nɛ aleewaa ñɩŋ pɩtɩŋa payɩ.

eng: All the brothers and sisters greet you. You in turn should greet each other with a holy kiss.
local: Taabalaa mbá pɛwɛ cɩnɛ yɔ, patɩŋa payɩ pɛsɛ-mɩ. Ɩsɛɛ ɖama ɖeu.

eng: This is so that we wont be taken advantage of by Satan, because we are well aware of his schemes.
local: Malabɩ ḿbʋ́ se Satanɩ ɛtaawɛnɩ waɖɛ ɖɔ-yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩtɛm sɩm mbʋ́ ɛcaɣ labʋ yɔ.

eng: It is necessary for him to rule until he puts all enemies under his feet .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛtɔɔ kewiyaɣ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ kpa e-koyindinaa tɩŋa payɩ nɛ ɛ́ɖʋzɩ-wɛ e-nesi taa yɔ.

eng: Here is my greeting in my own handwriting—Paul.
local: Ma Pɔɔlɩ, ma-maɣmaɣ mamaɣnɩ tɔm pee anɛ nɛ me-nesi. Ana lɛ se: mɛnsɛ-mɩ.

eng: When I came to Troas to preach Christs gospel, the Lord gave me an opportunity to preach.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mantalɩ Trowaasɩ se monsusuu Krɩstʋ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm yɔ, Kɩpaɣlʋ kaha-m nʋmɔʋ.

eng: Death is the last enemy to be brought to an end.
local: Koyindu weyi pɩɖɩkɩɣnɩ-ɩ tɔyɩtʋʋ yɔ lɛ sɩm.

eng: A curse on anyone who doesnt love the Lord. Come, Lord!.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛtɔsɔɔlɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se Ɛsɔ ɛwɩlɩ pʋdʋ kpalʋʋ! Maraana ta, Kɩpaɣlʋ! Kɔɔ .

eng: But I was worried because I couldnt find my brother Titus there. So I said good-bye to them and went on to Macedonia.
local: Ɛlɛ́, man-taa taawɛɛ leleŋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mantaana taabalʋ Tiiti. Peeɖe mankʋyɩ Trowaasɩ ñɩma ḿbá pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ mowolo Masedonii egeetiye taa.

eng: since he has brought everything under control under his feet. When it says that everything has been brought under his control, this clearly means everything except for the one who placed everything under his control.
local: Ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Ɛsɔ ɖʋzɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ e-nesi taa yɔ» pɩwɩlɩɣ kpayɩ se Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ ɛfɛyɩ pɩ-taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ ɖʋzɩnɩ-pʋ e-nesi taa.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
local: Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu ɛlabɩ-mɩ lootiye.

eng: But thank God, who is always leading us around through Christ as if we were in a parade. He releases the fragrance of the knowledge of him everywhere through us.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩsa Ɛsɔ se eyeba pɩtɩŋna ɖa nɛ Krɩstʋ ɖɛ-kpɛndʋʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩwakɩ tam. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, eyeki se pɩtɩŋnɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛyáa sɩm-ɩ paa le ɛzɩ tɩlaaɖɩ sɔzɩŋ ñɛyʋʋ yɔ.

eng: But when all things have been brought under his control, then the Son himself will also be under the control of the one who gave him control over everything so that God may be all in all.
local: Ɛsɔ ɖʋzʋʋ pɩtɩŋa payɩ Krɩstʋ nesi taa lɛ, ɛnʋ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ maɣmaɣ eluzi ɛ-tɩ Ɛsɔ weyi ɛɖʋzʋʋ-pʋ e-nesi taa yɔ ɛ-tɛɛ. Peeɖe Ɛsɔ kɛɣ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pi-wiyaʋ.

eng: My love is with all of you in Christ Jesus.
local: Mɔnsɔɔlɩ mbá mɩ mɩtɩŋa payɩ mɩ nɛ Krɩstʋ Yesu ɩkpɛndaa yɔ.

eng: We smell like the aroma of Christs offering to God, both to those who are being saved and to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɖɩwɛ ɛzɩ tɩlaaɖɩ kɩbaŋgʋ ŋgʋ́ Krɩstʋ ha Ɛsɔ yɔ. Kʋ-sɔzɩŋ ñɛyɩɣ mbá Ɛsɔ yakɩ pa-ñʋʋ yɔ nɛ mbá peleki yɔ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa.

eng: Otherwise, what are those who are getting baptized for the dead doing? If the dead arent raised, then why are they being baptized for them?.
local: Ɩmaɣzɩ mbá petisiɣ se palabɩ-wɛ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ sɩɖaa yɔɔ yɔ pɔ-tɔm. Yee toovenim se sɩɖaa eefeŋ kpem lɛ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma petisiɣ se palabɩ-wɛ-sɩ pɔ-yɔɔ?.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, and Timothy our brother. To Gods church that is in Corinth, along with all of Gods people throughout Achaia.
local: Ma Pɔɔlɩ weyi Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ se mɛnkɛ Krɩstʋ Yesu tiyiyu yɔ nɛ taabalʋ Timootee ɖɩmaɣnɩ-mɩ takayaɣ kanɛ. Đɩmaɣ-kɛ mbá mɩ ɩkɛ Ɛsɔ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma Kɔrɛntɩ yɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩma lɛlaa mbá patɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛ Akayɩ egeetiye tɩŋa taa yɔ.

eng: We smell like a contagious dead person to those who are dying, but we smell like the fountain of life to those who are being saved. Who is qualified for this kind of ministry?.
local: Mbá peleki yɔ, sɔzɩŋ ɛnʋ ɛkɛnɩ-wɛ sɔŋ weyi ɛkʋʋ ɛyáa yɔ. Mbá Ɛsɔ yakɩ pa-ñʋʋ yɔ, ɛkɛnɩ paba sɔzɩŋ weyi ɛhaɣ ɛyáa wezuu yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, apɩzɩɣ se ɛla tʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́?.

eng: And what about us? Why are we in danger all day every day?.
local: Đa-maɣmaɣ kpem yɔ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma alɩwaatʋ kpeekpe ɖileŋ ɖa-tɩ sɩm nɔɔ taa ḿbʋ́?.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and from our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Đɩsɛ-mɩ: ɖa-Caa Ɛsɔ nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ palabɩ-mɩ lootiye nɛ pɛhɛzɩ mɩ-laŋa.

eng: We arent like so many people who hustle the word of God to make a profit. We are speaking through Christ in the presence of God, as those who are sincere and as those who are sent from God.
local: Đɩfɛyɩ ɛzɩ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ mbá pakpakɩɣ Ɛsɔtɔm nɛ palakɩnɩ tadɩyɛ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɖa lɛ mbá Ɛsɔ tiyaa nɛ ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ toovenim ɛ-ɛsɩndaa ɖa nɛ Krɩstʋ ɖɛ-kpɛndʋʋ taa yɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I swear by the pride I have in you in Christ Jesus our Lord, Im facing death every day.
local: Taabalaa, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mankʋndʋʋnɩ-mɩ ma nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ ɖɛ-kpɛndʋʋ taa yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ mɛwɛ sɩm nɔɔ taa paa ɖooye.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! He is the compassionate Father and God of all comfort.
local: Đɩsa Ɛsɔ weyi ɛkɛ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ Caa nɛ ɛnaɣ ɖa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ nɛ ɛhɛzɩɣ ɖa-laŋa pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa yɔ.

eng: From a human point of view, what good does it do me if I fought wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead arent raised, lets eat and drink because tomorrow well die .
local: Yee ɛyáa lɩmaɣza tɩtɩŋa maawɛna nɛ mala you nɛ tɛtɛ kpɩna Efɛɛzɩ lɛ, pɩɩlɩ-m we pɩ-taa? Yee sɩɖaa eefeŋ yɔ, pʋbʋ ɖɩla ɛzɩ potuu eduuye se: «Đɩtɔɔ nɛ ɖɩñɔɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩkɔŋ sɩbʋ cee» yɔ.

eng: Hes the one who comforts us in all our trouble so that we can comfort other people who are in every kind of trouble. We offer the same comfort that we ourselves received from God.
local: Ɛhɛzɩɣ ɖa-laŋa ɖa-kʋñɔmɩŋ tɩŋa taa se pɩtɩŋnɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩpɩzɩ ɖɩhɛzɩ ɖɔɖɔ mbá pɛwɛ kʋñɔmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ taa yɔ pa-ñɩna.

eng: Dont be deceived, bad company corrupts good character.
local: Ɩtaayele nɛ nɔɔyʋ cɛtɩ-mɩ: «Ɛgbaɣdɩyɛ kɩdɛkɛdɩyɛ lesiɣ lakasɩ kɩbanzɩ.».

eng: That is because we receive so much comfort through Christ in the same way that we share so many of Christs sufferings.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɖɩtɔkɩ kʋñɔmɩŋ siŋŋ ɛzɩ Krɩstʋ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ tɩŋɩɣnɩ Krɩstʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛhɛzɩɣ ɖa-laŋa siŋŋ.

eng: Sober up by acting like you should and dont sin. Some of you are ignorant about God—I say this because you should be ashamed of yourselves!.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩmaɣzɩ mɩ-ñʋŋ taa camɩyɛ ɛzɩ pʋpɔzʋʋ yɔ nɛ iyele kɩwɛɛkɩm labʋ. Me-liu wɛɛ se fɛyɛ ɛkpa-mɩ yɔɔ mɔyɔɔdʋʋ se mɩ-taa nabɛyɛ tasɩ Ɛsɔ.

eng: So if we have trouble, it is to bring you comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is to bring you comfort from the experience of endurance while you go through the same sufferings that we also suffer.
local: Paa ɖɩtɔkɩ kʋñɔmɩŋ yɔ, ɖɩtɔkɩ-ɩ se Ɛsɔ ɛtɩŋnɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛhɛzɩ mɩ-laŋa nɛ ɛya mɩ-ñʋʋ. Yee Ɛsɔ ɛhɛzɩɣ ɖa-laŋa yɔ, ɛhɛzɩɣ-yɛ se ɖɩpɩzɩ ɖɩhɛzɩ mɩ-ñɩna ɖɔɖɔ nɛ ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ kʋñɔmɩŋ weyi ɩtɔkɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ɖa yɔ ɩ-taa.

eng: But someone will say, How are the dead raised? What kind of body will they have when they come back.
local: Nɔɔyʋ kaɣ pɔzʋʋ se: «Ɛzɩma sɩɖaa kɔŋ fem? Tomnaɣ ŋgá pɔkɔŋ wɛnaʋ?».

eng: Our hope for you is certain, because we know that as you are partners in suffering, so also you are partners in comfort.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩtaɣ-mɩ liu siŋŋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩtɛm naʋ se ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɖa nɛ mɩ ɖɩtɔkɩ kʋñɔmɩŋ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ hɛzɩɣ mɩ-laŋa ɛzɩ ɛhɛzʋʋ ɖa-ñɩna yɔ.

eng: Look, fool! When you put a seed into the ground, it doesnt come back to life unless it dies.
local: Kɩmɛlɛŋ ña! Ŋtatɩlɩ se piye nɖɩ́ ŋɖuu yɔ, ɖɩtaɖazɩ yɔ, ɖɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɖɩñɔ yaa we?.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we dont want you to be unaware of the troubles that we went through in Asia. We were weighed down with a load of suffering that was so far beyond our strength that we were afraid we might not survive.
local: Taabalaa, ɖe-liu wɛɛ se ɩtɩlɩ kʋñɔŋ sɔsɔʋ ŋgʋ́ ɖɩɩtɔɔ Aazii ɛjaɖɛ taa nɛ pɩnɩɩ-ɖʋ siŋŋ nɛ pɩɖɛlɛsɩ ɖa-hama yɔɔ yɔ. Halɩ ɖɩɩmaɣzaa se ɖɩtɛŋɛlɩɣ kɩ-taa.

eng: What you put in the ground doesnt have the shape that it will have, but its a bare grain of wheat or some other seed.
local: Piye yem ŋɖuu. Pʋtɔma yɔ, mɩɖɛ yaa tɔɔnaɣ nakɛyɛ piye, ńɖɩ́ ñɔʋna. Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ ŋɖuwa yɔ, pɩɩwɛɣ ɛzɩ mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ ñɔʋ yɔ.

eng: It certainly seemed to us as if we had gotten the death penalty. This was so that we would have confidence in God, who raises the dead, instead of ourselves.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɖa-maɣmaɣ ɖɩtɛm sɩm sɔŋ nɩʋ. Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se ɖɩtaatasɩ ɖa-tɩ taʋ liu. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩtaa Ɛsɔ weyi efeziɣ sɩɖaa yɔ e-ɖeke koŋ.

eng: God gives it the sort of shape that he chooses, and he gives each of the seeds its own shape.
local: Ɛsɔ haɣnɩ piye ńɖɩ́ tomnaɣ ŋgá e-liu wɛɛ yɔ. Paa pee wena lɛ, ɛhaɣ-yɛ tomnaɣ ŋgá kamʋnɩ-yɛ yɔ.

eng: Are we starting to commend ourselves again? We dont need letters of introduction to you or from you like other people, do we?.
local: Đɩyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pɩwɩlɩɣ se ɖɩɖaɣnɩɣ ɖa-tɩ wɩlʋʋ naa we? Yaa pʋpɔzʋʋ se pama takayɩsɩ ɛzɩ lɛlaa lakʋʋ yɔ nɛ pawɩlɩnɩ-mɩ mbʋ́ ɖɩkɛwa yɔ? Đɩɩpɔzʋʋ-mɩ ɖɔɖɔ se ɩma takayaɣ nakɛyɛ nɛ ɩwɩlɩnɩ-ɖʋ lɛlaa.

eng: God rescued us from a terrible death, and he will rescue us. We have set our hope on him that he will rescue us again.
local: Ɛnʋ yabɩnɩ ɖa-ñʋʋ sɩm ḿbʋ́ pɩ-taa. Ɛkɔŋ ɛtasɩ ɖa-ñʋʋ yabʋ. Toovenim ɖɩtaɣ-ɩ se ɛnʋ tasɩɣnɩ ɖa-ñʋʋ yabʋ.

eng: All flesh isnt alike. Humans have one kind of flesh, animals have another kind of flesh, birds have another kind of flesh, and fish have another kind.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋwɛ wezuu yɔ, pɩtɩŋa pu-tomnasɩ tɛkɛ kʋɖʋmaɣ: ɛyáa wɛnɩ po-tomnaɣ, kpɩna ñɛwɛnɩ a-ñɩŋga, sumasɩ ñɛwɛnɩ sɩ-ñɩŋga nɛ kpakpasɩ ñɛwɛnɩ sɩ-ñɩŋga ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: You are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.
local: Mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩwɛ ɛzɩ takayaɣ ŋgá Krɩstʋ mawa yɔ. Ŋ́gá wɩlɩɣnɩ ɛyáa mbʋ́ ɖɩkɛnaa yɔ. Kɛwɛ ɖa-laŋa taa. Ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ pɩzɩɣ pasɩm-kɛ nɛ pakalɩ-kɛ.

eng: since you are helping with your prayer for us. Then many people can thank God on our behalf for the gift that was given to us through the prayers of many people.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩsɩŋnɩ-ɖʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩsɛɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɔ-yɔɔ yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɛyáa sakɩyɛ sɛɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ecosi-ɖʋ nɛ pasam-ɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. The heavenly bodies have one kind of glory, and the earthly bodies have another kind of glory.
local: Tomnasɩ ńzɩ́ sɩ-ɖeu wɛ ndɩ nɛ wɩsɩ nɛ fenaɣ nɛ tɩɩnzɩ pa-ñɩmbʋ. Tataa tomnasɩ ńzɩ́ sɩ-ɖeu wɛ ndɩ nɛ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ pi-ɖeu.

eng: You show that you are Christs letter, delivered by us. You werent written with ink but with the Spirit of the living God. You werent written on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
local: Mɩwɛnɩ ɛzɩ takayaɣ ŋ́gá yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɔ-yɔɔ Krɩstʋ tɩŋnaa nɛ ɛma-kɛ. Pɩtɛkɛ tɔlɩm ɛmanɩ-kɛ, ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ wezuu tʋ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɛmanɩ-kɛ. Pɩtɛkɛ pɩtaahalanzɩ yɔɔ ɛma-kɛ, ɛlɛ́, ɛyáa laŋa taa ɛmawa.

eng: We have conducted ourselves with godly sincerity and pure motives in the world, and especially toward you. This is why we are confident, and our conscience confirms this. We didnt act with human wisdom but we relied on the grace of God.
local: Mbʋ́ ɖɩkʋndʋʋna yɔ lɛ se: ɖɩnaɣ ɖa-taa se ɖe-wezuu caɣʋ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ, kɔzɩkɔzɩ mɩ-ɛsɩndaa lɛ, ɖɩwɛ yayɩ nɛ ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ toovenim. Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ pɩlɩnɩ pɩtɩŋa. Pɩtɛkɛ ɛyáa lɔŋsɩnɖɛ yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ lootiye yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa.

eng: The sun has one kind of glory, the moon has another kind of glory, and the stars have another kind of glory .
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, wɩsɩ ɖeu wɛ ndɩ, fenaɣ ñɩmbʋ ñɛwɛɛ ndɩ nɛ tɩɩnzɩ ñɩmbʋ ñɛwɛɛ ndɩ ɖɔɖɔ. Halɩ tɩɩnzɩ tɩtɩŋa taa lɛ, paa ŋgá nɛ ke-ɖeu.

eng: This is the confidence that we have through Christ in the presence of God.
local: Đɩyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Krɩstʋ yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnaa nɛ ɖɩtaa Ɛsɔ liu.

eng: We dont write anything to you except what you can read and also understand. I hope that you will understand totally.
local: Maamaɣ-mɩ natʋyʋ yem nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ ndʋ́ ɩkalɩɣ nɛ ɩnɩɣ yɔ. Mantaɣ liu se ɩkɔŋ tɩtɩŋa tɩ-taa nɩʋ camɩyɛ. Halɩ ɩtɛm pazʋʋ tɩ-taa nɩʋ se puwiye nɖɩ́ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu pɩsɩɣ yɔ, ɩkʋndʋʋnɩ-ɖʋ ɛzɩ ɖɩkʋndʋʋnɩ-mɩ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ.

eng: Its the same with the resurrection of the dead: a rotting body is put into the ground, but what is raised wont ever decay.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ sɩɖaa wɛɣ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pefeŋ yɔ. Pepiŋ-wɛ lɛ, po-tomnaɣ ɖazɩ, pefeŋ lɛ, po-tomnaɣ ɛɛtasɩɣ ɖazʋʋ kaʋ.

eng: It isnt that we ourselves are qualified to claim that anything came from us. No, our qualification is from God.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩnawa se ɖɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɖɩla tʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ nɛ ɖa-maɣmaɣ ɖo-ɖoŋ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ yebina se ɖɩpɩzɩ ɖɩlabɩ-ɖɩ.

eng: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to visit you first so that you could have a second opportunity to see me.
local: Maatɛm naʋ siŋŋ se ɩnɩɣnɩ-m yɔɔ maaɖʋwa se mancalɩɣnɩ mɩ-tɛ kɔm nɛ mɩ-laŋa hʋlʋmɩ nabʋlɛ.

eng: Its degraded when its put into the ground, but its raised in glory. Its weak when its put into the ground, but its raised in power.
local: Pepiŋ-wɛ nɛ pʋtɔdɩyɛ, ɛlɛ́ pefemni koboyaɣ. Pepiŋ-wɛ nɛ ɛjandʋ, ɛlɛ́ pefemni ɖoŋ.

eng: He has qualified us as ministers of a new covenant, not based on what is written but on the Spirit, because what is written kills, but the Spirit gives life.
local: Eyeba ɖɔɖɔ se ɖɩkɛ nɔɔ haʋ kɩfam tʋmlaɖaa. Nɔɔ haʋ kɩfam ḿbʋ́, pɩtɛkɛ paɣtʋ ndʋ́ paamawa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩkɛ mbʋ́ pɩlɩnɩ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu cɔlɔ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paɣtʋ yeki nɛ ɛyáa sɩkɩ. Ɛlɛ́, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ñahaɣ wezuu.

eng: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and then come to you again on my way back from Macedonia, at which point I was hoping you would help me on my way to Judea.
local: Mankʋyʋʋ mɩ-tɛ lɛ, me-liu wɛɛ se mowolo Masedonii nɛ malɩɩnɩ peeɖe lɛ, mantasɩ mɩ-tɛ tɩŋʋʋ. Pʋbʋ ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩsɩnɩ-m nɛ mowolo Yudee.

eng: Its a physical body when its put into the ground, but its raised as a spiritual body. If theres a physical body, theres also a spiritual body.
local: Pepiŋ-wɛ nɛ tomnaɣ ŋgá panaɣ yɔ, ɛlɛ́ pefemni ɛsɔdaa ñɩŋga. Yee tomnaɣ ŋgá panaɣ yɔ kɛwɛɛ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛsɔdaa ñɩŋga wɛɣ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: The ministry that brought death was carved in letters on stone tablets. It came with such glory that the Israelites couldnt look for long at Moses face because his face was shining with glory, even though it was a fading glory.
local: Đooo lɔŋ Ɛsɔ kama paɣtʋ piye piye pɩtaahalanzɩ yɔɔ nɛ e-koboyaɣ ñɩlɩsɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, eeyeba nɛ Mooyiizi ɛsɩndaa ñɩlɩsɩ siŋŋ nɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma tapɩzɩɣ se pɔcɔnɩ-ɩ. Ŋgʋ́, koboyaɣ ŋ́gá kɛtɛwɛɣ tam. Yee paɣtʋ ndʋ́ tiyeki nɛ ɛyáa sɩkɩ yɔ, tɩwɩlɩ-wɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ koboyaɣ paɣlaa yɔ lɛ.

eng: So I wasnt unreliable when I planned to do this, was I? Or do I make decisions with a substandard human process so that I say Yes, yes and No, no at the same time?.
local: Mbʋ́ maaɖʋwa yɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ yem malaba-pʋ yaa we? Yaa ma-lɩmaɣza ana, ɛyáa lɩmaɣza akɛnaa se maapɩzɩ mɛwɛnɩ nɔsɩ naalɛ tɔm kʋɖʋmtʋ yɔɔ?.

eng: So it is also written, The first human, Adam, became a living person, and the last Adam became a spirit that gives life.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Kajalaɣ ɛyʋ weyi Ɛsɔ ma-ɩ nɛ payaɣ-ɩ se Aɖam yɔ, ɛpɩsɩ weyi ɛwɛ wezuu yɔ.» Ɛlɛ́, kɛtɛzaɣ Aɖam lɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ŋgʋ́ kɩhaɣ wezuu yɔ.

eng: Wont the ministry of the Spirit be much more glorious?.
local: ɛzɩma Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tʋmɩyɛ ñaawɩlɩɣ koboyaɣ ŋ́gá ka-paɣlɩka kpem nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ?.

eng: But as God is faithful, our message to you isnt both yes and no.
local: Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩkɛ toovenim se Ɛsɔ ɛɛlɛɣzɩɣ ɛ-tɔm kaʋ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ maayɔɔdʋʋnɩ-mɩ nɔsɩ naalɛ.

eng: But the physical body comes first, not the spiritual one—the spiritual body comes afterward.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ ɛsɔdaa tomnaɣ calɩnɩ wɛʋ, ɛlɛ́, kedeŋa yɔɔ ñɩŋga. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛsɔdaa ñɩŋga ñɔkɔɔ.

eng: If the ministry that brought condemnation has glory, how much more glorious is the ministry that brings righteousness?.
local: Yee paɣtʋ ndʋ́ tiyeki se Ɛsɔ ɛhʋʋnɩ ɛyáa nɛ ɛkʋ pɔ-tɔm yɔ, tɩwɩlɩ-ɖʋ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ e-koboyaɣ paɣlaa yɔ lɛ, ɛzɩma nɔɔ haʋ kɩfam ḿbʋ́ piyeki se Ɛsɔ eɖezi-ɖʋ yɔ, pɩɩwɩlɩɣ e-koboyaɣ paɣlɩka kpem nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ḿbʋ́?.

eng: Gods Son, Jesus Christ, is the one who was preached among you by us—through me, Silvanus, and Timothy—he wasnt yes and no. In him it is always yes.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ Krɩstʋ Yesu weyi ma nɛ Silaasɩ nɛ Timootee ɖisusi ɛ-tɔm mɩ-hɛkʋ taa yɔ, ɛtɔyɔɔdɩ nɔsɩ naalɛ tɔm kʋɖʋmtʋ yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ pɩkɛ toovenim yɔ «Ɛɛ» ɖeke ɛtɔŋ.

eng: The first human was from the earth made from dust; the second human is from heaven.
local: Tɛtʋ pamanɩ kajalaɣ ɛyʋ nɛ ɛkɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ ñɩnʋ. Naalɛ tʋ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ ñalɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa.

eng: In fact, what was glorious isnt glorious now, because of the glory that is brighter.
local: Halɩ ɖɩpɩzɩɣ ɖɩtɔ se koboyaɣ ŋgá kaañɩlɩsaa yɔ, kaatasɩɣ ñɩlɩsʋʋ ŋgá kañɩlɩsɩɣ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ kɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: All of Gods promises have their yes in him. That is why we say Amen through him to the glory of God.
local: Ɛnʋ wɩlɩɣnɩ-ɖʋ kaŋŋ se mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ Ɛsɔ heyi-ɖʋ se ɛlakɩ-ɖʋ yɔ, ɛlakɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ pɩtɩŋnɩ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɛ-yɔɔ ɖɩtɩŋɩɣna nɛ ɖiheyi Ɛsɔ se: «Pɩla ḿbʋ́!» Nɛ pɩkɛnɩ Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ koboyaɣ.

eng: The nature of the person made of dust is shared by people who are made of dust, and the nature of the heavenly person is shared by heavenly people.
local: Mbá pɛwɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ yɔ, pɛwɛ ɛzɩ weyi pamanɩ tɛtʋ yɔ. Mbá pɛkɛ ɛsɔdaa ñɩma yɔ, paba ñɛwɛ ɛzɩ weyi ɛlɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ.

eng: If the glory that fades away was glorious, how much more glorious is the one that lasts!.
local: Toovenim lɛ, yee mbʋ́ pɩɩwɛɣ tam yɔ, pɩwɩlɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ koboyaɣ paɣlaa yɔ lɛ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pʋbʋ ñɛwɛɣ tam yɔ, pɩɩwɩlɩɣ-kɛ kpem nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ḿbʋ́?.

eng: God is the one who establishes us with you in Christ and who anointed us.
local: Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ ɛ-maɣmaɣ eyekini se ɖa nɛ mɩ ɖɩkpa ɖoŋ ɖa nɛ Krɩstʋ ɖɛ-kpɛndʋʋ taa. Ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛlɩzɩnɩ-ɖʋ ɛsɩɩ hɔɔlʋʋ.

eng: We will look like the heavenly person in the same way as we have looked like the person made from dust.
local: Đa lɛ, ɖɩwɛ ɛzɩ weyi pamanɩ tɛtʋ yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ ɖɩkɔŋ wɛʋ ɛzɩ weyi ɛlɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ.

eng: So, since we have such a hope, we act with great confidence.
local: Đɩyɔɔdʋʋ kaalɛyɩtʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩtaɣ liu mbʋ́ pʋwɛɣ tam yɔ.

eng: God also sealed us and gave the Spirit as a down payment in our hearts.
local: Ɛlabɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ ɛ-yʋsaɣ nɛ ɛha-ɖʋ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu. Ḿbʋ́ wɩlɩɣna se ɛkɔŋ-ɖʋ haʋ ñɩm lɛɛbʋ mbʋ́ eheyi-ɖʋ se ɛhaɣ-ɖʋ yɔ.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: Flesh and blood cant inherit Gods kingdom. Something that rots cant inherit something that doesnt decay.
local: Taabalaa, ndʋ́ meheyiɣ-mɩ yɔ lɛ se: ɖo-tomnasɩ nzɩ́ sɩwɛ ɛzʋnɔ yɔ, sɩɩpɩzɩɣ sɩsʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mbʋ́ pɩɖazɩɣ yɔ, pɩɩpɩzɩɣ pɩpɩsɩ mbʋ́ pɩɩɖazɩɣ wiɖiyi kaʋ yɔ.

eng: We arent like Moses, who used to put a veil over his face so that the Israelites couldnt watch the end of what was fading away.
local: Đɩɩlakɩ ɛzɩ Mooyiizi kohuuzuu ɛ-ɛsɩndaa kɛtɛɛka yɔ. Ehuuzaɣ-kɛ se Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma ɩtaana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa ñɩlɩsʋʋ mʋkɩ yɔ.

eng: I call on God as my witness—I didnt come again to Corinth because I wanted to spare you.
local: Ŋgʋ́, Ɛsɔ mankpakɩɣ se ɛkɛ ma-aseɣɖe tʋ, yee mɛncɛtɩɣ yɔ, ɛkʋ-m. Mbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ mantatasɩ pɩsʋʋ mɩ-tɛ Kɔrɛntɩ yɔ lɛ se: maasɔɔlaa se malɩzɩ-mɩ pɩ-taa.

eng: Listen, Im telling you a secret: All of us wont die, but we will all be changed—.
local: Iwelesi nɛ ɩnɩɩ: mancɩkɩ-mɩ cɩkpalʋʋ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ tɔm natʋyʋ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ɖɩɩkɔŋ ɖatɩŋa sɩbʋ. Ɛlɛ́, pɔkɔŋ ɖatɩŋa payɩ lɛɣzʋʋ tomnaɣ.

eng: But their minds were closed. Right up to the present day the same veil remains when the old covenant is read. The veil is not removed because it is taken away by Christ.
local: Pa-ñʋŋ taa kakpa ɖoŋ nɛ halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ yee pakalɩɣ Nɔɔ Haʋ Kɩbɩm Takayaɣ yɔ, paanɩɣ kɔ-tɔbʋʋ. Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ kɛtɛɛka ŋgá kohuuzi pɛ-ɛsɩndaa tam yɔ. Weyi ɛ nɛ Krɩstʋ pɛkpɛndaa yɔ, e-ɖeke koŋ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa kɛtɛɛka ŋ́gá kamʋkɩ.

eng: It isnt that we are trying to control your faith, but we are working with you for your happiness, because you stand firm in your faith.
local: Đɩɩcɛyɩɣ-mɩ se ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtɛm wɛnaʋ lidaʋ siŋŋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɖe-liu wɛɛ se ɖa nɛ mɩ ɖɩla tʋmɩyɛ nɛ ɩwɛɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ taa.

eng: in an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the final trumpet. The trumpet will blast, and the dead will be raised with bodies that wont decay, and we will be changed.
local: tam kʋɖʋm taa yem ɛzɩ pacɩbɩyʋʋ ɛsɛ yɔ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ payaɣ kɛtɛzaɣ ɛgandɩyɛ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, payaɣ-ɖɩ lɛ, sɩɖaa feŋ nɛ papɩsɩ mbá paapɩzɩɣ patasɩ sɩbʋ wiɖiyi kaʋ yɔ nɛ mbá ɖa ɖɩwɛ wezuu yɔ, pɛlɛɣzɩ ɖatɩŋa payɩ ɖo-tomnaɣ.

eng: Even today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their hearts.
local: Halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ, yee pakalɩɣ Mooyiizi takayɩsɩ yɔ, pʋwɛ ɛzɩ kɛtɛɛka ŋ́gá kohuuzi pa-lɩmaɣza yɔɔ ḿbʋ́ yɔ.

eng: Its necessary for this rotting body to be clothed with what cant decay, and for the body that is dying to be clothed in what cant die.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɖo-tomnaɣ ŋgá kaɖazɩɣ yɔ, kapɩsɩ ŋgá kaaɖazɩɣ wiɖiyi kaʋ yɔ nɛ ɖo-tomnaɣ ŋgá kasɩkɩ yɔ, kapɩsɩ ɖɔɖɔ ŋgá kaasɩkɩ wiɖiyi kaʋ yɔ.

eng: But whenever someone turns back to the Lord, the veil is removed.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ nɔɔyʋ yeki ɛ-nʋmɔʋ nɛ ɛpɩsɩnɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔ yɔ, kɛtɛɛka ŋ́gá, kamʋkɩ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa» yɔ.

eng: And when the rotting body has been clothed in what cant decay, and the dying body has been clothed in what cant die, then this statement in scripture will happen: Death has been swallowed up by a victory .
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖo-tomnaɣ ŋgá kasɩkɩ nɛ kaɖazɩ yɔ, kapɩsɩɣ ŋgá kaasɩkɩ nɛ kaaɖazɩɣ wiɖiyi kaʋ yɔ lɛ, tɔm ndʋ́ pamawa yɔ tɩlakɩ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Pawabɩ sɩm nɛ pamʋʋzɩ-pʋ.

eng: The Lord is the Spirit, and where the Lords Spirit is, there is freedom.
local: Ŋgʋ́, Kɩpaɣlʋ ɛnʋ lɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu. Nɛ ɖenɖe Kɩpaɣlʋ Fezuu wɛɛ yɔ, peeɖe ñɩma wɛ pa-tɩ yɔɔ.

eng: Where is your victory, Death? Where is your sting, Death? .
local: Sɩm, ño-ɖoŋ wɛ le? Sɩm, ñɔ-sɔtʋ ndʋ́ ŋkʋʋna yɔ tɩwɛ le?».

eng: All of us are looking with unveiled faces at the glory of the Lord as if we were looking in a mirror. We are being transformed into that same image from one degree of glory to the next degree of glory. This comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mbá ɖa ɖatɩŋa payɩ pokuli ɖɛ-ɛsɛ yɔɔ kɛtɛɛka ŋ́gá yɔ, ɖɩnaɣ Kɩpaɣlʋ koboyaɣ tam nɛ ɖɩtɛzɩɣ wɛʋ ɛzɩ ɛnʋ yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ pɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔɔ nɛ e-koboyaɣ tɛzɩɣ ɖɔʋ ɖɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: Deaths sting is sin, and the power of sin is the Law.
local: Sɩm sɔtʋ lɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm nɛ paɣtʋ ñɛtɛzɩɣ kɩwɛɛkɩm ɖoŋ haʋ.

eng: Thanks be to God, who gives us this victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩsa Ɛsɔ weyi ɛtɩŋɩɣnɩ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛhaɣ-ɖʋ ɖoŋ nɛ ɖɩwakɩ yɔ.

eng: As a result of all this, my loved brothers and sisters, you must stand firm, unshakable, excelling in the work of the Lord as always, because you know that your labor isnt going to be for nothing in the Lord.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, taabalaa kɩbama, ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ camɩyɛ kpam. Pɩtaaliɣti-mɩ, ɩla Kɩpaɣlʋ tʋma paa ɖooye. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩtɛm naʋ se tʋma wena ɩlakɩ mɩ nɛ ɩ mɩ-kpɛndʋʋ taa yɔ, atɛkɛ falaa.

eng: This is why we dont get discouraged, given that we received this ministry in the same way that we received Gods mercy.
local: Ɛsɔ labɩnɩ-ɖʋ ɖeu nɛ ɛɖʋ-ɖʋ nɔɔ haʋ kɩfam tʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́. Pʋ-yɔɔ piitisiɣ ɖo-toma taa.

eng: Instead, we reject secrecy and shameful actions. We dont use deception, and we dont tamper with Gods word. Instead, we commend ourselves to everyones conscience in the sight of God by the public announcement of the truth.
local: Đikizi mɛsaɣ tɛɛ lakasɩ nzɩ́ sɩwɛ fɛyɛ yɔ. Điipeɣziɣ ɛyáa, ɖɩɩlɛɣzɩɣ Ɛsɔtɔm. Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩñɩnɩɣ se paa weyi ɛtaa-ɖʋ liu Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa yɔɔ ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ toovenim kpayɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Yee Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ndʋ́ ɖisusuu yɔ tɩ-taa ɛwɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ, mbá peleki yɔ tɩwɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ.

eng: We know that if the tent that we live in on earth is torn down, we have a building from God. Its a house that isnt handmade, which is eternal and located in heaven.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɖɩnawa se yee tomnaɣ ŋgá ɖɩwɛnɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ nɛ kɛwɛ ɛzɩ kɩɖanɖʋʋ ŋgʋ́ ɖɩwɛ kɩ-tɛɛ yɔ, kɔyɔlaa yɔ, ɖɩwɛnɩ tomnaɣ lɛɛka ɛsɔdaa, kɛwɛ ɛzɩ ɖɩɣa yɔ. Pɩtɛkɛ ɛyáa manɩ-kɛ, ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ maɣmaɣ manɩ-kɛ. Kɛwɛɣ tam.

eng: The god of this age has blinded the minds of those who dont have faith so they couldnt see the light of the gospel that reveals Christs glory. Christ is the image of God.
local: Laŋa kpɩzɩŋ tɩnaa mbá kedeŋa wiyaʋ kañadʋ huuzi pa-lɩmaɣza yɔɔ yɔ, ḿbá tɩwɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ. Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se pataana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ñalɩmɩyɛ wɩlɩɣ Krɩstʋ koboyaɣ yɔ. Ɛnʋ lɛ Ɛsɔ lɛzʋʋ.

eng: We groan while we live in this residence. We really want to dress ourselves with our building from heaven—.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɖisisiɣ kɩɖanɖʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-tɛɛ nɛ ɖe-liu wɛɛ kpem se Ɛsɔ ɛsʋzɩ-ɖʋ ɖa-ɖɩɣa ŋ́gá ka-taa nɛ pʋwɛɛ ɛzɩ posu-ɖʋ wondu yɔ.

eng: We dont preach about ourselves. Instead, we preach about Jesus Christ as Lord, and we describe ourselves as your slaves for Jesus sake.
local: Toovenim lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ ɖa-maɣmaɣ ɖɔ-tɔm ɖisusuu. Ɛlɛ́, Krɩstʋ Yesu weyi ɛkɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔ ɛ-tɔm ɖisusuu. Ndʋ́ ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ ɖa-maɣmaɣ ɖɔ-yɔɔ yɔ lɛ se: ɖɩkɛ mɩ-tʋmlaɖaa Yesu yɔɔ.

eng: since we assume that when we take off this tent, we wont find out that we are naked.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee ɖɩsʋ ka-taa yɔ, ɖɩɩwɛɣ tombolo.

eng: God said that light should shine out of the darkness. He is the same one who shone in our hearts to give us the light of the knowledge of Gods glory in the face of Jesus Christ.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ weyi ɛtɔm se: «Ñalɩmɩyɛ ɛñalɩ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa» yɔ, ɛnʋ ɖɔɖɔ yebina nɛ ɛ-ñalɩmɩyɛ ñalɩ ɖa-laŋa taa. Ńɖɩ́ yekina se ɖɩtɩlɩ e-koboyaɣ ŋgá kañɩlɩsɩɣ Krɩstʋ ɛsɩndaa yɔ.

eng: Yes, while we are in this tent we groan, because we are weighed down. We want to be dressed not undressed, so that what is dying can be swallowed up by life.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa ɖɩwɛɣ kɩɖanɖʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-tɛɛ yɔ, ɖisisiɣ nɛ pɩnɩɣ-ɖʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩɩcaɣ se Ɛsɔ ɛhɔzɩ tomnaɣ ŋgá ɖɩwɛ ka-taa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɖe-liu wɛɛ se esuu-ɖʋ ɖɔɖɔ ɛsɔdaa ñɩŋga. Peeɖe mbʋ́ pʋwɛ wezuu yɔ, pɩlɛɣzɩɣ mbʋ́ pɩsɩkɩ yɔ pu-lone taa.

eng: But we have this treasure in clay pots so that the awesome power belongs to God and doesnt come from us.
local: Đɩwɛ ɛzɩ cʋʋ pɛŋ weyi ɩ-taa wɛ ñɩm sɔsɔm ḿbʋ́ yɔ se paa weyi ɛna se ɖoŋ sɔsɔŋ ɛnʋ ɛtɛkɛ ɖa-ñɩŋ, ɛlɛ́ Ɛsɔ ñɩŋ ɛkɛnaa.

eng: Now the one who prepared us for this very thing is God, and God gave us the Spirit as a down payment for our home.
local: Ɛsɔ maɣmaɣ ɖʋna se pɩlɛɣzɩ-ɖʋ ḿbʋ́. Ɛha-ɖʋ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu. Ḿbʋ́ wɩlɩɣnɩ-ɖʋ se ɛhaɣ-ɖʋ ɛ-ñɩm lɛɛbʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: We are experiencing all kinds of trouble, but we arent crushed. We are confused, but we arent depressed.
local: Ɛyáa cɔ-ɖʋ pata nɛ panazɩɣ-ɖʋ, ɛlɛ́, pɩtakɩlɩ-ɖʋ. Đɩɩnaɣ se ɖɩla ɛzɩma, ɛlɛ́, ɖɩtɔlɔ liu.

eng: So we are always confident, because we know that while we are living in the body, we are away from our home with the Lord.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩtaɣ-ɩ liu paa ɖooye. Ŋgʋ́, ɖɩtɛm naʋ se yee ɖɩwɛ tomnaɣ kanɛ ka-taa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɖiposini Kɩpaɣlʋ ɖɩɣa.

eng: We are harassed, but we arent abandoned. We are knocked down, but we arent knocked out.
local: Pɛwɛ ɖa-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ, ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ tɔlɔ-ɖʋ. Payakɩ-ɖʋ nɛ tɛtʋ, ɛlɛ́, ɖɩɩsɩkɩ.

eng: We live by faith and not by sight.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɖɩtanata-pʋ nɛ ɖɛ-ɛsɛ, ɛlɛ́, ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔɔ ɖɩɖɔŋ ḿbʋ́.

eng: We always carry Jesus death around in our bodies so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies.
local: Yesu sɩm siziŋ wɛ ɖo-tomnaɣ taa tam se pɩwɩlɩ se e-wezuu wɛ ɖa-taa.

eng: We are confident, and we would prefer to leave the body and to be at home with the Lord.
local: Đɩtaɣ-ɩ liu siŋŋ nɛ ɖɩsɔɔlɩ se ɖɩlɩɩ tomnaɣ kanɛ ka-taa nɛ ɖiwolo ɖɩwɛɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ cɔlɔ.

eng: We who are alive are always being handed over to death for Jesus sake so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies that are dying.
local: Yesu yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩwɛ sɩm nɔɔ taa tam ɖe-wezuu caɣʋ taa se pɩwɩlɩ se paa ɖo-tomnaɣ ɛsɩkɩ yɔ, e-wezuu wɛ ka-taa tam.

eng: So our goal is to be acceptable to him, whether we are at home or away from home.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ ɖɩwɛɛ ɖɩlʋkɩ se paa ɖɩɩwɛ cɩnɛ yaa peeɖe yɔ yɔ, ɖɔ-tɔm ɛkɛdɩnɩ-ɩ.

eng: So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩwɛ sɩm nɔɔ taa tam. Ḿbʋ́ yebina ihiɣ wezuu.

eng: We all must appear before Christ in court so that each person can be paid back for the things that were done while in the body, whether they were good or bad.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɖatɩŋa payɩ ɖiwolo Krɩstʋ ɛsɩndaa nɛ ɛhʋʋnɩ-ɖʋ tɔm nɛ ɛhɛyɩ paa weyi kɩbandʋ yaa kɩdɛkɛdɩm mbʋ́ ɛɛlaba alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛɛwɛ e-tomnaɣ taa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: We have the same faithful spirit as what is written in scripture: I had faith, and so I spoke. We also have faith, and so we also speak.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɖɩwɛnɩ lɩmaɣza kʋɖʋma ɖe-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ taa ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Mɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ yɔɔ mɔyɔɔdaa» yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ.

eng: So we try to persuade people, since we know what it means to fear the Lord. We are well known by God, and I hope that in your heart we are well known by you as well.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ ɖɩnawa se pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩña Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔ ɖɩñɩnɩɣ se ɛyáa etisi ɖɔ-tɔm. Ɛsɔ sɩm-ɖʋ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ ɖa-taa lotu nɛ manaɣ se mɩ-lɩmaɣza taa lɛ, ɩsɩm-ɖʋ camɩyɛ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: We do this because we know that the one who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us with Jesus, and he will bring us into his presence along with you.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩnawa se Ɛsɔ weyi efezi Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu yɔ, ɛkɔŋ-ɖʋ fezuu ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ efezuu-i yɔ nɛ eyele ɖa nɛ mɩ ɖiwolo ɛ-cɔlɔ.

eng: We arent trying to commend ourselves to you again. Instead, we are giving you an opportunity to be proud of us so that you could answer those who take pride in superficial appearance, and not in what is in the heart.
local: Đɩɩɖaɣnɩɣ ɖa-tɩ wɩlʋʋ mɩ-cɔlɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩhaɣ-mɩ waɖɛ se ɩkʋndɩnɩ-ɖʋ nɛ ɩpɩzɩ icosi mbá pakpazɩɣ pa-tɩ ɛsɛ yɔɔ yem nɛ paanɩɣnɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɛyʋ taa yɔ.

eng: All these things are for your benefit. As grace increases to benefit more and more people, it will cause gratitude to increase, which results in Gods glory.
local: Mɩ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́ se Ɛsɔ ɛtɩŋnɩ e-lootiye yɔɔ nɛ ɛya ɛyáa sakɩyɛ ñʋʋ. Ɛlakɩ ḿbʋ́ se mbá pasaŋ-ɩ se ɛ-hɩɖɛ ɛsɛɛ yɔ, pɛtɛzɩ ɖɔʋ.

eng: If we are crazy, its for Gods sake. If we are rational, its for your sake.
local: Yee ɖɩɩwɛ ɛzɩ kɩmɛlɛmɩŋ yɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa ɖɩɩwɛ ḿbʋ́. Ŋgʋ́, ɖɩtɛkɛ kɩmɛlɛmɩŋ. Ḿbʋ́ wazɩɣnɩ-mɩ.

eng: So we arent depressed. But even if our bodies are breaking down on the outside, the person that we are on the inside is being renewed every day.
local: Tɔm ńdʋ́, tɩ-yɔɔ piitisiɣ ɖo-toma taa. Paa ɖo-ɖoŋ ɛɖɔŋ tɛm yɔ, ɖa-kalɩzaɣ tɛzɩɣ kpaʋ ɖoŋ tam.

eng: The love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this: one died for the sake of all; therefore, all died.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Krɩstʋ sɔɔlɩm seɣtiɣni mbá ɖa ɖitisaa se: ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm koŋ sɩbɩnɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ yɔɔ yɔ. Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ sɩba.

eng: Our temporary minor problems are producing an eternal stockpile of glory for us that is beyond all comparison.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kʋñɔmɩŋ cikpeŋ weyi ɖɩtɔkɩ ɖe-wezuu caɣʋ taa yɔ, ɩhaɣ-ɖʋ koboyaɣ sɔsɔɔ ŋgá kakɩlɩ ɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ kaatɛŋ wiɖiyi kaʋ yɔ.

eng: He died for the sake of all so that those who are alive should live not for themselves but for the one who died for them and was raised.
local: Krɩstʋ sɩbɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ yɔɔ se mbá pɛwɛ wezuu yɔ, pataatasɩ wezuu caɣʋ ɛzɩ pa-maɣmaɣ pe-liu wɛʋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pacaɣ-kʋ ɛzɩ weyi ɛsɩbɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ fezi-i yɔ, e-liu wɛʋ yɔ.

eng: We dont focus on the things that can be seen but on the things that cant be seen. The things that can be seen dont last, but the things that cant be seen are eternal.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ mbʋ́ panaɣ yɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩmaɣzɩɣ. Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ paanaɣ yɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩmaɣzɩɣ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mbʋ́ panaɣ yɔ, pɩwɛɣ alɩwaatʋ pazɩ yem nɛ mbʋ́ paanaɣ yɔ, pʋbʋ ñɛwɛɣ tam.

eng: So then, from this point on we wont recognize people by human standards. Even though we used to know Christ by human standards, that isnt how we know him now.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pɩkpaɣʋnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, ɖɩɩtasɩɣ nɔɔyʋ sɩm ɛsɛ yɔɔ. Paa ɛsɛ yɔɔ ɖɩɩsɩm Krɩstʋ yɔ, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɖɩsɩm mbʋ́ ɛkɛnaa kpem yɔ.

eng: So then, if anyone is in Christ, that person is part of the new creation. The old things have gone away, and look, new things have arrived!.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, yee nɔɔyʋ nɛ Krɩstʋ pɛkpɛndaa yɔ, pʋdʋ pɩsɩ ɛyʋ kɩfalʋ. Mbʋ́ pɩɩkɛ kɩbɩm yɔ, pɩɖɛwa nɛ kɩfam kɔɔ.

eng: All of these new things are from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ and who gave us the ministry of reconciliation.
local: Ɛsɔ weyi ɛtɩŋnɩ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖiceɣ yɔ, ɛ-cɔlɔ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩlɩnaa. Ɛnʋ ɖʋnɩ-ɖʋ se ɖiwoni ɛyáa lɛlaa ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ pa nɛ ɩ peceɣ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: In other words, God was reconciling the world to himself through Christ, by not counting peoples sins against them. He has trusted us with this message of reconciliation.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Krɩstʋ yɔɔ Ɛsɔ katɩŋnaa nɛ ɛ nɛ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ peceɣ. Pe-ceɣu ḿbʋ́ pɩ-taa lɛ, ɛtaakpaɣ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm nɛ ɛlabɩnɩ tʋmɩyɛ. Ɛɖʋ-ɖʋ se ɖisusi ceɣu ḿbʋ́ pʋ-tɔm.

eng: So we are ambassadors who represent Christ. God is negotiating with you through us. We beg you as Christs representatives, Be reconciled to God.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩkɛ Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa. Ɛsɔ tɩŋɩɣnɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ nɛ etendiɣ ɛyáa alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖiheyiɣ-wɛ se: «Đitendiɣ-mɩ, itisi nɛ mɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ iceɣ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ.

eng: God caused the one who didnt know sin to be sin for our sake so that through him we could become the righteousness of God.
local: Krɩstʋ tala kɩwɛɛkɩm kaʋ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ sʋʋ-ɩ ɖa-kɩwɛɛkɩm se ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɛ-kpɛndʋʋ taa lɛ, ɖɩpɩsɩ ɛyáa kʋtʋʋzaa Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa» yɔ.

eng: My dear friends, since we have these promises, lets cleanse ourselves from anything that contaminates our body or spirit so that we make our holiness complete in the fear of God.
local: Man-taabalaa kʋsɔɔlaa, mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ heyi-ɖʋ se ɛlakɩ-ɖʋ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pɩwɛɛ se ɖiyele ɛzaɣ́tʋ ndʋ́ tipisiɣ tomnaɣ yɔ nɛ ndʋ́ tipisiɣ kalɩzaɣ yɔ tɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ ɖɩwɛɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ. Pʋwɛɛ se ɖɩtɛzɩ kɛʋ ɛ-ñɩma siŋŋ ɖe-wezuu caɣʋ taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩñaŋ-ɩ.

eng: Make room in your hearts for us. We didnt do anything wrong to anyone. We didnt ruin anyone. We didnt take advantage of anyone.
local: Pɩfɛyɩ se ɩkpa-m nɛ mɩ-tɩ. Đɩtala nɔɔyʋ mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ. Đɩtaɖʋzɩ nɔɔyʋ, ɖitepeɣzi ɖɔɖɔ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɖikpizi e-nesi taa.

eng: Since we work together with him, we are also begging you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́, mbá ɖa ɖa nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩlakɩ tʋmɩyɛ yɔ, ɖɩsazɩɣ-mɩ se ɩtaawɛɛkɩ lootiye nɖɩ́ ɛlabɩ-mɩ yɔ yem.

eng: Im not saying this to make you feel guilty. Ive already said that you are in our hearts so that we die and live together with you.
local: Maayɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ se mankʋ mɩ-tɔm. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛzɩ mɛntɛm yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ, paa pʋwɛɛ se ɖa nɛ mɩ ɖɩsɩ yaa ɖɩwɛɛ wezuu yɔ, ɖɩsɔɔlɩ-mɩ siŋŋ.

eng: He says, I listened to you at the right time, and I helped you on the day of salvation. Look, now is the right time! Look, now is the day of salvation!.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ yɔɔdaa se: «Pɩtalɩ ɖeu labʋ lɛ, manɩnɩ-ŋ. Pɩtalɩ ñʋʋ yabʋ lɛ, mansɩnɩ-ŋ.» Ɩnɩɩ camɩyɛ se: lɛɛlɛɛyɔ lɛ Ɛsɔ ɖeu labʋ alɩwaatʋ. Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɛyakɩ ɛyáa ñʋʋ.

eng: I have every confidence in you. Im terribly proud of you. Im filled with encouragement. Im overwhelmed with happiness while in the middle of our problems.
local: Mɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ siŋŋ mɩ-yɔɔ, mankʋndʋʋnɩ-mɩ siŋŋ. Ma-laŋɩyɛ hɛ niɣ niɣ nɛ man-taa hʋlʋmɩ siŋŋ ɖa-kʋñɔmɩŋ tɩŋa taa.

eng: We dont give anyone any reason to be offended about anything so that our ministry wont be criticized.
local: Đɩcaɣ se nɔɔyʋ ɛtaapɩzɩ ɛkpɛzɩnɩ-ɖʋ tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩlakɩ yɔ ɖɩ-yɔɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩñakɩ pana se nɔɔyʋ ɛtaatɔlɩ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa.

eng: Even after we arrived in Macedonia, we couldnt rest physically. We were surrounded by problems. There was external conflict, and there were internal fears.
local: Đooo ɖɩtalʋʋ Masedonii egeetiye taa yɔ, ɖɩtatɩɩhɛzɩ kaʋ. Đɩlabɩ kʋñɔŋ pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa: ɖa nɛ ɛyáa lɛlaa ɖiyowa nɛ sɔɔndʋ kpa-ɖʋ.

eng: Instead, we commend ourselves as ministers of God in every way. We did this with our great endurance through problems, disasters, and stressful situations.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩtɩŋnɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɖɩɖɔkʋʋ ɖa-tɩ siŋŋ kʋñɔmɩŋ nɛ kɛtɛɖaɣ nɛ nɩɣzʋʋ pa-taa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩwɩlɩ se ɖɩkɛ Ɛsɔ tʋmlaɖaa.

eng: However, God comforts people who are discouraged, and he comforted us by Titus arrival.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ weyi ɛhɛzɩɣ mbá poluzuu pa-tɩ ɛ-tɛɛ yɔ pa-laŋa yɔ, eyeba nɛ Tiiti kɔɔ ɖɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ɖa-laŋa hɛɛ.

eng: We went through beatings, imprisonments, and riots. We experienced hard work, sleepless nights, and hunger.
local: Pamabɩ-ɖʋ nɛ paɖʋ-ɖʋ salaka nɛ palɩzɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ ñaɖʋʋ. Đɩlabɩ tʋma sɩm yɔɔ sɩm yɔɔ: nabʋyʋ taa, ɖitoɖoo, ɖɩtɔtɔɔ pʋyʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: We werent comforted only by his arrival but also by the comfort he had received from you. He told us about your desire to see me, how you were sorry, and about your concern for me, so that I was even happier.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ ɛ-kɔm ɖeke hɛzɩnɩ ɖa-laŋa. Ɛkɛdɩ-ɖʋ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩhɛzɩ ɛ-ñɩnɖɛ yɔ. Eheyi-ɖʋ se mi-liu wɛɛ kpem se ɩna-m, iwiɣ mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɩlʋkɩ. Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ man-taa tɛzɩ hʋlʋmʋʋ.

eng: We displayed purity, knowledge, patience, and generosity. We served with the Holy Spirit, genuine love.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɖɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ ɖa-cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ wɛtʋ nɛ ɖɔ-lɔŋsɩnɖɛ nɛ ɖa-tɩ ɖɔkʋʋ nɛ ɖa-camɩyɛ lakasɩ nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu nɛ ɖɔ-sɔɔlɩm siŋŋ.

eng: Even though my letter hurt you, I dont regret it. Well—I did regret it just a bit because I see that that letter made you sad, though only for a short time.
local: Manawa se takayaɣ ŋgá maama-mɩ yɔ, ka-taa tɔm wɛɛkɩ mɩ-laŋa, ɛlɛ́, maakpiɖiɣ. Paa maakpiɖaa se ka-taa tɔm kaawɛɛkɩ mɩ-laŋa alɩwaatʋ pazɩ yɔ.

eng: telling the truth, and Gods power. We carried the weapons of righteousness in our right hand and our left hand.
local: nɛ toovenim tɔm susuu nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖoŋ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩwɩlɩ se ɖɩkɛ Ɛsɔ tʋmlaɖaa. Đɩkpakɩɣ mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ ɖɩɖɔkɩ nɛ ɖɩlakɩnɩ you ɛzɩ you wondu ndʋ́ pɔɖɔkʋʋ nɩmɩyɛ nɛ nɩwaŋ pa-taa yɔ.

eng: Now Im glad—not because you were sad but because you were made sad enough to change your hearts and lives. You felt godly sadness so that no one was harmed by us in any way.
local: man-taa hʋlʋmɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ. Pɩtɛkɛ mɩ-laŋa wɛɛkaa yɔɔ man-taa hʋlʋmaa. Ɛlɛ́, man-taa hʋlʋmɩnɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɩ-laŋwɛɛkɩyɛ yeba nɛ ɩlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ sɔɔlɩna se mɩ-laŋa ɛwɛɛkɩ ḿbʋ́. Pɩtɛkɛ ɖalabɩnɩ-mɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩm nabʋyʋ.

eng: We were treated with honor and dishonor and with verbal abuse and good evaluation. We were seen as both fake and real.
local: Nabɛyɛ ɖʋʋ-ɖʋ hɩɖɛ nɛ lɛlaa ñaɖʋʋ-ɖʋ fɛyɛ. Nabɛyɛ saŋ-ɖʋ nɛ lɛlaa ñatʋʋ-ɖʋ. Pakpakɩɣ-ɖʋ ɛzɩ cɛtɩyaa yɔ, ŋgʋ́ toovenim ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ.

eng: Godly sadness produces a changed heart and life that leads to salvation and leaves no regrets, but sorrow under the influence of the world produces death.
local: Toovenim lɛ, laŋwɛɛkɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ, ɖiyeki nɛ ɛyʋ lɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ nɛ ehiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ, eekpiɖiɣ. Piyele kedeŋa laŋwɛɛkɩyɛ ñɔkɔŋnɩ sɩm.

eng: as unknown and well known, as dying—and look, we are alive! We were seen as punished but not killed.
local: Palakɩ ɛzɩ paasɩŋ-ɖʋ yɔ, ŋgʋ́ pasɩm-ɖʋ camɩyɛ. Pakpakɩɣ-ɖʋ ɛzɩ sɩɖaa yɔ, ŋgʋ́ ɖɩwɛ wezuu ɛzɩ ɩnaɣʋ yɔ. Pɔhɔŋ ɖa-nɩŋgbaaŋ, ɛlɛ́, paakʋʋ-ɖʋ.

eng: Look at what this very experience of godly sadness has produced in you: such enthusiasm, what a desire to clear yourselves of blame, such indignation, what fear, what purpose, such concern, what justice! In everything you have shown yourselves to be innocent in the matter.
local: Ɛsɔ tɩŋnɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɩ-laŋa wɛɛkaa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ, ɩɩnaɣ mbʋ́ pɩlɩ-mɩ pɩ-taa yɔ: ɩcɔnɩ ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩmaɣzɩɣ pɩtɩŋa pʋ-yɔɔ camɩyɛ yɔ! Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩlʋkɩ mɩ-tɩ yɔɔ yɔ! Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ fɛyɛ kpaɣ-mɩ nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɛkɛdɩ yɔ! Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ sɔɔndʋ kpaɣ-mɩ yɔ! Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mi-liu wɛɛ se ɩtasɩ-m naʋ yɔ! Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩlakɩ-mɩ hʋnʋm yɔ! Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩhɔm kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖʋ nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ yɔ! Ɩwɩlɩ pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa se ɩtɛwɛɛkɩ pʋyʋ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa.

eng: as going through pain but always happy, as poor but making many rich, and as having nothing but owning everything.
local: Papɩsɩɣ ɖɔ-hɔ, ɛlɛ́, ɖɩwɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ taa paa ɖooye. Đɩwɛ ɛzɩ kʋñɔndɩnaa yɔ, ɛlɛ́, ɖiyeki nɛ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ hikiɣ pa-tɩ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa. Đɩwɛ ɛzɩ mɛnfɛyɩpʋyʋwaa yɔ, ŋgʋ́ ɖɩwɛnɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ.

eng: So although I wrote to you, it wasnt for the sake of the one who did wrong, or for the sake of the one who was wronged, but to show you your own enthusiasm for us in the sight of God.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yee mama-mɩ takayaɣ kanɛ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ weyi ɛyʋsaa yaa weyi payʋsɩnaa yɔ, pa-taa nɔɔyʋ yɔɔ mama-mɩ-kɛ. Ɛlɛ́, mama-mɩ-kɛ se ɩna Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa camɩyɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩñakɩ pana ɖɔ-yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: Corinthians, we have spoken openly to you, and our hearts are wide open.
local: Taabalaa Kɔrɛntɩ ñɩma mɩ, ɖiheyi-mɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖa-taa yɔ kpayɩ. Đɩsɔɔlɩ-mɩ siŋŋ.

eng: Because of this we have been encouraged. And in addition to our own encouragement, we were even more pleased at how happy Titus was. His mind has been put at rest by all of you.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖa-laŋa hɛwa. Pɩtasɩɩnɩ ɖa-laŋa hɛʋ ɖeke. Ɛlɛ́, ɖa-taa kɛtɛzɩ hʋlʋmʋʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩnawa se Tiiti taa hʋlʋmɩnɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɩtɩŋa payɩ iyeba nɛ ɛ-lɩmaɣza caɣ a-ɖeɖe yɔ.

eng: There are no limits to the affection that we feel for you. You are the ones who placed boundaries on your affection for us.
local: Đitekizi se ɖɩɩsɔɔlʋʋ-mɩ, ɛlɛ́, mikizini se ɩɩsɔɔlʋʋ-ɖʋ.

eng: If Ive bragged about you to him in any way, I havent been embarrassed. Instead, our bragging to Titus has also been proven to be true, just like everything we said to you was true.
local: Maasam-mɩ ɛ-cɔlɔ, ɩtaɖʋ-m fɛyɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɖiheyiɣ-mɩ toovenim paa ɖooye yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ mɩ-tɔm ndʋ́ ɖiheyi Tiiti yɔ, tɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ ɖeyi ɖeyi.

eng: But as a fair trade—Im talking to you like you are children—open your hearts wide too.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ mɔyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-mɩ ɛzɩ men-piya yɔ: ɩsɔɔlɩ-ɖʋ ɛzɩ ɖɩsɔɔlʋʋ-mɩ yɔ.

eng: His devotion to you is growing even more as he remembers how all of you were obedient when you welcomed him with fear and trembling.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Tiiti kɔm mɩ-tɛ yɔ, mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩɩñam-ɩ nɛ ɩmʋ-ɩ camɩyɛ. Ɛtɔzʋʋ pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɛtɛzɩɣ-mɩ sɔɔlʋʋ.

eng: Dont be tied up as equal partners with people who dont believe. What does righteousness share with that which is outside the Law? What relationship does light have with darkness?.
local: Mɩ nɛ mbá paañaŋ Krɩstʋ yɔ, ɩtaakpɛndɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtatʋʋzɩ yɔ pɩpɩzɩɣ pɩkpɛndɩ? Ɛzɩma ñalɩmɩyɛ nɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ papɩzɩɣ pɛkpɛndɩ?.

eng: Im happy, because I can completely depend on you.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ man-taa hʋlʋmɩ poyi poyi, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, manpɩzɩɣ mantaa-mɩ liu pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa.

eng: What harmony does Christ have with Satan? What does a believer have in common with someone who doesnt believe?.
local: Ɛzɩma Krɩstʋ nɛ Eleeu papɩzɩɣ panɩɩnɩ ɖama? Yaa ɛbɛ Krɩstʋ tʋ nɛ weyi ɛtɛkɛ Krɩstʋ tʋ yɔ papɩzɩɣ pakaa?.

eng: What agreement can there be between Gods temple and idols? Because we are the temple of the living God. Just as God said, I live with them, and I will move among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Ɛbɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa nɛ kumola pakawa? Ŋgʋ́, ɖɩkɛ Ɛsɔ wezuu tʋ ɖɩɣa. Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Mɛwɛɣ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ ma nɛ wɛ ɖɩɖɔ. Mɛnkɛɣ pɛ-Ɛsɔ nɛ pɛkɛ mɛ-ɛyáa» yɔ.

eng: Therefore, come out from among them and be separated, says the Lord. Dont touch what is unclean. Then I will welcome you. .
local: Pamawa ɖɔɖɔ se: Kɩpaɣlʋ tɔŋ se: «ɩlɩɩ azaɣ́a ḿbá pa-taa nɛ iyebi-wɛ. Ɩtaatukuni azuluma pʋyʋ nɛ piyele mamʋ-mɩ.».

eng: I will be a father to you, and you will be my sons and daughters, says the Lord Almighty .
local: Kɩpaɣlʋ Đoŋ Tɩŋa Tʋ tasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Mɔnkɔŋ kɛʋ mɩ-Caa nɛ mɩkɛ man-pɩyalaa nɛ mɛn-pɛlaa.».

eng: So every single one of you who judge others is without any excuse. You condemn yourself when you judge another person because the one who is judging is doing the same things.
local: Weyi ña ŋmaɣzɩɣ se lɛlaa ɛɛlakɩ camɩyɛ yɔ, paa ŋkɛ anɩ yɔ, natʋyʋ fɛyɩ se ŋwaɣna. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ŋmaɣzɩɣ se lɛlaa ɛɛlakɩ camɩyɛ nɛ ŋlakɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ḿbá yɔ, ŋkʋʋ ña-maɣmaɣ ñɔ-tɔm.

eng: We know that Gods judgment agrees with the truth, and his judgment is against those who do these kinds of things.
local: Đɩnawa se Ɛsɔ hʋʋnɩ mbá palakɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ nɛ toovenim.
