eng: If you judge those who do these kinds of things while you do the same things yourself, think about this: Do you believe that you will escape Gods judgment?.
local: Weyi ña ŋmaɣzɩɣ se lɛlaa ɛɛlakɩ camɩyɛ nɛ ŋlakɩ ɛzɩ palakʋʋ yɔ, ŋmaɣzɩɣ se ŋkɔŋ lɩʋ Ɛsɔ tɔm hʋʋ taa yaa we?.

eng: Or do you have contempt for the riches of Gods generosity, tolerance, and patience? Dont you realize that Gods kindness is supposed to lead you to change your heart and life?.
local: Yaa ŋniŋ Ɛsɔ camɩyɛ lakasɩ sɔsɔsɩ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ eyeki ɛyáa nɛ ɛɛhɔŋ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ yɔ nɛ ɛ-hɔyɛ ɖɔkʋʋ pɔ-yɔɔ yaa we? Ŋtatɩlɩ se ɛ-camɩyɛ lakasɩ ńzɩ́ pʋwɛɛ se siyele nɛ ŋlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ naa we?.

eng: You are storing up wrath for yourself because of your stubbornness and your heart that refuses to change. Gods just judgment will be revealed on the day of wrath.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ña-laŋɩyɛ wɛ kpɩzɩŋ nɛ ŋŋcaɣ lɛɣzʋʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, Ɛsɔ pana tɛzɩɣ-ŋ kpanaʋ siŋŋ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛhʋʋnɩ ɛyáa tɔm siɣsiɣ yɔ.

eng: God will repay everyone based on their works .
local: Puwiye ɛhɛyɩɣ paa weyi mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ:.

eng: On the one hand, he will give eternal life to those who look for glory, honor, and immortality based on their patient good work.
local: ɛhaɣ mbá pañakɩ pana se pala camɩyɛ yɔ wezuu maatɛŋ. Palakɩ ḿbʋ́ se ɛha-wɛ e-koboyaɣ nɛ ɛɖʋ-wɛ hɩɖɛ nɛ ɛha-wɛ mbʋ́ pɩɩɖazɩɣ yɔ.

eng: But on the other hand, there will be wrath and anger for those who obey wickedness instead of the truth because they are acting out of selfishness and disobedience.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛ-pana kɔŋ kpanaʋ siŋŋ mbá pakaɖɩɣnɩ-ɩ nɛ paañaŋ mbʋ́ pɩkɛ toovenim nɛ panɩɣnɩ mbʋ́ pɩtatʋʋzɩ kpem yɔ.

eng: There will be trouble and distress for every human being who does evil, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Ɛɖʋʋ kʋñɔŋ mbá patɩŋa payɩ palakɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩm yɔ nɛ eyele pa-laŋa maa. Ɛpazɩɣnɩ Yuda ñɩma nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩtamnɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ!.

eng: But there will be glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does what is good, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Weyi ɛlakɩ camɩyɛ yɔ, ɛwɛɣ koboyaɣ nɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ pa-taa, paɖʋʋ-ɩ hɩɖɛ. Pɩpazɩɣnɩ Yuda ñɩma nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩtamnɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ!.

eng: God does not have favorites.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɛɛcɔŋnɩ nɔɔyʋ ɛsɩndaa se pʋcɔ ɛlabɩ-ɩ pʋyʋ.

eng: Those who have sinned outside the Law will also die outside the Law, and those who have sinned under the Law will be judged by the Law.
local: Mbá patɩŋa payɩ patasɩ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ nɛ palakɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ, pasɩkɩ, paahʋʋnɩ-wɛ nɛ tʋ. Ɛlɛ́, mbá pasɩm-tʋ nɛ pala kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ, pahʋʋnɩ-wɛ nɛ tʋ.

eng: It isnt the ones who hear the Law who are righteous in Gods eyes. It is the ones who do what the Law says who will be treated as righteous.
local: Toovenim lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ mbá pewelesiɣ mbʋ́ paɖʋ paɣtʋ taa yem yɔ lɛ mbá patʋʋzɩ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mbá pɔɖɔŋ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, ḿbá kɛɣnɩ kʋtʋʋzaa ɛ-ɛsɩndaa.

eng: Gentiles dont have the Law. But when they instinctively do what the Law requires they are a Law in themselves, though they dont have the Law.
local: Yee mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma nɛ paasɩŋ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ yɔ, pɔɖɔŋ mbʋ́ paɖʋ tɩ-taa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ yɔ, pɩwɩlɩɣ se pa-maɣmaɣ pasɩm mbʋ́ paɖʋ tɩ-taa se pala yɔ. Ŋgʋ́, patasɩm-tʋ.

eng: They show the proof of the Law written on their hearts, and their consciences affirm it. Their conflicting thoughts will accuse them, or even make a defense for them.
local: Pawɩlɩɣnɩ pa-lakasɩ se mbʋ́ paɖʋ paɣtʋ taa yɔ, pʋwɛ pa-laŋa taa ɖɔɖɔ. Pa-lɩmaɣza lɩzɩɣ aseɣɖe se nabʋyʋ taa, palabɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩm yaa camɩyɛ.

eng: on the day when, according to my gospel, God will judge the hidden truth about human beings through Christ Jesus.
local: Ḿbʋ́ kaɣnɩ lɩʋ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Ɛsɔ kɔŋ ɛyáa tɔm hʋnaʋ yɔ. Ɛtɩŋɩɣnɩ Krɩstʋ Yesu yɔɔ nɛ ɛhʋʋnɩ ɛyáa mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ palabɩ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ. Ḿbʋ́ wɛnɩ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ndʋ́ monsusuu yɔ tɩ-taa.

eng: But, if you call yourself a Jew; if you rely on the Law; if you brag about your relationship to God;.
local: Weyi ña ŋyaɣ ña-tɩ se Yuda tʋ yɔ, ŋtaɣ paɣtʋ liu nɛ ŋkʋndʋʋnɩ Ɛsɔ.

eng: if you know the will of God; if you are taught by the Law so that you can figure out the things that really matter;.
local: Ŋsɩm mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ yɔ, nɛ ŋtɩlɩ mbʋ́ pɩkɩlɩ wazaɣ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pawɩlɩ-ŋ paɣtʋ nɛ ñɛ-ɛsɛ kuli.

eng: if you have persuaded yourself that you are: a guide for the blind; a light to those who are in darkness;.
local: Ŋmaɣzɩɣ se ñɔhɔŋnɩ yʋlʋmaa nɛ ŋkɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa ñɩma ñalɩmɩyɛ.

eng: an educator of the foolish; a teacher of infants ;.
local: Ŋmaɣzɩɣ ɖɔɖɔ se ŋkɛ mbá paasɩŋ pʋyʋ yɔ nɛ mbá pɛwɛ ɛzɩ piya yɔ pa-wɩlɩyʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ŋhikiɣ paɣtʋ taa lɔŋsɩnɖɛ tɩŋa nɛ toovenim tɩŋa.

eng: then why dont you who are teaching others teach yourself? If you preach, No stealing, do you steal?.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ weyi ña ŋkpɛlɩkɩɣ lɛlaa yɔ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ŋŋkpɛlɩkɩɣ ña-maɣmaɣ ña-tɩ? Weyi ña ŋsusuu se pataamɩlɩ yɔ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ŋmɩlɩɣ?.

eng: If you say, No adultery, do you commit adultery? If you hate idols, do you rob temples?.
local: Weyi ña ŋtɔŋ se pataala acɛyɛ yɔ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ŋlakɩ-tʋ? Weyi ña ŋŋcaɣ agɔlɩma kpem yɔ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ŋmɩlɩɣ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ a-kuduyiŋ tɛɛ yɔ?.

eng: If you brag about the Law, do you shame God by breaking the Law?.
local: Ŋkʋndʋʋnɩ paɣtʋ, ɛlɛ́, ŋwɛɛkɩɣ Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ŋŋɖɔŋ mbʋ́ paɖʋ tɩ-taa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: As it is written: The name of God is discredited by the Gentiles because of you .
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ yɔ, pamawa se: «Mɩ-yɔɔ lɛ, Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ wɛɛkɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ.».

eng: Circumcision is an advantage if you do what the Law says. But if you are a person who breaks the Law, your status of being circumcised has changed into not being circumcised.
local: Toovenim lɛ, yee ŋɖɔŋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ yɔ, pɛlʋʋ wɛ wazaɣ. Ɛlɛ́, yee ŋŋɖɔŋ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pɛtɛpɛlɩ-ŋ yɔ.

eng: So if the person who isnt circumcised keeps the Law, wont his status of not being circumcised be counted as if he were circumcised?.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yee weyi pɛtɛpɛlɩ-ɩ yɔ ɛɖɔŋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kpakɩɣ-ɩ ɛzɩ weyi pɛpɛlɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: The one who isnt physically circumcised but keeps the Law will judge you. You became a lawbreaker after you had the written Law and circumcision.
local: Weyi pɛtɛpɛlɩ-ɩ tomnaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɛɖɔŋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛkɔŋ wɩlʋʋ weyi ña pɛpɛlɩ-ŋ tomnaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ŋsɩ paɣtʋ camɩyɛ nɛ ŋŋɖɔŋ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ se ñɔ-tɔm sɩba.

eng: It isnt the Jew who maintains outward appearances who will receive praise from God, and it isnt people who are outwardly circumcised on their bodies.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ ɛsɛ yɔɔ yem panaɣ ɛyʋ se ɛkɛ Yuda tʋ siŋŋ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ tomnaɣ yɔɔ pɛlʋʋ lɛ pɛlʋʋ siŋŋ.

eng: Instead, it is the person who is a Jew inside, who is circumcised in spirit, not literally. That persons praise doesnt come from people but from God.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɛyʋ taa yɔ, ḿbʋ́ wɩlɩɣna se ɛkɛ Yuda tʋ siŋŋ. Pɛlʋʋ siŋŋ lɛ mbʋ́ pɩlɩnɩ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu cɔlɔ nɛ pʋwɛɛ ɛyʋ taa yɔ. Pɩtɛkɛ mbʋ́ pɩlɩnɩ paɣtʋ taa yɔ. Yuda tʋ siŋŋ ɛnʋ, pɩtɛkɛ ɛyáa saŋnɩ-ɩ, ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ saŋnɩ-ɩ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we want to let you know about the grace of God that was given to the churches of Macedonia.
local: Taabalaa, ɖɩcaɣ se ɩtɩlɩ lootiye nɖɩ́ Ɛsɔ labɩ Masedonii egeetiye taa Yesu aŋgba taa ñɩma yɔ.

eng: While they were being tested by many problems, their extra amount of happiness and their extreme poverty resulted in a surplus of rich generosity.
local: Pɩtakɩ-wɛ nɛ kʋñɔmɩŋ siŋŋ, ɛlɛ́, pa-laŋa hʋlʋmɩ siŋŋ nɛ pahanɩ pa-laŋa tɩŋa pilim. Ŋgʋ́, pɛkɛ kʋñɔndɩnaa kpem.

eng: I assure you that they gave what they could afford and even more than they could afford, and they did it voluntarily.
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ toovenim se paha mbʋ́ papɩzɩɣ yɔ. Halɩ pɩɖɛɛ pʋ-yɔɔ. Pa-maɣmaɣ paasɔɔlɩnɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: They urgently begged us for the privilege of sharing in this service for the saints.
local: Paawɛɛ pɛcɛyɩɣ-ɖʋ se ɖɩha-wɛ waɖɛ nɛ pasɩnɩ ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ ñɩma.

eng: They even exceeded our expectations, because they gave themselves to the Lord first and to us, consistent with Gods will.
local: Palaba nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ɛzɩ ɖɩɩmaɣzʋʋ yɔ: pacalɩ pa-tɩ kpaɣʋ nɛ pɛcɛlɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, peyebini-ɖʋ pa-tɩ ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ liu wɛʋ yɔ.

eng: As a result, we challenged Titus to finish this work of grace with you the way he had started it.
local: Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Tiiti kapazɩ liidiye mʋ́ʋ mɩ-tɛ yɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ ɖitendi-i se ɛñaɣ pana ɛpɩsɩ mɩ-tɛ peeɖe nɛ ɛtɛzɩ sɔɔlɩm tʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ ɛzɩ ɛɛpazʋʋ-ɖɩ yɔ.

eng: Be the best in this work of grace in the same way that you are the best in everything, such as faith, speech, knowledge, total commitment, and the love we inspired in you.
local: Ɩkɛ ñɩm tɩnaa Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa: ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ siŋŋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ, isusuu Ɛsɔtɔm camɩyɛ. Mbʋ́ ɩsɩm yɔ pʋɖɔwa, ɩñakɩ pana Ɛsɔ tʋmɩyɛ taa nɛ ɩsɔɔlɩ-ɖʋ siŋŋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩla sɔɔlɩm tʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ mɩ-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa pilim.

eng: Im not giving an order, but by mentioning the commitment of others, Im trying to prove the authenticity of your love also.
local: Mɔyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ paɣtʋ natʋyʋ manɖʋʋ-mɩ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ lɛlaa ñakɩ pana yɔ pʋ-tɔm meheyiɣ-mɩ se mana se ɩwɛnɩ sɔɔlɩm siŋŋ yaa ɩfɛyɩna.

eng: You know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ. Although he was rich, he became poor for your sakes, so that you could become rich through his poverty.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩsɩm lootiye nɖɩ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ labɩ-ɖʋ yɔ. Ɛnʋ weyi ɛɛkɛ ñɩm tʋ yɔ, ɛpɩsɩ ɛ-tɩ kʋñɔndʋ mɩ-yɔɔ se pɩtɩŋnɩ ɛ-kʋñɔndʋtʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɩkɛ ñɩm tɩnaa.

eng: Im giving you my opinion about this. Its to your advantage to do this, since you not only started to do it last year but you wanted to do it too.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ lɛ, mbʋ́ mana liidiye haʋ pʋnɛ pʋ-tɔm taa yɔ meheyiɣ-mɩ. Pinde lɛ, mɩɩsɔɔlɩna nɛ ɩlɛyɩ pazʋʋ liidiye ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-haʋ.

eng: Now finish the job as well so that you finish it with as much enthusiasm as you started, given what you can afford.
local: Ɩwɛɛ pʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɩtɛzɩ tʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́. Ɩtɛzɩ-ɖɩ labʋ nɛ mɩ-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa ɛzɩ ɩɩɖʋʋ yɔ. Ɩlabɩ-ɖɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩpɩzɩɣ yɔ.

eng: A gift is appreciated because of what a person can afford, not because of what that person cant afford, if its apparent that its done willingly.
local: Yee ɛyʋ ɛhaɣ pʋyʋ nɛ ɛ-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa pilim yɔ, Ɛsɔ mʋ́ʋ-pʋ nɛ ɛmaɣzɩnɩ mbʋ́ pʋdʋ wɛna yɔ. Ɛɛmaɣzɩɣnɩ mbʋ́ ɛfɛyɩna yɔ.

eng: It isnt that we want others to have financial ease and you financial difficulties, but its a matter of equality.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɔtɔtɔ se ikpizi mi-nesi taa nɛ ɩkpazɩ lɛlaa nɛ piyele ɩsʋʋ kʋñɔŋ taa. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se mɩ nɛ mbá ɩsɩŋna yɔ, ɩwɛɛ kɩmaŋ.

eng: At the present moment, your surplus can fill their deficit so that in the future their surplus can fill your deficit. In this way there is equality.
local: Mbá mɩ ɩwɛnɩ ñɩm lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, ɩkpaɣ-pʋ nɛ ɩsɩnɩ mbá pɛwɛ kʋñɔŋ taa yɔ. Yee nabʋyʋ taa ɩkɔm ɩsʋʋ kʋñɔŋ taa nɛ paba wɛnɩ ñɩm yɔ, paasɩnɩ-mɩ. Ḿbʋ́ yekina nɛ mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩwɛɛ kɩmaŋ.

eng: As it is written, The one who gathered more didnt have too much, and the one who gathered less didnt have too little. .
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: «Weyi eeku tɔɔnaɣ sakɩyɛ yɔ, ɛtaawɛna nɛ pɩkɩlɩ mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ-ɩ yɔ nɛ weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ku pazɩ yɔ, nabʋyʋ taatɛɖɩɣ-ɩ.».

eng: But thank God, who put the same commitment that I have for you in Titus heart.
local: Đɩsa Ɛsɔ weyi eyeba nɛ Tiiti ñakɩ pana ɖɔɖɔ mɩ-yɔɔ ɛzɩ ɖa yɔ.

eng: Not only has he accepted our challenge but hes on his way to see you voluntarily, and hes excited.
local: Điheyi-i se ɛkɔɔ mɩ-tɛ nɛ etisi. Halɩ ɛ-maɣmaɣ kpem ɛɛɖʋwa se ɛkɔŋ mɩ-tɛ nɛ ɛla ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: We are sending the brother who is famous in all the churches because of his work for the gospel along with him.
local: Đitiyiɣ ɛ nɛ taabalʋ weyi esusi Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm siŋŋ nɛ pasaŋ-ɩ Yesu aŋgba tɩŋa payɩ taa yɔ.

eng: In addition to this, he is chosen by the churches to be our traveling companion in this work of grace, which we are taking care of for the sake of the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our desire to help.
local: Yesu aŋgba taa ñɩma lɩzɩnɩ-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ se ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩcɔɔ nɛ ɖɩmʋ liidiye nɖɩ́ pahawa yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ wɩlɩɣnɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ maɣmaɣ koboyaɣ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɖe-liu wɛɛ se ɖɩsɩnɩ lɛlaa yɔ.

eng: We are trying to avoid being blamed by anyone for the way we take care of this large amount of money.
local: Đɩɩcaɣ se nɔɔyʋ ɛhɔmnɩ-ɖʋ tɔm liidiye sɔsɔɖɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩcɔŋnɩ ɖɩ-yɔɔ yɔ ɖɩ-tɔm taa.

eng: We care about doing the right thing, not only in the Lords eyes but also in the eyes of other people.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩcaɣ se ɖɩla mbʋ́ pʋmʋnaa se pala Kɩpaɣlʋ ɛsɩndaa nɛ mbʋ́ pʋmʋnaa se pala ɛyáa ɛsɩndaa ɖɔɖɔ yɔ.

eng: We are sending our brother with them. We have tested his commitment in many ways and many times. Now hes even more committed, because he has so much confidence in you.
local: Đa-taabalʋ weyi ɖitiyiɣ pa nɛ ɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɖɩmaɣzɩ-ɩ tam sakɩyɛ nɛ ɖɩna se ɛñakɩ pana siŋŋ. Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, liu ŋgʋ́ ɛtaɣ-mɩ siŋŋ yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɛtɛzɩɣ pana ñaɣʋ.

eng: If there is any question about Titus, he is my partner and coworker among you. If there is any question about our brothers, they are the churches apostles and an honor to Christ.
local: Tiiti lɛ, ɛkɛ man-taabalʋ weyi ma nɛ ɩ ɖɩlakɩ tʋmɩyɛ mɩ-cɔlɔ yɔ. Pɩsɛɣ taabalaa lɛlaa mbá pasɩɣ-ɩ yɔ, Yesu aŋgba taa ñɩma tiyiɣni-wɛ. Palakɩ se Krɩstʋ hɩɖɛ ɛsɛɛ.

eng: So show them the proof of your love and the reason we are so proud of you, in such a way that the churches can see it.
local: Ɩwɩlɩ-wɛ nɛ pana se ɩsɔɔlɩ-wɛ nɛ pɩwɩlɩ Yesu aŋgba taa ñɩma nɛ patɩlɩ se ɖɩwɛnɩ toovenim se ɖɩkʋndɩnɩ-mɩ.

eng: Its unnecessary for me to write to you about this service for Gods people.
local: Pɩfɛyɩ se mancazɩ-mɩ maʋ nɛ meheyi-mɩ liidiye nɖɩ́ ɖitiyiɣnɩ Ɛsɔ ñɩma yɔ ɖɩ-tɔm.

eng: I know about your willingness to help. I brag about you to the Macedonians, saying, Greece has been ready since last year, and your enthusiasm has motivated most of them.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, manawa se mɩ-maɣmaɣ mi-liu wɛɛ se ɩsɩŋnɩ-wɛ. Pʋ-yɔɔ mansam-mɩ Masedonii ñɩma cɔlɔ nɛ meheyi-wɛ se: «Akayɩ egeetiye taa Yesu ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma ñɔɔzɩ pa-tɩ ɖooo pinde se pahaɣ. Mɩ-pana ñaɣʋ labɩnɩ pa-taa ɛyáa sakɩyɛ hʋnʋm se paha.».

eng: But Im sending the brothers so that our bragging about you in this case wont be empty words, and so that you can be prepared, just as I keep telling them you will be.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́, mentiyi Tiiti nɛ taabalaa ḿbá mɩ-tɛ se samtʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩsam-mɩ haɖɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, tɩtaakɔɔ tɩsayɩ yem. Me-liu wɛɛ se ɩñɔɔzɩ mɩ-tɩ nɛ ɩɖaŋ kpam ɛzɩ meheyuu-mɩ yɔ.

eng: If some Macedonians should come with me and find out that you arent ready, we would be embarrassed as far as this project goes.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, yee ma nɛ Masedonii ñɩma ɖɩkɔm ɖɩmaɣnɩ-mɩ nɛ ɩtɔñɔɔzɩdɩ haɖɛ ńɖɩ́ yɔ, fɛyɛ kpaɣ-ɖʋ liu ŋgʋ́ ɖɩɩta-mɩ yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ. Pɩkpaɣ-mɩ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: This is why I thought it was necessary to encourage the brothers to go to you ahead of time and arrange in advance the generous gift you have already promised. I want it to be a real gift from you. I dont want you to feel like you are being forced to give anything.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ mamaɣzaa nɛ mentendi Tiiti nɛ ɛ-taabalaa ḿbá se pañaɣ pana pɛɖɛ-m nɔɔ nɛ pɔkɔɔ mɩ-tɛ nɛ pɔñɔɔzɩ haɖɛ nɖɩ́ ɩɩɖʋwa se ɩhaɣnɩ mɩ-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa yɔ. Maatalɩɣ lɛ, pɔñɔɔzɩ pɩtɩŋa pasɩɩ. Ḿbʋ́ wɩlɩɣna se ɩhanɩ sɔɔlɩm, pɩtɛkɛnɩ kɩcɛyʋʋ.

eng: What I mean is this: the one who sows a small number of seeds will also reap a small crop, and the one who sows a generous amount of seeds will also reap a generous crop.
local: Ɩtaasɔɔ se weyi eɖuu pazɩ yɔ, ɛkʋŋ pazɩ nɛ weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ɖúu sakɩyɛ yɔ, ɛkʋ sakɩyɛ.

eng: Everyone should give whatever they have decided in their heart. They shouldnt give with hesitation or because of pressure. God loves a cheerful giver.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se paa weyi ɛha ɛzɩ ɛɖʋʋ ɛ-taa yɔ. Ɛtaahanɩ laŋpɩsɩyɛ yaa kɩcɛyʋʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ sɔɔlɩ weyi ɛhaɣnɩ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ yɔ.

eng: God has the power to provide you with more than enough of every kind of grace. That way, you will have everything you need always and in everything to provide more than enough for every kind of good work.
local: Ɛsɔ pɩzɩɣ ɛha-mɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ. Peeɖe ɩwɛɣnɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋpɔzʋʋ-mɩ yɔ alɩwaatʋ tɩŋa nɛ ɩlabɩnɩ tʋma kɩbana sakɩyɛ.

eng: As it is written, He scattered everywhere; he gave to the needy; his righteousness remains forever. .
local: Ḿbʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Ɛsɔ labɩ kʋñɔndɩnaa kʋjɔŋ nɛ ɛ-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa. Ɛ-camɩyɛ lakasɩ wɛ tam.».

eng: The one who supplies seed for planting and bread for eating will supply and multiply your seed and will increase your crop, which is righteousness.
local: Ɛsɔ cɛlɩɣnɩ haɖʋ ɖomaɣ se eɖuu nɛ ɛhaɣ-ɩ tɔɔnaɣ se ɛtɔɔ. Ɛnʋ kɔŋnɩ-mɩ cɛlʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋpɔzʋʋ-mɩ yɔ. Eyeki nɛ puhuu nɛ mɩ-camɩyɛ lakasɩ tɛzɩ ɖɔʋ.

eng: You will be made rich in every way so that you can be generous in every way. Such generosity produces thanksgiving to God through us.
local: Eyeki nɛ ɩwɛnɩ ñɩm sakɩyɛ tam nɛ ɩpɩzɩ ɩwɛɛ kʋjɔŋ labʋ yɔɔ nɛ mɩ-laŋa tɩŋa. Peeɖe ɛyáa sakɩyɛ saŋ Ɛsɔ kʋjɔŋ weyi ɩlakɩ-wɛ nɛ ɖɩcɛlɩɣ-wɛ yɔ ɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: Your ministry of this service to Gods people isnt only fully meeting their needs but it is also multiplying in many expressions of thanksgiving to God.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kʋjɔʋ ŋ́gʋ́, pɩtɛkɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩma ɖeke kɩwazɩɣ. Ɛlɛ́, kiyeki nɛ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ sɛɛ Ɛsɔ nɛ pasam-ɩ kʋ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: They will give honor to God for your obedience to your confession of Christs gospel. They will do this because this service provides evidence of your obedience, and because of your generosity in sharing with them and with everyone.
local: Sɩnaʋ mbʋ́ ɩsɩŋnɩ-wɛ yɔ, pɩwɩlɩɣ mbʋ́ ɩkɛnaa yɔ. Peeɖe ɛyáa sakɩyɛ saŋ Ɛsɔ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩɖɔŋ Krɩstʋ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm yɔɔ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ. Pasaŋ-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɩ nɛ wɛ nɛ lɛlaa tɩŋa payɩ ɩtayɩɣ mɩ-ñɩm nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: They will also pray for you, and they will care deeply for you because of the outstanding grace that God has given to you.
local: Pɔsɔɔlʋʋ-mɩ siŋŋ nɛ pɛsɛɣ Ɛsɔ mɩ-yɔɔ lootiye sɔsɔɖɛ nɖɩ́ ɛlabɩ-mɩ nɛ ɖɩfɛyɩ kaɣmaɣ yɔ ɖɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: Thank God for his gift that words cant describe!.
local: Đɩsa Ɛsɔ ɛ-kʋjɔʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩkɩlɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ!.

eng: I, Paul, make a personal request to you with the gentleness and kindness of Christ. Im shy when Im with you, but Im bossy when Im away from you!.
local: Ma Pɔɔlɩ manawa se mɔn-tɔm ɛyáa yɔɔdʋʋ se mɛwɛɣ pɔ-cɔlɔ lɛ, mañaŋ pɛ-ɛsɛ. Mɛnɖɛɣ poliŋ lɛ, mañazɩɣ-wɛ. Ma-maɣmaɣ mantɩŋɩɣnɩ Krɩstʋ leleŋ nɛ ɛ-camɩyɛ lakasɩ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ mentendiɣ-mɩ se:.

eng: I beg you that when Im with you in person, I wont have to boss you around. Im afraid that I may have to use that kind of behavior with those people who think we live by human standards.
local: ɩtaala nɛ mañazɩ-mɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mɔnkɔŋ mɩ-tɛ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mansɩm ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɔnkɔŋ ñazʋʋ mbá pamaɣzɩɣ se ma nɛ man-taabalaa ɖɩcakɩ wezuu ɛzɩ kedeŋa ñɩma yɔ.

eng: Although we live in the world, we dont fight our battles with human methods.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɖɩwɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ, ɛlɛ́, ɖɩɩlakɩ you ŋgʋ́ kedeŋa ñɩma lakɩ yɔ.

eng: Our weapons that we fight with arent human, but instead they are powered by God for the destruction of fortresses. They destroy arguments.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ hɔzɩŋ weyi kedeŋa ñɩma lakɩnɩ you yɔ ɖɩlakɩna. Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ ɖɩlakɩna. Ɩwɛ ɖoŋ, ɩpɩzɩɣ ɩyɔkɩ Ɛsɔ koyindinaa ɖɩmɛlɩyɛ. Ɩnʋ ɖiɖiɣzini cɛtɩm tɔm.

eng: and every defense that is raised up to oppose the knowledge of God. They capture every thought to make it obedient to Christ.
local: Ɩnʋ ɖɔɖɔ ɖɩyɔkʋʋnɩ mbʋ́ tɩ kpazɩyaa ɖɩkɩnɩ ɛyáa nʋmɔʋ se pataatɩlɩ Ɛsɔ yɔ. Đɩpaɣ ɛyáa se pɛlɛɣzɩ pa-lɩmaɣza nɛ paña Krɩstʋ.

eng: Once your obedience is complete, we are ready to punish any disobedience.
local: Đɩɖaŋ kpam se yee ɩtɛzɩ ñam nɛ pɩsɛɣ mɩ-taa nabɛyɛ yɔ, ɖɩhɔ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ.

eng: Look at what is right in front of you! If anyone is sure about belonging to Christ, that person should think again. We belong to Christ just like that person.
local: Ɩnaɣ wondu ɛsɛ yɔɔ yem. Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛna ɛ-maɣmaɣ se ɛkɛ Krɩstʋ tʋ lɛ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma ɛɛnaɣ se: ɖa ɖɔɖɔ ɖɩkɛ Krɩstʋ ñɩma ɛzɩ ɛnʋ yɔ?.

eng: Even if I went on to brag about our authority, I wouldnt be ashamed of it. The Lord gave us that authority to build you up and not to destroy you.
local: Paa maakɩlɩ kʋndɩnaʋ ɖoŋ weyi Kɩpaɣlʋ ha-ɖʋ yɔ yɔ, fɛyɛ ɛɛkpaɣ-m. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛha-ɖʋ-ɩ se ɖɩsɩnɩ-mɩ nɛ ɩpɩɩ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa. Ɛlɛ́, ɛtaha-ɖʋ-ɩ se ɖɩɖʋzɩnɩ-mɩ.

eng: I dont want it to seem like Im trying to intimidate you with my letters.
local: Maacaɣ se ɩmaɣzɩ se mamaɣ-mɩ takayɩsɩ se sɔɔndʋ ɛkpa-mɩ.

eng: I know what some people are saying: His letters are severe and powerful, but in person he is weak and his speech is worth nothing.
local: Nabɛyɛ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Takayɩsɩ nzɩ́ Pɔɔlɩ maɣ yɔ sɩwɛ kaɖɛ, sɩ-tɔm wɛ ɖoŋ. Ŋgʋ́ ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩwɛɣ lɛ, ɛwɛ ɛjandʋ, tɔm ndʋ́ ɛyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ tɩɩwazɩɣ pʋyʋ.».

eng: These people need to think about this—that when we are with you, our actions will show that we are the same as the words we wrote when we were away from you.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɛtɩlɩ camɩyɛ se: tɔm ndʋ́ ɖɩmaɣ-mɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩfɛyɩ mɩ-cɔlɔ yɔ, tɩfɛyɩ ndɩ nɛ mbʋ́ ɖɩlakɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩwɛɣ mɩ-cɔlɔ yɔ.

eng: We wont dare to place ourselves in the same league or to compare ourselves with some of those who are promoting themselves. When they measure themselves by themselves, and compare themselves with themselves, they have no understanding.
local: Đɩɩcazɩɣ ɖa-tɩ kpaɣʋ se ɖɩkɛdɩna yaa ɖɩmaɣzɩnɩ mbá pawɩlɩɣ pa-tɩ yem yɔ. Pa-ñʋŋ taa fɛyɩ yɔɔ pɛkɛdɩɣnɩ pa-tɩ yaa pamaɣzɩɣnɩ pa-tɩ nɛ pa-maɣmaɣ.

eng: We wont take pride in anything more than what is appropriate. Lets look at the boundaries of our work area that God has assigned to us. Its an area that includes you.
local: Đa lɛ, tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ Ɛsɔ cɛlɩ-ɖʋ yɔ, ɖi-ɖeke ɖɩkʋndʋʋna. Đɩɩkʋndʋʋnɩ nabʋyʋ ɖɔɖɔ. Ɛsɔ ɖʋzɩ ɖe-nesi taa tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ yɔ, ńɖɩ́ lɛ se halɩ ɖɩkɔɔ ɖooo mɩ-tɛ.

eng: We arent going out of bounds, as if our work area doesnt extend as far as you. We were the first ones to travel as far as Corinth with the gospel of Christ.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩkɔnaʋ Krɩstʋ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm mɩ-tɛ yɔ, ɖɩtɛɖɛɛ kámaɣ́ ŋ́gá Ɛsɔ ɖʋ-ɖʋ yɔ kɔ-yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɛkɛnɩ kajalaɣ ñɩma mbá pɔkɔnɩ-mɩ-tʋ yɔ.

eng: We dont take pride in what other people do outside of our boundaries. We hope that our work will be extended even more by you as your faith grows, until it expands fully .
local: Đɩɩkʋndʋʋnɩ lɛlaa tʋma. Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩtaɣ liu se ɩtɛzɩɣ lidaʋ wɛnaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ. Peeɖe ɖɩpɩzɩɣ ɖɩla tʋmɩyɛ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ pɩɖɛnɩ ɛsɩndaa. Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩɩlakɩ nɛ pɩɖɛɛ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋ-ɖʋ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: We hope that our work grows even to the point of the gospel being preached in places beyond Corinth, without bragging about what has already been done in another persons work area.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɖɩpɩzɩɣ ɖiwolo ɖisusi Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ajɛya wɛna akɩlɩ poliŋ mɩ-ñɩnɖɛ yɔ a-taa. Pɩfɛyɩ se ɖɩkʋndɩnɩ mbʋ́ lɛlaa tɛm labʋ yɔ.

eng: But, the one who brags should brag in the Lord. .
local: Ḿbʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Pʋwɛɛ se weyi ɛkʋndʋʋ yɔ, ɛkʋndɩnɩ mbʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ laba yɔ.».

eng: It isnt the person who promotes himself or herself who is approved but the person whom the Lord commends.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ weyi ɛsaŋ ɛ-tɩ yɔ, ɛkɛnɩnɩ kɩbanʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ ɛsɩndaa. Ɛlɛ́, weyi Kɩpaɣlʋ saŋ-ɩ yɔ, ɛnʋ lɛ kɩbanʋ.

eng: I hope that you will put up with me while I act like a fool. Well, in fact, you are putting up with me!.
local: Hayɩ! Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, mentendiɣ-mɩ se itisi man-kɩmɛlɛndʋ tɔm. Ɩmʋ-tʋ.

eng: Im deeply concerned about you with the same concern that God has. As your father, I promised you in marriage to one husband. I promised to present you as an innocent virgin to Christ himself.
local: Malakɩ mɩ-yɔɔ sʋsɩ ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩwɛ ɛzɩ pɛlɛ weyi ɛtasɩ abalʋ nɛ monsuu-i abalʋ kʋɖʋm yɔ. Abalʋ ɛnʋ lɛ Krɩstʋ.

eng: But Im afraid that your minds might be seduced in the same way as the snake deceived Eve with his devious tricks. You might be unable to focus completely on a genuine and innocent commitment to Christ.
local: Ɛlɛ́, sɔɔndʋ kpaɣ-m se ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɖʋm kowobi ɛcɛtɩ Ɛɛvɩ nɛ ɛ-lɛɣtʋ yɔ, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛtaakɔɔ eliɣti mɩ-lɩmaɣza nɛ iyele Krɩstʋ weyi ɩsɔɔlɩ-ɩ nɛ mɩ-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa yem fɛɛɛ yɔ.

eng: If a person comes and preaches some other Jesus than the one we preached, or if you receive a different Spirit than the one you had received, or a different gospel than the one you embraced, you put up with it so easily!.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee nɔɔyʋ ɛkɔm esusi-mɩ Yesu tɔm nɛ tɩwɛnɩ ndɩ nɛ ndʋ́ ɖiisusi-mɩ yɔ yaa ɛkɛdɩ-mɩ Fezuu ŋgʋ́ kɩwɛnɩ ndɩ nɛ ŋgʋ́ ɩɩmʋwa yɔ kʋ-tɔm yaa ɛkɛdɩ-mɩ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ndʋ́ tɩwɛnɩ ndɩ nɛ ndʋ́ paaheyi-mɩ nɛ itisi yɔ yɔ, itisiɣ yem kitisu.

eng: I dont consider myself as second-rate in any way compared to the super-apostles.
local: Ɛyáa ḿbá, ɩkpakɩɣ-wɛ se pɛkɛ Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa sɔsaa. Ŋgʋ́, mantasa se pakɩlɩ-m pʋyʋ!.

eng: But even if Im uneducated in public speaking, Im not uneducated in knowledge. We have shown this to you in every way and in everything we have done.
local: Paa mɛnfɛyɩ nɔɔ leleŋ yɔ, mansɩm nabʋyʋ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɩwɩlɩ-mɩ kpayɩ alɩwaatʋ kpeekpe pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa.

eng: Did I commit a sin by humbling myself to give you an advantage because I preached the gospel of God to you free of charge?.
local: Moluzi man-tɩ nɛ monsusi-mɩ Ɛsɔ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm falaa. Malabʋ ḿbʋ́ se pɩkpazɩ-mɩ yɔ, mayʋsaa yaa we?.

eng: I robbed other churches by taking a salary from them in order to serve you!.
local: Manɖʋzɩ Yesu aŋgba lɛɛna. Mentisaa se ahɛyɩ-m nɛ mansɩnɩ-mɩ.

eng: While I was with you, I didnt burden any of you even though I needed things. The believers who came from Macedonia gave me everything I needed. I kept myself from being a financial drain on you in any way, and I will continue to keep myself from being a burden.
local: Maawɛ mɩ-tɛ nɛ mɛwɛɛ liidiye kʋñɔŋ taa lɛ, mantasʋlʋmɩ mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ pʋyʋ. Ɛlɛ́, Yesu ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma mbá palɩnɩ Masedonii egeetiye taa nɛ pɔkɔɔ yɔ, ḿbá hanɩ-m mbʋ́ pɩtɛɖaɣ-m yɔ. Maaɖɔkɩ man-tɩ pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa se mantaakɛnɩ-mɩ sʋʋtʋ, mɔnɖɔkʋʋ man-tɩ ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Since Christs truth is in me, I wont stop telling the entire area of Greece that Im proud of what I did.
local: Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Krɩstʋ toovenim wɛ man-taa yɔ, mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se: nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ Akayɩ hɔɔlʋʋ nakʋyʋ taa se ɛɖaŋɩɣ-m se mantaakʋndɩnɩ tɔm ńdʋ́.

eng: Why? Is it because I dont love you? God knows that I do!.
local: Mɔntɔsɔɔlɩ-mɩ yɔɔ mɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́ yaa we? Aayɩ! Ɛsɔ nawa se mɔnsɔɔlɩ-mɩ.

eng: But Im going to continue to do what Im doing. I want to contradict the claims of the people who want to be treated like they are the same as us because of what they brag about.
local: Mbʋ́ malakɩ yɔ, mɛwɛɣ pɩ-labʋ yɔɔ se manɖɩɣ nʋmɔʋ mbá pañɩnɩɣ se pehiɣ mbʋ́ paawana nɛ pakʋndɩ se pa-tʋmɩyɛ labʋ wɛ ɛzɩ ɖa-ñɩnɖɛ yɔ.

eng: Such people are false apostles and dishonest workers who disguise themselves as apostles of Christ.
local: Ɛyáa ḿbá, pɛcɛtɩɣ kɩcɛtʋ se pɛkɛ Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa. Paayɔɔdʋʋ toovenim pa-tʋmɩyɛ labʋ taa. Papɩsɩ pa-tɩ Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa kɩpɩsʋ.

eng: And no wonder! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
local: Tɔm tʋnɛ tɩfɛyɩ piti. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Satanɩ maɣmaɣ ñapɩsɩɣ ɛ-tɩ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛñɩlɩsɩɣ yɔ.

eng: It is no great surprise then that his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will be what their actions deserve.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pɩtaaɖɩɣzɩ-mɩ se ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa ñapɩsɩɣ pa-tɩ Ɛsɔ tʋmlaɖaa toovenim ñɩma. Mbʋ́ palakɩ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ pɔkɔŋ tɛmnaʋ.

eng: I repeat, no one should take me for a fool. But if you do, then allow me to be a fool so that I can brag like a fool for a bit.
local: Mantasɩɣ-mɩ heyuu se mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛtaamaɣzɩ se mɛnkɛ kɩmɛlɛŋ. Tɛ yee ɩkpaɣ-m ɛzɩ kɩmɛlɛŋ yɔ yɔ, ɩha-m waɖɛ nɛ ma ɖɔɖɔ manpɩzɩ mankʋndɩ pazɩ.

eng: Im not saying what Im saying because the Lord tells me to. Im saying it like Im a fool. Im putting my confidence in this business of bragging.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ndʋ́ mɔyɔɔdʋʋ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ hanɩ-m nʋmɔʋ se mɔyɔɔdɩ-tʋ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩlabɩ-m se mankʋndɩ yɔɔ mɔyɔɔdʋʋ yem ɛzɩ kɩmɛlɛŋ yɔ.

eng: Since so many people are bragging based on human standards, that is how Im going to brag too.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ kʋndʋʋnɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ mɔnkɔŋ kʋndʋʋ.

eng: Because you, who are so wise, are happy to put up with fools.
local: Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩkɛ lɔŋsɩnɖaa yɔ, iitisiɣni amʋʋya kɩmɛlɛmɩŋ tɔm naa we?.

eng: You put up with it if someone enslaves you, if someone exploits you, if someone takes advantage of you, if someone places themselves over you, or if someone hits you in the face.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, itisiɣ se mbá pɛcɛtɩɣ se pɛkɛ Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa yɔ, palabɩ-mɩ ɛzɩ yomaa yɔ yaa pɔtɔɔ mɩ-yɔɔ yaa pɛcɛtɩ-mɩ yaa peniɣzi-mɩ yaa pɛcɛbɩ-mɩ kadanzɩ.

eng: Im ashamed to say that we have been weak in comparison! But in whatever they challenge me, I challenge them .
local: Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩkpaɣ-m fɛyɛ kpem se mɔyɔɔdɩ se ɖɩɩkɛ ajamaa yɔɔ ɖɩtanazɩ-mɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ! Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, yee pɩcɩbɩ nɔɔyʋ nɛ ɛkʋndɩnɩ nabʋyʋ yɔ, ma ɖɔɖɔ mankʋndʋʋnɩ-pʋ. Mɔyɔɔdʋʋ ɛzɩ kɩmɛlɛŋ yɔ.

eng: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
local: Ɛyáa ḿbá pɛkɛ Ebree ñɩma naa we? Ma ɖɔɖɔ mɛnkɛ Ebree tʋ. Pɛkɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma naa we? Ma ɖɔɖɔ mɛnkɛ Izrɛɛlɩ tʋ. Pɛkɛ Abraham liɖe taa ñɩma naa we? Ma ɖɔɖɔ mɛnkɛ e-liɖe taa tʋ.

eng: Are they ministers of Christ? Im speaking like a crazy person. What Ive done goes well beyond what theyve done. Ive worked much harder. Ive been imprisoned much more often. Ive been beaten more times than I can count. Ive faced death many times.
local: Pɛkɛ Krɩstʋ tʋmlaɖaa naa we? Tɔɔ! Mɔyɔɔdʋʋ ɛzɩ ma-ñʋʋ taa fɛyɩ yɔ. Mɛnkɛ Krɩstʋ tʋmlaɖʋ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ-wɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, malabɩ tʋmɩyɛ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ-wɛ. Paɖʋ-m salaka tam sakɩyɛ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ-wɛ. Pamabɩ-m siŋŋ. Manpam coɖuu sɩm ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: I received the forty lashes minus one from the Jews five times.
local: Yuda ñɩma mabɩ-m ɖaŋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ fɛyɩ nɩɩnaza tam kagbaanzɩ taa.

eng: I was beaten with rods three times. I was stoned once. I was shipwrecked three times. I spent a day and a night on the open sea.
local: Room ñɩma ñolu-m agbazɛɛ tam naadozo taa. Tam kʋɖʋm payaɣ-m pɛɛ se mansɩ. Tam naadozo ɖɛ-mɛlɛ sʋ lɩm. Mancaɣ lɩm taa wɩsɩ nɛ ɖoó pilim.

eng: Ive been on many journeys. I faced dangers from rivers, robbers, my people, and Gentiles. I faced dangers in the city, in the desert, on the sea, and from false brothers and sisters.
local: Ma-nʋmɔŋ ɖɔm taa paa ɖooye lɛ, moncoɖi sɩm pɔsɩ nɛ teŋgu nɛ tɛtʋ ndɩ ndɩ nɛ lakɩŋ pa-taa. Moncoɖi ɖɔɖɔ sɩm mɩ́láa nɛ mbá pɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ nɛ mbá pɛcɛtɩɣ se pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ yɔ pe-nesi taa.

eng: I faced these dangers with hard work and heavy labor, many sleepless nights, hunger and thirst, often without food, and in the cold without enough clothes.
local: Malabɩ tʋma wena awɛ kaɖɛ yɔ nɛ pɩnɩ-m siŋŋ. Nabʋyʋ taa lɛ, mancaɣnɩ mɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ ñɔɔsɩ nɛ lɔkɔtʋ pakʋ-m. Nabʋyʋ taa lɛ, mɛwɛɛ nɔɔ hɔkʋʋ yɔɔ. Mantaawɛnɩ wondu se monsuu nɛ niŋkaɣ kʋ-m.

eng: Besides all the other things I could mention, theres my daily stress because Im concerned about all the churches.
local: Maacazɩɣ yɔɔdʋʋ kʋñɔmɩŋ lɛɛŋ tɔm. Tɛʋ feŋuu yɔ, Yesu aŋgba tɩŋa payɩ lɩmaɣza kʋʋnɩ-m.

eng: Who is weak without me being weak? Who is led astray without me being furious about it?.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛwɛnɩ ɛjandʋ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa yɔ, pʋwɛ ɛzɩ mɛwɛnɩ tɩ-taa yɔ. Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛtɔlɩ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa yɔ, pɩcɛyɩɣ-m ɖɔɖɔ siŋŋ.

eng: If its necessary to brag, Ill brag about my weaknesses.
local: Yee pʋwɛɛ se mankʋndɩ yɔ, mɛ-ɛjandʋ mankʋndʋʋna.

eng: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, the one who is blessed forever, knows that Im not lying.
local: Ɛsɔ weyi ɛkɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Caa yɔ, ɛnawa se maacɛtɩɣ! Pasam-ɩ tam!.

eng: At Damascus the governor under King Aretas was guarding the city of Damascus in order to capture me.
local: Wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Aretaasɩ kasɩ nɔɔyʋ se ɛcɔŋnɩ Damaasɩ tɛtʋ yɔɔ. Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ maawɛ peeɖe yɔ, pʋdʋ ɛnʋ ɛɛɖʋnɩ ɛyáa se paɖaŋ tɛtʋ nɔnɔsɩ taa nɛ pakpa-m.

eng: but I got away from him by being lowered in a basket through a window in the city wall.
local: Ɛlɛ́, nabɛyɛ kpaɣ-m paɖʋ ɖɔkʋ taa nɛ petisi-m nɛ mundoɖe nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ tɛtʋ koloŋa sɔsɔ yɔɔ yɔ ɖɩ-wayɩ nɛ mantʋlɩ, etehiɣ-m.

eng: It is necessary to brag, not that it does any good. Ill move on to visions and revelations from the Lord.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se mankʋndɩ. Ŋgʋ́, kʋndʋʋ fɛyɩ ɖeu. Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, mɛnkɛdɩɣ-mɩ mbʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ yeba nɛ mana nɛ mbʋ́ ɛwɩlɩ-m ɖozee taa yɔ pʋ-tɔm.

eng: I know a man in Christ who was caught up into the third heaven fourteen years ago. I dont know whether it was in the body or out of the body. God knows.
local: Mansɩm Krɩstʋ tʋ nɔɔyʋ , pɩtalɩ pɩnzɩ hiu nɛ naanza yɔ Ɛsɔ kpazʋʋ-ɩ ɛ-cɔlɔ ɖooo ɛsɔdaa yɔ. Mantatɩlɩ se toovenim pɩkpazɩ-ɩ yaa ɛ-kalɩzaɣ ɖeke kpana. Ɛsɔ ɖeke sɩmna.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: Mantasɩɣ yɔɔdʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ se: mantatɩlɩ se toovenim pɩkpazɩ-ɩ yaa ɛ-kalɩzaɣ ɖeke kpana. Ɛsɔ ɖeke sɩmna. Ɛlɛ́, manawa se: alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ kpazɩ-ɩ ɛ-cɔlɔ koboyaɣ taa yɔ, ɛnɩ tɔm ndʋ́ ɛyáa ɛɛpɩzɩɣ pɔyɔɔdɩ-tʋ nɛ pɩtaha nʋmɔʋ se ɛyʋ ɛyɔɔdɩ-tʋ yɔ.

eng: Ill brag about this man, but I wont brag about myself, except to brag about my weaknesses.
local: Mɔnkɔŋ kʋndɩnaʋ ɛyʋ ɛnʋ. Pɩtɛkɛ ma-maɣmaɣ mankʋndʋʋna. Pʋtɔmaɣ yɔ, mɛ-ɛjandʋ mankʋndʋʋna.

eng: This is the third time that Im coming to visit you. Every matter is settled on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
local: Mowokuu mɩ-tɛ yɔ, tozo manɖʋʋ yɔ. Pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Pʋcɔ pakʋ nɔɔyʋ tɔm lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛyáa naalɛ yaa naadozo ɩlɩzɩ aseɣɖe kʋɖʋmɖɩyɛ tɩ-yɔɔ.».

eng: If I did want to brag, I wouldnt make a fool of myself because Id tell the truth. Im holding back from bragging so that no one will give me any more credit than what anyone sees or hears about me.
local: Yee me-liu ɛɛwɛɛ se mankʋndʋʋ yɔ, mantaakɛ kɩmɛlɛŋ. Pʋbʋ maakʋndɩnɩ mbʋ́ pɩlabɩ ɖeyi ɖeyi yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mɔnɖɔkʋʋ man-tɩ man-kʋndʋʋ ḿbʋ́ pɩ-taa se nɔɔyʋ ɛtaakpaɣ-m ɛsɩɩ ɖenɖe mantatalɩ yɔ. Me-liu wɛɛ se pañam-m mbʋ́ malakɩ nɛ panaɣ yɔ nɛ toovenim mbʋ́ mɔyɔɔdʋʋ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: When I was with you on my second visit, I already warned those who continued to sin. Now Im repeating that warning to all the rest of you while Im at a safe distance: if I come again, I wont spare anyone.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ maakɔm mɩ-tɛ nabʋlɛ ɖeɖe yɔ, maatɛm paɣ́ʋ mbá paalabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ nɛ ɛyáa lɛlaa tɩŋa payɩ. Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ mantatasɩ mɩ-cɔlɔ wɛʋ, paa ḿbʋ́ mantasɩɣ-wɛ paɣ́ʋ. Yee mantasɩ pɩsʋʋ mɩ-tɛ yɔ, maakpeɣu mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ.

eng: I was given a thorn in my body because of the outstanding revelations Ive received so that I wouldnt be conceited. Its a messenger from Satan sent to torment me so that I wouldnt be conceited.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pʋcɔ mantaakpazɩ man-tɩ mbʋ́ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ wɩlɩ-m yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, paɖʋ-m kʋdɔŋ nɛ kɩsalɩɣ-m, ŋ́gʋ́ lɛ Satanɩ tiyiyu.

eng: Since you are demanding proof that Christ speaks through me, Christ isnt weak in dealing with you but shows his power among you.
local: Ɩcaɣ se mawɩlɩ-mɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɔyɔɔdʋʋnɩ Krɩstʋ nɔɔ yɔ yaa we? Mawɩlɩɣ-mɩ ɖɔɖɔ. Krɩstʋ tɛkɛ ɛjam mɩ-hɛkʋ taa: ɛwɩlɩɣ-mɩ e-ɖoŋ.

eng: I pleaded with the Lord three times for it to leave me alone.
local: Mentendi Kɩpaɣlʋ tam naadozo se ɛlɩzɩ-m kʋdɔŋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-taa.

eng: Certainly he was crucified because of weakness, but he lives by the power of God. Certainly we also are weak in him, but we will live together with him, because of Gods power that is directed toward you.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-ɛjandʋ yɔɔ pakam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtɩŋnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖoŋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛwɛɛ wezuu. Đa nɛ ɩ ɖɛ-kpɛndʋʋ taa lɛ, ɖɩwɛ ɛjandʋ ɖɔɖɔ. Ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩkɔŋ mɩ-tɛ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɖoŋ ɖɩñɔɔzʋʋnɩ tɔm mɩ-hɛkʋ taa.

eng: He said to me, My grace is enough for you, because power is made perfect in weakness. So Ill gladly spend my time bragging about my weaknesses so that Christs power can rest on me.
local: Peeɖe ecosi-m se: lootiye nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa ŋwɛɛ yɔ, ɖɩɖɔ-ŋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ñɛ-ɛjandʋ taa mawɩlɩɣ mon-ɖoŋ tɩŋa payɩ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pɩkɩlɩ ɖeu se mankʋndɩ nɛ ma-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa mɛ-ɛjandʋ yɔɔ nɛ Krɩstʋ ɖoŋ wɛɛ mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: Examine yourselves to see if you are in the faith. Test yourselves. Dont you understand that Jesus Christ is in you? Unless, of course, you fail the test.
local: Mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩtakɩ mɩ-taa nɛ ɩna se ɩwɛ lidaʋ nʋmɔʋ taa yaa we? Ɩtatɩlɩ se mɩ nɛ Yesu Krɩstʋ ɩkpɛndaa yaa we? Yee ɩnawa se ɩfɛyɩnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ lɛ, ḿbʋ́ lɛ.

eng: Therefore, Im all right with weaknesses, insults, disasters, harassments, and stressful situations for the sake of Christ, because when Im weak, then Im strong.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ man-taa hʋlʋmɩnɩ ɛjandʋ nɛ tʋtʋ nɛ tɛɖʋʋ nɛ wayɩ wɛʋ kaŋ kaŋ nɛ siziŋ weyi manɩɣ Krɩstʋ nʋmɔʋ taa yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mɛwɛɣ ɛjandʋ taa yɔ mɛwɛɣ ɖoŋ.

eng: But I hope that you will realize that we dont fail the test.
local: Ɩkɔŋ naʋ se ɖa ɖatakɩ ɖa-taa nɛ ɖɩna se ɖɩmʋnaa.

eng: Ive become a fool! You made me do it. Actually, I should have been commended by you. Im not inferior to the super-apostles in any way, even though Im a nonentity.
local: Mɔyɔɔdaa ɛzɩ mɛnkɛ kɩmɛlɛŋ yɔ: ɛlɛ́, miyebina nɛ mɔyɔɔdɩ ḿbʋ́. Mɩ pɩɩmʋnaa se ɩlʋ mɔ-yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paa mɛntɛkɛ pʋyʋ yɔ, mbá ɩkpakaɣ-wɛ se pɛkɛ Krɩstʋ tiyiyaa sɔsaa yɔ, pa-taa nɔɔyʋ takɩlɩ-m pʋyʋ.

eng: We pray to God that you dont do anything wrong, not because we want to appear to pass the test but so that you might do the right thing, even if we appear to fail.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɖɩsɛɣ Ɛsɔ se mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛtaatasɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm nabʋyʋ labʋ. Đɩɩcaɣ se ɖɩtɩŋnɩ pʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩwɩlɩ se ɖɩmʋnaa. Ɛlɛ́, paa pɩlakɩ-mɩ ɛzɩ ɖɩtamʋna yɔ yɔ, mɩ mɩla mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ.

eng: The signs of an apostle were performed among you with continuous endurance through signs, wonders, and miracles.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ malakaɣ tʋmɩyɛ mɩ-tɛ yɔ, mɔnɖɔkaɣ man-tɩ pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa nɛ Ɛsɔ tɩŋɩɣnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛlakɩ ɛ-hama lakasɩ ndɩ ndɩ. Ḿbʋ́ wɩlɩɣna se mɛnkɛ Krɩstʋ tiyiyu.

eng: We cant do anything against the truth but only to help the truth.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩfɛyɩnɩ ɖoŋ nɩɩyɩ se ɖɩɩɖɩɣnɩ toovenim nʋmɔʋ. Toovenim ḿbʋ́ pʋ-tɔm taa ɖɩwɛ ɖoŋ.

eng: How were you treated worse than the other churches, except that I myself wasnt a financial burden on you? Forgive me for this wrong!.
local: Ɛbɛ malabɩ Yesu aŋgba lɛɛna taa ñɩma nɛ pɩkɩlɩ-mɩ? Yaa mentekpizi mi-nesi taa yɔɔ? Yee ḿbʋ́, ikpeɣ-m mɛ-ɛzaɣ́tʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: We are happy when we are weak but you are strong. We pray for this: that you will be made complete.
local: Paa ɖɩwɛ ɛjandʋ nɛ mɩ mɩwɛɛ ɖoŋ yɔ, pɩlakɩ-ɖʋ leleŋ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɩsɛɣ Ɛsɔ se eyele nɛ ɩtɛzɩ kpaʋ ɖoŋ ɛ-nʋmɔʋ taa.

eng: Look, Im ready to visit you a third time, and I wont be a burden on you. I dont want your things; I want you. It isnt the childrens responsibility to save up for their parents but parents for children.
local: Mɔñɔɔzɩ man-tɩ se mɔnkɔɔ mɩ-tɛ tozo ɖeɖe. Mantalɩɣ yɔ, maapɔzʋʋ mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ se ɛsɩnɩ-m. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ mɩ-ñɩm mañɩnɩɣ, ɛlɛ́, mɩ-maɣmaɣ mañɩnɩɣ. Toovenim lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ piya cazɩɣna se sɩcalɩ sɩ-ñɩma, ɛlɛ́, ñɩma cazɩɣna se pacalɩ pe-piya.

eng: This is why Im writing these things while Im away. Im writing so that I wont need to act harshly when Im with you by using the authority that the Lord gave me. He gave it to me so that I could build you up, not tear you down.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ mamaɣ-mɩ takayaɣ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mɛwɛ poliŋ yɔ se mɔnkɔm mantalɩ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa lɛ, mantaañazɩ-mɩ. Toovenim lɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ ha-m ɖoŋ se mañazɩnɩ-mɩ nɛ mɔñɔɔzɩnɩ mɩ-ŋgbɛyɛ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛtaha-m-ɩ se mɔyɔkɩnɩ-ɖɩ.

eng: I will very gladly spend and be spent for your sake. If I love you more, will you love me less?.
local: Ma lɛ, paa mankpaɣ ma-ñɩm nɛ mɛwɛɛkɩ mɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, pɩɩcɛyɩɣ-m. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, manpɩzɩɣ mankpaɣ man-tɩ maha nɛ pɩya mɩ-ñʋʋ. Ɛzɩ mankɩlɩ-mɩ sɔɔlʋʋ yɔɔ ɩtɔsɔɔlɩ-m camɩyɛ naa we?.

eng: Finally, brothers and sisters, good-bye. Put things in order, respond to my encouragement, be in harmony with each other, and live in peace—and the God of love and peace will be with you.
local: Taabalaa, pɩlabɩ wiɖiyi! Ɩñaɣ pana nɛ ɩtɛzɩ pɩʋ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa. Ɩhɛzɩ ɖama laŋa. Ɩla nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ. Ɩwɛɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa nɛ Ɛsɔ weyi ɛsɔɔlɩ ɛyáa nɛ ɛhɛzɩɣ pa-laŋa yɔ, ɛwɛɛ mɩ-cɔlɔ.

eng: We all know that I didnt place a burden on you, but in spite of that you think Im a con artist who fooled you with a trick.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩnawa se mantaapɔzɩ mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ se ɛsɩnɩ-m. Ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, nɔɔyʋ pɩzɩɣ ɛmaɣzɩ se mɛncɛtɩ-mɩ kɩcɛtʋ nɛ mehiɣ-mɩ.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All of Gods people say hello to you.
local: Ɩsɛɛ ɖama camɩyɛ ɛzɩ pʋmʋnaʋ yɔ. Ɛsɔ ñɩma tɩŋa payɩ sɛ-mɩ.

eng: I havent taken advantage of you through any of the people I sent to you, have I?.
local: Mantɩŋnɩ mbá mentiyini-mɩ yɔ pa-taa nɔɔyʋ yɔɔ nɛ mɔhɔɔzɩ mi-nesi taa yaa we?.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.
local: Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ ɛla mɩtɩŋa payɩ lootiye nɛ Ɛsɔ sɔɔlɩ-mɩ nɛ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu wɛɛ mɩtɩŋa payɩ mɩ-cɔlɔ nɛ kɩkpɛndɩ-mɩ.

eng: I strongly encouraged Titus to go to you and sent the brother with him. Titus didnt take advantage of you, did he? Didnt we live by the same Spirit? Didnt we walk in the same footsteps?.
local: Meheyi Tiiti se ɛñaɣ pana nɛ ɛkɔɔ mɩ-tɛ. Ɛ nɛ taabalʋ weyi ɩsɩma yɔ mentiyaa. Tiiti kɔm yɔ, ɛhɔɔzɩ mi-nesi taa yaa we? Ɛzɩ pɩtɛkɛ lɩmaɣza kʋɖʋma ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩɖɔmnɩ nʋmɔʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ taa yaa we?.

eng: Have you been thinking up to now that we are defending ourselves to you? Actually, we are speaking in the sight of God and in Christ. Dear friends, everything is meant to build you up.
local: Pʋtɔma ɩmaɣzaɣ mɩ-taa ɖooo lɔŋ se ɖɩcaɣ se ɖiɖezi ɖa-tɩ mɩ-cɔlɔ. Taabalaa kʋsɔɔlaa, ɖa nɛ Krɩstʋ ɖɩkpɛndaa yɔɔ ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa. Điheyiɣ-mɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ḿbʋ́ se ɩtɛzɩ pɩʋ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa.

eng: Im afraid that maybe when I come you will be different from the way I want you to be, and that Ill be different from the way you want me to be. Im afraid that there might be fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, backstabbing, gossip, conceit, and disorderly conduct.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, manpɩsɩɣ mɩ-tɛ. Ɛlɛ́, sɔɔndʋ kpaɣ-m se maamakɩnɩ-mɩ ɛzɩ me-liu wɛʋ yɔ nɛ mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩɩnaɣ-m ɛzɩ mi-liu wɛʋ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ. Tɩkpaɣ-m ɖɔɖɔ se mamakɩnɩ you nɛ ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ nɛ pana nɛ paɖɩyɛ nɛ tʋtʋ nɛ ɛyáa tɔm yɔɔdʋʋ nɛ tɩ kpazʋʋ nɛ yem yem lakasɩ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa.

eng: Im afraid that when I come again, my God may embarrass me in front of you. I might have to go into mourning over all the people who have sinned before and havent changed their hearts and lives from what they used to practice: moral corruption, sexual immorality, and doing whatever feels good.
local: Mantasʋʋ pɩsʋʋ mɩ-tɛ yɔ, sɔɔndʋ kpaɣ-m se mɛ-Ɛsɔ ɖʋʋ-m fɛyɛ mɩ-tɔm taa. Tɩkpaɣ-m ɖɔɖɔ se mɔnkɔŋ tɩmʋʋ mbá paalabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm ɖooo lɔŋ nɛ pataha acɛyɛ nɛ ɛzaɣ́tʋ siɣ́ye yɔ, pa-taa ɛyáa sakɩyɛ yɔɔ.

eng: From Paul, an apostle who is not sent from human authority or commissioned through human agency, but sent through Jesus Christ and God the Father who raised him from the dead;.
local: Ma Yesu tiyiyu Pɔɔlɩ nɛ man-taabalaa tɩŋa mbá pɛwɛ cɩnɛ yɔ, ɖɩmaɣnɩ Krɩstʋ aŋgba wena awɛ Galaatɩ egeetiye taa yɔ a-taa ñɩma takayaɣ kanɛ. Pɩtɛkɛ ɛyáa nabɛyɛ yaa ɛyʋ nɔɔyʋ lɩzɩnɩ-m se mɛnkɛ Yesu tiyiyu. Ɛlɛ́, Yesu Krɩstʋ weyi ɛsɩba nɛ Caa Ɛsɔ fezi-i yɔ, ɛ nɛ Caa Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ, palɩzɩnɩ-m.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Mɛnsɛ-mɩ: ɖa-Caa Ɛsɔ nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ palabɩ-mɩ lootiye nɛ pɛhɛzɩ mɩ-laŋa.

eng: Then after fourteen years I went up to Jerusalem again with Barnabas, and I took Titus along also.
local: Pɩnzɩ hiu nɛ naanza ɖɛwa lɛ, ma nɛ Barnaabaasɩ ɖɩpɩsɩ Yeruusalɛm. Đɩɩkpɛndɩnɩ Tiiti ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: He gave himself for our sins, so he could deliver us from this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father.
local: Krɩstʋ ɛnʋ ɛhanɩ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-tɩ ɖa-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ se ɛlɩzɩ-ɖʋ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ kedeŋa kɩdɛkɛdɩm nesi taa. Ɛlabɩ ḿbʋ́ ɛzɩ ɖa-Caa Ɛsɔ liu wɛʋ yɔ.

eng: I went there because of a revelation, and I laid out the gospel that I preach to the Gentiles for them. But I did it privately with the influential leaders to make sure that I wouldnt be working or that I hadnt worked for nothing.
local: Manpɩsɩ peeɖe, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ wɩlɩnɩ-m se pʋwɛ se mala ḿbʋ́. Mantalaa lɛ, ma nɛ Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ ñʋndɩnaa ɖɩkɛzɩ ɖa-tɩ hɔɔlʋʋ nɛ malɩzɩ-wɛ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ndʋ́ meheyaɣ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, tɩ-tɔbʋʋ. Mantaasɔɔlɩ se tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ mɛntɛm labʋ yɔ nɛ nɖɩ́ mɔnɖɔŋ labʋ yɔ pala aleɣwondu.

eng: To God be the glory forever and always! Amen.
local: Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ, ɛwɛɛ koboyaɣ taa tam! Pɩla ḿbʋ́!.

eng: However, not even Titus, who was with me and who was a Greek, was required to be circumcised.
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ se patatɩɩcɛyɩ man-taabalʋ Tiiti weyi ɛkɛ Grɛɛkɩ tʋ yɔ se ewolo pɛpɛlɩ-ɩ.

eng: Im amazed that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you by the grace of Christ to follow another gospel.
local: Pɩɖɩɣzɩ-m pɩŋŋ nɛ mɩ-tɔm. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩha Ɛsɔ weyi ɛtɩŋnɩ Krɩstʋ lootiye yɔɔ nɛ ɛ́yáa-mɩ se ɩkɛ ɛ-ñɩma yɔ siɣ́ye yem kɛlɛʋ nɛ ɩnɩɣnɩ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm lɛɛtʋ.

eng: But false brothers and sisters, who were brought in secretly, slipped in to spy on our freedom, which we have in Christ Jesus, and to make us slaves.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mbá pɛcɛtaɣ se pɛkɛ Krɩstʋ ñɩma nɛ paasʋ ɖɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa yɔ, ḿbá yɔɔdaɣna se pʋwɛɛ se pɛpɛlɩ-ɩ. Ŋgʋ́, ɛyáa ḿbá, mɛsaɣ tɛɛ paasʋ ɖa-taa se pana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋnɩ Krɩstʋ Yesu yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩwɛɛ ɖa-tɩ yɔɔ yɔ. Pañɩnaɣ se ɖɩɖaɣnɩ pɩsʋʋ yomaa.

eng: Its not really another gospel, but certain people are confusing you and they want to change the gospel of Christ.
local: Ŋgʋ́, tɩtɛkɛ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm siŋŋ. Ɛlɛ́, nabɛyɛ wɛɛ peliɣtiɣ-mɩ nɛ pacaɣ se pɛlɛɣzɩ Krɩstʋ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: We didnt give in and submit to them for a single moment, so that the truth of the gospel would continue to be with you.
local: Đɩtatɩɩhaʋ-wɛ waɖɛ paa ñɩɩɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩñɩnaɣ se Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm toovenim ɛwɛɛ mɩ-taa ḿbʋ́.

eng: However, even if we ourselves or a heavenly angel should ever preach anything different from what we preached to you, they should be under a curse.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, paa ɖa-maɣmaɣ ɖa-taa nɔɔyʋ yaa tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ ɛlɩnɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ ɛkɔɔ esusuu-mɩ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ndʋ́ tɩwɛ ndɩ nɛ ndʋ́ ɖisusi-mɩ yɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɛwɩlɩ-ɩ kpalʋʋ!.

eng: The influential leaders didnt add anything to what I was preaching—and whatever they were makes no difference to me, because God doesnt show favoritism.
local: Ɛyáa mbá pɔcɔŋaɣnɩ-wɛ se pɛkɛ ñʋndɩnaa yɔ, patakpa-m nɛ ɖoŋ se mala pʋyʋ. Halɩ mantakpaɣ pɔ-sɔsɔtʋ nɛ mayaa pʋyʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ wɛ kɩmaŋ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa.

eng: Im repeating what weve said before: if anyone preaches something different from what you received, they should be under a curse!.
local: Đɩtɛm-mɩ-tʋ heyuu nɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ mantasɩɣ-mɩ-tʋ heyuu se: yee nɔɔyʋ eheyi-mɩ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm natʋyʋ yem nɛ pɩtɛkɛnɩ ndʋ́ ɖiiheyi-mɩ nɛ itisi yɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɛwɩlɩ-ɩ kpalʋʋ!.

eng: But on the contrary, they saw that I had been given the responsibility to preach the gospel to the people who arent circumcised, just as Peter had been to the circumcised.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ñʋndɩnaa ḿbá, pa-maɣmaɣ peɖiɣzinaa se Ɛsɔ ɖʋnɩ-m tʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ se mɔncɔɔ meheyi kɩtɛpɛlɩŋ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm: ḿbá lɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ ɛɛɖʋ Pɩyɛ se ɛcɔɔ nɛ eheyi-tʋ mbá paba kɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ.

eng: Am I trying to win over human beings or God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I wouldnt be Christs slave.
local: Ɩmaɣzɩɣ se mɔyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, mancaɣ se ɛyáa ɩyɩɣyɩ-m yaa Ɛsɔ ɛyɩɣyɩnɩ-m? Ɩmaɣzɩɣ se malakɩ se pɩwɛɛ pɩkɛdɩɣnɩ ɛyáa naa we? Yee me-liu ɛɛwɛ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, mantaakɛnɩ Krɩstʋ tʋmlaɖʋ.

eng: The one who empowered Peter to become an apostle to the circumcised empowered me also to be one to the Gentiles.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ yeba nɛ manpɩsɩ Yesu tiyiyu nɛ etiyi-m mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ etiyuu Pɩyɛ Yuda ñɩma cɔlɔ yɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the gospel I preached isnt human in origin.
local: Taabalaa! Mankaɖɩɣ-mɩ se Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ndʋ́ monsusuu yɔ, tɩtalɩɩnɩ ɛyáa cɔlɔ.

eng: James, Cephas, and John, who are considered to be key leaders, shook hands with me and Barnabas as equals when they recognized the grace that was given to me. So it was agreed that we would go to the Gentiles, while they continue to go to the people who were circumcised.
local: Yakubu nɛ Pɩyɛ nɛ Yohanɛɛsɩ mbá Krɩstʋ ñɩma kpakaɣ se pɛkɛ sɔsaa yɔ, peɖiɣzinaa se Ɛsɔ ɖʋzɩnɩ tʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ me-nesi taa. Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ paha ma nɛ Barnaabaasɩ nesi nɛ pɩwɩlɩ se ɖa nɛ wɛ ɖɩkpɛndaa ɖɩla nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖatɩŋa ɖɩnɩnɩ ɖama nɛ ma nɛ Barnaabaasɩ ɖiwolo ɖɩla tʋmɩyɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ piyele ḿbá ñowolo Yuda ñɩma cɔlɔ.

eng: I didnt receive it or learn it from a human. It came through a revelation from Jesus Christ.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ ɛyʋ nɔɔyʋ kɛdɩnɩ-m-tʋ yaa nɔɔyʋ kpɛlɩkɩnɩ-m-tʋ, ɛlɛ́, Yesu Krɩstʋ maɣmaɣ wɩlɩnɩ-m-tʋ.

eng: They asked only that we would remember the poor, which was certainly something I was willing to do.
local: Tɔm kʋɖʋmtʋ koŋ peheyi-ɖʋ nɛ mɔnɖɔkɩ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se ɖɩtɔzɩ taabalaa kʋñɔndɩnaa yɔɔ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ mañaɣ pana nɛ mala.

eng: You heard about my previous life in Judaism, how severely I harassed Gods church and tried to destroy it.
local: Pɔyɔɔdaa nɛ ɩnɩɩ mbʋ́ malakaɣ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ malakaɣ Yuda ñɩma sɔnzɩ yɔ. Tɩ-taa lɛ, maawɛ Ɛsɔ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ nɛ mañɩnɩɣ se mankʋ patɩŋa.

eng: But when Cephas came to Antioch, I opposed him to his face, because he was wrong.
local: Pɩyɛ talɩ Antɩyɔɔsɩ lɛ, mankʋyɩ ɛ-yɔɔ ɛyáa tɩŋa ɛsɩndaa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛlabɩ mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ.

eng: I advanced in Judaism beyond many of my peers, because I was much more militant about the traditions of my ancestors.
local: Đɔ-ɖɔndɩyɛ taa lɛ, malakaɣ ɖɔ-cɔzɔnaa Yuda ñɩma sɔnzɩ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, maaɖɔkɩ-sɩ nesi naalɛ kpam.

eng: He had been eating with the Gentiles before certain people came from James. But when they came, he began to back out and separate himself, because he was afraid of the people who promoted circumcision.
local: Toovenim lɛ, Pɩyɛ nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, pɛkpɛndaɣ nɛ pɔtɔkɩ tɔɔnaɣ. Ɛlɛ́, Yakubu kɔm etiyi nabɛyɛ nɛ Pɩyɛ na-wɛ lɛ, ɛkɛzɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ hɔɔlʋʋ. Ɛ nɛ wɛ patatasɩ kpɛndʋʋ tɔɔnaɣ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, sɔɔndʋ kpa-ɩ nɛ mbá pɔtɔŋaɣ se pʋwɛɛ se pɛpɛlɩ paa weyi yɔ.

eng: But God had set me apart from birth and called me through his grace. He was pleased.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ ya-m. Pʋcɔ palʋlɩ-m lɛ, ɛɛtɛm-m lɩzʋʋ nɛ ɛlabɩ-m lootiye nɛ ɛya-m se mala ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ. Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɩmaɣ-ɩ.

eng: And the rest of the Jews also joined him in this hypocrisy so that even Barnabas got carried away with them in their hypocrisy.
local: Krɩstʋ ñɩma lɛlaa mbá pɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, ḿbá tisini Pɩyɛ tɔm nɛ palakɩ ɖɔɖɔ tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ lakasɩ nɛ piyele Barnaabaasɩ maɣmaɣ ñɛtɛzɩ pa-wayɩ tɩŋʋʋ.

eng: to reveal his Son to me, so that I might preach about him to the Gentiles. I didnt immediately consult with any human being.
local: se ɛlɩzɩɣ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ ɛwɩlɩ-m nɛ meheyi mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-tɔm lɛ, mɔntɔpɔzɩ nɔɔyʋ se ɛtasɩ-m tɔm natʋyʋ.

eng: But when I saw that they werent acting consistently with the truth of the gospel, I said to Cephas in front of everyone, If you, though youre a Jew, live like a Gentile and not like a Jew, how can you require the Gentiles to live like Jews.
local: Mɔnkɔm mana se Pɩyɛ ɛɛɖɔŋ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm yɔɔ nɛ toovenim lɛ, mɔnpɔzɩ-ɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa ɛsɩndaa kpayɩ se: «Weyi ña ŋkɛ Yuda tʋ yɔ, yee ña-lakasɩ ɛwɛ ɛzɩ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ lɛ, pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ŋcɛyɩɣ paba se pala ɛzɩ Yuda ñɩma yɔ?».

eng: I didnt go up to Jerusalem to see the men who were apostles before me either, but I went away into Arabia and I returned again to Damascus.
local: Montowolo ɖɔɖɔ Yeruusalɛm se mana mbá pɛkɛ Yesu tiyiyaa nɛ pʋcɔ pɩtalɩnɩ-m yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mɛnɖɛ kpaagbaa mowolo Araabii ɛjaɖɛ taa nɛ pʋcɔ manpɩsɩ Damaasɩ tɛtʋ taa.

eng: We are born Jews—were not Gentile sinners.
local: Đa lɛ, Yuda ñɩma liɖe taa palʋlɩ-ɖʋ. Đɩfɛyɩ ɛzɩ lila lɛɛna taa ñɩma. Paba ñaaɖɔŋ Ɛsɔ paɣtʋ yɔɔ.

eng: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and stayed with him fifteen days.
local: Pɩnzɩ naadozo ɖɛwa lɛ, mowolo Yeruusalɛm nɛ mansɩ Pɩyɛ nɛ ma nɛ ɩ ɖɩcaɣ kɩyakɩŋ hiu nɛ kagbaanzɩ.

eng: However, we know that a person isnt made righteous by the works of the Law but rather through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ. We ourselves believed in Christ Jesus so that we could be made righteous by the faithfulness of Christ and not by the works of the Law—because no one will be made righteous by the works of the Law.
local: Đɩnawa se pɩtɛkɛ weyi ɛɖɔŋ paɣtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ lɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa. Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ Yesu yɔɔ yɔ lɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖa ɖɔɖɔ ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ se pɩtɩŋnɩ kʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩkɛ kʋtʋʋzaa Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtaakɛna se ɖɩɖɔŋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛkɛɣ kʋtʋʋzʋ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa se ɛɖɔŋ ɛ-paɣtʋ yɔɔ.

eng: But I didnt see any other of the apostles except James the brother of the Lord.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mantana Yesu tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ ɛ nɛ Yakubu weyi ɛkɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu neu yɔ.

eng: But if it is discovered that we ourselves are sinners while we are trying to be made righteous in Christ, then is Christ a servant of sin? Absolutely not!.
local: Yee ɖɩcaɣ se ɖɩtɩŋnɩ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩkɛ kʋtʋʋzaa Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa nɛ piyeki se ɖɩna se ɖɩkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa ɛzɩ lɛlaa yɔ yɔ, pɩwɩlɩɣ se Krɩstʋ ɛnʋ ɛwɩlɩɣnɩ-ɖʋ kɩwɛɛkɩm nʋmɔʋ naa we? Toovenim lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́!.

eng: Before God, Im not lying about the things that Im writing to you!.
local: Ɛsɔ maɣmaɣ nawa se tɔm ndʋ́ meheyiɣ-mɩ yɔ tɩkɛ toovenim, maacɛtɩɣ-mɩ.

eng: If I rebuild the very things that I tore down, I show that I myself am breaking the Law.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee nɔɔyʋ eyebi paɣtʋ natʋyʋ se tɩfɛyɩ ɖeu nɛ ɛɖaɣnɩ tɩ-wayɩ pɩsʋʋ yɔ, pʋdʋ wɩlɩɣ se ɛwɛɛ ɛyʋsʋʋnɩ Ɛsɔ.

eng: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
local: Mankʋyɩ Yeruusalɛm lɛ, mowolo Siirii nɛ Siliisii pa-ageeta taa.

eng: I died to the Law through the Law, so that I could live for God.
local: Paɣtʋ yɔɔ lɛ, manpɩsɩ ɛzɩ sɩɖʋ yɔ nɛ tɩtatasɩ kɛʋ pʋyʋ mɛ-ɛsɩndaa. Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se manpɩzɩ mancaɣ wezuu Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa. Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pakaŋaɣ Krɩstʋ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ yɔ, ma nɛ ɩ pakama.

eng: but I wasnt known personally by the Christian churches in Judea.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa lɛ, Krɩstʋ aŋgba wena awɛ Yudee egeetiye taa yɔ, a-taa ñɩma tasɩmdɩ-m.

eng: I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me. And the life that I now live in my body, I live by faith, indeed, by the faithfulness of Gods Son, who loved me and gave himself for me.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, mɛwɛ wezuu. Pɩtɛkɛ mɔn-cɔlɔ pɩlɩnaa, ɛlɛ́, Krɩstʋ wezuu wɛnɩ man-taa. Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mɛwɛ wezuu kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ yɔ, Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ weyi ɛsɔɔlɩ-m nɛ ɛsɩ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-yɔɔ mɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ.

eng: They only heard a report about me: The man who used to harass us now preaches the faith that he once tried to destroy.
local: Ɛlɛ́, tɔm kʋɖʋmtʋ ndʋ́ paanɩwa yɔ lɛ se: «Đooo weyi ɛɛwɛ ɖa-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ yɔ, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɛnʋ susuuni Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ndʋ́ ɛñɩnaɣ se eɖezi yɔ.».

eng: I dont ignore the grace of God, because if we become righteous through the Law, then Christ died for no purpose.
local: Ma lɛ, maayeki nɛ Ɛsɔ lootiye pɩsɩ aleɣwondu kaʋ. Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa se ɛɖɔŋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ yɔ, pʋbʋ Krɩstʋ sɩbɩ falaa yem.

eng: So they were glorifying God because of me.
local: Panɩ mɔn-tɔm ńdʋ́ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɛwɛɛ pasaŋ Ɛsɔ mɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: You irrational Galatians! Who put a spell on you? Jesus Christ was put on display as crucified before your eyes!.
local: Galaatɩ ñɩma kɩmɛlɛmɩŋ mɩ! Ahɔkɩ-mɩ fadɩŋ nɛ ɩwɛɛ ḿbʋ́? Ŋgʋ́, palɩzɩ-mɩ mbʋ́ pʋ-yɔɔ Yesu Krɩstʋ sɩbɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ yɔ pʋ-tɔm taa kpayɩ kpayɩ.

eng: I just want to know this from you: Did you receive the Spirit by doing the works of the Law or by believing what you heard?.
local: Tɔm kʋɖʋmtʋ koŋ mɔnpɔzʋʋ-mɩ, ńdʋ́ lɛ se: ɩɖɔŋ Yuda ñɩma paɣtʋ yɔɔ camɩyɛ yɔɔ Ɛsɔ ha-mɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yaa ɩnɩ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm nɛ itisi-tʋ yɔɔ ɛha-mɩ-kʋ? Icosi-m nɛ manɩɩ.

eng: Are you so irrational? After you started with the Spirit, are you now finishing up with your own human effort?.
local: Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩwɛɛ kɩmɛlɛndʋ ḿbʋ́? Ɛsɔ ha-mɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu nɛ ɩpazɩ lɛ, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩcaɣ se ɩtɛzɩnɩ mɩ-maɣmaɣ mi-ɖoŋ?.

eng: Did you experience so much for nothing? I wonder if it really was for nothing.
local: Ɩmaɣzɩɣ se ɩtɔɔ kʋñɔmɩŋ ɩnʋ falaa yaa we? Pɩɩpɩzɩɣ pɩla ḿbʋ́.

eng: So does the one providing you with the Spirit and working miracles among you do this by you doing the works of the Law or by you believing what you heard?.
local: Ɩɖɔŋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ yɔɔ Ɛsɔ ha-mɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu nɛ ɛlakɩ hama lakasɩ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa yaa we? Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́! Ɩnɩ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm nɛ itisi-tʋ yɔɔ ɛha-mɩ-kʋ.

eng: Understand that in the same way that Abraham believed God and it was credited to him as righteousness, .
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Abraham laba. Pama ɛ-tɔm Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔɔ ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ-ɩ se ɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ.».

eng: those who believe are the children of Abraham.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se ɩtɩlɩ se mbá pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ, ḿbá kɛnɩnɩ Abraham sayɩnaa siŋŋ.

eng: But when it saw ahead of time that God would make the Gentiles righteous on the basis of faith, scripture preached the gospel in advance to Abraham: All the Gentiles will be blessed in you .
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa ɖooo lɔŋ se mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, pɔkɔŋ wɛnaʋ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ se ɛ́lɛ́ ɛkpaɣ-wɛ se pɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzaa. Pʋ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ kalayɩnɩ Abraham heyuu laŋhɛzɩyɛ tɔm. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Ñɔ-yɔɔ lɛ, mɔnkɔŋ wazʋʋ kedeŋa kpeekpe ɛyáa yɔɔ.».

eng: Therefore, those who believe are blessed together with Abraham who believed.
local: Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Abraham wɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ ɛwazɩ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ ɛkɔŋ wazʋʋ mbá patɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: All those who rely on the works of the Law are under a curse, because it is written, Everyone is cursed who does not keep on doing all the things that have been written in the Law scroll .
local: Mbá patɩŋa payɩ pamaɣzɩɣ se pehikiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ se pɔɖɔŋ Yuda ñɩma paɣtʋ yɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ wɩlɩɣ-wɛ kpalʋʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛɛɖɔŋ mbʋ́ pɩwɛ paɣtʋ takayaɣ taa yɔ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋ-yɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɛwɩlɩ pʋdʋ kpalʋʋ.».

eng: But since no one is made righteous by the Law as far as God is concerned, it is clear that the righteous one will live on the basis of faith .
local: Toovenim lɛ, Ɛsɔ ɛɛpɩsɩɣ nɔɔyʋ kʋtʋʋzʋ se pʋdʋ ɖɔŋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ. Ḿbʋ́ pama ka-taa se: «Weyi ɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ e-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛwɛɣ wezuu.».

eng: The Law isnt based on faith; rather, the one doing these things will live by them .
local: Piyele paɣtʋ tɔm taa lɛ, lidaʋ tɔm fɛyɩnɩ wazaɣ nakɛyɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pama tɩ-tɔm ɖɔɖɔ se: «Weyi ɛɖɔŋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ yɔ, pɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ tɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛcaɣ wezuu.».

eng: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the Law by becoming a curse for us—because it is written, Everyone who is hung on a tree is cursed .
local: Pɩwɩlɩɣ se Ɛsɔ tɛm ɖɔ-tɔm kʋʋ. Ɛlɛ́, Krɩstʋ pɩsɩnɩ weyi Ɛsɔ wɩlɩ kpalʋʋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́ɣzɩ ɖo-lone taa se ɛ́lɛ́ ɛtaatasɩ-ɖʋ-kʋ wɩlʋʋ. Ḿbʋ́ ɛlaba nɛ ɛya ɖa-ñʋʋ. Pʋwɛ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pama ka-taa ɖɔɖɔ se: «Weyi popuzuu-i tɩʋ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ Ɛsɔ wɩlɩɣ kpalʋʋ.».

eng: He redeemed us so that the blessing of Abraham would come to the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, and that we would receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
local: Ɛsɔ labɩ ḿbʋ́ se pɩtɩŋnɩ Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛha ñɩm kɩbam mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ Abraham yɔ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ ɖɔɖɔ. Ḿbʋ́ ɛtɩŋnɩ lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ɖɩwɛnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛha-ɖʋ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ŋgʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ-ɖʋ yɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Ill use an example from human experience. No one ignores or makes additions to a validated will.
local: Taabalaa! Đɩkpaɣ mbʋ́ ɛyáa lakɩ nɛ ɖɩkɛdɩnɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ laba yɔ: yee nɔɔyʋ ɛɖʋ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ patayɩ ɛ-ñɩm ɛ-sɩm wayɩ nɛ ɛ-ñɩɣ niye yɔ, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ekizini-i yaa ɛɖʋ nɔɔ pɩ-taa.

eng: The promises were made to Abraham and to his descendant. It doesnt say, and to the descendants, as if referring to many rather than just one. It says, and to your descendant, who is Christ.
local: Ɛsɔ ñaɖʋwa ɖɔɖɔ se ɛlakɩ Abraham nɛ ɛ-sayɩ pʋyʋ. Ɛlɛ́, patama se: «Abraham nɛ ɛ-sayɩnaa.» Ɛlɛ́, pamawa se: «Abraham nɛ ɛ-sayɩ.» Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ tɔm pamawa, ɛlɛ́, kʋɖʋm koŋ tɔm pamawa. Ɛnʋ lɛ Krɩstʋ.

eng: Im saying this: the Law, which came four hundred thirty years later, doesnt invalidate the agreement that was previously validated by God so that it cancels the promise.
local: Iwelesi nɛ ɩnɩɩ tɔm ndʋ́ mancaɣ-mɩ heyuu yɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: Ɛsɔ kaɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ Abraham pʋyʋ nɛ ɛñɩɣ niye. Pɩnzɩ nasɩnaza nɛ niidozo ɖɛwa lɛ, ɛɖʋ Yuda ñɩma paɣtʋ. Ɛlɛ́, tɩɩpɩzɩɣ tɩwɛɛkɩ mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: If the inheritance were based upon the Law, it would no longer be from the promise. But God has given it graciously to Abraham through a promise.
local: Yee pɩɩkɛ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɛɛlaba ɛyáa kʋjɔʋ se pɔɖɔŋ paɣtʋ yɔɔ. Pʋbʋ pɩɩwɩlaa se mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ Abraham yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ɛɖɔŋ. Ŋgʋ́, mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ-ɩ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ ɛtɩŋnaa kpem nɛ ɛlabɩ-ɩ lootiye.

eng: Im saying that as long as the heirs are minors, they are no different from slaves, though they really are the owners of everything.
local: Ndʋ́ mancaɣ yɔɔdʋʋ yɔ lɛ se: weyi ɛtɩɣyɩɣ ɛ-caa ñɩm yɔ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛtapaɣlɩda yɔ, ɛfɛyɩ ndɩ nɛ yom. Ɛlɛ́, ɛnʋ kɔŋna tɩɣyʋʋ ɛ-caa ñɩm tɩŋa.

eng: So why was the Law given? It was added because of offenses, until the descendant would come to whom the promise had been made. It was put in place through angels by the hand of a mediator.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛbɛ yɔɔ Ɛsɔ ɖʋ paɣtʋ yɔ? Ɛɖʋ-tʋ se pɩtɩŋnɩ tɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛyáa tɩlɩ mbʋ́ pɩkɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ. Ɛɖʋ-tʋ ɖɔɖɔ se tɩwɛɛ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ palʋlɩ Abraham sayɩ weyi Ɛsɔ kaɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ-ɩ pʋyʋ yɔ. Paɣtʋ ńdʋ́ ɛcɛlɩ e-tiyiyaa. Paba ñatɩŋnɩ Mooyiizi yɔɔ nɛ powoni-tʋ ɛyáa.

eng: However, they are placed under trustees and guardians until the date set by the parents.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛwɛ pɩcatʋ taa yɔ, ɛyáa nabɛyɛ nesi taa ɛwɛɛ nɛ pɔcɔŋnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛ-caa kaɖʋwa yɔ.

eng: Now the mediator does not take one side; but God is one.
local: Ɛyáa naalɛ hɛkʋ taa weyi patɩŋɩɣnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ ɛwɛɛ. Weyi patɩŋɩɣnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛfɛyɩ wazaɣ ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm tɔm taa. Ŋgʋ́, Ɛsɔ ñɛwɛ e-ɖeke.

eng: In the same way, when we were minors, we were also enslaved by this worlds system.
local: Đa lɛ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ ɖɩɩwɛ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩɩwɛ ɛzɩ piya yɔ. Đɩɩkɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ɖoŋ yomaa.

eng: So, is the Law against the promises of God? Absolutely not! If a Law had been given that was able to give life, then righteousness would in fact have come from the Law.
local: Pɩwɩlɩɣ se paɣtʋ kʋyʋʋ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ yaa we? Toovenim lɛ, pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́! Yee paaɖʋ paɣtʋ natʋyʋ nɛ tɩhaɣ ɛyáa wezuu yɔ, tɩɩpɩzaa nɛ tɩpɩsɩ-wɛ kʋtʋʋzaa Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa. Ŋgʋ́, pataɖʋ natʋyʋ ḿbʋ́.

eng: But when the fulfillment of the time came, God sent his Son, born through a woman, and born under the Law.
local: Ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ talaa lɛ, Ɛsɔ yele nɛ pɩtɩŋnɩ halʋ nɔɔyʋ yɔɔ nɛ palʋlɩ Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ nɛ Yuda ñɩma paɣtʋ wɛnɩ ɖoŋ ɛ́lɛ́ yɔɔ.

eng: Christ has set us free for freedom. Therefore, stand firm and dont submit to the bondage of slavery again.
local: Krɩstʋ holi-ɖʋ se ɖɩwɛɛ ɖa-tɩ yɔɔ kpem. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩñaɣ pana nɛ ɩwɛɛ mɩ-tɩ yɔɔ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɩtaatasɩ pɩsʋʋ yomaa.

eng: But scripture locked up all things under sin, so that the promise based on the faithfulness of Jesus Christ might be given to those who have faith.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔtɔm wɩlɩ kpayɩ se kedeŋa yɔɔ ɛyáa kpeekpe wɛ kɩwɛɛkɩm nesi taa se pɩtɩŋnɩ Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔɔ lidaʋ wɛnaʋ yɔɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ la mbá pɛwɛnɩ-kʋ yɔ mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ yɔ.

eng: This was so he could redeem those under the Law so that we could be adopted.
local: se eholi mbá tɩɩwɛnɩ ɖoŋ pɔ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ yekina nɛ ɖɩpɩsɩ Ɛsɔ piya.

eng: Look, I, Paul, am telling you that if you have yourselves circumcised, having Christ wont help you.
local: Iwelesi nɛ ɩnɩɩ, ma Pɔɔlɩ meheyiɣ-mɩ se: yee itisaa nɛ pɛpɛlɩ-mɩ yɔ, pʋbʋ Krɩstʋ tatasɩ-mɩ wɛʋ wazaɣ nakɛyɛ.

eng: Before faith came, we were guarded under the Law, locked up until faith that was coming would be revealed.
local: Pʋcɔ ɖɩwɛnɩ-kʋ lɛ, pɩwɛ ɛzɩ ɖɩɩwɛ salaka taa nɛ paɣtʋ cɔŋnɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ wɩlɩ-ɖʋ kʋ-nʋmɔʋ yɔ.

eng: Because you are sons and daughters, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
local: Mbʋ́ pɩwɩlɩɣ se ɖɩkɛ e-piya siŋŋ yɔ lɛ se eyeba nɛ ɛ-Pɩyalʋ Fezuu sʋʋ ɖa-taa nɛ kɩyɔɔdʋʋ se: «Man-Caa!».

eng: Again I swear to every man who has himself circumcised that he is required to do the whole Law.
local: Mantasɩɣ-mɩ heyuu se: weyi etisiɣ nɛ pɛpɛlɩ-ɩ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛɖɔ paɣtʋ tɩŋa yɔɔ camɩyɛ.

eng: so that the Law became our custodian until Christ so that we might be made righteous by faith.
local: Ḿbʋ́ paɣtʋ kɔcɔnɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Krɩstʋ kɔma yɔ. Ɛkɔm se pɩtɩŋnɩ ɖe-lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ɖɩwɛnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩpɩsɩ kʋtʋʋzaa Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa.

eng: Therefore, you are no longer a slave but a son or daughter, and if you are his child, then you are also an heir through God.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ŋtatasɩ kɛʋ yom, ɛlɛ́, ŋpɩsɩ Ɛsɔ pɩɣa. Ŋpɩsɩ ɛ-pɩɣa yɔ, ɛhaɣ-ŋ ñɩm mbʋ́ ɛsɩwa se ɛhaɣ e-piya yɔ.

eng: You people who are trying to be made righteous by the Law have been estranged from Christ. You have fallen away from grace!.
local: Mbá mɩ ɩɖɔŋ tɩ-yɔɔ se ɩpɩsɩ kʋtʋʋzaa Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa yɔ, mɩ nɛ Krɩstʋ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa tayaa, ilesi Ɛsɔ lootiye.

eng: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a custodian.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, paɣtʋ ɛɛtasɩɣ ɖɔ-yɔɔ cɔnaʋ.

eng: At the time, when you didnt know God, you were enslaved by things that arent gods by nature.
local: Đooo lɛ, ɩtaasɩ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɩkɛ agɔlɩma yomaa. Ŋgʋ́, atɛkɛ Ɛsɔ.

eng: We eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness through the Spirit by faith.
local: Đa lɛ, ɖɩtaɣ liu se ɛkɔŋ-ɖʋ pɩsʋʋ kʋtʋʋzaa. Ḿbʋ́ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yeba nɛ ɖɩɖaŋ nɛ ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: You are all Gods children through faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩkɛ Ɛsɔ piya: ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ Yesu yɔɔ.

eng: But now, after knowing God , how can you turn back again to the weak and worthless world system? Do you want to be slaves to it again?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɩsɩm Ɛsɔ nɛ halɩ ɛ́lɛ́ ñasɩm-mɩ. Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ ɩcaɣ se ɩɖaɣnɩ pɩsʋʋ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ɖoŋ weyi ɩɩpɩzɩɣ pʋyʋ nɛ ɩɩwazɩɣ pʋyʋ yɔ ɩ-wayɩ se ɩpɩsɩ-mɩ yomaa?.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt matter in Christ Jesus, but faith working through love does matter.
local: Yee ɖa nɛ Krɩstʋ Yesu ɖɩkpɛndaa yɔ, paa pɛpɛlɩ-ɖʋ yaa pɛtɛpɛlɩ-ɖʋ yɔ, pɩfɛyɩnɩ wazaɣ nakɛyɛ: mbʋ́ pʋwɛ wazaɣ yɔ lɛ lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ɖɩwɛnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ kiyeki se ɖɩsɔɔlɩ ɖama yɔ.

eng: All of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
local: Mbá mɩ mɩtɩŋa payɩ palabɩ lɩm sɔʋ sɔnzɩ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ yɔ, mɩ nɛ ɩ ɩpɩsɩ kʋɖʋm.

eng: You observe religious days and months and seasons and years.
local: Mɩ-ɛsɩyɛ kɩlɩɣ sɛnaʋ kɩyakɩŋ nɩɩyɩ nɛ fenasɩ nasɩyɩ nɛ alɩwaatʋ natʋyʋ nɛ pɩnzɩ nasɩyɩ!.

eng: You were running well—who stopped you from obeying the truth?.
local: Đooo ɩñakaɣ pana nɛ ɩɖɔŋ toovenim nʋmɔʋ taa camɩyɛ lɛ, pɩla ɛzɩma ɩɩtasɩɣ labʋ ḿbʋ́ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ? Acɔlɔ pɩlɩnaa?.

eng: There is neither Jew nor Greek; there is neither slave nor free; nor is there male and female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
local: Tayʋʋ nabʋyʋ fɛyɩ Yuda ñɩma nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, yomaa nɛ mbá pɛtɛkɛ yomaa yɔ, nɛ halaa nɛ abalaa, pɛ-hɛkʋ taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩpɩsɩ kʋɖʋm, mɩ nɛ Krɩstʋ Yesu mɩ-kpɛndʋʋ taa.

eng: Im afraid for you! Perhaps my hard work for you has been for nothing.
local: Ḿbʋ́ yebina nɛ sɔɔndʋ kpaɣ-m se tʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩtɩŋa malabɩ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa yɔ, ɖɩtaakɔɔ ɖɩla aleɣwondu.

eng: This line of reasoning doesnt come from the one who calls you.
local: Ɛsɔ weyi ɛyaɣ-mɩ yɔ, pɩtalɩɩnɩ ɛ-cɔlɔ!.

eng: Now if you belong to Christ, then indeed you are Abrahams descendants, heirs according to the promise.
local: Yee ɩkɛ Krɩstʋ ñɩma yɔ, pɩwɩlɩɣ ɖɔɖɔ se ɩkɛ Abraham sayɩnaa. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, Ɛsɔ haɣ-mɩ mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ Abraham nɛ e-liɖe taa ñɩma yɔ.

eng: I beg you to be like me, brothers and sisters, because I have become like you! You havent wronged me.
local: Taabalaa! Mentendiɣ-mɩ se mɩ-wɛtʋ ɛwɛɛ ɛzɩ ma-ñɩndʋ yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ma ɖɔɖɔ mɛwɛ ɛzɩ mɩ yɔ. Ɩtalabɩ-m kɩdɛkɛdɩm nabʋyʋ.

eng: A little yeast works through the whole lump of dough.
local: Ɛyáa yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Sɔsɔɔna pazɩ yekina nɛ somtu huúu.».

eng: You know that I first preached the gospel to you because of an illness.
local: Ɩtɔzɩ se kʋdɔŋ taa maawɛɛ yɔɔ mancalɩ-mɩ susuu Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm.

eng: Im convinced about you in the Lord that you wont think any other way. But the one who is confusing you will pay the penalty, whoever that may be.
local: Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔ lɛ, mantaɣ-mɩ liu se ɩkɔŋ maɣzʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ma yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, weyi eliɣtiɣ-mɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, paa ɛtɩɩkɛ anɩ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kɔŋ hɔm ɛ-nɩŋgbaaŋ.

eng: Though my poor health burdened you, you didnt look down on me or reject me, but you welcomed me as if I were an angel from God, or as if I were Christ Jesus!.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɩwɩɣaɣ-m ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɩlabɩ kʋñɔŋ mɔ-yɔɔ siŋŋ, iteniɣzi-m yaa ɩtɔlɔ-m yem. Ɛlɛ́, ɩmʋ-m ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ yɔ, halɩ ɩmʋ-m ɛzɩ Krɩstʋ Yesu maɣmaɣ yɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if Im still preaching circumcision, why am I still being harassed? In that case, the offense of the cross would be canceled.
local: Taabalaa! Ma lɛ, yee toovenim maawɛɛ monsusuu se pʋwɛɛ se pɛpɛlɩ ɛyáa yɔ, Yuda ñɩma taawɛɛ ma-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ ḿbʋ́. Pʋbʋ, tɔm ndʋ́ mɔyɔɔdaɣ Krɩstʋ weyi pakam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, tɩtaakɛnɩ nɔɔyʋ piti tɔm.

eng: Where then is the great attitude that you had? I swear that, if possible, you would have dug out your eyes and given them to me.
local: Mɩ-taa kɛwɛ leleŋ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, pɩla ɛzɩma lɛɛlɛɛyɔ? Mɩ-tɔm taa lɛ, manpɩzaɣ mɔntɔ se: paa mɩ-ɛsɛ yɔ, ɩpɩzaɣ se ɩhɔɔzɩ-yɛ nɛ ɩcɛlɩ-m.

eng: I wish that the ones who are upsetting you would castrate themselves!.
local: Mbá pɛwɛɛ peliɣtiɣ-mɩ ḿbʋ́ se pɛpɛlɩ-mɩ yɔ, pɛtɛzɩ wobu nɛ palɩzɩ pa-tɩ nɛ sɛsɩ!.

eng: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?.
local: Meheyiɣ-mɩ toovenim yɔɔ manpɩsɩ mi-koyindu naa we?.

eng: You were called to freedom, brothers and sisters; only dont let this freedom be an opportunity to indulge your selfish impulses, but serve each other through love.
local: Taabalaa! Mɩ lɛ, Ɛsɔ ya-mɩ se ɩwɛɛ mɩ-tɩ yɔɔ. Paa ḿbʋ́, pɩtaha-mɩ waɖɛ se ɩla mbʋ́ mɩ-maɣmaɣ mi-liu wɛɛ yɔ mi-wezuu caɣʋ taa. Ɛlɛ́, ɩsɔɔlɩ ɖama nɛ piyele ɩsɩnɩ ɖama camɩyɛ.

eng: They are so concerned about you, though not with good intentions. Rather, they want to shut you out so that you would run after them.
local: Ɛyáa ḿbá, pɛ-ɛsɩyɛ sɛnɩ mbʋ́ palakɩ-mɩ yɔ, ɛlɛ́, pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pañɩnɩɣ se ɩha-m siɣ́ye nɛ ɩnɩɩnɩ pe-ɖeke.

eng: All the Law has been fulfilled in a single statement: Love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paɣtʋ natʋyʋ wɛɛ, ɛyʋ ɛɖɔm tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, pʋbʋ, ɛɖɔm lɛɛtʋ tɩŋa payɩ yɔɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Sɔɔlɩ ña-taabalʋ ɛzɩ ŋsɔɔlʋʋ ña-maɣmaɣ ña-tɩ yɔ.».

eng: However, its always good to have people concerned about you with good intentions, and not just when Im there with you.
local: Toovenim lɛ, pɩwɛ ɖeu se ɛyáa ɛsɩyɛ ɛsɛɛnɩ camɩyɛ lakasɩ tam. Pɩtaakɛ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ mɛwɛ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa yɔ, ti-ɖeke.

eng: But if you bite and devour each other, be careful that you dont get eaten up by each other!.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee ɩwɛɛ nɛ ɩlakɩ ɛzɩ tɛtɛ kpɩna nɛ ɩpaŋ ɖama kela nɛ ɩnazɩɣ ɖama yɔ, pʋbʋ, ɩkpa mɩ-tɩ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, ɩkɔŋ ɩtɛ mɩtɩŋa yem.

eng: My little children, Im going through labor pains again until Christ is formed in you.
local: Men-piya! Mantasɩɣ nɩʋ siziŋ mɩ-yɔɔ ɛzɩ halʋ lʋlʋʋ yɔ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩtɛzɩ pɩʋ nɛ ɩwɛɛ ɛzɩ Krɩstʋ yɔ.

eng: I say be guided by the Spirit and you wont carry out your selfish desires.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, meheyiɣ-mɩ se: iyele nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu wɩlɩ-mɩ nʋmɔʋ mi-wezuu caɣʋ taa. Yee itisi ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɩɩtasɩɣ tɩŋʋʋ nʋmɔŋ kɩdɛkɛdɩŋ weyi mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩsɔɔlaa yɔ.

eng: But I wish I could be with you now and change how I sound, because Im at a loss about you.
local: Me-liu wɛɛ siŋŋ se mɛwɛɛ mɩ-cɔlɔ alɩwaatʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-taa nɛ mana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ maayɔɔdɩnɩ-mɩ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɩcɛyɩɣ-m mɩ-tɔm taa!.

eng: A persons selfish desires are set against the Spirit, and the Spirit is set against ones selfish desires. They are opposed to each other, so you shouldnt do whatever you want to do.
local: Mbʋ́ mɩ-maɣmaɣ mi-liu wɛɛ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu liu wɛɛ. Mbʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu liu ñɛwɛɛ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́ mi-liu ñɛwɛɛ. Mbʋ́ ki-liu wɛɛ yɔ nɛ kɩdɛkɛdɩm mbʋ́ ɛyʋ sɔɔlɩ labʋ yɔ, pɩtata. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɩla mbʋ́ mɩ-maɣmaɣ mi-liu wɛɛ yɔ.

eng: Tell me—those of you who want to be under the Law—dont you listen to the Law?.
local: Mbá mɩ mi-liu wɛɛ se paɣtʋ ɛwɛnɩ ɖoŋ mɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, iheyi-m nɛ mana se ɩnɩ ndʋ́ paɖʋ tɩ-taa yɔ yaa ɩtanɩɩ?.

eng: But if you are being led by the Spirit, you arent under the Law.
local: Yee ɩlakɩ mbʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu wɩlɩɣ-mɩ yɔ yɔ, pʋbʋ, paɣtʋ tatasɩ wɛnaʋ ɖoŋ mɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: Its written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and one by the free woman.
local: Pama tɩ-takayaɣ taa se Abraham wɛnɩ piya naalɛ: halʋ weyi ɛkɛ yom nɛ payaɣ-ɩ se Agaarɩ yɔ, ɛnʋ lʋlɩnɩ lɛɛka nɛ weyi payaɣ se Saara nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ wɛ ɛ-tɩ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ ñalʋlɩ lɛɛka.

eng: The actions that are produced by selfish motives are obvious, since they include sexual immorality, moral corruption, doing whatever feels good.
local: Kɩdɛkɛdɩm mbʋ́ ɛyʋ sɔɔlɩ labʋ yɔ, pɩnaɣ keɣlee. Ḿbʋ́ yɔ: acɛyɛ nɛ mbʋ́ pipisiɣ ɛyáa yɔ nɛ yem yem lakasɩ.

eng: The son by the slave woman was conceived the normal way, but the son by the free woman was conceived through a promise.
local: Yom ñaalʋlɩ ɛ-pɩɣa ɛzɩ halaa tɩŋa lʋlʋʋ yɔ. Piyele weyi ɛwɛ ɛ-tɩ yɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɖʋna nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ ñalʋlɩ ɛ-ñɩŋga.

eng: idolatry, drug use and casting spells, hate, fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, conflict, selfishness, group rivalry.
local: nɛ agɔlɩma tɛɛ luŋuu nɛ fadɩŋ labʋ nɛ koyindiye nɛ you nɛ ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ nɛ pana kɔʋ kadadayaɣ nɛ ɖama yɔɔ kʋyʋʋ nɛ tayʋʋ.

eng: These things are an allegory: the women are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, which gives birth to slave children; this is Hagar.
local: Tɔm tʋnɛ tɩwɩlɩɣ nabʋyʋ, ḿbʋ́ lɛ: nɔsɩ naalɛ haʋ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa yɔ. Agaarɩ nɛ Saara pawɩlɩɣnɩ-sɩ. Yom Agaarɩ ñawɩlɩɣ nɔɔ haʋ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ labɩ Sinayii pʋʋ ñʋʋ taa yɔ, e-piya lɛ yomaa.

eng: jealousy, drunkenness, partying, and other things like that. I warn you as I have already warned you, that those who do these kinds of things wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: nɛ lɛlaa ñɩm yɔɔ ɛsɩyɛ ɖʋʋ nɛ sʋlʋm kpaŋʋʋ nɛ kazandʋ labʋ kadadayaɣ nɛ lakasɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩsɩ lɛɛsɩ ndɩ ndɩ ɖɔɖɔ. Maatɛm-mɩ heyuu nɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ mantasɩɣ-mɩ heyuu se: mbá patɩŋa payɩ palakɩ pɩtɛ lakasɩ ńzɩ́ yɔ, pa-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛɛkaɣ sʋʋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ taa kaʋ.

eng: Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and she corresponds to the present-day Jerusalem, because the city is in slavery with her children.
local: Ɛwɛ ɛzɩ Araabii pʋʋ ŋgʋ́ payaɣ se Sinayii yɔ. Ɛwɛ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ tɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩwɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ nɛ payaɣ-tʋ se Yeruusalɛm yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɩ nɛ tɩ-taa ñɩma tɩŋa pɛwɛ ɛzɩ yomaa yɔ.

eng: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ñahaɣ yɔ lɛ: sɔɔlɩm nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ nɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ nɛ hɔyɛ ɖɔkʋʋ nɛ ɖeu labʋ nɛ camɩyɛ lakasɩ nɛ mbʋ́ ɛyʋ ɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ labʋ.

eng: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, and she is our mother.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Yeruusalɛm ŋgʋ́ kʋkʋ wɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ kɩ-taa ñɩma lɛ mbá pɛwɛ pa-tɩ yɔɔ yɔ, ŋ́gʋ́ wɛna ɛzɩ ɖo-ɖoo yɔ.

eng: gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against things like this.
local: nɛ hɛɛɛ wɛtʋ nɛ tɩ ɖɔkʋʋ. Paɣtʋ tekizi pɩtɛ wɛtʋ ńdʋ́.

eng: Its written: Rejoice, barren woman, you who have not given birth . Break out with a shout, you who have not suffered labor pains; because the woman who has been deserted will have many more children than the woman who has a husband .
local: Ḿbʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Halʋ weyi ña ŋkɛ kaalʋlaɣ nɛ ŋtanɩɩ lʋlʋʋ siziŋ yɔ, ña-taa ɛla leleŋ nɛ ŋtee hendu nɛ ŋmayɩ amaya! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, halʋ weyi ña pekizi-ŋ yɔ, ŋkɔŋ wɛnaʋ piya nɛ pɩkɩlɩ weyi ɛwɛ ɛ-walʋ cɔlɔ yɔ.».

eng: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the self with its passions and its desires.
local: Mbá pɛkɛ Krɩstʋ Yesu ñɩma yɔ, pɛtɛm kpaɣʋ pa-lakasɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩsɩ nzɩ́ paasɔɔlɩ labʋ kpem yɔ sɩtɩŋa payɩ nɛ paka kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, you are children of the promise like Isaac.
local: Taabalaa! Toovenim lɛ, Ɛsɔ ɖʋna nɛ ɩkɛ e-piya ɛzɩ ɛɖʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ palʋlɩ Izaakɩ yɔ.

eng: If we live by the Spirit, lets follow the Spirit.
local: Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu haɣnɩ-ɖʋ wezuu. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se ɖiyele nɛ kɩwɩlɩ-ɖʋ nʋmɔʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: But just as it was then, so it is now also: the one who was conceived the normal way harassed the one who was conceived by the Spirit.
local: Izaakɩ ɛnʋ, paalʋlɩ-ɩ nɛ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖoŋ. Piyele lɛɛka lɛ, palʋlɩ-kɛ ɛzɩ palʋlʋʋ ɛyáa lɛlaa yɔ. Kaawɛ Izaakɩ ɛnʋ ɛ-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛ sɔnɔ yɔ.

eng: Lets not become arrogant, make each other angry, or be jealous of each other.
local: Đɩtaakpazɩ ɖa-tɩ, ɖɩɖɔkɩ ɖa-tɩ nɛ ɖa-pana ɛtaakpanɩ ɖama yaa ɖɩtaaɖʋ ɛsɩyɛ ɖama yɔɔ.

eng: But what does the scripture say? Throw out the slave woman and her son, because the slave womans son wont share the inheritance with the free womans son .
local: Ɛlɛ́, pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se we? Pamawa se: «Đɔɔnɩ halʋ weyi ɛkɛ yom yɔ nɛ ɛ-pɩɣa. Pɩtaha nʋmɔʋ se yom nɛ halʋ weyi ɛwɛ ɛ-tɩ yɔɔ yɔ pe-piya ɛkpɛndɩ patɩɣyɩ pa-caa ñɩm.».

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, we arent the slave womans children, but we are the free womans children.
local: Taabalaa! Yee ḿbʋ́, ɖɩtɛkɛnɩ halʋ weyi ɛkɛ yom yɔ e-piya, ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛwɛ ɛ-tɩ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-ñɩnzɩ ɖɩkɛnaa.

eng: At one time you were like a dead person because of the things you did wrong and your offenses against God.
local: Mɩ lɛ, ɖooo lɛ, ɩɩsɩbɩ mɩ-kɩdɛkɛdɩm nɛ mɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: This is why I, Paul, am a prisoner of Christ for you Gentiles.
local: Tɔm ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ meheyi-mɩ yɔ, tɩ-yɔɔ paɖʋ ma Pɔɔlɩ salaka se malakɩ Krɩstʋ Yesu tʋmɩyɛ mbá mɩ ɩtɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa.

eng: You used to live like people of this world. You followed the rule of a destructive spiritual power. This is the spirit of disobedience to Gods will that is now at work in persons whose lives are characterized by disobedience.
local: Ɩcakaɣ wezuu kɩwɛɛkɩm taa ɛzɩ pʋwɛʋ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ yɔ nɛ ɩñaŋ ɛsɔdaa nɛ tataa pɛ-hɛkʋ taa ɖoŋ wiyaʋ. Wiyaʋ ɛnʋ lɛ Satanɩ. Ɛnʋ seɣtiɣni mbá paañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ.

eng: Youve heard, of course, about the responsibility to distribute Gods grace, which God gave to me for you, right?.
local: Paa pɩɩla ɛzɩma, ɩnɩ lootiye nɖɩ́ Ɛsɔ labɩ-m nɛ ɛɖʋ-m se mala tʋmɩyɛ mɩ-hɛkʋ taa yɔ ɖɩ-tɔm.

eng: At one time you were like those persons. All of you used to do whatever felt good and whatever you thought you wanted so that you were children headed for punishment just like everyone else.
local: Đa ɖɔɖɔ ɖatɩŋa payɩ ɖɩɩwɛ pa-taa. Đɩɖɔŋaɣ ɛzɩ ɖa-maɣmaɣ ɖe-liu wɛʋ yɔ nɛ ɖɩlakɩ mbʋ́ pɩkɛdɩnɩ-ɖʋ yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ ɖɩmaɣzɩɣ yɔ. Pɩɩwɛɛ se Ɛsɔ ɛhʋʋnɩ-ɖʋ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ lɛlaa yɔ wɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-taa ɖɩwɛɛ yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: God showed me his secret plan in a revelation, as I mentioned briefly before .
local: Ńdʋ́ lɛ se ɛwɩlɩ-m mbʋ́ pɩɩwɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ nɛ mansɩm-pʋ ɛzɩ mamaʋ tɔm pee ana lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ.

eng: However, God is rich in mercy. He brought us to life with Christ while we were dead as a result of those things that we did wrong. He did this because of the great love that he has for us. You are saved by Gods grace!.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛnaɣ ɛyáa pʋtɔdɩyɛ pɩŋŋ, ɛsɔɔlɩ-ɖʋ kámaɣ́ fɛyɩ.

eng: when you read this, youll understand my insight into the secret plan about Christ.
local: Ɩkalɩɣ mbʋ́ mamawa yɔ lɛ, ɩna mbʋ́ ɛwɩlɩ-m Krɩstʋ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩɩwɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ tɩ-taa yɔ.
