local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɛmɩzɩ Ɛsɔ kɩɖanɖʋʋ nɛ wondu ndʋ́ tɩtɩŋa payɩ palakɩnɩ tʋmɩyɛ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ poluŋuu ɛ-tɛɛ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ calɩm.

eng: Almost everything is cleansed by blood, according to the Laws regulations, and there is no forgiveness without blood being shed.
local: Paɖʋ paɣtʋ taa se calɩm ñalɩɣ wondu sakɩyɛ nɛ tɩwɛɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ. Calɩm ɛtɛkpɛdɩ yɔ, kpeɣu ɛɛwɛɣ.

eng: The Law is a shadow of the good things that are coming, not the real things themselves. It never can perfect the ones who are trying to draw near to God through the same sacrifices that are offered continually every year.
local: Ñɩm kɩbam mbʋ́ Krɩstʋ kɔnaa yɔ, Mooyiizi paɣtʋ kɛnɩ pɩ-kɩlɛmʋʋ yem. Tɩtɛkɛ pɩ-maɣmaɣ. Tɩɩpɩzɩɣ tɩñɔɔzɩ ɛyáa nɛ papɩsɩ kɩbama nɛ powolo Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ: ɛzɩma tɩpɩzɩɣ tɩtɩŋnɩ kɩlaŋ weyi palaɣ pɩnzɩ tɩŋa payɩ nɛ pɩfɛyɩ tɩnaɣ yɔ ɩ-yɔɔ nɛ tɩñɔɔzɩ ɛyáa?.

eng: So it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be cleansed with these sacrifices, but the heavenly things had to be cleansed with better sacrifices than these.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́, ḿbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se pañalɩ mbʋ́ pɩwɩlɩɣ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩlakɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ nɛ pʋwɛɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ. Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, pʋpɔzʋʋ-pʋ kɩlaŋ weyi ɩkɩlɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ.

eng: Otherwise, wouldnt they have stopped being offered? If the people carrying out their religious duties had been completely cleansed once, no one would have been aware of sin anymore.
local: Yee tɩɩpɩzaɣ yɔ, paayeba laʋ. Pʋbʋ, tiiyeba nɛ papɩsɩ ɛyáa kɩbama kpaagbaa pɩsʋʋ nɛ pataatasɩ tɔzʋʋ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm nabʋyʋ yɔɔ.

eng: Christ didnt enter the holy place made by human hands, but into heaven itself, so that he now appears in Gods presence for us.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Krɩstʋ tasʋʋ kɩɖanɖʋʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛyáa siɣaa yɔ kɩ-tɛɛ. Ŋ́gʋ́ lɛ ɛsɔdaa ñɩŋgʋ lɛzʋʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛkpa ɛsɔdaa maɣmaɣ se ewolo Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ nɛ ɖɔ-nɔɔ.

eng: Instead, these sacrifices are a reminder of sin every year.
local: Ɛlɛ́, kɩlaŋ ɩnʋ ɩtɔzʋʋnɩ ɛyáa pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ paa pɩnaɣ ŋgá.

eng: He didnt enter to offer himself over and over again, like the high priest enters the earthly holy place every year with blood that isnt his.
local: Ɛtasʋʋ kɩ-tɛɛ se ɛlaa ɛ-tɩ tam sakɩyɛ ɛzɩ cɔjɔnaa ñʋʋdʋ kpɛndʋʋnɩ kpɩnɛ calɩm paa pɩnaɣ ŋ́gá nɛ ɛsʋʋ yɔ.

eng: because its impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, naŋ abalaa nɛ tuliŋ pa-calɩm ɛɛpɩzɩɣ pɩñalɩ ɛyáa kɩwɛɛkɩm kaʋ.

eng: If that were so, then Jesus would have to suffer many times since the foundation of the world. Instead, he has now appeared once at the end of the ages to get rid of sin by sacrificing himself.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ pɩɩwɛɛ yɔ, ɖooo ɛzɩ palabʋ kedeŋa yɔ, Krɩstʋ kalabɩ kʋñɔŋ tam sakɩyɛ. Toovenim lɛ, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɛlɩ kpaagbaa lɩʋ kɛtɛzaɣ alɩwaatʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-taa se ɛha ɛ-tɩ nɛ palaa-ɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ hɛzɩ ɛyáa kɩwɛɛkɩm.

eng: Therefore, when he comes into the world he says, You didnt want a sacrifice or an offering, but you prepared a body for me; .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Krɩstʋ heyi Ɛsɔ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛkɔŋaɣ kedeŋa yɔɔ yɔ se: «Ŋtɔsɔɔlɩ kɩlaʋ yaa haɖɛ. Ɛlɛ́, ŋyeba nɛ mɛwɛnɩ tomnaɣ.

eng: People are destined to die once and then face judgment.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se paa weyi ɛsɩ tam kʋɖʋm koŋ nɛ pʋwayɩ lɛ, Ɛsɔ hʋʋnɩ-ɩ tɔm.

eng: you werent pleased with entirely burned offerings or a sin offering. .
local: Ŋtɔsɔɔlɩ se pɔhɔ kpɩna nɛ paha-ŋ. Ŋtɔsɔɔlɩ ɖɔɖɔ kɩlaŋ weyi ɛyáa laɣ-ŋ se ŋkpeɣ-wɛ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ.

eng: In the same way, Christ was also offered once to take on himself the sins of many people. He will appear a second time, not to take away sin but to save those who are eagerly waiting for him.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Krɩstʋ ha ɛ-tɩ kpaagbaa haʋ se palaa-ɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ kpeɣ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɛkɔŋʋʋ nabʋlɛ ɖeɖe yɔ, kɩwɛɛkɩm tɔm ɛɛtasɩɣ wɛʋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛkɔŋ se ɛya mbá paɖaŋ-ɩ kpam yɔ pa-ñʋʋ.

eng: So then I said, Look, Ive come to do your will, God. This has been written about me in the scroll. .
local: Peeɖe mɔntɔm se: ‹Hayɩ Ɛsɔ! Ma yɔ: mɛwɛɛ se mala mbʋ́ ñe-liu wɛɛ yɔ ɛzɩ pamaʋ mɔn-tɔm paɣtʋ takayaɣ taa yɔ.› ».

eng: Faith is the reality of what we hope for, the proof of what we dont see.
local: Ɛsɔ yɔɔ lidaʋ wɛnaʋ lɛ se ɖɩtɩlɩ se mbʋ́ ɖɩɖaŋ kpam yɔ, pʋkɔŋ labʋ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ mbʋ́ ɖɩɩnaɣnɩ ɖɛ-ɛsɛ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ.

eng: He says above, You didnt want and you werent pleased with a sacrifice or an offering or with entirely burned offerings or a purification offering, which are offered because the Law requires them.
local: Cɩnɛ lɛ, Krɩstʋ calɩ kaɖʋʋ se: «Ŋtɔsɔɔlɩ se ŋmʋ kɩlaŋ yaa haɖɛ yaa ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɔhɔkɩ kpɩna se paha-ŋ yɔ. Ŋtɔsɔɔlɩ ɖɔɖɔ kɩlaŋ weyi ɛyáa laɣ-ŋ se ŋkpeɣ-wɛ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ.» Ŋgʋ́, kɩlaŋ ɩnʋ palaɣ-ɩ ɛzɩ paɖʋʋ paɣtʋ taa yɔ.

eng: The elders in the past were approved because they showed faith.
local: Lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ ɖooo lɔŋ taa ñɩma kɛwɛnɩ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ, kʋ-yɔɔ pɔ-tɔm kɛdɩnɩ-ɩ.

eng: Then he said, Look, Ive come to do your will . He puts an end to the first to establish the second.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛtasɩ se: «Hayɩ Ɛsɔ! Ma yɔ: mɛwɛɛ se mala mbʋ́ ñe-liu wɛɛ yɔ.» Ḿbʋ́ Krɩstʋ ɖeziɣ kajalaɣ kɩlaŋ tɩŋa nɛ ɛlɛɣzɩ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-ñɩŋgʋ i-lone taa.

eng: By faith we understand that the universe has been created by a word from God so that the visible came into existence from the invisible.
local: Đɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔɔ ɖɩtɩlaa se ɛ-tɔm ɛlabɩnɩ kedeŋa. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩtɩlaa ɖɔɖɔ se ɛkpaɣ mbʋ́ paanaɣ yɔ nɛ ɛlabɩnɩ mbʋ́ panaɣ yɔ.

eng: We have been made holy by Gods will through the offering of Jesus Christs body once for all.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Yesu Krɩstʋ labɩ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu kɛwɛɛ yɔ nɛ palaa-ɩ kpaagbaa laʋ. Ḿbʋ́ hanɩ-ɖʋ waɖɛ nɛ ɖɩpɩsɩ Ɛsɔ ñɩma.

eng: By faith Abel offered a better sacrifice to God than Cain, which showed that he was righteous, since God gave approval to him for his gift. Though he died, hes still speaking through faith.
local: Aabɛɛlɩ wɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔɔ ɛla-ɩ kɩlaʋ nɛ kɩkɩlɩ ɛ-ɖalʋ Kayɛnɩ ñɩŋgʋ. Kʋ-yɔɔ ɛ́lɛ́ kaɖaa se ɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-haɖɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩkɛdɩnɩ-ɩ. Aabɛɛlɩ ɛnʋ, mbʋ́ ɛlaba nɛ pɩtɩŋnɩ e-lidaʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kʋ-yɔɔ yɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ pɛkɛdɩɣ-ɖʋ ɛ-tɔm halɩ nɛ sɔnɔ nɛ pʋwɛɛ ɛzɩ ɛnʋ yɔɔdʋʋnɩnɩ-ɖʋ yɔ. Ŋgʋ́ ɛsɩba.

eng: Every priest stands every day serving and offering the same sacrifices over and over, sacrifices that can never take away sins.
local: Paa cɔjɔ weyi lɛ, ɛwɛ ɛ-tʋmɩyɛ ɖɩlaɖɛ paa kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ se ɛlabɩ-ɖɩ. Ɛpaŋ laʋ kpɩna, ŋgʋ́, kɩlaŋ ɩnʋ ɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɩhɛzɩ ɛyáa kɩwɛɛkɩm kaʋ.

eng: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he didnt see death, and he wasnt found because God took him up. He was given approval for having pleased God before he was taken up.
local: Enɔɔkɩ ɖɔɖɔ ñaawɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ-ɩ nɛ ɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ ewoni-i ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ nɔɔyʋ tatasɩ-ɩ naʋ. Pʋcɔ ɛkpazɩ-ɩ lɛ, paalɩzɩ ɛ-aseɣɖe se ɛ-tɔm kɛdɩnɩ ɛ́lɛ́ pɩŋŋ.

eng: But when this priest offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, he sat down at the right side of God.
local: Piyele Krɩstʋ yɔ, ɛkpaɣ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-tɩ ɛha nɛ palaa-ɩ kpaagbaa laʋ ɛyáa kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ewolo ɛcaɣ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa.

eng: Its impossible to please God without faith because the one who draws near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards people who try to find him.
local: Ŋgʋ́, yee nɔɔyʋ ɛwɛɛ nɛ ɛfɛyɩnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-tɔm ɛɛkɛdɩɣnɩ-ɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛñɔtʋʋnɩ-ɩ yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se etisi se ɛwɛɛ. Etisi ɖɔɖɔ se ɛlakɩ mbá pañɩnɩɣ-ɩ yɔ kʋjɔʋ.

eng: Since then, hes waiting until his enemies are made into a footstool for his feet.
local: nɛ ɛɖaŋ se ɛ́lɛ́ ɛkpa e-koyindinaa nɛ eluzi ɛ-tɛɛ.

eng: By faith Noah responded with godly fear when he was warned about events he hadnt seen yet. He built an ark to deliver his household. With his faith, he criticized the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Nowee kɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ etisi tɔm ndʋ́ ɛ́lɛ́ heyaɣ-i yɔ se tɩkɔŋ labʋ. Ŋgʋ́, ɛtanada. Ɛlɛ́, ɛñam-ɩ nɛ ɛsaɣ mɛlɛ sɔsɔʋ se ɛ-hɔʋ taa ñɩma ehiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ. Mbʋ́ Nowee lakaɣ yɔ, pɩwɩlaɣ se kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ñɩma kɛ kañadɩnaa. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtɩŋnɩ e-lidaʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛnʋ ñapɩsɩ kʋtʋʋzʋ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa.

eng: because he perfected the people who are being made holy with one offering for all time.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kɩlaʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ koŋ yɔɔ ɛtɩŋnaa nɛ ɛtɛzɩ pɩsʋʋ mbá pɛkɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩma yɔ kɩbama tam.

eng: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was going to receive as an inheritance. He went out without knowing where he was going.
local: Abraham ɖɔɖɔ ñaawɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ ɛñam-ɩ nɛ etisi tɔm ndʋ́ eheyi-i yɔ. Eeheyi-i se: «Kʋyɩ ŋwolo ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ mawɩlɩɣ-ŋ yɔ ɖɩ-taa nɛ ŋtɩɣyɩ-ɖɩ.» Peeɖe Abraham kʋyɩ ɛ-ɛjaɖɛ taa nɛ ɛɖɛɛ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛtatɩlɩ ɖenɖe ewokaɣ yɔ.

eng: The Holy Spirit affirms this when saying.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tasɩ-ɖʋ heyuu. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kɩcalɩ-ɖʋ kaɖʋʋ se:.

eng: By faith he lived in the land he had been promised as a stranger. He lived in tents along with Isaac and Jacob, who were coheirs of the same promise.
local: Ɛɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ ɛkʋyaa nɛ ewolo ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ ɛ́lɛ́ ɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ-ɩ yɔ ɖɩ-taa nɛ ɛwɛɛ ɛzɩ ɛgɔm yɔ. Ɛtalɩ peeɖe lɛ, ɛsʋʋ kɩɖanɖɩŋ tɛɛ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Izaakɩ nɛ Yakɔɔbʋ paba ñasʋʋ kɩɖanɖɩŋ tɛɛ ɖɔɖɔ: kʋjɔʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́ Ɛsɔ kaɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ-wɛ.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with them. After these days, says the Lord, I will place my laws in their hearts and write them on their minds. .
local: «Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔdaa se: ‹Ɩɩnaɣ nɔɔ haʋ mbʋ́ mɔnkɔŋ ɖʋʋ ma nɛ wɛ ɖɛ-hɛkʋ taa kɩyakɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-wayɩ yɔ. Manɖʋʋ man-paɣtʋ pa-laŋa taa nɛ mama-tʋ ɖɔɖɔ pa-lɩmaɣza taa.› ».

eng: He was looking forward to a city that has foundations, whose architect and builder is God.
local: Abraham ɛnʋ, ɛɛɖaŋaa se ɛsʋʋ tɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-taa kuduyiŋ tigila wɛ ɖoŋ yɔ. Ɛsɔ maɣzɩna nɛ ɛma-ɩ.

eng: And I wont remember their sins and their lawless behavior anymore. .
local: Ɛtasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Maatasɩɣ tɔzʋʋ pa-kɩwɛɛkɩm yaa pɛ-ɛzaɣ́tʋ lakasɩ pɔ-yɔɔ kaʋ.».

eng: By faith even Sarah received the ability to have a child, though she herself was barren and past the age for having children, because she believed that the one who promised was faithful.
local: Saara kɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ Ɛsɔ ha-ɩ ɖoŋ se ɛpɩzɩ ɛlʋlɩ pɩɣa alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛkpadɩyɩ kuŋgu yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ se ɛlakɩ mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: When there is forgiveness for these things, there is no longer an offering for sin.
local: Toovenim lɛ, yee pekpeɣ kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ, pɩtatasɩ pɔzʋʋ se palaa kɩlaʋ nakʋyʋ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: So descendants were born from one man . They were as many as the number of the stars in the sky and as countless as the grains of sand on the seashore.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ pɩtɩŋnɩ ɛyʋ kʋɖʋm weyi ɛñɔtɩnɩ pɩlaʋ nɔɔ kpam yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ palʋlɩ ɛ-sayɩnaa sakɩyɛ ɛzɩ tɩɩnzɩ yɔ. Pɔɖɔ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ teŋgu nɔɔ kañɩŋa yɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we have confidence that we can enter the holy of holies by means of Jesus blood.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, taabalaa! Đɩna siŋŋ se Yesu calɩm haɣ-ɖʋ waɖɛ se ɖɩsʋʋ lone nɖɩ́ ɖɩkɩlɩ kaɖɛ yɔ ɖɩ-taa kaalɛyɩtʋ.

eng: All these people died in faith without receiving the promises, but they saw the promises from a distance and welcomed them. They confessed that they were strangers and immigrants on earth.
local: Mbá paawɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ, pɔɖɔkɩ-kʋ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pʋcɔ pɔkɔɔ pasɩ patɩŋa payɩ. Ɛlɛ́, petehiɣ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ kaɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ-wɛ yɔ. Polosi-pʋ kulosuu poliŋ taa nɛ pɔyɔɔdɩ se pɛkɛ agɔmaa nɛ ñɩtʋ sʋyaa yem kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ.

eng: through a new and living way that he opened up for us through the curtain, which is his body.
local: Ɛtɩŋnɩ pɩsaʋ ŋgʋ́ pakaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛtʋlɩ-ɖʋ nʋmɔʋ kɩfalʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩhaɣ wezuu yɔ. Pɩsaʋ ŋ́gʋ́ lɛ e-tomnaɣ.

eng: People who say this kind of thing make it clear that they are looking for a homeland.
local: Mbá pɔyɔɔdʋʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pawɩlɩɣ kpaagbaa se pañɩnɩɣ pɔ-cɔzɔnaa tɛtʋ natʋyʋ se pɛwɛɛ tɩ-taa.

eng: and we have a great high priest over Gods house.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́, ɖɩwɛnɩ cɔjɔ sɔsɔ nɛ ɛcɔŋnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa yɔɔ.

eng: If they had been thinking about the country that they had left, they would have had the opportunity to return to it.
local: Yee paakpiɖaɣ ndʋ́ palɩnɩ tɩ-taa yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, pɩɩha-wɛ waɖɛ se papɩsɩ ɖɩ-taa.

eng: Therefore, lets draw near with a genuine heart with the certainty that our faith gives us, since our hearts are sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies are washed with pure water.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩñɔtɩnɩ Ɛsɔ nɛ laŋa kɩbana nɛ lidaʋ wɛnaʋ siŋŋ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɖa-taa ɛwɛɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ nɛ pɔsɔ-ɖʋ lɩm mbʋ́ pʋwɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ yɔ.

eng: But at this point in time, they are longing for a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore, God isnt ashamed to be called their God—he has prepared a city for them.
local: Toovenim lɛ, pe-liu kɛwɛ ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩkɩlɩ ɖeu yɔ. Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ fɛyɛ ɛɛkpaɣ Ɛsɔ se payaa-ɩ se pɛ-Ɛsɔ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɛñɔɔzɩ-wɛ tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ natʋyʋ.

eng: Lets hold on to the confession of our hope without wavering, because the one who made the promises is reliable.
local: Đɩɖɔkɩ ɖaŋʋʋ tɔm ndʋ́ ɖisusuu yɔ nesi naalɛ kpam. Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ-ɖʋ pʋyʋ. Ɛlakɩ-ɖʋ-pʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: By faith Abraham offered Isaac when he was tested. The one who received the promises was offering his only son.
local: Abraham kɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ ɛkpaɣ ɛ-pɩɣa ɛgbamɩyɛ Izaakɩ se ɛlaɣ-ɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛ́lɛ́ takɩ-ɩ se ɛna ɛ-wayɩ yɔ. Etisaa se ɛlaɣ-kɛ. Ŋgʋ́, ɛɖʋwa se kɔ-yɔɔ ɛkɔŋ tɩŋnaʋ nɛ ɛha-ɩ sayɩnaa.

eng: And let us consider each other carefully for the purpose of sparking love and good deeds.
local: Đɩmaɣzɩ ɖama yɔɔ nɛ pɩlabɩ-ɖʋ hʋnʋm nɛ ɖɩtɛzɩ ɖama sɔɔlʋʋ nɛ ɖɩla tʋma kɩbana.

eng: He had been told concerning him, Your legitimate descendants will come from Isaac. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, eeheyi-i se: «Izaakɩ yɔɔ pɩtɩŋɩɣna nɛ ŋwɛnɩ sayɩnaa ɛzɩ maaɖʋʋ yɔ.».

eng: Dont stop meeting together with other believers, which some people have gotten into the habit of doing. Instead, encourage each other, especially as you see the day drawing near.
local: Nabɛyɛ wɛ ɖa-taa, pɩpɩsɩ-wɛ sɔnzɩ se pataakpeɣli nɛ poluŋ Ɛsɔ tɛɛ. Pɩfɛyɩ se ɖɩla ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩsazɩ ɖama nɛ pɩlabɩ-ɖʋ hʋnʋm. Đɩwɛɛ pʋ-yɔɔ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩtɛm naʋ se kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ kɔŋ yɔ, kɩwɩzɩ talʋʋ.

eng: He figured that God could even raise him from the dead. So in a way he did receive him back from the dead.
local: Abraham maɣzaɣ se Ɛsɔ wɛnɩ ɖoŋ nɛ paa sɩɖʋ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ pɩzɩɣ efezi-i. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛɖaɣnɩ ɛ-pɩyalʋ naʋ nɛ pʋwɛɛ ɛzɩ ɛ́lɛ́ fezi-i nɛ sɩm taa yɔ.

eng: If we make the decision to sin after we receive the knowledge of the truth, there isnt a sacrifice for sins left any longer.
local: Yee ɖitisi toovenim tɔm nɛ ɖɩwɛɛ ɖɩlakɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm ɛzɩ pɩmaɣʋ-ɖʋ yɔ yɔ, kɩlaʋ nakʋyʋ tatasɩ kazʋʋ se palaɣ nɛ Ɛsɔ kpeɣ-ɖʋ-pʋ.

eng: By faith Isaac also blessed Jacob and Esau concerning their future.
local: Izaakɩ wɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ ɛwazɩ Yakɔɔbʋ nɛ Ezaayuu yɔɔ cee wayɩ yɔɔ.

eng: Theres only a scary expectation of judgment and of a burning fire thats going to devour Gods opponents.
local: Mbá palakɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, sɔɔndʋ kɔŋ-wɛ kpaʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-wɛ tɔm hʋnaʋ nɛ ɛɖʋ-wɛ miŋ sɔsɔŋ weyi ɛñakɩ e-koyindinaa yɔ ɛ-taa.

eng: By faith Jacob blessed each of Josephs sons as he was dying and bowed in worship over the head of his staff. .
local: Yakɔɔbʋ ɖɔɖɔ kɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ ɛwazɩ paa Yozɛɛfʋ pɩyalʋ weyi ɛ-yɔɔ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛcaɣaɣ sɩbʋ yɔ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɛɛñɩɣ ɛ-ɖaʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛɖʋ Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ.

eng: When someone rejected the Law from Moses, they were put to death without mercy on the basis of the testimony of two or three witnesses.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛtɔɖɔ Mooyiizi paɣtʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛyáa naalɛ yaa naadozo lɩzɩ ɛ-aseɣɖe yɔ, pakʋʋ-ɩ, pʋtɔdɩyɛ ɛɛwɛɣ.

eng: By faith Joseph recalled the exodus of the Israelites at the end of his life, and gave instructions about burying his bones.
local: Yozɛɛfʋ ɖɔɖɔ ñaawɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛyɔɔdɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma kɔŋ lɩʋ Ejiptɩ ɛjaɖɛ taa yɔ kʋ-tɔm alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛwɩzɩ sɩbʋ yɔ. Ɛyɔɔdɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se pala ɛ-mɔɔ ki-wiye yɔ.

eng: How much worse punishment do you think is deserved by the person who walks all over Gods Son, who acts as if the blood of the covenant that made us holy is just ordinary blood, and who insults the Spirit of grace?.
local: Yee pʋwɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, weyi ɛyakɩ Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ naŋgbaŋaɣ nɛ ɛtʋʋ-ɩ yɔ, ɛbɛ pɩtɛŋnɩ-ɩ? Ɛtakpaɣ nɔɔ haʋ calɩm nɛ ɛ́yáa pʋyʋ. Ŋgʋ́, ḿbʋ́ pɩsɩnɩ-ɩ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ tʋ. Ɛtʋ ɖɔɖɔ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ŋgʋ́ kɩlakɩ-ɖʋ lootiye yɔ.

eng: By faith Moses was hidden by his parents for three months when he was born, because they saw that the child was beautiful and they werent afraid of the kings orders.
local: Mooyiizi caa nɛ e-ɖoo paawɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ pɛmɛsɩ-ɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ palʋlɩ-ɩ yɔ fenasɩ naadozo. Pataase mbʋ́ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ ɖʋwa yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, panawa se pɩɣa ŋ́gá keɖewa.

eng: We know the one who said, Judgment is mine; I will pay people back. And he also said, The Lord will judge his people. .
local: Đɩsɩm Ɛsɔ, ɛnʋ yɔɔdɩna se: «Ma pʋwɛɛ se mankpa ɛyáa pa-kɩma. Mɛhɛyɩɣnɩ-wɛ mbʋ́ palaba yɔ.» Ɛtasɩ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Ma Kɩpaɣlʋ mɔnkɔŋnɩ hʋnaʋ ma-ñɩma.».

eng: By faith Moses refused to be called the son of Pharaohs daughter when he was grown up.
local: Mooyiizi kɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ ekizi alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛpaɣlaa yɔ se ɛɛtasɩɣ kɛʋ Faraɔɔ pɛlɛ pɩyalʋ.

eng: Its scary to fall into the hands of the living God!.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́, pʋtɔɔ weyi ɛtɔlʋʋ Ɛsɔ wezuu tʋ nesi taa yɔ!.

eng: He chose to be mistreated with Gods people instead of having the temporary pleasures of sin.
local: Ɛsɔɔlaa siŋŋ se ɛ nɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩma pɛwɛɛ nɛ panazɩ-wɛ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ɛzɩ ɛ-laŋɩyɛ kahʋlʋmɩnɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm alɩwaatʋ pazɩ yɔ.

eng: But remember the earlier days, after you saw the light. You stood your ground while you were suffering from an enormous amount of pressure.
local: Ɩtɔzɩ kajalaɣ kɩyakɩŋ weyi ɩ-taa Ɛsɔ ñalɩmɩyɛ ñalɩ mɩ-yɔɔ yɔ ɩ-yɔɔ. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ʋ́ ɩtɔɔ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ. Ɛlɛ́, ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ kpam mɩ-lʋbɩyɛ taa.

eng: He thought that the abuses he suffered for Christ were more valuable than the treasures of Egypt, since he was looking forward to the reward.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɛmaɣzaɣ se yee peniɣzi-i ɛzɩ Krɩstʋ yɔ yɔ, pʋwɛɣ wazaɣ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ ñɩm mbʋ́ pʋwɛ Ejiptɩ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛcɔŋaɣnɩ kɩhɛyʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kʋwɛnɩ ɛsɩndaa yɔ ki-ɖeɖe.

eng: Sometimes you were exposed to insults and abuse in public. Other times you became partners with those who were treated that way.
local: Nabʋyʋ taa, patʋ-mɩ nɛ paɖʋ-mɩ kʋñɔŋ ɛyáa samaɣ taa. Nabʋyʋ taa ɖɔɖɔ lɛ, ɩsɩŋaɣnɩ mbá paɖʋwaɣ-wɛ kʋñɔŋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ.

eng: By faith he left Egypt without being afraid of the kings anger. He kept on going as if he could see what is invisible.
local: E-lidaʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kʋ-yɔɔ ɛkʋyɩ Ejiptɩ nɛ sɔɔndʋ takpa-ɩ nɛ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ pana. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛɖɔkɩ ɛ-tɩ kpam nɛ pʋwɛɛ ɛzɩ ɛnaɣ Ɛsɔ yɔ, ŋgʋ́, paapɩzɩɣ pana-ɩ.

eng: You even showed sympathy toward people in prison and accepted the confiscation of your possessions with joy, since you knew that you had better and lasting possessions.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɩna salaka tɩnaa pʋtɔdɩyɛ. Mɩ-laŋa kahʋlʋmɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pamʋwaɣ mɩ-ñɩm yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtɛm naʋ se ɩwɛnɩ ñɩm mbʋ́ pɩkɩlɩ ḿbʋ́ nɛ pʋwɛɛ tam yɔ.

eng: By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, in order that the destroyer could not touch their firstborn children.
local: Kʋ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ ɛɖʋ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma Paska Kazandʋ. Ɛha nɔɔ se pataa calɩm nɔnɔsɩ yɔɔ nɛ tiyiyu weyi ɛkɔŋ kʋʋ kajalaɣ piya yɔ, ɛkɔma lɛ, ɛtaatukuni Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma ñɩnzɩ yɔɔ.

eng: So dont throw away your confidence—it brings a great reward.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩtaalɔ liu: ɩkɔŋ hiɣu kɩhɛyʋʋ sɔsɔʋ.

eng: By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if they were on dry land, but when the Egyptians tried it, they were drowned.
local: Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma wɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ pɛtɛzɩ Teŋgu Kɩsɛmʋʋ ɛzɩ ɛyáa ɖɔŋʋʋ tataa ɖenɖe pʋwɛ waɣwaɣ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Ejiptɩ ñɩma mbá patɩɣzɩɣnɩ-wɛ yɔ, pilibi-wɛ.

eng: You need to endure so that you can receive the promises after you do Gods will.
local: Ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-hɔ kpam nɛ ɩla mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ liu wɛɛ yɔ nɛ ihiɣ mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ-mɩ yɔ.

eng: By faith Jerichos walls fell after the people marched around them for seven days.
local: Pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔɔ pɔcɔ Yeriikoo tɛtʋ kɩyakɩŋ lʋbɛ nɛ ti-kolonzi tɔlɩ.

eng: In a little while longer, the one who is coming will come and wont delay; .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Pɩkazɩ alɩwaatʋ kpaagbaa pazɩ nɛ weyi pʋwɛɛ se ɛkɔɔ yɔ, ɛkɔɔ. Ɛtacaɣ wayɩ.

eng: By faith Rahab the prostitute wasnt killed with the disobedient because she welcomed the spies in peace.
local: Rahaabɩ kɛ halʋ weyi ɛpɛdaɣ ɛ-tɩ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ nɛ ɛmʋ feŋiyaa nabɛyɛ camɩyɛ ɛ-tɛ. Pʋ-yɔɔ ɛ nɛ mbá paañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ pɛtɛkpɛndɩ sɩbʋ.

eng: but my righteous one will live by faith, and my whole being wont be pleased with anyone who shrinks back. .
local: Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔdaa se: Ma-ñɩnʋ weyi ɛtʋʋzɩ mɛ-ɛsɩndaa yɔ, e-lidaʋ yɔɔ lɛ, ɛwɛɣ wezuu. Ɛlɛ́, yee ɛpɩsɩnɩ ɛ-tɩ nɛ wayɩ yɔ, ɛ-tɔm ɛɛkɛdɩɣnɩ-m.».

eng: What more can I say? I would run out of time if I told you about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
local: Ndʋ́ mantasɩɣ yɔɔdʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ yɔ? Alɩwaatʋ ɛɛtalɩɣ-m se mɛnkɛdɩ Jedeyɔɔ nɛ Baraakɩ nɛ Samsɔɔ nɛ Yeftee nɛ Daaviidi nɛ Samʋyɛɛlɩ nɛ náyaa pɔ-tɔm.

eng: But we arent the sort of people who timidly draw back and end up being destroyed. Were the sort of people who have faith so that our whole beings are preserved.
local: Đa lɛ, ɖɩfɛyɩ mbá papɩsɩɣnɩ pa-tɩ nɛ wayɩ se pele yɔ pa-taa. Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ se ɖa-ciyamnaa ehiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ.

eng: Through faith they conquered kingdoms, brought about justice, realized promises, shut the mouths of lions.
local: Pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔɔ poyoni ajɛya lɛɛna taa ñɩma nɛ pawabɩ-wɛ nɛ pamʋ pɛ-tɛtʋ. Palabɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ nɛ pehiɣ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ-wɛ yɔ. Posuli tɔɔyɩŋ nɔsɩ.

eng: put out raging fires, escaped from the edge of the sword, found strength in weakness, were mighty in war, and routed foreign armies.
local: nɛ peɖezi miŋ sɔsɔŋ. Pesewa nɛ patʋlɩ, patakʋ-wɛ nɛ sɩɣa. Paakɛ ajamaa nɛ pʋwayɩ lɛ, pakpa ɖoŋ. Paawɛ abalɩtʋ you taa nɛ pawa sɔɔjanaa agɔmaa nɛ pɔɖɔɔnɩ-wɛ.

eng: Women received back their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured and refused to be released so they could gain a better resurrection.
local: Halaa nabɛyɛ kɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, pʋ-yɔɔ efezi pa-ñɩma mbá pasɩba yɔ nɛ ɛcɛlɩ-wɛ. Lɛlaa ñaatisaa nɛ paɖʋ-wɛ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ nɛ pɔkɔɔ pasɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paakizaa se pataayebi-wɛ. Paawɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ se paa pasɩba yɔ, efeziɣ-wɛ nɛ pacaɣ wezuu ŋgʋ́ kɩkɩlɩ ɖeu ŋgʋ́ kɩ-taa paawɛɛ yɔ.

eng: But others experienced public shame by being taunted and whipped; they were even put in chains and in prison.
local: Peniɣzi lɛlaa ɖɔɖɔ nɛ polu-wɛ agbazɛɛ. Pɔhɔkɩ lɛlaa yoŋgbasɩ nɛ paɖʋ-wɛ salaka.

eng: They were stoned to death, they were cut in two, and they died by being murdered with swords. They went around wearing the skins of sheep and goats, needy, oppressed, and mistreated.
local: Pakʋ lɛlaa nɛ pɛɛ nɛ patayɩ lɛlaa titilee naalɛ naalɛ nɛ pakʋ lɛlaa nɛ sɩɣa. Lɛlaa kɛkɛ kʋñɔndɩnaa, posuu heŋ nɛ pʋŋ pɔ-tɔna wondu nɛ pɛwɛɛ pɔcɔʋ paa le. Ɛyáa kɛwɛ pa-wayɩ kaŋ kaŋ nɛ panazɩɣ-wɛ.

eng: The world didnt deserve them. They wandered around in deserts, mountains, caves, and holes in the ground.
local: Pɩtɛ ɛyáa ḿbá, kedeŋa taamʋnɩ-wɛ! Pɔcɔwaɣ lakɩŋ nɛ pʋŋ pa-taa nɛ pasʋʋ ɛwasɩ pɔŋ nɛ tɛtɛɛ ñɩŋ pa-taa.

eng: All these people didnt receive what was promised, though they were given approval for their faith.
local: Mbá patɩŋa payɩ paawɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛlɩzɩ pa-aseɣɖe se pɔ-tɔm kɛdɩnɩ-ɩ pɩŋŋ. Ɛlɛ́, petehiɣ mbʋ́ ɛɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ-wɛ yɔ.

eng: God provided something better for us so they wouldnt be made perfect without us.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kaɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ-ɖʋ mbʋ́ pɩkɩlɩ ɖeu ḿbʋ́ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛtaasɔɔlɩ se pɛkɛ mbá pamʋnaa yɔ nɛ piyebi-ɖʋ.

eng: So then, with endurance, lets also run the race that is laid out in front of us, since we have such a great cloud of witnesses surrounding us. Lets throw off any extra baggage, get rid of the sin that trips us up.
local: Đa lɛ, ḿbʋ́ aseɣɖe tɩnaa samaɣ sɔsɔɔ ŋgá kɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ yɔ, kɔcɔ-ɖʋ kata. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩlɔ ɖa-sʋʋtʋ nɛ ɖa-kɩwɛɛkɩm patɩŋa payɩ nɛ ɖɩpɩzɩ ɖise seya wena paɖʋ-ɖʋ yɔ siŋŋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ḿbá yekina se ɖɩtaala mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ-ɖʋ yɔ.

eng: and fix our eyes on Jesus, faiths pioneer and perfecter. He endured the cross, ignoring the shame, for the sake of the joy that was laid out in front of him, and sat down at the right side of Gods throne.
local: Đɩkɛzɩ ɛsɛ nɛ Yesu yɔɔ: ɛnʋ sɩnɩ ɖe-lidaʋ kiɖe, ɛnʋ kɔŋnɩ-kʋ tɛzʋʋ. Etisaa nɛ pakam-ɩ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɛsɩ. Ɛlɛ́, fɛyɛ takpa-ɩ nɛ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ sɩm ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnawa se ɛkɔŋ wɛʋ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ taa. Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ɛcaɣ Ɛsɔ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ cɔlɔ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa.

eng: Think about the one who endured such opposition from sinners so that you wont be discouraged and you wont give up.
local: Ɩmaɣzɩ weyi etisaa nɛ ɛnɩɩ siziŋ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa nesi taa yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ. Pʋbʋ, piitisiɣ mi-toma taa nɛ ɩlɔ liu.

eng: In your struggle against sin, you havent resisted yet to the point of shedding blood.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩwɛɛ iyouni kɩwɛɛkɩm. Ɛlɛ́, mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ calɩm tɛkpɛdɩdɩ you ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-taa.

eng: and you have forgotten the encouragement that addresses you as sons and daughters: My child, dont make light of the Lords discipline or give up when you are corrected by him, .
local: Ɩtaasɔɔ tɔm ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ heyi-mɩ ɛzɩ e-piya se pɩlabɩ-mɩ hʋnʋm yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Man-pɩɣa, Kɩpaɣlʋ ɛhɔŋ ña-nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ yɔ, pɩtaakɛnɩ-ŋ aleɣya. Pɩtaatisi ño-toma taa alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛkalɩɣ-ŋ yɔ.

eng: because the Lord disciplines whomever he loves, and he punishes every son or daughter whom he accepts. .
local: Weyi Kɩpaɣlʋ sɔɔlɩ-ɩ yɔ, ɛ-nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ ɛhɔŋ, weyi ɛsɩm-ɩ se ɛkɛ ɛ-pɩɣa yɔ, ɛnʋ ɛmakɩ.».

eng: Bear hardship for the sake of discipline. God is treating you like sons and daughters! What child isnt disciplined by his or her father?.
local: Itisi nɛ Ɛsɔ hɔ mɩ-nɩŋgbaaŋ: pɩwɩlɩɣ se ɛlakɩ-mɩ ɛzɩ e-piya yɔ. Pɩɣa nakɛyɛ wɛɛ se ka-caa ɛɛhɔŋ ka-nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ naa we?.

eng: But if you dont experience discipline, which happens to all children, then you are illegitimate and not real sons and daughters.
local: Yee ɛɛhɔŋ mɩ-nɩŋgbaaŋ ɛzɩ piya lɛɛsɩ yɔ yɔ, pʋbʋ, ɩtɛkɛ ɛ-maɣmaɣ e-piya. Ɛlɛ́, habɩŋ taa piya ɩkɛnaa.

eng: Whats more, we had human parents who disciplined us, and we respected them for it. How much more should we submit to the Father of spirits and live?.
local: Đɩtɔzɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ɖa-caanaa yɔɔ: pɔhɔŋaɣ ɖa-nɩŋgbaaŋ nɛ ɖɩñaŋ-wɛ. Yee ḿbʋ́ lɛ, ɛzɩma ɖɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɖitisi ɖa-tɩ ɖa-Caa weyi ɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ ɛ-tɛɛ nɛ ɖɩwɛɛ wezuu yɔ?.

eng: Our human parents disciplined us for a little while, as it seemed best to them, but God does it for our benefit so that we can share his holiness.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɖa-caanaa hɔŋaɣ ɖa-nɩŋgbaaŋ alɩwaatʋ pazɩ ɛzɩ pamaɣzʋʋ se pʋmʋnaa yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ ñɔhɔŋ-ɩ ɖɛ-kɛzɛɣa maɣmaɣ yɔɔ se ɖɩtɛzɩ wɛʋ ɛzɩ ɛnʋ yɔ.

eng: No discipline is fun while it lasts, but it seems painful at the time. Later, however, it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness for those who have been trained by it.
local: Kajalaɣ taa lɛ, nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ hɔm wɛ ɛzɩ ɛsɛñɩŋɩyɛ pʋyʋ yɔ, pɩfɛyɩ ɛzɩ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ pʋyʋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, mbá pɩsɩɣzɩ-wɛ lɔŋ yɔ, pʋkɔŋ-wɛ sɛʋ: pacakɩ wezuu ŋgʋ́ kʋtʋʋzaa yɔ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa.

eng: So strengthen your drooping hands and weak knees!.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mbá mɩ pitibi mi-toma taa yɔ, ɩñaɣ pana.

eng: Make straight paths for your feet so that if any part is lame, it will be healed rather than injured more seriously.
local: Ɩɖɔ nʋmɔŋ weyi ɩtʋʋzaa yɔ ɩ-taa nɛ ɛjandʋ ndʋ́ ɩwɛna yɔ tɩtaatɛzɩ ɖɔʋ, ɛlɛ́, tɩtɛ.

eng: Pursue the goal of peace along with everyone—and holiness as well, because no one will see the Lord without it.
local: Ɩñaɣ pana nɛ mɩ nɛ paa weyi mɩ-laŋa hɛnɩ ɖama nɛ ɩcaɣ wezuu ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ liu wɛʋ yɔ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, ɛyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛna Kɩpaɣlʋ.

eng: Make sure that no one misses out on Gods grace. Make sure that no root of bitterness grows up that might cause trouble and pollute many people.
local: Ɩcɔnɩ ɖama yɔɔ camɩyɛ nɛ mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛtaakɔɔ ɛha lootiye nɖɩ́ Ɛsɔ labɩ-mɩ yɔ siɣ́ye. Mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛtaawɛɛ ɛzɩ tɩʋ ñaŋ ñɩŋgʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩwɛɛ nɛ kʋ-sɔtʋ ɖɛɣ ɛyáa sakɩyɛ nɛ tɩwɛɛkɩɣ-wɛ yɔ.

eng: Make sure that no one becomes sexually immoral or ungodly like Esau. He sold his inheritance as the oldest son for one meal.
local: Mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛtaala acɛyɛ yaa ɛtaakpaɣ Ɛsɔ ñɩm se pɩtɛkɛ pʋyʋ ɛzɩ Ezaayuu yɔ. Tam kʋɖʋm tɔɔnaɣ maɣmaɣ yɔɔ ɛɛpɛdɩ ɛ-sɔsɔtʋ.

eng: You know that afterward, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected because he couldnt find a way to change his heart and life, though he looked for it with tears.
local: Ɩtɛm naʋ se pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛɛñɩnaa ɖɔɖɔ se ɛ-caa ɛwazɩ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ ɖɔɔnɩ-ɩ. Ŋgʋ́, ɛñɩnaɣnɩ ɛsɩtɛɣlɩm kpem se ɛlɛɣzɩ mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtaha-ɩ waɖɛ.

eng: You havent drawn near to something that can be touched: a burning fire, darkness, shadow, a whirlwind.
local: Ɩtɔñɔtɩnɩ Ɛsɔ ɛzɩ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma yɔ. Paba kɔñɔtɩnɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ pʋʋ nakʋyʋ: ŋ́gʋ́ lɛ Sinayii Pʋʋ. Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ lɛ, miŋ sɔsɔŋ nɩɩyɩ mʋwaɣ kʋ-yɔɔ nɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ yu kpemm nɛ heɣlim sɔsɔm nabʋyʋ makɩ.

eng: a blast of a trumpet, and a sound of words that made the ones who heard it beg that there wouldnt be one more word.
local: Panɩɣaɣ ɛgandɩyɛ naɖɩyɛ nɛ nɔɔ nakɛyɛ pa-taŋ. Mbá paanɩ nɔɔ ŋ́gá yɔ, pɔtɔm se pataatasɩ-wɛ natʋyʋ heyuu nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ ndʋ́ panɩwa yɔ.

eng: They couldnt stand the command, If even a wild animal touches the mountain, it must be stoned. .
local: mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɩkɩlɩ-wɛ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Paa kpɩnɛ maɣmaɣ etukunini pʋʋ yɔ, ɩkʋ-ɖɩ nɛ pɛɛ.».

eng: The sight was so frightening that Moses said, Im terrified and shaking.
local: Mbʋ́ pɩlabɩ peeɖe yɔ, pɩɩwɛ sɔɔndʋ siŋŋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ Mooyiizi tɔm se: «Sɔɔndʋ kɩlɩ-m kpem nɛ menseliɣ.».

eng: But you have drawn near to Mount Zion, the city of the living God, heavenly Jerusalem, to countless angels in a festival gathering.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mɩ mɩñɔtɩnɩ Siiyɔɔ Pʋʋ nɛ Ɛsɔ wezuu tʋ tɛtʋ: ńdʋ́ lɛ ɛsɔdaa Yeruusalɛm, Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ wɛ tɩ-taa.

eng: to the assembly of Gods firstborn children who are registered in heaven, to God the judge of all, to the spirits of the righteous who have been made perfect.
local: Iwobi Ɛsɔ kajalaɣ piya nzɩ́ pama sɩ-hɩla ɛsɔdaa nɛ sikpeɣli ɖama nɛ sɩtɔkɩ kazanɖʋ yɔ sɩ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa. Iwobi Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ ɖɔɖɔ, ɛnʋ hʋʋnɩnɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ: toovenim tɩnaa mbá pasɩba nɛ ɛtɛzɩ-wɛ pɩsʋʋ mbá pamʋnaa yɔ.

eng: to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks better than Abels blood.
local: Iwobi Yesu nɛ ɛ-calɩm mbʋ́ pɩkpɛdaa nɛ pɩkɩlɩ wazaɣ Aabɛɛlɩ ñɩmbʋ yɔ pɔ-cɔlɔ. Ɛnʋ lɛ ceyiyu nɔɔ haʋ kɩfam mbʋ́ pʋwɛ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛyáa pɛ-hɛkʋ taa yɔ pɩ-taa.

eng: See to it that you dont resist the one who is speaking. If the people didnt escape when they refused to listen to the one who warned them on earth, how will we escape if we reject the one who is warning from heaven?.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́, ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ! Ɩtaakizi se iiwelesiɣni Ɛsɔ weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-mɩ yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma kizaa se paawelesiɣni weyi eheyaɣ-wɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ mbʋ́ ɛwɩlɩ-ɩ yɔ nɛ patatʋlɩ nɩŋgbaŋʋʋ hɔm taa. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yee ɖa ɖahaɣ siɣ́ye weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-ɖʋ nɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ yɔ, ɖɩɩpɩzɩɣ ɖɩlɩɩ pɩ-taa ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: His voice shook the earth then, but now he has made a promise: Still once more I will shake not only the earth but heaven also .
local: Ɛɛyɔɔdɩnɩ-wɛ nɛ tɛtʋ ñamsɩ, ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ eheyi-ɖʋ se: «Mɔnkɔŋ tasʋʋ tɛtʋ ñamsʋʋ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩɩsɩɣnɩ ti-ɖeke, mañamsɩɣ ɖɔɖɔ ɛsɔdaa.».

eng: The words still once more reveal the removal of what is shaken—the things that are part of this creation—so that what isnt shaken will remain.
local: Tɔm pee anɛ, atasɩɣ wɩlʋʋ se mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ, pɩñamsɩɣ-pʋ nɛ pʋmʋʋ nɛ piyele mbʋ́ pɩfɛyɩ ñamsʋʋ yɔ pʋwɛɛ.

eng: Therefore, since we are receiving a kingdom that cant be shaken, lets continue to express our gratitude. With this gratitude, lets serve in a way that is pleasing to God with respect and awe.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩwɛnɩ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ ɛsɩmɩyɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛha-ɖʋ kewiyaɣ ŋgá kɛfɛyɩ ñamsʋʋ yɔ: ɖiluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ ɛzɩ pɩkɛdɩnaʋ-ɩ yɔ nɛ ñamtʋ nɛ sɔɔndʋ.

eng: because our God really is a consuming fire.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɛ-Ɛsɔ kɛ ɖɔɖɔ miŋ weyi ɛñakɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ yɔ.

eng: Keep loving each other like family.
local: Ɩwɛɛ ɖama sɔɔlʋʋ yɔɔ ɛzɩ pʋmʋnaʋ yɔ.

eng: Dont neglect to open up your homes to guests, because by doing this some have been hosts to angels without knowing it.
local: Ɩtaasɔɔ ɛyáa mʋ́ʋ yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, nabɛyɛ mʋwaɣ ɛyáa nɛ pɔkɔɔ pamʋ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa nabɛyɛ pɛ-tɛ nɛ patatɩlɩ.

eng: Remember prisoners as if you were in prison with them, and people who are mistreated as if you were in their place.
local: Ɩtɔzɩ salaka tɩnaa yɔɔ ɛzɩ mɩ nɛ wɛ ɩwɛnɩ ka-taa yɔ. Ɩtɔzɩ ɖɔɖɔ mbá panazɩɣ-wɛ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ ɛzɩ mɩ nɛ wɛ panazɩɣ yɔ.

eng: Marriage must be honored in every respect, with no cheating on the relationship, because God will judge the sexually immoral person and the person who commits adultery.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ ɛɖʋ halɩkpaɣyɛ hɩɖɛ. Abalʋ ɛsʋnɩ ɛ-halʋ ɖeke nɛ halʋ ɖɔɖɔ ḿbʋ́. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ kɔŋ hʋnaʋ hayitu nɛ acɛyɛ pa-laɖaa.

eng: Your way of life should be free from the love of money, and you should be content with what you have. After all, he has said, I will never leave you or abandon you .
local: Ɩtaawɛnɩ liidiye laɣzaɣ mi-wezuu caɣʋ taa. Mɩ-laŋa ɛhʋlʋmɩnɩ ñɩm mbʋ́ ɩwɛnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ maɣmaɣ yɔɔdaa se: «Maakaɣ-ŋ lɔʋ, maahaɣ-ŋ siɣ́ye kaʋ.».

eng: This is why we can confidently say, The Lord is my helper, and I wont be afraid. What can people do to me? .
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ ɖɩpɩzɩɣ ɖɩyɔɔdɩnɩ lidaʋ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ lɛ weyi ɛsɩŋnɩ-m yɔ. Sɔɔndʋ ɛɛkpaɣ-m kaʋ. Ɛyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛlabɩ-m pʋyʋ!».

eng: Remember your leaders who spoke Gods word to you. Imitate their faith as you consider the way their lives turned out.
local: Ɩtɔzɩ mɩ-ñʋndɩnaa mbá paasusi-mɩ Ɛsɔtɔm yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ. Ɩcɔnɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pacaɣ wezuu nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ pa-sɩm wiye yɔ nɛ ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ ɛzɩ ḿbá yɔ.

eng: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever!.
local: Yesu Krɩstʋ wɛʋ yɔ, ɛɛlɛɣzɩɣ kaʋ. Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛɛwɛ ɖooo lɔŋ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɛwɛɛ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ ɛwɛɣ tam.

eng: Dont be misled by the many strange teachings out there. Its a good thing for the heart to be strengthened by grace rather than by food. Food doesnt help those who live in this context.
local: Ɛyáa lɩzɩ tɔm ndɩ ndɩ nɛ pawɩlɩɣ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, itaayele nɛ pepeɣzi-mɩ nɛ tʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛ ɖeu se pɩtɩŋnɩ Ɛsɔ lootiye yɔɔ nɛ ɛyʋ kpa ɖoŋ. Ɛlɛ́, pɩtaatɩŋnɩ tɔɔnaɣ paɣtʋ yɔɔ. Mbá pɔɖɔm paɣtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, pɩtawazɩ-wɛ pʋyʋ.

eng: We have an altar, and those who serve as priests in the meeting tent dont have the right to eat from it.
local: Đɩwɛnɩ kɩlaʋ lone naɖɩyɛ. Pɩtaha nʋmɔʋ Yuda ñɩma cɔjɔnaa mbá palakɩ tʋmɩyɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa yɔ se pɔtɔɔ mbʋ́ pala ɖɩ-taa yɔ.

eng: The blood of the animals is carried into the holy of holies by the high priest as an offering for sin, and their bodies are burned outside the camp.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kpɩna wena cɔjɔ sɔsɔ kpɛdɩ a-calɩm kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔɔ lone kaɖɛ ñɩnɖɛ taa yɔ, paɖʋʋ-yɛ miŋ nɛ tɛtʋ wayɩ.

eng: And so Jesus also suffered outside the city gate to make the people holy with his own blood.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ Yesu ñasɩbɩ tɛtʋ koloŋa wayɩ se ɛñalɩ ɛyáa kɩwɛɛkɩm nɛ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛ-calɩm nɛ pɛwɛɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ.

eng: So now, lets go to him outside the camp, bearing his shame.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɖɩlɩɩ ɖiwolo ɛ-cɔlɔ tɛtʋ koloŋa wayɩ peeɖe nɛ ɖa nɛ ɩ ɖɩkpɛndɩ ɖɩtɔɔ ɛ-fɛyɛ.

eng: We dont have a permanent city here, but rather we are looking for the city that is still to come.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩfɛyɩnɩ tataa cɩnɛ tɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩwɛɣ tam yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɖɩñɩnɩɣ ndʋ́ tɩkaɣ kɔm yɔ.

eng: So lets continually offer up a sacrifice of praise through him, which is the fruit from our lips that confess his name.
local: Đɩtɩŋnɩ Yesu yɔɔ nɛ ɖɩsa Ɛsɔ tam. Samtʋ ndʋ́ tɩlɩɣnɩ ɖɔ-nɔɔ taa alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ se ɛnʋ lɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔ, tɩwɛ ɛzɩ kɩlaʋ yɔ. Pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se ɖɩsam-ɩ paa ɖooye.

eng: Dont forget to do good and to share what you have because God is pleased with these kinds of sacrifices.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɩtaasɔɔ camɩyɛ lakasɩ nɛ ɖama sɩnaʋ pɔ-yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, lakasɩ ńzɩ́ sɩwɛnɩ ɛzɩ kɩlaŋ weyi ɩkɛdɩnɩ Ɛsɔ yɔ.

eng: Rely on your leaders and defer to them, because they watch over your whole being as people who are going to be held responsible for you. They need to be able to do this with pleasure and not with complaints about you, because that wouldnt help you.
local: Ɩña mɩ-ñʋndɩnaa nɛ itisi mɩ-tɩ pɛ-tɛɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ḿbá cɔŋnɩnɩ mi-ciyamnaa yɔɔ. Ḿbá kɔŋnɩ Ɛsɔ heyuu ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɔcɔnɩ mɩ-yɔɔ yɔ. Yee ɩñaŋ-wɛ yɔ, palakɩ pa-tʋmɩyɛ nɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ. Ɛlɛ́, yee ɩtañam-wɛ yɔ, palakɩ-ɖɩ nɛ hɔpɩsɩyɛ nɛ pɩɩwazɩɣ-mɩ pʋyʋ.

eng: Pray for us. Were sure that we have a good conscience, and we want to do the right thing in every way.
local: Ɩsɛɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɔ-yɔɔ tam. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩnawa se ɖa-taa wɛ ɖeu. Đe-liu wɛɛ se ɖɩɖɔ ɖɔnɛ kɩbanɖɛ pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa.

eng: Im particularly asking you to do this so that I can be returned to you quickly.
local: Mentendiɣ-mɩ se ɩsɛɛ-ɩ nɛ ɛha-m nʋmɔʋ nɛ manpɩsɩ mɩ-cɔlɔ lɔŋ.

eng: May the God of peace, who brought back the great shepherd of the sheep, our Lord Jesus, from the dead by the blood of the eternal covenant.
local: Ɛsɔ lɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ tʋ. Mentendiɣ-i se ɛtɩŋnɩ calɩm mbʋ́ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu kpɛdaa nɛ piyele nɔɔ haʋ wɛɛ tam yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ nɛ efezi-i nɛ sɩɖaa taa. Yesu ɛnʋ lɛ heŋ ɖiyiyu sɔsɔ.

eng: From James, a slave of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ. To the twelve tribes who are scattered outside the land of Israel. Greetings!.
local: Ma Yakubu weyi ɛkɛ Ɛsɔ nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ pa-tʋmlaɖʋ yɔ, mamaɣnɩ takayaɣ kanɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩma mbá mɩ ɩmɩzɩnɩ kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ yɔ. Ɩsɛɛ ɖeu!.

eng: equip you with every good thing to do his will, by developing in us what pleases him through Jesus Christ. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ, eyele nɛ ɩpɩzɩ ɩla mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ nɛ piyele ɩla mbʋ́ e-liu wɛɛ yɔ. Mentendiɣ-i ɖɔɖɔ se ɛtɩŋnɩ Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛñɔɔzɩ ɖa-laŋa taa ɛzɩ pɩkɛdɩnaʋ-ɩ yɔ. Yesu Krɩstʋ ɛwɛɛ koboyaɣ taa tam! Pɩla ḿbʋ́!.

eng: My brothers and sisters, think of the various tests you encounter as occasions for joy.
local: Man-taabalaa! Ɩkpaɣ mɩ-tɩ se ɩwɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ taa siŋŋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩsʋʋ takʋʋ ndɩ ndɩ taa yɔ.

eng: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to put up with this message of encouragement, since Ive only written a short letter to you!.
local: Taabalaa, meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-hɔ nɛ itisi sazʋʋ tɔm tʋnɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɔm tobi yem mama-mɩ.

eng: After all, you know that the testing of your faith produces endurance.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtɛm naʋ se yee pɩtakɩɣ-mɩ se pana mi-lidaʋ tɩnaɣ nɛ ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ yɔ, piyeki nɛ ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-hɔ.

eng: You should know that our brother Timothy has been set free. If he comes soon, we will travel together to see you.
local: Ɩtɩlɩ se palɩzɩ ɖa-taabalʋ Timootee nɛ salaka taa. Yee ɛtalɩ lɔŋ yɔ, ma nɛ ɩ ɖɩkɔŋna nɛ ɖɩna-mɩ.

eng: Let this endurance complete its work so that you may be fully mature, complete, and lacking in nothing.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se mɩ-hɔ ɖɔkʋʋ ḿbʋ́, pɩtɛzɩ kpaʋ ɖoŋ mi-wezuu caɣʋ taa nɛ piyele ɩtɛzɩ wɛnaʋ wɛtʋ kɩbandʋ tɩŋa, nabʋyʋ ɛtaatɛɖɩ-mɩ.

eng: Greet your leaders and all of Gods holy people. The group from Italy greets you.
local: Ɩsɛɛ mɩ-ñʋndɩnaa nɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩma patɩŋa payɩ. Itaalii taabalaa sɛ-mɩ.

eng: But anyone who needs wisdom should ask God, whose very nature is to give to everyone without a second thought, without keeping score. Wisdom will certainly be given to those who ask.
local: Yee mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ ɛfɛyɩnɩ lɔŋ yɔ, ɛsʋlʋmɩ Ɛsɔ, ɛhaɣ-ɩ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛhaɣ paa weyi nɛ laŋɩyɛ kɩbanɖɛ, ɛɛkalɩɣ.

eng: May grace be with all of you.
local: Ɛsɔ ɛla mɩtɩŋa payɩ lootiye!.

eng: Whoever asks shouldnt hesitate. They should ask in faith, without doubting. Whoever doubts is like the surf of the sea, tossed and turned by the wind.
local: Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se ɛ-sʋlʋmʋʋ taa lɛ, ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ se Ɛsɔ haɣ-ɩ, pʋtʋʋ ɛtaawɛɛ. Weyi ɛpʋtʋʋ yɔ, ɛwɛ ɛzɩ teŋgu taa lɩm mbʋ́ heɣlim kpakɩɣ nɛ pɩtaŋgatɩɣ-pʋ yɔ.

eng: People like that should never imagine that they will receive anything from the Lord.
local: Pɩfɛyɩ se ɛcazɩ maɣzʋʋ se Kɩpaɣlʋ haɣ-ɩ nabʋyʋ.

eng: They are double-minded, unstable in all their ways.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛfɛyɩnɩ lɩmaɣzɩyɛ kʋɖʋmɖɩyɛ, ɛɖɔŋ taa nɛ wayɩ.

eng: Brothers and sisters who are poor should find satisfaction in their high status.
local: Krɩstʋ tʋ weyi ɛkɛ kʋñɔndʋ yɔ, ɛkʋndɩ se Ɛsɔ kpazɩ-ɩ.

eng: Those who are wealthy should find satisfaction in their low status, because they will die off like wildflowers.
local: Krɩstʋ tʋ weyi ɛkɛ ñɩm tʋ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ ɛkʋndɩ ɖɔɖɔ se Ɛsɔ tisi-i. Toovenim lɛ, ɛkɔŋ ɖɛʋ kɛlɛʋ ɛzɩ tɛyɛ ñɩtʋ hɛtʋ yɔ.

eng: The sun rises with its scorching heat and dries up the grass so that its flowers fall and its beauty is lost. Just like that, in the midst of their daily lives, the wealthy will waste away.
local: Wɩsɩ kpaɣ nɛ sɩñaɣ ñɩtʋ nɛ tiloo nɛ tɩ-hɛtʋ tɔ, tɩɩtasɩɣ wɛʋ ɖeu. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛnɩ ñɩm tʋ. Ɛ-ñɩm taa sɩm kɔŋ-ɩ tuduu.

eng: Those who stand firm during testing are blessed. They are tried and true. They will receive the life God has promised to those who love him as their reward.
local: Weyi ɛɖɔkʋʋ ɛ-tɩ takʋʋ taa yɔ, ehiɣ ɛ-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛtɛŋ wɩlʋʋ ɛ-tɩ ɖɔkʋʋ lɛ, Ɛsɔ cɛlɩ-ɩ ɛ-kɩhɛyʋʋ: ŋ́gʋ́ lɛ wezuu maatɛŋ. Ŋ́gʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛhɛyɩɣ mbá pɔsɔɔlɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: No one who is tested should say, God is tempting me! This is because God is not tempted by any form of evil, nor does he tempt anyone.
local: Yee pɩtakɩɣ nɔɔyʋ se pʋsʋzɩ-ɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm taa yɔ, pɩfɛyɩ se ɛtɔ se: «Ɛsɔ takɩɣnɩ-m.» Toovenim lɛ, nabʋyʋ ɛɛtɩɩpɩzɩɣ pɩtakɩ Ɛsɔ se pʋɖʋzɩ-ɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm taa, ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛɛtakɩɣ nɔɔyʋ se ɛɖʋzɩ-ɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm taa.

eng: Everyone is tempted by their own cravings; they are lured away and enticed by them.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mbʋ́ ɛyʋ sɔɔlɩ ɛ-maɣmaɣ kpem nɛ pɩkɩlɩ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ hɔŋnɩ-ɩ nɛ puwokini-i takʋʋ taa.

eng: Once those cravings conceive, they give birth to sin; and when sin grows up, it gives birth to death.
local: Ɛwɛɛ nɛ ɛtɛzɩɣ-pʋ sɔɔlʋʋ kpem nɛ pɩkɩlɩɣ yɔ, puwokini-i kɩwɛɛkɩm taa. Pʋbʋ ñɔkɔŋ pɩkɩlɩ-ɩ lɛ, puwoni-i sɩm taa.

eng: Dont be misled, my dear brothers and sisters.
local: Man-taabalaa kʋsɔɔlaa! Ɩtaacɛtɩ mɩ-tɩ.

eng: Every good gift, every perfect gift, comes from above. These gifts come down from the Father, the creator of the heavenly lights, in whose character there is no change at all.
local: Mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pahaɣ ɛyʋ nɛ pʋwɛ ɖeu pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ yɔ, Ɛsɔ weyi ɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ ɛ-cɔlɔ pɩlɩnaa. Ɛnʋ labɩnɩ wɩsɩ nɛ fenaɣ nɛ tɩɩnzɩ. Ɛɛlɛɣzɩɣ kaʋ: ɛfɛyɩ ɛzɩ kɩlɛmʋʋ yɔ.

eng: He chose to give us birth by his true word, and here is the result: we are like the first crop from the harvest of everything he created.
local: Ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛsɔɔlɩna nɛ ɛtɩŋnɩ toovenim tɔm yɔɔ nɛ ɛha-ɖʋ wezuu se ɖɩkɛ kajalaɣ ñɩma, mbʋ́ mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ pɩ-taa.

eng: Know this, my dear brothers and sisters: everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to grow angry.
local: Man-taabalaa kʋsɔɔlaa! Ɩtɩlɩ se: pʋwɛɛ se paa weyi ɛɖaŋ kpam nɛ ewelesini lɛlaa. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɛtaakpedesi se ɛyɔɔdʋʋ, ɛ-pana ɛtaakpa yem yem.

eng: This is because an angry person doesnt produce Gods righteousness.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, yee ɛyʋ pana ɛkpawa yɔ, ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛla mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzɩ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa yɔ.

eng: Therefore, with humility, set aside all moral filth and the growth of wickedness, and welcome the word planted deep inside you—the very word that is able to save you.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, iyele mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pipisiɣ ɛyáa yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu yɔ. Itisi tɔm ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖu mɩ-laŋa taa yɔ nɛ ñamtʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɩpɩzɩɣ tɩya mɩ-ñʋʋ.

eng: You must be doers of the word and not only hearers who mislead themselves.
local: Ɩtaacɛtɩɣ mɩ-tɩ nɛ Ɛsɔtɔm welesuu yem. Ɛlɛ́, pʋwɛɛ se ɩɖɔ tɩ-yɔɔ camɩyɛ.

eng: Those who hear but dont do the word are like those who look at their faces in a mirror.
local: Weyi ewelesiɣ-tʋ yem nɛ ɛɛɖɔŋ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛwɛ ɛzɩ weyi ɛcɔŋnɩ ɛ-tɩ ñɩnʋʋ taa nɛ ɛnaɣ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛwɛɛ yɔ.

eng: They look at themselves, walk away, and immediately forget what they were like.
local: Ɛtɛm ɛ-tɩ cɔnaʋ nɛ ɛɖɛɛ lɛ, ɛsɔɔ ɛzɩma ɛwɛɛ yɔ kpaagbaa.

eng: But there are those who study the perfect law, the law of freedom, and continue to do it. They dont listen and then forget, but they put it into practice in their lives. They will be blessed in whatever they do.
local: Weyi ɛ́lɛ́ ñatazɩɣ paɣtʋ maɣmaɣ taa siŋŋ yɔ, ehikiɣ ɛ-tɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛlakɩ yɔ pɩ-taa. Ńdʋ́ yekina nɛ ɛyʋ wɛɛ ɛ-tɩ yɔɔ. Eewelesiɣ-tʋ yem nɛ ɛsɔɔ-tʋ, ɛlɛ́, ɛɖɔŋ tɩ-yɔɔ siŋŋ.

eng: If those who claim devotion to God dont control what they say, they mislead themselves. Their devotion is worthless.
local: Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛmaɣzɩɣ se ɛkɛ Ɛsɔ wayɩ tɩŋɩyʋ nɛ ɛɛɖɔkʋʋ ɛ-tɩ ɛ-yɔɔdaɣ taa nɛ ɛcɛtɩɣ ɛ-tɩ kɩcɛtʋ yɔ, ɛ-Ɛsɔ wayɩ tɩŋʋʋ ɛɛwazɩɣ pʋyʋ.

eng: True devotion, the kind that is pure and faultless before God the Father, is this: to care for orphans and widows in their difficulties and to keep the world from contaminating us.
local: Weyi ɛɖɔkʋʋ suluwaa nɛ lelaa pa-kʋñɔmɩŋ taa nɛ ɛɖɔkʋʋ ɛ-tɩ se kedeŋa yɔɔ kɩdɛkɛdɩm ɛtaapisi-i yɔ, ɛnʋ tɩŋɩɣnɩ Caa Ɛsɔ wayɩ ɛzɩ pʋmʋnaʋ yɔ nɛ pɩkɛdɩɣnɩ ɛ́lɛ́.

eng: My brothers and sisters, when you show favoritism you deny the faithfulness of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has been resurrected in glory.
local: Man-taabalaa! Đenɖe ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ weyi ɛwɛ Ɛsɔ koboyaɣ taa yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɩtaacɔnɩ nɔɔyʋ ɛsɩndaa nɛ pʋcɔ ɩlabɩ-ɩ pʋyʋ.

eng: Imagine two people coming into your meeting. One has a gold ring and fine clothes, while the other is poor, dressed in filthy rags.
local: Pʋtɔma yɔ, ikpeɣlaa nɛ ñɩm tʋ nɔɔyʋ nɛ kʋñɔndʋ nɔɔyʋ pɔkɔɔ. Ñɩm tʋ lɛ ñɩɣlɩ́m kokooye nɛ esuu ñɩɣlɩ́m wondu nɛ piyele kʋñɔndʋ ñosuu cɛkɩŋ.

eng: Then suppose that you were to take special notice of the one wearing fine clothes, saying, Heres an excellent place. Sit here. But to the poor person you say, Stand over there; or, Here, sit at my feet.
local: Yee ɩsam weyi esu wondu kɩbandʋ yɔ nɛ ɩyaa-ɩ se: «Đajaa! Kɔɔ cɩnɛ nɛ ŋcaɣ lone kɩbanɖɛ taa» nɛ piyele iheyi kʋñɔndʋ se: «Sɩŋ peeɖe yɔ» yaa: «Kɔɔ ŋcaɣ ɖa-naŋgbanzɩ tɛɛ cɩnɛ tataa» yɔ.

eng: Wouldnt you have shown favoritism among yourselves and become evil-minded judges?.
local: tayʋʋ lɩmaɣza fɛyɩ mɩ-maɣmaɣ mɩ-ñʋŋ taa yaa we? Ɩtapɩsɩ mbá pɔcɔŋnɩ ɛyáa ɛsɩndaa nɛ pʋcɔ pahʋʋ tɔm yɔ yaa we?.

eng: My dear brothers and sisters, listen! Hasnt God chosen those who are poor by worldly standards to be rich in terms of faith? Hasnt God chosen the poor as heirs of the kingdom he has promised to those who love him?.
local: Man-taabalaa kʋsɔɔlaa! Iwelesi camɩyɛ nɛ ɩnɩɩ: Ɛsɔ lɩzɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ kʋñɔndɩnaa se pɛkɛ ñɩm tɩnaa pe-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ taa nɛ patɩɣyɩ e-Kewiyaɣ ŋgá ɛɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ mbá pɔsɔɔlɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: But you have dishonored the poor. Dont the wealthy make life difficult for you? Arent they the ones who drag you into court?.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mɩ mɩɩkpakɩɣ kʋñɔndɩnaa se pɛkɛ pʋyʋ! Pɩtɛkɛ ñɩm tɩnaa ɖʋʋnɩ-mɩ kʋñɔŋ naa we? Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbá ɖɔɖɔ yaɣnɩ-mɩ tɔm hʋyaa tɛ yaa we?.

eng: My brothers and sisters, not many of you should become teachers, because we know that we teachers will be judged more strictly.
local: Man-taabalaa! Itaakpedesi mɩtɩŋa se ɩpɩsɩ Ɛsɔtɔm wɩlɩyaa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtɛm naʋ se mbá ɖa ɖɩkɛ wɩlɩyaa yɔ, Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-ɖʋ hʋnaʋ nɛ pɩkɩlɩ lɛlaa.

eng: Arent they the ones who insult the good name spoken over you at your baptism?.
local: Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbá ɖɔɖɔ wɛɛkɩɣnɩ hɩɖɛ kɩbanɖɛ nɖɩ́ Ɛsɔ ya-mɩ yɔ yaa we?.

eng: We all make mistakes often, but those who dont make mistakes with their words have reached full maturity. Like a bridled horse, they can control themselves entirely.
local: Pɩpaŋ coɖuu ɖatɩŋa payɩ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa. Yee putocoɖi nɔɔyʋ kaʋ ɛ-yɔɔdaɣ taa yɔ, nabʋyʋ fɛyɩ se paawanɩ-ɩ. Ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛtɛzɩ ɛ-tɩ ɖɔkʋʋ kpam.

eng: You do well when you really fulfill the royal law found in scripture, Love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Toovenim lɛ, yee ɩɖɔŋ Ɛsɔ Kewiyaɣ paɣtʋ yɔɔ yɔ, pʋbʋ, ɩlabɩ camɩyɛ. Pama-tʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa, ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Sɔɔlɩ ña-taabalʋ ɛzɩ ŋsɔɔlʋʋ ña-maɣmaɣ ña-tɩ yɔ.».

eng: When we bridle horses and put bits in their mouths to lead them wherever we want, we can control their whole bodies.
local: Ɩcɔnɩ kpaŋnɔ nɛ ɩna: ɖɩɖʋʋ ɛ-nɔɔ taa ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ cikpeluu nakʋyʋ kpaagbaa ñɩɩɩ. Ŋ́gʋ́ yekina se ɛñam-ɖʋ camɩyɛ kpem nɛ ɛtɩŋ nʋmɔʋ ŋgʋ́ ɖɩsɔɔlaa yɔ.

eng: But when you show favoritism, you are committing a sin, and by that same law you are exposed as a lawbreaker.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee ɩcɔŋnɩ nɔɔyʋ ɛsɩndaa se pʋcɔ ɩlabɩ-ɩ pʋyʋ yɔ, ɩlabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm. Mbʋ́ paɖʋ paɣtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa yɔ, pɩwɩlɩɣ se ɩtɔɖɔ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: Consider ships: They are so large that strong winds are needed to drive them. But pilots direct their ships wherever they want with a little rudder.
local: Ɩcɔnɩ ɖɔɖɔ mɛlɛnaa nɛ ɩna: paa paapaɣlɩ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ nɛ paa heɣlim sɔsɔm ɛmakɩ nɛ pɩɖɛɣnɩ-wɛ yɔ, ɖaʋ cikpeluu kpaagbaa ñɩɩɩ nakʋyʋ wɛ pɛ-tɛɛ. Kʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, weyi ɛsaɣ yɔ, ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛtɩŋnɩ-wɛ nʋmɔʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛsɔɔlaa yɔ.

eng: Anyone who tries to keep all of the Law but fails at one point is guilty of failing to keep all of it.
local: Paɣtʋ wɛʋ yɔ, paa tɩ-taa kʋɖʋmtʋ koŋ yɔɔ nɔɔyʋ ɛtɔɖɔ yɔ, ɛyʋsɩnɩ tɩtɩŋa payɩ.

eng: In the same way, even though the tongue is a small part of the body, it boasts wildly. Think about this: A small flame can set a whole forest on fire.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛnɩ nzʋlʋmɩyɛ, ɖɩkɛ ɛyʋ tomnaɣ hɔɔlʋʋ cikpeluu, ɛlɛ́, ńɖɩ́ ɛyʋ kpazɩɣnɩ ɛ-tɩ. Ɩcɔnɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mamacɩka yeki nɛ lakʋ ñaɣ miŋ kɩtɩŋa payɩ yɔ.

eng: The one who said, Dont commit adultery , also said, Dont commit murder . So if you dont commit adultery but do commit murder, you are a lawbreaker.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛtɔm se: «Taala acɛyɛ» yɔ, ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛtɔmna ɖɔɖɔ se: «Taakʋ ɛyʋ.» Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yee ɩtala acɛyɛ, ɛlɛ́, ɩkʋ ɛyʋ yɔ, ɩtɔɖɔ paɣtʋ yɔɔ.

eng: The tongue is a small flame of fire, a world of evil at work in us. It contaminates our entire lives. Because of it, the circle of life is set on fire. The tongue itself is set on fire by the flames of hell.
local: Nzʋlʋmɩyɛ wɛ ɛzɩ miŋ ɛnʋ yɔ. Tomnaɣ hɔɔlɩŋ tɩŋa taa lɛ, ńɖɩ́ pisiɣni katɩŋa payɩ: ɖɩlakɩ mbʋ́ pɩtatʋʋzɩ yɔ. Mbʋ́ piyeba nɛ ɖɩwɛɛ ɛzɩ miŋ yɔ lɛ se: miŋ lɩnɩ ɖenɖe kʋñɔŋ maatɛŋ tɩtɩŋa wɛɛ yɔ nɛ ɛñɔtɩ-ɖɩ nɛ ɖɩwɛɛkɩ ɛyáa wezuu caɣʋ tɩŋa.

eng: In every way, then, speak and act as people who will be judged by the law of freedom.
local: Paɣtʋ ndʋ́ tiyeki se ɛyʋ ɛwɛɛ ɛ-tɩ yɔɔ yɔ, tɩ-yɔɔ pɔkɔŋ-mɩ hʋnaʋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mɩ-yɔɔdaɣ nɛ mɩ-lakasɩ pawɩlɩ se ɩsɩm paɣtʋ ńdʋ́.

eng: People can tame and already have tamed every kind of animal, bird, reptile, and fish.
local: Ɛyʋ pɩzɩɣ ɛkpa tɛyɛ kpɩna nɛ sumasɩ nɛ tɛtɛ ñɩtʋ nɛ kpakpasɩ nɛ lɩm taa kpɩna patɩŋa payɩ nɛ ɛcɔnɩ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: There will be no mercy in judgment for anyone who hasnt shown mercy. Mercy overrules judgment.
local: Weyi ɛɛnaɣ lɛlaa pʋtɔdɩyɛ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɛɛkɔŋ ɛ-ñɩnɖɛ naʋ ɖɔɖɔ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɛhʋʋnɩ-ɩ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛ́lɛ́ naɣ lɛlaa pʋtɔdɩyɛ yɔ, piyeki nɛ ɛlɩɩ tɔm hʋʋ taa.

eng: No one can tame the tongue, though. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.
local: Ɛlɛ́, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛkpa ɛ-nzʋlʋmɩyɛ nɛ ɛ́ɖɔ́kɩ-ɖɩ: ɖɩkɛ kɩdɛkɛdɩm, ɖɩɩcakɩ ɖi-ɖeɖe, sɔtʋ su ɖɩ-nɔɔ taa ɖo.

eng: My brothers and sisters, what good is it if people say they have faith but do nothing to show it? Claiming to have faith cant save anyone, can it?.
local: Man-taabalaa! Yee ɛyʋ ɛwɛɛ nɛ ɛyɔɔdʋʋnɩ nɔɔ yem se: «Mɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ» nɛ ɛɛwɩlɩɣ-kʋ nɛ lakasɩ kɩbanzɩ lɛ, pɩwazɩɣ-ɩ we? Lidaʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩpɩzɩɣ kɩya ɛ-ñʋʋ naa we?.

eng: With it we both bless the Lord and Father and curse human beings made in Gods likeness.
local: Nzʋlʋmɩyɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩsaŋnɩ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Caa Ɛsɔ. Ńɖɩ́ ɖɔɖɔ ɖɩwɩlɩɣnɩ lɛlaa kpalʋʋ, ŋgʋ́, Ɛsɔ labɩ-wɛ ɛzɩ ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛwɛʋ yɔ:.

eng: Imagine a brother or sister who is naked and never has enough food to eat.
local: Yee Krɩstʋ tʋ halʋ ñɩnʋ yaa abalʋ ñɩnʋ nɔɔyʋ ɛlɩɣ ndaakpeɖe nɛ tɔɔnaɣ tɛɖɩɣ-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Blessing and cursing come from the same mouth. My brothers and sisters, it just shouldnt be this way!.
local: samtʋ nɛ kpalʋʋ wɩlʋʋ palɩɣnɩ nɔɔ kʋɖʋmaɣ taa. Man-taabalaa! Pɩfɛyɩ se pʋwɛɛ ḿbʋ́.

eng: What if one of you said, Go in peace! Stay warm! Have a nice meal!? What good is it if you dont actually give them what their body needs?.
local: nɛ mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ heyi-i se: «Wolo, ña-laŋɩyɛ ɛhɛɛ, suu wondu camɩyɛ nɛ ŋtɔɔ nɛ ŋhaɣ!» Nɛ ɛtaha-ɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɛɖɩɣ-ɩ yɔ lɛ, pɩwazɩɣ we?.

eng: Both fresh water and salt water dont come from the same spring, do they?.
local: Lɩm mbʋ́ pʋwɛ leleŋ yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ñaŋ yɔ paapɩzɩɣ palɩɩnɩ hiɖe kʋɖʋmɖɩyɛ taa.

eng: In the same way, faith is dead when it doesnt result in faithful activity.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛnɩ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ lidaʋ wɛnaʋ: yee nɔɔyʋ ɛtɔm se ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ nɛ ɛɛwɩlɩɣ-kʋ nɛ lakasɩ kɩbanzɩ yɔ, e-lidaʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩsɩba.

eng: My brothers and sisters, can a fig tree produce olives? Can a grapevine produce figs? Of course not, and fresh water doesnt flow from a saltwater spring either.
local: Man-taabalaa! Hiyuú lʋlʋʋ sɩŋkpatʋ naa we? Pɩɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́! Tɩʋ ŋgʋ́ popuu ki-pee sʋlʋm yɔ kʋkʋ ñalʋlʋʋ hiyikajásɩ naa we? Pɩɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ! Ḿbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ: lɩm mbʋ́ pʋwɛ leleŋ yɔ, pɩɩpɩzɩɣ pɩlɩɩnɩ hiɖe nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa lɩm wɛ salaʋ yɔ ɖɩ-taa.

eng: Someone might claim, You have faith and I have action. But how can I see your faith apart from your actions? Instead, Ill show you my faith by putting it into practice in faithful action.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee nɔɔyʋ ɛtɔm se: «ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ nɛ ma malakɩ lakasɩ kɩbanzɩ yɔ» mɔnpɔzʋʋ pʋdʋ se: «Wɩlɩ-m ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ŋwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ nɛ ŋŋlakɩ lakasɩ kɩbanzɩ yɔ nɛ ma mawɩlɩ-ŋ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ nɛ ma-lakasɩ kɩbanzɩ yɔ.».

eng: Are any of you wise and understanding? Show that your actions are good with a humble lifestyle that comes from wisdom.
local: Mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ sɩm lɔŋ nɛ ɛwɛɛ lɛɣtʋ naa we? Pʋdʋ eyele pasɩm-ɩ nɛ ɛ-wɛtʋ kɩbandʋ nɛ ɛ-lakasɩ kɩbanzɩ nɛ ɛ-hɛɛ wɛtʋ nɛ ɛ-lɔŋsɩnɖɛ.

eng: Its good that you believe that God is one. Ha! Even the demons believe this, and they tremble with fear.
local: Ŋwɛnɩ lidaʋ se Ɛsɔ kʋɖʋm koŋ wɛna, pʋwɛ camɩyɛ. Aleewaa ñɛwɛnɩ-kʋ ɖɔɖɔ, ɛlɛ́ sɔɔndʋ kpaɣ-wɛ nɛ peseliɣ.

eng: However, if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, then stop bragging and living in ways that deny the truth.
local: Ɛlɛ́, yee ɩlakɩ ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ kadadaɣyaɣ nɛ ɩkɛɣyɩɣ ɖama yɔ, ɩtaakʋndɩ, ɩtaakpɛzɩ mbʋ́ pɩkɛ toovenim yɔ.

eng: Are you so slow? Do you need to be shown that faith without actions has no value at all?.
local: Kɩmɛlɛŋ ña! Ɛyʋ ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ nɛ ɛɛwɩlɩɣ-kʋ nɛ ɛ-lakasɩ kɩbanzɩ yɔ, pɩɩwazɩɣ pʋyʋ. Ŋcaɣ mawɩlɩ-ŋ nɛ ŋna yaa we?.

eng: This is not the wisdom that comes down from above. Instead, it is from the earth, natural and demonic.
local: Mbá palakɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pɔ-lɔŋsɩnɖɛ talɩɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ. Ɛlɛ́, kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ ɖɩlɩnaa, ɛyáa lɔŋsɩnɖɛ pɩkɛnaa, aleewaa cɔlɔ ɖɩlɩnaa.

eng: What about Abraham, our father? Wasnt he shown to be righteous through his actions when he offered his son Isaac on the altar?.
local: Ɛbɛ yɔɔ Ɛsɔ tɔm se ɖɔ-cɔzɔ Abraham kɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa? Ɛ-lakasɩ kɩbanzɩ yɔɔ: ɛkpaɣ ɛ-pɩyalʋ Izaakɩ se ewokini-i nɛ ɛlaa-ɩ-ɩ.

eng: Wherever there is jealousy and selfish ambition, there is disorder and everything that is evil.
local: Đenɖe ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ nɛ ɖama kɛɣyʋʋ pɛwɛɛ yɔ, ɛyáa ɛɛnɩɣnɩ ɖama. Lakasɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩsɩ ndɩ ndɩ wɛɣ peeɖe ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: See, his faith was at work along with his actions. In fact, his faith was made complete by his faithful actions.
local: Ŋnawa: e-lidaʋ nɛ ɛ-lakasɩ kɩbanzɩ pɛkpɛndaa nɛ palakɩ tʋmɩyɛ. Ɛ-lakasɩ ńzɩ́ sɩwɩlɩnɩ se ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ siŋŋ.

eng: What of the wisdom from above? First, it is pure, and then peaceful, gentle, obedient, filled with mercy and good actions, fair, and genuine.
local: Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛwɛɣnɩ lɔŋ weyi ɛlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ yɔ, kajalaɣ lɛ, ɛ-taa wɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ. Nabʋlɛ lɛ, ɛɛcaɣ tɔm, ɛwɛ hɛɛ, ɛnɩɣnɩ ɛyáa nɛ ɛnaɣ pa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ siŋŋ nɛ ɛlakɩ tʋma kɩbana, ɛɛlakɩ tɛ yɔɔ tɛ yɔɔ, ɛɛlakɩ tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ lakasɩ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: So the scripture was fulfilled that says, Abraham believed God, and God regarded him as righteous . What is more, Abraham was called Gods friend.
local: Ḿbʋ́ tɔm ndʋ́ pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ, tɩlaba. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Abraham wɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ kpaɣ-ɩ se ɛkɛ kʋtʋʋzʋ.» Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɩtɩlɩ se Ɛsɔ ya-ɩ se ɛ-taabalʋ.

eng: Those who make peace sow the seeds of justice by their peaceful acts.
local: Mbá pañɩnɩɣ se lɛlaa ɛwɛɛ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa yɔ, laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa poɖuu po-ɖomaɣ. Mbʋ́ pakʋŋ yɔ lɛ se Ɛsɔ pɩsɩɣ-wɛ kʋtʋʋzaa.

eng: So you see that a person is shown to be righteous through faithful actions and not through faith alone.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́, ɛyʋ lakasɩ kɩbanzɩ yɔɔ ɛpɩsɩɣ kʋtʋʋzʋ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa, pɩtɛkɛ e-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ yɔɔ ɖeke.

eng: In the same way, wasnt Rahab the prostitute shown to be righteous when she received the messengers as her guests and then sent them on by another road?.
local: Ɩtɔzɩ ɖɔɖɔ Rahaabɩ weyi ɛpɛdɩɣ ɛ-tɩ yɔ ɛ-yɔɔ. Feŋiyaa kowobi pɛmɛlɩ ɛ-tɛ nɛ ɛsɩnɩ-wɛ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-wɛ nʋmɔʋ lɛɛkʋ nɛ pekpe. Ɛ-lakasɩ ńzɩ́ sɩ-yɔɔ ɛpɩsɩ kʋtʋʋzʋ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa.

eng: As the lifeless body is dead, so faith without actions is dead.
local: Toovenim lɛ, ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛyʋ ɛfɛyɩnɩ fezuu, ɛsɩba yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ yee nɔɔyʋ ɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ nɛ ɛɛlakɩ lakasɩ kɩbanzɩ yɔ, e-lidaʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩsɩba.

eng: What is the source of conflict among you? What is the source of your disputes? Dont they come from your cravings that are at war in your own lives?.
local: You nɛ lʋbɩyɛ palɩnɩ le mɩ-hɛkʋ taa? Mbʋ́ ɩsɔɔlɩ labʋ kpem nɛ pʋwɛɛ mɩ-taa nɛ piseɣtiɣ-mɩ yɔ, pʋ-cɔlɔ pɩlɩnaa.

eng: You long for something you dont have, so you commit murder. You are jealous for something you cant get, so you struggle and fight. You dont have because you dont ask.
local: Mi-liu wɛ nabʋyʋ, ɛlɛ́, ɩɩpɩzɩɣ se ihiɣ-pʋ. Ɩɖʋʋ ɛsɩyɛ lɛlaa ñɩm yɔɔ nɛ ɩcaɣ se ɩkʋ-wɛ, ɛlɛ́, pɩɩlɩɣ-mɩ pʋyʋ. Ḿbʋ́ labɩna nɛ ɩwɛɛ iyou nɛ ɩlʋkɩ. Ŋgʋ́, ɩtasʋlʋmɩ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ itehiɣ-pʋ.

eng: You ask and dont have because you ask with evil intentions, to waste it on your own cravings.
local: Paa ɩsʋlʋmɩ-ɩ yɔ, ɛɛhaɣ-mɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩɩsɩŋ sʋlʋmʋʋ: ɩsʋlʋmʋʋ mbʋ́ ɩcaɣ se ɩkpaɣ nɛ ɩlabɩnɩ mbʋ́ ɩsɔɔlaa kpem yɔ.

eng: You unfaithful people! Dont you know that friendship with the world means hostility toward God? So whoever wants to be the world's friend becomes God's enemy.
local: Mɩ, ɩwɛ ɛzɩ acɛyɛ tɩnaa yɔ! Ɩtatɩlɩ se yee nɔɔyʋ ɛsɔɔlɩ kedeŋa ñɩm kpem yɔ, ɛpɩsɩɣ Ɛsɔ koyindu naa we? Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, weyi ɛsɔɔlʋʋ kedeŋa ñɩm yɔ, ɛpɩsɩɣ Ɛsɔ koyindu kpaagbaa.

eng: Or do you suppose that scripture is meaningless? Doesnt God long for our faithfulness in the life he has given to us?.
local: Ɩmaɣzɩɣ se Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa tɔm tʋnɛ, tɩfɛyɩ wazaɣ naa we? Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Ɛsɔ ñɩnɩɣ fezuu ŋgʋ́ ɛha-ɖʋ yɔ nɛ sʋsɩ.».

eng: But he gives us more grace. This is why it says, God stands against the proud, but favors the humble .
local: Ŋgʋ́, ɛnʋ lakɩnɩ-ɖʋ lootiye siŋŋ. Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ ka-taa ɖɔɖɔ yɔ. Pamawa se: «Ɛsɔ kʋyʋʋ mbá pakpazɩɣ pa-tɩ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛlakɩ mbá paba ñetisiɣ pa-tɩ yɔ lootiye.».

eng: Therefore, submit to God. Resist the devil, and he will run away from you.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, itisi mɩ-tɩ Ɛsɔ tɛɛ. Ɩkaɖɩnɩ eleeu, pʋbʋ, eseɣ eyebi-mɩ.

eng: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Wash your hands, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you double-minded.
local: Ɩkpɩtɩnɩ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ kpɩtɩnɩ-mɩ. Kɩwɛɛkɩm laɖaa mɩ! Ɩlɩzɩ mi-nesi pʋ-tɔm taa. Nɔsɩ naalɛ tɩnaa mɩ! Iyele mɩ-lɩmaɣza kɩdɛkɛdɛɛ.

eng: Cry out in sorrow, mourn, and weep! Let your laughter become mourning and your joy become sadness.
local: Ɩtɩlɩ mɩ-kʋñɔŋ nɛ iwili nɛ ɩtɩmɩ. Iyele mi-woŋa nɛ iwili, iyele ɖɔɖɔ mɩ-laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ nɛ mɩ-laŋa wɛɛkɩ.

eng: Pay attention, you wealthy people! Weep and moan over the miseries coming upon you.
local: Mbá mɩ ɩkɛ ñɩm tɩnaa yɔ, iwelesini-m lɛɛlɛɛyɔ! Iwili nɛ isisi kʋñɔmɩŋ weyi ɩkɔŋ-mɩ talʋʋ yɔ ɩ-yɔɔ!.

eng: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will lift you up.
local: Itisi mɩ-tɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ tɛɛ nɛ ɛkpazɩ-mɩ.

eng: Your riches have rotted. Moths have destroyed your clothes.
local: Mɩ-ñɩm ɖazaa nɛ ŋgbam tɔɔ mi-tokonaa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont say evil things about each other. Whoever insults or criticizes a brother or sister insults and criticizes the Law. If you find fault with the Law, you are not a doer of the Law but a judge over it.
local: Taabalaa! Ɩtaayɔɔdɩ ɖama yɔɔ tɔm ɛzaɣ́tʋ. Weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋ lɛlʋ yɔɔ tɔm ɛzaɣ́tʋ yaa ɛkpɛzɩɣnɩ-ɩ yɔ, ɛyɔɔdɩ ɖɔɖɔ mbʋ́ paɖʋ paɣtʋ taa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ tɔm ɛzaɣ́tʋ nɛ ɛtɛ ɛkpɛɛnɩ-tʋ. Yee ŋkpɛɣnɩ paɣtʋ yɔ, ŋŋɖɔŋ tɩ-yɔɔ, ɛlɛ́, ŋkpaɣ ña-tɩ se ŋkɛ tɔm hʋyʋ.

eng: Your gold and silver have rusted, and their rust will be evidence against you. It will eat your flesh like fire. Consider the treasure you have hoarded in the last days.
local: Mi-sika nɛ mi-liidiye ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ pamʋ kálɩkɔ́daɣ. Kálɩkɔ́daɣ ŋ́gá kɔkɔŋnɩ lɩzʋʋ aseɣɖe se ɩtala camɩyɛ. Halɩ kɔkɔŋ yebu ɖɔɖɔ nɛ mɩ-maɣmaɣ ɩpʋʋ ɛzɩ miŋ ñakʋʋ pʋyʋ yɔ. Ikpeɣli ñɩm pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ, ŋgʋ́, kɛtɛzaɣ alɩwaatʋ taa ɖɩwɛɛ.

eng: There is only one lawgiver and judge, and he is able to save and to destroy. But you who judge your neighbor, who are you?.
local: Ŋgʋ́, Ɛsɔ ɖeke koŋ ɖʋʋnɩ paɣtʋ, ɛnʋ lɛ tɔm hʋyʋ. E-ɖeke koŋ ɛpɩzɩɣnɩ ɛya ɛyáa ñʋʋ yaa ɛkʋ-wɛ. Weyi ña ñɛkpɛzɩɣnɩ lɛlaa yɔ, ŋmaɣzɩɣ se ña lɛ anɩ kpem?.

eng: Listen! Hear the cries of the wages of your field hands. These are the wages you stole from those who harvested your fields. The cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of heavenly forces.
local: Ɩtɛhɛyɩ tʋmlaɖaa mbá palakɩ tʋmɩyɛ mɩ-hayɩm taa yɔ pe-liidiye. Ɩnɩɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ patɩmɩɣnɩ-mɩ yɔ! Mbá pakʋŋ mɩ-tɔɔnaɣ yɔ, pamabɩ kobusi nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ Caabiyuu nɩɩ.

eng: Pay attention, you who say, Today or tomorrow we will go to such-and-such a town. We will stay there a year, buying and selling, and making a profit.
local: Mbá mɩ ɩlakɩ tamaɣ se: «Sɔnɔ yaa cee ɖiwoki tɛtʋ tʋnɛ tɩ-taa nɛ ɖɩcaɣ peeɖe pɩnaɣ nɛ ɖɩla tadɩyɛ nɛ ɖihiɣ liidiye» yɔ, iwelesi lɛɛlɛɛyɔ nɛ ɩnɩɩ.

eng: You have lived a self-satisfying life on this earth, a life of luxury. You have stuffed your hearts in preparation for the day of slaughter.
local: Mi-wezuu caɣʋ taa kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ lɛ, ɩwɛ leleŋ taa nɛ ɩcanɩɣ mɩ-tɩ. Ɩtɔɔ camɩyɛ nɛ ɩpaɣlɩ ɛzɩ kpɩna wena pɔkɔŋ kʋʋ se pɔtɔɔ yɔ .

eng: You dont really know about tomorrow. What is your life? You are a mist that appears for only a short while before it vanishes.
local: Ɩtatɩɩsɩm mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ-mɩ tɛmnaʋ cee yɔ! Ɩwɛ ɛzɩ hulumiye nɖɩ́ ɖɩñɛyɩɣ alɩwaatʋ pazɩ nɛ pʋwayɩ ɖɩcɛ nɛ paatasɩɣ-ɖɩ naʋ yɔ.

eng: You have condemned and murdered the righteous one, who doesnt oppose you.
local: Ɩkʋ ɛyáa kʋtʋʋzaa tɔm nɛ ɩtɛ ɩkʋ-wɛ nɛ pasɩ: patapɩzɩ pakaɖɩnɩ-mɩ.

eng: Heres what you ought to say: If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that.
local: Pɩɩwɛɛ se ɩtɔ se: «Yee Kɩpaɣlʋ liu ɛwɛɛ yɔ, ɖɩwɛɣ wezuu nɛ ɖɩla pʋnɛ yaa pʋnɛ.».

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, you must be patient as you wait for the coming of the Lord. Consider the farmer who waits patiently for the coming of rain in the fall and spring, looking forward to the precious fruit of the earth.
local: Taabalaa! Pʋ-yɔɔ meheyiɣ-mɩ se ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-hɔ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ kɔŋ yɔ. Ɩcɔnɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ haɖʋ ɖɔkʋʋ ɛ-hɔyɛ nɛ ɛɖaŋ se ɛkɔŋ tɔɔnaɣ kɩbaŋga kʋm ɛ-hayɩm taa yɔ: ɛɖɔkʋʋ ɛ-hɔyɛ nɛ kajalaɣ tɛŋ nɛ kɛtɛzaɣ ñɩŋ panɩɩ.

eng: But now you boast and brag, and all such boasting is evil.
local: Ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, mɩ mɩkʋndʋʋ nɛ ɩɩsɩɣ mɩ-tɩ pʋ-cɔlɔ. Pɩtɛ kʋndʋʋ ḿbʋ́, pɩfɛyɩ ɖeu.

eng: You also must wait patiently, strengthening your resolve, because the coming of the Lord is near.
local: Mɩ ɖɔɖɔ, ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-hɔ ḿbʋ́, iyele nɛ mɩ-lɩmaɣza kpa ɖoŋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ kɔŋ yɔ, tɩwɩzɩ talʋʋ.

eng: It is a sin when someone knows the right thing to do and doesnt do it.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, yee nɔɔyʋ ɛsɩm mbʋ́ pʋmʋnaa se ɛla yɔ nɛ ɛɛlakɩ-pʋ yɔ, ɛlabɩ kɩwɛɛkɩm.

eng: Dont complain about each other, brothers and sisters, so that you wont be judged. Look! The judge is standing at the door!.
local: Taabalaa! Ɩtaatɩmnɩ ɖama, pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, Ɛsɔ hʋʋnɩ-mɩ nɛ ɛkʋ mɩ-tɔm. Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna: tɔm hʋyʋ talɩ nɔnɔɔ taa!.

eng: Brothers and sisters, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord as an example of patient resolve and steadfastness.
local: Taabalaa! Ɩtɔzɩ náyaa mbá pɔyɔɔdaɣnɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ nɔɔ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ, ɩla ɛzɩ palabʋ yɔ. Pɔɖɔkɩ pɔ-hɔ siŋŋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɔtɔkaɣ kʋñɔŋ yɔ.

eng: Look at how we honor those who have practiced endurance. You have heard of the endurance of Job. And you have seen what the Lord has accomplished, for the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
local: Đɩtɔŋ se pehiɣ pa-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paaɖɔkɩ pa-tɩ kpam. Ɩtɛm nɩʋ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Yɔɔbʋ kɔɖɔkɩ ɛ-tɩ ɛ-kʋñɔŋ taa yɔ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɩna mbʋ́ Kɩpaɣlʋ ha-ɩ pʋwayɩ yɔ. Ɛlabɩ-ɩ ḿbʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnaɣ ɛyáa pʋtɔdɩyɛ nɛ ɛlakɩ-wɛ camɩyɛ.

eng: Most important, my brothers and sisters, never make a solemn pledge—neither by heaven nor earth, nor by anything else. Instead, speak with a simple Yes or No, or else you may fall under judgment.
local: Man-taabalaa! Pɩtɩŋa pɩ-taa lɛ, ɩtaaɖuu: ɩtaaɖuuni ɛsɔdaa yaa tɛtʋ yaa nabʋyʋ. Yee ɩtɔm se: «Ɛɛ» yɔ, pɩkɛ «Ɛɛ.» Ɩtɔm se: «Aayɩ» yɔ, pɩkɛ ɖɔɖɔ «Aayɩ.» Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, Ɛsɔ hʋʋnɩ-mɩ nɛ ɛkʋ mɩ-tɔm.

eng: If any of you are suffering, they should pray. If any of you are happy, they should sing.
local: Mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ wɛ kʋñɔŋ taa yaa we? Pʋdʋ ɛsɛɛ Ɛsɔ. Mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ wɛ laŋhʋlʋmɩyɛ taa yaa we? Pʋdʋ etee hendu nɛ ɛsa Ɛsɔ.

eng: If any of you are sick, they should call for the elders of the church, and the elders should pray over them, anointing them with oil in the name of the Lord.
local: Pɩwɩɣ mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ yaa we? Pʋdʋ ɛ́yáa Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa sɔsaa nɛ powolo petendi Ɛsɔ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ pasayɩ-ɩ num Kɩpaɣlʋ hɩɖɛ taa.

eng: Prayer that comes from faith will heal the sick, for the Lord will restore them to health. And if they have sinned, they will be forgiven.
local: Pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ pe-tenduu taa yɔ, pɩyakɩ kʋdɔndʋ ñʋʋ: Kɩpaɣlʋ lakɩ nɛ ɛ-yɔɔ cɛ nɛ yee ɛɛlaba kɩwɛɛkɩm yɔ, ekpeɣ-i-pʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: For this reason, confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of the righteous person is powerful in what it can achieve.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ meheyiɣ-mɩ se iheyi ɖama mɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm nɛ itendi Ɛsɔ ɖama yɔɔ nɛ mɩ-yɔɔ cɛ. Weyi ɛtʋʋzɩ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa yɔ, yee ɛsɛ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛ-laŋɩyɛ tɩŋa yɔ, ɛ-Ɛsɔ sɛʋ wɛɣ ɖoŋ pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ.

eng: Elijah was a person just like us. When he earnestly prayed that it wouldnt rain, no rain fell for three and a half years.
local: Ɩtɔzɩ Elii yɔɔ, ɛkɛ ɛyʋ yem ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ɖa yɔ: eetendi Ɛsɔ siŋŋ se ɛtaayele nɛ tɛʋ nɩɩ. Toovenim, pɩkpaɣnɩ puwiye ńɖɩ́ lɛ, tɛʋ yele nɩʋ pɩnzɩ naadozo nɛ hɔɔlʋʋ.

eng: He prayed again, God sent rain, and the earth produced its fruit.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, ɛtasɩ-ɩ tenduu se kɩnɩɩ. Peeɖe ɛ́lɛ́ yeba nɛ kɩpazɩ nɩʋ nɛ tɔɔnaɣ la ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, if any of you wander from the truth and someone turns back the wanderer.
local: Man-taabalaa! Mɩ-taa lɛ, nɔɔyʋ pɩzɩɣ ele toovenim nʋmɔʋ taa, nɛ lɛlʋ pɩsɩnɩ-ɩ nʋmɔʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-taa.

eng: recognize that whoever brings a sinner back from the wrong path will save them from death and will bring about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: Ɩtɩlɩ se: yee mɩ-taa nɔɔyʋ eleba toovenim nʋmɔʋ nɛ nɔɔyʋ hɔm-ɩ nɛ ɛpɩsɩnɩ-ɩ kɩ-taa yɔ, ɛlɩzɩ-ɩ nɛ sɩm taa. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, lakasɩ ńzɩ́ sɩlakɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ kpeɣ ɛyáa kɩwɛɛkɩm sakɩyɛ .

eng: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To Gods chosen strangers in the world of the diaspora, who live in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
local: Ma Pɩyɛ weyi ɛkɛ Yesu Krɩstʋ tiyiyu yɔ, mamaɣnɩ takayaɣ kanɛ. Mamaɣ-kɛ mbá mɩ Ɛsɔ lɩzaa se ɩkɛ ɛ-ñɩma nɛ ɩfɛyɩ mɩ-tɛ yɔ. Ɩmɩzɩ ɖama ageeta wena payaɣ se Pɔntɩ nɛ Galaasii nɛ Kapaadoosi nɛ Aazii nɛ Bitinii yɔ a-taa.

eng: God the Father chose you because of what he knew beforehand. He chose you through the Holy Spirits work of making you holy and because of the faithful obedience and sacrifice of Jesus Christ. May Gods grace and peace be multiplied to you.
local: Caa Ɛsɔ kalɩzɩ-mɩ ɛzɩ ɛɛɖʋʋ yɔ se ɩkɛ ɛ-ñɩma. E-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ɖoŋ yebina nɛ ɩkɛ ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɩña Yesu Krɩstʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-calɩm pɔsɔ-mɩ se ɩpɩzɩ ɩwɛɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa. Mɛnsɛ-mɩ: Caa Ɛsɔ ɛtɛzɩ-mɩ labʋ lootiye nɛ ɛtɛzɩ mɩ-laŋa hɛzʋʋ.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! On account of his vast mercy, he has given us new birth. You have been born anew into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead.
local: Đɩsa Ɛsɔ, ɛnʋ lɛ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ Caa. Ɛna ɖa-pʋtɔdɩyɛ siŋŋ nɛ ɛtɩŋnɩ Yesu Krɩstʋ fem nɛ ɛlɩɩnɩ sɩɖaa taa yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛha-ɖʋ wezuu kɩfalʋʋ. Mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ labɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, piyebina nɛ ɖɩtaɣ liu siŋŋ.

eng: You have a pure and enduring inheritance that cannot perish—an inheritance that is presently kept safe in heaven for you.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɛhaɣ-ɖʋ ɛ-ñɩm mbʋ́ pɩɩɖazɩɣ yaa piipiyiɣ yaa pɩɩwɛɛkɩɣ kaʋ nɛ ɛsɩɩ-pʋ ɛsɔdaa nɛ ɛɖaŋnɩ ɛ-ñɩma yɔ.

eng: Through his faithfulness, you are guarded by Gods power so that you can receive the salvation he is ready to reveal in the last time.
local: Mbá mɩ ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Krɩstʋ yɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ huuzi mɩ-yɔɔ nɛ e-ɖoŋ se ihiɣ ñʋʋ yabʋ. Ɛtɛm ɖaŋʋʋ kpam se ɛwɩlɩ-pʋ ɛyáa kɛtɛzaɣ alɩwaatʋ taa.

eng: You now rejoice in this hope, even if its necessary for you to be distressed for a short time by various trials.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, paa pɩtɩŋɩɣnɩ kʋñɔmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ yɔɔ nɛ mɩ-ɛsɛ ñɩŋ alɩwaatʋ pazɩ yɔ, mɩ-laŋa ɛhʋlʋmɩ siŋŋ.

eng: This is necessary so that your faith may be found genuine. Your genuine faith will result in praise, glory, and honor for you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: Pɩlakɩ-mɩ ḿbʋ́ se pana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ siŋŋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ yɔ. Sika wɛʋ yɔ, kɔkɔŋ mʋʋ́ʋ. Paa ḿbʋ́ yɔ, paɖʋʋ-kɛ miŋ taa se pana se kɛkɛ kɩbaŋga naa kɛtɛkɛ. Ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ maɣzɩɣ mbá mɩ mi-lidaʋ kɩlɩ sika wazaɣ yɔ se ɛna kɩ-tɩnaɣ. Ḿbʋ́ yekina nɛ ɛsam-mɩ nɛ ɛɖʋ-mɩ hɩɖɛ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ Yesu Krɩstʋ lɩzɩɣ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ yɔ.

eng: Although youve never seen him, you love him. Even though you dont see him now, you trust him and so rejoice with a glorious joy that is too much for words.
local: Ɩtana-ɩ nɛ mɩ-ɛsɩyɛ, ɛlɛ́ ɩsɔɔlɩ-ɩ. Paa ɩtana-ɩ yɔ, ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ mɩ-laŋa hʋlʋmɩ siŋŋ, pɩfɛyɩ yɔɔdʋʋ.

eng: Therefore, get rid of all ill will and all deceit, pretense, envy, and slander.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, iyele kɩwɛɛkɩm tɩŋa payɩ labʋ: kañatʋ nɛ cɛtʋʋ nɛ tɛɛ nɛ pʋ-yɔɔ lakasɩ nɛ ɛsɩsɛmɩyɛ nɛ lɛlaa tɔm ɛzaɣ́tʋ yɔɔdʋʋ.

eng: You are receiving the goal of your faith: your salvation.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ yakɩ mɩ-ñʋʋ yɔɔ ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ.

eng: Instead, like a newborn baby, desire the pure milk of the word. Nourished by it, you will grow into salvation.
local: Ɛsɔtɔm ɛlabɩ-mɩ liu tam ɛzɩ hɩlɩm lakʋʋ piya ajamaa liu yɔ nɛ pɩtɩŋnɩ tɩ-yɔɔ nɛ ɩtɛzɩ pɩʋ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩya mɩ-ñʋʋ.

eng: Wives, likewise, submit to your own husbands. Do this so that even if some of them refuse to believe the word, they may be won without a word by their wives way of life.
local: Mɩ halaa lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se paa mɩ-taa weyi ɛña ɛ-walʋ. Yee ɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, mɩ-taa mbá pa-walaa kiziɣ Ɛsɔtɔm yɔ, panaɣ mɩ-ɖɔnɛ kɩbanɖɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛ, pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ, paa iteheyi-wɛ-tʋ.

eng: The prophets, who long ago foretold the grace that youve received, searched and explored, inquiring carefully about this salvation.
local: Náyaa kosusi ñʋʋ yabʋ mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa se ɛhaɣ-mɩ yɔ pʋ-tɔm ɖooo lɔŋ. Pañɩnaa nɛ patazɩ pɩ-taa se pana.

eng: since you have tasted that the Lord is good.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɩtɛm naʋ se Kɩpaɣlʋ lɛ kɩbanʋ.

eng: After all, they will have observed the reverent and holy manner of your lives.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, panaɣ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mɩ-ɖɔnɛ wɛ ɖeu yɔ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ iseɣ Ɛsɔ yɔ.

eng: They wondered what the Spirit of Christ within them was saying when he bore witness beforehand about the suffering that would happen to Christ and the glory that would follow. They wondered what sort of person or what sort of time they were speaking about.
local: Krɩstʋ Fezuu ŋgʋ́ kɩɩwɛ pa-taa yɔ, kiiheyi-wɛ kʋñɔŋ ŋgʋ́ ɛkɔŋ tɔɔʋ yɔ nɛ koboyaɣ ŋgá ɛkɔŋ mʋ́ʋ yɔ pɔ-tɔm. Pʋ-yɔɔ pɛcɛyaɣ pa-tɩ se patɩlɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pʋkɔŋ labʋ yɔ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɩlakɩ yɔ.

eng: Now you are coming to him as to a living stone. Even though this stone was rejected by humans, from Gods perspective it is chosen, valuable.
local: Ɩkpɩtɩnɩ-ɩ. Ɛnʋ lɛ pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩhaɣ wezuu yɔ, ɛyáa kizi-ɖɩ se ɖɩɩwazɩɣ pʋyʋ. Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ ñalɩzɩ-ɖɩ se ńɖɩ́ kɩlɩnɩ wazaɣ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa.

eng: Dont try to make yourselves beautiful on the outside, with stylish hair or by wearing gold jewelry or fine clothes.
local: Ɩtaanɩɩnɩ tomnaɣ yɔɔ ajana ñɩlɩ ñɩlɩ. Ana lɛ ñɔsɩ hɔkʋʋ nɛ sika nɩŋgbacaɣlɩnaa nɛ sika lɩtʋ pɛ-lɛʋ nɛ wondu kɩbandʋ suu.

eng: It was revealed to them that in their search they were not serving themselves but you. These things, which even angels long to examine, have now been proclaimed to you by those who brought you the good news. They did this in the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven.
local: Ɛsɔ wɩlɩ-wɛ kpayɩ se tɔm ndʋ́ posusaɣ yɔ, pɩtɛkɛ pa-tɩ posusaɣ-tʋ. Ɛlɛ́, mɩ posusaɣ-tʋ. Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm susiyaa heyi-mɩ-tʋ. Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu ŋgʋ́ kɩlɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, ki-ɖoŋ paba heyini-mɩ-tʋ. Halɩ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa maɣmaɣ ñɩnaɣ se patɩlɩ-tʋ.

eng: Therefore, since Christ suffered as a human, you should also arm yourselves with his way of thinking. This is because whoever suffers is finished with sin.
local: Ɩtɛm naʋ se Krɩstʋ nɩ siziŋ e-tomnaɣ taa. Ɩmaɣzɩ pʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɩkpa ɖoŋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, weyi ɛnɩ siziŋ e-tomnaɣ taa yɔ, ɛha kɩwɛɛkɩm siɣ́ye.

eng: You yourselves are being built like living stones into a spiritual temple. You are being made into a holy priesthood to offer up spiritual sacrifices that are acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
local: Pʋwɛɛ se ɩwɛɛ ɛzɩ pɛɛ wena awɛ wezuu yɔ. Ana Ɛsɔ kpakɩɣ nɛ ɛtɩŋnɩ e-Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu yɔɔ nɛ ɛmanɩ ɛ-ɖɩɣa. Peeɖe ɩkɛɣ cɔjɔnaa mbá ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ nɛ patɩŋɩɣnɩ Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ palaɣ-ɩ kɩlaŋ weyi paanaɣ nɛ e-liu wɛɛ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: Instead, make yourselves beautiful on the inside, in your hearts, with the enduring quality of a gentle, peaceful spirit. This type of beauty is very precious in Gods eyes.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɩnɩɩnɩ mɩ-laŋa canʋʋ. Ajana ana, aacadɩɣ kaʋ. Ana lɛ mi-leleŋ nɛ mɩ-hɛɛ pɛ-wɛtʋ. Ḿbʋ́ wɛnɩ wazaɣ siŋŋ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa.

eng: Therefore, once you have your minds ready for action and you are thinking clearly, place your hope completely on the grace that will be brought to you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɩmaɣzɩ mbʋ́ ɩlakɩ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ camɩyɛ. Ɩɖɔkɩ mɩ-tɩ nɛ ɩtaa mɩ-ñʋʋ yabʋ liu siŋŋ. Ḿbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa se ɛkɔŋ-mɩ haʋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Yesu Krɩstʋ lɩzɩɣ ɛ-tɩ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ yɔ.

eng: Dont be conformed to your former desires, those that shaped you when you were ignorant. But, as obedient children.
local: Ɩña Ɛsɔ ɛzɩ piya nzɩ́ sɩñaŋ sɩ-caa yɔ. Iyele mbʋ́ mi-liu kɛwɛ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩtasɩmdɩ-ɩ yɔ.

eng: For it was in this way that holy women who trusted in God used to make themselves beautiful, accepting the authority of their own husbands.
local: Ḿbʋ́ ɖooo lɔŋ taa halaa mbá paawɛnɩ lidaʋ Ɛsɔ yɔɔ nɛ pataɣ-ɩ liu yɔ, pacanaɣ pa-tɩ: pañaŋaɣ pa-walaa.

eng: Thus it is written in scripture, Look! I am laying a cornerstone in Zion, chosen, valuable. The person who believes in him will never be shamed. .
local: Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: «Ɩnaɣ! Malɩzɩ pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩkɩlɩ wazaɣ yɔ nɛ mansɩɩ-ɖɩ Siiyɔɔ tɛtʋ taa se ɖɩkɛ pɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ. Weyi ɛwɛɣnɩ lidaʋ ɖɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛkaɣ fɛyɛ tɔɔʋ.».

eng: As a result, they dont live the rest of their human lives in ways determined by human desires but in ways determined by Gods will.
local: se ɛtaatasɩ wezuu caɣʋ ɛzɩ ɛyáa liu wɛʋ yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, ɛcaɣ-kʋ ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ liu wɛʋ yɔ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ ɛkʋyaa yɔ.

eng: you must be holy in every aspect of your lives, just as the one who called you is holy.
local: Ɛsɔ wɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ nɛ ɛ́yáa-mɩ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, mɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɩwɛɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ mɩ-lakasɩ tɩŋa taa.

eng: For example, Sarah accepted Abrahams authority when she called him master . You have become her children when you do good and dont respond to threats with fear.
local: Pa-taa lɛlʋ yɔ Saara. Ɛnɩɣaɣnɩ ɛ-walʋ Abraham nɛ ɛyaɣ-ɩ se ɛ-caa. Yee ɩlakɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ nɛ nabʋyʋ ɛɛkpaɣ-mɩ sɔɔndʋ yɔ, ɩpɩsɩ e-liɖe taa ñɩma.

eng: So God honors you who believe. For those who refuse to believe, though, the stone the builders tossed aside has become the capstone.
local: Mbá mɩ ɩwɛnɩ lidaʋ Kɩpaɣlʋ yɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩwɛ-mɩ wazaɣ siŋŋ. Piyele mbá pɛfɛyɩnɩ lidaʋ ɛ-yɔɔ yɔ, pʋwɛ-wɛ ɛzɩ pamaʋ Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa yɔ. Pamawa se: «Pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ kuduyiŋ mayaa kpaɣaɣ pɔlɔ yɔ, ńɖɩ́ pɩsɩnɩ ɖɩkɩlɩ wazaɣ atɩŋa payɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ.».

eng: It is written, You will be holy, because I am holy. .
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pama Ɛsɔtɔm Takayaɣ taa se: «Ɩtaañɔtɩnɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩm, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, maañɔtʋʋnɩ-pʋ.».

eng: Husbands, likewise, submit by living with your wife in ways that honor her, knowing that she is the weaker partner. Honor her all the more, as she is also a coheir of the gracious care of life. Do this so that your prayers wont be hindered.
local: Mɩ walaa lɛ, ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ pʋwɛnɩ-mɩ: pʋwɛɛ se paa weyi ɛ́ɖɔ́kɩ ɛ-halʋ camɩyɛ. Ɩtɩlɩ se halaa fɛyɩ ɖoŋ ɛzɩ mɩ yɔ. Ɩɖʋ-wɛ hɩɖɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, wezuu ŋgʋ́ Ɛsɔ ha-mɩ falaa yɔ, mɩ nɛ wɛ ɛha-kʋ. Yee ɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, nabʋyʋ ɛɛcɛyɩɣ-mɩ mɩ-Ɛsɔ sɛʋ taa.

eng: You have wasted enough time doing what unbelievers desire—living in their unrestrained immorality and lust, their drunkenness and excessive feasting and wild parties, and their forbidden worship of idols.
local: Đooo lɔŋ, ɩɩkpaɣ alɩwaatʋ siŋŋ nɛ ɩla ɛzɩ mbá paañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ palakʋʋ mbʋ́ pe-liu wɛɛ yɔ. Pɩmaɣ ḿbʋ́. Ɩɖɔŋaɣ ɛzaɣ́tʋ nʋmɔʋ taa nɛ ɩɖʋʋ ɛsɛ lɛlaa ñɩm yɔɔ nɛ ɩkpaŋɩɣ sʋlʋm nɛ ɩɖɛlɛsɩɣ niye tɔkɩyɛ nɛ ñɔtʋ pa-taa nɛ mɩ nɛ mɩ-taabalaa ɩñɔʋ nɛ pɩɖɛlɛsɩɣ niye nɛ iluŋuu agɔlɩma tɛɛ.

eng: Since you call upon a Father who judges all people according to their actions without favoritism, you should conduct yourselves with reverence during the time of your dwelling in a strange land.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɩsɛɣ Ɛsɔ yɔ, ɩyaɣ-ɩ se Caa. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se ɩñam-ɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩlakɩ yɔ pɩ-taa mi-wezuu caɣʋ taa kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, mɩ-Caa Ɛsɔ hʋʋnɩ ɛyáa tɩŋa payɩ mbʋ́ palaba yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ. Ɛɛcɔŋnɩ nɔɔyʋ ɛsɩndaa nɛ pʋcɔ ɛhʋʋnɩ-ɩ.

eng: This is a stone that makes people stumble and a rock that makes them fall. Because they refuse to believe in the word, they stumble. Indeed, this is the end to which they were appointed.
local: Pamawa ɖɔɖɔ se: «Ńɖɩ́ tuuluuni ɛyáa, ńɖɩ́ lɛ ɛwaɣ ŋgá kɛpɛɖɩɣ-wɛ yɔ.» Đituuli-wɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, paatisiɣ Ɛsɔtɔm. Ḿbʋ́ pɩɩtɩɩɖʋ ɖooo lɔŋ se pɩlabɩ-wɛ.

eng: Finally, all of you be of one mind, sympathetic, lovers of your fellow believers, compassionate, and modest in your opinion of yourselves.
local: Ndʋ́ mɛntɛzɩɣnɩ yɔ lɛ se: mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩwɛnɩ lɩmaɣza kʋɖʋma, ɩwɛnɩ ɖɔɖɔ hɔyɛ kʋɖʋmɖɩyɛ. Ɩsɔɔlɩ ɖama ɛzɩ Krɩstʋ ñɩma sɔɔlʋʋ ɖama yɔ. Ɩmaɣzɩ ɖama yɔɔ siŋŋ nɛ itisi mɩ-tɩ ɖama tɛɛ.

eng: They think its strange that you dont join in these activities with the same flood of unrestrained wickedness. So they slander you.
local: Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, pɩɖɩɣzɩɣ mbá paañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɩɩtaasɩɣ labʋ ɛzaɣ́tʋ nɛ ɩɖɛlɛsɩɣ niye ɛzɩ ḿbá yɔ. Pʋ-yɔɔ patʋʋ-mɩ.

eng: Live in this way, knowing that you were not liberated by perishable things like silver or gold from the empty lifestyle you inherited from your ancestors.
local: Ɩtɛm naʋ se pɩtɛkɛ mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛkɩɣ ɛzɩ liidiye yaa sika yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ patɩŋnaa nɛ paya mɩ-ñʋʋ nɛ palɩzɩ-mɩ mɩ-cɔzɔnaa lakasɩ nzɩ́ sɩɩwazɩɣ pʋyʋ yɔ sɩ-taa.

eng: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people who are Gods own possession. You have become this people so that you may speak of the wonderful acts of the one who called you out of darkness into his amazing light.
local: Piyele mɩ yɔ, mɩ lɛ liɖe nɖɩ́ Ɛsɔ lɩzaa yɔ ɖɩ-taa ñɩma. Ɩkɛ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ cɔjɔnaa. Mɩ lɛ Ɛsɔ ɛjaɖɛ taa ñɩma, mɩ-ñʋʋ ɛyaba. Mɩ ɛlɩzɩ cɩkpɛndʋʋ taa nɛ ewoni ɛ-ñalɩmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩkɩlɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ yɔ ɖɩ-taa se isusi ɛ-tʋma sɔsɔna tɔm.

eng: Dont pay back evil for evil or insult for insult. Instead, give blessing in return. You were called to do this so that you might inherit a blessing.
local: Ɩtaakpa ɖama kɩmɩyɛ. Nɔɔyʋ ɛtʋ-mɩ lɛ, ɩtaatʋʋ-ɩ. Ɛlɛ́, itendi Ɛsɔ se ɛ́wazɩ pʋdʋ yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛya-mɩ se ɩla ḿbʋ́ nɛ ɛ́wazɩ mɩ-yɔɔ ɛzɩ ɛɖʋʋ yɔ.

eng: They will have to reckon with the one who is ready to judge the living and the dead.
