Ɛnʋ tɩŋnɩnɩ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu Krɩstʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛya ɖa-ñʋʋ. Pasam-ɩ nɛ paɖʋ-ɩ hɩɖɛ. Ɛwɛnɩ ɖoŋ tɩŋa nɛ ɛcɔŋnɩ pɩtɩŋa pʋ-yɔɔ. Ḿbʋ́ pɩɩwɛ ɖooo kiɖe tɛɛ. Pʋwɛɛ ḿbʋ́ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, pʋwɛɛ ɖɔɖɔ ḿbʋ́ tam! Pɩla ḿbʋ́!.

eng: A revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show his servants what must soon take place. Christ made it known by sending it through his angel to his servant John.
local: Mbʋ́ mbʋ́ Yesu Krɩstʋ wɩlaa yɔ, pʋ-tɔm pama takayaɣ kanɛ ka-taa. Ɛsɔ wɩlɩnɩ-ɩ-pʋ se ɛwɩlɩ ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa nɛ pana mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ labʋ alɩwaatʋ pazɩ taa yɔ nɛ ɛtɩŋnɩ e-tiyiyu yɔɔ nɛ ɛwɩlɩ-pʋ ɛ-tʋmlaɖʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ.

eng: who bore witness to the word of God and to the witness of Jesus Christ, including all that John saw.
local: Ɛ́lɛ́ lɩzɩnɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛnawa yɔ pɩ-aseɣɖe: ɛlɩzɩ-ɖɩ Ɛsɔtɔm nɛ nɖɩ́ Yesu Krɩstʋ ñalɩzaa yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: Favored is the one who reads the words of this prophecy out loud, and favored are those who listen to it being read, and keep what is written in it, for the time is near.
local: Weyi ɛkalɩɣ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ nɛ pama-tʋ takayaɣ kanɛ ka-taa yɔ, ehiɣ ɛ-tɩ! Mbá paba ñewelesiɣ-tʋ nɛ pɔɖɔkʋʋ-tʋ yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, alɩwaatʋ wɩzɩ talʋʋ.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Sardis: These are the words of the one who holds Gods seven spirits and the seven stars: I know your works. You have the reputation of being alive, and you are in fact dead.
local: Ɛyʋ ɛnʋ ɛtasɩ-m heyuu se: «Ma Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛcɔŋnɩ Saardɩ Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma yɔɔ yɔ takayaɣ nɛ ŋheyi-i se: ‹Weyi ma mɛwɛnɩ Ɛsɔ feziŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-lʋbɛ nɛ tɩɩnzɩ ńzɩ́ sɩ-lʋbɛ yɔ, mɔntɔŋna se: mansɩm mbʋ́ ñɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma lakɩ yɔ: pa-hɩɖɛ sɛwa se pɛwɛ wezuu, ɛlɛ́, pasɩba.

eng: John, to the seven churches that are in Asia: Grace and peace to you from the one who is and was and is coming, and from the seven spirits that are before Gods throne.
local: Ma Yohanɛɛsɩ, mɛnsɛɣnɩ Krɩstʋ aŋgba lʋbɛ wena awɛ Aazii hɔɔlʋʋ taa yɔ a-taa ñɩma: Ɛsɔ nɛ feziŋ lʋbɛ weyi ɩwɛ e-kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ ɛsɩndaa yɔ, palabɩ-mɩ lootiye nɛ pɛhɛzɩ mɩ-laŋa. Ɛnʋ wɛna, ɛnʋ kɛwɛna, ɛnʋ kaɣnɩ kɔm.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Ephesus: These are the words of the one who holds the seven stars in his right hand and walks among the seven gold lampstands:.
local: Ɛyʋ ɛnʋ ɛtasɩ-m heyuu se: «Ma Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛcɔŋnɩ Efɛɛzɩ Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma yɔɔ yɔ takayaɣ nɛ ŋheyi-i se: ‹Weyi ma mɔnɖɔkɩ tɩɩnzɩ ńzɩ́ sɩ-lʋbɛ me-nesi kɩbanzɩ taa nɛ mɔnɖɔŋ sika kanɖʋnaa ḿbá pa-lʋbɛ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa yɔ, mɔntɔŋna se:.

eng: Wake up and strengthen whatever you have left, teetering on the brink of death, for Ive found that your works are far from complete in the eyes of my God.
local: Pefe nɛ peyele mbʋ́ pɩkazɩ-wɛ yɔ, pɩkpa ɖoŋ nɛ pɩtaatɛzɩ sɩbʋ: manawa se mbʋ́ palakɩ yɔ, mɛ-Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa lɛ, pɩtamʋna.

eng: and from Jesus Christ—the faithful witness, the firstborn from among the dead, and the ruler of the kings of the earth. To the one who loves us and freed us from our sins by his blood.
local: Yesu Krɩstʋ weyi ɛkɛ aseɣɖe tʋ weyi ɛlakɩ mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa se ɛlakɩ yɔ, ɛlabɩ-mɩ ḿbʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ. Ɛnʋ pacalɩ fezuu nɛ sɩɖaa taa, ɛkɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ awiya taa Wiyaʋ. Ɛsɔɔlɩ-ɖʋ nɛ ɛtɩŋnɩ ɛ-calɩm mbʋ́ ɛkpɛdɩ ɖɔ-yɔɔ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛlɩzɩ-ɖʋ ɖa-kɩwɛɛkɩm taa.

eng: I know your works, your labor, and your endurance. I also know that you dont put up with those who are evil. You have tested those who say they are apostles but are not, and you have found them to be liars.
local: mansɩm mbʋ́ ñɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma lakɩ yɔ nɛ pa-pana ñaɣʋ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɔɖɔkʋʋ pa-tɩ yɔ. Manawa se paacaɣ se pa nɛ kañadɩnaa pɛkpɛndɩ. Pamaɣzɩ mbá pɔtɔŋ se pɛkɛ Yesu tiyiyaa yɔ nɛ pana se pɛkɛ cɛtɩyaa.

eng: who made us a kingdom, priests to his God and Father—to him be glory and power forever and always. Amen.
local: nɛ eyele ɖɩkɛ kewiyaɣ taa ñɩma nɛ cɔjɔnaa ɛ-Caa Ɛsɔ tʋmɩyɛ taa. Ɛnʋ lɛ, ɛwɛɛ koboyaɣ taa nɛ ɛwɛɛ ɖoŋ pɩnzɩ ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ! Pɩla ḿbʋ́!.

eng: So remember what you received and heard. Hold on to it and change your hearts and lives. If you dont wake up, I will come like a thief, and you wont know what time I will come upon you.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pɔtɔzɩ tɔm ndʋ́ peheyi-wɛ yɔ nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ petisi-tʋ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ. Pɔɖɔkɩ-tʋ nɛ pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ. Yee patacaɣ pɛ-ɛsɛ yɔɔ yɔ, mɔnkɔŋ ɛzɩ mɩ́lʋ́ yɔ nɛ montudi-wɛ, paasɩŋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ mɔnkɔŋ yɔ.

eng: You have shown endurance and put up with a lot for my names sake, and you havent gotten tired.
local: Pɔɖɔkɩ pa-tɩ nɛ pala kʋñɔŋ mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pɩtɛɖɛɛ-wɛ.

eng: But you do have a few people in Sardis who havent stained their clothing. They will walk with me clothed in white because they are worthy.
local: Ŋgʋ́, nabɛyɛ wɛ mɩ-tɛ Saardɩ peeɖe nɛ petepisi po-wondu. Pɔkɔŋ suu wondu kʋhʋlʋmʋtʋ nɛ ma nɛ wɛ ɖɩɖɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pamʋnɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: But I have this against you: you have let go of the love you had at first.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ndʋ́ man-taa wɛnɩ-wɛ yɔ lɛ se: patatasɩ-m sɔɔlʋʋ ɛzɩ ɖooo paasɔɔlʋʋ-m yɔ.

eng: Look, he is coming with the clouds! Every eye will see him, including those who pierced him, and all the tribes of the earth will mourn because of him. This is so. Amen.
local: Ɩcɔnɩ ɩna, ɛkɔŋ naɣ yɔ ɛsɔmɩndʋ yɔɔ! Ɛyáa tɩŋa kɔŋ-ɩ naʋ, halɩ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ mbá paasɔbɩ ɛ-hʋyʋʋ taa kɩkpɛzɩŋ yɔ. Ajɛya kpeekpe taa ɛyáa kɔŋ wiu ɛ-yɔɔ. Toovenim, pʋkɔŋ labʋ ḿbʋ́! Pɩla ḿbʋ́!.

eng: Those who emerge victorious will wear white clothing like this. I wont scratch out their names from the scroll of life, but will declare their names in the presence of my Father and his angels.
local: ‹Weyi ɛwakɩ yɔ, ɛkɔŋ suu wondu kʋhʋlʋmʋtʋ ḿbʋ́. Maahɩzɩɣ ɛ-hɩɖɛ wezuu takayaɣ taa. Mɔnkɔŋ kaɖʋʋ man-Caa nɛ e-tiyiyaa pɛ-ɛsɩndaa se pʋdʋ kɛ ma-ñɩnʋ.

eng: After this I looked and there was a door that had been opened in heaven. The first voice that I had heard, which sounded like a trumpet, said to me, Come up here, and I will show you what must take place after this.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mɔncɔna nɛ manaɣ nɔnɔɔ nakɛyɛ ɛsɔdaa, katʋlaa nɛ manɩɣ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-m ɛzɩ pɩcalʋʋ-m yɔɔdɩnaʋ ɛzɩ pahʋlʋʋ ɛgandɩyɛ yɔ se: «Kpa cɩnɛ nɛ mawɩlɩ-ŋ mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ labʋ pʋnɛ pʋ-wayɩ yɔ.».

eng: Then I saw a scroll in the right hand of the one seated on the throne. It had writing on the front and the back, and it was sealed with seven seals.
local: Mana ɖɔɖɔ weyi ɛcaɣ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ yɔ e-nesi kɩbanzɩ taa takayaɣ nakɛyɛ, pama ka-taa nɛ ka-wayɩ nɛ pataaɖɩ-kɛ nɛ nesi ñɩɣʋ tam lʋbɛ taa.

eng: So remember the high point from which you have fallen. Change your hearts and lives and do the things you did at first. If you dont, Im coming to you. I will move your lampstand from its place if you dont change your hearts and lives.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pɔtɔzɩ ɖenɖe pucoɖi-wɛ yɔ, pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ nɛ pala mbʋ́ palakaɣ kajalaɣ taa yɔ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, mɔnkɔŋ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ mɛlɛɣ pa-kanɖʋ.

eng: I am the Alpha and the Omega, says the Lord God, the one who is and was and is coming, the Almighty.
local: Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ Đoŋ Tɩŋa Tʋ tɔŋna se: «Ma lɛ Nʋmɔɖɛyʋ nɛ Ɛɖɩɣyɛ.» Ɛnʋ wɛna, ɛnʋ kɛwɛna, ɛnʋ kaɣnɩ kɔm.

eng: At once I was in a Spirit-inspired trance and I saw a throne in heaven, and someone was seated on the throne.
local: Kpaagbaa ḿbʋ́ lɛ, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tii mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ manaɣ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ nakɛyɛ ɛsɔdaa peeɖe. Nɔɔyʋ caɣ kɔ-yɔɔ.

eng: But you have this in your favor: you hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, which I also hate.
local: Piyele mbʋ́ palaba nɛ pʋwɛ ɖeu yɔ lɛ se: paacaɣ mbʋ́ Niikolaa ñɩma lakɩ yɔ kpem ɛzɩ ma ɖɔɖɔ yɔ.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: ‹Weyi ɛwɛnɩ nɩŋgbaaŋ lɛ, ɛnɩɩ tɔm ndʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu heyiɣ Krɩstʋ aŋgba taa ñɩma yɔ!› ».

eng: I saw a powerful angel, who proclaimed in a loud voice, Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals.
local: Mana ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu ɖoŋ tʋ nɔɔyʋ, ɛpɔzaɣnɩ hola se: «Amʋnaa se ɛkpɛzɩ ɖenɖe ɖenɖe pañɩɣ nesi yɔ nɛ ekuli takayaɣ ŋ́gá».

eng: I, John, your brother who shares with you in the hardship, kingdom, and endurance that we have in Jesus, was on the island called Patmos because of the word of God and my witness about Jesus.
local: Ma mɩ-taabalʋ Yohanɛɛsɩ lɛ, ma nɛ mɩ ɖɩtɔkɩnɩ kʋñɔŋ nɛ kewiyaɣ nɛ ɖɩñakɩ pana ɖa nɛ Yesu ɖɛ-kpɛndʋʋ taa. Monsusi Ɛsɔtɔm nɛ malɩzɩ Yesu aseɣɖe yɔɔ pɛkɛzɩ-m tɛtʋ ɛjaɖɛ nɖɩ́ lɩm cɔ-ɖɩ pɩta nɛ payaɣ-ɖɩ se Patmoosi yɔ ɖɩ-taa.

eng: Then, when the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Heɣa ŋ́gá kɛkpɛzɩ nesi ñɩɣʋ lʋbɛ ñɩmbʋ nɛ pusu ɛsɔdaa ɖiɣ ɖiɣ nɛ pɩtalɩ ɛzɩ cacasɩ niidozo yɔ.

eng: The one seated there looked like jasper and carnelian, and surrounding the throne was a rainbow that looked like an emerald.
local: Pʋdʋ ñɩlɩsaɣ pelu pelu ɛzɩ ñɩɣlɩ́m pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ maɖʋ maɖʋ nɛ ɖɩwɛɛ kpʋzʋyɛɛ yɔ. Tɩŋgaɣyʋʋ nakʋyʋ kɔcɔ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ ŋ́gá nɛ kɩta. Kɩñɩlɩsɩɣ ɛzɩ ñɩɣlɩ́m pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ yuulee ɛzɩ tɩŋ hatʋ yɔ.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. I will allow those who emerge victorious to eat from the tree of life, which is in Gods paradise.
local: ‹Weyi ɛwɛnɩ nɩŋgbaaŋ lɛ, ɛnɩɩ ndʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu heyiɣ Krɩstʋ aŋgba taa ñɩma yɔ! ‹Weyi ɛwakɩ yɔ, mahaɣ-ɩ waɖɛ se ɛtɔɔ tɩʋ ŋgʋ́ kʋwɛ Ɛsɔ kaɖaɣ taa nɛ kɩhaɣ wezuu yɔ ki-pee.› ».

eng: Then I looked on as the Lamb opened one of the seven seals. I heard one of the four living creatures say in a voice like thunder, Come.
local: Mantasɩ cɔnaʋ lɛ, mana Heɣa kpɛzɩ nesi ñɩɣʋ ḿbʋ́ pʋ-lʋbɛ taa kajalaɣ ñɩmbʋ nɛ manɩɣ kpɩna ana a-naanza a-taa lɛɛɖɛ yɔɔdʋʋ ɛzɩ tɛʋ nazʋʋ yɔ se: «Kɔɔ!».

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: These are the words of the one who is holy and true, who has the key of David. Whatever he opens, no one will shut; and whatever he shuts, no one opens.
local: Ɛyʋ ɛnʋ ɛtasɩ-m heyuu se: «Ma Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛcɔŋnɩ Filaadɛlfii Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma yɔɔ yɔ takayaɣ nɛ ŋheyi-i se: ‹Weyi ma mɛnkɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩnʋ nɛ toovenim tʋ nɛ mɔnɖɔkɩ wiyaʋ sɔsɔ Daaviidi nɔnɔɔ ñɩmɩyɛ nɛ yee mantʋlaa, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛɖɩɣ nɛ yee manɖɩɣaa, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛtʋlɩ yɔ, mɔntɔŋna se:.

eng: But no one in heaven or on earth or under the earth could open the scroll or look inside it.
local: Ɛlɛ́, nɔɔyʋ fɛyɩ: paa ɛsɔdaa yaa tataa yaa tɛtɛɛ se ɛpɩzɩɣ ekuli-kɛ yaa ɛcɔnɩ ka-taa.

eng: I was in a Spirit-inspired trance on the Lords day, and I heard behind me a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
local: Peeɖe Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tibi mɔ-yɔɔ Kɩpaɣlʋ kɩyakʋ nakʋyʋ wiye nɛ manɩɣ puholuu nɛ ma-wayɩ ɖoŋ ɛzɩ pahʋlʋʋ ɛgandɩyɛ yɔ se:.

eng: Then I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and seven trumpets were given to them.
local: Mɔncɔnaa lɛ, manaɣ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa mbá pasɩŋɩɣ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa yɔ, pɛwɛ lʋbɛ. Pɛcɛlaɣaɣ-wɛ agandɛɛ lʋbɛ.

eng: Twenty-four thrones, with twenty-four elders seated upon them, surrounded the throne. The elders were dressed in white clothing and had gold crowns on their heads.
local: Kewiyaɣ kpelisi nɩɩlɛ nɛ naanza kɔcɔ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ ŋ́gá nɛ sɩta nɛ sɔsaa nɩɩlɛ nɛ naanza nabɛyɛ caɣ sɩ-yɔɔ. Posu wondu kʋhʋlʋmʋtʋ nɛ paba sika hulasi.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Smyrna: These are the words of the one who is the first and the last, who died and came back to life:.
local: Ɛyʋ ɛnʋ ɛtasɩ-m heyuu se: «Ma Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛcɔŋnɩ Smiirnɩ Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma yɔɔ yɔ takayaɣ nɛ ŋheyi-i se: ‹Weyi ma mɛnkɛ Nʋmɔɖɛyʋ nɛ Ɛɖɩɣyɛ nɛ maasɩba nɛ menfe yɔ, mɔntɔŋna se:.

eng: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth. They held back the earths four winds so that no wind would blow against the earth, the sea, or any tree.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mana Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa naanza, pasɩŋ kedeŋa hɔɔlɩŋ naanza kʋlɔmɩŋ taa. Paakpa kedeŋa yɔɔ heɣlim naanza mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ yɔ se pɩtaama tataa yaa teŋgu yɔɔ yaa tɩʋ nakʋyʋ yɔɔ.

eng: So I looked, and there was a white horse. Its rider held a bow and was given a crown. And he went forth from victory to victory.
local: Peeɖe mana kpaŋnɔ kʋhʋlʋm nɔɔyʋ. Weyi ɛcaɣ-ɩ yɔ, ɛɖɔkɩ tɔʋ nɛ mana pɛcɛlɩ-ɩ wabɩyɛ hulaɣ nɛ ɛɖɛɛ ɛzɩ waɖʋ yɔ se ɛwɛɛ wabʋ yɔɔ.

eng: I know your works. Look! I have set in front of you an open door that no one can shut. You have so little power, and yet you have kept my word and havent denied my name.
local: Mansɩm mbʋ́ ñɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma lakɩ yɔ. Na: mantʋlɩ nɔnɔɔ ŋgá nɔɔyʋ ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛɖɩɣ yɔ ñɛ-ɛsɩndaa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pɛwɛnɩ ɖoŋ pazɩ, ɛlɛ́, pɔɖɔkɩ mɔn-tɔm nɛ pɛtɛkpɛzɩ se patasɩm-m.

eng: Because of this I will make the people from Satans synagogue —I will make them come and bow down at your feet and realize that I have loved you.
local: Na! Mɔnkɔŋ ɖʋzʋʋ Satanɩ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma nabɛyɛ pe-nesi taa. Pɔtɔŋ se pɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma, ɛlɛ́, pɛtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, pɛkɛ cɛtɩyaa. Na! Mɔnkɔŋ yebu nɛ pɔkɔɔ poluŋ ñɛ-tɛɛ nɛ pɔyɔɔdɩnɩ pɔ-nɔsɩ se mɔnsɔɔlɩ-ŋ.

eng: From the throne came lightning, voices, and thunder. In front of the throne were seven flaming torches, which are the seven spirits of God.
local: Tɛʋ ñɩlɩsʋʋ nɛ kɔkɔɖɛ nɛ tɛʋ nazʋʋ palɩɣaɣnɩ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ ŋgá kɛwɛ hɛkʋ taa yɔ kɔ-yɔɔ. Pɔsɔbɩ kanɖʋnaa lʋbɛ mbá panaɣ camɩyɛ yɔ kɛ-ɛsɩndaa: ḿbá lɛ Ɛsɔ feziŋ lʋbɛ.

eng: Something like a glass sea, like crystal, was in front of the throne. In the center, by the throne, were four living creatures encircling the throne. These creatures were covered with eyes on the front and on the back.
local: Nabʋyʋ kɛwɛ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ ŋ́gá kɛ-ɛsɩndaa ɖɔɖɔ. Pʋwɛ ɛzɩ teŋgu yɔ nɛ pɩñɩlɩzɩɣ ɛzɩ ñɩñʋʋ yɔ. Pɩnɩɣzɩnɩ pɩyɛ kʋhʋlʋmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩñɩlɩzɩɣ pelu pelu yɔ. Kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ ŋ́gá, kɛ-hɛkʋ taa nɛ kɔ-cɔlɔ kpɛɛɛ lɛ, kpɩna naanza naayɛ kɛwɛɛ, awɛnɩ ɛsɛ tɔtɔ, taa nɛ wayɩ.

eng: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, Come.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, kɛkpɛzɩ nesi ñɩɣʋ naalɛ ñɩmbʋ nɛ manɩɣ kpɩna ana a-taa naalɛ ñɩnɖɛ tɔŋ se: «Kɔɔ!».

eng: Another angel came and stood at the altar, and he held a gold bowl for burning incense. He was given a large amount of incense, in order to offer it on behalf of the prayers of all the saints on the gold altar in front of the throne.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ wolo ɛsɩŋ tɩlaaɖɩ ɖɩhɔɖɛ. Ɛɖɔkɩ ñɩɣlɩ́m ñanaɣ ŋgá patɩɩhɔkɩ ka-taa tɩlaaɖɩ yɔ. Pɛcɛlɩ-ɩ tɩlaaɖɩnaa sakɩyɛ nɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩma Ɛsɔ sɛtʋ se ewoni ɛha Ɛsɔ peeɖe. Đɩhɔɖɛ ńɖɩ́, paalabɩ-ɖɩ nɛ sika nɛ ɖɩwɛɛ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ ɛsɩndaa.

eng: I know your hardship and poverty . I also know the hurtful things that have been spoken about you by those who say they are Jews .
local: mana ñɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma siziŋ nɩʋ nɛ pa-kʋñɔndʋtʋ. Ŋgʋ́, pɛkɛ ñɩm tɩnaa! Manɩ tɔm ɛzaɣ́tʋ ndʋ́ mbá pɛcɛtɩɣ se pɛkɛ Yuda ñɩma yɔ, pɔyɔɔdɩ-wɛ yɔ: pɛkɛ Satanɩ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma!.

eng: I saw another angel coming up from the east, holding the seal of the living God. He cried out with a loud voice to the four angels who had been given the power to damage the earth and sea.
local: Mana ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu lɛlʋ, ɛlɩnɩ wɩsɩ ɖɩlɩyɛ nɛ ɛkpaɣ. Ɛɖɔkɩ Ɛsɔ wezuu tʋ nesi ñɩɣʋ. Peeɖe eholuuni Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa naanza mbá paha-wɛ ɖoŋ se pɛwɛɛkɩ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ tataa nɛ teŋgu taa yɔ se:.

eng: Then I saw another powerful angel coming down from heaven. He was robed with a cloud, with a rainbow over his head. His face was like the sun, and his feet were like fiery pillars.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mantasɩ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ naʋ, ɛwɛ ɖoŋ. Ɛlɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ etiki, ehu ɛsɔmɩndʋ. Ɛ-ñʋʋ taa wɛ tɩŋgaɣyʋʋ nɛ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa wɛɛ ɛzɩ wɩsɩ yɔ nɛ ɛ-naŋgbanzɩ ñɛwɛɛ ɛzɩ miŋ sɔsɔŋ nzʋlʋmɛɛ yɔ.

eng: Because you kept my command to endure, I will keep you safe through the time of testing that is about to come over the whole world, to test those who live on earth.
local: Ɛzɩma mbʋ́ meheyi-wɛ se pɔ́ɖɔ́kɩ pa-tɩ nɛ petisi pɔ́ɖɔ́kɩ tɔm ńdʋ́ yɔ, ma ɖɔɖɔ mɔnɖɔkʋʋ-wɛ. Mɔnɖɔkʋʋ-wɛ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pʋkɔŋ takʋʋ kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ ɛyáa se pɩna pa-wayɩ yɔ.

eng: It said, Write down on a scroll whatever you see, and send it to the seven churches: to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.
local: «Ma mbʋ́ ŋnaɣ yɔ pʋ-tɔm takayaɣ nakɛyɛ taa nɛ ŋtiyini Krɩstʋ aŋgba lʋbɛ anɛ a-taa ñɩma. Ana yɔ: Efɛɛzɩ ñɩnɖɛ nɛ Smiirnɩ ñɩnɖɛ nɛ Pɛɛrgam ñɩnɖɛ nɛ Tiyaatiiri ñɩnɖɛ nɛ Saardɩ ñɩnɖɛ nɛ Filaadɛlfii ñɩnɖɛ nɛ Laodisee ñɩnɖɛ.».

eng: The first living creature was like a lion. The second living creature was like an ox. The third living creature had a face like a human being. And the fourth living creature was like an eagle in flight.
local: Kajalaɣ ñɩnɖɛ wɛ ɛzɩ tɔɔyʋʋ yɔ, naalɛ ñɩnɖɛ ñɛwɛɛ ɛzɩ neɣye yɔ, tozo ñɩnɖɛ ɛsɩndaa ñɛwɛɛ ɛzɩ ɛyʋ yɔ nɛ naza ñɩnɖɛ ñɛwɛɛ ɛzɩ keziye nɖɩ́ ɖɩɖɔŋ kʋyʋʋ yɔ.

eng: The smoke of the incense offered for the prayers of the saints rose up before God from the angels hand.
local: Peeɖe ɛhɔbɩ-kʋ nɛ ñɔsɩ ñɛyɩ e-nesi taa nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ Ɛsɔ ñɩma Ɛsɔ sɛtʋ nɛ pɩkpa Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa.

eng: Out came another horse, fiery red. Its rider was allowed to take peace from the earth so that people would kill each other. He was given a large sword.
local: Peeɖe kpaŋnɔ lɛlʋ lɩwa, ɛsɛ ɛzɩ miŋ yɔ. Weyi ɛcaɣ-ɩ yɔ, pɛcɛlɩ-ɩ sɩɣa sɔsɔɔ nakɛyɛ nɛ paha-ɩ ɖoŋ se ɛla nɛ ɛyáa ɛtaatasɩ wɛʋ laŋhɛzɩyɛ taa kedeŋa yɔɔ nɛ piyele pakʋ ɖama.

eng: Then the fifth angel blew his trumpet, and I saw a star that had fallen from heaven to earth, and he was given the key to the shaft of the abyss.
local: Kagbaanzɩ ñɩnʋ hʋlɩ ɛgandɩyɛ lɛ, mana tɩɩŋa nakɛyɛ, kɛkpɛzɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ kɔtɔlɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ. Pɛcɛlɩ-kɛ asayʋʋ pɔʋ nɔnɔɔ ñɩmɩyɛ.

eng: He said, Dont damage the earth, the sea, or the trees until we have put a seal on the foreheads of those who serve our God.
local: «Ɩtaawɛɛkɩ tataa yaa teŋgu taa yaa tɩŋ yɔɔ nabʋyʋ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ ɖɩñɩɣ nesi ɖɛ-Ɛsɔ tʋmlaɖaa kila taa yɔ.».

eng: Im coming soon. Hold on to what you have so that no one takes your crown.
local: Pɩkazɩ pazɩ, mɔnkɔŋ. Pɔɖɔkɩ mbʋ́ pɛwɛna yɔ kpam nɛ nɔɔyʋ ɛtaakɔɔ ɛlɛɣ pa-wabɩyɛ hulaɣ.

eng: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and each was covered all around and on the inside with eyes. They never rest day or night, but keep on saying, Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is coming.
local: Kpɩna ana, a-naanza aawɛnɩ keŋ loɖo loɖo. Aawɛnɩ ɛsɛ yʋʋʋ a-taa nɛ a-yɔɔ nɛ awɛɛ ateɣ hendu wɩsɩ nɛ ɖoó se: «Cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ, cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ, Ɛsɔ wɛnɩ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ. Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ Đoŋ Tɩŋa Tʋ weyi ɛɛwɛɛ yɔ, ɛnʋ wɛna, ɛkaɣ kɔm nee.».

eng: Then the angel took the incense container and filled it with fire from the altar. He threw it down to the earth, and there were thunder, voices, lightning, and an earthquake.
local: Ɛlɛ́, tiyiyu ɛnʋ ɛkpaɣ tɩlaaɖɩ ñanaɣ nɛ ekuu tɩlaaɖɩ ɖɩhɔɖɛ miŋ ɖo nɛ ɛlɔ kedeŋa yɔɔ. Peeɖe tɛʋ nazaa nɛ kitili nɛ kɩñɩlɩsɩ nɛ tɛtʋ ñamsɩ.

eng: I turned to see who was speaking to me, and when I turned, I saw seven oil lamps burning on top of seven gold stands.
local: Menkisaa se mana weyi ɛyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-m yɔ lɛ, manaɣ kanɖʋnaa nabɛyɛ lʋbɛ, palabɩ-wɛ nɛ sika.

eng: So I began to weep and weep, because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or to look inside it.
local: Peeɖe mewilaa siŋŋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, nɔɔyʋ tamʋnɩ se ɛpɩzɩɣ ekuli-kɛ yaa ɛcɔnɩ ka-taa.

eng: Then a great sign appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet and a crown of twelve stars on her head.
local: Pɩwɩlɩ kawɩlɩsɩ sɔsɔsɩ nasɩyɩ ɛsɔdaa: halʋ nɔɔyʋ, wɩsɩ huuzi ɛ-yɔɔ ɛzɩ toko esuwa yɔ nɛ ɛsɩŋ fenaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɛpa hulaɣ nakɛyɛ. Kɔ-yɔɔ nɛ tɩɩnzɩ hiu nɛ naalɛ.

eng: He held an open scroll in his hand. He put his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
local: Ɛɖɔkɩ takayaɣ cikpelaɣ nakɛyɛ e-nesi taa, kokulaa. Ɛsɩ ɛ-nɩwaŋ naŋgbaŋaɣ teŋgu yɔɔ nɛ nɩmɩyɛ ñɩŋga tɛtʋ yɔɔ.

eng: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, Come! So I looked, and there was a black horse. Its rider held a balance for weighing in his hand.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, kɛkpɛzɩ nesi ñɩɣʋ tozo ñɩmbʋ nɛ manɩɣ tozo ñɩnɖɛ tɔŋ se: «Kɔɔ!» Mɔncɔnaa lɛ, mana kpaŋnɔ kɩkpɛdʋ nɔɔyʋ. Weyi ɛcaɣ-ɩ yɔ, ɛɖɔkɩ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ kɩmaɣzʋʋ e-nesi taa.

eng: Then I was given a measuring rod, which was like a pole. And I was told, Get up and measure Gods temple, the altar, and those who worship there.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pɛcɛlɩ-m kañɩŋ́gbayaɣ́ ŋgá kɛwɛ ɛzɩ ɖaʋ kɩmaɣzʋʋ yɔ nɛ peheyi-m se: «Kʋyɩ ŋmaɣzɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa nɛ tɩlaaɖɩ ɖɩhɔɖɛ nɛ ŋkalɩ mbá poluŋuu Ɛsɔ tɛɛ ka-taa yɔ.

eng: Dont be afraid of what you are going to suffer. Look! The devil is going to throw some of you into prison in order to test you. You will suffer hardship for ten days. Be faithful even to the point of death, and I will give you the crown of life.
local: Sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-wɛ nɛ kʋñɔŋ ŋgʋ́ pɔkɔŋ tɔɔʋ yɔ. Nɩɩ se eleeu kɔŋ ñɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma nabɛyɛ ɖʋʋ salaka se ɛtakɩ-mɩ nɛ ɛna. Ɩkɔŋ nɩʋ siziŋ kɩyakɩŋ hiu. Ɩla mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ yɔ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ mɩ-sɩm wiye nɛ maha-mɩ wezuu hulaɣ.

eng: Whenever the living creatures give glory, honor, and thanks to the one seated on the throne, who lives forever and always.
local: A-naanza asaŋ weyi ɛcaɣ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ nɛ ɛwɛɛ wezuu pɩnzɩ ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ yɔ nɛ aɖʋʋ-ɩ hɩɖɛ nɛ asɛɣ-ɩ. Alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ alakɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ.

eng: In the middle of the lampstands I saw someone who looked like the Human One. He wore a robe that stretched down to his feet, and he had a gold sash around his chest.
local: Peeɖe mana nɔɔyʋ sɩŋ pɛ-hɛkʋ taa. Esu toko nɛ kitii ɛ-naŋgbanzɩ yɔɔ waaa nɛ ɛta ɛ-laŋɩyɛ taa sika tambaɖɛ.

eng: Then I heard the number of those who were sealed: one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed from every tribe of the Israelites:.
local: Peeɖe manɩ mbá pañɩɣ pe-kila taa nesi yɔ pa-ñʋʋ. Pɛkpɛndɩ kutokiŋ mɩnʋʋ nɛ nɩɩnaza nɛ naanza. Pɛwɛ Izrɛɛlɩ cejewee tɩŋa taa:.

eng: As for those who emerge victorious, I will make them pillars in the temple of my God, and they will never leave it. I will write on them the name of my God and the name of the city of my God, the New Jerusalem that comes down out of heaven from my God. I will also write on them my own new name.
local: ‹Weyi ɛwakɩ yɔ, meyeki nɛ ɛkɛ mɛ-Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa sisika, ɛɛtasɩɣ lɩʋ. Mamaɣ ɛ-yɔɔ mɛ-Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ ɛ-hɩɖɛ nɛ ɛ-tɛtʋ ñɩnɖɛ: ńdʋ́ʋ́ lɛ Yeruusalɛm kɩfalʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩlɩɣnɩ ɛsɔdaa mɛ-Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ nɛ kitiki yɔ. Mamaɣ ɛ-yɔɔ ɖɔɖɔ ma-hɩɖɛ kɩfaɖɛ.

eng: He called out with a loud voice like a lion roaring, and when he called out, the seven thunders raised their voices.
local: nɛ eholi ɖoŋ ɛzɩ tɔɔyʋʋ wiluu yɔ. Eholi ḿbʋ́ lɛ, tɛʋ nazɩ tam lʋbɛ.

eng: But dont measure the court outside the temple. Leave that out, because it has been given to the nations, and they will trample the holy city underfoot for forty-two months.
local: Ɛlɛ́, taamaɣzɩ taɣ, yebi-kɛ nɛ hɔɔlʋʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, peyebini-kɛ nɛ mbá paañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ, ḿbá kɔŋna yaɣʋ Ɛsɔ tɛtʋ naŋgbanzɩ fenasɩ nɩɩnaza nɛ naalɛ.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. Those who emerge victorious wont be hurt by the second death.
local: ‹Weyi ɛwɛnɩ nɩŋgbaaŋ lɛ, ɛnɩɩ ndʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu heyiɣ Krɩstʋ aŋgba taa ñɩma yɔ! ‹Weyi ɛwakɩ yɔ, sɩm naalɛ ñɩmbʋ ɛɛnaɣ ɛ-yɔɔ kaʋ.› ».

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Dont weep. Look! The Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has emerged victorious so that he can open the scroll and its seven seals.
local: Ɛlɛ́, sɔsaa taa lɛlʋ heyi-m se: «Taawii! Na, Yuda cejewiye taa tɔɔyʋʋ Daaviidi liɖe taa tʋ waba: ɛpɩzɩɣ ɛkpɛzɩ nesi ñɩɣʋ ḿbʋ́ pʋ-lʋbɛ nɛ ekuli takayaɣ ŋ́gá.».

eng: the twenty-four elders fall before the one seated on the throne. They worship the one who lives forever and always. They throw down their crowns before the throne and say.
local: sɔsaa ḿbá pa-nɩɩlɛ nɛ naanza paba ñoluŋuu weyi ɛwɛ wezuu pɩnzɩ ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ yɔ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa. Ɛcaɣ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ nɛ pasaŋ-ɩ nɛ pɔlɔʋ po-hulasi kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ ɛsɩndaa nɛ pɔtɔŋ se:.

eng: I heard what sounded like a voice from among the four living creatures. It said, A quart of wheat for a denarion, and three quarts of barley for a denarion, but dont damage the olive oil and the wine.
local: nɛ manɩɣ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ kpɩna ana a-naanza a-hɛkʋ taa se: «Payakɩ mɩla kiiloo kɩyakʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ tʋmɩyɛ liidiye nɛ saamɩla kiiloonaa naadozo kɩyakʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ tʋmɩyɛ liidiye ɖɔɖɔ. Piyele num nɛ sʋlʋm lɛ, taawɛɛkɩ-wɛ.».

eng: Then the seven angels who held the seven trumpets got ready to blow them.
local: Peeɖe Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa lʋbɛ mbá pɔɖɔkɩ agandɛɛ lʋbɛ yɔ, pɔñɔɔzɩ pa-tɩ se pahʋlɩ-yɛ.

eng: He opened the shaft of the abyss; and smoke rose up from the shaft, like smoke from a huge furnace. The sun and air were darkened by the smoke from the shaft.
local: Peeɖe katʋlɩ kʋ-nɔɔ nɛ kokuli nɛ ñɔsɩ nasɩyɩ lɩɣnɩ kɩ-taa. Sɩwɛ ɛzɩ momoɖe sɔsɔɖɛ naɖɩyɛ taa ñɩnzɩ yɔ nɛ sɩñɛyɩ nɛ sihuuzi wɩsɩ yɔɔ kpemm.

eng: She was pregnant, and she cried out because she was in labor, in pain from giving birth.
local: Ɛhaɣ howa nɛ ɛcaɣ lʋlʋʋ nɛ ɛnɩɣ siziŋ nɛ ɛmakɩ kubusi.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: ‹Weyi ɛwɛnɩ nɩŋgbaaŋ lɛ, ɛnɩɩ tɔm ndʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu heyiɣ Krɩstʋ aŋgba taa ñɩma yɔ!› ».

eng: His head and hair were white as white wool—like snow—and his eyes were like a fiery flame.
local: Ɛ-ñʋʋ nɛ ɛ-ñɔsɩ pahʋlʋmɩ poyi poyi ɛzɩ kpootu yɔ nɛ ɛ-ɛsɛ ɖeɣ miŋ pelu pelu ɛzɩ miŋ nzʋlʋmɩyɛ yɔ.

eng: When the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write, but I heard a voice from heaven say, Seal up what the seven thunders have said, and dont write it down.
local: Kɩnaza ḿbʋ́ lɛ, manɩɩ pɩ-taa nɛ mancaɣ se mama. Ɛlɛ́, manɩɣ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-m nɛ ɛsɔdaa se: «Mɛsɩ tɛʋ nazʋʋ lʋbɛ tɔm ńdʋ́ ña-taa, taama-tʋ.».

eng: From the tribe of Judah, twelve thousand were sealed; from the tribe of Reuben, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Gad, twelve thousand;.
local: Yuda liɖe taa lɛ, ɛyáa kutokiŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ kila taa pañɩɣ nesi. Rubɛɛnɩ ñɩnɖɛ taa kutokiŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ, Gaadɩ ñɩnɖɛ taa kutokiŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Pergamum: These are the words of the one who has the sharp, two-edged sword:.
local: Ɛyʋ ɛnʋ ɛtasɩ-m heyuu se: «Ma Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛcɔŋnɩ Pɛɛrgam Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma yɔɔ yɔ takayaɣ nɛ ŋheyi-i se: ‹Weyi ma mɛwɛnɩ sɩɣa ŋgá kɔ-nɔɔ taa cʋlʋtaa nɛ kapɩɣ cenje yɔ, mɔntɔŋna se:.

eng: And I will allow my two witnesses to prophesy for one thousand two hundred sixty days, wearing mourning clothes.
local: Meyeki nɛ ma-aseɣɖe tɩnaa naalɛ suu kʋñɔŋ wondu nɛ pɛkɛ náyaa kɩyakɩŋ kutoku nɛ nasɩlɛ nɛ nɩɩnʋwa nɛ hiu.».

eng: You are worthy, our Lord and God, to receive glory and honor and power, because you created all things. It is by your will that they existed and were created.
local: «Hayɩ Kɩpaɣlʋ yee, ɖɛ-Ɛsɔ, ŋmʋnaa se ña-hɩɖɛ ɛsɛɛ, paɖʋ-ŋ pɩ-taa yee pɛcɛlɩ-ŋ ɖoŋ nee. Ñalabɩna pɩtɩŋa payɩ ñe-liu wɛna nɛ pala pɩtɩŋa nɛ pʋwɛɛ.».

eng: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, Come.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, kɛkpɛzɩ nesi ñɩɣʋ naanza ñɩmbʋ nɛ manɩɣ kpɩnɛ naanza ñɩnɖɛ ñɔtɔŋ se: «Kɔɔ!».

eng: The first angel blew his trumpet, and hail and fire mixed with blood appeared, and was thrown down to the earth. A third of the earth was burned up. A third of the trees were burned up. All the green grass was burned up.
local: Ɛsɔ tiyiyu kajalaɣ ñɩnʋ hʋlɩ ɛgandɩyɛ lɛ, pɔlɔ ɖɔmbɛɛ nɛ miŋ kedeŋa yɔɔ, pisidini calɩm. Pʋtɔlʋʋ yɔ, yee pɩtayɩ tɛtʋ tam naadozo yɔ, pɩñaɣ hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ. Yee pɩtayɩ ɖɔɖɔ tɩŋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pɩñaɣ hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ, pɩñaɣ ɖɔɖɔ ñɩtʋ lɛŋ ñɩndʋ tɩŋa payɩ.

eng: Then locusts came forth from the smoke and onto the earth. They were given power like the power that scorpions have on the earth.
local: Peeɖe kudoloŋ nakʋyʋ lɩnɩ sɩ-taa nɛ kusu kedeŋa yɔɔ. Paha-kʋ ɖoŋ ɛzɩ pɩcasɩ yɔ.

eng: Then, in between the throne and the four living creatures and among the elders, I saw a Lamb, standing as if it had been slain. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are Gods seven spirits, sent out into the whole earth.
local: Peeɖe mana ɖɔɖɔ Heɣa nakɛyɛ sɩŋ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ nɛ kpɩna ana a-naanza nɛ sɔsaa pɛ-hɛkʋ taa. Kɛwɛ ɛzɩ pala-kɛ yɔ. Kɔñɔ hɩŋ lʋbɛ nɛ kɛwɛnɩ ɛsɛ lʋbɛ. Ana lɛ Ɛsɔ feziŋ lʋbɛ weyi etiyi-i kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Laodicea: These are the words of the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the ruler of Gods creation.
local: Ɛyʋ ɛnʋ ɛtasɩ-m heyuu se: «Ma Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛcɔŋnɩ Laodisee Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma yɔɔ yɔ takayaɣ nɛ ŋheyi-i se: ‹Weyi ma payaɣ se Pɩla Ḿbʋ́ nɛ mɛnkɛ aseɣɖe tʋ weyi ɛlakɩ mbʋ́ pʋpɔzʋʋ yɔ nɛ ɛtɛ ɛkɛ toovenim tʋ yɔ, weyi ma mɛnkɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ Ɛsɔ laba yɔ pi-kiɖe yɔ, mɔntɔŋna se:.

eng: His feet were like fine brass that has been purified in a furnace, and his voice sounded like rushing water.
local: Ɛ-naŋgbanzɩ wɛ ɛzɩ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ kazɩnzɩŋa ñɩŋgʋ ŋgʋ́ paɖʋ miŋ taa nɛ kɩsɛɛ yɔ nɛ panɩɣ ɛ-nɔɔ ɛzɩ lɩm sɔsɔm falʋʋ yɔ.

eng: Then the angel I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven.
local: Peeɖe Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi mana ɛsɩŋ teŋgu nɛ tɛtʋ pɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛkpazɩ e-nesi kɩbanzɩ nɛ Ɛsɔ.

eng: I know that you are living right where Satans throne is. You are holding on to my name, and you didnt break faith with me even at the time that Antipas, my faithful witness, was killed among you, where Satan lives.
local: Mansɩm ɖenɖe ñɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma wɛɛ yɔ: peeɖe Satanɩ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ wɛɛ. Pɔɖɔkɩ ma-hɩɖɛ kpam nɛ pɔtɔlɔ lidaʋ ŋgʋ́ pɛwɛnɩ mɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, halɩ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ kɩyakɩŋ weyi ɩ-taa pakʋ ma-aseɣɖe tʋ Antiipaa yɔ. Ɛlakaɣ mbʋ́ ɛɖʋwa yɔ. Đenɖe Satanɩ wɛɛ yɔ, peeɖe pɩtɩŋa payɩ pɩɩlaba ḿbʋ́.

eng: These are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand before the Lord of the earth.
local: Ḿbá lɛ somiŋ naalɛ nɛ kanɖʋnaa naalɛ mbá pasɩŋ kedeŋa yɔɔ Kɩpaɣlʋ ɛsɩndaa yɔ.

eng: Then another sign appeared in heaven: it was a great fiery red dragon, with seven heads and ten horns, and seven royal crowns on his heads.
local: Ɩna! Pɩwɩlɩ ɖɔɖɔ kawɩlɩsɩ lɛɛsɩ ɛsɔdaa: coojo sɔsɔ nɔɔyʋ, ɛsɛ cɩɩɩ ɛzɩ miŋ yɔ. Ɛwɛnɩ ñʋŋ lʋbɛ nɛ hɩŋ hiu nɛ ɛpa hulaɣ paa ɛ-ñʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩ-taa.

eng: So I looked, and there was a pale green horse. Its riders name was Death, and the Grave was following right behind. They were given authority over a fourth of the earth, to kill by sword, famine, disease, and the wild animals of the earth.
local: Mɔncɔnaa lɛ, mana kpaŋnɔ nɔɔyʋ, epiyaa ɛzɩ tɩŋ hatʋ yɔ. Weyi ɛcaɣ-ɩ yɔ, payaɣ-ɩ se Sɩm nɛ ɖenɖe sɩɖaa wɛɛ yɔ pɩtɩŋɩɣ ɛ-wayɩ. Patayɩ kedeŋa tam naanza yɔ, paha-wɛ hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ se pakʋ kɩ-taa ɛyáa. Pakʋʋ-wɛ nɛ sɩɣa nɛ ñɔɔsɩ nɛ kʋdɔmɩŋ nɛ tɛtɛ kpɩna wena awɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: from the tribe of Asher, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Naphtali, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Manasseh, twelve thousand;.
local: Asɛɛrɩ ñɩnɖɛ taa kutokiŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ, Nɛftalii ñɩnɖɛ taa kutokiŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ, Manaasee ñɩnɖɛ taa kutokiŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ.

eng: Then the second angel blew his trumpet, and something like a huge mountain burning with fire was thrown down into the sea. A third of the sea became blood.
local: Naalɛ ñɩnʋ ñahʋlɩ ɛ-ñɩnɖɛ lɛ, pɩsɛlɩ nabʋyʋ, pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pʋʋ sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ yɔ nɛ pʋlɔ teŋgu taa, pɩɖaɣnɩ mʋ́ʋ miŋ. Yee pɩtayɩ teŋgu tam naadozo yɔ, hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ taa lɩm pɩsɩ calɩm.

eng: They were told not to hurt the grass of the earth or any green plant or any tree. They could only hurt the people who didnt have the seal of God on their foreheads.
local: Peheyi-kʋ se kɩtaatukuni kedeŋa yɔɔ ñɩyʋʋ nakʋyʋ yaa tɩʋ nakʋyʋ yaa mbʋ́ pʋñɔwa yɔ, pɩ-taa nabʋyʋ. Ɛlɛ́, kɩ-ɛsɩyɛ ɛsɛɛnɩ mbá pe-kila taa fɛyɩ Ɛsɔ nesi ñɩɣʋ yɔ.

eng: He came forward and took the scroll from the right hand of the one seated on the throne.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Heɣa ŋ́gá kowolo kamʋ takayaɣ weyi ɛcaɣ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ yɔ e-nesi kɩbanzɩ taa.

eng: He swore by the one who lives forever and always, who created heaven and what is in it, the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it, and said, The time is up.
local: nɛ eɖuuni weyi ɛwɛ wezuu pɩnzɩ ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ yɔ. Ɛnʋ labɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pʋwɛ kɩ-taa yɔ nɛ tɛtʋ nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pʋwɛ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ nɛ teŋgu nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pʋwɛ kɩ-taa yɔ. Eɖuwaa se: «Paatasɩɣ pʋyʋ ɖaŋʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: But I have a few things against you, because you have some there who follow Balaams teaching. Balaam had taught Balak to trip up the Israelites so that they would eat food sacrificed to idols and commit sexual immorality.
local: Ɛlɛ́, man-taa wɛnɩ-wɛ natʋyʋ: pɛ-tɛ ɛyáa nabɛyɛ tisiɣ tɔm ndʋ́ Balaam wɩlaɣ yɔ. Balaam wɩlaɣ Balaakɩ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ɛɛla nɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma tɔɔ kɩlaʋ nandʋ nɛ pala acɛyɛ nɛ piyele pɔtɔlɩ Ɛsɔ nʋmɔʋ taa yɔ.

eng: He held seven stars in his right hand, and from his mouth came a sharp, two-edged sword. His appearance was like the sun shining with all its power.
local: Ɛɖɔkɩ tɩɩnzɩ lʋbɛ e-nesi kɩbanzɩ taa nɛ ɛ-nɔɔ taa lɩɣ sɩɣa ŋgá kɔ-nɔɔ taa cʋlʋtaa nɛ kapɩɣ cenje yɔ. Ɛ-ɛsɩndaa ñɩlɩsaɣ ɛzɩ wɩsɩ ñakʋʋ ɖoŋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ sisiɣa yɔ.

eng: His tail swept down a third of heavens stars and threw them to the earth. The dragon stood in front of the woman who was about to give birth so that when she gave birth, he might devour her child.
local: E-suu kahazɩ tɩɩnzɩ nasɩyɩ nɛ kʋlɔ tataa. Yee patayɩ tɩɩnzɩ tɩŋa tam naadozo yɔ, pɩmakɩnɩ hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ. Coojo ɛnʋ, ɛsɩŋ halʋ weyi ɛcaɣ lʋlʋʋ yɔ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa se ɛlʋlɩ ɛ-pɩɣa lɛ, ɛtɩŋ ɛsalɩ-kɛ.

eng: I know your works. You are neither cold nor hot. I wish that you were either cold or hot.
local: mansɩm mbʋ́ ñɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma lakɩ yɔ: pɛfɛyɩ niŋkaɣ, pɛfɛyɩ ɖɔɖɔ miŋ. Yee paatɩɩwɛ niŋkaɣ yaa miŋ yɔ!.

eng: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar those who had been slaughtered on account of the word of God and the witness they had given.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Heɣa ŋ́gá kɛkpɛzɩ nesi ñɩɣʋ kagbaanzɩ ñɩmbʋ nɛ manaɣ lone nɖɩ́ palaɣ ɖɩ-taa yɔ, ɖɩ-tɛɛ mbá paakʋ-wɛ yɔ pa-ciyamnaa. Ɛsɔtɔm nɛ aseɣɖe nɖɩ́ palɩzaa yɔ, pɔ-yɔɔ paakʋ-wɛ.

eng: If anyone wants to hurt them, fire comes out of their mouth and burns up their enemies. So if anyone wants to hurt them, they have to be killed in this way.
local: Yee po-koyindinaa ɛcaɣ se palabɩ-wɛ kɩdɛkɛdɩm nabʋyʋ yɔ, miŋ nɩɩyɩ lɩɣnɩ pɔ-nɔsɩ taa nɛ enezi-wɛ. Toovenim lɛ, yee nɔɔyʋ ɛcaɣ se ɛlabɩ-wɛ kɩdɛkɛdɩm yɔ, pʋwɛɛ se pʋkʋ-ɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: from the tribe of Simeon, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Levi, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Issachar, twelve thousand;.
local: Siimeyɔɔ ñɩnɖɛ taa kutokiŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ, Leevi ñɩnɖɛ taa kutokiŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ, Isakaarɩ ñɩnɖɛ taa kutokiŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ.

eng: a third of the creatures living in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
local: Yee pɩtayɩ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ Ɛsɔ laba nɛ pʋwɛɛ wezuu teŋgu taa yɔ tam naadozo yɔ, hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ sɩba. Yee pekpeɣli mɛlɛnaa nɛ pɩtayɩ-wɛ ɖɔɖɔ tam naadozo yɔ, pʋwɛɛkɩ hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ.

eng: The locusts werent allowed to kill them, but only to make them suffer for five months—and the suffering they inflict is like that of a scorpion when it strikes a person.
local: Peheyi-kʋ se kɩtaakʋ-wɛ, ɛlɛ́, kɩnazɩ-wɛ fenasɩ kagbaanzɩ. Nazʋʋ mbʋ́ kɩnazɩɣ-wɛ yɔ, pʋwɛ ɛzɩ pɩcaka yakʋʋ ɛyʋ nɛ ɛnɩɣ siziŋ yɔ.

eng: When he took the scroll, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb. Each held a harp and gold bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints.
local: Kamʋ-kɛ lɛ, kpɩna ana a-naanza nɛ sɔsaa ḿbá pa-nɩɩlɛ nɛ naanza poluŋ kɛ-ɛsɩndaa. Paa weyi ɛɖɔkɩ cɩmʋʋ nɛ sika caɖasɩ, sɩ-taa nɛ tɩlaaɖɩnaa mbá pɔhɔkɩ yɔ ɖo. Ḿbá lɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩma Ɛsɔ sɛtʋ.

eng: In the days when the seventh angel blows his trumpet, Gods mysterious purpose will be accomplished, fulfilling the good news he gave to his servants the prophets.
local: Ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu lʋbɛ ñɩnʋ hʋlʋʋ ɛ-ɛgandɩyɛ yɔ, mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ɖʋwa nɛ pʋwɛɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ, pɩtɛzɩɣ labʋ. Ḿbʋ́ eeheyi ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa mbá pɛkɛ náyaa yɔ.».

eng: In the same way, you have some who follow the Nicolaitans teaching.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, nabɛyɛ wɛɛ nɛ petisiɣ tɔm ndʋ́ Niikolaa ñɩma wɩlɩɣ yɔ ɖɔɖɔ ḿbʋ́.

eng: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead man. But he put his right hand on me and said, Dont be afraid. Im the first and the last.
local: Mana-ɩ lɛ, mɔntɔlɩ ɛ-tɛɛ ɛzɩ mansɩba yɔ. Peeɖe ɛtɩnɩ e-nesi kɩbanzɩ mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Sɔɔndʋ ɛtaakpa-ŋ! Ma lɛ Nʋmɔɖɛyʋ nɛ Ɛɖɩɣyɛ.

eng: She gave birth to a son, a male child who is to rule all the nations with an iron rod. Her child was snatched up to God and his throne.
local: Peeɖe halʋ ɛnʋ ɛlʋlɩ abalɩbɩɣa nɛ Ɛsɔ cobi-kɛ nɛ ɛkpazɩ ɛ nɛ e-kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ pɔ-cɔlɔ. Ŋ́gá kɔŋnɩ ɖiyuu ajɛya kpeekpe taa ñɩma nɛ awiya nɛ ɖaʋ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ ñɩŋgʋ.

eng: So because you are lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, Im about to spit you out of my mouth.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ ɖenɖe pɛwɛ hɛkʋ nɛ hɛkʋ, pɛfɛyɩ niŋkaɣ yaa pɛfɛyɩ miŋ yɔ, mɔntɔʋ-wɛ nɛ mɔ-nɔɔ.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice, Holy and true Master, how long will you wait before you pass judgment? How long before you require justice for our blood, which was shed by those who live on earth.
local: Pa-ciyamnaa makaɣ kubusi ɖoŋ se: «Hayɩ Caa cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ tʋ nɛ toovenim tʋ! Đooye ŋcaɣ se ŋkʋyɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ ñɩma mbá pakʋ-ɖʋ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ŋhʋʋnɩ-wɛ nɛ ŋhɔ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ».

eng: They have the power to close up the sky so that no rain will fall for as long as they prophesy. They also have power over the waters, to turn them into blood, and to strike the earth with any plague, as often as they wish.
local: Náyaa ḿbá, pɛwɛnɩ ɖoŋ se paɖɩɣ ɛsɔdaa nɛ tɛʋ ɛtaanɩɩ kɩyakɩŋ weyi ɩ-taa posusuu pɔ-tɔm yɔ. Pɛwɛnɩ ɖoŋ ɖɔɖɔ se papɩsɩ lɩm calɩm nɛ peyele kʋñɔmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ tɩŋa sʋʋ kedeŋa yɔɔ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɩmaɣ-wɛ yɔ.

eng: from the tribe of Zebulun, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Joseph, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Benjamin, twelve thousand were sealed.
local: Zabulɔɔ ñɩnɖɛ taa kutokiŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ, Yozɛɛfʋ ñɩnɖɛ taa kutokiŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ, Bɛnjamɛɛ ñɩnɖɛ taa ɖɔɖɔ kutokiŋ hiu nɛ naalɛ.

eng: Then the third angel blew his trumpet, and a great star, burning like a torch, fell from heaven. It fell on a third of the rivers and springs of water.
local: Naadozo ñɩnʋ hʋlɩ ɛ-ñɩnɖɛ lɛ, tɩɩŋa sɔsɔɔ nakɛyɛ kpɛzɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ miŋ ɛzɩ kooloŋa yɔ nɛ kɔtɔlɩ. Yee pɩtayɩ pɔsɩ nɛ hila tam naadozo yɔ, kɔtɔlɩ hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ yɔɔ.

eng: In those days people will seek death, but they wont find it. They will want to die, but death will run away from them.
local: Kɩyakɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-taa lɛ, ɛyáa kɔŋ ñɩnʋʋ sɩm, ɛlɛ́, paanaɣ-pʋ kpem. Pe-liu wɛɣ se pasɩ, ɛlɛ́, sɩm seɣ-wɛ nɛ puwolo poliŋ.

eng: They took up a new song, saying, You are worthy to take the scroll and open its seals, because you were slain, and by your blood you purchased for God persons from every tribe, language, people, and nation.
local: Peteɣaɣ hendu kɩfatʋ natʋyʋ. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Heɣa, ŋmʋnaa se ŋmʋ takayaɣ nee. Ŋmʋnaa se ŋkpɛzɩ ɖenɖe pataaɖaa yɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ña Ɛsɔ yeba nɛ palaa nɛ pɩtɩŋnɩ ña-calɩm yɔɔ nɛ ŋya ɛyáa ñʋʋ cejewee tɩŋa taa, kʋnʋmɩŋ tɩŋa taa, samaɣ tɩŋa taa, ajɛya tɩŋa taa nɛ ŋwoni-wɛ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ.

eng: Then the voice I heard from heaven spoke to me again and said, Go, take the opened scroll from the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, nɔɔ ŋgá kaayɔɔdɩnɩ-m nɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, katasɩ-m yɔɔdɩnaʋ se: «Wolo ŋmʋ takayaɣ cikpelaɣ ŋgá kokulaa yɔ, Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛsɩŋ teŋgu nɛ tɛtʋ pɔ-yɔɔ yɔ e-nesi taa.».

eng: and I saw a beast coming up out of the sea. It had ten horns and seven heads. Each of its horns was decorated with a royal crown, and on its heads were blasphemous names.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mana kpɩnɛ naɖɩyɛ, ɖɩlɩɣaɣnɩ teŋgu taa. Đɩñɔ hɩŋ hiu nɛ ɖɩwɛnɩ ñʋŋ lʋbɛ. Paa hɩlʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kʋ-nɔɔ taa nɛ awiya hulaɣ, pama paa ñʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kʋ-yɔɔ hɩla wena atʋʋ Ɛsɔ yɔ.

eng: So change your hearts and lives. If you dont, I am coming to you soon, and I will make war on them with the sword that comes from my mouth.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ. Pɩtɛkɛ ḿbʋ́, mɔnkɔŋ pɛ-tɛ kɩyakɩŋ ɩnɛ ɩ-taa nɛ moyoni-wɛ nɛ sɩɣa ŋgá kalɩɣnɩ mɔ-nɔɔ taa yɔ.

eng: and the living one. I was dead, but look! Now Im alive forever and always. I have the keys of Death and the Grave.
local: Ma lɛ weyi ɛwɛ wezuu yɔ. Maasɩba, ɛlɛ́, ma yɔ, mɛwɛɣnɩ mɛ-ɛsɛ tam. Mɔnɖɔkɩ sɩm nɛ ɖenɖe sɩɖaa wɛɛ yɔ pɔ-nɔnɔɔ ñɩma.

eng: Then the woman fled into the desert, where God has prepared a place for her. There she will be taken care of for one thousand two hundred sixty days.
local: Ɛlɛ́, halʋ ɛnʋ ese nɛ ɛsʋʋ lakʋ taa ɖenɖe Ɛsɔ kɔñɔɔzɩ-ɩ ɖɩjaɣyɛ se pɔcɔnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ kɩyakɩŋ kutoku nɛ nasɩlɛ nɛ nɩɩnʋwa nɛ hiu yɔ.

eng: After all, you say, Im rich, and Ive grown wealthy, and I dont need a thing. You dont realize that you are miserable, pathetic, poor, blind, and naked.
local: Pɔtɔŋ se pɛkɛ ñɩm tɩnaa, pehiɣ ñɩm nɛ nabʋyʋ ɛɛtɛɖɩɣ-wɛ. Ŋgʋ́, patatɩlɩ se pɛwɛ kɛtɛɖaɣ taa nɛ pɛwɛɛ pʋtɔdɩyɛ nɛ pɛkɛ kʋñɔndɩnaa nɛ payʋlʋmɩ nɛ pɛwɛɛ tombolo.

eng: Each of them was given a white robe, and they were told to rest a little longer, until their fellow servants and brothers and sisters—who were about to be killed as they were—were finished.
local: Peeɖe pɛcɛlɩ paa pa-taa weyi toko kʋhʋlʋmʋʋ kitibuu waaa. Ɛlɛ́, peheyi-wɛ se pɛwɛɛ pe-ɖeɖe nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɛtɛzɩ kʋʋ pa-taabalaa nɛ mbá pa nɛ wɛ palakɩ tʋmɩyɛ, nɛ pɩɩwɛɛ se pakʋ-wɛ ɛzɩ ḿbá yɔ.

eng: When they have finished their witnessing, the beast that comes up from the abyss will make war on them, gain victory over them, and kill them.
local: Pɔkɔŋ pɛtɛ pa-aseɣɖe lɩzʋʋ lɛ, kpɩnɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩlɩɣnɩ asayʋʋ pɔʋ taa yɔ, ɖiyooni-wɛ nɛ ɖɩwabɩ-wɛ nɛ ɖɩkʋ-wɛ pasɩ.

eng: After this I looked, and there was a great crowd that no one could number. They were from every nation, tribe, people, and language. They were standing before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands.
local: Pʋwayɩ mantasɩ cɔnaʋ lɛ, manaɣ ɛyáa samaɣ nakɛyɛ, kɔɖɔwa pɩtɩɩfɛyɩ, kɛfɛyɩ kalʋʋ: ajɛya kpeekpe nɛ cejewee tɩŋa taa ɛyáa nɛ samaɣ tɩŋa nɛ kʋnʋmɩŋ tɩŋa. Pasɩŋ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ nɛ Heɣa pɛ-ɛsɩndaa. Posu tokonaa kʋhʋlʋmaa waa waa nɛ pɔ́ɖɔ́kɩ paatʋ pe-nesi taa.

eng: The stars name is Wormwood, and a third of the waters became wormwood, and many people died from the water, because it became so bitter.
local: Payaɣ-kɛ se: «Sɔtʋ ndʋ́ tɩwɛ ñaŋ yɔ». Yee pɩtayɩ pɔsɩ tam naadozo yɔ, hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ taa lɩm wɛ ñaŋ nɛ mbá pɔñɔ-pʋ yɔ, pa-taa sakɩyɛ sɩba.

eng: The locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads were what seemed to be gold crowns. Their faces were like human faces.
local: Kutoloŋ ŋ́gʋ́, kɩnɩɣzɩnɩ kpaŋnaŋ weyi pɔñɔɔzɩ-ɩ se ɩɖɛɛ you yɔ. Nabʋyʋ kɛwɛ kʋ-ñʋŋ taa ɛzɩ sika hulasi yɔ nɛ kɩ-ɛsɩndaa wɛɛ ɛzɩ ɛyáa yɔ.

eng: You made them a kingdom and priests to our God, and they will rule on earth.
local: Ŋyeba nɛ pɛkɛ kewiyaɣ taa ñɩma. Ŋyeba nɛ pɛkɛ cɔjɔnaa ɖɛ-Ɛsɔ tʋmɩyɛ taa. Pɔkɔŋ tɔɔʋ kewiyaɣ tataa.».

eng: So I went to the angel and told him to give me the scroll. He said to me, Take it and eat it. It will make you sick to your stomach, but sweet as honey in your mouth.
local: Peeɖe mowobi ɛ-cɔlɔ nɛ meheyi-i se ɛcɛlɩ-m takayaɣ cikpelaɣ ŋ́gá. Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-m se: «Mʋ-kɛ nɛ ŋtɔɔ: keyeki nɛ ño-lotu taa wɛɛ ñaŋ. Ɛlɛ́, ñɔ-nɔɔ taa wɛɣ leleŋ ɛzɩ tʋŋ num yɔ.».

eng: The beast I saw was like a leopard. Its feet were like a bears, and its mouth was like a lions mouth. The dragon gave it his power, throne, and great authority.
local: Kpɩnɛ nɖɩ́ manawa yɔ, ɖɩwɛ ɛzɩ kpou yɔ. Đɩ-naŋgbanzɩ wɛ ɛzɩ kpemiɣa ñɩnzɩ yɔ nɛ ɖɩ-nɔɔ ñɛwɛɛ ɛzɩ tɔɔyʋʋ ñɩŋga yɔ. Coojo ha-ɖɩ e-ɖoŋ nɛ ɛ-abalɩtʋ sɔsɔtʋ nɛ ɛcɛlɩ-ɖɩ e-kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. I will give those who emerge victorious some of the hidden manna to eat. I will also give to each of them a white stone with a new name written on it, which no one knows except the one who receives it.
local: ‹Weyi ɛwɛnɩ nɩŋgbaaŋ lɛ, ɛnɩɩ tɔm ndʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu heyiɣ Krɩstʋ aŋgba taa ñɩma yɔ! ‹Weyi ɛwakɩ yɔ, mahaɣ-ɩ tɔɔnaɣ ŋgá kalɩɣnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ payaɣ-kɛ se maanɩ yɔ. Mahaɣ-ɩ ɖɔɖɔ pɩyɛ kʋhʋlʋmɩyɛ. Pama ɖɩ-yɔɔ hɩɖɛ kɩfaɖɛ, nɔɔyʋ tasɩm-ɖɩ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ weyi pɛcɛlɩɣ-ɩ-ɖɩ yɔ.› ».

eng: So write down what you have seen, both the scene now before you and the things that are about to unfold after this.
local: Yee ḿbʋ́ ma mbʋ́ ŋnawa yɔ pʋ-tɔm. Ḿbʋ́ lɛ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ pʋkɔŋ labʋ pʋwayɩ yɔ.

eng: Then there was war in heaven: Michael and his angels fought the dragon. The dragon and his angels fought back.
local: Peeɖe you nakʋyʋ pazɩ ɛsɔdaa. Miicɛɛlɩ nɛ e-tiyiyaa poyouni coojo nɛ coojo nɛ ɛ́lɛ́ ñɩma paba ñoyouni-wɛ.

eng: My advice is that you buy gold from me that has been purified by fire so that you may be rich, and white clothing to wear so that your nakedness wont be shamefully exposed, and ointment to put on your eyes so that you may see.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ mantasɩɣ-wɛ tɔm se pɔkɔɔ mɔn-cɔlɔ nɛ paya sika ŋgá pɔhɔbɩ-kɛ miŋ nɛ kañɩlɩsɩɣ camɩyɛ yɔ nɛ papɩsɩ ñɩm tɩnaa. Pɔkɔɔ paya wondu kʋhʋlʋmʋtʋ nɛ posuu nɛ pataawɛɛ tombolo nɛ pana pɛ-fɛyɛ. Pɔkɔɔ paya ɖɔɖɔ kɔyɛ nɛ pelizi pɛ-ɛsɛ taa nɛ papɩzɩ pana.

eng: I looked on as he opened the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake. The sun became black as funeral clothing, and the entire moon turned red as blood.
local: Mantasɩ cɔnaʋ lɛ, mana kɛkpɛzɩ nesi ñɩɣʋ loɖo ñɩmbʋ, peeɖe tɛtʋ ñamsɩ siŋŋ nɛ wɩsɩ piyi ɛzɩ kʋñɔŋ wondu yɔ nɛ fenaɣ sɛɛ katɩŋa payɩ ɛzɩ calɩm yɔ.

eng: Their dead bodies will lie on the street of the great city that is spiritually called Sodom and Egypt, where also their Lord was crucified.
local: Po-tomnasɩ wɛɣ tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ ndʋ́ pamaɣzɩnɩ Sodom nɛ Ejiptɩ nɛ payaa-tʋ ḿbʋ́ yɔ tɩ-habɛɛ yɔɔ. Peeɖe pakam pa-Kɩpaɣlʋ kaamaŋkaɣ yɔɔ.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice: Victory belongs to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb.
local: nɛ poholuu ɖoŋ se: «Đɛ-Ɛsɔ weyi ɛcaɣ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ yɔ nɛ Heɣa pɔ-cɔlɔ ñʋʋ yabʋ lɩɣna!».

eng: Then the fourth angel blew his trumpet, and a third of the sun was struck, and a third of the moon, and a third of the stars so that a third of them became dark. The day lost a third of its light, and the night lost a third of its light too.
local: Tiyiyu naanza ñɩnʋ ñahʋlɩ ɛ-ñɩnɖɛ lɛ, yee pɩtayɩ wɩsɩ tam naadozo yɔ, pʋtʋʋ hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ. Yee pɩtayɩ fenaɣ nɛ tɩɩnzɩ ḿbʋ́ yɔ, pʋtʋʋ ɖɔɖɔ hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ. Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́ se ñalɩmɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ evemiye taa yɔ, yee pɩtayɩ-ɖɩ tam naadozo yɔ, hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ pɩsɩ cɩkpɛndʋʋ nɛ ɖoó taa ñɩnɖɛ ɖɔɖɔ ḿbʋ́.

eng: their hair was like womens hair, and their teeth were like lions teeth.
local: Kʋ-ñɔsɩ wɛ ɛzɩ halaa ñɔsɩ yɔ nɛ ki-kela ñɛwɛɛ ɛzɩ tɔɔyɩŋ ñɩna yɔ.

eng: Then I looked, and I heard the sound of many angels surrounding the throne, the living creatures, and the elders. They numbered in the millions—thousands upon thousands.
local: Mɔncɔnaa lɛ, mana ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa sakɩyɛ nɛ manɩɩ pɔ-nɔɔ. Pɔcɔ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ nɛ kpɩna nɛ sɔsaa pata. Pɔɖɔwa pɩfɛyɩ kalʋʋ, pɛwɛ kutokiŋ ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ.

eng: So I took the scroll from the angels hand and ate it. And it was sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I swallowed it, it made my stomach churn.
local: Peeɖe mamʋ-kɛ e-nesi taa mɔntɔɔ nɛ mɔ-nɔɔ taa wɛɛ leleŋ ɛzɩ tʋŋ num yɔ. Melibi-kɛ lɛ, mo-lotu taa kpa ñaŋ.

eng: One of its heads appeared to have been slain and killed, but its deadly wound was healed. So the whole earth was amazed and followed the beast.
local: Ɛlɛ́, mana ɖɩ-ñʋʋ lɛɛkʋ, pʋkʋ-kʋ sɩm yɔɔ sɩm yɔɔ nɛ pʋwaa. Ɛyáa tɩŋa na-ɖɩ lɛ, pɩɖɩɣzɩ-wɛ nɛ patɩŋɩɣ-ɖɩ.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Thyatira: These are the words of Gods Son, whose eyes are like a fiery flame, and whose feet are like fine brass.
local: Ɛyʋ ɛnʋ ɛtasɩ-m heyuu se: «Ma Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛcɔŋnɩ Tiyaatiiri Krɩstʋ ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma yɔɔ yɔ takayaɣ nɛ ŋheyi-i se: ‹Ma Ɛsɔ Pɩyalʋ, weyi ma mɛ-ɛsɛ ɖeɣ miŋ pelu pelu ɛzɩ miŋ nzʋlʋmɩyɛ nɛ ma-naŋgbanzɩ wɛɛ ɛzɩ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ kazɩnzɩŋa ñɩŋgʋ yɔ, mɔntɔŋna se:.

eng: As for the mystery of the seven stars that you saw in my right hand and the seven gold lampstands, here is what they mean: the seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.
local: Ŋnaɣ tɩɩnzɩ lʋbɛ nzɩ́ ŋna me-nesi kɩbanzɩ taa yɔ nɛ kanɖʋnaa lʋbɛ mbá palabɩ-wɛ nɛ sika yɔ, pɔ-tɔm ndʋ́ tɩwɛ mɛsaɣ tɛɛ yɔ tɩ-tɔbʋʋ: tɩɩnzɩ lʋbɛ ńzɩ́ lɛ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa mbá ɛsɩwa se pɔcɔnɩ Krɩstʋ aŋgba lʋbɛ ana a-yɔɔ yɔ. Piyele kanɖʋnaa lʋbɛ lɛ aŋgba ana a-lʋbɛ.».

eng: but they did not prevail, and there was no longer any place for them in heaven.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Miicɛɛlɩ nɛ e-tiyiyaa pawabɩ-wɛ. Pʋ-yɔɔ pɔɖɔnɩ-wɛ ɛsɔdaa nɛ patatasɩ wɛnaʋ ɖɩjaɣyɛ peeɖe.

eng: I correct and discipline those whom I love. So be earnest and change your hearts and lives.
local: Ma lɛ, mɔhɔŋ mbá patɩŋa payɩ mɔnsɔɔlɩ-wɛ yɔ pa-nɩŋgbaaŋ nɛ mansɩɣzɩ-wɛ lɔŋ. Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, pakpa pa-tɩ nɛ pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ.

eng: The stars of the sky fell to the earth as a fig tree drops its fruit when shaken by a strong wind.
local: Tɩɩnzɩ kpɛzaɣnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ sɩtɔlʋʋ tataa ɛzɩ heɣlim sɔsɔm ñamsʋʋ hiyuú ɖoŋ nɛ ki-hiwee kazɩŋ tɔʋ yɔ.

eng: And for three and a half days, members of the peoples, tribes, languages, and nations will look at their dead bodies, but they wont let their dead bodies be put in a tomb.
local: Samaɣ nɛ cejewee nɛ kʋnʋmɩŋ nɛ ajɛya pa-taa ɛyáa nabɛyɛ kɔŋ cɔnaʋ po-tomnasɩ kɩyakɩŋ naadozo nɛ hɔɔlʋʋ. Paakɔŋ haʋ nʋmɔʋ se pepim-wɛ.

eng: All the angels stood in a circle around the throne, and around the elders and the four living creatures. They fell facedown before the throne and worshipped God.
local: Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa tɩŋa kɔcɔ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ nɛ sɔsaa nɛ kpɩna ana a-naanza nɛ pata. Peeɖe poluŋ Ɛsɔ tɛɛ kɛ-ɛsɩndaa nɛ paka pɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ tɛtʋ nɛ pasaŋ-ɩ.

eng: Then I looked and I heard an eagle flying high overhead. It said with a loud voice, Horror, horror, oh! The horror for those who live on earth because of the blasts of the remaining trumpets that the three angels are about to blow.
local: Mantasɩ cɔnaʋ lɛ, manaɣ keziye naɖɩyɛ, ɖɩkʋyaɣ ɖooo ɛsɔdaa nɛ manɩɣ ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ ɖoŋ se: «Pʋtɔɔ-wɛ! Pʋtɔɔ-wɛ! Pʋtɔɔ kedeŋa yɔɔ ñɩma, agandɛɛ wena Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa naadozo mbá pɩkazaa yɔ, pɔkɔŋ hʋlʋʋ yɔ a-yɔɔ!».

eng: In front they had what seemed to be iron armor upon their chests, and the sound of their wings was like the sound of many chariots and horses racing into battle.
local: Nabʋyʋ wɛ kɩ-laŋa taa, pʋwɛ ɛzɩ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ ŋgʋ́ pohuuzuu lotu yɔɔ you taa yɔ. Kʋkʋyaɣ nɛ ki-keŋ taa wiɣ ɛzɩ you lɔɔɖa wena kpaŋnaŋ sakɩyɛ hɔŋ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ iseɣ you taa yɔ.

eng: They said in a loud voice, Worthy is the slaughtered Lamb to receive power, wealth, wisdom, and might, and honor, glory, and blessing.
local: Poholaɣ se: «Heɣa ŋgá Ɛsɔ yeba nɛ palaa yɔ, kamʋnaa se ɛha-kɛ ɖoŋ nɛ ñɩm, nɛ lɔŋsɩnɖɛ, nɛ abalɩtʋ, nɛ ɛɖʋ-kɛ hɩɖɛ, nɛ eyele kɛwɛɛ koboyaɣ taa nɛ pasam-kɛ.».

eng: I was told, You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.
local: Ɛlɛ́, nɔɔ ŋ́gá keheyi-m se: «Pʋwɛɛ se ŋɖaɣnɩ susuu samaɣ ndɩ ndɩ nɛ ajɛya sakɩyɛ nɛ kʋnʋmɩŋ sakɩyɛ nɛ awiya sakɩyɛ pɔ-tɔm ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ kɔŋ-ŋ heyuu yɔ.».

eng: They worshipped the dragon because it had given the beast its authority. They worshipped the beast and said, Who is like the beast, and who can fight against it.
local: Poluŋaɣ coojo tɛɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛnʋ hanɩ kpɩnɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖoŋ. Poluŋaɣ ɖɔɖɔ kpɩnɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-tɛɛ nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: «Anɩɣzɩnɩ kpɩnɛ ɖɩnɛ? Apɩzɩɣ eyoni-ɖɩ?».

eng: I know your works, your love and faithfulness, your service and endurance. I also know that the works you have done most recently are even greater than those you did at first.
local: mansɩm mbʋ́ ñɛ-ŋgbɛyɛ taa ñɩma lakɩ yɔ, nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɛwɛnɩ sɔɔlɩm yɔ, nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ yɔ, nɛ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ pɔɖɔkʋʋ pa-tɩ yɔ. Mansɩm ɖɔɖɔ se mbʋ́ ɩlakɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, pɩkɩlɩ ɖooo mbʋ́ ɩlakaɣ yɔ ɖɔʋ.

eng: So the great dragon was thrown down. The old snake, who is called the devil and Satan, the deceiver of the whole world, was thrown down to the earth; and his angels were thrown down with him.
local: Coojo sɔsɔ ɛnʋ lɛ ɖʋm kɩbɩnʋ weyi payaɣ se Eleeu nɛ pɛtɛ patasɩ-ɩ se Satanɩ yɔ. Ɛnʋ cɛzɩɣnɩ ɛyáa kpeekpe kedeŋa yɔɔ. Potuzi ɛ nɛ e-tiyiyaa nɛ ɛsɔdaa nɛ pɛpɛɖɩ-wɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ.

eng: Look! Im standing at the door and knocking. If any hear my voice and open the door, I will come in to be with them, and will have dinner with them, and they will have dinner with me.
local: Pewelesi nɛ panɩɩ: mansɩŋ nɔnɔɔ taa nɛ mamakɩ pɔɔyʋʋ. Yee nɔɔyʋ ɛnɩ man-taŋ nɛ ɛtʋlɩ yɔ, mansʋʋ ɛ-tɛ nɛ ma nɛ ɩ ɖɩkpɛndɩ ɖɩtɔɔ.

eng: Those who live on earth will rejoice over them. They will celebrate and give each other gifts, because these two prophets had brought such pain to those who live on earth.
local: Kedeŋa yɔɔ ñɩma taa hʋlʋmʋʋnɩ náyaa naalɛ ḿbá pa-sɩm nɛ pɛwɛɛ amaya yɔɔ. Pɔkɔŋ ɖama tiyinaʋ kʋjɔŋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pa-naalɛ panazaɣnɩ-wɛ.

eng: And I heard every creature in heaven and on earth and under the earth and in the sea—I heard everything everywhere say, Blessing, honor, glory, and power belong to the one seated on the throne and to the Lamb forever and always.
local: Manɩ ɖɔɖɔ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ ɛsɔdaa nɛ tataa nɛ tɛtɛɛ nɛ teŋgu taa nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ pʋwɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ yɔ, pɔyɔɔdaɣ se: «Pasa weyi ɛcaɣ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ yɔ nɛ Heɣa nɛ paɖʋ-wɛ hɩɖɛ, ḿbá tɩnɩ koboyaɣ nɛ ɖoŋ pɩnzɩ ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ!».

eng: The sky disappeared like a scroll being rolled up, and every mountain and island was moved from its place.
local: Ɛsɔdaa pazɩ mʋʋ́ʋ ɛzɩ pekpiyuu ñalʋʋ yɔ nɛ pʋŋ tɩŋa nɛ tɛtʋ ndʋ́ ndʋ́ lɩm cɔ-tʋ pɩta yɔ, pɛkpɛzɩɣ po-lona taa.

eng: saying, Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might be to our God forever and always. Amen.
local: se: «Pɩla ḿbʋ́! Đɛ-Ɛsɔ ɛtɩɣyɩ samtʋ nɛ koboyaɣ nɛ lɔŋsɩnɖɛ nɛ ɛsɩmɩyɛ nɛ hɩɖɛ ɖʋʋ nɛ ɖoŋ nɛ abalɩtʋ pɩnzɩ ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ! Pɩla ḿbʋ́!».

eng: Then I heard a loud voice in heaven say, Now the salvation and power and kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Christ have come. The accuser of our brothers and sisters, who accuses them day and night before our God, has been thrown down.
local: Peeɖe manɩɣ puholuuni ɛsɔdaa ɖoŋ se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, ñʋʋ yabʋ alɩwaatʋ talaa! Pɩtalaa se ɖɛ-Ɛsɔ ɛwɩlɩ e-Kewiyaɣ ɖoŋ! Pɩtalaa ɖɔɖɔ se Weyi Ɛlɩzaa Yɔ, ɛwɩlɩ ɛ-abalɩtʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, potuzi weyi ɛkʋyaɣ ɖa-taabalaa yɔɔ wɩsɩ nɛ ɖoó ɖɛ-Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa yɔ nɛ ɛsɔdaa nɛ pɛpɛɖɩ-ɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ.

eng: They also have tails with stingers, just like scorpions; and in their tails is their power to hurt people for five months.
local: Kʋñɔ suŋ ɛzɩ pɩcasɩ yɔ nɛ ɩ-nɔsɩ taa wɛɛ ɖɔɖɔ ḿbʋ́. Ɩ-nɔsɩ taa peeɖe ku-ɖoŋ weyi kɩnazɩɣnɩ ɛyáa fenasɩ kagbaanzɩ yɔ, ɛwɛɛ.

eng: But I have this against you: you put up with that woman, Jezebel, who calls herself a prophet. You allow her to teach and to mislead my servants into committing sexual immorality and eating food sacrificed to idols.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ndʋ́ man-taa wɛnɩ-mɩ yɔ lɛ se: iyeki halʋ weyi payaɣ se Yezaabɛɛlɩ nɛ ɛtɔŋ se ɛkɛ náyʋ yɔ. Ŋgʋ́, ɛwɩlɩɣ nɛ eyiliɣ man-tʋmlaɖaa se pala acɛyɛ nɛ pɔtɔɔ kɩlaʋ nandʋ.

eng: But after three and a half days, the breath of life from God entered them, and they stood on their feet. Great fear came over those who saw them.
local: Ɛlɛ́, kɩyakɩŋ naadozo nɛ hɔɔlʋʋ ɩnʋ ɩ-wayɩ lɛ, wezuu fezuu nakʋyʋ lɩɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ nɛ kɩsʋʋ sɩɖaa naalɛ ḿbá pa-taa nɛ pakʋyɩ pasɩŋ pa-naŋgbanzɩ yɔɔ nɛ sɔɔndʋ kpa mbá pɔcɔŋaɣnɩ-wɛ yɔ siŋŋ.

eng: As for those who emerge victorious, I will allow them to sit with me on my throne, just as I emerged victorious and sat down with my Father on his throne.
local: ‹Weyi ɛwakɩ yɔ, meyeki nɛ ma nɛ ɩ ɖɩcaɣ men-kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ ɛzɩ ma mawabʋ nɛ ma nɛ man-Caa ɖɩcaɣ ɛ-ñɩŋga yɔɔ yɔ.

eng: The beast was given a mouth that spoke boastful and blasphemous things, and it was given authority to act for forty-two months.
local: Coojo sɔsɔ ɛnʋ, ɛha-ɖɩ nɔɔ nɛ ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ cɛjɛyɛ tɔm nɛ ɖɩtʋʋ Ɛsɔ. Paha-ɖɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɖoŋ se ɖɩlabɩnɩ tʋmɩyɛ fenasɩ nɩɩnaza nɛ naalɛ.

eng: Then the four living creatures said, Amen, and the elders fell down and worshipped.
local: Peeɖe kpɩna ana a-naanza acosaa se: «Pɩla ḿbʋ́!» Nɛ sɔsaa ñoluŋ pa-nɩmbɔla yɔɔ nɛ pasaŋ-wɛ.

eng: Then the kings of the earth, the officials and the generals, the rich and the powerful, and everyone, slave and free, hid themselves in caves and in the rocks of the mountains.
local: Piyele kedeŋa yɔɔ awiya sɔsɔna nɛ ñʋndɩnaa nɛ sɔɔjanaa ñʋndɩnaa nɛ ñɩm tɩnaa nɛ ɖoŋ tɩnaa nɛ yomaa nɛ mbá pɛwɛ pa-tɩ yɔɔ yɔ, patɩŋa payɩ pɛmɛlɩɣ pɔŋ nɛ pʋŋ ɛwasɩ pa-taa.

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Who are these people wearing white robes, and where did they come from.
local: Peeɖe sɔsaa taa lɛlʋ pɔzɩ-m se: «Mbá lɛ mbá posu tokonaa kʋhʋlʋmaa waa waa yɔ. Palɩnɩ le».

eng: They gained the victory over him on account of the blood of the Lamb and the word of their witness. Love for their own lives didnt make them afraid to die.
local: Pɩtɩŋnɩ Heɣa calɩm nɛ aseɣɖe nɖɩ́ ɖa-taabalaa lɩzaa yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ nɛ pawabɩ-ɩ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, patakpaɣ pe-wezuu payaa pʋyʋ nɛ petisi se yee paa sɩm yɔ, pasɩ.

eng: Their king is an angel from the abyss, whose Hebrew name is Abaddon, and whose Greek name is Apollyon.
local: Kɩwɛnɩ wiyaʋ nɔɔyʋ, ɛnʋ lɛ asayʋʋ tiyiyu. Payaɣ-ɩ nɛ Ebree taa se: Abaadɔɔ nɛ Grɛɛkɩ taa se: Apoliyɔɔ .

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: ‹Weyi ɛwɛnɩ nɩŋgbaaŋ lɛ, ɛnɩɩ tɔm ndʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu heyiɣ Krɩstʋ aŋgba taa ñɩma yɔ!› ».

eng: I gave her time to change her heart and life, but she refuses to change her life of prostitution.
local: Maha-ɩ alɩwaatʋ se ɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ, ɛlɛ́, ɛɛcaɣ se eyele ɛ-acɛyɛ lakasɩ.

eng: It opened its mouth to speak blasphemies against God. It blasphemed Gods name and his dwelling place .
local: Peeɖe kpɩnɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖikuli ɖɩ-nɔɔ nɛ ɖɩtʋʋ Ɛsɔ. Đɩwɛɛkɩ ɛ-hɩɖɛ nɛ ɖɩkpɛndɩnɩ ɖenɖe ɛwɛɛ yɔ nɛ mbá pɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ.

eng: Then they heard a loud voice from heaven say to them, Come up here. And they went up to heaven in a cloud, while their enemies watched them.
local: Peeɖe náyaa ḿbá, panɩ puholuuni ɛsɔdaa se: «Ɩkpa cɩnɛ!» Ɛlɛ́, pɩkpazɩ-wɛ ɛsɔdaa yem nɛ pasʋʋ mɩndʋ, po-koyindinaa cɔŋaɣnɩ-wɛ.

eng: They called to the mountains and the rocks, Fall on us and hide us from the face of the one seated on the throne and from the Lambs wrath!.
local: Peheyaɣ pʋŋ nɛ ɛwasɩ se: «Ɩkpɛzɩ ɩɖa ɖɔ-yɔɔ nɛ ihuuzi ɖɔ-yɔɔ nɛ weyi ɛcaɣ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛtaana-ɖʋ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, Heɣa pana ɛtaakpanɩ-ɖʋ.

eng: I said to him, Sir, you know. Then he said to me, These people have come out of great hardship. They have washed their robes and made them white in the Lambs blood.
local: Ɛlɛ́, moncosi-i se: «Đajaa! Ñasɩmna.» Peeɖe eheyi-m se: «Ḿbá tɔɔnɩ kʋñɔŋ sɔsɔʋ. Pɔcɔtɩ po-tokonaa kitibaa waa waa nɛ pahʋlɩsɩ-wɛ Heɣa calɩm taa.

eng: The first horror has passed. Look! Two horrors are still coming after this.
local: Kajalaɣ kʋñɔŋ ɖɛwa. Kʋ-wayɩ lɛ, kʋñɔmɩŋ naalɛ tasɩɣ kɔm.

eng: Therefore, rejoice, you heavens and you who dwell in them. But oh! The horror for the earth and sea! The devil has come down to you with great rage, for he knows that he only has a short time.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ lɛ, ɛsɔdaa nɛ mbá mɩ ɩwɛ peeɖe yɔ, mɩ-taa ɛhʋlʋmɩ! Mbá mɩ ɩwɛ tɛtʋ yɔɔ nɛ teŋgu taa yɔ, pʋtɔɔ-mɩ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, eleeu tibi mɩ-cɔlɔ. Ɛnawa se alɩwaatʋ pazɩ yem pɩkazɩ-ɩ, pʋ-yɔɔ ɛ-pana kpawa pɩŋŋ.».

eng: Look! Im throwing her onto a sickbed. I am casting those who have committed adultery with her into terrible hardship—if they dont change their hearts from following her practices—.
local: Na! Manɖʋʋ-ɩ kʋdɔŋ nɛ ɛnɩɩ siziŋ siŋŋ nɛ pɩkpɛndɩnɩ mbá pa nɛ ɩ palakɩ acɛyɛ yɔ. Yee peyebi pa-lakasɩ ńzɩ́ yɔ, kalɩpiɖeke.

eng: It was also allowed to make war on the saints and to gain victory over them. It was given authority over every tribe, people, language, and nation.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ɛha-ɖɩ waɖɛ se ɖiyooni Ɛsɔ ñɩma nɛ ɖɩwabɩ-wɛ. Ɛha-ɖɩ ɖɔɖɔ ɖoŋ cejewee tɩŋa nɛ samaɣ tɩŋa nɛ kʋnʋmɩŋ tɩŋa nɛ ajɛya tɩŋa pa-taa ñɩma yɔɔ.

eng: At that hour there was a great earthquake, and a tenth of the city fell. Seven thousand people were killed by the earthquake, and the rest were afraid and gave glory to the God of heaven.
local: Alɩwaatʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ tɛtʋ ñamsɩ siŋŋ nɛ yee patayɩ tɛtʋ ndʋ́ tɩ-taa paawɛɛ yɔ tam hiu yɔ, hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ yɔkaa. Ɛyáa kutokiŋ lʋbɛ sɩbɩnɩ asayʋʋ ŋ́gʋ́ kɩ-taa. Piyele sɔɔndʋ kpa mbá palɩ kɩ-taa yɔ nɛ pasa Ɛsɔ weyi ɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ.

eng: The great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to stand.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kɩyakʋ sɔsɔʋ ŋgʋ́ pa-pana kpaɣ yɔ, kɩtalaa. Apɩzɩɣ ɛlɩɩ pɩ-taa».

eng: This is the reason they are before Gods throne. They worship him day and night in his temple, and the one seated on the throne will shelter them.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ pasɩŋ Ɛsɔ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ ɛsɩndaa nɛ palakɩ-ɩ tʋmɩyɛ wɩsɩ nɛ ɖoó ɛ-ɖɩɣa taa. Weyi ɛcaɣ kɔ-yɔɔ yɔ, ɛkɔŋ pɔ-yɔɔ kenduu.

eng: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet, and I heard a voice from the four horns of the gold altar that is before God.
local: Tɩlaaɖɩ ɖɩhɔɖɛ nɖɩ́ palabɩnɩ sika nɛ ɖɩwɛɛ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa yɔ, ɖɩɩwɛnɩ hɩŋ naanza. Tiyiyu loɖo ñɩnʋ hʋlɩ ɛgandɩyɛ lɛ, manɩɣ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ tɩlaaɖɩ ɖɩhɔɖɛ taa.

eng: When the dragon saw that he had been thrown down to the earth, he chased the woman who had given birth to the male child.
local: Coojo ɛnʋ, ɛnawa se potuzi-i nɛ pɛpɛɖɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ lɛ, ɛɖɔɔnʋʋ halʋ weyi ɛlʋlɩ abalɩbɩɣa yɔ.

eng: Then I saw another great and awe-inspiring sign in heaven. There were seven angels with seven plagues—and these are the last, for with them Gods anger is brought to an end.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mana yʋsaɣ sɔsɔɔ lɛɛka nakɛyɛ ɛsɔdaa, kɛfɛyɩ yɔɔdʋʋ: Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa lʋbɛ nabɛyɛ ɖɔkɩ kɛtɛzaɣ kʋñɔmɩŋ lʋbɛ. Ɩnʋ tɛŋna nɛ Ɛsɔ pana hɛɛ.

eng: All who live on earth worshipped it, all whose names hadnt been written—from the time the earth was made—in the scroll of life of the Lamb who was slain.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ kedeŋa yɔɔ ɛyáa kpeekpe kɔŋ luŋuu ɖɩ-tɛɛ. Ḿbá lɛ mbá patɩŋa payɩ patama pa-hɩla wezuu takayaɣ taa ɖooo ɛzɩ Ɛsɔ labʋ kedeŋa yɔ. Takayaɣ ŋ́gá lɛ Heɣa ŋgá palawa yɔ ka-ñɩŋga.

eng: and I will even put her children to death with disease. Then all the churches will know that Im the one who examines minds and hearts, and that I will give to each of you what your actions deserve.
local: Mɔnkɔŋ kʋʋ halʋ ɛnʋ e-piya nɛ piyele ma-aŋgba tɩŋa taa ñɩma tɩlɩ se ma lɛ weyi ɛtazɩɣ ɛyáa lɩmaɣza nɛ mbʋ́ pʋwɛ pa-taa yɔ pa-taa. Mɔnkɔŋ hɛyʋʋ paa mɩ-taa weyi mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: The second horror is over. The third horror is coming soon.
local: Kʋñɔŋ naalɛ ñɩŋgʋ ɖɛwa. Pɩkazɩ pazɩ, tozo ñɩŋgʋ kɔŋ.

eng: But the woman was given the two wings of the great eagle so that she could fly to her place in the desert. There she would be taken care of—out of the snakes reach—for a time and times and half a time.
local: Peeɖe pɛcɛlɩ halʋ ɛnʋ keziye naɖɩyɛ keŋ naalɛ se ɛkʋyɩ nɛ ewolo lakʋ taa e-lone taa nɛ pɔcɔnɩ ɛ-yɔɔ pɩnzɩ naadozo nɛ hɔɔlʋʋ nɛ ɖʋm ɛnʋ ɛtaana-ɩ.

eng: They wont hunger or thirst anymore. No sun or scorching heat will beat down on them.
local: Ñɔɔsɩ ɛɛtasɩɣ-wɛ wɩʋ nɛ lɔkɔtʋ ɛɛtasɩɣ-wɛ ɖɔkʋʋ kaʋ. Wɩsɩ ɛɛtasɩɣ-wɛ ñaɣʋ yaa soŋaɣ nakɛyɛ ɛɛtasɩɣ-wɛ kpaʋ kaʋ.

eng: It said to the sixth angel, who had the trumpet, Release the four angels who are bound at the great river Euphrates.
local: Pʋyɔɔdaɣnɩ tiyiyu ɛnʋ se: «Yɛɖɩ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa naanza mbá paɖɩ-wɛ yoŋgbasɩ pɔɔ sɔsɔɔ ŋgá payaɣ se Efraatɩ yɔ kɔ-nɔɔ yɔɔ yɔ.».

eng: Then I saw what appeared to be a sea of glass mixed with fire. Those who gained victory over the beast, its image, and the number of its name were standing by the glass sea, holding harps from God.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, mana nabʋyʋ, pʋwɛ ɛzɩ teŋgu yɔ, pɩñɩlɩzɩɣ ɛzɩ ñɩnʋʋ yɔ, pisidini miŋ. Mana ɖɔɖɔ mbá pawabɩ kpɩnɛ nɛ ɖɩ-sɩʋ kpelaɣ nɛ ɖɩ-hɩɖɛ ñʋʋ yɔ, pasɩŋ teŋgu ŋ́gʋ́ kʋ-nɔɔ. Pɔɖɔkɩ pe-nesi taa Ɛsɔ cɩmɩŋ.

eng: Whoever has ears must listen:.
local: Weyi ɛwɛnɩ nɩŋgbaaŋ lɛ, ɛnɩɩ!.

eng: Then I looked, and there was the Lamb, standing on Mount Zion. With him were one hundred forty-four thousand who had his name and his Fathers name written on their foreheads.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mantasɩ cɔnaʋ nɛ manaɣ Heɣa sɩŋ Siiyɔɔ pʋʋ yɔɔ, ka nɛ ɛyáa kutokiŋ mɩnʋʋ nɛ nɩɩnaza nɛ naanza. Pama ka-hɩɖɛ nɛ ka-Caa ñɩnɖɛ pe-kila taa.

eng: As for the rest of you in Thyatira—those of you who dont follow this teaching and havent learned the so-called deep secrets of Satan—I wont burden you with anything else.
local: ‹Ɛlɛ́, meheyiɣ Tiyaatiiri ñɩma mbá mɩ ɩɩɖɔŋ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩ-yɔɔ ɛzɩ lɛlaa yɔ nɛ ɩtatazɩ Satanɩ ñɩndʋ ndʋ́ lɛlaa mbá pɔtɔŋ se tɩ-taa wɛ cukaŋ yɔ tɩ-taa yɔ se maatasɩɣ-mɩ sɔzʋʋ sʋʋtʋ natʋyʋ.

eng: Then from his mouth the snake poured a river of water after the woman so that the river would sweep her away.
local: Đʋm ɛnʋ, ɛlɩzaɣ lɩm nɛ ɛ-nɔɔ taa ɛzɩ pɔɔ sɔsɔɔ yɔ halʋ ɛnʋ ɛ-wayɩ se pɩkpaɣ-ɩ.

eng: Then the seventh angel blew his trumpet, and there were loud voices in heaven saying, The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and his Christ, and he will rule forever and always.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, Ɛsɔ tiyiyu lʋbɛ ñɩnʋ hʋlɩ ɛgandɩyɛ nɛ puholuuni ɛsɔdaa kpaŋŋ kpaŋŋ se: «Pɛcɛlɩ kedeŋa kewiyaɣ ɖa-Kɩpaɣlʋ nɛ Weyi Ɛlɩzaa Yɔ. Ɛtɔkɩ-kɛ pɩnzɩ ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ!».

eng: because the Lamb who is in the midst of the throne will shepherd them. He will lead them to the springs of life-giving water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Heɣa ŋgá kɛwɛ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ hɛkʋ taa yɔ, keɖiyiɣ-wɛ nɛ kowoni-wɛ wezuu lɩm hiɖe cɔlɔ nɛ Ɛsɔ hɛzɩ pɛ-ɛsɩtɛɣlɩm tɩŋa.».

eng: Then the four angels who had been made ready for that hour, day, month, and year were released to kill a third of humankind.
local: Peeɖe pɛhɛɖɩ pa-naanza. Paañɔɔzɩ pa-tɩ nɛ paɖaŋ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ ŋ́gʋ́, kɩyakʋ ŋ́gʋ́, fenaɣ ŋ́gá, pɩnaɣ ŋ́gá ka-taa se yee patayɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ ɛyáa tam naadozo yɔ, pakʋ hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ.

eng: They sing the song of Moses, Gods servant, and the song of the Lamb, saying, Great and awe-inspiring are your works, Lord God Almighty. Just and true are your ways, king of the nations.
local: Peteɣaɣ hendu ndʋ́ Ɛsɔ tʋmlaɖʋ Mooyiizi ketewa yɔ. Ńdʋ́ ɖɔɖɔ Heɣa ketewa. Ńdʋ́ lɛ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ, Đoŋ Tɩŋa Tʋ! Tʋma wena ŋlakɩ yɔ, apaɣlaa kpem, pɩfɛyɩ yɔɔdʋʋ! Ajɛya Wiyaʋ! Ña-nʋmɔŋ tʋʋzaa, ɩnʋ lɛ nʋmɔŋ siŋŋ.

eng: If any are to be taken captive, then into captivity they will go. If any are to be killed by the sword, then by the sword they will be killed. This calls for endurance and faithfulness on the part of the saints.
local: Yee pʋɖʋwa se nɔɔyʋ ɛsʋʋ salaka yɔ, ɛsʋʋ ka-taa ɖɔɖɔ. Yee pʋɖʋwa se pakʋ nɔɔyʋ nɛ sɩɣa yɔ, pakʋʋ-ɩ nɛ kɛ ɖɔɖɔ. Pʋnɛ pɩ-taa panaɣ Ɛsɔ ñɩma tɩ ɖɔkʋʋ nɛ pe-lidaʋ wɛnaʋ.

eng: I heard a sound from heaven that was like the sound of rushing water and loud thunder. The sound I heard was like that of harpists playing their harps.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, manɩ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ ɛsɔdaa ɛzɩ lɩm sɔsɔm falʋʋ yɔ yaa ɛzɩ tɛʋ nazʋʋ ɖoŋ yɔ. Kɔkɔɖɛ nɖɩ́ manɩɣaɣ yɔ, ɖɩwɛ ɛzɩ cɩmɩŋ maɖaa makʋʋ cɩmɩŋ yɔ.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice from the temple say to the seven angels, Go and pour out the seven bowls of Gods anger on the earth.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, manɩɩ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ tiyiyaa ḿbá pa-lʋbɛ nɛ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa se: «Iwolo ihuti kedeŋa yɔɔ Ɛsɔ pana ana a-caɖasɩ lʋbɛ!».

eng: Just hold on to what you have until I come.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɩɖɔkɩ mbʋ́ ɩwɛna yɔ kpam nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ kɩyakʋ ŋgʋ́ mɔnkɔŋ yɔ.

eng: But the earth helped the woman. The earth opened its mouth and swallowed the river that the dragon poured out of his mouth.
local: Ɛlɛ́, tɛtʋ sɩnɩ halʋ ɛnʋ: tɩha tɩ-nɔɔ nɛ tili lɩm mbʋ́ coojo lɩzaɣnɩ ɛ-nɔɔ taa yɔ, pɩtɩŋa.

eng: Then the twenty-four elders, who were seated on their thrones before God, fell on their faces and worshipped God.
local: Peeɖe sɔsaa nɩɩlɛ nɛ naanza mbá paacaɣ Ɛsɔ ɛsɩndaa pe-kewiyaɣ kpelisi yɔɔ yɔ, poluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ nɛ paka pɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ tɛtʋ.

eng: The number of cavalry troops was two hundred million. I heard their number.
local: You laɖaa mbá pacakɩ kpaŋnaŋ yɔ, manɩwa se pɛkpɛndɩ ɛgbaŋ nasɩlɛ.

eng: Who wont fear you, Lord, and glorify your name? You alone are holy. All nations will come and fall down in worship before you, for your acts of justice have been revealed.
local: Kɩpaɣlʋ! Awɛɛ se ɛɛsɔɔndʋʋ-ŋ nɛ eeyeki se ña-hɩɖɛ ɛsɛɛ? Ñe-ɖeke koŋ lɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ tʋ. Ajɛya kpeekpe taa ɛyáa kaɣ kɔm nɛ poluŋ ñɛ-tɛɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pana ɛzɩma mbʋ́ ŋtʋʋzɩ ña-lakasɩ taa yɔ.».

eng: Then I saw another beast coming up from the earth. It had two horns like a lamb, but it was speaking like a dragon.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mana kpɩnɛ lɛɛɖɛ, ɖɩlɩɣaɣnɩ tɛtɛɛ. Đɩñɔ hɩŋ naalɛ ɛzɩ heɣa yɔ nɛ ɖɩyɔɔdʋʋ ɛzɩ coojo yɔ.

eng: So the first angel poured his bowl on the earth, and a nasty and terrible sore appeared on the people who had the beasts mark and worshipped its image.
local: Peeɖe kajalaɣ tʋ woba nɛ ehuti ɛ-caɖaɣ kedeŋa yɔɔ nɛ piyele kɩcɩkpɩŋ kɩdɛkɛdɩŋ weyi ɩnazɩɣ ɛyáa yɔ, ɩkpa mbá palabɩ pɔ-yɔɔ kpɩnɛ yʋsaɣ nɛ poluŋuu ɖɩ-sɩʋ kpelaɣ tɛɛ yɔ.

eng: To those who emerge victorious, keeping my practices until the end, I will give authority over the nations—.
local: ‹Weyi ɛwakɩ nɛ ɛ́ɖɔ́kɩ ɛ-tɩ man-tʋma taa kpam nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩtalɩ tɩnaɣ yɔɔ yɔ, mahaɣ-ɩ ɖoŋ se ɛcɔnɩ ajɛya taa ñɩma yɔɔ ɛzɩ man-Caa haʋ-m ɖɔɖɔ yɔ. Eɖiyiɣ-wɛ nɛ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ ɖaʋ nɛ ɛmɩzɩ-wɛ ɛzɩ pɔyɔkʋʋ cʋʋ pɛŋ yɔ. Mɔnkɔŋ-ɩ haʋ ɖɔɖɔ tɩmbalʋʋ.

eng: They sing a new song in front of the throne, the four living creatures, and the elders. And no one could learn the song except the one hundred forty-four thousand who had been purchased from the earth.
local: Ɛyáa ḿbá, peteɣ hendu kɩfatʋ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ nɛ kpɩna ana a-naanza nɛ sɔsaa pɛ-ɛsɩndaa. Nɔɔyʋ tapɩzɩɣ se ɛkpɛlɩkɩ-tʋ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ-wɛ. Pa-ñʋʋ paayabɩnɩ tataa.

eng: So the dragon was furious with the woman, and he went off to make war on the rest of her children, on those who keep Gods commandments and hold firmly to the witness of Jesus.
local: Peeɖe ɛ-pana kpanɩ halʋ ɛnʋ nɛ ewolo eyouni e-liɖe taa ñɩma mbá pakazaa yɔ. Ḿbá ɖɔmnɩ Ɛsɔ paɣtʋ yɔɔ nɛ palɩzɩ Yesu yɔɔ aseɣɖe.

eng: They said, We give thanks to you, Lord God Almighty, who is and was, for you have taken your great power and enforced your rule.
local: nɛ pasaŋ-ɩ se: «Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ, Đoŋ Tɩŋa Tʋ! Ñɛwɛna, ñaawɛna, ɖɩsaŋ-ŋ se ŋtɔɔ ñe-kewiyaɣ nɛ ño-ɖoŋ sɔsɔŋ.

eng: And this is the way I saw the horses and their riders in the vision: they had breastplates that were fiery red, dark blue, and yellow as sulfur. The horses heads were like lions heads, and out of their mouths came fire, smoke, and sulfur.
local: Ɩɩnaɣ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ mana kpaŋnaŋ nɛ ɩ-yɔɔ caɣyaa mon-ɖoziye taa yɔ: posu ñɩɣtʋ wondu ndʋ́ tɩsɛ ɛzɩ miŋ nɛ tipiyi ɛzɩ tɛʋ piyuu yɔ nɛ tɩwɛɛ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ kazɩnzɩŋa yɔ. Kpaŋnaŋ ɩnʋ, ɩ-ñʋŋ wɛ ɛzɩ tɔɔyɩŋ ñʋŋ yɔ, miŋ nɛ ñɔsɩ nɛ tɩm palɩɣaɣnɩ ɩ-nɔsɩ taa.

eng: After this I looked, and the temple in heaven—that is, the tent of witness—was opened.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, manaɣ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa ŋgá kɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa nɛ ka-taa nɛ kɩɖanɖʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩlɩzɩɣ aseɣɖe yɔ, kɔ-nɔnɔɔ tʋlaa.

eng: It exercises all the authority of the first beast in its presence. It also makes the earth and those who live in it worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.
local: Đɩɩwɛnɩ kajalaɣ ñɩnɖɛ ɖoŋ tɩŋa nɛ ɖɩlakɩ tʋmɩyɛ nɛ ɖɩ-nɔɔ ɖɩ-ɛsɩndaa. Điyekaɣ nɛ kedeŋa nɛ kɔ-yɔɔ ñɩma poluŋuu kajalaɣ ñɩnɖɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-kɩcɩkpʋʋ kɔŋaɣnɩ-ɖɩ sɩm nɛ kʋwaa yɔ ɖɩ-tɛɛ.

eng: The second angel poured his bowl into the sea, and the sea turned into blood, like the blood of a corpse, and every living thing in the sea died.
local: Naalɛ tʋ ñohuti ɛ-caɖaɣ teŋgu taa nɛ lɩm pɩsɩ ɛzɩ sɩɖʋ calɩm yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa pʋwɛ wezuu pɩ-taa yɔ, pɩsɩ.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: ‹Weyi ɛwɛnɩ nɩŋgbaaŋ lɛ, ɛnɩɩ tɔm ndʋ́ Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu heyiɣ Krɩstʋ aŋgba taa ñɩma yɔ!› ».

eng: They werent defiled with women, for these people who follow the Lamb wherever he goes are virgins. They were purchased from among humankind as early produce for God and the Lamb.
local: Pɩtɛ ɛyáa ḿbá lɛ mbá petepisi pa-tɩ nɛ halaa yɔ, pototukuni-wɛ kaʋ. Paa Heɣa ewoki le yɔ, patɩŋɩɣ-kɛ. Payabɩ pa-ñʋʋ nɛ ɛyáa hɛkʋ taa se pɛkɛ ɛzɩ kajalaɣ kʋmtʋ ndʋ́ pahaɣ Ɛsɔ nɛ Heɣa yɔ.

eng: Then the dragon stood on the seashore.
local: Coojo sɔsɔ ɛnʋ ɛsɩŋ teŋgu kañɩŋa yɔɔ.

eng: The nations were enraged, but your wrath came. The time came for the dead to be judged. The time came to reward your servants, the prophets and saints, and those who fear your name, both small and great, and to destroy those who destroy the earth.
local: Ajɛya taa ñɩma pana kakpawa, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ña-ñɩna ñakpawa! Pɩtalaa se ŋhʋʋnɩ sɩɖaa: ŋhɛyɩɣ ña-tʋmlaɖaa, ḿbá yɔ: náyaa nɛ ña-ñɩma nɛ mbá pañaŋ-ŋ yɔ, paa paakɛ ajamaa yaa ɖoŋ tɩnaa nɛ piyele ŋkʋ mbá paba wɛɛkɩɣ kedeŋa yɔ.».

eng: By these three plagues a third of humankind was killed: by the fire, smoke, and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
local: Yee patayɩ kedeŋa kpeekpe ɛyáa tam naadozo yɔ, miŋ nɛ ñɔsɩ nɛ tɩm mbʋ́ pɩlɩɣaɣnɩ kpaŋnaŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-nɔsɩ taa yɔ, pʋkʋ hɔɔlʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ.

eng: The seven angels, who have the seven plagues, came out of the temple. They were clothed in pure bright linen and had gold sashes around their waists.
local: nɛ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa lʋbɛ mbá pɔɖɔkɩ kʋñɔmɩŋ lʋbɛ yɔ, palɩɣnɩ ka-taa. Posu wondu ndʋ́ tɩwɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ nɛ tɩñɩlɩzɩɣ siŋŋ yɔ nɛ pata tambala pa-laŋa taa.

eng: It does great signs so that it even makes fire come down from heaven to earth in the presence of the people.
local: Đɩlakaɣ hama lakasɩ sɔsɔsɩ nɛ halɩ ɖɩkɔɔ ɖɩla nɛ miŋ nɩɩyɩ lɩɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ etii tataa nɛ ɛyáa na.

eng: The third angel poured his bowl into the rivers and springs of water, and they turned into blood.
local: Tozo tʋ ñohuti ɛ-ñɩŋga pɔsɩ nɛ hila pa-taa nɛ lɩm pɩsɩ calɩm.

eng: No lie came from their mouths; they are blameless.
local: Cɛtɩm tɔm talɩɩ pɔ-nɔsɩ taa wiɖiyi kaʋ, natʋyʋ fɛyɩ se paawanɩ-wɛ.

eng: The horses power is in their mouths and their tails, for their tails are like snakes with heads that inflict injuries.
local: Kpaŋnaŋ ɩnʋ, i-ɖoŋ wɛ ɩ-nɔsɩ nɛ i-suŋ pa-taa. I-suŋ wɛ ɛzɩ ɖʋmaa yɔ: ɩwɛnɩ ñʋŋ nɛ ɩnʋ ɩnazɩɣnɩ ɛyáa.

eng: Then Gods temple in heaven was opened, and the chest containing his covenant appeared in his temple. There were lightning, voices, thunder, an earthquake, and large hail.
local: Peeɖe Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa ŋgá kɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, kɔ-nɔnɔɔ tʋlaa nɛ panaɣ ɛ-nɔɔ haʋ aɖakaɣ ka-taa. Tɛʋ ñɩlɩsaa nɛ kɔkɔɖɛ ya nɛ tɛʋ nazɩ nɛ tɛtʋ ñamsɩ nɛ ɖɔmbɛɛ tɔlɩ siŋŋ.

eng: Then one of the four living creatures gave the seven angels seven gold bowls full of the anger of the God who lives forever and always.
local: Peeɖe kpɩna naanza ana a-taa lɛɛɖɛ cɛlɩ Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa ḿbá pa-lʋbɛ sika caɖasɩ lʋbɛ. Sɩ-taa su Ɛsɔ pana ɖo. Ɛsɔ ɛnʋ ɛwɛnɩ wezuu pɩnzɩ ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ.

eng: It deceives those who live on earth by the signs that it was allowed to do in the presence of the beast. It told those who live on earth to make an image for the beast who had been wounded by the sword and yet came to life again.
local: Đɩcɛzaɣ kedeŋa yɔɔ ɛyáa nɛ hama lakasɩ nzɩ́ coojo ha-ɖɩ waɖɛ se ɖɩla kajalaɣ ñɩnɖɛ ɛsɩndaa yɔ. Điheyaɣ-wɛ ɖɔɖɔ se pama sɩʋ kpelaɣ nakɛyɛ nɛ paɖʋnɩ kpɩnɛ nɖɩ́ paalom sɩɣa nɛ ɖɩɖaɣnɩ wɛʋ wezuu yɔ hɩɖɛ.

eng: Then I heard the angel of the waters say, You are just, holy one, who is and was, because you have given these judgments.
local: Peeɖe manɩ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛcɔŋnɩ lɩm yɔɔ yɔ, ɛyɔɔdaɣ se: «Weyi ña ŋwɛɛ nɛ ŋŋwɛɛ yɔ, ŋtʋʋzaa. Weyi ña ŋwɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ yɔ, mbʋ́ ŋlaba ɛzʋnɔ nɛ ŋhʋʋnɩ ɛyáa yɔ, pʋtʋʋzaa.

eng: Then I saw another angel flying high overhead with eternal good news to proclaim to those who live on earth, and to every nation, tribe, language, and people.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mana Ɛsɔ tiyiyu lɛlʋ, ɛkʋyaɣ ɖooo ɛsɔdaa. Ɛɛwɛnɩ Laŋhɛzɩyɛ Tɔm ndʋ́ tɩwɛ tam yɔ se esusuu kedeŋa yɔɔ ɛyáa nɛ ajɛya tɩŋa nɛ cejewee tɩŋa nɛ kʋnʋmɩŋ tɩŋa nɛ samaɣ tɩŋa.

eng: The rest of humankind, who werent killed by these plagues, didnt change their hearts and lives and turn from their handiwork. They didnt stop worshipping demons and idols made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood—idols that cant see or hear or walk.
local: Piyele ɛyáa mbá pɩkazɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ se kʋñɔmɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩtakʋ-wɛ yɔ, peteyele mbʋ́ palaɣkaɣ yɔ. Peteyele aleewaa nɛ sɩʋ kpelisi nzɩ́ palabɩnɩ sika nɛ nzɩ́ palabɩnɩ liidiye nɛ nzɩ́ palabɩnɩ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ kazɩnzɩŋa ñɩŋgʋ yɔ nɛ nzɩ́ pasaɣnɩ pɛɛ nɛ nzɩ́ pasaɣnɩ ɖasɩ yɔ, sɩ-tɛɛ luŋuu. Ŋgʋ́, sɩɩpɩzɩɣ sɩna yaa sɩnɩɩ yaa sɩɖɔ.

eng: The temple was filled with smoke from Gods glory and power, and no one could go into the temple until the seven plagues of the seven last angels were brought to an end.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ koboyaɣ nɛ e-ɖoŋ peyele nɛ ñɔsɩ nasɩyɩ ñɛyɩ ɖɩɣa ŋ́gá ka-taa. Nɔɔyʋ tapɩzɩɣ se ɛsʋʋ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ kʋñɔmɩŋ lʋbɛ weyi tiyiyaa ḿbá pɔkɔnɩ pa-lʋbɛ yɔ, ɩtɛma yɔ.

eng: They poured out the blood of saints and prophets, and you have given them blood to drink. They deserve it.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, pakʋ Ɛsɔ ñɩma nɛ náyaa nɛ pɛkpɛdɩ pa-calɩm. Pʋ-yɔɔ ŋcɛlɩ-wɛ calɩm se pɔñɔɔ, pamʋnɩ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: It was allowed to give breath to the beasts image so that the beasts image would even speak and cause anyone who didnt worship the beasts image to be put to death.
local: Ɛha-ɖɩ waɖɛ se ɖɩla nɛ sɩʋ kpelaɣ ŋgá pama ɛzɩ kajalaɣ kpɩnɛ ńɖɩ́ yɔ, kɛwɛɛ wezuu nɛ kɔyɔɔdɩ nɛ kala nɛ mbá patɩŋa payɩ paaluŋuu kɛ-tɛɛ yɔ, pasɩ.

eng: He said in a loud voice, Fear God and give him glory, for the hour of his judgment has come. Worship the one who made heaven and earth, the sea and springs of water.
local: Eholaɣ ɖoŋ se: «Ɩsɔɔndɩ Ɛsɔ nɛ ɩɖʋ-ɩ hɩɖɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛ-tɔm hʋʋ alɩwaatʋ talaa. Iluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ! Ɛnʋ labɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ tɛtʋ nɛ teŋgu nɛ hila.».

eng: They didnt turn away from their murders, their spells and drugs, their sexual immorality, or their stealing.
local: Peteyele ɖɔɖɔ ɛyáa kʋʋ, nɛ pe-efeletu nɛ pa-acɛyɛ nɛ pa-mɩlɩm se pɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ.

eng: It forces everyone—the small and great, the rich and poor, the free and slaves—to have a mark put on their right hand or on their forehead.
local: Điiyeba nɛ payʋsɩ ajamaa nɛ ɖoŋ tɩnaa nɛ ñɩm tɩnaa nɛ kʋñɔndɩnaa nɛ mbá pɛwɛ pa-tɩ yɔɔ yɔ nɛ yomaa patɩŋa payɩ pe-nesi kɩbanzɩ yɔɔ yaa pe-kila taa.

eng: And I heard the altar say, Yes, Lord God Almighty, your judgments are true and just.
local: Ɛlɛ́, manɩɩ tɩlaaɖɩ ɖɩhɔɖɛ yɔɔdʋʋ se: «Ɛɛ, Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ Đoŋ Tɩŋa Tʋ! Ŋhʋʋ tɔm nɛ toovenim, ñɔ-tɔm hʋʋ tʋʋzaa.».

eng: Another angel, a second one, followed and said, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She made all the nations drink the wine of her lustful passion.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mantasɩ lɛlʋ naʋ, ɛtɩŋaɣ ɛ-wayɩ nɛ ɛtɔŋ se: «Tɩtɔlaa! Babilooni tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ tɔlaa kpem! Ńdʋ́ ñɔzɩnɩ ajɛya kpeekpe taa ɛyáa tɩ-sʋlʋm nɛ pʋkʋʋ-wɛ, ḿbʋ́ lɛ tɩ-acɛyɛ ndʋ́ tɩlakɩ yɔ.».

eng: It will not allow anyone to make a purchase or sell anything unless the person has the mark with the beasts name or the number of its name.
local: Yee yʋsaɣ ŋ́gá yaa kpɩnɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-hɩɖɛ yaa ɖɩ-hɩɖɛ ñʋʋ ɛfɛyɩ nɔɔyʋ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛɛpɩzɩɣ ɛya pʋyʋ yaa ɛpɛdɩ.

eng: Then another angel, a third one, followed them and said in a loud voice, If any worship the beast and its image, and receive a mark on their foreheads or their hands.
local: Peeɖe mana tozo tʋ, ɛtɩŋaɣ kajalaɣ ñɩma wayɩ nɛ eholuu ɖoŋ se: «Yee nɔɔyʋ eluŋ kpɩnɛ nɛ ɖɩ-sɩʋ kpelaɣ pɛ-tɛɛ nɛ pala e-kiɖe taa yaa e-nesi yɔɔ yʋsaɣ yɔ.

eng: The fourth angel poured his bowl on the sun, and it was allowed to burn people with fire.
local: Naanza tʋ ñohuti ɛ-ñɩŋga wɩsɩ yɔɔ nɛ paha-sɩ waɖɛ se sɩ-miŋ ɛñaɣ ɛyáa.

eng: This calls for wisdom. Let the one who understands calculate the beasts number, for its a human beings number. Its number is six hundred sixty-six.
local: Cɩnɛ panaɣ lɔŋsɩnɖɛ: weyi ɛwɛnɩ lɛɣtʋ yɔ, ɛla kpɩnɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖɩ-hɩɖɛ ñʋʋ akɔnta. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kɩkɛ ɛyʋ mayaɣ. Ŋ́gʋ́ lɛ taŋga nɛ mɩnʋʋ nɛ nɩɩnʋwa nɛ hiu nɛ loɖo.

eng: they themselves will also drink the wine of Gods passionate anger, poured full strength into the cup of his wrath. They will suffer the pain of fire and sulfur in the presence of the holy angels and the Lamb.
local: pʋdʋ kɔŋ ñɔʋ Ɛsɔ pana sʋlʋm mbʋ́ papɩyɩ ɛ-pana caɖaɣ taa nɛ pataca pɩ-taa pʋyʋ kaʋ se pu-ɖoŋ ɛpasɩ yɔ: pɩtɛ ɛyáa kɔŋ tɔɔʋ kʋñɔŋ siŋŋ miŋ sɔsɔŋ nɛ tɩm pa-taa, Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ ñɩma nɛ Heɣa pɛ-ɛsɩndaa.

eng: The people were burned by intense heat, and they cursed the name of the God who had power over these plagues. But they didnt change their hearts and lives and give him glory.
local: Peeɖe hohou sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ kpa-wɛ nɛ pɛwɛɛkɩɣ Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ. Ŋgʋ́, ɛnʋ wɛnɩnɩ ɖoŋ kʋñɔmɩŋ ɩnʋ ɩ-yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɛtɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ se peyele nɛ ɛ-hɩɖɛ sɛɛ.

eng: The smoke of their painful suffering goes up forever and always. There is no rest day or night for those who worship the beast and its image, and those who receive the mark of its name.
