local: Miŋ sɔsɔŋ ɛnʋ, ɛ-ñɔsɩ ñɛyɩɣ pɩnzɩ ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ. Mbá poluŋuu kpɩnɛ nɛ ɖɩ-sɩʋ kpelaɣ pɛ-tɛɛ yɔ nɛ mbá palabɩ pɔ-yɔɔ ɖɩ-hɩɖɛ yʋsaɣ yɔ, paahɛzɩɣ wɩsɩ nɛ ɖoó.».

eng: The fifth angel poured his bowl over the beasts throne, and darkness covered its kingdom. People bit their tongues because of their pain.
local: Kagbaanzɩ tʋ ñohuti ɛ-ñɩŋga kpɩnɛ ńɖɩ́ ɖi-kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ nɛ cɩkpɛndʋʋ yu ɖi-kewiyaɣ yɔɔ. Ɛyáa nɩɣaɣ siziŋ nɛ pasalɩɣ pe-kela.

eng: This calls for the endurance of the saints, who keep Gods commandments and keep faith with Jesus.
local: Cɩnɛ panaɣ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ ñɩma mbá pɔɖɔŋ ɛ-paɣtʋ yɔɔ nɛ pɛwɛnɩ lidaʋ Yesu yɔɔ yɔ pɔɖɔkɩ pa-tɩ yɔ.

eng: and they cursed the God of heaven because of their pains and sores; but they didnt turn away from what they had done.
local: nɛ pɛwɛɛkɩɣ Ɛsɔ weyi ɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ ɛ-hɩɖɛ pe-siziŋ nɩʋ nɛ pa-kɩcɩkpɩŋ pɔ-yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɛtɛlɛɣzɩ wɛtʋ.

eng: And I heard a voice from heaven say, Write this: Favored are the dead who die in the Lord from now on. Yes, says the Spirit, so they can rest from their labors, because their deeds follow them.
local: Peeɖe manɩ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-m nɛ ɛsɔdaa se: «Ma se pɩkpaɣʋnɩ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ yɔ, mbá pasɩkɩ pa nɛ Kɩpaɣlʋ pɛ-kpɛndʋʋ taa yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ!» Ɛlɛ́, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tisi se: «Ḿbʋ́ lɛ! Piyeki nɛ pɛhɛzɩnɩ pa-tʋma: pɔkɔŋ-wɛ hɛyʋʋ a-yɔɔ.».

eng: Then the sixth angel poured his bowl on the great river Euphrates. Its water was dried up so that the way was ready for the kings from the east.
local: Loɖo tʋ ñohuti ɛ-ñɩŋga pɔɔ sɔsɔɔ ŋgá payaɣ se Efraatɩ yɔ ka-taa nɛ lɩm sɔɔ se awiya wena alɩɣnɩ wɩsɩ ɖɩlɩyɛ yɔ, apɩzɩ akɔɔ.

eng: Then I looked, and there was a white cloud. On the cloud was seated someone who looked like the Human One. He had a gold crown on his head and a sharp sickle in his hand.
local: Mantasɩ cɔnaʋ lɛ, mana ɛsɔmɩnʋʋ kʋhʋlʋmʋʋ nakʋyʋ. Nɔɔyʋ caɣ kʋ-yɔɔ, ɛpam sika hulaɣ nɛ ɛɖɔkɩ loozo ŋgʋ́ kɩpɩɣ camɩyɛ yɔ e-nesi taa.

eng: Then I saw three unclean spirits, like frogs, come from the dragons mouth, the beasts mouth, and the mouth of the false prophet.
local: Peeɖe mana feziŋ kɩdɛkɛdɩŋ nɩɩyɩ naadozo, ɩwɛ ɛzɩ sayɛɛ yɔ. Ɩlɩɣaɣnɩ coojo nɛ kpɩnɛ nɛ weyi ɛcɛtɩɣ se ɛkɛ náyʋ yɔ pɔ-nɔsɩ taa.

eng: Another angel came out of the temple, calling in a loud voice to the one seated on the cloud: Use your sickle to reap the harvest, for the time to harvest has come, and the harvest of the earth is ripe.
local: Ɛsɔ tiyiyu lɛlʋ ñalɩnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa nɛ ɛmakɩ kubusi ɖoŋ nɛ ɛyɔɔdʋʋnɩ weyi ɛcaɣ ɛsɔmɩnʋʋ yɔɔ yɔ se: «Tisi ño-loozo nɛ ŋkʋ. Alɩwaatʋ talaa se pakʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɔɔnaɣ pɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ kpem!».

eng: These are demonic spirits that do signs. They go out to the kings of the whole world, to gather them for battle on the great day of God the Almighty. .
local: Ɩkɛ aleewaa feziŋ weyi ɩwɩlɩɣ kawɩlɩsɩ yɔ. Iwoki kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ awiya cɔlɔ se ikpeɣli-wɛ nɛ pala you Ɛsɔ Đoŋ Tɩŋa Tʋ kɩyakʋ sɔsɔʋ wiye.

eng: So the one seated on the cloud swung his sickle over the earth, and the earth was harvested.
local: Peeɖe weyi ɛcaɣ mɩnʋʋ yɔɔ yɔ, etisi e-loozo nɛ ɛkʋ kedeŋa yɔɔ tɔɔnaɣ tɩŋa.

eng: Look! Im coming like a thief! Favored are those who stay awake and clothed so that they dont go around naked and exposed to shame.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Kɩpaɣlʋ tɔ se: «Ɩna, montuduu-mɩ ɛzɩ mɩ́lʋ́ yɔ. Weyi ɛcakɩnɩ ɛ-ɛsɛ nɛ ɛɖaŋ e-wondu se ɛtaaɖɔ ndaakpeɖe nɛ pana ɛ-fɛyɛ yɔ, ehiɣ ɛ-tɩ!».

eng: Then another angel came out of the temple in heaven, and he also had a sharp sickle.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, lɛlʋ lɩɩnɩ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa ŋgá kɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ ka-taa, ɛɖɔkɩ ɖɔɖɔ loozo ŋgʋ́ kɩpɩɣ yɔ.

eng: The spirits gathered them at the place that is called in Hebrew, Harmagedon.
local: Piyele feziŋ ɩnɩ, ikpeɣli awiya ana lone nɖɩ́ payaɣnɩ Ebree taa se Armaagedɔɔ yɔ ɖɩ-taa.

eng: Still another angel, who has power over fire, came out from the altar. He said in a loud voice to the one who had the sharp sickle, Use your sharp sickle to cut the clusters in the vineyard of the earth, because its grapes are ripe.
local: Tiyiyu lɛlʋ weyi ɛwɛnɩ ɖoŋ miŋ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ ñalɩnɩ tɩlaaɖɩ ɖɩhɔɖɛ nɛ eholuuni weyi ɛɖɔkɩ loozo ŋgʋ́ kɩpɩɣ yɔ se: «Tisi ño-loozo ŋgʋ́ kɩpɩɣ yɔ nɛ ŋkoyi tɩŋ weyi popuu i-pee sʋlʋm yɔ i-pee kedeŋa yɔɔ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, apɩwa.».

eng: Then the seventh angel poured his bowl into the air, and a loud voice came out from the temple, from the throne, saying, It is done.
local: Lʋbɛ tʋ ñohuti ɛ-ñɩŋga ɛsɔdaa nɛ puholuuni Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa taa kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ cɔlɔ ɖoŋ se: «Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́!».

eng: So the angel swung his sickle into the earth, and cut the vineyard of the earth, and he put what he reaped into the great winepress of Gods passionate anger.
local: Peeɖe Ɛsɔ tiyiyu ɛnʋ etisi e-loozo nɛ ekoyi tɩŋ ɩnʋ i-pee kedeŋa yɔɔ nɛ ɛlɔ-yɛ Ɛsɔ pana pulone sɔsɔɖɛ taa ɖenɖe pɔhɔyʋʋ-yɛ yɔ.

eng: There were lightning strikes, voices, and thunder, and a great earthquake occurred. The earthquake was greater than any that have occurred since there have been people on earth.
local: Peeɖe tɛʋ ñɩlɩsaa nɛ kɔkɔɖɛ ya nɛ tɛʋ nazɩ nɛ tɛtʋ ñamsɩ ñamsʋʋ nabʋyʋ kpem. Đooo ɛzɩ ɛyáa wɛʋ kedeŋa yɔɔ yɔ, wiɖiyi tɩtañamsɩ ḿbʋ́.

eng: Then the winepress was trampled outside the city, and the blood came out of the winepress as high as the horses bridles for almost two hundred miles.
local: Ɛlɛ́, palɩzɩ pulone ńɖɩ́ nɛ tɛtʋ wayɩ nɛ pɔhɔyɩ-yɛ nɛ calɩm lɩɩnɩ ɖɩ-taa nɛ pikpe nɛ pɩtalɩ kiloomɛtanaa nasidozo nɛ cukaŋ taa ñɛwɛ ɛzɩ mɛta nɛ hɔɔlʋʋ yɔ.

eng: The great city split into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell. God remembered Babylon the great so that he gave her the wine cup of his furious anger.
local: Tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ tayɩ tam naadozo taa nɛ ajɛya taa ñɩndʋ ñɛkpɛzɩɣ tɩɖakɩ. Ḿbʋ́ Ɛsɔ tɔzɩnɩ Babilooni tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ yɔɔ nɛ ɛñɔzɩ tɩ-taa ñɩma ɛ-pana sɔsɔna sʋlʋm caɖaɣ.

eng: Every island fled, and the mountains disappeared.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, ajɛya wena atɩŋa payɩ lɩm cɔ-yɛ pɩta yɔ, ase nɛ paatasɩɣ pʋŋ naʋ.

eng: Huge hailstones weighing about one hundred pounds came down from heaven on the people. They cursed God for the plague of hail, because the plague was so terrible.
local: Đɔmbɛɛ sɔsɔna naayɛ lɩɣaɣnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ atɔlʋʋ ɛyáa yɔɔ. Kʋɖʋmɖɩyɛ yuŋ talɩ ɛzɩ kiiloonaa nɩɩnaza yɔ nɛ ɛyáa wɛɛkɩɣ Ɛsɔ hɩɖɛ kʋñɔŋ ŋgʋ́ ɖɔmbɛɛ kɔnɩ-wɛ yɔ kʋ-yɔɔ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kɩɩɖɛlɛsɩ niye.

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the judgment upon the great prostitute, who is seated on deep waters.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa lʋbɛ mbá pɔɖɔkɩ sʋlʋm caɖasɩ ńzɩ́ sɩ-lʋbɛ yɔ, pa-taa lɛlʋ kɔɔ ɛyɔɔdʋʋnɩ-m se: «Kɔɔ, mawɩlɩɣ-ŋ ɛzɩma mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ hʋʋnɩ abiɖe nɖɩ́ ɖɩpɛdɩɣ ɖɩ-tɩ yɔ. Ńɖɩ́ lɛ tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ ndʋ́ tɩwɛ pɔsɩ nɔsɩ yɔ.

eng: The kings of the earth have committed sexual immorality with her, and those who live on earth have become drunk with the wine of her whoring.
local: Tɩ nɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ awiya palabɩ acɛyɛ nɛ kɔ-yɔɔ ñɩma ñɔɔ ɖɩ-acɛyɛ sʋlʋm nɛ pʋkʋʋ-wɛ.».

eng: Then he brought me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a desert. There I saw a woman seated on a scarlet beast that was covered with blasphemous names. It had seven heads and ten horns.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tii mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ e-tiyiyu ɛnʋ ɛkpaɣ-m nɛ ewoni lakʋ nakʋyʋ taa. Peeɖe mana halʋ nɔɔyʋ, ɛcaɣ kpɩnɛ kɩsɛmɖɩyɛ cɩɩɩ naɖɩyɛ yɔɔ. Đɩwɛnɩ hɩla wena atʋʋ Ɛsɔ yɔ ɖɩ-yɔɔ tɔtɔ nɛ ñʋŋ lʋbɛ nɛ hɩŋ hiu.

eng: The woman wore purple and scarlet clothing, and she glittered with gold and jewels and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup full of the vile and impure things that came from her activity as a prostitute.
local: Halʋ ɛnʋ, esu wondu ndʋ́ tiwobini sɛmɩŋ yɔɔ yɔ nɛ ndʋ́ tɩsɛ cɩɩɩ yɔ nɛ ɛlɛɛ sika nɩŋgbacaɣlɩnaa nɛ ñɩɣlɩ́m pɛɛ nɛ lɩtʋ. Ɛɖɔkɩ sika caɖaɣ nakɛyɛ, ɛ-azuluma nɛ ɛ-acɛyɛ lakasɩ nzɩ́ sipisiɣ-i yɔ posuni ka-taa ɖo.

eng: A name—a mystery—was written on her forehead: Babylon the great, the mother of prostitutes and the vile things of the earth.
local: Pama e-kiɖe taa hɩɖɛ naɖɩyɛ. Paanɩɣ ɖɩ-tɔbʋʋ, ńɖɩ́ lɛ se: «Babilooni tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ, halaa mbá pɛpɛdɩɣ pa-tɩ yɔ nɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ azuluma po-ɖoo.».

eng: I saw that the woman was drunk on the blood of the saints and the blood of Jesus witnesses. I was completely stunned when I saw her.
local: Peeɖe manawa se halʋ ɛnʋ ɛñɔ Ɛsɔ ñɩma nɛ Yesu aseɣɖe tɩnaa mbá pakʋ-wɛ yɔ pa-calɩm nɛ pʋkʋʋ-ɩ ɛzɩ sʋlʋm yɔ. Mana-ɩ lɛ, pɩɖɩɣzɩ-m yem ɖɩɣ ɖɩɣ.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Why are you amazed? I will tell you the mystery of the woman and the seven-headed, ten-horned beast that carries her.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ tiyiyu pɔzɩ-m se: «Pɩlabɩ ɛzɩma nɛ pɩɖɩɣzɩɣ-ŋ ḿbʋ́? Malɩzɩɣ-ŋ halʋ nɛ kpɩnɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛnɩ ñʋŋ lʋbɛ nɛ hɩŋ hiu nɛ ɛcaɣ-ɖɩ yɔ pɛ-mɛsaɣ tɛɛ tɔm tɔbʋʋ.

eng: The beast that you saw was and is not, and is about to come up out of the abyss and go to destruction. Those who live on earth, whose names havent been written in the scroll of life from the time the earth was made, will be amazed when they see the beast, because it was and is not and will again be present.
local: Kpɩnɛ nɖɩ́ ŋna ḿbʋ́ yɔ, ɖɩɩwɛɛ, ɛlɛ́, lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɖɩfɛyɩ. Đɩkɔŋ lɩnaʋ asayʋʋ pɔʋ taa nɛ ɖiwolo tɛŋɛlʋʋ taa. Ɛlɛ́, kedeŋa yɔɔ ñɩma mbá patama pa-hɩla wezuu takayaɣ taa ɖooo ɛzɩ kedeŋa wɛʋ yɔ, panaɣ-ɖɩ lɛ, pɩɖɩɣzɩɣ-wɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖɩɩwɛɛ nɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɖɩfɛyɩ, ɛlɛ́, ɖɩkɔŋ ɖaɣnʋʋ lɩʋ.

eng: This calls for an understanding mind. The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman is seated. They are also seven kings.
local: «Pʋpɔzʋʋ cɩnɛ lɛɣtʋ nɛ lɔŋsɩnɖɛ: ñʋŋ lʋbɛ ɩnʋ lɛ pʋŋ lʋbɛ weyi ɩ-yɔɔ halʋ ɛnʋ ɛcaɣaɣ yɔ. Ɩnʋ ɖɔɖɔ lɛ awiya lʋbɛ:.

eng: Five kings have fallen, the one is, and the other hasnt yet come. When that king comes, he must remain for only a short time.
local: a-taa kagbaanzɩ tɔm tɛm ɖɛʋ, lɛlʋ wɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ, lɛlʋ ñɔtɔkɔmda. Ɛkɔŋ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se ɛcaɣ alɩwaatʋ pazɩ yem.

eng: As for the beast that was and is not, it is itself an eighth king that belongs to the seven, and it is going to destruction.
local: Kpɩnɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩɩwɛɛ nɛ lɛɛlɛɛyɔ ɖɩfɛyɩ yɔ, ɖɩ-maɣmaɣ ɖɩkɛ wiyaʋ lutozo ñɩnʋ. Đɩkɛ ɖɔɖɔ awiya lʋbɛ ana a-taa lɛlʋ nɛ ɖiwoki tɛŋɛlʋʋ taa.

eng: The ten horns that you saw are ten kings, who havent yet received royal power. But they will receive royal authority for an hour, along with the beast.
local: «Hɩŋ hiu weyi ŋnawa yɔ, ɩkɛ awiya sɔsɔna hiu wena pɛtɛcɛlɩdɩ-yɛ kewiyisi yɔ. Ɛlɛ́, a nɛ kpɩnɛ pɔkɔŋ tɔɔʋ kewiyaɣ alɩwaatʋ pazɩ yem.

eng: These kings will be of one mind, and they will give their power and authority to the beast.
local: Pɛwɛnɩ lɩmaɣzɩyɛ kʋɖʋmɖɩyɛ. Ńɖɩ́ lɛ se: paha kpɩnɛ po-ɖoŋ nɛ pa-abalɩtʋ.

eng: They will make war on the Lamb, but the Lamb will emerge victorious, for he is Lord of lords and King of kings. Those with him are called, chosen, and faithful.
local: Pɔkɔŋ yonaʋ Heɣa, ɛlɛ́, kawakɩ-wɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kɛkɛ kɩpaɣlaa taa Kɩpaɣlʋ nɛ awiya taa Wiyaʋ. Mbá Ɛsɔ ya-wɛ nɛ ɛlɩzɩ-wɛ nɛ pɔɖɔŋ ɛ-nʋmɔʋ taa nɛ pa nɛ kɛ pɛwɛɛ yɔ, pawakɩ-wɛ ɖɔɖɔ.».

eng: Then he said to me, The waters that you saw, where the prostitute is seated, are peoples, crowds, nations, and languages.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ tiyiyu ɛnʋ eheyi-m se: «Lɩm mbʋ́ ŋnawa nɛ halʋ weyi ɛpɛdɩɣ ɛ-tɩ yɔ ɛcaɣ pʋ-yɔɔ yɔ, ḿbʋ́ lɛ samaɣ nɛ ɛyáa tikasɩ nɛ ajɛya nɛ kʋnʋmɩŋ ndɩ ndɩ pa-taa ñɩma.

eng: As for the ten horns that you saw, they and the beast will hate the prostitute. They will destroy her and strip her bare. They will devour her flesh and burn her with fire.
local: Piyele hɩŋ hiu weyi ŋnawa yɔ nɛ kpɩnɛ pɔkɔŋ halʋ ɛnʋ paɖʋʋ: pɔkɔŋ mʋ́ʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ɛwɛna yɔ nɛ pɔhɔzɩ e-wondu nɛ piyebi-i ndaakpeɖe. Pɔkɔŋ tɔɔʋ ɛ-nandʋ nɛ pɩkazɩ pɔsɔ miŋ.

eng: because God moved them to carry out his purposes. That is why they will be of one mind and give their royal power to the beast, until Gods words have been accomplished.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ ɖʋ pa-taa se pala mbʋ́ e-liu wɛɛ yɔ. Ḿbʋ́ lɛ se panɩɩnɩ ɖama nɛ pɛcɛlɩ kpɩnɛ pe-kewiyisi nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ Ɛsɔtɔm laba yɔ.

eng: The woman whom you saw is the great city that rules over the kings of the earth.
local: «Halʋ weyi ŋnawa yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ lɛ tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ ndʋ́ tinuutuu kedeŋa yɔɔ awiya yɔ.».

eng: After this I saw another angel coming down from heaven. He had great authority, and the earth was filled with light because of his glory.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mana Ɛsɔ tiyiyu lɛlʋ, ɛlɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ etiki. Ɛwɛnɩ ɖoŋ siŋŋ nɛ e-koboyaɣ ñalɩmɩyɛ ñalɩ kedeŋa yɔɔ.

eng: He called out with a loud voice, saying, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She has become a home for demons and a lair for every unclean spirit. She is a lair for every unclean bird, and a lair for every unclean and disgusting beast.
local: Eholaɣ ɖoŋ se: «Tɩtɔlaa! Babilooni tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ tɔlaa! Tɩpɩsɩ aleewaa ɖɩjaɣyɛ. Peeɖe feziŋ kɩdɛkɛdɩŋ tɩŋa nɛ sumasɩ kɩdɛkɛdɩsɩ nɛ nzɩ́ ɛyáa ɛɛcaɣ kpem yɔ patɩŋa pɛwɛɛ.

eng: because all the nations have fallen due to the wine of her lustful passion. The kings of the earth committed sexual immorality with her, and the merchants of the earth became rich from the power of her loose and extravagant ways.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ajɛya kpeekpe taa ɛyáa ñɔ tɩ-acɛyɛ sʋlʋm nɛ pʋkʋʋ-wɛ. Tɩ nɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ awiya palabɩ acɛyɛ nɛ kɔ-yɔɔ tadɩyɛ laɖaa hiɣ ñɩm tɩ-ajana kadadaɣyaɣ yɔɔ.».

eng: Then I heard another voice from heaven say, Come out of her, my people, so that you dont take part in her sins and dont receive any of her plagues.
local: Peeɖe mantasɩ nɩʋ nɔɔ lɛɛka nɛ ɛsɔdaa se: «Ma-ñɩma! Ɩlɩɩ tɩ-taa nɛ ɩtaakɔɔ ɩɖʋ mi-nesi tɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm taa. Kʋñɔmɩŋ weyi ɩkɔŋ-tʋ talʋʋ yɔ, ɩ-taa nakʋyʋ ɩtaatalɩ-mɩ.

eng: Her sins have piled up as high as heaven, and God remembered her unjust acts.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɩ-kɩwɛɛkɩm ka huuye nɛ pɩkpa ɛsɔdaa nɛ Ɛsɔ na mbʋ́ tɩlaba nɛ pɩtatʋʋzɩ yɔ.

eng: Give her what she has given to others. Give her back twice as much for what she has done. In the cup that she has poured, pour her twice as much.
local: Mɩ lɛ, ɩlabɩ-tʋ ɛzɩ tɩlabʋ lɛlaa yɔ, ɩkpa-tʋ kɩmɩyɛ pʋ-tam naalɛ. Ɩpɩyɩ-tʋ sʋlʋm ɖoŋ ñɩmbʋ mbʋ́ pɩpɩyɩ lɛlaa se pʋkʋ-wɛ yɔ pʋ-tam naalɛ.

eng: To the extent that she glorified herself and indulged her loose and extravagant ways, give her pain and grief. In her heart she says, I sit like a queen! Im not a widow. Ill never see grief.
local: Ɩcɔzɩ-tʋ nɛ iyele tɩ-ɛsɛ ñɩŋ tam ɛzɩma mbʋ́ tɩkʋndaa nɛ tɩsʋʋ ajana taa yɔ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɩmaɣzɩɣ tɩ-taa se: ‹Weyi ma mɛnkɛ abiɖe yɔ, mancaɣnɩ cɩnɛ. Mɛntɛkɛ lelu, maakaɣ caɣʋ leɣa nakɛyɛ kaʋ.›.

eng: This is why her plagues will come in a single day—deadly disease, grief, and hunger. She will be consumed by fire because the Lord God who judges her is powerful.
local: Pʋ-yɔɔ tɩ-kʋñɔmɩŋ kaɣ kɔm kɩyakʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ. Ɩnʋ lɛ: sɩm nɛ leɣa caɣʋ nɛ ñɔɔsɩ. Miŋ kɔŋ-tʋ ñaɣʋ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ weyi ɛhʋʋnɩ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ yɔ, ɛwɛ ɖoŋ.

eng: The kings of the earth, who committed sexual immorality with her and shared her loose and extravagant ways, will weep and mourn over her when they see the smoke from her burning.
local: «Kedeŋa yɔɔ awiya wena a nɛ tʋ palabɩ acɛyɛ nɛ ajana yɔ, akɔŋ wiluu atɩŋa payɩ nɛ atɩmɩɣ tɩ-yɔɔ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ anaɣ miŋ weyi ɛñakɩ-tʋ yɔ ɛ-ñɔsɩ yɔ.

eng: They will stand a long way off because they are afraid of the pain she suffers, and they will say, Oh, the horror! Babylon, you great city, you powerful city! In a single hour your judgment has come.
local: Kʋñɔŋ ŋgʋ́ kɩtalɩɣ-tʋ yɔ kʋ-sɔɔndʋ yɔɔ lɛ, pasɩŋɩɣ poliŋ nɛ pɔtɔŋ se: ‹Babilooni tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ, tɛtʋ ɖoŋ ñɩndʋ, pʋtɔɔ-ŋ! Pʋtɔɔ-ŋ! Pahʋʋnɩ-ŋ nɛ pakʋ ñɔ-tɔm yem kɛlɛʋ!›.

eng: The merchants of the earth will weep and mourn over her, for no one buys their cargoes anymore—.
local: «Piyele kedeŋa yɔɔ tadɩyɛ laɖaa lɛ, pewiliɣ nɛ pacaɣ ti-leɣa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, nɔɔyʋ ɛɛtasɩɣ po-wondu yabʋ.

eng: cargoes of gold, silver, jewels, and pearls; fine linen, purple, silk, and scarlet; all those things made of scented wood, ivory, fine wood, bronze, iron, and marble;.
local: Mbʋ́ pɛpɛdaɣ yɔ lɛ: sika wondu nɛ ndʋ́ palabɩnɩ liidiye yɔ nɛ ñɩɣlɩ́m pɛɛ nɛ lɩtʋ nɛ pɩsaŋ fɛlɛ fɛlɛ ñɩŋ nɛ sotu ñɩŋ nɛ weyi iwobini sɛmɩŋ yɔɔ yɔ nɛ kɩsɛmɩŋ cɩɩɩ nɛ mbʋ́ pasaɣnɩ tɩŋ weyi ɩlɩɣ sɔzɩŋ yɔ nɛ weyi ɩwɛ liidiye yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ palabɩnɩ tu kela yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ palabɩnɩ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ kazɩnzɩŋa ñɩŋgʋ yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ palabɩnɩ ñɩɣtʋ yɔ nɛ mbʋ́ palabɩnɩ pɛɛ wena payaɣ se maarbɩɖɩ yɔ.

eng: cinnamon, incense, fragrant ointment, and frankincense; wine, oil, fine flour, and wheat; cattle, sheep, horses, and carriages; and slaves, even human lives.
local: Pɛpɛdaɣ ɖɔɖɔ mbʋ́ pacanɩɣnɩ ɖozi yɔ nɛ tɩlaaɖɩnaa ndɩ ndɩ nɛ tɩlaaɖɩ ŋgʋ́ pɔhɔkɩ yɔ nɛ kɔyɛ nɖɩ́ payaɣ se miiri yɔ nɛ sʋlʋm nɛ num nɛ mʋlʋm nɛ mɩla nɛ naŋ nɛ heŋ nɛ kpaŋnaŋ nɛ lɔɔɖa wena kpaŋnaŋ hɔŋ yɔ nɛ yomaa nɛ ɛyáa lɛlaa.

eng: The fruit your whole being craved has gone from you. All your glitter and glamour are lost to you, never ever to be found again.
local: Tadɩyɛ laɖaa ḿbá, paba heyiɣ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ se: ‹Mbʋ́ pɩtɩŋa payɩ ñe-liu kɛwɛɛ kpem nɛ ŋwɛnɩ-pʋ yɔ, pʋmʋʋ ñɛ-tɛ. Ña-ñɩm tɩŋa payɩ nɛ ña-ajana peleba. Ŋŋtasɩɣ-wɛ naʋ kaʋ.›.

eng: The merchants who sold these things, and got so rich by her, will stand a long way off because they fear the pain she suffers. They will weep and mourn, and say.
local: Tadɩyɛ laɖaa mbá pɩtɩŋnɩ tɩ-yɔɔ nɛ pehiɣ ñɩm yɔ, tɩ-kʋñɔŋ sɔɔndʋ kɔŋ-wɛ kpaʋ nɛ pasɩŋ poliŋ nɛ pewili nɛ pacaɣ leɣa.

eng: Oh, the horror! The great city that wore fine linen, purple, and scarlet, who glittered with gold, jewels, and pearls—.
local: Pɔkɔŋ yɔɔdʋʋ se: ‹Pʋtɔɔ tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ! Pʋtɔɔ-tʋ! Tisu wondu fɛlɛ fɛlɛ ñɩndʋ nɛ ndʋ́ tiwobini sɛmɩŋ yɔɔ yɔ nɛ kɩsɛmtʋ cɩɩɩ nɛ tɩlɛɛ sika nɩŋgbacaɣlɩnaa nɛ ñɩɣlɩ́m pɛɛ nɛ lɩtʋ.

eng: in just one hour such great wealth was destroyed. Every sea captain, every seafarer, sailors, and all who make their living on the sea stood a long way off.
local: Ɛlɛ́, tɩ-ñɩm sakɩyɛ ḿbʋ́, pʋmʋʋ yem alɩwaatʋ pazɩ taa.› «Mɛlɛnaa sayaa tɩŋa payɩ nɛ pa-sʋyaa nɛ mbá palakɩ tʋmɩyɛ pa-taa yɔ nɛ mbá patɩŋa payɩ paba ñalakɩ tʋmɩyɛ teŋgu yɔɔ yɔ, pasɩŋ poliŋ.

eng: They cried out as they saw the smoke from her burning and said, What city was ever like the great city.
local: nɛ pamakɩ kubusi alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ panaɣaɣ miŋ weyi ɛñakɩ-tʋ yɔ ɛ-ñɔsɩ yɔ se: ‹Tɛtʋ natʋyʋ taawɛɛ nɛ tɩtalɩ tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ tʋnɛ!›.

eng: They threw dust on their heads, and they cried out, weeping and mourning. They said, Oh, the horror! The great city, where all who have ships at sea became so rich by her prosperity—in just one hour she was destroyed.
local: Pokuwaɣ mʋzʋʋ nɛ paɖʋʋ pa-ñʋŋ taa nɛ pewiliɣ nɛ pacaɣ leɣa nɛ pamakɩ kubusi se: ‹Pʋtɔɔ tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ! Pʋtɔɔ-tʋ! Tɩ-ñɩm yɔɔ pɩtɩŋaɣnaa nɛ mbá patɩŋa payɩ pɛwɛnɩ mɛlɛnaa teŋgu taa yɔ, pehikiɣ ñɩm. Ɛlɛ́, alɩwaatʋ pazɩ pakpaɣ pɔyɔkɩnɩ-tʋ!› ».

eng: Rejoice over her, heaven—you saints, apostles, and prophets—because God has condemned her as she condemned you.
local: Mbá mɩ ɩwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, mɩ-taa ɛhʋlʋmɩ! Pɔyɔkɩ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́! Mɩ Ɛsɔ ñɩma nɛ Yesu tiyiyaa nɛ náyaa mɩ-taa ɛhʋlʋmɩnɩ tɩ-yɔkʋʋ! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ hʋnɩ-tʋ tɔm kɩdɛkɛdɩm mbʋ́ tɩlabɩ-mɩ yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ.

eng: Then a powerful angel picked up a stone that was like a huge millstone and threw it into the sea, saying, With such violent force the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, and it wont be found anymore.
local: Peeɖe Ɛsɔ tiyiyu ɖoŋ tʋ nɔɔyʋ kpaɣ pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ ɛzɩ namɩyɛ sɔsɔɖɛ yɔ nɛ ɛlɔ teŋgu taa nɛ ɛtɔ se: «Ḿbʋ́ pɔkɔŋ lɔʋ Babilooni tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ, nɛ tɩyɔɔkɩ tam kʋɖʋmʋm yem kɛlɛʋ! Paatasɩɣ-tʋ naʋ kaʋ.

eng: The sound of harpists and musicians, of pipers and trumpeters, will never be heard among you again. No craftsman of any kind will ever be found among you again. The sound of the hand mill will never be heard among you again.
local: Nɔɔyʋ ɛɛtasɩɣ nɩʋ tɩ-taa kogoye naɖɩyɛ kaʋ nɔɔyʋ ɛɛtasɩɣ nɩʋ cɩmɩŋ maɖaa taŋ nɔɔyʋ ɛɛtasɩɣ nɩʋ hɩsɩ hʋlɩyaa nɛ agandɛɛ hʋlɩyaa pa-taŋ Paatasɩɣ naʋ mbá palakɩ nesi tʋma yɔ pa-taa nɔɔyʋ kaʋ Paatasɩɣ nɩʋ ɖɔɖɔ se nɔɔyʋ naŋɩɣ.

eng: The light of a lamp will never shine among you again. The sound of a bridegroom and bride will never be heard among you again because your merchants ran the world, because all the nations were deceived by the spell you cast, and because.
local: Kanɖʋnaa ñalɩmɩyɛ ɛɛtasɩɣ naʋ. Paatasɩɣ nɩʋ mbá pakpaɣ ɖama fam nɛ fam yɔ pa-taŋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ: tɩ-taa tadɩyɛ laɖaa kɛkɛnɩ sɔsaa kedeŋa yɔɔ, nɛ tiyili ajɛya tɩŋa payɩ nɛ ti-efeletu.

eng: the blood of prophets, of saints, and of all who have been slaughtered on the earth was found among you.
local: Ɖɔɖɔ lɛ, pana tɩ-taa náyaa nɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩma nɛ mbá patɩŋa payɩ pɛcɛbɩ pe-liŋ tɛɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ cɩnɛ yɔ pa-calɩm.».

eng: Then I saw an angel coming down from heaven, holding in his hand the key to the abyss and a huge chain.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mana Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ lɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ etiki. Ɛɖɔkɩ asayʋʋ pɔʋ nɔɔ ñɩmɩyɛ nɛ yoŋgbasɩ sɔsɔsɩ nasɩyɩ e-nesi taa.

eng: After this I heard what sounded like a huge crowd in heaven. They said, Hallelujah! The salvation and glory and power of our God!.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, manɩɩ puholuuni ɛsɔdaa ɛzɩ samaɣ sɔsɔɔ nakɛyɛ nɔɔ yɔ se: «Aleluuya! Đɛ-Ɛsɔ tɩnɩ ñʋʋ yabʋ nɛ koboyaɣ nɛ ɖoŋ!.

eng: He seized the dragon, the old snake, who is the devil and Satan, and bound him for a thousand years.
local: Etiba lɛ, ewolo ɛkpa coojo nɛ ɛhɔkɩ-ɩ-sɩ se ɛwɛɛ ḿbʋ́ pɩnzɩ kutoku. Ɛnʋ lɛ ɖʋm kɩbɩnʋ weyi payaɣ se eleeu nɛ pɛtɛ patasɩ-ɩ se Satanɩ yɔ.

eng: His judgments are true and just, because he judged the great prostitute, who ruined the earth by her whoring, and he exacted the penalty for the blood of his servants from her hand.
local: Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɛhʋʋ tɔm nɛ toovenim, ɛ-tɔm hʋʋ tʋʋzaa. Ɛhʋnɩ abiɖe nɖɩ́ ɖɩpɛdaɣ ɖɩ-tɩ nɛ ɖɩwɛɛkɩ tɛtʋ nɛ ɖi-acɛyɛ lakasɩ yɔ nɛ ɛkpa-ɖɩ kɩmɩyɛ ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa mbá ɖɩkʋwa yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ.».

eng: He threw him into the abyss, then locked and sealed it over him. This was to keep him from continuing to deceive the nations until the thousand years were over. After this he must be released for a little while.
local: Ɛhɔkɩ-ɩ lɛ, ɛpɛɖɩ-ɩ asayʋʋ pɔʋ taa nɛ ɛɖɩɣ ɛ-yɔɔ nɛ ɛtaaɖɩ nɛ ɛñɩɣ nesi ɛ-yɔɔ se ɛtaatasɩ lɩʋ nɛ epeɣzi ajɛya taa ñɩma. Ɛwɛɣ peeɖe nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɩnzɩ kutoku ɖɛwa yɔ. Pʋwayɩ lɛ, pʋwɛɛ se patʋlɩ-ɩ nɛ pɛyɛɖɩ-ɩ alɩwaatʋ pazɩ.

eng: Then they said a second time, Hallelujah! Smoke goes up from her forever and always.
local: Mantasɩ nɩʋ se: «Aleluuya! Miŋ weyi ɛñakɩ tɛtʋ sɔsɔtʋ yɔ, ɛ-ñɔsɩ kʋyʋʋ pɩnzɩ ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ.».

eng: Then I saw thrones, and people took their seats on them, and judgment was given in their favor. They were the ones who had been beheaded for their witness to Jesus and Gods word, and those who hadnt worshipped the beast or its image, who hadnt received the mark on their forehead or hand. They came to life and ruled with Christ for one thousand years.
local: Mana ɖɔɖɔ kewiyisi kpelisi nasɩyɩ: mbá powokaɣ pacakɩ sɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, paha-wɛ ɖoŋ se pahʋʋnɩ ɛyáa tɔm. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, mana mbá pɛsɛtɩ pa-ñʋŋ yɔ pa-ciyamnaa. Paakʋ-wɛ , mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, palɩzaɣ Yesu aseɣɖe nɛ posusuu Ɛsɔtɔm. Ḿbá taaluŋni kpɩnɛ yaa ɖɩ-sɩʋ kpelaɣ pɛ-tɛɛ, patala pe-kila taa yaa pe-nesi yɔɔ ɖɩ-yʋsaɣ. Pefema nɛ pa nɛ Krɩstʋ pɔtɔɔ kewiyaɣ pɩnzɩ kutoku.

eng: The twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshipped God, who is seated on the throne, and they said, Amen. Hallelujah.
local: Peeɖe sɔsaa ḿbá pa-nɩɩlɛ nɛ naanza nɛ kpɩna ana a-naanza poluŋ Ɛsɔ weyi ɛcaɣ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ yɔ ɛ-tɛɛ nɛ pasaŋ-ɩ se: «Aleluuya! Pɩla ḿbʋ́!».

eng: The rest of the dead didnt come to life until the thousand years were over. This is the first resurrection.
local: Ḿbʋ́ lɛ kajalaɣ fem. Piyele sɩɖaa lɛlaa yɔ, paafeŋ nɛ pʋkɔɔ pɩsɩɩnɩ alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ pɩnzɩ kutoku ŋ́gʋ́ kɩtɛma yɔ.

eng: Then a voice went out from the throne and said, Praise our God, all you his servants, and you who fear him, both small and great.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, manɩɣ pʋyɔɔdʋʋnɩ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ se: «Mbá mɩ mɩtɩŋa payɩ ɩkɛ ɖɛ-Ɛsɔ tʋmlaɖaa nɛ ɩsɔɔndʋʋ-ɩ yɔ, ajamaa nɛ ɖoŋ tɩnaa ɩsam-ɩ!».

eng: Favored and holy are those who have a share in the first resurrection. The second death has no power over them, but they will be priests of God and of Christ, and will rule with him for one thousand years.
local: Mbá pefeŋ kajalaɣ fem ḿbʋ́ pɩ-taa yɔ, pɛkɛ Ɛsɔ ñɩma, pehiɣ pa-tɩ! Sɩm naalɛ ñɩmbʋ fɛyɩnɩ ɖoŋ pɔ-yɔɔ. Ɛlɛ́, pɛkɛɣ Ɛsɔ nɛ Krɩstʋ pɔ-cɔjɔnaa nɛ pa nɛ Krɩstʋ ɛnʋ, pɔtɔɔ kewiyaɣ pɩnzɩ kutoku.

eng: And I heard something that sounded like a huge crowd, like rushing water and powerful thunder. They said, Hallelujah! The Lord our God, the Almighty, exercised his royal power!.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, manɩɩ pʋyɔɔdʋʋ ɛzɩ samaɣ sɔsɔɔ nakɛyɛ nɔɔ yɔ yaa lɩm sɔsɔm falʋʋ yɔ yaa tɛʋ nazʋʋ ɖoŋ yɔ se: «Aleluuya! Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Kɩpaɣlʋ ɖɛ-Ɛsɔ Đoŋ Tɩŋa Tʋ sɩ e-Kewiyaɣ.

eng: When the thousand years are over, Satan will be released from his prison.
local: Pɩnzɩ kutoku ŋ́gʋ́ kʋkɔŋ kɩɖɛɛ lɛ, palɩzɩ Satanɩ nɛ ɛ-salaka taa.

eng: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the former heaven and the former earth had passed away, and the sea was no more.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mana ɛsɔdaa kɩfalʋʋ nakʋyʋ nɛ tɛtʋ kɩfatʋ natʋyʋ. Kajalaɣ ɛsɔdaa nɛ kajalaɣ tɛtʋ pamʋwaa, teŋgu tatasɩ wɛʋ.

eng: Let us rejoice and celebrate, and give him the glory, for the wedding day of the Lamb has come, and his bride has made herself ready.
local: Đa-laŋa ɛhʋlʋmɩ, ɖɩmayɩ amaya nɛ ɖiyele ɛ-hɩɖɛ sɛɛ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Heɣa halɩkpaɣyɛ kazandʋ talaa nɛ ka-halʋ ñɔɔzɩ ɛ-tɩ.

eng: He will go out to deceive the nations that are at the four corners of the earth—Gog and Magog. He will gather them for battle. Their number is like the sand of the sea.
local: Ɛlɩɣ lɛ, epeɣzi Gɔɔgɩ nɛ Maagɔɔgɩ, pʋ-tɔbʋʋ se kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ ajɛya taa ñɩma. Ekpeɣliɣ-wɛ se pala you. Pɔɖɔ ɛzɩ teŋgu nɔɔ kañɩŋa yɔ.

eng: I saw the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, made ready as a bride beautifully dressed for her husband.
local: Mana ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ tɛtʋ: Yeruusalɛm kɩfalʋʋ, kɩlɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ nɛ kitiki, kɩɩcanɩ kɩ-tɩ ɛzɩ pɛlɛ canʋʋ ɛ-tɩ se ewoki walʋʋ yɔ.

eng: She was given fine, pure white linen to wear, for the fine linen is the saints acts of justice.
local: Paha-ɩ waɖɛ se esuu wondu ndʋ́ tɩwɛ fɛlɛ fɛlɛ nɛ tɩñɩlɩzɩɣ nɛ tɩwɛɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ yɔ.» Fɛlɛ fɛlɛ wondu ńdʋ́ lɛ tʋma kɩbana wena Ɛsɔ ñɩma laba yɔ.

eng: They came up across the whole earth and surrounded the saints camp, the city that God loves. But fire came down from heaven and consumed them.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, manaɣ palɩ kedeŋa kpeekpe yɔɔ nɛ pɔcɔʋ Ɛsɔ ñɩma pɩlʋʋ nɛ tɛtʋ kʋsɔɔlɩtʋ nɛ pataŋ. Ɛlɛ́, miŋ nɩɩyɩ lɩɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa nɛ etii enezi-wɛ.

eng: I heard a loud voice from the throne say, Look! Gods dwelling is here with humankind. He will dwell with them, and they will be his peoples. God himself will be with them as their God.
local: Pɩkɛdaa lɛ, manɩɩ puholuuni kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ se: «Lɛɛlɛɛyɔ Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa wɛ ɛyáa cɔlɔ! Ɛcakɩ pɔ-cɔlɔ nɛ pɛkɛ ɛ-ñɩma. Ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛwɛɣ pɔ-cɔlɔ.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Write this: Favored are those who have been invited to the wedding banquet of the Lamb. He said to me, These are the true words of God.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Ɛsɔ tiyiyu heyi-m se: «Ma se: mbá paya-wɛ Heɣa halɩkpaɣyɛ kazandʋ tɔɔnaɣ ɖeɖe yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ!» Ɛtasɩ-m heyuu se: «Tɔm ńdʋ́, tɩkɛ Ɛsɔtɔm maɣmaɣ siŋŋ.».

eng: Then the devil, who had deceived them, was thrown into the lake of fire and sulfur, where the beast and the false prophet also were. There painful suffering will be inflicted upon them day and night, forever and always.
local: Eleeu weyi epeɣzaɣ-wɛ yɔ, pɛpɛɖɩ-ɩ miŋ sɔsɔŋ weyi ɛ-taa nɛ tɩm yɔ ɛ-taa. Peeɖe kpɩnɛ nɛ weyi ɛcɛtɩɣ se ɛkɛ náyʋ yɔ, pɛwɛɛ. Ḿbʋ́ panazɩɣ-wɛ wɩsɩ nɛ ɖoó pɩnzɩ ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ.

eng: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more. There will be no mourning, crying, or pain anymore, for the former things have passed away.
local: Ɛhɛzɩɣ pɛ-ɛsɩtɛɣlɩm tɩŋa. Sɩm yaa leɣa caɣʋ yaa atɩma yaa wɩzasɩ pa-taa nabʋyʋ ɛɛtasɩɣ wɛʋ. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, kajalaɣ wondu mʋwaa.».

eng: Then I fell at his feet to worship him. But he said, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters who hold firmly to the witness of Jesus. Worship God! The witness of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy.
local: Peeɖe mɔntɔlɩ ɛ-naŋgbanzɩ tɛɛ se moluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ. Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-m se: «Taala ḿbʋ́! Mɛnkɛ Ɛsɔ tʋmlaɖʋ ɛzɩ ña nɛ ña-taabalaa mbá pɔɖɔkɩ aseɣɖe nɖɩ́ Yesu lɩzaa yɔ. Ɛsɔ tɛɛ pʋwɛɛ se ŋluŋ!» Aseɣɖe nɖɩ́ Yesu lɩzɩɣ yɔ, ńɖɩ́ seɣtiɣni náyaa.

eng: Then I saw a great white throne and the one who is seated on it. Before his face both earth and heaven fled away, and no place was found for them.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mana kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ sɔsɔɔ kʋhʋlʋmaɣ nakɛyɛ nɛ weyi ɛcaɣ kɔ-yɔɔ yɔ. Ɛsɔdaa nɛ tɛtʋ pese ɛ-ɛsɩndaa, patatasɩ-wɛ naʋ.

eng: Then the one seated on the throne said, Look! Im making all things new. He also said, Write this down, for these words are trustworthy and true.
local: Peeɖe weyi ɛɛcaɣ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ yɔɔ yɔ, ɛtɔm se: «Na! Malakɩ wondu tɩŋa payɩ nɛ tɩpɩsɩ kɩfatʋ. Ma tɔm ńdʋ́, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, tɩkɛ toovenim, tɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́.».

eng: Then I saw heaven opened, and there was a white horse. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he judges and makes war justly.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, mana ɛsɔdaa tʋlaa nɛ kpaŋnɔ kʋhʋlʋm nɔɔyʋ lɩɩ. Weyi ɛcaɣ-ɩ yɔ, payaɣ-ɩ se Weyi Ɛlakɩ Mbʋ́ Ɛɖʋwa yɔ nɛ Toovenim Tʋ. Ɛhʋʋ tɔm nɛ ɛlakɩ you nɛ toovenim.

eng: I saw the dead, the great and the small, standing before the throne, and scrolls were opened. Another scroll was opened too; this is the scroll of life. And the dead were judged on the basis of what was written in the scrolls about what they had done.
local: Mana ɖɔɖɔ sɩɖaa: ɖoŋ tɩnaa nɛ ajamaa pasɩŋ kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ ŋ́gá kɛ-ɛsɩndaa. Pukuli takayɩsɩ nasɩyɩ. Pukuli lɛɛka ɖɔɖɔ, ŋ́gá lɛ wezuu takayaɣ. Mbʋ́ sɩɖaa laba yɔ, pʋ-tɔm paama takayɩsɩ ńzɩ́ sɩ-taa, pʋ-yɔɔ pahʋʋnɩ-wɛ.

eng: Then he said to me, All is done. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the thirsty I will freely give water from the life-giving spring.
local: Ɛtasɩ-m heyuu se: «Pɩlabɩ ḿbʋ́! Ma lɛ Nʋmɔɖɛyʋ nɛ Ɛɖɩɣyɛ, manpazɩnɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ, mɛntɛzɩɣnɩ-pʋ. Weyi lɔkɔtʋ ɖɔkɩ-ɩ yɔ, mahaɣ-ɩ hiɖe nɖɩ́ ɖɩ-taa lɩm haɣ wezuu yɔ ɖɩ-taa lɩm falaa.

eng: His eyes were like a fiery flame, and on his head were many royal crowns. He has a name written on him that no one knows but he himself.
local: Ɛ-ɛsɛ ɖeɣ miŋ pelu pelu ɛzɩ miŋ nzʋlʋmɩyɛ yɔ nɛ papam-ɩ awiya hulasi sakɩyɛ. Pama ɛ-yɔɔ hɩɖɛ naɖɩyɛ, nɔɔyʋ tasɩm-ɖɩ nɛ pɩtasɩnɩ-ɩ.

eng: The sea gave up the dead that were in it, and Death and the Grave gave up the dead that were in them, and people were judged by what they had done.
local: Teŋgu lɩzɩ sɩɖaa mbá paawɛ kɩ-taa yɔ nɛ sɩm nɛ ɖenɖe sɩɖaa wɛɛ yɔ, paba ñɛcɛlɩ mbá paawɛ pɔ-cɔlɔ yɔ nɛ pahʋʋnɩ paa weyi mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: Those who emerge victorious will inherit these things. I will be their God, and they will be my sons and daughters.
local: Weyi ɛwakɩ yɔ, malakɩ-ɩ kʋjɔʋ ŋ́gʋ́. Mɛnkɛɣ ɛ-Ɛsɔ nɛ ɛkɛ man-pɩɣa.

eng: He wore a robe dyed with blood, and his name was called the Word of God.
local: Esu toko sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ, peli-kʋ calɩm. Payaɣ-ɩ se: Ɛsɔtɔm.

eng: Then Death and the Grave were thrown into the fiery lake. This, the fiery lake, is the second death.
local: Pɛpɛɖɩ sɩm nɛ ɖenɖe sɩɖaa wɛɛ yɔ miŋ sɔsɔŋ taa. Miŋ sɔsɔŋ ɛnʋ lɛ: sɩm naalɛ ñɩmbʋ.

eng: But for the cowardly, the faithless, the vile, the murderers, those who commit sexual immorality, those who use drugs and cast spells, the idolaters and all liars—their share will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur. This is the second death.
local: Piyele sɔɔndʋ tɩnaa nɛ mbá paañaŋ Ɛsɔ yɔ nɛ kʋsɔɔtʋ tɩnaa nɛ ɛyáa kʋyaa nɛ acɛyɛ laɖaa nɛ fadɩŋ laɖaa nɛ agɔlɩma layaa nɛ cɛtɩyaa tɩŋa payɩ pe-ɖeɖe lɛ miŋ sɔsɔŋ weyi ɛ-taa nɛ tɩm nɛ ɛmʋʋ siŋŋ yɔ ɛ-taa: ḿbʋ́ lɛ sɩm naalɛ ñɩmbʋ.».

eng: Heavens armies, wearing fine linen that was white and pure, were following him on white horses.
local: Sɔɔjanaa sakɩyɛ mbá pɛwɛ ɛsɔdaa yɔ, posu wondu kʋhʋlʋmʋtʋ ndʋ́ tɩwɛ fɛlɛ fɛlɛ nɛ tɩwɛɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ yɔ nɛ pacaɣ kpaŋnaŋ kʋhʋlʋmɩŋ nɛ patɩŋɩɣ-ɩ.

eng: Then anyone whose name wasnt found written in the scroll of life was thrown into the fiery lake.
local: Weyi patana ɛ-hɩɖɛ wezuu takayaɣ taa yɔ, pɛpɛɖɩ-ɩ miŋ sɔsɔŋ taa.

eng: Then the angel showed me the river of life-giving water, shining like crystal, flowing from the throne of God and the Lamb.
local: Ɛsɔ tiyiyu ɛnʋ, ɛwɩlɩ-m ɖɔɖɔ pɔɔ ŋgá ka-taa lɩm haɣ wezuu yɔ. Lɩm ḿbʋ́, pʋwɛ kele kele ɛzɩ ñɩnʋʋ yɔ. Pɩlɩɣaɣnɩ Ɛsɔ nɛ Heɣa pe-kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ tɛɛ.

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the bride, the Lambs wife.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Ɛsɔ tiyiyaa lʋbɛ mbá paaɖɔkɩ kɛtɛzaɣ kʋñɔmɩŋ lʋbɛ caɖasɩ lʋbɛ taa ɖo yɔ, pa-taa lɛlʋ kɔɔ ɛya-m se: «Kɔɔ mawɩlɩ-ŋ halɩmvalʋ, ɛnʋ lɛ Heɣa halʋ.».

eng: From his mouth comes a sharp sword that he will use to strike down the nations. He is the one who will rule them with an iron rod. And he is the one who will trample the winepress of the Almighty Gods passionate anger.
local: Sɩɣa ŋgá kɛwɛ lɔŋ yɔ, kalɩɣaɣnɩ ɛ-nɔɔ taa se ɛwabɩnɩ ajɛya taa ñɩma. Ɛ-maɣmaɣ ɛkɔŋ-wɛ ɖiyuu nɛ ɖaʋ ñɩ́ɣ́yʋ́ʋ ñɩŋgʋ. Ɛhɔyʋʋ-wɛ Ɛsɔ Đoŋ Tɩŋa Tʋ pana sɔsɔna pulone taa ɛzɩ pɔhɔyʋʋ tɩŋ weyi popuu i-pee sʋlʋm yɔ i-pee yɔ.

eng: through the middle of the citys main street. On each side of the river is the tree of life, which produces twelve crops of fruit, bearing its fruit each month. The trees leaves are for the healing of the nations.
local: Pɔɔ ŋ́gá, kekpeŋaɣ tɛtʋ taa samaɣ ɖikpeɣliye hɛkʋ taa. Tɩŋ weyi ɩhaɣ wezuu yɔ, ɩɩwɛ ke-kidiilimiŋ yɔɔ nɛ ɩlʋlʋʋ pee tam hiu nɛ naalɛ pɩnaɣ taa. Ɩlʋlʋʋ tam kʋɖʋm fenaɣ taa. Pakpakɩɣ ɩ-hatʋ nɛ pawazɩɣnɩ ajɛya taa ɛyáa pa-kʋdɔmɩŋ.

eng: He took me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a great, high mountain, and he showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God.
local: Ɛlɛ́, Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu tii mɔ-yɔɔ nɛ e-tiyiyu ɛnʋ ɛkpaɣ-m nɛ ɛkpazɩ-m pʋʋ sɔsɔʋ nakʋyʋ ñʋʋ taa. Peeɖe ɛwɩlɩ-m Ɛsɔ tɛtʋ, Yeruusalɛm ŋgʋ́ kɩlɩnɩ ɛsɔdaa Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ nɛ kitiki yɔ.

eng: He has a name written on his robe and on his thigh: King of kings and Lord of lords.
local: Pama e-toko sɔsɔʋ nɛ e-yuye pɔ-yɔɔ hɩɖɛ naɖɩyɛ. Ńɖɩ́ lɛ se: awiya taa Wiyaʋ nɛ kɩpaɣlaa taa Kɩpaɣlʋ.

eng: There will no longer be any curse. The throne of God and the Lamb will be in it, and his servants will worship him.
local: Mbʋ́ Ɛsɔ wɩlɩ kpalʋʋ yɔ, pɩɩtasɩɣ wɛʋ tɩ-taa. Ɛsɔ nɛ Heɣa pe-kewiyaɣ kpelaɣ wɛɣ tɩ-taa. Ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa kɔŋ ɛ-tɛɛ luŋuu.

eng: The city had Gods glory. Its brilliance was like a priceless jewel, like jasper that was as clear as crystal.
local: Ɛsɔ koboyaɣ kɛwɛ kɩ-taa nɛ kɩñɩlɩsɩɣ siŋŋ ɛzɩ ñɩɣlɩ́m pɩyɛ kpem yɔ. Kɩñɩlɩsaɣ ɖɔɖɔ ɛzɩ ñɩɣlɩ́m pɩyɛ nɖɩ́ ɖɩwɛ maɖʋ maɖʋ nɛ ɖɩñɩlɩzɩɣ ɛzɩ ñɩnʋʋ yɔ.

eng: Then I saw an angel standing in the sun, and he called out with a loud voice and said to all the birds flying high overhead, Come and gather for Gods great supper.
local: Mana ɖɔɖɔ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu nɔɔyʋ, ɛsɩŋ wɩsɩ taa nɛ eholuuni sumasɩ nzɩ́ sɩkʋyaɣ ɖooo ɛsɔdaa yɔ sɩtɩŋa payɩ se: «Ɩkɔɔ ikpeɣli Ɛsɔ kazandʋ sɔsɔtʋ tɔɔnaɣ ɖeɖe!.

eng: They will see his face, and his name will be on their foreheads.
local: Pɔkɔŋ naʋ ɛ-ɛsɩndaa nɛ ɛ-hɩɖɛ wɛɛ pe-kila taa.

eng: It had a great high wall with twelve gates. By the gates were twelve angels, and on the gates were written the names of the twelve tribes of Israels sons.
local: Pama koloŋa sɔsɔɔ kʋkʋyaɣ nakɛyɛ nɛ pakʋ nɔnɔsɩ sɔsɔsɩ hiu nɛ naalɛ nɛ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu sɩŋ paa ŋgá ka-taa. Paama paa ŋgá kɔ-yɔɔ hɩɖɛ naɖɩyɛ. Hɩla ana lɛ Izrɛɛlɩ ñɩma cejewee hiu nɛ naalɛ hɩla.

eng: Come and eat the flesh of kings, the flesh of generals, the flesh of the powerful, and the flesh of horses and their riders. Come and eat the flesh of all, both free and slave, both small and great.
local: Ɩkɔɔ ɩtɔɔ awiya sɔsɔna nɛ sɔɔjanaa sɔsaa nɛ ɖoŋ tɩnaa nɛ kpaŋnaŋ nɛ ɩ-yɔɔ caɣyaa nɛ mbá pɛwɛ pa-tɩ yɔɔ yɔ nɛ yomaa nɛ ajamaa nɛ ɖoŋ tɩnaa patɩŋa payɩ pa-nandʋ.».

eng: Night will be no more. They wont need the light of a lamp or the light of the sun, for the Lord God will shine on them, and they will rule forever and always.
local: Đoo ɛɛtasɩɣ yuu. Paatasɩɣ tɛɖʋʋ kanɖʋ ñalɩmɩyɛ yaa wɩsɩ ñɩnɖɛ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ ñalɩmɩyɛ kɔŋ pɔ-yɔɔ ñalʋʋ. Đɔɖɔ lɛ, pɔkɔŋ tɔɔʋ kewiyaɣ pɩnzɩ ɛgbaŋ ɛgbaŋ.

eng: There were three gates on the east, three gates on the north, three gates on the south, and three gates on the west.
local: Paakʋm nɔnɔsɩ naadozo nɛ wɩsɩ ɖɩlɩyɛ nɛ naadozo nɛ hayo kiŋ nɛ naadozo nɛ hadɛ kiŋ nɛ naadozo nɛ wɩsɩ ɖɩɖʋyɛ.

eng: Then I saw that the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies had gathered to make war against the rider on the horse and his army.
local: Mana ɖɔɖɔ kpɩnɛ nɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ awiya sɔsɔna nɛ a-sɔɔjanaa pekpeɣlaa se poyouni weyi ɛcaɣ kpaŋnɔ yɔ nɛ ɛ-sɔɔjanaa.

eng: Then he said to me, These words are trustworthy and true. The Lord, the God of the spirits of the prophets, sent his angel to show his servants what must soon take place.
local: Pʋwayɩ lɛ, Ɛsɔ tiyiyu ɛnʋ, eheyi-m se: «Tɔm tʋnɛ, tɩkɛ toovenim, tɩlakɩ ḿbʋ́. Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ weyi ɛwɩlɩɣ náyaa ɛ-lɩmaɣza yɔ, etiyi e-tiyiyu se ɛwɩlɩ ɛ-tʋmlaɖaa mbʋ́ pʋwɛɛ se pala alɩwaatʋ pazɩ taa yɔ.».

eng: But the beast was seized, along with the false prophet who had done signs in the beasts presence. The two of them were thrown alive into the fiery lake that burns with sulfur.
local: Peeɖe paba kpa kpɩnɛ nɛ weyi ɛcɛtɩɣ se ɛkɛ náyʋ nɛ ɛɛlaba hama lakasɩ ɖɩ-ɛsɩndaa yɔ. Sɩ-yɔɔ ɛtɩŋnaa nɛ ɛcɛtɩ mbá paalabɩ pɔ-yɔɔ kpɩnɛ yʋsaɣ nɛ poluŋuu ɖɩ-sɩʋ kpelaɣ tɛɛ yɔ. Pakpa-wɛ lɛ, pɔlɔ pa-naalɛ nɛ pɛ-ɛsɛ miŋ sɔsɔŋ weyi ɛ-taa wɛ tɩm nɛ ɛmʋʋ siŋŋ yɔ ɛ-taa.

eng: The city wall had twelve foundations, and on them were the twelve names of the Lambs twelve apostles.
local: Tɛtʋ ńdʋ́, ti-koloŋa kɛwɛnɩ tigila hiu nɛ naalɛ, pama a-yɔɔ Heɣa tiyiyaa hiu nɛ naalɛ hɩla.

eng: Look! Im coming soon. Favored is the one who keeps the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll.
local: Yesu tɔŋ se: «Na, pɩkazɩ pazɩ, mɔnkɔŋ! Weyi ɛɖɔŋ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ nɛ pama takayaɣ kanɛ ka-taa yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, ehiɣ ɛ-tɩ!».

eng: The rest were killed by the sword that comes from the mouth of the rider on the horse, and all the birds ate their fill of their flesh.
local: Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛcaɣ kpaŋnɔ yɔ, ɛkʋ lɛlaa mbá pɩkazaa yɔ patɩŋa payɩ nɛ sɩɣa ŋgá kalɩɣaɣnɩ ɛ-nɔɔ taa yɔ nɛ sumasɩ tɩŋa tɔɔ pa-nandʋ nɛ sɩhaɣ.

eng: I, John, am the one who heard and saw these things. When I heard and saw them, I fell down to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown them to me.
local: Ma Yohanɛɛsɩ, manɩnɩ wondu ńdʋ́ tɩ-tɔm. Manɩ tɩ-tɔm nɛ mana-tʋ lɛ, mɔntɔlɩ Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛwɩlaɣ-m-tʋ yɔ ɛ-naŋgbanzɩ tɛɛ se moluŋ ɛ-tɛɛ.

eng: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring rod with which to measure the city, its gates, and its wall.
local: Ɛsɔ tiyiyu weyi ɛyɔɔdaɣnɩ-m yɔ, ɛɖɔkɩ sika ɖaʋ kɩmaɣzʋʋ se ɛmaɣzɩ tɛtʋ nɛ tɩ-nɔnɔsɩ nɛ ti-koloŋa.

eng: But he said to me, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters, the prophets, and those who keep the words of this scroll. Worship God.
local: Ɛlɛ́, eheyi-m se: «Taala ḿbʋ́! Mɛnkɛ Ɛsɔ tʋmlaɖʋ ɛzɩ ña nɛ ña-taabalaa náyaa nɛ mbá pɔɖɔŋ takayaɣ kanɛ ka-taa tɔm yɔɔ yɔ. Ɛsɔ tɛɛ pʋwɛɛ se ŋluŋ!».

eng: Now the city was laid out as a square. Its length was the same as its width. He measured the city with the rod, and it was fifteen hundred miles. Its length and width and height were equal.
local: Tɛtʋ ńdʋ́, tɩ-ɖaɣlɩkɩŋ nɛ tɩ-walaanzɩ paawɛ kɩmaŋ. Ɛmaɣzɩ-tʋ nɛ ɖaʋ ŋ́gʋ́ lɛ, ɖaɣlɩkɩŋ wɛ kiloomɛtanaa kutokiŋ naalɛ nɛ nasɩlɛ. Walaanzɩ nɛ kʋkʋyɩŋ paawɛ kɩmaŋ ɖɔɖɔ.

eng: Then he said to me, Dont seal up the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll, because the time is near.
local: Ɛlɛ́, ɛtasɩ-m heyuu se: «Taamɛsɩ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ nɛ pama takayaɣ kanɛ ka-taa yɔ ña-taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, alɩwaatʋ ndʋ́ tɔm ńdʋ́ tɩlakɩ yɔ, tɩwɩzɩ talʋʋ.

eng: He also measured the thickness of its wall. It was two hundred sixteen feet thick, as a person—or rather, an angel—measures things.
local: Ɛmaɣzɩ koloŋa lɛ, pɩlɩɩ mɛtanaa nɩɩnʋwa nɛ hiu nɛ kagbaanzɩ. Mbʋ́ ɛyáa maɣzɩɣna yɔ, ḿbʋ́ ɛmaɣzɩnɩ-tʋ.

eng: Let those who do wrong keep doing what is wrong. Let the filthy still be filthy. Let those who are righteous keep doing what is right. Let those who are holy still be holy.
local: Weyi ɛtatʋʋzɩ yɔ, ɛwɛɛ mbʋ́ pɩtatʋʋzɩ yɔ pɩ-labʋ yɔɔ ḿbʋ́ nɛ azuluma tʋ ñɛwɛɛ ɛ-azuluma taa ḿbʋ́. Ɛlɛ́, weyi ɛtʋʋzaa yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ ɛwɛɛ mbʋ́ pʋtʋʋzaa yɔ pɩ-labʋ yɔɔ nɛ weyi ɛ́lɛ́ wɛ cɩɖɩcɩɖɩ yɔ, ɛ́lɛ́ ñɛwɛɛ ḿbʋ́!».

eng: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was pure gold, like pure glass.
local: Pama koloŋa ŋ́gá nɛ pɛɛ wena payaɣ se yaaspɩ yɔ nɛ pama tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa ɖɩsɩ nɛ sika siŋŋ nɛ pɩñɩlɩzɩɣ ɛzɩ ñɩnʋʋ yɔ.

eng: Look! Im coming soon. My reward is with me, to repay all people as their actions deserve.
local: Yesu tɔŋ se: «Na! Pɩkazɩ pazɩ, mɔnkɔŋ! Mɛnkpɛndɩɣnɩ kɩhɛyʋʋ nɛ mɛhɛyɩ paa weyi mbʋ́ ɛlaba yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ.

eng: The city walls foundations were decorated with every kind of jewel. The first foundation was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was chalcedony, and the fourth was emerald.
local: Pacanɩ ti-koloŋa tigila nɛ ñɩɣlɩ́m pɛɛ ndɩ ndɩ: wena pasɩ kiɖe tɛɛ yɔ, payaɣ-yɛ se yaaspɩ, wena patɩnɩ a-yɔɔ yɔ, payaɣ alɛ se saafiirɩ, tozo ɖeɖe lɛ, patɩnɩ wena payaɣ se agaatɩ yɔ nɛ naza ɖeɖe wena payaɣ se emeroodɩ yɔ.

eng: I am the alpha and the omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
local: Ma lɛ Nʋmɔɖɛyʋ nɛ Ɛɖɩɣyɛ, manpazɩnɩ pɩtɩŋa payɩ, mɛntɛzɩɣnɩ-pʋ. Ma ɖɔɖɔ lɛ Đɩpazɩyɛ nɛ Đɩtɛzɩyɛ.

eng: The fifth was sardonyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, and the eighth was beryl. The ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
local: kagbaanzɩ ɖeɖe wena payaɣ se oniksɩ yɔ nɛ loɖo ɖeɖe wena payaɣ se saardowanɩ yɔ nɛ lʋbɛ ɖeɖe wena payaɣ se krizooliitɩ yɔ nɛ lutozo ɖeɖe wena payaɣ se beeriilɩ yɔ nɛ nakʋ ɖeɖe wena payaɣ se toopaazɩ yɔ nɛ hiu ɖeɖe wena payaɣ se krizoopraazɩ yɔ nɛ hiu nɛ kʋɖʋm ɖeɖe wena payaɣ se iyaasɛntɩ yɔ nɛ hiu nɛ naalɛ ɖeɖe wena payaɣ alɛ se ameetiistɩ yɔ.

eng: Favored are those who wash their robes so that they may have the right of access to the tree of life and may enter the city by the gates.
local: Mbá pɔcɔtʋʋ po-tokonaa se pɩha-wɛ waɖɛ nɛ pɔtɔɔ tɩʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩhaɣ wezuu yɔ ki-pee nɛ patɩŋnɩ nɔnɔsɩ nɛ pasʋʋ tɛtʋ taa yɔ, pehiɣ pa-tɩ!.

eng: The twelve gates were twelve pearls; each one of the gates was made from a single pearl. And the citys main street was pure gold, as transparent as glass.
local: Piyele nɔnɔsɩ hiu nɛ naalɛ ńzɩ́ lɛ lɩtʋ hiu nɛ naalɛ. Paa ŋgá lɛ, palabɩ-kɛ nɛ lɩyʋʋ kʋɖʋmʋʋ koŋ. Pɔpɔ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa samaɣ ɖikpeɣliye sika siŋŋ nɛ pɩñɩlɩzɩɣ ɛzɩ ñɩnʋʋ yɔ.

eng: Outside are the dogs, the drug users and spell-casters, those who commit sexual immorality, the murderers, the idolaters, and all who love and practice deception.
local: Piyele kʋsɔɔtʋ laɖaa nɛ fadɩŋ laɖaa nɛ acɛyɛ laɖaa nɛ ɛyáa kʋyaa nɛ agɔlɩma layaa nɛ mbá pɔsɔɔlɩ cɛtʋʋ nɛ pɛcɛtɩɣ ɛyáa yɔ, paba ñɛwɛɣnɩ tɩ-wayɩ!.

eng: I didnt see a temple in the city, because its temple is the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb.
local: Ɛsɔ ɖɩɣa fɛyɩ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Kɩpaɣlʋ Ɛsɔ Đoŋ Tɩŋa Tʋ nɛ Heɣa lɛ tɩ-ɖɩɣa.

eng: I, Jesus, have sent my angel to bear witness to all of you about these things for the churches. Im the root and descendant of David, the bright morning star.
local: Ma Yesu lɛ, meheyi men-tiyiyu se ewolo ma-aŋgba taa nɛ ɛlɩzɩ-mɩ aseɣɖe ɖɩnɛ. Mɛnkɛ Daaviidi liɖe taa tʋ, ma lɛ tɩmbalʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩñɩlɩsɩɣ yɔ.».

eng: The city doesnt need the sun or the moon to shine on it, because Gods glory is its light, and its lamp is the Lamb.
local: Pɩtatasɩ pɔzʋʋ se wɩsɩ yaa fenaɣ ɛñaɣ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa. Mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ koboyaɣ ñalɩmɩyɛ ñalɩɣ, tɩ-kanɖʋ lɛ Heɣa.

eng: The Spirit and the bride say, Come! Let the one who hears say, Come! And let the one who is thirsty come! Let the one who wishes receive life-giving water as a gift.
local: Fezuu Kiɖeɖeu nɛ Halɩmvalʋ pɔtɔŋ se: «Kɔɔ!» Weyi ɛnɩɣ yɔ, ɛtɔ ɖɔɖɔ se: «Kɔɔ!» Weyi lɔkɔtʋ ɖɔkɩ-ɩ yɔ, ɛkɔɔ: weyi e-liu wɛ wezuu lɩm yɔ, ecozi-pʋ falaa!.

eng: The nations will walk by its light, and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it.
local: Ajɛya taa ñɩma kɔŋ ɖɔm tɩ-ñalɩmɩyɛ taa nɛ kedeŋa yɔɔ awiya ñowoni pa-ñɩm tɩ-taa.

eng: Now I bear witness to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll: If anyone adds to them, God will add to that person the plagues that are written in this scroll.
local: Mankaŋ paa weyi ɛnɩɣ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ nɛ pama-tʋ takayaɣ kanɛ ka-taa yɔ ɛ-ñʋʋ taa se: yee nɔɔyʋ ɛsɔzɩ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ sɔzʋʋ mbʋ́ pɩtɛŋnɩ pʋdʋ yɔ pʋ-yɔɔ kʋñɔmɩŋ weyi pama ɩ-tɔm takayaɣ kanɛ ka-taa yɔ.

eng: Its gates will never be shut by day, and there will be no night there.
local: Paakaɣ ɖɩɣʋ tɩ-nɔnɔsɩ, mbʋ́ pʋyɔɔ yɔ, ɖoó ɛɛkaɣ yuu tɩ-taa.

eng: If anyone takes away from the words of this scroll of prophecy, God will take away that persons share in the tree of life and the holy city, which are described in this scroll.
local: Đɔɖɔ lɛ, yee nɔɔyʋ ɛpasɩ tɔm ndʋ́ tɩlɩnɩ Ɛsɔ cɔlɔ nɛ pama-tʋ takayaɣ kanɛ ka-taa yɔ tɩ-yɔɔ yɔ, Ɛsɔ pasɩɣ tɩʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩhaɣ wezuu yɔ kʋ-hɔɔlʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩkɛ pʋdʋ ñɩŋgʋ yɔ nɛ ɛ-tɛtʋ hɔɔlʋʋ ŋgʋ́ kɩkɛ pʋdʋ ñɩŋgʋ yɔ pɔ-yɔɔ. Tɩʋ ŋ́gʋ́ nɛ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ pɔ-tɔm pama takayaɣ kanɛ ka-taa.

eng: They will bring the glory and honor of the nations into it.
local: Pɔkɔŋ wonaʋ ñɩm nɛ mbʋ́ pʋɖʋʋ ajɛya hɩɖɛ yɔ tɩ-taa.

eng: The one who bears witness to these things says, Yes, Im coming soon. Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!.
local: Yesu weyi ɛtɔm se tɔm tʋnɛ tɩkɛ toovenim yɔ, ɛtɔm se: «Ɛɛ, pɩkazɩ pazɩ, mɔnkɔŋ.» Pɩla ḿbʋ́! Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu, kɔɔ!.

eng: Nothing unclean will ever enter it, nor anyone who does what is vile and deceitful, but only those who are registered in the Lambs scroll of life.
local: Azuluma pʋyʋ yaa weyi ɛlakɩ kʋsɔɔtʋ yɔ yaa weyi ɛcɛtɩɣ yɔ, ɛɛkaɣ sʋʋ tɛtʋ ńdʋ́ tɩ-taa. Ɛlɛ́, mbá pama pa-hɩla wezuu takayaɣ ŋgá kɛkɛ Heɣa ñɩŋga yɔ ka-taa yɔ, pe-ɖeke koŋ pasʋʋna.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all.
local: Kɩpaɣlʋ Yesu ɛla mɩtɩŋa payɩ lootiye!.
